name: A certain man
bio: During his childhood, Varys was the victim of a certain man, a sorcerer who gelded him in a demonic ritual. The sorcerer's true name is not mentioned. In the television adapation Game of Thrones, the sorcerer is portrayed by Harold James McMullan. When Varys was an orphan boy, he was apprenticed to a traveling folly. The folly's master owned a fat little cog, and they sailed up and down the narrow sea performing in all the Free Cities, and from time to time in Oldtown and King’s Landing. One day at Myr, a certain man came to the folly. After the performance, he made an offer for young Varys that his master found too tempting to refuse. As he was sold, Varys was in terror, afraid that the man meant to use him as he had heard men used small boys. However, the only part of Varys the man had need of was his manhood. The man gave Varys a potion that made him powerless to move or speak, but did nothing to dull his senses as his manhood was cut off at the stem with a long hooked blade, then burnt in a brazier in a bloodmagic ritual while the sorcerer chanted. Varys saw the flames turn blue and heard a voice within them answer the man's call, but he did not understand the words they spoke. With the ritual complete, the man had no further use for Varys and tossed him out on the streets. The mummers' ship had sailed away by then, so Varys asked the man what he should do. The sorcerer replied that he supposed Varys should die. To spite him, Varys vowed to live.

name: Abelar Hightower
bio: Abelar Hightower was a knight from House Hightower during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Abelar took part in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC, where he challenged Prince Valarr_Targaryen, one of the tourney champions, in the first joust. Abelar and Valarr both broke lances against each other on the first tilt, but on the second, the prince knocked Abelar off his horse with a lance blow to the chest. Abelar's squire had to call two servingmen to carry the dazed knight off the field and back to his tent, the first of Valarr's nine victories against "undistinguished foes".

name: Abelon
bio: Abelon was an archmaester of the Citadel and the author of When Women Ruled: Ladies of the Aftermath.

name: Addam of Duskendale
bio: Addam was a merchant of Duskendale and the subject of the True Account of Addam of Duskendale's Journeys. Addam visited the cities of Bayasabhad, Samyriana, and Kayakayanaya.

name: Addam Frey
bio: Addam Frey was a knight from House Frey during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. He was a cousin to the daughter of Lord_Frey of the Crossing. Addam was considered a promising lance in his youth. Addam and his squire attended the marriage of his cousin to Lord Ambrose_Butterwell. He participated in the wedding tourney at Whitewalls, where he was unhorsed by Ser Uthor_Underleaf in his second joust.

name: Addam Hightower
bio: Addam Hightower was Lord of the Hightower and head of House Hightower during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. In 10 AC, during the First Dornish War, Lord Fowler led an army into the Reach. By the time the marcher lords realized the threat, a dozen villages had already been burned and Nightsong had been captured. When word reached Oldtown, Addam was sent by his father with a strong force to retake the castle. When Addam arrived at Nightsong, Lord Fowler had already put the castle to the torch and the garrison to the sword, taking Lord Caron, his wife, and their children back to Dorne as captives. Worse, while Addam had marched from Oldtown to Nightsong, a second Dornish force had come down to attack the city, burning fields, farms and villages nearby, killing Addam's younger brother Garmon_Hightower in battle. Instead of pursuing the Dornish host that had taken the Carons, Addam returned to Oldtown to relieve the city, only to find the Dornish host gone upon their arrival. Soon after Lord Manfred_Hightower_(Aegon's_Conquest) died, and Addam succeeded his father as the Lord of the High Tower. Addam was eventually succeeded by his son, Manfred_Hightower_(son_of_Addam).

name: Addam Marbrand
bio: Addam Marbrand is a knight of House Marbrand, and the son and heir of Lord Damon_Marbrand of Ashemark. He is one of the chief knights in the service of Tywin_Lannister, Lord of Casterly Rock. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Addam is portrayed by B.J. Hogg. During his youth, Addam was a page at Casterly Rock, and a childhood friend of Ser Jaime_Lannister.

name: Addam Osgrey
bio: Addam Osgrey was a squire from House Osgrey during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He was the third son of Ser Eustace_Osgrey of Standfast. Addam served as a page and then as a squire at Coldmoat, the seat of Lord Wyman_Webber, his father's friend. During his time at Coldmoat, a love grew between Addam and Lord Wyman's daughter, Rohanne_Webber, two years younger than him. Addam would pick blackberries from the thicket on Standfast's hill for Rohanne, and they would eat them with cream. However, their innocent romance never went beyond a few kisses. Addam's father, Ser Eustace, came to Coldmoat to propose a marriage between the two children. Lord Wyman refused him, however, possibly due to Eustace's growing traitorous leanings towards the Blackfyre cause. Though his refusal was polite, as Eustace left he heard Lord Wyman laughing with his castellan, Ser Lucas_Inchfield. Lord Wyman then had Rohanne married to another of his squires, while Ser Eustace refused to ever return to Coldmoat for thirteen years. During the First Blackfyre Rebellion in 196 AC, Ser Eustace fought on the side of the pretender Daemon_I_Blackfyre. His three sons went to war with him, including Addam, twelve years old, as his father's squire. During the Battle of the Redgrass Field, Addam was trying to protect his wounded brother, Ser Harrold_Osgrey, when a knight of House Smallwood cut off his arm with an ax. Addam's brothers also died in the battle, and afterwards his sister Alysanne_Osgrey was sent to King's Landing as a hostage for Eustace's pardon. After Eustace brought home the bones of his sons and allowed Alysanne to be taken away, Addam's mother committed suicide by throwing herself from the top of Standfast. Eustace buried his wife and sons at Standfast by the blackberry bushes his sons had loved to pick. Lord Wyman Webber also fought in the battle, on the side of House Targaryen, and his squire (Rohanne's husband) was slain, making her a widow at the age of ten. However, Rohanne mourned only for Addam, not for her husband; she blamed Ser Eustace for his death, and held a grudge against the knight thereafter. In 211 AC, due to growing tensions with Lady Rohanne Webber, Ser Eustace Osgrey took on Ser Duncan_the_Tall as a sworn sword. Duncan's squire, Aegon_V_Targaryen, was outfitted in Addam's old squire's clothing. On Ser Eustace's behalf, Duncan went to Coldmoat to pay a blood price for his fellow sworn sword's assault on Wolmer, and to request that Lady Rohanne take down the dam on the Chequy Water that blocked the stream from reaching Osgrey lands. However, she refused both petitions. Duncan, having heard the story of Addam and Rohanne's childhood fondness from Ser Eustace, made the mistake of appealing to Rohanne in Addam's name, for which she struck him in fury. Duncan eventually defeated Rohanne's Lucas_Inchfield in a trial by combat in the Chequy Water, and afterwards Eustace and Rohanne reconciled. She visited Addam's grave at Standfast, and when she knelt before the blackberries and began to weep, Eustace comforted her. They spent the night talking of their families, and married the next day, which allowed Rohanne to keep ruling Coldmoat per her father's will.

name: Addam Rivers
bio: Addam Rivers was a knight and a bastard of the Riverlands who claimed the title of River King or King of the Trident in the generations after the riverlands were conquered by the Storm King Arlan_III_Durrandon. Addam had a brief, inglorious reign, before being put down by the might of Storm's End.

name: Addam Velaryon
bio: Addam Velaryon, born Addam of Hull, was a legitimized bastard from Driftmark of dragonseed descent, like his younger brother Alyn_Velaryon. During the Dance of the Dragons, Addam became a knight and the heir to House Velaryon of Driftmark. He was a dragonrider whose dragon was Seasmoke. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Addam is portrayed by Clinton Liberty. According to Archmaester Gyldayn, Addam's origins remain a matter of dispute among historians to this day. His mother Marilda claimed he and his brother Alyn_Velaryon were fathered by Ser Laenor_Velaryon - a fact that many found remarkable due to Laenor's known sexual preference for men. The court fool, Mushroom, claimed that they were fathered by Laenor's father, Lord Corlys_Velaryon, but kept unacknowledged and far from court in order to not offend Corlys's fiery-tempered wife, Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon). Addam and Alyn grew up in the shipyard of Marilda's father, and the boys served on her trading cog, Mouse.

name: Addam Whitehead
bio: Addam Whitehead is a knight from House Whitehead and the son and heir of old Lord Whitehead.

name: Addison Hill
bio: Addison Hill, known as the Bastard of Cornfield, was a bastard knight from House Swyft and a Lord Commander of the Kingsguard. In 10 AC, Ser Addison was chosen by Queen Visenya_Targaryen to be one of the first knights of the Kingsguard when the order was founded by King Aegon_I_Targaryen. He would serve his king with honor and valor until the end of his life. Despite his bastard origin, Addison eventually rose to become Lord Commander of the Kingsguard. It is unknown when it happened, however. It could have been during the reign of Aegon I or during one of his sons's, Aenys_I_Targaryen and Maegor_I_Targaryen.

name: Adrack Humble
bio: Adrack Humble is an ironborn raider and a member of House Humble. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Adrack is portrayed by Jody Halse. Adrack lost one of his arms in battle. He reportedly has a rock wife and three salt wives, three of whom were big with child when Adrack left the Iron Islands.

name: Adrian Redfort
bio: Adrian Redfort was a knight from House Redfort and a member of Rhaenyra_Targaryen's Queensguard. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Adrian is portrayed by Barry Sloane. Ser Adrian was inducted into the Queensguard after Rhaenyra seized King's Landing and commanded Lord Commander Lorent_Marbrand to recruit six knights to serve alongside him. Ser Adrian was killed in defense of Rhaenyra by the men of Ser Alfred_Broome at Dragonstone. He was stabbed in the back by a spear.

name: Adrian Tarbeck
bio: Adrian Tarbeck was a knight from House Tarbeck who supported King Aegon_II_Targaryen during the Dance of the Dragons. During the Dance of the Dragons, Adrian took command of the Lannister host after Lord Jason_Lannister fell in the Battle at the Red Fork. Thrice repelled by the rivermen, Adrian and a hundred handpicked knights stripped off their heavy armor and swam across the river, upstream of the battle. Adrian then led the knights in circling around to take Lord Tristan_Vance's lines in the rear, personally slaying the riverlord. The ranks of the rivermen shattered, allowing the westermen to cross into the riverlands. The Lannister host continued to march east towards Harrenhal, winning victories under Adrian's command. The westermen were briefly checked at Acorn Hall by Lord Joseth_Smallwood and Lord Petyr_Piper, but Lord Piper died in battle and Lord Smallwood retreated back to his castle. Three days later, the rivermen regrouped under a hedge knight, Ser Harry_Penny, and gave battle to the westermen again. While the Lannister host once again prevailed, Ser Adrian and Ser Harry killed each other in the fighting. With Adrian's death, command of the westermen passed to the aged and wounded Lord Humfrey_Lefford.

name: Adrian Thorne
bio: Adrian Thorne was a knight from House Thorne who served as Commander of the City Watch during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Adrian was the captain of the Lion Gate during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 135 AC, he was the only one of Commander Lucas_Leygood's seven captains not accused of involvement in the plot against House Rogare. After the death of Ser Lucas, he was chosen as the new Commander of the City Watch.

name: Aegon Ambrose
bio: Aegon Ambrose was a knight of House Ambrose and a member of the Warrior's Sons at the start of the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. In 42 AC, Ser Aegon stationed at the King's Landing chapter of the Warrior's Sons, based in the Sept of Remembrance. He fought in the trial of seven in which seven of the Warrior's Sons faced King Maegor_I_Targaryen and his six champions. Aegon died during the fight.

name: Aegon Blackfyre
bio: Aegon Blackfyre was the eldest son of Daemon_I_Blackfyre and the twin of Aemon_Blackfyre. Aegon was Daemon's heir. Aegon was the oldest of the children of Daemon_I_Blackfyre and Rohanne_of_Tyrosh of Tyrosh. He had a younger twin brother, Aemon_Blackfyre, and five other younger brothers: Daemon_II_Blackfyre, Haegon, Aenys_Blackfyre, and two whose names are currently unknown. He also had at least two sisters. The twins often bullied their younger brother Daemon, and Daemon's friend, Alyn_Cockshaw. Daemon once had a prophetic dream about the deaths of Aegon and Aemon, but the disbelieving twins only laughed at their brother. During the First Blackfyre Rebellion, when Aegon was twelve years old, he served as a squire to his father. Aegon was killed at the Battle of the Redgrass Field by the arrows of Brynden_Rivers and his Raven's Teeth. Daemon remained by his fallen son's side, and was also struck down by arrows. Aegon's twin, Aemon, took up the sword Blackfyre from his dying father, but was then slain by the archers as well.

name: Aegon Frey
bio: Aegon Frey, called Jinglebell, is the lackwit son of Ser Stevron_Frey and Jeyne_Lydden, and the fool of the Twins. Lord Walder_Frey claims to have hosted three kings at the Twins, but Jinglebell was not introduced to them.

name: Aegon Frey
bio: Aegon Frey, better known as Aegon Bloodborn, is the eldest son of Ser Aenys_Frey and Tyana_Wylde.

name: Aegon I Targaryen
bio: Aegon I Targaryen, also known as Aegon the Conqueror and Aegon the Dragon, was the first Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and king on the Iron Throne, having conquered six of the Seven Kingdoms during the Conquest. The dragonlord was the founder of the ruling Targaryen dynasty of Westeros. Aegon was born on Dragonstone in 27 BC to Lord Aerion_Targaryen_(son_of_Daemion) and Lady Valaena_Velaryon. He had an older sister, Visenya_Targaryen, and a younger sister, Rhaenys_Targaryen. According to Valyrian and Targaryen tradition, Aegon married within the family, to keep the bloodlines pure; however, instead of taking only one sister to wife, as was expected, Aegon married both of them. It is said that he married Visenya out of duty, and Rhaenys out of desire. The taking of a second wife was unusual, but not without precedent among the dragonlords. Aegon had claimed the dragon Balerion for his own prior to his marriage. When Pentos and Tyrosh asked Aegon for his aid in their struggle against Volantis, Aegon, Lord of Dragonstone, flew to Pentos atop of Balerion to meet with the Prince of Pentos and the city's magisters. Aegon next flew to Lys, where he burned a Volantene fleet before it could attempt to invade the city. Shortly after, Aegon returned to Dragonstone.

name: Aegon II Targaryen
bio: Aegon II Targaryen, also known as Aegon the Elder, was the sixth Targaryen king to sit the Iron Throne, succeeding his father, Viserys_I_Targaryen, as Lord of the Seven Kingdoms. His ascent was disputed by his older half-sister, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, who had been their father's designated heir. Aegon and Rhaenyra fought for the throne in the civil war the singers called the Dance of the Dragons, in which both siblings perished. Aegon was married to his younger sister, Helaena_Targaryen, and had three children by her, though none reached adulthood. His dragon was Sunfyre, also known as "the Golden". During the Dance, Aegon took a three-headed golden dragon for his personal sigil instead of the red three-headed Targaryen dragon, indicating his mount. Aegon's supporters used his personal heraldry as their battle-flag. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Aegon is portrayed by Tom Glynn-Carney, Ty Tennant (young), and an uncredited child actor (infant).

name: Aegon III Targaryen
bio: Aegon III Targaryen, also known as Aegon the Younger, and later as Aegon the Unlucky, Aegon the Unhappy, the Broken King, and most famously as Aegon the Dragonbane, was the seventh Targaryen king to sit the Iron Throne. He succeeded his uncle, Aegon_II_Targaryen, at the conclusion of the Dance of the Dragons, which saw the victorious supporters of his late mother Rhaenyra_Targaryen install him on the throne. When he was a child, his dragon was Stormcloud. Aegon kept the kingdoms united following the divisions of the civil war, with the aid of his brother, Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, whom he eventually named Hand of the King. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Aegon is portrayed by the uncredited actors Jake and Rory Heard (child).

name: Aegon IV Targaryen
bio: Aegon IV Targaryen, known as Aegon the Unworthy, was the eleventh Targaryen to sit the Iron Throne, and is considered to be one of the worst Targaryen kings. He sired numerous bastards, legitimizing them on his deathbed, an act that led to five Blackfyre Rebellions. Aegon was the eldest son of Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, and his wife, Larra_Rogare. He was born during the third moon of the year 135 AC in King's Landing, less than a fortnight after his aunt, Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon), rode her dragon, Morning, for the first time. Aegon grew up during the rule of his uncle, King Aegon_III_Targaryen, after whom he was named. Septon Bernard anointed him with the seven oils in the royal sept, and the bells of the city rang in celebration of his birth and following the custom a dragon egg was placed in his cradle. His grandfather, Lysandro_Rogare even declared a day of feasting in Lys in his honor. In 139 AC, his mother Larra returned to her native Lys, where she died in 145 AC. In 153 AC, Viserys had Aegon married to his sister, Princess Naerys_Targaryen, with Aegon III's blessing. Their marriage was an unhappy one. Naerys had a better relationship with her second brother, Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II), for he knew how to make her laugh and had something of the piety she had, while Aegon did not. When Aegon and Naerys were wed in the early months of 153 AC, Aemon quarreled with Aegon at the wedding feast, and Naerys wept during the bedding. Aegon's first child with Naerys, Prince Daeron_II_Targaryen, was born on the last day of 153 AC. As a young prince, Aegon accompanied his cousin, King Daeron_I_Targaryen, in his conquest of Dorne, as did Aegon's younger brother, Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II), who had joined the Kingsguard. After the submission of Sunspear, Aegon was tasked by King Daeron I to escort the highborn Dornish hostages to King's Landing. One of these hostages, Lady Cassella_Vaith, became one of Aegon's mistresses for a few years. When Baelor_I_Targaryen became king after Daeron's death in Dorne, Baelor dissolved his marriage to his sister-wife, Daena_Targaryen, and imprisoned her and his other sisters in comfortable confinement of the Maidenvault so the sight of her would not tempt him or the men of his court to carnal thoughts. That did not stop Daena from escaping her confinement on three occasions, one time with the help of her cousin, Aegon. Daena became pregnant, refused to say who the father was, and was dubbed "Daena the Defiant" for her willfulness. In time she gave birth to Aegon's son whom she named Daemon_I_Blackfyre. Daemon was not the first of Aegon's bastards, however. Aegon had already acknowledged multiple children by two of his four mistresses, and more children would follow. In his marriage, however, childbirth went less easily. During the years of their marriage, Princess Naerys had several difficult pregnancies. In 161 AC, Naerys gave birth to twins who died shortly after the birth. This caused the new king, Baelor_I_Targaryen, to fast for a moon's turn. Because Naerys nearly died during this pregnancy, King Baelor sent Prince Aegon to Braavos on a diplomatic mission. Accounts at the time suggest it was an excuse to make certain Aegon left Naerys alone as she recovered from the failed childbirth. In 172 AC, after another troubled labor, Naerys gave birth to a daughter named Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_IV). Daenerys' twin brother, however, was stillborn. Eventually Baelor starved himself to death during one of his pious fasts, and Daena and her sisters were passed over in the succession. Aegon's father Viserys became king, but he only ruled for a year before passing away himself. So, in 172 AC, the throne passed to Aegon, the Fourth of His Name. Some historians suspect that the sudden death of Aegon's father, King Viserys, was not natural and that his successor and son Aegon poisoned him in order to hasten his inheritance to the Iron Throne.

name: Aegon V Targaryen
bio: Aegon V Targaryen was a Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and the fifteenth Targaryen to sit the Iron Throne as King of the Andals, the Rhoynar, and the First Men. In his youth, he went by the nickname Egg and squired for Ser Duncan_the_Tall. Aegon became king after a Great Council bypassed a number of candidates earlier in the line of succession, and after his elder brother, Maester Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I), refused the throne. As the fourth son of Maekar_I_Targaryen, himself a fourth son, Aegon was called Aegon the Unlikely. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Aegon is portrayed by Dexter Sol Ansell. Aegon was born the fourth son and fifth child of Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen and his wife, Dyanna_Dayne. Aegon served at court in King's Landing for two years as a page, sometimes attending to the small council. Aegon owned a dragon egg, which was colored with green and white swirls and had been placed in his cradle with him when he was a babe. Aegon and his elder brother, Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I), hoped their eggs would hatch so they could become dragonriders. Aegon kept his egg at Summerhall. The brothers recreated the Battle of the Redgrass Field with painted soldiers on Maester Melaquin's table. Aegon loathed his brother, the cruel and sadistic Aerion_Targaryen, who threw Aegon's pet cat down a well. Aerion once visited Aegon in his bedroom during the dead of night, put a knife to Aegon's privates, and joked about removing his genitals so he would become a girl whom Aerion could marry. Once Aegon's sister Rhae_Targaryen slipped a love potion into his drink so that he would marry her and not their sister Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Maekar_I), to whom he might have been betrothed.[N 1] Aegon did not wish to marry, instead dreaming of becoming a knight of the Kingsguard, who are sworn not to wed. In 219 AC, Aegon served alongside his father Prince Maekar and elder brother Prince Aerion in the Third Blackfyre Rebellion, where he demonstrated great courage. Aegon married Lady Betha_Blackwood in 220 AC. The marriage at the time provoked no opposition as Aegon was very low in the line of succession.  They married for love and eventually had five children together. Aegon named his first-born son Duncan_Targaryen, presumably to honor his friend, Ser Duncan_the_Tall (however, when asked about it, George R. R. Martin refused to answer). In 221 AC, Aegon's uncle, King Aerys_I_Targaryen died with no issue and was followed on the Iron Throne by his last living brother, Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen. Aegon and his brothers were summoned to court by King Maekar. According to a semi-canon source, Aegon participated in the suppression of the Peake Uprising in 233 AC, with Tion_Lannister as his squire. The prince knighted the mortally wounded Tywald_Lannister, Tion's twin, during the battle. King Maekar I was also killed at Starpike during the rebellion. With Maekar dead, it was unclear who should be king since two of Aegon's older brothers had died before his father. Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) left a feeble-witted daughter named Vaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daeron) and Aerion_Targaryen an Maegor_Targaryen_(son_of_Aerion), so a Great Council was called to choose the king. Aegon seemed the logical choice but many lords considered him "half a peasant" due to his youth among the smallfolk. The council approached Aegon's older brother, Maester Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I), but he refused, stating the crown should be given to Aegon.

name: Aegon Targaryen
bio: Aegon Targaryen was a member of House Targaryen during the Century of Blood, the son of Lord Gaemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenar) and Lady Daenys Targaryen of Dragonstone. Aegon married his sister, Elaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Gaemon), and together they had two sons: Maegon_Targaryen and Aerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon). Aegon and Elaena also had a younger sister who married a petty lord. Aegon and Elaena inherited the lordship of Dragonstone from their father and mother, they jointly ruled Dragonstone and were succeeded by Maegon.

name: Aegon Targaryen
bio: Prince Aegon Targaryen was a member of House Targaryen and was the second child, and eldest son and heir, of King Aenys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alyssa_Velaryon. After his father's death, he became a dragonrider and rode his late father's dragon, Quicksilver. He became known to history as Aegon the Uncrowned after his Maegor_I_Targaryen usurped the Throne.

name: Aegon Targaryen
bio: Prince Aegon Targaryen was the firstborn child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. Aegon was born too early. He was named by Alysanne in honor of both her grandfather Aegon_I_Targaryen and her deceased and much mourned elder brother Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I). The entire realm rejoiced at the child's birth, most of all his father King Jaehaerys. The joy was short-lived, however, as Aegon died three days after his birth. Alysanne was so deeply affected by her son's death that the maesters feared for her life as well. Alysanne blamed her son's early birth and subsequent death on the women who had attacked her at Jonquil's Pool at Maidenpool, believing that if she had she been allowed to bathe in the pool and its healing waters, Aegon would have lived.

name: Aegon Targaryen
bio: Prince Aegon Targaryen was the youngest child of Prince Baelon Targaryen and Princess Alyssa_Targaryen. He was named after Aegon_I_Targaryen. His older brothers were King Viserys_I_Targaryen and Prince Daemon_Targaryen. Alyssa never recovered from the difficult labor, and perished the same year of Aegon's birth. Aegon died the following year, just short of his first nameday.

name: Aegon Targaryen
bio: Prince Aegon Targaryen was the thirdborn son of King Aerys_II_Targaryen and Queen Rhaella_Targaryen. Prince Aegon was born in 272 AC, two months premature. As his brother Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Aerys_II) before him, Aegon did not live long. He died in 273 AC.

name: Aegon Targaryen
bio: Prince Aegon Targaryen was the second child and only son of Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen and Princess Elia_Martell.

name: Aegor Rivers
bio: Aegor Rivers, frequently called Bittersteel, was one of the Great Bastards, a son of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen by his fifth mistress, Lady Barba_Bracken. The knight was a major supporter of the pretender king Daemon_I_Blackfyre and the later Blackfyre pretenders. Aegor was the founder of the Golden Company, a famed group of exiled sellswords. In his personal arms, he combined the red stallion of House Bracken with black dragon wings from House Blackfyre, on a golden field. In 172 AC, Aegor was born at King's Landing to the fifth of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen's mistresses, Barba_Bracken. Aegor was born only a fortnight before Aegon's wife, Queen Naerys_Targaryen, nearly died in childbirth. With Naerys lingering near death, Barba's father, the Hand of the King, began to speak openly of Barba marrying King Aegon IV. However, his words became a scandal when Queen Naerys recovered her health. Naerys's brother, Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II), and her son, Prince Daeron_II_Targaryen, forced King Aegon to send both Barba and her bastard son away from court. Aegor was raised at Stone Hedge in the riverlands by the Brackens. King Aegon IV was visiting Aegor at Stone Hedge in 177 AC when he met Barba's younger sister, Bethany_Bracken, and took her for his seventh mistress. However, Bethany's affair with Ser Terrence_Toyne, a Kingsguard, soon resulted in the execution of Terrence, Bethany, and her father, Aegor's grandfather. The angry Aegor reserved particular loathing for his half-brother Brynden_Rivers, whose mother, Melissa_Blackwood, had replaced his own as their father's favorite mistress. Aegor loathed Brynden, who was known as Bloodraven, even further when Shiera_Seastar, their beautiful half-sister and a fellow Great Bastard, chose Brynden over him. Their other half-brother, Daemon_I_Blackfyre, agreed to wed his daughter Calla_Blackfyre to Aegor; however, it is unknown if and when the marriage took place. Aegor often urged Daemon to press his own claim to the Iron Throne.

name: Aelinor Penrose
bio: Queen Aelinor Penrose was a noblewoman from House Penrose and the queen consort of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Aelinor was born in her family's seat, Parchments. Aelinor was married to her cousin, Prince Aerys_I_Targaryen. He never showed any interest in getting her with child, instead shunning her bed. Rumor had it that Aerys never even consummated the marriage. When Aerys ascended to the throne as King Aerys I in 209 AC, his small council urged the king to put Aelinor aside and marry another, hoping that Aerys's behavior was caused by dislike of her. However, King Aerys would not listen, and so Aelinor remained his wife. During the reign of Aerys I, it was said that Queen Aelinor would pray at the Great Sept of Baelor daily, beseeching the Mother Above to bless her with a child. Aelinor and Aerys had no children.

name: Aelor Targaryen
bio: Prince Aelor Targaryen was the son of Prince Rhaegel_Targaryen and Lady Alys_Arryn_(wife_of_Rhaegel). He had a twin sister, Aelora_Targaryen, to whom he was married, and a younger sister, Daenora_Targaryen. When Duncan_the_Tall first encounters Aelor's cousin, Prince Aerion_Targaryen, at Ashford Meadow, he initially wonders if Aerion might be Prince Valarr_Targaryen, Matarys_Targaryen, or one of their cousins, as he was aware that three of King Daeron_II_Targaryen's sons had sons of their own who were youths around his own age. Aelor, the only son of Daeron's third son Prince Rhaegel_Targaryen, was one of these sons. During the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen, some in the realm suspected the King's Hand Brynden_Rivers held the true power. Septon Sefton_Staunton at Coldmoat lamented that none in House Targaryen could stand against him, including Aelor, who was only a child at the time. Ser Kyle the Cat too distrusted Bloodraven and believed Aerys' eventual death would result in war between Bloodraven and  Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen, who he mistook as heir. Ser Maynard_Plumm reminded him that Rhaegel and his children came before Maekar in the line of succession. Ser Kyle dismissed all three, claiming that Aelor and his twin Aelora were as good as dead, doomed to fall victim to Bloodraven or Maekar. Aelor's cousin, Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen, fiercely insisted that his father would never harm Rhaegel or any of his children. After the death of Aelor's father in 215 AC, Aelor was named Prince of Dragonstone and heir to the throne by his uncle, King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Aelor was married to his twin sister, Princess Aelora_Targaryen. He died in 217 AC at the hand of his sister-wife through a freak accident, which left her mad with grief.

name: Aelora Targaryen
bio: Princess Aelora Targaryen was the daughter of Prince Rhaegel_Targaryen and Lady Alys_Arryn_(wife_of_Rhaegel). She had a twin brother, Aelor_Targaryen, to whom she was married, and a younger sister, Daenora_Targaryen. Aelora was married to her twin brother, Prince Aelor_Targaryen. When their father, Prince Rhaegel_Targaryen, died in 215 AC, Aelor was named Prince of Dragonstone and heir to the Iron Throne by their uncle, King Aerys_I_Targaryen. As Rhaegel was often disregarded on account of his mental fragility, both he and his children were sometimes forgotten as heirs to Aerys who came ahead of Aelora's uncle, Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen. Those more knowledgeable of the true line of succession were quick to point out that Rhaegel and his children all preceded Maekar. During the reign of Aerys I, his Hand of the King, Lord Brynden_Rivers, was the subject of much mistrust. Bloodraven's detractors worried that nobody in House Targaryen was fit to stand up to him This anti-Bloodraven sentiment ran so deep that some even suggested Rhaegel and his children might be killed by his magic. Even Prince Maekar was not free of such suspicions, though his youngest son, Aegon_V_Targaryen, was quick to protest that Maekar loved his brother and would never harm him or his children. In 217 AC, Aelora accidentally caused Aelor's death in a "grotesque mishap", which left her mad with grief. Following Aelor's death, King Aerys recognized Aelora as his new heir to the throne. Some time later, three men known as The_Rat,_the_Hawk,_and_the_Pig attacked Aelora at a masked ball, after which she committed suicide.

name: Aelyx Targaryen
bio: Aelyx Targaryen was a member of House Targaryen during the Century of Blood, the first son of Lord Aerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon) of Dragonstone. He had two younger brothers, Baelon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aerys) and Daemion_Targaryen. Aelyx inherited the lordship of Dragonstone from his father and was himself succeeded by his brother Baelon.

name: Aemma Arryn
bio: Aemma Arryn was the queen consort and first wife of King Viserys_I_Targaryen, and a granddaughter of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and his sister-wife, Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Aemma is portrayed by Sian Brooke. Aemma was the fifth child of Lord Rodrik_Arryn, and the only one by his second wife, Princess Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I), who was the daughter of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and his sister-wife Alysanne_Targaryen. Aemma had four elder half-siblings from her father's first marriage, two unnamed half-brothers and her half-sisters Elys_Arryn and Amanda_Arryn. In 82 AC, Daella went into labor a fortnight too early, and had a long and troubled labor. Although Aemma was born healthy, Daella became ill and died from childbed fever within a day of her birth. In 93 AC, at the age of eleven, Aemma married Prince Viserys_I_Targaryen, her cousin. The marriage was not consummated until Aemma flowered, two years later. In the early years of her marriage, Aemma became pregnant several times, but suffered multiple miscarriages and gave birth to a son who died in the cradle. Some maesters believed that these difficulties were because Aemma had been married and bedded too young. However, in 97 AC, Aemma gave birth to a healthy daughter, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen. Aemma and Viserys both adored their only living child. Viserys's father and King Jaehaerys's heir, Prince Baelon Targaryen, unexpectedly passed from an illness in 101 AC, leading to a succession crisis. During the Great Council of 101 AC, Viserys was chosen as King Jaehaerys's new heir, over a female cousin and her children. Upon her grandfather Jaehaerys's death in 103 AC, Aemma became Viserys's queen consort. Questions arose regarding the inheritance of the throne, with many opposing Viserys's younger brother and potential heir, the roguish Prince Daemon_Targaryen. However, when asked about the subject, Viserys only answered that he expected Aemma would soon give him a son. In early 105 AC, Aemma became pregnant once more, which Viserys joyously announced to the court and small council. Late in the year, she gave birth to a son, Prince Baelon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I). Unfortunately Aemma died during the birth, and her son died a day later, leaving Viserys bereft.

name: Aemon Blackfyre
bio: Aemon Blackfyre was the second son of Daemon_I_Blackfyre. He was Aegon_Blackfyre's younger twin brother. Aemon was the secondborn of the children of Daemon_I_Blackfyre and Rohanne_of_Tyrosh of Tyrosh. He had an older twin brother, Aegon_Blackfyre, and five younger brothers: Daemon_II_Blackfyre, Haegon, Aenys_Blackfyre, and two whose names are currently unknown. He also had at least two sisters. The twins often bullied their younger brother Daemon, and Daemon's friend, Alyn_Cockshaw. Daemon once had a prophetic dream about the deaths of Aemon and Aegon, but the disbelieving twins only laughed at their brother. During the First Blackfyre Rebellion, when Aemon was twelve years old, he served as a squire to his father. At the Battle of the Redgrass Field, Aemon's twin, Aegon, was killed by the arrows of Brynden_Rivers and his Raven's Teeth. Unwilling to leave him, Daemon remained by his fallen son's side, and was also struck down by arrows, supposedly driven by Bloodraven's magic. Aemon took up the sword Blackfyre from his dying father, but was then slain by the archers as well.

name: Aemon Costayne
bio: Aemon Costayne is a member of House Costayne and is a traveling singer.

name: Aemon Estermont
bio: Aemon Estermont is a knight of House Estermont. He is the son of Eldon_Estermont and the father of Alyn_Estermont.

name: Aemon Rivers
bio: Aemon Rivers is a knight and the only son of Ser Walder_Rivers, the oldest of Lord Walder_Frey's bastard sons.

name: Aemon Steelsong
bio: Aemon Steelsong is the name to be given to the free folk son of Mance_Rayder and his wife Dalla when he is two years of age.

name: Aemon Targaryen
bio: Prince Aemon Targaryen was a member of House Targaryen. He was the third born child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. Aemon was married to Lady Jocelyn_Baratheon. Together, they had a daughter, Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon). Aemon was a dragonrider whose mount was Caraxes. Aemon was born in 55 AC on Dragonstone to King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. The night following his birth, at the suggestion of Queen Alysanne, King Jaehaerys I placed a dragon's egg in Aemon's cradle, a practise that was started by Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) who put a dragon egg in Jaehaerys and Alysanne's own cradles. From the moment of his birth, King Jaehaerys I openly called Aemon his heir. Queen Alysanne, on the other hand, would reply that Princess Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I) was older than Aemon, and should therefore be queen, to which Jaehaerys would respond that, once Aemon and Daenerys married, they would rule together as king and queen. The answer never entirely pleased Queen Alysanne. The smallfolk of King's Landing lined the streets outside the Red Keep when Jaehaerys and Alysanne returned to the city a month after Aemon's birth, hoping to catch a glimpse of the baby. Jaehaerys eventually climbed the ramparts of the main gates of the castle, raising Aemon over his head so all could see him. It was said that in response, a roar went up that was so loud that it could be heard across the narrow sea. Aemon's brother Prince Baelon was born in 57 AC. The two children became as close as could be. As soon as Baelon could walk, he began to follow Aemon around everywhere he went. In 58 AC, Aemon attended a tourney at King's Landing on the anniversary of Jaehaerys's coronation. There, he shared the royal box with his parents, his sister Daenerys, and his brother Baelon.

name: Aemon Targaryen
bio: Prince Aemon Targaryen, also known as the Dragonknight, was the second son of King Viserys_II_Targaryen and his wife, Lady Larra_Rogare. Aemon joined the Kingsguard during the reign of his uncle, King Aegon_III_Targaryen, at the age of seventeen, and eventually rose to become Lord Commander. After Aegon III, Aemon served four other kings. He died saving the life of his brother, King Aegon_IV_Targaryen. Prince Aemon has been referred to as the noblest knight who ever lived, and his skill with a sword is legendary throughout the Seven Kingdoms. Even over a hundred years after his death, he is a popular figure among the highborn and smallfolk in songs, stories, and fables. Aemon was born to Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen and Lady Larra_Rogare on the eve of Smith's Day in 136 AC. A dragon's egg was placed in his cradle. All rejoiced at his birth, except his older brother Aegon_IV_Targaryen, who was found by their mother hitting the newborn Aemon with Aemon's egg. Aemon was inseparable from his sister Naerys_Targaryen when they were young. Stories speak of Aemon's doomed love for his sister. Aemon and Naerys supposedly loved each other. According to the singers, both Aemon and Naerys cried the day Naerys married Aegon in 153 AC, although historians report that Naerys had only cried during the bedding, while Aemon had quarreled with Aegon at the wedding feast. Aemon grew to be so accomplished in the lists and with the sword that he was deemed worthy of carrying the Valyrian steel sword Dark Sister. Shortly after Naerys and Aegon's wedding, Aemon joined the Kingsguard, taking his vows at the age of seventeen during the reign of his uncle, King Aegon_III_Targaryen. During his life, Aemon would serve under five kings: his uncle Aegon III, his cousins Daeron_I_Targaryen and Baelor_I_Targaryen, his father Viserys_II_Targaryen, and lastly his brother Aegon_IV_Targaryen. Aemon once fought Lord Cregan_Stark of Winterfell and claimed he never faced a finer swordsman.

name: Aemon Targaryen
bio: Maester Aemon, born Aemon Targaryen, is the third son of King Maekar_I_Targaryen and his wife Dyanna_Dayne. He has served as a maester to the Night's Watch at Castle Black for so long that the rest of the Seven Kingdoms has forgotten about his Targaryen blood. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Aemon is portrayed by Peter Vaughan.

name: Aemond Targaryen
bio: Prince Aemond Targaryen, also known as Aemond One-Eye and later as Aemond the Kinslayer, was a member of House Targaryen. He was the second son of King Viserys_I_Targaryen by his second wife, Queen Alicent_Hightower, and the younger brother of King Aegon_II_Targaryen. Like his siblings, Aemond was a dragonrider, and rode the dragon Vhagar. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Aemond is portrayed by Ewan Mitchell and Leo Ashton (young). Aemond was born in 110 AC to King Viserys_I_Targaryen and his second wife, Queen Alicent_Hightower. He had two older sisters, Rhaenyra_Targaryen, Viserys's daughter by his first wife, and Helaena_Targaryen, and an older brother, Aegon_II_Targaryen. Aemond would later also have a younger brother, Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I). The births of Aemond and his older brother Aegon were the cause of some strife at the court of Viserys I. The king had named Rhaenyra, his only surviving child by his first marriage, his official heir in 105 AC, but refused to change the order of succession after the birth of two healthy sons. This eventually led to the rise of the "queen's party" at the royal court, as opposed to the "party of the princess". Additionally, Prince Daemon_Targaryen, the younger brother of King Viserys and Aemond's uncle, was said to be notably cool towards Aemond and Aegon, as their births had pushed him back even further in the line of succession as well. The enmity between Aemond's mother, Alicent, and older half-sister, Rhaenyra, was passed on to their children. Aemond and his siblings became bitter rivals to Rhaenyra's three sons, Princes Jacaerys_Velaryon, Lucerys_Velaryon and Joffrey_Velaryon, resenting them for stealing their birthright, the Iron Throne. The time the six boys were forced to spend together (e.g. attending feasts, sometimes training together at arms or studying under the same maester) only caused to increase this dislike. In 120 AC, when Aemond was ten years old, his brother-in-law, Ser Laenor_Velaryon, died. Aemond accompanied his parents and siblings to Driftmark to attend Laenor's funeral. King Viserys had suggested traveling to Dragonstone after the funeral, and informed Aemond, who out of all his siblings was the only one yet without a dragon of his own, that he could claim a dragon egg or hatchling on Dragonstone for his own, if he was "bold enough". Annoyed by his father's comment, Aemond resolved to claim Vhagar, the eldest of the dragons, who had recently become riderless with the death of Laena_Velaryon. Vhagar was present on Driftmark, and Aemond, who knew that his parents would not allow him near the dragon, went to make his attempt in secret. Aemond was caught by his three-year-old nephew, Prince Joffrey. In an attempt to keep him quiet, Aemond slapped and threatened the younger boy, before shoving him into a pile of dragon droppings. When Joffrey began to yell, Aemond climbed on top of Vhagar's back. The dragon broke free of her chains, and Aemond flew two circles before landing. Joffrey, however, had alarmed his two older siblings, Jacaerys and Lucerys Velaryon. The four boys fought with wooden training swords, until Aemond called his nephews "Strongs". Jacaerys, old enough to understand the insult, attacked Aemond again. When Aemond gained the upper hand and began to beat Jacaerys savagely, Lucerys drew his dagger and slashed at Aemond's face, taking out his right eye. After the incident, Queen Alicent called for Lucerys to lose an eye as penance, which King Viserys refused, while Princess Rhaenyra demanded that Aemond was questioned, "sharply", until he revealed where he heard her sons  being described as "Strongs"—as naming them such was suggesting they were bastards, and therefore that Rhaenyra was guilty of high treason by sleeping with Ser Harwin_Strong. Forced by his father, Aemond eventually admitted hearing the name from his brother, Prince Aegon. While he was an unforgiving person, Aemond would later say that he considered losing an eye to be a fair trade in exchange for obtaining Vhagar. Despite the loss of his eye, Aemond developed into a dangerous swordsman under the guidance of Ser Criston_Cole, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard. On the first day of 127 AC, King Viserys held a feast to celebrate the return of his health. After the king had left the feast, Aemond toasted his three Velaryon nephews, speaking of their appearance, mocking them as "three strong boys".

name: Aenys Blackfyre
bio: Aenys Blackfyre was the fifth son of Daemon_I_Blackfyre and Rohanne_of_Tyrosh. After the death of King Maekar_I_Targaryen in 233 AC, there was no definitive heir to ascend the Iron Throne. When the Hand of the King, Lord Brynden_Rivers, called a Great Council, Aenys put forth his claim to the throne by writing from exile. Bloodraven offered him safe conduct to King's Landing, so Aenys could present his claim in person. Aenys accepted this proposal, but as soon as he entered the capital he was seized by the gold cloaks and dragged to the Red Keep, where he was executed. His head was presented to the lords of the Great Council, as a warning to any who might still have Blackfyre sympathies. After King Aegon_V_Targaryen was crowned king, he had Bloodraven arrested for the murder of Aenys, as Brynden had broken the word of the Iron Throne with the act. Brynden was sentenced to death but later sent to the Wall instead, to live out the remainder of his life as a Brother of the Night's Watch.

name: Aenys Frey
bio: Aenys Frey is a knight from House Frey and is the third son of Walder_Frey, Lord of the Crossing, and his first spouse, Lady Perra_Royce. Aenys is married to Tyana_Wylde, and has two sons with her, Aegon and Rhaegar_Frey.

name: Aenys I Targaryen
bio: Aenys I Targaryen was Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and the second Targaryen king to sit the Iron Throne. He was the son of King Aegon_I_Targaryen and his younger sister-wife, Queen Rhaenys_Targaryen. He had a younger half-brother, Maegor_I_Targaryen. Aenys was born on Dragonstone in 7 AC to King Aegon_I_Targaryen and Queen Rhaenys_Targaryen. He was weak and sickly at birth and cried all the time. He refused to nurse from wet nurses and would only nurse at his mother's breast. He was so unlike the king that it was rumored that the boy could not be the son of Aegon the Conqueror, who was a peerless warrior, and that perhaps his father had been one of the singers or mummers that Rhaenys delighted in. However, when Aenys was given the hatchling dragon Quicksilver in the same year, his condition quickly improved, and "as the dragon grew, so too did Aenys." The rumors that he was not Aegon's son ended at this time. Aenys was three years old when Queen Rhaenys died in Dorne. The death of his mother shattered him: he went back to crawling around as if he were a baby. Aenys was five years older than his half-brother, Maegor_I_Targaryen. He was not close with Maegor while growing up, as Aenys was kept by his father's side in King's Landing, while Visenya and Maegor lived on Dragonstone. Aenys was trained by his father's Kingsguard, and although he was an adequate fighter, he lacked his father's size and strength. From time to time Aegon would let him practice with the Valyrian steel sword Blackfyre. Aenys was married to Alyssa_Velaryon in 22 AC. Their marriage was a political match. Together they had six children: three sons, (Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I), Viserys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen), and three daughters, (Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), Alysanne_Targaryen and Vaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I)). By all accounts, Aenys had a good and loving relationship with his wife and their children. Aenys accompanied his father to Sunspear to celebrate the tenth anniversary of the peace between the Iron Throne and Dorne. When King Aegon I turned sixty, Aenys took over the royal progresses together with his wife, Alyssa. In 37 AC, when King Aegon I died of a stroke at Dragonstone, Aenys was at Highgarden. He swiftly flew Quicksilver to Dragonstone for the funeral and his coronation. He was proclaimed king by Grand Maester Gawen. After Maegor had spoken the eulogy for their father and Aegon had been cremated, Aenys gifted his brother with the sword Blackfyre, admitting that Maegor was more fit to wield the blade than he was. Aenys asked Maegor to wield the sword in service to him, and told his brother that they would rule together. After Aegon's funeral, Aenys traveled to King's Landing, where he claimed his father's throne amidst the rubble and mud of the torn-down Aegonfort and the foundations of the Red Keep. He was cheered by many. There are conflicting written accounts about his coronation by the maesters. According to Archmaester Gyldayn, Aenys was crowned with his father's circlet of Valyrian steel and rubies on Dragonstone, and later, when he traveled with his wife and children to Oldtown by the way of Riverrun, Lannisport, and Highgarden, was presented by the High_Septon_(Aenys_I) at the Starry Sept with a crown of yellow gold, with the faces of the Seven inlaid in jade and pearl. However, according to Maester Yandel, Aenys was crowned during a ceremony in the Red Keep with an ornate golden crown.

name: Aerea Targaryen
bio: Princess Aerea Targaryen was the eldest daughter of Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) and Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). She had a younger twin sister, Rhaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon).

name: Aerion Targaryen
bio: Prince Aerion Targaryen, also known as Aerion the Monstrous, or Aerion Brightflame as he liked to call himself, was the second son of King Maekar_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Aerion is portrayed by Finn Bennett. Aerion was born the second son to Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen and his wife, Lady Dyanna_Dayne. He had one older brother, Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I), two younger brothers, Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) and Aegon_V_Targaryen, and two younger sisters, Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Maekar_I) and Rhae_Targaryen. Aerion owned a dragon egg that was gold and silver. Aerion threw Aegon's pet cat down a well, though he claimed otherwise. Aerion once visited Aegon in his bedroom during the dead of night, and put a knife to Aegon's privates and joked about removing his genitals so Aegon would become a sister he could marry. Aerion stayed in Lys for a few years. According to a semi-canon source, he may have fathered some bastard children while living there, and his descendants may still be living in Lys. Aerion served with the Second Sons for a time while in exile. Aerion returned to Westeros at some point, and he fought in the Third Blackfyre Rebellion in 219 AC. Aerion performed some actions there that are "known well". After Ser Aegor_Rivers was captured, Aerion and the Hand of the King, Lord Brynden_Rivers, urged King Aerys_I_Targaryen to put Aegor to death, but Aerys decided to send Bittersteel to the Night's Watch instead. According to a semi-canon source, at some point after Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I)'s death, Aerion was appointed as Prince of Dragonstone by his father, King Maekar_I_Targaryen. Aerion was married to his cousin, Daenora_Targaryen. Together, they had one child, a son ominously named Maegor_Targaryen_(son_of_Aerion), born in 232 AC. The same year as his son's birth, a drunken Aerion died screaming after drinking a cup of wildfire, believing it would transform him into a dragon. A year later, King Maekar died, and in the Great Council Aerion's son Maegor was passed over in the succession as it was feared that the boy might have inherited his father's cruelty and madness.

name: Aerion Targaryen
bio: Aerion Targaryen was a member of House Targaryen during the Century of Blood, the only son of Lord Daemion_Targaryen of Dragonstone. He was married to Lady Valaena_Velaryon of Driftmark and together they had three children: Visenya_Targaryen, Aegon_I_Targaryen, and Rhaenys_Targaryen. Aerion was also rumored to have fathered Orys_Baratheon on an unknown woman. Aerion inherited the lordship of Dragonstone from his father and was himself succeeded by his son, Aegon_I_Targaryen. Lord Aerion kept a maester on Dragonstone.

name: Aeron Greyjoy
bio: Aeron Greyjoy is the youngest surviving son of the late Lord Quellon_Greyjoy and Lady Sunderly, and is a younger brother of Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands. As a result of a near-death experience, Aeron became a priest of the Drowned God, and he is now called the Damphair because of his wet hair. His acolytes are known as the drowned men. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Aeron is portrayed by Michael Feast. Aeron was born the youngest son from Lord Quellon_Greyjoy's second of three marriages. At six-and-ten he called himself a man, but in truth he had been a ribald drunk, who would sing, dance, play pipes, juggle and ride horses. He drank much, and claimed no man could piss longer or farther than him. He once wagered his ship against a flock of goats that he could douse a hearth-fire by urinating on it, and subsequently outraged his brother, Lord Balon_Greyjoy, by naming the ship Golden Storm. During Greyjoy's Rebellion, Aeron joined his brothers Victarion_Greyjoy and Euron_Greyjoy in raiding the coasts. Aeron nearly drowned when Golden Storm was sunk by the war galley Fury during the sea battle off Fair Isle, when the ironborn fleet was caught in a trap set by the master of ships, Lord Stannis_Baratheon.  Aeron was initially believed to be lost at sea, but he washed ashore and was discovered by local fishermen, who led him to Lannisport in chains. Aeron spent the rest of the rebellion as a prisoner of the Lannisters, but he was later returned to the Iron Islands. Aeron  held pissing contests with "lions, boars, and chickens" while in captivity. An unknown amount of time after Greyjoy's Rebellion, Aeron went down in a storm, but washed up ashore, safely and unharmed. Due to his near-death experience, he underwent a spiritual reawakening, and dedicated himself as a priest of the Drowned God and prophet. He became hard, humorless, and ascetic, living as a hermit on the beach, spreading the word of their god, and becoming known primarily by his new name, Damphair. The acolytes serving under Aeron, called drowned men, do his bidding without question, and the Damphair is greatly respected by the ironborn in general. He has never failed to resuscitate any of his followers with the kiss of life. Aeron fears his brother, Euron, for reasons that are not made clear. He has recurring memories and nightmares of a door with screeching hinges, which he associates with Euron. Aeron is still afflicted with guilt over the death of another brother, Urrigon_Greyjoy, who died from the infection of a wound that occurred while he and Aeron performed a finger dance, a traditional axe-juggling game among the ironborn.

name: Aerys Targaryen
bio: Aerys Targaryen was a member of House Targaryen during the Century of Blood, the second son of Lord Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Gaemon) and Lady Elaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Gaemon) of Dragonstone. He had one older brother, Maegon_Targaryen, and three sons, Aelyx_Targaryen, Baelon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aerys), and Daemion_Targaryen. Aerys inherited the lordship of Dragonstone from Maegon and was himself succeeded by Aelyx.

name: Aerys I Targaryen
bio: Aerys I Targaryen was Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and the thirteenth Targaryen king to sit the Iron Throne. He succeeded his father, King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Aerys was the second son of King Daeron_II_Targaryen and Queen Myriah_Martell. Ser Quentyn_Ball, the master-at-arms of the Red Keep, taught Aerys and his brothers how to fight. However, Aerys lacked martial prowess. From an early age Aerys was obsessed with books about ancient prophecy and the higher mysteries. At some point, Aerys read a prophecy that spoke about the return of dragons.  Aerys and his younger brother Rhaegel seldom left the court, leading their father to pass the castle of Summerhall instead to their youngest brother, Maekar. Aerys was married to his cousin, Lady Aelinor_Penrose, but he never showed any interest in getting her with child, instead shunning her bed. According to rumor, Aerys did not even consummate the marriage.

name: Aerys II Targaryen
bio: Aerys II Targaryen, also called the Mad King and King Scab, was the seventeenth and last member of the Targaryen dynasty to sit the Iron Throne, ruling from 262 AC to 283 AC. His children who lived to adulthood, by his sister-wife, Rhaella_Targaryen, were Rhaegar_Targaryen, Viserys_Targaryen, and Daenerys_Targaryen. Aerys showed great promise at the start of his reign, bringing peace and prosperity to the Seven Kingdoms. His later descent into insanity, however, was caused by, amongst other factors, multiple miscarriages and stillbirths, the deaths of three sons, and a brief uprising known as the Defiance of Duskendale, in which he was held prisoner for half a year by Denys_Darklyn. The king's paranoia and cruelty grew out of control, and Aerys was eventually killed by a member of his own Kingsguard, Ser Jaime_Lannister, in the Sack of King's Landing during Robert's Rebellion. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Aerys is portrayed by David Rintoul in a flashback scene. He was previously played by Liam Burke in the rejected pilot episode of the series. Aerys was born to Prince Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen, the second son of King Aegon_V_Targaryen, and his sister-wife, Princess Shaera_Targaryen. While still a teenager, Aerys was married to his sister Rhaella_Targaryen, in accordance with the family tradition of dynastic incest. While Aerys's grandfather, King Aegon_V_Targaryen was against this incestuous marriage (as he had been against incestuous marriages for his own children), Jaehaerys was allowed to arrange it. Jaehaerys's motivation for the marriage was a prophecy made by a Ghost_of_High_Heart, which had been interpreted to mean that the prince that was promised would be born of the line of Aerys and Rhaella. According to Ser Barristan_Selmy, who was present at the wedding, there was no fondness between the two siblings. During his youth, Aerys befriended the young heir to Casterly Rock, Tywin_Lannister, who served as a page at the royal court. Aerys also befriended the heir to Storm's End, his cousin Steffon_Baratheon. The three became inseparable. In 259 AC, Rhaella gave birth to their firstborn son and heir, Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen, during the tragedy of Summerhall, which killed King Aegon V, his eldest son Prince Duncan_Targaryen, and many others. Aerys's father, Jaehaerys, ascended the Iron Throne after this tragedy. It would later be rumored that, on the night of Jaehaerys's coronation, Aerys took the maidenhead of Lady Joanna_Lannister, who had come to court for the celebrations. Aerys was a squire during the War of the Ninepenny Kings. He fought on the Stepstones, and received his knighthood there at the age of sixteen. Aerys chose to be dubbed a knight by his friend, Tywin. When King Jaehaerys II passed away from illness in 262 AC, Aerys was crowned as King Aerys II Targaryen.

name: Aerys II Targaryen's last mistress
bio: Aerys II Targaryen's last mistress was the daughter of one of King Aerys_II_Targaryen's household knights. After the death of his son, Prince Jaehaerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aerys_II), King Aerys went into a black rage and blamed Jaehaerys' wet nurse for the death of the princeling. Aerys later announced Jaehaerys had been poisoned by his own mistress. She and all her kin were tortured to death. Afterward Aerys, who was known for his infidelities, vowed to stay faithful to his sister-wife, Queen Rhaella_Targaryen. According to the chronicles, Aerys respected his vow and did not take another mistress.

name: Aethan
bio: Aethan is a ranger of the Night's Watch of Castle Black.

name: Aethan Velaryon
bio: Aethan Velaryon was the powerful Lord of the Tides, Master of Driftmark, and head of House Velaryon during the reigns of kings Aegon_I_Targaryen, Aenys_I_Targaryen, and Maegor_I_Targaryen. He was married to Lady Alarra_Massey and had several sons, including Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan), and at least one daughter, Alyssa_Velaryon.

name: Aethelmure
bio: Aethelmure was a Grand Maester of the Citadel. His life was noteworthy enough for a biography to appear in Winterfell's library.

name: Aggar
bio: Aggar is one of Theon_Greyjoy's crew members. He takes part in the capture of Winterfell and accompanies Theon during the search for Bran_Stark and his companions. Aggar, Gelmarr, and Gynir go with Theon and "Ramsay_Snow" and are present for the false execution of Bran and Rickon_Stark at the mill on the Acorn Water. Gelmarr dies the night the group returns to Winterfell, while Aggar is found with his throat cut the following day. Gynir is also eventually found dead. Although he has no evidence, Theon condemns Farlen for the deaths and personally executes the kennelmaster. Theon later recollects that Reek was responsible for the murders. As more men turn up mysteriously dead in Winterfell Theon Greyjoy thinks to himself that it all seems so familiar, like a mummer show that he had seen before.  Except this time the mummers have changed, Roose_Bolton is playing the part that Theon once played and the dead men are playing the parts of Aggar, Gynir Rednose, and Gelmarr the Grim.

name: Aggo
bio: Aggo  is a Dothraki horserider in the khalasar of Khal Drogo. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Aggo is portrayed by Niccolai McKinsky.

name: Aglantine
bio: Aglantine is a septa of the Faith of the Seven in King's Landing. She might be one of the Most Devout.[N 1]

name: Agnes Blackwood
bio: Agnes Blackwood was a Lady of Raventree Hall and head of House Blackwood. Agnes led the Blackwoods when King Harwyn_Hoare of the Iron Islands invaded the northern riverlands. After Samwell_Rivers' small force was destroyed by Harwyn on the Tumblestone, Lord Tommen_Tully joined his strength to that of Agnes at Raventree Hall. When she marched on Harwyn's ironborn, however, Agnes was attacked from behind by her neighboring rival, Lord Lothar_Bracken. Lothar delivered Agnes and two of her sons to Harwyn, who strangled the boys himself. Harwyn offered to make Agnes a salt wife, as he was impressed with her demeanor, but she said she would rather die; he stabbed her after her rejection. The defeat of the Blackwoods ended the rivermen's resistance to Harwyn, but the Storm King Arrec_Durrandon then marched on the ironborn.

name: Agramore Cobb
bio: Agramore Cobb was a knight from House Cobb and a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. During the regency of Aegon III, an attempted coup against the Rogare family and the Hand of the King, Lord Thaddeus_Rowan, resulted in the deaths of Ser Marston_Waters, the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, and two of his sworn brothers, Ser Mervyn_Flowers and Ser Amaury_Peake. Ser Agramore, Ser Dennis_Whitfield, and Ser Edmund_Warrick were given the white cloaks of the Kingsguard to replace the deceased.

name: Agrivane
bio: Archmaester  Agrivane is an archmaester at the  Citadel in Oldtown.

name: Aladale Wynch
bio: Aladale Wynch is a knight of House Wynch. He is a ranger of the Night's Watch, stationed at Castle Black.

name: Aladore Florent
bio: Aladore Florent was a knight from House Florent and the heir to Brightwater Keep during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Ser Aladore is listed in the roll of arms as participating in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Alan o' the Oak
bio: Alan o' the Oak, also known as the Blind Bowman, is a legendary figure from the Age of Heroes that founded House Yew.

name: Alan of Rosby
bio: Alan of Rosby is a ranger of the Night's Watch, stationed at Castle Black. He comes from Rosby in the crownlands.

name: Alan Beesbury
bio: Alan Beesbury was the Lord of Honeyholt and the head of House Beesbury during the Dance of the Dragons. He was the heir of his grandfather, Lord Lyman_Beesbury, at the start of the war. He and Alan_Tarly are remembered as the Two Alans. Alan's grandsire, Lord Lyman_Beesbury, was the first to die during the Dance of the Dragons, although his cause of death is disputed. Alan was unaware of his grandfather's death. Instead, he believed him to languish in the black cells, and loudly called for his grandfather's release. Alan fought during the war as a supporter of Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen and the blacks. Alan and Lord Alan_Tarly (the "Two Alans") harassed the host of Lord Ormund_Hightower after it departed Oldtown. Along with Lord Owen_Costayne, the two Alans fought Ormund's army along the banks of the Honeywine, trapping the greens with a second army of blacks led by Lord Thaddeus_Rowan and Tom_Flowers. Lord Hightower's lines started to crumble and defeat seemed all but a foregone conclusion. However, Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I) joined the Battle on the Honeywine upon his dragon, Tessarion, turning the battle for the greens. Both Alans were captured after the battle.

name: Alan Tarly
bio: Alan Tarly was the Lord of Horn Hill and the head of House Tarly during the Dance of the Dragons. He and Alan_Beesbury are remembered as the Two Alans. Alan supported Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen and the blacks. Joined by Lord Owen_Costayne and Ser Alan_Beesbury, Lord Tarly trapped Lord Ormund_Hightower's forces along the banks of the Honeywine between them and another army of blacks led by Lord Thaddeus_Rowan and Tom_Flowers. Ormund's lines started to crumble and defeat seemed all but a forgone conclusion. However, Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I) attacked on his dragon, Tessarion, turning the battle for the greens. Alan was captured after the Battle of the Honeywine.

name: Alannys Harlaw
bio: Alannys Harlaw, also known as Lady Greyjoy, is the wife of Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands. Originally from House Harlaw, she is the sister of Gwynesse_Harlaw and Rodrik_Harlaw, Lord of Harlaw. Alannys had four children with Balon; Rodrik_Greyjoy, Maron_Greyjoy, Asha_Greyjoy, and Theon_Greyjoy. She is nicknamed Lanny by Rodrik. During Greyjoy's Rebellion, Balon_Greyjoy declared himself King of the Iron Islands, with Alannys as his queen. Her eldest two sons, Rodrik_Greyjoy and Maron_Greyjoy, were killed during the rebellion, however, and her youngest son, Theon_Greyjoy, was taken by Lord Eddard_Stark as a hostage when Balon submitted to King Robert_I_Baratheon. With Theon away at Winterfell, Alannys brought five boys, including Tristifer_Botley, to Pyke to foster. Alannys raised her daughter, Asha_Greyjoy, to be bold. As time passed, Alannys began to roam the halls of Pyke in search of her lost sons, even walking the rope bridge to the Sea Tower while barefoot; a maester often had to draw splinters from her heels. Alannys eventually moved to Ten Towers to live with her sister, Gwynesse_Harlaw, as Harlaw is less raw than Pyke.

name: Alaric of Eysen
bio: Alaric of Eysen is a far-traveled singer from Eysen.

name: Alaric Stark
bio: Alaric Stark was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Alaric attended the Golden Wedding in King's Landing with his father, Lord Brandon_Stark_(father_of_Walton), and older brother, Walton_Stark_(son_of_Brandon), in 49 AC. Brandon died soon after returning to the north and was succeeded by Walton. While pursuing rebels from the Night's Watch in 50 AC, Lord Walton was slain beyond the Wall by giants. Now Lord of Winterfell, Alaric blamed King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen for his brother's death, as the rebels had been spared by the young king and sent to the Wall after the wars of Maegor_I_Targaryen. Lord Alaric was wed to a Lady_Mormont_(wife_of_Alaric_Stark) from Bear Island. They had two sons and a daughter, Alarra_Stark. Alaric said his wife had taken an axe to a pack of wolves when she had been twelve, had killed two of them and had sewed a cloak from their skins. Alaric's wife died in 55 AC. Alaric and his sons alone received Queen Alysanne_Targaryen during the royal progress to Winterfell in 58 AC. He asked the queen not to take her dragon, Silverwing, within the castle walls, and declined her initial offers to arrange marriage alliances for his sons with daughters of great southern lords, by pointing out that House Stark keeps the old gods. After Alysanne pointed out that several southern houses kept the old gods, he promised to discuss the matter with his sons. Alaric also scoffed at not wanting to have to feast a large royal delegation, citing that "winter is coming", even though it was summer, because they could not feed a thousand men for long. Alaric was uncertain on how to entertain a queen, and unhappy that Jaehaerys had not come with Alysanne. However, he warmed to the queen, and eventually took her hunting in the wolfswood for elk and wild boar, and showed her the bones of a giant. He also allowed her to rummage as she pleased through his modest castle library. When Alysanne continued her journey north, Alaric sent word ahead to Lothor_Burley, Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, informing him of her upcoming arrival at Castle Black. Alaric was cold toward Jaehaerys when the king arrived, showing him Walton's tomb in the crypt of Winterfell. According to Archmaester Gyldayn, Alysanne charmed a reluctant Alaric into granting the New Gift to the Night's Watch because, even though Alaric was a strong friend to the Watch, he knew the northern lords who already held the lands in question would not be happy to give them away. According to Maester Yandel, however, the Starks were opposed to the royal demand, and Alaric's sons wrote letters to the Citadel asking the maesters about precedents against forced donation of property. Alaric did not attend the tourney in King's Landing in which was celebrated the tenth anniversary of Jaehaerys's coronation, sending his two sons and daughter, Alarra, instead. Alaric ordered his bannermen to put aside half of their harvests in anticipation of the winter of 59–60 AC, but not all of the northmen obeyed, resulting in famine. Lord Alaric died in 72 AC and was succeeded by his grandson, Edric_Stark, as Alaric's two sons had preceded him in death.

name: Alarra Massey
bio: Alarra Massey was a noblewoman of House Massey during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. She was married to Lord Aethan_Velaryon, Lord of the Tides and the Master of Driftmark. Alarra was married to Lord Aethan_Velaryon of Driftmark. During the marriage, she gave birth to several sons, including Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan), and at least one daughter, Alyssa_Velaryon, in 7 AC.

name: Alarra Stark
bio: Alarra Stark was the daughter of Lord Alaric_Stark and his Lady_Mormont_(wife_of_Alaric_Stark) from House Mormont. When Queen Alysanne_Targaryen visited Winterfell in 58 AC, Alarra and Alysanne became particularly close. Near the end of 58 AC, Alarra came to King's Landing with her two brothers to attend the tourney celebrating the tenth anniversary of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen's coronation. At this time, Alarra also became a lady-in-waiting to the Queen.

name: Alastor Reyne
bio: Alastor Reyne was a knight from House Reyne during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and the master-at-arms at Casterly Rock. During the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, Ser Alastor was the champion of the westerlands and the master-at-arms at Casterly Rock, in the service of Lord Lyman_Lannister. Ser Alastor was one of the notables that participated in the grand melee in the Dragonpit in the tourney for its completion in 55 AC.

name: Alayaya
bio: Alayaya, sometimes called Yaya, is a prostitute working at the whorehouse owned by her mother, Chataya, in King's Landing. She is a Summer Islander, but was born in King's Landing.

name: Alayne Baelish
bio: Alayne was the mother of Petyr_Baelish.

name: Alayne Royce
bio: Alayne Royce was a noblewoman of House Royce in the reigns of kings Aenys_I_Targaryen, Maegor_I_Targaryen, and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Alayne was the daughter of Lord Allard_Royce of Runestone. She became the favorite of Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), who cherished her so much that she sometimes took Alayne flying on the back of her dragon, Dreamfyre. During Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) and Princess Rhaena's royal progress, Alayne and Melony_Piper were the only lady companions of the princess who stayed loyal to her after King Maegor_I_Targaryen usurped the inheritance of Prince Aegon. Alayne arrived on Fair Isle at the beginning of 49 AC for Rhaena's wedding to Androw_Farman. She stayed with the princess at Faircastle, where she, Rhaena, Samantha_Stokeworth, and Elissa_Farman became nigh inseparable and often rode Dreamfyre together. Ser Franklyn_Farman dubbed the four friends, "the Four-Headed Beast". When Rhaena left Fair Isle upon the ascension of Lord Franklyn, Alayne came with her to her new court upon Dragonstone. After Elissa betrayed the princess with her theft of Dreamfyre's dragon eggs, Alayne observed that the marriage between Rhaena and Androw had become loveless. Alayne died at the end of 54 AC. At first, it was thought she had died of an illness. Later, it was revealed that she had been poisoned by Androw Farman, who was jealous of Rhaena's friendships with her ladies and weary of their mockery toward him.

name: Albar Royce
bio: Albar Royce is a knight and the son and heir of Lord Nestor_Royce, Keeper of the Gates of the Moon.

name: Albett
bio: Albett is a young recruit of the Night's Watch. He is called Pimple by Ser Alliser_Thorne.

name: Albin Manwoody
bio: Albin Manwoody was the last king of House Manwoody in Dorne. Albin was defeated in Nymeria's War and was one of the six kings sent to the Wall by Nymeria.

name: Albin Massey
bio: Albin Massey was the Lord of Stonedance and the head of House Massey during the reigns of King Maegor_I_Targaryen and King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He served King Jaehaerys as master of laws from 50 AC until his death in 59 AC. Albin Massey was the third son of the Lord of Stonedance. Born with a limp, Albin had been sent to the Citadel to forge a chain and become a maester. However, in 47 AC a fever killed Albin's older brothers and lord father, making him the new Lord of Stonedance. In 48 AC, Prince Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen pressed his claim to the throne against his usurping uncle, King Maegor_I_Targaryen, and high lords of the realm flocked to his cause. Maegor commanded a score of lesser lords, including Albin, to gather at King's Landing, but their numbers were few and many soon lost heart. Shortly afterwards, Maegor was found dead upon the Iron Throne, and the prince ascended as King Jaehaerys I Targaryen. In the chaos that followed Maegor's death, Albin fled back to Stonedance. During Jaehaerys' minority, Albin was the first lord to visit the young king at Dragonstone, with other noblemen following his lead. In 50 AC, King Jaehaerys came of age, and gave Lord Albin a seat on the small council, naming him lord justiciar and master of laws. After Jaehaerys dismissed Septon Mattheus from court, Albin was rumored to have remarked that the king required three persons to replace the corpulent prelate. After Mattheus was feared to become the High Septon, Albin suggested silencing the septon with a sudden, mysterious death. Jaehaerys quickly dismissed this suggestion. After Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan) resigned as Hand of the King, Jaehaerys considered Albin for the office, but found him to be unsuitable. Albin himself counseled the king that he served him better from the shadows, for his twisted appearance would strike the ignorant as somehow sinister. In 55 AC, Albin assisted King Jaehaerys in codifying, reorganizing, and reforming the kingdom's laws, creating a single book of law for the Seven Kingdoms. He initially justified the right of the first night by noting that the practice originated from the Age of Heroes when it was considered a blessing for an ancient warrior to sleep with a maiden on her wedding night. Lord Albin perished during the harsh winter of 59 AC from the Shivers. Jaehaerys did not find it easy to replace him as master of laws, but Albin was ultimately succeeded in the office by Lord Rodrik_Arryn.

name: Alchemist
bio: The alchemist is a man encountered by Pate_(novice), a novice of the Citadel, in Oldtown. It is unknown if he is connected with the Alchemists' Guild of King's Landing.

name: Alebelly
bio: Alebelly is a fat guard of Winterfell.

name: Alec Hunter
bio: Alec Hunter was the Lord of Longbow Hall and the head of House Hunter during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Alec travelled to King's Landing to attend the Golden Wedding in 49 AC. King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen took the opportunity to entertain the assembled lords in the intimacy of his solar, encouraging them to speak freely and share their views on the problems of the realm and how they might best be overcome. Alec thought the young king to be stern.

name: Alekyne Florent
bio: Alekyne Florent is the only son of Lord Alester_Florent and Lady Melara_Crane, and the heir to Brightwater Keep. He has two younger sisters, Melessa_Florent and Rhea_Florent.

name: Alequo Adarys
