bio: Alequo Adarys, known as Silvertongue and the Tyrant of Tyrosh, was a wealthy and ambitious Tyroshi merchant and a key member of the Band of Nine. The Band of Nine were a group of merchants, sellswords and pirates who joined together to carve kingdoms out for themselves. After conquering the Disputed Lands, Alequo convinced the others to take Tyrosh. After successfully sacking the city and deposing the Archon_of_Tyrosh_(War_of_the_Ninepenny_Kings) Archon, Alequo was installed as its head of state. The Band of Nine next meant to conquer Westeros for Maelys I Blackfyre, and fought a war called the War of the Ninepenny Kings on the Stepstones against the Westerosi forces. The Band of nine was defeated there, and Maelys killed. While the remainder of the Band of Nine was defeated half a year later, Alequo, known as the Tyrant of Tyrosh, would rule the Free City for six more years, until he was poisoned by his queen. With Alequo's death, the Archon of Tyrosh was restored.

name: Alerie Hightower
bio: Lady Alerie Hightower is the daughter of Lord Leyton_Hightower of Oldtown, and the spouse of Lord Mace_Tyrell of Highgarden. She is the mother of his four children, Willas_Tyrell, Garlan_Tyrell, Loras_Tyrell, and Margaery_Tyrell.

name: Alesander Frey
bio: Alesander Frey is a singer and the son of Symond_Frey, the seventh son of Lord Walder_Frey, and Betharios_of_Braavos.

name: Alesander Staedmon
bio: Alesander Staedmon, called Pennylover, is the Lord of Broad Arch and the head of House Staedmon.

name: Alesandor Torrent
bio: Alesandor Torrent is the Lord of Littlesister and the head of House Torrent.

name: Alester II Arryn
bio: Alester II Arryn was a King of Mountain and Vale from House Arryn. Unlike kings Hugh_Arryn and Hugo_Arryn who used force to extend their realm to the islands off the coast of the Vale, Alester brought the Witch Isle, seat of House Upcliff, into his realm by taking Arwen_Upcliff for his bride.

name: Alester Florent
bio: Alester Florent is the Lord of Brightwater Keep and the head of House Florent, sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden. He is married to Lady Melara_Crane, and they have three children, Alekyne_Florent, Melessa_Florent, and Rhea_Florent. His niece, Selyse_Florent, is married to Stannis_Baratheon, Lord of Dragonstone. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Alester is merged with Axell_Florent (taking the latter's name) and portrayed by James McHale. Alester was born at Brightwater Keep. He and a Tarly were once unhorsed in the same tourney by Ser Denys_Mallister.

name: Alester Norcross
bio: Alester Norcross is the eldest son of Ser Hosman_Norcross and Delena_Florent. He has a younger brother, Renly_Norcross, and a bastard half-brother, Edric_Storm. He is probably named after his mother's uncle, Lord Alester_Florent.

name: Alester Oakheart
bio: Alester Oakheart was a member of House Oakheart.

name: Alester Wynch
bio: Alester Wynch was a nobleman from House Wynch during the Dance of the Dragons. After Lord Dalton_Greyjoy was killed by his own salt wife Tess at Faircastle in 133 AC, the smallfolk and remaining knights of Fair Isle rose up in rebellion against the ironborn. Alongside Gunthor_Goodbrother, Alester was one of the commanders of the Greyjoy garrison at Faircastle, composed of the Red Kraken's close companions and brothers-in-battle. While the ironborn fled the island or were otherwise murdered by its people, the garrison at Faircastle continued to stubbornly hold out, until a quarrel over one of the Red Kraken's salt widows, Lysa_Farman, led to Gunthor slaying Alester.

name: Alf of Runnymudd
bio: Alf of Runnymudd is a builder of the Night's Watch from Runnymudd.

name: Alfador
bio: Alfador was a maester who served House Hightower at the Hightower in Oldtown during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. After Grand Maester Mellos passed away, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen recommended that her own maester, Gerardys, take his place as Grand Maester. Queen Alicent_Hightower, on the other hand, wanted Maester Alfador named to the office. Beset from both sides, King Viserys chose neither, reminding both his wife and daughter that the Citadel would decide who filled the office. Archmaester Orwyle, in the end, was selected by the conclave as the new Grand Maester.

name: Alford
bio: Alford was a Grand Maester of the Citadel during the late reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Alford was Grand Maester in 153 AC. When Prince Daeron_II_Targaryen was born to Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen and Princess Naerys_Targaryen on the last day of the year, Alford warned them that another pregnancy might kill the princess.

name: Alfred Broome
bio: Alfred Broome was a knight from House Broome, sworn to House Targaryen during the Dance of the Dragons. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Alfred is portrayed by Jamie Kenna. Alfred joined the garrison of Dragonstone during the reign of the Old King, Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. By the time civil war broke out between Rhaenyra_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen, Alfred was the most senior knight of Dragonstone. When Rhaenyra led the blacks to capture King's Landing, she named Ser Robert_Quince as castellan of Dragonstone, rather than the sullen Alfred. Unbeknownst to Rhaenyra, Aegon and later his dragon, Sunfyre, went into hiding on the island of Dragonstone. Aegon's supporters, the greens, were able to find potential allies amongst the castle's garrison. Ser Alfred was convinced by promises of lordship, lands, and wealth. He participated in the coup against Robert, personally slaying the castellan with a spear. After the duel between Sunfyre and Moondancer, Ser Marston_Waters prevented Alfred from slaying Moondancer's dragonrider, Baela_Targaryen. When Rhaenyra and her few remaining companions returned to Dragonstone after the storming of the Dragonpit, Alfred led the forty-strong escort that accompanied her from the port to the castle. When they arrived at the castle, Rhaenyra was shocked to see the charred corpse of Robert. Alfred had his men slay Rhaenyra's remaining Queensguard—Ser Harrold_Darke, Ser Adrian_Redfort, and Ser Loreth_Lansdale—although the latter knight slew two of Alfred's guards. Alfred's cloak caught fire when Sunfyre bathed Rhaenyra with flame. Alfred advised Aegon II to kill Rhaenyra's son, Aegon_III_Targaryen, but the king decided to keep the boy as a hostage. Ser Alfred accompanied Aegon II when the king returned to King's Landing. He beheaded Trystane_Truefyre after the latter was sentenced to die for treason. Ser Alfred was present at the council meeting following the green defeat at the Battle of the Kingsroad. After Aegon II refused to surrender and Queen Alicent_Hightower suggested mutilating one of the ears of the hostage Aegon the Younger, the king ordered Alfred to carry out the order. However, when Alfred crossed the drawbridge to Maegor's Holdfast, he found Ser Perkin_the_Flea and six of his gutter knights blocking the way. Alfred demanded they move aside in the king's name.  Perkin answered that there was a new king, put a hand upon on Alfred's shoulder, and shoved him off the drawbridge onto the iron spikes below. Alfred agonized for two days, writhing and twisting, before dying.

name: Alfyn
bio: Alfyn, also known as Alfyn Crowkiller, is an infamous wildling leader. He has at least four sons.

name: Alfyn
bio: Alfyn was a septon of the Faith of the Seven during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, later becoming the High Septon. After King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen wed his sister, Alysanne_Targaryen, Jaehaerys sent seven preachers to walk the realm and win the smallfolk over to the marriage, not with swords, but with words. Septon Alfyn was among these seven. They traveled the realm alone, walking from village to village, telling the smallfolk of Jaehaerys's wisdom and Alysanne's kindness. When challenged on the issue of incest, the seven would say the Valyrians mounted dragons and thus were not like other men, and as such, an exception had to be made. When the High Septon died in 54 AC, the king and queen hoped to prevent the election of a High Septon that would denounce their marriage as incestuous and begin an uprising. Septon Mattheus of King's Landing was a likely candidate, and fiercely opposed to the Targaryen practice of incest. King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne flew to Oldtown, and according to Septon Barth's account there struck a deal with Lord Donnel_Hightower to support Alfyn, a fierce champion of the Doctrine of Exceptionalism, as the new High Septon. King Jaehaerys promised Lord Donnel that, as Alfyn's old age would likely mean he would soon die, Alfyn's successor would be a Hightower on the condition that the Hightowers would support the king's doctrine. At Ashford, Alfyn was astonished to learn he had been elected High Septon. It took him a fortnight to travel to Oldtown and take up the crystal crown. Alfyn and High_Septon_(brother_of_Donnel_Hightower), a brother of Lord Donnel Hightower, made the Doctrine of Exceptionalism an official tenet of the Faith of the Seven. In 59 AC, the ruling High Septon died of the Shivers. It is currently unknown if this High Septon is Alfyn or one of his successors.

name: Alia
bio: Alia of Braavos is an old singer from the Free City of Braavos.

name: Aliandra Martell
bio: Aliandra Nymeros Martell was the ruling Princess of Dorne and the head of House Nymeros Martell during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. The daughter of Prince Qoren_Martell, Aliandra became Princess of Dorne by the age of seventeen. She saw herself as "the new Nymeria" and encouraged her lords and knights to prove themselves to her by raiding in the Dornish Marches. Aliandra's younger brother, Prince Qyle_Martell, was suggested as a possible husband for Lady Baela_Targaryen, but she wed her cousin Lord Alyn_Velaryon instead. In 133 AC, when Aliandra was eighteen, she met Alyn Velaryon during his first great journey, when his campaign to defend the westerlands from Lord Dalton_Greyjoy brought him to Sunspear. The book Bastard Born claims that Alyn had to provide intimate services to Aliandra before she allowed him provisions for his fleet, although another biography of Alyn, Hard as Oak, contradicts that story. Aliandra met Alyn again when he returned from the Sunset Sea, and Drazenko_Rogare, a Lysene envoy, informed the Oakenfist that Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen was a captive of his brother, Lysandro_Rogare. Aliandra showed great favor to Alyn during his visits. Alyn's wife, Baela Targaryen, disliked hearing about Aliandra. Aliandra was one of the greatest beneficiaries of the Daughters' War, with Dorne taking dominion over most of the Stepstones. During the Lysene Spring, Aliandra married Drazenko and named him Prince Consort of Dorne and Lord of the Stepstones. When Drazenko suspiciously choked to death on bacon in 135 AC, his death was attributed by some to the Faceless Men of Braavos.

name: Alicent Hightower
bio: Alicent Hightower was a member of House Hightower who became the second wife to King Viserys_I_Targaryen. She was the daughter of Ser Otto_Hightower, who had been Hand of the King to Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, Viserys I, and later his grandson Aegon_II_Targaryen. She was the mother of King Aegon II, Queen Helaena_Targaryen, Prince Aemond_Targaryen and Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I). In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Alicent is portrayed by Olivia Cooke and Emily Carey (young). Alicent was born in 88 AC to Otto_Hightower, the younger brother of Lord_Hightower, the Lord of the Hightower. Alicent had several brothers, including Gwayne_Hightower. When her father was named Hand of the King by King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen in 101 AC, thirteen year-old Alicent accompanied him to court together with her brothers and mother. In 103 AC, Alicent became the constant companion of King Jaehaerys, whose health was failing.[N 1] She fetched his meals, helped him wash and dress, and read to him. In the end, King Jaehaerys sometimes mistook her for one of his daughters, especially Saera_Targaryen. Jaehaerys eventually died the same year, while Alicent was reading to him. Alicent stayed at court, as her father continued to occupy the position as Hand under Jaehaerys's grandson and successor, King Viserys_I_Targaryen. Already early in the reign of Viserys, Ser Otto and Prince Daemon_Targaryen were at odds with each other.[N 2] Rhaenyra_Targaryen's ten-year-old son took the throne as King Aegon_III_Targaryen in 131 AC. When the Lads arrived at the Red Keep, they found that the late Aegon II Targaryen had been laid out on a bier before the Iron Throne, while Alicent stood crying beside it. During the Hour of the Wolf, King Aegon III's Hand, Lord Cregan_Stark, sentenced to death the men who had arrested Alicent. The Dowager Queen opposed the marriage of her granddaughter Jaehaera_Targaryen to the new king, and was absent from the ceremony. Aegon's new regents were divided on what to do with Alicent. While they did not desire Alicent's own execution, some from compassion and others for fear that doing so would reignite the war, they felt she could not be allowed to participate in court life. If she did, she was likely to curse at the young king, or snatch a dagger from a guard and try to attack him. Alicent could not even be trusted around her granddaughter; once when she was allowed to share a meal with the little queen, she terrified Jaehaera by suggesting she slit Aegon's throat while he was sleeping. Since Alicent refused to be reconciled, Ser Tyland_Lannister, the Hand of the King, ordered a gentle imprisonment, confining her within her own apartments in Maegor's Holdfast. Alicent remained in her chambers for the last year of her life. Her only company was her septa, serving girls, and guards. She was given books as well as needles and thread, but Alicent's guards reported she spent more time weeping than reading or sewing. One day she ripped all her clothing into pieces. By the end of the year, Alicent had begun talking to herself, and was known to abhor the color green. A few days into 133 AC, the Winter Fever epidemic arrived in King's Landing, and Alicent became one of the victims of the disease. Though most infected became delirious, she regained some sanity, confessing her sins and telling her septa of her wish to see her children again. In her final hours, Alicent also often spoke of hoping to see King Jaehaerys, but oddly never of her husband, King Viserys. Dowager Queen Alicent Hightower died of the fever on a rainy night, at the hour of the wolf, and her death was considered a mercy.

name: Alios Qhaedar
bio: Alios Qhaedar is a candidate to become a triarch of Volantis.

name: All-for-Joffrey
bio: All-for-Joffrey is an old man held captive by Ser Gregor_Clegane and his men in a village at the Gods Eye. His real name is not mentioned. Other prisoners start calling him All-for-Joffrey because he keeps insisting that he and his son do all for Joffrey_Baratheon.

name: Alla Tyrell
bio: Alla Tyrell is the eldest daughter of Ser Leo_Tyrell_(son_of_Victor) and Lady Alys_Beesbury. She is a granddaughter of Ser Victor_Tyrell, a cousin of Lord Mace_Tyrell of Highgarden.

name: Allaquo
bio: Allaquo is a mummer of the Ship.

name: Allar
bio: Allar was a maester of the Citadel who served as Grand Maester during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Allar was appointed Grand Maester in 97 AC during the late reign of King Jaehaerys I Targaryen, after the death of Grand Maester Elysar. He served for four years until he died of a stroke in 101 AC. His successor as Grand Maester was Runciter.

name: Allar Deem
bio: Allar Deem is a member of the City Watch of King's Landing and one of the most ardent supporters of Janos_Slynt, Commander of the City Watch.

name: Allard Royce
bio: Allard Royce was the Lord of Runestone and the head of House Royce during the reign of King Aenys_I_Targaryen. In 37 AC, at the start of King Aenys I's reign, Jonos_Arryn rose up in rebellion against the Targaryens and his own older brother, Lord Ronnel_Arryn_(king). He imprisoned Ronnel in the Eyrie and declared himself King of Mountain and Vale. King Aenys's response was confused and vacillating: first ordering his Hand, Lord Alyn_Stokeworth, to sail to the Vale to deal with the usurper, but then recalling the order at the last moment, for fear that Harren_the_Red, a rebel from the riverlands, might infiltrate King's Landing. Taking matter into own hands, Lord Allard Royce of Runestone gathered "twoscore loyal lords" and led them to sweep away Jonos's supporters, penning him at the Eyrie. However, this led Jonos to send his brother flying out of the Moon Door, earning him the nickname Jonos the Kinslayer. Jonos and his garrison remained defiant until Prince Maegor_I_Targaryen flew to the Eyrie atop the dragon Balerion. Rather than face the fire, Jonos was seized by his garrison and delivered to Lord Royce through the Moon Door. Surrender saved Jonos's followers from burning, but not from death. All of them were hanged per the orders of Maegor, who denied the highborn rebels the honor of beheading. House Arryn continued through a kinsman after Ronnel and Jonos's deaths, a cousin, Hubert_Arryn. Given that Hubert Arryn's wife was a Royce of Runestone, who had already produced six half-Royce sons to one day rule the Eyrie after him, the succession was seen as secure (and was not contested by Lord Allard). Allard was rewarded by King Aenys with gold, titles, and honors.

name: Allard Seaworth
bio: Allard Seaworth is the second son of Ser Davos_Seaworth and his wife Marya_Seaworth. He is the captain of the Lady Marya, named for his mother.

name: Alleras
bio: Alleras, known as the Sphinx, is an acolyte studying at the Citadel in Oldtown. His mother is a Summer Islander trader, and he claims not to be a lord's son, despite the claims of a few others. Alleras has a Dornishman for a father and a Summer Islander for a mother. He has been at the Citadel for a year, and has forged three links in his maester's chain, the most recent of which is copper. At some point in their time at the Citadel, Leo Tyrell gave Alleras the nickname "Sphinx", as he was "a bit of this, a bit of that", like a sphinx is made of multiple creatures.

name: Alliser Thorne
bio: Alliser Thorne is a knight from House Thorne who now serves as the master-at-arms of the Night's Watch at Castle Black. While capable, he is bitter and humorless, and is particularly hated by many recruits of the Night's Watch who have been trained by him. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Alliser is portrayed by Owen Teale. Prior to joining the Night's Watch, Alliser was a knight who fought on the side of House Targaryen during Robert's Rebellion. Following the Sack of King's Landing and the defeat of the Targaryens, surviving knights like Alliser and Ser Jaremy_Rykker were given the choice by Lord Tywin_Lannister between death or taking the black.

name: Allyria Dayne
bio: Allyria Dayne is a noblewoman of House Dayne, and according to semi-canon sources the younger sibling of Ser Arthur_Dayne and Lady Ashara_Dayne. She is the aunt of the young Edric_Dayne, Lord of Starfall, whose Lord_Dayne_(father_of_Edric) was her older brother. Allyria was betrothed to Beric_Dondarrion, Lord of Blackhaven.

name: Alton Beesbury
bio: Alton, born Alton Beesbury, is a maester of the Citadel hailing from House Beesbury in the Reach. According to semi-canon sources, Alton was the only son of Ben_Beesbury, the Lord of Honeyholt. Alton joined the Citadel to become a maester and renounced his inheritance despite his father's protests. Upon Ben's death, he was succeeded by Alton's cousin, Warryn_Beesbury.

name: Alton Butterwell
bio: Alton Butterwell was the Lord of Lord Harroway's Town and the head of House Butterwell during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. He became Maegor's master of coin. After King Maegor exterminated House Harroway in 44 AC, he divided up the Harroway holdings among his remaining supporters. He granted Harrenhal to House Towers, but gave rule over Lord Harroway's Town to Lord Alton and the rest of the Harroway holdings to Lord Darnold_Darry. King Maegor's wars were ruinously expensive, exhausting the royal treasury. As master of coin, Alton continuously raised taxes and imposed new ones, but these measures failed to bring in sufficient revenue. Maegor than demanded a tribute of gold from the high lords. These measures only made Maegor more loathed than he already was. The execution of Queen Alys_Harroway, and soon Queen Tyanna_of_the_Tower as well, left Maegor in need of new brides, as he had so far failed to produce an heir. Lord Alton suggested his stout, widowed sister as a match, who, while no great beauty, had given birth to seven children, her fertility being beyond doubt. King Maegor was scornful of Alton's offer, but with Alton's remark on fertility in mind, the three Black Brides wed by the king in 47 AC were proven to be fertile.

name: Alton Celtigar
bio: Alton Celtigar was the Lord of Claw Isle and the head of House Celtigar during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. Alton was named Hand of the King by King Aegon_I_Targaryen, following the resignation of Lord Edmyn_Tully in 9 AC. Alton served ably as Hand until he died from natural causes in 17 AC. He was succeeded as Hand by Ser Osmund_Strong.

name: Alton Greyjoy
bio: Alton Greyjoy, known as the Holy Fool, was a Lord of the Iron Islands and head of House Greyjoy. According to Archmaester Haereg's History of the Ironborn, Alton wanted to conquer new lands beyond the Lonely Light, but no one has ever successfully crossed the Sunset Sea, with the possible exception of Elissa_Farman.

name: Alvyn Sharp
bio: Alvyn Sharp is a member of House Sharp and an ironborn captain.

name: Alyce
bio: Alyce is a serving girl at the Peach in Stoney Sept.

name: Alyce Graceford
bio: Alyce Graceford is the Lady of Holyhall and the head of House Graceford.

name: Alyn
bio: Alyn is a member of the household guard of Lord Eddard_Stark at Winterfell.

name: Red Alyn of the Rosewood
bio: Red Alyn of the Rosewood is a steward of the Night's Watch. He comes from the Rosewood.

name: Sour Alyn
bio: Sour Alyn is a man-at-arms sworn to House Bolton. He is one of the Bastard's Boys.

name: Alyn Ambrose
bio: Alyn Ambrose is a squire from House Ambrose. The son of Lord Arthur_Ambrose and Lady Alysanne_Hightower, Alyn is betrothed to Elinor_Tyrell and is to wed her when he becomes a knight.

name: Alyn Ashford
bio: Alyn Ashford was a knight of House Ashford during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 73 AC, Alyn took part in a tourney at Old Oak, held by Lord Oakheart. Alyn was defeated by a mystery knight known as the Silver Fool, who was later revealed to be Prince Baelon Targaryen.

name: Alyn Blackwood
bio: Alyn Blackwood is the fifth son of Lord Tytos_Blackwood of Raventree Hall.

name: Alyn Bullock
bio: Alyn Bullock was a knight in service to House Targaryen. His father, Ser Merrell_Bullock, was the commander of the castle garrison at Dragonstone during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Alyn and his brother Ser Howard_Bullock served alongside their father. When King Jaehaerys was on Dragonstone during his regency, he spent every morning until midday in the castle yard training with sword, shield, and any other armament. Ser Alyn, along with his Merrell_Bullock and Howard_Bullock, Ser Elyas_Scales, and knights of the Kingsguard would spar with the young king.

name: Alyn Cockshaw
bio: Alyn Cockshaw was the Lord and the head of House Cockshaw during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Alyn was a childhood friend of Daemon_II_Blackfyre. He and Daemon were bullied by Daemon's older twin brothers, Aegon_Blackfyre and Aemon_Blackfyre. He wept when Daemon went into exile after the First Blackfyre Rebellion. Alyn was ecstatic at Daemon's return to Westeros. He was part of the conspiracy to start the Second Blackfyre Rebellion at the wedding tourney at Whitewalls in 212 AC. Alyn hoped to become the Lord Commander of Daemon's Kingsguard. On the way to Whitewalls, while part of Lord Gormon_Peake's entourage accompanying Ser John the Fiddler (Daemon in disguise), Alyn first encountered Ser Duncan_the_Tall. He did not care for Dunk and was jealous every time "John" approached him. During the tourney, Alyn was unhorsed by Ser Glendon_Flowers, and his mood towards hedge knights took an even darker turn. Alyn deeply resented Duncan for the loss of Daemon's attention and became his secret enemy. He arranged for Ser Uthor_Underleaf to kill Duncan "accidentally" in the tourney joust. However, Duncan survived the blow to his head, much to Alyn's dismay. After Aegon_V_Targaryen went missing at the tourney, Alyn convinced Dunk to follow him if he wanted to find his squire. Alyn lured Duncan to a well, where he intended to kill him. Duncan foiled his plan, and instead it was Alyn who fell into the well, though not before badly injuring Dunk's arm with his dagger. Alyn was left to drown in the well by Duncan and Ser Maynard_Plumm, who had seen the entire episode.

name: Alyn Connington
bio: Alyn Connington, also known as the Pale Griffin, was a knight from House Connington, who eventually became Lord Commander of the Kingsguard at an unknown time.

name: Alyn Estermont
bio: Alyn Estermont is a knight of House Estermont. He is the son of Aemon_Estermont and the grandson of Eldon_Estermont.

name: Alyn Frey
bio: Alyn Frey is the son of Ser Arwood_Frey and Ryella_Royce. He is the twin brother of Androw_Frey. His older sister is Ryella_Frey, and his younger sister is Hostella_Frey.

name: Alyn Garner
bio: Alyn Garner was a knight of House Garner during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Alyn participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Alyn Haigh
bio: Alyn Haigh is the youngest son of Ser Leslyn_Haigh and Perriane_Frey. He has two older brothers; Ser Harys_Haigh and Ser Donnel_Haigh.

name: Alyn Hunt
bio: Alyn Hunt is a knight of House Hunt. He is the cousin of Ser Hyle_Hunt.

name: Alyn Marbrand
bio: Alyn Marbrand was the Lord of Ashemark and the head of House Marbrand during the reign of kings Maekar_I_Targaryen and Aegon_V_Targaryen. He was the father of Lady Jeyne_Marbrand, the wife of Lord Tytos_Lannister.

name: Alyn Orkwood
bio: Alyn Orkwood, called Orkwood of Orkmont, is the head of House Orkwood and is a lord from Orkmont.

name: Alyn Stackspear
bio: Alyn Stackspear is a knight from House Stackspear and the second son of Lord Selmond_Stackspear.

name: Alyn Stokeworth
bio: Alyn Stokeworth was the Lord of Stokeworth and the head of House Stokeworth during the reign of kings Aegon_I_Targaryen and Aenys_I_Targaryen. He served as Hand of the King for both of them. Lord Alyn was named Hand of the King by King Aegon_I_Targaryen after the death of Ser Osmund_Strong, the previous Hand, in 34 AC. When Aegon ordered the Aegonfort torn down and a stone castle to be built in its place in 35 AC, he assigned the task to Alyn and Queen Visenya_Targaryen, while he moved to Dragonstone with the rest of his court. Lord Alyn brought his daughter Samantha_Stokeworth to court, who became a close friend and companion of Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). Aegon the Conqueror died at Dragonstone in 37 AC and Alyn remained as Hand for his son and heir, King Aenys_I_Targaryen. Upon Aenys' coronation, rebellions rose throughout the Seven Kingdoms. The first of these challenges was by a bandit outlaw in the riverlands known as Harren_the_Red, who claimed to be a grandson of King Harren_Hoare. First, Aenys commanded Lord Stokeworth to take a fleet and army to the Vale of Arryn to put down a rebellion instigated by Jonos_Arryn and restore his brother Ronnel_Arryn_(king) to his lordship. As Alyn was about to set sail to the Vale, Aenys countermanded the order, fearing that Stokeworth's departure would leave King's Landing undefended. Instead, Alyn was sent to the riverlands with a few hundred men to hunt down Harren the Red. Alyn personally led the campaign against Red Harren and successfully caught and cornered the outlaw king in a village west of the Gods Eye. In the final fight, Harren killed Alyn but was subsequently slain by Alyn's squire, Bernarr_Brune. A grateful King Aenys conferred knighthood on Brune.

name: Alyn Tarbeck
bio: Alyn Tarbeck was the Lord of Tarbeck Hall and the head of House Tarbeck during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. After declaring his uncle Maegor_I_Targaryen a usurper and a tyrant, Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) gathered his supporters at Pinkmaiden Castle in the riverlands. Lord Alyn Tarbeck was one of the several lords from the westerlands to join Aegon's cause. However, Lord Alyn died with the rebels at the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye in 43 AC, slain by Maegor's loyalists alongside the prince. His wife, Lady Jeyne_Westerling_(wife_of_Maegor_I) of House Westerling, gave him a posthumous Lord_Tarbeck_(son_of_Alyn) a few months later who succeeded him as Lord of Tarbeck Hall.

name: Alyn Terrick
bio: Alyn Terrick was a knight of House Terrick during the Faith Militant uprising. Ser Alyn Terrick, alongside Lord Rupert_Falwell and other knights of the westerlands and riverlands, fought on behalf of the Starry Sept and the High_Septon_(Aenys_I) against Maegor_I_Targaryen's royal army at the battle at the Great Fork. It is unknown if he survived.

name: Alyn Velaryon
bio: Alyn Velaryon, born Alyn of Hull and later called Alyn Oakenfist, was Lord of the Tides, Master of Driftmark, and head of House Velaryon. Alyn was a legitimized bastard of dragonseed descent, born in Hull on the island of Driftmark. His brother was Ser Addam_Velaryon, a dragonrider. An admiral during the reigns of the kings Aegon_III_Targaryen and Daeron_I_Targaryen, Alyn participated in the Dance of the Dragons, the Daughters' War, and the conquest of Dorne. His life is recounted in Six Times to Sea: Being an Account of the Great Voyages of Alyn Oakenfist, as well as in Hard as Oak and Bastard Born. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Alyn is portrayed by Abubakar Salim. According to Archmaester Gyldayn, Alyn's origins remain a matter of dispute among historians to this day. His mother, Marilda of Hull, claimed he and his brother Addam_Velaryon were fathered by Ser Laenor_Velaryon—a fact that many found remarkable due to Laenor's known sexual preference for men. The court fool Mushroom claimed they were fathered by Laenor's father, Lord Corlys_Velaryon, but kept unacknowledged and far from court in order to not offend Corlys's fiery-tempered wife, Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon). Addam and Alyn grew up in the shipyard of Marilda's father, and the boys served on her trading cog, Mouse. Alyn and Baela's marriage was a stormy one. The Velaryons had lost much of their wealth to the Rogare Bank, so in 136 AC, Alyn assembled a fleet of merchantmen and war galleys led by Bold Marilda for a trading expedition to Volantis. When Alyn first made his plans to leave Driftmark, Baela quarreled with him, as she had heard the rumors of her husband's involvement with Aliandra Martell. However, they reconciled as always before Alyn left Driftmark, where Baela remained, pregnant with their second child. While Corlys Velaryon had made all of his nine great voyages on the Sea Snake, Alyn's six great voyages were made on six different ships, which he called "my ladies". The Oakenfist achieved great acclaim battling pirates from the Stepstones and the narrow sea. In 138 AC, Addam_Velaryon's bones were returned to Driftmark from Raventree Hall. Alyn used only a single word, "LOYAL", as the epitaph on his brother's new tomb, along with carvings of a mouse and a seahorse. In 157 AC, Alyn took a decisive part in King Daeron_I_Targaryen's conquest of Dorne as master of ships. He commanded a fleet that broke the Planky Town, and swept halfway up the Greenblood river while the main Dornish strength was engaged with Daeron in the Prince's Pass. Alyn returned to subdue the Planky Town and the Greenblood in 160 AC after the Dornishmen rebelled, but the Young Dragon Daeron was slain the following year while under a peace banner. His successor, King Baelor_I_Targaryen, made peace with the Dornish. Alyn had an unknown number of children with his wife, Lady Baela_Targaryen. Although he was married, he loved the Princess Elaena_Targaryen, and would have two bastards by her, the twins Jon_Waters and Jeyne_Waters.  Alyn disappeared at sea circa 171–176 AC. A year after his disappearance, Elaena, who had hoped to marry him, agreed to wed another. Two biographies, considered to be vulgar and unreliable, if colorful, were written about Alyn. The first, Hard as Oak, was written by Ser Russell_Stillman, who squired for and was knighted by Alyn. The other, Bastard Born, was written by Rue, who may have been a septa and may have been one of the Oakenfist's paramours. The book Six Times to Sea: Being an Account of the Great Voyages of Alyn Oakenfist, by Maester Bendamure, is considered to be the most complete and authoritative source on Alyn's life and voyages.

name: Alynne Connington
bio: Alynne Connington is the younger sister of Ser Ronnet_Connington, the Knight of Griffin's Roost. She resides in Griffin's Roost.

name: Alys Arryn
bio: Alys Arryn was a noblewoman of House Arryn, the only daughter of Lord Jasper_Arryn. Alys had two brothers Lord Jon_Arryn and Ser Ronnel_Arryn_(son_of_Jasper). She was married to Ser Elys_Waynwood. Elys and Alys had nine children together, one son and eight daughters. Alys died not long after having her ninth child. Of their nine children, Jasper_Waynwood died at three years old, kicked in the head by a horse. The eldest daughter married Ser Denys_Arryn but died soon after he did. Two daughters died while children from the pox, and another was left scarred by the same disease and became a septa. Another daughter was seduced by a sellsword, but after her bastard died in infancy, she joined the Silent Sisters. One daughter married the Lord_Elesham, but was barren. One daughter was carried off by Burned Men on her way to marry a Bracken. The youngest daughter married a landed knight of House Hardyng and died after giving him a son, Harrold_Hardyng.

name: Alys Arryn
bio: Alys Arryn was the wife of Prince Rhaegel_Targaryen, the third son of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Lady Alys was the third match to be made between House Targaryen and House Arryn, following the past unions of Rodrik_Arryn to Princess Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I) and Daella's daughter Aemma_Arryn to Viserys_I_Targaryen. She married Prince Rhaegel_Targaryen and bore him three children; the twins Aelor_Targaryen and Aelora_Targaryen, and daughter  Daenora_Targaryen.

name: Alys Beesbury
bio: Alys Beesbury is a noblewoman of House Beesbury. She is married to Ser Leo_Tyrell_(son_of_Victor). They have five children: Alla_Tyrell, Leona_Tyrell, Lyonel_Tyrell_(son_of_Leo), Lucas_Tyrell and Lorent_Tyrell.

name: Alys Frey
bio: Alys Frey was a descendant of a minor branch of House Frey. She was married to her cousin Ser Jared_Frey, the fourth son of Lord Walder_Frey, with whom she had two children; Tytos_Frey and Kyra_Frey. She is deceased.

name: Alys Harroway
bio: Queen Alys Harroway was a noblewoman from House Harroway during the reign of kings Aegon_I_Targaryen, Aenys_I_Targaryen and Maegor_I_Targaryen. She was the daughter of Lord Lucas_Harroway of Harrenhal and Maegor's second wife.

name: Alys Karstark
bio: Alys Karstark is a noblewoman of House Karstark and is the only daughter of Rickard_Karstark, Lord of Karhold. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Alys is portrayed by Megan Parkinson. When she was six, Alys and her father, Lord Rickard_Karstark, visited Winterfell. Despite Alys's young age, Rickard wanted her to charm Robb_Stark in hopes of arranging a betrothal. She danced with Robb, whom she considered courteous, and with Jon_Snow, who was sullen. Alys is now betrothed to Daryn_Hornwood, heir to the Hornwood; they are waiting for her to flower before they marry.

name: Alys Karstark
bio: Alys Karstark was a noblewoman from House Karstark. She married Lord Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan), youngest son of Lord Cregan_Stark. Together they had three children: Rodwell_Stark, Beron_Stark and Arsa_Stark.

name: Alys Oakheart
bio: Alys Oakheart was a member of House Oakheart during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. The daughter of Lord Oakheart, Alys married Ser Jon_Cafferen, heir to Fawnton in 12 AC. During the wedding, Lord Wyl_of_Wyl turned up uninvited and was admitted through the postern gate by a treacherous servant. Lord Oakheart and most of the guests were slain, and Alys was forced to watch as her husband was gelded. Afterwards, she and her handmaids were gang-raped by Wyl's men and then sold off to a Myrish slaver. This was the most infamous act of the First Dornish War.

name: Alys Rivers
bio: Alys Rivers was a bastard of House Strong, a wet nurse at Harrenhal, and a bedmate of Prince Aemond_Targaryen. She could allegedly see visions of the future, and she was considered a witch queen by some. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Alys is portrayed by Gayle Rankin.

name: Alys Rivers's son
bio: Alys Rivers's son was a boy claimed by Alys_Rivers to be her child by Prince Aemond_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. During the Dance of the Dragons, Prince Aemond_Targaryen ordered the deaths of the remaining members of House Strong at Harrenhal. The dragonrider spared the life of Alys_Rivers, however, and took the older wet nurse for his bedmate as a spoil of war. Septon Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons) disagrees with Mushroom's claim that Alys used love potions and philtres to entice Aemond and Ser Criston_Cole. While Criston marched south along the Gods Eye, Aemond began burning the riverlands with Vhagar. When Lady Sabitha Frey seized the undefended Harrenhal, she found only Alys within, with the wet nurse claiming to be pregnant with Aemond's child. Alys eventually escaped with Aemond when the prince returned to Harrenhal and chased away Sabitha. Later in the civil war, the pregnant Alys watched the Battle Above the Gods Eye atop Kingspyre Tower in Harrenhal below. Aemond and Prince Daemon_Targaryen were both slain in their duel in 130 AC. In 132 AC during the regency of Aegon III, Alys gathered a number of broken men and outlaws to Harrenhal. Ser Tyland_Lannister, Hand of the King, sent Ser Regis_Groves of the Kingsguard and Ser Damon_Darry with a hundred men to deal with the so-called "witch queen". Alys presented a boy to the knights, claiming that she was Aemond's widow and that the child was his trueborn son. Alys declared the boy was the rightful king and ordered Regis to submit to him. Refusing to kneel, Regis instead insulted Alys and her son. Regis abruptly died then under questionable circumstances, and Alys's followers forced Damon's host to retreat to Darry. When informed of Regis's death, the regents of King Aegon_III_Targaryen decided that a stronger force would be needed to deal with Alys's outlaws at Harrenhal. Their plans were interrupted by the outbreak of Winter Fever in 133 AC, however.

name: Alys Stackspear
bio: Alys Stackspear was a noblewoman of House Stackspear, and the first wife of Jason_Lannister_(son_of_Gerold). She was the mother of Damon_Lannister_(son_of_Jason). According to a semi-canon source, the fifteen-year-old Jason_Lannister_(son_of_Gerold) deflowered the maiden Alys in 244 AC, getting her pregnant. Jason and Alys were married because of this, but Alys would eventually die giving birth to the child, named Damon_Lannister_(son_of_Jason).

name: Alys Stark
bio: Alys Stark was a member of House Stark. She was the second daughter of Lord Cregan_Stark and Lady Alysanne_Blackwood.

name: Alys Stone
bio: Alys Stone is the eldest bastard daughter of Ser Harrold_Hardyng. Her mother is Cissy, a lowborn woman in the service of House Waynwood. Alys is two years old.

name: Alys Turnberry
bio: Alys Turnberry, called Sweetberry, was a lady companion to Princess Saera_Targaryen. Alys was a maiden of an age with Princess Saera_Targaryen, attending the royal court at the Red Keep in King's Landing. In 82 AC, Alys became a favorite and dearest friend of the princess, along with Perianne_Moore. Saera nicknamed Alys "Sweetberry". Saera and her five favorites — Alys, Perianne, Jonah_Mooton, Lord Roy_Connington, and Ser Braxton_Beesbury, called Stinger — became inseparable at every feast and ball. They hunted and hawked together, and once sailed across Blackwater Bay to Dragonstone together. When the young lords rode at rings or crossed swords in the yards, the three girls cheered them on. King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen was pleased at his daughter's popularity and friendship, but Queen Alysanne_Targaryen was concerned about the character of Alys, Perianne, and the three boys. Nevertheless, King Jaehaerys felt they could not get up to trouble, with all the servants attending them watching. In 84 AC, an incident occurred between Saera's male favorites and Tom_Turnip, King Jaehaerys's fool, at the Blue Pearl, a brothel in King's Landing, something Jonah claimed had been the princess's idea. Alys and Perianne were woken in the middle of the night and questioned by Queen Alysanne, telling them to tell the truth or they would join their male friends in the dungeons. Before long, the girls were weeping and pleading for forgiveness, although their confessions said almost nothing about the incident they were being questioned about. Perianne explained that Saera had been teaching Alys how to kiss, and she asked to be taught to kiss too, to train for their future marriages. Alys revealed that one night the girls had started kissing each other with their clothes off, taking turns pretending they were boys. Then, Saera dared Alys to kiss a real boy, and she dared Perianne, and both dared Saera, who said she would kiss a man grown, a knight. This began the ladies' affairs with Red Roy, Jonah, and Stinger. Perianne explained that Stinger did the "training" for all of them. Queen Alysanne then demanded to know where their septas and servants had been during all of this. Perianne, confused, explained that servants do what you tell them, and they had sent them out of the room. As for the ones who knew, Stinger had threatened to have their tongues out if they talked. This caused Alys to break down, sobbing and tearing at her dressing gown. She apologized to the queen, and claimed that Saera and Stinger had pressured her into sexual behavior. She revealed she was with child, and that she did not know who the father was. Alysanne sent both girls to bed, and told them that the next morning they would make a confession to a septa and be forgiven by the Mother. Alys knew that her own mother would not forgive her, however. Hasty marriages were arranged for the two girls. However, Roy Connington refused to marry Alys, declaring that she was pregnant with Stinger's child, whom he would not make heir to Griffin's Roost and raise as his own. Alys was sent to the Motherhouse of Maris in Gulltown, where she gave birth to a girl with bright red hair like Roy's. Afterwards, she was wed to Lord Dunstan_Pryor of Pebble.

name: Alysane Mormont
bio: Alysane Mormont, known as Aly and the She-Bear, is a member of House Mormont. She is the second daughter of Maege_Mormont, Lady of Bear Island. Alysane is in her mid-twenties and has two children, a nine-year-old daughter and a two-year-old son. She is unwed, and claims her children were fathered by a bear. Though Alysanne started having children at an age she feels was too young, she also feels that it was better than to wait too long.

name: Alysanne
bio: Alysanne was the eldest child of Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen and his mistress, Megette. Alysanne's mother Megette was the mistress of Prince Aegon for four years. Alysanne was the first of four daughters. After Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen discovered this, Megette was returned to her husband in the riverlands, while Alysanne and her three sisters were given to the Faith to be trained as a septa.

name: Alysanne Blackwood
bio: Alysanne Blackwood, also known as Black Aly, was a member of House Blackwood. She was the aunt of Lord Benjicot_Blackwood of Raventree Hall,[N 1] and the second wife of Lord Cregan_Stark of Winterfell. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Alysanne is portrayed by Annie Shapero.

name: Alysanne Bracken
bio: Alysanne Bracken is a noblewoman of House Bracken, the fifth daughter of Lord Jonos_Bracken of Stone Hedge, and the third by his Lady_Bracken.

name: Alysanne Bulwer
bio: Alysanne Bulwer is the Lady of Blackcrown and the head of House Bulwer. She may have a Lady_Bulwer's_brother. Born in 291 AC, Alysanne is the daughter of Lord Jon_Bulwer and Lady Victaria_Tyrell, and she is a distant cousin of Lord Mace_Tyrell. She succeeded her father after he died of summer fever.

name: Alysanne Farman
bio: Alysanne Farman was a noblewoman of House Farman, and the first wife of Gerold_Lannister. They had no issue. Gerold Lannister was courting Rohanne_Webber in 211 AC, but it is unknown whether he was a widower at the time, or whether after Rohanne married Eustace_Osgrey, Gerold married Alysanne. Gerold became Lord of Casterly Rock in 213 AC, and if he were married to Alysanne at the time, she would have become Lady of Casterly Rock. However, this is unknown. Nevertheless, at some point Alysanne did die, and at some point after her death, Gerold married the widowed Rohanne.

name: Alysanne Hightower
bio: Alysanne Hightower is a daughter of Lord Leyton_Hightower. She is married to Lord Arthur_Ambrose. Together, they have a son, Alyn_Ambrose.

name: Alysanne Lefford
bio: Alysanne Lefford is a member of House Lefford of the Golden Tooth.

name: Alysanne Osgrey
bio: Alysanne Osgrey was the youngest child and only daughter of Ser Eustace_Osgrey, the head of House Osgrey during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. After the defeat of Daemon_I_Blackfyre on the Redgrass Field, all of his supporters who survived the war and remained in Westeros were made to pay for their treasons. As Alysanne's brothers were all dead, she became a hostage at King's Landing at the age of seven to ensure the good behavior of her rebel father, Ser Eustace Osgrey. At some point she became a silent sister and died in the capital at the age of twenty of the Great Spring Sickness.

name: Alysanne Stark
bio: Alysanne Stark was a noblewoman of House Stark, the fifth child and second daughter of Beron_Stark, Lord of Winterfell, and his wife Lady Lorra_Royce.

name: Alysanne Targaryen
bio: Queen Alysanne Targaryen, also known as Good Queen Alysanne, was the queen consort of her brother, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Alysanne was a dragonrider, whose mount was Silverwing.

name: Alysanne of Tarth
bio: Alysanne Tarth was a daughter of Lord Selwyn_Tarth of Evenfall. She died while still in the cradle.

name: Alyse Ladybright
bio: Alyse Ladybright is a noblewoman of House Ladybright and the lord treasurer at Sunspear.

name: Alyssa Arryn
bio: Alyssa Arryn is a half-legendary figure of House Arryn. Alyssa's Tears, a waterfall in the Vale of Arryn, is named for her. Maesters are reasonably certain that Alyssa Arryn did exist, but are not exactly sure when she lived. Most people believe her to have lived six thousand years ago, but the book True History states four thousand years ago. Maester Denestan, in his Questions, states two thousand years. According to legend, Alyssa saw her husband, her brothers, and her children killed in front of her, but she did not shed a tear. As a consequence, after her own death, the gods determined that she would not know rest until her tears reached the earth of the Vale of Arryn, where all those she had loved were buried. The waterfall that flows from the Giant's Lance, known as Alyssa's Tears, pours from such a height that the water turns into mist and is dispersed by wind before it ever reaches the ground below. Per the legend, no drop of the waterfall has ever reached the valley.

name: Alyssa Blackwood
bio: Alyssa Blackwood was a noblewoman from House Blackwood, and the fourth wife of Lord Walder_Frey. Alyssa married Walder Frey after his third wife, Lady Amarei_Crakehall, died in 264 AC. Alyssa and Walder had five children together: Lothar_Frey, Ser Jammos_Frey, Ser Whalen_Frey, Morya_Frey, and Tyta_Frey.

name: Alyssa Royce
bio: Alyssa Royce was a member of House Royce during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 133 AC, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Many maidens across Westeros came to King's Landing in hopes of marrying the young king. However, the event was not a true ball, but merely a procession of maidens, each woman curtsying before the king while a herald announced her name and lineage. Alyssa told King Aegon III that she had come all the way from Runestone to be with him.

name: Alyssa Targaryen
bio: Princess Alyssa Targaryen was the fifth child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen, and their second daughter. Alyssa was a dragonrider whose dragon was Meleys, the Red Queen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Alyssa is portrayed by Emeline Lambert in a dream scene. Princess Alyssa was born in late 60 AC to King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. She was named for her grandmother, Alyssa_Velaryon, who had died six years before. As a young child, Alyssa followed her brother Baelon everywhere since she was able to walk. Baelon, four years older, complained that she was like a puppy, and was further annoyed that she was a girl, as he preferred the company of his older brother Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I). Soon after Alyssa's sister Maegelle_Targaryen's birth in 62 AC, she began to cling to Alyssa the same way as Alyssa had to Baelon, to Alyssa's annoyance and Baelon's amusement. Alyssa avoided her younger sister as well she could. When Alyssa was six years old, she was playing in the training yard when she was accidentally hit in the face by a wooden sword, which broke her nose. It healed crooked, but this did not seem to bother her. When marriage options were being considered for Jaehaerys and Alysanne's eldest son, Prince Aemon, some thought he should marry Alyssa, as she was his eldest living sister following the death of Princess Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I). However, Queen Alysanne Targaryen firmly refused, saying that Alyssa was meant for Baelon, and Aemon instead married Jocelyn_Baratheon in 70 AC. In 72 AC, Alyssa attended a tourney at Duskendale, held in honor of the wedding of Lord Darklyn to a daughter of Lord Theomore_Manderly. Alyssa and Baelon finally became inseparable, and by 73 AC plans for their eventual marriage were already being made. That same year, when her brother Vaegon_Targaryen severely insulted their sister Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I), Alyssa poured a flagon of wine over his head. Over the following year, Baelon began training Vaegon at arms; he once brought Alyssa to the training yard, dressed in a man's mail, hoping that having to face a girl would lead to Vaegon making more of an effort. Alyssa, still remembering the incident between Vaegon and Daella, mocked and laughed at Vaegon as she danced around him, humiliating him, whilst Daella watched. Vaegon, embarrassed too much to bear, threw down his sword, left the yard, and never came back.

name: Alyssa Velaryon
bio: Alyssa Velaryon was a member of House Velaryon during the first century after Aegon's Conquest. She was the wife and queen of King Aenys_I_Targaryen, and they had six children together. Some years after Aenys's death, Alyssa served as Queen Regent for her youngest son, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. She married again to Lord Rogar_Baratheon, and gave birth to two more children during the marriage. Alyssa was born in 7 AC to Lord Aethan_Velaryon, King Aegon_I_Targaryen's master of ships, and Lady Alarra_Massey. She had several brothers and cousins in House Velaryon. Alyssa married her cousin, Prince Aenys_I_Targaryen, in 22 AC. The marriage was a political match. She gave birth to six children during this marriage: Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) (23 AC), Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) (26 AC), Viserys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) (29 AC), Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen (34 AC), Alysanne_Targaryen (36 AC), and Vaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) (39 AC, died in the cradle). When Queen Visenya_Targaryen suggested a betrothal between her own son Maegor_I_Targaryen and Alyssa's firstborn Rhaena in 24 AC, Alyssa and her husband were opposed the idea. King Aegon_I_Targaryen eventually agreed to wed Maegor to Ceryse_Hightower. In the later years of Aegon I's reign, when Maegor refused half a dozen dragon hatchlings, Alyssa teased him, asking him whether he was afraid of dragons. Following King Aegon I's sixtieth nameday in 33 AC, Aenys and Alyssa took over the royal progresses, traveling across the realm half of every year. Upon the coronation of her husband Aenys in 37 AC, Alyssa became queen. It has been suggested that the birth of Alyssa's sixth child, Vaella, in 39 AC is what drove Prince Maegor to take a second wife that same year. Aenys and Alyssa lived in a manse on Visenya's Hill during his reign, as the Red Keep was still not yet complete. In 41 AC, following the first rebellious actions of the Warrior's Sons and Poor Fellows, King Aenys I decided that the manse was not safe for his family, and decided to relocate to Dragonstone. Aenys died the following year on Dragonstone. At his funeral, Alyssa sang a dirge for him.

name: Alyx Frey
bio: Alyx Frey is the only daughter of Symond_Frey, the seventh son of Lord Walder_Frey, and Betharios_of_Braavos.

name: Amabel
bio: Amabel is a servant in Lady Shella_Whent's household at Harrenhal.

name: Amanda Arryn
bio: Amanda Arryn was a noblewoman from House Arryn during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Amanda was the daughter of Lord Rodrik_Arryn and his first wife. She had three siblings, including an elder sister, Elys_Arryn. After her mother died, her father married again to Princess Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I). Amanda largely wrote letters on behalf of her step-mother to Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. Daella died in childbirth, giving Amanda a younger half-sister, Aemma_Arryn.

name: Amarei Crakehall
bio: Amarei Crakehall was a noblewoman of House Crakehall, and the third wife of Lord Walder_Frey. They had seven children together. Amarei was born as a daughter of House Crakehall. She was married to Lord Walder_Frey as his third wife, after his second wife, Cyrenna_Swann, had died. Amarei gave her husband seven children: Ser Hosteen_Frey, Lythene_Frey, Symond_Frey, Ser Danwell_Frey, Merrett_Frey, Ser Geremy_Frey, and Ser Raymund_Frey. Amarei's children and grandchildren often maintain the Crakehall look, tending to be big-boned and strong. Her granddaughters are strong, with wide hips and large breasts. Merrett was sent to Crakehall to serve his mother's family as a page, and later squired for Lord Sumner_Crakehall.

name: Amarys
bio: Amarys was a septa who was a religious tutor of Baela_Targaryen. Amarys was given the religious instruction of Baela_Targaryen, but neither she, nor Septon Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons), could curb her wild ways.

name: Amaury Peake
bio: Amaury Peake was a knight from House Peake and a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. He was a nephew of Lord Unwin_Peake of Starpike. During the regency of Aegon III in 133 AC, Ser Willis_Fell, the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, died of the Winter Fever alongside two of his sworn brothers of the Kingsguard. In their stead, King Aegon_III_Targaryen appointed Ser Robin_Massey as Lord Commander and Ser Robert_Darklyn to the white cloaks. With the support of Grand Maester Munkun, Lord Unwin_Peake, the Hand of the King, dismissed all the appointments and arrangements of the twelve-year-old king, who had not consulted with his regents. His Kingsguard appointments were reversed, with Ser Marston_Waters being elevated to Lord Commander while Lord Peake's bastard half-brother, Ser Mervyn_Flowers, and nephew, Ser Amaury, were made knights of the Kingsguard. In 135 AC Amaury and a dozen men-at-arms were ordered to seize Maegor's Holdfast by Ser Marston, the newly-named King's Hand, to arrest Lady Larra_Rogare when the Rogare family were accused of plotting to overthrow Aegon. However, Larra's husband, Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, and the king refused to give her up. Viserys planted a battleaxe upon the drawbridge and warned Amaury and his men not to cross it, before retreating back into the castle. Amaury ordered his men to take the boys gently and to see no harm come to them. Larra's guard, a former pit fighter named Sandoq_the_Shadow, emerged to face Amaury and his men. As Sandoq dueled Amaury, he wounded the Kingsguard and then slew another guard. Amaury rushed Sandoq after the pit fighter was disarmed of his sword, but Sandoq used Viserys's battleaxe to split the knight's head and helm in half. Amaury's corpse toppled onto the spikes below the drawbridge of the keep.

name: Ambrode
bio: Ambrode is a shipwright in the Iron Islands.

name: Ambrose Butterwell
bio: Ambrose Butterwell was the Lord of Whitewalls and the head of House Butterwell during the reigns of Kings Aegon_IV_Targaryen, Daeron_II_Targaryen, and Aerys_I_Targaryen. He served as master of coin for Aegon IV and later as Hand of the King for Daeron II. Lord Ambrose was master of coin under King Aegon_IV_Targaryen. He was raised to Hand of the King under Aegon's successor, King Daeron_II_Targaryen, for a short time, but his ability to lead was questioned when his attempt to deal with the burgeoning Blackfyre Rebellion failed miserably. He was replaced by Lord Lord_Hayford. During the First Blackfyre Rebellion Butterwell attempted to keep one foot in each camp, he sent his eldest son to fight for King Daeron II and his second son to fight for Daemon_I_Blackfyre, while he remained home with his full strength. Both sons perished at the Battle of the Redgrass Field, and his youngest son died of the Great Spring Sickness. With his sons dead and his daughters married off, Lord Butterwell despaired of siring a new son, or risked his name dying with him. In 212 AC, the recently-widowed Lord Butterwell hosted a wedding tourney at Whitewalls to celebrate his marriage to a daughter of House Frey, but the tourney was also secretly an attempt to foment the Second Blackfyre Rebellion in the name of Daemon_II_Blackfyre. The winner would receive the dragon egg that had been given to Ambrose's Butterwell_(Hand_of_the_King) by King Aegon_IV_Targaryen. However, the egg was stolen from Whitewalls before it could be awarded. Egg, the squire of Ser Duncan_the_Tall and secretly Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen in disguise, attempted to send a message to his father, Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen, by presenting his father's ring to Maester Lothar and asking to send a raven. After being informed, Lord Ambrose brought the boy to the castle sept with a few loyal guardsmen; most of the Butterwell garrison were supportive of Ser Tommard_Heddle, their commander and Ambrose's treasonous son-in-law. Aegon bluffed Ambrose by claiming that he was a spy and that an army was coming, so Lord_Frey departed Whitewalls with his retinue and Ambrose recanted his allegiance to House Blackfyre. Duncan slew Tom Heddle in the sept when he attempted to take Aegon into custody. Ambrose then departed Whitewalls with Aegon, who was instructed by Duncan to escape when he could. However, before Lords Butterwell and Frey could get far, Lord Brynden_Rivers appeared with an army to put an end to the nascent rebellion. Lord Ambrose lost Whitewalls (which was razed and the earth salted), and was only allowed to keep a tenth of House Butterwell's fortunes along with his head and his new bride.

name: Amerei Frey
bio: Amerei Frey, called Ami, is a noblewoman of House Frey and the eldest daughter of Merrett_Frey and Mariya_Darry. Her promiscuity has earned her the nickname Gatehouse Ami. Some time before the start of the series, Amerei was discovered in the stables of the Twins having sex with three grooms at the same time. Her parents were forced to marry her to Ser Pate_of_the_Blue_Fork, a hedge knight of humble beginnings. Pate's cousin, Tom_of_Sevenstreams, sang at the wedding.

name: Amethyst Empress
bio: The Amethyst Empress was the legendary eighth ruler of the mythic Great Empire of the Dawn and one of its last monarchs. She succeeded her father, the Opal_Emperor but was cast down and slain by her envious younger brother, who crowned himself the Bloodstone_Emperor. According to the annals of the Further East, it was the Blood Betrayal, as his usurpation was named, that ushered in the Long Night.

name: Amory Lorch
bio: Amory Lorch is a knight of House Lorch, and bannerman of House Lannister. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Amory is portrayed by Fintan McKeown. According to a semi-canon source, during the Reyne-Tarbeck rebellion in 261 AC, Ser Amory threw the Last_Lord_Tarbeck, a three-year-old boy, down a well in Tarbeck Hall. It is disputed whether this was done per the orders of Ser Tywin_Lannister, or without his knowledge. In 283 AC, Amory was responsible for the murder of the three-year old Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar) during the Sack of King's Landing. Per the order of Tywin Lannister, Amory and Gregor_Clegane scaled the walls of Maegor's Holdfast to access the royal apartments. Alongside and in front of several House Lannister men-at-arms, he dragged the screaming toddler from under her father Rhaegar_Targaryen bed, and when she kicked him, stabbed her dozens of times.

name: Amos Bracken
bio: Amos Bracken was a knight from House Bracken. His father was Lord Humfrey_Bracken of Stone Hedge and his bastard half-brother was Ser Raylon_Rivers. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Amos is portrayed by Tim Faraday. Amos defeated Samwell_Blackwood in a duel when Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen visited the Trident in 112 AC, as the rivals unsuccessfully fought for her hand. House Bracken declared for the greens during the Dance of the Dragons, while House Blackwood supported the blacks. Amos cut down Lord Samwell during the Battle of the Burning Mill, only to then be slain by a weirwood arrow allegedly fired by Samwell's sister, Alysanne_Blackwood. Ser Raylon_Rivers led the Bracken survivors back to the Stone Hedge, which had been stormed in the meantime by Prince Daemon_Targaryen.

name: Andahar
bio: Andahar was a presumed river king in the vicinity of Lord Harroway's Town in the riverlands. He may be related to House Roote.

name: Andar Royce
bio: Andar Royce is a knight from House Royce and is the eldest son and heir of Yohn_Royce, Lord of Runestone.

name: Anders Yronwood
bio: Anders Yronwood is the Lord of Yronwood and head of House Yronwood. He also holds the titles the Bloodroyal and Warden of the Stone Way. He has a son, Cletus_Yronwood and two daughters, Ynys_Yronwood, and Gwyneth_Yronwood. Prince Quentyn_Martell had previously been his ward. Prince Quentyn_Martell was fostered with Lord Yronwood and served as first his page and then his squire. Quentyn chose to be knighted by Anders instead of by his uncle, Prince Oberyn_Martell.

name: Andrew Estermont
bio: Andrew Estermont is a knight from House Estermont. The only son of Ser Lomas_Estermont, Andrew is a former squire of Lord Stannis_Baratheon.

name: Andrew Tollett
bio: Andrew Tollett  is a knight from House Tollett. His relation to the head of the House, Lord Uthor_Tollett, is unknown.

name: Andrey Charlton
bio: Andrey Charlton is a knight of House Charlton. His squire is young Lucas_Roote.

name: Andrey Dalt
bio: Andrey Dalt, called  Drey by his friends, is a knight of House Dalt. He is the brother and heir of Ser Deziel_Dalt, the Knight of Lemonwood. Andrey has been a close friend of Princess Arianne_Martell, daughter of Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne, since childhood. Both he and his brother Deziel_Dalt had desired to marry her. Andrey experimented sexually with Arianne and Tyene_Sand in their youth, though he got too excited too early.

name: Andrik
bio: Andrik the Unsmiling is an ironborn warrior serving Lord Dunstan_Drumm and Balon_Greyjoy.

name: Andros Brax
bio: Andros Brax is the Lord of Hornvale and the head of House Brax. He has three knighted sons: Tytos_Brax, Robert_Brax, and Flement_Brax. Lord Andros once visited Riverrun in hopes of making an offer for the hand of Catelyn_Stark or Lysa_Arryn for one of his sons.

name: Androw Ashford
bio: Androw Ashford was a knight from House Ashford, the eldest son and heir of Lord_Ashford during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Androw participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC. Along with his younger brother, Ser Robert_Ashford, he was one of Lord_Ashford's_daughter champions in the tourney. He was defeated in the first tilt by Ser Tybolt_Lannister, after six broken lances.

name: Androw Farman
bio: Androw Farman was a member of House Farman during the reign of kings Maegor_I_Targaryen and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He was the second son of Lord Marq_Farman of Fair Isle. Androw was a failed squire in his youth, possessing no skill at arms. His father had considered sending him to Oldtown to become a maester, until his own maester told him that Androw was simply not clever enough. From 43–47 AC, Marq Farman hosted Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) on Fair Isle. Rhaena and Androw reportedly grew more than fond of each other during the princess's stay at Faircastle. After the death of Rhaena's second husband, King Maegor_I_Targaryen, and the ascension of her brother, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, Queen Rhaena returned to Fair Isle in 48 AC. She and Androw married in the second week of 49 AC, in a quick and small ceremony. Lord Marq presided over the wedding, and Lord Lyman_Lannister, his wife, Lady Jocasta_Lannister, and two of Rhaena's friends, Samantha_Stokeworth and Alayne_Royce, attended the wedding. Rhaena was twenty-six and twice widowed, and Androw was seventeen. He was said to be utterly besotted with his new wife. As the second son of a minor lord, Androw was not considered particularly worthy of a former queen and the mother of the king's Aerea_Targaryen. When asked why she had chosen such an unpromising husband, Rhaena replied that he had been kind to her. Some wondered if perhaps Rhaena married Androw to repay Marq Farman for his kindnesses in sheltering her from King Maegor_I_Targaryen, for Lord Farman was fond of his son. However, Lord Farman's maester, Smike, speculated that the princess had wed Androw not because she loved him, but because she had fallen in love with his sister Elissa_Farman. Elissa, Rhaena, and Rhaena's other companions Alayne Royce and Samantha Stokeworth were very close, to the point they were called "the Four-Headed Beast". Rhaena would frequently take her ladies flying on the back of her dragon Dreamfyre. Though Androw was admitted within their circle, he was never considered a fifth head, and never went flying with Rhaena. It is possible that Rhaena offered, but Androw was not adventurous enough to agree. There were tensions between the couple and Androw's brother Ser Franklyn_Farman. A fortnight after Rhaena and Androw's first anniversary in 50 AC, Marq Farman died, and Franklyn became Lord of Fair Isle. He immediately commanded Androw and Rhaena to leave Fair Isle, but to leave Elissa behind. Androw, angered, offered to face his brother in single combat, but Rhaena gently dissuaded him, saying he was no match for Franklyn and she did not wish to be widowed again. Rhaena, Androw, and Elissa fled the island for Casterly Rock, along with Rhaena's entourage. At Casterly Rock, while Lord Lyman Lannister and his wife Jocasta were openly hospitable, it became apparent the couple were being spied upon. A castle septa asked if the marriage had ever been consummated, and who had witnessed the bedding. Ser Tyler_Hill, Lyman's bastard son, was openly scornful of Androw and tried to charm Rhaena. The Lannisters also suggested a way to repay them would be to give them one of Dreamfyre's eggs. Rhaena and her court took their leave of Casterly Rock and traveled the westerlands and riverlands, where Androw found himself forgotten and ignored by their hosts, and Rhaena discovered they all feared Dreamfyre or desired her eggs. In 51 AC, Rhaena visited King's Landing to attend the second wedding of her siblings King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen, and explained her difficulties. She was eventually granted Dragonstone as her own seat. However, on Dragonstone, Androw found life to be no better. Rhaena was called the "Queen in the East", but nobody considered him a king, or even a lord consort. He sat at his wife's side at meals, but did not share her bed; that honor went to her companions and favorites. They slept apart in different towers, and though the gossip at Dragonstone was that his wife told him he should find a pretty maid to share his bed, he never did. Having no skill at arms, he would remain abed while knights trained in the yard. He could neither read nor write, so could not enjoy the Dragonstone library. Though Androw could ride a horse passably well, and would trot around the yard, he was too timid to leave the castle to explore the island. Men at Dragonstone paid him no mind; the ladies laughed at him behind his back; servants sometimes ignored his commands. Children taunted him, the cruelest being his step-daughter Aerea_Targaryen, who once threw the contents of a chamber pot over his head just because she was angry at her mother. Androw drank a great deal, and was known to spend whole days in the Chamber of the Painted Table, moving painted wooden soldiers around the map. Rhaena's companions would joke that he was planning his conquest of Westeros. Androw's loneliness and discontent only grew worse after his sister Elissa Farman fled Dragonstone in 54 AC, as she had been his closest friend, perhaps his only friend. When it was later discovered Elissa had stolen three dragon eggs from the Dragonstone hatcheries, Rhaena summoned Androw and furiously demanded to know if he knew anything about his sister's betrayal. His tearful denials only enraged Rhaena further, and she found it hard to believe he had played no role in the crime. Androw offered to accompany her to see King Jaehaerys at King's Landing to report the theft of the eggs, but she scornfully refused, saying he was incapable of anything but falling off the dragon. After the theft, Rhaena dismissed Ser Merrell_Bullock, and Androw asked her to make him the new commander of the garrison in his place. She and her ladies burst out laughing at the request. When news arrived that Rhaena's Alyssa_Velaryon health was failing, Androw announced he would accompany Rhaena to Storm's End so he could comfort her, as a husband should. Rhaena refused his offer as she had before, and not gently. A loud argument preceded her departure. By the time Rhaena returned from her mother's deathbed, Androw no longer had any desire to comfort her. The marriage between the two, though never passionate, became loveless. Alayne Royce called it a mummer's farce, "and not an entertaining one." Sullen and cold, Androw sat silent at meals and avoided his wife's company. Rhaena was not bothered by his sulks, finding comfort in her ladies instead, both old and new. At the end of 54 AC, a mysterious epidemic came to Dragonstone. The elderly Maester Culiper was the first to die, followed by Rhaena's ladies Cassella_Staunton, Septa Maryam, Alayne Royce, and Samantha Stokeworth. Aside from Culiper, the disease only killed women, striking down Rhaena's friends and dear companions. The castle closed its gates to protect others, and the royal fleet quarantined the island. When Rhaena's young cousin Lianna_Velaryon died in her arms, Andrew saw her weeping and asked if she would weep for him the same way. Rhaena struck him across the face and told him to leave her, as she wished to be alone. He replied that she would be, as Lianna was the last of them. When King Jaehaerys and his small council discussed the epidemic, Lord Rego_Draz recognized the symptoms as those of poison, the tears of Lys. As soon as Rhaena read the message from King's Landing, she knew that the poisoner was Androw. Her guards tried to find him within the castle, and discovered Maester Anselm, stabbed in the back with a dagger. Androw was finally tracked down within the Chamber of the Painted Table, where he confessed to Rhaena that he had been jealous of her friendships with her ladies and tired of their mockery, and frustrated that he had never been seen as a true husband to her. Rhaena did not reply, but ordered her guards that Androw be gelded, and his penis and testicles fed to him. Androw leaped from the room's window, denying her revenge. Rhaena had his body cut into pieces and fed to her dragons. Afterwards, just the mention of his name would provoke her to fits of rage.

name: Androw Frey
bio: Androw Frey is a member of House Frey, a son of Ser Arwood_Frey and Ryella_Royce. He has a twin brother, Alyn_Frey, and two sisters, Ryella_Frey and Hostella_Frey.

name: Anguy
bio: Anguy, called the Anguy the Archer or simply Archer, is a commoner from the Dornish Marches who is skilled with the bow. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Anguy is portrayed by Philip McGinley.

name: Annara Farring
bio: Annara Farring was a noblewoman of House Farring, and the seventh wife of Lord Walder_Frey. They had six children. She is deceased. Though invited to the wedding of Annara and Lord Walder_Frey, Lord Hoster_Tully of Riverrun did not attend. Rumors around the Twins claim that Annara may have been sleeping with Walder's great-grandson, Walder_Frey_(son_of_Ryman), casting doubts on her children's parentage.

name: Anselm
bio: Anselm was a maester of the Citadel who served House Targaryen at Dragonstone during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 54 AC when a mysterious illness killed Maester Culiper and several of Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) close friends on Dragonstone, Maester Anselm sent a raven to King's Landing with a description of the disease. When the small council realized that the description matched the poison known as the tears of Lys, it sent a warning to the castle. Anselm was found in the rookery with a dagger in his back. Rhaena's alienated husband, Androw_Farman, poisoned his wife's friends in revenge against her, who he felt had belittled him.

name: Anson
bio: Anson was a maester from the Citadel of Oldtown. Anson wrote Truth, in which he expressed the belief that the idea that dragons can change sex at need is erroneous. According to Anson, this is a misunderstanding of the metaphor which was used by Septon Barth, when discussing the higher mysteries.

name: Antario Jast
bio: Antario Jast is the Lord and the head of House Jast in the westerlands. He is married to Lady Lanna_Lannister, a cousin to the main line of House Lannister. He has sons, but it is unconfirmed whether they are from Lanna or an earlier wife.

name: Anya Waynwood
bio: Anya Waynwood is the Lady of Ironoaks and head of House Waynwood. She is widowed and has three sons: Morton_Waynwood, Donnel_Waynwood, and Wallace_Waynwood, and an unspecified number of daughters, granddaughters, and grandsons. Her wards include the young Harrold_Hardyng, her cousin and the heir-presumptive of the Vale of Arryn, and Cynthea_Frey, whose mother was Carolei_Waynwood. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Anya is portrayed by Paola Dionisotti. Lord Nestor_Royce hoped to marry his daughter, Myranda_Royce, to Harrold_Hardyng, but Lady Waynwood refused. She does not want her ward to marry against his will. Myranda thinks that Harry might marry one of Anya's granddaughters.

name: Anya Weatherwax
bio: Anya Weatherwax was a member of House Weatherwax during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 133 AC, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Many maidens across Westeros came to King's Landing in hopes of marrying the young king. However, the event was not a true ball, but merely a procession of maidens, each woman curtsying before the king while a herald announced her name and lineage. Anya, only seven years old, was one of these maidens. She informed King Aegon that her horse was named Twinklehoof, and that she loved him very much. When Anya asked the king if he had a good horse, Lord Unwin_Peake grew impatient with her, and told her that "His Grace has a hundred horses".

name: Archibald Yronwood
bio: Archibald Yronwood, called Arch and the big man, is a knight from House Yronwood and a nephew to Anders_Yronwood, Lord of Yronwood. Archibald lost all his hair from a childhood malady. He was first called "the big man" by his cousin, Ser Cletus_Yronwood.

name: Archon of Tyrosh (War of the Ninepenny Kings)
bio: This Archon of Tyrosh, was the ruler of Tyrosh in the time preceding the War of the Ninepenny Kings. The ruling Archon of Tyrosh was deposed when the Band of Nine and its armies sacked Tyrosh at the behest of one of its members, the merchant prince Alequo_Adarys, was then installed as the ruler of the city. Adarys reigned until 266 AC, when he was poisoned by his queen. Afterward, the legitimate Archon was restored to power.

name: Archon's daughter (Jaehaerys I)
bio: The Archon's daughter was the daughter of the Archon of Tyrosh during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. She was the wife of Ser Orryn_Baratheon. Her given name is unknown. In 49 AC, the maiden came to King's Landing at the age of fifteen with her father, the Archon of Tyrosh, to attend the Golden Wedding. After the wedding, Lord Rogar_Baratheon proposed to the other members of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen's small council that she wed the young king, in order to forge an alliance with Tyrosh and extend the power of the Iron Throne to Essos. However, Queen Regent Alyssa_Velaryon argued that the smallfolk of Westeros would never accept a foreign woman with dyed hair as their queen. She felt a Tyroshi, who kept foreign gods, would also be strongly opposed by those pious to the Faith of the Seven. She had her own ideal candidates for Jaehaerys's bride, which other council members opposed, proposing their own suggestions as they argued for a month without reaching a consensus. In 50 AC, King Jaehaerys sentenced Ser Orryn_Baratheon to a ten-year exile for attempting to kidnap Princess Rhaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon). Orryn entered the service of the Archon in Tyrosh, and the following year he married the Archon's daughter. She soon gave birth to a girl, although some doubted that Orryn was the father.

name: Archon's green-haired daughter
bio: The Archon's green-haired daughter was the daughter of the Archon of Tyrosh during the reign of King Robert Baratheon. Her given name is unknown. Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne, planned to send his young daughter, Arianne_Martell, to Tyrosh to serve as a cupbearer to the Archon of Tyrosh, and to secretly meet with her betrothed, Prince Viserys_Targaryen. The Archon's green-haired daughter was sent to Dorne in exchange for Arianne. However, Doran abandoned the plan due to suicidal threats from Arianne's mother Mellario, who was already deeply upset that her son Quentyn_Martell had been sent away to be fostered.
