Arianne was left ignorant of the plan and of her secret betrothal, and played with the Archon's green-haired daughter in the pools of the Water Gardens, though she did not know the girl's parentage. When they played the shoulder-riding game, if Arianne rode the shoulders of her friend Garin_(orphan), nobody could defeat them, not even Nymeria_Sand and the Archon's green-haired daughter.

name: Ardrian Celtigar
bio: Ardrian Celtigar, known as the Red Crab, is the Lord of Claw Isle and the head of House Celtigar.

name: Areo Hotah
bio: Areo Hotah is the captain of the household guard at Sunspear for the Prince of Dorne, Doran_Martell. He is originally from the Free City of Norvos, where he was trained by the bearded priests. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Areo is portrayed by DeObia Oparei. Areo was born in Norvos as the youngest of six children, with two brothers and three sisters, and he was sold to the bearded priests at a young age. When he was sixteen years old, Areo completed his training. He was wedded to his axe and branded on his chest to signify the event. Areo became a guardsman to the family of Lady Mellario, and was present the day she first met her future husband, Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne. He escorted Mellario when she traveled with Doran to Dorne, having learned Westerosi speech. Upon arrival in Dorne, Areo found it a strange place; the Dornish women were lewd, Dornish wine was sour, and Dornish food was full of queer hot spices. He also thought the Dornish sun was hotter than the pale, wan sun of Norvos. Areo entered the service of House Martell, and Mellario married Doran. Areo watched all of Doran and Mellario's children grow up, and developed a father-like love for Princess Arianne_Martell, whom he refers to as "little princess." Despite this affection, however, he is utterly loyal to Prince Doran. Areo eventually became the captain of Prince Doran's guard, and remained in Dorne to serve the Martell's even after Mellario returned to Norvos. Even after all his years in Dorne, Areo still reminds himself of the vows he took when he was sixteen, "Serve. Obey. Protect." When Doran Martell moved from Sunspear to the Water Gardens due to his deteriorating health, Areo accompanied him. On occasion, Areo is sent to Sunspear by Doran.

name: Argella Durrandon
bio: Princess Argella Durrandon was the daughter and only child of Argilac_Durrandon, the last Storm King. After her father was killed in Aegon's Conquest, she married Orys_Baratheon, the first Lord of Storm's End, founding the new House Baratheon. Argilac_Durrandon, the Storm King, was afraid of the threat of Harren_Hoare, the King of the Isles and the Rivers. In 2 BC, he reached out to Aegon_I_Targaryen, Lord of Dragonstone, offering the hand of his only daughter, Argella, with all the lands east of the Gods Eye from the Trident to the Blackwater Rush as her dowry. Lord Aegon rejected the offer, as he had two wives already and did not need a third, and the lands offered had belonged to House Hoare for a generation. He countered with an offer of his own: those lands plus Massey's Hook, and the lands from the Blackwater south to the river Wendwater and the headwaters of the Mander. The pact would be sealed with the marriage of Argella to Orys_Baratheon, Lord Aegon's childhood friend and champion. King Argilac angrily rejected the terms, as Orys was rumored to be Aegon's bastard half-brother. Enraged, he had the hands of Aegon's envoy cut off and returned to him in a box, with the message, "These are the only hands your bastard shall have of me." This spurred the start of Aegon Targaryen's War of Conquest, where Aegon announced that he would be the only king of the Seven Kingdoms. King Aegon's wife, Queen Rhaenys_Targaryen, and his Hand of the King, Orys Baratheon, were sent to take Storm's End. In the battle called the Last Storm, Orys slew Argilac Durrandon in single combat, and the remaining stormlander army fled. At the approach of Orys Baratheon and the Targaryen host, Argella barred the gates of Storm's End and declared herself Storm Queen. When Queen Rhaenys flew Meraxes into the castle to parley, Argella announced that the defenders of Storm's End would die to the last man. However, her garrison refused to share the fate of Harrenhal and revolted, raising a peace banner and delivering Argella to Orys's camp, gagged, chained, and naked. Orys, however, removed the chains with his own hands, wrapped his cloak around her, and gave her food and wine. He spoke to her gently, telling her of her father Argilac's courage in death. Aegon rewarded Orys Baratheon with Storm's End, the rule of the Stormlands, and Argella. On his marriage to Argella Durrandon, and to honor Argilac's valor, Orys Baratheon adopted the sigil and words of House Durrandon as his own. Argella and Orys had at least a son, Davos_Baratheon.[N 1]

name: Argelle Stark
bio: Argelle Stark was a noblewoman of House Stark during the latter half of the second century after Aegon's Conquest. She was the younger daughter of Edric_Stark_(son_of_Cregan) and Serena_Stark.[N 1] Argelle was married to Lord Robard_Cerwyn, the Lord of Cerwyn. Together, the couple had children.

name: Argilac Durrandon
bio: Argilac Durrandon, also known as Argilac the Arrogant, was the last Storm King from House Durrandon who reigned in the stormlands before the Conquest by Aegon_I_Targaryen.

name: Argos Sevenstar
bio: Argos Sevenstar was an Andal warlord. Argos was one of many warlords during the coming of the Andals to Westeros. He led the great of the Andal threats to the north, but was smashed by an alliance between King Theon_Stark and House Bolton at the Battle of the Weeping Water. In the aftermath of victory, King Theon raised his own fleet and crossed the narrow sea to the shores of Andalos in western Essos, with Argos's corpse lashed to the prow of his flagship. Theon's northmen burned a score of villages, captured three tower houses and a fortified sept, and put hundreds to the sword. When he returned to the north, the Hungry Wolf planted the heads of the slain on spikes along his coasts as a warning for other potential invaders.

name: Argoth Stone-Skin
bio: Argoth Stone-Skin, called the Grey Giant, is a character in the legend of Maris_the_Maid. Among fifty lords, Argoth won Maris's hand in the first tourney held in Westeros. Maris wed King Uthor_of_the_High_Tower before Argoth could claim her, however, and Argoth spent the rest his life roaring for Maris outside the walls of Oldtown. It is unconfirmed if Argoth was a man or a giant.

name: Argrave the Defiant
bio: Ser Argrave the Defiant was a knight from Nunny in service to Lord Ambrose_Butterwell during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. During the Whitewalls tourney, Argrave was unhorsed by Ser Glendon_Flowers in his first tilt.

name: Arianne Martell
bio: Princess Arianne Nymeros Martell, better known simply as Arianne Martell, is a member of House Martell and is the eldest daughter of Doran_Martell, the ruling Prince of Dorne, and his consort, Lady Mellario of the Free City of Norvos. According to Dornish customs, she is the heiress of Sunspear, and the future ruling Princess of Dorne. Arianne is a POV character in A Feast for Crows and The Winds of Winter. Born in 276 AC, Arianne is the eldest child of her father, Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne, and his wife, Lady Mellario of Norvos. As her father's firstborn, she is Doran's heiress, according to Rhoynish customs and Dornish laws. Arianne spent several years of her early life at the Water Gardens. She has two younger brothers, but since Quentyn_Martell was sent away as a page to Yronwood at an early age and Trystane_Martell is eleven years younger than herself, Arianne has never been close to them. Instead, her best companions during childhood were Andrey_Dalt, Garin_(orphan) (whose mother had been her wet nurse), Sylva_Santagar, and her cousin Tyene_Sand. While playing the shoulder-riding game in the Water Gardens, Arianne sometimes rode the shoulders of Jeyne_Fowler or Jennelyn_Fowler, or her friend Frynne. Her favorite steed was Garin, since if she rode him, nobody could defeat them, not even Nymeria_Sand and the Archon's_green-haired_daughter. After breaking one of Garin's teeth, Arianne bought him a golden replacement. Tyene in particular was Arianne's best friend, since they were the same age, and they became close as sisters. Arianne and Tyene shared everything, including learning how to read, ride, and dance; they even shared a flagon of wine they had stolen at the age of ten, and an early attempted sexual experience with Andrey. Arianne's older cousin Nymeria joined Arianne and her four companions on occasion, and sometimes her cousin Sarella_Sand attempted to join them as well. Arianne crossed the Mander in the Reach when she and three of her cousins went to visit Tyene's mother. Arianne once held her young cousin, Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar), but she was so young at the time that she no longer remembers. Arianne does remember her great-uncle, Prince Lewyn_Martell of the Kingsguard, of whom she has cherished memories, and who used to tickle her until she could not stop laughing. When Robert's Rebellion ended, Arianne was seven years old. At the age of eight, Arianne became afflicted with redspots, and she learned that the disease, while harmless for children, could be lethal for adults. From a young age, Arianne was half in love with her uncle, Prince Oberyn_Martell, the father of her cousins. At some point, Oberyn brought Arianne, Garin, and her cousins Tyene and Sarella_Sand to the abandoned holdfast of Shandystone. While Oberyn showed Tyene the best way to milk a snake's venom and Sarella went exploring, Arianne sat on a rock, dreaming that a tall, hard robber knight, with black eyes and a widow's peak, had taken her there to have his way with her.

name: Arianne Tarth
bio: Arianne Tarth was a daughter of Lord Selwyn_Tarth of Evenfall. She died while still in the cradle.

name: Arlan of Pennytree
bio: Arlan of Pennytree was a hedge knight and the master of Dunk, the later Ser Duncan_the_Tall. Arlan's coat-of-arms was a winged chalice, silver on brown. It is unknown if he had any connection with House Hersy, whose arms also feature a winged chalice. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Arlan is portrayed by Danny Webb. Arlan was born in the village of Pennytree in the riverlands. When Arlan was a little boy, his grandfather took him to King's Landing, where he saw the last dragon. The dragon died a year later. In 193 AC, Ser Arlan unhorsed Lord Stokeworth and the Bastard_of_Harrenhal in a melee at King's Landing. Years prior to that, he had unhorsed Ser Damon_Lannister_(lord) in a tourney at Lannisport. In 200 AC, Arlan was defeated by Prince Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II), Prince of Dragonstone, in a tourney at Storm's End. Arlan later said he broke seven lances against Baelor, while Baelor remembered four lances. Arlan chose never to joust again after that prestigious showing. Arlan's nephew, Roger_of_Pennytree, was his squire. During the First Blackfyre Rebellion, Arlan and Roger fought beneath Lord_Hayford's banners, taking part in the Battle of the Redgrass Field, during which Roger was killed. Arlan was next to Lord Hayford when the lord was slain by Lord Gormon_Peake. At some later point Arlan met Duncan_the_Tall in the slums of Flea Bottom in King's Landing, and took him for his squire. The two of them remained together for years. Arlan and Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield were among knights hired by a Dornish merchant to escort him from Lannisport to the Prince's Pass. Arlan and Dunk also served at Highgarden for a time, and they also marched with a few peasant levies. Arlan once helped hang an outlaw who had been stealing rings. Arlan also served a minor lord in the stormlands in a regional dispute. Three years before the tourney at Ashford Meadow, Arlan and Dunk served Lord Dondarrion in the battle with the Vulture_King_(Daeron_II). Also three years before the Ashford tourney, Arlan spent a half year in service to Lord_Florent. Once, a drunken Arlan promised to take Dunk to a brothel, but the knight did not remember the promise once sober. Arlan died of a chill on the road leading to Ashford Meadow in 209 AC. Dunk buried Arlan's body on the western slope of a hill, facing the sunset. Dunk continued on to the tourney. At Ashford he claimed to Plummer that Arlan had knighted him as Ser Duncan the Tall before his passing, although there were no witnesses to the ceremony. Two years later, Duncan also claimed to Lady Rohanne_Webber that he had been knighted by Arlan.

name: Arlan I Durrandon
bio: Arlan I Durrandon, also known as Arlan the Avenger, was a Storm King of House Durrandon who ruled during the Age of the Hundred Kingdoms. Arlan extended the borders of the Kingdom of the Storm as far as the Blackwater Rush and the headwaters of the Mander. His great-grandson, Arlan_III_Durrandon, annexed the riverlands to the kingdom.

name: Arlan III Durrandon
bio: Arlan III Durrandon was the Storm King from House Durrandon who conquered the riverlands. Arlan married one of the daughters of Lord Roderick_Blackwood, wedding her beneath Raventree Hall's great weirwood. When Roderick rebelled against King Humfrey_I_Teague, Arlan supported Roderick and intended to restore the riverlands to the Blackwoods. Arlan's stormlanders crushed the Teagues in the Battle of Six Kings, but Roderick was slain in battle. Roderick's heir was a boy of only eight and Arlan distrusted Roderick's brothers. He suggested crowning Roderik's eldest child, Shiera_Blackwood, who was wed to his own son, but the riverlords rejected this suggestion. Arlan then decided to add the riverlands to the realm of the Storm Kings. Arlan was able to plant the banner of the Durrandons on the shores of the Sunset Sea. Arlan's successors were not able to hold his gains, however.

name: Arlan V Durrandon
bio: Arlan V Durrandon was a Storm King of the stormlands from House Durrandon during the Century of Blood. Arlan tried to take back the riverlands from House Hoare but died in the attempt.

name: Armen
bio: Armen, better known as Armen the Acolyte, is an acolyte at the Citadel. Armen has earned four links to his maester's chain: tin, lead, copper and pewter. It took him one year to earn each link.

name: Armen Peake
bio: Armen Peake was the Lord of Starpike, Dunstonbury, and Whitegrove, and the head of House Peake during Aegon's Conquest. During Aegon's Conquest, Lord Armen joined his strength to King Mern_IX_Gardener's host against Aegon_I_Targaryen. However, Armen and his sons perished in the resulting Field of Fire, along with King Mern IX and the male line of House Gardener. With Armen's death, the slow decline of House Peake began, as the Peakes were no longer favored by their new liege lords, House Tyrell. House Peake protested against the rise of the Tyrells on account of their own distinguished lineage. However, they found no success, perhaps because Lord Armen had taken up arms against House Targaryen on the Field of Fire.

name: Armistead Vance
bio: Armistead Vance was a member of House Vance and the mightiest of the Andal warlords that invaded the riverlands. Armistead was presumably one of the seven Andal kings who defeated Tristifer_IV_Mudd, King of the Rivers and the Hills. Afterward he gained the allegiance of Edmure_Tully_(father_of_Axel) and his sons. Armistead granted Edmure's son Axel_Tully lands at the juncture of the Red Fork and the Tumblestone, where Lord Axel raised Riverrun.

name: Armond Caswell
bio: Armond Caswell was the Lord of Bitterbridge and the head of House Caswell during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He had at least one son, Lord Joffrey_Caswell. Armond fought for the Targaryens during the First Blackfyre Rebellion. Ser Kyle claimed to have saved Lord Armond's life during the war.

name: Armond Connington
bio: Armond Connington was the Lord of Griffin's Roost and the head of House Connington during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. He was the father of Jon_Connington. Lord Armond often liked to take his son Jon_Connington up to the tallest tower of Griffin's Roost to show him the view of their ancestral lands. When Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen was still a boy, he visited Griffin's Roost after a trip to Dorne, Armond attempted to win the young prince to his side in an argument he had with Lord Morrigen. Armond died a few years before Robert's Rebellion and is buried beneath Griffin's Roost's sept.

name: Arnel
bio: Arnel was an archmaester of the Citadel and the author of Mountain and Vale.

name: Arneld
bio: Arneld is a knight in service at King's Landing. He may be a member of the gold cloaks.

name: Arnell
bio: Arnell is a knight and a brother of the Night's Watch. His mother was a green-apple Fossoway, but it is unknown if he is a legitimate son or a bastard.

name: Arnold Arryn
bio: Arnold Arryn, called the Mad Heir, was a knight from House Arryn and a claimant to the Eyrie. Ser Arnold was a first cousin of Jeyne_Arryn, the Lady of the Eyrie. In his youth, he served as a squire at Runestone. Arnold twice rose against his cousin to contest her inheritance, claiming that women were too soft to rule. After his second rising sometime before the Dance of the Dragons, he was imprisoned in one of the sky cells of the Eyrie. Jeyne died in 134 AC. In her last testament, she named her fourth cousin and her Knight of the Gate, Ser Joffrey_Arryn, as heir, though Arnold was much closer by blood. Though Arnold had gone mad during his time imprisoned in the sky cells, his sane, shrewd, and ambitious son, Ser Eldric_Arryn, came forward to press his father's claim. Many lords supported Arnold's claim, insisting that the laws of inheritance could not be put aside by "the whim of a dying woman." Additionally, a third claimant came forward in the person of Isembard_Arryn, head of House Arryn of Gulltown. Ser Corwyn_Corbray, one of the regents of young King Aegon_III_Targaryen, ruled that Jeyne's will must prevail and declared Joffrey the rightful Lord of the Eyrie. Isembard and his sons were imprisoned and Eldric was executed, but Arnold eluded Corwyn and fled to Runestone. When Corwyn arrived at the castle, a crossbowman of Runestone killed Corwyn after he drew Lady Forlorn against Lord Gunthor_Royce, Arnold's protector. Striking down the king's regent was an act of treason, and war began anew across the Vale of Arryn. Houses Royce, Templeton, Tollett, Coldwater, Dutton, and the lords of the Three Sisters supported Arnold's claim, though the Hand of the King, Lord Thaddeus_Rowan, commanded the lords supporting Arnold to lay down their arms. When no reply was forthcoming, the crown sent men through the Mountains of the Moon and launched a sea attack to support Joffrey's claim. By the time the new regents were chosen by lot in 136 AC, Lord Alyn_Velaryon and Lord Benjicot_Blackwood had at last forced the supporters of Isembard and Arnold to do homage to Lord Joffrey as their liege.

name: Arnolf Karstark
bio: Arnolf Karstark is a member of House Karstark and the uncle of Lord Rickard_Karstark. He is the castellan of Karhold, but the followers of Stannis_Baratheon refer to him as Lord Arnolf as a courtesy.

name: Aron Santagar
bio: Aron Santagar is a knight from House Santagar and the master-at-arms at the Red Keep.

name: Arra Norrey
bio: Arra Norrey was a noblewoman of House Norrey during the second century after Aegon's Conquest, and the first wife of Lord Cregan_Stark of Winterfell. Arra was a childhood friend of Cregan_Stark. Shortly after Cregan overthrew his regent uncle, Bennard_Stark, and took control of the north in 126 AC, she married him. In 128 AC, Arra died while giving birth to their only son together, Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan).

name: Arrana Stark
bio: Arrana Stark was a noblewoman of House Stark during the latter half of the second century after Aegon's Conquest. She was the older daughter of Edric_Stark_(son_of_Cregan) and Serena_Stark. Arrana was married to Osric_Umber, the Lord of the Last Hearth, and together they had children.

name: Arrec Durrandon
bio: Arrec Durrandon was a Storm King of the stormlands from House Durrandon who lost the wealthy riverlands to the Iron Islands. He was also the grandsire of the last Storm King, Argilac_Durrandon. Arrec was the last Storm King to rule the riverlands, an area conquered by his ancestor Arlan_III_Durrandon some three hundred years earlier. Harwyn_Hoare, King of the Iron Islands, used the mobility of ironborn longships to defeat the stormlanders in the riverlands. Arrec's greatest defeat was in the Battle at Fairmarket. Later in life, Arrec tried twice to cross the Blackwater and take back the lost lands, but failed. Arrec was succeeded by his eldest son, Arlan_V_Durrandon.

name: Arron
bio: Arron is a Night's Watch recruit. He is the twin brother of Emrick, and is from Fair Isle.

name: Arron Qorgyle
bio: Arron Qorgyle is a knight of House Qorgyle, the second son of Lord Quentyn_Qorgyle of Sandstone.

name: Arryk
bio: Arryk is one of the two personal guards of Olenna Tyrell, the dowager Lady of Highgarden. He is the twin of Erryk_(guard), her other guard. Lady Olenna cannot tell them apart, so she calls them Left and Right.

name: Arryk Cargyll
bio: Arryk Cargyll was a knight from House Cargyll. He and his twin brother, Ser Erryk_Cargyll, were members of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. During the Dance of the Dragons, Arryk sided with King Aegon_II_Targaryen, while his brother Erryk sided with Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Arryk is portrayed by Luke Tittensor. Ser Arryk and Ser Erryk_Cargyll, knights of the Kingsguard, were both unhorsed by Ser Criston_Cole at the tourney for King Viserys I's accession at Maidenpool in 105 AC. Arryk visited Dragonstone often during the reign of King Viserys, and was very familiar with the castle. According to Septon Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons), Arryk found Prince Daemon_Targaryen and Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen in bed together, a few months after the tourney to celebrate King Viserys's and Queen Alicent_Hightower fifth anniversary in 111 AC. Court fool Mushroom claims that Daemon was instructing his niece Rhaenyra so she could better seduce Ser Criston. This led to Viserys exiling Daemon from the Seven Kingdoms.

name: Lord Arryn (father of Jeyne)
bio: Lord Arryn was the Lord of the Eyrie and the head of House Arryn during the late reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Lord Arryn and his sons were killed by Stone Crows in 97 AC. He was succeeded as ruler of the Vale of Arryn by his daughter, Jeyne_Arryn. As Jeyne was three years old at the time of her father's death, Lord Yorbert_Royce ruled in her stead as regent and Lord Protector of the Vale during her minority.

name: Arsa Stark
bio: Arsa Stark was a noblewoman from House Stark during the latter half of the second century after Aegon's Conquest. She was the only daughter of Lord Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan) and his wife, Lady Alys_Karstark_(wife_of_Brandon). Arsa had two full older brothers, Rodwell_Stark and Beron_Stark and an older bastard brother as well, Lonnel_Snow.

name: Arson
bio: Arson Iceaxe was a wildling raider. Arson tried to cross the Wall by digging through it's base. When he was midway under the ice, he was discovered by a patrol from the Nightfort. Instead of trying to stop the wildling, they decided to wall him in alive. The legend says that by listening the ear against the Wall, one can still hear the sound of Arson's axe digging away at the ice. Since this time, the Night's Watch sends infrequent patrols along the base of the Wall on its northern face to look for traces of excavation.

name: Arstan Selmy
bio: Arstan Selmy is the Lord of Harvest Hall and the head of House Selmy.

name: Arthor Celtigar
bio: Arthor Celtigar was a member of House Celtigar during the Dance of the Dragons. When riots broke out in King's Landing, Arthor was in the manse of his kinsman, Lord Bartimos_Celtigar, who served as master of coin to Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen. The building was overrun by a mob, and Arthor made a brave stand in a doorway, holding the mob at bay briefly with a sword, until a treacherous serving girl let rioters in from a back way. Arthor was slain when he was stabbed in the back with a spear wielded by one of the throng.

name: Arthor Karstark
bio: Arthor Karstark is a member of House Karstark and the second son of Arnolf_Karstark.

name: Arthor Oakheart
bio: Arthor Oakheart was a knight of House Oakheart during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Arthor took part in a tourney celebrating Jaehaerys's tenth year on the Iron Throne, and was unhorsed by the young Ser Ryam_Redwyne of the Kingsguard.

name: Arthur Ambrose
bio: Arthur Ambrose is the Lord and the head of House Ambrose. He is married to Alysanne_Hightower and has a son named Alyn_Ambrose.

name: Arthur Dayne
bio: Arthur Dayne, also known by his title as the Sword of the Morning, was a famed and legendary knight from House Dayne and a member of Aerys_II_Targaryen's Kingsguard. Many considered him to have been the most chivalrous warrior of the Seven Kingdoms. Arthur was Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen's closest friend. He was the brother of Lady Ashara_Dayne and Lady Allyria_Dayne. His nephew, the son of Arthur's unknown Lord_Dayne_(father_of_Edric), is Lord Edric_Dayne. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Arthur is portrayed by Luke Roberts. Arthur wielded the greatsword called Dawn as part of his office as Sword of the Morning, carrying it slung across his back. To defeat the Kingswood Brotherhood, he gained the trust of the smallfolk of the kingswood by paying for what he and his forces took and taking their grievances before King Aerys_II_Targaryen. In the end Arthur slew the Smiling_Knight in single combat and ended the threat of the outlaws. Following that victory, he knighted Jaime_Lannister, who would later become a sworn brother of the Kingsguard. Arthur was the champion in the tournament in honor of Viserys's birth in 276 AC, defeating Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen. In another tourney, Arthur broke twelve lances against Rhaegar, losing to the prince.[N 1] At the tourney at Harrenhal in 281 AC, he was defeated by Rhaegar once again. According to a semi-canon source, Arthur, along with his other sworn brother Ser Oswell_Whent, aided Rhaegar in the kidnapping of Lyanna_Stark near Harrenhal as the year of the false spring was ending. Robert's Rebellion began shortly after. After the end of the rebellion, Arthur, Oswell, and Ser Gerold_Hightower were found by Lord Eddard_Stark at the tower of joy. Arthur died there, fighting Eddard and his companions, but the exact circumstances of his death are unclear. The recorded history describes Arthur as being killed by Lord Eddard in single combat, however. Bran_Stark recalls his father, Eddard, describing Arthur as the finest knight he ever saw and elaborating further that Arthur would have killed him if not for Howland_Reed. Stones from the tower were used to build his cairn upon a nearby ridge. Arthur is remembered as not only a skilled fighter but a valiant and true knight. After Arthur's death, Eddard rode to Starfall, the seat of House Dayne, to return the sword Dawn to Arthur's sister, Lady Ashara_Dayne, as a sign of respect.

name: Arthur Goodbrother
bio: Arthur Goodbrother was a member of House Goodbrother. In 134 AC during Lady Johanna_Lannister's invasion of the Iron Islands, Arthur slew Ser Leo_Costayne, the lord admiral of the Reach, on Great Wyk.

name: Artos the Strong
bio: Artos the Strong is one of the many legendary heroes of the riverlands, whose tales date back to the Age of Heroes. It is unknown if Artos is related to House Strong, a family from the riverlands with First Men origin.

name: Artos Flint
bio: Artos Flint is the second son of Lord Torghen_Flint, known as The Flint, or Old Flint clan chief of the first Flints, a mountain clan in the North. He is the half-brother of Black Donnel_Flint.

name: Artos Stark
bio: Artos Stark, also known as Artos the Implacable, was a member of House Stark during the reign of King Maekar_I_Targaryen. He was the third son of Lord Beron_Stark and Lady Lorra_Royce. When King-Beyond-the-Wall Raymun_Redbeard went over the Wall in 226 AC, Lord Willam_Stark called his banners and marched north. Artos was among those who went with him. During the battle at Long Lake, Willam was killed. Artos slew Raymun during the battle, ending the wildling threat. The Night's Watch arrived too late to take part in the battle and for their tardiness, Artos ordered them to dispose of the dead. Although he was never Lord of Winterfell, he has a statue in the crypts beneath Winterfell.

name: Artys I Arryn
bio: Artys I Arryn was the first King of Mountain and Vale and the founder of House Arryn in Westeros. Prior to his coronation, Ser Artys, called the Falcon Knight, was an Andal knight who defeated the First Men of the Vale in the Battle of the Seven Stars. He is sometimes combined in tales with the Winged Knight, a legendary hero of the Vale from thousands of years before the Andal invasion. Artys was born of pure Andal blood in the Vale in the shadow of the Giant's Lance. On his shield he bore the moon-and-falcon and a pair of falcon's wings decorated his silver warhelm, earning him the nickname of the Falcon Knight. He was esteemed as the finest warrior of his day, skilled with sword, lance and morningstar. Artys was a cunning and resourceful leader, beloved by all who fought beside him. After King Robar_II_Royce united the First Men of the Vale, the Mountains of the Moon, and the Fingers under his rule and struck against the divided Andal houses and petty kings, the Andals unified under the leadership of Ser Artys, who was of an age with King Robar. He led the Andal army in the Battle of Seven Stars, taking part in its final stage. Artys led a force of over five hundred knights through a goat track on the Giant's Lance to take the already weakened First Men in the rear, leaving a decoy, who was slain by Robar, among the main fighting force. It is possible he slew Robar himself, though other candidates have been put forth over the centuries. After the Andal victory, the First Men houses bent the knee to Ser Artys, who was proclaimed King of Mountain and Vale, thus founding the Arryn dynasty. In later centuries, singers would conflate him with the legendary "Winged Knight".

name: Arwen Upcliff
bio: Arwen Upcliff was a lady of House Upcliff who married Alester_II_Arryn, King of Mountain and Vale. Arwen's marriage to Alester_II_Arryn brought the Witch Isle, seat of House Upcliff, into the realm of House Arryn.

name: Arwood Frey
bio: Arwood Frey is a knight of House Frey, the only son of Ser Hosteen_Frey and Bellena_Hawick. He is married to Ryella_Royce and they have four children, Ryella_Frey, Androw_Frey, Alyn_Frey and Hostella_Frey.

name: Arwyn Frey
bio: Arwyn Frey is the sixth daughter of Lord Walder_Frey. She is the first born and eldest daughter of Lord Walder and his seventh spouse, Lady Annara_Farring. However according to Merrett_Frey, Walder_Frey_(son_of_Ryman) was rumored to be sleeping with Lady Annara casting doubts on the parentage of her children.

name: Arwyn Oakheart
bio: Arwyn Oakheart is the head of House Oakheart and the Lady of Old Oak. Her husband is deceased and gave her several sons, including the youngest, Ser Arys_Oakheart, a member of the Kingsguard of King Robert_I_Baratheon since 290 AC.

name: Arya Flint
bio: Arya Flint was a noblewoman of House Flint of the mountains. She was married to Rodrik_Stark_(son_of_Beron), the fifth son of Lord Beron_Stark, with whom she had two daughters; Branda_Stark and Lyarra_Stark. Arya died before her grandson Eddard_Stark was born.

name: Arya Stark
bio: Arya Stark is the third child and second daughter of Lord Eddard_Stark and Lady Catelyn_Stark. A member of House Stark, she has five siblings: brothers Robb_Stark, Bran_Stark, Rickon_Stark, half-brother Jon_Snow, and older sister Sansa_Stark. Arya is one of the major POV characters in A Song of Ice and Fire. Like some of her siblings, Arya sometimes dreams that she is a direwolf. Her own direwolf is Nymeria_(direwolf), who is named in reference to the Rhoynar warrior-queen Nymeria. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Arya is portrayed mainly by Maisie Williams, and by various additional actors when she is wearing other faces, including Hattie Gotobed, Sabrina Bartlett, and David Bradley. Born in 289 AC at Winterfell, Arya is the youngest daughter and third child of Lord Eddard_Stark, the head of House Stark and Warden of the North, and Lady Catelyn_Stark. Arya has an older sister, Sansa_Stark, an older brother, Robb_Stark, and two younger brothers, Bran_Stark and Rickon_Stark. She also has an older bastard half-brother, Jon_Snow. Arya has spent her entire life at Winterfell, though she did accompany her father on two occasions to White Harbor. Arya has been tutored by Septa Mordane in the womanly arts (including needlework), and received lessons from Maester Luwin as well. Arya was also taught to ride a horse, but due to being called "Arya Horseface" by Jeyne_Poole because of her long face, she could not enjoy the fact that riding horses was one of the few things she was better at than her older sister. The House Stark guards call her "Arya Underfoot". Jon_Snow, having covered himself with flour to appear as a ghost, once tried to scare his younger siblings in the crypt of Winterfell. While Sansa and Bran were frightened, Arya instead punched her half brother. Lord Eddard often eats in the same hall as his staff, and always keeps a seat next to him reserved, inviting a different servant or advisor to dine with him each night. Arya loves listening to their stories.

name: Arys Oakheart
bio: Arys Oakheart is a knight from House Oakheart and a member of the Kingsguard of Robert_I_Baratheon. Arys is the youngest son of Lady Arwyn_Oakheart of Old Oak. He becomes a POV character in A Feast for Crows. Arys always dreamed of joining the Kingsguard. His dream became reality by 290 AC.[N 1]

name: Ash
bio: Ash is one of the children of the forest in service to the last greenseer. Their true name is too long for the human tongue, according to Leaf.

name: Asha Greyjoy
bio:
Asha Greyjoy is a member of House Greyjoy, and is the daughter of Lord Balon_Greyjoy by his wife, Alannys_Harlaw. She becomes a POV character in A Feast For Crows. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, she is renamed Yara Greyjoy to avoid confusion with the wildling Osha, and portrayed by Gemma Whelan. Asha was fairly unattractive in her youth. Her uncle, Victarion_Greyjoy, remembers Asha as a little girl swimming naked in the sea and playing with her doll. Asha's eldest siblings, Rodrik_Greyjoy and Maron_Greyjoy, died during Greyjoy's Rebellion, and her younger brother Theon_Greyjoy departed for Winterfell to be Eddard_Stark's hostage and ward. Asha thus became Lord Balon_Greyjoy's only remaining child in Pyke. Asha had her first flowering during the war. Asha's mother, Lady Alannys_Harlaw, fostered five boys at Pyke after Theon's departure. Asha experimented with Tristifer_Botley, the boy closest to Asha in age, during puberty, although his feeling were stronger than hers. Tris was sent away to Blacktyde after Maester Qalen caught the youths together. Asha grew into an attractive and bold young woman, and Balon effectively raised his wild and headstrong daughter as his heir. However, Asha preferred Ten Towers, the seat of her uncle Rodrik_Harlaw, to Pyke. She lost her virtue at age 16 to a sailor from Lys. 
In 293 AC or 294 AC Asha sailed Black Wind on a trading voyage which included stops at Fair Isle, Lannisport, and the Arbor, where she introduced peaches to one of her lovers, Qarl_the_Maid. Asha was away raiding Lysene pirates at the Stepstones when her father exiled her uncle, Euron_Greyjoy, from the Iron Islands in 297 AC for having impregnated Victarion's Victarion_Greyjoy's_third_wife. Asha has visited Tyrosh in her travels.

name: Ashara Dayne
bio: Ashara Dayne was a noblewoman of House Dayne and a sister of Ser Arthur_Dayne, the Sword of the Morning. She committed suicide shortly after the end of Robert's Rebellion. Ashara was a lady-in-waiting to Princess Elia_Martell, the wife of Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen. She had not been long at court by the false spring of 281 AC. She was present at the tourney at Harrenhal that year, where she danced with a knight of the Kingsguard, Prince Oberyn_Martell, and Lord Jon_Connington. After Brandon_Stark approached her on behalf of his younger brother Eddard_Stark, Ashara danced with Eddard as well. According to Allyria_Dayne, Ashara's younger sister, Ashara and Eddard Stark fell in love at Harrenhal. Ser Barristan_Selmy of the Kingsguard was also in love with Ashara, although he never told her so. Barristan recalls that, had he won the tourney, he would have named Ashara his queen of love and beauty. According to Barristan, Ashara was "dishonored" by a man at Harrenhal. In addition, Barristan states that Ashara "looked" at "Stark" following the final tilts at the tourney, although he does not identify the Stark in question. Ashara's brother Arthur_Dayne died the war, fighting during the combat at the tower of joy. Eddard traveled to Starfall to return Arthur's greatsword Dawn to House Dayne. Some time after his visit, Ashara jumped from the top of the Palestone Sword, one of the towers of Starfall, into the sea. Several characters have cited possible reasons for Ashara's decision to commit suicide: a broken heart, because of her stillborn daughter, because of a stolen child, for the "man who had dishonored her at Harrenhal", or due to grief over Arthur's death. Her body was never found. At Winterfell, Eddard Stark's soldiers spread the tale that Ashara was the mother of Eddard's bastard son Jon_Snow. When Eddard's wife, Catelyn_Stark, heard these stories from her maids, she confronted Eddard, who refused to confirm or deny anything regarding Jon's mother, and subsequently silenced all talk of Ashara at Winterfell. Her name was never mentioned at the castle again.

name: Lord Ashford
bio: Lord Ashford was the Lord of Ashford and the head of House Ashford during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Lord Ashford had two sons, Ser Androw_Ashford and Ser Robert_Ashford, and Lord_Ashford's_daughter. In A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, he is portrayed by Paul Hunter. In 209 AC Lord Ashford organized the tourney at Ashford Meadow for the thirteenth name day of Lord_Ashford's_daughter. The Lord of Ashford gave his chambers to Prince Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II), Hand of the King, during the tourney. According to a semi-canon source, Lord Ashford hoped to curry favor with Baelor by hosting the tourney. Alongside princes from House Targaryen, Lord Ashford watched the competition from a viewing stand. His sons, Ser Androw_Ashford and Ser Robert_Ashford, were defeated in the lists but competed nobly against Ser Tybolt_Lannister and Ser Lyonel_Baratheon, respectively. Lord Ashford enraged Prince Aerion_Targaryen by declaring Ser Humfrey_Hardyng the victor of their bout and awarding him Aerion's courser. This tourney entered history by becoming the theater of the first trial of seven in more than a hundred years. Mud landed on Lord Ashford and his daughter when Ser Duncan_the_Tall shook the defeated Aerion before the viewing stand. Baelor Breakspear was slain during the trial.

name: Lord Ashford's daughter
bio: Lord Ashford's daughter, called the fair maid, was a lady from House Ashford and the daughter of Lord_Ashford. Her given name is unknown. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, she is named Gwin Ashford and is portrayed by Cara Harris. In 209 AC, Lord_Ashford hosted the tourney at Ashford Meadow to celebrate his daughter's thirteenth name day. Five champions wore a wisp of orange silk as her favor and defended her position as the reigning Queen of Love and Beauty; these included her brothers Ser Androw_Ashford and Ser Robert_Ashford, Lord Leo_Tyrell_(Longthorn), Ser Humfrey_Hardyng, and Prince Valarr_Targaryen. Lord Ashford and his daughter watched the tourney from a viewing stand alongside princes of House Targaryen. During the trial of seven, mud landed on Lord Ashford and the fair maid when Ser Duncan_the_Tall shook the defeated Prince Aerion_Targaryen before the viewing stand.

name: Assadora of Ibben
bio: Assadora, also known as Assadora of Ibben, is an Ibbenese prostitute at the Happy Port in Braavos.

name: Aubrey Ambrose
bio: Aubrey Ambrose was a knight from House Ambrose during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Aubrey supported Daemon_I_Blackfyre during the First Blackfyre Rebellion.

name: Aubrey Crakehall
bio: Aubrey Crakehall was a knight from House Crakehall who briefly ruled as King of the Iron Islands. The Shrike, a priest of the Drowned God, led a revolt against King Harmund_III_Hoare which replaced him with his younger brother, Hagon_Hoare. Their mother, the Dowager Queen Lelia_Lannister, was mutilated and sent back to Casterly Rock. Her nephew, the King of the Rock, raised his banners in vengeance and Ser Aubrey commanded the westermen in the war. In the seventh year of fighting, Aubrey defeated the army of Hagon the Heartless on Great Wyk. He considered restoring the throne to Harmund, who had been found in the dungeon of Hoare Castle, but instead gave the broken man the gift of mercy. Aubrey had Hagon mutilated the same way that Lelia had been, and then had him hanged. Aubrey also ordered that Hoare Castle be razed. Instead of returning to the westerlands, however, surprisingly Aubrey declared himself King of the Iron Islands. The Lannisters did not support this decision, and King Aubrey was overthrown by the ironborn after less than six months. The Shrike drowned him as a sacrifice to the Drowned God.

name: Aurane Waters
bio: Aurane Waters, called the Bastard of Driftmark, is a bastard member of House Velaryon. He is the half-brother of Monford_Velaryon, Lord of the Tides and Master of Driftmark.

name: Aurion
bio: Aurion was one of the few dragonlords who survived the Doom of Valyria. According to histories of Qohor, he raised forces from Qohorik colonists and declared himself first Emperor of Valyria. Atop his dragon, he flew south to lay claim to what remained of Valyria and reestablish the Freehold along with his thirty thousand men. Neither Emperor Aurion nor his host were ever seen again.

name: Axel Tully
bio: Axel Tully was a head of House Tully and the first Lord of Riverrun. His father was Ser Edmure_Tully_(father_of_Axel), a turncoat who supported the Andal warlord Armistead_Vance after the death of Tristifer_IV_Mudd, King of the Rivers and the Hills. Axel received a grant of lands at the juncture of the Red Fork and the Tumblestone from Armistead Vance. There, he raised a red castle he named Riverrun.

name: Axell Florent
bio: Axell Florent is a knight of House Florent who serves as the castellan of Dragonstone for his nephew-in-law, Lord Stannis_Baratheon. He is the younger brother of Lord Alester_Florent of Brightwater Keep. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Axell is merged with Alester Florent and portrayed by James McHale. By 299 AC, Axell had been castellan of Dragonstone for ten years while Lord Stannis_Baratheon has been away in King's Landing to serve on the small council of King Robert_I_Baratheon.

name: Ayrmidon
bio: Ayrmidon is the author of Engines of War, a Valyrian scroll hard to find in its entirety in Westeros. A complete version can be found in the Winterfell library.

name: Azor Ahai
bio: Azor Ahai was a legendary hero who wielded a burning sword called Lightbringer, according to tales from Asshai and followers of R'hllor. In some other cultures this warrior is called Hyrkoon the Hero, Yin Tar, Neferion, and Eldric Shadowchaser. Melisandre has mentioned the prince that was promised and Azor Ahai interchangeably, although she tends to use the name Azor Ahai far more often. It is unknown if Azor Ahai is connected with the Westerosi legend of the Last_hero, who lived during the Long Night and helped to defeat the Others. When Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen was young, he and Maester Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) believed that Rhaegar was the prince that was promised. When he was older, Rhaegar came to believe that his infant son, Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar), fit the prophecy, since a comet was seen above King's Landing when the boy was born.

name: Azzak zo Ghazeen
bio: Azzak is a boy from the House of Ghazeen, one of the noble ruling families of Meereen.

name: Bael the Bard
bio: Bael the Bard was a King-Beyond-the-Wall. According to legend, he was one of the greatest free folk raiders of his time, a man who outwitted the northmen and impregnated the daughter of Lord Brandon_Stark_(the_daughterless). He was also a singer who wrote songs about his own life, which are still sung to this day. There are questions as to whether Bael ever truly existed. While the wildlings say he did, attributing many of their songs to him, the old chronicles of Winterfell do not mention him at all. Whether this was due to the defeats and humiliations he was said to have visited upon them or because he never existed is a matter of debate.

name: Baelish (hedge knight)
bio: The grandfather of Petyr_Baelish was a hedge knight of the Vale of Arryn and the founder of House Baelish. His given name is unknown. His Baelish_(sellsword) was a Braavosi sellsword in the service of Lord Corbray, and he took the head of the Titan of Braavos as his sigil when he was knighted.

name: Baelish (lord)
bio: The father of Petyr_Baelish was "the smallest of small lords" in the Vale of Arryn. His first name is unknown. His Baelish_(sellsword) was a Braavosi Baelish_(sellsword) in the service of Lord Corbray, and his Baelish_(hedge_knight) was a hedge knight who took the head of the Titan of Braavos as his sigil when he was knighted. During the War of the Ninepenny Kings, Lord Baelish became friends with Lord Hoster_Tully. He later "cashed in" on the friendship to have his son Petyr_Baelish fostered at Riverrun. Lord Baelish died several years before the start of the series and was succeeded by his son, Petyr.

name: Baelish (sellsword)
bio: The great-grandfather of Petyr_Baelish was a Braavosi sellsword that came to the Vale of Arryn in the service of Lord Corbray. His line was continued by his Baelish_(hedge_knight), who became a hedge knight and took the head of the Titan of Braavos as his sigil. His first name is unknown.

name: Baela Targaryen
bio: Lady Baela Targaryen, also known as Baela Velaryon, was a daughter of Prince Daemon_Targaryen and Lady Laena_Velaryon. She had a younger twin sister, Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon), and two younger half-brothers, Aegon_III_Targaryen and Viserys_II_Targaryen. She was a dragonrider who rode Moondancer. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Baela is portrayed by Bethany Antonia and Shani Smethurst (young).

name: Baelon Targaryen
bio: Baelon Targaryen was a member of House Targaryen during the Century of Blood, the second son of Lord Aerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon) of Dragonstone. He had one older brother, Aelyx_Targaryen and one younger brother Daemion_Targaryen. Baelon inherited the lordship of Dragonstone from Aelyx and was himself succeeded by Daemion. King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen named Prince Baelon Targaryen, the second of his sons who survived past infancy, after Lord Baelon, who was himself a second son.

name: Baelon Targaryen
bio: Prince Baelon Targaryen was the secondborn son of King Viserys_I_Targaryen and his wife, Queen Aemma_Arryn. Queen Aemma_Arryn died giving birth to Baelon. He was named after his grandfather, Prince Baelon the Brave, but the infant died one day after his birth. While the court was grieving and bereft over the deaths of Baelon and Aemma, Prince Daemon_Targaryen was observed in the Street of Silk with his highborn cronies, making drunken japes about the "heir for a day". When word of this reached King Viserys_I_Targaryen, he became livid and named his daughter, Rhaenyra_Targaryen, as his heir.

name: Baelor Blacktyde
bio: Baelor Blacktyde is the Lord of Blacktyde and the head of House Blacktyde. Baelor captains the Nightflyer. Baelor's father, Lord_Blacktyde, died during the rebellion launched by Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands. After the war, Baelor spent eight years as a hostage in Oldtown. By the time he came back to the Iron Islands, he had converted to the Faith of the Seven. To this day, Baelor still resents Balon for his father's death, calling him "Balon the Widowmaker". Balon sent Tristifer_Botley to ward with Baelor after Tris was caught sexually experimenting with Balon's daughter, Asha_Greyjoy.

name: Baelor Hightower
bio: Baelor Hightower, also known as Baelor Brightsmile, is a knight from House Hightower. He is the eldest son and heir of Lord Leyton_Hightower of Oldtown. Baelor is wed to Rhonda_Rowan. Prince Oberyn_Martell and Princess Elia_Martell visited Oldtown on their way to Casterly Rock where their Princess_of_Dorne_(mother_of_Doran) was to broker their marriage to the Lannister twins, Cersei_Lannister and Jaime_Lannister. While there they met Baelor, whom Oberyn considered Elia's best suitor. Even though he liked Baelor more than the others, he called him 'Baelor Breakwind' after he farted in their presence and Elia could no longer look at him without laughing.

name: Baelor I Targaryen
bio: Baelor I Targaryen, known as Baelor the Beloved and Baelor the Blessed, was the ninth Targaryen king to reign on the Iron Throne as Lord of the Seven Kingdoms. Known for his piety, Baelor was a septon as well as a king. He was the second son of King Aegon_III_Targaryen and Queen Daenaera_Velaryon, and ruled after the death of his older brother, King Daeron_I_Targaryen. Prince Baelor Targaryen was born in 144 AC, the second child of King Aegon_III_Targaryen and Queen Daenaera_Velaryon. He had an older brother, Prince Daeron_I_Targaryen, and three younger sisters, the princesses Daena_Targaryen, Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_III), and Elaena_Targaryen. Baelor's uncle, Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, served as Aegon's Hand of the King, and he had three children, Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen, Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II), and Princess Naerys_Targaryen. In 157 AC, King Aegon III Targaryen died of consumption, and his eldest son Daeron took the throne. Prince Viserys continued to serve as Daeron's Hand, ruling in King's Landing while Daeron led a conquest of Dorne. In 160 AC, Baelor married his sister, Princess Daena, when he was sixteen and she was fifteen. However, he refused to consummate their marriage. In an attempt to shame him, Daena, who usually dressed all in black, swore to wear nothing but white until she was properly bedded. The plan did not work, as Baelor liked to see his sister all in white, feeling that it made her look more innocent.

name: Baelor Targaryen
bio: Prince Baelor Targaryen, known as Baelor Breakspear, was a member of House Targaryen and the Prince of Dragonstone. He was the eldest son and heir of King Daeron_II_Targaryen and Queen Myriah_Martell. Named after his Aegon_IV_Targaryen's cousin, King Baelor_I_Targaryen, Prince Baelor served as his father's Hand of the King after the Battle of the Redgrass Field until his death in 209 AC. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Baelor is portrayed by Bertie Carvel. Prince Baelor was born in 170 AC, the first child of King Daeron_II_Targaryen and Myriah_Martell. After the death of his grandfather, King Aegon_IV_Targaryen, in 184 AC, Prince Baelor became the heir to the Iron Throne and was later given the title of Prince of Dragonstone by his father. Baelor learned how to fight under the Red Keep's master-at-arms, Ser Quentyn_Ball, alongside his brothers and the Great Bastards. In 187 AC, the seventeen-year-old Baelor won the name "Breakspear", following his famous victory at his Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_IV)'s wedding tourney, where he defeated Ser Daemon_I_Blackfyre, Daeron's bastard-born half-brother, in the final tilt.

name: Black Balaq
bio: Black Balaq is a sellsword in service to the Golden Company, in command of the company's archers.

name: Balder
bio: Balder was a maester of the Citadel in service to the Night's Watch at Eastwatch-by-the-Sea near 400 BC. He wrote the book The Edge of the World when Osric_Stark served as Lord Commander.

name: Baldric I Durrandon
bio: Baldric I Durrandon, known as Baldric the Cunning, was a Storm King of the stormlands from House Durrandon. During the Andal invasion of the stormlands, the Andals conquered part of Cape Wrath. To prevent them from conquering all of the rainwood, Baldric I manipulated the petty Andal kings and warlords into attacking each other.

name: Baldrick
bio: Baldrick was a septon of the Faith of the Seven during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. After King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen wed his sister, Alysanne_Targaryen, Jaehaerys sent seven preachers to walk the realm and win the small folk over to the marriage, not with swords, but with words. Cunning and quick-witted Septon Baldrick was among these seven. They traveled the realm alone, walking from village to village, telling the smallfolk of Jaehaerys's wisdom and Alysanne's kindness. When challenged on the issue of incest, the seven would say the Valyrians mounted dragons and thus were not like other men, and as such, an exception had to be made.

name: Balerion Otherys
bio: Balerion Otherys was the youngest of the three bastard children of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen and his fourth mistress, Bellegere_Otherys. After Princess Naerys_Targaryen had almost died, giving birth in 161 AC, her husband, Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen was sent to Braavos as an envoy, so Naerys would have sufficient time to recover. In Braavos, Aegon met Bellegere Otherys, with whom he started an affair. Aegon and Bellegere had an affair for ten years, during which Bellegere gave birth to three children: Bellenora_Otherys, Narha_Otherys and Balerion. Their paternity remains doubtful, as Bellegere is said to have had a husband in every port she visited.

name: Ballabar
bio: Ballabar is the maester assigned to House Redwyne in the Arbor.

name: Balman Byrch
bio: Balman Byrch is a knight from House Byrch and the husband of Lady Falyse_Stokeworth, the heir to Stokeworth. Ser Balman was once a renowned tourney jouster, and in his youth he was considered one of the most handsome knights. He jousted in the tourney at Lannisport in 289 AC after Greyjoy's Rebellion, as well as in a tourney at Duskendale in 294 AC. Balman has been married to Falyse for ten years, and they have no children. It is rumored that Balman shuns his wife's bed, preferring maidens.

name: Balon Blackskin
bio: Balon Blackskin is a legendary ironborn raider who supposedly lived during the Age of Heroes. According to legend, Balon fought with an axe in his left hand and a hammer in his right. He was impervious to injuries from any man-made weapon: swords glanced off leaving no mark, while axes shattered against his skin.

name: Balon Botley
bio: Balon Botley is the eldest son of Lord Germund_Botley.

name: Balon Greyjoy
bio: Balon Greyjoy is the Lord of the Iron Islands and the head of House Greyjoy of Pyke. He is the father of Asha_Greyjoy and Theon_Greyjoy, as well as is the elder brother of Euron_Greyjoy, Victarion_Greyjoy, and Aeron_Greyjoy. The captain of the Great Kraken, Balon is a follower of the Old Way of the ironborn and desires to bring them back to prominence. In 289 AC, Balon declared himself King of the Iron Islands and proclaimed the independence of the Iron Islands from the Iron Throne, but Greyjoy's Rebellion was defeated by King Robert_I_Baratheon's forces. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Balon is portrayed by Patrick Malahide. Balon is the eldest surviving son of Quellon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands, and a lady of House Sunderly. It is not clear when he was born. Balon learned to sail when still young and was a very skilled captain. Balon scaled the Flint Cliffs to the Blind_Lord's haunted tower when he was ten. By his thirteenth year, he had become an expert oarsman. At fifteen he sailed with Dagmer to the Stepstones on a reaving, killing his first man and taking his first two salt wives there. By seventeen he captained his own longship. Later he often went raiding and was known for his fierceness and fearlessness. Balon and two of his brothers, Euron_Greyjoy and Victarion_Greyjoy, wanted their father Quellon to join Robert's Rebellion. Quellon was peaceful and cautious, however, and desired neutrality. After Crown Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen was slain in the Battle of the Trident, Quellon was convinced by his sons to join the rebellion or else gain nothing from the war. The ironmen's contribution to the war turned out to be minimal. Quellon was slain fighting the longships of the Shield Islands in a battle at the Mander, after which Balon brought the Greyjoy ships back to Pyke so he could claim the Seastone Chair. When Balon returned to Pyke he found out his younger brother, Urrigon_Greyjoy, had died of gangrene after being injured in the finger dance. The maester at Pyke had tried to save the hand of the boy, but his potions had failed and the fourteen-year-old Urrigon lost his life instead. Balon commanded the same treatment be used on the maester, who died along with Balon's stepmother. Balon is married to Alannys_Harlaw, who gave him three sons and a daughter: Rodrik_Greyjoy, Maron_Greyjoy, Asha_Greyjoy, and Theon_Greyjoy. In 297 AC one of Balon's brothers, Euron_Greyjoy, seduced the Victarion_Greyjoy's_third_wife of another brother, Victarion_Greyjoy. To prevent bloodshed between the brothers Balon sent Euron into exile and swore he would be killed if he returned. To keep his honor Victarion killed his wife.

name: Balon V Greyjoy
bio: Balon V Greyjoy, known as Coldwind, was a High King of the Iron Islands from House Greyjoy. Balon V was chosen at a kingsmoot. During his reign he destroyed the feeble fleets of the King in the North.

name: Balon Swann
bio: Balon Swann is a knight from House Swann and the second son of Lord Gulian_Swann of Stonehelm.

name: Balon Tawney
bio: Balon Tawney is the Lord and the head of House Tawney on Orkmont in the Iron Islands.

name: Bambarro Bazanne
bio: Bambarro Bazanne was a Lysene magister who had custody of Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen during the time he was presumed dead by those in Westeros. After Sharako_Lohar captured Prince Viserys after the Battle in the Gullet, he sold him to Bambarro Bazanne in exchange for the prince's weight in gold and a promise of support. With Lohar soon murdered and the Triarchy beginning to collapse, Magister Bambarro thought it prudent to keep his prize hidden away. Magister Bambarro forbade Viserys from leaving his manse, though he gave the prince his own suite, had the prince sup with him and his family, and had a tutor instruct him in many skills. It is believed that Bambarro's intent was to wait out the Dance of the Dragons and either ransom him to his mother Rhaenyra_Targaryen or sell his head to his uncle, King Aegon_II_Targaryen, depending who emerged as the victor. As Lys suffered defeats as the Daughters' War ensued, Bambarro died in 132 AC when the sellsword company he led against Tyrosh turned against him over a matter of back pay. Upon his death, it was discovered he was enormously in debt. His creditors seized his manse, sold his wife and children into slavery, and all his possessions (including Prince Viserys) passed to Lysandro_Rogare.

name: Bandy
bio: Bandy is the twin sister of Shyra and daughter of Joseth, the master of horse at Winterfell.

name: Bannen
bio: Bannen is a ranger of the Night's Watch at Castle Black.

name: Barba Bolton
bio: Barba Bolton was a member of House Bolton during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 133 AC, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Many maidens across Westeros came to King's Landing in hopes of marrying the young king. However, the event was not a true ball, but merely a procession of maidens, each woman curtsying before the king while a herald announced her name and lineage. Barba was one of these maidens, but she took the opportunity to ask Aegon that if he did not choose her, to at least send food to the north, as the region was suffering greatly from winter.

name: Barba Bracken
bio: Barba Bracken was a noblewoman of House Bracken during the reigns of kings Baelor_I_Targaryen, Viserys_II_Targaryen, and Aegon_IV_Targaryen. She was the fifth of Aegon the Unworthy's mistresses. In 161 AC, King Baelor_I_Targaryen imprisoned his sisters — the Princesses Daena_Targaryen, Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_III), and Elaena_Targaryen — within the Maidenvault in the Red Keep. The three princesses were kept in comfortable confinement, and to gain favor with Baelor, lords and knights sent their maiden relatives to the Maidenvault to be their lady companions. Near the end of Baelor's reign, one of these maidens was Barba Bracken, daughter to Lord Bracken of Stone Hedge. When King Baelor I died in 171 AC, King Viserys_II_Targaryen released the princesses from their confinement, and sixteen-year-old Barba met Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen, who became entranced with her. When King Viserys II died in 172 AC, Prince Aegon ascended the throne as King Aegon IV Targaryen, named Barba's father his Hand of the King, and openly took Barba as his mistress. Barba gave birth to a bastard son of Aegon, Aegor_Rivers, only a fortnight before Queen Naerys_Targaryen nearly died in childbirth. With Naerys lingering near death, Barba's father began to speak openly of Barba marrying King Aegon IV. However, Queen Naerys recovered, and the talk about Barba marrying the king became a scandal. Princes Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II) and Daeron_II_Targaryen, Naerys's brother and son, forced King Aegon to send both Barba and her bastard son away from court. They returned to Stone Hedge, where Barba raised her son Aegor. After Barba, King Aegon IV took Melissa_Blackwood as his mistress, who was younger, prettier, and more modest. In response, Barba and her father began grooming Barba's younger sister, Bethany_Bracken, to replace Melissa. This eventually worked in 177 AC, when Bethany caught Aegon's eye while he was visiting Stone Hedge to see Aegor. However, in 178 AC, Bethany was caught abed with the handsome knight of the Kingsguard, Ser Terrence_Toyne, and both Bethany and Lord Bracken were executed.

name: Barbara Bracken
bio: Barbara Bracken is a noblewoman of House Bracken, the first daughter of Lord Jonos_Bracken of Stone Hedge by his first wife.

name: Barbrey Dustin
bio: Barbrey Dustin, née Ryswell, is the Lady of Barrowton as the widow of Willam_Dustin, Lord of Barrowton. She was born into House Ryswell as the younger daughter of Rodrik_Ryswell, Lord of the Rills. Barbrey is the surviving daughter of Rodrik_Ryswell, Lord of the Rills, and is the sister of Roger_Ryswell, Rickard_Ryswell, and Roose_Ryswell. Their sister was Bethany_Bolton, the late second wife of Roose_Bolton, Lord of the Dreadfort. Brandon_Stark, the eldest son of Rickard_Stark, Lord of Winterfell, often rode the Rills while being fostered at Barrowton with old Lord Dustin. Infatuated with the heir of Winterfell, young Barbrey had an affair with Brandon, who took her maidenhead. Lord Rodrik hoped to wed his daughter to a Stark, but Lord Rickard betrothed his heir to Catelyn_Stark of Riverrun. Barbrey claims Brandon did not want to wed Catelyn and that the betrothal was the idea of Walys_Flowers, Rickard's maester at Winterfell. Rodrik then hoped to wed Barbrey to Brandon's younger brother, Eddard_Stark, but that was not to be. Barbrey instead wed Willam_Dustin, the young Lord of Barrowton. The abduction of Lyanna_Stark by Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen led to the executions of Brandon and Lord Rickard Stark by King Aerys_II_Targaryen in King's Landing. Now Lord of Winterfell, Eddard returned to the north to call his banners for Robert's Rebellion. Not yet half a year into marriage, Willam rode off to war against Barbrey's objections. Barbrey gifted him with a fine red steed, the pride of her father's herd, and Willam swore he would return to her mounted on the horse. However, Lord Dustin was slain in the combat at the tower of joy, and Ned built cairns for the fallen. After the war ended, Lord Stark returned Willam's steed to Barbrey. She bears a grudge against Eddard, who brought the bones of his sister, Lyanna, to Winterfell for burial in its crypt, but not the bones of her husband, Lord Dustin, to Barrowton. In accordance with the Widow's Law, Barbrey remained at Barrowton after her husband's passing and rules the town in her own name. She was fond of her sister Bethany's son, Domeric_Bolton, who served at Barrowton for four years as her page. Since Domeric's death in 297 AC, Barbrey has nursed suspicions about Ramsay_Snow's involvement.

name: Barneby
bio: Barneby is a maester of the Citadel of Oldtown who researched the First Men. His translations of runes support the claim of House Blackwood that they had once ruled most of the wolfswood before being driven from the north by the Kings of Winter.

name: Barquen Peake
bio: Barquen Peake, known as Barquen the Besieger, was a knight and an illustrious and legendary ancestor of House Peake.

name: Barra
bio: Barra is a bastard daughter of King Robert_I_Baratheon and an Barra's_mother. She is being raised in Chataya's brothel in King's Landing. Barra's_mother was a young virgin prostitute, who became pregnant when King Robert Baratheon visited Chataya's brothel. She named her daughter "Barra" after him.

name: Barra's mother
bio: Barra's mother  is a prostitute from Chataya's brothel in King's Landing, whose infant daughter is one of King Robert_I_Baratheon's bastard children. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, she is named Mhaegen and portrayed by Antonia Christophers.

name: Barre
bio: Barre is a septon who administers the sept at Dragonstone.

name: Barris Hightower
bio: Barris Hightower was a head of House Hightower and Lord of the Hightower. Barris succeeded his father, Lord Triston_Hightower. Barris gave the first crystal crown to the High Septon.

name: Barristan Selmy
bio: Barristan Selmy, called Barristan the Bold, is a knight from House Selmy and the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard of Robert_I_Baratheon. A celebrated hero, he has served in the Kingsguard since a time when these knights were still considered to be the greatest of the Seven Kingdoms. Barristan becomes a POV character in A Dance with Dragons. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Barristan is portrayed by Ian McElhinney. Barristan was born the first son of Ser Lyonel_Selmy. He squired for Ser Manfred_Swann in his youth. At the age of ten, Barristan donned the armor of a mystery knight and entered a tourney at Blackhaven. Prince Duncan_Targaryen took pity on the small mystery knight while others laughed. Duncan jousted Barristan when no one else would and it was Duncan who gave Barristan his epithet. Again competing as a mystery knight, Barristan received his knighthood at the age of sixteen from King Aegon_V_Targaryen after unhorsing Prince Duncan and Ser Duncan_the_Tall, the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, during the winter tourney at King's Landing. An eminently successful tourney knight throughout his career, Barristan also proved his worth on the battlefield. During the War of the Ninepenny Kings Barristan slew Maelys I Blackfyre in single combat, ending the male line of the Blackfyre Pretenders. Barristan cut a bloody path through the Golden Company to reach Maelys. The remaining members of the Band of Nine had little to no interest in conquering Westeros, and soon returned to their own domains. Barristan's deeds during the war gained him undying fame and his name was well known throughout the Seven Kingdoms afterwards. Barristan was named to the Kingsguard at the age of twenty-three and swore his vows before Ser Gerold_Hightower, while King Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen bestowed the white cloak on him. In joining the Kingsguard, he gave up the claim to his family seat, and the girl he was to wed was married to his cousin instead.

name: Barron Durrandon
bio: Barron Durrandon, also known as Barron the Beautiful, was a Storm King of House Durrandon and the successor of Durran_XI_Durrandon. Barron is remembered for having yielded up territories his Monfryd_I_Durrandon had conquered and more.

name: Barsena Blackhair
bio: Barsena Blackhair is a pit fighter famed in Meereen. Her origin is unknown, but she supports the pit fighting culture of the city. It is said that Barsena has slain every woman she has faced in the fighting pits of Meereen in the last eight years.

name: Barth
bio: Barth was a septon who served as Hand of the King for King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Barth was a blacksmith's son. He was given to the Faith of the Seven when he was young, and eventually took a septon's vows. Barth served at first at Highgarden. Thanks to his brilliance, in 50 AC Barth was sent to King's Landing to see to the library of the Red Keep. According to legend, Barth traveled on his own on a donkey. In 51 AC, Barth officiated the second marriage of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen, and along with Septon Oswyck he aided the king in writing the Doctrine of Exceptionalism. After Oswyck died in early 54 AC, Barth took over his duties. By 54 AC, Barth had become one of Jaehaerys's most trusted advisors. When the High_Lickspittle died that year, Jaehaerys entertained the idea of trying to persuade the Most Devout to name Barth to the office; Barth personally persuaded him not to do so. Barth was horrified by the suggestions of influencing the choosing with gold, force of arms, and secret assassinations, and wrote down an account on how King Jaehaerys managed to influence the choosing by traveling to Oldtown, expressing regret about the corruption among the Most Devout. When Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan) resigned as Hand of the King, King Jaehaerys at first looked at Barth, among others, to fill the office. However, due to his low birth, and due to the fact that it was suspected that Barth was more loyal to the Faith than to the Iron Throne, he was not chosen. In 55 AC, Barth, King Jaehaerys, Queen Alysanne, Grand Maester Benifer, and Lord Albin_Massey began creating the first unified code of law for the entire Seven Kingdoms. It would take them decades, and Barth alone contributed three times more than any other on his Great Code. When the severely-ill Princess Aerea_Targaryen returned to King's Landing in 56 AC on Balerion after having been missing for more than a year, Barth helped Benifer care for her. He announced to Jaehaerys and Alysanne, who waited outside the Grand Maester's chambers, when Aerea had died. Barth wrote two accounts on Aerea's death, which speak about the horrors that came forth from the girl, and in which he speculates about what had happened to her. They were kept in Barth's private papers, and were only discovered a hundred years later. The incident with Aerea left a strong impression on Barth, and afterwards he began research that eventually led to his book Dragons, Wyrms, and Wyverns: Their Unnatural History.

name: Barth
bio: Barth is the brewer at Winterfell. Barth's wife took Theon_Greyjoy's virginity.

name: Barthogan Stark
bio: Barthogan Stark, also called Barth Blacksword, was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He was the fourthborn son of Lord Cregan_Stark, the third by his last wife, Lady Lynara_Stark. Barthogan became Lord of Winterfell after the death of his brother Jonnel_Stark, who had no issue. Barthogan was killed when Skagosi rose up against Winterfell during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Barthogan is entombed in the crypts of Winterfell. He was succeeded as Lord of Winterfell by his younger brother, Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan).

name: Bartimos Celtigar
bio:
Bartimos Celtigar was the Lord of Claw Isle and the head of House Celtigar during the Dance of the Dragons. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Bartimos is portrayed by Nicholas Jones. Lord Bartimos was a member of Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen's black council during the Dance of the Dragons. He urged the queen to use her numerical advantage in dragons to take King's Landing from King Aegon_II_Targaryen, but Rhaenyra agreed with the cautious Prince Daemon_Targaryen.
After Rhaenyra seized control of King's Landing, Bartimos was named lord treasurer and master of coin on her small council. Despite the fact that King Viserys_I_Targaryen had left the treasure vaults full of gold upon his death, the treasury was completely empty by the time Rhaenyra took the capital, for the greens had sent three quarters of the gold away for safekeeping and spent the rest prior to the fall of the capital. Rhaenyra was in desperate need of coin and to rectify this, Lord Bartimos put in place the ruinous taxes his ancestor Lord Edwell_Celtigar once enacted, although Bartimos went a step further. Taxes on wine and ale were doubled, port fees tripled. Every shopkeeper was assessed a fee to keep his doors open. Innkeepers were required to pay a silver stag for each bed in their inn. The entry and exit fees once put on the seven city gates by Rego_Draz, the "Lord of Air", were brought back, but at triple the cost. A property tax was put in place that smallfolk and wealthy alike were obliged to pay depending on how much space they took up. Bartimos even made executions a source of coin, by having them take place in the Dragonpit and charging those wishing to see the spectacle three pennies. Merchants and traders who couldn't pay had their property, ships, or cargo seized. Though these taxes replenished Queen Rhaenyra's coffers, they quickly made both the queen and Lord Bartimos despised by the populace. After the Treasons of Tumbleton by the Two Betrayers, Bartimos urged Rhaenyra to arrest the other two baseborn dragonriders, Ser Addam_Velaryon and Nettles. He later argued for a tax to be placed for any child born out of wedlock, which would help fill the royal coffers and would discourage more bastards being born. When riots broke out in King's Landing, Lord Bartimos's manse was overrun by a mob, his servants turned on him or fled, and his kinsman Arthor_Celtigar was cut down. Bartimos himself was bound to a post and tortured until he revealed all the locations of his wealth. Then a tanner named Wat_the_Tanner declared to Bartimos that he had not paid the "cock tax" and thus his manhood was forfeit. Wat the Tanner later rode through the streets upon a stolen horse, displaying Lord Bartimos's severed head and genitals to the crowds and declaring an end to all taxes.

name: Bartimus
bio: Bartimus is an old knight, the chief gaoler and castellan of the Wolf's Den. Ser Bartimus lost his leg to an unmanned horse falling on it and the Maester's saw. Wyman_Manderly gave the Wolf's Den to him as a reward for saving his life at the Battle of the Trident.

name: Bass
bio: Bass is a member of the Night's Watch at Castle Black, where he acts as kennelmaster.

name: Bastard of Blackhaven
bio: The Bastard of Blackhaven was a bastard of House Dondarrion during the reign of the Storm King Argilac_Durrandon. His given name has not been published, although "Storm" is the surname for bastards of noble origin in the stormlands. The Bastard of Blackhaven and Dickon_Morrigen were slain by the dragonflame of Meraxes while leading the vanguard of Argilac during the Last Storm.

name: Bastard of Harrenhal
bio: The Bastard of Harrenhal was a bastard of House Lothston during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. His given name has not been published, although "Rivers" is the traditional surname for bastards of noble origin in the riverlands. He was defeated by Ser Arlan_of_Pennytree in the melee at a tourney at King's Landing in 193 AC. In 209 AC, Ser Duncan_the_Tall was trying to prove his knightly status in order to participate in the tourney at Ashford, claiming that he was knighted by his late master, Ser Arlan of Pennytree. Lord_Ashford said they had no proof of Arlan's existence or, if he did exist, that Duncan ever squired for him. However, Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II), Prince of Dragonstone, stated that he recalled Ser Arlan, citing the tourney at King's Landing where he defeated the Bastard of Harrenhal, as well as from other tourneys. Prince Baelor asked Duncan further questions to prove his story, which he answered correctly.

name: Bastard's bastard
bio: The bastard's bastard is how Victarion_Greyjoy refers to a thrall and oarsman in the Iron Fleet. He's the bastard son of a bastard.

name: Bathi Ma Lo
bio: Empress Bathi Ma Lo was the wife of God-Emperor Lo_Doq, called Lo Lackwit, ruler of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti. Some suggest Bathi was the true ruler behind her simpleton husband, who despite his affliction ruled wisely for more than thirty years.

name: Bayard Norcross
bio: Bayard Norcross is a knight of House Norcross.

name: Bean
bio: Bean was a crossbowman during the Dance of the Dragons. Bean claimed to have killed Syrax in the aftermath of the Storming of the Dragonpit. One of Rhaenyra_Targaryen's loyalists cut his tongue.

name: Beans
bio: Beans is a sellsword and member of the Windblown. His true name is Baqq which means Beans.

name: Bearded woman
bio: A bearded woman is a slave owned by Yezzan_zo_Qaggaz. She is a part of Yezzan's grotesquerie, one of his "special treasures".

name: Beastmaster
bio: The Beastmaster is a name the Windblown, a sellsword company hired by Yunkai, mockingly gave to a Yunkish nobleman commanding some of the Yunkai slave solders.

name: Beatrice Butterwell
bio: Beatrice Butterwell was a noblewoman from House Butterwell during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Beatrice was the closest friend of Princess Viserra_Targaryen. In 87 AC, the princess complained to Beatrice that her brother, Prince Baelon, showed no desire in marrying her after the death of his wife, their sister Princess Alyssa_Targaryen, and told Beatrice how she believed herself to be far more beautiful than Alyssa.

name: Bearded Ben
bio: Bearded Ben is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Beardless Dick
bio: Beardless Dick is a man of the riverlands.

name: Becca
bio: Becca is a former lover of Robert_I_Baratheon.

name: Becca the Baker
bio: Becca, better known as Becca the Baker, is an inhabitant of the Riverlands.

name: Beck
bio: Goodman Beck was an inhabitant of Donnelwood.

name: Bedwyck
bio: Bedwyck, nicknamed Giant due to his short height, is an experienced ranger of the Night's Watch. He is the smallest of the black brothers. Bedwyck is the son of a crofter. He was sent to the Wall for poaching, and has been a Night's Watch ranger for over a dozen years.

name: Belandra
bio: Belandra is a servant of House Martell at Sunspear. Belandra was once a bedmaid to Lady Mellario.

name: Belaquo Bonebreaker
bio: Belaquo Bonebreaker is a pit fighter famed in Meereen. His origin is unknown, but he supports the pit fighting culture of the city. Belaquo wields a flail.

name: Beldecar
