bio: Beldecar is the author of the History of the Rhoynish Wars. A copy can be found in the Red Keep's library. Beldecar's book is considered to be without equal in describing the Rhoynish Wars.

name: Beldon
bio: Beldon was a maester of the Citadel who served at Casterly Rock for Lord Gerold_Lannister and later his son Lord Tytos_Lannister. Beldon wrote in detail about the rivalry between Ellyn_Reyne and Jeyne_Marbrand, the lady wives of Ser Tion_Lannister and Tytos_Lannister, two sons of the Lord of Casterly Rock, Gerold_Lannister. According to a semi-canon source, Beldon surmised that Lady Ellyn named her two daughters by her Walderan_Tarbeck, after the late Rohanne_Webber and Cerelle_Lannister_(daughter_of_Tybolt) of Lord Gerold, who both died mysteriously, to hurt him. Beldon was relieved when Ser Jason_Lannister_(son_of_Gerold), another son of Lord Gerold, left Casterly Rock for Feastfires after his marriage to Marla_Prester as he could have caused problems for Lord Tytos.

name: Lord Belgrave
bio: Lord Belgrave was the head of House Belgrave during the reign of King Baelor_I_Targaryen. His given name has not been published. History recorded Lord Belgrave as being a proud man. He was known to have been commanded by King Baelor_I_Targaryen to wash the ulcerous feet of a beggar.

name: Belicho
bio: Belicho was one of the ruling triarchs of the Free City of Volantis. Belicho was a renowned Volantene patriot whose famous exploits are recorded in the series The Life of the Triarch Belicho. His unbroken succession of conquests and triumphs ended rather abruptly when he was eaten by giants.

name: Belicho Staegone
bio: Belicho Staegone is a candidate to become a triarch of Volantis. He appears to have been named after a legendary triarch, Belicho.

name: Belis
bio: Wisdom Belis was a member of the Alchemists' Guild of King's Landing during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. With Wisdoms Rossart and Garigus, Belis took part in King Aerys's wildfire plot during Robert's Rebellion. According to Ser Jaime_Lannister, Belis was as enthusiastic about the plan as the other two pyromancers. The three secretly deployed wildfire caches across the whole city, to be ignited at the command of the king. A couple of days after he had killed Rossart and Aerys at the Red Keep, Jaime hunted down Belis and Garigus and slew them as well. Belis tried to bribe Jaime with gold in exchange for his life.

name: Bella
bio: Bella is a young prostitute at the Peach in Stoney Sept. She is a bastard daughter of King Robert_I_Baratheon. According to Leslyn, Bella's mother was the favorite prostitute of Lord Robert_I_Baratheon at the Peach when he hid in Stoney Sept during Robert's Rebellion. Bella is named after the Battle of the Bells, which occurred when Lord Jon_Connington attacked the town in search of the future king.

name: Bellegere Otherys
bio: Bellegere Otherys, also known as the Black Pearl of Braavos, was a smuggler, trader, occasional pirate, and the captain of the Widow Wind. Bellegere was born of the union between the son of a Sealord of Braavos and a Summer Islander princess.[N 1] After Princess Naerys_Targaryen almost died in 161 AC after giving birth, King Baelor_I_Targaryen sent her husband, Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen, to Braavos on a diplomatic mission. Aegon met Bellegere in the Free City. The Black Pearl's ensuing affair with the prince continued for ten years, though it was said that she had a husband in every port and that Aegon was one of many.

name: Bellegere Otherys
bio: Bellegere Otherys is the courtesan in Braavos called the Black Pearl. She is descended from the first Black Pearl of Braavos, also named Bellegere_Otherys, and Aegon_IV_Targaryen. Her mother, Bellonara_Otherys, was the previous Black Pearl.

name: Bellena Hawick
bio: Bellena Hawick is a noblewoman of House Hawick and the wife of Ser Hosteen_Frey, sixth son of Lord Walder_Frey of the Crossing. They have one child, Ser Arwood_Frey.

name: Bello
bio: Bello is the author of the book The End of the Tall Men.

name: Bellenora Otherys
bio: Bellenora Otherys was the eldest of the three bastard children of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen and his fourth mistress, Bellegere_Otherys. After Princess Naerys_Targaryen had almost died giving birth in 161 AC, her husband, Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen was sent to Braavos as an envoy, so Naerys would have sufficient time to recover. In Braavos, Aegon met Bellegere Otherys, with whom he started an affair. Aegon and Bellegere had an affair for ten years, during which Bellegere gave birth to three children: Bellenora, Narha_Otherys and Balerion_Otherys. Their paternity remains doubtful, as Bellegere is said to have had a husband in every port she visited. Bellenora became a courtesan and took the name of "Black Pearl of Braavos", acclaimed by singers of her day as the most beautiful woman in all the world. The courtesan tradition was continued by her daughter.

name: Bellonara Otherys
bio: Bellonara Otherys, once the Black Pearl of Braavos, is the mother of Bellegere_Otherys_(Courtesan), the courtesan who is currently called the Black Pearl of Braavos. Bellonara had been the Black Pearl of Braavos before her daughter, Bellegere_Otherys_(Courtesan). Bellonara had succeeded the position from her own mother, who had been a Black Pearl as well, and whose own mother, in turn, had been a Black Pearl too.

name: Belthasar Bolton
bio: Belthasar Bolton was a member of House Bolton who participated in the Rape of the Three Sisters. The Chronicles of Longsister state that during the Rape of the Three Sisters by the Kings of Winter, Belthasar had a "Pink Pavilion" made from the flayed skins of one hundred Sistermen.

name: Belwas
bio: Belwas, commonly known as  Strong Belwas, is a eunuch former pit fighter and slave. His nickname comes from the fighting pits of Meereen, where he used to fight. Belwas claims he has never lost a fight. He allows his opponents to cut him once before they die, so his scars can be counted to see how many he has slain. Belwas was a slave bred and trained in Meereen. He is a veteran pit fighter who earned a reputation in the fighting pits of Meereen. He clams to have killed many Dothraki in the fighting pits, and that their bells jingle when they die. Belwas was eventually sold from Meereen to Qohor, and from there to Pentos. His latest owner is Illyrio_Mopatis, whom he describes as a "fat man with sweet stink in his hair".

name: Ben
bio: Ben is a young boy from the riverlands. He is an orphan.

name: Ben
bio: Big Belly Ben was a famous member of the Kingswood Brotherhood. During battle against the forces of King Aerys_II_Targaryen, Ben was about to smash in the head of Lord Sumner_Crakehall. Jaime_Lannister rescued Lord Sumner, but Ben was able to escape.

name: Ben Beesbury
bio: Ben Beesbury, called Blind Ben, was the Lord of Honeyholt and the head of House Beesbury during the reign of King Robert_I_Baratheon. Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne, attempted to betroth his daughter, Princess Arianne_Martell, to Ben, a minor lordling who was blind, toothless, and eighty years old at the time. To Arianne's relief, Ben died a few years later before the betrothal was completed. According to semi-canon sources, Ben had one son named Alton_Beesbury. Alton joined the Citadel to become a maester and renounced his inheritance despite Ben's protests. Upon Ben's death, he was succeeded by his nephew, Warryn_Beesbury.

name: Ben Blackthumb
bio: Ben Blackthumb is an old blacksmith in the service of Lady Shella_Whent of Harrenhal. Ben worked for Lady Shella_Whent's father and grandfather, as well as for the last lord of House Lothston.

name: Ben Bones
bio: Ben Bones is a kennelmaster at the Dreadfort sworn to House Bolton. He is one of the Bastard's Boys.

name: Ben Bushy
bio: Ben Bushy, known as Big Ben Bushy, is a knight of House Bushy.

name: Ben Buttercakes
bio: Ben Buttercakes was the owner of Hogs Head, a disreputable inn in Bitterbridge, during the Dance of the Dragons. The disguised Rickard_Thorne of the Kingsguard and his charge, Prince Maelor_Targaryen, tried to stay overnight in the Hogs Head while journeying to the safety of Lord Ormund_Hightower's host at the nearby siege of Longtable. Ben Buttercakes instructed his stableboy, Sly_(stableboy), to search for coins in Rickard's belongings, but Sly found Maelor's dragon egg wrapped in Rickard's white cloak instead. When he informed the inn's common room of his discovery, Rickard fled the inn with Maelor, slaying Ben on his way out.

name: Ben Coldwater
bio: Ben Coldwater is a knight from House Coldwater.

name: Ben Plumm
bio: Ben Plumm, better known as Brown Ben Plumm, is a descendant of House Plumm, and the captain and commander of the Second Sons. Ben's mother is a Dothraki, and his grandmother was half-Ibbenese and half-Qohorik. His grandfather was killed by a Dothraki prior to Ben's birth. Ben says he is part Braavosi, Summer Islander, Ibbenese, Qohorik, Dornish, Dothraki, and Westerosi. He claims to have a drop of Targaryen blood, which must be why dragons, particularly Viserion, have an affinity for him. He states an Ossifer_Plumm of House Plumm married a "Elaena_Targaryen". He vaguely says that the wedding took place in the reign of Aegon_IV_Targaryen. Daenerys_Targaryen points out that there were five Aegons who reigned in Westeros and asks which one, but Ben fails to remember the regnal number requested. Tyrion_Lannister suspects him to be a distant descendant of a younger son of Viserys_Plumm and a possible distant relation to the famous Ossifer_Plumm, and that Ben thus has "two drops" of dragon blood, not just one, as Viserys Plumm was rumored to have been fathered by King Aegon_IV_Targaryen on Princess Elaena_Targaryen, Ossifer's wife. Ben once served as a bodyguard to the uncle of Oznak_zo_Pahl in Meereen, before joining the Second Sons. When someone Ben knew was killed by Oznak for looking at a woman the wrong way, Ben fled, escaping the city through its sewer system, an experience that haunts him. While plundering after his first battle with the Second Sons, Plumm took some leather armor from a dead sellsword, and discovered a fortune of coins the man had sewn into the lining of the armor. In his naivety, Ben told a fellow sellsword whom he believed a friend. The fellow sellsword told their sergeant, and his colleagues relieved Ben of his fortune. Ben loves gold, but this incident made him realize that it is useless if one is not alive to enjoy it. Ben claims to have fought in a hundred battles, but he does not claim to have fought bravely in all of them.

name: Bendamure
bio: Bendamure was a maester and the author of Six Times to Sea: Being an Account of the Great Voyages of Alyn Oakenfist. He may or may not have been the maester at Driftmark during Lord Alyn_Velaryon's time.

name: Benedar Belmore
bio: Benedar Belmore is the Lord of Strongsong and the head of House Belmore.

name: Benedict
bio: Archmaester Benedict serves at the Citadel in Oldtown. Benedict insists that there never was a War of the Five Kings because Renly_Baratheon was dead when Balon_Greyjoy crowned himself.

name: Benedict Blackmont
bio: Benedict Blackmont was the last king of House Blackmont in Dorne. Benedict was defeated in Nymeria's War and was one of the six kings sent to the Wall by Nymeria.

name: Benedict Broom
bio: Benedict Broom is a knight of House Broom, who serves as the master-at-arms at Casterly Rock.

name: Benedict I Justman
bio: Benedict I Justman, born Benedict Rivers and known as Benedict the Bold and Benedict the Just, was a King of the Trident and the founder of House Justman. A bastard from Houses Blackwood and Bracken, Benedict Rivers was despised as a boy. He became a great knight, however, and his prowess earned him the support of his parents' families and then other riverlords. Benedict was able to defeat all petty kings in the riverlands over more than thirty years, after which he allowed himself to be crowned. As king, Benedict the Bold became known as Benedict the Just, which led to him naming his family House Justman. During his twenty-three-year reign, Benedict I expanded east to Maidenpool and north to the Neck. He was succeeded by his son, King Benedict_II_Justman.

name: Benedict II Justman
bio: Benedict II Justman was the second King of the Trident from House Justman. Benedict II was the son and heir of the founder of House Justman, King Benedict_I_Justman. Benedict II ruled for sixty years, adding Duskendale, Rosby, and the mouth of the Blackwater Rush to the Justman realm.

name: Benedict Royce
bio: Benedict Royce was a member of the junior branch of House Royce that may or may not have become House Royce of the Gates of the Moon in 300 AC.[N 1] Benedict was a younger son of Lord Raymar_Royce. He was married to Jocelyn_Stark and had three daughters. According to Catelyn Stark, one married a Waynwood, another a Corbray, and the third might have married a Templeton.

name: Benerro
bio: Benerro is the High Priest at the Temple of the Lord of Light in the Free City of Volantis. His right hand is the red priest Moqorro. Benerro was bought by the red temple while enslaved as a child.

name: Benethon Scales
bio: Benethon Scales is a knight of House Scales, in service to King Stannis_Baratheon. He is one of the queen's men.

name: Benfred Tallhart
bio: Benfred Tallhart is the eldest son and heir of Ser Helman_Tallhart, the head of House Tallhart. Benfred frequently visited Winterfell with his father, Ser Helman_Tallhart, and was friendly with Robb_Stark and Theon_Greyjoy during these visits. When visiting Torrhen's Square with Lord Eddard_Stark, Theon Greyjoy used to mock Benfred for his thick neck, to see how angry he could make Benfred.

name: Benfrey Frey
bio: Benfrey Frey is a knight of House Frey. He is the sixteenth son of Lord Walder_Frey, the second by his sixth wife, Lady Bethany_Rosby. Benfrey married his cousin Jyanna_Frey with whom he has two children, Della_Frey and Osmund_Frey.

name: Benifer
bio: Benifer  was a maester of the Citadel who served as Grand Maester during the reigns of kings Maegor_I_Targaryen and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Benifer became Grand Maester after the execution of Grand Maester Desmond_(Grand_Maester). When Maegor decided to take another wife, Benifer suggested a match with Lady Clarisse_Dayne of Starfall in the hopes of detaching her lands and house from Dorne. In 48 AC when the realm had enough of Maegor's tyranny and were flocking to join their support to the claim put forth by Prince Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, Maegor ordered Grand Maester Benifer to send forth his ravens summoning all Maegor's leal lords and bannermen to gather at King's Landing. Benifer instead abandoned Maegor and secretly fled on a ship to Pentos. Benifer was the only Grand Maester to have survived Maegor's reign as Maegor had beheaded the previous three. Upon Maegor's death, the new young king Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen sent an invitation to Benifer asking him to return to King's Landing. Although Benifer was fearful he might be executed for treason, he returned and was reinstated to his office by Jaehaerys. When Balerion returned to King's Landing in 56 AC with the afflicted Princess Aerea_Targaryen barely clinging to him. The princess was carried to Benifer by Ser Lucamore_Strong of the Kingsguard. Septon Barth aided Benifer in attempting to heal the girl, but their efforts failed and the princess perished, both men were traumatized by the girl's death. Benifer was one of the victims of the Shivers when that disease came to King's Landing in 59 AC. He died after serving as Grand Maester for a total of fifteen years under two different kings. Elysar, who was twenty years younger than him, succeeded him as Grand Maester.

name: Benjamin Avery
bio: Ser Benjamin Avery was a knight during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Benjamin participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Benjen Stark
bio: Benjen Stark, also known as Ben Stark, is the First Ranger of the Night's Watch and the younger brother of Lord Eddard_Stark of Winterfell. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Benjen is portrayed by Joseph Mawle and in a Season 6 flashback by Matteo Elezi.

name: Benjen the Bitter
bio: Benjen Stark, better known as Benjen the Bitter, was King in the North of House Stark. He is buried in the crypts beneath Winterfell.

name: Benjen the Sweet
bio: Benjen Stark, better known as Benjen the Sweet, was King in the North of House Stark. He is buried in the crypts beneath Winterfell.

name: Benjen Stark
bio: Benjen Stark was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. He married Lady Lysa_Locke, with whom he had two sons, Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Benjen) and Bennard_Stark. Benjen succeeded Lord Ellard_Stark as the Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North and was in turned succeeded by his eldest son, Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Benjen).

name: Benjen Stark
bio: Benjen Stark was a member of House Stark. He was the youngest son of Artos_Stark and Lysara_Karstark. His older twin brother was Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Artos). He had children.

name: Benjen Stark
bio: Benjen Stark was a member of House Stark. He was the eldest son of Bennard_Stark and Lady Margaret_Karstark. He was probably named after his grandfather, Lord Benjen_Stark_(lord). Benjen was imprisoned in 126 AC with his father and brothers by his cousin Lord Cregan_Stark.

name: Benjicot
bio: Benjicot is a man employed at the Gates of the Moon, possibly as the fool and jester. It is unknown which house employs his services.

name: Benjicot Branch
bio: Benjicot Branch is a member of House Branch and is a man of the wolfswood sworn to House Glover of Deepwood Motte.

name: Benjicot Blackwood
bio: Benjicot Blackwood, known as Bloody Ben by his men, was the Lord of Raventree Hall and the head of House Blackwood during the Dance of the Dragons and during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Benjicot had a long life. In 138 AC, he allowed Lord Alyn_Velaryon to bring the bones of his brother Addam_Velaryon, which had been taken to Raventree Hall after Second Tumbleton in 130 AC, to Driftmark so they could be entombed in Hull.

name: Bennard Brune
bio: Bennard Brune, the Knight of Brownhollow, is the head of House Brune of Brownhollow in the Crackclaw Point area of the Crownlands. He is the cousin of Lord Eustace_Brune of the Dyre Den.

name: Bennard Stark
bio: Bennard Stark was a member of House Stark and the second son of Lord Benjen_Stark_(lord) and Lady Lysa_Locke. He married Margaret_Karstark, with whom he had three sons; Benjen_Stark_(son_of_Bennard), Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Bennard) and Elric_Stark. When his elder brother Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Benjen), Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North, died in 121 AC, Bennard ruled the north as regent during the minority of his lord nephew, Cregan_Stark. When Cregan came of age in 124 AC, Bennard was slow to surrender his power. Relations grew strained between Bennard and Cregan as the young Lord of Winterfell chafed under the limits imposed on him by his uncle. Finally, in 126 AC, Cregan rose up against his uncle, and imprisoned Bennard and his sons, taking the rule of the north into his own hands.

name: Bennarion Botley
bio: Bennarion Botley is the sixth son of Lord Sawane_Botley.

name: Bennet
bio: Bennet is an old septon of the Faith of the Seven. A resident of Saltpans, he hears the confessions of the penitent who dwell at the Quiet Isle.

name: Bennifer Blackwood
bio: Bennifer Blackwood was a member of House Blackwood and the heir of Raventree Hall during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Bennifer participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.[N 1]

name: Bennis
bio: Bennis of the Brown Shield was a hedge knight during the reigns of kings Daeron_II_Targaryen and Aerys_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Bennis will be portrayed by Babou Ceesay. Bennis met Ser Arlan_of_Pennytree and his squire, Duncan_the_Tall, when a group of hedge knights were hired by a Dornish merchant to guard him between Lannisport and the Prince's Pass. Bennis was cruel to the young Dunk, pinching and taunting him. After one of the other knights went missing, it was rumored he had been murdered by Bennis following an argument. By 211 AC, Bennis served Ser Eustace_Osgrey at Standfast together with Ser Duncan the Tall, Arlan's former squire. He is insulting toward Duncan's squire, Egg, but does not realize the boy is actually Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen. Bennis accompanied Duncan in an attempt to discover why the Chequy Water had dried up, and he injured Wolmer, one of the workers of Lady Rohanne_Webber of Coldmoat, when they refused to break down the dam they had built. After Bennis and Duncan informed Eustace about the events that had transpired, Eustace realized that Rohanne would take the wounding of one of her men as a slight to her honor. Preparing for conflict, Bennis and Duncan began to train Eustace's villagers for battle. Hoping to prevent retaliation, Duncan traveled to Coldmoat to pay a blood price, but Rohanne refused to accept it. She instead threatened to come for Bennis with fire and steel if Eustace would not give him to her by the next day. On the condition that Bennis deliver himself for punishment, Rohanne planned to only slice his nose, but Bennis refused to allow it, even after the woods near Standfast were burned on Rohanne's orders. Duncan sent home Bennis's poorly-trained recruits. Preparing for Rohanne's arrival, Bennis decided to hold up in the tower of Standfast. When Eustace, Duncan, and Egg rode to the river to meet Rohanne and her men, Bennis stayed behind because the sight of him would likely infuriate the Lady of Coldmoat. While Duncan settled the dispute in a duel with Ser Lucas_Inchfield at the river, Bennis tied up Sam_Stoops and Sam_Stoops'_wife at Standfast. The brown knight stole all the valuable items at the tower and fled.

name: Bent-backed man
bio: A bent-backed man is a ferryman, and an inhabitant of Lord Harroway's Town in the riverlands.

name: Benton Glover
bio: Benton Glover was a member of House Glover who joined the Night's Watch and served as First Ranger. When Queen Alysanne_Targaryen visited the Night's Watch at Castle Black in 58 AC, Benton escorted her atop the Wall to the Nightfort.

name: Beony Beesbury
bio: Beony Beesbury is a noblewoman of House Beesbury, the wife of Ser Raymund_Frey. They have seven children.

name: Beqqo
bio: Blind Beqqo is a fishmonger from Braavos.

name: Beren Tallhart
bio: Beren Tallhart is a member of House Tallhart. He is the second son of Leobald_Tallhart, the castellan of Torrhen's Square, and Berena_Hornwood.

name: Berena Hornwood
bio: Berena Hornwood is a member of House Hornwood, the wife of Leobald_Tallhart and the sister of Lord Halys_Hornwood. She is mother of Brandon_Tallhart and Beren_Tallhart.

name: Berena Stark
bio: Berena Stark was a noblewoman of House Stark, the fourth child and first daughter of Beron_Stark, Lord of Winterfell, and his wife Lady Lorra_Royce.

name: Beric Dondarrion
bio: Beric Dondarrion, also known as the lightning lord because of his sigil, is the Lord of Blackhaven and head of House Dondarrion. He is a marcher lord. He is betrothed to Lady Allyria_Dayne of Starfall. His squire is the young Edric_Dayne, Lord of Starfall, Allyria's nephew. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Beric is portrayed by David Michael Scott (Season 1) and by Richard Dormer (Seasons 3 - 8). In either 294 AC or 295 AC,[N 1] Beric was betrothed to Lady Allyria_Dayne. Following their betrothal, Beric took Allyria's nephew Edric_Dayne, the seven-year-old Lord of Starfall, back with him to Blackhaven to serve as his page. He later makes Edric his squire.

name: Bernard
bio: Bernard was a septon during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. When Lord Unwin_Peake became Hand of the King in 133 AC, he decided to replace Septon Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons) with a younger man, his cousin Bernard. After Unwin resigned and left the Red Keep in 134 AC, Bernard remained behind. When Larra_Rogare, the wife of Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, gave birth to Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen, Septon Bernard anointed the child with the seven sacred oils in the royal sept. During the plot against House Rogare in 135 AC, Septon Bernard tried to persuade King Aegon_III_Targaryen to leave Maegor's Holdfast during the secret siege. Bernard wrote down Lord Thaddeus_Rowan's confession as the man was being tortured. Bernard was later named as being an accomplice to the plot, and eventually confessed to his involvement, citing his hatred of the Lyseni and their foreign gods. He claimed that it had been intended for Larra to die, so Viserys could ascend the throne and take a "proper Westerosi queen." During the treason trials, the High_Septon_(Regency_of_Aegon_III) wrote asking that Bernard be spared execution so that he might repent. King Aegon obliged, and Bernard was gelded and sent to walk barefoot from King's Landing to Oldtown with his manhood hung around his neck. He spent the rest of his life as a scribe copying holy books at the Starry Sept, under a vow of silence.

name: Black Bernarr
bio: Black Bernarr is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Brown Bernarr
bio: Brown Bernarr is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Bernarr Brune
bio: Bernarr Brune was a knight from House Brune[N 1] during the reigns of the kings Aenys_I_Targaryen and Maegor_I_Targaryen. Bernarr was the squire of Lord Alyn_Stokeworth, who was the Hand of the King for the newly crowned King Aenys_I_Targaryen in 37 AC. When Lord Alyn was slain by the rebel Harren_the_Red, Bernarr avenged him by killing Harren. Bernarr was rewarded with a knighthood by the grateful King Aenys. In 42 AC, Ser Bernarr was present at Visenya's Hill when King Maegor_I_Targaryen was challenged by Ser Damon_Morrigen, Grand Captain of the Warrior's Sons, to a trial of seven to settle his legitimacy to the Iron Throne. Bernarr kept quiet when Maegor called upon those who would stand beside him, but was shamed into action by the volunteering of Dick_Bean, a simple man-at-arms. Ser Bernarr spoke out, offering to stand beside King Maegor, and noting that all present were no true knights. Bernarr's appeal succeeded, and many offered their swords. During the fight against the Warrior's Sons, Ser Bernarr was killed.

name: Bernarr II Justman
bio: Bernarr II Justman was the last King of the Trident from the line of House Justman. Bernarr's three sons were taken as hostages after the river lords were defeated by Qhored Hoare, King of the Iron Islands. Three years later Qhored murdered Bernarr's sons, whilst they were held captive in a keep of Pyke, known afterward as the Bloody Keep, because Bernarr was slow to pay tribute. Qhored's ironborn crushed Bernarr's hopeless war for vengeance, and Bernarr was drowned as a sacrifice to the Drowned God. The fall of the Justmans led the riverlands into a century of anarchy.

name: Beron
bio: Beron is a serjeant of the guard sworn to House Dustin.

name: Beron Stark
bio: Beron Stark was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Beron was a younger grandson of Cregan_Stark. Beron became Lord of Winterfell after his older brother, Rodwell_Stark, died without issue. Lord Beron gathered swords to throw Lord Dagon_Greyjoy's ironmen back into the sea and made common cause with the Lord of Casterly Rock for it. According to a semi-canon source, he was mortally wounded in the ensuing clash, however, and House Stark faced a crisis of succession involving several Stark widows and possibly their children. Despite the crisis, Beron's eldest son, Donnor_Stark, succeeded him as Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North. Beron is buried in the crypts of Winterfell.

name: Beron Blacktyde
bio: Beron Blacktyde, known as Blind Beron Blacktyde, is a priest of the Drowned God from House Blacktyde.

name: Bertram Beesbury
bio: Bertram Beesbury is a knight from House Beesbury.

name: Bertrand Tyrell
bio: Bertrand Tyrell was the Lord of Highgarden and head of House Tyrell during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. When Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan) resigned the office of Hand of the King in 54 AC, several names were brought up and then quickly dismissed as potential replacements. Lord Bertrand was among them, for he was known to be a drunkard and the small council feared that his unruly sons would bring disgrace to the Crown. In the terrible winter of 59 AC, the Shivers hit Highgarden. Bertrand sickened but survived, only to perish, drunk, in a fall from his horse four days after his recovery. He was succeeded as Lord of Highgarden and Lord Paramount of the Mander by his eldest son.

name: Bess Bracken
bio: Bess Bracken is a noblewoman of House Bracken, the fourth daughter of Lord Jonos_Bracken of Stone Hedge, and the second by his Lady_Bracken.

name: Bessa
bio: Bessa was a girl from Hag's Mire. Bessa was once propositioned by Chett, who had picked wildflowers for her. Bessa rejected and mocked him, however, on account of his ugliness. The angry Chett then stabbed the girl repeatedly with his knife and watched her die. Chett fled Hag's Mire, but was caught at Sevenstreams and forced by Ser Walder_Rivers to join the Night's Watch.

name: Bessa
bio: Bessa is a tavern wench who works at the Smoking Log in the winter town outside of Winterfell.

name: Bessaro Reyaan
bio: Bessaro Reyaan is a keyholder of the Iron Bank from Braavos and a member of House Reyaan.

name: Beth Cassel
bio: Beth Cassel is a noblewoman of House Cassel and is the only surviving child of Ser Rodrik_Cassel, Winterfell's master-at-arms. Ser Rodrik Cassel was married three times, and his wives gave birth to several daughters, including Beth's mother. However, he was widowed, and Beth was his only surviving child. Beth was raised at Winterfell with the Stark children. Though closer in age to Arya_Stark, she tends to hover around Sansa_Stark's more glamorous retinue.

name: Betha Blackwood
bio: Betha Blackwood, known as Black Betha, was a noblewoman from House Blackwood who became queen consort to King Aegon_V_Targaryen. The daughter of Lord Blackwood of Raventree Hall, Betha married Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen in 220 AC at the age of nineteen. Their union was a love match which saw no opposition as at the time Aegon was far down the line of the succession for the Iron Throne. Betha assisted in arranging politically advantageous betrothals for their four eldest children. The youths resisted Betha's and Aegon's plans, however, and the children instead married for love as their parents had done. It is unknown if Betha was in attendance during the tragedy at Summerhall.

name: Blushing Bethany
bio: Blushing Bethany is a prostitute at the Happy Port whorehouse in Braavos.

name: Bethany Blackwood
bio: Bethany Blackwood is a noblewoman of House Blackwood and is the only daughter of Tytos_Blackwood, Lord of Raventree Hall. Bethany has never been more than a day's ride from Raventree Hall.

name: Bethany Bolton
bio: Bethany Bolton, née Ryswell, was the elder daughter of Lord Rodrik_Ryswell of House Ryswell and, as the second wife of Lord Roose_Bolton, the Lady of the Dreadfort. According to Roose, Bethany never made a sound in bed. Bethany had one son with Roose, Domeric_Bolton. During his youth, Domeric served as a page at Barrowton with Bethany's sister, Lady Barbrey_Dustin. Reek, a servant at the Dreadfort, was once caught stealing perfume from Bethany's bedchamber in order to his mask his unpleasant scent; the following year Reek was caught drinking the perfume. Bethany died of a fever. Domeric died in 297 AC,[N 1] allegedly poisoned by her husband's bastard, Ramsay_Snow.

name: Bethany Bracken
bio: Bethany Bracken was a member of House Bracken. She became Aegon_IV_Targaryen's seventh mistress. Barba_Bracken was King Aegon_IV_Targaryen's fifth mistress, and her father, Lord Bracken of Stone Hedge, had been his Hand, but they were sent away in 172 AC and Melissa_Blackwood had become Aegon's mistress. Barba and her father groomed her younger sister, Bethany, expressly to win the king's favor and displace Melissa Blackwood as his mistress. In 177 AC, Bethany caught Aegon's eye when he visited Stone Hedge to see his and Barba's bastard son, Aegor_Rivers. By then, the king was fat and foul-tempered, but Bethany delighted him, and he took her back with him to King's Landing. However, Bethany found Aegon's embraces distressing, and for comfort she turned to a handsome knight of the Kingsguard, Ser Terrence_Toyne. The pair was discovered abed by Aegon himself in 178 AC. Even though they proclaimed their love, Bethany was forced to watch as Terrence was dismembered piece by piece and died screaming. Both Bethany and her father were then executed.

name: Bethany Fair-Fingers
bio: Bethany Fair-fingers is a wandering singer of the Seven Kingdoms.

name: Bethany Hightower
bio: Bethany Hightower was a noblewoman from House Hightower during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Bethany was the daughter of Lord Ormund_Hightower and his first wife, who died in childbed at some point. She had three brothers, Lyonel_Hightower, Martyn_Hightower_(son_of_Ormund), and Garmund_Hightower. During the Dance of the Dragons, her father married again to Samantha_Tarly, but Ormund died in the First Battle of Tumbleton. Years later, Bethany's step-mother would become her good-sister when Sam married again to Ormund's heir, Lyonel. After the death of Queen Jaehaera_Targaryen in 133 AC and the subsequent planned betrothal between King Aegon_III_Targaryen and Myrielle_Peake, Lady Samantha wrote a bold letter proposing that Aegon marry both Bethany and Sam's sister, Sansara_Tarly.

name: Bethany Redwyne
bio: Bethany Redwyne is the wife of Mathis_Rowan, Lord of Goldengrove. They have three children, including at least one daughter. Lord Hoster Tully intended to ask for Bethany's hand for his brother Ser Brynden_Tully, but Brynden stubbornly refused to marry.

name: Bethany Rosby
bio: Bethany Rosby was a member of House Rosby, and was the sixth wife of Lord Walder_Frey. She is deceased. Although Rosbys are not known for their robustness, Bethany was healthy enough to have many children, one each year she was married to Walder. Of these, five survived to adulthood: Ser Perwyn_Frey, Ser Benfrey_Frey, Maester Willamen_Frey, Olyvar_Frey and Roslin_Frey.

name: Betharios of Braavos
bio: Betharios, better known as Betharios of Braavos, is the wife of Symond_Frey. She comes from Braavos.

name: Bhakaz zo Loraq
bio: Bhakaz zo Loraq is a child from the House of Loraq, one of the noble ruling families of Meereen. He is kin to Hizdahr_zo_Loraq.

name: Bharbo
bio: Bharbo was a Dothraki khal and the father of Khal Drogo.

name: Big Boil
bio: Big Boil is a member of the free folk.

name: Lord Bigglestone
bio: Lord Bigglestone was the head of House Bigglestone during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. During the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Bigglestone sided with the blacks and Rhaenyra_Targaryen. Lord Bigglestone, alongside with Lord_Chambers and Lord_Perryn, joined Ser Pate_of_Longleaf and his ragged band of survivors from earlier battles and fought together in the Battle by the Lakeshore. He did not survive the battle.

name: Muttering Bill
bio: Muttering Bill is a steward of the Night's Watch.

name: Old Bill Bone
bio: Old Bill Bone is a sellsword from the Summer Isles and a member of the Windblown.

name: Billy Burley
bio: Billy Burley was a longbowman in the service of House Blackwood during the Dance of the Dragons. His name indicates some relationship to House Burley a mountain clan in the north. Following the Second Battle of Tumbleton, Lord Benjicot_Blackwood had Billy mercy kill Tessarion, the she-dragon of the late Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I). From one hundred yards away Billy fired three shafts through the eye of the wounded dragon.

name: Biter
bio: Biter is one of the criminals Yoren recruits to the Night's Watch when he cleans out the dungeons of King’s Landing. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Biter is portrayed by Gerard Jordan. According to a semi-canon source, Biter was found as an orphaned child by Rorge, who raised him ferally to fight in Rorge's dog and bear fighting pit in Flea Bottom.

name: Black Fist
bio: Black Fist is an Unsullied sworn to Daenerys_Targaryen.

name: Black Knife
bio: Black Knife is one of the children of the forest in service to the last greenseer. Their true name is too long for the human tongue, according to Leaf.

name: Blackshield
bio: Blackshield was the identity taken by a mystery knight during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. Although his personal arms are not explicitly described, the knight's moniker suggests that his sigil was sable and uncharged. Blackshield participated as a mystery knight at a tourney held at Oldtown sometime between 278 AC and 281 AC. He was unhorsed by Ser Barristan_Selmy of the Kingsguard, who unmasked him and revealed him as the Bastard of Uplands. Blackshield's given name is unknown. His surname is presumably "Flowers", the bastard name of the Reach, as that is where House Mullendore's seat of Uplands is located.

name: Lord Blacktyde
bio: Lord Blacktyde was the Lord of Blacktyde and the head of House Blacktyde during the Greyjoy Rebellion. He had at least one son, Baelor_Blacktyde. His given name has not been mentioned. Lord Blacktyde died during the Greyjoy Rebellion, fighting for Balon_Greyjoy. After the rebellion was over, Baelor_Blacktyde was made a hostage to ensure the Blacktydes' loyalty to the Iron Throne, and taken to Oldtown.

name: Lord Blackwood (Aerys II)
bio: Lord Blackwood was the Lord of Raventree Hall and the head of House Blackwood during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. In 276 AC or 277 AC, Lord Blackwood and Lord_Bracken_(Aerys_II) travelled to Riverrun to lay their feud before their liege lord, Hoster_Tully. During their stay, Lord Hoster announced the betrothal of his twelve-year-old daughter, Catelyn Tully, to the heir of Winterfell, Brandon_Stark. When a border dispute over a mill arose, the two lords came to King's Landing to seek judgment from the Crown. The Hand of the King, Lord Tywin_Lannister, arbitrated in favor of Lord Blackwood but was overruled by King Aerys_II_Targaryen who gave the disputed mill to Lord Bracken.

name: Blane
bio: Blane is an officer of the Night's Watch assigned to the Shadow Tower.

name: Blind Lord
bio: The Blind Lord was a noble of the north who lived near the Flint Cliffs. When he was ten, Balon_Greyjoy scaled the cliffs to the Blind Lord's haunted tower.

name: Blood (the butcher)
bio: Blood is the alias of a butcher and former Serjeant of the City Watch of King's Landing during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. Blood's true name is lost to history. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Blood is portrayed by Sam C. Wilson. Once a serjeant in the City Watch of King's Landing, Blood lost his gold cloak for beating a whore to death whilst in a drunken rage. He spent time in Flea Bottom. After Prince Aemond_Targaryen killed Prince Lucerys_Velaryon at Storm's End, the blacks wanted vengeance against the greens. Blood and the ratcatcher called Cheese were employed by Mysaria, Prince Daemon_Targaryen's spymaster, to slay one of the sons of Aemond's older brother, King Aegon_II_Targaryen. Cheese led Blood into the Red Keep through a forgotten passageway, and creeping through walls, they entered the chambers of Aegon's mother, Queen Alicent_Hightower, in the Tower of the Hand. After Cheese bound and gagged the Dowager Queen and Blood strangled her bedmaid, the assassins waited for Aegon's wife, Queen Helaena_Targaryen, and her children to arrive. Blood killed Helaena's guardsman once she arrived, and Cheese grabbed her youngest son, Prince Maelor_Targaryen. Cheese asked Helaena which of her sons she wanted them to kill. Helaena offered herself but Blood refused, stating it had to be a son. When Helaena prevaricated, Cheese warned her that Blood would rape her six-year-old daughter, Princess Jaehaera_Targaryen. Although Helaena eventually named her youngest son, Maelor, Blood beheaded Prince Jaehaerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon_II) instead. Blood and Cheese then fled through the secret passages with the prince's head in hand. Blood was seized two days later at the Gate of the Gods while attempting to bring Jaehaerys's head to Daemon at Harrenhal and collect his reward. Under torture, he admitted to having been hired by a woman whose description matched Mysaria. Blood died on the thirteenth day of torture. Ser Luthor_Largent had the gold cloaks search for Mysaria and Cheese, but no trace was found of them. Aegon II wanted to attack Dragonstone to avenge his son's murder, but Ser Criston_Cole instead suggested sending Ser Arryk_Cargyll to infiltrate the castle.

name: Bloodbeard
bio: Bloodbeard is the commander of the Company of the Cat. His given name is unknown. Bloodbeard and the Tattered_Prince, commander of the Windblown, despise each other, as the Windblown and the Company of the Cat have fought on opposing sides in the Disputed Lands in the recent past.

name: Bloodstone Emperor
bio: The Bloodstone Emperor was the legendary ninth and last ruler of the mythic Great Empire of the Dawn. He was the son of the Opal_Emperor and the younger brother of the Amethyst_Empress, who succeeded their father. Envious, he slew his sister and proclaimed himself the Bloodstone Emperor. The Bloodstone Emperor's reign was a reign of terror. His usurpation became known as the Blood Betrayal in the annals of the Further East, which claim that the act of usurping his sister's throne ushered in the Long Night. He practiced torture, dark arts, and necromancy. He enslaved his own people, took a tiger-woman for wife, feasted on human flesh and cast down the true gods of Yi Ti to worship a black stone that fell from the sky. Some scholars believe him to have been the first High Priest of the sinister Church of Starry Wisdom.

name: Bluetooth
bio: Bluetooth is a tall ironborn captain sworn to Balon_Greyjoy. His given name is unknown.

name: Bobono
bio: Bobono is a dwarf mummer in the troupe performing at the Gate in the Free City of Braavos. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is portrayed by Leigh Gill.

name: Bodger
bio: Bodger is a young raider of the free folk.

name: Bokkoko
bio: Bokkoko is a sellsword of the Second Sons. He is an axeman of formidable repute. He is homosexual.

name: Bonifer Hasty
bio: Bonifer Hasty, also known as Bonifer the Good, is an old landed knight from House Hasty, and is the commander of the Holy Hundred. In his youth, Ser Bonifer Hasty was a promising tourney knight. He and Princess Rhaella_Targaryen were infatuated with one another prior to the princess's official betrothal to her brother, Prince Aerys_II_Targaryen. According to Ser Barristan_Selmy, Bonifer once wore the princess's favor in a tourney in which he defeated all challengers to name Rhaella his queen of love and beauty. Their love was ultimately a brief thing and could never have been otherwise, as Bonifer was of far too low birth to be considered as a suitor for a princess of the royal House Targaryen. When Rhaella married Aerys, the knight found solace in religion, finding only the Maiden could replace Rhaella in his heart. Not many today know of Bonifer's brief relationship with the late Queen Rhaella in their youths; Jaime Lannister only knows that something had happened to Bonifer, a defeat or a disgrace or a near brush with death, and that he stopped jousting afterwards, deciding it was an empty vanity. At one point Bonifer served under Lord Owen_Merryweather, a former Hand of the King to Aerys_II_Targaryen. Bonifer founded the Holy Hundred, a group of one hundred pious men-at-arms.

name: Books
bio: Books is a Volantene sellsword and a member of the Windblown.

name: Borcas
bio: Borcas is a steward of the Night's Watch.

name: Boremund Baratheon
bio: Boremund Baratheon was the Lord of Storm's End and the head of House Baratheon during the reign of kings Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Viserys_I_Targaryen. The only son of Lord Rogar_Baratheon and Dowager Queen Alyssa_Velaryon, Boremund was the half-brother of King Jaehaerys I. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Boremund is portrayed by Julian Lewis Jones.

name: Boremund Harlaw
bio: Boremund Harlaw, known as Boremund the Blue, is a member of House Harlaw. He is the master of the castle Harridan Hill. His personal sigil is the Harlaw scythe on a pale blue field. He has three sons.

name: Boros Blount
bio: Boros Blount is a knight from House Blount, who serves in the Kingsguard of Robert_I_Baratheon. It is unknown when Boros joined the Kingsguard. He may have joined in the aftermath of Robert's Rebellion, when the White Swords had five vacancies. Blount is confirmed to have been a Kingsguard by the time of the Greyjoy Rebellion of 289 AC, however. Boros took part in the tourney at Lannisport in 289 AC, during which he was defeated by the eventual champion, Ser Jorah_Mormont, Lord of Bear Island.

name: Lord Borrell (father of Godric)
bio: Lord Borrell was the Lord of Sweetsister, Shield of Sisterton, Master of Breakwater Castle, Keeper of the Night Lamp, and the head of House Borrell during Robert's Rebellion. During Robert's Rebellion, Lord Eddard_Stark washed up on Sweetsister while trying to sail to the north to raise his banners. Lord Borrell could easily have turned Eddard over to King Aerys_II_Targaryen for a reward, but he let him go.

name: Borroq
bio: Borroq is a free folk skinchanger who can inhabit the body of a boar. Borroq was one of those present at a meeting of skinchangers that Haggon took Varamyr to in his youth.

name: Borros Baratheon
bio: Borros Baratheon was the Lord of Storm's End and the head of House Baratheon during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. He had four daughters—Cassandra_Baratheon, Maris_Baratheon, Ellyn_Baratheon, and Floris_Baratheon—and a son, Royce_Baratheon, with his wife, Lady Elenda_Caron. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Borros is portrayed by Roger Evans. Borros clashed with Dornishmen on numerous occasions. Borros married Elenda_Caron, the daughter of Lord Royce_Caron of Nightsong. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, it was thought that Borros would support Queen  Rhaenyra_Targaryen over King Aegon_II_Targaryen, as his father Boremund_Baratheon had always been a staunch supporter of his niece Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon), Borros's cousin. Therefore, the green council sent Prince Aemond_Targaryen on Vhagar to Storm's End, to win the hand of one of Borros's four eligible daughters, in order to bring House Baratheon and the stormlands over to the greens. In the meanwhile, the black council sent Rhaenyra's son Prince Lucerys_Velaryon on Arrax to win Borros to the blacks' cause, and it was expected that Borros would give him a warm welcome. However, when Aemond arrived, Storm's End welcomed him with feasts and hunts and jousting, and Borros was more than willing to entertain his suit, telling him to pick any one of his daughters to marry. Borros told Aemond he felt that Rhaenyra had taken House Baratheon for granted for too long. He misremembered his relationship to Rhaenys (saying "some great-aunt I never knew was married to her father", instead of his aunt), and noted that Rhanenyra was not Rhaenys. He claimed he had nothing against women, but that he supported a male heir over a daughter. He felt that when Rhaenyra learned she had lost Storm's End, she would make peace with Aegon II, as the way his daughters did when they fought. It is uncertain which of Borros's daughters Aemond chose, but Borros and the prince were haggling over dates and dowries the morning when Lucerys Velaryon arrived. Eyewitness accounts varied, some saying Borros was ashamed for Lucerys to find him treating with Aemond, while others say he relished the prospect of both factions competing for his aid. Though neither was present, Mushroom wrote that Borros was drunk, and Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons) wrote that he was fearful. When Aemond confronted Lucerys in the Round Hall of Storm's End, trying to snatch Rhaenyra's message from his hand, Borros commanded his knights to separate the princes and bring him the letter. After his maester read the message to him, Borros asked Lucerys if he joined the black cause, which of his daughters would Lucerys marry, and commanded him to pick one. Lucerys replied he could not, as he was betrothed to Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon), and Borros told him to go home and tell his mother not to take him for granted. As Lucerys left, Aemond continued to confront him, demanding recompense for his lost eye, but Lucerys refused to fight, saying he came as an envoy. Borros prohibited the princes fighting beneath his roof at Storm's End, saying he wanted no bloodshed, and had his knights escort them out to the yard. Reportedly provoked by the words of Borros's daughter Maris_Baratheon, Aemond again attempted to attack Lucerys, asking for Borros's permission, and Borros replied he could not tell him what to do outside his roof. Aemond flew Vhagar to combat Lucerys on Arrax, leading to the death of Lucerys and his dragon. As the war began in earnest, Storm's End officially declared for the greens. Borros gathered an army of six thousand men to Storm's End, but he marched into the Red Mountains to confront a Vulture_King_(Aegon_II) instead of fighting Rhaenyra_Targaryen and her blacks. When King's Landing fell to Rhaenyra, Princess Jaehaera_Targaryen, daughter of King Aegon_II_Targaryen, was sent to Storm's End under the charge of Ser Willis_Fell of the Kingsguard. After Rhaenyra's death at Dragonstone, Lord Baratheon left his pregnant wife, Elenda Baratheon, at Storm's End and marched with six hundred knights and four hundred foot to King's Landing. Borros seized the capital and ended the Moon of the Three Kings. Borros agreed that his eldest daughter, Cassandra_Baratheon, would wed the widowed King Aegon II, and a younger daughter, Floris_Baratheon, would marry Lord Larys_Strong. The Shepherd is said to have cursed Borros before being captured. Aegon II sent Borros to subdue the crownlands, and the stormlanders achieved the submissions of Rosby, Stokeworth, and Duskendale. The king's demand for heavy ransoms angered the former blacks, however. Borros marched up the kingsroad to confront the host of rivermen led by the Lads. Discounting the black host because they were led by youths and women, Borros led the charge in the Battle of the Kingsroad. Borros attempted to retreat when the battle turned against the stormlanders, but the crownlands men betrayed him turning the battle into a rout. A heavily-wounded Borros is said to have killed twelve knights, along with Lords Roland_Darry and Jorah_Mallister, before falling against Lord Kermit_Tully. Borros's wife Elenda gave birth to a son seven days later, and did not name him Aegon like Borros wished. Instead she named him Royce_Baratheon after her father, Lord Royce_Caron.

name: Bors
bio: Bors is a servant of House Martell at Sunspear.

name: Bors the Breaker
bio: Bors the Breaker is a legendary son of Garth_Greenhand and founder of House Bulwer of Blackcrown. According to legend, he gained the strength of twenty men by drinking only bull's blood. Some tales state Bors drank so much blood he grew a pair of shiny black horns.

name: Borys Baratheon
bio: Borys Baratheon was a knight from House Baratheon during the reigns of kings Aegon_I_Targaryen, Aenys_I_Targaryen, Maegor_I_Targaryen and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He was the eldest of Lord Rogar_Baratheon's four younger brothers.

name: Bowen Marsh
bio: Bowen Marsh, also known as the Old Pomegranate, is the Lord Steward of the Night's Watch. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Bowen is portrayed by Michael Condron.

name: Boy
bio: Boy is an orphan of war staying with Sharna, the innkeep of the Inn of the Kneeling Man and her husband, Husband. Husband and his wife Sharna took in Boy after he lost his mother to the Bloody Mummers.

name: Braavosi daggerman
bio: A Braavosi daggerman is one of the foul-looking mercenaries that Varys gets for Tyrion_Lannister to guard Shae's manse in King's Landing.

name: Lady Bracken
bio: Lady Bracken is the third and current wife of Jonos_Bracken, the ruling Lord of the Stone Hedge and the head of House Bracken. Together they have three daughters: Catelyn_Bracken, Bess_Bracken and Alysanne_Bracken. Her given name and birth house are unknown.

name: Lord Bracken
bio: Lord Bracken was the Lord of the Stone Hedge and the head of House Bracken during the reigns of kings Baelor_I_Targaryen, Viserys_II_Targaryen, and Aegon_IV_Targaryen. His given name is as yet unknown. He had two daughters, Barba_Bracken and Bethany_Bracken. When the Princesses Daena_Targaryen, Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_III), and Elaena_Targaryen were confined to the Maidenvault in the Red Keep, they were accompanied only by maidens who had been sent to court by lords and knights who wanted to gain favor with King Baelor_I_Targaryen. Near the end of Baelor's reign, one of these maidens was Lord Bracken's daughter Barba_Bracken. When King Baelor I died in 171 AC, King Viserys_II_Targaryen released Daena, Rhaena, and Elaena from their confinement, and Barba met Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen, who became entranced with her. On King Aegon IV Targaryen's ascent to the Iron Throne in 172 AC, he named Lord Bracken as Hand of the King and openly took Barba as his fifth mistress. Later that year, when Queen Naerys_Targaryen was lingering near death from a difficult childbirth, Lord Bracken talked openly of wedding Barba to the king. However, these talks became Lord Bracken's undoing, as Naerys's son, Prince Daeron_II_Targaryen, and her brother, Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II), forced Aegon to send Barba and the king's newborn bastard Aegor_Rivers away from court. After Lord Bracken's daughter was replaced as the king's mistress by Melissa_Blackwood, Lord Bracken and Barba started grooming his younger daughter, Bethany_Bracken, to win the king's favor. In 177 AC, Bethany caught the king's eye, and he took her as his seventh mistress. However, in 178 AC, Bethany was caught in bed with Ser Terrence_Toyne, a knight of the Kingsguard. Both Bethany and Lord Bracken were executed.

name: Lord Bracken (father of Otho)
bio: Lord Bracken was the Lord of the Stone Hedge and the head of House Bracken during the reign of kings Daeron_II_Targaryen and Aerys_I_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. During the First Blackfyre Rebellion, a Lord Bracken (presumably, the father of Ser Otho_Bracken) declared for House Blackfyre. He sailed to Myr to hire crossbowmen to aid Daemon_I_Blackfyre and Bittersteel, but was delayed by storms. In 211 AC, Lord Bracken is dying. As he outlived his eldest son, who died during the Great Spring Sickness, his second son, the belligerent Ser Otho, was set to inherit Stone Hedge. It is unclear if he died by 212 AC or not. The conspirators of the Second Blackfyre Rebellion hoped for Otho to join the forces of House Bracken to their own, which may imply Otho had succeeded his father.

name: Lord Bracken (Aerys II)
bio: Lord Bracken was the Lord of the Stone Hedge and the head of House Bracken during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. In 276 AC or 277 AC, Lord Bracken and Lord_Blackwood_(Aerys_II) travelled to Riverrun to lay their feud before their liege lord, Hoster_Tully. During their stay, Lord Hoster announced the betrothal of his twelve-year-old daughter, Catelyn Tully, to the heir of Winterfell, Brandon_Stark. When a border dispute over a mill arose, the two lords came to King's Landing to seek judgment from the Crown. The Hand of the King, Lord Tywin_Lannister, arbitrated in favor of Lord Blackwood but was overruled by King Aerys_II_Targaryen who gave the disputed mill to Lord Bracken. Lord Bracken offered to Lord Hoster the hand of his daughter in marriage for his younger brother, Ser Brynden_Tully. Brynden refused the match, however.

name: Bradamar Frey
bio: Bradamar Frey is a member of House Frey, the second son of Symond_Frey and Betharios_of_Braavos. Bradamar was sent to the Free City of Braavos, to be fostered as a ward by a Braavosi merchant named Oro_Tendyris.

name: Bramm of Blackhull
bio: Bramm of Blackhull was a hedge knight during the final days of King Aenys_I_Targaryen and the coronation of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Ser Bramm was present at Visenya's Hill when King Maegor was challenged by Ser Damon_Morrigen, Grand Captain of the Warrior's Sons, to a trial of seven. Bramm kept quiet when Maegor called upon those who would stand beside him but was shamed by the example of Dick_Bean, a simple man-at-arms, and the appeal of Ser Bernarr_Brune and became one of the king's champions. During the fight against the Warrior's Sons, Bramm was killed.

name: Branda Stark
bio: Branda Stark was a noblewoman from House Stark during the first half of the third century after Aegon's Conquest. She was the eldest daughter of Rodrik_Stark_(son_of_Beron) and his wife, Arya_Flint. She had a younger sister, Lyarra_Stark, the mother of Lord Eddard_Stark. Branda was married to Ser Harrold_Rogers.

name: Brandon of the Bloody Blade
bio: Brandon of the Bloody Blade is a legendary son of Garth_Greenhand. He is credited with driving giants away from the Reach and warring against children of the forest, slaying so many at Blue Lake it became known as Red Lake. In some tales he is mentioned as the ancestor or father of Brandon_the_Builder, making him a possible ancestor of House Stark.

name: Brandon Norrey
bio: Brandon Norrey, called the Norrey, is the clan chief of House Norrey, one of the mountain clans in the north.

name: Brandon Norrey
bio: Brandon Norrey, called Brandon the Younger, is the son and heir of Brandon_Norrey, the Norrey, the clan chief of House Norrey, a mountain clan in the north.

name: Brandon Snow
bio: Brandon Snow was the bastard half-brother of Torrhen_Stark, King in the North. During Aegon_I_Targaryen's conquest, Aegon was preparing to march on Dorne. But when he received word that Torrhen Stark was marching south with thirty thousand northmen at his back, Aegon led forty-five thousand troops to meet him at the Trident, and also his and his sisters' dragons, Balerion, Meraxes, and Vhagar. At the Trident, Torrhen took advice from his bannermen, while considering what had happened to Harrenhal and the Field of Fire. His bastard brother Brandon Snow offered to cross the Trident alone at night, to sneak into the Targaryen camp and assassinate the dragons while they slept. However, instead Torrhen sent Brandon and three maesters across the river to negotiate with Aegon, and the next day knelt and swore fealty.

name: Brandon the Builder
bio:
Brandon the Builder, also known as Bran the Builder, was
a legendary figure from the Age of Heroes who is said to have founded House Stark and been the ancestor of the Kings of Winter. Several feats of engineering and architecture in Westeros are attributed to Brandon in various tales, including the Wall, Winterfell, the Hightower, and Storm's End. Scholars, however, believe an ancient king's actions have been exaggerated into legend, or the reigns of multiple kings have been remembered as one through the passage of time. Brandon the Builder is said to have lived during the Age of Heroes. Legends from the Reach claim that he was a descendant of Brandon_of_the_Bloody_Blade, a son of the mythical Garth_Greenhand. Tales from the stormlands claim that when Brandon was a boy, he helped Durran Godsgrief build Storm's End. Legends claim that Brandon built the Wall after the Long Night. According to a ballad in Maester Childer's Winter's Kings, or the Legends and Lineages of the Starks of Winterfell, Brandon sought assistance from the children of the forest in building the Wall, and he learned to understand the True Tongue in order to do so. The children are said to have have used magic during the construction of the Wall, which was composed of huge blocks of ice moved with the aid of giants. Bran the Builder is also said to have raised Winterfell, the seat of House Stark in the north, again with the help of giants, and legends claim that Brandon prayed in the castle's godswood. The Kings of Winter and Lords of Winterfell descend from Brandon the Builder. It is said that he gave Brandon's Gift, a stretch of land twenty-five leagues wide south of the Wall, to the Night's Watch, though some maesters argue this was done by another Brandon of House Stark. Some stories claim King Uthor_of_the_High_Tower commissioned Brandon to design the stone Hightower at Oldtown, while others state it was Bran the Builder's son, who was also named Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Bran_the_Builder). Brandon the Builder is not as beloved among singers and storytellers as Lann_the_Clever, the legendary founder of House Lannister.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark was a member of House Stark and a son of the legendary Brandon_the_Builder. Some stories claim that King Uthor_of_the_High_Tower commissioned Bran the Builder to design the stone Hightower, while others claim it was instead Bran's son, Brandon. It is unknown if Brandon was a King of Winter.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark, known as Brandon the Breaker, was a King of Winter. Brandon was the King of Winter when the Night's_King ruled with his corpse queen at the Wall. He formed an alliance with Joramun, the King-Beyond-the-Wall, to end the thirteen-year long rule of the Night's King and free the Night's Watch from bondage. Thereafter he obliterated the Night's King's name from memory.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark, known as Brandon the Shipwright, was a King in the North and a head of House Stark. His son, Brandon_Stark_(Burner), followed him on the throne. King Brandon was known as Brandon the Shipwright due to his love of the sea. Thousands of years before Aegon's Conquest, King Brandon attempted to sail across the Sunset Sea, but was never seen again. It is known that he never visited Asshai. His tomb in the crypts of Winterfell is empty. His son burned the remaining ships in his grief, and is thus known as King Brandon_Stark_(Burner).

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark, known as Brandon the Burner, was a King in the North and a head of House Stark. He was the son of Brandon_Stark_(Shipwright). Brandon became King in the North after the disappearance in the Sunset Sea of his father, King Brandon_Stark_(Shipwright), thousands of years before Aegon's Conquest. In his grief, the new king set fire to his father's shipyards and remaining ships. Brandon's tomb is located in the crypt of Winterfell.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark, called Ice Eyes, was a King in the North of House Stark. He was the great-grandson of Edrick_Stark. Brandon recaptured the Wolf's Den from slavers originating from the Stepstones during a long, cruel winter. He stripped the slavers naked and gave them to the ex-slaves chained up in the dungeon.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark, better known as Brandon the Bad, was King in the North. He is buried in the crypts beneath Winterfell.

name: Brandon IX Stark
bio: Brandon IX Stark was King in the North and head of House Stark. King Brandon IX stopped Skagosi raids on the Bay of Seals. He broke their power, destroyed their ships and forbade them the sea.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark, known as Brandon the Boisterous, was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the first half of the first century after Aegon's Conquest. Brandon succeeded Torrhen_Stark as Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North, making him the second Stark to hold these titles.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark, also called Brandon the Daughterless, was supposedly the Lord of Winterfell and head of House Stark when the legendary Bael_the_Bard was just a common wildling raider.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark, called Brandon the Boastful, was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Brandon succeeded Roderick_Stark as Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North. In 49 AC, Lord Brandon attended the wedding of Rogar_Baratheon and Alyssa_Velaryon in King's Landing with his sons Walton_Stark_(son_of_Brandon) and Alaric_Stark, despite his ailing. They were accompanied by a dozen bannermen and thirty members of the Night's Watch. While there, Brandon spoke with the young king Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, profoundly praising him by claiming "I see his Aegon_I_Targaryen in him." Brandon died soon after returning to Winterfell, the northmen believing that the strenuous journey was too much for him. Brandon was succeeded as Lord of Winterfell by his eldest son Walton.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark was a member of House Stark, the second son of Bennard_Stark and Lady Margaret_Karstark. Brandon was imprisoned in 126 AC with his father and brothers by his cousin Lord Cregan_Stark.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark was the Lord of Winterfell and head of House Stark during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He was the youngest son of Lord Cregan_Stark and Lady Lynara_Stark. Brandon succeeded his brother Barthogan_Stark as Lord of Winterfell at some time during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Brandon married Lady Alys_Karstark_(wife_of_Brandon), with whom he had three children: Rodwell_Stark, Beron_Stark and Arsa_Stark. Before his marriage, he had an affair with Wylla_Fenn which resulted in a bastard son, Lonnel_Snow. Brandon is buried in the crypts of Winterfell.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark was a member of House Stark, the only son of Lord Willam_Stark and his first wife, Lady Lyanne_Glover. Brandon's mother died in childbirth, so Nan came to Winterfell to be his wet nurse. He died at age three of a summer chill.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark was a member of House Stark. He was the eldest of Artos_Stark and Lysara_Karstark's twin sons. His younger brother was Benjen_Stark_(son_of_Artos). Brandon had children.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark was the oldest son of Lord Rickard_Stark and Lady Lyarra_Stark and the heir to Winterfell prior to Robert's Rebellion. His younger siblings were Eddard_Stark, Lyanna_Stark, and Benjen_Stark. Born in 262 AC at Winterfell, Brandon was the eldest son and heir of Lord Rickard_Stark and Lady Lyarra_Stark. Brandon was fostered at Barrowton with Lord Dustin, father of Willam_Dustin, and he spent most his time riding in the Rills. He took the maidenhead of Barbrey_Dustin, who was infatuated with him, and her father Rodrik_Ryswell, Lord of the Rills, desired to wed her to the heir of Winterfell. However, Lord Stark eventually betrothed Brandon to Catelyn_Stark of Riverrun. Brandon swore to Barbrey he did not want to marry Catelyn, but Rickard's ambitions did not allow him to marry the daughter of a northern bannerman. In 281 AC, Brandon attended the tourney at Harrenhal, where he was introduced by his sister, Lyanna_Stark, to Howland_Reed, a crannogman from the Neck. Brandon laughed during the festivities. At the opening feast of the tourney, Brandon asked Ashara_Dayne to dance with his younger brother Eddard_Stark, who was too shy to ask her himself. Brandon entered the lists, but he was unhorsed by Rhaegar_Targaryen, Prince of Dragonstone. After Rhaegar won the tournament, he crowned Lyanna as his queen of love and beauty, passing over his own wife, Princess Elia_Martell. Brandon had to be restrained from confronting Rhaegar, as he took the prince's action as a slight upon Lyanna's honor, since Lyanna was already betrothed to Eddard's friend, Lord Robert_I_Baratheon. In 282 AC, Brandon was present at Riverrun when the date for his wedding to Catelyn was announced. Petyr_Baelish, the ward of Catelyn's father, Lord Hoster_Tully, challenged Brandon to a duel for her hand. Catelyn begged Brandon to spare Petyr's life, and Brandon promised her to do so. Catelyn gave Brandon her favor to wear during the duel. Brandon armored himself for battle, aided by Catelyn's young brother, Edmure_Tully, who served as Brandon's squire. When Brandon saw that the smaller Petyr wore only a helm, a breastplate, and mail, he took off most of his own armor. Brandon was more than a match for the much younger Petyr. He offered the boy several chances to yield during their duel in the castle's lower bailey, but when Petyr kept refusing, Brandon ended the duel by injuring Petyr severely. Afterwards he departed Riverrun, promising Catelyn to return soon so they could wed.

name: Brandon Stark
bio: Brandon Stark, typically called Bran, is the second son of Lord Eddard_Stark and Lady Catelyn_Stark. He is one of the major POV characters in the books. He has four older siblings—Robb_Stark, Jon_Snow, Sansa_Stark, Arya_Stark—and one younger—Rickon_Stark. Like his siblings, he is constantly accompanied by his direwolf, Summer, with whom he shares a strong warg connection. He is seven years old at the beginning of A Game of Thrones. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Bran is portrayed by Isaac Hempstead-Wright. Born at Winterfell in 290 AC at the height of summer, Bran is the second son and fourth child of Catelyn_Stark and Eddard_Stark, head of House Stark. He is named after his late uncle, Eddard's elder brother Brandon_Stark, whose death was one of the major events that led to Robert's Rebellion in 282 AC. Bran has three older siblings (Robb_Stark, Sansa_Stark, and Arya_Stark) and one younger (Rickon_Stark). He also has a bastard half-brother, Jon_Snow. As a child, Bran was educated by Maester Luwin and trained with weapons by the master-at-arms, Ser Rodrik_Cassel. A year before Bran's birth, in 289 AC, Eddard brought to Winterfell a hostage, Theon_Greyjoy, the last surviving son of Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands. Although Robb and Theon became close after Greyjoy's Rebellion, Bran never warmed to his father's ward. Bran enjoys exploring the castle, as he believes it allows him to know its secrets. Since he was a babe, Bran often climbed the walls of Winterfell, scaring Catelyn to death. Once, she pleaded for him to stop, and extracted a promise for him to do so, which he kept for barely a fortnight. When Bran, wracked with guilt, confessed this to Eddard, he ordered his son to the Winterfell godswood to cleanse himself, where Bran was eventually found asleep in the tallest sentinel tree. Ned then accepted his climbing, but told him to try to not let his lady mother see him. Lady Stark then turned to others in the castle: first, Maester Luwin, who once made a boy out of pottery and threw it from Winterfell's walls to show Bran what would happen if the boy were to fall while climbing, then, to Old Nan, who told him a story of a boy who climbed too high, was struck by lightning and had his eyes eaten by crows, and finally the castle guards, who chased Bran from tower to tower in an attempt to haul him down. Each attempt was fruitless, and Bran continued his climbing. The boy enjoys swinging from the gargoyles of the First Keep  and feeding corn to the crows atop the broken tower. Bran loves hearing Old Nan's stories, preferring the scary ones above all else concerning the children of the forest, the Long Night, and the Others. He is also enthralled by tales of mystery knights and the Kingsguard, and asks to hear their stories over and over until their names become like music to him. However, he is disappointed to be informed by his father that the new Kingsguard is not as it once was.

name: Brandon Tallhart
bio: Brandon Tallhart is a member of House Tallhart. He is the eldest son of Leobald_Tallhart and Berena_Hornwood.

name: Branston Cuy
bio: Branston Cuy is the Lord of Sunflower Hall and the head of House Cuy in the Reach.

name: Brass-seller
bio: A brass-seller is a merchant who sells brass in the port of Qarth.

name: Braxton Beesbury
bio: Braxton Beesbury, known as Stinger, was a knight from House Beesbury and the heir to Honeyholt during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. It was rumored Braxton had fathered two bastards before he turned nineteen, one in the Reach and one in King's Landing. Braxton became a close companion to Princess Saera_Targaryen, Alys_Turnberry, Perianne_Moore, Jonah_Mooton and Roy_Connington. The six became inseparable at every feast and ball, hunted and hawked together, and once sailed across Blackwater Bay to Dragonstone. In 84 AC, Roy, Jonah, and Braxton were found drunk at the Blue Pearl, a brothel in King's Landing, laughing as the king's fool, Tom_Turnip, was surrounded by whores. When asked by men of the City Watch, Roy admitted they thought it would be funny to see the fool do the deed, and Jonah admitted it was Princess Saera's idea. When Perianne and Alys were questioned about the incident, it was revealed that Alys was pregnant outside of wedlock, and that Saera had coerced them into sexual acts with her male favorites. Roy, Jonah, and Braxton were then imprisoned in the dungeons for one night. When questioned by King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen, Saera confessed to her parents that she had bedded all three of her male favorites, with each of them thinking he was the first to take her maidenhead. She proposed a solution of redemption by marrying all three of her favorites, like King Maegor_I_Targaryen and his six wives, at which the king grew furious. The harshest punishment was reserved for Ser Braxton since he was the most guilty of the three, the first to introduce sexual activity to Saera and her friends. Seated on the Iron Throne, Jaehaerys condemned Braxton to have his tongue and nose removed, as well as to be gelded. The king also ordered that Braxton's legs and arms would be broken and healed in a way to ensure he was a cripple for the rest of his life. However, Jaehaerys then allowed Braxton to invoke trial by combat instead of that punishment. While Stinger expected to face a Kingsguard, the king chose to fight the knight himself. The nineteen-year-old Braxton faced the forty-nine-year-old king the next morning, with both men equipped with shields and full armor. Jaehaerys was armed with Blackfyre, while Ser Braxton chose a morningstar, hoping the king was unaccustomed to defending himself against that weapon. The youthful Stinger hoped to overwhelm the older king, but Jaehaerys took every blow on his shield. When the exhausted Braxton was unable to raise his arm, Jaehaerys cut him several times in weak points and then stabbed Stinger through his visor after the knight fell. Princess Saera was forced to watch the duel by Jonquil_Darke.

name: Brea
bio: Brea is the oldest daughter of Brusco, a Braavosi fishmonger. She has one younger sister, Talea, and a couple of brothers.

name: Brella
bio: Brella is a servant in King's Landing. Brella was originally in service to Renly_Baratheon, running his household while he stayed in King's Landing.

name: Brendel Byrne
bio: Brendel Byrne is a sellsword and knight in service to the Golden Company.

name: Brenett
bio: Brenett is a maester who serves House Frey at the Twins.

name: Briar
bio: Briar was a female wildling skinchanger who could inhabit the body of a shadowcat. Briar was one of the skinchangers that Varamyr met at a meeting of wargs. Varamyr wanted to have a shadowcat as soon as he saw Briar's. She is long dead.

name: Brienne Tarth
bio: Brienne Tarth, commonly called Brienne of Tarth after her island home, is the only living child and heir of Lord Selwyn_Tarth of Evenfall. Brienne is sometimes called the Maid of Tarth and mocked as Brienne the Beauty. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Brienne is portrayed by Gwendoline Christie. Brienne was born to Lord Selwyn_Tarth, Lord of Evenfall Hall, on the island of Tarth. Her mother died when she was so young that she does not remember her. She had a single older brother, Galladon_Tarth, who drowned when he was eight and she was four, and two sisters, both of whom died in the cradle. This makes her the only living offspring of Lord Selwyn, a fact that naturally encouraged him to find a suitable match for her. Given her ugly and ungainly appearance and attitude, however, the task was a difficult one, especially since Brienne was bent on a martial life rather than a marital one, more comfortable with a sword than in the company of court society. She was tutored by her septa, Roelle, and had the equivalent of a knight's training by a master-at-arms, Ser Goodwin. Brienne has known Ser Cortnay_Penrose since she was a girl. Brienne has learned many songs by heart since Evenfall Hall always had singers in her youth. Brienne's father successfully found three men willing to betroth her, though all the betrothals fell through. A younger son of Lord Bryen_Caron was Brienne's first betrothed. They met only once, on the occasion of their betrothal, when she was seven and he was ten. The boy died two years later from a chill, along with his father, mother and sisters. Had he not died, Brienne would have been wed to him within the year of her first flowering. The second betrothal was to Ser Ronnet_Connington, who was six years older than her. On their meeting, Red Ronnet gave her a rose, and told her it was all she would have of him, breaking the betrothal as he felt she was a "freak". The last man Brienne was betrothed to, Ser Humfrey_Wagstaff, warned her he would insist she act like "a proper woman" once they were wed. Though only sixteen, Brienne replied she would only accept such a demand from a man who could beat her in combat. In the effort, Humfrey ended up with three broken bones and a broken betrothal. After that, Lord Selwyn stopped trying to arrange a marriage for Brienne. Brienne's only positive encounter with a man occurred when House Tarth's liege, Lord Renly_Baratheon, visited their island during his coming of age progress. He crossed paths with Brienne and treated her courteously, and she fell in love with him. Sometime later, Lord Selwyn sent his daughter to Storm's End. Renly's fool has made cruel japes about Brienne.

name: Briony
bio: Briony is a common woman. She is the mother of Joy_Hill, the bastard daughter of Gerion_Lannister.

name: Brogg
bio: Brogg has black hair, and is described as being a man of few words. Brogg is among the wildling leaders who accompany Tormund to the Wall in declaring a truce with the Night's Watch. Like the other leaders, Brogg gives a son as hostage to the Watch to assure his and his people's good behavior. Lord Commander Jon_Snow later considers giving Brogg the command of one of the abandoned castles along the Wall but Bowen_Marsh rejects the idea. Brogg is present in the Shieldhall when Jon_Snow announces the letter purportedly from Ramsay Bolton entitled 'Bastard'.

name: Broken Nose
bio: Broken Nose is how Arya_Stark refers to a member of the gold cloaks of King's Landing. His given name is unknown.

name: Bronn
bio: Bronn is a low-born sellsword of great skill and cunning. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is played by Jerome Flynn.

name: Brude
bio: Brude was an archmaester of the Citadel. Brude was born in the shadow city of Sunspear. He famously observed that Dorne has more in common with the north than the rest of the Seven Kingdoms lying between them. Both regions are thinly peopled and cling stubbornly to their laws and traditions, and neither was actually conquered by House Targaryen.
