In addition, Brude observed that Dorne and the north are derided as 'savages' by the other five 'civilized' kingdoms.

name: Brus Buckler
bio: Brus Buckler is a knight from House Buckler. He is the cousin of Ralph_Buckler, Lord of Bronzegate.

name: Brusco
bio: Brusco is a fishmonger from Braavos. He has two daughters, Brea and Talea, and at least two sons.

name: Big Brusco
bio: Big Brusco is a mummer from Braavos. He is part of the group of mummers led by Izembaro at The Gate.

name: Brute
bio: The brute is how Victarion_Greyjoy refers to a thrall and oarsman in the Iron Fleet. He has been rowing for twenty years.

name: Bryan Frey
bio: Bryan Frey is the youngest son of Walton_Frey and Deana_Hardyng. He is one of the numerous great-grandchildren of Lord Walder_Frey.

name: Bryan Fossoway
bio: Bryan Fossoway is a knight from House Fossoway of Cider Hall.

name: Bryan of Oldtown
bio: Bryan of Oldtown was a merchant-adventurer and a ship captain who once journeyed through the Shivering Sea. Hailing from Oldtown in the Reach, Bryan was the captain of the cog Spearshaker. Bryan reported that the Dothraki language name for the lost race that inhabited the Kingdom of the Ifequevron means "those who walk in the woods". He also reported that the Ibbenese he met claimed these woods walkers bless households that leave offerings of leaf, stone, and water.

name: Bryce Caron
bio: Bryce Caron is the Lord of Nightsong and the head of House Caron. Bryce became the head of House Caron after Bryen_Caron, mother, brother, and all his sisters succumbed to a terrible chill in 289 AC. Bryce's only known living relative is his bastard half-brother, Ser Rolland_Storm.

name: Bryen
bio: Bryen is the captain of the guards for House Baelish at their tower in the Fingers.

name: Bryen Caron
bio: Bryen Caron was the Lord of Nightsong and the head of House Caron during the early reign of King Robert_I_Baratheon. Lord Bryen was the father of Bryce_Caron, a second, younger legitimate son, as well as a bastard son, Rolland_Storm. He also had several daughters. He, along with his wife, second son, and all his daughters died of a chill in 289 AC. Bryen's second son was twelve and had been engaged to Brienne of Tarth for two years at his time of death.

name: Bryen Farring
bio: Bryen Farring is a member of House Farring, and a squire of Stannis_Baratheon. He is the son of Ser Gilbert_Farring.

name: Bryndemere Tarth
bio: Bryndemere Tarth was the Evenstar and the Lord of Tarth, and the head of House Tarth during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. When the Dance of the Dragons broke out in 129 AC, Ser Otto_Hightower suspected that the Evenstar of Tarth may side with Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen and the blacks. It is unknown if he did or if he followed his liege lord, Borros_Baratheon, and supported the greens. In 133 AC, Ser Gedmund_Peake and Ned_Bean were sent against Racallio_Ryndoon, the self-proclaimed King of the Narrow Sea. On their way to the Stepstones, they were joined first by the Velaryon fleet commanded by Lord Alyn_Velaryon and then by Lord Bryndemere who brought a dozen longships to further swell their ranks. At Tarth, Lord Bryndemere told them Racallio had allied himself with the Archon of Tyrosh and the Sealord of Braavos.

name: Brynden Blackwood
bio: Brynden Blackwood is the eldest son and heir of Lord Tytos_Blackwood of Raventree Hall.

name: Brynden Rivers
bio: Brynden Rivers, called Lord Bloodraven, was the bastard son of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen by his sixth mistress, Lady Melissa_Blackwood. He had two older sisters, Mya_Rivers and Gwenys_Rivers, and numerous half-siblings. As his mother was highborn, Brynden was counted among the Great Bastards. Like his bastard siblings, Brynden was legitimized by King Aegon IV in 184 AC. Brynden's personal arms were a white dragon with red eyes breathing red flame on a black field. Brynden was a Targaryen loyalist during the Blackfyre Rebellions. He served as the Hand of the King to King Aerys_I_Targaryen and King Maekar_I_Targaryen. He was eventually sent to the Night's Watch by King Aegon_V_Targaryen, where he rose to the position of Lord Commander. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Brynden is portrayed by Struan Rodger (Season 4) and Max von Sydow (Season 6). In House of the Dragon, he is portrayed by Joshua Ben-Tovim.[N 1]

name: Brynden Tully
bio: Brynden Tully, also known as Brynden Blackfish, Brynden the Blackfish, or simply the Blackfish, is a knight from House Tully. He is the younger brother of Hoster_Tully, Lord Paramount of the Trident, and the uncle of Lady Catelyn_Stark, Lady Lysa_Arryn, and Ser Edmure_Tully. The Blackfish has served as the Knight of the Gate for House Arryn since Robert's Rebellion. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Brynden is portrayed by Clive Russell. Brynden is the brother of Hoster_Tully, younger than him by five years. The brothers were close through their childhood. Together with Norbert_Vance, Brynden squired for Lord Darry, and was eventually knighted. In 260 AC, Brynden fought in the War of the Ninepenny Kings against the  Band of Nine on the Stepstones. There, he proved himself a great and worthy knight, and gained fame and renown throughout the Seven Kingdoms. After their Lord_Tully_(father_of_Hoster) died, Brynden's older brother Hoster inherited the rule of Riverrun. Hoster sought to betroth Brynden to Bethany_Redwyne, but Brynden refused, straining their relationship. While other lords like Lord_Bracken_(Aerys_II) and Walder_Frey made offers as well, Brynden remained unwed. George R. R. Martin has thus far not shed any light on why Brynden has chosen to remain unwed. When Catelyn_Stark was eight, Hoster called Brynden the "black goat of the Tully flock" during an argument. Brynden pointed out that their sigil was a fish, which made him a black fish instead of a black goat. Brynden took for his personal sigil the Tully emblem, but with a black trout jumping, instead of a silver trout, and he became known as the "Blackfish". While Brynden's relationship with Hoster was strained, he had a good relationship with Hoster's children, Catelyn, Lysa_Arryn and Edmure_Tully, as well as Hoster's young ward, Petyr_Baelish. When Hoster was too busy and Lady Minisa_Whent too ill, the children ran to Brynden with their problems and stories. On the night when Catelyn's betrothal to Brandon_Stark was announced, Petyr, who had been in love with Catelyn, passed out drunk on a table after Catelyn rejected a kiss from him. In order to prevent Hoster from seeing his young ward like this, Brynden carried Petyr away. Hoster and Tywin_Lannister, Lord of Casterly Rock, considered betrothing Hoster's younger daughter, Lysa, to Tywin's heir, Jaime_Lannister. As such, Lord Sumner_Crakehall sent Jaime, his squire, with a message to Riverrun. Hoster took a fortnight to write his reply, during which he sat Jaime next to Lysa with every meal. Jaime ignored the shy Lysa, however, instead pressing Brynden for stories about the War of the Ninepenny Kings.

name: Bryndon Hightower
bio: Bryndon Hightower[N 1] was a knight from House Hightower during the Dance of the Dragons. During the First Battle of Tumbleton in the Dance of the Dragons, Ser Bryndon put himself between his liege lord and cousin, Ormund_Hightower, and the advancing Winter Wolves, lead by Roderick_Dustin, the Lord of Barrowton. Bryndon took off Roddy the Ruin's shield arm with his longaxe, but the northman still killed both Hightowers.

name: Bu Gai
bio: Bu Gai, seventeenth of the azure emperors, is the current God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti. Gai's seat is located at the city of Yin, in a palace said to be larger than all of King's Landing. His rule is challenged by imperial general Pol_Qo in Trader Town, who claims to be the first of the orange emperors. Another pretender is a Sorcerer_lord, claiming to be the sixty-ninth yellow emperor despite the fall of that dynasty a thousand years ago, who is exiled to the city of Carcosa, far to the east of Yi Ti.

name: Lady Buckler
bio: Lady Buckler was the Lady of Bronzegate and the head of House Buckler during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Her given name is unknown. In 133 AC, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Lady Buckler came to King's Landing with her two daughters, hoping that one of the two maidens would be chosen as Aegon III's new queen. However, the three ladies did not reach the capital, as they drowned when the boat that was carrying them across the Blackwater Rush foundered and sank. Some considered their deaths to be part of the "Maiden's Day curse". It is possible that this accident was orchestrated by Lord Unwin_Peake, the Hand of the King, who wished to promote his Myrielle_Peake as Aegon III's second queen.

name: Lord Buckler
bio: Lord Buckler was the Lord of Bronzegate and the head of House Buckler during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Buckler was arrested and put in the Red Keep's dungeons by Aegon_II_Targaryen's supporters for supporting his half-sister, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen. When given the choice between swearing his allegiance to Aegon II or being killed by the King's Justice, Lord Buckler was one of the nobles who chose to keep their sworn words and were beheaded for it. Their heads were mounted on spikes above the city’s gates.

name: Buford Bulwer
bio: Buford Bulwer (or Theomore Bulwer[N 1]), better known as the Old Ox, was a knight of House Bulwer during the reigns of kings Daeron_II_Targaryen and Aerys_I_Targaryen. Buford is said to have slain forty men in the Battle of the Redgrass Field in 196 AC. However, according to Ser Maynard_Plumm, the number Buford had slain on the Redgrass Field grew higher every single passing year. Ser Buford attended the Whitewalls tourney in 212 AC. He faced Ser Uthor_Underleaf in the lists, but was defeated in the fifth tilt. He was injured upon being unhorsed and taken to the maester.

name: Lady Bulwer's brother
bio: Lady Bulwer's brother is a member of House Bulwer.

name: Bump
bio: Bump was the younger brother of Varamyr and Meha. He was attacked and killed by dogs. Bump was born a healthy child to the parents of Varamyr Sixskins. He was nicknamed Bump by his elder sister Meha. Varamyr, resentful of his little brother and that their mother was going to name him after their father, skinchanged into one of the family's dogs (Loptail, Sniff or Growler) and attacked and killed Bump. When Varamyr's father found the dogs sniffing around the child's body, he did not know which one had killed his son, so he put down all three. Varamyr slipped inside Loptail as his father killed him, and screamed when the dog died, which is how Varamyr's parents discovered that he was a warg. Bump was two years old when he died, three days shy of his third name day.

name: Burning Knight
bio: The Burning Knight was a man living at King's Landing during the Dance of the Dragons. His real name is not known. During the Storming of the Dragonpit, the Burning Knight slew the dragon Morghul. Encased in heavy armor, he rushed headlong into the dragonflame with spear in hand, thrusting the point into the dragon's eye repeatedly, even as the flames melted the steel and burning his flesh, thus giving him his nickname.

name: Burton Crakehall
bio: Burton Crakehall is a knight of House Crakehall. He is the younger brother of Lord Roland Crakehall.

name: Burton Humble
bio: Burton Humble is an ironborn raider in Victarion_Greyjoy's crew aboard the Iron Victory.

name: Butterbumps
bio: Butterbumps is the fool and jester of House Tyrell.

name: Butterwell (Hand of the King)
bio: Lord Butterwell was the Lord of Whitewalls and the head of House Butterwell during the reign of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen. He served as Hand of the King to Aegon IV. When King Aegon_IV_Targaryen visited Lord Butterwell's castle he gifted him with a dragon egg in return for "access" to his three maiden daughters. According to tales, Aegon impregnated all three girls the same night. He had Whitewalls built, the pale castle was commonly called the Milkhouse by the smallfolk who lived near it. Its walls, keep, and towers were made of white stone quarried in the Vale of Arryn, and its floors and pillars were crafted from white marble veined with gold. The rafters were carved from the trunks of weirwoods.

name: Lord Butterwell
bio: Lord Butterwell was the head of House Butterwell during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Butterwell was one of the nobles arrested and put in the Red Keep's dungeons by Aegon_II_Targaryen's supporters for supporting his half-sister, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen. When given the choice between swearing his allegiance to Aegon II or being killed by the King's Justice, Lord Butterwell, along with Lord_Rosby and Lord_Stokeworth, pledged his support to Aegon II. While Rosby and Stokeworth were executed by Rhaenyra after the fall of King's Landing, Lord Butterwell is not mentioned again. It is unknown if he survived the war.

name: Butts
bio: Butts is one of the wildlings who Jon_Snow allows to cross the Wall and settle in Mole's Town.

name: Buu
bio: Buu is a resident of King's Landing.

name: Byam Flint
bio: Byam Flint is a knight from House Flint and a brother of the Night's Watch. It is unknown from which branch of House Flint he comes from.[N 1] He is stationed at the Shadow Tower.

name: Byan Votyris
bio: Byan Votyris is a Norvoshi merchant and the captain of a great trading caravan.

name: Byren Flowers
bio: Byren Flowers, known as Black Byren Flowers, was a supporter of Daemon_I_Blackfyre during the First Blackfyre Rebellion. He was considered one of the finest knights of his time.

name: Byron Swann
bio: Byron Swann was a knight from House Swann and the second son of the Lord of Stonehelm, during the reign of King Aegon_II_Targaryen. In 129 AC, Ser Byron Swann was among the two dozen knights present while Lord Borros_Baratheon and Prince Aemond_Targaryen haggled over the betrothal of the prince to one of Lord Baratheon's daughters. Byron witnessed Prince Lucerys_Velaryon's arrival, and the confrontation between the two princes. Shortly after, both princes left, resulting in the fight above Shipbreaker Bay and the beginning of the Dance of the Dragons. In 130 AC, about the same time of the Butcher's Ball, Byron and his squire set out to slay a dragon. The knight tried to accomplish the feat by sneaking up upon the dragon, while hiding behind his silvered shield so the beast would see only its reflection, as Serwyn_of_the_Mirror_Shield legendarily did to the dragon Urrax. The identity of the dragon is disputed. The court fool Mushroom, who was with Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen, asserted in his Testimony that Byron had attempted to kill Rhaenyra's dragon Syrax; the same thing was written in an unspecified text, presumably a letter, by Ser Byron's squire.[N 1] However, Grand Maester Munkun stated in his book The Dance of the Dragons, A True Telling, that it was Prince Aemond's dragon Vhagar who Byron tried to kill, in an effort to end the prince's raids across the riverlands. Nevertheless, this is unlikely since House Swann supported the prince's brother, King Aegon_II_Targaryen, and the greens. Munkun largely drew his information from Grand Maester Orwyle, who was imprisoned at the time. Another chronicler, Septon Eustace, claimed that the knight tried to slay Sunfyre, the dragon of King Aegon II, though this is certainly a mistake, as Sunfyre's whereabouts were unknown at the time. All accounts agree that the dragon stirred upon the knight's approach and unleashed its fire, burning through the mirror shield and roasting Ser Byron Swann, who died screaming.

name: Byron the Beautiful
bio: Ser Byron the Beautiful is a hedge knight.

name: Bywin Strong
bio: Bywin Strong was a knight from House Strong and the Lord of Harrenhal. He was the first Strong to hold Harrenhal. In 73 AC, after the death of the widowed Queen Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), who had resided at Harrenhal, her brother King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen granted the castle to Bywin Strong. At the end of the year, it was discovered that Bywin's brother, Ser Lucamore_Strong of the Kingsguard, had disgraced his vows by marrying three times and fathering sixteen children. When matters were brought up to the king, Jaehaerys put Lucamore's first wife and their children in Lord Bywin's charge. By 105 AC, the lordship of Harrenhal had passed to Lyonel_Strong.

name: Cadwyl
bio: Cadwyl[N 1] is an ironborn warrior.

name: Cadwyn
bio: Cadwyn is a knight from the westerlands.

name: Lord Cafferen
bio: Lord Cafferen was the head of House Cafferen and Lord of Fawnton during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. During Robert's Rebellion, Lord Cafferen, faithful to House Targaryen, was captured by his liege lord, Robert_I_Baratheon, in the battles at Summerhall. He became a loyal friend of Robert until he was slain by Lord Randyll_Tarly at the battle of Ashford. His head was brought to King Aerys_II_Targaryen.

name: Caggo
bio: Caggo, also known as Caggo Corpsekiller, is a sellsword of Dothraki origin and a captain of the Windblown. He is the right hand man of the Tattered_Prince. Ser Lucifer_Long resents that Caggo once took a slave girl from the knight, and the Dothraki then killed the girl because he considered her ugly.

name: Caleotte
bio: Caleotte is a maester in service to House Martell, along with the younger Myles. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Caleotte is portrayed by Colin Azzopardi. Caleotte did not have redspots when he was a child. The maester has been serving House Martell since Doran_Martell's Princess_of_Dorne_(mother_of_Doran) ruled as Princess of Dorne. Caleotte designed a rolling chair with goose-down cushions and wheels of ebony and iron for Doran.

name: Calla Blackfyre
bio: Calla Blackfyre was the eldest daughter of Daemon_I_Blackfyre and Rohanne_of_Tyrosh. Prior to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, Calla's father Daemon_I_Blackfyre agreed to wed her to her half-uncle, Ser Aegor_Rivers. However, it is unknown if and when the marriage took place.

name: Calon
bio: Calon is the son of Cayn, one of the House Stark guards at Winterfell.

name: Calor
bio: Calor is the father of Crawn. He is a member of the Moon Brothers.

name: Camarron of the Count
bio: Camarron of the Count is a pit fighter famed in Meereen. His origin is unknown, but he supports the pit fighting culture of the city.

name: Cameron Tarth
bio: Cameron Tarth was the Lord of Tarth, Lord of Evenfall, and the head of House Tarth during the late reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 92 AC, Tarth was invaded by Myrish exiles fleeing the Myrish Bloodbath, the rogues had conquered the eastern half of Tarth. Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I) left for Tarth on the ninth day of the third month that year to come to the Evenstar's aid. On Tarth, Aemon met with Lord Cameron in the mountains located at the center of the island, where Cameron had made camp. A pair of Myrish scouts eventually discovered the camp, and recognized Lord Cameron. One of them took a shot at Lord Cameron with his crossbow. Although he missed Lord Tarth, Aemon, who had been standing by Lord Cameron's side, was struck in his throat, and drowned in his own blood. The island was eventually liberated by Aemon's younger brother, Prince Baelon.

name: Canker Jeyne
bio: Canker Jeyne is a dockside whore in Braavos.

name: Canty
bio: Canty is a man-at-arms in Stannis_Baratheon's army. It is unknown if he is a King's men or a Queen's men.

name: Captain's sister
bio: The captain's sister is a resident of Duskendale. She is the sister of the captain of the guardsmen at the town gatehouse. Her name is not mentioned.

name: Captain of the Myraham
bio: The captain of the Myraham is a ship captain and a merchant from Oldtown. He has one Daughter_of_the_captain_of_the_Myraham.

name: Captain of the Prince Aemon
bio: The captain of the Prince Aemon is a ship captain of the royal fleet, in service at King's Landing.

name: Captain of the River Arrow
bio: The captain of the River Arrow is a ship captain of the royal fleet, in service at King's Landing.

name: Captain of the Selaesori Qhoran
bio: The captain of the Selaesori Qhoran is a ship captain and a merchant operating in the Summer Sea.

name: Captain of the Wildwind
bio: The captain of the Wildwind is a ship captain of the royal fleet, in service at King's Landing.

name: Carellen Smallwood
bio: Carellen Smallwood is a noblewoman of House Smallwood and is the daughter of Lord Theomar_Smallwood and Lady Ravella_Swann.

name: Carolei Waynwood
bio: Carolei Waynwood is a noblewoman of House Waynwood and the widow of Ser Geremy_Frey. They have two children: Sandor_Frey, a squire to Ser Donnel_Waynwood, and Cynthea_Frey, a ward of Lady Anya_Waynwood.

name: Carrot
bio: Carrot is one of the mule-handlers in service to House Royce of the Gates of the Moon.

name: Casper Straw
bio: Casper Straw was a former knight from House Straw who served the Faith of the Seven during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Casper was once a knight who served as a captain in the Warrior's Sons. After their abolition in the aftermath of the Faith Militant uprising, Straw served as steward to the High_Lickspittle. In 50 AC, Ser Orryn_Baratheon and a dozen men-at-arms came to the motherhouse attached to the Starry Sept in Oldtown. On orders from his brother Lord Rogar_Baratheon, Orryn demanded that Princess Rhaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon), a novice of the Faith, be given into his custody. The suspicious Septa Karolyn, under the guise of summoning the girl, instead sent for the High Septon. The stormlanders were soon opposed by the alert and wary Casper and thirty armed septons, who falsely claimed that forty Hightower knights were on their way, upon which Orryn and his men surrender and were taken into custody.

name: Casper Wylde
bio: Casper Wylde is the Lord of the Rain House and the head of House Wylde.

name: Caspor Hill
bio: Caspor Hill is a serjeant in service to the Golden Company. As in the free companies a man can call himself whatever he chooses, it is unknown if Caspor is a bastard from the Westerlands.

name: Cass
bio: Cass is a serving wench and prostitute at the Peach in Stoney Sept.

name: Cassana Estermont
bio: Cassana Estermont was a noblewoman of House Estermont during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. She was the wife of Lord Steffon_Baratheon of Storm's End, and they had three sons together: Robert_I_Baratheon, Stannis_Baratheon, and Renly_Baratheon. Cassana was the daughter of Lord Estermont, Lord of Greenstone. In 262 AC, Robert_I_Baratheon was born to Cassana and her husband, Lord Steffon_Baratheon, at Storm's End. Their son Stannis_Baratheon followed in 264 AC, and Renly_Baratheon in 277 AC. In 278 AC, Steffon Baratheon was sent to the Free Cities by his cousin, King Aerys_II_Targaryen, to search for a bride for his son, Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen. Steffon and Cassana's quest was unsuccessful, but in Volantis they did find a remarkable fool, called Patchface. During the return journey, their ship, the Windproud, was caught in a severe storm. Within sight of Robert and Stannis, who were standing on the parapets of Storm's End, the ship was smashed on the rocks and sank in Shipbreaker Bay. Cassana, Steffon, and hundreds of men drowned; Patchface was the only survivor.

name: Cassander
bio: Archmaester Cassander is an Archmaester of the Citadel and the author of Song of the Sea: How the Lands Were Severed, where he argues that the Arm of Dorne was not shattered by the children of the forest's greenseers with the Hammer of the Waters, but rather covered by the Song of the Sea, a slow rising of the waters caused by a series of long, hot summers and short, warm winters that melted the ice in the frozen lands north of the Shivering Sea..

name: Cassandra Baratheon
bio: Cassandra Baratheon was a noblewoman from House Baratheon during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen and Aegon_III_Targaryen. She was the eldest of the four daughters of Lord Borros_Baratheon of Storm's End with his wife, Lady Elenda_Caron.  Cassandra and her three sisters, Maris_Baratheon, Ellyn_Baratheon, and Floris_Baratheon, were known as the Four Storms. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Cassandra is portrayed by Emma O'Hara.

name: Cassella Vaith
bio: Cassella Vaith was a member of House Vaith and a Lady of Vaith. She was the third mistress of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen, although prior to his ascent to the Iron Throne. In 158 AC, after the Submission of Sunspear during the conquest of Dorne, Cassella was one of the hostages sent to King's Landing, as she was the daughter of Lord_Vaith_(father_of_Cassella). She was escorted by Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen, who kept Cassella a "hostage" in his own chambers. After the Dornishmen revolted and killed King Daeron_I_Targaryen, all the hostages were to be killed. Aegon, by then bored of her, returned Cassella to her place with the other prisoners. The new king, Baelor_I_Targaryen, pardoned all the hostages and personally took them back to Dorne. Cassella never wed or had children. In her old age, the deluded Lady Vaith believed she had been Aegon's one true love and that he would soon send for her. At Vaith circa 209–210 AC, Ser Duncan_the_Tall met an aged Lady Vaith, and spoke to her with such lack of grace that he put his own life and Aegon_V_Targaryen, his squire, in danger. According to Egg, Lady Vaith was mad, so it is unclear if that danger could have been avoided, but that Duncan could have been more gallant.[N 1] For a brief time before he met Rohanne_Webber, Lady of Coldmoat, Duncan worried that she was as old as Lady Vaith. After taking a blow to the head during a joust with Ser Uthor_Underleaf, Dunk sees the faces of many people he'd met on his travels when he wakes up, including Lady Vaith.

name: Cassella Staunton
bio: Cassella Staunton was a noblewoman from House Staunton during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. She was the daughter of Lord_Staunton_(father_of_Cassella) of Rook's Rest. At the age of fourteen, Cassella became a companion to Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) on Dragonstone in 54 AC. She died at the end of the year. At first, it was thought she had died of an illness. Later, it was revealed that she had been poisoned by Androw_Farman using the tears of Lys, who had grown jealous of his wife Rhaena's friendships with her ladies and weary of their mockery toward him.

name: Casso Mogat
bio: Casso Mogat is the captain of the Merry Midwife. His mother was a Sisterton whore, and his father an Ibbenese whaler.

name: Caster
bio: Caster is a figure from the Dawn Age and the father of Corlos, the first lord of House Casterly, who took their name from Corlos's father.

name: Castos
bio: Archmaester Castos is an archmaester at the Citadel in Oldtown.

name: Lady Caswell
bio: Lady Caswell was the wife of Lord_Caswell_(Dance), the ruling Lord of Bitterbridge and the head of House Caswell during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. Her given name and birth house are unknown. After the execution of her Lord_Caswell_(Dance), Lady Caswell ruled Bitterbridge in the name of her young son. Lady Caswell had the gates of her castle barred and refused hospitality to anyone, knight or lord, who was not from her own house. One evening, a riot broke out on the stone bridge of the city. Lady Caswell sent her knights to disperse the mob, who discovered that the origin of the troubles was none other than the presence of Prince Maelor_Targaryen, son of King Aegon_II_Targaryen, and his sworn shield, Ser Rickard_Thorne of the Kingsguard, at an inn named the Hogs Head. Both having been massacred by the mob, she would have exclaimed, according to Mushroom's Testimony, that the curse of the Seven was going to pursue them all because of this crime. She therefore ordered the hangings of three of people implicated in the death of the prince: the stable boy Sly_(stableboy), the washerwoman Willow_Pound-Stone and the owner of the horse on which Ser Rickard Thorne had tried to flee. Lady Caswell sent Maelor's head and Rickard's body to Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen at King's Landing. Maelor's dragon egg was sent to Lord Ormund_Hightower, a supporter of Aegon II, who at that time was besieging Lady_Merryweather at Longtable thirty leagues away. Lady Caswell hoped that this diplomatic gesture would appease the fury of Lord Ormund. Lord Ormund and his army soon arrived at Bitterbridge. Lady Caswell offered to capitulate in exchange for a safe conduct for her and her family, but Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I), still furious after the death of his nephew, did not intend to grant any mercy to the murderers. Bitterbridge was sacked and burned, while the population was executed or pushed into the Mander to drown. After observing the spectacle from the ramparts of her castle, Lady Caswell ordered the gates of the castle opened, knowing that no wall would protect her family from the dragonfire of Daeron's dragon, Tessarion. When Lord Ormund rode up, he saw Lady Caswell standing on the roof of the gatehouse with a noose around her neck. She begged him to spare her children, and threw herself from the ramparts, hanging herself. Lord Ormund captured Lady Caswell's sons and daughter. He obliged to her last wish and sent them to Oldtown as hostages. The garrison of Bitterbridge was not so fortunate, however. They were all put to the sword.

name: Lord Caswell
bio: Lord Caswell was the Lord of Bitterbridge and the head of House Caswell during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. Old Lord Caswell was Lord of Bitterbridge when his son, Lorent_Caswell, took a longsword made by the castle's armorer for his own son, Rolly_Duckfield. After taking revenge by wounding Lorent, Rolly fled the Reach for the exiles of the Golden Company. After his death, Lorent succeeded him as Lord of Bitterbridge.

name: Lord Caswell (Dance)
bio: Lord Caswell was the Lord of Bitterbridge and the head of House Caswell during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, he is named Allun Caswell and portrayed by Paul Hickey. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Caswell was arrested and put in the Red Keep's dungeons by Aegon_II_Targaryen's supporters for supporting his half-sister, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen. When given the choice between swearing his allegiance to Aegon II or being killed by the King's Justice, Lord Caswell was one of the nobles who chose to keep their sworn words and were beheaded for it. Their heads were mounted on spikes above the city’s gates. As Lord Caswell's son and successor was still a young child, the Bastard of Bitterbridge, Tom_Flowers, took command of the Caswell men in the field, while Lord Caswell's widow, Lady_Caswell, ruled Bitterbridge in the name of her young son.

name: Catelyn Bracken
bio: Catelyn Bracken is a noblewoman of House Bracken, the third daughter of Lord Jonos_Bracken of Stone Hedge, and the first by his Lady_Bracken.

name: Catelyn Stark
bio: Catelyn Stark, née Tully, is the wife of Eddard_Stark, Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North. Together they have five children: Robb_Stark, Sansa_Stark, Arya_Stark, Bran_Stark, and Rickon_Stark. Catelyn is known as Cat to close family and friends. Catelyn was born into House Tully of Riverrun, the liege lords of the riverlands. She is the eldest daughter of Lord Hoster_Tully and Lady Minisa Whent, and has two younger siblings: Lysa_Arryn and Ser Edmure_Tully. She is a major POV character in the books. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Catelyn is portrayed by Michelle Fairley. She was previously played by Jennifer Ehle in the rejected pilot episode of the series. Catelyn is the eldest daughter of Hoster_Tully, the Lord Paramount of the Trident, Lord of Riverrun, and head of House Tully, and his wife, Lady Minisa_Whent. She was anointed with the seven oils and named in the sept of Riverrun. Catelyn had two older brothers, who both died in infancy, causing her father to regard her as his heir until the birth of Catelyn's younger brother, Edmure_Tully. Catelyn also has a sister, Lysa_Arryn, who is two years younger than her. Her mother Minisa died in childbirth with her fourth son and sixth child, who also died shortly after. Catelyn was raised at Riverrun. She was close with her sister Lysa, and would play with her two younger siblings, as well as with her father's young ward, Petyr_Baelish. Just as her younger siblings and Petyr were wont to do, Catelyn would go to her uncle, Ser Brynden_Tully, with her tears and tales, whenever Lord Hoster was too busy, and Lady Minisa was too ill. When Catelyn's mother died in childbirth, along with her son, Lord Hoster told his daughter that she had to be the Lady of Riverrun in her mother's stead. Catelyn does not have many memories of her mother, though she still remembers her warm smile and soft hands. Catelyn's relationship with her father was a close one, and according to Catelyn, Lord Hoster cherished her best of all his children. Hoster taught her how to ride in the godswood of Riverrun. Whenever Hoster would ride off to court or a battle, he would tell Catelyn to watch for him from the battlements of the castle, which she did. When his eyes tired, Hoster would have Catelyn read to him in his solar. Catelyn and Lysa played together as girls, and even had a secret language. Once, they made mud pies and served them to Petyr, who ate them and as a consequence was sick for a week. Catelyn and Lysa also practiced kissing with Petyr in the godswood, where Petyr attempted to stick his tongue in their mouths - an attempt which Catelyn halted, but Lysa both allowed and enjoyed it. Catelyn often accompanied her father on his travels, and had visited the inn at the crossroads on multiple occasions. Once, Catelyn, Lysa and Petyr accompanied Hoster to Seagard. During the journey to the seat of House Mallister, they stopped at Oldstones, where Catelyn had played at being Jenny_of_Oldstones, while Petyr had pretended to be her Duncan_Targaryen. On their way back from Seagard to Riverrun, Catelyn and Lysa got lost in the mist, and were eventually found by Petyr. As the Lady of Winterfell, Catelyn became familiar with the lords of the north, and she once comforted a fellow southron, Lady Lynesse_Hightower, by telling her how she had once felt out of place in the north, but had come to love it all the same. Eddard had a small sept built at Winterfell for Catelyn, so she could pray to her own gods. Catelyn saw her nephew, Lysa's son Robert_Arryn, when the boy was less than a year old. Catelyn met Stannis_Baratheon when he was less bald than he is now.

name: Catspaw
bio: An unnamed catspaw or footpad is an assassin who tries to kill Bran_Stark. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, the catspaw is portrayed by Lalor Roddy.

name: Cayn
bio: Cayn is a guard sworn to House Stark. He has a son, Calon.

name: Cedra
bio: Cedra is a servant of House Martell at Sunspear. Garin_(orphan) once claimed to have bedded Cedra.

name: Cedric Payne
bio: Cedric Payne was a knight from House Payne. Ser Cedric took Podrick_Payne in after he was abandoned by his mother. Podrick squired for Cedric until the War of the Five Kings where during Lord Tywin_Lannister's campaign in the riverlands, Cedric died.

name: Cedrik Storm
bio: Cedrik Storm, also known as the Bastard of Bronzegate, was a bastard of House Buckler. In the White Book, Ser Barristan_Selmy's victory over Cedrik is recorded.

name: Celia Tully
bio: Celia Tully was a noblewoman from House Tully during the reign of King Aegon_V_Targaryen. She was a daughter of the ruling Lord of Riverrun.[N 1] King Aegon_V_Targaryen and his wife, Queen Betha_Blackwood, made several betrothals for their children in 237 AC, amongst them was Celia and Prince Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen. However, the betrothal was broken by Jaehaerys three years later, and he married his sister, Princess Shaera_Targaryen, for love instead.

name: Cellador
bio: Cellador is the septon of Castle Black, where he maintains a small sept.

name: Cerelle Lannister
bio: Cerelle Lannister was the Lady of Casterly Rock and the head of House Lannister during the beginning of King Aerys_I_Targaryen's reign. Cerelle was the only child of Lord Tybolt_Lannister and his wife, Lady Teora_Kyndall. When Tybolt died in 212 AC, Cerelle succeeded him as the Lady of Casterly Rock, at the age of three. Her uncle, Gerold_Lannister, served as her regent. Cerelle's rule would last for less than a year, as she herself suddenly passed away in 213 AC. After her death, she was succeeded by her uncle Gerold as Lord of Casterly Rock. Since both Lady Cerelle and her father Tybolt died suddenly and under suspicious circumstances, it was whispered that their deaths had been caused by Gerold, so that he would inherit the rule of House Lannister and the westerlands. According to a semi-canon source, the most common tale was that Gerold had murdered his niece himself, smothering her with a pillow as she slept. Gerold ruled the westerlands for thirty-one years, earning the sobriquet Gerold the Golden. Despite all his accomplishments, some of his lords and many of his smallfolk had no love for him, as they blamed him for his niece's death. According to a semi-canon source, Lady Ellyn Tarbeck later named Cyrelle_Tarbeck after Lady Cerelle to slight Gerold.

name: Cerelle Lannister
bio: Cerelle Lannister was a member of House Lannister during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Cerelle was the daughter of Lord Jason_Lannister and his wife, Lady Johanna_Lannister. She had four sisters, including Tyshara_Lannister, and one brother, Lord Loreon. After the death of Queen Jaehaera_Targaryen in 133 AC, Lady Johanna set aside her war with the ironmen long enough to write Unwin_Peake that her daughters Cerelle and Tyshara were maidens of noble birth and marriageable age.

name: Cerenna Lannister
bio: Cerenna Lannister is a noblewoman of House Lannister and the daughter of Ser Stafford_Lannister and Myranda_Lefford. She is the sister of Daven_Lannister and Myrielle_Lannister.

name: Cerion Lannister
bio: Cerion Lannister was a King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Cerion extended the rule of the Kingdom of the Rock as far east as the Golden Tooth and its surrounding hills, defeating three lesser kings when they made an alliance against him.

name: Cerissa Brax
bio: Cerissa Brax was a noblewoman from House Brax. She was married to Damon_Lannister_(lord), Lord of Casterly Rock. Cerissa married Damon_Lannister_(lord) known as the Grey Lion. She was the mother of Tybolt_Lannister and Gerold_Lannister.

name: Cerrick
bio: Cerrick was the maester at Coldmoat at the start of the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. He was in the service of Lady Rohanne_Webber. Cerrick came from the Iron Islands. The maester designed the dam of the Chequy Water, which caused a conflict over the river between Lady Rohanne_Webber of Coldmoat and Ser Eustace_Osgrey of Standfast. After Ser Duncan_the_Tall defeated Ser Lucas_Inchfield in a trial by battle, Maester Cerrick treated his wounds. Cerrick was able to resuscitate Duncan after he had drowned in the river during the battle, due to his study of ironborn priests' kiss of life. Duncan's squire, Aegon_V_Targaryen, worried about poison, forced Cerrick to first taste anything he wanted to give the knight while Duncan was still unconscious.

name: Cersei Frey
bio: Cersei Frey, also know as Little Bee, is the second daughter of Ser Raymund_Frey and Beony_Beesbury.

name: Cersei Lannister
bio: Queen Cersei Lannister is the only daughter and eldest child of Lord Tywin_Lannister of Casterly Rock and his wife, Lady Joanna_Lannister. She is the twin of her younger brother, Ser Jaime_Lannister. After Robert's Rebellion, Cersei married King  Robert_I_Baratheon and became Queen of the Seven Kingdoms. She is the mother of Prince Joffrey_Baratheon, Princess Myrcella_Baratheon, and Prince Tommen_Baratheon of House Baratheon of King's Landing. Cersei becomes a POV character in A Feast for Crows. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Cersei is portrayed by Lena Headey, and by Nell Williams as a child in a Season 5 flashback. Cersei was born in 266 AC as the first-born child and only daughter to Ser Tywin_Lannister, heir to Casterly Rock and Hand of the King, and his wife, Lady Joanna_Lannister. Cersei was born shortly before her twin brother Jaime_Lannister, who was holding her foot. King Aerys_II_Targaryen sent the twins their weights in gold as a name day gift, and commanded Tywin to bring the children to court when they were old enough to travel. It is currently unknown whether this visit to King's Landing actually occurred, as the royal court came to Casterly Rock following the death of Lord Tytos_Lannister in 267 AC. Cersei and Jaime did visit King's Landing in 272 AC, when their mother brought them from Casterly Rock for the Anniversary Tourney held to celebrate the tenth year of King Aerys's reign. During their early childhood, Cersei and Jaime were inseparable. They looked so similar as children that not even their father was able to keep them apart. Sometimes they would dress in each other's clothes, and spend a whole day as each other. Occasionally when Cersei wore Jaime's clothes, she took lessons from the master-at-arms in his stead, without anyone realizing. The twins played in the bowels of Casterly Rock, where the caged lions of Cersei's late grandfather, Tytos, were kept. Cersei and Jaime would dare each other to climb into the cage. Cersei once dared to go as far as touch one of the lions, until her brother pulled her away. Cersei and Jaime slept together in the same bed when they were very young, and experimented sexually at a young age. During one of these encounters, they were caught by a servant, who informed their mother. A guard was placed outside Cersei's bedchamber, while Jaime's bedchamber was moved to the other side of the castle. The twins were told never to do anything like that ever again, as Joanna would otherwise be forced to tell their father. Cersei's mother died not long thereafter, in 273 AC, when giving birth to Cersei's younger brother, Tyrion_Lannister. Cersei, then seven years old, despised her youngest sibling for killing her mother. Shortly after Joanna's death, while the family was still in mourning, the Princess_of_Dorne_(mother_of_Doran) arrived at Casterly Rock with her two youngest children, Elia_Martell and Oberyn_Martell. Though Tyrion was kept out of sight during the visit of the Martells, Cersei promised to show him to Elia. On the last day of their visit, Cersei threatened her brother's wet nurse and sent her away, then undid Tyrion's swaddling clothes to better display his dwarfism to the siblings. When Oberyn remarked that Tyrion was not much of a monster, Cersei replied that he had killed her mother, and then attacked the baby, twisting his penis while he screamed, until Jaime stopped her. After being separated from Jaime following their mother's discovery of their sexual experimenting, Cersei had numerous bedmaids and companions, girls her age who were daughters of Tywin's bannermen and household knights. While Cersei occasionally appreciated the company of these girls in keeping her bed warm at night in the cold Rock, she did not like any of them. She felt they were dull, weepy, sneakily telling tales about her, and was convinced they were trying to come between her and Jaime. Few lasted very long.

name: Ceryse Hightower
bio: Queen Ceryse Hightower was a noblewoman from House Hightower and was the daughter of Lord Manfred_Hightower_(son_of_Addam) of Oldtown. Her maternal uncle was one of the High_Septon_(Aegon_I) during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. She eventually married Prince Maegor_I_Targaryen, becoming his first (of many) wives. Ceryse's uncle, the High_Septon_(Aegon_I), protested strongly when Queen Visenya_Targaryen suggested that her son Prince Maegor_I_Targaryen should be wed to Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), and instead suggesting his own niece. Ceryse and Maegor were wed in 25 AC. The ceremony was held at the Starry Sept of Oldtown, with Ceryse's uncle, the High Septon, performing the rites. Ceryse was twenty-three, whereas Maegor was thirteen years old. Maegor boasted to having consummated the marriage a dozen times the night of the wedding, and those who had seen the bedding agreed that Maegor was a lusty husband. While the marriage of Ceryse and Maegor remained childless, Maegor's half-brother Aenys_I_Targaryen had five healthy children. After the birth of Aenys's sixth child, Vaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), in 39 AC, Maegor decided that Ceryse was barren, and he took Alys_Harroway to wife, entering a polygamous marriage. The High_Septon_(Aenys_I) demanded that Maegor should leave the "whore of Harroway" and return to Ceryse. When Maegor was presented with choosing between setting Alys aside, or being exiled for five years, he chose exile and left for Pentos, leaving Ceryse behind in Westeros. Septon Murmison was tasked with making Ceryse fertile, but failed. Ceryse eventually returned to her father in Oldtown. Maegor returned to Westeros to claim the Iron Throne after the death of his brother, King Aenys I, in 42 AC. Later in the same year, Maegor I announced his intent to marry again, taking Tyanna_of_the_Tower as his third wife, who had become his paramour while in exile in Pentos. Grand Maester Myros spoke against the proposed wedding, stating that Maegor's "one true wife" awaited him at the Hightower. Ceryse, still at Oldtown, continued to insist that she was Maegor's only lawful queen. In 43 AC King Maegor and Dowager Queen Visenya_Targaryen moved towards Oldtown, threatening to incinerate the Starry Sept in response to the High Septon's continued condemnation of his polygamous marriages. The High Septon instead died suddenly shortly before they arrived, however. Maegor remained at Oldtown for half a year while presiding over the trials of the Warrior's Sons chapter at Oldtown, and in that time Ceryse reconciled with Maegor. Ceryse agreed to accept Maegor's other two wives, with Maegor in turn swearing to restore Ceryse to all the rights, incomes, and privileges due her as lawful queen. They celebrated their reunion with a great feast and had a second consummation. She later returned to court at King's Landing. Shortly after the completion of the Red Keep in 45 AC, Ceryse died of a sudden illness. It was rumored she had said something to offend Maegor and he ordered Ser Owen_Bush of the Kingsguard to remove her tongue. While Ser Maladon_Moore held her, Ceryse allegedly struggled so much that Owen's knife slipped and he accidentally slashed her throat. Though never proven, this story was widely believed at the time. Most historians believe it was a slander concocted by the king's enemies to further blacken Maegor's repute. Maladon denied the story and stated that Ceryse died of "shrewishness".

name: Cetheres
bio: Archmaester Cetheres is an archmaester at the  Citadel in Oldtown.

name: Chai Duq
bio: Chai Duq was a God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti and the fourth member of his dynasty. Chai Duq took to wife a noblewoman of Valyria and kept a dragon at his court.

name: Chain
bio: Chain is a sellsword and a serjeant in service to the Golden Company. His given name is unknown. Chain's great-grandsire had fought for Daemon_I_Blackfyre in the Battle of the Redgrass Field and fled to exile across the narrow sea with Bittersteel. His father was a sellsword of the Company, and fathered Chain on a camp follower. He was raised to speak the Common Tongue and think of himself as Westerosi.

name: Lord Chambers
bio: Lord Chambers was the Lord and the head of House Chambers during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. During the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Chambers sided with the blacks and Rhaenyra_Targaryen. Lord Chambers, alongside with Lord_Bigglestone and Lord_Perryn, joined Ser Pate_of_Longleaf and his ragged band of survivors from earlier battles and fought together in the Battle by the Lakeshore. It is unknown if he survived the war.

name: Charioteer
bio: The Charioteer is a name the Windblown, a sellsword company hired by Yunkai, mockingly gave to a Yunkish nobleman commanding some of the Yunkish slave solders.

name: Chatana Qo
bio: Chatana Qo, the Arrow of Jhahar, was a warrior woman of the Summer Isles and the daughter and successor of Xanda_Qo, Princess of Sweet Lotus Vale. Chatana fought in a series of wars, which came to be known as the Slavers' Wars. The unity of the Summer Isles, that her mother achieved, did not survive Chatana's reign however, for she wed unwisely and did not rule as well as she fought.

name: Chataya
bio: Chataya is the owner of an upscale brothel in King's Landing. She has a daughter named Alayaya, who works in her establishment. The secret tunnel from Chataya's brothel, to a stable three blocks away, was dug for an unspecified Hand of the King whose honor would not let him openly visit her establishment. Chataya has closely guarded the knowledge of the tunnel's existence. Chataya's daughter, Alayaya, was born in King's Landing, near the time of Robert's Rebellion.

name: Chayle
bio: Chayle is a septon from the Faith of the Seven. He is the guardian of Lady Catelyn's sept at Winterfell, where he hangs carved masks of the Seven, and the librarian of the castle's library tower. Chayle grew up on the shores of the White Knife and is a very good swimmer.

name: Cheese (the rat-catcher)
bio: Cheese was the alias of a ratcatcher turned assassin during the Dance of the Dragons. Cheese's true name is lost to history. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Cheese is portrayed by Mark Stobbart. Cheese was a ratcatcher in the Red Keep, and he also spent time in Flea Bottom. After Prince Aemond_Targaryen killed Prince Lucerys_Velaryon in a fight above Storm's End, the blacks wanted vengeance against the greens. Cheese and the swordsman called Blood were employed by Mysaria, Prince Daemon_Targaryen's spymaster, to slay one of the sons of Aemond's older brother, King Aegon_II_Targaryen. Cheese led Blood into the Red Keep through a forgotten passageway, and creeping through walls, they entered the chambers of Aegon's mother, Queen Alicent_Hightower, in the Tower of the Hand. After Cheese bound and gagged the Dowager Queen and Blood strangled her bedmaid, the assassins waited for Aegon's wife, Queen Helaena_Targaryen, and her children to arrive. Blood killed Helaena's guardsman once she arrived, and Cheese grabbed her youngest son, Prince Maelor_Targaryen. Cheese asked Helaena which of her sons she wanted them to kill. Helaena offered herself but Blood refused, stating it had to be a son. Although Helaena eventually named her youngest son, Maelor, Blood beheaded Prince Jaehaerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon_II) instead. Blood and Cheese then fled through the secret passages with the prince's head in hand. While Blood was captured at the Gate of the Gods and tortured to death, no trace of Cheese was found by the gold cloaks of Ser Luthor_Largent. In his fury, Aegon II ordered all of King's Landing's ratcatchers to be hanged. Afterward, Ser Otto_Hightower brought one hundred cats into the Red Keep to replace them. Aegon II wanted to attack Dragonstone to avenge his son's murder, but Ser Criston_Cole instead suggested sending Ser Arryk_Cargyll to infiltrate the castle.

name: Chella
bio: Chella is the daughter of Cheyk and clan chief of the Black Ears, one of the Vale mountain clans. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Chella is portrayed by Natalia Lee.

name: Chett
bio: Chett is a steward of the Night's Watch. Before he was sent to the Wall, he was a leechman's son in the riverlands. He is the POV character in the prologue of A Storm of Swords. Chett was born around Hag's Mire. His father collected leeches and sold them to maesters, and Chett learned the trade from his father. He was an ugly boy and so girls did not take interest in him. Chett picked flowers for a girl named Bessa_(Hag's_Mire) who had slept with every boy in their village, but she spurned and insulted him. Chett became so angry that he killed her with his knife. After being caught near Sevenstreams, Chett was judged by Ser Walder_Rivers, one of Lord Walder_Frey's bastards, and sent to the Wall. Chett has been a steward assisting Maester Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) for four years. The only women he has slept with have been prostitutes from Mole's Town.

name: Cheyk
bio: Cheyk is a member of the Black Ears, one of the Vale mountain clans. Chella is the daughter of Cheyk, but Cheyk's gender and living status are unknown.

name: Chezdhar zo Rhaezn
bio: Chezdhar zo Rhaezn is a Yunkish nobleman.

name: Chiggen
bio: Chiggen is a sellsword who rides with Bronn.

name: Childer
bio: Maester Childer was a maester of the Citadel. He wrote the book Winter's Kings, or the Legends and Lineages of the Starks of Winterfell.

name: Chiswyck
bio: Chiswyck is a man-at-arms from the westerlands. He is one of the Mountain's men in service to Ser Gregor_Clegane.

name: Choq Choq
bio: Choq Choq was a God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti and the fifteenth and last member of his dynasty. Choq kept a hundred wives and a thousand concubines and sired daughters beyond count, but was never able to produce a son.

name: Lord Chyttering (father of Lucos)
bio: Lord Chyttering is the head of House Chyttering. He is the father of Lucos_Chyttering.

name: Cissy
bio: Cissy is a lowborn woman in the service of House Waynwood. Cissy is the mother of Alys_Stone, the eldest bastard daughter of Ser Harrold_Hardyng. Cissy gained weight from her pregnancy, causing Harrold to lose interest in her. Lady Anya_Waynwood arranged a marriage between Cissy and one of the Waynwood men-at-arms.

name: Clarence Cargyll
bio: Clarence Cargyll was a knight of House Cargyll during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Clarence attended the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Clarence Charlton
bio: Clarence Charlton was a knight from House Charlton during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. In 212 AC, Ser Clarence took part in the tourney held by Lord Ambrose_Butterwell at his castle of Whitewalls to celebrate his second marriage to the daughter of Lord_Frey. Clarence was soundly defeated in the lists by Ambrose's commander and son-in-law, Ser Tommard_Heddle.

name: Clarence Crabb
bio: Clarence Crabb is a legendary hero from House Crabb of Crackclaw Point. Ser Clarence's seat was called the Whispers, so named due to the disembodied heads that he would collect and bring home to his wife, who would raise them from the dead. The dead heads would give him counsel, and talk among themselves in a whisper. Ser Clarence collected the heads from a variety of people, including pirates, lords, wizards, knights, and even one king from Duskendale. Clarence is also said to have fought the squisher king.

name: Clarence Crabb
bio: Clarence Crabb, better known as Clarence the Short, was a knight from House Crabb, who became a member of the Kingsguard at an unknown time.

name: Clarent Crakehall
bio: Clarent Crakehall was a knight from House Crakehall during the Dance of the Dragons. Ser Clarent joined Lord Jason_Lannister's host in support of King Aegon_II_Targaryen during the Dance of the Dragons. Clarent later participated in the Battle by the Lakeshore under Lord Humfrey_Lefford, where he was slain in 130 AC.

name: Clarice Osgrey
bio: Clarice Osgrey was a noblewoman of House Osgrey during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. She was mistress of the households of Aegon's two queens, Jaehaera_Targaryen and Daenaera_Velaryon. Clarice was the widowed aunt of Unwin_Peake, Lord of Starpike. It is unknown if she was a maternal or paternal aunt, and whether she was an Osgrey by birth or by marriage. When her nephew became Hand of the King in 133 AC, he brought his own trusted kin to court, and Unwin put Clarice in charge of Queen Jaehaera_Targaryen's household, supervising her maids, servants, and lady companions. After Jaehaera's death in the 9th moon of 133 AC, Clarice managed the household of the new queen, Daenaera_Velaryon, and stayed in her position after her nephew resigned as Hand in 134 AC. In 135 AC, the king and queen suffered an attempted poisoning, and a conspiracy was made to frame Lady Larra_Rogare, the wife of Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen. When the conspirators were exposed, three of the queen's ladies were accused of being among them. Clarice was also suspected, as she supervised the ladies in Daenaera's household, and was well-acquainted with all the confessed conspirators, including George Graceford. If Clarice had been involved, it was likely Unwin Peake had been in the conspiracy as well. However, the three accused ladies, Cassandra_Baratheon, Lucinda_Penrose, and Priscella_Hogg, all denied Clarice's involvement, only shaking their heads when asked directly.

name: Clarisse Dayne
bio: Clarisse Dayne was the Lady of Starfall and the head of House Dayne during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. When King Maegor_I_Targaryen decided to take another wife, Grand Maester Benifer suggested Clarisse as a possible match in the hopes of detaching House Dayne and their lands from Dorne. Maegor dismissed his advice, however.

name: Clayton Suggs
bio: Clayton Suggs is a knight from House Suggs, and a supporter of Stannis_Baratheon. His coat of arms is a winged pig. Clayton was a hedge knight of lowborn origin, hailing from Flea Bottom in King's Landing. According to Ser Justin_Massey, Clayton gambled with Dragonstone's torturers, and aided them in the questioning of prisoners, especially young women.

name: Clegane (father of Gregor and Sandor)
bio: The second Clegane was the father of Gregor_Clegane and Sandor_Clegane. His first name is unknown. Clegane was born the son of the kennelmaster at Casterly Rock. One autumn year, Clegane_(founder) saved Lord Tytos_Lannister from a lioness. Tytos rewarded the kennelmaster with a knighthood, lands and a towerhouse, and took his son as his squire. Some time later, Clegane inherited the keep and lands from his father. When his son Sandor_Clegane was seven years old, and his son Gregor_Clegane was twelve, a woodcarver set up shop in the village by the keep, and gifted toys to the Cleganes to buy favor. Sandor desired his brother's gift, a jointed wooden knight, and took it to play with, though he was afraid of what the brutal Gregor might do. When Gregor found him, he shoved the side of Sandor's face into the burning coals of a brazier and held him there while he screamed, and only three grown men could drag Gregor off his brother. The Cleganes' maester attempted to heal the burns with ointments, and Ser Clegane explained the burns to others by claiming that his son's bedding had caught fire. However, there are still rumors that Gregor was involved in Sandor's burning. Ser Clegane's young daughter died mysteriously, and Gregor is rumored to have been involved with that as well. Finally, the knight himself died in a hunting accident, and it is implied he was actually killed by his son Gregor. Sandor left home the day of his father's death to become a sworn sword of House Lannister, and he has never returned.

name: Clegane (founder)
bio: Clegane was the founder of House Clegane and the first Knight of Clegane's Keep. His first name is unknown. Clegane was originally the kennelmaster at Casterly Rock. One autumn year, Lord Tytos_Lannister came between a lioness and her prey. The lioness killed Tytos's horse and was about to attack him as well, when the kennelmaster came to his rescue with his hounds, losing his leg and three dogs in the effort. As a reward, Tytos granted the kennelmaster a knighthood, lands and a towerhouse, and took Clegane_(father_of_Gregor_and_Sandor) as his squire, thereby creating House Clegane. For his sigil, Ser Clegane chose the three dogs he lost saving Tytos, on a yellow field like autumn grass.

name: Clegg
bio: Clegg was a Grand Maester of the Citadel who served on the small council under an unknown Targaryen King. At the end of the First Dornish War, Princess Deria_Martell presented to King Aegon_I_Targaryen a letter from Nymor_Martell. Although the content of the letter was never revealed, many have speculated on its content. Grand Maester Clegg, who came to King’s Landing many years later, concluded that Dorne no longer had the strength to fight. Clegg suggested Prince Nymor might have threatened that, should his peace be refused, he would engage the Faceless Men of Braavos to kill King Aegon’s son and heir, Aenys_I_Targaryen, then but six years old. Clegg's suggestion was never proven.

name: Clement
bio: Clement is a brother of the order on the Quiet Isle. He is forty-eight years old.

name: Clement Celtigar
bio: Clement Celtigar was the Lord of Claw Isle and the head of House Celtigar during the late part of the Dance of the Dragons. He was the son and heir of Lord Bartimos_Celtigar. Clement became the Lord of Claw Isle after the death of his father during the riot in King's Landing in 130 AC. After the ascension of King Aegon_III_Targaryen, Lord Clement rode to King's Landing from Duskendale with Ser Corwyn_Corbray and Lady_Staunton.

name: Clement Crabb
bio: Clement Crabb was a knight from House Crabb, and a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In the great tourney of King's Landing in 98 AC, to celebrate the fiftieth year of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen's reign, Ser Clement and Ser Ryam_Redwyne broke thirty lances against each other, before Jaehaerys proclaimed them co-champions. Their competition is often called the finest display of jousting in the history of Westeros.

name: Clement Piper
bio: Clement Piper is the Lord of Pinkmaiden Castle and the head of House Piper.

name: Cleoden I Durrandon
bio: Cleoden I Durrandon was a Storm King of the stormlands from House Durrandon. He ruled a generation after Durran_XXI_Durrandon. During the coming of the Andals to the stormlands, he made an unlikely alliance with three Dornish kings and won a major victory over Drox_the_Corpse-Maker on the river Slayne near Stonehelm.

name: Cleon
bio: Cleon is a butcher and cook enslaved to Grazdan_mo_Ullhor.

name: Cleon the Second
bio: Cleon the Second was the brief King of Astapor, succeeding the first Cleon.

name: Cleos Frey
bio: Cleos Frey is a knight of House Frey and the eldest son of Emmon_Frey and Genna_Lannister. He is married to Jeyne_Darry and has two young sons, Tywin_Frey and Willem_Frey. His personal coat-of-arms is the twin towers of House Frey and the golden lion of House Lannister, quartered. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Cleos is replaced by the original character Alton Lannister. Cleos was born at Casterly Rock to Genna_Lannister and Ser Emmon_Frey.

name: Cletus Yronwood
bio: Cletus Yronwood is a knight of House Yronwood. He is the son and heir of Lord Anders_Yronwood of Yronwood. When Prince Quentyn_Martell admitted to having kissed one of the Drinkwater twins, Cletus did not mind and suggested that his friend could take one or both of the girls as paramours. Cletus once shot down a heron while hunting with Ser Archibald_Yronwood and Ser Gerris_Drinkwater.

name: Cley Cerwyn
bio: Cley Cerwyn is a member of House Cerwyn. He is the son of Lord Medger_Cerwyn and the heir to Castle Cerwyn. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Cley is portrayed by Tom Varey.

name: Cleyton Caswell
bio: Cleyton Caswell was a knight of House Caswell during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Cleyton was a suitor of Lady Rohanne_Webber, a four-time widow. He attended her at Coldmoat, the seat of House Webber.

name: Clifford Conklyn
bio: Clifford Conklyn was the head of House Conklyn during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Clifford had an old claim on Leafy Lake which was the property of House Webber.

name: Clifford Swann
bio: Clifford Swann is a member of House Swann. In the appendix of A Dance with Dragons he is listed as the Lord of Stonehelm, despite the fact that the earlier established Lord of Stonehelm, Lord Gulian_Swann, had two sons, Donnel_Swann and Balon_Swann, the former of which is still eligible to inherit.

name: Clubfoot Karl
bio: Clubfoot Karl is a ranger or steward of the Night's Watch. Although Clubfoot Karl does not appear in the television adaptation Game of Thrones, the character Karl Tanner, played by Burn Gorman, is based on both Clubfoot Karl and Dirk.

name: Clydas
bio: Clydas is a steward of the Night's Watch. For years Clydas and Chett have helped tend to Maester Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) needs and helped with the ravens in the rookery at Aemon's tower at Castle Black.

name: Coals
bio: Coals is one of the children of the forest in service to the last greenseer. Their true name is too long for the human tongue, according to Leaf.

name: Cohollo
bio: Cohollo is a bloodrider to Khal Drogo. Cohollo saved Drogo from sellswords when he was still young.

name: Coldhands
bio: Coldhands is a mysterious figure from beyond the Wall. "Coldhands" is a name given to him by Samwell_Tarly; his actual name is unknown. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Coldhands is merged with Benjen_Stark and portrayed by Joseph Mawle.[N 1]

name: Colemon
bio: Colemon is a maester in service to House Arryn at the Eyrie. As the maester in service to Jon_Arryn, Lord of the Eyrie, Colemon stayed in King's Landing during Jon's time in the capital as Hand of the King to Robert_I_Baratheon. When Jon became ill with stomach pain, Colemon purged him with wasting potions and pepper juice. Pycelle, the Grand Maester at the Red Keep, sent Colemon away, but Lord Arryn's condition continued to worsen and he soon died. After Jon's death, Colemon returned to the Eyrie with the rest of Lady Lysa_Arryn's household.

name: Colen of Greenpools
bio: Colen of Greenpools is a knight from Greenpools. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Colen is portrayed by Darren Killeen.

name: Colin Florent
bio: Colin Florent is a knight from House Florent and the younger brother to Alester_Florent, Lord of Brightwater Keep. During the wedding of his niece Selyse_Florent and Lord Stannis_Baratheon, Colin's daughter Delena_Florent bedded King Robert_I_Baratheon. The match led to the birth of Edric_Storm, whom Robert acknowledged due to Delena's highbirth. Lord Tywin_Lannister once offered to betroth his son Tyrion_Lannister to Delena, though Colin instead married her to Ser Hosman_Norcross, one of his household knights.

name: Collio Quaynis
bio: Collio Quaynis is a Tyroshi singer.

name: Colloquo Votar
bio: Colloquo Votar was a Volantene adventurer and the author of the Jade Compendium, a collection of stories and legends from the east. At some point in his life, Colloquo traveled east and visited all the lands of the Jade Sea.

name: Colmar Frey
bio: Colmar Frey is the twentieth son of Lord Walder_Frey, the second son born of his marriage to Annara_Farring. However, the rumor at the Twins is that Annara Farring was sleeping with Walder_Frey_(son_of_Ryman), making Colmar's parentage dubious. Colmar is promised to the Faith.

name: Conn
bio: Conn, known as Conn son of Coratt because of Coratt, is a member of the Stone Crows, one of the Vale mountain clans found in the Mountains of the Moon.

name: Conwy
bio: Conwy[N 1] is a recruiter or wandering crow of the Night's Watch. Jeor_Mormont, Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, would be willing to have Yoren or Conwy collect Craster's sons, but the Night's Watch instead allows Craster to give his sons to the wood so that Craster's Keep remains a refuge beyond the Wall.

name: Coratt
bio: Coratt is one of the Stone Crows from the Mountains of the Moon in the Vale of Arryn. He is the father of Conn.

name: Lady Corbray
bio: Lady Corbray is a merchant's daughter who becomes the second wife of Lyonel_Corbray, the Lord of Heart's Home and the head of House Corbray. Her given name is unknown. Lady Corbray is the sixteen-year-old daughter of a rich Gulltown merchant of lowborn birth.

name: Lord Corbray (father of Lyonel)
bio: Lord Corbray was the Lord of Heart's Home and the head of House Corbray during Robert's Rebellion. Lord Corbray fought for Lord Robert Baratheon during the Battle of the Trident. He was wounded during the battle and his eldest son and heir, Lyonel_Corbray, carried him back to the maesters in the rear, while his second son, Ser Lyn_Corbray, grabbed his sword, Lady Forlorn, and led a charge against the Dornishmen led by Prince Lewyn_Martell. Lord Corbray was eventually succeeded by Lyonel, but gave Lady Forlorn to Lyn.

name: Corenna Swann
bio: Corenna Swann was a noblewoman of House Swann during the reign of King Aegon_V_Targaryen. She was the first wife of Ser Stevron_Frey, and had one son by him, Ser Ryman_Frey. Corenna died of a wasting illness.

name: Corliss Penny
bio: Corliss Penny is a knight of House Penny, and a supporter of Stannis_Baratheon.

name: Corlos
bio: Corlos is a figure from the Dawn Age and the founder of House Casterly. His father's name was Caster. According to legend, Corlos was a huntsman who lived in a village near to where Lannisport would eventually stand. When a lion began preying on the village's sheep, Corlos tracked it back to its den, a cave in the base of the Rock. Armed with only a spear, he slew the lion and his mate, but spared the newborn cubs. This act of mercy so pleased the old gods that they sent a sudden ray of sunlight deep into the cave, revealing the gleam of yellow gold in a vein as thick as a man's waist. Maester Yandel believes Corlos soon moved inside the cave and fortified its entrance and as centuries passed, his descendants delved deeper and deeper into the earth to mine the gold but also carving halls, galleries and tunnels, transforming the gigantic Rock into a castle that dwarfed every other seat in Westeros.

name: Corlys Velaryon
bio: Corlys Velaryon, known as the Sea Snake, was a fabled Lord of the Tides, Master of Driftmark, and head of House Velaryon. In his younger years he made nine great voyages on Sea Snake, a ship he designed and built himself. Corlys was the husband of Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon), the Queen Who Never Was. During the Dance of the Dragons, he became Hand to Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Corlys is portrayed by Steve Toussaint. Corlys was born in 53 AC at Driftmark to Corwyn_Velaryon and his lady wife. Corwyn in turn was the eldest son of Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan). He was named after his great-granduncle, Ser Corlys_Velaryon_(son_of_Daemon), who served King Aegon_I_Targaryen as the first Lord Commander of the Kingsguard. Corlys took to the sea at a young age, first crossing the narrow sea to Pentos with an uncle when he was only six. Corlys would make such voyages every year that followed, working on the ships as a member of the crew. According to the captains he served under, they had never seen such a natural sailor as Corlys. He became a captain himself at the age of sixteen, when he sailed the Cod Queen from Driftmark to Dragonstone and back. By the age of twenty-three, Corlys was already a celebrated mariner. Corlys sailed to Oldtown, Lannisport, Lordsport, Lys, Tyrosh, Pentos, and Myr. He sailed the Summer Maid to Volantis and the Summer Isles, and he took Ice Wolf to Braavos, Eastwatch-by-the-Sea, Hardhome, Lorath, and the Port of Ibben. He also took Ice Wolf beyond the Wall, but was unsuccessful in finding a northerly route in the Shivering Sea around Westeros. Corlys was knighted at some point. Corlys sailed aboard his Sea Snake, a ship he designed and built, on nine great voyages to Essos. During his first voyage, he sailed beyond the Jade Gates at Qarth to Yi Ti and Leng. Corlys returned with treasures like silk, spices, and jade, doubling the wealth of House Velaryon. His second voyage took him to Asshai, where he "lost his love and half his crew", according to the stories. At Asshai, Corlys also saw a ship docked in the harbor that he believed to have been the Sun Chaser of Elissa_Farman. Corlys's third voyage led to him becoming the first Westerosi to navigate the Thousand Islands in the Shivering Sea and visit Nefer in N'ghai and Mossovy. During his ninth voyage on Sea Snake, Corlys filled the ship's hold with gold and bought twenty more ships at Qarth, loading them with spices, elephants, and silk. Only fourteen ships reached Driftmark and all the elephants died, but Corlys became vastly wealthy from the venture. He took his nickname "Sea Snake" from his famous ship. Corlys's expeditions on the Sea Snake have been described by Maester Mathis in his book The Nine Voyages.

name: Corlys Velaryon
bio: Corlys Velaryon  was a knight from House Velaryon and a son of Daemon_Velaryon, the Lord of the Tides and Master of Driftmark. He was a younger brother to Aethan_Velaryon. Corlys was the first Lord Commander of the Kingsguard. After a Dornish assassination attempt on the king, Corlys was chosen by Queen Visenya_Targaryen to be the first Lord Commander of the Kingsguard when the order was founded in 10 AC by King Aegon_I_Targaryen, a position in which he served nobly and with honor until the end of his days. Lord Corlys_Velaryon, a famed descendant known as the Sea Snake, was named after Ser Corlys of the Kingsguard, his great-great uncle.

name: Corsair king
bio: A corsair king, a leader of pirates, is reported as being active in the Summer Sea.

name: Cortnay Penrose
bio: Cortnay Penrose is a knight of House Penrose. The son of Lord Penrose, he is named by Renly_Baratheon as castellan of Storm's End. While Lord Renly_Baratheon was performing his duties in King's Landing as master of laws, Ser Cortnay was named the castellan of Storm's End. He took a part in raising Edric_Storm, the acknowledged bastard of Renly's brother, King Robert_I_Baratheon. Cortnay first met Brienne of Tarth when she was a child while visiting Evenfall Hall, and again when Lord Selwyn_Tarth sent her to Storm's End. Lord Mathis_Rowan considers Ser Cortnay to be a seasoned battle commander.

name: Corwin Rogers
bio: Corwin Rogers was a knight from House Rogers during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Corwin was present at the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Corwyn Corbray
bio: Corwyn Corbray was a knight from House Corbray during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons and the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. During the regency, Corwyn became one of Aegon III's regents, whilst his older brother, Lord Leowyn_Corbray, was the Protector of the Realm. Born in 100 AC, Ser Corwyn was the second son of the Lord of Heart's Home, and a younger brother of Leowyn_Corbray. A famed knight, his lord father had gifted him Lady Forlorn, the ancient Valyrian steel sword of House Corbray. Corwyn had two daughters from his first marriage. By the time of the Dance of the Dragons, his wife had passed, however. At the Eyrie, he met Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon), a ward of Lady Jeyne_Arryn, growing close to the Targaryen princess.

name: Corwyn Velaryon
bio: Corwyn Velaryon was the eldest son and heir of Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan), the Lord of the Tides and the head of House Velaryon during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen and King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. When King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen flew their dragons to Driftmark and toured the shipyards, they were escorted by Corwyn and his brothers Jorgen_Velaryon and Victor_Velaryon. As Corwyn predeceased his father, his eldest son, Ser Corlys_Velaryon, succeeded Lord Daemon as the Lord of the Tides and Master of Driftmark.

name: Coryanne Martell
bio: Coryanne Martell was a noblewoman from House Martell during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. She was a younger daughter of Qoren_Martell, Prince of Dorne, and younger sister of Aliandra_Martell, Princess of Dorne. Coryanne and her brother, Prince Qyle_Martell, were angered by the attentions their flirtatious older sister, Princess Aliandra, lavished upon Lord Alyn_Velaryon.

name: Coryanne Wylde
bio: Coryanne Wylde was a noblewoman from House Wylde during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. The events of her life are a matter of dispute between historical records and her alleged biography, a scandalous book called A Caution for Young Girls. Coryanne Wylde was the daughter of Lord Morgan_Wylde of the Rain House. At the age of thirteen, she was deflowered by a "Surly_lad" from the stables. According to A Caution for Young Girls, her lover was a handsome boy her own age, but according to the Rain House maester, he was thirty years old and pox-scarred. Per both accounts, he was gelded and sent to the Wall shortly afterwards. Coryanne was confined to her chambers, where in 48 AC, she gave birth to a bastard son, who was soon sent away to Storm's End to be fostered by a castle steward and his wife. Few beyond the walls of the Rain House knew of her shame. In 50 AC, Coryanne was summoned to King's Landing. Queen Regent Alyssa_Velaryon was sending septas and girls of noble birth to Dragonstone to be a household for her daughter, Princess Alysanne_Targaryen, and Coryanne was chosen as a representative of the stormlands. When the raven came for Coryanne, her mother told her to let everyone believe she was a maiden, and to never speak of her child. However, the truth was that Queen Alyssa and the Hand of the King, Lord Rogar_Baratheon, were attempting to undo the secret marriage of young King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and his sister-wife Alysanne, which at the time was unconsummated, and the ladies' mission was to try and persuade Alysanne to annul the marriage. It is not certain why Coryanne was included, but A Caution for Young Girls states that Lord Rogar or his brother, Ser Borys_Baratheon, placed Coryanne among these ladies with the instruction to bed King Jaehaerys, in order to anger Alysanne into undoing the marriage. If this was the case, the attempt failed. On the fifteenth day of the sixth moon of 50 AC, Coryanne eloped with the married Ser Howard_Bullock, the younger son of the Merrell_Bullock. A fishing boat carried them to Driftmark, where they took ship to Pentos, and then to the Disputed Lands, where Ser Howard joined the Free Company. In 53 AC, he died in Myr from a fall from his horse after a night of drinking, and Coryanne was left alone and penniless. The rest of Coryanne's life has not been recorded in history, but it is believed the tales she told to the sellswords of the Free Company and the magisters of Pentos helped spread gossip across the narrow sea about the marriage of Jaehaerys and Alysanne.

name: Cosgrove
bio: Cosgrove was the steward of House Butterwell during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. During the wedding tourney at Whitewalls, Cosgrove acted as the master of the games. Ser Duncan_the_Tall sent his squire, Aegon_V_Targaryen, to meet with Cosgrove and enter the hedge knight in the lists as a mystery knight called the Gallows Knight. Ser Uthor_Underleaf bribed Cosgrove for favorable matches.
