Cosgrove was reluctant to admit Ser Glendon_Flowers to Lord Ambrose_Butterwell's feast within Whitewalls, insisting that Ser Quentyn_Ball did not have a son and disregarding Glendon's claim of having been knighted by Ser Morgan_Dunstable. Ser Kirby_Pimm spoke with Cosgrove, however, and Glendon was eventually allowed to attend the feast.

name: Cossomo
bio: Cossomo the Conjurer is a patron of the Happy Port.

name: Lord Costayne (Daeron II)
bio: Lord Costayne was the Lord of the Three Towers and the head of House Costayne during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Lord Costayne supported Daemon_I_Blackfyre during his rebellion in 196 AC. He fought on the left side of the Blackfyre host at the Battle of the Redgrass Field. As a young boy, Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen would re-enact the battle with his brother Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) using small painted soldiers and banners. From these childhood games, he recognized the previous Lord Costayne and his lineage as Blackfyre rebels and knew where their forces fought in the battle. By 212 AC, Lord Costayne had been succeeded as head of the house by his Lord_Costayne_(Aerys_I).

name: Lord Costayne (Aerys I)
bio: Lord Costayne was the Lord of the Three Towers and the head of House Costayne during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. He was married to one of Lord Ambrose_Butterwell's daughters. During the First Blackfyre Rebellion, Lord Costayne's Lord_Costayne_(Daeron_II) fought for Daemon_I_Blackfyre in the Battle of the Redgrass Field. In 212 AC, Lord Costayne came with his retinue to Whitewalls to attend the wedding of his father-in-law, Lord Ambrose_Butterwell, and his second wife, the daughter of Lord_Frey. On his way, Lord Costayne had to cross the Gods Eye via a ferry. He quarreled with Lord_Shawney on the matter of which lord should cross first; Shawney argued that as the older of the two he should go first, whereas Costayne believed that as his House was nobler then Shawney's, he had the precedence. During the wedding feast, Lord Costayne was seated on the dais. The next day, he competed in the wedding tourney, but lost to Ser Glendon_Flowers. He sent his squire to ransom the sorrel he lost but Glendon decided to keep the horse. The angry lord told Glendon he had no right to use Quentyn_Ball's coat of arms. When Glendon was falsely accused of stealing a dragon egg, Lord Costayne advocated for Glendon to die. The fate of Lord Costayne following the arrival at Whitewalls of the Hand of the King, Brynden_Rivers, and the suppression of the Second Blackfyre Rebellion is unknown.

name: Cotter Pyke
bio: Cotter Pyke is a member of the Night's Watch and the commander of Eastwatch-by-the-Sea. Cotter is the bastard son of a tavern wench from the Iron Islands. According to Ser Denys_Mallister, Cotter has been violent since his childhood. He is a veteran of many battles.

name: Courtenay Greenhill
bio: Courtenay Greenhill is knight of House Greenhill. He is a household knight in King's Landing.

name: Crackbones
bio: Crackbones is a legendary hero from Crackclaw Point. His given name is unknown. Old Crackbones is remembered for bringing peace to Crackclaw Point before Aegon's Conquest, though the peace did not last long beyond his death. Crackbones allegedly tied the neck of a dragon into a knot.

name: Craghas Drahar
bio: Craghas Drahar, called Craghas Crabfeeder, was a prince admiral from Myr who served the Triarchy. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Craghas is portrayed by Daniel Scott-Smith. The Triarchy—the alliance of Lys, Myr, and Tyrosh—drove Volantis from the Disputed Lands with the Battle of the Borderland in 96 AC. Craghas then led the Triarchy's armies and fleets in conquering the Stepstones from the islands' nests of pirates and outlaws. During this campaign he earned his sobriquet, "Craghas Crabfeeder", by ordering hundreds of pirates staked out on beaches to drown in the rising tide, with their bodies then consumed by crabs. For a time the Westerosi lords were pleased by the pirates' defeat, and were happy to pay the Triarchy's tolls for safe passage. Several years later, however, the avarice of Craghas and his Lysene and Tyroshi co-admirals had driven up the tolls to outrageous levels. Corlys_Velaryon, Lord of the Tides, was most hurt by these tolls, and he partnered with the glory-hungry Prince Daemon_Targaryen to drive the Triarchy's forces from the Stepstones and carve out a realm, attacking in 106 AC. The forces of the Triarchy lost many battles over the next two years during the War for the Stepstones, before Craghas was finally defeated in battle by Daemon, who beheaded him with his Valyrian steel longsword, Dark Sister.

name: Craghorn Hoare
bio: Craghorn Hoare, known as Craghorn of the Red Smile, was a King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. The Hoare kings of his era were opposed by the priests of the Drowned God because of their support for the Faith of the Seven, discouraging of reaving, and promotion of trade.

name: Cragorn
bio: Cragorn is a member of Euron_Greyjoy's crew aboard the Silence.

name: Crake the Boarkiller
bio: Crake the Boarkiller is a legendary figure from the Age of Heroes that founded House Crakehall.

name: Craster
bio: Craster is a wildling and is the master of Craster's Keep, located beyond the Wall. Despite his unsavory reputation, he is regarded as a friend to the Night's Watch. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Craster is portrayed by Robert Pugh. Craster is the bastard son of a man of the Night's Watch and a wildling woman from the village of Whitetree. His father abandoned his mother after Craster was conceived, and when his mother tried to bring him to Castle Black, she was chased off by members of the Watch. His father's name is never mentioned. Craster's Keep is located in the haunted forest, where he lives with his nineteen wives, and he has other daughters too young to wed. Any sons he has are sacrificed to cold gods. Jeor_Mormont, Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, once gifted Craster with a crossbow. Craster cut out the tongue of an envoy sent by Mance_Rayder, King-Beyond-the-Wall. Gilly drank sweet mead when she wed Craster in the summer.

name: Crawn
bio: Crawn, known as Crawn son of Calor because of Calor, is a member of the Moon Brothers, one of the Vale mountain clans.

name: Cregan Karstark
bio: Cregan  Karstark is a member of House Karstark. He is the eldest son of Arnolf_Karstark and the cousin of Rickard_Karstark, Lord of Karhold.

name: Cregan Stark
bio: Cregan Stark, known as the Wolf of the North and later in life the Old Man of the North, was the Lord of Winterfell and head of House Stark during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen, Aegon_III_Targaryen and Daeron_I_Targaryen. He briefly served as Hand of the King for Aegon III after the Dance of the Dragons, a series of days that would be remembered as the Hour of the Wolf. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Cregan is portrayed by Tom Taylor. Cregan was born in 108 AC as the eldest son of Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Benjen), Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North, and Lady Gilliane_Glover. He had a younger brother, who died in 119 AC, and, according to The Testimony of Mushroom, a bastard half-sister, Sara_Snow. When Lord Rickon passed away in 121 AC, he was succeeded by the thirteen-year-old Cregan. Rickon's brother, Bennard_Stark, ruled the north as regent during Cregan's minority. Bennard was slow to relinquish power when Cregan turned sixteen and came of age in 124 AC, however, and relations were tense between uncle and nephew. In 126 AC, Cregan took up rule of the north by having Bennard and his three sons imprisoned. The young lord then married Lady Arra_Norrey, a friend from childhood, but she died in 128 AC while giving birth to a son. Cregan named his infant heir Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan) after his late father.

name: Cregard Stark
bio: Cregard Stark was a member of House Stark during the latter half of the second century after Aegon's Conquest. He was the younger twin brother of Torrhen_Stark_(son_of_Edric), and the youngest son of Edric_Stark_(son_of_Cregan) and Serena_Stark.[N 1]

name: Creighton Longbough
bio: Creighton Longbough is a hedge knight. His coat of arms is a green field with a brown chief. His traveling partner is Ser Illifer the Penniless.

name: Creighton Redfort
bio: Creighton Redfort is a knight of House Redfort and is the son of Lord Horton_Redfort.

name: Cressen
bio: Cressen is a maester of the Citadel in service to Stannis_Baratheon, Lord of Dragonstone. He is the POV character in the prologue of A Clash of Kings. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Cressen is portrayed by Oliver Ford Davies. At some point, Cressen and Walgrave knew each other. Maester Cressen served as the maester of Storm's End. In 278 AC, following the wreck of the Windproud in Shipbreaker Bay, Cressen helped identify the deceased. When the fool Patchface washed ashore three days after the shipwreck, Ser Harbert, the castellan of Storm’s End at the time, suggested that the mad boy be given the gift of mercy with milk of the poppy, but Cressen refused to agree. In the end, Cressen won the argument. Because Lord Steffon_Baratheon and his wife Cassana_Estermont had died in the wreck, Cressen raised the Baratheon children. His favorite was Stannis_Baratheon, who needed Cressen more because he lacked the charm of his brothers. Cressen came to regard Stannis as the son he never had. Cressen was at Storm's End when it was besieged during Robert's Rebellion in 282–263 AC. When Ser Gawen_Wylde and three other knights were caught trying to sneak out a postern gate to surrender, Stannis, who held command, prepared to have them hurled at the Tyrell host via catapult. Cressen convinced him instead to imprison the men, pointing out that the castle might at some point be forced to eat their dead, and the four knights were "good meat". Cressen attended the knighting of Ser Davos_Seaworth by Stannis after the siege. When Stannis was named Lord of Dragonstone by his brother, King Robert_I_Baratheon, Cressen followed him to the ancient stronghold. Circa 289–290 AC, Stannis's infant daughter Shireen_Baratheon contracted greyscale. Though Cressen was able to save her life, the disease almost killed her, and left her disfigured. Cressen feels that her sadness is a mark of his failure. In 297 AC, Cressen fell and broke his hip, and afterwards was no longer able to climb to the rookery. In 298 AC, when Cressen fell ill, the Citadel sent young Maester Pylos to Dragonstone. It was said that he was to be Cressen's assistant, but Cressen was aware that truly meant that Pylos was to be his replacement after he died.

name: Lord Cressey
bio: Lord Cressey was the head of House Cressey during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Lord Cressey was one of the six lords who accompanied Lord Manfryd_Mooton to Braavos in 133 AC. As the Crown's envoy, Manfryd was tasked to make peace with the Sealord of Braavos after the recent conflict between the Free City and Lord Alyn_Velaryon. While Manfryd treated with the Sealord, Lord Cressey spread bribes among the Braavosi keyholders, magisters, and other notables.

name: Crey
bio: Crey was an archmaester of the Citadel during the late reign of Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Archmaester Crey is dismissive of the story told by Coryanne_Wylde in a A Caution for Young Girls that she was ordered by Lord Rogar_Baratheon to try and seduce King Jaehaerys while he was in his minority on Dragonstone. Crey insists that it was a calumny to try and blacken Lord Rogar's name and suspects that Rogar's traitorous brother, Ser Borys_Baratheon was the one who started the lie.

name: Creylen
bio: Creylen is a maester of the Citadel assigned to Casterly Rock.

name: Crispian Celtigar
bio: Crispian Celtigar was a member of House Celtigar during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. He served as the first master of coin. The Celtigars were closely allied with House Targaryen of Dragonstone and House Velaryon of Driftmark, families of Valyrian descent. Crispian was a vassal of the Targaryens and joined his strength to Lord Aegon_I_Targaryen when he sought to conquer the Seven Kingdoms of Westeros. After Aegon was crowned as King of All Westeros, he established the first small council and Crispian was named the first master of coin. In that office, he advised Aegon on matters relating to taxation, debts, and incomes.

name: Criston Cole
bio: Criston Cole was a knight from House Cole who rose to become Lord Commander of the Kingsguard for Viserys_I_Targaryen. His relationship with Viserys's daughter, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, was first friendly and then adversarial. Criston convinced Viserys's son, Prince Aegon_II_Targaryen, to claim the rule of the Seven Kingdoms as his father lay dead. This led to the Dance of the Dragons, a civil war between Aegon II and his elder half-sister, Rhaenyra, whom Viserys had long groomed as his successor. For this the knight became known as Criston the Kingmaker. Criston also served as Hand of the King for Aegon II during part of the war. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Criston is portrayed by Fabien Frankel. Ser Criston was the son of Lord Dondarrion's steward; according to a semi-canon source, Criston was common-born. In 104 AC, Criston won a melee at the tourney at Maidenpool to celebrate the ascension of King Viserys_I_Targaryen to the Iron Throne, drawing the attention of the royal court by knocking Dark Sister from the hands of Prince Daemon_Targaryen with his morningstar. He gave the victor's laurel to the seven-year-old Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, whose favor he wore while unhorsing Daemon in the joust, as well as the twins Ser Arryk_Cargyll and Ser Erryk_Cargyll of the Kingsguard, before being unhorsed by Lord Lymond_Mallister. Thereafter, King Viserys indulged Rhaenyra by naming Criston her personal sworn shield. In 105 AC, the twenty-three-year-old Criston became a member of the Kingsguard, taking the place of the legendary Ser Ryam_Redwyne. Criston wore Rhaenyra's favor in a tournament in 111 AC where he unhorsed Ser Gwayne_Hightower, younger brother and champion to King Viserys's second wife and queen, Alicent_Hightower. Half a year later, Daemon departed the capital after quarreling with his royal brother. According to Mushroom, Daemon had given explicit lessons to Rhaenyra so that she could seduce the noble Criston. When Rhaenyra finally approached the knight, however, the horrified Criston rejected the girl.

name: Criston Wylde
bio: Criston Wylde was a knight of House Wylde during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Ser Criston is listed on the roll of arms as a competitor in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Cromm
bio: Cromm is one of the crew on Asha_Greyjoy's ship, Black Wind.

name: Lord Commander Crowl
bio: Lord Commander Crowl was a member of House Crowl who served as Lord Commander of the Night's Watch over a thousand years ago. His given name has not been revealed.

name: Cuger
bio: Cuger is a recruit of the Night's Watch.

name: Culiper
bio: Culiper was a maester of the Citadel who served House Targaryen at Dragonstone during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Culiper served at Dragonstone since Queen Visenya_Targaryen's day. By 50 AC, he was being assisted by two younger maesters.[N 1] Culiper wrote about King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, during the king's time on Dragonstone during his minority. He later wrote to the Citadel about Androw_Farman, the husband of Queen Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). In 54 AC, when a sickness struck Dragonstone, Culiper ruled out the sweating sickness, the shaking sickness, and greyscale, and informed Rhaena that a number of diseases were suspect. Culiper was the first to die of the illness, two days after he first felt ill. After Culiper's death, all the female close friends of Queen Rhaena died of the same illness. Once the symptoms of the illness were described to the small council of King Jaehaerys by Maester Anselm, Culiper's successor, Lord Rego_Draz recognized it as the symptoms of the tears of Lys, a strong poison. Draz hypothesized that Culiper had been poisoned first because he would have recognized the signs of the poison. Queen Rhaena's husband, Androw Farman, later admits that he had poisoned Culiper and the others, as revenge on Rhaena and her friends, who had belittled him.

name: Cutjack
bio: Cutjack is one of the recruits for the Night’s Watch found by Yoren and is part of the band marching north to the Wall. Cutjack used to be a stonemason.

name: Cynthea Frey
bio: Cynthea Frey is the only daughter of Ser Geremy_Frey and Carolei_Waynwood. She is a ward of Lady Anya_Waynwood.

name: Cyrelle Tarbeck
bio: Cyrelle Tarbeck was a member of House Tarbeck and the younger daughter of Lord Walderan_Tarbeck and Ellyn Tarbeck. According to a semi-canon source, Lady Ellyn_Reyne named her second daughter after Cerelle_Lannister_(daughter_of_Tybolt), as an attack against Gerold_Lannister, Lord of Casterly Rock and the late Cerelle's uncle, who had been suspected of causing his niece's death. During the Reyne-Tarbeck rebellion, Lord Tywin_Lannister ordered the executions of Cyrelle's unnamed husband and her father, Lord Walderan_Tarbeck. Cyrelle and her sister Rohanne_Tarbeck were taken captive during Tywin's assault on Tarbeck Hall. Some stories claim that Tywin ordered their tongues removed before they were forced to join the silent sisters.

name: Cyrenna Swann
bio: Cyrenna Swann was a noblewoman from House Swann and the second wife of Lord Walder_Frey, with whom she had two sons, Ser Jared_Frey and Septon Luceon_Frey. Her relation to Gulian_Swann, Lord of Stonehelm, is unknown.

name: Daario Naharis
bio: Daario Naharis is a Tyroshi captain of the sellsword company, the Stormcrows. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Daario is portrayed by Ed Skrein (Season 3) and Michiel Huisman (Seasons 4 - 6).

name: Dacey Mormont
bio: Dacey Mormont is the heir of House Mormont. She is the daughter of Maege_Mormont, Lady of Bear Island, and the niece of Jeor_Mormont, Lord Commander of the Night's Watch. At an age when most girls would receive dolls, Dacey was given a morningstar.

name: Dacks
bio: Dacks was a squire for a Redfort of Redfort. Dacks was the squire of a Redfort knight, possibly Lord Horton_Redfort, during the time when Robert_I_Baratheon and Eddard_Stark were fostering at the Eyrie with Lord Jon_Arryn. Robert once threw a rotten orange at Dacks, beginning a food fight in the castle's High Hall.

name: Daegon Shepherd
bio: Daegon Shepherd, known as the Black Shepherd, is the captain of the ship Dagger in the Iron Fleet. He is under the command of Victarion_Greyjoy.

name: Daella Targaryen
bio: Princess Daella Targaryen was the eighth born child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. She was married to Lord Rodrik_Arryn, with whom she had one child, Aemma_Arryn. Daella was born in 64 AC. She was a shy and frightened girl, who considered her sister Maegelle_Targaryen as her "guiding star". When she was young, Daella had a kitten whom she loved, but after it scratched her she did not dare to go near cats anymore. Daella was the victim of some of the pranks of her younger sister, Princess Saera_Targaryen. Saera always sneaked cats into Daella's bedchamber, knowing that Daella was afraid of them, and she once filled Daella's chamberpot with bees. King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen assumed that Daella would one day marry her brother, Prince Vaegon_Targaryen, who was only one year older than she was. However, as time passed it became clear that the two children did not like one another. Vaegon tolerated Daella's presence, but no more, and considered her to be stupid. Daella, on the other hand, was frightened of Vaegon, and considered him to be mean. Although Jaehaerys believed that the two children would eventually warm to one another, in truth their dislike only increased. In 73 AC, one of Alysanne's lady companions asked Daella and Vaegon when they would be wed. Vaegon's public reaction was a grave insult to Daella, insisting that she needed to find a husband who wanted stupid children, as those were the only kind she would be able to birth. After Daella fled the hall in tears, followed by her mother, their sister Alyssa emptied a flagon of wine over Vaegon's head. Following this incident, Jaehaerys and Alysanne agreed that they would need to find other spouses for their children. Finding a husband for Daella proved a difficult task for Queen Alysanne. At the age of thirteen, Daella traveled to Driftmark to meet Corlys_Velaryon. However, Daella became seasick when she crossed Blackwater Bay, and after her return home, complained that Corlys had seemed to like his boats better than he liked her. At the age of fourteen, Daella kept company with several promising squires of a similar age: Denys_Swann, Simon_Staunton_(squire), Gerold_Templeton, and Ellard_Crane. By the end of the year, after Simon attempted to get her to drink wine and Crane had kissed her on the lips, Daella hated them all. At the age of fifteen, Daella traveled by wheelhouse with Queen Alysanne to the riverlands where Royce_Blackwood, the son of Lord Blackwood, paid court to her. Daella was smitten with the boy, who would sing ballads he had composed for her, and Alysanne and Lord Blackwood began to discuss plans for the wedding. However, when Daella learned that House Blackwood did not follow the Faith of the Seven but instead worshiped the old gods, and that she would be expected to wed in front of a weirwood, she was horrified. The betrothal subsequently did not happen. On the first day of 80 AC, King Jaehaerys told Queen Alysanne that he wanted Daella wed before the year was over, declaring he no longer cared to whom she was wed. Alysanne, worried that Daella would not accept a husband, inquired what would happen to her if she failed the deadline, as joining the Faith was not an option, since they did not accept a girl that could not read the prayers. In response, Jaehaerys proposed the silent sisters, imploring Alysanne not to let it come to that. Alysanne explained to Daella what Jaehaerys had commanded, and allowed her to choose between three men: Boremund_Baratheon, the Lord of Storm's End, Ser Tymond_Lannister, the heir to Casterly Rock, and Rodrik_Arryn, the Lord of the Eyrie. Daella eventually chose Lord Rodrik, declaring to Alysanne that he seemed “good and wise, like Father". Concerning Rodrik's four children from an earlier marriage, Daella declared "I'm to be their new mother!" According to Archmaester Gyldayn, Queen Alysanne's response to this was not recorded, the account written by Grand Maester Elysar about that day only said "Gods be good".

name: Daella Targaryen
bio: Princess Daella Targaryen was the eldest daughter and fourth child of King Maekar_I_Targaryen and his wife Dyanna_Dayne. Born in 199 AC, Daella was the eldest daughter of Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen and his wife, Lady Dyanna_Dayne. She had three older brothers, Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I), Aerion_Targaryen, and Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I), one younger brother, Aegon_V_Targaryen, and one younger sister, Rhae_Targaryen. Once, in their youth, Daella's sister Rhae_Targaryen gave a love potion to their youngest brother, Aegon_V_Targaryen, so he would marry Rhae, instead of Daella. Daella may have been betrothed to her youngest brother, Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen.[N 1] Like most of their siblings, Daella called Aegon "Egg," the nickname coined by Aemon. Later in life, Daella was married, and had children.

name: Daemion Velaryon
bio: Daemion Velaryon was a member of House Velaryon during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen and Aegon_III_Targaryen. He was a son of Ser Vaemond_Velaryon and his lady wife, and had a brother, Daeron_Velaryon. In 126 AC, after the death of his father at the hands of Prince Daemon_Targaryen, Daeron sailed to King's Landing with his mother, his brother Daeron_Velaryon, and his father's five cousins to seek justice from King Viserys_I_Targaryen and put forward their claims on Driftmark. In 132 AC, following the death of Corlys_Velaryon, Daemion and his brother Daeron contested the claim of Alyn_Velaryon as Lord of the Tides and put forth their own claims before the council of regents in King's Landing. When the Hand of the King, Ser Tyland_Lannister, and the regents ruled against them, they chose to accept the decision and be reconciled with Lord Alyn, who rewarded them with lands on Driftmark on the condition that they contribute ships to his fleet.

name: Daemion Targaryen
bio: Daemion Targaryen was a member of House Targaryen during the Century of Blood, the third son of Lord Aerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon) of Dragonstone, and the father of Aerion_Targaryen_(son_of_Daemion). Daemion became Lord of Dragonstone after his two brothers. He was succeeded by his only son, Aerion_Targaryen_(son_of_Daemion). Lord Daemion kept a maester on Dragonstone.

name: Daemon I Blackfyre
bio: Daemon I Blackfyre, born Daemon Waters, was the bastard son of Princess Daena_Targaryen and King Aegon_IV_Targaryen. Daemon founded House Blackfyre, named after the Targaryen Valyrian steel sword. He fought against his trueborn half-brother, King Daeron_II_Targaryen, for the Iron Throne in the First Blackfyre Rebellion. Daemon was the first and greatest of the five Blackfyre Pretenders. As a bastard of House Targaryen Daemon bore the arms of House Targaryen with the colors reversed: a black three-headed dragon on a red field. Thus he became known as the Black Dragon. Supporters to his claim to the Iron Throne dubbed him the King Who Bore the Sword while those that supported his half-brother Daeron referred to him as Daemon the Pretender. In late 170 AC[N 1] Princess Daena_Targaryen gave birth to Daemon in King's Landing. His mother named him for her grandfather, Prince Daemon_Targaryen, "for the Prince Daemon who had been the wonder and the terror of his age". In later years, some considered this name a warning of what he became. Although Daena refused to name her son's father, some people suspected the involvement of her cousin, Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen, even at the time of Daemon's birth. Daemon was raised in the Red Keep and was taught arms by Ser Quentyn_Ball, the master-at-arms at the Red Keep. In 182 AC or 183 AC, when he was twelve years old, Daemon participated in a squire's tourney, which he won. King Aegon_IV_Targaryen personally knighted Daemon and publicly bestowed the Targaryen Valyrian steel sword Blackfyre on him, finally acknowledging him as his son. There were some who felt that the sword symbolized the monarchy as it had been the sword of King Aegon_I_Targaryen, the Conqueror, and had been passed on from king to king ever since. Aegon's public gift of the sword would eventually start the first talk that Daemon should be king. Following the acquisition of the sword, Daemon took the name "Blackfyre" for himself. King Aegon IV negotiated a betrothal for Daemon to Rohanne_of_Tyrosh. Daemon's supporters would later claim that Daemon had desired to marry his younger half-sister, Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_IV), instead and that King Aegon, who desired the marriage alliance to Tyrosh, had promised Daemon that he could have more than one wife. In 184 AC, while Aegon IV was on his deathbed, he legitimized all of his bastards; those born to noblewomen, including Daemon, became known as the Great Bastards. At this point Daemon may have been given the title Prince since he is referred to as such by Ser Eustace_Osgrey, however Ser Eustace was one of his followers so this may just be his reverence for Daemon showing instead of Daemon actually being granted the title of Prince. Daemon's trueborn half-brother, Daeron_II_Targaryen, ascended the throne following Aegon's death. He refused to allow Daemon more than one wife, paid the dowry his late father had promised to the Archon of Tyrosh, and saw Daemon wed to Rohanne in early 184 AC,[N 2] granting him a tract of land near the Blackwater on his wedding day, with the right to raise a castle. Daemon and Rohanne had a fertile marriage. Their twin sons, Aegon_Blackfyre and Aemon_Blackfyre, were born later that same year. They were followed by five more sons, and at least two daughters. In 187 AC, Daemon participated in Princess Daenerys's wedding tourney. He was defeated in the final tilt by Prince Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II), Daeron II's eldest son. Meanwhile, the growing influence of the Dornish at court, the Dornish appearance of Daeron II's heir, the sudden change of Dornish enemies to Dornish allies, Daeron's rumored illegitimacy (started by King Aegon IV during his reign), and Aegon IV's previous public gift of Blackfyre, the "sword of kings", to Daemon caused several nobles and knights to start mistrusting Daeron II and Baelor, while seeing promise in Daemon. Men began to approach Daemon, but it took them many years to convince Daemon to declare his claim. Maester Yandel considers it likely that it had been Aegor_Rivers, Daemon's younger half-brother, who had managed to convince Daemon in the end. According to King Maekar_I_Targaryen, it had been both Aegor Rivers and Ser Quentyn_Ball, the master-at-arms at the Red Keep, who had played the largest role in convincing Daemon. Aegor pressed him to proclaim his claim for the Iron Throne, and after Daemon agreed to a betrothal between his eldest daughter, Calla_Blackfyre, and Aegor, his efforts increased. Many lords and knights joined in. Besides the influences of his councillors, Daemon had come to resent having the status of bastard and what it represented. Following the Blackfyre defeat in the first rebellion (which included the death of Daemon himself and his eldest two children, Aegon_Blackfyre and Aemon_Blackfyre), Daemon's bastard half-brother, Ser Aegor_Rivers, fled to Tyrosh with Daemon's remaining children and widow. From there, Daemon's descendants continued to plot. Four more rebellions would follow, until 260 AC, when Maelys I Blackfyre, the last male of House Blackfyre, was slain on the Stepstones during the War of the Ninepenny Kings.

name: Daemon II Blackfyre
bio: Daemon II Blackfyre, also known as Daemon the Younger, was the third son of Daemon_I_Blackfyre. He used the alias John the Fiddler, a hedge knight, while traveling incognito through the Seven Kingdoms. He led the brief Second Blackfyre Rebellion. Daemon was born in Westeros, the third son of Daemon_I_Blackfyre and Rohanne_of_Tyrosh. He was named after his father. As a child, he became close friends with Alyn_Cockshaw, and they were bullied by Daemon's older brothers, Aegon_Blackfyre and Aemon_Blackfyre. In 196 AC, when he was seven years old, his father began the First Blackfyre Rebellion. Daemon dreamed that Aegon and Aemon would die, and told them this, but they only laughed at him. After the death of Daemon's father and his older brothers at the Battle of the Redgrass Field, Daemon went into exile in Tyrosh with his surviving family and with his uncle Aegor_Rivers. Bloodraven kept Daemon alive as a hostage to prevent Aegor_Rivers from crowning Daemon's younger brother, Haegon. However, Daemon the Younger died a few years later while a hostage in the Red Keep, and Haegon and Bittersteel launched the Third Blackfyre Rebellion.

name: Daemon III Blackfyre
bio: Daemon III Blackfyre was the eldest son of Haegon I Blackfyre.

name: Daemon Blackfyre
bio: Daemon Blackfyre was a member of House Blackfyre. He was a cousin of Maelys I Blackfyre and a captain-general of the Golden Company. Daemon fought with his cousin, Maelys, over the command of the Golden Company. Maelys killed Daemon's destrier with a single punch, and then twisted Daemon's head until he tore it from his shoulders.

name: Daemon Sand
bio: Daemon Sand, known as the Bastard of Godsgrace, is the bastard son of Ser Ryon_Allyrion, the heir of House Allyrion. The knight is one of Dorne's finest swords. Daemon is not much older than Princess Arianne_Martell. He took her maidenhead when they were both fourteen years old, and their couplings had been both clumsy and ardent. Arianne recalls that it had been sweet. Although her father Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne, eventually learned of this, he did not react to it. Daemon asked Doran for Arianne's hand, but was refused, and relations between Daemon and Arianne were not the same after. Daemon squired for Prince Oberyn_Martell, and received his knighthood from the Red Viper himself. There are rumors he also became Oberyn's lover, though this is seldom said to his face.

name: Daemon Targaryen
bio: Prince Daemon Targaryen was a member of House Targaryen, being a younger son of Prince Baelon Targaryen and a brother of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. Daemon was the second husband of Rhaenyra_Targaryen, Princess of Dragonstone, following Ser Laenor_Velaryon. Daemon was the most experienced warrior of his time and wielded the Valyrian steel blade Dark Sister. A dragonrider whose dragon was Caraxes, Daemon declared himself the King of the Stepstones and the Narrow Sea. During the Dance of the Dragons, he was a prominent supporter of his wife, Rhaenyra. Much of Daemon's life was written about by Archmaester Gyldayn in The Rogue Prince, or, A King's Brother. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Daemon is portrayed by Matt Smith. Daemon was born as the second son of Prince Baelon Targaryen and his sister-wife, Princess Alyssa_Targaryen, in 81 AC, during the reign of his grandfather, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He had one older brother, Viserys_I_Targaryen, as well as one younger brother, Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Baelon), who died in infancy. Within a fortnight of Daemon's birth, Alyssa took the child flying on Meleys. Daemon was free spirited and adventurous in his youth. Daemon was knighted at sixteen, and at some point King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen gave him Dark Sister.[N 1] In 97 AC, Daemon married Lady Rhea_Royce, the heir to Runestone, but he disliked both her and the Vale of Arryn; Daemon referred to his wife as his "bronze bitch". During the Great Council of 101 AC, Daemon supported the claim of Prince Viserys, as it would make Daemon the heir after his brother. Daemon gathered a small army of sworn swords and men-at-arms when it was rumored that Lord Corlys_Velaryon was readying a fleet to defend the rights of his son, Laenor_Velaryon.

name: Daemon Velaryon
bio: Daemon Velaryon was the Lord of the Tides, Master of Driftmark, and head of House Velaryon during Aegon's Conquest. Amongst the oldest supporters and closest friends of House Targaryen, Daemon served as the first master of ships to Aegon_I_Targaryen. Lord Daemon was the father of Ser Corlys_Velaryon_(son_of_Daemon) and Aethan_Velaryon. Aethan's Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan) shared his name with Lord Daemon, his grandfather. A wealthy vassal of House Targaryen, Daemon was among the closest friends of Aegon_I_Targaryen, Lord of Dragonstone, and his sisters, Visenya_Targaryen and Rhaenys_Targaryen. After deciding to invade and conquer Westeros, Aegon summoned his bannermen and friends to Dragonstone for counsel, Daemon among them. After planning his conquest with Daemon and his allies, the Lord of Dragonstone announced his intentions to the Seven Kingdoms, beginning Aegon's Conquest. Daemon's ships carried Aegon's soldiers across Blackwater Bay, landing his army at the mouth of the Blackwater Rush. After Aegon was crowned as King of All Westeros shortly after landing on the mainland, Daemon was counted as one of the oldest supporters of House Targaryen. Daemon was therefore given the honor of becoming Aegon's master of ships and given control of the new royal fleet, the bulk of which was formed from the warships of the Velaryon fleet. After House Arryn refused to bend the knee to House Targaryen, the king sent his admiral to take the city of Gulltown on the coast of the Vale of Arryn. A hastily assembled Arryn fleet, augmented by a dozen Braavosi warships, managed to repel the royal fleet, and Daemon perished during the battle in the waters off Gulltown. While the Valemen sank a third of the royal fleet, Visenya avenged Daemon by burning the Arryn fleet with her dragon, Vhagar.

name: Daemon Velaryon
bio: Daemon Velaryon was the Lord of the Tides, Master of Driftmark, and the head of House Velaryon during the reigns of King Maegor_I_Targaryen and King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He served as master of ships and lord admiral for both kings, as well as Hand of the King for his nephew, King Jaehaerys I. Daemon was the son of Lord Aethan_Velaryon of Driftmark, and was brother to Queen Alyssa_Velaryon, wife of King Aenys_I_Targaryen. He had several sons and daughters. Daemon was the eldest son and heir of Lord Aethan_Velaryon and Lady Alarra_Massey. He had several brothers, and a sister, Alyssa_Velaryon, who wed their cousin Prince Aenys_I_Targaryen in 22 AC, and later became the Queen of the Seven Kingdoms. After King Aenys' death, his brother Maegor_I_Targaryen usurped the throne from Alyssa's eldest son Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I). Fleeing to Driftmark, Alyssa named her son the rightful king, but a visit from Dowager Queen Visenya_Targaryen and her dragon Vhagar convinced Daemon's family to return to court. In 42 AC, Daemon, Alyssa, and their brothers and cousins did homage to Maegor as the true king. In late 58 AC, Lord Daemon attended the tourney at King's Landing commemorating the tenth anniversary of the king's coronation. During the winter of 59 AC, when the Shivers spread to the crownlands, Daemon sickened on Driftmark and was at the point of death, but eventually recovered. However, his second son (possibly Jorgen_Velaryon) and three of his daughters perished. In 73 AC, Ser Lucamore_Strong of the Kingsguard was disgraced for having broken his vows by marrying three women and fathering children with them. The knight's second wife and her children were sent to Driftmark to be fostered with Daemon. Daemon eventually passed away at the age of eighty-eight. As he had outlived his eldest son, Daemon was succeeded as Lord of the Tides by his grandson, Ser Corlys_Velaryon.

name: Daena
bio: Daena is a mummer at the Gate in Braavos.

name: Daena Targaryen
bio: Princess Daena Targaryen, also known as Daena the Defiant, was the eldest daughter of King Aegon_III_Targaryen and Queen Daenaera_Velaryon. She was the sister-wife of King Baelor_I_Targaryen, and was the mother of Daemon_I_Blackfyre, her bastard son by the future King Aegon_IV_Targaryen.

name: Daenaera Velaryon
bio: Daenaera Velaryon was the queen consort and second wife of King Aegon_III_Targaryen, and a member of House Velaryon. Daenaera was the daughter of Daeron_Velaryon, a cousin of Lord Alyn_Velaryon, and his wife, Hazel_Harte. Daenaera's mother, Hazel_Harte, was one of the casualties of the Winter Fever. Her father, Daeron_Velaryon, drowned in the Stepstones during Lord Alyn's attack in the Daughters' War in 133 AC.  Daenaera was left an orphan at the age of six. Lady Baela_Targaryen and her husband, Lord Alyn_Velaryon, took her as their ward afterward. While Aegon and Daenaera were married when he was thirteen and she only six years old, the marriage was not consummated until long after Daenaera had flowered. Aegon was very melancholy, and had come to dislike being touched, even by the hand of his beautiful queen. But eventually Aegon called Daenaera to his bed, and they had five children in their marriage: Daeron_I_Targaryen (born 143 AC), Baelor_I_Targaryen (born 144 AC), Daena_Targaryen (born 145 AC), Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_III) (born 147 AC), and Elaena_Targaryen (born 150 AC). King Aegon III died in 157 AC. It is currently unknown if Daenaera outlived her husband.

name: Daenerys Targaryen
bio: Princess Daenerys Targaryen was the eldest daughter and secondborn child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. Born during the seventh moon of 53 AC in King's Landing, Daenerys was the eldest child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen, her older brother Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I) having died some years before. All of the Seven Kingdoms were delighted by her birth, save for Princess Aerea_Targaryen, the eldest daughter of Jaehaerys's older sister Rhaena, as Aerea had been the king's heir until then. Daenerys began to talk well before her first nameday. By the time she was one and a half years old, she could already walk and run. In 55 AC, Daenerys was crowned the queen of love and beauty by Ser Simon_Dondarrion of Blackhaven. That same year, when Queen Alysanne became pregnant, Daenerys was firm in telling her mother that she wanted a sister. When Alysanne gave birth, the child turned out to be a boy, Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I). Daenerys eventually warmed to Aemon. By the time she was four, her favorite toy had become a broomstick dragon, on which she "flew" everywhere. Daenerys delighted in telling her younger brothers Aemon and Baelon what to do. Queen Alysanne believed that Daenerys, as the eldest of their three living children, should be the king's heir and first in line to the Iron Throne. King Jaehaerys, however, regarded Prince Aemon as his heir since the day he had been born. Though Alysanne would voice her displeasure about this to the king, reminding him that Daenerys was older and should therefore be queen, Jaehaerys would reply telling her that Daenerys would become queen after she married Aemon, and rule the kingdoms by his side. According to Grand Maester Benifer, the king's response did not entirely please Alysanne. Princess Daenerys became one of the many victims of the Shivers in 60 AC. The first night of the new year, she woke her mother at the hour of the owl, complaining of feeling cold. All that could be done was done for her. She was given poultices, hot soups and scalding baths, blankets, furs, hot stones, nettle tea, mother's milk. Septons and septas prayed, and maesters came and went, but her shivering grew worse. Near dawn, King Jaehaerys suddenly insisted that his daughter needed a dragon and had ravens sent to Dragonstone, instructing the Dragonkeepers to immediately bring a hatchling to the Red Keep. It was too late. Daenerys died a day and a half after she had first complained of feeling cold. After her daughter's death, Alysanne collapsed in Jaehaerys's arms, shaking so hard that some worried that she had the Shivers too. Jaehaerys had her put to bed with milk of the poppy, then flew to Dragonstone on Vermithor to inform the Dragonkeepers that there was no need for the hatchling. On his return to the Red Keep, Jaehaerys asked Septon Barth why the gods had taken his daughter, but the wise Barth had no answer. According to Archmaester Gyldayn, though many other children died of the Shivers that winter, the death of Daenerys must have seemed more cruel to Jaehaerys and Alysanne, as it struck at the very heart of the Doctrine of Exceptionalism that Jaehaerys had created to validate his marriage to his sister. The doctrine stated that Targaryens never became ill with common diseases, and indeed it was unknown in history for any to succumb to such.

name: Daenerys Targaryen
bio: Princess Daenerys Targaryen was the daughter of Aegon_IV_Targaryen and Naerys_Targaryen, and the younger sister of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Daenerys_Targaryen, daughter of Aerys_II_Targaryen and Rhaella_Targaryen, was named after her.

name: Daenerys Targaryen
bio: Daenerys Targaryen, also known as Daenerys Stormborn, is one of the last confirmed members of House Targaryen, along with her older brother Viserys_Targaryen, who refers to her as Dany. She is the youngest child of King Aerys_II_Targaryen and his sister-wife, Queen Rhaella_Targaryen. She is one of the major POV characters in A Song of Ice and Fire. In the television adaptations Game of Thrones and House of the Dragon, Daenerys is portrayed respectively by Emilia Clarke and Imogen Ruby Little. She was previously played by Tamzin Merchant in the rejected pilot episode of Game of Thrones. Daenerys is the youngest child of King Aerys_II_Targaryen and his sister-wife, Queen Rhaella_Targaryen. Daenerys was conceived during the last month of Robert's Rebellion, the war which ended House Targaryen's reign over the Seven Kingdoms. Shortly thereafter, her mother was sent with the young prince Viserys_Targaryen to the family ancestral seat of Dragonstone to escape the rebel army which was marching towards King's Landing. Daenerys was born nine months after their flight, while a great summer storm raged above Dragonstone, sinking what remained of the Targaryen fleet; for this reason she is known as "Daenerys Stormborn". Her mother died in labor, but not before naming her. The war had been lost shortly after Rhaella had fled the city, after which Robert_I_Baratheon had claimed the Iron Throne. With Aerys, his eldest son Rhaegar_Targaryen, and Rhaegar's young children Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar) and Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar) dead, Daenerys and her older brother Viserys were the only known living Targaryen heirs. As Viserys had been crowned king on Dragonstone by their mother before her death, Daenerys received the title "Princess of Dragonstone" as Viserys's heir. Robert's brother, Stannis_Baratheon, built a new royal fleet in preparation for an assault on Dragonstone. With the Targaryen fleet destroyed by the storm during which Daenerys had been born, the garrison at Dragonstone were prepared to sell the Targaryen children to Robert. However, before they could act on this plan, Ser Willem_Darry and several other loyal retainers took the children from the nursery and smuggled them into exile, sailing to the Braavosian coast. Viserys and Daenerys lived in Braavos in a house with a red door, where Daenerys had her own room, and a lemon tree underneath her window. Willem was old and sickly, but Daenerys remembers that he always treated her kindly. When Daenerys was five years old, Willem fell sick and slowly wasted away. After his death, the servants he had hired stole all they could. The young Targaryens were put out of the house a while after. As they were forced out, Daenerys wept. Viserys and Daenerys left Braavos, and wandered the Free Cities for years. They visited nearly all the Free Cities, including Myr, Tyrosh, Qohor, Volantis, and Lys, and returned to Braavos at least once. While at first they were welcomed by magisters and archons and merchant princes, with time their interests in the last Targaryens decreased. Daenerys and Viserys had to sell their last few treasures, and eventually their mother's crown. Selling their mother's crown took the last of Viserys's joy, leaving only rage. Daenerys remembers how Viserys once feasted the Golden Company, hoping they would take up his cause, but to no avail. Considering himself to be the rightful ruler, Viserys grew bitter and obsessed with the birthright he had been denied. Daenerys was a frequent target for his frustrations. He had never forgiven her for their mother's death in childbirth, and frequently warned her not to "wake the dragon" by angering him. Once, when Daenerys and Viserys were on their way to Braavos by ship, Daenerys expressed to her brother how fine it would be to be a sailor. Angry, Viserys twisted her hair until she cried. Because Viserys often told her that their line must be "kept pure", Daenerys grew up believing that she would marry him once she came of age. Though Viserys's growing bitterness made their relationship more troubled, Daenerys also has fond memories of her brother from times when he had not yet been so angry. He let her crawl into bed with him and told her stories about the Seven Kingdoms, about their family and their history, their dragons, and Robert's Rebellion. Almost everything Daenerys knows about Westeros she learned from Viserys, making her feel as if she knew what it was like without ever having been there. Viserys is intent on reclaiming their father's throne, and he promised Daenerys a thousand times that he will take her back to Westeros, and that their lives will be much better once he has. Viserys also told her about siege towers and the Faith of the Seven. In 297 AC, Viserys and Daenerys were taken in by Illyrio_Mopatis, a magister of the Free City of Pentos, who offered his help in reclaiming the Iron Throne for Viserys. They have lived with Illyrio for almost half a year.

name: Daenora Targaryen
bio: Daenora Targaryen was the younger daughter of Prince Rhaegel_Targaryen and Lady Alys_Arryn_(wife_of_Rhaegel). She had an older brother and sister, the twins Aelor_Targaryen and Aelora_Targaryen. Daenora was married to her cousin, Prince Aerion_Targaryen. Although Daenora's older sister Aelora_Targaryen was Princess of Dragonstone and heir to the Iron Throne until her death, Daenora was passed over for unknown reasons and her uncle, Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen, was the next heir instead. Daenora married her cousin, Prince Aerion_Targaryen, at an unknown point in time. Together, they had one son, Prince Maegor_Targaryen_(son_of_Aerion), who was born in 232 AC, and whose claim to the throne was disputed and ultimately disregarded by the Great Council of 233 AC.

name: Daeron I Targaryen
bio: Daeron I Targaryen, known as the Young Dragon, was the eldest son of King Aegon_III_Targaryen and Queen Daenaera_Velaryon, and the eighth Targaryen king to sit the Iron Throne. He ascended to the throne at the age of fourteen and is famous for invading Dorne, which he wrote about in his The Conquest of Dorne.

name: Daeron II Targaryen
bio: Daeron II Targaryen, known as Daeron the Good, was the twelfth Targaryen to sit the Iron Throne. Daeron brought Dorne peacefully into the realm, uniting all the lands of Westeros south of the wall. His bastard half-brother, Daemon_I_Blackfyre, challenged his rule and the result was a terrible civil war, the First Blackfyre Rebellion. Daeron died during the Great Spring Sickness in 209 AC. Born on the last day of 153 AC during the final years of the reign of his great-uncle King Aegon_III_Targaryen, Daeron was the eldest son of Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen and Aegon's sister and wife, Naerys_Targaryen. After the failed conquest of Dorne during the short reign of Daeron_I_Targaryen, King Baelor_I_Targaryen ascended the Iron Throne and arranged the marriage of his cousin, Prince Daeron, to Princess Myriah_Martell of Dorne as part of a dual marriage pact between the two kingdoms to make peace. In 170 AC Daeron and Myriah had their first son, whom they named Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II) in Baelor the Blessed's honor. Three other sons would later follow.

name: Daeron Targaryen
bio: Prince Daeron Targaryen, also known as Daeron the Daring, was the third and youngest son of King Viserys_I_Targaryen and his second wife, Queen Alicent_Hightower. He was a younger brother to King Aegon_II_Targaryen. Like his siblings, Daeron was a dragonrider, and he rode the she-dragon Tessarion. Late in 114 AC, Daeron was born to King Viserys I Targaryen and his second wife, Queen Alicent Hightower. He had two elder brothers, Aegon_II_Targaryen and Aemond_Targaryen, and an older sister, Helaena_Targaryen, as well as an older half-sister, Rhaenyra_Targaryen. Daeron was born only shortly after Rhaenyra's eldest son, Prince Jacaerys_Velaryon. However, Rhaenyra did not get along with Daeron's mother, Alicent. Trying to prevent a similar enmity between his youngest son and eldest grandson, Viserys had the two children share a wet nurse until they were weaned, hoping that them being milk brothers would make a difference. The king's hope was in vain, because just as his older siblings, Daeron grew up to be a bitter rival to Jacaerys and his younger siblings, Lucerys_Velaryon and Joffrey_Velaryon. While Daeron and his older siblings were forced to spend time with their cousins (i.e. attending the same feasts, and occasionally training at arms or studying with a maester together), it is said that this only increased the enmity. By the age of six, Daeron had a dragon of his own, Tessarion, though he had yet to ride her. When Daeron was twelve in 126 AC, he was sent to serve as a cupbearer and squire to Lord Ormund_Hightower, his mother Alicent's cousin, in Oldtown.

name: Daeron Targaryen
bio: Prince Daeron Targaryen, sometimes referred to as Daeron the Drunken, was the eldest son of King Maekar_I_Targaryen and his wife Dyanna_Dayne. Daeron was one of the rare Targaryens who had prophetic dragon dreams. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Daeron is portrayed by Henry Ashton. Daeron's father ascended to the throne as Maekar_I_Targaryen in 221 AC after the death of King Aerys_I_Targaryen, who had no other living heirs. This made Daeron the Prince of Dragonstone and heir to the Iron Throne, though he preferred to be called Prince of Summerhall as he considered Dragonstone "such a gloomy abode". Daeron's brother Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) served as his maester. Daeron married Kiera_of_Tyrosh, the widow of his cousin, Prince Valarr_Targaryen. Their daughter, Princess Vaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daeron) was born in 222 AC, and proved to be sweet but simple-minded. Daeron died some time before 233 AC, reputedly from a pox he caught from a whore. His brother Aemon claimed the dreams of dragons that plagued Daeron killed him, as they did their brothers Aerion_Targaryen and Aegon_V_Targaryen. Daeron predeceasing his father was part of what led to the succession crisis after Maekar's death, which was resolved in the Great Council of 233 AC.

name: Daeron Targaryen
bio: Prince Daeron Targaryen was the youngest son and fourthborn child of King Aegon_V_Targaryen and Queen Betha_Blackwood. Prince Daeron was the youngest son of Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen and his wife, Lady Betha_Blackwood. He had three older siblings, Duncan_Targaryen, Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen, and Shaera_Targaryen, and a younger sister, Rhaelle_Targaryen. Daeron's grandfather, King Maekar_I_Targaryen, died in 233 AC, when Daeron was five years old, and the Great Council selected his father to be the new king, Aegon V Targaryen, called "Aegon the Unlikely" as he was the fourth son of a fourth son. When the Fourth Blackfyre Rebellion erupted in 236 AC as the self-styled King Daemon_III_Blackfyre sailed across the Narrow Sea with Ser Aegor_Rivers and the Golden Company, Daeron rode out to Massey's Hook to meet the invaders alongside his father and both his elder brothers. It had long been the custom of House Targaryen to wed brother to sister to keep the blood of the dragon pure, but Aegon V had become convinced that such unions did more harm than good. Instead, he chose to betroth his children to some of the Great Houses of the Seven Kingdoms, in hopes of winning their support for his reign. In 237 AC, Daeron's mother Queen Betha arranged his betrothal to Olenna_Redwyne of the Arbor, when both children were nine years old. However, Daeron's three older siblings broke their betrothals; Duncan fell in love with and married Jenny_of_Oldstones in 239 AC, and Jaehaerys and Shaera married each other in 240 AC. In 246 AC, corrupted by the example of his brothers, the eighteen-year-old Daeron broke his betrothal to Olenna as well. While his brothers had broken their betrothals for other women, this was not the case for Daeron, who remained unwed for the rest of his life. He preferred the companionship of Ser Jeremy_Norridge, a dashing young knight who had been with Daeron since they were squires together at Highgarden. In 251 AC, Prince Daeron led an army against the rebels known as The_Rat,_the_Hawk,_and_the_Pig. Though they managed to quash the rebellion, Daeron was killed in battle, as was Jeremy, who died by his side.

name: Daeron Targaryen
bio: Prince Daeron Targaryen was the secondborn son of King Aerys_II_Targaryen and Queen Rhaella_Targaryen. He lived for only half a year.

name: Daeron Vaith
bio: Daeron Vaith is the Lord of the Red Dunes and the head of House Vaith.

name: Daeron Velaryon
bio: Daeron Velaryon was a member of House Velaryon during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen and Aegon_III_Targaryen. He was a son of Ser Vaemond_Velaryon and his lady wife, and had a brother, Daemion_Velaryon. His grandson, King Daeron_I_Targaryen, was presumably named after him. In 126 AC, after the death of his father at the hands of Prince Daemon_Targaryen, Daeron sailed to King's Landing with his mother, his brother Daemion_Velaryon, and his father's five cousins to seek justice from King Viserys_I_Targaryen and put forward their claims on Driftmark. In 132 AC, following the death of Lord Corlys, Daeron and Daemion contested the claim of their illegitimate cousin Alyn_Velaryon as Lord of the Tides and put forth their own claims before the council of regents in King's Landing. When the Hand of the King, Ser Tyland_Lannister, and the regents ruled against them, they chose to accept the decision and be reconciled with Lord Alyn, who rewarded them with lands on Driftmark on the condition that they contribute ships to his fleet. During the Daughters' War, Daeron joined his cousin Alyn in the attack on the Braavosi fleet in the Stepstones, stationed around Bloodstone. The attack was a complete victory for the Velaryon fleet, but Daeron perished when his ship True Heart went down in battle against the Braavosi.

name: Daeryssa
bio: Princess Daeryssa  is a character in a song. According to the songs, she was once saved by Serwyn_of_the_Mirror_Shield from giants.

name: Dafyn Vance
bio: Dafyn Vance is a knight of House Vance. He is the husband of Maegelle_Frey. They have three children, Marianne_Vance, Walder_Vance and Patrek_Vance. It is unknown if he comes from the Atranta or the Wayfarer's Rest branch of House Vance.

name: Dagmer
bio: Dagmer, known as Cleftjaw and Dagmer Cleftjaw, is the ironborn captain of the longship Foamdrinker and the master-at-arms for House Greyjoy of Pyke. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Dagmer is portrayed by Ralph Ineson. Dagmer is the descendant of a bastard from House Greyjoy from four or five generations ago. When Dagmer was a boy, a longaxe blow nearly killed him, splintering his jaw, smashing his front teeth, and leaving him four lips instead of two. Dagmer once spent a summer reaving in the Stepstones with a young Balon_Greyjoy. He was one of the most skilled warriors of the ironborn when he was younger. Dagmer trained Lord Balon's son, Theon_Greyjoy, in arms, riding, and sailing. Theon gets on well with the old raider, whom he calls "Uncle". Dagmer Cleftjaw is the subject of stories that Old Nan told the Stark children. In her stories, Dagmer could not be killed. According to Old Nan, a foe had cut Dagmer's head in two with an axe, but Dagmer was so fierce that he simply pushed the two halves back together and held them in place until they healed.

name: Dagon Codd
bio: Dagon Codd is an ironborn warrior and a member of House Codd.

name: Dagon Ironmaker
bio: Dagon Ironmaker is a member of House Ironmaker and a grandson of Erik_Ironmaker.

name: Dagon Greyjoy
bio: Dagon Greyjoy, called the Last Reaver, was Lord of the Iron Islands and Lord Reaper of Pyke during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. According to his great-grandson, Victarion_Greyjoy, he last sat in the Seastone Chair "almost a hundred years" before the events of A Song of Ice and Fire. By 211 AC, Lord Dagon and his ironborn were raiding along the Sunset Sea and the western coast of Westeros. They burned Little Dosk, carried off half the wealth of Fair Isle and a hundred women, and raided as far south as the Arbor. The ironmen also also attacked the north, so Lord Beron_Stark gathered swords in an attempt to drive them off. Dagon and his forces retreated to the sea and Pyke whenever his adversaries retaliated on land, so Lord Tybolt_Lannister began building ships for an attack on the Iron Islands. Erik_Ironmaker sailed with Dagon. The Lord of the Iron Islands frustrated the Starks and the Lannisters for some time, but the Last Reaver was eventually defeated by House Targaryen. Lord Dagon and Dagon's Feast, longships of the Iron Fleet, are named after Dagon.

name: Dagon Greyjoy
bio: Dagon Greyjoy, called Dagon the Drunkard, is a cousin of Asha_Greyjoy, even more distantly related to her than her distant cousin Quenton_Greyjoy_(cousin). He is named after the famous Dagon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands.

name: Dagos Manwoody
bio: Dagos Manwoody is the Lord of Kingsgrave and the head of House Manwoody.

name: Dake
bio: Dake was a peasant who served House Osgrey. After being trained by Ser Eustace_Osgrey's sons with other peasants, Dake went off to fight for House Osgrey during the First Blackfyre Rebellion. Eustace considered him to be an expert forager, and Dake was able enough to provide for the whole group as they marched. Dake was one of the only three surviving men that returned with Ser Eustace, along with Pate_(Standfast) and Lem_(Standfast). Years later, Dake was accused of stealing sheep by members of House Webber, whose lands bordered House Osgrey's. Lady Rohanne_Webber sent her knights across the Chequy Water to seize Dake and bring him to Coldmoat for punishment. When Eustace Osgrey arrived at Coldmoat to demand his release, he learned that Dake had been sewn into a sack with rocks, which was sunk in the moat of Coldmoat.

name: Dake
bio: Dake is a guard in service to House Lannister.

name: Dalbridge
bio: Dalbridge, called Squire Dalbridge, is a veteran ranger of the Night's Watch stationed at the Shadow Tower.

name: Dale
bio: Droopeye Dale is an ironborn sailor and a member of the crew on Asha_Greyjoy's ship, Black Wind.

name: Dale Drumm
bio: Dale Drumm, known as Dale the Dread, was a member of House Drumm.

name: Dale Seaworth
bio: Dale Seaworth is the eldest son of Ser Davos_Seaworth and his wife Marya_Seaworth. He is the captain of the Wraith. Dale was married. He and his wife were praying for a child.

name: Dalla
bio: Dalla is a wildling and the wife of Mance_Rayder. She has a sister, Val. Val's fine white clothes were given to her by her sister.

name: Dalla
bio: Dalla is a serving woman at Dragonstone. She is in service to House Baratheon of Dragonstone.

name: Dalton Greyjoy
bio: Dalton Greyjoy, known as the Red Kraken, was the Lord of the Iron Islands, Lord Reaper of Pyke, and the head of House Greyjoy during the late reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen and then the Dance of the Dragons.

name: Damion Lannister
bio: Damion Lannister is a knight of House Lannister, a cousin of the main branch. He is married to Shiera_Crakehall. They have two children, Ser Lucion_Lannister and Lanna_Lannister.

name: Damon
bio: Damon, known as Damon Dance-for-Me, is a man-at-arms sworn to House Bolton. He is one of the Bastard's Boys.

name: Damon Darry
bio: Damon Darry was a knight from House Darry. He may have been a Lord of Darry. During the regency of Aegon III, Ser Tyland_Lannister, the Hand of the King, sent fifty men with Ser Regis_Groves of the Kingsguard to suppress the rabble of Alys_Rivers at Harrenhal. Regis was joined at Castle Darry with another fifty men led by Ser Damon. Alys refused to surrender Harrenhal, however, and Regis was killed in the fighting which ensued. Only thirty-two of the king's men reached the safety of Darry. Alys sent another survivor as a messenger to Castle Darry, claiming he was cursed and doomed to die if anyone laughed at his message. Although Damon reassured the messenger, one of Lord Darry's men did laugh at the messenger's talk of dragons and the envoy is said to have abruptly choked to death. Lord Unwin_Peake, one of the regents, discounted Damon's talk of sorcery.

name: Damon Hightower
bio: Damon Hightower, known as Damon the Devout, was a head of House Hightower and Lord of the Hightower. Damon was a grandson of Lord Dorian_Hightower, the first Hightower to wed an Andal princess. Damon was the first Hightower to convert to the Faith of the Seven, building the first sept in Oldtown and six elsewhere in the Kingdom of the Reach. When Damon prematurely died of a bad belly, Septon Robeson became regent for Damon's newborn son, Triston_Hightower.

name: Damon Lannister
bio: Damon Lannister, better known as the Grey Lion, was the Lord of Casterly Rock and the head of House Lannister during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He wed Cerissa_Brax and was the father of Tybolt_Lannister and Gerold_Lannister. Damon, who was not lord at the time, was unhorsed by Ser Arlan_of_Pennytree in a tourney at Lannisport. He was also once unhorsed in a tourney by Ser Tommard_Heddle. During the First Blackfyre Rebellion, Lord Damon was defeated by Ser Quentyn_Ball. Lord Damon participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC, beginning the tourney as a challenger. In his first tilt, he challenged Lord Leo_Tyrell_(Longthorn). He yielded after Leo ripped his helm off with a well placed lance. Lord Damon died of the Great Spring Sickness in 210 AC and his eldest son Tybolt_Lannister succeeded him as Lord of Casterly Rock.

name: Damon Lannister
bio: Damon Lannister is a nobleman of House Lannister and the only son of Ser Jason_Lannister_(son_of_Gerold) by his first wife, Alys_Stackspear. He is a cousin and half-brother-in-law to Lord Tywin_Lannister of Casterly Rock. In 244 AC, the young Jason_Lannister_(son_of_Gerold) deflowered and impregnated Alys_Stackspear, the maiden daughter of Lord_Stackspear_(father_of_Alys). Jason was compelled by his brother Lord Tytos_Lannister to wed Alys, likely at the behest of Tytos' wife, Lady Jeyne_Marbrand. However, Alys died giving birth to Damon. It is currently unknown whether Damon is still alive in A Song of Ice and Fire. The fact that Cersei_Lannister appointed his son Damion as castellan instead of him might indicate Damon has passed but it is not a definitive argument.

name: Damon Marbrand
bio: Damon Marbrand is the Lord of Ashemark and the head of House Marbrand in the westerlands. He is the father of Ser Addam_Marbrand.

name: Damon Morrigen
bio: Damon Morrigen, called Damon the Devout, was a pious knight from House Morrigen who was a devout member of the Warrior's Sons. He became Grand Captain of the Warrior's Sons, and led the chapter in King's Landing based at the Sept of Remembrance early in the Faith Militant uprising. After King Maegor_I_Targaryen was crowned and flew on Balerion to Visenya's Hill to raise his standard, his mother Dowager Queen Visenya_Targaryen challenged those who questioned her son's right to rule to prove themselves, a challenge that the Warrior's Sons accepted. Ser Damon proposed a trial of seven, as had been done in Andalos. A trial of seven took place with Damon and six of his Warrior's Sons against King Maegor and his six champions. Damon and his comrade Ser Willam_the_Wanderer were the last two of the Warrior's Sons standing against Maegor, whose champions had all been killed. Badly wounded, Damon fell to the king and Blackfyre.

name: Damon Paege
bio: Damon Paege is a knight of House Paege. His squire is Hoster_Frey, a kinsman and son of Sylwa_Paege.

name: Damon Shett
bio: Damon Shett is the Knight of Gull Tower and the head of House Shett of Gull Tower.

name: Damon Teague
bio: Damon Teague was the last monarch of House Teague. He was the younger brother and champion of King Humfrey_I_Teague, who instigated a massive uprising of Riverlords by attempting to suppress worship of the old gods. In the campaign's final battle, Humfrey I perished, passing the crown to his heir Humfrey_II_Teague, only for the latter to be killed. The bloody crown then passed to Damon's second nephew, Hollis_Teague, who died shortly after, thus making Tyler_Teague, the youngest of Humfrey I's sons, the new king. However, Tyler was also killed. And so, the crown passed to Damon, who also perished in battle. Thus by the end of the day, House Teague was extinct.

name: Damon Vypren
bio: Damon Vypren is a knight from House Vypren, the son and heir of Lord Lucias_Vypren.

name: Dan
bio: Hempen Dan is a free folk raider.

name: Dancy
bio: Dancy is a whore at Chataya's brothel in King's Landing.

name: Danelle Lothston
bio: Danelle Lothston, known as Mad Danelle, was the Lady of Harrenhal and the head of House Lothston, during the reigns of kings Aerys_I_Targaryen and Maekar_I_Targaryen. When Lord Brynden_Rivers, the Hand of the King to Aerys_I_Targaryen, marched on Whitewalls to quash the Second Blackfyre Rebellion, Lady Danelle was among the riverlords who came in support, marching in great strength. She led her men herself, wearing black armor. Lady Danelle turned to the black arts during the reign of Maekar_I_Targaryen, causing madness and chaos and the downfall of the Lothston line.

name: Dannel
bio: Dannel is a steward of the Night's Watch.

name: Danny Flint
bio: Danny Flint was a girl from House Flint who dressed up as a boy to become a member of the Night's Watch. Danny was raped and murdered at the Nightfort. She is remembered for being brave and young. "Brave Danny Flint" is a sad and pretty song written about her. In some versions of the song her ghost still walks the Nightfort.

name: Danos Slynt
bio: Danos Slynt is the third son of Janos_Slynt.

name: Danwell Frey
bio: Danwell Frey is a knight of House Frey, the eighth son of Lord Walder_Frey, the third son born of Walder's marriage to Amarei_Crakehall. He is married to Wynafrei_Whent, with many stillbirths and miscarriages. Ser Danwell rode in the tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day held in King's Landing in 298 AC. He was unhorsed by a hedge knight, which embarrassed his father, Lord Walder_Frey.

name: Dareon
bio: Dareon is a recruit of the Night's Watch, known primarily for his excellent singing voice. Ser Alliser_Thorne refers to him as Lover. While an apprentice singer in the Reach, Dareon was caught in bed with Mathis_Rowan's_daughter of Lord Mathis_Rowan of Goldengrove. Though Dareon maintains that the girl was waiting for him naked and helped him into her room, under her father's eye she named it rape. Dareon was sent to the Wall, though he maintains his innocence.

name: Darke (Seven Swords)
bio: Darke is a servant at the Seven Swords inn in Duskendale.

name: Lord Darklyn (father of Jonquil Darke)
bio: Lord Darklyn was the Lord of Duskendale and the head of House Darklyn during the reigns of King Maegor_I_Targaryen and King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 48 AC, Prince Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen pressed his claim to the throne against his usurping uncle, King Maegor_I_Targaryen, and high lords of the realm flocked to his cause. Maegor commanded a score of lesser lords, including Lord Darklyn, to gather at King's Landing, but their numbers were few and many soon lost heart. Shortly afterwards, Maegor was found dead upon the Iron Throne, and the prince ascended as King Jaehaerys I Targaryen. In the chaos that followed Maegor's death, many lords fled the capital, but Lord Darklyn remained. Along with Lord Jordan_Towers and Lord_Staunton_(father_of_Cassella), he yielded the Red Keep to Prince Jaehaerys, Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), and Princess Alysanne_Targaryen. The three lords were sent to the black cells, but were eventually pardoned by King Jaehaerys I after surrendering some of their land and sending a son to court to serve as a hostage. In 50 AC, Lord Darklyn was among the lords who visited Jaehaerys at Dragonstone. This displeased Jaehaerys' Hand, Lord Rogar_Baratheon, who referred to them as the "lords lickspittle". Lord Darklyn died that same year or the one after, and he was succeeded as Lord of Duskendale by his son. It is unknown if it was the father or the son who hosted King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen at Duskendale during their first royal progress in 51 AC. Lord Darklyn's bastard daughter, Jonquil_Darke, was named the queen's sworn shield after an assassination attempt on Alysanne failed when the royal couple visited Jonquil's Pool at Maidenpool.

name: Darla Deddings
bio: Lady Darla Deddings was a member of House Deddings during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. During the riots of King's landing, rioters attempted to rape Lady Darla. Her brother Davos_Deddings was stabbed in the eye defending her.

name: Darlessa Marbrand
bio: Darlessa Marbrand is the widow of Ser Tygett_Lannister. She had one son with him, Tyrek_Lannister.

name: Darnold Darry
bio: Darnold Darry[N 1] was a Lord of Darry and head of House Darry during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. After King Maegor I exterminated House Harroway in 44 AC, he divided up the Harroway holdings among his remaining supporters. Harrenhal was granted to Lord Walton_Towers, Lord Harroway's Town was given to Lord Alton_Butterwell, and the rest of the Harroway holdings were given to Lord Darnold. Lord Darry, possibly Darnold, attended the Golden Wedding in 49 AC and approved of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen.

name: Darnold Arryn
bio: Darnold Arryn was the Lord of the Eyrie, Defender of the Vale, Warden of the East, and the head of House Arryn at the beginning of the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Lord Arryn was present at the wedding of Lord Rogar_Baratheon and Dowager Queen Alyssa_Velaryon in 49 AC, but it is unknown if this was Darnold. In 54 AC, Lord Darnold and his brother, Ser Rymond_Arryn, were killed at the hands of mountain clan raiders they had unwisely pursued into the Mountains of the Moon. He was succeeded as Lord of the Eyrie by his  nephew, the ten-year-old Rodrik_Arryn.

name: Lord Darry (Dance)
bio: Lord Darry was the Lord of Darry and the head of House Darry during the reign of King Aegon_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Lord Darry is portrayed by John-Paul Hurley. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Darry declared for Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen and supported the blacks. Lord Darry was part of the host led by Rhaenyra's consort, Prince Daemon_Targaryen, who took Stone Hedge. Later on, Lord Darry alongside Lord Roote and Ser Oswald_Wode lost a short battle against the vanguard of Ser Criston_Cole's host on the lakeshore of the God's Eye. This was meant as a feint to lure them closer to Harrenhal and away from King's Landing which gave Daemon the opportunity to take the capital for the blacks. After hearing the news, Prince Aemond_Targaryen, who had escorted Cole's men on his dragon Vhagar, became enraged and retaliated against the riverlords by burning their lands and castles. Aemond first hit Castle Darry where Lord Darry, his son and heir, and forty Darry men died burning on the castle's battlements. Fortunately, Lady Darry and their younger children survived the attack by hiding in the castle's vaults. Derrick_Darry succeeded him as Lord of Darry.

name: Lord Darry (father of Raymun)
bio: Lord Darry is the Lord of Darry and head of House Darry.[N 1] He is the father of Ser Raymun_Darry, Mariya_Darry and Jeyne_Darry. His given name has not been mentioned yet. In Lord Darry's days, House Darry stood high in King Aerys_II_Targaryen's favor. At the start of Robert's Rebellion, the Darrys sided with House Targaryen instead of their liege lord, House Tully. Three of Lord Darry's sons died in the Battle of the Trident. With the fall of the Targaryens, the Darrys lost half their lands, most of their wealth, and almost all their power. In 298 AC, Castle Darry is run by Ser Raymun_Darry. It is possible that Lord Darry was still alive, having retired from active rule in the years after the rebellion, with Raymun being the de facto lord.

name: Daryn Hornwood
bio: Daryn Hornwood is a member of House Hornwood and the heir to the Hornwood. He is the son of Lord Halys_Hornwood and Lady Donella_Hornwood. He has a bastard half-brother, Larence_Snow.

name: Daughter of the captain of the Myraham
bio: The captain's daughter is Theon_Greyjoy's bedmate during his journey back to the Iron Islands. Her father is the Captain_of_the_Myraham of the Myraham out of Oldtown.  Her name is never mentioned. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, she is portrayed by Amy Dawson.

name: Daughter of the Dusk
bio: The Daughter of the Dusk is a famous courtesan in Braavos. Her given name is unknown.

name: Daven Lannister
bio: Daven Lannister is a knight from House Lannister. He is the son of Ser Stafford_Lannister, a cousin and brother-in-law of Lord Tywin_Lannister of Casterly Rock. Ser Stafford_Lannister had been negotiating to betroth his son Daven to Desmera_Redwyne, although an agreement had not been reached with Lord Paxter_Redwyne.

name: Davos
bio: Davos the Dragonslayer is a legendary figure of the Reach who lived during the Age of Heroes. Tales describe him as a knight even though knights came to Westeros thousands of years later with the Andal invasion.

name: Davos Baratheon
bio: Davos Baratheon was a son of Lord Orys_Baratheon and Lady Argella_Durrandon. Davos fought beside his father during the Vulture Hunt in 37 AC. During the battle at Stonehelm, Orys's forces crushed a part of the Vulture_King_(Aenys_I) host. Davos was present when Orys died on the march home to Storm's End, from wounds he had taken in the battle. According to the accounts of Davos, his father had died content, having avenged the loss of his hand by cutting off the hands and feet of Lord Walter_Wyl after the battle. Later that year, a grateful King Aenys_I_Targaryen rewarded Davos with gold, offices, and honors together with several lords. Because of that, it is possible that Davos was the son of Orys who succeeded him as Lord of Storm's End and Lord Paramount of the Stormlands.

name: Davos Darklyn
bio: Davos Darklyn was a knight of the Kingsguard, one of the seven knights from House Darklyn to serve that order. Ser Davos was a knight of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. In 43 AC, Davos marshalled five thousand swords in King's Landing for his king and struck out west to meet the rebels under the command of Maegor's nephew, Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I). As Prince Aegon marched towards King's Landing from the Gods Eye, he found Davos' Kingslanders blocking his path, sitting on high ground behind a wall of spearmen. Hoping to break through Davos' host before other royalist armies fell upon his flanks, Prince Aegon commanded a charge. In the resulting Battle Beneath the Gods Eye, Prince Aegon was killed by Maegor in an aerial dragon battle, while Davos was slain by Lord Qarl_Corbray of Heart's Home with the Valyrian steel longsword Lady Forlorn, the only notable casualty among the loyalists. The following year, Davos' aged father was slain by the Warrior's Sons who remained loyal to Ser Joffrey_Doggett after the order was outlawed by Maegor.

name: Davos Dayne
bio: Davos Dayne was a knight from House Dayne and the Sword of the Morning. During Nymeria's War, Vorian_Dayne, the last King of the Torrentine, was defeated by Princess Nymeria and Lord Mors_Martell. Afterwards, the Daynes became vassals of House Martell, and supported Nymeria and her husband against King Yorick_V_Yronwood. After the death of her second husband, the aged Lord_Uller_(husband_of_Nymeria), Ser Davos married Princess Nymeria and became the Prince Consort of Dorne, and the couple had one son together. Nymeria remained the unquestioned ruler of Dorne, with Davos serving only as a counselor and consort. After Nymeria's death, she was succeeded by her eldest daughter by Lord Martell rather than by her son with Ser Davos, for the Dornish had come to adopt the Rhoynish custom of equal primogeniture.

name: Davos Deddings
