bio: Davos Deddings was a member of House Deddings during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. During the riots of King's landing, three rioters attempted to rape Davos's sister, Lady Darla_Deddings. Davos was stabbed in the eye when he tried to stop them.

name: Davos Seaworth
bio: Davos Seaworth, commonly called the Onion Knight, and sometimes Davos Shorthand, is the head of House Seaworth. He was once a smuggler, but after Robert's Rebellion he became a landed knight and Stannis_Baratheon's most honest and loyal supporter. Davos created his own house sigil, a black ship with an onion on the sails upon a grey field. Davos becomes a POV character starting with A Clash of Kings. Davos captains Black Betha. He has seven sons, many of them serving on ships in Stannis's fleet. His wife Marya_Seaworth tends his lands on Cape Wrath. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Davos is portrayed by Liam Cunningham. Davos is a man of low birth, born in the wretched Flea Bottom ward of King's Landing to a life of hardship. In his youth, he found service on the Cobblecat under Roro_Uhoris, a Tyroshi smuggler and pirate. Eventually, Uhoris was caught by the Night's Watch fleet from Eastwatch, where he was executed for trading weapons to wildlings. While impressed by this event, Davos did not refuse to trade with the members of the Night's Watch later. After his time aboard the Cobblecat, Davos made his own way and secured his own small ship, becoming one of the most infamous smugglers of the Seven Kingdoms. He piloted his black-sailed ship into harbors in the dead of night, and navigated treacherous shallows. As a smuggler, he visited the Three Sisters. Davos is also known to have visited White Harbor often, most recently in 294 AC.[N 1] The smuggler has spent time in gaols and dungeons. Davos married a woman named Marya_Seaworth; together they had seven sons: Dale_Seaworth, Allard_Seaworth, Matthos_Seaworth, Maric_Seaworth, Devan_Seaworth, Stannis_Seaworth and Steffon_Seaworth. During Robert's Rebellion, almost a year into the siege of Storm's End, Davos evaded the Redwyne fleet's blockade of Shipbreaker Bay to smuggle onions and salted fish into the castle, knowing the starving people would pay him the best price. The food allowed Stannis_Baratheon's men to hold on until Lord Eddard_Stark arrived to break the siege. As a reward for his service, Stannis knighted Davos, giving him choice lands on Cape Wrath and allowing him to choose Seaworth as the name of his new house. However, as payment for his past crimes, Davos lost the first joint from each finger of his left hand. Davos found the ruling just, and agreed to the terms if Stannis would swing the blade himself. Davos keeps the bones of his joints in a pouch around his neck because he believes they are lucky, having bought a future for his family. Davos now captains the Black Betha, a war galley, in service of Stannis, who was named Lord of Dragonstone by his brother, King Robert_I_Baratheon. Davos was in King's Landing with Stannis for the tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day in 298 AC.

name: Lord Dayne (father of Edric)
bio: Lord Dayne was the Lord of Starfall and the head of House Dayne during the early reign of King Robert_I_Baratheon.[N 1] At some point during Robert_I_Baratheon's early reign,[N 2] Lord Dayne died and was succeeded by his young son, Edric_Dayne.

name: Deana Hardyng
bio: Deana Hardyng is a noblewoman of House Hardyng and the wife of Walton_Frey. They have three children: Steffon_Frey, Walda_Frey_(daughter_of_Walton), and Bryan_Frey.

name: Lady Deddings
bio: Lady Deddings is the wife of Lord_Deddings.

name: Lord Deddings
bio: Lord Deddings is the head of House Deddings in the riverlands.

name: Del
bio: Del is a young free folk raider.

name: Delena Florent
bio: Delena Florent is a noblewoman of House Florent, niece to Lord Alester_Florent. She is the mother of Edric_Storm, one of the bastards of King Robert Baratheon. She is married to Ser Hosman_Norcross, and has two children by him, Alester_Norcross and Renly_Norcross. Delena was a bedmaid for her cousin, Selyse_Florent, and attended her wedding to Lord Stannis_Baratheon. During the wedding feast, while the bride and groom were still dancing, Stannis's brother, King Robert_I_Baratheon, carried Delena upstairs to the bedroom of the newlyweds and broke in the wedding bed. Robert later told Stannis he had not recognized the bedchamber because he was drunk, and had not meant to cause shame. However, Stannis felt his brother's actions were an insult to House Florent. The coupling eventually led to the birth of Edric_Storm. Because Delena had been a maiden of noble birth when Robert slept with her, he had to acknowledge the child as his bastard.  Stannis had the infant sent to Storm's End to be fostered by his younger brother, Lord Renly_Baratheon. Lord Tywin_Lannister once offered to betroth his son Tyrion_Lannister to Delena, but her father, Ser Colin_Florent, refused, and instead married her to one of his household knights, Ser Hosman_Norcross.

name: Della Frey
bio: Della Frey, known as Deaf Della among her kin, is a member of House Frey. She is the eldest child of Ser Benfrey_Frey and his wife and cousin, Jyanna_Frey.

name: Dellono
bio: Dellono is a dyer  from Braavos.

name: Delonne Allyrion
bio: Delonne Allyrion is the Lady of Godsgrace and the head of House Allyrion. She is the mother of Ser Ryon_Allyrion.

name: Delp
bio: Delp is on duty at the River Gate when Tyrion_Lannister's plot to break Ser Jaime_Lannister free is carried out. Although the attempted escape fails, Delp is wounded while recapturing Jaime. His wounds are so severe that Maester Vyman fears he will die. Shortly after Delp's wound has healed and he returns to duty, he is killed during Lord Rickard_Karstark's murder of Willem_Lannister and Tion_Frey.

name: Demon of Darry
bio: The  Demon of Darry was a famous knight who eventually became Lord Commander of the Kingsguard at an unknown time, likely between the reigns of King Aegon_III_Targaryen and King Aegon_V_Targaryen.[Note 1] His given name has not been published, but he presumably came from Darry.

name: Denestan
bio: Maester Denestan is the author of the book Questions. In his book, Denestan argues that it has been two thousand years since the last kingsmoot, and Alyssa_Arryn's lifetime. He also speculates that the runic armor of House Royce is far less ancient than it appears to be.

name: Dennet
bio: Dennet (or Dennett) is a member of the brotherhood without banners. He is a longbowman.

name: Dennis Plumm
bio: Dennis Plumm is a knight from House Plumm and is the eldest son and heir of Lord Philip_Plumm. He has two younger brothers, Peter_Plumm and Harwyn_Plumm.

name: Dennis the Lame
bio: Dennis, known as Dennis the Lame, was a leader of the Poor Fellows during the Faith Militant uprising against King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Following the Faith Militant's defeats in the battles at Stonebridge and the Great Fork of the Blackwater the previous year, new leaders arose across the Seven Kingdoms in 43 AC, one of which was Dennis the Lame. He and his followers were hardly distinguishable from outlaws, ambushing royal forces then disappearing into the greenwood. Smallfolk gave them aid wherever they went, while the knights and men-at-arms who were sent out to bring them to justice often disappeared. The exact region that Dennis the Lame and his followers were active in hasn't been stated. Dennis was still alive at the time of Maegor's death, but his ultimate fate has not been revealed.

name: Dennis Whitfield
bio: Dennis Whitfield was a knight of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. During the regency of Aegon III, an attempted coup against the Rogare family and the Hand of the King, Lord Thaddeus_Rowan, resulted in the deaths of Lord Commander Marston_Waters of the Kingsguard and two of his sworn brothers, Ser Mervyn_Flowers and Ser Amaury_Peake. Ser Dennis, Ser Agramore_Cobb and Ser Edmund_Warrick were given the white cloaks of the Kingsguard to replace the deceased.

name: Denyo Terys
bio: Denyo Terys is the youngest son of Ternesio_Terys, captain of the Titan's Daughter. Denyo works aboard ship as the ship's boy.

name: Denys
bio: Silver Denys was a man living on Dragonstone at the outset of the Dance of the Dragons. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Denys is portrayed by Robert Rhodes. When Prince Jacaerys_Velaryon called for dragonriders, Denys had his arm ripped off in his attempt to mount Sheepstealer. While his sons were trying to staunch the wound, the Cannibal descended on them and devoured Denys along with his sons.

name: Denys Arryn
bio: Denys Arryn, known as the Darling of the Vale, was a knight of House Arryn. A distant cousin to the ruling line, he married Lord Jon_Arryn's niece. Denys married a distant cousin who descended from the main Arryn branch. Since Denys had "Arryn" as a surname and had married the eldest surviving daughter of Alys_Arryn he was considered the next heir of the house after Ser Elbert_Arryn. He was named Keeper of the Gates of the Moon by Lord Jon_Arryn, replacing Jon's brother, Ser Ronnel_Arryn_(son_of_Jasper). When Elbert was murdered by King Aerys_II_Targaryen Denys was made heir. During the Battle of the Bells, a major battle in Robert's Rebellion, Ser Denys fought Lord Jon_Connington and was slain by him. His wife and son both died shortly afterwards.

name: Denys Darklyn
bio: Denys Darklyn was the Lord of Duskendale and the head of House Darklyn during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. He and his family were executed after the Defiance of Duskendale. Lord Denys asked King Aerys_II_Targaryen for certain rights for his citizens and a new town charter for Duskendale. The king refused and Denys refused to pay taxes to the crown. This led to an escalation of events that saw the capture of Aerys and the death of Ser Gwayne_Gaunt of the Kingsguard by Ser Symon_Hollard during the taking. Denys continued his defiance, even as the might of the Seven Kingdoms, led by the King's Hand Lord Tywin_Lannister, sat outside his walls. Tywin's forces were strong enough to storm Duskendale, but Denys threatened to kill the king if that happened. The Defiance ended when Ser Barristan_Selmy sneaked into the Dun Fort, the seat of House Darklyn, and rescued his king. Denys, no longer having a hostage, immediately opened his gates and begged for mercy. He was beheaded, as was the rest of his family, while his wife, Serala of Myr, was burned. Denys was still young at the time of his death. Not one lordly Darklyn remained alive.

name: Denys Drumm
bio: Denys Drumm is a member of House Drumm, a son of Lord Dunstan_Drumm of Old Wyk.

name: Denys Harte
bio: Denys Harte was a knight from House Harte during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Denys was part of Lord Manfryd_Mooton's diplomatic mission to Braavos in 133 AC. According to Mushroom, Denys hired the Faceless Men to assassinate a rival in King's Landing.

name: Denys Mallister
bio: Denys Mallister is a knight of House Mallister and a member of the Night's Watch. He has served as commander of the Shadow Tower since 267 AC. Denys is an uncle of Jason_Mallister, Lord of Seagard. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Denys is portrayed by J.J. Murphy. Denys was born at Seagard to a lord of House Mallister. He was a squire at twelve, a knight at eighteen, and a champion at twenty-two years old. He once unhorsed both of Samwell_Tarly's grandfathers (Alester_Florent and the father of Randyll_Tarly) at the same tourney. When Denys joined the Night's Watch, he was assigned to the order of rangers. He has been the commander of the Shadow Tower since 267 AC. The knight has considered becoming Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, but in the last two elections he yielded to first Lord_Commander_Qorgyle and then Jeor_Mormont. Mance_Rayder, one of the rangers at the Shadow Tower, was once wounded by a shadowcat during an expedition north of the Wall. A wildling woman nursed him back to health and repaired tears in his cloak with scarlet silk. When Mance returned to the Shadow Tower, Denys ordered him to burn the cloak and wear only the black cloaks of the Night's Watch. Denys's stern order influenced Mance's decision to desert the Shadow Tower and the Night's Watch the next day, joining the free folk living beyond the Wall.

name: Denys Oakheart
bio: Denys Oakheart was a knight of House Oakheart during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He was the eldest son and heir of Lord Oakheart. In 73 AC, Denys took part in a tourney held by his father to celebrate the birth of another son. Denys was defeated by a mystery knight called the Silver Fool, who was later revealed to be Prince Baelon Targaryen.

name: Denys Redwyne
bio: Denys Redwyne is the son of Ser Desmond_Redwyne and Denyse_Hightower.

name: Denys Snow
bio: Denys Snow, known as the Bastard of Barrowton, was a bastard from House Dustin. Denys fought in the army of Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) when he rebelled against his uncle, King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Denys was killed in the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye in 43 AC.

name: Denys Strong
bio: Denys Strong is a sellsword in service to the Golden Company. As a serjeant, he is a high-ranking officer. Denys is a Westerosi exile. His surname Strong is of a house that had once loomed large in the histories of the Seven Kingdoms. House Strong once held the infamous castle of Harrenhal until they were extinguished during the Dance of the Dragons. He may or may not be of House Strong, as in the free companies a man can call himself whatever he chooses, though not all are genuine. He may be related to another serjeant in the company, Duncan_Strong.

name: Denys Swann
bio: Denys Swann was a squire from House Swann during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Denys was among the group of squires who kept company with Princess Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I). By the end of 78 AC the princess fell out with Denys and the other squires, as she decided she hated them.

name: Denys Woodwright
bio: Denys Woodwright was a knight from House Woodwright and a sworn sword to Corlys_Velaryon, Lord of the Tides and Hand of the Queen to Rhaenyra_Targaryen, during the Dance of the Dragons. After Lord Corlys was imprisoned under charges of treason for warning Ser Addam_Velaryon of his impending arrest, Ser Denys and Ser Thoron_True, another sworn sword to the Sea Snake, cut their way into the dungeons of the Red Keep to liberate their lord. However, their plans had been betrayed to Mysaria by a whore Ser Thoron had been bedding. Ser Denys and Ser Thoros were taken and hanged from the walls of the Red Keep, dying at dawn.

name: Denyse Hightower
bio: Denyse Hightower is a daughter of Lord Leyton_Hightower. She is married to Ser Desmond_Redwyne and they have one child, Denys_Redwyne.

name: Denzo D'han
bio: Denzo D'han is a sellsword captain of the Windblown. A warrior bard, he is the left hand of the Tattered_Prince. Denzo is a seasoned warrior who does not come from Westeros. He is a veteran of hundred battles. He is a poet.

name: Deremond
bio: Deremond is the subject of a song. He took part in a battle at the Bloody Meadow. He may be a former Lord of Darry.

name: Deria Martell
bio: Deria Martell was the ruling Princess of Dorne and the head of House Martell during the reigns of kings Aegon_I_Targaryen and Aenys_I_Targaryen.

name: Dermot of the Rainwood
bio: Dermot of the Rainwood is a famed hedge knight from the rainwood.

name: Derrick Darry
bio: Derrick Darry was a Lord of Darry and head of House Darry during the Dance of the Dragons. A Lord_Darry_(Dance) and his heir were slain during the Dance when Prince Aemond_Targaryen burned Darry with the dragonflame of Vhagar, but Lady Darry and her younger children survived in the castle's vaults. Lord Derrick gathered men for Ser Addam_Velaryon's host before the Second Battle of Tumbleton. Derrick may have died during the battle, as a Lord Roland_Darry later participated in the Battle of the Kingsroad.

name: Derrick Fossoway
bio: Derrick Fossoway, dubbed the Bad Apple, was a member of House Fossoway. It is unknown if he was a red or green Fossoway. Derrick became one of the infamous Band of Nine, the instigators of the War of the Ninepenny Kings. It's unknown if Derrick survived the war or not.

name: Desmera Redwyne
bio: Desmera Redwyne is a noblewoman of House Redwyne and is the daughter and youngest child of Lord Paxter_Redwyne and Mina_Tyrell. Lord Randyll_Tarly hoped for his son and heir, Samwell_Tarly, to be taken as Lord Paxter_Redwyne's page at the Arbor and in due time be betrothed to Desmera. Her brothers Horas_Redwyne and Hobber_Redwyne bullied Sam during his visit, however, and Randyll brought his son back to Horn Hill.

name: Desmond
bio: Desmond  was a maester of the Citadel who served as Grand Maester during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. After Queen Alys_Harroway went into premature labor in 44 AC, Desmond assisted in the delivery. When the queen gave birth to a severely-deformed stillborn child, the furious King Maegor_I_Targaryen blamed everyone who had assisted in the delivery, and later Alys herself for her supposed infidelity. The king eventually ordered the executions of Alys, her lord father Lucas_Harroway, her septas and midwives, and Desmond. Desmond was one of three Grand Maesters who were executed during Maegor's reign, the others being Gawen and Myros.

name: Desmond
bio: Desmond is a member of the household guard of Lord Eddard_Stark at Winterfell.

name: Desmond Darry
bio: Desmond Darry was a knight from House Darry during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Desmond was a participant in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Desmond Grell
bio: Desmond Grell is a knight of House Grell and the master-at-arms at Riverrun. He has spent the better part of his life in service to House Tully. Desmond was born of House Grell, one of the minor houses of the riverlands. He entered the service of Lord Hoster_Tully at a young age. He was a squire when Hoster's daughter Catelyn_Stark was born, a knight by the time she learned to ride, and the master-at-arms at Riverrun before she was wed to Lord Eddard_Stark. During Catelyn's bedding on her wedding night, Desmond drunkenly apologized for each bawdy joke he made.

name: Desmond Mallister
bio: Desmond Mallister was a knight from House Mallister. According to Archmaester Haereg's History of the Ironborn, Desmond had a great and tragic friendship with Lord Loron_Greyjoy_(lord).

name: Desmond Redwyne
bio: Desmond Redwyne is a knight of House Redwyne. He is married to Denyse_Hightower and they have one child, Denys_Redwyne. His relation to Paxter_Redwyne, Lord of the Arbor and the head of the house, is currently unknown.

name: Devan Seaworth
bio: Devan Seaworth is the fifth son of Ser Davos_Seaworth and his wife Marya_Seaworth. He is a squire of King Stannis_Baratheon.

name: Devyn Sealskinner
bio: Devyn Sealskinner is a free folk leader.

name: Deziel Dalt
bio: Deziel Dalt is the Knight of Lemonwood and the head of House Dalt. He is the brother of Ser Andrey_Dalt. Deziel had once aspired to marry Princess Arianne_Martell, as had his younger brother, Ser Andrey_Dalt.

name: Dhako
bio: Dhako, also known as the Dragon of the North, was a Dothraki khal who lived during the Century of Blood. He was the grandson of Khal Onqo. When Dhako approached the city of Ibbish to sack it, its inhabitants took to their ships and fled to Ib. Dhako put the city to the torch and named it Vaes Aresak, or City of Cowards. In his wroth, Dhako burned not only the abandoned city, but also much of the surrounding countryside. Thereafter he was known as the Dragon of the North. The Braavosi adventurer Terrio_Erastes was a guest of Khal Dhako when Ibbish was destroyed. He wrote in his chronicle Fire Upon the Grass that Dhako took great pride in being known as the Dragon of the North, but came to rue it when his khalasar was defeated in battle by that of Khal Temmo, who took the elder Dhako captive and fed him to the flames after cutting off his hands, feet, and genitals, and roasting them before his eyes, after first burning his wives and sons in the same manner.

name: Dhazzar
bio: Dhazzar is a child of one of the noble ruling families of Meereen.

name: Dick
bio: Fletcher Dick was a famous member of the Kingswood Brotherhood. Hailing from a village near Stonehelm, he taught Ulmer how to use a bow and is regarded by some as the greatest archer ever.

name: Dick
bio: Yellow Dick is a man-at-arms sworn to House Bolton. He is one of the Bastard's Boys.

name: Dick Bean
bio: Dick Bean was a man-at-arms in service of House Targaryen in 42 AC. Dick served House Targaryen since he was a boy. When King Maegor_I_Targaryen was challenged to a trial of seven by Ser Damon_Morrigen, Grand Captain of the Warrior's Sons, Dick was the first to answer Maegor's call to those who would stand beside him, shaming many knights and noble lords into answering Maegor's summons. One account claimed that Dick was the first to die during the trial, cut down by Ser Lyle_Bracken.

name: Dick Cole
bio: Dick Cole is a sellsword in service to the Golden Company. As a serjeant, he is a high-ranking officer in the company. Dick is a Westerosi exile. His surname Cole is of a house that was once prominent in the histories of the Seven Kingdoms. House Cole served as stewards to House Dondarrion in the years preceding the Dance of the Dragons, and their most prominent member was Ser Criston_Cole, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard under Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. By the present day, their arms are obscure, and they may be extinct. Dick may or may not be of House Cole, as in the free companies a man can call himself whatever he chooses, and not all are genuine. He may be related to another serjeant in the company, Will_Cole.

name: Dick Crabb
bio: Dick Crabb, better known as Nimble Dick, is a former soldier, originally from Crackclaw Point. Dick was born and bred at Crackclaw Point and knows the terrain well, as well as much of its folklore and history. According to him, he carries the blood of Ser Clarence_Crabb. He has a sister who was bedded by a knight's son and then went off to King's Landing to take a job as a whore. Nimble Dick deserted the service of an unnamed lord.[N 1]

name: Dick Follard
bio: Dick Follard, called Deaf Dick, is a ranger of the Night's Watch from House Follard.

name: Dick Straw
bio: Dick Straw is a member of House Straw and of the Windblown. The Tattered_Prince has had Dick whipped more than any man in the Windblown. Dick has had sex with Pretty Meris a few times.

name: Dickon Flowers
bio: Dickon Flowers, known as the Bastard of Beesbury, was a bastard from House Beesbury and a knight of the Warrior's Sons at the start of the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. In 42 AC, Ser Dickon was stationed at the King's Landing chapter of the Warrior's Sons, based in the Sept of Remembrance. He fought in the trial of seven in which seven of the Warrior's Sons faced King Maegor_I_Targaryen and his six champions. Dickon died during the fight.

name: Dickon Frey
bio: Dickon Frey is a member of House Frey. He is the son of Ser Jammos_Frey and Sallei_Paege, and the twin brother of Mathis_Frey.

name: Dickon Manwoody
bio: Dickon Manwoody is the second son of Lord Dagos_Manwoody of Kingsgrave.

name: Dickon Morrigen
bio: Dickon Morrigen was a member of House Morrigen during the reign of the Storm King Argilac_Durrandon. Dickon and the Bastard_of_Blackhaven were slain by the dragonflame of Meraxes while leading the vanguard of Argilac during the Last Storm.

name: Dickon Tarly
bio: Dickon Tarly is the second son and fifth child of Lord Randyll_Tarly and Lady Melessa_Florent. Since his less martial older brother Samwell_Tarly has joined the Night's Watch, he is the heir to Horn Hill. Dickon is a young squire. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Dickon is portrayed by Freddie Stroma in Season 6 and Tom Hopper in Season 7. Dickon was born at Horn Hill, the second son of Lord Randyll_Tarly and Lady Melessa_Florent. As a babe, Dickon's mother used to sing her son to sleep, alongside her eldest son Samwell_Tarly. Randyll soon put an end to it, fearing that the "soft septon's songs" would make his second son soft, like Samwell. Lord Randyll eventually turned his attention to Dickon and started to groom him as his heir after he was repeatedly disappointed by Samwell. This grooming included taking Dickon along when Randyll left Horn Hill and requesting his presence when Lord Randyll held audiences. After Lord Randyll forces Samwell to join the Night's Watch, Dickon becomes the heir apparent to Horn Hill.

name: Dirk
bio: Dirk is a ranger of the Night's Watch. Although Dirk does not appear in the television adaptation Game of Thrones, the character Karl Tanner, played by Burn Gorman, is based on both Dirk and Clubfoot_Karl.

name: Dobber
bio: Dobber is one of the criminals Yoren picked for the Night’s Watch when he cleaned out the dungeons of King’s Landing and is part of the group marching north to the Wall.

name: Dogkiller
bio: Dogkiller is a captain of the Unsullied sworn to Daenerys_Targaryen.

name: Dolf
bio: Dolf, known as Dolf son of Holger because of Holger, is the father of Shagga and a member of the Stone Crows. He taught Shagga everything he knew about warfare. It is unknown if Dolf is still alive.

name: Domeric Bolton
bio: Domeric Bolton was a member of House Bolton, the sole trueborn son of Lord Roose_Bolton and Lady Bethany_Bolton, and heir to the Dreadfort. Domeric served four years as a page in Barrowton to his aunt, Lady Barbrey_Dustin, and then squired for three years for Lord Horton_Redfort in the Vale of Arryn. Domeric enjoyed the company of Horton's sons and began to consider them brothers. Lord Redfort thought the lad had the making of a tourney champion. After returning from the Vale to the Dreadfort, Domeric heard he had a bastard half-brother, Ramsay_Snow, and wished to seek him out, against his father's wishes, since he had always wanted a brother of his own. Soon after the visit, Domeric died in 297 AC from a sickness of the bowels, according to Maester Uthor. However, Roose believes Ramsay poisoned Domeric, in order to become Roose's only son. The heirless Roose subsequently brought Ramsay to live at the Dreadfort.

name: Donal Noye
bio: Donal Noye is an armorer, smith, and steward of the Night's Watch. He used to be a smith and armorer for House Baratheon of Storm's End. Before he joined the Night's Watch, Donal was in service to House Baratheon of Storm's End. He knew the late Lord Steffon_Baratheon and saw his sons when they were children. Donal worked as a smith at Storm's End and made the warhammer for Robert_I_Baratheon which killed Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen at the Trident, as well as the first sword for Stannis_Baratheon. Donal left the service of House Baratheon after Robert's Rebellion, having lost an arm which festered from an axe wound suffered during the siege of Storm's End. Jon_Snow thinks Donal has traveled the Seven Kingdoms and participated in many battles.

name: Lord Dondarrion (father of Simon)
bio: Lord Dondarrion was the Lord of Blackhaven and the head of House Dondarrion during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. It is unknown if this is the same individual as Lord Harmon_Dondarrion. In 54 AC, Lord Dondarrion hosted King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen at Blackhaven during their return from a royal progress in the Reach. The royal couple stayed longer than they had intended but left immediately on dragonback when they learned their mother, Queen Alyssa_Velaryon, was dying at Storm's End. By 61 AC, he was succeeded as Lord of Blackhaven by his son, Ser Simon_Dondarrion.

name: Lord Dondarrion (father of Manfred)
bio: Lord Dondarrion was the Lord of Blackhaven and the head of House Dondarrion during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In 206 AC, Lord Dondarrion and Lord Pearse_Caron raised a host and defeated the Vulture_King_(Daeron_II), burning him out of the Red Mountains. Ser Arlan_of_Pennytree and his squire, Duncan_the_Tall, were in his service during this campaign.

name: Donel Greyjoy
bio: Donel Greyjoy was a member of House Greyjoy, the third son of Lord Quellon_Greyjoy and his first wife, Lady_Stonetree_(wife_of_Quellon_Greyjoy).

name: Donella Hornwood
bio: Donella Hornwood is the Lady of the Hornwood, wife of Lord Halys_Hornwood, and mother of Daryn_Hornwood. Originally from House Manderly, she is a cousin of Wyman_Manderly, Lord of White Harbor. Donella did not receive much attention from men when she was a maid.

name: Doniphos Paenymion
bio: Doniphos Paenymion is one of the current ruling triarchs of the Free City of Volantis.

name: Donnel Arryn
bio: Donnel Arryn was the Lord of the Eyrie and the head of House Arryn during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen, whom he staunchly supported during the First Blackfyre Rebellion. When Daemon_I_Blackfyre declared himself the true king of the Seven Kingdoms and sought to overthrow his half-brother King Daeron II in 196 AC, Lord Donnel called his banners in support of King Daeron and marched his army to the Redgrass Field in support. Donnel boldly lead the vanguard, which was on the verge of being overwhelmed by the Blackfyre forces until the arrival of Ser Gwayne_Corbray of the Kingsguard with reinforcements. In 208 AC, Donnel participated in a tourney at Maidenpool, where he was overthrown in the lists by Ser Humfrey_Hardyng, alongside Lord Royce and Ser Donnel_of_Duskendale. During the Great Spring Sickness, Donnel ordered the Vale of Arryn sealed off, shutting down traffic from the high road and by sea, preventing the illness from spreading into the region.

name: Donnel Drumm
bio: Donnel Drumm is a member of House Drumm, a son of Lord Dunstan_Drumm of Old Wyk.

name: Donnel Flint
bio: Donnel Flint, also known as Black Donnel Flint, is the son and heir of Lord Torghen_Flint, the clan chief of the first Flints. Donnel has a half-brother, Artos_Flint.

name: Donnel Haigh
bio: Donnel Haigh is a knight from House Haigh. The second son of Ser Leslyn_Haigh and Perriane_Frey, Donnel has two brothers, Ser Harys_Haigh and Alyn_Haigh. Donnel has been beaten many times in tourneys by Sandor_Clegane, who almost killed him in a mêlée.

name: Donnel Hightower
bio: Donnel Hightower, called Donnel the Delayer, was Lord of the Hightower and head of House Hightower during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Martyn_Hightower was lord of House Hightower at the beginning of Maegor the Cruel's reign. He later died in unspecified circumstances, so that by the end of Maegor's reign, Donnel had succeeded him as lord of House Hightower. Donnel's exact relationship to Martyn has not been stated. When Septon Moon_(septon) and his followers appeared at Oldtown in 48 AC, Lord Donnel closed his gates against them. Moon and his followers remained outside the city's walls, but Donnel refused to take up arms against them. Many believed Donnel was not willing to battle against Lords Torgen_Oakheart and Rickard_Rowan, who had joined Septon Moon. Due to his reluctance, Lord Hightower was dubbed Donnel the Delayer by the maesters of the Citadel. Most of Moon's followers fled after his death. By the time less than four hundred men remained of the original host of five thousand, Lord Donnel rode forth and killed those who had remained behind. In 49 AC, Lord Donnel came to King's Landing for the Golden Wedding, escorting the High_Lickspittle with a hundred knights and seventy-seven of the Most Devout. Ser Orryn_Baratheon traveled to Oldtown to seize the novice Rhaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon) in 50 AC. He was arrested by Donnel, however, despite the fact that the High Septon had heard Orryn's confession and had forgiven him for his intended crime. Donnel sent a full account of what transpired to King's Landing. In 54 AC, following the death of the High Septon, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen traveled to Oldtown on their dragons, disregarding the small council objection to their traveling alone. Donnel wished for one of his relatives serving the Faith to be elected as the new High Septon, to which Jaehaerys had no objection, as long as they spoke in favor of the king's Doctrine of Exceptionalism. When the king and Donnel reached an agreement, they sealed it with a large feast. Septon Alfyn_(septon) was eventually elected, but as he was quite old, it seemed likely that he would die sooner rather than later. It was expected Alfyn would be succeeded by a Hightower, provided the Hightowers aligned themselves firmly with the Exceptionalists. Alfyn was eventually succeeded by High_Septon_(brother_of_Donnel_Hightower). In 55 AC, Lord Donnel received Queen Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) when she arrived in Oldtown searching for her daughter, Princess Aerea_Targaryen. In 56 AC, Donnel received summons from King Jaehaerys to arrest Lady Elissa_Farman, now going by the name of Alys Westhill, commanding him to take her into custody and deliver her to King's Landing. Either the ravens did not reach Oldtown in time, or Donnel delayed on purpose, but Alys had already taken ship by the time Donnel moved against her. He sent a dozen of his swiftest ships after her and his two grandsons, Eustace_Hightower and Norman_Hightower, who had joined her. None of Donnel's vessels were able to capture them, however. When Ser Eustace turned up in Oldtown on the seventh day of 59 AC, Hightower knights escorted him to Donnel in the high hall of the Hightower, where the lord listened to his grandson's tale. Later that same year, Lord Donnel died from the Shivers.

name: Donnel Hill
bio: Donnel Hill, known as Sweet Donnel, is a steward of the Night's Watch and is the squire of Ser Mallador_Locke. He claims to be a bastard of the Lannisters. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Donnel is portrayed by Jack Roth.

name: Donnel Locke
bio: Donnel Locke is a knight from House Locke.

name: Donnel of Duskendale
bio: Donnel of Duskendale was a knight of the Kingsguard during the reigns of the kings Daeron_II_Targaryen and Aerys_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Donnel is portrayed by Bill Ward. At the tourney at Maidenpool in 208 AC, Ser Donnel of Duskendale was among those overthrown by Ser Humfrey_Hardyng in the lists. The following year, Donnel, along with his two fellow brothers of the Kingsguard, Ser Roland_Crakehall_(Kingsguard) and Ser Willem_Wylde, escorted the Targaryen princes to the tourney at Ashford Meadow. As the princes participated in the tourney, the Kingsguard knights did not join in the lists, as it would not be fitting to have them ride against those they are sworn to protect. After the princes Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) and Aegon_V_Targaryen disappeared, Donnel accompanied Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen in searching for them. The three of them fought on the side of Prince Aerion_Targaryen during the trial of seven of Ser Duncan_the_Tall. In the first charge, Donnel slew Ser Humfrey_Beesbury. After King Daeron II passed away during the Great Spring Sickness, Ser Donnel continued to serve in the Kingsguard of his successor, King Aerys_I_Targaryen.

name: Donnel Swann
bio: Donnel Swann is a knight of House Swann and the eldest son and heir of Gulian_Swann, Lord of Stonehelm.

name: Donnel Waynwood
bio: Donnel Waynwood is a knight from House Waynwood and the second son of Lady Anya_Waynwood, Lady of Ironoaks. His squire is Sandor_Frey, a kinsman of some relation. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Donnel is portrayed by Alisdair Simpson.

name: Donnis
bio: Donnis is a member of the guard at Winterfell.

name: Donnor Stark
bio: Donnor Stark was a Lord of Winterfell and head of House Stark. He was the eldest son of Lord Beron_Stark and his wife, Lady Lorra_Royce. He had four younger brothers (Willam_Stark, Artos_Stark, Errold_Stark and Rodrik_Stark_(son_of_Beron)) and two younger sisters (Berena_Stark and Alysanne_Stark). According to a semi-canon source, Donnor's father, Lord Beron_Stark, was mortally wounded by ironborn, and House Stark faced a crisis of succession as his wife and four other recent Stark widows made claim for their own children. In the end, Donnor succeeded his father as Lord of Winterfell. As Donnor died without issue, his brother Willam_Stark inherited Winterfell after Donnor's death. Donnor is buried in the crypts of Winterfell.

name: Donnor Saltcliffe
bio: Donnor Saltcliffe is a lord from Saltcliffe in the Iron Islands and is the head of House Saltcliffe.

name: Dontos Hollard
bio: Dontos Hollard, called Dontos the Red, is a knight and the last surviving member of House Hollard. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Dontos is portrayed by Tony Way. Dontos was the son of Ser Steffon_Hollard, who died of a fever when Dontos was young. Dontos is the only surviving member of House Hollard, once close allies and vassals of House Darklyn. As a result of the Defiance of Duskendale, both houses were destroyed and their blood exterminated. Only young Dontos was spared, because Ser Barristan_Selmy asked for clemency. As Barristan had rescued Aerys_II_Targaryen during the Defiance, the king granted Barristan's wish. Young Dontos was taken to King's Landing to serve as a squire. He never returned to Duskendale. Dontos has won no renown or tourneys as a knight.

name: Donyse
bio: Donyse is a septa of the Faith of the Seven in service at King's Landing.

name: Doran Martell
bio: Prince Doran Nymeros Martell, also known simply as Doran Martell, is the head of House Martell, the Prince of Dorne, and the Lord of Sunspear. Married to Lady Mellario, of the Free City of Norvos, he has three children: Arianne_Martell, Quentyn_Martell, and Trystane_Martell. He is also the elder brother of Elia_Martell and Oberyn_Martell. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Doran is portrayed by Alexander Siddig. Doran was the firstborn child of his parents, and thus heir to Princess_of_Dorne_(mother_of_Doran), the Princess of Dorne. For years, he was an only child, as his mother had several miscarriages. While he would have four siblings, Mors_Martell_(brother_of_Doran) and Olyvar_Martell, both died in the cradle. Doran spent several years of his childhood at the Water Gardens, where he played games in the pools and fountains. By the age of nine, Doran was a squire to Lord Gargalen at Salt Shore. While there, his mother gave birth to his sister, Elia_Martell. As Elia had been born one month premature, Doran was convinced that his sister too would die, as their brothers had done before, but she survived. A year later, Doran's brother Oberyn_Martell was born. As a young man, Doran took a tour through the Free Cities. He visited Volantis and next went to Norvos, where he met his future wife, Lady Mellario. They fell in love and Mellario returned to Dorne with Doran, where they were betrothed and would soon after marry. Areo_Hotah escorted Mellario and entered the service of House Martell. In 273 AC, when his mother, her consort, and Doran's two younger siblings traveled through the south of Seven Kingdoms on a quest to find suitable matches for Elia and Oberyn, Doran stayed behind as castellan of Sunspear, with Mellario. Although Mellario was highborn enough to become his wife, most marriages between nobles in Westeros are political. A love match is very uncommon. In 276 AC, Doran and Mellario's first child, Arianne_Martell, was born. Around 279 AC, Doran inherited the rule of Dorne. An unknown amount of time after his siblings had traveled to find suitable marriage candidates, Doran's brother Oberyn fought in a duel with Lord Edgar_Yronwood. Due to Oberyn's high birth and young age, the two men had agreed to duel until first blood. Both men were injured, but when Oberyn's cuts healed, and Edgar's festered, eventually resulting in his death, it was whispered Oberyn had fought with a poisoned blade. Doran acted swiftly, sending Oberyn into temporary exile.

name: Dorcas
bio: Dorcas is a servant of House Lannister in King's Landing.

name: Dorden the Dour
bio: Ser Dorden the Dour is a knight in the service of King Stannis_Baratheon. He is one of the queen's men.

name: Dorea Sand
bio: Dorea Sand is the thirdborn bastard daughter of Prince Oberyn_Martell and his paramour Ellaria_Sand. She is one of the famous Sand Snakes.

name: Doreah
bio: Doreah is a Lysene handmaid to Daenerys_Targaryen. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Doreah is portrayed by Roxanne McKee. Magister Illyrio_Mopatis found Doreah in a pleasure house in Lys. Viserys_Targaryen bedded her while he was a guest at Illyrio's manse in Pentos.

name: Dorian Hightower
bio: Dorian Hightower was a head of House Hightower and Lord of the Hightower. During the coming of the Andals to the Reach, Lord Dorian welcomed the Andals and set aside his wife of twenty years and the mother of his children to take an Andal princess as wife.

name: Dormund
bio: Dormund, together with his father Tormund, brother Toregg, and sister Munda, and others join Ygritte when she sings "The Last of the Giants". When Ryk comes to 'steal' Munda, Dryn and Dormund give Ryk a fight to defend their sister's honor. Tormund reveals to Jon_Snow that Dormund was cut down during the battle beneath the Wall by a knight under Stannis_Baratheon who had moths on his shield.[N 1]

name: Dorna Swyft
bio: Dorna Swyft is the daughter of Ser Harys_Swyft, the Knight of Cornfield, and the wife of Ser Kevan_Lannister. They have four children together: Lancel_Lannister, Willem_Lannister, Martyn_Lannister, and Janei_Lannister. When Dorna was younger, her father borrowed large sums of money from Lord Tytos_Lannister. In 260 AC, after the War of the Ninepenny Kings, Ser Tywin_Lannister, the young heir of Casterly Rock, demanded that all those who took loans from House Lannister had to repay them immediately. Those unable to do so instead had to surrender a family member as a hostage until their debt was settled. When debt collectors arrived at Cornfield, Ser Harys was unable to pay, and was forced to hand Dorna over to the custody of Ser Kevan_Lannister. By 266 AC, Dorna and Kevan had wed. Their eldest son, Lancel, was born in 282 AC.

name: Dornish Dilly
bio: Dornish Dilly is a member of the Night's Watch.

name: Doro Golathis
bio: Doro Golathis was a Norvoshi historian. He stated there are remnants of Rhoynish outposts in Andalos.

name: Dorren Stark
bio: Dorren Stark was a King in the North and head of House Stark before the War of Conquest. Doren is mentioned in an account of the Night's Watch. During his rule the Night's Watch fought giants and traded with the children of the forest.

name: Doshi
bio: Doshi was a Dothraki woman and the mother of Khal Mengo, who united all Dothraki under his command during the Century of Blood. She was a purported witch queen. Mengo compelled all other Dothraki to accept his rule at Doshi's counsel.

name: Blind Doss
bio: Blind Doss is a free folk leader.

name: Drako Rogare
bio: Drako Rogare was a member of House Rogare, a Lysene noble banking family. He was the son of Lysandro_Rogare, the First Magister of Lys. Drako was the son of Lysandro Rogare. He had five trueborn brothers, Lysaro_Rogare, Moredo_Rogare, Lotho_Rogare, Fredo_Rogare, and Roggerio_Rogare, and three trueborn sisters, Larra_Rogare, Lysara_Rogare, and Marra_Rogare as well as sixteen bastard siblings. After the death of his father, Lysandro_Rogare, Drako inherited his ships. When the Rogare Bank ran dry, Drako escaped to Volantis on one of his galleys before he could be tried like many of his siblings. However, for every ship saved, nine were lost, together with their cargos and the Rogare wharves and storehouses. When his brother Lysaro_Rogare arrived in Volantis after being captured at Volon Therys, the Triarchs offered to ransom him to Drako, but he declined, suggesting instead that the Volantenes sell him back to Lys.

name: Draper's wife
bio: A draper's wife was the paramour of Ser Preston_Greenfield. She met him while her husband was away.

name: Draqaz
bio: Draqaz is a child of one of the noble ruling families of Meereen.

name: Drazenko Rogare
bio: Drazenko Rogare was a member of House Rogare, a wealthy noble family of Lys dedicated to banking. Drazenko was one of the envoys from Lys to the court of the Princess of Dorne, Aliandra_Martell, at Sunspear. Drazenko, alongside his brother Lysandro_Rogare, were seen as the architects of the alliance between Dorne and the Triarchy. It is therefore likely that Drazenko was already part of Sunspear's court under Prince Qoren_Martell, Aliandra's father. On Maiden's Day of 133 AC, Drazenko approached Lord Alyn_Velaryon during the feast organized to celebrate the Maiden, and asked for a private word. He whispered in Alyn's ear, informing him that Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, believed dead ever since the Battle of the Gullet, was in fact alive and with Drazenko's brother in Lys. Lord Alyn, who was returning to King's Landing after his expedition to the westerlands to deal with the threat of Lord Dalton_Greyjoy's ironborn, changed his plans and sailed towards the Free City the very next day. At some point, Drazenko married Aliandra_Martell, and became the Prince Consort of Dorne. Aliandra even conferred him the title of "Lord of the Stepstones". This marriage, and the return of Prince Viserys to King's Landing, marked the apex of House Rogare's power in Westeros, called the Lysene Spring. In 135 AC, he and his brother died within a day of one another, beginning the decline of the Rogare family in both Lys and the Seven Kingdoms. Drazenko died first, choking upon a piece of bacon, and Lysandro drowned when his barge sank. Their deaths were widely believed at the time to have been the act of hired Faceless Men.

name: Drennan
bio: Drennan is an ironborn raider. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Drennan is portrayed by David Coakley.

name: Drinkwater twins
bio: The Drinkwater twins are members of House Drinkwater and the twin sisters of Ser Gerris_Drinkwater. Their given names have not been revealed. One of the girls gave Prince Quentyn_Martell his first kiss. Ser Cletus_Yronwood suggested that Quentyn take one or both of them as paramours, but the prince instead avoided the pair afterwards.

name: Drogo
bio: Drogo is a powerful khal of the Dothraki. Daenerys_Targaryen is promised to the warlord at the beginning of A Song of Ice and Fire. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Drogo is portrayed by Jason Momoa. Drogo's father was Khal Bharbo. Cohollo was pledged to the khalakka, or prince, while Drogo was still a child, and he saved young Drogo's life from sellswords on one occasion. Bharbo gave his son a whip when Drogo reached manhood. By the age of thirty, Drogo leads a khalasar with forty thousand warriors, the largest on the Dothraki sea. The khal has never been defeated in battle. He has given a tall grey stallion to Jhogo. Drogo developed a taste for good wine from his visits to the Free Cities.

name: Drox the Corpse-Maker
bio: Drox the Corpse-Maker was an Andal warlord who fought during the coming of the Andals to the stormlands. He was defeated by a Storm King, Cleoden_I_Durrandon, and three Dornish kings in a battle on the river Slayne.

name: Drunken Daughter
bio: The Drunken Daughter  is a dockside whore in Braavos.

name: Druselka
bio: Druselka was a Rhoynish priestess that took part in the Rhoynar migration. When Nymeria had her people abandon the Abulu, one of the Summer Isles, Druselka and her followers abandoned Nymeria and returned to Essos, as Druselka claimed to have heard Mother Rhoyne calling her children home. Druselka and the others returned to the abandoned and ruined Rhoynish cities, only to find the Valyrians waiting to hunt them down or enslave them.

name: Dryn
bio: Dryn is a son of Tormund. He is about the same age as Bran_Stark.

name: Dudley
bio: Dudley is a knight in the riverlands.

name: Dunaver
bio: Dunaver is a knight in service to House Lannister. His squire is Jodge.

name: Duncan the Tall
bio: Duncan the Tall was a knight during the reigns of Kings Daeron_II_Targaryen, Aerys_I_Targaryen, Maekar_I_Targaryen, and Aegon_V_Targaryen. Raised as Dunk in Flea Bottom, he became the squire of a hedge knight, Ser Arlan_of_Pennytree. Duncan then traveled the Seven Kingdoms as a hedge knight himself, with the young Prince Aegon, called "Egg", as his squire. The early exploits of "Dunk and Egg" are chronicled in A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, which collects The Hedge Knight, The Sworn Sword, and The Mystery Knight. Duncan then served Aegon after he ascended the Iron Throne, eventually becoming Lord Commander of the Kingsguard for Aegon V. Duncan's personal arms were a green shooting star above an elm tree proper on sunset, but he also briefly used, while competing as a mystery knight in a tournament, the sigil of an unknown knight, "a hanged man swinging grim and gray beneath a gallows tree." In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Duncan is portrayed by Peter Claffey. Duncan's earliest memories were of living as a street urchin in the Flea Bottom slums of King's Landing. His closest friends went by the names of Ferret, Rafe, and Pudding. By his own admission, they were "little monsters" with Duncan, at the time still known as "Dunk", the worst of all. Sometimes they would sell or even dump pieces of meat of dubious origin to the local pot shops that made the bowls of brown. Dunk was an orphan who never knew either of his parents. It may be that he is a bastard. He believed his father might have been a criminal, and once hoped to someday go to the north and eventually the Wall, where he could perhaps meet some tall old man who looked like him. Ser Arlan_of_Pennytree, a hedge knight, took Dunk as his new squire three or four years after the death of his previous squire, Roger_of_Pennytree, during the final battle of the First Blackfyre Rebellion in 196 AC.[N 2] He found the young Dunk, aged five or six, in King's Landing chasing a pig and took him on. Arlan raised Dunk, and together they traveled all over Westeros, except the north. Though they never visited Pennytree, where Arlan was from, they served Lord Leo_Tyrell_(Longthorn) at Highgarden during one of the first few years that Dunk served Arlan, visited King's Landing in 205 AC, served Lord_Florent for half a year and joined Lord_Dondarrion_(father_of_Manfred) in the hunt for the Vulture_King_(Daeron_II), both in 206 AC, and visited Lannisport in 208 AC, amongst other places. As well, Duncan accompanied Arlan when he was one of several knights hired by a Dornish merchant who wanted to travel from Lannisport to the Prince's Pass in Dorne. Dunk learned sword-fighting from Ser Arlan, though he still recalls his fighting experience from Flea Bottom as well. Ever since the first time that Arlan had let Duncan hold a sword, Duncan had dreamt of becoming a knight of the Kingsguard. Dunk was not sure exactly how old he was because he grew up as an abandoned orphan in the streets of King's Landing, and his unusually large height from a young age made it difficult to guess. By 209 AC he loosely thought he must be about 16 or 17 years old, which would place his birth somewhere between 191 to 193 AC. An unknown amount of time after Whitewalls, Duncan and Aegon visited Winterfell. Presumably in honor of Ser Duncan, Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen named his firstborn son and heir Duncan_Targaryen (however, when asked about it, George R. R. Martin refused to answer). Prince Duncan was called "Duncan the Small". Both he and Aegon had some involvement during the Peake Uprising, where Aegon's father was killed. In late 233 AC, after Aegon had ascended the throne as Aegon_V_Targaryen, Duncan escorted the king's brother, Maester Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I), to Eastwatch-by-the-Sea, where Aemon was going to join the Night's Watch. Duncan eventually joined the Kingsguard. While it is unknown when exactly he took his vows, the first known mention of him as a Kingsguard knight is in 236 AC. In 236 AC, Ser Duncan fought in the Fourth Blackfyre Rebellion and personally slew Daemon_III_Blackfyre, the Blackfyre Pretender. In 239 AC, when Prince Duncan_Targaryen broke his betrothal to Lord Lyonel_Baratheon's daughter and married a commonborn girl known as Jenny_of_Oldstones instead, the enraged Lyonel renounced his fealty to the Iron Throne. Duncan served as King Aegon V's champion in the trial by combat, in which he bested Lyonel, thereby ending his short rebellion. In 252 AC or 253 AC Duncan, by then Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, participated in a winter tourney at King's Landing, where he was defeated by a sixteen-year-old Barristan_Selmy. In 259 AC, Duncan apparently perished in the tragedy of Summerhall,  along with King Aegon V and Prince Duncan, among others. Little is known about the events of the tragedy, but it seems that the Lord Commander's valor saved some of the survivors from dying in the great fire that consumed Summerhall.

name: Duncan Liddle
bio: Duncan Liddle, known as Big Liddle, is a ranger of the Night's Watch. He is the eldest son of Lord Torren_Liddle, known as the Liddle, the clan chief of House Liddle, one of the northern mountain clans.

name: Duncan Strong
bio: Duncan Strong is a sellsword in service to the Golden Company. As a serjeant, he is a high-ranking officer. Duncan is a Westerosi exile. His surname Strong is of a house that had once loomed large in the histories of the Seven Kingdoms. House Strong once held the infamous castle of Harrenhal until they were extinguished during the Dance of the Dragons. He may or may not be of House Strong, as in the free companies a man can call himself whatever he chooses, though not all are genuine. He may be related to another serjeant in the company, Denys_Strong.

name: Duncan Targaryen
bio: Prince Duncan Targaryen, also known as Duncan the Small and the Prince of Dragonflies, was a member of House Targaryen. He was the first son of King Aegon_V_Targaryen and his Queen Betha_Blackwood, but gave up the throne for love. Prince Duncan was the first child and eldest son born to then-Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen and his wife Betha_Blackwood. He was presumably named for his father's dearest friend, Ser Duncan_the_Tall, and so was nicknamed "Duncan the Small" (however, when asked about it, George R. R. Martin refused to answer). In 233 AC, Duncan's grandfather King Maekar_I_Targaryen died in the Peake Uprising, and through the decision of the Great Council of 233 AC, Maekar's fourth son Aegon became king of Westeros. Duncan, Aegon's eldest son, became Prince of Dragonstone and heir to the Iron Throne. Prince Duncan defeated Ser Barristan_Selmy in the tourney at Blackhaven in 247 AC, when Barristan was only a ten-year-old squire and participating in the tourney as a mystery knight. Prince Duncan took pity on the small knight and jousted against him when no one else would, giving Barristan the nickname "the Bold". Years later, in 253 AC, Duncan was defeated by Barristan at the winter tourney at King's Landing. In 259 AC, Prince Duncan died in the tragedy at Summerhall along with his father, King Aegon_V_Targaryen, his namesake Ser Duncan_the_Tall, and several other members of the royal court.

name: Dunsen
bio: Dunsen is one of the Mountain's men, the men-at-arms in service to Ser Gregor_Clegane.

name: Dunstan Drumm
bio: Dunstan Drumm, also known as The Drumm and the Bone Hand, is head of House Drumm, Lord of Old Wyk, and captain of Thunderer.

name: Dunstan Pryor
bio: Dunstan Pryor was the Lord of Pebble and the head of House Pryor during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. After Princess Saera_Targaryen's companion Alys_Turnberry was disgraced and gave birth to a bastard daughter in the Vale, she was arranged to marry Lord Dunstan.

name: Duram Bar Emmon
bio: Duram Bar Emmon is the Lord of Sharp Point and the head of House Bar Emmon.

name: Duran
bio: Duran is an Unsullied sworn to Daenerys_Targaryen.

name: Durran
bio: Durran, called Durran Godsgrief and the Godsgrief, was the mythic first Storm King and the founder of House Durrandon. Legend has that he was married to Elenei and was the builder of Storm's End. According to legend, in the Age of Heroes, Durran won the love of Elenei, the daughter of the sea god and the goddess of the wind. Her divine parents forbade their love, but Durran and Elenei wed despite them. When she gave her maidenhead to him on their wedding night, committing herself to a mortal life, the gods' wrath was terrible to behold. They destroyed Durran's keep, killing all his family and the wedding guests, but Elenei protected Durran from the storm. Enraged, Durran declared war on the gods, who replied by hammering his kingdom with massive storms. Each time Durran built a castle to face the sea, the gods destroyed it. King Durran's lords pleaded with him to build inland, his priests told him he must placate the gods by giving Elenei back to the sea, and even his smallfolk begged him to relent. But Durran refused, and persisted building larger and more powerful fortifications. Finally, the seventh castle stayed in place at Durran's Point and resisted the storms of Shipbreaker Bay. Some believe this is because the children of the forest took a hand in its construction, shaping the stones with magic. Others believe that a young boy, who grew up to be Bran the Builder, advised Durran on its construction. The truth of the matter is unknown. Nevertheless, Storm's End resisted everything the gods threw at it, and Durran reigned with Elenei at his side for a thousand years. Storm's End has been called Durran's Defiance. Though Durran supposedly ruled for a thousand years, Archmaester Glaive proposed that the "King of a Thousand Years" was in fact a succession of monarchs all bearing the same name. The Storm Kings of old from House Durrandon named their firstborn son and heir after Durran Godsgrief, complicating the research of maesters. Durran Godsgrief claimed the rainwood, but much of it was returned to the children of the forest by his son, Durran_II_Durrandon.

name: Durran II Durrandon
bio: Durran II Durrandon, also known as Durran the Devout, was a Storm King of House Durrandon and the son and successor of King Durran. Durran returned most of the Rainwood to the children of the forest.

name: Durran Durrandon
bio: Durran Durrandon, also known as Durran Bronze-Axe, was a Storm King of House Durrandon. A century after Durran_II_Durrandon returned most of the Rainwood to the children of the forest, Bronze-Axe took it back for good.

name: Durran Durrandon
bio: Durran Durrandon, also known as Durran the Dour, was a Storm King of House Durrandon. Durran is remembered for slaying Lun the Last, King of the Giants, at the Battle of Crookwater. Scholars debate whether he was Durran V or Durran VI.

name: Durran Durrandon
bio: Durran Durrandon, also known as Durran the Ravenfriend, was a Storm King of House Durrandon. Under Durran's rule, Maldon_Massey built Stonedance and established his lordship over Massey's Hook.

name: Durran Durrandon
bio: Durran Durrandon, also known as Durran the Young and the Butcher Boy, was a Storm King of House Durrandon. Durran dammed the river Slayne with Dornish corpses after turning back Yoren_Yronwood and the warrior maid Wylla_of_Wyl in the Battle by the Bloody Pool. Scholars also debate whether he was the same king who became besotted with his own niece later in life and died at the hands of his brother Erich_Durrandon_(Kin-Killer).

name: Durran Durrandon
bio: Durran Durrandon, also known as Durran the Fair, was a Storm King of House Durrandon. Durran brought the independent island Kingdom of Tarth into the Kingdom of the Storm by taking the daughter of King Edwyn_Tarth as his wife. He was also the grandfather of Erich the Sailmaker

name: Durran X Durrandon
bio: Durran Durrandon, to most scholars Durran X, was a Storm King of House Durrandon. Durran extended the Kingdom of the Storm northward to the Blackwater Rush. He was the father of Monfryd_I_Durrandon.

name: Durran XI Durrandon
bio: Durran XI Durrandon, also known as Durran the Dim, was a Storm King of House Durrandon and the sucessor of Monfryd_I_Durrandon. Durran is remembered for having yielded up territories his Monfryd_I_Durrandon had conquered and more. He was succeeded by his son Barron_Durrandon.

name: Durran XXI Durrandon
bio: Durran XXI Durrandon was a Storm King of House Durrandon during the Andal Invasion. During the Andal invasion of the stormlands, he sought out the remaining Children of the Forest taking refuge in caves and hollow hills, and made common cause with them. This Weirwood Alliance dealt the Andals a series of stinging defeats in such battles as the Battle at Black Bog, the Battle in the Misty Wood, and the Battle beneath the Howling Hill, and checked the decline of the Kingdom of the Storm for a time.

name: Durran XXIV Durrandon
bio: Durran XXIV Durrandon, also known as Durran Half-Blood, was a Storm King of House Durrandon. Durran was born during the late Andal invasion of the stormlands, from the union of his father Maldon_IV_Durrandon with an Andal maiden he took as his wife, thus earning Durran his nickname. He followed the example of his father and also took a wife of pure Andal blood. During his reign, and the following ones, Andal warlords became lords and petty kings and married the daughters of Stormlords and gave them their daughters in return, swearing fealty and their swords for lands.

name: Durran the Third
bio: Durran Durrandon, also known as Durran the Third,[N 1] was a Storm King of House Durrandon. Durran attempted two invasions of Dorne, but was thrown back by Princess Nymeria.

name: Durwald I Durrandon
bio: Durwald I Durrandon, also known as Durwald the Fat, was a Storm King of House Durrandon. During his long rule House Massey broke away from the Kingdom of the Storm, Tarth revolted thrice, and at Cape Wrath a woods witch known only as the Green Queen held the rainwood for the best part of a generation. Thus, it was said that Durwald's rule extended no farther than the distance a man could urinate off the walls of Storm's End.

name: Dusky woman
bio: The "dusky woman" is a comely mute given by Euron_Greyjoy as a bedwarmer to his brother Victarion_Greyjoy. The woman's origins and name are unknown. She may come from Naath, as the Naathi people are described as having "dusky skin" and are widely sought after by slavers who consider them to make the best slaves. Euron_Greyjoy takes the woman from a slaver bound for Lys, and he has her tongue sliced and torn out.

name: Dwarf brother
bio: The dwarf brother is a holy brother and a member of the sparrows. His given name is unknown.

name: Dwarf juggler
bio: The dwarf juggler was an entertainer in Tyrosh. His given name is unknown. The juggler entertained people every day for years at the square by the Fountain of the Drunken God in Tyrosh. He was known to a pair of dwarf entertainers, Oppo and Penny.

name: Dyah
bio: Dyah is a wildling. She is one of the nineteen wives of Craster.

name: Dyanna Dayne
bio: Dyanna Dayne was a noblewoman from House Dayne during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. She married Daeron's fourth son, Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen, and together they had six children: Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I), Aerion_Targaryen, Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I), Aegon_V_Targaryen, Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Maekar_I) and Rhae_Targaryen. Dyanna died before 209 AC, well before Maekar's ascendance to the Iron Throne in 221 AC, thus she never became queen consort. On his way to the Ashford tourney in 209 AC, Ser Duncan_the_Tall mistook young Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen for a stableboy. Aegon asked him to take him with Ashford and, presuming the innkeep was his mother, asked him what his mother would say to the request. When Aegon stated that she wouldn't say anything because his mother was dead, Dunk assumed the boy was an orphan.

name: Dykk Harlaw
bio: Dykk Harlaw is an ironborn raider from House Harlaw. His relation to Lord Rodrik_Harlaw, the head of the house, is currently unknown.

name: Dywen
bio: Dywen is a veteran ranger and forester of the Night's Watch.

name: Dywen Shell
bio: Dywen Shell was a petty king of the First Men from House Shell who claimed the title King of the Fingers before the Andal invasion. Dywen paid an Andal warlord to cross the narrow sea in order to use his swords against Jon_Brightstone, who also claimed to be King of the Fingers. Eventually the warlord Shell had hired turned against him and he was roasted alive inside his wooden longhall. An Andal knight named Corwyn Corbray took his wife for a bedwarmer.

name: Earl Harlaw
bio: Earl Harlaw is a member of House Harlaw and one of the crew on Asha_Greyjoy's ship Black Wind. His relation to Lord Rodrik_Harlaw, the head of the house, is currently unknown.

name: Easy
bio: Easy is a steward of the Night's Watch.

name: Ebben
bio: Ebben (or Eggen) is a veteran ranger of the Night's Watch stationed at the Shadow Tower.

name: Ebrose
bio: Ebrose is the archmaester at the Citadel who tests novices seeking their link in the arts of healing. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Ebrose is portrayed by Jim Broadbent. Ebrose has studied all known accounts of the "butterfly fever" of Naath and concluded that it is spread by a large black-and-white variety of butterflies with wings as big as a man's hand. Ebrose has also examined the accounts of dangerous diseases of Sothoryos. The archmaester praises the healthful qualities of mother's milk. Ebrose informed Maester Yandel that Archmaester Edgerran allowed Yandel to be raised at the Citadel so that Edgerran could test theories involving infants. Qyburn desired to surpass Ebrose as a healer, so he began opening the bodies of the living. Qyburn was thus expelled from the maesters for disobeying their rules.

name: Eddara Tallhart
bio: Eddara Tallhart is the daughter of Ser Helman_Tallhart.

name: Eddard Karstark
bio: Eddard Karstark, affectionately called Edd by his sister Alys_Karstark, is the second son of Lord Rickard_Karstark of Karhold. He is named after Eddard_Stark.

name: Eddard Stark
bio: Eddard Stark, also called "Ned", is the head of House Stark, Lord of Winterfell, and Warden of the North. He is a close friend to King Robert_I_Baratheon, with whom he was raised. Eddard is one of the major POV characters in A Song of Ice and Fire. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Eddard is portrayed by Sean Bean, Sebastian Croft (as a child), and Robert Aramayo (as a young man).

name: Edderion Stark
bio: Edderion Stark, better known as Edderion the Bridegroom, was King in the North. Edderion was buried in the crypts beneath Winterfell.

name: Eddison Peake (the Elder)
bio: Eddison Peake, known as Eddison the Elder, was a Lord of Starpike. He was an illustrious and legendary ancestor of House Peake.

name: Eddison Peake (the Younger)
bio: Eddison Peake, known as Eddison the Younger, was a Lord of Starpike. He was an illustrious and legendary ancestor of House Peake.

name: Eddison Tollett
bio: Eddison Tollett, better known as Dolorous Edd, is a squire from House Tollett and a steward of the Night's Watch. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Eddison is portrayed by Ben Crompton. Dolorous Edd appears to have been born to a lesser branch of House Tollett, as he speaks of being raised in a crude house such as the poorer of the smallfolk might have. Per George R. R. Martin, he was recruited to the Night's Watch by Yoren, who told him women could not resist a man in uniform, leaving out the Night's Watch vow of celibacy. Edd once lost track of his white horse while it was snowing.

name: Eden Risley
bio: Eden Risley was a knight of House Risley during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Eden participated in the Whitewalls tourney in 212 AC. His leg was crushed beneath his horse during a fall.

name: Edgar Yronwood
bio: Edgar Yronwood was the Lord of Yronwood and the head of House Yronwood during the early reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. He was the father of Lord Ormond_Yronwood and the grandfather of Lord Anders_Yronwood. When Prince Oberyn_Martell was sixteen, he was found abed with Lord Edgar's paramour. A duel between the two ensued, though only to first blood due to Oberyn's youth and high birth. Both men took cuts. However, while Prince Oberyn soon recovered, Edgar's wounds festered and he died. Afterward it was rumored that Oberyn had poisoned his sword, gaining his nickname of "the Red Viper".

name: Edgar Sloane
bio: Edgar Sloane was a nobleman of House Sloane who served as Hand of the King for Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen. Edgar served as Hand of the King for Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen, one of the many seasoned, older men serving the king. Upon the king's death, he was retired from office by Aerys_II_Targaryen when the latter ascended to the Iron Throne and Ser Tywin_Lannister, the heir of Casterly Rock, was named in his place.

name: Edgerran
bio: Archmaester Edgerran was one of the archmaesters at the Citadel. Edgerran was the Seneschal of the Citadel in 272 AC. He wrote a treatise on the swaddling of infants and tested his theories on the infant Maester Yandel.

name: Edgerran Oakheart
bio: Edgerran Oakheart, better known as Edgerran the Open-Handed, was the Lord of Old Oak and the head of House Oakheart at an unknown time. Edgerran fought against Dornishmen.

name: Edmund Ambrose
bio: Edmund Ambrose is a knight from House Ambrose. His relation to Lord Arthur_Ambrose, the head of House Ambrose, is unknown.

name: Edmund Blackwood
bio: Edmund Blackwood, called Ben by family and friends, is the fourth son of Lord Tytos_Blackwood of Raventree Hall.

name: Edmund Breakstone
bio: Edmund Breakstone is a knight of House Breakstone.

name: Edmund Gardener
bio: Prince Edmund Gardener was a member of House Gardener of Highgarden and the heir to Mern_IX_Gardener, the last King of the Reach. When his father, King Mern_IX_Gardener, made an alliance with Loren_I_Lannister, King of the Rock, to join their armies against Aegon_I_Targaryen, Edmund was given the vanguard. Edmund burned in the Field of Fire alongside his father, brothers, and sons when Aegon and his sisters, Visenya_Targaryen and Rhaenys_Targaryen, unleashed the fury of their dragons.

name: Edmund Warrick
bio: Edmund Warrick was a knight of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. During the regency of Aegon III, an attempted coup against the Rogare family and the Hand of the King, Lord Thaddeus_Rowan, resulted in the deaths of Ser Marston_Waters, the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, and two of his sworn brothers, Ser Mervyn_Flowers and Ser Amaury_Peake. Ser Edmund, Ser Agramore_Cobb, Ser Dennis_Whitfield were given the white cloaks of the Kingsguard to replace the deceased.

name: Edmund Waxley
bio: Edmund Waxley is the Knight of Wickenden and the head of House Waxley.

name: Edmure Tully
bio: Edmure Tully is a knight and the heir of House Tully of Riverrun, the liege lords of the riverlands. He is the only living son of Hoster_Tully, the Lord Paramount of the Trident, and his wife Lady Minisa Whent. Edmure is the younger brother of Catelyn_Stark and Lysa_Arryn. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Edmure is portrayed by Tobias Menzies. Edmure was born as the third child and only living son of Lord Hoster_Tully and Lady Minisa Whent. Edmure's mother died while giving birth to his baby brother, who also died. As a youth, Edmure was known as hot-headed but good-hearted. He had a number of friends who had similar characteristics, most notably Marq_Piper. Edmure once broke his arm after falling from an elm in the godswood of Riverrun. Edmure gave Petyr_Baelish, Hoster's ward, the nickname "Littlefinger", because of House Baelish's holdings on the Fingers and because of Petyr's short stature. When Lords Lord_Blackwood_(Aerys_II) and Lord_Bracken_(Aerys_II) visited Hoster to discuss their feud, young Edmure, his sisters, and Petyr drank wine while the adults were preoccupied. When Petyr challenged Brandon_Stark, the betrothed of Edmure's sister Catelyn_Stark, for her hand, Edmure served as Brandon's squire. Brandon easily defeated Petyr in their duel, and the wounded Littlefinger sent Edmure away when he tried to visit afterward. Hoster refused an offer by Lord Walder_Frey to wed Edmure to one of his daughters. Princess Arianne_Martell wanted to accept Hoster's offer for her to meet Edmure at Riverrun, but Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne, declined because of a secret marriage pact.

name: Edmure Tully
bio: Edmure Tully was a knight and a head of House Tully during the coming of the Andals. He was the father of Axel_Tully, who would become the first Lord of Riverrun. Edmure and his sons fought under Tristifer_IV_Mudd, King of the Rivers and the Hills, in many of his ninety-nine victories. However, after the Hammer of Justice's death, Edmure went over to the mightiest of the Andal conquerors, Armistead_Vance.

name: Edmyn Tully
bio: Edmyn Tully was the Lord of Riverrun and the head of House Tully during Aegon's Conquest. The first of the riverlords to join Aegon_I_Targaryen against King Harren_Hoare, Aegon rewarded Edmyn by raising him to Lord Paramount of the Trident when Harren and his line perished. Edmyn helped repair damage done to the riverlands by Harren the Black, and he also served as Hand of the King to King Aegon I. He had three daughters, one of whom married Quenton_Qoherys, Lord of Harrenhal.

name: Edric Dayne
bio: Edric Dayne, also known as Ned, is the Lord of Starfall and the head of House Dayne. His Lord_Dayne_(father_of_Edric), whose name is unknown, was the elder brother of Ser Arthur_Dayne, Lady Ashara_Dayne, and Lady Allyria_Dayne. Edric serves as the squire of Beric_Dondarrion, Lord of Blackhaven. Edric was born at Starfall, in Dorne, to Lord_Dayne_(father_of_Edric) and his wife. Edric's mother did not have enough milk for him when he was born, so he was nursed by Wylla, a servant at Starfall. At some point, Edric was told that Wylla was the mother of Jon_Snow, the bastard of Lord Eddard_Stark. When Edric was seven, his aunt Allyria_Dayne was betrothed to Lord Beric_Dondarrion, and Edric went to Blackhaven with Beric to serve as his page. Beric raised Edric to squire when he turned ten. He once won a prize riding at rings. Edric has never killed anyone.

name: Edric Stark
bio: Edric Stark was a Lord of Winterfell and head of House Stark during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Edric was a grandson of Lord Alaric_Stark. Edric became Lord of Winterfell after Alaric's death in 72 AC, as Alaric's two sons had died previously.

name: Edric Stark
bio: Edric Stark was the third son of Lord Cregan_Stark, second by his last wife Lynara_Stark. He was married to Serena_Stark, the daughter of his half-brother Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan). They had four children; twin sons Torrhen_Stark_(son_of_Edric) and Cregard_Stark, and daughters Arrana_Stark and Argelle_Stark.

name: Edric Storm
bio: Edric Storm is the bastard son of King Robert_I_Baratheon and Delena_Florent. Edric was conceived by King Robert_I_Baratheon and Delena on the wedding night of Lord Stannis_Baratheon to Selyse_Florent, Delena's cousin, in the couple's wedding bed. Stannis saw this as an insult to his honor, so he sent Edric to Storm's End to foster with the boy's other uncle, Renly_Baratheon. Selyse believes that Edric's existence has cursed her to have no sons. As Delena was a noblewoman, Edric was acknowledged by his father. He was raised in relative comfort at Storm's End under the guardianship of Ser Cortnay_Penrose, Renly's castellan, who is fond of the boy. Edric was educated by maesters, castellans, and masters-at-arms. Edric has been sent a gift from King Robert every year on his name day. These have included a pony, a sable cloak, and a child-sized warhammer similar to his father's weapon. Edric would write to King's Landing to thank his father for the gifts, but upon receiving the letters Robert would laugh and ask Varys what he had sent that year.

name: Edrick Stark
bio: Edrick Stark, known as Edrick Snowbeard, was a King in the North from House Stark. Edrick ruled the north for almost a hundred years. His reign was marked by quarrels between his descendants, as well as external threats from the ironborn, wildlings, and the Starks' ancient rivals, House Bolton. The outer walls of Winterfell were thus raised during Edrick's time. Slavers from the Stepstones captured the Wolf's Den once Edrick grew feeble. He is buried in the crypts beneath Winterfell.

name: Edwell Celtigar
bio: Edwell Celtigar was the Lord of Claw Isle and the head of House Celtigar during the reigns of kings Maegor_I_Targaryen and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Edwell was named Hand of the King by Maegor_I_Targaryen in 44 AC, following the execution of the previous Hand, Lord Lucas_Harroway. King Maegor left Edwell to rule King's Landing along with Maegor's wife, Queen Tyanna_of_the_Tower, when Maegor himself left to embark on a new campaign against the Faith Militant uprising in 45 AC. In 47 AC Edwell suggested to Maegor that the heirless king should take more than one new wife so as to increase his chances of producing an heir. Edwell suggested his  two maiden daughters, Prunella_Celtigar and Prudence_Celtigar, who were of twelve and thirteen years. Although Maegor eventually took Edwell's advice, he did not choose Edwell's daughters. Lord Celtigar resigned the office of Hand in 48 AC, when even previously loyal lords of the realm were now turning against the king. He safely returned to Claw Isle. Upon the ascension of the young Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen to the Iron Throne, Lord Celtigar was pardoned and named lord treasurer and master of coin by Lord Rogar_Baratheon, the new Hand. The wars fought by Maegor had near bankrupted the crown. Lord Celtigar was unwilling to risk offending the high lords, so he instead enforced a number of new taxes upon the smallfolk, such as tripling the port fees of King's Landing, Lannisport, and Oldtown. Certain goods were taxed both coming in and going out of the city, and new levies were asked of innkeepers and builders. These measures made Edwell deeply despised by the populace and failed to generate sufficient revenue. Building slowed to a halt, the inns emptied, and trade declined significantly in the capital, while the Lannisters and Hightowers ignored Lord Celtigar's demand to raise their port fees. Upon King Jaehaerys's sixteenth name day, he returned to King's Landing and kindly dismissed Lord Edwell from his office, thanking him for his services. Three days later the king undid most of the taxes Edwell had placed on the smallfolk, much to the populace's delight. During the winter of 59 AC when the Shivers spread to the crownlands, Lord Edwell was one of the first high lords to die from the disease.

name: Edwyd Fossoway
bio: Edwyd Fossoway is a knight from House Fossoway of Cider Hall.

name: Edwyle Stark
bio:
Edwyle Stark was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the late reign of King Maekar_I_Targaryen and the reign of King Aegon_V_Targaryen. He was the only son of Lord Willam_Stark and his second wife, Lady Melantha_Blackwood. Edwyle married Marna_Locke and had only one child with her, Rickard_Stark.
Edwyle was buried in the crypt of Winterfell. There is a statue of him placed with his tomb. Edwyle was succeeded as Lord of Winterfell by his son, Rickard_Stark.

name: Edwyn Frey
