bio: Edwyn Frey is a member of House Frey. He is the eldest son of Ser Ryman_Frey, a grandson of Ser Stevron_Frey, and a great-grandson of Walder_Frey, Lord of the Crossing. Edwyn is wed to Janyce_Hunter, and they have a daughter, Walda_Frey_(daughter_of_Edwyn). Edwyn has always been at odds with his brother Walder_Frey_(son_of_Ryman), considering him a threat due to his position as next in the line of succession. Additionally, according to Merrett_Frey, it is common knowledge at the Twins that Janyce had an affair with Black Walder.

name: Edwyn Osgrey
bio: Edwyn Osgrey was a knight of House Osgrey during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He was the eldest son of Ser Eustace_Osgrey. Edwyn joined his father in supporting Daemon_I_Blackfyre in his rebellion in 196 AC. Edwyn was killed during the Battle of the Redgrass Field and was buried among the blackberries at Standfast.

name: Edwyn Stark
bio: Edwyn Stark, better known as Edwyn the Spring King, was a King in the North. He is buried in the crypt of Winterfell and has a statue.

name: Edwyn Tarth
bio: Edwyn Tarth, known as Edwyn Evenstar, was a King of Tarth from House Tarth. Maesters believe the island kingdom of Tarth became part of the dominion of Storm's End when Edwyn's daughter married a Storm King, Durran_Durrandon_(the_Fair).

name: Edyth
bio: Edyth was a novice, and later septa, who was a beloved religious tutor of Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. In 50 AC, Queen Alyssa_Velaryon sent three women to see to Queen Alysanne_Targaryen's religious instruction: Septa Ysabel, and the novices Lyra and Edyth, alongside the famously pious Lady Lucinda_Broome. It was the hope of the Queen Regent that the fiercely righteous women would convince Alysanne and perhaps even King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen that brother lying with sister was an abomination in the eyes of the Faith before their incestuous union could tear the realm apart again. Instead, they were moved by the queen's devotion to her husband. By 51 AC, she had sworn her vows to become a septa. She attended Queen Alysanne at Maidenpool where she was slashed across the face defending the queen when she was attacked by women attending Jonquil's Pool who wished to kill the "Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I)" Alysanne was carrying. After Queen Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I)'s beloved Septa Maryam was poisoned by Androw_Farman in 54 AC, Edyth arrived on Dragonstone to assume her place. In 55 AC, she accompanied Queen Alysanne during her pregnancy with Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I). Edyth died of the Shivers of 59 AC and 60 AC.

name: Egbert
bio: Egbert is a maester of the Citadel. He wrote the book Justice and Injustice in the North: Judgments of Three Stark Lords.

name: Eggon
bio: Eggon is an armsman in service to Ser Gregor_Clegane.

name: Eglantine
bio: Eglantine is a septa of the Faith of the Seven. She is the personal septa of Princess Myrcella_Baratheon.

name: Egon Emeros
bio: Egon Emeros, also known as Egon Emeros the Exquisite, is a member of the Pureborn of Qarth.

name: Eladon Goldenhair
bio: Eladon Goldenhair is an Unsullied sworn to Daenerys_Targaryen.

name: Elaena Targaryen
bio: Princess Elaena Targaryen was the third daughter and youngest child of King Aegon_III_Targaryen and his wife, Queen Daenaera_Velaryon. Born in 150 AC, Elaena was the third daughter and youngest child of King Aegon_III_Targaryen and Queen Daenaera_Velaryon of House Velaryon. She had two older brothers, Daeron_I_Targaryen and Baelor_I_Targaryen, and two older sisters, Daena_Targaryen and Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_III). Elaena's most cherished possession was a dragon's egg whose stony shell showed the same colors as her hair. After being freed from the Maidenvault, Elaena outlived her siblings and led a tumultuous life. Although Elaena had three husbands during her lifetime, the great love of her life was her cousin, Lord Alyn_Velaryon. She gave birth to two bastard children by him, the twins Jon_Waters and Jeyne_Waters. Elaena had hoped to marry Alyn, but he was lost at sea; a year after his disappearance, she gave up hope and agreed to marry another. In 176 AC, during the reign of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen, Elaena married her first husband, the wealthy but elderly Lord Ossifer_Plumm. He died on their wedding night (supposedly after seeing her naked), but Elaena became pregnant and gave birth to a son, called Viserys_Plumm. There were those who were dubious of the boy's parentage, and had suspicions on who might actually be the boy's Aegon_IV_Targaryen. None dared speak their suspicions aloud, however. Elaena married her second husband, Lord Ronnel_Penrose, in the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He sat on the king's small council as master of coin; but it was widely known that Elaena was the one performing the duties, as she was shrewd and intelligent with money, while Ronnel did not have a great facility for numbers. King Daeron trusted her with many important matters of state, and she grew influential. Elaena birthed Ronnel four children: Robin_Penrose, Laena_Penrose, Jocelyn_Penrose, and Joy_Penrose. After the birth of her seventh child, Elaena declared that if seven was sufficient for the gods, it would do for her as well. Not long after Ronnel's death, Elaena married once more, for love and by her own choosing. With King Daeron II's blessing, Elaena was married to Ser Michael_Manwoody, a Dornishman who had attended the court of Queen Myriah_Martell. Michael was sent as an envoy to Braavos on several occasions, and there is record of correspondence between him and the keyholders of the Iron Bank. These letters, while signed with Michael's name and fixed with his seal, appear to have been in the handwriting of Elaena. While her husband was a cultured man of great wit and learning, Elaena said it was not those traits that had caused her to fall in love with him, but his love of music, and he was known to play the harp for her. When Michael died, Elaena commanded his effigy to be carved with him holding a harp, instead of the sword and spurs of knighthood.

name: Elaena Targaryen
bio: Elaena Targaryen was a member of House Targaryen during the Century of Blood, the eldest daughter of Lord Gaemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenar) and Lady Daenys Targaryen of Dragonstone. Elaena married her brother, Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Gaemon), and together they had two sons: Maegon_Targaryen and Aerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon). Elaena and Aegon also had a younger sister who married a petty lord. Elaena and Aegon inherited the lordship of Dragonstone from their father and mother, they jointly ruled Dragonstone and were succeeded by Maegon.

name: Elbert Arryn
bio: Elbert Arryn was a member of House Arryn and nephew of Lord Jon_Arryn. At the time of his death he was the heir to the Vale of Arryn. Elbert was born at roughly the same time his father Ser Ronnel_Arryn_(son_of_Jasper) was dying. After the death of his father, Elbert became Lord Jon_Arryn's heir. When Brandon_Stark learned that his sister Lyanna_Stark had been abducted by Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen, Brandon raced off to King's Landing accompanied by a party that included Elbert. The party rode into the Red Keep and demanded Rhaegar's head. King Aerys_II_Targaryen had them all arrested and summoned their fathers to King's Landing to answer for their sons' crimes. Aerys had all the men killed except for Ethan_Glover.

name: Elder Brother
bio: The Elder Brother is the leader of a septry on the Quiet Isle. He is reputed to have powers as a healer. This elder brother's given name is unknown. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, the Elder Brother is merged with Meribald as Ray, and portrayed by Ian McShane. The Elder Brother was born the third son of a family of knights, and his father, grandfather, and brothers were all knights. He was trained to fight from the day he was old enough to hold a wooden sword. He saw many battles, and did well in them, without disgrace. However, he had some women by rape, which he now considers shameful. When he was not fighting, he was drunk. He wished to marry a younger daughter of a petty lord, but as a third son, he had nothing to offer her but the shield, sword, and horse of his knighthood. He fought for House Targaryen at the Battle of the Trident, serving a lord who served a lord who chose to support King Aerys_II_Targaryen rather than Robert Baratheon. He was shot in the thigh and the foot, and his horse was killed, but he still fought, while desperate to find another horse, as he could not afford a new one and could not be a knight without a horse. A blow from behind knocked him unconscious, and he fell into the Trident. Others thought him dead, so they stripped his armor and possessions and pushed his body into deeper waters. He floated downstream where he woke up naked on the Quiet Isle. He spent the next ten years as a penitent under a vow of silence. At some point he became the Elder Brother of the Quiet Isle, and took residence in the cave called the Hermit's Hole. He gained a reputation as a great healer.

name: Elderly man
bio: An old man is a ship insurer operating in Braavos. His given name is unknown. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is identified as the "thin man" and portrayed by Oengus MacNamara.

name: Eldiss
bio: Eldiss was an ironborn sailor. He had a brother, Skyte.

name: Eldon Estermont
bio: Eldon Estermont is a knight of House Estermont.

name: Eldred Codd
bio: Eldred Codd is an ironborn raider and a member of House Codd.

name: Eldric Arryn
bio: Eldric Arryn was a knight from House Arryn during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. He attempted to claim the Eyrie for his father, Ser Arnold_Arryn. Ser Eldric was the son of Ser Arnold_Arryn, himself a first cousin of Jeyne_Arryn, Lady of the Eyrie. Twice his father rose against her to contest her inheritance, claiming that women were too soft to rule. After his second rising, Arnold was imprisoned in the Eyrie's sky cells, where he went mad. In 134 AC, Lady Jeyne died. In her last testament, she named her fourth cousin, Ser Joffrey_Arryn as heir, though Arnold was much closer by blood. With Arnold still imprisoned, Eldric came forward to press his father's claim. Many lords supported his endeavor, insisting that the laws of inheritance could not be put aside by "the whim of a dying woman." Additionally, a third and more distant claimant came forward in the person of Isembard_Arryn, the head of House Arryn of Gulltown. Ser Corwyn_Corbray, one of the regents of young King Aegon_III_Targaryen, ruled that Jeyne's will must prevail and declared Joffrey the rightful Lord of the Eyrie. However, when the claimants refused to accept his ruling, Corwyn ordered the imprisonment of Isembard and his sons and the execution of Eldric. Arnold was able to flee to Runestone.

name: Eleanor Mooton
bio: Eleanor Mooton is a noblewoman of House Mooton. She is the eldest daughter of Lord William_Mooton of Maidenpool, and his heir.[N 1] She has at least two brothers and an unknown number of sisters.

name: Elenda Caron
bio: Elenda Baratheon, née Elenda Caron, was the Lady of Storm's End through her marriage to Lord Borros_Baratheon during the Dance of the Dragons. During the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen, she ruled Storm's End as regent. Lady Elenda was the daughter of Royce_Caron, Lord of Nightsong and Marshal of the Marches. She married Borros_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End, and had four daughters: Cassandra_Baratheon, Floris_Baratheon, Ellyn_Baratheon, and Maris_Baratheon. During the Dance of the Dragons, Elenda fell pregnant. Seven days after her husband died in the Battle of the Kingsroad, she gave birth to his posthumous son. Although Lord Borros had instructed her to name him Aegon, after King Aegon_II_Targaryen, Elenda named the child Royce_Baratheon after her lord father. Following her husband's death, Elenda ruled as regent for her newborn son. Seeing her men broken by battle, she agreed to terms of peace. Still weak from childbirth, she gave three of her daughters as hostages to the crown. They were accompanied by Ser Willis_Fell and Princess Jaehaera_Targaryen, who had been given refuge at Storm's End during the war. Lady Elenda sent Cassandra, Ellyn, and Floris to represent House Baratheon at the coronation of King Aegon_III_Targaryen and his wedding to Princess Jaehaera. Maris had become a silent sister, and though Mushroom claims that Elenda had Maris's tongue removed, Gyldayn notes that this can be discounted, as the silent sisters supposedly being tongueless is a myth. In 132 AC, Lady Elenda married again, to Ser Steffon_Connington, second son of the Lord of Griffin's Roost and a man twenty years her junior. With Royce but an infant, Elenda felt that she needed a strong man's hand to keep the peace against the increasingly frequent raids from Dornishmen. However, in 133 AC Steffon was slain by Wyland_Wyl in the Dornish Marches, and Elenda was widowed once more. After the death of Queen Jaehaera in 133 AC, Elenda wrote to the Crown, offering her daughters Cassandra and Ellyn as potential brides for King Aegon III. Three years later, Cassandra was tried for attempting to poison the king and his new queen, Daenaera_Velaryon. Elenda sent three stormlords to speak for Storm's End, being too ill to attend herself. They arranged for Cassandra to marry Ser Walter_Brownhill, a widower, as Elenda correctly believed that caring for Walter's thirteen surviving children would cease Cassandra's scheming and plotting.

name: Elenei
bio: Elenei is said to have been the daughter of the sea god and the goddess of the wind. According to legend, she married Durran Godsgrief, the first Storm King. According to legend, in the Age of Heroes, the first Storm King, Durran Godsgrief, won the love of fair Elenei. Her divine parents forbade their love, but Durran and Elenei wed despite them. When she gave her maidenhead to him on their wedding night, committing herself to a mortal life, the gods' wrath was terrible to behold. They destroyed Durran's keep, killing all his family and the wedding guests, but Elenei protected Durran from the storm. Enraged, Durran declared war on the gods, who replied by hammering his kingdom with massive storms. Each time Durran built a castle to face the sea, the gods destroyed it. King Durran's lords pleaded with him to build inland, his priests told him he must placate the gods by giving Elenei back to the sea, and even his smallfolk begged him to relent. But Durran refused, and persisted building larger and more powerful fortifications. Finally, the seventh castle stayed in place and resisted the storms of Shipbreaker Bay. Some believe this is because the children of the forest took a hand in its construction, shaping the stones with magic. Others believe that a young boy who grew up to be Bran the Builder advised Durran on its construction. The truth of the matter is unknown. Nevertheless, Storm's End resisted everything the gods threw at it, and Durran reigned with Elenei at his side for a thousand years.

name: Lord Elesham
bio: Lord Elesham was a Lord of the Paps and a head of House Elesham. Lord Elesham wed a daughter of Ser Elys_Waynwood and Alys_Arryn. Their marriage produces no children, however.

name: Eleyna Westerling
bio: Eleyna Westerling[N 1] is a noblewoman of House Westerling. She is the youngest daughter of Lord Gawen_Westerling and Lady Sybell_Spicer.

name: Elia Martell
bio: Princess Elia Nymeros Martell, also known simply as Elia Martell or Elia of Dorne, was a Dornish princess from House Martell. She was married to the crown prince, Rhaegar_Targaryen, and by him birthed Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar) and Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar). Elia was raped and murdered by Ser Gregor_Clegane in the Sack of King's Landing during Robert's Rebellion.

name: Elia Sand
bio: Elia Sand, known as El by her family and Lady Lance by others, is the oldest bastard daughter of Prince Oberyn_Martell by his paramour, Ellaria_Sand. She is one of the Sand Snakes. Elia was born in 285 AC or 286 AC the fifthborn daughter of Prince Oberyn_Martell, and the first by his longtime paramour Ellaria_Sand. Elia was most likely named after her late aunt, Elia_Martell, whom Oberyn was close to. Elia and Obella_Sand worship their eldest sisters—Obara_Sand, Nymeria_Sand, and Tyene_Sand—and in turn are worshipped by their younger sisters, Dorea_Sand and Loreza_Sand.

name: Elinda Massey
bio: Elinda Massey was a noblewoman of House Massey and a lady-in-waiting to Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen during the Dance of the Dragons. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Elinda is portrayed by Jordon Stevens. Elinda Massey was among the four ladies-in-waiting who accompanied Rhaenyra during her flight from King's Landing to Dragonstone. Supposedly she gouged out her own eyes at the sight of her queen being devoured by the dragon Sunfyre. After Rhaenyra's death, Elinda and the three other ladies-in-waiting were put in cells in Sea Dragon Tower to await ransom.

name: Elinor Costayne
bio: Elinor Costayne was a noblewoman from House Costayne and one of the many brides of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Along with Lady Jeyne_Westerling_(wife_of_Maegor_I) and Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), she was one of the so-called "Black Brides". Elinor enjoyed a quiet life with her three children by her first husband, Ser Theo_Bolling. However, in 47 AC, Theo was arrested by knights of the Kingsguard, accused of conspiring with Queen Alyssa_Velaryon to place her son, Prince Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, on the Iron Throne, and was executed. After seven days of mourning, Elinor was summoned to wed King Maegor_I_Targaryen, who sought to marry three women of proven fertility. Her sons' presence at the wedding ensured she would play her part in the ceremony. According to one tale, Elinor scratched Maegor's back to bloody ribbons as they coupled. Elinor's sons were sent away from her after her wedding to Maegor; her eldest was fostered at the Eyrie, her second son at Highgarden, and her youngest was given to a wet nurse. In 48 AC, When Queen Tyanna_of_the_Tower admitted to poisoning Queen Alys_Harroway during her pregnancy, Tyanna promised the same would happen to the pregnant Elinor. Two months later, Tyanna was proven correct when Elinor gave birth to a stillborn abomination said to have been born eyeless and with small wings. That year, Prince Jaehaerys Targaryen pressed his claim to the Iron Throne. Maegor's response was sluggish and confused, but though he was heavily outnumbered, he refused to surrender. At the end of his war council, Maegor remained behind alone in the throne room to brood. He was found the next morning by Queen Elinor, dead upon the Iron Throne with his wrists cut and one of the swords pointing through his neck. His death remains a mystery; some say he was killed by Queen Elinor, others that he had been killed by a knight of his own Kingsguard, yet others say he was killed by a builder who escaped the massacre the king had ordered three years prior and desired revenge, and many believe that Maegor had been killed by the throne itself for his unworthiness. Others believe that Maegor killed himself by opening his wrists on the blades of the Iron Throne. Elinor was one of two wives who survived the king, the other being Queen Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). After King Maegor's death, Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan) proposed that King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen marry Queen Elinor to reconcile with Maegor's supporters when a bride was being considered for the king, but nothing came of the proposal. After Jaehaerys's ascent, Elinor departed King's Landing dressed in the robes of a penitent. She visited her two elder sons at the Eyrie and Highgarden before retiring to her father's seat at the Three Towers with her youngest son. In 51 AC, King Jaehaerys commanded Elinor to be one of his Seven Speakers, three men and four women he sent out to spread his Doctrine of Exceptionalism to the peoples of the Seven Kingdoms. Elinor dressed in queenly raiment, which became shabbier and more threadbare each day she traveled through the Reach, but she eloquently testified to the evils of her late husband Maegor and the goodness of Jaehaerys and Alysanne. Years later, Elinor gave up all claims to nobility, becoming Mother Elinor at the great motherhouse in Lannisport.

name: Elinor Massey
bio: Elinor Massey was a member of House Massey during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 133 AC, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Many maidens across Westeros came to King's Landing in hopes of marrying the young king. Elinor was one of the many maidens who suffered what was called the "Maiden's Day curse" by being injured or scandalized after the ball was announced. In Elinor's case, a tale of her deflowering became common gossip in the court. It is possible that this was a rumor fabricated by Lord Unwin_Peake, the Hand of the King, who wished to promote his Myrielle_Peake as Aegon III's second queen.

name: Elinor Tyrell
bio: Elinor Tyrell is a member of a junior branch of House Tyrell and is a lady-in-waiting of Lady Margaery_Tyrell. Elinor is betrothed to Alyn_Ambrose, whom she is to marry once he becomes a knight.

name: Elissa Farman
bio: Elissa Farman was a noblewoman of House Farman and the daughter of Lord Marq_Farman during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. The daughter of Lord Marq_Farman of Fair Isle, Elissa had two brothers: Franklyn_Farman and Androw_Farman. As a child, it was said that she spent more time at sea than upon the land. She sailed her own boat around Fair Isle at the age of fourteen. By twenty, she had voyaged as far north as Bear Island and as far south as the Arbor. Elissa was betrothed twice, once at twelve and once at sixteen, but she scared off both boys. Thus, it came as little surprise that she became a close confidant to Queen Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). Elissa attended the wedding of her brother Androw to Queen Rhaena on Fair Isle in 49 AC. Elissa quickly grew close to the queen, as well as Rhaena's old friends Alayne_Royce and Samantha_Stokeworth. Her brother Franklyn dubbed the four friends "the Four-Headed Beast", and the friends often rode the dragon Dreamfyre together. Lord Farman's maester, Smike, speculated that the queen had only married Androw not because she loved him, but instead because of her love for Elissa. When Marq died in 50 AC, Franklyn became lord and ordered Rhaena, the so-called "Queen in the West", to leave Fair Isle, with the dragonrider departing for Casterly Rock. Though Elissa attempted to leave as well, Franklyn confronted her at the dock and commanded she stay on Fair Isle to be wed. Elissa spat defiance at Lord Franklyn before he and his men were overwhelmed, with Rhaena's companions then sailing with Elissa on her ship, Maiden's Fancy, for Lannisport. Realizing that most lords wanted to take advantage of her or were afraid of her, Rhaena and her friends briefly stayed at Casterly Rock, Ashemark, Castamere, the Golden Tooth, Wayfarer's Rest, and Pinkmaiden Castle. Elissa remained with Rhaena after she established her court on Dragonstone. She quickly warmed to Rhaena's daughter, Princess Aerea_Targaryen, as both were unhappy with their lives at the island fortress. Elissa often spoke of returning to the western seas with the young princess. As Elissa was denied the incomes of Fair Isle by Lord Franklyn, she petitioned Rhaena for gold sufficient to build a ship at Driftmark that could sail the Sunset Sea. Rhaena denied her request, and a rift thus opened in Elissa and Rhaena's relationship. In 54 AC, Elissa petitioned Rhaena to leave once more. Rhaena permitted her request stone-faced, while Aerea wept for Elissa. Neither Targaryen princess knew of Elissa's destination, and she discreetly stole three dragon eggs from the Dragonstone hatcheries. She departed for Driftmark, where she took ship to Pentos and made her way overland to Braavos under the name Alys Westhill and secured an audience with the Sealord of Braavos, selling him the eggs. With the gold, Elissa financed the building of her carrack, the Sun Chaser. She set sail with a word of caution from the Sealord, due to the scandal that the theft of the dragon eggs had caused the Targaryens. Androw fell into depression at Dragonstone, as Elissa had been his closest friend. Septon Barth, the Hand of the King to Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, was sent to Braavos to investigate the missing dragon eggs. The Sealord denied having them, but agreed to reduce the Iron Throne's debts to the Iron Bank of Braavos. In Oldtown in 56 AC, Elissa searched for a crew for her ship for her voyage across the Sunset Sea, as she had believed since childhood that undiscovered lands existed west of Westeros. Words of her efforts reached Lord Donnel_Hightower, and his grandsons, Ser Eustace_Hightower and Ser Norman_Hightower, pledged their ships, Lady Meredith and Autumn Moon, to her voyage. King Jaehaerys ordered Donnel to take Elissa into custody, but Elissa's three swift ships evaded the twelve pursuers sent by Lord Hightower. When he returned to Oldtown on Lady Meredith in 59 AC, Eustace Hightower explained that they had sailed south by southwest years ago but lost Norman and his Autumn Moon to a storm. Elissa's crew towed Lady Meredith to three exotic islands, which she named Aegon, Rhaenys, and Visenya. Despite the protests of Eustace, Elissa continued westward on Sun Chaser and was never seen again. Many years later, during his second great voyage on Sea Snake, Corlys_Velaryon believed he saw the old, weathered Sun Chaser in Asshai. It is therefore possible that Elissa reached Essos by sailing west from Westeros.

name: Elkin
bio: Maester Elkin is a maester from the Citadel at Oldtown. He has translated The Red Book, many centuries after it was originally written.

name: Ella Broome
bio: Ella Broome was a noblewoman from House Broome. She was the younger sister of Lady Lucinda_Broome. Ella was briefly offered as a match for Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Later on, she became a lady-in-waiting to his wife, Queen Alysanne_Targaryen.

name: Ella Lannister
bio: Ella Lannister is a noblewoman of House Lannister of Lannisport.[N 1] It is currently unknown if she is still alive in the main series. Ella was married to Damon_Lannister_(son_of_Jason), and gave birth to Damon's son, Damion_Lannister.

name: Ellard Stark
bio: Ellard Stark was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the late reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. At the Great Council of 101 AC, Lord Ellard supported first Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon) and then her son, Laenor_Velaryon. This may have been because House Stark was angry with King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen for earlier transferring the New Gift from northern control to the Night's Watch. Prince Viserys_I_Targaryen, Rhaenys's cousin, was chosen by the assembled lords as the new heir, however.

name: Ellard Crane
bio: Ellard Crane was a squire from House Crane during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Ellard was among the group of squires who kept company with Princess Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I). The princess fell out with Ellard after he kissed her on the lips without her leave.

name: Ellaria Sand
bio: Ellaria Sand is the beloved paramour of Prince Oberyn_Martell, and the mother of the four youngest "Sand Snakes," Oberyn's bastard daughters. Ellaria herself is the bastard daughter of Harmen_Uller, Lord of Hellholt. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Ellaria is portrayed by Indira Varma.

name: Ellendor
bio: Ellendor was a Grand Maester serving King Aegon_V_Targaryen. Ellendor was elected Grand Maester when he was close to 90 years old and was sent to serve the court of King Aegon_V_Targaryen to replace the late Grand Maester Kaeth. Ellendor died within a year of his appointment and was succeeded by Grand Maester Merion.

name: Ellery Vance
bio: Ellery Vance is a knight from House Vance of Atranta and the third son of Lord Norbert_Vance. He has four brothers; Ser Ronald_Vance, Ser Hugo_Vance, Ser Kirth_Vance and Maester Jon_Vance.

name: Ellyn Baratheon
bio: Ellyn Baratheon was a noblewoman from House Baratheon during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen and Aegon_III_Targaryen. She was one of the four daughters of Lord Borros_Baratheon of Storm's End with his wife, Lady Elenda_Caron. Ellyn and her sisters, Cassandra_Baratheon, Maris_Baratheon, and Floris_Baratheon, were known as the Four Storms. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Ellyn is portrayed by Laura Lake. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Prince Aemond_Targaryen arrived at Storm's End. Lord Borros offered the hand of any of his daughters. Prince Aemond agreed to a betrothal, though the identity of the daughter is not known, save it was not Maris. The lord and prince were haggling over dates and dowries the morning Prince Lucerys_Velaryon arrived, who claimed he could not marry Ellyn or any of her sisters due to his betrothal to Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon), and was sent away by Lord Borros. Lady Elenda sent Cassandra, Ellyn, and Floris to represent House Baratheon at the coronation of Aegon_III_Targaryen and his wedding to Princess Jaehaera_Targaryen. Ellyn remained at court as a companion of Queen Jaehaera_Targaryen while her mother departed the capital. After Queen Jaehaera died, Lady Elenda put forward Ellyn and Cassandra as potential brides for Aegon III. She attended the Maiden's Day Ball and Ellyn asked the king if he liked her gown. Her sister later put it about that her question was if he liked her breasts.

name: Ellyn Caron
bio: Ellyn Caron was the Lady of the Marches, Lady of Nightsong, and the head of House Caron during the early reign of Aenys_I_Targaryen. When the Vulture_King_(Aenys_I) raided the Dornish Marches in the Second Dornish War, he led half of his host against Nightsong and Horn Hill. The outlaw "king" was unable to capture Nightsong and abandoned the siege, marching his army west. However, Lady Ellyn sallied forth behind him and joined her strength with a strong force led by Lord Harmon_Dondarrion. Meanwhile, Lord Samwell_Tarly_(lord) joined the battle athwart the Dornish line of march. Although the Vulture King's army was twice the size of his three foes combined, his host shattered when surrounded by armored knights. Afterward, Ellyn was one of the many nobles rewarded by a grateful King Aenys_I_Targaryen with gold, offices, and honors.

name: Ellyn Ever Sweet
bio: Ellyn Ever Sweet is a legendary daughter of Garth_Greenhand and mother of House Beesbury. According to legend, Ellyn loved honey so much that she sought out the King of the Bees in his vast mountain hive and made a pact with him, to care for his children and his children's children for all time, becoming the first beekeeper.

name: Ellyn Reyne
bio: Ellyn Reyne, also known as Ellyn Tarbeck, was a noblewoman of House Reyne of Castamere, who married into House Lannister and later House Tarbeck. She was the daughter of Lord Robert_Reyne and the sister of Lord Roger "The Red Lion" Reyne and Ser Reynard_Reyne. Ellyn was described as a spirited, strong-willed, hot-tempered, and ambitious woman, who dreamed of not only marrying into the wealthy House Lannister, but of also making herself the Lady of Casterly Rock. Due to House Reyne's position and wealth, Lord Robert_Reyne had little trouble arranging his daughter's betrothal to his squire, Tywald_Lannister, the heir of Gerold_Lannister, Lord of Casterly Rock. However, Ellyn's betrothed and father were slain during the Peake Uprising in 233 AC. Ellyn, unwilling to give up her dream, seduced Tywald's younger twin brother and the new heir, Tion_Lannister, persuading him to give up his betrothal to a daughter of Lord Rowan. Gerold, having witnessed how quickly Ellyn acted after the deaths of Robert and Tywald, opposed this match, but the ailing lord eventually let his son follow his heart. According to a semi-canon source, this was after Tion revealed that Tywald's last words were begging him to "take care of Lady Ellyn".

name: Elmar Frey
bio: Elmar Frey is a member of House Frey and is the youngest son of Lord Walder_Frey. Elmar is the twenty-second-born son of Walder and the fourth son born of Walder's marriage to Annara_Farring. There is a rumor in the Twins that Lady Annara_Farring slept with Walder_Frey_(son_of_Ryman), a great-grandson of Lord Walder_Frey, casting doubts on Elmar's parentage.

name: Elmo Tully
bio: Elmo Tully was the Lord of Riverrun and the head of House Tully during the Dance of the Dragons, briefly succeeding his grandfather, Lord Grover_Tully, before passing away forty-nine days later. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Elmo and Kermit_Tully are merged with Oscar_Tully, who is portrayed by Archie Barnes. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Grover_Tully wanted to support King Aegon_II_Targaryen and the greens, but no word of support left Riverrun. Fearing the wroth of the dragons that both the greens and blacks held, Ser Elmo kept the Tully banners at Riverrun. While Grover was furious with his grandson, he was too old and bedridden to oppose him. The Tullys finally joined the war in support of Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen, after Ser Addam_Velaryon visited Riverrun atop Seasmoke, much to Lord Grover's wroth. Joining Ser Addam's army, Elmo led the riverlords to victory at the Second Battle of Tumbleton. Grover passed away soon after the battle, with Elmo succeeding his grandfather as Lord of Riverrun. As the host of Lord Borros_Baratheon began attacking the late Rhaenyra's loyalists around King's Landing, Elmo summoned the riverlords to war and marched a host of six thousand men towards the capital. While marching to King's Landing, Elmo died from drinking bad water, only forty-nine days after succeeding his grandfather. He was succeeded by his eldest son, Ser Kermit_Tully, who led the riverlords to victory against the Baratheon host.

name: Elric Stark
bio: Elric Stark was a member of House Stark. He was the third son of Bennard_Stark and Lady Margaret_Karstark. Elric was imprisoned in 126 AC with his father and brothers by his cousin Lord Cregan_Stark.

name: Elron
bio: Elron is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Elston Tully
bio: Elston Tully was the Lord of Riverrun and the head of House Tully during the reign of King Humfrey_I_Teague. When King Humfrey attempted to suppress the worship of the old gods, Lord Elston rose in rebellion along with Lord Roderick_Blackwood. He did not survive to see the end of the rebellion and the extinction of House Teague.

name: Elwood
bio: Elwood is a guard at Riverrun.

name: Elwood Blackbar
bio: Elwood Blackbar was a knight of House Blackbar during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Elwood attended the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Elwood Harte
bio: Elwood Harte is a knight from House Harte.

name: Elwood Meadows
bio: Elwood Meadows is the Lord of Grassfield Keep and the head of House Meadows. He is a cousin of the Fossoways.

name: Elyana Vypren
bio: Elyana Vypren is a member of House Vypren, a daughter of Lord Lucias_Vypren. She is married to Ser Jon_Wylde and has one child, Rickard_Wylde.

name: Elyas Willum
bio: Elyas Willum is the son of Lord_Willum. He has at least one brother, Josua_Willum.

name: Elyas Scales
bio: Elyas Scales was a knight from House Scales in service to House Targaryen. He was the master-at-arms at Dragonstone during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. When young King Jaehaerys was on Dragonstone during his regency, he spent every morning until midday in the castle yard training with sword, shield, and any other armament. Ser Elyas, along with Ser Merrell_Bullock the commander of the castle garrison, Ser Merrell's Alyn_Bullock Howard_Bullock, and knights of the Kingsguard, would spar with the king.

name: Elyn Norridge
bio: Elyn Norridge is a member of House Norridge and the wife of Ser Luthor_Tyrell_(son_of_Moryn). They have three children, Ser Theodore_Tyrell, Olene_Tyrell, and Medwick_Tyrell.

name: Elys Arryn
bio: Elys Arryn was a noblewoman from House Arryn during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Elys was the eldest child of Lord Rodrik_Arryn and his first wife. She had three siblings: a sister, Amanda_Arryn and two brothers. After her mother died, her father married again to Princess Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I), who was three years younger than her. The two of them clashed from the first. Queen Alysanne_Targaryen visited the Eyrie when Princess Daella was pregnant. Elys was dismissive, telling the queen, "You would think she was the first woman ever to have a baby," for the princess burst into tears for the slightest reason, and sometimes no reason at all. Daella died in childbirth, giving Elys a younger half-sister, Aemma_Arryn.

name: Elys Waynwood
bio: Elys Waynwood was a knight from House Waynwood. He was the uncle of the current head of the House, Lady Anya_Waynwood. Ser Elys wed Alys_Arryn, the sister of Lord Jon_Arryn. They had nine children together, one son and eight daughters. Of their nine children, Jasper_Waynwood died at three years old, kicked in the head by a horse. The eldest daughter married Ser Denys_Arryn but died soon after he did. Two daughters died while children from the pox, and another was left scarred by the same disease and became a septa. Another daughter was seduced by a sellsword, but after her bastard died in infancy, she joined the Silent Sisters. One daughter married the Lord of the Paps, but was barren. One daughter was carried off by Burned Men on her way to marry a Bracken. The youngest daughter married a landed knight of House Hardyng and died after giving him a son, Harrold_Hardyng.

name: Elys Westerling
bio: Elys Westerling was a knight from House Westerling during the reign of Aerys_II_Targaryen. His relation to Gawen_Westerling, the Lord of the Crag, is unknown. Elys participated in the Sack of King's Landing. He and Lord Roland_Crakehall_(Lord) were the first into the throne room where Ser Jaime_Lannister slew King Aerys_II_Targaryen.

name: Elysar
bio: Elysar was a maester of the Citadel who served as Grand Maester during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. After the Shivers ended in 60 AC, the conclave elected Elysar as Grand Maester to succeed Benifer who had passed away from the disease the previous year. Elysar was twenty years younger than Benifer had been when he first took the office. He was known to be very opinionated, with some suspecting the Citadel named him to the office to be rid of him from Oldtown. Elysar and Septon Barth were present when King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen confronted their daughter, Princess Saera_Targaryen, about her indiscretions. Elysar passed away in 97 AC and was succeeded by Allar.

name: Elza
bio: Elza (or Ella) was a very fine weaver slave from Meereen owned by Grazdan_zo_Galare. When Elza began to get old, Grazdan_zo_Galare purchased half a dozen young girls and commanded Elza to instruct them in the secrets of her craft. An unknown amount of time later, Elza died.

name: Emberlei Frey
bio: Emberlei Frey is a noblewoman of House Frey, the third daughter of Lothar_Frey and Leonella_Lefford.

name: Emerick Peake
bio: Emerick Peake, known as Emerick the Avenger, was a Lord of Starpike. He was an illustrious and legendary ancestor of House Peake.

name: Emma
bio: Emma is one of the oldest servers at the Quill and Tankard, a drinking tavern in Oldtown. She has a daughter, Rosey. Emma has determined that the maidenhead of her fifteen-year old daughter Rosey will cost a golden dragon.

name: Emmett
bio: Emmett, called Iron Emmett, is a young ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Emmon Cuy
bio: Emmon Cuy, also known as Emmon the Yellow, is a knight of House Cuy.

name: Emmon Frey
bio: Emmon Frey is a knight from House Frey and is the second son of Lord Walder_Frey and Lady Perra_Royce. He is married to Lady Genna_Lannister, with whom he has four sons: Cleos_Frey, Lyonel_Frey, Tion_Frey, and Walder_Frey_(son_of_Emmon). Emmon Frey is the second son of Walder_Frey, Lord of the Crossing, and his first wife, Lady Perra_Royce. Sensing a weakness and a desire for approval in Lord Tytos_Lannister, Lord Walder traveled to Casterly Rock and convinced him to betroth his only daughter, Lady Genna_Lannister, to his second son, Emmon. A third son himself, Lord Tytos agreed and announced the betrothal at a feast, scandalizing the west and outraging Lord Roger_Reyne and Genna's brother, the ten-year-old Tywin_Lannister. While Lord Reyne stormed out from the feast and Lady Ellyn Tarbeck laughed, Tywin denounced the marriage in the strongest terms in front of half the nobility of the west, but still the betrothal remained. Emmon has since been fearful of Tywin, now the Lord of Casterly Rock and Warden of the West. Following their marriage, Emmon remained at Casterly Rock with his wife and children. At many a feast, Emmon would nervously poke at his food while his wife jested with whatever household knight happened to be seated to her left. The marriage resulted in four sons, Cleos_Frey, Lyonel_Frey, Tion_Frey, and Walder_Frey_(son_of_Emmon). Genna claims that all four boys are Emmon's and none dare to question her, least of all Emmon.

name: Emmond
bio: Emmond is a boy of the Iron Islands whom Aeron_Greyjoy drowns and brings back to life.

name: Emory Hill
bio: Emory Hill,[N 1] called the Bastard of Lannisport, was a knight from the westerlands, likely a bastard of the Lannisters of Lannisport. Emory joined Lord Jason_Lannister's host during the Dance of the Dragons, in support of King Aegon_II_Targaryen and the greens. Emory was among the notable casualties during the Battle by the Lakeshore in 130 AC, where the westermen host was defeated by the riverlords.

name: Emphyria Vance
bio: Emphyria Vance is a noblewoman of House Vance of Wayfarer's Rest. She is the third and youngest daughter of the heir of Wayfarer's Rest, Ser Karyl_Vance.

name: Emrick
bio: Emrick is a Night's Watch recruit. He is the twin brother of Arron, and is from Fair Isle.

name: Endehar
bio: Endehar is an ironborn raider.

name: Endrew Tarth
bio: Endrew Tarth is a knight from House Tarth and member of the Night's Watch. His relation to Lord Selwyn_Tarth of Evenfall is unknown. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Endrew is portrayed by Dez McMahon.

name: Enger
bio: Enger is on duty at the River Gate when Tyrion_Lannister's plot to break Ser Jaime_Lannister free is carried out.

name: Eon
bio: Maester Eon is a maester of the Citadel of Oldtown. He wrote Account of the War of the Ninepenny Kings, filled with details on the fighting that occured. The book is considered one of the finest works of its kind.

name: Eon Hunter
bio: Eon Hunter is the Lord of Longbow Hall and the head of House Hunter. He has three quarrelsome sons: Gilwood_Hunter, Eustace_Hunter and Harlan_Hunter. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Eon is portrayed by Barrington Cullen. Lord Eon attended the tourney at Harrenhal and was talking with Lords Robert_I_Baratheon and Jon_Arryn when Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen gave Lyanna_Stark a crown of flowers as the queen of love and beauty.

name: Erena Glover
bio: Erena Glover is the daughter of Robett_Glover and Sybelle_Glover.

name: Erich Durrandon
bio: Erich Durrandon, also known as Erich Kin-Killer, was a member of House Durrandon. Erich slew his own brother, King Durran. Scholars debate whether this King Durran, who had become besotted with Erich's daughter, was the same as King Durran_Durrandon_(the_Young).

name: Erich III Durrandon
bio: Erich Durrandon, also known as Erich the Sailmaker, was a Storm King of House Durrandon. Erich was the first to claim Estermont and the lesser isles farther south for the Kingdom of the Storm. Scholars speculate he was most likely Erich III. He was the grandson of King Durran_Durrandon_(the_Fair).

name: Erich VII Durrandon
bio: Erich VII Durrandon, also known as Erich the Unready, was a Storm King of House Durrandon during the beginnings of the Andal invasion. Erich took little note of the invaders, declaring he had no interest in "the quarrels of strangers in a land far away", as he was embroiled in his own war to reconquer Massey's Hook from pirate king Justin_Massey_(Milk-Eye) and whilst fending off incursions of King Olyvar_Yronwood. He died while the Andals conquered the Vale. His grandson was Qarlton_II_Durrandon.

name: Erich Hoare
bio: Erich Hoare, known as Erich the Eagle, was an ironborn raider from House Hoare. He was a grandson of Harrag_Hoare, a King of the Iron Islands. Erich led an attack against the western shores of the north, which had been liberated from occupation by King Theon_Stark.

name: Erich I Greyiron
bio: Erich I Greyiron, known as Erich the Ugly, was a High King of the Iron Islands from House Greyiron. He was the son of Urras_Greyiron, the first High King. After the death of his father, Urras_Greyiron, Erich seized his driftwood crown and proclaimed himself High King of the Iron Islands. However, the priest Galon_Whitestaff, despite being half-blind and feeble, arose in fury and declared Erich an unlawful king, as he had not been chosen at a kingsmoot. He assembled all ironborn "captains and kings" to Old Wyk and Erich was quickly unmade and condemned to death. He avoided this fate by casting his father's crown to the sea, a sign of his submission to the Drowned God. In his place, the kingsmoot raised Regnar_Drumm.

name: Erich V Harlaw
bio: Erich V Harlaw was a High King of the Iron Islands from House Harlaw. Erich V was chosen at a kingsmoot. He retook Fair Isle in his youth, only to lose it again in his old age.

name: Erik Ironmaker
bio: Erik Ironmaker, known as Erik the Just and Erik Anvil-Breaker, is an ironborn raider and the head of House Ironmaker. In his youth, Erik was a fearsome reaver who sailed with Lord Dagon_Greyjoy.

name: Ermesande Hayford
bio: Ermesande Hayford is the Lady of Hayford and the head of House Hayford, a noble house of the crownlands. She is still a baby and is the last of her line.

name: Ernest Dabell
bio: Ser Ernest Dabell was a knight during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He had two brothers: Ser Lester_Dabell and Ser Pascal_Dabell. He participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Eroeh
bio: Eroeh is a Lhazareen girl who lives in Lhazar.

name: Erreck
bio: Maester Erreck is the bastard son of a hedge knight. He is considered to have considerable ability as a maester.

name: Erreg
bio: Erreg the Kinslayer was a legendary Andal king. According to song, King Erreg attacked High Heart, a place sacred to the children of the forest of the riverlands and protected by First Men river kings. Erreg's men killed the children and First Men, and then cut down the hill's weirwood grove. It is believed by smallfolk that High Heart is still haunted by the dead children. In contrast to the legend, True History insists that the children of the forest had already abandoned the riverlands before the Andal invasion. Archmaester Perestan suggests in A Consideration of History that "Erreg" was not a given name but corruption of an Andal title. Perestan suggests this unnamed chieftain who attacked High Heart did so on behalf of a river king's rival.

name: Erren Florent
bio: Erren Florent is knight of House Florent, the son of Ser Ryam_Florent and brother to Selyse_Florent and Imry_Florent. Erren is brother-in-law to Stannis_Baratheon. In A Clash of Kings, Davos II, Erren's name is erroneously written as Errol.

name: Errok
bio: Errok is a thin man. He is quiet. Errok is part of Jarl's group of raiders who climbs the Wall to take Castle Black from behind. He leads a group of four raiders to the top of Wall. After Jarl's death, Errok follows Styr's commands. Together with Del, he goes ahead to scout the ruins of Queenscrown. When he returns to the other raiders, he reports that there's an old man with a horse present in the town.

name: Errold Stark
bio: Errold Stark was a member of House Stark. He was the fourth son of Lord Beron_Stark and wife, Lady Lorra_Royce. He had three older brothers (Donnor_Stark, Willam_Stark and Artos_Stark), two older sisters (Berena_Stark and Alysanne_Stark), and one younger brother (Rodrik_Stark_(son_of_Beron)).

name: Erryk
bio: Erryk is one of the two personal guards of Olenna Tyrell, the dowager Lady of Highgarden. He is the twin of Arryk_(guard), her other guard. Lady Olenna cannot tell them apart, so she calls them Left and Right.

name: Erryk Cargyll
bio: Erryk Cargyll was a knight from House Cargyll. He and his twin brother, Ser Arryk_Cargyll, were members of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. During the Dance of the Dragons, Erryk sided with Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen, while his brother Arryk sided with King Aegon_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Erryk is portrayed by Elliott Tittensor. Ser Erryk and Ser Arryk_Cargyll, knights of the Kingsguard, were both unhorsed by Ser Criston_Cole at the tourney for King Viserys I's accession at Maidenpool in 105 AC. In 120 AC, King Viserys_I_Targaryen made Erryk the sworn shield of Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, replacing Ser Harwin_Strong.

name: Erwin Lannister
bio: Erwin Lannister was a knight from House Lannister during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. During Lady Johanna_Lannister's war against the ironborn, she hoped to retake Fair Isle from the Dalton_Greyjoy, and Ser Erwin was given command of a ragtag fleet of fishing boats and cogs at Feastfires, along with Lord Tarbeck and Lord Prester. A hundred knights and three thousand men-at-arms were at their command, but someone had betrayed them, and the longships were waiting. Ser Erwin's head was sent back to Casterly Rock afterward, with Lord Greyjoy calling it "payment for his uncle."

name: Esgred
bio: Esgred is the mother of Sigrin, a shipwright at Lordsport. Sigrin named Esgred, the first ship that he built, after his mother.

name: Essie
bio: Essie was a whore working at the House of Kisses in King's Landing, and the mother of Gaemon_Palehair. During the Dance of the Dragons, Essie claimed her four-year old son, Gaemon, was a bastard son of King Aegon_II_Targaryen. This led to the boy being installed as a King in the House of Kisses after the flight of Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen, during the Moon of the Three Kings. From the brothel, the boy issued a series of edicts, while the whores offered their favors to any men who swore allegiance to Gaemon. When King's Landing was taken back in the name of King Aegon II, Essie was arrested, and confessed under torture that Gaemon was actually the son of a silver-haired oarsman from Lys. She was hanged for treason. According to Mushroom, Essie had a paramour, Sylvenna_Sand, who was also a whore at the House of Kisses. She was hanged alongside Essie.

name: Estermont (cousin of Robert)
bio: The Estermont female cousin of King Robert_I_Baratheon is a noblewoman of House Estermont. In her childhood, she played with the young Robert_I_Baratheon. Both her husband and father died at Storm's End when the castle was besieged during the rebellion that made her cousin king. In the beginning of his reign, Robert stayed at Greenstone for a fortnight. During his visit, he consoled the young widow and bedded her.

name: Ethan Glover
bio: Ethan Glover was a member of House Glover during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. He was the squire for Brandon_Stark, the heir of Winterfell. When Brandon_Stark learned of his sister Lyanna_Stark abduction at the hands of Rhaegar_Targaryen, Prince of Dragonstone, he took off for King's Landing with Jeffory_Mallister, Kyle_Royce, Elbert_Arryn, and his squire, Ethan. When Brandon entered the Red Keep and demanded that Rhaegar come out and die, King Aerys_II_Targaryen had Brandon's party arrested for threatening the life of the crown prince. The fathers of Brandon and his companions were called to King's Landing to answer for the crimes of their sons. The king had the fathers and sons all executed, with only Ethan spared. Aerys then demanded that Jon_Arryn, Lord of the Eyrie, execute his wards, Robert_I_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End, and Brandon's younger brother Eddard_Stark, now Lord of Winterfell. However, Jon refused the king's command, and Robert's Rebellion started shortly thereafter. After Aerys's death in the Sack of King's Landing, Eddard lifted the siege of Storm's End. Lord Stark then rode to the Red Mountains with Ethan, Martyn_Cassel, Theo_Wull, Howland_Reed, Ser Mark_Ryswell, and Willam_Dustin, Lord of Barrowton. At the tower of joy, they were confronted by the last three members of Aerys's Kingsguard, Ser Arthur_Dayne, Ser Oswell_Whent, and Lord Commander Gerold_Hightower. All but Eddard and Howland were slain in the ensuing fight. Afterwards, Eddard had the tower pulled down, and used its stones for cairns for the fallen.

name: Eunuch strangler
bio: A eunuch strangler is one of the foul-looking, impotent mercenaries that Varys gets for Tyrion_Lannister to guard Shae's manse in King's Landing.

name: Euron Greyjoy
bio: Euron Greyjoy, known as Crow's Eye and Euron Crow's Eye, is a member of House Greyjoy and is the eldest of Lord Balon_Greyjoy's younger brothers. Euron is captain of the Silence, a ship crewed entirely by mutes whose tongues he ripped out. The personal coat of arms of the Crow's Eye is a red eye with a black pupil beneath a black iron crown supported by two crows. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Euron is portrayed by Pilou Asbæk.

name: Eustace
bio: Eustace is a disgraced and defrocked septon of the Faith of the Seven who used to be attached to the Sept-Beyond-the-Sea in Braavos.

name: Eustace
bio: Eustace was a septon attached to the royal sept of the Red Keep during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. The writings of Eustace, among them The Reign of King Viserys, First of His Name, and the Dance of the Dragons That Came After, were used as a source for The Princess and the Queen as well as The Rogue Prince by Archmaester Gyldayn. Eventually, he rose to the ranks of the Most Devout.

name: Eustace Brune
bio: Eustace Brune is the Lord of the Dyre Den and the head of House Brune of the Dyre Den.

name: Eustace Hightower
bio: Eustace Hightower was a knight from House Hightower during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He was a grandson of Lord Donnel_Hightower and an accomplished mariner. In 56 AC, Eustace and his brother, Ser Norman_Hightower, accompanied Lady Elissa_Farman, under the alias of Alys Westhill, on her voyage to discover new lands. Lord Donnel_Hightower received a raven from the Iron Throne that Elissa was to be arrested and brought back to King's Landing in chains, but by the time Donnel acted, Eustace and his brother had already set sail from Oldtown. After twelve days of sailing west and south of Westeros, the expedition began to suffer fierce storms. Some sailors on Eustace's ship, Lady Meredith, claimed that Norman's Autumn Moon was brought down by a kraken during one such storm. Elissa's Sun Chaser escorted Lady Meredith to three small uninhabited islands, Aegon, Rhaenys, and Visenya. Eustace turned east for home, while Elissa continued west for the unknown. The hull of Lady Meredith was cracked by a leviathan during their return, so Eustace set sail for the Summer Isles but landed on Sothoryos. The knight spent a year repairing his ship on the continent, all the while his crew dwindled from diseases or unexplained disappearances. Hiring a new crew of Summer Islanders at high wages, Eustace eventually sailed Lady Meredith to Tall Trees Town. Nearly three years after departing, Eustace returned to Oldtown on Lady Meredith, gaunt and sunburnt but with very valuable cargo. Eustace's wife, children, and grandfather perished from the winter disease known as the Shivers in 59 AC.

name: Eustace Hunter
bio: Eustace Hunter is a knight from House Hunter. He is the second son of Lord Eon_Hunter.

name: Eustace Osgrey
bio: Eustace Osgrey was the knight of Standfast and the head of House Osgrey during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Eustace will be portrayed by Peter Mullan.

name: Evlyn
bio: Maester Evlyn is a maester of the Citadel. He opposes Archmaester Harmune_(archmaester)'s belief that the Andals carved axes as a symbol of the Faith of the Seven in addition to seven-pointed stars, only for the latter to gain greater devotion whereas the former fell by the wayside. Evlyn argues that what Harmune calls axes were in fact hammers, the sign of the Smith, explaining the irregularity of the depictions of these hammers as the results of the Andals being warriors, not artisans.

name: Eyron
bio: Eyron is an archmaester of the Citadel. He wrote that the crannogmen of old believed their Marsh Kings were the first among equals and that they were influenced by the old gods.

name: Eyron Stark
bio: Eyron Stark was King in the North. He is buried in the crypts beneath Winterfell.

name: Ezzara
bio: Ezzara is a Ghiscari priestess of Meereen. She is a Blue Grace.

name: Ezzelyno
bio: Ezzelyno is a red priest of R'hllor in Braavos, who is oft drunk, yet cheerful.

name: Faezhar zo Faez
bio: Faezhar zo Faez is a Yunkai'i nobleman. The men of the Windblown mock him as the Rabbit.

name: Falena Stokeworth
bio: Falena Stokeworth was a noblewoman of House Stokeworth during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen, Aegon_III_Targaryen, Daeron_I_Targaryen, Baelor_I_Targaryen, Viserys_II_Targaryen and Aegon_IV_Targaryen. Falena came to court during the early years of the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen, during which time she would sometimes play at dolls with young Queen Jaehaera_Targaryen. Unfortunately, Queen Jaehaera died in 133 AC, allegedly by suicide. Afterwards, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Many maidens across Westeros came to King's Landing in hopes of marrying the young king. Falena was one of the many maidens who suffered what was called the "Maiden's Day curse" by being injured or scandalized after the ball was announced. In Falena's case, she took a tumble down the serpentine steps of the Red Keep and broke a leg. According to Archmaester Gyldayn, Lord Unwin_Peake, the Hand of the King, may have engineered the fall to promote his Myrielle_Peake as the king's second wife.

name: Falia Flowers
bio: Falia Flowers is the bastard daughter of Lord Humfrey_Hewett. Falia is made to be a servant in Lord Hewett's castle by her trueborn sisters and her step-mother.

name: Falyse Stokeworth
bio: Lady Falyse Stokeworth is the heir of Lady Tanda_Stokeworth of Stokeworth. She has been married to Ser Balman_Byrch for ten years, but they remain childless.

name: Farlen
bio: Farlen is the kennelmaster at Winterfell. His daughter is Palla. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Farlen is portrayed by Peter Balance. The kennelmaster once told Sansa_Stark that an animal takes after its master. Theon_Greyjoy learned all he knows about hounds from Farlen.

name: Lord Farman (father of Lysa)
bio: Lord Farman was the Lord of Fair Isle and the head of House Farman during the Dance of the Dragons. His given name is unknown. During the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Dalton_Greyjoy landed longships on Fair Isle. Lord Farman was unable to repel the ironborn attack and retreated to his castle, Faircastle, where he sent out pleas for help. No one came, however, and Faircastle eventually fell to the ironborn in 130 AC. The Red Kraken claimed four of Lord Farman's daughters as salt wives, including Lysa_Farman, while giving the fifth "homely one" to his brother, Veron_Greyjoy. Lord Farman and his sons were ransomed back to Casterly Rock for their weights in silver. After the fall of Faircastle, Lord Dalton took Lord Farman's bedchamber as his own. By 133 AC, Lord Farman and two of his daughters had perished.

name: Fat fellow
bio: The fat fellow is a secret servant of the Many-Faced God in the House of Black and White in Braavos and a Faceless Man.

name: Fearless Ithoke
bio: Fearless Ithoke is a pit fighter famed in Meereen. His origin is unknown, but he supports the pit fighting culture of the city.

name: Lady Fell
bio: Lady Fell was the Lady of Felwood and the head of House Fell during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. Her given name is unknown. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, she is portrayed by Miriam Lucia. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Lady Fell was arrested and put in the Red Keep's dungeons by Aegon_II_Targaryen's supporters for supporting his half-sister, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen. When given the choice between swearing her allegiance to Aegon II or being killed by the King's Justice, Lady Fell was one of the nobles who chose to keep their sworn words and were beheaded for it. Their heads were mounted on spikes above the city’s gates.

name: Lord Fell
bio: Lord Fell was the Lord of Felwood and the head of House Fell during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen, and the early stages of Robert's Rebellion. His given name is not yet known. When Lord Robert_I_Baratheon returned to Storm's End after the taking of Gulltown and called his banners to rebel against King Aerys_II_Targaryen, Lord Fell remained loyal to the Iron Throne and marched against his liege lord. He was to be joined by Lord_Cafferen and Lord_Grandison at Summerhall, but Robert, having caught wind of their plan, marched to meet them separately. In one of the ensuing battles at Summerhall, Robert slew Lord Fell in single combat and took his son Silveraxe_Fell as prisoner.

name: Silveraxe Fell
bio: Silveraxe was the nickname of a famous member of House Fell during Robert's Rebellion. His given name is not yet known. Silveraxe participated in the battles at Summerhall as a Targaryen loyalist against Lord Robert_I_Baratheon. Robert slew Silveraxe's father, Lord_Fell, in single combat and captured Silveraxe in the battle. It is unknown if Silveraxe was the heir to Felwood. Following Summerhall, Robert won Silveraxe to his cause.

name: Fergon Hoare
bio: Fergon Hoare, known as Fergon the Fierce, was a King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. The Hoare kings of his era were opposed by the priests of the Drowned God because of their support for the Faith of the Seven, discouraging of reaving, and promotion of trade.

name: Fern
bio: Fern is a peasant who lives in Lambswold in the riverlands.

name: Ferny
bio: Ferny is a wildling and one of the many wives of Craster.

name: Ferrego Antaryon
bio: Ferrego Antaryon is a member of House Antaryon who serves as the current Sealord of Braavos.

name: Ferret
bio: Ferret was a street child from Flea Bottom in King's Landing during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. When Ser Duncan the Tall was an orphan child in Flea Bottom, Ferret was one of his friends, along with Rafe and Pudding. They once stole a rotting head from a spike atop one of the walls of King's Landing. Ferret climbed the wall to grab the head, after Rafe and Pudding dared him, but when the guards came, he threw the head down and Dunk caught it. The four friends used the head to terrorize the girls of Flea Bottom, chasing them through the alleys and making them give the head a kiss before they let them go. Dunk and his friends kept the head until it turned black and the flesh started falling off, which made it less fun to chase girls. One night, they broke into a pot shop and threw what was left of the head into the kettle. In 212 AC, Duncan saw the decapitated head of the Hunchbacked_septon on the walls of Stoney Sept, and was reminded of his childhood friends' adventures with the head in King's Landing.

name: Ferris Fowler
bio: Ferris Fowler was a King of Stone and Sky from House Fowler in Dorne. King Ferris invaded the Reach with ten thousand men through the Wide Way, but he was turned back by Garth_VII_Gardener, King of the Reach.

name: Fingers
bio: Fingers is an ironman and one of the crew on Asha_Greyjoy's ship, Black Wind.

name: First King
bio: The First King was a sovereign who once reigned supreme over all First Men and led them to Westeros, according to legends from the north. His given name is unknown. It is unclear if he is related to Garth_Greenhand. Lord Dustin showed the barrow of the First King to King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen in 58 AC.

name: Flement Brax
bio: Flement Brax is a knight from House Brax and the third son of Lord Andros_Brax of Hornvale. He is married to Morya_Frey and they have three sons together: Robert_Brax_(son_of_Flement), Walder_Brax and Jon_Brax.

name: Florence Fossoway
bio: Florence Fossoway was a noblewoman of House Fossoway during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. She was married to Martyn_Tyrell. Florence married young Martyn_Tyrell, a younger son of Lord Bertrand_Tyrell. After her marriage, Florence took charge of the accounts of Highgarden and increased the incomes of House Tyrell by over a third. When a new master of coin was needed by King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, his Hand of the King, Septon Barth, put forward Martyn's name. In truth, he intended that Florence would perform the duties of the office, as she would be brought to court with her husband. The king also hoped that Lady Florence's presence at court would be welcomed by Queen Alysanne_Targaryen, who had not been attending council sessions since the death of Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I). Although her husband officially sat on the small council and was given the title master of coin, Florence was effectively in charge of the treasury. Under Lady Florence and her lord husband, the realm saw prosperity, and the couple was nearly as effective as the late Rego_Draz.

name: Lord Florent
bio: Lord Florent was the Lord of Brightwater Keep and the head of House Florent during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Ser Arlan_of_Pennytree and his squire, Duncan_the_Tall, spent half of the year 206 AC in Lord Florent's service.

name: Florian the Fool
bio: Florian, called Florian the Fool, is a legendary hero of the riverlands from the Age of Heroes. He is known for falling in love with a maiden named Jonquil. Singers compared the sudden marriage of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen to the great romance of Florian and Jonquil. During the tourney at Ashford Meadow, Ser Duncan_the_Tall observed Tanselle performing a puppet show of Florian and Jonquil. While playing as children at Winterfell, Jon_Snow and Robb_Stark would pretend to be Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II) and Florian the Fool. Songs about Florian and Jonquil are favorites of Sansa_Stark. Brienne of Tarth learned about Florian and other heroes from her nurse.

name: Florian Greysteel
bio: Florian Greysteel was a knight and the personal champion of Manfryd_Mooton, Lord of Maidenpool, during the Dance of the Dragons. Lord Manfryd received a raven from Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen ordering him to execute Nettles. As she was his guest, Lord Manfryd was dubious of what to do. He assembled his Maester, Norren_(House_Mooton), his brother, his captain of the guard and his champion, Ser Florian, and listened to their advices. Ser Florian was shocked the queen would demand him to do such unhonorable act.

name: Florian V Mooton
bio: Florian V Mooton, known as Florian the Brave, was a petty king of Maidenpool from House Mooton. The boy king Florian was killed in the Fall of Maidenpool during the Andal invasion.

name: Floris Baratheon
bio: Floris Baratheon was a noblewoman from House Baratheon during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen and Aegon_III_Targaryen. She was one of the four daughters of Lord Borros_Baratheon of Storm's End with his wife, Lady Elenda_Caron. Floris and her sisters, Cassandra_Baratheon, Maris_Baratheon, and Ellyn_Baratheon, were known as the Four Storms. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Floris is portrayed by Megan Barter. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Prince Aemond_Targaryen arrived at Storm's End. Lord Borros offered the hand of any of his daughters, noting that Floris was the prettiest. Prince Aemond agreed to a betrothal, though the identity of the daughter is not known, save it was not Maris. The lord and prince were haggling over dates and dowries the morning Prince Lucerys_Velaryon arrived, who claimed he could not marry Floris or any of her sisters due to his betrothal to Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon), and was sent away by Lord Borros. Later, in return for the support of Ser Perkin_the_Flea and the stormlords in restoring King Aegon_II_Targaryen to the Iron Throne, Floris was betrothed to Lord Larys_Strong of Harrenhal. However, Lord Strong was instead executed by Lord Cregan_Stark at the end of the war. Lady Elenda sent Cassandra, Ellyn, and Floris to represent House Baratheon at the coronation of Aegon_III_Targaryen and his wedding to Princess Jaehaera_Targaryen. After Lady Baela_Targaryen rebuffed Lord Thaddeus_Rowan's proposal of marriage, he was betrothed to the fourteen-year-old Floris in 132 AC, appeasing his wounded pride. She died two years later, in 134 AC or 135 AC, during childbirth, leaving her husband in a black state.

name: Florys the Fox
bio: Florys the Fox is a legendary daughter of Garth_Greenhand and the cleverest of his children. According to myth, she kept three husbands, each ignorant of the existence of the other two. Her sons became the founders of House Florent, House Ball and House Peake. In some tales of the Reach, Lann_the_Clever was a bastard born to Florys or her sister, Rowan_Gold-Tree.

name: Fogo
bio: Fogo is the khalakka, or son and heir of Khal Ogo.

name: Lord Follard
bio: Lord Follard was the head of House Follard during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Lord Follard was one of the six lords who accompanied Lord Manfryd_Mooton to Braavos in 133 AC. As the Crown's envoy, Manfryd was tasked to make peace with the Sealord of Braavos after the recent conflict between the Free City and Lord Alyn_Velaryon. While Manfryd treated with the Sealord, Lord Follard spread bribes among the Braavosi keyholders, magisters, and other notables. Rather than return to Westeros, he chose to remain in Braavos with a courtesan he had become enamored with.

name: Fomas
bio: Archmaester Fomas was an archmaester of the Citadel. He wrote the book Lies of the Ancients. He argues that the mythic Others were nothing more than a tribe of First Men established on the far north and forced by the Long Night to engage in conquests to the south, becoming monstrous in tales told thereafter by House Stark and the Night's Watch to give themselves a heroic background as saviors of mankind.

name: Lord Footly
bio: Lord Footly was the head of House Footly and the Lord of Tumbleton during the Dance of the Dragons. His given name has not been revealed. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, he is named Glendon Footly and portrayed by Adam Brown. Lord Footly hosted the blacks at Tumbleton during the civil war. When the town was betrayed in the First Battle of Tumbleton, Lord Footly's soldiers surrendered to the victorious greens, but they were instead bound and beheaded. Ser Jon_Roxton became enamored of the lord's beautiful wife, Lady Sharis_Footly, and claimed her as a "prize of war." When her lord husband protested, Bold Jon cut him nigh in two with Orphan-Maker, saying, "She can make widows too," as he tore the gown from the weeping lady. Lord Footly was succeeded by his posthumous son, born six months after the Second Battle of Tumbleton. Though claimed by Lady Sharis as her late lord husband's trueborn heir, it was far more likely that he had been fathered by Bold Jon.

name: Forley Prester
bio: Forley Prester is a knight of House Prester. He is a cousin to Garrison_Prester, Lord of Feastfires.

name: Fornio
bio: Fornio is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Forrest Frey
bio: Forrest Frey, called Fool Frey, was the head of House Frey and the Lord of the Crossing during the Dance of the Dragons. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Forrest is portrayed by Kenneth Collard. In his youth, Forrest had once been a suitor to Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen. When Rhaenyra visited the riverlands in 112 AC, he got dubbed "Fool Frey" for boldly asking openly for the Princess of Dragonstone's hand. He later married Lady Sabitha_Vypren, and became the Lord of the Crossing. Lord Forrest was among the prominent riverlords that sided with Princess Rhaenyra and the blacks during the Dance of the Dragons, despite initial reluctance among the riverlords. Forrest added his strength to Prince Daemon_Targaryen's host and participated in capturing Stone Hedge from Lord Humfrey_Bracken. He brought two hundred knights and six hundred infantry when he joined Lord Roderick_Dustin and Robb_Rivers by the Gods Eye. In the ensuring battle, Forrest died fighting the remains of House Lannister's army from the westerlands in the Battle by the Lakeshore. His young son succeeded him as Lord of the Crossing and his wife, Lady Sabitha, took command of the Frey levies.

name: Foss the Archer
bio: Foss the Archer is a legendary son of Garth_Greenhand and founder of House Fossoway of Cider Hall.

name: Fossoway twins
bio: The Fossoway twins were members of House Fossoway of Cider Hall during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Their given names have not been revealed. In 73 AC, the Fossoway twins entered the list in a tourney at Old Oak hosted by Lord Oakheart in honor of the birth of a son. Both of them were defeated by a mystery knight known as the "Silver Fool", which was later revealed to be Prince Baelon Targaryen.

name: Fralegg
bio: Fralegg the Strong is an ironborn raider and captain.

name: Franklyn Farman
bio: Franklyn Farman was the first-born son and heir of Lord Marq_Farman of Fair Isle. He became the head of House Farman and Lord of Fair Isle during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Franklyn fought with his grandfather and uncle in the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye on the side of Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) in 43 AC. He had been wounded during the battle, while his grandfather and uncle died, leaving Franklyn's father Marq_Farman to inherit Faircastle. Franklyn returned the bodies of his grandfather and uncle to Fair Isle after the battle. Franklyn grew to dislike Queen Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), the widow of Prince Aegon and the new wife to Franklyn's younger brother, Androw_Farman. He believed Rhaena had not shown enough gratitude for all he had suffered whilst fighting for her first husband, nor enough remorse for the pain she had caused House Farman. Additionally, he resented the fact that Rhaena would not encourage his sister Elissa_Farman to take a husband and have children, as well as the fact that the Queen and her companions had become the center of attention at Fair Isle, whilst he and Lord Marq were increasingly disregarded. In 50 AC, when Franklyn inherited Faircastle after his father choked on a fish bone, he summoned Rhaena and informed her that she was no longer welcome on the island. He stated that she was allowed to take her husband and her household with her, but decreed that Elissa was to remain as Fair Isle. Rhaena defied him, however, and Franklyn awaited them at the docks with a dozen of his men to stop them. There, he argued with his sister as the smallfolk of the island looked on. When he attempted to seize his sister, the smallfolk rushed at him and threw him in the hold of a ship's hold full of freshly caught cod. Franklyn continued to deny his sister any part of the income of Fair Isle, and eventually disowned her. In 55 AC Queen Rhaena returned to Fair Isle on her dragon Dreamfyre in search of her missing daughter Aerea_Targaryen. Franklyn received her coldly, and insisted that Aerea was well rid of Rhaena, promising to "see off" the girl if she ever showed up, just as he had seen off Rhaena. To that, Rhaena replied that Balerion, the dragon Aerea had claimed, had once enjoyed eating Franklyn's brother Androw, and suggested that the dragon might "desire another course".

name: Franklyn Flowers
bio: Franklyn Flowers, also known as the Bastard of Cider Hall, is a knight and a captain in service to the Golden Company. Franklyn claims that his mother was a washerwoman at Cider Hall, who was raped by a son of House Fossoway's head. Due to this, Franklyn loathes the Fossoways. Franklyn knows Jon_Connington, and remembers him from when he served with Golden Company twelve years ago. Franklyn was knighted some time after Griff left the company.

name: Franklyn Fowler
bio: Franklyn Fowler, called the Old Hawk, is the Lord of Skyreach and the head of House Fowler. He also holds the title Warden of the Prince's Pass. He is the father of the Fowler twins.

name: Franklyn Frey
bio: Franklyn Frey was a knight from House Frey and the brother to Lord_Frey of the Crossing during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. According to a a semi-canon source, Ser Franklyn participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC. As part of the retinue of his brother, Lord_Frey, Franklyn attended the marriage of his niece to Lord Ambrose_Butterwell at Whitewalls in 212 AC. A drunken Franklyn nearly dropped the bride during the bedding custom. He took part in the wedding tourney at Whitewalls. On the back of his sorrel, Franklyn defeated Ser Harbert_Paege after five tilts. While riding his dapple grey, however, Franklyn was defeated by Ser John the Fiddler in his next joust, and the victorious hedge knight helped Franklyn to his feet. According to a semi-canon source, Franklyn's defeat may have been suspect because of Lord Frey's involvement in the Second Blackfyre Rebellion, which intended for John, actually Daemon_II_Blackfyre, to win the competition and claim a dragon egg. When Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen revealed his identity to Lord Butterwell, Lord Frey departed Whitewalls with his retinue to avoid participating in the faltering rebellion.

name: Fredo Rogare
bio: Fredo Rogare was a member of House Rogare, a Lysene noble banking family. He was the son of Lysandro_Rogare, the First Magister of Lys. Fredo was the son of Lysandro Rogare. He had five trueborn brothers, Lysaro_Rogare, Moredo_Rogare, Lotho_Rogare, Drako_Rogare, and Roggerio_Rogare, and three trueborn sisters, Larra_Rogare, Lysara_Rogare, and Marra_Rogare as well as sixteen bastard siblings. After the death of his father, Lysandro_Rogare, Fredo inherited his pillow house, the Perfumed Garden. When the Rogare Bank ran dry in 135 AC, Lysaro Rogare's brothers and sisters claimed to have no part in it, but many doubted their claims of innocence, and Fredo was put on trial. The Perfumed Garden was seized, even as Fredo tried to sell it, and his slaves were sold. When that proved insufficient to pay more than a tenth of the debts, the Rogares themselves were sold as slaves, and Fredo's daughters were made bed slaves in the Perfumed Garden. It is written that Fredo availed himself of being able to whip his brother Lysaro when he was chained naked to a pillar before the Temple of Trade.

name: Frenken
bio: Frenken is the maester assigned to House Stokeworth of Stokeworth.

name: Frenya
bio: Frenya is a wildling spearwife.

name: Lady Frey (wife of Ambrose Butterwell)
bio: Lady Butterwell was the second wife of Ambrose_Butterwell, Lord of Whitewalls.
