Originally from House Frey, she was the daughter of Lord_Frey of the Crossing, elder sister to Walder_Frey, niece to Ser Franklyn_Frey, and cousin to Ser Addam_Frey. In 212 AC when she was fifteen, she married Ambrose in the wedding tourney at Whitewalls. It was rumored that Lady Frey was married off after her younger brother, Walder_Frey, caught her having intimate relations with a servant, a scullion at the Twins. The story was told that they were found in the kitchens on the marble slab where dough is rolled out, both naked and covered in flour. Lady Frey wed Lord Ambrose_Butterwell at Whitewalls, and during the feast the couple used a sword to cut open a large wedding pie and release the dozens of birds within it. Ser Duncan_the_Tall was volunteered by Ser John the Fiddler to carry her from the great hall to Ambrose's bedchamber as part of the bedding custom. Men made ribald jokes about "flouring her up and kneading her well" while they disrobed her, and Duncan became attracted to the new bride. During the tourney thrown to celebrate the marriage, Lady Butterwell's hair was limp from perspiration and she appeared uncomfortable and bored. When Duncan, competing as the Gallows Knight, greeted Lord and Lady Butterwell before his joust, the girl pushed out her chest in an attempt to impress the mystery knight. When her cousin, Ser Addam_Frey, was unhorsed by Ser Uthor_Underleaf, Lady Butterwell fainted and had to be helped back to her new chambers. As a result of the failed Second Blackfyre Rebellion, Ambrose was stripped of Whitewalls by Lord Brynden_Rivers, and the Hand of the King allowed Lord Butterwell to keep his new bride and only a tenth of his fortune.

name: Lord Frey
bio: Lord Frey was the Lord of the Crossing and the head of House Frey during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. In 212 AC, Lord Frey came to Whitewalls to attend the wedding of his daughter to Lord Ambrose_Butterwell. His retinue included his four-year-old son and heir Walder_Frey, his married daughters and their husbands, his brother Ser Franklyn_Frey, and his nephew Ser Addam_Frey. It was rumored that Lord Frey married off his fifteen-year-old daughter after Walder caught her having intimate relations with a scullion at the Twins. The marriage was used by House Blackfyre sympathizers as an opportunity to gather those who had fought for the Daemon_I_Blackfyre during his rebellion and those who resented the rule of Hand of the King, Brynden_Rivers. The organizers of the wedding tourney at Whitewalls, mainly Ser Tommard_Heddle and Lord Gormon_Peake, convinced Lord Butterwell to name his dragon egg as the prize for the winner of the tourney. Their plan was for the Black Dragon's eldest living son, Daemon_II_Blackfyre, to win the tourney and claim the egg for himself. Once the egg hatched like Daemon dreamed, the conspirators would use House Butterwell's gold and the swords of House Frey to rebel against King Aerys_I_Targaryen. They hoped the Lothstons of Harrenhal and the Brackens of Stone Hedge, who did not attend the wedding, would be swayed by those events and join their effort in putting a Blackfyre on the Iron Throne. Although both Lords Frey and Butterwell were aware of this plan and let it happen, they doubted it would work. During the wedding feast, Lord Frey was seated on the dais and led multiple toasts. The next day, he attended the tourney from his seat next to Lord and Lady Butterwell in the viewing stand. When the squire Egg revealed himself as Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen and told Lords Frey and Butterwell that an army led by his father, Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen, was marching on Whitewalls, Lord Frey fled the castle with his retinue. Though Egg made up the story to frighten both lords, a real army led by Brynden Rivers quickly arrived and took control of the castle. Lord Frey was presumably taken captive by this army while fleeing, as he was later seen drinking wine in the Hand's pavilion outside Whitewalls. Bloodraven allowed Lord Frey to go home, but warned him they would speak again. It is unknown what punishment, if any, Lord Frey suffered for his part in the would-be Second Blackfyre Rebellion. After his death, Lord Frey was succeeded by his son, Walder_Frey.

name: Frynne
bio: Frynne is the daughter of a smith in Dorne. Frynne played in the pools at the Water Gardens when Princess Arianne_Martell spent time there. They would often team up in the pools, with Arianne riding Frynne's shoulders.

name: Fulk the Flea
bio: Fulk the Flea is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Gael Targaryen
bio: Princess Gael Targaryen was the thirteenth and last child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. Born during winter, Gael was also called the Winter Child. Gael was born in 80 AC, a winter year, giving her the sobriquet the "Winter Child". (According to some, the name was because Queen Alysanne was "in the winter of her childbearing years", 44 years old at the time of Gael's birth.) Gael was cared for by Grand Maester Elysar and Septa Lyra, who nursed her through her first year. After her sisters Alyssa_Targaryen, Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I), and Viserra_Targaryen had died within the span of five years (82–87 AC), Gael became a comfort for her mother Queen Alysanne, along with her older sister Maegelle_Targaryen. Gael became Alysanne's constant shadow, and even slept with her in her bed. In 99 AC, Gael disappeared from court. It was announced that she had died of a summer fever. After the deaths of King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne, it was revealed that Gael had been seduced and impregnated by a traveling singer. Gael had given birth to a stillborn son, and overwhelmed by her grief, she had walked into Blackwater Bay and drowned. According to some, Queen Alysanne never recovered from losing Gael. Following Gael's death, Alysanne could no longer stand to be in King's Landing and at the Red Keep. Alysanne herself would die on Dragonstone less than a year after Gael, in 100 AC.

name: Gaemon Palehair
bio: Gaemon Palehair was a pretender king during the Dance of the Dragons. His mother Essie, a whore in the House of Kisses, claimed he was a bastard son of Aegon_II_Targaryen. After the war, Gaemon became the cupbearer, food taster, and whipping boy of his friend, King Aegon_III_Targaryen.

name: Gaemon Targaryen
bio: Gaemon Targaryen, known as Gaemon the Glorious, was the second Lord of Dragonstone of House Targaryen. Gaemon was the son of Lord Aenar Targaryen and had at least one sister, Daenys Targaryen. He came to Dragonstone along his family in 114 BC. Gaemon was married to Daenys and together they had several children, Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Gaemon), Elaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Gaemon), and at least one younger daughter. Aegon and Elaena eventually inherited the rule of Dragonstone from Gaemon.

name: Gaemon Targaryen
bio: Prince Gaemon Targaryen was the eleventh child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. Gaemon was born to King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen in 73 AC at the Red Keep in King's Landing. Gaemon was born early, following a long and difficult labor. Though his mother eventually recovered despite the maesters' fears for her life, Gaemon did not and died short of the new year, less than three months old. As with the deaths of his elder siblings, Alysanne took the loss of Gaemon hard and questioned whether his passing was the result of her own failing. Septa Lyra, the queen's close confidant and companion, comforted her and assured her he was in the care of the Mother now. Years later in 83 AC when Jaehaerys and Alysanne's final child Gael_Targaryen was born small and frail, Grand Maester Elysar was so determined that she not suffer the fate of Gaemon or their brother Valerion_Targaryen that he personally nursed her through her first year of life.

name: Gage
bio: Gage is the head cook at Winterfell. He has a child, Turnip.

name: Galazza Galare
bio: Galazza Galare is the Green Grace, the high priestess of the Ghiscari at the Temple of the Graces in Meereen. She is from the House of Galare.

name: Galbart Glover
bio: Galbart Glover is the head of House Glover and the unwed Master of Deepwood Motte. Larence_Snow, the bastard of Hornwood, is a ward of Galbart. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Galbart is portrayed by Mark Coney. The unwed Galbart may be a widower. The appendices of A Clash of Kings and A Storm of Swords state that Galbart's nephew Gawen_Glover is the heir to Deepwood Motte, while the appendices of A Feast for Crows and A Dance with Dragons state that Galbart's brother Robett_Glover is the heir.

name: Galendro
bio: Galendro was an historian who wrote the book The Fires of the Freehold recording the History of Valyria. No complete copy of the book is known to Westeros - even the Citadel's copy lacks 27 scrolls.

name: Galeo
bio: Galeo is a sausage-maker from Braavos.

name: Galladon of Morne
bio: Galladon of Morne, called the Perfect Knight, was a legendary knight from Morne on the isle of Tarth. Many nobles and smallfolk from the island claim to be descended from him. Galladon_Tarth, the late son of Lord Selwyn_Tarth, was presumably named after the Perfect Knight.

name: Galladon Tarth
bio: Galladon Tarth was the only son of Lord Selwyn_Tarth of Evenfall. He was presumably named after Ser Galladon_of_Morne, a legendary figure of the island of Tarth. Galladon drowned when he was eight years old.

name: Gallard
bio: Gallard is an archmaester at the Citadel in Oldtown.

name: Galon Whitestaff
bio: Galon Whitestaff was a priest of the Drowned God who lived during the Age of Heroes. Galon called for the unification of the Iron Islands as a single kingdom, decreeing that ironborn must not make war on their own kind or carry off each other's women or raid each other's shores. He summoned a kingsmoot at Old Wyk to choose a high king to rule over the salt and rock kings alike. The kingsmoot chose Urras_Greyiron, who was crowned with a driftwood crown by Galon himself. After Urras Ironfoot's death, his son Erich_I_Greyiron seized the crown and proclaimed himself king rather than be chosen in a kingsmoot. Though half-blind and feeble, Galon arose in fury and summoned all "captains and kings" to unmake Erich the Ugly and condemn him to death. Instead, Erich saved his life by breaking down his father's crown and casting it into the sea. The kingsmoot then chose Regnar_Drumm.

name: Galt
bio: Galt is a member of the Stone Crows, one of the Vale mountain  clans.

name: Galtry the Green
bio: Galtry the Green was a hedge knight during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Galtry participated in the wedding tourney at Whitewalls. He faced Ser Daemon_II_Blackfyre in his final joust, losing after ten tilts.

name: Galyeon of Cuy
bio: Galyeon of Cuy is a singer from Cuy in the Reach.

name: Gared
bio: Gared is a ranger of the Night's Watch. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Gared is portrayed by Dermot Keaney. Gared joined the Night's Watch as a boy and spent forty years as a ranger. When Gared's ears had frostbite one winter, Maester Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) had to cut them off. While drinking wine in the barracks of Castle Black, Gared joked that Ser Waymar_Royce personally killed the sables for his cloak. Shortly before A Song of Ice and Fire starts, Waymar, Will, and Gared were sent ranging into the haunted forest by Jeor_Mormont, Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, in search of a band of wildling raiders. Jeor gave the command to Waymar, although he was the least experienced of the three, whereas Jeor counted Gared and Will among his best men. However, Waymar felt it was his due to have a command because he was a knight, and the Lord Commander accepted because he did not want to offend Waymar's father, Lord Yohn_Royce.

name: Gareth
bio: Gareth was a maester who wrote the book Red Sands. In it, he chronicles several Dornish letters, which suggest that it was Lord Qorgyle of Sandstone who had arranged the murder of Lord Lyonel_Tyrell_(lord) during the conquest of Dorne.

name: Gareth Clifton
bio: Gareth Clifton is a knight and the head of House Clifton. He is sworn to the Farmans of Fair Isle. He is married to Jeyne_Farman, the sister of his overlord, Lord Sebaston_Farman. They have twelve children.

name: Gareth II Gardener
bio: Gareth II Gardener, called the Grim, was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. Gareth was slain in battle by Harron Harlaw, High King of the Iron Islands, beneath the walls of Oldtown.

name: Gareth Long
bio: Gareth Long was a knight and master-at-arms in the service of House Peake during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. It is unconfirmed if he was from House Long, a family from the north. Ser Gareth served Lord Unwin_Peake as the master-at-arms at Starpike in the Reach. When Unwin became Hand of the King, he made Gareth the new master-at-arms of the Red Keep. Gareth trained King Aegon_III_Targaryen, a sullen student who often ignored instructions or refused to cooperate. Since Gareth was not allowed to harm the king, he advised Lord Peake to make Aegon's friend, Gaemon_Palehair, the king's whipping boy. Gareth quickly became despised in the Red Keep, especially by the king. Gareth's regard of Aegon improved slightly after the king wed his second wife, Queen Daenaera_Velaryon. Gareth remained in King's Landing after Lord Peake resigned as Hand in 134 AC. During the secret siege in 135 AC, Gareth's entreaty for Aegon to leave Maegor's Holdfast only angered the king. Lord George_Graceford named Gareth as an accomplice to a plot against House Rogare in the capital. The master-at-arms never denied the charges, claiming Aegon was a weakling unfit to sit the Iron Throne. He also accused Ser Victor_Risley, the King's Justice, of being a conspirator. Gareth and George were condemned to death, but both were allowed to join the Night's Watch. Victor instead demanded a trial by combat, so his accuser, Gareth, was chosen to face him before leaving for the Wall. Gareth promptly killed Victor in the throne room. Ser Lucas_Lothston_(husband_of_Falena) replaced Gareth as master-at-arms at the Red Keep.

name: Gareth the Grey
bio: Gareth the Grey was a knight who supported Daemon_I_Blackfyre during the First Blackfyre Rebellion.

name: Gareth Tyrell
bio: Gareth Tyrell was a head of House Tyrell and the first High Steward of the Reach for House Gardener. He was the second son of Ser Alester Tyrell. Gareth and his son Leo_Tyrell_(son_of_Gareth) performed their duties so well that the Gardeners made the office of High Steward hereditary.

name: Gargon Qoherys
bio: Gargon Qoherys, also known as Gargon the Guest, was the Lord of Harrenhal and the head of House Qoherys during the reigns of Kings Aegon_I_Targaryen and Aenys_I_Targaryen. When his grandsire, Quenton_Qoherys, received the lordship of Harrenhal in 2 AC from King Aegon_I_Targaryen, the succession of the newly-created House Qoherys was assured as Quenton had two strong sons and one plump grandson, Gargon. However, when Lord Quenton died from a fall from his horse in 9 AC, Gargon directly succeeded him. During the rebellion of Harren_the_Red in 37 AC, the father of a maid deflowered by Gargon allowed Harren to enter Harrenhal. Harren and his outlaws dragged Gargon from his bed to the castle godswood, where he sliced off Gargon's genitals, and fed them to a dog while Gargon bled to death. As Gargon was the sole living Qoherys, the house became extinct and Harrenhal was then granted to House Harroway.

name: Garibald
bio: Ser Garibald is a sellsword of the Second Sons.

name: Garibald Grey
bio: Garibald Grey was a knight from House Grey during the Targaryen civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. Ser Garibald led a group of rivermen in the Battle by the Lakeshore. Garibald refused the challenge of Ser Criston_Cole before the Butcher's Ball began, and Criston was instead slain by arrows. During the First Battle of Tumbleton, Garibald commanded six thousand blacks who faced the army of Lord Ormund_Hightower. Garibald's men were forced to retreat after a rain of arrows and a cavalry charge, however. When the Two Betrayers began attacking the blacks, Garibald was struck by a crossbow bolt and then burned by the dragonflame of Tessarion.

name: Garibald of the Seven Stars
bio: Garibald of the Seven Stars, known as the septon knight, was a septon knight of the Warrior's Sons at the start of the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. In 42 AC, Ser Garibald was stationed at the King's Landing chapter of the Warrior's Sons, based at the Sept of Remembrance. He fought in the trial of seven in which seven of the Warrior's Sons faced King Maegor_I_Targaryen and his six champions. According to one chronicler, Ser Garibald sang a paean as he fought. He died during the fight.

name: Garibald Shells
bio: Garibald Shells is a young knight from Sunspear.

name: Garigus
bio: Wisdom Garigus was a member of the Alchemists' Guild of King's Landing during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. Garigus, together with Wisdoms Rossart and Belis, took part in King Aerys_II_Targaryen's wildfire plot, the plan to destroy the capital, during Robert's Rebellion. According to Ser Jaime_Lannister, Garigus was as enthusiastic about the plan as the other two pyromancers. The three secretly deployed wildfire caches across the whole city, to be ignited at the command of King Aerys. Their constant consultations with the king, both day and night, made the Hand of the King, Lord Qarlton_Chelsted, suspicious, leading to him confronting the king. A couple of days after he had killed Rossart and Aerys at the Red Keep, Jaime Lannister hunted down Garigus and Belis and slew them as well. Garigus wept for mercy, and was giving a quick death.

name: Garin
bio: Garin is an orphan of the Greenblood. He is named after Prince Garin_the_Great, a famous leader of the Rhoynar. Garin was the milk-brother of Princess Arianne_Martell, as his mother was Arianne's wet nurse, and has been a close friend and companion to her since their early childhood. When Arianne and her friends played the shoulder-riding game in the Water Gardens, her favorite steed was Garin, since if she rode him, nobody could defeat them, not even Nymeria_Sand and the Archon's_green-haired_daughter. When Prince Oberyn_Martell brought his niece, Arianne, and his daughters Tyene_Sand and Sarella_Sand, to Shandystone, Garin accompanied them. At some point, Arianne broke one of Garin's teeth; she gave him a golden tooth to replace it. Garin claims to have bedded Cedra, a servant at Sunspear.

name: Garin the Great
bio: Garin the Great was a noble of the Rhoynar, and the Prince of Chroyane. During the Second Spice War, Prince Garin gathered a great army of the Rhoynar at Chroyane to oppose the Valyrian Freehold. According to Beldecar's History of the Rhoynish Wars, Garin had a quarter of a million men in his command. The prince declared that as long as the army remained beside the Rhoyne, they would be protected from the dragons of Valyria by the Rhoynish water wizards . Garin began to be called the Great after victories at Selhorys, Valysar, and Volon Therys. However, after Garin the Great threatened Volantis, his Rhoynish host was defeated by the dragonlords of Valyria. According to legend, the men of Volantis and Valyria hung Garin in a golden cage, and carried him north to his city of Chroyane, to witness its destruction and the enslavement of his people. Garin called upon Mother Rhoyne to destroy his captors, but they only mocked him. Nevertheless, that very night, the waters of the Rhoyne flooded out of season and with great force, drowning the invaders. Additionally, a thick fog fell upon Chroyane, and the Valyrian conquerors began to die of greyscale. Legend says that the fallen conquerors have never rested since that day, down beneath the waters of the Sorrows. It is said that their cold breath rises from the murk to make the fogs that infest the ruined city of Chroyane, and their flesh is said to have turned as stony as their hearts. Some also believe that Garin rose from his watery grave and became the Shrouded_Lord. Some say Garin's Curse brought about the Doom of Valyria.

name: Gariss
bio: Gariss is a man sworn to House Stark.

name: Garizon
bio: Archmaester  Garizon is an archmaester at the  Citadel in Oldtown.

name: Garlan Tyrell
bio: Garlan Tyrell, known as Garlan the Gallant, is a knight of House Tyrell and the second son of Lord Mace_Tyrell and Lady Alerie_Hightower. His personal coat of arms is two golden roses on green, to denote his status as second son. Garlan is married to Lady Leonette_Fossoway of House Fossoway. Garlan was plump in his youth. His elder brother, Willas_Tyrell, shrewdly dubbed him "the Gallant" to save him from the indignity that befell an uncle, nicknamed Garth_Tyrell.

name: Garland II Gardener
bio: Garland II Gardener, called Garland the Bridegroom, was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. Garland brought House Hightower of Oldtown and their domains peacefully into the Kingdom of the Reach through long negotiations and marriage by wedding his daughter to Lord Lymond_Hightower whilst putting aside his own wives to marry Lord Lymond's daughter.

name: Garland VI Gardener
bio: Garland VI Gardener was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. During his minority, a member of House Tyrell ruled the Reach in his name as Regent.

name: Garmon Hightower
bio: Garmon Hightower was a nobleman of House Hightower and a younger son of Lord Manfred_Hightower_(Aegon's_Conquest). In 10 AC during the First Dornish War, Lord Manfred_Hightower_(Aegon's_Conquest) dispatched his eldest son, Ser Addam_Hightower, to lead a host against Lord Fowler at Nightsong. With most of the Hightower strength away, Ser Joffrey_Dayne led a second army against Oldtown. The young Garmon led a sortie against the Dornishmen, but he was slain in battle by Ser Joffrey.

name: Garmund Hightower
bio: Garmund Hightower was a member of House Hightower during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. He was the third son of Lord Ormund_Hightower. Shortly before the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Garmund's father, Lord Ormund_Hightower, married a second time to Samantha_Tarly, known as Lady Sam, who would later marry his brother Lyonel_Hightower. During the war, Garmund served as a page and cupbearer to House Tyrell at Highgarden. In 131 AC, Lord Lyonel accepted Lord Corlys_Velaryon's peace terms for the end of the conflict, despite initially resisting them. According to Grand Maester Munkun's True Telling, this is because the Tyrells forbade him to raise a host or go to war without their leave, with the implicit threat that Garmund would pay for Lyonel's disobedience with his life, as every ward is also a hostage. Garmund later married Lady Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon), and had six daughters by her.

name: Garon Baratheon
bio: Garon Baratheon was a knight from House Baratheon during the reigns of kings Aegon_I_Targaryen, Aenys_I_Targaryen, Maegor_I_Targaryen and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He was a younger brother of Lord Rogar_Baratheon of Storm's End.[N 3] After both plots of Garon's eldest brother, Lord Rogar_Baratheon, to set aside King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and crown another in his place, Rogar wrote a will and confession absolving Garon and his brothers Ser Borys_Baratheon and Ser Ronnal_Baratheon of having played any role in his plots. When Rogar was summoned to King's Landing by King Jaehaerys in 50 AC, Garon and his brother Ser Ronnal urged Rogar to defy the summons. In 51 AC, Garon accompanied his brothers Lord Rogar and Ser Ronnal and Rogar's wife, the Dowager Queen Alyssa_Velaryon, to King's Landing for the second wedding of King Jaehaerys to Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. After Lady Alyssa died in childbirth, Garon was sent by Lord Rogar to invite King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne to remain at Storm's End for a feast in honor of the birth of Rogar's daughter, Jocelyn_Baratheon. In 55 AC, Garon participated in the melee of the tourney held at King's Landing to celebrate the completion of the Dragonpit. Garon became a widower when his wife succumbed of the Shivers.[N 1] In 61 AC, Garon was made regent for Rogar's heir, Boremund_Baratheon, as Rogar left to fight their brother Ser Borys and the Vulture_King_(Jaehaerys_I) in the Third Dornish War. Garon called Rogar's plan "a fool's errand". Garon was present at his brother's side when Rogar died half a year after returning from battle. In 73 AC, Garon and his nephew Lord Boremund were given charge of the third wife of Ser Lucamore_Strong and their children.

name: Garrett Flowers
bio: Garrett Flowers is a bastard of House Tyrell, and the first cousin to Mace_Tyrell, the current Lord of Highgarden. He is the youngest son of Garth_Tyrell, and he has an older brother, Garse_Flowers.

name: Garrett Greenspear
bio: Garrett Greenspear is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Garrett Paege
bio: Garrett Paege is a squire from House Paege. He is the son of Ser Halmon_Paege,[N 1] the head of the house.

name: Garrick Hall
bio: Garrick Hall was a landed knight from Middleton along the Blackwater Rush during the Dance of the Dragons. When the host of blacks led by Ser Garibald_Grey and Lord Roderick_Dustin marched south from the riverlands to Tumbleton, they were joined by men from the Blackwater Rush, including Garrick. It is unknown if he survived the First Battle of Tumbleton.

name: Garris the Grey
bio: Garris, called Garris the Grey, was a legendary Rhoynish prince of Ny Sar. Garris drove the hairy men away from the land where Norvos stands today. However, the Rhoynar did not linger in those lands, as they preferred the more temperate climes of the lower river to the dark skies and cold winds of the hills.

name: Garrison Fowler
bio: Garrison Fowler, known as the Blind King, was a head of House Fowler and the last King of Stone and Sky in Dorne. Garrison was defeated in Nymeria's War and was one of six kings sent to the Wall by Nymeria.

name: Garrison Prester
bio: Garrison Prester is the Lord of Feastfires and the head of House Prester. His cousin is Ser Forley_Prester.

name: Garse
bio: Garse is a guardsman at Castle Black.

name: Garse Flowers
bio: Garse Flowers is a bastard of House Tyrell, and the first cousin to Mace_Tyrell, the current Lord of Highgarden. He is the eldest son of Garth_Tyrell, and he has a younger brother, Garrett_Flowers.

name: Garse VII Gardener
bio: Garse VII Gardener was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener during the Century of Blood. His relation to King Mern_IX_Gardener is unknown. Garse was slain by King Argilac_Durrandon, the last of the Storm Kings, in the Battle of Summerfield during the Century of Blood.

name: Garse Goodbrook
bio: Garse Goodbrook is  a knight of House Goodbrook. He is the husband of Kyra_Frey with whom he has two children, Walder_Goodbrook and Jeyne_Goodbrook. His relation to Lord Lymond_Goodbrook, the head of the House is unknown.

name: Garth of Greenaway
bio: Garth of Greenaway is a ranger of the Night's Watch. He comes from Greenaway and carries a knife.

name: Garth the Gardener
bio: Garth the Gardener was the legendary first King of the Reach and the founder of House Gardener. Garth was the eldest son of the mythical Garth_Greenhand, the High King of the First Men said to have been the ancestor of numerous houses of the Reach. According to legend, Garth the Gardener made his home on the hill atop the Mander that in time would become known as Highgarden, and wore a crown of vines and flowers. From his loins sprang House Gardener, whose kings would rule for many thousands of years.

name: Garth III Gardener
bio: Garth III Gardener, called Garth the Great, was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. Garth extended the borders of the Reach northward, winning Old Oak, Red Lake, and Goldengrove with pacts of friendship and mutual defense. In the annuals of history the name of Garth III is writ large.

name: Garth V Gardener
bio: Garth V Gardener was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. He was dubbed Hammer of the Dornish for his legendary conflicts with Dornishmen.

name: Garth VI Gardener
bio: Garth VI Gardener, called the Morningstar, was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. Garth died in battle against the ironborn.

name: Garth VII Gardener
bio: Garth VII Gardener, called the Goldenhand or Garth Goldenhand, was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. As a boy, Garth turned back a Dornish invasion when King Ferris_Fowler led ten thousand men through the Wide Way intent on conquest. Soon after he drove the last of the ironborn from their strongholds on the Misty Islands. He then renamed them the Shield Islands, and resettled them with his own fiercest fighters and finest seamen, granting them special dispensations with the goal of turning them into a first line of defense against ironborn raiders. This proved a great success. In his final and greatest war, Garth VII faced an alliance between the Storm King and the King of the Rock, who intended to divide the Reach between them. The Goldenhand defeated them both, and then with cunning words he sowed such strife between the two that they turned on each other with great slaughter at the Battle of Three Armies. In the aftermath Garth married his daughters to their heirs and signed a pact with each, thereby fixing the borders between their three kingdoms. But Garth's greatest achievement was to give his kingdom and people three-quarters of a century of golden peace. During the long years of his reign, the Reach was at war for less than ten. Generations of boys were born, grew to manhood, and sired children of their own and died without ever having to know what it was to grasp a spear and shield and march away to war. And with this long peace came an unprecedented prosperity. This period became known as the Golden Reign, and was when the Reach truly flowered. Garth VII died when he was ninety-three, frail of body but sound of wits, having reigned for eighty-one years. He was succeeded on the Oakenseat by a great-grandson. The golden peace of Garth Goldenhand never came again. Highgarden reached its apex of power during the reign of King Gyles_III_Gardener, however.

name: Garth IX Gardener
bio: Garth IX Gardener was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. Along with his son Merle_I_Gardener and grandson Gwayne_V_Gardener, he is remembered as one of the Three Sage Kings who dealt with the coming of the Andals to the Reach with a policy of accord and assimilation rather then armed resistance. Instead of fighting off the Andals, Garth IX brought a septon to his court and made him part of his councils. The first sept at Highgarden was built during Garth's reign, although he continued to worship the old gods in the godswood of Highgarden. Garth took Andals into his service as household knights and retainers, and he bequeathed lands and wives and lordships for the more powerful of the Andal kings and other Andal adventurers descending on the Reach in exchange for pledges of fealty. Garth also sought after Andal craftsmen, especially blacksmiths and stonemasons, to settle throughout the Reach, supplying him and his bannermen with iron instead of bronze and strengthening their castles and holdfasts with Andal masonry.

name: Garth X Gardener
bio: Garth X Gardener, called Garth Greybeard, was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. During his long reign House Gardener reached the nadir of its power. Garth came into the crown at the age of seven and died at ninety-six, reigning longer than his forebear Garth_VII_Gardener. Though vigorous in his youth, Garth X was neither wise nor clever. Vain and frivolous, he surrounded himself with fools and flatterers and lost his wits entirely in old age. During the long years of his senility, he became the tool of first one faction and then another as those around him vied for wealth and power. Garth sired no sons and only daughters; one of whom had married Lord Manderly and another to Lord Peake and each lord was determined that his wife should succeed Garth Greybeard. The rivalry between them was marked by betrayal, conspiracy, and murder, finally escalating into open war with other lords joining the cause on both sides. With the Kingdom of the Reach in chaos and the king too feeble to grasp what was occurring, much less stop it, the Storm King and the King of the Rock seized the opportunity and took large swathes of territory, whilst Dornish raids grew bolder and more frequent. One Dornish king besieged Oldtown, whilst another crossed the Mander and sacked Highgarden. The Oakenseat was chopped into pieces and burned, whilst the senile King Garth X was found tied to his bed, whimpering and covered in his own filth. The Dornish cut his throat - in an act of mercy one of them would later say - then put Highgarden to the torch after stripping it of all its wealth. Almost a decade of anarchy followed, until Ser Osmund_Tyrell, the High Steward, made common cause with the other lords of the Reach and defeated both the Peakes and Manderlys, reclaimed the ruined seat of the Gardeners, and placed a second cousin of the late and unlamented Garth Greybeard on its new throne who ascended as King Mern_VI_Gardener.

name: Garth XI Gardener
bio: Garth XI Gardener, also known as Garth the Painter, was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. He was the son and successor of Mern_VI_Gardener. While his father had done much to restore the Reach and Highgarden after the civil war instigated by House Peake and House Manderly, and the Dornish raiding that sacked Highgarden, Garth XI did the rest, taking such a terrible vengeance upon the Dornish that Lord Hightower said the Red Mountains had been green until Garth painted them with Dornish blood. For the remainder of his long reign, the king was known as Garth the Painter.

name: Garth XII Gardener
bio: Garth XII Gardener was a King of the Reach and a head of House Gardener.

name: Garth Greenfield
bio: Garth Greenfield is the Knight of Greenfield and head of House Greenfield.

name: Garth Greenhand
bio: Garth Greenhand, also called Garth Greenhair and Garth the Green, was a mythical High King of the First Men said to have been the common ancestor of numerous houses from the Reach. His firstborn son, Garth_the_Gardener, founded House Gardener, the Kings of the Reach. Garth supposedly wore a crown of vines and flowers and made the land bloom. It is unclear if he is related to the First_King.

name: Garth Greyfeather
bio: Garth Greyfeather is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Garth the Harelip
bio: Garth the Harelip was a captain of the City Watch of King's Landing during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. The knight commanded the Dragon Gate. During the riots of King's landing, Garth led the men of the Dragon Gate garrison against the rioters and managed to restore some semblance of order to the streets north and east of Rhaenys's Hill. When a mob marched for the storming of the Dragonpit, Rhaenyra sent riders to Garth and Ser Balon Byrch, commanding them to lead their respective garrisons to disperse the rioters, seize the Shepherd, and protect House Targaryen's dragons. It is unknown if the riders reached their destination. After Rhaenyra fled the capital, Garth was charged with defense of the Red Keep. However, not half a day passed before he struck Rhaenyra's banners and opened his gates to Ser Perkin_the_Flea and his gutter knights, trusting to their mercy. Garth was dragged before the Flea and beheaded, however, along with twenty knights still loyal to the queen.

name: Garth Hightower
bio: Garth Hightower, called Greysteel, is a knight of House Hightower and the second son of Lord Leyton_Hightower.

name: Garth of Oldtown
bio: Garth of Oldtown is a ranger of the Night's Watch. He comes from Oldtown.

name: Garth Oldflowers
bio: Garth Oldflowers was a knight and member of House Oldflowers during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Ser Garth is listed as a participant of the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Garth Tyrell
bio: Garth Tyrell, known as Garth the Gross, is a member of House Tyrell and an uncle of Lord Mace_Tyrell of Highgarden. He serves his nephew as the Lord Seneschal. Garth has two bastard sons, Garse_Flowers and Garrett_Flowers. Garth served Lord Luthor_Tyrell as Lord Seneschal of Highgarden.

name: Garth
bio: Garth is a gaoler, headsman, and torturer in the Wolf's Den at White Harbor.

name: Gascoyne of the Greenblood
bio: Gascoyne of the Greenblood is a knight and the sworn shield of Prince Trystane_Martell. He comes from the banks of the Greenblood.

name: Gaven Greywolf
bio: Gaven Greywolf was a First Men skinchanger. Gaven was killed along his kin by a King of Winter of House Stark during "the savage War of the Wolves".

name: Gavin the Trader
bio: Gavin the Trader is a free folk leader.

name: Gawen
bio: Gawen was a maester of the Citadel who served as Grand Maester during the reigns of Kings Aegon_I_Targaryen, Aenys_I_Targaryen, and, albeit briefly, Maegor_I_Targaryen. Gawen served as Grand Maester for King Aegon_I_Targaryen, succeeding Grand Maester Lyonce in 12 AC. Gawen and Ser Osmund_Strong, Aegon's Hand of the King, were charged with raising walls around King's Landing. Gawen continued to hold the office of Grand Maester during the reign of Aegon I's heir, King Aenys_I_Targaryen. When Aenys fell ill at Dragonstone in late 41 AC, Gawen despaired of improving the king's condition. Following the death of Aenys I, the elderly and grey-headed Gawen protested the coronation of Aenys's half-brother, Maegor_I_Targaryen, by his mother, Visenya_Targaryen. Gawen stated that following the laws of inheritance the crown should pass to Aenys's oldest son, Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I). Maegor beheaded Gawen for his protests with Blackfyre. After Gawen's death, there were no further protests at Dragonstone. Gawen was the first of three Grand Maesters who were executed during Maegor's reign, the others being Myros and Desmond_(Grand_Maester).

name: Gawen Corbray
bio: Gawen Corbray was a knight of House Corbray during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. On the account of his prowess at arms and reputation, Ser Gawen was chosen by Queen Visenya_Targaryen as the first master-at-arms for her son, Prince Maegor_I_Targaryen, on Dragonstone. Under Corbray's tutelage, Prince Maegor took to swordplay at once.

name: Gawen Gardener
bio: Prince Gawen Gardener was a member of House Gardener of Highgarden and the second son of Mern_IX_Gardener, King of the Reach. When his father, King Mern_IX_Gardener, made an alliance with Loren_I_Lannister, King of the Rock, to join their armies against Aegon_I_Targaryen, Gawen rode beside him carrying the Gardener banner. Gawen burned in the Field of Fire alongside his father, brothers, and his father's grandsons when Aegon and his sisters, Visenya_Targaryen and Rhaenys_Targaryen, unleashed the fury of their dragons.

name: Gawen Glover
bio: Gawen Glover is a member of House Glover, the son of Robett_Glover and Sybelle_Glover. The appendices of A Clash of Kings and A Storm of Swords state that Gawen is the heir to Deepwood Motte, while the appendices of A Feast for Crows and A Dance with Dragons state that his father Robett is the heir of Galbart_Glover.

name: Gawen Swann
bio: Gawen Swann was the Lord of Stonehelm and the head of House Swann during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Lord Gawen's fool had named Prince Matarys_Targaryen the "Even Younger Prince". Lord Gawen participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC, where he challenged Prince Valarr_Targaryen. Although he nearly defeated the prince in the first tilt, Valarr knocked him from his saddle in the second tilt and Gawen quickly yielded to the prince, the second of Valarr's nine hollow victories against "undistinguished foes". When Ser Duncan_the_Tall searched for a seventh knight for his trial of seven, Gawen ignored his call for help, laughing softly at a comment from Lord Pearse_Caron.

name: Gawen Westerling
bio: Gawen Westerling is the Lord of the Crag and the head of House Westerling. As the Lord of the Crag, Gawen headed an old and proud, though impoverished, family. Gawen wed Sybell_Spicer of House Spicer, a wealthy House of lowborn origins. This marriage is said to have somewhat sordid origins, and Gawen is rumored to have been entrapped into the marriage. Gawen once offered his elder daughter, Jeyne_Westerling, as a match for either Martyn_Lannister or Willem_Lannister, but was rejected because of Jeyne's mother.

name: Gawen Wylde
bio: Gawen Wylde was a knight from House Wylde and the master-at-arms at Storm's End during Robert's Rebellion. During Robert's Rebellion, when the siege of Storm's End was at its worst, Ser Gawen and three other knights tried to sneak out of Storm's End through a postern gate to surrender. However, the four knights were intercepted and captured. Stannis_Baratheon, who had command of the castle, ordered them flung from the walls using the catapults. As they were being strapped down, Maester Cressen suggested that they not send them to their deaths, as the remaining rations were low and there was no need to waste good meat. Gawen was sent to the cells where he later died shortly before Storm's End received food from the smuggler Davos_Seaworth.

name: Gedmund Peake
bio: Gedmund Peake, called Gedmund Great-Axe, was a knight from House Peake who became commander of the royal fleet, and later lord admiral and master of ships during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. He was the younger brother of Lord_Peake_(father_of_Unwin) and uncle of Unwin_Peake of Starpike. When Lord Unwin_Peake became Hand of the King, in 133 AC he made his uncle Gedmund commander of the royal fleet with the more experienced Ned_Bean as his second-in-command. During the Daughters' War, Gedmund and Ned were sent against Racallio_Ryndoon, the self-proclaimed King of the Narrow Sea. The commander became so seasick during the voyage, however, that sailors called him "Gedmund Green-Sick". When it was learned that Racallio had allied with the Sealord of Braavos and the Archon of Tyrosh, Gedmund remained at Tarth to await further instructions from Unwin. Lord Alyn_Velaryon protested they would lose time, however, and left Gedmund behind to continue south. The Braavosi ships in the Stepstones were then defeated in Lord Alyn's attack, an action which outraged Unwin. The Hand recalled Gedmund's fleet from Tarth to defend the Gullet and Blackwater Bay from Braavosi retaliation. Gedmund remained in his post after his nephew resigned as Hand in 134 AC. After the attempted coup against the Rogare family and the King's Hand, Lord Thaddeus_Rowan in 135 AC, Gedmund was named lord admiral and master of ships to appease Lord Peake and his supporters.

name: Gella
bio: Gella is the granddaughter of Lord Godric_Borrell, through one of his daughters.

name: Gelmarr
bio: Gelmarr, also known as Gelmarr the Grim, is an ironborn raider. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Gelmarr is portrayed by Jonny Magee.

name: Gendel
bio: Gendel was a King-Beyond-the-Wall, a title he shared with his brother, Gorne. He lived three thousand years ago and led an invasion into the north with his brother. Maester Herryk's History of the Kings-Beyond-the-Wall has a transcription of a wildling song about Gendel and Gorne in which the brothers mediated a dispute between a clan of children of the forest and a family of giants over control of a cave. After discovering the cave was part of a larger system of caverns which extended beneath the Wall, Gendel and Gorne resorted to trickery and convinced both parties to abandon the cave. Maesters are skeptical of the wildling tale, however. Three thousand years before the War of the Five Kings, Gendel and Gorne ruled as Kings-Beyond-the-Wall. The brother kings gathered the free folk to them and marched them through Gorne's Way, their path through caves beneath the Wall. The wildlings evaded the Night's Watch, but when they emerged on the far side, the King in the North was waiting for them and attacked them. Gorne slew the king from House Stark, but his son took up his crown and killed Gorne in turn. While the battle raged, the Night's Watch, made aware of the battle, sortied from their castles and took the wildlings in the rear. With the Starks to the south, the Umbers the east, and the Watch to the north, Gendel was also killed, according to the traditions of northmen and of rangers. According to the free folk, however, Gendel survived the fighting. Cutting his way through the Night's Watch, he attempted to return beyond the Wall via the same route the wildlings had come. Gendel did not know the way as well as Gorne, however, and supposedly became lost forever trying to find his way out. Free folk legend says that the descendants of Gendel's people still dwell in those caves, attacking anyone who tries to find Gorne's Way and eating them.

name: Gendry
bio: Gendry is a blacksmith apprentice for Master Tobho_Mott in King's Landing. He does not know he is a bastard son of King Robert_I_Baratheon. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Gendry is portrayed by Joe Dempsie. Gendry was born in King's Landing, but he does not know that his father is King Robert_I_Baratheon. Gendry's_mother, a worker at an alehouse, died when he was still a young boy. All he remembers of her was that she had blond hair and would sometimes sing to him. Gendry saw King Robert from afar at tourneys and once at the Great Sept of Baelor. A drunken Robert also once nearly rode down Gendry when the boy was playing near the Mud Gate. Tobho_Mott, a master smith, took Gendry on as an apprentice after being paid double the customary fee by a Varys. Lords Jon_Arryn and Stannis_Baratheon called upon Tobho and Gendry during their investigation into Robert's children.

name: Gendry's mother
bio: Gendry's mother was a tavern wench from King's Landing who was the mother of Gendry. Her given name is unknown. The woman worked in an alehouse and would bring home drunks from the establishment. She had a black-haired bastard named Gendry with King Robert_I_Baratheon, though she never revealed to the boy the identity of his father. She sometimes sang to Gendry but died when the boy was little. Lord Jon_Arryn asked Gendry about his mother during the Hand of the King's investigation into the parentage of Robert's various children.

name: Genna Lannister
bio: Genna Lannister is a noblewoman of House Lannister wed to Ser Emmon_Frey, with whom she has four children: Ser Cleos_Frey, Lyonel_Frey, Tion_Frey, and Walder_Frey_(son_of_Emmon). Genna is the only daughter of the late Lord Tytos_Lannister and Lady Jeyne_Marbrand, and she is the younger sister of Tywin_Lannister, Lord of Casterly Rock. Genna is the aunt of Queen Cersei_Lannister, Ser Jaime_Lannister of the Kingsguard, and Tyrion_Lannister, having taken on a maternal role for the siblings after the death of Tywin's wife, Lady Joanna_Lannister.

name: Geoff
bio: Geoff, called the Squirrel, is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: George Graceford
bio: George Graceford was the Lord of Holyhall and the head of House Graceford during the Dance of the Dragons. He was later named Lord Confessor during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. During the Dance of the Dragons, George was a member of the Caltrops, thirteen nobles who plotted to kill the upstart Two Betrayers. When Lord Unwin_Peake came to power as Hand of the King, Lord Graceford and Ser Victor_Risley were the only other members of the Caltrops still living. As a supporter of Lord Peake, George was installed as Lord Confessor. According to Mushroom, Lord Graceford and Clarice_Osgrey were lovers. He claims she was aroused by torture, and sometimes joined the Lord Confessor in the dungeons to assist him with his work. After an attempted poisoning of Queen Daenaera_Velaryon, a dozen cooks, bakers, scullions, and serving girls were delivered to the custody of the Lord Confessor. Seven confessed to having plotted to poison the boy king Aegon_III_Targaryen, but each account differed as to the poisoned dish and the source of the poison, and thus Lord Thaddeus_Rowan dismissed these confessions. Amid the Lysene Spring in 135 AC, Lord Rowan was tortured by George for plotting to kill the king. When a bruised Thaddeus was brought before the court, he did not answer unless Lord Graceford pricked him with a dagger. However, King Aegon III questioned Lord Rowan, making him confess that Aegon himself was part of the plot, and that Lord Rowan had killed King Viserys_I_Targaryen. George was greatly displeased that Rowan's confession was proven unreliable by the king. The king then ordered the Lord Confessor to be seized, as he had committed treason by forcing a false confession from the Hand. At the mere sight of instruments of torture, Lord Graceford revealed the conspirators, including many other supporters of Lord Peake. Tessario the Tiger implicated Lord Graceford under his own torture. George's trial was one of three of the conspirators that the king attended. George choose to take the black, rather than be executed. Maester Rowley replaced him as Lord Confessor in 136 AC.

name: Gerald Gower
bio: Gerald Gower is a knight of House Gower in service to King Stannis_Baratheon.

name: Gerardys
bio: Gerardys was a maester who served Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen on Dragonstone. He later became the Grand Maester of Queen Rhaenyra, and sat on her black council. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Gerardys is portrayed by Phil Daniels. Gerardys served Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen on Dragonstone. He also flew with Prince Daemon_Targaryen to help his wife Lady Laena_Velaryon after a difficult childbirth, but they arrived too late. When King Viserys_I_Targaryen fell and sliced his hand open on the Iron Throne, Rhaenyra flew Gerardys, accounted a more gifted healer, to King's Landing after Mellos was unsuccessful in treating the laceration. Gerardys amputated two of the king's fingers, saving his life. After Mellos passed away, Rhaenyra recommended that Gerardys take his place as Grand Maester. Alicent_Hightower, on the other hand, wanted Maester Alfador, who served House Hightower, named to the office. Beset from both sides, Viserys chose neither, reminding both his wife and daughter that the Citadel would decide who filled the office. Archmaester Orwyle was in the end selected by the conclave as the new Grand Maester.[N 1]

name: Geremy Frey
bio: Geremy Frey was a knight of House Frey. The tenth-born son of Lord Walder_Frey, and fifth son by his third wife Lady Amarei_Crakehall. He was married to Carolei_Waynwood and had two children, Sandor_Frey and Cynthea_Frey.

name: Gerion Lannister
bio: Gerion Lannister was a member of House Lannister, and was the youngest brother of Tywin_Lannister, Lord of Casterly Rock.

name: Germund Botley
bio: Germund Botley is a member of House Botley and the younger brother of Lord Sawane_Botley.

name: Gerold
bio: Gerold is a Westerosi given name. It can refer to several people:

name: Gerold Dayne
bio: Gerold Dayne, known as Darkstar, is the Knight of High Hermitage. His house  is a lesser branch of the Daynes of Starfall, and he is a cousin to Lord Edric_Dayne. At some point, Gerold was one of the lovers of Arianne_Martell.

name: Gerold Grafton
bio: Gerold Grafton is the Lord of Gulltown and the head of House Grafton.

name: Gerold Grafton
bio: Gerold Grafton was an Andal knight from Andalos who established the rule of House Grafton over Gulltown. Ser Gerold made an alliance with First Man King Osgood_III_Shett, King of the True Men, to fight against Bronze King Yorwyck_VI_Royce. Gerold took Osgood's daughter as wife, gave the King his eldest daughter as bride, and gave a younger daughter to Osgood's son and heir. All this marriages were performed by septons in rites according to the Faith of the Seven. Ser Gerold aided King Osgood in winning against King Yorwyck VI, but afterward, it was rumored, he struck down his good-father. After returning to Gulltown, Gerold claimed Osgood's crown and confined Osgood's heir to his bedchambers until he had gotten Gerold's daughter with child. Afterward the prince vanished from history. Gulltown rose against King Gerold, who brutally put down the protests, executing men, women and children and having their bodies thrown in the bay to feed the crabs. In the following years, House Grafton's rule was uncontested, for King Gerold proved to be a sage and clever ruler, making Gulltown prosper enough to grow into the first and only city in the Vale.

name: Gerold Hightower
bio: Gerold Hightower, known as the White Bull, was a knight from House Hightower and the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard during the reigns of kings Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen and Aerys_II_Targaryen. He was the uncle of Lord Leyton_Hightower of Oldtown. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Gerold Hightower is merged with Oswell_Whent, keeping the former's name, and portrayed by Eddie Eyre.

name: Gerold Lannister
bio: Gerold Lannister, also known as Gerold the Golden, was the Lord of Casterly Rock, the Shield of Lannisport, the Warden of the West, and the head of House Lannister during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen, King Maekar_I_Targaryen, and part of the reign of King Aegon_V_Targaryen. Born at Casterly Rock, Gerold Lannister was the youngest son of Lord Damon_Lannister_(lord), the Grey Lion and his wife, Lady Cerissa_Brax. After Damon passed in 210 AC, Gerold's older brother Ser Tybolt_Lannister became the Lord of Casterly Rock. By 211 AC, Gerold was a suitor to Lady Rohanne_Webber of Coldmoat, and she was fond of his letters. However, she believed Gerold would never willingly forsake the pleasures of Lannisport and the splendor of Casterly Rock for some little lordship, as he wielded more influence at Casterly Rock as his brother's advisor than he could ever hope for as the husband of Lady Rohanne. Rohanne Webber would eventually marry Eustace_Osgrey, but some time after the passing of her fifth husband, she and Gerold were married after all. Although Gerold had no children by his first wife, Lady Alysanne_Farman, he would have four sons with Rohanne: Tywald_Lannister, Tion_Lannister, Tytos_Lannister (born 220 AC), and Jason_Lannister_(son_of_Gerold) (born 229 AC). Tybolt passed away in 212 AC under suspicious circumstances, and his young daughter Cerelle_Lannister_(daughter_of_Tybolt) succeeded him as Lady of Casterly Rock, with Gerold serving as her regent. However, in less than a year, Lady Cerelle also passed away. The suddenness of Cerelle's death led to rumors that Gerold had murdered his niece, the most common tale claiming that he had smothered her with a pillow. Some of his lords and many of his smallfolk held no love for him, believing him a kinslayer. Some said he had murdered his brother Tybolt as well.

name: Gerold Lannister
bio: Gerold Lannister, called Gerold the Great, was a King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock. His regnal number is undetermined, but he is known to be a successor to King Gerold_III_Lannister. In an ultimate indignity to the declining power of the driftwood kings, Gerold the Great sailed his fleet to the Iron Islands in a daring raid, taking a hundred ironborn hostages. He kept them at Casterly Rock afterwards, hanging one every time his shores were raided. True to his word, Gerold hanged more than twenty of the hostages.

name: Gerold II Lannister
bio: Gerold II Lannister was a King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Like his father, Tyrion_III_Lannister, Gerold decided to arrange marriages for the more powerful Andal warchiefs that were entering the Westerlands with the daughters of the great local Houses. Aware of Andal betrayals in the Vale, he also took the sons and daughters of the new Andal lords as wards and fosterlings to serve as pages and cupbearers, as well as hostages, at Casterly Rock.

name: Gerold III Lannister
bio: Gerold III Lannister was a King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Gerold died without male issues, thus a council crowned his only daughter's husband, Ser Joffrey_Lannister, who took the Lannister name and became the first Andal to rule Casterly Rock.

name: Gerold Templeton
bio: Gerold Templeton was a squire from House Templeton during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Gerold was among the group of squires who kept company with Princess Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I). By the end of 78 AC the princess fell out with Gerold and the other squires, as she decided she hated them.

name: Gerren
bio: Gerren is one of the criminals Yoren picked for the Night's Watch when he cleaned out the dungeons of King's Landing.

name: Gerrick Kingsblood
bio: Gerrick Kingsblood is a raider from the free folk and the founder of House Redbeard. He has a son and three daughters. Gerrick boasts of descent from Raymun_Redbeard, a King-Beyond-the-Wall who invaded the north but was killed along with his sons in the battle at Long Lake. Tormund claims Gerrick actually descends from Raymun's younger brother, the Red_Raven_(free_folk).

name: Gerrick Kingsblood's son
bio: Gerrick Kingsblood's son is a member of the newly fashioned House Redbeard.

name: Gerrick Kingsblood's eldest daughter
bio: Gerrick_Kingsblood's first daughter is a member of the newly fashioned House Redbeard.

name: Gerrick Kingsblood's second daughter
bio: Gerrick_Kingsblood's second daughter is a member of the newly fashioned House Redbeard.

name: Gerrick Kingsblood's youngest daughter
bio: Gerrick_Kingsblood's youngest daughter is a member of the newly fashioned House Redbeard.

name: Gerris Drinkwater
bio: Gerris Drinkwater is a knight from House Drinkwater and a long-time friend of Prince Quentyn_Martell. Gerris is sworn to House Yronwood. Gerris once led Quentyn_Martell and some other friends into the Red Mountains to find the lair of the Vulture King. Quentyn's first kiss was with one of Gerris's Drinkwater_twins.

name: Black Gerrold
bio: Black Gerrold is a sellsword and a member of the Windblown.

name: Gerrold Redback
bio: Gerrold Redback is a sellsword and a member of the Windblown. He is so named to differentiate from Black_Gerrold.

name: Gessio Haratis
bio: Gessio Haratis was the author of Before the Dragons, which claims that the city of Pentos already existed before the Valyrians expanded to the Narrow Sea. He lived during the Century of Blood, when Volantis threatened to restore the Valyrian empire under their rule.

name: Gevin Harlaw
bio: Gevin Harlaw is a member of House Harlaw and an ironborn raider. His relation to Lord Rodrik_Harlaw, the head of the house, is currently unknown.

name: Ghael
bio: Ghael is an envoy of King Cleon of Astapor. He is a former slave.

name: Gharak Squint-Eye
bio: Gharak Squint-Eye was a jhattar of the Jogos Nhai. According to legend Gharak slew the last of the Jhogwin at the Battle in the Howling Hills.

name: Ghazdor zo Ahlaq
bio: Ghazdor zo Ahlaq is a Yunkish nobleman. The men of the Windblown mock him as Lord Wobblecheeks.

name: Ghost of High Heart
bio: The ghost of High Heart is an old, stooped, very short woman who has visions of the future and the past. She lives at High Heart in the riverlands. When Jenny_of_Oldstones came to court after marrying Prince Duncan_Targaryen, she was accompanied by her friend, a dwarf albino woman who was reputed to be a woods witch in the riverlands. Lady Jenny claimed that she was one of the children of the forest, although this was not true. After hearing the woods witch's prophecy that the prince that was promised would come from the line of Aerys_II_Targaryen and Rhaella_Targaryen, their father Prince Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen insisted that they marry. The woods witch was present at the tragic destruction of Summerhall in 259 AC, and was believed to have died there. However, she somehow survived and returned to the riverlands, where she mourns for her friend Jenny.

name: Giant of the Trident
bio: The Giant of the Trident was a robber knight who troubled the Trident in the riverlands during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. Prince Maegor_I_Targaryen won renown by defeating the Giant of the Trident in 31 AC.

name: Gilbert Farring
bio: Gilbert Farring is a knight of House Farring. His son is Bryen_Farring, a young squire of Stannis_Baratheon. He is the cousin of Ser Godry_Farring.

name: Gilbert of the Vines
bio: Gilbert of the Vines was a legendary son of Garth_Greenhand and the founder of House Redwyne. He taught the men of the Arbor to make wine.

name: Gillam
bio: Gillam is a brother of the order on the Quiet Isle.

name: Gilliane Glover
bio: Gilliane Glover was a noblewoman from House Glover. She was married to Lord Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Benjen) of Winterfell, with whom she had two sons: Cregan_Stark and an unnamed younger son.

name: Gilly
bio: Gilly is a wildling girl, a pregnant daughter of Craster and the youngest of his wives. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Gilly is portrayed by Hannah Murray. Gilly was born to Craster and one of his many wives. She was named for the gillyflower.[N 1] Gilly's_mother raised her to be honest, beating the lying out of the girl when she was very young. Gilly lived the first decade and a half of her life without seeing anything other than Craster's Keep and the woods around it, never going further than half a league away. Although familiar with streams and rivers, she never saw a lake. When Gilly was old enough, at some point during the summer, she married her father. On the day of their wedding, they drank the sweetest mead. At some point after the wedding, Gilly became pregnant with Craster's child. By 299 AC, Gilly is Craster's youngest wife.

name: Gilly's mother
bio: Gilly's mother is a wildling and one of the many wives of Craster. She is the mother of Gilly, and her given name is unknown.

name: Gilwood Hunter
bio: Gilwood Hunter is a knight of House Hunter of Longbow Hall. He is the eldest son and heir of Lord Eon_Hunter.

name: Girl bought to please the king
bio: The girl bought to please the king is one of Illyrio_Mopatis's slaves in his manse in Pentos. Her given name is not mentioned. The girl was trained as a bed slave in Lys and was taught the Common Tongue at some point. She was bought to please the king, Illyrio_Mopatis's one-time guest Viserys_Targaryen, in the bedchamber.

name: Gladden Wylde
bio: Gladden Wylde is a knight of House Wylde.

name: Glaive
bio: Archmaester Glaive was an archmaester at the Citadel born in the stormlands. He proposed that the "King of a Thousand Years", Durran Godsgrief, was actually a succession of monarchs all bearing the same name.

name: Glendon Flowers
bio: Glendon Flowers was a hedge knight during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. He referred to himself as Glendon Ball, but was mockingly referred to as the Knight of the Pussywillows. Glendon claimed to be the son of Ser Quentyn_Ball, a renowned knight known as "Fireball", and used the personal coat-of-arms of his alleged father, a fireball blazing red and yellow across a night black field, as his own. Glendon was born to a camp follower, known as Penny_Jenny. Penny claimed that Glendon's father was Ser Quentyn_Ball. After the war, Jenny came to work in a brothel called the Pussywillows, where Glendon and his sister were raised. After Penny died, Glendon and his sister were looked after by the other women working at the Pussywillows. Glendon was taught the knightly arts by an old squire that lived nearby the brothel. In 211 AC, half a year before the Whitewalls tourney, Glendon was knighted by Ser Morgan_Dunstable in front of two dozen witnesses, supposedly in exchange for Glendon's sister giving up her maidenhead to Morgan.

name: Glendon Goode
bio: Glendon Goode was a knight from House Goode during the Dance of the Dragons, who became a member of Rhaenyra_Targaryen's Queensguard. During the Dance of the Dragons, Ser Glendon sided with the blacks. After Rhaenyra seized King's Landing, he was made one of her Queensguard, at twenty years of age. A month later, he became Lord Commander after Ser Lorent_Marbrand, whose body Glendon recovered, was killed during the first day of the Riot of King's Landing. He passed into history as one of the Seven Who Rode, seven knights that went after Prince Joffrey_Velaryon when the prince mounted the dragon Syrax to fly to the Dragonpit to protect it from the rioting mob. Glendon and his fellow knights managed to reclaim the body of the fallen prince, but he was attacked from behind by a man who set his white cloak afire with a torch. He was then thrown out of the saddle by his horse and swarmed by a mob, which tore him to pieces. He had been Lord Commander for less than a day.

name: Glendon Hewett
bio: Glendon Hewett is a knight of House Hewett and a member of the Night's Watch stationed at Eastwatch-by-the-Sea. His relation to Lord Humfrey_Hewett of Oakenshield is unknown.

name: Glover steward
bio: The Glover steward is the steward for House Glover at Deepwood Motte.

name: Goady
bio: Goady is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Goat boy
bio: The goat boy is a deformed slave owned by Yezzan_zo_Qaggaz.  He is a part of Yezzan’s grotesquerie, one of Yezzan's "special treasures".

name: God-on-Earth
bio: The God-on-Earth was the legendary first ruler of the mythic Great Empire of the Dawn. According to the tales told by the priestly scribes of Yin, he was the only begotten son of the Maiden-Made-of-Light and the Lion of Night, and traveled about his domains in a palanquin carved from a single pearl and carried by a hundred queens, his wives. He ruled for ten thousand years, in which the empire flourished, until at last he ascended to the stars to join his forebears. He was succeeded by his eldest son, the Pearl_Emperor.

name: Godric Borrell
bio: Godric Borrell is the Lord of Sweetsister, Shield of Sisterton, Master of Breakwater Castle, Keeper of the Night Lamp, and the head of House Borrell. During Robert's Rebellion, Eddard_Stark washed up on Sweetsister while trying to sail to the north to raise his banners. Godric's father, Lord_Borrell_(father_of_Godric), could easily have turned Eddard over to King Aerys_II_Targaryen for a reward. Godric was in attendance when his father instead freed Ned. Because the Three Sisters have long tolerated smugglers, Lord Stannis_Baratheon, master of ships for King Robert_I_Baratheon, once sent a fleet to Sisterton without Godric's leave and made him hang a dozen friends. Stannis threatened to hang Godric as well should some ship sink because the Night Lamp had gone black. Godric has daughters but no sons, and his granddaughters include Gella.

name: Godry Farring
bio: Godry Farring, called Godry the Giantslayer, is a knight of House Farring and a supporter of Stannis_Baratheon. He is the cousin of Ser Gilbert_Farring.

name: Godwyn
bio: Godwyn is a guard loyal to House Lannister. He is one of the red cloaks assigned to the royal family in King's Landing.

name: Goghor the Giant
bio: Goghor the Giant is a pit fighter famed in Meereen. His origin is unknown, but he supports the pit fighting culture of the city.

name: Goodwife
bio: The Goodwife was the first fool at the royal court in the Red Keep. His given name is unknown. The Goodwife was among the many artists brought to the Red Keep by Queen Alysanne_Targaryen after her husband, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, began to rule in his own right. When Alysanne relocated to Dragonstone following the death of Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I), the queen was accompanied by her lady attendants and the entertaining Goodwife.

name: Goodwin
bio: Goodwin was a knight and the master-at-arms at Evenfall Hall, in service to House Tarth. Ser Goodwin fought in the Stepstones. He had a friend during the War of the Ninepenny Kings, a fierce warrior in the yard who died young when he hesitated in killing a man. Goodwin taught Brienne of Tarth how to fight. He has been dead for a long time.

name: Goodwyn
bio: Goodwyn was an archmaester of the Citadel during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Fearing that Septon Mattheus might become the next High Septon after the death of the High_Lickspittle in 54 AC, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and his sister-wife, Queen Alysanne_Targaryen flew to Oldtown to oversee the election of the new High Septon. When the royal couple arrived at Oldtown, the High Septon had already been interred in the crypts beneath the Starry Sept. Jaehaerys gave a eulogy nonetheless, addressing a huge crowd of septons, maesters, and smallfolk in the plaza. At the end of his remarks, he announced that he and the queen would remain in Oldtown until the Alfyn_(septon) had been chosen. Afterward, Goodwyn would wrote about the reaction of the crowd: "The smallfolk cheered, the maesters nodded sagely, and the septons looked at one another and thought on dragons."

name: Gordan I Gardener
bio: Gordan I Gardener, called Grey-Eyes, was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener.

name: Goren Greyjoy
bio: Goren Greyjoy was the Lord of the Iron Islands, Lord Reaper of Pyke, and the head of House Greyjoy during the final years of the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen, as well as the reign of King Aenys_I_Targaryen. Goren was the son and heir of Lord Vickon_Greyjoy, who ruled the Iron Islands in the name of Aegon_I_Targaryen. Having allowed septons of the Faith of the Seven to preach on the islands, Vickon's decree angered pious ironborn and priests of the Drowned God, but Vickon reminded Goren that he was Aegon's man and that only a fool would rise against House Targaryen and their dragons. In 33 AC, Goren succeeded his father as Lord of the Iron Islands and Lord Reaper of Pyke. Not long after, he easily put down a clumsy conspiracy which attempted to crown Qhorin_Volmark's son. In 37 AC, King Aenys_I_Targaryen succeeded Aegon the Conqueror on the Iron Throne. Among the multiple rebellions which broke out in the Seven Kingdoms, one took place on the Iron Islands, where a man claimed to be Lodos_the_Twice-Drowned, returned at last from the watery halls of the Drowned God. To end Lodos the Twice-Drowned's revolt, Goren quickly descended on Old Wyk and Great Wyk, where most of Lodos's followers could be found, with a hundred longships and put thousands of them to the sword. Goren sent to Aenys the head of Lodos pickled in brine. In return, Aenys granted Goren any boon within his power to give. Wanting to prove himself a true son of the Drowned God, Goren requested permission to expel the septons and septas who had come to the Iron Islands since Aegon's Conquest, preaching the Faith of the Seven. Aenys was unable to refuse. Goren was among those granted gold, honors, and offices by a grateful King Aenys I Targaryen.

name: Gorghan of Old Ghis
bio: Gorghan of Old Ghis was an ancient scholar from Old Ghis.

name: Gorm
bio: Gorm the Wolf was one of the four chieftains who ruled Hardhome in the time prior to its destruction. Under Gorm the Wolf's protection, Maester Wyllis established himself in Hardhome as a healer and counselor. However, Gorm was killed in a drunken brawl, forcing Wyllis to flee back to Oldtown.

name: Gormon Massey
bio: Gormon Massey was the Lord of Stonedance and the head of House Massey during the reigns of King Viserys_I_Targaryen and Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Gormon is portrayed by James Dreyfus. As predicted by Ser Otto_Hightower, upon the death of King Viserys_I_Targaryen, Lord Gormon Massey sided with Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen and the blacks during the Dance of the Dragons, sitting among the lesser lords of the black council at Dragonstone. Lord Massey counted himself among Rhaenyra's loyalists, well-aware that King Aegon_II_Targaryen would name him a traitor, having already received a summons from King's Landing demanding an oath of loyalty from him. When Prince Jacaerys_Velaryon called for dragonriders, Gormon made an attempt to ride Vermithor, but was burned to death on approach.

name: Gormon Peake
bio: Gormon Peake, called Gormy by his close friend Daemon_II_Blackfyre, was the Lord of Starpike and the head of House Peake during the first two Blackfyre Rebellions.

name: Gormon Tyrell
bio: Gormon, born Gormon Tyrell, is a maester serving at the Citadel. He is an uncle of Mace_Tyrell, Lord of Highgarden.

name: Gormond Drumm
bio: Gormond Drumm, known as Gormond the Oldfather, was a member of House Drumm. Gormond is famous for fathering a hundred sons.

name: Gormond Goodbrother
bio: Gormond Goodbrother is the third son of Lord Gorold_Goodbrother. Gormond was ritually drowned in service to the Drowned God on his name day.

name: Gorne
bio: Gorne was a King-Beyond-the-Wall, a title that he shared with his brother Gendel, three thousand years ago. Maester Herryk's History of the Kings-Beyond-the-Wall transcribes how Gendel and Gorne were asked to mediate a dispute between a clan of children of the forest and a family of giants, who argued over the ownership of a cavern. Gorne and Gendel eventually used trickery to resolve the issue, after discovering the cavern was part of a chain of caverns that passed beneath the Wall. According to Maester Herryk's history, Gorne and Gendel made both the giants and the children swear no longer to desire the cavern. The passage underneath the Wall became known as Gorne's Way. In search of conquest, Gorne and Gendel led the free folk south through Gorne's Way, allowing them to evade the Night's Watch. Gorne and Gendel fought against the King in the North, and Gorne killed the King in the North. However, the king's son took up his fallen father's crown and killed Gorne. According to northern stories, Gendel died as well, although both maesters and wildlings claim Gendel survived the battle and led the survivors back underneath the Wall to the lands north of the Wall. However, not knowing the caves as well as his brother had, Gendel became lost, and none of his people ever emerged.

name: Gorold Goodbrother
bio: Gorold Goodbrother is the Lord of Hammerhorn and the head of House Goodbrother. He has twelve daughters and three sons.

name: Gorys Edoryen
bio: Gorys Edoryen is a Volantene sellsword and the paymaster of the Golden Company. Gorys is originally from Volantis. He replaced Ser Harry_Strickland as the Golden Company's paymaster after Strickland's promotion to captain-general.

name: Gorzhak zo Eraz
bio: Gorzhak zo Eraz, also called Pudding Face, is a Yunkish nobleman.

name: Gowen Baratheon
bio: Gowen Baratheon was the third son of a reigning Lord of Storm's End. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Gowen is portrayed by Connan Aaron. Gowen was married to Lady Tya_Lannister. Their wedding, circa 208 AC, was the last between houses Lannister and Baratheon until the marriage of King Robert_I_Baratheon and Queen Cersei_Lannister some seventy-six years later.[N 1] Gowen and Tya had one son, a "large and lusty lad" born with a full head of black hair, who died in his infancy.

name: Gramyon
bio: Archmaester Gramyon was an Archmaester at the Citadel of Oldtown. He wrote Remnants of the Dragonlords, in which he claimed the Temple of the Lord of Light in Volantis was thrice as large as King's Landing's Great Sept of Baelor.

name: Gran Goodbrother
bio: Gran Goodbrother is the second son of Lord Gorold_Goodbrother.

name: Grance Morrigen
bio: Grance Morrigen was a knight from House Morrigen during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen.[N 1] Grance participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC. Ser Steffon_Fossoway offered to practice with him.

name: Lord Grandison
bio: Lord Grandison was the Lord of Grandview and the head of House Grandison during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. During Robert's Rebellion, Lord Grandison remained faithful to House Targaryen but was captured by his liege lord, Robert_I_Baratheon, in the battles at Summerhall. He became a loyal ally of Robert and fought for him during the rest of the war. He was wounded at the battle of the Trident and died of his wounds a year later.

name: Gravedigger
bio: A gravedigger is a novice on the Quiet Isle. His true name is not known. As his moniker suggests he digs graves on the island.

name: Grazdan
bio: Grazdan is a Good Master of Astapor. He was presumably named after Grazdan_the_Great, who founded Old Ghis. He is fat.

name: Grazdan
bio: Grazdan is a Good Master of Astapor. He was presumably named after Grazdan_the_Great, who founded Old Ghis. He has a spiked beard, and speaks the Common Tongue, though not well.

name: Grazdan
bio: Grazdan is a Good Master of Astapor. He was presumably named after Grazdan_the_Great, who founded Old Ghis.

name: Grazdan the Great
bio: Grazdan the Great is the legendary founder of Old Ghis. According to legend, Grazdan founded the lockstep legions, with which Old Ghis colonized its surroundings and subjugated its neighbors, forming the first empire. Grazdan is also credited with raising the ancient brick walls of Old Ghis.

name: Grazdan mo Eraz
bio: Grazdan mo Eraz is one of the Wise Masters of Yunkai. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is renamed Razdal mo Eraz and portrayed by George Georgiou.

name: Grazdan mo Ullhor
bio: Grazdan mo Ullhor is one of the Good Masters of Astapor. He was presumably named after Grazdan the Great, who founded Old Ghis. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is renamed Greizhen mo Ullhor and portrayed by Clifford Barry. Among Grazdan's many slaves is Cleon, a very talented butcher.

name: Grazdan zo Galare
bio: Grazdan zo Galare is a former slave merchant in Meereen. He is the cousin of Galazza_Galare, the Green Grace.

name: Grazhar zo Galare
bio: Grazhar (or Grazdar) is a boy of the House of Galare, one of the noble ruling families of Meereen. His sister is Qezza. He is a cousin to Galazza_Galare, the Green Grace of Meereen.

name: Grazdhan zo Rhaezn
bio: Grazdhan zo Rhaezn is a Yunkish nobleman.

name: Great Walrus
bio: The Great Walrus is a free folk leader among the the people of the Frozen Shore.

name: Greatheart
bio: The Greatheart was a member of the Kingsguard. He is considered one of the greatest to ever don the white cloak.

name: Green Gergen
bio: Green Gergen is a man-at-arms. He was part of the Iron Throne host assembled at Eddard_Stark's command and sent to stop Ser Gregor_Clegane's attacks against the Riverlands.

name: Greenbeard
bio: Pello of Tyrosh, better known as Greenbeard, is a Tyroshi member of the brotherhood without banners and one of its leaders. Tyrion_Lannister learned some of the tongue of Tyrosh from a sellsword at Casterly Rock. It is unknown if he is related to Greenbeard or Tyroshi_sellsword.

name: Gregor Clegane
bio: Gregor Clegane, the Knight of Clegane's Keep and the head of House Clegane, is a landed knight and bannerman to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Gregor is a freakishly large man and for this he is known as the Mountain That Rides or simply the Mountain. His soldiers, the Mountain's men, often refer to him simply as Ser. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Gregor is played by Conan Stevens (Season 1), Ian Whyte (Season 2), and Hafþór Júlíus Björnsson (Season 4 - Season 8). As a young boy, eleven or twelve years old, Gregor caused his brother Sandor_Clegane's facial scars by holding the younger boy's face to a hot brazier as punishment for playing with a toy he had discarded. Their father spread the story that the injuries were caused by bedding that had caught fire.
