Four years later, Gregor was knighted by Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen, presumably prior to the tourney of Harrenhal. Gregor is a suspected kinslayer; there are rumors that Gregor killed his father, sister, and his first two wives. His keep is said to be a grim place where servants vanish unaccountably and even the dogs are afraid to enter the halls. On the day that Gregor came into his inheritance, Sandor entered the service of House Lannister, never to return. At the age of seventeen, Gregor participated in the Sack of King's Landing during Robert's Rebellion. He and Ser Amory_Lorch scaled Maegor's Holdfast when the Lannister troops reached the Red Keep. Gregor entered the nursery of the infant Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar), son of Prince Rhaegar, and killed the baby present by dashing the boy's head against a wall. Then he raped and murdered Aegon's mother, Princess Elia_Martell, supposedly with the boy's blood and brains still on his hands. The identities of the two murderers have not been publicly confirmed in the recorded history, although Gregor's involvement is common knowledge at Casterly Rock. Gregor participated in the suppression of Greyjoy's Rebellion.

name: Gregor Goode
bio: Gregor Goode was a knight from House Goode during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. He had a brother, Ser Griffith_Goode. Ser Gregor was chosen by Queen Visenya_Targaryen, alongside his brother Ser Griffith_Goode, to be one of the first sworn brothers of the Kingsguard when the order was founded in 10 AC by King Aegon_I_Targaryen.

name: Grenn
bio: Grenn is a recruit of the Night's Watch and friend of Jon_Snow. He is given the nickname Aurochs by Ser Alliser_Thorne. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Grenn is portrayed by Mark Stanley.

name: Gretchel
bio: Gretchel is a serving woman in service to House Arryn at the Eyrie.

name: Grey King
bio: The Grey King is a legendary monarch of the Iron Islands who is said to have ruled for 1,007 years during the Age of Heroes. Almost all of the great houses of the isles claim descent from him, including the ruling Greyjoys. The exceptions are the Goodbrothers, who claim descent from the Grey King's leal eldest brother. The Grey King's given name is unknown. Each great house from the Iron Islands claims to be descended from the Grey King, aside from the Goodbrothers. At a kingsmoot on Old Wyk called by Galon_Whitestaff, Urras_Greyiron was chosen as the first High King of the Iron Islands and the first monarch to rule all of the ironborn since the Grey King. Vickon_Greyjoy, a descendent of the Grey King, was chosen as the new Lord of the Iron Islands after Aegon's Conquest.

name: Grey Worm
bio: Grey Worm is an Unsullied purchased by and sworn to Daenerys_Targaryen. He is the commander of Daenerys's Unsullied and one of her trusted advisors. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Grey Worm is portrayed by Jacob Anderson.

name: Greydon
bio: Greydon was a Grand Maester of the Citadel who wrote a definitive history of the Triarchy in 182 AC, fifty-three years after the beginning of the Dance of the Dragons.

name: Greydon Gardener
bio: Greydon Gardener was a King of the Reach and a head of House Gardener. Greydon attempted an invasion of Dorne, but was thrown back by Princess Nymeria.

name: Greydon Goodbrother
bio: Greydon Goodbrother is the eldest son and heir of Gorold_Goodbrother, Lord of Hammerhorn.

name: Griffin King
bio: The Griffin King is a legendary figure of the Vale who lived thousands of years ago. His connection to griffins is unknown. According to legend, Ser Artys Arryn, the Winged Knight, flew to the top of Giant's Lance on the back of a giant falcon to slay the Griffin King and thereby claim the Vale. Maesters are skeptical, however, and believe that the actual Artys Arryn, an Andal warlord, became conflated with the legends of a First Men hero who may have lived thousands of years before the coming of the Andals.

name: Griffith Goode
bio: Griffith Goode was a knight from House Goode during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. He had a brother, Ser Gregor_Goode. Ser Griffith was chosen by Queen Visenya_Targaryen, alongside his brother Ser Gregor_Goode, to be one of the first sworn brothers of the Kingsguard when the order was founded in 10 AC by King Aegon_I_Targaryen.

name: Grigg the Goat
bio: Grigg the Goat is a free folk raider.

name: Grimtongue
bio: Grimtongue is one of the crew on Asha_Greyjoy's ship Black Wind.

name: Grisel
bio: Grisel is a member of House Baelish's household staff. Grisel was Lord Petyr_Baelish's wet nurse but now keeps his tower.

name: Grisella
bio: Grisella was a female wildling skinchanger who could inhabit the body of a goat. Grisella was one of the skinchangers that Varamyr met at a meeting of wargs. She is long dead.

name: Groleo
bio: Groleo is the captain of the cog Saduleon, renamed the Balerion.

name: Grover Tully
bio: Grover Tully was the Lord of Riverrun, Lord Paramount of the Trident, and the head of House Tully during the late reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen and the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. Lord Grover supported Prince Viserys_I_Targaryen instead of Laenor_Velaryon at the Great Council in 101 AC, favoring the rights of the male claimant. When Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen visited the Trident in 112 AC, a son of Lord Tully attempted to court the young princess, vying for her favor. When the Dance of the Dragons broke out in 129 AC, Grover wanted to support King Aegon_II_Targaryen and the greens, but he was bedridden and too aged to act. His grandson and heir, Ser Elmo_Tully, who preferred the claim of Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, kept the Tully banners at Riverrun at the start of the war. Grover passed away soon after the Second Battle of Tumbleton and he was succeeded by Elmo. According to Mushroom, Grover died of apoplexy at his grandson's actions.

name: Grubbs
bio: Grubbs is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Grunt
bio: Grunt is a man-at-arms sworn to House Bolton. He  is one of the Bastard's Boys.

name: Gueren
bio: Gueren is a member of the Night's Watch. He is a wandering crow, a recruiter who scours the Seven Kingdoms in search of new recruits for the Watch.

name: Gulian
bio: Maester Gulian is a maester of the Citadel.

name: Gulian Qorgyle
bio: Gulian Qorgyle is a knight of House Qorgyle, the first son and heir of Lord Quentyn_Qorgyle of Sandstone. He has a younger brother, Ser Arron_Qorgyle.

name: Gulian Swann
bio: Gulian Swann is Lord of Stonehelm and head of House Swann.

name: Guncer Sunglass
bio: Guncer Sunglass is the Lord of Sweetport Sound and head of House Sunglass. He has at least Lord_Sunglass.

name: Gundor Goodbrother
bio: Gundor Goodbrother was a member of House Goodbrother who founded Kayce. According to a semi-canon source, Gundor founded Kayce on the coast of the westerlands when House Casterly still held Casterly Rock.

name: Gunthor
bio: Gunthor, known as Gunthor son of Gurn because of Gurn, is a leader of the Stone Crows, one of the Vale mountain clans from the Mountains of the Moon.

name: Gunthor Darklyn
bio: Gunthor Darklyn was the Lord of Duskendale and the head of House Darklyn during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. His uncle was Ser Steffon_Darklyn of the Kingsguard. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Gunthor is portrayed by Steven Pacey. At the outbreak of the Dance of the Dragons, Gunthor was among the lesser lords that gathered at Dragonstone and sat on the black council in support of Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen. Lord Darklyn counted himself among Rhaenyra's greatest supporters, well-aware that King Aegon_II_Targaryen would name him a traitor, having already received a summons from King's Landing demanding an oath of loyalty from him. Shortly after, his uncle, Ser Steffon_Darklyn, defected from Aegon II's Kingsguard with the crown of Rhaenyra's father, King Viserys_I_Targaryen. Soon after, Ser Criston_Cole led a host from King's Landing to move against the nearby lords sworn to Rhaenyra. Duskendale was among the first targets and the town's defenders were taken by surprise. In the subsequent sack of Duskendale, the town was sacked, the harbor was set afire, and Lord Darklyn was beheaded by Cole. Most of Gunthor's garrison chose to swear fealty to Aegon II, although a few followed Lord Darklyn in death. With Gunthor's death, his widow, Lady Meredyth_Darklyn, took control of Duskendale. Although amongst Rhaenyra's greatest supporters, House Darklyn suffered heavily for its loyalty to the princess, with Gunthor and Steffon both dying for her cause. As a result, when Rhaenyra was forced to flee to Dragonstone, Lady Meredyth reluctantly hosted her at Duskendale, but only for a brief time.

name: Gunthor Estermont
bio: Gunthor Estermont was a knight of House Estermont during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He was the son and heir of the Lord Estermont of Greenstone Gunthor competed in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Gunthor Goodbrother
bio: Gunthor Goodbrother was a nobleman from House Goodbrother during the Dance of the Dragons. After Lord Dalton_Greyjoy was killed by his own salt wife Tess at Faircastle in 133 AC, the smallfolk and remaining knights of Fair Isle rose up in rebellion against the ironborn. Alongside the sly Alester_Wynch, Gunthor was one of the commanders of the Greyjoy garrison at Faircastle, composed of the Red Kraken's close companions and brothers-in-battle. While the ironborn fled the island or were otherwise murdered by its people, the garrison at Faircastle held out stubbornly. In a dispute over another of the Red Kraken's salt widows, Lysa_Farman, Gunthor slew Alester Wynch. The longships thus fled and Fair Isle was free.

name: Gunthor Hightower
bio: Gunthor Hightower is a knight of House Hightower. He is the third son of Lord Leyton_Hightower and is married to Jeyne_Fossoway. Ser Gunthor studied at the Citadel for several years, during which time he learned to speak the Summer Tongue.

name: Gunthor Royce
bio: Gunthor Royce, called the Bronze Giant, was the Lord of Runestone and the head of House Royce during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. After her death in 115 AC, the Lady of Runestone, Rhea_Royce, was succeeded by her nephew. It is unknown when and how Gunthor came to his lordship. During the dispute over succession after Lady Jeyne_Arryn's death, the claimant to the Eyrie, Ser Arnold_Arryn, eluded Ser Corwyn_Corbray, who claimed that Lady Jeyne's testament must be upheld and that her more distant cousin Ser Joffrey_Arryn should become Lord of the Eyrie. Ser Arnold was given sanctuary at Runestone by Lord Gunthor, having served as a squire there in his youth. When Ser Corwyn tried to winkle him out, Lord Gunthor rode out to confront him in his ancient bronze armor. Words grew heated, then turned to curses, then threats, and Ser Corwyn drew Lady Forlorn, though whether as a threat or to strike at Lord Royce is unknown. A crossbowman on Runestone's battlements loosed a quarrel, and pierced Ser Corwyn through the breast. Striking down one of the king's regents was an act of treason, and war began anew across the Vale of Arryn. House Royce, joined by their vassals, the Coldwaters and the Tolletts, supported Ser Arnold's claim. By the time the new regents were to be chosen by lot in 136 AC, Lord Alyn_Velaryon and Lord Benjicot_Blackwood had at last forced the Bronze Giant and the supporters of Isembard_Arryn and Arnold Arryn to do homage to Lord Joffrey as their liege. Gunthor made his way to King's Landing for the lottery to select new regents with Lord Joffrey Arryn, Lord Quenton_Corbray, and Isembard Arryn.

name: Gurn
bio: Gurn is the father of Gunthor, a member of the Stone Crows in the Mountains of the Moon. It is unknown if Gurn is still alive.

name: Guthor Grimm
bio: Guthor Grimm is the Lord of Greyshield in the Shield Islands and the head of House Grimm.

name: Guy Lothston
bio: Guy Lothston, known as Guy the Glutton, was a knight from House Lothston during the final days of King Aenys_I_Targaryen and the coronation of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Ser Guy was present at Visenya's Hill when King Maegor was challenged by Ser Damon_Morrigen, Grand Captain of the Warrior's Sons, to a trial of seven. He kept quiet when Maegor called upon those who would stand beside him but was shamed by the example of Dick_Bean, a simple man-at-arms, and the appeal of Ser Bernarr_Brune and became one of the king's champions. According to one chronicler, when he was cut open during the fight, the remains of forty half-digested pies spilled out of his belly.

name: Guyard Morrigen
bio: Guyard Morrigen, also known as Guyard the Green, is a famous knight from House Morrigen in the stormlands. He is a younger brother of Lester_Morrigen, Lord of Crow's Nest.

name: Guyle the Cunning
bio: Guyle the Cunning was an infamous hedge knight during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Guyle participated in the grand mêlée in the Dragonpit in the tourney for the completion of the Dragonpit in 55 AC.

name: Guyne
bio: Archmaester  Guyne is an archmaester at the  Citadel in Oldtown.

name: Gwayne Corbray
bio: Gwayne Corbray was a knight from House Corbray and member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He wielded the Valyrian steel sword Lady Forlorn. Ser Gwayne fought during the First Blackfyre Rebellion in 196 AC, at the Battle of the Redgrass Field in Lord Donnel_Arryn's vanguard. Lord Arryn's lines were shattered and nearly overrun by Daemon_I_Blackfyre, until Gwayne appeared with reinforcements. Gwayne came across Daemon and the two dueled ahorsed for nearly an hour. The duel was considered a sight to behold, partly because of the clashing of two Valyrian steel swords against each other, with Gwayne wielding Lady Forlorn and Daemon with Blackfyre. Ultimately, Daemon cut through Gwayne's helm and left him blind and bleeding. Daemon dismounted to ensure the wounded knight was not trampled and ordered Redtusk to bring Gwayne to the maesters in the rear to see to his wounds. Daemon's duel and subsequent compassion to Gwayne was a fatal error, as it allowed for Lord Brynden_Rivers to position his Raven's Teeth atop a ridge and rain arrows upon Daemon and his twin sons, Aegon_Blackfyre and Aemon_Blackfyre.

name: Gwayne I Gardener
bio: Gwayne I Gardener, called the Gallant, was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener.

name: Gwayne III Gardener
bio: Gwayne III Gardener, called the Fat, was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. Gwayne persuaded Lord Peake and Lord Manderly to accept his judgement on their quarrel and do fealty for their lands without any bloodshed. In the annals of history the name of Gwayne III Gardener is writ large.

name: Gwayne IV Gardener
bio: Gwayne IV Gardener, called the Gods-fearing, was a King of the Reach and a head of House Gardener. Generations before the Andals arrived in the Reach, Gwayne sent out his warriors in search for the children of the Forest in the hope that their greenseers and magic could halt the invaders.

name: Gwayne V Gardener
bio: Gwayne V Gardener was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. Along with his Garth_IX_Gardener and Merle_I_Gardener, he is remembered as one of the Three Sage Kings who dealt with the Andal invasion of the Reach with a policy of accord and assimilation rather then armed resistance. Whereas his father converted to the Faith of the Seven, Gwayne V was the first Gardener born into the new Faith, as well as the first to be made a knight. Like his father, he took an Andal maiden to wife as a means of binding the bride's father to the realm. He also took Andals into his service as retainers and household knights, among them Ser Alester Tyrell, whose prowess at arms was such that he was made the king's champion and sworn shield. Gwayne bequeathed lands and wives and lordships for the more powerful of the Andal kings and other Andal adventurers descending on the Reach in exchange for pledges of fealty. Like his grandfather and father, Gwayne V likewise sought after Andal craftsmen, especially blacksmiths and stonemasons, to settle throughout the Reach, supplying him and his bannermen with iron instead of bronze and strengthening their castles and holdfasts with Andal masonry.

name: Gwayne Gardener
bio: Gwayne Gardener was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. Gwayne Gardener was the son of King Perceon_III_Gardener. His own numeric is unknown. He was married to the daughter of Lord Lorimar_Peake, who became the seventh Peake maiden to become Queen of All the Reach.

name: Gwayne Gaunt
bio: Gwayne Gaunt was a knight from House Gaunt and member of King Aerys_II_Targaryen's Kingsguard. In 277 AC, when King Aerys_II_Targaryen accepted the invitation of Lord Denys_Darklyn and traveled to Duskendale, Ser Gwayne accompanied him as part of the king's small escort. Upon arrival, however, the king was taken captive, and Gwayne was slain by Ser Symon_Hollard, the master-at-arms to Lord Denys, in an attempt to defend his king. His death was later avenged by his sworn brother, Ser Barristan_Selmy.

name: Gwayne Hightower
bio: Gwayne Hightower was a knight from House Hightower and an officer of the City Watch of King's Landing. He was the youngest son of Ser Otto_Hightower, and one of the brothers of Queen Alicent_Hightower, the second wife of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Gwayne is portrayed by Freddie Fox in the second season. In the first season, he was portrayed by stunt performer Will Willoughby. Gwayne came to court at the Red Keep in King's Landing in 101 AC with his siblings and mother when his father, Ser Otto_Hightower, was named Hand of the King by King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 111 AC, Gwayne participated in a tourney in King's Landing to celebrate the fifth anniversary of King Viserys_I_Targaryen's marriage to Gwayne's sister, Queen Alicent_Hightower. Gwayne served as one of his sister's champions during the tourney, but he was unhorsed by Ser Criston_Cole. When the Dance of the Dragons began in 129 AC, Gwayne was named the second-in-command of the City Watch of King's Landing by his father, who instructed him to keep an eye on Ser Luthor_Largent, the Commander of the City Watch, for any signs of disloyalty towards his nephew and the newly crowned King Aegon_II_Targaryen. Gwayne and Ser Arryk_Cargyll were in the retinue of Grand Maester Orwyle when he traveled from King's Landing to Dragonstone in an unsuccessful attempt to reconcile Aegon II and his half-sister, Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen. When King's Landing was attacked by Queen Rhaenyra's blacks in 130 AC, Gwayne rushed to the stables to sound the alarm, but he was seized, disarmed, and dragged before his own commander, Luthor Largent, who was still loyal to Prince Daemon_Targaryen. When Gwayne denounced him as a turncloak, Ser Luthor drove his sword through Hightower's belly and ordered the city gates to be opened to the blacks.

name: Gwayne Oakheart
bio: Gwayne Oakheart was a knight of House Oakheart during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Gwayne participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Gwenys Rivers
bio: Gwenys Rivers was the second of the three bastard children of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen and his sixth mistress, Lady Melissa_Blackwood. Melissa Blackwood "reigned" for five years as the mistress of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen, during which she gave birth to three children: Mya_Rivers, Gwenys, and Brynden_Rivers. Aegon's bastards born of noblewomen were legitimized as Great Bastards on the king's deathbed.

name: Gwin Goodbrother
bio: Gwin Goodbrother is one of the twelve daughters of Lord Gorold_Goodbrother.

name: Gwynesse Harlaw
bio: Gwynesse Harlaw is a noblewoman of House Harlaw. She is a sister of Lord Rodrik_Harlaw. Gwynesse's husband died in the sea battle off Fair Isle during Greyjoy's Rebellion in 289 AC. Following his death, Gwynesse returned to Ten Towers, where she has remained since. One of the towers of the castle was renamed the Widow's Tower in her honor. She believes that the castle should be hers since she is older than Lord Rodrik_Harlaw, and constantly reminds her brother of this.

name: Gwyneth Yronwood
bio: Gwyneth Yronwood is the youngest daughter of Lord Anders_Yronwood. While Prince Quentyn_Martell was fostered at Yronwood, Gwyneth became fond of him and hoped to marry him after she had flowered.

name: Gylbert Farman
bio: Gylbert Farman was a nobleman from House Farman of Fair Isle. Though the Farmans had ruled as petty kings of Fair Isle, the island had fallen to the rule of the Iron Islands. As the power of the driftwood kings dwindled and the strength of the westerlands grew, Fair Isle was the first to break free from the Iron Islands when Gylbert Farman led the smallfolk of Fair Isle in an uprising to expel their ironborn overlords from their island.

name: Gylbert Farwynd
bio: Gylbert Farwynd is the Lord of the Lonely Light and the head of House Farwynd of the Lonely Light.

name: Gyldayn
bio: Gyldayn was an archmaester of the Citadel known for his works about the history of House Targaryen. Gyldayn began writing Fire & Blood, Being a History of the Targaryen Kings of Westeros, which includes The Sons of the Dragon, about Kings Aenys_I_Targaryen and Maegor_I_Targaryen; The Rogue Prince, about Prince Daemon_Targaryen; The Princess and the Queen, a history of the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons; and other sections. Currently only the first volume of Gyldayn's Fire & Blood, Being a History of the Targaryen Kings of Westeros is known to have been completed. Gyldayn shared his uncompleted work with Archmaester Perestan, however, and he permitted Maester Yandel to use fragments of his work in The World of Ice and Fire. According to a semi-canon source, Gyldayn lived into the reign of King Robert_I_Baratheon.

name: Gyleno Dothare
bio: Gyleno Dothare is a patron of the Happy Port. He has a brother, Gyloro_Dothare.

name: Gyles Greycloak
bio: Gyles Greycloak was a knight of the Kingsguard. Ser Gyles is famously considered a turncloak.

name: Gyles Belgrave
bio: Gyles Belgrave was a knight from House Belgrave and a member of Aegon_II_Targaryen's Kingsguard. Ser Gyles served King Aegon_II_Targaryen during the Dance of the Dragons. Following the defeat of Lord Borros_Baratheon of Storm's End and his army at the Battle of the Kingsroad, Gyles commanded the Aegon's escort after his meeting with the small council. The fatigued-looking king drank a cup of Arbor red and before closing the curtains to keep out the chill, ordered Ser Gyles to take his palanquin to the Red Keep's sept. When Aegon did not respond after they arrived at the sept, Gyles threw back the hangings and found Aegon dead with blood dribbling from his lips. When the victorious riverlords arrived at the capital two days later, Gyles and four other Kingsguard were present as Aegon's body was laid on a bier beneath the Iron Throne. During the Hour of the Wolf, Gyles and three other white cloaks who had been present when Aegon died were accused of treason. Lord Cregan_Stark of Winterfell, the Hand of the King, accused Gyles of either poisoning the king's wine himself or failing in his duty to prevent it from happening. On the day scheduled for the executions, most of those condemned chose to take the black. Gyles, however, accepted death as he had failed in his duty to protect Aegon. The knight was personally executed by Lord Stark, who took his head off with Ice. The heads of Gyles and Lord Larys_Strong, who was accused of leading the conspiracy against Aegon, were put on pikes by the gates to the Red Keep.

name: Gyles Farwynd
bio: Gyles Farwynd is the son of Lord Gylbert_Farwynd.

name: Gyles I Gardener
bio: Gyles I Gardener, called the Woe, was a King of the Reach and a head of House Gardener. Gyles once sacked Oldtown and reportedly sold three quarters of its population into slavery, but failed to breach the defenses of the Hightower on Battle Isle.

name: Gyles II Gardener
bio: Gyles II Gardener was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. Gyles was taken captive in a naval battle near the Misty Islands and tortured to death by Joron_I_Blacktyde, an ironborn High King of the Iron Islands. Gyles corpse was then cut into small pieces to bait his captor's fishhooks with "a chunk of king".

name: Gyles III Gardener
bio: Gyles III Gardener was a King of the Reach and a head of House Gardener. While the Reach enjoyed an unprecedented prosperity, called the Golden Reign, under King Garth_VII_Gardener, it was during the reign of Gyles III that Highgarden reached the apex of its power. Leading a glittering host of armored knights, Gyles went to war against the aged Storm King in the east, defeated the armies of the stormlanders, and conquered all the lands north of the rainwood except for Storm's End, which he besieged for two years. While the King of the Reach was away with his army in the east, the Reach was attacked by the King of the Rock, Lancel_IV_Lannister, forcing Gyles to lift his siege of Storm's End and hurry home to deal with the westermen. House Osgrey were the Marshalls of the Northmarch at the time. Since his elder brothers were all with Gyles, Ser Wilbert_Osgrey called up the reserve levies and marched to stop King Lancel. During the battle between the two forces, Wilbert met the King of the Rock and slew him at the cost of his own life, stopping the attack in its tracks. The westermen retreated and the Reach was saved. Maester Yandel writes that had Lancel not invaded the Reach, Gyles might have been able to complete his conquest in the stormlands. A broader war followed involving three Dornish kings and two from the riverlands. The fighting eventually ended with Gyles dead of a bloody flux and the borders between the realms restored to more or less what they had been before hostilities began.

name: Gyles Grafton
bio: Gyles Grafton is a squire of House Grafton and is the youngest son of Lord Gerold_Grafton of Gulltown.

name: Gyles Morrigen
bio: Gyles Morrigen was a knight from House Morrigen. He served in the Kingsguard as Lord Commander during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Ser Gyles Morrigen was a skilled knight of the stormlands. He was a nephew to Ser Damon_Morrigen, the Grand Captain of the Warrior's Sons. During the late reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen, he supported the claim of Prince Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen when Lord Rogar_Baratheon declared for the prince against Maegor's tyranny. After Jaehaerys took the Iron Throne, he gave the remaining members of his uncle's Kingsguard a choice between execution or taking the black. Ser Harrold_Langward demanded a trial by combat, and his request was granted. Jaehaerys wished to fight Harrold himself, but his council overruled him, and Ser Gyles acted as the crown's champion instead. Morrigen made short work of Ser Harrold, and Jaehaerys decided to make him the first member of his new Kingsguard, naming him Lord Commander. During the Golden Wedding, Gyles announced that King Jaehaerys would grant an audience in his solar with any lord or knight who wished a private word with their new king. Gyles remained in the solar guarding his king while he talked to the six-score who accepted the king's offer. When Princess Alysanne_Targaryen discovered her betrothal plans, she informed Jaehaerys, who acted immediately and ordered Gyles to discreetly travel to Dragonstone under the cover of darkness with the rest of the white cloaks. Jaehaerys and Alysanne later flew to the island. On Dragonstone, Morrigen and the Kingsguard stood witness as Jaehaerys and Alysanne married, with Septon Oswyck performing the ceremony. When Lord Rogar_Baratheon and Queen Regent Alyssa_Velaryon arrived and tried to take the royal children back, Gyles and his sworn brothers defied the King's Hand by forming a wall in front of their king and queen, informing Rogar that he would be the first to die if Rogar's men attacked. During Jaehaerys's minority on Dragonstone, the Lord Commander, along with the rest of the Kingsguard, sparred with their young king, who spent every morning until midday in the castle yard training with sword, shield and any other weapon. When the king came of age and went on his royal progresses with the queen, Gyles accompanied them. Gyles and his sworn brother Ser Joffrey_Doggett guarded the entrance of Jonquil's Pool in Maidenpool when Queen Alysanne and her ladies entered to bathe in the sacred waters. Both men rushed in when they heard screams. Three sisters of the order who attended the pools had tried to kill Alysanne, but her ladies shielded her so the queen was unharmed. Morrigen and Ser Joffrey slew two of the attackers and spared the third for questioning. In 57 AC Jaehaerys sent his new Hand of the King, Septon Barth, to Braavos, to negotiate with the Sealord of Braavos. The Lord Commander accompanied Barth on the diplomatic mission as his protector. After the death of two of his sworn brothers in 59 AC from the winter plague known as the Shivers, Gyles personally selected in the following year their replacements, Ser Ryam_Redwyne and Ser Robin_Shaw, who were both given a white cloak by King Jaehaerys. Late in 73 AC, Ser Ryam Redwyne discovered that Ser Lucamore_Strong had broken his sworn vows: he had wed in secret, not once, but thrice, with each woman being ignorant of the other two. On these three wives, he had fathered a total of sixteen children. Incensed, Gyles had Ser Lucamore seized and brought before the Iron Throne, along with his three wives and all his children. He requested that the king put Ser Lucamore to death, but Jaehaerys instead ordered Strong to be gelded and sent to the Wall to join the Night's Watch. Morrigen and Ser Robert_Redwyne, the Commander of the City Watch, escorted Princess Saera_Targaryen before the throne in 84 AC upon the truths that came to light after an incident at the Blue Pearl. Ser Gyles passed away at an unknown date.

name: Gyles Rosby
bio: Gyles Rosby is the Lord of Rosby and the head of House Rosby. Gyles has no immediate heir, though he has a Ward_of_Gyles_Rosby. Gyles has been married at least twice but remains childless. The aunt of his second wife is Lady Tanda_Stokeworth, who is herself third cousin to Gyles. Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne, once suggested Lord Rosby as a potential husband for his daughter, Princess Arianne_Martell.

name: Gyles Yronwood
bio: Gyles Yronwood was an exiled knight from House Yronwood who was loyal to the blacks during the Dance of the Dragons. During the Storming of the Dragonpit, Ser Gyles was one of the Seven Who Rode, men sent by Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen to recover her son, Prince Joffrey_Velaryon, as he tried to save the dragons from the Dragonpit. Joffrey was killed after falling off the dragon Syrax, however, with a mob tearing apart his body. While trying to recover the boy's remains, Gyles was one of three rescuers who were killed, being pulled from his horse and bludgeoned to death.

name: Gylo Rhegan
bio: Gylo Rhegan is the captain and commander of the Long Lances.

name: Gyloro Dothare
bio: Gyloro Dothare is a patron of the Happy Port in Braavos. He has a brother, Gyleno_Dothare.

name: Gynir
bio: Gynir Rednose is an ironborn raider.

name: Gysella Goodbrother
bio: Gysella Goodbrother is one of the twelve daughters of Lord Gorold_Goodbrother.

name: Haegon Blackfyre
bio: Haegon I Blackfyre was the fourth son of Daemon_I_Blackfyre and Rohanne_of_Tyrosh. He was the heir to his older brother, Daemon_II_Blackfyre. After the battle of the Redgrass Field and the failure of the First Blackfyre Rebellion, Haegon was taken to Tyrosh by his uncle, Aegor_Rivers. In exile he had at least two sons, the eldest of which he named Daemon_III_Blackfyre. In 219 AC Haegon launched the Third Blackfyre Rebellion, which ended in failure. He was slain treacherously after he gave up his sword.

name: Haereg
bio: Haereg was an archmaester who wrote the exhaustive History of the Ironborn. Haereg suggested that the ancestors of the ironborn were not First Men, but instead a race of unknown origin from beyond the Sunset Sea who created the Seastone Chair. He ultimately rejected the idea for lack of evidence, however. Archmaester Hake_(archmaester) believed the Kings of the Iron Islands from House Hoare were ungodly men, but Haereg believed they were resented by the ironborn because of their tolerance for the Faith of the Seven.

name: Hagen
bio: Hagen the Horn is one of the ironborn. He and Hagen's_daughter are among the crew of Asha_Greyjoy's ship, Black Wind.

name: Hagen's daughter
bio: Hagen's daughter is the ironborn daughter of Hagen the Horn. Both are crew members on Asha_Greyjoy's ship Black Wind. Her given name is unknown.

name: Haggo
bio: Haggo is a bloodrider to Khal Drogo.

name: Haggon
bio: Haggon was a free folk skinchanger. Haggon instructed Varamyr Sixskins in the ways of the forest and skinchanging, after Varamyr's ability manifested itself and his father abandoned him to his own kind. Haggon raised Varamyr, teaching him the differences between the animals and which were better and how to survive. He insisted that using the warg ability to control another person's body was an "abomination". Varamyr never had much respect for such traditions and resented Haggon's rules. When Haggon lay dying, Varamyr repaid him by killing him and then forcing him out of his wolf, Greyskin, denying him his second life.

name: Hagon the Terrible
bio: Hagon the Terrible was a High King of the Iron Islands. It is unknown whether he was a king from House Hoare, like the later Hagon_Hoare, or if he came from a different family. Hagon the Terrible and Joron_I_Blacktyde were ironborn kings who succeeded Qhored_I_Hoare and fought against the Kings of the Reach from House Gardener.

name: Hagon Hoare
bio: Hagon Hoare, known as Hagon the Heartless, was a King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. Hagon took the throne when his elder brother, King Harmund_III_Hoare, was overthrown and mutilated by the Shrike and other priest. Hagon quickly expelled the Faith of the Seven from the Iron Islands. He allowed his mother, Queen Lelia_Lannister, to also be mutilated by the Shrike and then sent back to her family home, Casterly Rock. Her outraged nephew, the King of the Rock, responded by invading the Iron Islands. Ser Aubrey_Crakehall, the commander of the westermen, defeated Hagon's army on Great Wyk and captured Hoare Castle in the seventh year of fighting. Before being hanged, Hagon was mutilated as Lelia had been.

name: Hake
bio: Hake is a steward and cook of the Night's Watch.

name: Hake
bio: Hake was an archmaester from Harlaw who wrote about the history of the ironborn.

name: Hairy Hal
bio: Hairy Hal is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Hal
bio: Hal the Hog is a guard on Dragonstone.

name: Halder
bio: Halder is a recruit of the Night's Watch. He is nicknamed Stone Head by Ser Alliser_Thorne. Sixteen years old in 298 AC, Halder is the son of a quarryman and apprenticed as a stonemason.

name: Haldon
bio: Haldon, called Haldon Halfmaester or the Halfmaester, is a healer and the tutor for Young Griff.

name: Hali
bio: Hali has a broad, flat face and yellow hair. She is shorter than Robb_Stark and Osha. Hali travels south of the Wall with Osha, two unnamed wildling men, and Stiv and Wallen, two deserters of the Night's Watch. When they encounter Bran_Stark in the wolfswood, Hali insists Bran give them his horse. When Bran threatens them, Hali suggests killing the boy, which is protested by Osha. Hali then mocks Robb_Stark when he arrives on the scene. During the fighting that ensues, Summer bites Hali's calf. She manages to free herself from the direwolf for a second, but when she stumbles and falls, Summer slams her backwards and tears at her belly, killing her.

name: Halleck
bio: Halleck is a free folk raider. He is the brother of Harma.

name: Halleck Hoare
bio: Halleck Hoare was the second King of the Isles and the Rivers from House Hoare. Halleck succeeded his father, King Harwyn_Hoare, who had conquered the riverlands from Arrec_Durrandon, one of the Storm Kings from Storm's End. As King of the Isles and the Rivers, Halleck visited the Iron Islands only three times for a total of two years. The remainder of King Halleck's time was spent on mainland Westeros warring against the neighbors of the riverlands. Halleck was not as successful as his father, Harwyn Hardhand, as he was defeated in wars against the Kingdom of the Rock and the Kingdom of the Storm. Halleck also failed three times in attacks on the Bloody Gate, the bulwark of the Kings of Mountain and Vale. Halleck did succeed in extending control east to Duskendale and Rosby, however. When not at war Halleck ruled from a modest tower house in Fairmarket. His son and successor, King Harren_Hoare, built Harrenhal as a grander seat on the shores of the Gods Eye in the riverlands. Another son of Halleck was Lord_Commander_Hoare of the Night's Watch.

name: Hallis Hornwood
bio: Hallis Hornwood, known as Mad Hal, was a member of House Hornwood during the Dance of the Dragons. Hallis accompanied Lord Cregan_Stark south to King's Landing during the Dance of the Dragons. After war's end, Hal and Timotty_Snow founded the Wolf Pack, a free company which sailed from Westeros for the Free Cities.

name: Hallis Mollen
bio: Hallis Mollen, called Hal, is a member of the household guard of Lord Eddard_Stark at Winterfell.

name: Hallyne
bio: Hallyne, also known as Hallyne the Pyromancer, is the head of the Alchemists' Guild.  He is a third generation Alchemist. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Hallyne is portrayed by Roy Dotrice. Hallyne's family has been involved in the Alchemists' Guild for three generations. His father was wisdom in the days of King Aerys_II_Targaryen and his grandfather in those of Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen. An acolyte during the reign of Aerys II, Hallyne was trained by Wisdom Pollitor. He was likely unaware of the wildfire plot being planned by Rossart and the other Wisdoms, as according to Ser Jaime_Lannister the alchemists did all the work in secret and did not trust their own apprentices with the knowledge.

name: Halmon Paege
bio: Halmon Paege is a knight and the current head of House Paege.

name: Halys Hornwood
bio: Halys Hornwood[N 1] is Lord of the Hornwood and head of House Hornwood. He is the husband of Lady Donella_Hornwood and the father of a trueborn heir, Daryn_Hornwood, and a natural son, Larence_Snow.

name: Hammer
bio: Hammer is a drunken blacksmith and armorer in the service of the sellsword company the Second Sons. His apprentice is called Nail.

name: Hammer of the Hills
bio: The Hammer of the Hills was an Andal king  during the Andal Invasion. His given name is unknown. He controlled part of the eastern Vale during the Andal invasion. He died near Ironoaks fighting the army of Robar_II_Royce, a First Men high king.

name: Hamish the Harper
bio: Hamish the Harper is a singer.

name: Handsome man
bio: The handsome man is a secret servant of the Many-Faced God in the House of Black and White in Braavos and a Faceless Man.

name: Hanna Harroway
bio: Hanna Harroway was one of the daughters of Lord Lucas_Harroway of Harrenhal, and the sister of Queen Alys_Harroway, one of King Maegor_I_Targaryen's many wives. When her sister Alys_Harroway became pregnant in 44 AC, Hanna and her other sister Jeyne_Harroway assisted Grand Maester Desmond with Alys's care as the queen was confined to her bed. When Alys gave birth to a monstrous stillborn boy three months later, Hanna and Jeyne were the only ones spared from Maegor's wroth. Later, when the Kingsguard arrived at Alys's chambers to arrest her, Hanna and her sister Jeyne were cut down trying to protect Alys.

name: Har Loi
bio: Har Loi was a God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti and the founding member of his dynasty. Loi's throne was said to be a saddle, for he spent his entire reign at war, riding from one battle to another.

name: Harbert
bio: Harbert  was a knight and the castellan of Storm's End for Lord Steffon_Baratheon. He was either a Baratheon, an Estermont, or a bastard of either House. During his time as castellan, Ser Harbert counseled Maester Cressen to kill Patchface because he thought that the fool was suffering due to his deteriorated mental state. Cressen disagreed and won the argument. Stannis_Baratheon once found a wounded goshawk, which he named Proudwing, and nursed it back to health. He tried to teach the bird to hunt but had no success. Harbert persuaded Stannis to abandon the goshawk. Since Ser Cortnay_Penrose was castellan when Renly was made master of laws, it is clear that Harbert had died by the time Renly left for King's Landing.

name: Harbert Paege
bio: Harbert Paege was a knight from House Paege during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. In 212 AC, Harbert was present at the wedding of Lord Ambrose_Butterwell to the daughter of Lord_Frey at Whitewalls. During the the tourney held to celebrate the marriage, Harbert was defeated by Ser Franklyn_Frey after five tilts.

name: Hareth
bio: Hareth, nicknamed Horse, is a stablehand in Mole's Town.

name: Harghaz
bio: Harghaz is a Ghiscari animal handler in the fighting pits of Meereen.

name: Six-Toed Harl
bio: Six-Toed Harl is one of the crew on Asha_Greyjoy's ship, Black Wind.

name: Harlan Grandison
bio: Harlan Grandison was a knight from House Grandison. He was a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. Ser Harlan was a member of the Kingsguard under Aerys_II_Targaryen and presumably his father Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen before him, as he was an old man when Aerys reigned. He died in his sleep in his cell in the White Sword Tower of the Red Keep. Harlan's slot in the white cloaks was taken by a young Ser Jaime_Lannister.

name: Harlan Hunter
bio: Harlan Hunter is a knight from House Hunter and the youngest son of Lord Eon_Hunter.

name: Harlan Hoare
bio: Harlan Hoare was a prince of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. He was the middle son of Qhorwyn_Hoare, King of the Iron Islands. Harlan was the heir of King Qhorwyn_Hoare, as Qhorwyn's eldest son had died of greyscale. When Qhorwyn the Cunning was dying, Harlan died suddenly, allegedly due to falling from a horse. Some suggested that Harlan's younger brother, Harwyn_Hoare was actually to blame, while others attributed Harlan's death to a Faceless Man. Harwyn succeeded as King of the Iron Islands six days after Qhorwyn's own death.

name: Harlan Tyrell
bio: Harlan Tyrell was the head of House Tyrell and High Steward of the Reach. He was the first Tyrell to rule the Reach as Lord of Highgarden after Aegon's Conquest.

name: Harle the Handsome
bio: Harle the Handsome is a leader of the free folk.

name: Harle the Huntsman
bio: Harle the Huntsman is a leader of the free folk.

name: Harlon Botley
bio: Harlon Botley is the fourth son of Lord Sawane_Botley.

name: Harlon Greyjoy
bio: Harlon Greyjoy was the first son of Lord Quellon_Greyjoy and his first wife, Lady_Stonetree_(wife_of_Quellon_Greyjoy). Harlon died from greyscale as a child.

name: Harlon Stark
bio: Harlon Stark was a King in the North and head of House Stark. Hundreds of years ago, House Bolton rose up against the Starks. Harlon laid siege to the Dreadfort for two years to starve them out.

name: Harlon the Hunter
bio: Harlon the Hunter is a legendary son of Garth_Greenhand and ancestor of House Tarly. According to legend, Harlon and his twin brother Herndon_of_the_Horn built their castle atop Horn Hill and took to wife the beautiful woods witch who dwelled there, sharing her favors for a hundred years. The brothers did not age so long as they had intercourse with her whenever the moon was full.

name: Harma
bio: Harma Dogshead, also known as Harma the Dogshead and Dogshead, is a known free folk raider. She has a brother, Halleck. Before Mance_Rayder made peace between them, Harma once warred with Rattleshirt. Harma has become a well known raider among the brothers of the Night's Watch.

name: Harmen Uller
bio: Harmen Uller is the Lord of Hellholt and the head of House Uller.

name: Harmon
bio: Harmon is the author of On Miasmas, describing the effects of poluted waters in Asshai.

name: Harmon Dondarrion
bio: Harmon Dondarrion, called No-Nose Dondarrion, was the Lord of Blackhaven and the head of House Dondarrion during the reign of King Aenys_I_Targaryen. In 37 AC, a pretender outlaw called the Vulture_King_(Aenys_I) gathered thousands to his banners, calling on all "true Dornishmen" to avenge themselves on House Targaryen and the Seven Kingdoms for the First Dornish War as they poured into the Dornish Marches. Writing to King Aenys_I_Targaryen, Lord Harmon discounted the Vulture King's followers as undisciplined rabble. Not long after, the Vulture King's men stormed and seized Blackhaven, and the Vulture King personally sliced off Dondarrion's nose before putting the castle to the torch. As the war escalated, the Vulture King attempted to march west after a failed siege of Nightsong, only for Lady Ellyn_Caron to sally forth behind him to join her strength to a strong force of marchers led by Lord Dondarrion. In the resulting "Vulture Hunt", Lord Samwell_Tarly_(lord) cut off the Dornish retreat, and the three marcher lords shattered the Vulture King's host, riding after them into the Red Mountains and capturing the Vulture King. With the Vulture King executed by Lord Tarly, a grateful King Aenys rewarded Harmon and the marcher lords with gold, offices, and honors. The Lord of Blackhaven was present at the Golden Wedding in 49 AC, but it is unknown if this was Lord Harmon.

name: Harmon of the Reeds
bio: Harmon of the Reeds, called Iron-Banger, was a knight loyal to the blacks during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. During the Storming of the Dragonpit, Ser Harmon was one of the Seven Who Rode, men sent by Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen to recover her son, Prince Joffrey_Velaryon, as he tried to save the dragons. Joffrey was killed after falling off Syrax, however, and a mob tore apart his body. Iron-Banger survived the battle with smallfolk over the prince's remains. Harmon and twenty other knights loyal to Rhaenyra were executed by Ser Perkin_the_Flea after the city gates were opened by Ser Garth_the_Harelip.

name: Harmond Umber
bio: Harmond Umber, known as the Drunken Giant, was a Lord of the Last Hearth and head of House Umber during the reign of King Maekar_I_Targaryen. When King-Beyond-the-Wall Raymun_Redbeard and the his free folk army went over the Wall and marched south, Lord Harmond joined with Lord Willam_Stark with the intent to meet them. Harmond and Lord Willam caught the wildling army between them and Long Lake, defeating the host in battle.

name: Harmund I Hoare
bio: Harmund I Hoare, known as Harmund the Host, was a King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. The Hoare kings of his era were opposed by the priests of the Drowned God because of their support for the Faith of the Seven, discouraging of reaving, and promotion of trade. Harmund I was the first literate iron king and a man fond of books. From his seat on Great Wyk he welcomed travelers and merchants to the Iron Islands. He also protected septons and septas of the Faith.

name: Harmund II Hoare
bio: Harmund II Hoare, known as Harmund the Haggler, was a King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. The Hoare kings of his era were opposed by the priests of the Drowned God because of their support for the Faith of the Seven, discouraging of reaving, and promotion of trade. Harmund spent his youth as a ward of House Lannister of Casterly Rock. A great traveler, Harmund II is said to have been the first iron king to visit mainland Westeros in peace. Once king, he married Lady Lelia_Lannister, the daughter of the King of the Rock. He also visited Highgarden and Oldtown to discuss trade. Harmund followed both the Drowned God and the Faith of the Seven. He spoke of the "Eight Gods" and believed that the Drowned God was an aspect of the Stranger, which angered many of the ironborn. His children were raised in the Faith.

name: Harmund III Hoare
bio: Harmund III Hoare, known as Harmund the Handsome, was a King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. Harmund III was raised in the Faith of the Seven by his parents, King Harmund_II_Hoare and Queen Lelia_Lannister. He outraged the ironborn and the priests of the Drowned God by outlawing reaving and the taking of salt wives. He was also opposed to the practice of thralldom. A priest known as the Shrike led a rapid rebellion, and Harmund was cast down and mutilated, losing his eyes, tongue, and nose. Harmund's brother, Hagon_Hoare, took the throne and restored traditional ironborn practices. Harmund was confined to the dungeons of Hoare Castle and their mother, Lelia, was mutilated and returned to Casterly Rock, which led to war with House Lannister. When the westermen conquered Great Wyk in the seventh year of fighting, Ser Aubrey_Crakehall considered restoring Harmund to the throne but instead decided to give the broken man the gift of mercy.

name: Harmund Sharp
bio: Harmund Sharp is a member of House Sharp.

name: Harmune
bio: Harmune is the maester in service to the Night's Watch at Eastwatch-by-the-Sea. Maester Harmune has read and written the correspondence for Cotter_Pyke, the illiterate commander of Eastwatch, for years.

name: Harmune
bio: Harmune was an archmaester at the Citadel. He wrote Watchers on the Wall, which recounts many legends of the Nightfort. He also wrote Etched in Stone, a catalog of Andal stone carvings found throughout the Vale. He theorizes that, over time, Andals became more devoted to the symbol of the seven-pointed star and the symbol of the axe fell by the wayside. However, Maester Evlyn argues that what Harmune calls axes were in fact hammers, the sign of the Smith, explaining the irregularity of the depictions of these hammers as the results of the Andals being warriors, not artisans.

name: Haro
bio: Haro was a Dothraki khal who lived during the Century of Blood. He was one of the four khals that fought in the Field of Crows. Khal Haro joined forces with Khals Qano, Loso, and Zhako to fight against High King Mazor_Alexi, who had united all of the Sarnori lesser kings to defend their homeland against the Dothraki hordes. The battle took place halfway between Sarnath and the ruins of Kasath. The Sarnori chariots smashed the center of the Dothraki horde. Haro was trampled and died, while his Dothraki fled. However, the escape was a trap and Alexi and his forces were attacked on all sides and destroyed.

name: Harodon
bio: Harodon is an archmaester at the  Citadel in Oldtown.

name: Harra
bio: Harra is a servant in service to House Whent.

name: Harrag Hoare
bio: Harrag Hoare was a High King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. Ravos_Hoare was his son. According to Aeron_Greyjoy, Harrag was chosen by a kingsmoot.[N 1] Unlike many kings of his time, Harrag's reign resembled those of the reavers of the Age of Heroes. During the reign of Theon_Stark, the King of Winter known as the Hungry Wolf, Harrag led a fleet of longships from Great Wyk, Old Wyk, Pyke, and Orkmont against the western coast of the north. Harrag conquered the Stony Shore and burned swathes of the wolfswood, while Bear Island became a base for reaving for Harrag's son, Ravos_Hoare. Theon slew Ravos with his own hand, however, and expelled the ironmen from his shores. Although Harrag's invasion was repulsed, the ironborn would return during the reign of Harrag's grandson, King Erich_Hoare.

name: Harrag Sharp
bio: Harrag Sharp, known as Harrag Sheepstealer, is an ironborn raider from House Sharp.

name: Harrald Halfdrowned
bio: Harrald Halfdrowned was an ironborn king. Although unconfirmed, he was probably a hereditary King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare, since at the time of his reign the Westerlands and the Reach were ruled by Andal kings. Harrald and his heir were beheaded by Lancel_IV_Lannister, King of the Rock, with a single stroke of Brightroar, the Valyrian steel greatsword of House Lannister, during the Battle of Lann's Point (however, it was probably a common steel sword of the same name, which has been conflated with the Valyrian blade).[N 1]

name: Harras Harlaw
bio: Harras Harlaw, known as the Knight, is the head of House Harlaw of Grey Garden and the knight of Grey Garden. A knight, Harras is the heir of House Harlaw of Ten Towers, as its current lord, Rodrik_Harlaw, has no surviving children. In his youth Harras was a good friend of Rodrik_Greyjoy. Victarion_Greyjoy enjoyed thrashing him when they were younger.

name: Harras Hoare
bio: Harras Hoare, known as Harras Stump-hand, was a King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. During the coming of the Andals of the Iron Islands, the Hoares joined other ironborn houses and Andals in bringing down King Rognar_II_Greyiron. According to legend, they performed the finger dance afterward to choose a new king. Harras was chosen king, although he lost two fingers in the process and became known as Harras Stump-hand. Archmaester Haereg instead suggested that Harras came to power by marrying the daughter of an Andal warlord. Harras ruled for thirty years.

name: Harren Botley
bio: Harren Botley is the eldest son of Lord Sawane_Botley and is the heir of House Botley. He has a younger brother, Tristifer_Botley.

name: Harren Half-Hoare
bio: Harren Half-Hoare is an ironborn raider. His relation to House Hoare is unknown.

name: Harren Hoare
bio: Harren Hoare, known as Harren the Black or Black Harren, was last of the Kings of the Isles and the Rivers to rule over the Iron Islands and the riverlands, and the last member of House Hoare to rule. He completed the grand castle, Harrenhal, which would serve to be his doom.

name: Harren the Red
bio: Harren, known as Harren the Red and Red Harren, was an outlaw and bandit of the riverlands. He claimed to be a grandson of the late King Harren_Hoare, who had been known as Harren the Black. During the first year of the reign of King Aenys_I_Targaryen, Harren the Red was the first of four rebels who rose against the Iron Throne. With the aid of a castle servant, Red Harren seized both Harrenhal and its infamous lord, Gargon_Qoherys, slicing off Gargon's genitals in the castle godswood and feeding them to a dog while Gargon bled to death. Harren proclaimed himself Lord of Harrenhal and King of the Rivers. King Aenys I was present at Riverrun when he learned about Red Harren. Lord Tully urged him to mount his dragon Quicksilver and burn out the rebels at Harrenhal, but Aenys refused. Instead, he commanded Tully to raise his banners and marched to Harrenhal with a thousand men. However, aware of the slow advance of the royal army, Harren and his outlaws had put all the loyal castle servants to the sword and fled into the woods. Continuing on to King's Landing, Aenys ordered his Hand, Lord Alyn_Stokeworth, to take a fleet north to the Vale and deal with Jonos_Arryn. Aenys prevaricated, however, and at the last moment recalled the order, fearing that Harren the Red might attack King's Landing. After this waste of time, he instead dispatched Alyn into the riverlands with several hundred men to hunt down Harren the Red. Alyn succeeded, cornering Harren in a village west of the Gods Eye. In the battle that followed, Harren killed Lord Stokeworth, but was killed in turn by Alyn's squire, Bernarr_Brune. The grateful King Aenys subsequently knighted Brune, and rule over Harrenhal was granted to House Harroway. Harren's was the first of the four local rebellions that broke out against Aenys, but also the last one dealt with.

name: Harrion Karstark
bio: Harrion Karstark, affectionately called Harry by his sister Alys_Karstark, is a member of House Karstark. He is the eldest son and heir of Rickard_Karstark, Lord of Karhold.

name: Harrold Darke
bio: Harrold Darke was a knight from House Darke and a member of Rhaenyra_Targaryen's Queensguard. Harrold was likely from a minor branch of House Darklyn of Duskendale. He served as a squire to Ser Steffon_Darklyn. Harrold sided with the blacks during the Dance of the Dragons. After Rhaenyra_Targaryen seized King's Landing, Harrold was made one of her Queensguard. He was one of the Seven Who Rode, seven knights that went after Prince Joffrey_Velaryon when the prince mounted the dragon Syrax to fly to the Dragonpit, during the riot of King's Landing. When Queen Rhaenyra fled the capital, Harrold managed to persuade Lady Meredyth_Darklyn to allow the queen within her walls at Duskendale upon the condition that she would not remain for long. After Rhaenyra sold her crown Harrold urged her to seek refuge with Lady Jeyne_Arryn in the Vale. She refused his council and she and her party sailed for Dragonstone. Upon arriving at the island Ser Alfred_Broome's men fell upon the queen's protectors and an axe split Harrold's head before his sword could clear its scabbard. Prince Aegon_III_Targaryen picked up Harrold's sword to defend his mother, but Alfred knocked it away.

name: Harrold Hardyng
bio: Harrold Hardyng, often called Harry the Heir and sometimes the Young Falcon, is a squire from House Hardyng and a ward of Lady Anya_Waynwood. He is the cousin and heir presumptive of Robert_Arryn, Lord of the Eyrie. Harrold is the son and only child of a landed knight of House Hardyng sworn to House Waynwood, and the youngest daughter of Alys_Arryn and Ser Elys_Waynwood. He was taken in as the ward of Lady Anya_Waynwood at Ironoaks, where he was raised. Shortly after Robert's Rebellion, the infant Harrold became the heir presumptive of his great-uncle Jon_Arryn, Lord of the Eyrie. Jon had lost three heirs in succession: first, his nephew, Ser Elbert_Arryn, was executed on orders of King Aerys_II_Targaryen in 282 AC; second, his nephew by marriage, Ser Denys_Arryn, died at the Battle of the Bells the following year; and third, Denys's only son, who died shortly after his father. This left Harrold Hardyng as the last male descendant of Jon's father, Lord Jasper_Arryn. In 292 AC, Robert_Arryn was born to Jon, becoming his heir. Robert became Lord of the Eyrie on Jon's death in 298 AC, and Harrold was confirmed as Robert's heir. Despite his youth, Harrold has already fathered a bastard daughter, Alys_Stone, on a lowborn servant named Cissy. He has another bastard on the way by a different woman, Saffron, a spice merchant's daughter from Gulltown. Lady Waynwood declined Lord Nestor_Royce's offer to betroth Harry to Myranda_Royce.

name: Harrold Langward
bio: Harrold Langward was a knight from House Langward and a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Ser Harrold served in the Kingsguard during Maegor's reign. After King Maegor's mysterious death upon the Iron Throne, the new young king Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen offered Maegor's surviving Kingsguard a choice between execution or taking the black. Harrold was the only one of the surviving white cloaks who did not take the black, but instead asked for trial by combat. He fought and was subsequently slain by Ser Gyles_Morrigen, a young knight from the stormlands.

name: Harrold Osgrey
bio: Harrold Osgrey was a knight of House Osgrey during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He was the second son of Ser Eustace_Osgrey. Harrold joined his father in supporting Daemon_I_Blackfyre in his rebellion in 196 AC and was slain at the Battle of the Redgrass Field. He is buried at Standfast.

name: Harrold Rogers
bio: Harrold Rogers was a knight from House Rogers during the first half of the third century after Aegon's Conquest. Harrold married Branda_Stark, the first cousin of Lord Edwyle_Stark of Winterfell.

name: Harrold Swyft
bio: Harrold Swyft was a knight from House Swyft during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Ser Harrold was listed as a participant at the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Harrold Westerling
bio: Harrold Westerling was a knight from House Westerling and the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard under King Viserys_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Harrold is portrayed by Graham McTavish. It is unknown when Harrold became Lord Commander of the Kingsguard. At the earliest he could have been the successor of Ser Ryam_Redwyne who died in 105 AC. Upon his own death in 112 AC, Harrold was succeeded as Lord Commander by Ser Criston_Cole.

name: Harry Merrell
bio: Harry Merrell, known as Hot Harry Merrell, is one of Ser Jaime_Lannister's men during his campaign to bring the riverlands back to the authority of the Iron Throne.

name: Harry Penny
bio: Harry Penny was a hedge knight of the riverlands from House Penny. During the Dance of the Dragons, Harry regrouped rivermen after the battle at Acorn Hall. Three days after that encounter, Harry and Ser Adrian_Tarbeck died fighting each other in a new battle.

name: Harry Rivers
bio: Harry Rivers, known as the Bastard of Bracken, is the bastard son of Lord Jonos_Bracken of Stone Hedge.

name: Harry Sawyer
bio: Harry Sawyer is a knight of House Sawyer.

name: Harry Strickland
bio: Harry Strickland, also known as Homeless Harry Strickland, is a captain-general of the Golden Company and an exiled knight from House Strickland. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Harry is portrayed by Marc Rissmann. Harry's great-grandsire lost his lands when he rose in the First Blackfyre Rebellion with Daemon_I_Blackfyre. Harry was born in exile and raised with the Golden Company, as was his father and his grandfather. After the death of the previous commander, Ser Myles_Toyne, Harry was made the new captain-general, and Gorys_Edoryen replaced Harry as paymaster. Ser Rolly_Duckfield was once Harry's squire, and his current squire is Watkyn.

name: Harsley
bio: Harsley was an inhabitant of the Riverlands.

name: Lord Harte
bio: Lord Harte was the head of House Harte during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Harte was arrested and put in the Red Keep's dungeons by Aegon_II_Targaryen's supporters for supporting his half-sister, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen. When given the choice between swearing his allegiance to Aegon II or being killed by the King's Justice, Lord Harte was one of the nobles who chose to keep their sworn words and were beheaded for it. Their heads were mounted on spikes above the city’s gates.

name: Harwin
bio: Harwin is a member of the household guard of Lord Eddard_Stark at Winterfell. Harwin is the son of Hullen, the master of horse at Winterfell. Harwin often led Arya_Stark's pony around the yard of Winterfell during her youth, rode at quintain with Jon_Snow and Robb_Stark, and was known for drinking too much on feast days. Harwin remembers Lyanna_Stark's skills at horse-riding and was privy to the rumors of Lord Eddard_Stark and Lady Ashara_Dayne's love affair as a young boy no older than Arya, suggesting he has been a stable-boy and guard of Winterfell in the service of House Stark since his youth.

name: Harwin Strong
bio: Harwin Strong, known as Breakbones, was a knight from House Strong and the heir to Harrenhal. He also served as a captain in the City Watch of King's Landing. The rumored lover of Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, Harwin is believed by some to have been the real father of her three sons with her first husband Ser Laenor_Velaryon, (Jacaerys_Velaryon, Lucerys_Velaryon, and Joffrey_Velaryon). In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Harwin is portrayed by Ryan Corr. In 105 AC, Ser Harwin was brought to court by his father, Lord Lyonel_Strong, alongside his younger brother Larys_Strong and his two sisters. Harwin was made a captain in the gold cloaks of King's Landing. During 112 AC, Harwin was one of many suitors to pay court to Rhaenyra_Targaryen, the Princess of Dragonstone. According to Mushroom in 113 AC, Harwin one night returning to the Red Keep from a night of reveling in the capital happened upon a spurned Princess Rhaenyra Targaryen and she took solace in his company, being found abed together by Mushroom the following morning. In 114 AC, Princess Rhaenyra granted Harwin her favor at the tourney celebrating her wedding with Ser Laenor_Velaryon. At that tourney, Ser Criston_Cole broke his collarbone and shattered his elbow. Mushroom mocked him as "Brokenbones" after this event. Thereafter Harwin was a consistent presence at Rhaenyra's side and was the foremost member among the blacks. In late 115 AC, according to Septon Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons), during the birth of Rhaenyra's second son, Lucerys_Velaryon, both Rhaenyra's husband Ser Laenor and Breakbones were in attendance at her bedside. When Rhaenyra moved to Dragonstone after the rivalry with Queen Alicent_Hightower and the greens grew to be too much, Breakbones went with her as her personal sworn shield. In 120 AC, after Prince Aemond_Targaryen called Rhaenyra's sons "Strongs" and a brawl broke out, Harwin was removed as Princess Rhaenyra's champion by King Viserys_I_Targaryen and he returned to Harrenhal, being replaced by Ser Erryk_Cargyll of the Kingsguard. Harwin died in a fire at Harrenhal that same year, with it also killing his father, Lord Lyonel Strong. The fire was said to be an accident and blamed on the curse of Harrenhal. Other non-mystical suspects included Prince Daemon_Targaryen, whose motive would have been eliminating a rival for Princess Rhaenyra's affections; Lord Corlys_Velaryon, as revenge for Harwin cuckolding his son; Harwin's brother, Larys, to claim the inheritance of Harrenhal; and King Viserys I, to eliminate the man who brought dishonor on his House.

name: Harwood Fell
bio: Harwood Fell is the Lord of Felwood and the head of House Fell. Harwood's Lord_Fell was killed by Lord Robert_I_Baratheon at Summerhall during Robert's Rebellion. Harwood admires Robert for his martial prowess.

name: Harwood Stout
bio: Harwood Stout is the Lord of Goldgrass and the head of House Stout. He is a vassal of Lady Barbrey_Dustin of Barrowton.

name: Harwyn Hoare
bio: Harwyn Hoare, known as Harwyn Hardhand, was a King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare who conquered the riverlands and became the first King of the Isles and the Rivers. The ironborn longship Hardhand is presumably named in his honor.

name: Harwyn Plumm
bio: Harwyn Plumm, called Hardstone, is a knight from House Plumm. He is the third son of Lord Philip_Plumm.

name: Harys Cobb
bio: Harys Cobb is a knight from House Cobb. He is a sworn sword in service to King Stannis_Baratheon.

name: Harys Graceford
bio: Harys Graceford was a knight of House Graceford during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Harys was listed as a participant of the tourney at Ashford Meadow of 209 AC.

name: Harys Haigh
bio: Harys Haigh is a knight from House Haigh. He is son of Ser Leslyn_Haigh and Perriane_Frey.

name: Harys Hogg
bio: Harys Hogg, also known as Harry the Ham, was a knight from House Hogg during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Harys was regarded as an upjumped squire in the writings of Archmaester Gyldayn. In 55 AC, Harys became a favorite of the smallfolk during the great melee celebrating the completion of the Dragonpit. In 58 AC, Harys took part in a tourney celebrating King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen tenth year on the Iron Throne, and was unhorsed by the young Ser Ryam_Redwyne of the Kingsguard.

name: Harys Horpe
bio: Harys Horpe, known as Death's Head Harry, was a knight from House Horpe, and a member of the Warrior's Sons at the start of the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. In 42 AC, Horpe was stationed at the King's Landing chapter of the Warrior's Sons, based in the Sept of Remembrance. He fought in the trial of seven in which seven of the Warrior's Sons faced King Maegor_I_Targaryen and his six champions. According to several chronicles of the event, Ser Harys lost his arm to Lord Lucifer_Massey, one of the king's champions. One of these accounts state that he then tossed his battle-axe into his remaining hand and buried it between Lord Lucifer's eyes. Other chroniclers suggest Death's Head Harry simply died.

name: Harys Swyft
bio: Harys Swyft is the Knight of Cornfield and the head of House Swyft. He is the good-father to Ser Kevan_Lannister. When Tytos_Lannister was Lord of Casterly Rock, Ser Harys borrowed large sums of money from House Lannister. Upon Ser Tywin_Lannister's return from the War of the Ninepenny Kings, the young heir of Casterly Rock was determined to return his House's status and began by demanding repayment of gold lent by his father, Tytos. Those who could not repay immediately had to send a hostage to the Rock until their debt was settled. Harys hastened to obey, stating, "The lion has awoken." Unable to pay immediately, Harys surrendered his daughter, Dorna_Swyft, into the custody of Tywin's younger brother Ser Kevan_Lannister until the debt was settled. Kevan eventually married Dorna.

name: Lord Hayford
bio: Lord Hayford was the head of House Hayford and the Lord of Hayford during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. A noted loyalist to the Targaryens, Lord Hayford was made Hand of the King shortly before the battle at the Redgrass Field during the First Blackfyre Rebellion, replacing Lord Ambrose_Butterwell, who had done such a dismal job that many questioned his loyalty. Lord Hayford, however, had been stalwart from the first. Amongst the knights fighting in the battle of the Redgrass Field in 196 AC underneath the Hayford banner was Ser Arlan_of_Pennytree, with his squire and nephew, Roger_of_Pennytree. Lord Gormon_Peake killed both Lord Hayford and Roger in the battle.

name: Lord Hayford (Dance)
bio: Lord Hayford was the Lord of Hayford and the head of House Hayford during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Hayford was arrested and put in the Red Keep's dungeons by Aegon_II_Targaryen's supporters for supporting his half-sister, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen. When given the choice between swearing his allegiance to Aegon II or being killed by the King's Justice, Lord Hayford was one of the nobles who chose to keep their sworn words and were beheaded for it. Their heads were mounted on spikes above the city’s gates.

name: Hayhead
bio: Hayhead is a guard sworn to House Stark.

name: Hazel Harte
bio: Hazel Harte was a noblewoman from House Harte, during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen, and Aegon_III_Targaryen. Hazel was married to Daeron_Velaryon. They had one child together, Lady Daenaera_Velaryon, born in 127 AC. Hazel was one of the casualties of the Winter Fever. In 133 AC, her husband Daeron drowned in the Stepstones during the Daughters' War. Daenaera was left an orphan at the age of six, and was taken in as a ward by Lady Baela_Targaryen and her husband, Lord Alyn_Velaryon. Daenaera would become the queen consort of King Aegon_III_Targaryen, and the mother of his five children.

name: Hazrak zo Loraq
bio: Hazrak zo Loraq, known as Hazrak the Handsome, was a famous Ghiscari citizen of the city of Meereen. Hazrak is an ancestor of Hizdahr_zo_Loraq.

name: Hazzea
bio: Hazzea is a Ghiscari child of the plains near the city of Meereen. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, she is renamed Zalla. Hazzea was a Hazzea's_father's daughter.

name: Hazzea's father
bio: Hazzea's father is a Ghiscari farmer who lives on the plains near the city of Meereen. Hazzea is apparently killed by Drogon. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, where his daughter is renamed Zalla, he is portrayed by Darren Kent.

name: Helaena Targaryen
bio: Queen Helaena Targaryen was the sister-wife to King Aegon_II_Targaryen. She was the only daughter of King Viserys_I_Targaryen by his second wife, Queen Alicent_Hightower, and his second daughter overall. Helaena and Aegon had three children: the twins Jaehaerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon_II) and Jaehaera_Targaryen, and Maelor_Targaryen. Like her siblings, Helaena was a dragonrider, who rode the dragon Dreamfyre. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Helaena is portrayed by Phia Saban and Evie Allen (young). In 109 AC, Helaena was born to King Viserys_I_Targaryen and his second wife, Queen Alicent_Hightower. She had an older half-sister, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, and an older brother, Prince Aegon_II_Targaryen, and would have two younger brothers, Prince Aemond_Targaryen and Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I). By the age of eleven, Helaena had become a dragonrider, and rode the dragon Dreamfyre. In 122 AC, at the age of thirteen, Helaena was married to her older brother Prince Aegon, following the ancient tradition of House Targaryen. Barely a year after her wedding, Helaena gave birth to twins: Jaehaerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon_II) and Jaehaera_Targaryen. On the first day of 127 AC, Helaena's father, King Viserys I, held a feast to celebrate the recovery of his health. According to the court fool Mushroom, after the king had left the feast, Helaena's nephew, Prince Jacaerys_Velaryon, asked her for a dance, which her husband and brother, Aegon, took offense to. Later that same year, Helaena gave birth to a third child, a boy named Maelor_Targaryen. On the third day of the third month of 129 AC, Helaena brought her three children to her father's chambers, where the king, the night before his death, entertained her children with a fictional tale[N 1] about their great-great-grandsire, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen.

name: Helicent Uffering
bio: Helicent Uffering was a noblewoman from House Uffering. She was the sister to Ser Rolland_Uffering. Helicent was one of the courtiers at Coldmoat during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. She came to Coldmoat with her brother, Ser Rolland_Uffering, who was the fourth husband of Lady Rohanne_Webber. When Ser Duncan_the_Tall came to Coldmoat, Ser Lucas_Inchfield introduced Helicent as Rohanne in order to embarrass Duncan. Helicent did not understand Duncan's meaning when he asked her about the dam on the Chequy Water, and was frightened when one of the other courtiers shouted that Duncan wanted to sleep with her.

name: Helliweg
bio: Helliweg is a maester of the Citadel in service at Runestone to House Royce. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Helliweg is portrayed by Joe Hewetson.

name: Helly
bio: Helly is a serving girl at the Peach, an inn and brothel in Stoney Sept.

name: Helman Tallhart
bio: Helman Tallhart is a knight, the Master of Torrhen's Square, and the head of House Tallhart. Ser Helman and his son, Benfred_Tallhart, have often visited Winterfell to see Lord Eddard_Stark, and Theon_Greyjoy once accompanied Lord Stark on a visit to see Helman at Torrhen's Square.

name: Helya
bio: Helya is a bentback old woman. Helya greets Theon_Greyjoy upon his arrival at Pyke. She informs him of the death of the old steward Sylas and her position in the household. When Theon entertains the idea of taking "Esgred" (in reality Asha_Greyjoy) to Pyke with him, he tells her Helya will arrange a place for her in the castle at his request.

name: Hendry Bracken
bio: Hendry Bracken is a member of House Bracken and nephew to Lord Jonos_Bracken of Stone Hedge.

name: Henk the Helm
bio: Henk the Helm is a free folk raider.

name: Henly
bio: Henly is a young maester of the Citadel in service to Lord_Slate at Blackpool.

name: Henly
bio: Henly was a stableman in the town of Ashford during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Henly purchased Sweetfoot from Ser Duncan_the_Tall.

name: Young Henly
bio: Young Henly is a builder of the Night's Watch.

name: Old Henly
bio: Old Henly is a steward of the Night's Watch.

name: Henrietta Woodhull
bio: Henrietta Woodhull was a member of House Woodhull, the daughter of a landed knight from the Paps, during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 133 AC, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Many maidens across Westeros came to King's Landing in hopes of marrying the young king. However, the event was not a true ball, but merely a procession of maidens, each one curtsying before the king while a herald announced her name and lineage. Henrietta was the last maiden to be presented. After the appearance of Lady Daenaera_Velaryon, who was evidently the king's choice, the last few maidens were brought forward hurriedly. Aegon's desire to end the parade was so palpable that Henrietta was sobbing as she curtsied.

name: Herbert Bolling
bio: Herbert Bolling is a knight of House Bolling.

name: Heriston
bio: Maester Heriston was a maester of the Citadel who served at White Harbor. He speculated that beyond the White Waste may lie a warm summer sea.

name: Herman Rollingford
bio: Herman Rollingford was a knight from House Rollingford. Herman accompanied Lord Manfryd_Mooton during his diplomatic mission to Braavos in 133 AC. Herman was killed in a duel by a bravo who objected to the color of Herman's doublet.

name: Herndon of the Horn
bio: Herndon of the Horn is a legendary son of Garth_Greenhand and ancestor of House Tarly. According to legend, Herndon and his twin brother Harlon_the_Hunter built their castle atop Horn Hill and took to wife the beautiful woods witch who dwelled there, sharing her favors for a hundred years. The brothers did not age so long as they had intercourse with her whenever the moon was full.

name: Hero
bio: Hero is an Unsullied purchased by and sworn to Daenerys_Targaryen. He is the second in command of Daenerys's Unsullied after Grey_Worm.

name: Herrock Kenning
bio: Herrock Kenning, known as Herrock the Whoreson, was an ironborn warrior of House Kenning of Harlaw who founded House Kenning of Kayce in the westerlands. His warhorn, the Horn of Herrock, has been passed down by his descendants. During the wars between the driftwood kings and the Kings of the Rock, Herrock the Whoreson used his horn as a signal for the whores of Kayce to open a postern gate. His men then captured the town for House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Three different ironborn kings attempted to retake the town over the years; Herrock personally killed two of them.

name: Herryk
bio: Herryk was a maester of the Citadel. He wrote the book History of the Kings-Beyond-the-Wall.

name: Heward
bio: Heward  is a member of the household guard of Lord Eddard_Stark at Winterfell.

name: Hibald
bio: According to Ser Shadrich, Hibald is niggardly and fearful. Hibald travels from King's Landing to Duskendale with his serving men and a hedge knight, Ser Shadrich. On the road, he comes upon Brienne_Tarth and her two companions, Ser Illifer and Ser Creighton_Longbough. After Shadrich declares the three are no threat, Hibald agrees they can travel together. They all spend the night at the Old Stone Bridge, where Hibald confirms to one of the locals that Jaime_Lannister has lost his sword hand.

name: High Lickspittle
bio: The High Lickspittle, as he was dubbed by the Faith Militant during the uprising, was the third High Septon of the Faith of the Seven during the reign of Maegor_I_Targaryen and the early reign of Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He replaced the elderly and amiable High Septon after his death in 44 AC. The Faith Militant dubbed him "the High Lickspittle," because he never spoke against King Maegor while hundreds of Poor Fellows were hunted and murdered for the bounty Maegor put on their scalps. In defiance, a band of Poor Fellows crowned one of their leaders, Septon Moon_(septon), as their own High Septon.
