Cersei and Jaime slept together in the same bed when they were very young, and even went as far as to experiment together in a sexual manner at a young age. During one of these encounters, they were caught by a servant who informed their mother. Jaime's bedchamber was immediately moved to the other side of the castle, whilst a guard was placed near Cersei's bedchamber. The twins were told never to do anything like that ever again, as Joanna would otherwise be forced to tell their father. Jaime's mother died not long thereafter, in 273 AC, when giving birth to Jaime's younger brother, Tyrion_Lannister. Even though Tyrion is a malformed dwarf, and both his father and sister despise the young Lannister, Jaime developed an affection for his youngest sibling, and treated him with kindness and respect. Shortly after Joanna's death, the Princess_of_Dorne_(mother_of_Doran) visited Casterly Rock with her two youngest children, Elia_Martell and Oberyn_Martell. When she eventually proposed a betrothal between Elia and Jaime, she was refused. In 276 AC, when Jaime was ten years old, Lord Tywin hosted a tourney to celebrate the birth of young Prince Viserys_Targaryen. During this tourney, Tywin suggested to King Aerys that Jaime could squire for Rhaegar_Targaryen, Prince of Dragonstone, but Aerys refused and named the sons of other lords to the position instead. According to Cersei, young Jaime was only interested in dogs, horses, swords, and her. Jaime went to Crakehall at the age of eleven, and spent four years squiring for Lord Sumner_Crakehall, alongside Merrett_Frey. He occasionally visited Casterly Rock during this time, and raged when, in 278 AC, his sister Cersei was brought to King's Landing by their father. At the age of thirteen, while still a squire, Jaime won his first tourney melee. Whilst a squire to Lord Sumner, Jaime was sent to Riverrun, as Lord Tywin and Lord Hoster_Tully had been considering betrothing Jaime to Hoster's younger daughter, Lysa_Arryn. According to Sumner, the message Jaime was to bring to Hoster could not be entrusted to a raven. He spent a fortnight at Riverrun, being seated next to Lysa during meals. Jaime ignored her, however, being more interested in the stories of Hoster's younger brother, Ser Brynden_Tully, a famous warrior who had fought in the War of the Ninepenny Kings, and whom Jaime asked for stories about the war. Ser Addam_Marbrand, the heir of Lord Damon_Marbrand of Ashemark, is a childhood friend of Jaime's. The two have remained friends to this day.

name: Jalabhar Xho
bio: Jalabhar Xho, Prince of the Red Flower Vale, is an exiled prince from the Summer Isles residing in the Red Keep. Jalabhar Xho is the exiled Prince of the Red Flower Vale, likely exiled for losing a highly ritualized war as per the tradition of the Summer Isles. Following his exile, Jalabhar came to King's Landing in the Seven Kingdoms to request gold and swords from King Robert_I_Baratheon to help him regain the Red Flower Vale. Robert always entertained the notion of conquering the Summer Isles, but always put off Jalabhar's request for help. However, Robert never firmly declined him, telling him "Next year" instead of "No". While Jalabhar continues to occasionally petition the Iron Throne to win back his seat, he has become a noted courtier at King's Landing. Having no other place to seek refuge, the exiled prince is one of the most colorful figures at court, alongside Thoros_of_Myr.

name: Jammos Frey
bio: Jammos Frey is a knight of House Frey and the thirteenth son of Lord Walder_Frey, the second by his fourth wife, Lady Alyssa_Blackwood. Jammos is married to Sallei_Paege and has three children: Walder_Frey_(son_of_Jammos), Dickon_Frey, and Mathis_Frey.

name: Janei Lannister
bio: Janei Lannister is a member of House Lannister. She is the only daughter of Ser Kevan_Lannister and his wife, Dorna_Swyft. She is their youngest child, and her older siblings are Ser Lancel_Lannister and the squires Willem_Lannister and Martyn_Lannister.

name: Janna Tyrell
bio: Janna Fossoway, née Tyrell, is the wife of Ser Jon_Fossoway of the green-apple Fossoways. She is the youngest daughter of Lord Luthor_Tyrell and Lady Olenna Tyrell. Janna has two siblings: Mace_Tyrell, the current Lord of Highgarden, and an older sister, Mina_Tyrell.

name: Janos Slynt
bio: Janos Slynt is the founder of House Slynt and commander of the City Watch of King's Landing. His father was a butcher. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Janos is portrayed by Dominic Carter. Janos was born the son of a butcher in King's Landing. While captain of the Iron Gate, he was raised to Commander of the City Watch of King's Landing, the so-called gold cloaks, after the death of the previous commander, Manly_Stokeworth. During his rule as commander of the gold cloaks, Janos was known for taking bribes and selling positions and promotions. By the end of his tenure, over half the officers were paying him part of their salaries. Lord Jon_Arryn, the Hand of the King to Robert_I_Baratheon, discovered this, and had two men who were willing to come forward to testify against Janos, but both were found dead a short time later. Robert ackowledged Janos's corruption, but allowed him to remain in his position, arguing his successor might be worse. The king's brother, Lord Stannis_Baratheon, was certain Lord Petyr_Baelish had convinced Robert to retain Janos.

name: Janyce Hunter
bio: Janyce Hunter is a noblewoman of House Hunter, and the wife of Edwyn_Frey. They have one daughter, Walda_Frey_(daughter_of_Edwyn).

name: Jaqen H'ghar
bio: Jaqen H'ghar is a sellsword from Lorath. He is taken as a criminal from the black cells by Yoren to join the Night's Watch. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Jaqen is portrayed mainly by Tom Wlaschiha, and a variety of additional actors when he is wearing different faces, including Patrick O'Kane, Cedric Henderson, and Faye Marsay. Jaqen claims he was once of the Free City of Lorath, and that his sire is long dead.

name: Jar Han
bio: Jar Han was a God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti and the eight member of his dynasty. Under Han's rule, the Golden Empire reached the apex of its power that had began under the rule of his father, Jar_Har and then his elder brother, Jar_Joq. Jar Han exacted tribute from Qarth, Old Ghis, and Asshai.

name: Jar Har
bio: Jar Har was a God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti and the sixth member of his dynasty. Under Har's rule, the Golden Empire began the apex of its power with the conquest of Leng. He had at least two sons: Jar_Joq, his successor, and Jar_Han. The periodic slaughter of foreigners on Leng was ended by Jar Har. He also sent warriors to the subterranean ruins of Leng, though they returned mad, or not at all. Thus he finally decreed the vast underground ruins of the island should be sealed up and forgotten and entering such places be punished with torture and death.

name: Jar Joq
bio: Jar Joq was a God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti and the seventh member of his dynasty. Under Joq's rule, the Golden Empire continued the apex of its power with the conquest of Great Moraq. He succeeded his father, Jar_Har, and was in turn succeeded by his younger brother, Jar_Han.

name: Jared Frey
bio: Jared Frey is a knight of House Frey. He is the fourth son of Lord Walder_Frey, the first son born from his second wife, Cyrenna_Swann. He is married to his cousin Alys_Frey and has two children, Ser Tytos_Frey and Kyra_Frey. Jared was too old to ride in the tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day held in King's Landing in 298 AC.

name: Jaremy Rykker
bio: Jaremy Rykker is a ranger of the Night's Watch and a knight from House Rykker. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Jaremy is portrayed by Jeffrey O'Brien. Jaremy remained loyal to Aerys_II_Targaryen during Robert's Rebellion, and fought on the battlements during the Sack of King's Landing. Afterwards, he was given the choice by Lord Tywin_Lannister between the Wall or execution. Both Jaremy and Ser Alliser_Thorne joined the Night's Watch.

name: Jarl
bio: No more than twenty years old by 299 AC, Jarl has been a raider for eight years, having gone over the Wall with the Weeper and Alfyn_Crowkiller. He has led his own band of raiders.

name: Jarman Buckwell
bio: Jarman Buckwell is a knight from House Buckwell. He is a senior ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Jason Hightower
bio: Jason Hightower was a head of House Hightower and Lord of the Hightower. Jason doubled the wealth of Oldtown, after his father, Lord Jeremy_Hightower, had already done the same in his time. Jason also rebuilt the Hightower a hundred feet taller.

name: Jason Mallister
bio: Jason Mallister is the Lord of Seagard and the head of House Mallister. He has at least one son, Patrek_Mallister. Jason is known to have been the Lord of Seagard since about 278 AC.[N 1] Lord Jason is a renowned tourney knight, participating in several tourneys over the years. He was defeated in Lord Steffon's tourney at Storm's End by Ser Barristan_Selmy.[N 2] During Robert's Rebellion, Jason attended the weddings of Catelyn and Lysa Tully at Riverrun, where his gifts were lavish. Jason jested with Ser Brynden_Tully during the wedding feast. Lord Mallister fought in the Battle of the Trident, slaying three of Rhaegar's bannermen. During Greyjoy's Rebellion in 289 AC, Jason killed Rodrik_Greyjoy beneath the walls of Seagard, throwing the ironborn reavers back into the sea. After the defeat of the rebellion, King Robert_I_Baratheon ordered a tourney at Lannisport to celebrate his victory. There, Lord Jason was defeated by the eventual champion, Lord Jorah_Mormont.

name: Jason Lannister
bio: Jason Lannister was the Lord of Casterly Rock, the Shield of Lannisport, the Warden of the West, and the head of House Lannister during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. He was the older twin of Ser Tyland_Lannister. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Jason is portrayed by Jefferson Hall. When Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen visited the westerlands in 112 AC, Ser Jason and his younger twin, Ser Tyland_Lannister, vied for the hand of the princess during a feast at Casterly Rock. Both the twins showered her with gifts without success. When the Dance of the Dragons began in 129 AC, Ser Tyland served as master of coin to King Aegon_II_Targaryen. His brother's close association with the Iron Throne and his favored position at court brought Lord Jason into the Dance supporting the greens against Rhaenyra's claim. Lord Jason gathered a formidable host of a thousand knights and seven thousand archers and men-at-arms. Prince Aemond_Targaryen had Jason and his host descend on the riverlands from the Red Fork. In the Battle at the Red Fork, the riverlords rushed to meet Jason's host in battle. The westermen tried to cross the ford three times, but were turned back. In the third attempt, Lord Jason was mortally wounded by Pate_of_Longleaf, a grizzled squire. While Jason was dying of his wounds, Ser Adrian_Tarbeck took command of the westermen and shattered the riverlords' host. Lord Jason died from his wounds not long after, leaving Casterly Rock in disarray. Jason was succeeded as Lord of Casterly Rock by his young son, Loreon. For his actions, Pate was knighted by Lord Petyr_Piper and dubbed "Longleaf the Lionslayer". Jason's widow, Lady Johanna_Lannister, defended the westerlands from the Red Kraken, Lord Dalton_Greyjoy. With most of the Lannister strength in the riverlands, the ironborn descended on the thinly defended westerlands, sacking Lannisport and seizing hundreds of women, including Lord Jason's favorite mistress and his natural daughters.

name: Jason Lannister
bio: Jason Lannister was a knight from House Lannister. He was the fourth and youngest son of Lord Gerold_Lannister and his second wife, Lady Rohanne_Webber. Jason was born the youngest of four children to Lord Gerold_Lannister and his second wife, Lady Rohanne_Webber. His mother disappeared within a year after his birth. Jason grew up at Casterly Rock where he often quarreled with his weak-willed brother, Lord Tytos_Lannister. At age fourteen, he sired a bastard daughter, Lynora_Hill, by a serving girl. At fifteen, he deflowered Lord Lord_Stackspear_(father_of_Alys)'s maiden daughter, the Lady Alys_Stackspear, getting her with child as well. In a rare display of firmness, Lord Tytos insisted that his brother marry the girl, though most believe this was done at the behest of Tytos's wife, the Lady Jeyne_Marbrand. Lady Alys died in childbirth, giving Jason a son named Damon_Lannister_(son_of_Jason). Anxious to avoid further scandals and bastards, Tytos and Jeyne gave Jason a fortnight to mourn, and then compelled him to wed again, this time to Lord Prester's daughter, the Lady Marla_Prester. Though she was twice his age, she gave Jason three daughters and three sons. After the marriage, Jason departed Casterly Rock to reside with his lady wife at Feastfires, to the relief of even Casterly Rock's maester Beldon. When the War of the Ninepenny Kings broke out in 260 AC, as Lord Tytos was no warrior, Ser Jason was given command of the westerlands host—a thousand knights and ten thousand men-at-arms—at the Stepstones. Jason died on Bloodstone. By some later accounts, he was slain by the Blackfyre pretender, Maelys the Monstrous, but contemporary accounts suggest that it was a flux of the bowels that killed Ser Jason. After his death, Lord Roger_Reyne seized command of the westermen host.

name: Jasper Arryn
bio: Jasper Arryn was a head of House Arryn whose titles included Lord of the Eyrie, Defender of the Vale, and Warden of the East. He was the father of Jon_Arryn, Alys_Arryn and Ronnel_Arryn_(son_of_Jasper).

name: Jasper Redfort
bio: Jasper Redfort is a knight of House Redfort. He is the eldest son and heir of Lord Horton_Redfort of Redfort.

name: Jasper Waynwood
bio: Jasper Waynwood  was a nobleman of House Waynwood, the only son of Ser Elys_Waynwood and Lady Alys_Arryn. He was presumably named after his maternal grandfather, Lord Jasper_Arryn of the Eyrie. Jasper died at the age of three after getting kicked in the head by a horse.

name: Jasper Wylde
bio: Jasper Wylde, called Ironrod by the smallfolk, was the Lord of the Rain House and the head of House Wylde during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. He served as master of laws for both kings. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Jasper is portrayed by Paul Kennedy. When King Viserys_I_Targaryen died in his sleep in 129 AC, Lord Jasper was called to the small council convened in the chambers of Queen Alicent_Hightower. Plans were made to crown Alicent's son as Aegon_II_Targaryen, instead of Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, who had been named Viserys's heir. With Lord Lyman_Beesbury the only member of the council in support of Rhaenyra, Jasper reminded him of the Great Council of 101 AC, and the Old King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen in choosing Prince Baelon as his heir over Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon) in 92 AC. Jasper also discussed Aegon_I_Targaryen and his sister-wives Visenya_Targaryen and Rhaenys_Targaryen, and the Andal tradition wherein the rights of a trueborn son come before a daughter. When Grand Maester Orwyle asked about those not willing to bend the knee to Aegon II, Ironrod responded they would die as traitors. As the Dance of the Dragons progressed, Jasper advised Prince Regent Aemond_Targaryen to summon Lord Ormund_Hightower and Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I) from the Reach. Aemond, however, disregarded Jasper's caution, and instead headed for Harrenhal. Jasper was taken captive by Rhaenyra during the fall of King's Landing in 130 AC. Rhaenyra had several nobles executed for treason; Jasper was the second one beheaded, after Ser Otto_Hightower. To his death, Ironrod insisted that the Iron Throne goes to a king's son before a daughter.

name: Jate
bio: Jate serves with the force gathered to besiege Riverrun. After Ser Brynden_Tully escapes Riverrun, Jate is sent north under the command of Ser Dermot of the Rainwood to search for him. While Jate is on picket duty, the camp is attacked by wolves. Jate falls victim to the wolves, but informes Ser Dermot of seeing a Nymeria_(direwolf) leading the pack before dying.

name: Jate Blackberry
bio: Jate Blackberry is a member of House Blackberry and is the captain of the gate at Dragonstone.

name: Jax
bio: Jax is a member of the free folk of the haunted forest who joins the Night's Watch. Well past forty, Jax has sons and grandsons.

name: Jayde
bio: Jayde is a whore at Chataya's brothel in King's Landing.

name: Jayne Bracken
bio: Jayne Bracken is a noblewoman of House Bracken, the second daughter of Lord Jonos_Bracken of Stone Hedge by his first wife.

name: Jayne Ladybright
bio: Jayne Ladybright is Dornish noblewoman from House Ladybright. Her relation to Alyse_Ladybright, Sunspear's Lord treasurer, is unknown.

name: Jeffory Mallister
bio: Jeffory Mallister was a member of House Mallister during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. When news of Lyanna_Stark's kidnapping by Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen reached her brother, Brandon_Stark, he raced to King's Landing accompanied by his party, which included Jeffory. Brandon and his party rode into the Red Keep demanding Rhaegar's head. Rhaegar's father, King Aerys_II_Targaryen, had them arrested and demanded that their fathers come to King's Landing to answer for their sons' crimes. Aerys had all of them killed save for Ethan_Glover.

name: Jeffory Norcross
bio: Jeffory Norcross, better known as Neveryield, was a knight of House Norcross. He was a member of the Kingsguard at an unknown time.

name: Jellicoe
bio: Maester Jellicoe was a maester of the Citadel that supposedly lived among the children of the forest. He suggested that the settlement at the top of the Whispering Sound that would eventually become Oldtown began as a trading post where ships from Valyria, Old Ghis, and the Summer Isles put in to replenish their provisions, make repairs, and trade with the "elder races"[N 1]. According to this theory, Oldtown would date to the Dawn Age and predate even the arrival of the First Men to Westeros.

name: Jena Dondarrion
bio: Jena Dondarrion was a member of House Dondarrion during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Although the Marcher lords mistrusted King Daeron II and his heir, Jena married the Prince of Dragonstone, Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II). Together they had two sons: Valarr_Targaryen and Matarys_Targaryen.

name: Jennelyn Fowler
bio: Jennelyn Fowler is a daughter of Lord Franklyn_Fowler, and a twin to Jeyne_Fowler. Jennelyn is the youngest of the two. In her youth, Princess Arianne_Martell played in the pools of the Water Gardens with Jeyne and Jennelyn, riding one of the twins' shoulders.

name: Jennis Templeton
bio: Jennis Templeton was a noblewoman from House Templeton, companion to Queen Alysanne_Targaryen, and later Lady of Uplands during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Jennis Templeton was a companion to Queen Alysanne_Targaryen on Dragonstone from 51 AC. During the second wedding of King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne, Jennis helped to disrobe the king as part of the bedding ceremony. During Alysanne's pregnancy with Princess Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I), she sent Jennis to hold women's courts in her place. In 55 AC, Jennis married Lord Mullendore of Uplands as arranged by Alysanne. It was considered an exceptional match for her, and a triumph for the queen.

name: Jenny of Oldstones
bio: Jenny of Oldstones was the wife of Duncan_Targaryen, the Prince of Dragonflies. There are many songs written about her, including Jenny's song, which the Ghost_of_High_Heart always wants to hear. Jenny claimed descent from long-vanished kings of the First Men. The ruined castle of Oldstones was once the seat of House Mudd. Duncan_Targaryen, the Prince of Dragonstone, met Jenny while traveling the riverlands in 239 AC. The prince loved Jenny so much he married her against the wishes of his father, King Aegon_V_Targaryen, breaking his betrothal to the daughter of Lyonel_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End. King Aegon tried to have the marriage undone, but Duncan refused to give Jenny up, ultimately relinquishing his rights to the Iron Throne for her, while the outraged Lyonel led a short-lived rebellion. Duncan became known as the Prince of Dragonflies, while his younger brother Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen became Prince of Dragonstone and the new heir. Jenny was titled "Lady" by courtesy, and was eventually accepted at court. The smallfolk throughout the Seven Kingdoms cherished her, and she and Duncan were a favorite subject of singers for many years. Jenny was friends with a Ghost_of_High_Heart, a woods witch, whom she believed to be one of the children of the forest. The woman, whom Jenny brought to court, prophesied that the prince that was promised would be born from the line of Prince Aerys_II_Targaryen and Princess Rhaella_Targaryen. When Prince Jaehaerys heard the prophecy, he arranged a marriage between his two children. King Aegon and Prince Duncan were killed in the tragedy at Summerhall in 259 AC. It is uncertain if Jenny died in the fire or survived; according to a song, Jenny danced with her ghosts in the hall of kings, though this may be speaking of her life at Oldstones. When Catelyn_Stark was twelve, she camped at Oldstones while traveling to Seagard with her father, Lord Hoster_Tully. Catelyn and Petyr_Baelish, her father's ward, pretended to be Jenny and Duncan, with Catelyn winding flowers in her hair.

name: Penny Jenny
bio: Jenny, better known as Penny Jenny, and later known as Redgrass Jenny, was a whore. Jenny was a camp follower, known as Penny Jenny. Among her known lovers was Ser Quentyn_Ball. The night before the Battle of the Redgrass Field, she slept with dozens of men, which earned her a new nickname, Redgrass Jenny. Within nine months after the battle, she gave birth to a son, Glendon_Flowers, who she claimed had been fathered by the famous Quentyn Ball. Some point after the war, Jenny established herself at a brothel, the Pussywillows. There she raised her son and daughter. After she died, the other women working at the Pussywillows raised her two children.

name: Jeor Mormont
bio: Jeor Mormont, also known as the Old Bear, is the 997th Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, which he commands from their headquarters, Castle Black. Jeor, previously head of House Mormont and Lord of Bear Island, abdicated his seat in favor of his son, Ser Jorah_Mormont, and joined the Night's Watch. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Jeor is portrayed by James Cosmo. Presumably prior to Robert's Rebellion,[N 1] Jeor abdicated his seat, leaving the lordship of Bear Island to his son, Ser Jorah_Mormont, and joined the Night's Watch. Jeor rapidly rose through the ranks and was elected Lord Commander in 288 AC; Ser Denys_Mallister removed his name from consideration when Jeor's was offered. Since the Mallisters distrust ironborn, Lord Commander Mormont kept Denys and Cotter_Pyke at opposite ends of the Wall. Jeor was deeply concerned by the declining power of the Watch and the rising threat of the wildlings living beyond the Wall. On the advice of his First Ranger, Benjen_Stark, he gave up the regular patrols used by his predecessor, Lord_Commander_Qorgyle, in favor of a more random system, varying the number of patrols and the days of their departure, to make it more difficult for the wildlings to know their comings and goings. Sometimes a larger force was sent to garrison one of the abandoned castles for a fortnight or a moon's turn as well. When Lord Jorah fled to the Free Cities to escape Lord Eddard_Stark's punishment for selling poachers into slavery, Jeor regained Longclaw, the Valyrian steel sword of House Mormont, which his son left behind. Jeor's sister, Maege_Mormont, became Lady of Bear Island with the exile of Jorah. Jeor has written cautionary notes about problematic brothers of the Watch for his eventual successor.

name: Jeremy Hightower
bio: Jeremy Hightower was a Lord of the Hightower and a head of House Hightower. Jeremy fostered trade and built ever more ships to protect it, doubling Oldtown's wealth. He was succeeded by his son Jason_Hightower.

name: Jeremy Norridge
bio: Jeremy Norridge was a knight from House Norridge during the reign of King Aegon_V_Targaryen. In his youth, Jeremy was a squire at Highgarden, where he befriended Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon_V), third son of King Aegon_V_Targaryen. The two became constant companions, to the point that Daeron broke his betrothal to Olenna_Redwyne in 246 AC. In 251 AC, Prince Daeron led an army against the rebels known as The_Rat,_the_Hawk,_and_the_Pig. Though they managed to quash the rebellion, Daeron was killed in battle, as was Jeremy, who died by his side.

name: Jeren
bio: Jeren is a recruit of the Night's Watch.

name: Jessamyn Manderly
bio: Jessamyn Manderly was a noblewoman from House Manderly and the daughter of Lord Theomore_Manderly. She had at least three brothers and a sister. When Queen Alysanne_Targaryen visited White Harbor in 58 AC during a royal progress, Jessamyn acted as Alysanne's cupbearer.

name: Jessamyn Redfort
bio: Jessamyn Redfort was a noblewoman from House Redfort during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Jessamyn was a "dear companion" of Jeyne_Arryn, the Lady of the Eyrie. Jeyne, who never married, died in Jessamyn's arms at the Motherhouse of Maris in 134 AC.

name: Grey Jeyne
bio: Grey Jeyne is one of the bastard's girls.

name: Red Jeyne
bio: Red Jeyne is one of the bastard's girls.

name: Squinty Jeyne
bio: Jeyne, better known as Squinty Jeyne, was a peasant who lived on House Osgrey lands during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. She had a lackwit son, Big Rob.

name: Jeyne Arryn
bio: Jeyne Arryn, called the Maiden of the Vale, was the Lady of the Eyrie and the head of House Arryn during the reign of kings Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen, and Aegon_III_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Jeyne is portrayed by Amanda Collin. Jeyne became Lady of the Eyrie in 97 AC when she was three years old, after her Lord_Arryn_(father_of_Jeyne) and older brothers were killed at the hands of the Stone Crows. She did not attend the Great Council of 101 AC. In her stead went Lord Yorbert_Royce, who served as Lord Protector and regent during Jeyne's minority. Jeyne's inheritance was twice contested by her cousin, Ser Arnold_Arryn. Consequently, he was imprisoned in one of the sky cells, while he and his descendants were disinherited from the Arryn line of succession by her last testament. Jeyne would later say that "thrice" her own kin had tried to replace her, but it was also clearly stated that Arnold only rebelled against her twice; possibly the third time was when she was initially named ruler of the Vale at only three years old, or when she came of age in 110 AC. On the death of Lady Rhea_Royce in 115 AC, her husband Prince Daemon_Targaryen appealed to Lady Jeyne in the hopes of claiming Runestone for himself. Jeyne not only rejected his claim, but also warned him that his presence in the Vale of Arryn was unwelcome.

name: Jeyne Beesbury
bio: Jeyne Beesbury is a noblewoman of House Beesbury and the wife of Rhaegar_Frey, with whom she has three children, Robert_Frey_(son_of_Rhaegar), Walda_Frey_(daughter_of_Rhaegar), and Jonos_Frey.

name: Jeyne Darry
bio: Jeyne Darry is the wife of Ser Cleos_Frey, the eldest son of Lady Genna_Lannister and Lord Emmon_Frey. They have two sons: Tywin_Frey and Willem_Frey. Jeyne's father was the lord of House Darry. She is the younger sister of Mariya_Darry and a sister of Ser Raymun_Darry.

name: Jeyne Farman
bio: Jeyne Farman is a noblewoman of House Farman. She is the sister of Lord Sebaston_Farman, the Lord of Fair Isle, and is the wife of Ser Gareth_Clifton. Jeyne was a childhood friend and bedmaid of Cersei_Lannister at Casterly Rock, along with another bedmaid and friend, Melara_Hetherspoon. In 276 AC, the girls attended the tourney hosted by Lord Tywin_Lannister in Lannisport in honor of Prince Viserys's birth. The first night of the tourney, Cersei decided to visit the witch woman Maggy_the_Frog with Melara and Jeyne to have their fortunes told, after Melara told them tales of Maggy's powers she had heard from the Casterly Rock serving girls, and because Cersei wanted to show her friends that a queen and a lioness had no fears. That night, Cersei and her friends sneaked out of their beds to seek Maggy's tent on the tourney grounds, with the timid Jeyne hanging back behind them. They entered the tent to find that the sorceress was sleeping, and Cersei kicked her bed and demanded she wake up and tell their fortunes. However, when Maggy opened her yellow and crusted eyes, the sight of them caused Jeyne to flee into the night, shrieking with fear, and she never heard her future. Later, Jeyne married one of her brother Lord Sebaston_Farman's bannermen, Ser Gareth_Clifton, and had a dozen children.

name: Jeyne Fossoway
bio: Jeyne Fossoway is a member of House Fossoway of New Barrel. She is the wife of Ser Gunthor_Hightower.

name: Jeyne Fowler
bio: Jeyne Fowler is a daughter of Lord Franklyn_Fowler, and a twin to Jennelyn_Fowler. Jeyne is the elder of the two. In her youth, Princess Arianne_Martell played in the pools of the Water Gardens with Jeyne and Jennelyn_Fowler, riding one of the twins' shoulders.

name: Jeyne Goodbrook
bio: Jeyne Goodbrook is the only daughter of Ser Garse_Goodbrook and Kyra_Frey.

name: Jeyne Harroway
bio: Jeyne Harroway was one of the daughters of Lord Lucas_Harroway of Harrenhal, and the sister of Queen Alys_Harroway, one of King Maegor_I_Targaryen's many wives. When her sister Alys_Harroway became pregnant in 44 AC, Jeyne and her other sister Hanna_Harroway assisted Grand Maester Desmond with Alys's care as the queen was confined to her bed. When Alys gave birth to a monstrous stillborn boy three months later, Jeyne and Hanna were the only ones spared from Maegor's wroth. Later, when the Kingsguard arrived at Alys's chambers to arrest her, Jeyne and her sister Hanna were cut down trying to protect Alys.

name: Jeyne Heddle
bio: Jeyne Heddle, called Long Jeyne for her height, is a member of the Heddle family. She has a younger sister, Willow_Heddle. According to the Elder Brother, the sisters are nieces of Masha_Heddle, the innkeep of the inn at the crossroads. According to a semi-canon source, Masha is their great-aunt.

name: Jeyne Lothston
bio: Jeyne Lothston was a noblewoman from House Lothston and the daughter of Lord Lucas_Lothston_(husband_of_Falena) and Lady Falena_Stokeworth. She was King Aegon_IV_Targaryen's eighth mistress, and rumored to have been possibly fathered by the king himself. Jeyne was brought to court by her mother, Falena_Stokeworth, in 178 AC, when she was fourteen. King Aegon_IV_Targaryen made Lord Lucas_Lothston_(husband_of_Falena) of Harrenhal his new Hand, and it was said (but never proved) that he enjoyed mother and daughter together in the same bed. Aegon soon gave Jeyne a pox he had caught from the whores he had seen after Lady Bethany_Bracken's execution, and the Lothstons were then all sent from court.

name: Jeyne Lydden
bio: Jeyne Lydden was a noblewoman from House Lydden. Her relation to Lewys_Lydden, the Lord of the Deep Den, is unknown. She was the second wife of Ser Stevron_Frey. They had two children, Aegon and Maegelle_Frey. Jeyne died of a fall from a horse.

name: Jeyne Manderly
bio: Jeyne Manderly was a noblewoman from House Manderly during the second century of the Targaryen era. She married Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan), the heir of Lord Cregan_Stark of Winterfell. Together they had two daughters, Sansa_Stark_(daughter_of_Rickon) and Serena_Stark.

name: Jeyne Marbrand
bio: Jeyne Marbrand was a member of House Marbrand who became Lady of Casterly Rock. She was the wife of Lord Tytos_Lannister and mother to his five children, including the present lord, Tywin_Lannister. The daughter of Alyn_Marbrand,[N 1] Lord of Ashemark, Jeyne was married to Tytos_Lannister in 235 AC, in a double ceremony with Tytos's older brother, Ser Tion_Lannister, who married Lady Ellyn_Reyne.

name: Jeyne Merryweather
bio: Jeyne Merryweather was a member of House Merryweather during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 133 AC, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Many maidens across Westeros came to King's Landing in hopes of marrying the young king. Jeyne was one of the many maidens who suffered what was called the "Maiden's Day curse" by being injured or scandalized after the ball was announced. In Jeyne's case, a rumor spread that she, Jeyne_Mooton, and Jeyne Smallwood (known as the "three Jeynes") liked to dress in squire's garb, visiting the brothels along the Street of Silk to kiss and fondle the women there as if they were boys. It is possible that this was a rumor fabricated by Lord Unwin_Peake, the Hand of the King, who wished to promote his Myrielle_Peake as Aegon III's second queen.

name: Jeyne Mooton
bio: Jeyne Mooton was a member of House Mooton during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 133 AC, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Many maidens across Westeros came to King's Landing in hopes of marrying the young king. Jeyne was one of the many maidens who suffered what was called the "Maiden's Day curse" by being injured or scandalized after the ball was announced. In Jeyne's case, a rumor spread that she, Jeyne_Merryweather, and Jeyne Smallwood (known as the "three Jeynes") liked to dress in squire's garb, visiting the brothels along the Street of Silk to kiss and fondle the women there as if they were boys. It is possible that this was a rumor fabricated by Lord Unwin_Peake, the Hand of the King, who wished to promote his Myrielle_Peake as Aegon III's second queen.

name: Jeyne Poole
bio: Jeyne Poole is a member of House Poole and is the daughter of the steward of Winterfell, Vayon_Poole.

name: Jeyne Poore
bio: Jeyne Poore, known as Poxy Jeyne Poore, was a leader of the Poor Fellows during the Faith Militant uprising against King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Following the Faith Militant's defeats in 42 AC in the battles at Stonebridge and the Great Fork of the Blackwater, new leaders arose across the Seven Kingdoms in 43 AC. While the Warrior's Sons were all knights, the lower order of Poor Fellows contained male and female members. Poxy Jeyne led the Poor Fellows in the forested lands between King's Landing and Storm's End, making travel impassable. The forested terrain made Jeyne's cell more difficult to fight than others, and the kingswood was in close proximity to King's Landing. When King Maegor_I_Targaryen launched a new military campaign against the Faith Militant late in the year 45 AC, he targeted Jeyne's Poor Fellows. Scores of them were hunted down, and any taken alive were hanged if they refused to join the Night's Watch. Although Jeyne eluded Maegor's forces for some time, she was eventually betrayed by three followers who received pardons and knighthoods for turning her over to the king. Three septons with Maegor declared Jeyne to be a witch, and the king ordered her to be burned in a field by the Wendwater. On the day of Jeyne's execution, three hundred Poor Fellows and peasants under the leadership of Ser Horys_Hill attempted to rescue her. Jeyne's public execution was a trap to lure out her remaining followers, however, with Maegor's forces waiting and ready for them in an ambush. The rescuers were surrounded and slaughtered. After a year of campaigning, Maegor and his army returned to King's Landing in 46 AC in triumph, along with two thousand skulls which he questionably claimed to all have been Faith Militant. Other leaders of the Faith Militant had meanwhile regrown their strength, however. Septon Moon_(septon) openly preached against Maegor the Cruel to huge crowds in the riverlands and the Reach, and Ser Joffrey_Doggett had become the ruler in all but name of the hill country north of the Golden Tooth in the westerlands. Dennis_the_Lame and Ragged_Silas continued their efforts as well.

name: Jeyne Rivers
bio: Jeyne Rivers is a natural daughter of Lord Walder_Frey. Her mother has not been mentioned.

name: Jeyne Royce
bio: Jeyne Royce was the first wife of Lord Jon_Arryn. Jeyne died giving birth to a stillborn daughter.

name: Jeyne Rowan
bio: Jeyne Rowan was the Lady of Horn Hill during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. Lady Jeyne was married to Lord Donald Tarly. They had at least two daughters, Samantha_Tarly and Sansara_Tarly. As Samantha and Sansara lived during the Dance of the Dragons and shortly after, Lady Jeyne may have been the mother of Alan_Tarly, who was head of House Tarly during the Dance. It is unknown if she was still alive during the Dance.

name: Jeyne Swann
bio: Jeyne Swann was a member of House Swann during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. It is currently unknown if Jeyne is still alive in the main series. Her relation to Lord Gulian_Swann of Stonehelm, the current head of the House, is also unknown. Ser Barristan_Selmy rescued Lady Jeyne and her septa from the Kingswood Brotherhood defeating the Smiling_Knight and killing Simon_Toyne.

name: Jeyne Waters
bio: Jeyne Waters was the bastard daughter of Lord Alyn_Velaryon and Princess Elaena_Targaryen. Jeyne was the bastard daughter of the seafarer Lord Alyn_Velaryon, known as the Oakenfist, and Princess Elaena_Targaryen. She had a twin brother, Jon_Waters.

name: Jeyne Westerling
bio: Queen Jeyne Westerling was a member of House Westerling. She was one of the six wives of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Jeyne was married to Lord Alyn_Tarbeck. She was widowed when Alyn died during the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye in 43 AC. Jeyne was pregnant when her husband died, and gave him a posthumous Lord_Tarbeck_(son_of_Alyn) a few months later. In 47 AC, Jeyne was being courted by a younger son of Lyman_Lannister, the Lord of Casterly Rock, when King Maegor_I_Targaryen sent for her to be wed to him. She married Maegor in a ceremony at King's Landing, along with Lady Elinor_Costayne and Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). As all three women had been widowed due to Maegor, they became known as the "Black Brides". Jeyne's son was present for the wedding, to ensure that she would take part in the ceremony as desired. The stories told of the wedding night claim that Jeyne was given a fertility potion by Queen Tyanna_of_the_Tower, and either drank it, or threw it in Tyanna's face. After the wedding, Queen Jeyne's son was confirmed as Lord of Tarbeck Hall, and sent to Casterly Rock to be raised as a ward of Lyman Lannister. Lord Edwell_Celtigar, the Hand of the King, announced half a year after the wedding that Queen Jeyne was pregnant, and Queen Elinor's pregnancy was announced shortly afterwards. Maegor, joyful, showered both his wives with gifts and honors, and granted new lands and offices to their fathers, brothers, and uncles. Unfortunately, Jeyne's labor began three months early, and she gave birth to a stillborn child, monstrous, lacking arms and legs but possessing both male and female genitalia. Jeyne herself died soon after. In 48 AC, Tyanna_of_the_Tower confessed to having poisoned Jeyne's child in the womb.

name: Jeyne Westerling
bio: Jeyne Westerling is the eldest daughter of Lord Gawen_Westerling and Lady Sybell_Spicer. In the television adaptation  Game of Thrones, her character is replaced by Talisa Maegyr of Volantis, played by Oona Chaplin. House Westerling is an old house who descend from the First Men. Despite their ancient heritage and nobility, the family is not rich, as their mines have failed. Possibly to better his finances, Lord Gawen_Westerling wed Sybell_Spicer, the daughter of an ennobled family, House Spicer, who descend from a spice merchant and Maggy_the_Frog, a maegi of Essos who provided potions to the people of Lannisport. Gawen once offered his daughter Jeyne to Ser Kevan_Lannister as a match for one of his sons, Willem_Lannister or Martyn_Lannister, but Kevan rejected the offer because of the "doubtful blood" of the Spicers.

name: Jez
bio: Jez is one of the bastard's girls.

name: Jezhene
bio: Jezhene (or Jhezane) is the child of one of the noble ruling families of Meereen. Jezhene is older than Qezza_Galare.

name: Jhaqo
bio: Jhaqo is a ko in the khalasar commanded by Khal Drogo.

name: Jhiqui
bio: Jhiqui is a Dothraki handmaid of Daenerys_Targaryen. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Jhiqui is portrayed by Sarita Piotrowski. Jhiqui was born to a khal of a khalasar. It was destroyed by Khal Drogo, and she was enslaved.

name: Jhogo
bio: Jhogo is a Dothraki horserider in the khalasar of Khal Drogo. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is renamed Kovarro[N 1] and portrayed by Steven Cole.

name: Jirelle Mooton
bio: Jirelle Mooton was the Lady of Maidenpool and the head of House Mooton during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Lady Jirelle died from the Shivers during the winter of 59–60 AC.

name: Joanna Lannister
bio: Joanna Lannister was a member of House Lannister who became Lady of Casterly Rock, the wife of Lord Tywin_Lannister, and the mother of Queen Cersei_Lannister, Ser Jaime_Lannister, and Tyrion_Lannister. She died giving birth to Tyrion in 273 AC. Joanna's father, Ser Jason_Lannister_(son_of_Gerold), was the younger brother of Tytos_Lannister, Lord of Casterly Rock. She had one older sibling, Damon_Lannister_(son_of_Jason), a son from her father's first marriage, and five younger siblings (three brothers, two sisters), the eldest of whom was Stafford_Lannister. She also had an older bastard sister, Lynora_Hill. Tywin_Lannister, the eldest son of Lord Tytos, met his cousin Joanna at Casterly Rock while they were children. Joanna went to King's Landing in 259 AC, for the coronation of King Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen, and remained as a lady-in-waiting for the future queen, Princess Rhaella_Targaryen. There were rumors that Joanna gave her maidenhead to Prince Aerys_II_Targaryen the night of Jaehaerys's coronation, and that she briefly became Aerys's paramour after he ascended the Iron Throne. However, Grand Maester Pycelle insists these tales are baseless, as Tywin would not have married Joanna if they were true, "for he was ever a proud man and not one accustomed to feasting on another man's leavings." Joanna married her first cousin, Tywin_Lannister, now serving as Aerys's Hand of the King, in 263 AC in a lavish ceremony in the Great Sept of Baelor. On their wedding day, the oft-grim Tywin smiled, a rare occurrence. At the wedding feast, Aerys drunkenly japed about how it was a pity the first night tradition was banned, and he took certain "unwonted liberties" with Joanna in the bedding ritual when the men at the feast had to disrobe the bride. Not long thereafter, Joanna was dismissed by Queen Rhaella_Targaryen from her service in King's Landing. While Rhaella turned a blind eye towards most of her husband's infidelities, she did not approve when it concerned one of her own ladies, several of whom were sent away from court. Joanna departed for Casterly Rock at once, and she seldom visited the capital afterwards. Joanna's marriage to Tywin was reportedly a happy one, and she became Tywin's trusted counselor and companion. Her influence on her husband was such that people say that Tywin ruled the Seven Kingdoms as Hand, "but was ruled at home by his lady wife." In 266 AC, Joanna gave birth to Cersei_Lannister and Jaime_Lannister, with Tywin present. King Aerys ordered Tywin to bring Joanna and the twins to King's Landing when the children were old enough to travel, but Lord Tytos_Lannister died before this could occur. Instead, Tywin, Aerys, Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen, and half the court went to the westerlands in 267 AC, where they remained for most of the next year. Joanna attended the Anniversary Tourney in King's Landing in 272 AC, held to celebrate Aerys's tenth year on the Iron Throne. Aerys insulted Joanna by asking her if nursing her children had ruined her breasts, and the king refused the outraged Tywin's resignation the next day. At some point within the next year, Joanna's servant caught Jaime and Cersei together, engaged in some kind of sexual activity. Joanna sent the maid away, moved Jaime's chamber to the other side of Casterly Rock, and put a guard outside of Cersei's chamber. She then warned her twins that they must never do such a thing again, or else she would be forced to tell their father.

name: Joanna Swyft
bio: Joanna Swyft is a member of House Swyft, the daughter of Ser Steffon_Swyft, the heir of Cornfield.

name: Jocasta Tarbeck
bio: Jocasta Lannister, born Jocasta Tarbeck, was the wife of Lord Lyman_Lannister of Casterly Rock during the reign of kings Maegor_I_Targaryen and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Jocasta's husband, Lord Lyman_Lannister, protected Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) and Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) during the early years of the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen, extending them guest right. During their stay at Casterly Rock, Jocasta was the first to recognize that Rhaena was pregnant. In early 49 AC, Jocasta and her husband attended the small wedding of Rhaena to Androw_Farman. After Rhaena was exiled from Fair Isle in early 50 AC, Jocasta and Lyman welcomed them to Casterly Rock, staging a lavish feast in their honor. During the time Rhaena and her court remained at Casterly Rock, Jocasta suggested to Rhaena that one of Rhaena's dragon eggs would make a fine gift to House Lannister, to thank the Lannisters for providing them with a place to stay. Rhaena was not responsive to the suggestion, and departed from the Rock shortly after.

name: Jocelyn Baratheon
bio: Jocelyn Baratheon was a noblewoman from House Baratheon. She was the daughter of Lord Rogar_Baratheon, the Lord of Storm's End, and Dowager Queen Alyssa_Velaryon. She had an older brother, Boremund_Baratheon, and six elder half-siblings, Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I), Viserys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I), Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, Alysanne_Targaryen, and Vaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), born to Alyssa Velaryon from an earlier marriage. Jocelyn was born one month premature in 54 AC. Her mother, Alyssa_Velaryon, fell severely ill due to the pregnancy, and was unable to give birth by herself. According to Maester Kyrie, he could not save Alyssa, but by cutting her open he might be able to save Jocelyn. Lord Rogar_Baratheon then told the maester to save his child. Some days after her birth, Rogar named his daughter. In 58 AC, Jocelyn came to King's Landing with her father Rogar and her older brother Boremund_Baratheon for the tourney held on the anniversary of King Jaehaerys I's tenth anniversary on the throne. Jocelyn was raised mostly by the wives of her father's brothers, but they both favored their own children, and died eventually of Winter Fever. In 61 AC, at the age of six, Jocelyn was once again brought to court by her father, together with two daughters of her father's brother Ronnal_Baratheon. Lord Rogar requested to King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen that Jocelyn be raised at court as their ward. The king and queen gladly accepted. In 62 AC, during the feast held to celebrate the investiture of Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I) as Prince of Dragonstone, Jocelyn was seated beside Prince Aemon by Queen Alysanne. The two young people ignored all those around them all evening.

name: Jocelyn Penrose
bio: Jocelyn Penrose was a member of House Penrose and the second daughter of Lord Ronnel_Penrose and his wife Princess Elaena_Targaryen.

name: Jocelyn Stark
bio: Jocelyn Stark was the only daughter of Lord Willam_Stark and Lady Melantha_Blackwood. She was married to Benedict_Royce and had children. According to Catelyn_Stark, she had three daughters; one married a Waynwood, another a Corbray, and the third might have married a Templeton.

name: Jocelyn Swyft
bio: Jocelyn Swyft is a noblewoman of House Swyft. She is the companion and lady attendant of Queen Cersei_Lannister.

name: Jodge
bio: Jodge is a squire for Ser Dunaver.

name: Joffrey Arryn
bio: Joffrey Arryn was a Knight of the Gate from House Arryn who later became Lord of the Eyrie during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Ser Joffrey became the Knight of the Gate in 124 AC and served his fourth cousin Jeyne_Arryn, the Lady of the Eyrie, for ten years before her death in 134 AC. When she died, Lady Jeyne named Ser Joffrey as her heir in her last testament, although Ser Arnold_Arryn was more closely related to her. However, as Ser Arnold had twice tried to depose her and had been imprisoned for it, he was disinherited from the line of Arryn succession. However, tensions brewed on Jeyne's death between those who supported Jeyne's chosen successor and those who supported Ser Arnold. Many lords flocked to Ser Arnold's son, Ser Eldric_Arryn, insisting that the laws of inheritance could not be put aside by "the whim of a dying woman." Additionally, Isembard_Arryn, head of House Arryn of Gulltown, emerged as a third claimant to the Eyrie. One of the young king Aegon_III_Targaryen's regents, Ser Corwyn_Corbray, ruled that Lady Jeyne's will must prevail and declared Ser Joffrey the rightful Lord of the Eyrie. Isembard Arryn and his sons were imprisoned, and Ser Eldric was executed, but Ser Arnold eluded the knight and fled to Runestone, where Ser Corwyn was struck down by a crossbowman once he drew Lady Forlorn. Striking down the king's regent was an act of treason, and war began anew across the Vale of Arryn. Houses Corbray, Hunter, Crayne and Redfort supported Ser Joffrey's claim, and the Hand of the King, Lord Thaddeus_Rowan, commanded the lords supporting Ser Arnold and Isembard to lay down their arms. When no reply was forthcoming, the crown sent men through the Mountains of the Moon and launched a sea attack to support Ser Joffrey's claim. By the time the new regents were chosen by lot in 136 AC, Lord Alyn_Velaryon and Lord Benjicot_Blackwood had at last forced the supporters of Isembard and Arnold to do homage to Lord Joffrey as their liege.

name: Joffrey Baratheon
bio: Prince Joffrey Baratheon is known to the Seven Kingdoms as the eldest son and heir of King Robert_I_Baratheon and Queen Cersei_Lannister. A member of House Baratheon of King's Landing, his siblings are Princess Myrcella_Baratheon and Prince Tommen_Baratheon. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Joffrey is portrayed by Jack Gleeson. Joffrey grew up as a spoiled and indulgent child with a cruel streak within him. His father, King Robert_I_Baratheon, is deeply disappointed with his son and feels little affection for Joffrey, nor did he like the fact that Joffrey would always cry whenever Robert picked him up. It is implied that Joffrey craves Robert's respect and approval, and many of his acts are done in an effort to live up to his father's standards. Once after learning a kitchen cat was pregnant, Joffrey killed the animal and cut open its belly to see the kittens inside. He showed one of the unborn kittens to his father. Robert was so shocked and angry he hit Joffrey so hard it knocked out two of his baby teeth. Queen Cersei_Lannister believes Robert would have beaten the boy if she had allowed it. It is also implied Joffrey bullied his younger brother, Tommen_Baratheon. Cersei assigned Sandor_Clegane to be her son's sworn shield. While Sandor is widely known as the Hound, Joffrey refers to his guardian as "dog". For Joffrey's twelfth name day, King Robert organized a tourney in King's Landing in 298 AC, where, amongst others, Lord Walder_Frey, Lord Jon_Arryn, Lord Stannis_Baratheon, and Ser Davos_Seaworth were present. Following the tourney, Joffrey and his siblings traveled with Cersei to Casterly Rock with their grandfather, Lord Tywin_Lannister.

name: Joffrey Caswell
bio: Joffrey Caswell was the Lord of Bitterbridge and the head of House Caswell during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. At the time of the Second Blackfyre Rebellion, he was twenty years of age. Ser Kyle the Cat, a hedge knight, once served Lord Armond_Caswell as a sworn sword. He made the first sword for three-year-old Joffrey, carving a small wooden sword out of pine to fit his hand. Lord Joffrey attended the wedding tourney at Whitewalls in 212 AC, and he fell asleep in his wine at the wedding feast. The morning after, he faced Ser Kyle in his first joust of the tourney. The hedge knight planned to throw the match in order to compliment Joffrey on his growth as a warrior and then become a Caswell man again. Though Joffrey's horse was slow, his lance was wobbly, and he barely struck Kyle's shoulder, the Cat fell off his horse into the dirt. Joffrey excitedly pumped his lance in the air after his defeat of Kyle. When Kyle came to Joffrey to pay the forfeit, however, and told him about how he had once made his first sword, Joffrey did not recognize him. Lord Caswell said there was no place at Bitterbridge for knights as feeble as Kyle the Cat had shown himself to be. He still took Kyle's arms, armor, and horse, since Kyle could not pay the ransom for them. While investigating the Second Blackfyre Rebellion, Ser Duncan_the_Tall found an inebriated Joffrey among a group of men leaving the great hall of Whitewalls after a feast.

name: Joffrey Dayne
bio: Joffrey Dayne was a knight of House Dayne during the reign of Aegon_I_Targaryen. In 10 AC during the First Dornish War, King Aegon_I_Targaryen's attempt to conquer Dorne, Lord Fowler captured Nightsong. When Ser Addam_Hightower led a strong host to retake the castle, Ser Joffrey marched an army from Starfall to Oldtown. Unable to breach the city's walls, Joffrey had the fields, farms, and villages burned for twenty leagues around the city. When Lord Manfred_Hightower_(Aegon's_Conquest)'s younger son Garmon_Hightower led a sortie against Dayne, Joffrey slew the boy. By the time Addam returned to Oldtown, Joffrey's army had melted back into the Red Mountains. In response to Ser Joffrey's attack, Queen Visenya_Targaryen and her dragon, Vhagar, burned Starfall.

name: Joffrey Doggett
bio: Joffrey Doggett, known as the Red Dog of the Hills, was a knight from House Doggett during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen, and a member of the Lannisport chapter of the Warrior's Sons. He was later named to the Kingsguard by King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen.

name: Joffrey Lannister
bio: Joffrey Lannister, born Joffrey Lydden, was the first King of the Rock of Andal descent. Ser Joffrey, a knight of House Lydden, was married to the only daughter of King Gerold_III_Lannister, who was of First Men ancestry. When the king died without male issue, Joffrey was crowned by a council and took the name of House Lannister.

name: Joffrey Lonmouth
bio: Joffrey Lonmouth, known as the Knight of Kisses, was a knight from House Lonmouth during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Joffrey is portrayed by Solly McLeod. Ser Joffrey was Laenor_Velaryon's favorite knight and companion. At Laenor's wedding to Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, Joffrey rode as Laenor's champion in the tourney while Ser Harwin_Strong rode for the princess. During the tourney, Ser Criston_Cole attacked him with a morningstar and cracked his helm. He died six days later without recovering consciousness; Laenor never left his side and wept at his death. Ser Laenor wanted to name his first son, Jacaerys_Velaryon, after him, but was overruled by his father, Lord Corlys_Velaryon. Instead, Laenor's third son, Joffrey_Velaryon, bore his name.

name: Joffrey Staunton
bio: Joffrey Staunton was a knight from House Staunton and a member of the Kingsguard, during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 155 AC, Ser Joffrey was riding with Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen near Fairmarket, when the prince's horse threw a shoe. After they found the local smith, the prince liked his buxom young wife, Megette. The prince absconded with the blacksmith's wife, buying her for seven gold dragons, along with the threat of Ser Joffrey.

name: Joffrey Velaryon
bio: Prince Joffrey Velaryon, also known as Joff, was the thirdborn son of Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen and her first husband, Ser Laenor_Velaryon. He was a dragonrider whose dragon was Tyraxes. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Joffrey is portrayed by Oscar Eskinazi. He was named after his father's favorite knight and companion, Ser Joffrey_Lonmouth. When Joffrey was three years old, his ten-year-old dragonless half-uncle, Prince Aemond_Targaryen, slapped him in an attempt to claim Vhagar for himself. Joffrey called for his two older brothers, and in the ensuing fight, Aemond lost an eye. Joffrey was eleven years old at the start of the civil war which became known as the Dance of the Dragons. He asked to ride Tyraxes and help call the lords of the realm to his mother's claim, but was denied on account of his age. However, his elder brothers went forth, and Jacaerys arranged his betrothal to the youngest daughter of Lord Desmond Manderly in exchange for White Harbor's support for the blacks. After learning that Lucerys had died in the Dance over Shipbreaker Bay, Joffrey swore a terrible oath of vengeance against Prince Aemond and Lord Borros_Baratheon, and only his grandparents, Lord Corlys_Velaryon and Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon), kept him from mounting his own dragon at once. Jacaerys ordered Joff to Gulltown to defend the Vale of Arryn against King Aegon_II_Targaryen's dragons, with Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon) chosen to accompany him. Grand Maester Munkun suggests that it was Jacaerys's desire to keep his brother far away from the fighting. He was named the Prince of Dragonstone after the death of Jacaerys at the Battle of the Gullet. After the Fall of King's Landing, Rhaenyra felt secure enough to send for her sons, and a fleet set out from Gulltown with Joffrey. His mother planned a lavish celebration for Joffrey's formal installation as Prince of Dragonstone and heir to the Iron Throne. Joffrey was eager to prove himself as a warrior and defeat the armies of the greens, but swore solemnly to help his mother hold the Red Keep in the event of an attack when told to remain in the city with Tyraxes. During the Storming of the Dragonpit, he donned squire's armor and begged the queen to let him ride to the Dragonpit and mount Tyraxes, but she forbade him from leaving the castle out of her grief for his brothers' deaths. When the Shepherd and his savage flock began to march on the Dragonpit, he pleaded with his mother to let him ride forth with knights, anxious for the life of his own dragon, though Rhaenyra refused to believe that such rabble would be able to kill the dragons. Mushroom claims to have seen Joffrey sulking off, but to have held his tongue. In haste to be away before he could be stopped, Joffrey vaulted onto Syrax without benefit of a saddle or whip, never having ridden her before. When the watchers on the roof heard Syrax's roar, they realized that Joffrey was missing, and the queen sent the knights later known as the Seven Who Rode after the prince, demanding that they bring him back, as he "did not know". Once in the air, Syrax twisted beneath him, fighting to be free of her unfamiliar rider, and stones and spears flew at him from the Shepherd's lambs below. He plunged down into Flea Bottom from two hundred feet above, crashing into a steep-pitched roof before rolling off to fall another forty feet amidst a shower of broken tiles. The fall broke his back, shards of slate rained down him like knives, and his own sword tore loose from his hand and pierced him through the belly. According to legend, a candlemaker's daughter named Robin_(Flea_Bottom) comforted him as he died, and his last words were "Mother, forgive me," with debate over whether he was speaking of Rhaenyra or the Mother Above. The mob descended on Joffrey's corpse, and looters tore the boots from his feet and the sword from his belly. More savage rioters tore at his body, cutting off his hands so that the rings on his fingers may be claimed; his right foot was hacked through the ankle, and a butcher's apprentice was sawing at his neck to claim his head when the seven knights arrived. His remains were reclaimed, aside from his foot, although Ser Gyles_Yronwood, Ser Willam_Royce, and Lord Commander Glendon_Goode were killed trying to retrieve the body. His half-brother, Aegon_III_Targaryen, became his mother's heir following his death.

name: Johanna Swann
bio: Johanna Swann, known as the Black Swan, was a noblewoman from House Swann during the reign of kings Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen, and Aegon_III_Targaryen. She became an influential courtesan in Lys. After driving pirates from the Stepstones in 96 AC, the Triarchy gradually became greedier with their tolls for passage. Along with money, Lysene captains also began taking women, girls, and boys to turn into slaves for their pillow houses. Johanna was enslaved when she was fifteen, but her stingy uncle, Lord Swann, refused to ransom her. After being sold to a pillow house, Johanna became a celebrated courtesan known as the Black Swan, the eventual ruler of Lys in all but name. In 131 AC the Lysene admiral Sharako_Lohar was slain by a rival in competition for Johanna's affections. Since he had been blamed by Myrmen and Tyroshi for the disastrous Battle of the Gullet in the previous year, Sharako's death was initially viewed by Lyseni as a political murder orchestrated by the Myrish. The Triarchy collapsed into the Daughters' War. Johanna's fate is unknown.

name: Johanna Westerling
bio: Johanna Lannister, née Westerling, was the Lady of Casterly Rock through her marriage to Lord Jason_Lannister. Lady Johanna was the daughter of Roland_Westerling, Lord of the Crag. During the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons, Johanna's husband, Lord Jason_Lannister, and his twin brother, Ser Tyland_Lannister, supported the greens. Jason led his banners and marched to the field in support of King Aegon_II_Targaryen, but he was slain in the Battle at the Red Fork in 130 AC. After Jason's death, Johanna ruled Casterly Rock as a regent for their young son, Lord Loreon_Lannister. Dalton_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands, took advantage of the situation and attacked the westerlands, causing much damage to the region. Lady Johanna barred the gates of the Rock, but the Red Kraken captured most of the Lannister fleet, burned the rest, and sacked nearby Lannisport. As one of the Three Widows, Johanna agreed to make peace with the blacks after the death of Aegon II in return for the Iron Throne ordering Dalton to return to the Iron Islands, free Fair Isle, and return all captured women. Johanna and her father attended the wedding of King Aegon_III_Targaryen and Jaehaera_Targaryen in King's Landing. Dalton ignored the commands of the regency of Aegon III to vacate the westerlands. When Johanna began building a new fleet of warships, Dalton attacked the shipyards and captured another hundred women. Johanna donned mail to lead the remaining Lannister forces in the struggle to drive the ironborn from their shores. Leading a host from Casterly Rock and Lannisport, Johanna routed the ironborn beneath Kayce's walls, with Dalton's uncle slain in the fighting. Songs claim Johanna slew twelve ironmen in the battle at Kayce, although scholars believe she only carried a banner. Johanna next arranged an expedition to recover Fair Isle, but her plan was betrayed to the ironborn. The ragtag fleet sent by the regent was destroyed in an ambush at Fair Isle. There was a struggle for power among the ironborn after the Red Kraken was murdered at Faircastle a few years later. Since the Lannister fleet had been destroyed by the Greyjoys, Johanna allied with the lord admiral of the Reach, Ser Leo_Costayne, to have the westermen invade the Iron Islands in 134 AC. When Rodrik_Greyjoy_(son_of_Dalton) was taken to Casterly Rock as a captive, Johanna had him gelded and made him her son's fool. Johanna reclaimed the dwindling glory of House Lannister and also lent gold to King's Landing to help rebuild the extensive damages caused by the previous civil war.

name: John the Oak
bio: John the Oak is a legendary son of Garth_Greenhand and a giantess. John is credited with bringing chivalry to Westeros. His descendants became House Oakheart.

name: Young John Mudd
bio: Young John Mudd is a sellsword in service to the Golden Company. He holds the rank of serjeant and claims to be a knight. John has brown skin and wears brown clothes. He decorates his ears with golden coins. He has scars and weathered face. John is a brusque, brutal man who wields sword and dagger. He is not well spoken. He is skilled with dice. Although he is not a young man, John is known as Young John because his father, Old_John_Mudd, was also a member of the Golden Company. Their surname is of a house that had once loomed large in the histories of the Seven Kingdoms. House Mudd ruled as Kings of the Rivers and the Hills thousands of years ago until their line was extinguished with the death of King Tristifer_V_Mudd at the hands of the Andals. He may or may not be of House Mudd, as in the free companies a man can call himself whatever he chooses, and not all such names are genuine. He may also be related to another serjeant in the company, Lorimas_Mudd.

name: Old John Mudd
bio: Old John Mudd was a sellsword in service to the Golden Company. He was the father of Young_John_Mudd. Old John's surname is of a house that had once loomed large in the histories of the Seven Kingdoms. House Mudd ruled as Kings of the Rivers and the Hills thousands of years ago until their line was extinguished with the death of King Tristifer_V_Mudd at the hands of the Andals. He may or may not have been of House Mudd, as in the free companies a man can call himself whatever he chooses, and not all were genuine. Besides his son, Young_John_Mudd, Old John may have been related to a serjeant in the company, Lorimas_Mudd.

name: John II Gardener
bio: John II Gardener, called the Tall, was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. John sailed his barge up the Mander to its very headwaters, planting the Gardener banner wherever he went and receiving homage from lords and petty kings whose lands lined the river's banks. In the annals of history the name of John II Gardener is writ large.

name: Jojen Reed
bio: Jojen Reed is a member of House Reed. He is Lord Howland_Reed's only son and Meera_Reed's younger brother. Jojen has greensight, the power of prophetic green dreams. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Jojen is portrayed by Thomas Brodie-Sangster. In his childhood, Jojen nearly died of greywater fever. While he was near death, he was visited in a dream by a three-eyed crow. Jojen has since had the gift of greensight, causing him to experience prophetic dreams known as green dreams. One such dream involved a winged wolf bound with stone chains, which the three-eyed crow was trying to free. When Jojen told his father, Howland_Reed, about the dream, Lord Howland sent him and his sister Meera to Winterfell. Even though he experiences green dreams, Jojen is not a greenseer, since he lacks the abilities of a warg and cannot commune with weirwood trees.

name: Jokin
bio: Jokin is a member of the Stormcrows and the commander of the company's archers.

name: Jommo
bio: Jommo is a khal of a khalasar. He is a sometime ally of Khal Drogo.

name: Jommy
bio: Jommy was a man in service to House Baratheon at Storm's End during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. When Lord Steffon_Baratheon's ship, the Windproud, sank during a storm in Shipbreaker Bay, Jommy was one of the men sent to reclaim the corpses that washed up on the shore. He was the one who found Patchface and to his dying day, Jommy claimed that Patchface was dead when he found him. However, when Jommy grabbed his ankles, Patchface spat up water and sat up.

name: Jon Arryn
bio: Jon Arryn was the Lord of the Eyrie, the Defender of the Vale, the Warden of the East and the head of House Arryn during the reign of King Robert_I_Baratheon. He also served Robert as Hand of the King from his ascension to the Iron Throne in 283 AC until his unexpected death in 298 AC. Jon was the husband of Lady Lysa_Arryn and father of Lord Robert_Arryn, and he acted as a second father to Lord Eddard_Stark and King Robert. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Jon is portrayed by John Standing.

name: Jon Bettley
bio: Jon Bettley, better known as Beardless Jon Bettley, is a knight of House Bettley.

name: Jon Brax
bio: Jon Brax is the youngest son of Ser Flement_Brax and Morya_Frey.

name: Jon Brightstone
bio: Jon Brightstone was a petty king of the First Men from House Brightstone who claimed the title King of the Fingers before the Andal invasion. Jon paid an Andal warlord to cross the narrow sea in order to use his swords against Dywen_Shell, who also contested control of the Fingers. The warlord eventually turned against Jon, however, and the king was tortured and beheaded. An Andal knight named Corwyn Corbray took Jon's daughter for his bride.

name: Jon Bulwer
bio: Jon Bulwer was the Lord of Blackcrown and the head of House Bulwer during the early reign of King Robert_I_Baratheon. He married Victaria_Tyrell and had one child with her, Alysanne_Bulwer. Jon died of a summer fever. His daughter Alysanne_Bulwer succeeded him as Lady of Blackcrown and head of House Bulwer. Jon may have a Lady_Bulwer's_brother.

name: Jon Cafferen
bio: Jon Cafferen was a knight from House Cafferen and the heir to Fawnton during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. Ser Jon married Alys_Oakheart, the daughter of the Lord of Old Oak, in 12 AC. During the wedding, Lord Wyl_of_Wyl turned up uninvited and was admitted through the postern gate by a treacherous servant. Lord Oakheart and most of the guests were slain, and Jon's wife was forced to watch him be castrated. His wife and her handmaids were gang-raped by Wyl's men and then sold off to a Myrish slaver. This was the most infamous act of the First Dornish War.

name: Jon Connington
bio: Jon Connington, also known as Griff, was the Lord of Griffin's Roost, the head of House Connington, towards the end of the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. He served for a short time as Hand of the King to Aerys during Robert's Rebellion. Jon Connington was the only son of Armond_Connington, Lord of Griffin's Roost, and his wife. Jon served as a squire in King's Landing, first alongside Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen, and later for the crown prince. Jon was considered to be one of Prince Rhaegar's dear friends. Some felt Jon was the most headstrong of the young lordlings who had gathered around Rhaegar, seeking his favor. During a time when Jon's father was still alive, Rhaegar was returning from a trip to Dorne, and visited Griffin's Roost for a fortnight. At the welcoming feast, Rhaegar played a song on his harp that made every woman in the hall cry, but per Jon's recollection, none of the men. Rhaegar visited the tallest tower of the castle with Jon, and complimented the beauty of the Connington lands. After Armond died, Jon inherited the rule to Griffin's Roost. When Jon left to serve at King's Landing, he named his cousin, Ser Ronald_Connington, as castellan. In 280 AC, Jon was present for the wedding of Rhaegar to Princess Elia_Martell of Dorne. In 281 AC, Jon was present at the tourney at Harrenhal, where he danced with Ashara_Dayne. In a tourney at Storm's End, he was unhorsed in a joust by Ser Barristan_Selmy of the Kingsguard.

name: Jon Cupps
bio: Jon Cupps is a knight of House Cupps. He is married to Leyla_Hightower, one of the daughters of Lord Leyton_Hightower.

name: Jon Florent
bio: Jon Florent was a knight from House Florent during the reign of Daeron_II_Targaryen. His relation to the main branch is not known. Ser Jon is listed in the roll of arms as participating in the tourney at Ashford Meadow, 209 AC.

name: Jon Fossoway
bio: Jon Fossoway is a knight from House Fossoway of New Barrel. He is married to Lady Janna Fossoway, the sister of Lord Mace_Tyrell of Highgarden.

name: Jon Heddle
bio: Jon Heddle, better known as Long Jon Heddle, was a knight who owned the Crossroads Inn. Long Jon Heddle took over the inn at the crossroads when he retired from adventuring. He also took up ironworking, and forged a sign in the shape of a three-headed dragon. The black iron sign would clank in the wind, and gave his inn its new name, the Clanking Dragon. In 196 AC, when Long Jon's son was an old man, the First Blackfyre Rebellion broke out. Lord Darry, who was a fierce Targaryen supporter, saw the inn's sign and thought it resembled the coat of arms of the rebels (a three-headed black dragon on a red field), so he cut it down and hacked it to pieces. The inn at the crossroads remained in the hands of Jon Heddle's descendants for several generations. One of these descendants, Masha_Heddle, told the tale of Jon Heddle and his family's connection to the inn to Septon Meribald, who himself passed it along to Brienne_Tarth of Tarth, Ser Hyle_Hunt, and Podrick_Payne.

name: Jon Hightower
bio: Jon Hightower was a Lord of the Hightower and head of House Hightower during the reign of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen. Jon served as Hand of the King during the final years of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen's reign. He brought Serenei_of_Lys, the last of Aegon the Unworthy's Aegon_IV_Targaryen, to court at King's Landing.

name: Jon Hogg
bio: Jon Hogg, known as Big Jon Hogg and later as Blind John Hogg, was a member of House Hogg during the Faith Militant uprising. During the Faith Militant uprising, Jon remained loyal to the Iron Throne. He fought in the battle at the Great Fork, under the command of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Early in the battle, he was blinded by a sword slash, yet he managed to rally his men and led  a charge that broke through the lines of the Faith Militant and put the Poor Fellows to flight. He was later killed by the Warrior's Sons who remained loyal to Ser Joffrey_Doggett.

name: Jon Hollard
bio: Jon Hollard was a knight from House Hollard, the steward of Duskendale. He was the brother of Ser Symon_Hollard and Ser Steffon_Hollard,[N 1] and married to the sister of Lord Denys_Darklyn. In the aftermath of the Defiance of Duskendale, Ser Jon was executed along with his wife and his son.

name: Jon Lothston
bio: Jon Lothston is a sellsword in service to the Golden Company. As a serjeant, he is a high-ranking officer. Jon is a Westerosi exile. His surname Lothston was a house that once loomed large in the histories of the Seven Kingdoms. He may or may not be of House Lothston, as in the free companies a man can call himself whatever he chooses. His claimed surname was one of the houses who once held the infamous castle of Harrenhal and was widely believed to have died out long ago.

name: Jon Lychester
bio: Jon Lychester was the Lord of Castle Lychester and the head of House Lychester during the Faith Militant uprising. During the Faith Militant uprising, Lord Jon took up arms on behalf of the High_Septon_(Aenys_I) and the Starry Sept against the Iron Throne. He fought in the battle at the Great Fork alongside Lord Rupert_Falwell and other puissant knights from the westerlands and riverlands, as well as hundreds of Poor Fellows, against the royal army led by King Maegor_I_Targaryen himself. It is unknown if he survived.

name: Jon Lynderly
bio: Jon Lynderly is the Lord of the Snakewood and the head of House Lynderly.

name: Jon Mertyns
bio: Jon Mertyns was the Lord of Mistwood and head of House Mertyns during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Lord Jon attended the Golden Wedding, where the king entertained the lords in the intimacy of his solar, encouraging them to speak freely and share their views on the problems of the realm and how they might best be overcome. Lord Jon approved of Jaehaerys, saying, "He laughs often and freely, even at himself."

name: Jon Mooton
bio: Jon Mooton was the Lord of Maidenpool and the head of House Mooton during Aegon's Conquest. Jon became Lord of Maidenpool when his older brother was killed in combat against Aegon_I_Targaryen. Jon yielded Maidenpool to House Targaryen after the battle, becoming one of the first adversaries to join Aegon's cause. He commanded the Targaryen army during the Field of Fire, as Aegon took to the skies on Balerion.

name: Jon Myre
bio: Jon Myre, known as Pinchface Jon Myre, is a member of House Myre.

name: Jon Penny
bio: Jon Penny is a boy orphaned by the War of the Five Kings. He might have been a member of House Penny.

name: Jon Penrose
bio: Jon Penrose was a knight from House Penrose during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Jon took part in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC, where he challenged Ser Tybolt_Lannister, the heir of Casterly Rock. Ser Tybolt was unhorsed and broke his sword in his fall, but fought back with shield alone to win the bout.

name: Jon Piper
bio: Jon Piper was the Lord of Pinkmaiden Castle and the head of House Piper during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. He supported the claim of Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) and fought for him during the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye. He had at least two younger brothers and a sister, Lady Melony_Piper. After declaring his uncle Maegor_I_Targaryen a usurper and a tyrant, Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) and Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) lost many of their supporters while they remained at Casterly Rock, as they left to bend the knee to Maegor at King's Landing. However, Lord Jon Piper, his brothers, and his sister Melony_Piper, a friend and former favorite to Rhaena, arrived at Lannisport to swear the loyalty of House Piper. After claiming their dragons, Aegon and Rhaena gathered their supporters at Pinkmaiden Castle. Lord Jon had pledged his sword to Aegon's cause, though many believed it was Melony that convinced her brother to rally under the prince. When Aegon's gathered host began marching on King's Landing, the Piper levies were not led by Lord Jon or his brothers, but by Melony, who died with the prince and many of the rebels at the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye in 43 AC, alongside three of her brothers. Jon's fate is unclear.[N 1] When King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen reclaimed the throne from his uncle Maegor, Dowager Queen Alyssa_Velaryon argued for wedding the young king to a Piper, rewarding House Piper's loyalty to her late son Aegon.

name: Jon Pox
bio: Jon Pox was the friend of the brother of Septon Meribald. He died in the War of the Ninepenny Kings. Willam and his brothers, Meribald, Robin, Owen_(brother_of_Meribald) and Owen's friend Jon Pox all went off to fight in the War of the Ninepenny Kings. During the War, Willam and Robin died from a fever. Owen died in battle and Jon was hanged for rape. Meribald survived to become a septon.

name: Jon Redfort
bio: Jon Redfort is a knight of House Redfort and the third son of Lord Horton_Redfort of Redfort.

name: Jon Rosby
bio: Jon Rosby was the Lord of Rosby and the head of House Rosby during the early reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. During the First Dornish War, after capturing the major strongholds of the Dornish nobility, King Aegon_I_Targaryen marched upon House Martell's seat of Sunspear.
