Upon finding it half-deserted and the Martells absent, Aegon declared victory and informed the remaining Dornish dignitaries that they were now part of the Seven Kingdoms. Lord Jon Rosby was named Castellan of Sunspear and Warden of the Sands, commanded to rule Dorne in the king's name, while stewards and castellans were named for other castles and lands. Soon after, Aegon and his host departed Dorne. While ruling from Sunspear, Rosby was to be supported by Lord Harlan_Tyrell's garrison at the Hellholt. When the Dornishmen soon returned from the shadow city, Rosby was quickly bound and captured. While Aegon's other castellans and stewards were tortured, Rosby had a kinder end than most in the resulting Defenestration of Sunspear, with the aged Princess Meria_Martell throwing him from the top of the Spear Tower. Lord Tyrell set out from Hellholt with his host to avenge Rosby, but disappeared soon after in 5 AC.

name: Jon Roxton
bio: Jon Roxton, also known as Bold Jon Roxton, (also spelled John) was a knight and the head[N 1] of House Roxton during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Jon is portrayed by Joplin Sibtain. Ser Jon fought on the side of King Aegon_II_Targaryen and the greens during the Dance of the Dragons, a civil war between Aegon II and his half-sister, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, over the Iron Throne of Westeros. At the Battle on the Honeywine, Lord Owen_Costayne was mortally wounded by Jon's black Valyrian steel blade, Orphan-Maker. Jon knighted Titus_Peake_(son_of_Unwin), heir of Lord Unwin_Peake, after the battle, but Ser Titus died six days later in a skirmish. Following the death of Lord Ormund_Hightower in the First Battle of Tumbleton, Jon was among those who desired to take leadership of their host. Jon also claimed Lady Sharis_Footly as a "prize of war", slew Lord_Footly when he objected, and resided in his bedchamber. Jon was among those who wanted to quickly advance on King's Landing after the Storming of the Dragonpit and Rhaenyra's flight from the capital. Other green leaders were cautious, however, and the host remained at Tumbleton. When Ser Ulf_White and Ser Hugh_Hammer demanded Highgarden and the Iron Throne, respectively, Jon joined the Caltrops conspiracy which wanted the dragonriders dead. During the Second Battle of Tumbleton, Bold Jon slew Hugh Hammer when he least suspected it, splitting him from groin to throat with Orphan-Maker. Shortly thereafter he was attacked by a dozen of Hugh's men, but managed to kill three of them. Legend says Jon slipped on Hugh's entrails and was then slain. Sharis gave birth to a lusty dark-haired child which she claimed was the son of her late lord husband, but it was widely believed the boy had actually been fathered by Bold Jon. Unwin Peake revealed that Orphan-Maker was in his possession when he became Hand of the King during the regency of Aegon_III_Targaryen.

name: Jon Snow
bio: Jon Snow is the bastard son of Eddard_Stark, Lord of Winterfell. He has five half-siblings: Robb_Stark, Sansa_Stark, Arya_Stark, Bran_Stark, and Rickon_Stark. Unaware of the identity of his mother, Jon was raised at Winterfell. At the age of fourteen, Jon joins the Night's Watch, where he earns the nickname Lord Snow. Jon is one of the major POV characters in A Song of Ice and Fire. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Jon is portrayed by Kit Harington. Jon was born in 283 AC, near the end of Robert's Rebellion,[N 1] and he was named by Lord Eddard_Stark. One tale says Jon was named after Lord Jon_Arryn, Eddard's foster father. The identity of Jon's mother is a mystery, and several suggestions have been made by those who know the Starks. Jon is unaware of his mother's identity and Eddard refuses to speak of her. When Eddard returned from the war, he brought the newborn Jon to Winterfell, insisting on raising him with the rest of his family. Jon and his wet nurse were installed in the castle before the arrival of Eddard's new wife, Catelyn_Stark, and his young son and heir, Robb_Stark, from Riverrun, which Catelyn did not take well. Lord Eddard was fiercely protective of Jon and refused to send him away. Jon was raised at Winterfell with his half-siblings. He was tutored by Maester Luwin, including learning sums and astronomy, and was trained at arms by the master-at-arms, Ser Rodrik_Cassel. Jon has trained at swordplay since he was old enough to walk, together with Robb, whom he came to view as his "best friend, rival and constant companion," and later also with Theon_Greyjoy, after the latter came to Winterfell following the conclusion of Greyjoy's Rebellion. Jon learned how to swim in the great moat at Winterfell; he is considered a strong swimmer. Jon has possibly visited the Dreadfort. Jon grew close to his true-born siblings, especially Robb and Arya_Stark. Because they look alike, Arya briefly believed that she was bastard-born as well, but Jon reassured his half-sister this was not the case. When Jon was a young child,[N 2] he and Robb built a great mountain of snow on top of a gate, hoping to push it on someone passing by. They were discovered by Mance_Rayder, a ranger from the Night's Watch who had accompanied Lord_Commander_Qorgyle to Winterfell. Mance promised not to tell anyone, allowing Jon and Robb to succeed in their ploy. The boys dumped the snow upon Fat Tom, who chased the boys around the yard afterwards until all three were tired. Since he was young, Jon's hero was King Daeron_I_Targaryen, who had conquered Dorne at the age of fourteen. Jon and Robb would often play a game of swordplay in which they would pretend to be great heroes (including Florian_the_Fool, Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II), King Daeron the Young Dragon, and Ser Ryam_Redwyne). Once, when Jon called out that he was "Lord of Winterfell," Robb informed him that was impossible due to his bastardy, which became a sore memory for Jon. Jon once covered himself in flour and hid in one of the empty tombs in the crypt of Winterfell, and when Robb brought Sansa_Stark, Arya, and Bran_Stark down to the crypts, Jon jumped out of the tomb and moaned, pretending to be a spirit. Sansa screamed and ran back upstairs, Bran clung to Robb's leg and cried, and Arya punched Jon for scaring baby Bran with his prank. Jon and Robb laughed, however, until Bran and Arya began laughing as well. Lord Eddard had dreamed about raising new lords and settling them in the abandoned holdfasts in the New Gift, and Jon believes that if winter had come and gone more quickly, he might have been chosen to hold one of the settlements in his father's name.

name: Jon Stark
bio: Jon Stark was a King in the North and head of House Stark before the War of Conquest. There is a statue of Jon within the crypt of Winterfell. When sea raiders landed in the east, Jon drove them out and built a castle, the Wolf's Den, at the mouth of the White Knife, so as to be able to defend the mouth of the river. His son, Rickard_Stark_(king), followed him on the throne and annexed the Neck to the north.

name: Jon Tollett
bio: Jon Tollett was a knight from House Tollett and a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Ser Jon served as a knight of the Kingsguard during King Maegor_I_Targaryen's reign. In 48 AC, Maegor was found mysteriously slain on the Iron Throne; some attributed his death to knights of his own Kingsguard, as two knights were posted outside the door of the Iron Throne. With his uncle dead, the new young king Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen arrested Maegor's surviving Kingsguard and imprisoned them in the black cells with the rest of Maegor's court. Jaehaerys had intended to sentence Maegor's surviving Kingsguard to death, deciding that they had failed in their duties by allowing their king to die. However, at the urging of Princess Alysanne_Targaryen, the knights were given the choice of death or taking the black. Jon chose the latter.

name: Jon Umber
bio: Jon Umber, known as Greatjon Umber and the Greatjon, is Lord of the Last Hearth and head of House Umber, a vassal family to the Starks of Winterfell. His eldest son, also named Jon, is called the Smalljon. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, the Greatjon is portrayed by Clive Mantle.

name: Jon Umber
bio: Jon Umber, known as the Smalljon and Smalljon Umber in comparison to his father, is the eldest son of Lord Greatjon Umber and is the heir to the Last Hearth. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Smalljon is portrayed by Dean S. Jagger.

name: Jon Umber
bio: Jon Umber was the Lord of the Last Hearth and the head of House Umber during the latter half of the second century after Aegon's Conquest. Jon married Serena_Stark, the daughter of Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan), the heir to Winterfell, and the granddaughter of Lord Cregan_Stark, but they had no issue. After his death, Serena married again; her father's half-brother, Edric_Stark_(son_of_Cregan).

name: Jon Vance
bio: Jon, born Jon Vance, is a member of House Vance of Atranta and a maester of the Citadel. He is the fifth and youngest son of Lord Norbert_Vance, his four older brothers are: Ser Ronald_Vance, Ser Ellery_Vance, Ser Hugo_Vance and Ser Kirth_Vance.

name: Jon Waters
bio: Jon Waters was a knight during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He was the bastard son of Lord Alyn_Velaryon and Princess Elaena_Targaryen. Jon Waters was the bastard son of the seafarer Lord Alyn_Velaryon, called "the Oakenfist", and Princess Elaena_Targaryen. He had a twin sister, Jeyne_Waters. Elaena had hoped to marry Alyn, but he was lost at sea. Jon's Longwaters, also a great knight, changed the family name to Longwaters to remove the taint of bastardy. His line continues until Rennifer_Longwaters.

name: Jon Wylde
bio: Jon Wylde is a knight of House Wylde. He is married to Elyana_Vypren and has one child, Rickard_Wylde.

name: Jonah Mooton
bio: Jonah Mooton was the heir to, and later Lord of Maidenpool and head of House Mooton during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Jonah Mooton, Roy_Connington, and Braxton_Beesbury became the three male favorites of Princess Saera_Targaryen in 82 AC. Jonah, Roy, Braxton, Saera, Alys_Turnberry, and Perianne_Moore became inseparable at every feast and ball, hunted and hawked together, and once sailed across Blackwater Bay to Dragonstone. In 84 AC, Jonah, Roy, and Braxton were found drunk at the Blue Pearl, a brothel in King's Landing, laughing as the king's fool, Tom_Turnip, was surrounded by whores. When asked by men of the City Watch, Roy admitted they thought it would be funny to see the fool do the deed, and Jonah laughed, saying it had been Saera's notion, praising her cleverness. When Perianne and Alys were questioned about the incident, it was revealed that Alys was pregnant outside of wedlock, and that Saera had coerced them into sexual acts with her male favorites. Jonah, Roy, and Braxton were then imprisoned in the dungeons for one night. When questioned by King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen, Saera defended herself by saying Jonah Mooton said he loved her. She confessed that she had bedded all three of her male favorites, with each of them thinking he was the first to take her maidenhead. She proposed a solution of redemption by marrying all three of her favorites, like King Maegor_I_Targaryen and his six wives, at which the king grew furious. A hasty marriage was arranged for Perianne to Jonah, with the king telling him he could play a part in her redemption as he had played a part in her ruin. The marriage was a success, and they later ruled together as Lord and Lady of Maidenpool. King Jaehaerys later asked Lord Jonah and Lady Perianne to keep watch for Saera after she fled from her punishment in Oldtown.

name: Jonelle Cerwyn
bio: Jonelle Cerwyn is a member of House Cerwyn and is the daughter of Lord Medger_Cerwyn.

name: Jonnel Stark
bio: Jonnel Stark, also known as One-Eye, was a Lord of Winterfell and a head of House Stark in the latter half of the second century after Aegon's Conquest. He was the second son of Lord Cregan_Stark, and the first by his third and final wife, Lynara_Stark. Jonnel became the heir to Winterfell after the death of his older brother Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan) in the conquest of Dorne, passing over Rickon's daughters Sansa_Stark_(daughter_of_Rickon) and Serena_Stark. Jonnel also married Sansa, his half-niece. Jonnel became the Lord of Winterfell after the death of his father, Cregan_Stark. The north lamented the death of Rickon, for the reigns of Jonnel and his brothers after him were troublesome. Jonnel's marriage with Sansa was childless. After her death, he married Robyn_Ryswell, but also had no issue. He was succeeded by his brother, Barthogan_Stark, as Lord of Winterfell. Jonnel was interred in the crypts of Winterfell after his death.

name: Jonos Arryn
bio: Jonos Arryn was a member of House Arryn and the younger brother of Ronnel_Arryn_(king), the last King of Mountain and Vale and first Lord of the Vale after Aegon's Conquest. In 37 AC, at the start of the reign of King Aenys_I_Targaryen, Jonos rose in rebellion. He deposed and imprisoned his own brother and declared himself King of Mountain and Vale. In response King Aenys I commanded his Hand of the King, Lord Alyn_Stokeworth, to sail to the Vale and deal with the usurper. That order was recalled out of fear that Harren_the_Red, a rebel from the riverlands, might infiltrate King's Landing. Taking matter into own hands, Lord Allard_Royce of Runestone gathered forces to sweep away Jonos's supporters, penning him at the Eyrie. However, this led Jonos to send his brother flying out of the Moon Door, earning him the nickname Jonos the Kinslayer. Jonos and his garrison remained defiant until Prince Maegor_I_Targaryen flew to the Eyrie atop the dragon Balerion. Rather than face the fire, Jonos was seized by his garrison and delivered to Lord Royce through the Moon Door. Surrender saved Jonos's followers from burning, but not from death. All of them were hanged per the orders of Maegor, who denied the highborn rebels the honor of beheading. House Arryn continued through a kinsman after Ronnel and Jonos' deaths, a cousin, Hubert_Arryn.

name: Jonos Bracken
bio: Jonos Bracken is the Lord of the Stone Hedge and the head of House Bracken. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Jonos is portrayed by Gerry O'Brien. Jonos has five daughters from his three marriages, and a bastard son, Harry_Rivers. However, his rival, Lord Tytos_Blackwood, does not believe that Harry was fathered by Jonos.

name: Jonos Frey
bio: Jonos Frey is a member of House Frey, the second son of Rhaegar_Frey and Jeyne_Beesbury. He is one of the numerous great-grandchildren of Lord Walder_Frey.

name: Jonos Stark
bio: Jonos Stark was a King in the North. He is buried in the crypts beneath Winterfell.

name: Jonothor Darry
bio: Jonothor Darry, also known as Jon Darry, was a knight from House Darry and a member of King Aerys_II_Targaryen's Kingsguard. According to semi-canon sources, he was the brother of Ser Willem_Darry, master-at-arms at the Red Keep. Jonothor was present at the tourney at Harrenhal in 281 AC. After the Battle of the Bells during Robert's Rebellion in 283 AC, Jonothor and Ser Barristan_Selmy of the Kingsguard were sent to rally the remnants of the forces commanded by Lord Jon_Connington, the Hand of the King. On the night that King Aerys_II_Targaryen burned his next Hand, Lord Qarlton_Chelsted, Jon was standing watch outside the royal apartments with Ser Jaime_Lannister when Aerys began raping his sister-wife, Queen Rhaella_Targaryen. When Jaime stated they should protect her as well, Jon allowed that they should, but not from him. Jonothor was one of Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen's three Kingsguard who rode with him from the Red Keep to lead the royal army at the Battle of the Trident. Before departing, Jaime pleaded with Rhaegar to command Jon to remain at the Red Keep and guard Aerys in his stead. In response, Jon snapped at his younger sworn brother and reminded him of his duty. Jonothor was slain at the Trident, cut down in the midst of the conflict.

name: Jonquil
bio: Jonquil is a legendary character from the Age of Heroes. Associated with Jonquil's Pool at Maidenpool in the riverlands, she is known known for falling in love with a knight named Florian_the_Fool. Singers compared the sudden marriage of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen to the great romance of Jonquil and Florian_the_Fool. During the tourney at Ashford Meadow, Ser Duncan_the_Tall observed Tanselle performing a puppet show of Florian and Jonquil. Songs about Florian and Jonquil are favorites of Sansa_Stark.

name: Jonquil Darke
bio: Jonquil Darke, known as the Scarlet Shadow, was a noblewoman from House Darke, born a bastard daughter of Lord_Darklyn_(father_of_Jonquil_Darke) of Duskendale. At the Golden Wedding in 49 AC, a test of arms that became known as the War for the White Cloaks was held, with hundreds of knights competing for the honor of serving in the Kingsguard of Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Jonquil took part as a slender mystery knight known as the Serpent in Scarlet. She was eventually defeated and unmasked, and became a great favorite with the smallfolk. In 51 AC, after an attempted assassination of Queen Alysanne_Targaryen at the sacred waters known as Jonquil's Pool in Maidenpool, it was decided that the queen needed a protector who could accompany her where men couldn't go. Jaehaerys sent a raven to Duskendale, commanding the new Lord Darklyn to send his bastard half-sister to court. Jonquil arrived in King's Landing a few days later, and gladly accepted the position of Queen Alysanne's sworn shield and protector. In time she became known as the Scarlet Shadow, so closely did she guard her lady. When the High_Lickspittle passed away in 54 AC, Jaehaerys and Alysanne traveled to Oldtown upon their dragons to pay their respects, and to influence the choice of the next High Septon. They took two protectors with them. To balance the weight for the dragons, Jonquil rode with King Jaehaerys on Vermithor, while Ser Joffrey_Doggett of the Kingsguard rode with Alysanne on Silverwing. Jonquil traveled with Queen Alysanne when she went on a progress of the North in 58 AC. A small tourney was staged in White Harbor by House Manderly in the queen's honor. One of the competitors was a wildling woman, captured by Night's Watch rangers north of the Wall, and given to one of Lord Theomore_Manderly's knights to foster. Impressed with the woman's daring, Alysanne had Jonquil spar with her. The two women dueled with sword and spear, while the northmen roared with approval. When the scandals committed by Princess Saera_Targaryen came to light in 84 AC, and the young princess was confined to her room only to later attempt to escape to the Dragonpit, the king confined Saera to a tower cell, with Jonquil guarding her all day and night, even in the privy. When King Jaehaerys faced one of Saera's lovers, Ser Braxton_Beesbury, in a trial by combat, Jonquil made sure Saera watched from the window of her cell as her father slew him with Blackfyre.

name: Jorah Mallister
bio: Jorah Mallister was the Lord of Seagard and the head of House Mallister during the Dance of the Dragons. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Jorah Mallister is not present. He has been replaced by Lady Mallister, who is portrayed by Anna Francolini. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons in 129 AC, Lord Mallister was among the prominent riverlords that supported Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen and the blacks, despite initial reluctance. Lord Jorah joined the host of Lord Elmo_Tully as they marched to King's Landing against the army of Lord Borros_Baratheon, resulting in the Battle of the Kingsroad. During the battle, Jorah and Lord Roland_Darry were slain by the unhorsed Lord Baratheon.

name: Jorah Mormont
bio: Jorah Mormont is an exiled knight, the former head of House Mormont and Lord of Bear Island. He fled Westeros to escape execution for trading in slaves and took up work as a mercenary in the Free Cities, eventually entering the service of the last Targaryens. He becomes one of the most trusted companions of Daenerys_Targaryen. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Jorah is portrayed by Iain Glen.

name: Jorah Stark
bio: Jorah Stark was a King in the North. He is buried in the crypts beneath Winterfell.

name: Joramun
bio: Joramun was a legendary King-Beyond-the-Wall. According to myth, in ancient days he blew the Horn of Winter and "woke the giants from the earth". Joramun brought down the Night's_King alongside the King of Winter, Brandon_Stark_(Breaker).

name: Jordan Towers
bio: Jordan Towers was the Lord of Harrenhal and the head of House Towers during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Ser Walton_Towers, a household knight of the king, was granted Harrenhal by King Maegor_I_Targaryen in 44 AC after winning a melee in Lord Harroway's Town, but Walton died a fortnight later from his wounds. Harrenhal thus passed to Jordan, Walton's eldest son. The Harrenhal holdings that passed to Jordan were much diminished as the holdings of House Harroway were granted to Lord Alton_Butterwell and Lord Darnold_Darry. Over the course of the king's wars, Lord Towers remained staunchly loyal to Maegor, though his elder sons all died fighting in the king's wars. In 48 AC, Prince Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen pressed his claim to the throne against his uncle Maegor, and high lords of the realm flocked to his cause. Maegor commanded a score of lesser lords, including Lord Towers, to gather at King's Landing, but their numbers were few and many soon lost heart. Shortly afterwards, Maegor was found dead upon the Iron Throne; Lords Towers and Rosby were the last to see the king alive. Along with Lords Lord_Darklyn_(father_of_Jonquil_Darke) and Lord_Staunton_(father_of_Cassella), Jordan was the only one of Maegor's supporters to have the courage to yield the Red Keep to Prince Jaehaerys and sisters, Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) and Princess Alysanne_Targaryen. The three lords were sent to the black cells in chains, but were eventually pardoned by King Jaehaerys I after surrendering certain lands and sending a son to court to serve as a hostage. Jordan eventually died of a chest congestion in 53 AC. Harrenhal passed to Jordan's last surviving son, the sickly and impoverished Maegor_Towers.

name: Jorelle Mormont
bio: Jorelle Mormont, also called Jory, is the fourth daughter of Lady Maege_Mormont.

name: Jorgen
bio: Jorgen was a peasant sworn to House Webber of Coldmoat. Jorgen was a peasant in service to the Lady of Coldmoat, Rohanne_Webber, during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. He was one of the peasants building a dam across the Chequy Water. After Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield slashed the face of another peasant, Wolmer, Jorgen voiced his belief that the peasants could take Bennis and his fellow knight, Duncan_the_Tall. Wolmer convinced him that it would be best to go to Lady Webber.

name: Jorgen Velaryon
bio: Jorgen Velaryon was one of the children of Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan), the Lord of the Tides and the head of House Velaryon during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen and King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. When King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen flew their dragons to Driftmark and toured the shipyards, they were escorted by Jorgen and his brothers Corwyn_Velaryon and Victor_Velaryon.

name: Jorl the Whale
bio: Jorl the Whale is a legendary ironborn raider from the Age of Heroes.

name: Joron I Blacktyde
bio: Joron I Blacktyde was a High King of the Iron Islands from House Blacktyde. He was dubbed Maidensbane. Joron I captured Gyles_II_Gardener, King of the Reach, when their fleets clashed off the Misty Islands. After torturing him to death, Joron had Gyles's corpse cut to pieces and used them to bait his fishhooks with "a chunk of king". Later in his reign, Joron raided the Arbor and supposedly carried off every woman under thirty, earning the name "Maidensbane", by which he is best remembered.

name: Jorquen
bio: Jorquen was a septon of the Faith who served on the Wall. He wrote the Annals of the Black Centaur about Lord Commander Orbert_Caswell.

name: Jory Cassel
bio: Jory Cassel is a member of House Cassel and the captain of the  household guard at Winterfell for House Stark. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Jory is portrayed by Jamie Sives. Jory is the son of Martyn_Cassel and the nephew of Ser Rodrik_Cassel, master-at-arms of Winterfell. During Robert's Rebellion, Jory attended the weddings of Catelyn and Lysa Tully at Riverrun. During the bedding custom, he ripped Catelyn_Stark gown in haste. At the end of the war, Jory's father died fighting alongside Eddard in the combat at the tower of joy. Jory has served as Lord Eddard's captain of the guard for more than eight years. Jory once brought Robb_Stark, Bran_Stark, and Jon_Snow into the wolfswood to fish for trout in a rushing stream near Winterfell. Robb and Jon have often hunted in the forest with their father and Jory. The captain has also often sparred in Winterfell's yard with Robb, Jon, and Theon_Greyjoy. A laughing Jory once pulled apart Sansa_Stark and Arya_Stark when the sisters were having a snowball fight after a summer's snow. Jory has chased Bran across the roofs of Winterfell on multiple occasions.

name: Joseran
bio: Joseran was the maester assigned to House Blacktyde. When Tristifer_Botley was shipped to Blacktyde, he wanted to send letters to Asha_Greyjoy. Maester Joseran refused to send them, however.

name: Joseth
bio: Joseth is a stableman at Winterfell. Joseth has groomed the horses of Theon_Greyjoy.

name: Joseth
bio: Joseth was a maester of the Citadel of Oldtown who is known for writing the book Battles and Sieges of the Century of Blood.

name: Joseth Mallister
bio: Joseth Mallister was a knight from House Mallister during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In 209 AC, Ser Joseth participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow. He challenged Ser Humfrey_Hardyng in the second tilt, but was defeated and carried from the field unconscious.

name: Joseth Smallwood
bio: Joseth Smallwood was the Lord of Acorn Hall and the head of House Smallwood during the Dance of the Dragons. During the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Joseth Smallwood sided with the blacks and Rhaenyra_Targaryen. In 130 AC, the advance of the westermen through the riverlands was briefly halted at Acorn Hall when Lord Joseth sallied forth to join Lord Petyr_Piper and the remains of his defeated host, but Petyr died in the ensuring battle and Joseth fell back inside his castle. At the Second Battle of Tumbleton, Lord Smallwood joined the host led by Ser Addam_Velaryon and scraped together fresh levies of greybeards and green boys, despite the grievous losses he had suffered in the autumn battles.

name: Josmyn Peckledon
bio: Josmyn Peckledon, called Peck by Ser Jaime_Lannister, is a squire from House Peckledon.

name: Joss
bio: Joss is a brewer in the town of Sherrer in the riverlands. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Joss is portrayed by Niall Cusack.

name: Joss Hood
bio: Joss Hood is a young knight from Sunspear.

name: Joss Stilwood
bio: Joss Stilwood is one of the Mountain's men and serves as squire of Ser Gregor_Clegane, along with a Pimply_squire.

name: Joss the Gloom
bio: Joss the Gloom is a mummer of the Ship.

name: Josua Massey
bio: Josua Massey, also known as the Softspear, was the last King of House Massey. Josua was slain by the Storm King Qarlton_II_Durrandon. His daughter was taken as wife by Togarion_Bar_Emmon, while his son was installed as a puppet lord, after Togarion drove the stormlanders away from Stonedance.

name: Josua Willum
bio: Josua Willum is the son of Lord_Willum. He has at least one brother, Elyas_Willum.

name: Joth Quickbow
bio: Joth Quickbow is a man who lives in the riverlands.

name: Jothos Slynt
bio: Jothos Slynt is the second son of Janos_Slynt.

name: Joy Hill
bio: Joy Hill is the ten-year-old bastard daughter of Gerion_Lannister. Gerion_Lannister fathered Joy on Briony, a common woman. Gerion was lost at sea nearly a decade ago, when Joy was two or three years old.

name: Joy Penrose
bio: Joy Penrose was a member of House Penrose and the third and youngest daughter of Lord Ronnel_Penrose and his wife Princess Elaena_Targaryen.

name: Joyeuse Erenford
bio: Joyeuse Erenford is a noblewoman of House Erenford. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, she is portrayed by Kelly Long.

name: Julian Wormwood
bio: Julian Wormwood was a knight from House Wormwood during the reign of King Aegon_II_Targaryen. Julian fought for the greens during the Dance of the Dragons. After King's Landing was taken back by the greens in 130 AC, Ser Tyland_Lannister, Ser Marston_Waters and Ser Julian were sent to the Free Cities of Pentos, Tyrosh, and Myr to hire sellswords to fight for King Aegon_II_Targaryen. By the time Ser Tyland's party returned empty handed to King's Landing, Aegon II was dead.

name: Jurne
bio: Maester Jurne[N 1] is the maester in service to Lord Renly_Baratheon at Storm's End.

name: Justin Massey
bio: Justin Massey is a knight from House Massey and one of the queen's men in service to Stannis_Baratheon. Justin once squired for Robert_I_Baratheon, from whom it is said he acquired his appetite for women.

name: Justin Massey
bio: Justin Massey, called Justin Milk-Eye, was a monarch of House Massey and an infamous pirate-king. When the Andal invasion began, Justin was embroiled in a war over Massey's Hook with Erich_VII_Durrandon, the Storm King.

name: Jyana
bio: Jyana is a crannogwoman and the wife of Lord Howland_Reed. Together they have two children: Meera_Reed and Jojen_Reed.

name: Jyanna Frey
bio: Jyanna Frey is the wife and cousin of Ser Benfrey_Frey. They have two children; Della_Frey and Osmund_Frey.

name: Jyck
bio: Jyck is a guard sworn to House Lannister who serves as escort to Tyrion_Lannister when he travels to the Wall. In his service for Tyrion_Lannister, one of Jyck's regular tasks was to hire prostitutes for him. He sometimes failed to warn the women that Tyrion is a dwarf, leading to some unpleasant situations for Tyrion when the prostitutes first saw him.

name: Jynessa Blackmont
bio: Jynessa Blackmont is a noblewoman of House Blackmont. She is the eldest child and heir of Larra_Blackmont, the Lady of Blackmont.

name: Jyzene
bio: Jyzene is a serving wench at the Peach in Stoney Sept.

name: Kaeth
bio: Kaeth was a Grand Maester of the Citadel. About 257 AC Kaeth was made Grand Maester at the age of eighty and sent to serve the court of King Aegon_V_Targaryen in King's Landing. He died within a year of his appointment and was succeeded by Grand Maester Ellendor. Grand Maester Kaeth wrote the well-regarded work Lives of Four Kings, a detailed history of the reigns of Daeron_I_Targaryen, Baelor_I_Targaryen, Aegon_IV_Targaryen, and Daeron_II_Targaryen. Kaeth scants Viserys_II_Targaryen terribly, however, as his short reign as king came after Baelor's. The book is considered to be an excellent treatise of the art of rulership. Ser Kevan_Lannister and his nephew Tyrion_Lannister are amongst its appreciators, as is Prince Oberyn_Martell.

name: Karlon Stark
bio: Karlon Stark was a younger son of Winterfell. He was the first Lord of Karl's Hold and the founder of House Karstark. About thousand years ago (possibly before 700 BC)[N 1] after putting down a rebel lord, Karlon was granted lands for his success and bravery. Originally the castle he built was named Karl's Hold but eventually the name changed to Karhold and the Karhold Starks became the Karstarks.

name: Karolyn
bio: Karolyn was a septa at the motherhouse attached to the Starry Sept in Oldtown during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 50 AC Ser Orryn_Baratheon arrived at the motherhouse demanding Princess Rhaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon), a novice of the Faith, be given into his custody. The ploy might well have worked but for Septa Karolyn, who had the door of the motherhouse that day. Whilst placating Orryn with the pretext of sending for the girl, she sent instead to the High_Lickspittle. The High Septon sent his steward, Casper_Straw with thirty armed septons to the motherhouse and Ser Orryn and his men were captured.

name: Karyl Vance
bio: Karyl Vance is a knight of House Vance of Wayfarer's Rest. He has three daughters, Liane_Vance, Rhialta_Vance, and Emphyria_Vance.

name: Kasporio
bio: Kasporio, who styles himself Kasporio the Cunning, is a bravo who serves as the second-in-command of the Second Sons, a sellsword company.

name: Kedge Whiteye
bio: Kedge Whiteye is a ranger of the Night's Watch. Kedge is a forty-odd year old ranger who has been at the Wall since he was ten years old. His left eye is blind, his right eye mean. Beyond the Wall he is as a good as any ranger alone with an axe and garron, but he has never gotten along well with other men.

name: Kedry
bio: Kedry is a maester of the Citadel in service to Anders_Yronwood, Lord of Yronwood. Kedry has spent half his life studying the Free Cities.

name: Kegs
bio: Kegs is a builder of the Night's Watch. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Kegs is portrayed by Tim Landers.

name: Kella
bio: Kella is a servant sworn to House Baelish.

name: Kem
bio: Kem is a young sellsword of the Second Sons. Kem comes from Flea Bottom. He once saw Lord Tywin_Lannister riding up a hill in King's Landing, and the lad liked the little lions on the helms of Tywin's red cloaks. Kem claims that he and his brother, Kennet, fought in the army of Stannis_Baratheon during the Battle of the Blackwater, during which Kennet was killed. Kem dropped his spear and tried to escape after the appearance of Renly_Baratheon's ghost (Garlan_Tyrell), but he and thousands more were left behind by the fleeing ships. As he feared being sent to the Wall by Tywin after the battle, Kem traveled across the narrow sea and joined the Second Sons, a sellsword company, as a means to survive.

name: Kemmett Pyke
bio: Kemmett Pyke, known as Kemmett the Bastard, is an ironborn raider and a bastard.

name: Kenned the Whale
bio: Kenned the Whale is an ironborn raider.

name: Lord Kenning
bio: Lord Kenning is the head of House Kenning of Harlaw and a bannerman to Lord Rodrik_Harlaw of Harlaw.

name: Kennos of Kayce
bio: Kennos of Kayce is a knight in service to House Kenning of Kayce. Lord Terrence_Kenning entrusts him with the Horn of Herrock, a warhorn passed down through the family as an heirloom.

name: Kermit Tully
bio: Kermit Tully was the Lord of Riverrun, Lord Paramount of the Trident, and the head of House Tully at the end of the Dance of the Dragons and during the reign of Aegon_III_Targaryen. The young knight became the head of House Tully after the death of his father, Lord Elmo_Tully, during the Dance of the Dragons. Kermit was the most prominent of Tully lords, bringing them to the height of their power. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Kermit and Elmo_Tully are merged with Oscar_Tully, who is portrayed by Archie Barnes.

name: Kerwin
bio: Kerwin is a maester of the Citadel in service to House Chester of Greenshield.

name: Ketter
bio: Ketter is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Kevan Lannister
bio: Kevan Lannister is a knight from House Lannister and is the younger brother of Tywin_Lannister, Lord of Casterly Rock. He is the POV character for the epilogue of A Dance with Dragons. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Kevan is portrayed by Ian Gelder.

name: Kezmya Pahl
bio: Kezmya Pahl is a child from the House of Pahl, one of the noble ruling families of Meereen.

name: Khiara the Great
bio: Khiara the Great was a God-Empress of Leng and the founder of the current dynasty that rules the island. She was of pure Lengii descent, but to please her subjects she took two husbands, one Lengii and one YiTish. This custom was followed by her daughters and their daughters in turn. By tradition the Lengii consort commands the imperial armies whereas the YiTish one commands the fleets.

name: Khorane Sathmantes
bio: Khorane Sathmantes is a Lysene captain of Shayala's Dance, a galley owned by Salladhor_Saan. Khorane has spent many years trading with the Seven Kingdoms.

name: Khrazz
bio: Khrazz is a celebrated pit fighter from Meereen. Khrazz is of humble Meereenese birth.

name: Kiera of Tyrosh
bio: Kiera of Tyrosh was the wife of Prince Valarr_Targaryen. An unknown amount of time after Valarr's death, she was married to Valarr's cousin, Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I). During her marriage to Prince Valarr_Targaryen, Kiera gave birth to sons, though unfortunately they were stillborn. Prince Valarr died during the Great Spring Sickness, making Kiera a widow. An unknown amount of time later, Kiera was married to Valarr's cousin, Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I). Together with Prince Daeron, Kiera had a daughter, Princess Vaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daeron), born in 222 AC. Vaella proved to be sweet, but sadly simple-minded.

name: Kindly Man
bio: The kindly man is a servant of the Many-Faced God and the priest in charge of the House of Black and White in Braavos. One of the Faceless Men, he does not give his name.

name: King Cutthroat
bio: King Cutthroat is a barber who became King of Astapor after killing Cleon_the_Second by slitting his throat.

name: Kirby Pimm
bio: Kirby Pimm was a knight, during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Ser Kirby was present when Ser Morgan_Dunstable knighted Ser Glendon_Flowers at the Pussywillows. In 212 AC, Kirby attended the Whitewalls Tourney. He intervened when Ser Glendon Flowers was denied entry into the tourney and explained how Ser Glendon came about his knighthood to the stewards.

name: Kirth
bio: Kirth was a maester of the Citadel. He wrote the book Songs the Drowned Men Sing. He suggested that the Seastone Chair, which the original First Men settlers of the uninhabited Iron Islands discovered on Old Wyk, was left by visitors from across the Sunset Sea.

name: Kirth Vance
bio: Kirth Vance is a knight from House Vance of Atranta and the fourth son of Lord Norbert_Vance. He has four brothers; Ser Ronald_Vance, Ser Ellery_Vance, Ser Hugo_Vance and Maester Jon_Vance. Kirth is a good friend of Ser Edmure_Tully.

name: Knight of Ninestars
bio: This unidentified Knight of Ninestars, was the head of House Templeton during the latter stage of the First Blackfyre Rebellion. The Knight of Ninestars supported the loyalists during the Battle of the Redgrass Field, fighting in the vanguard led by Lord Donnel_Arryn. He was killed by Daemon_I_Blackfyre when the rebel cut through the van.

name: Knight of the Red Chicken
bio: The Knight of the Red Chicken supposedly fought at the Battle of the Blackwater.

name: Knight Without Armor
bio: The Knight Without Armor is a legendary knight of the Reach who lived during the Age of Heroes. Tales describe him as a knight even though those warriors came to Westeros thousands of years later with the coming of the Andals.

name: Kojja Mo
bio: Kojja Mo is the daughter of Quhuru_Mo, the captain of the Cinnamon Wind.

name: Korra
bio: Korra, known as Lady Korra and Korra the Cruel, is a female pirate on Dagger Lake. Her ship, Hag's Teeth, is supposedly crewed by beautiful young maids who geld every man they capture.

name: Koss
bio: Koss is a criminal taken from the dungeons of King's Landing by Yoren to join the Night's Watch. Koss had been arrested as a poacher.

name: Kraznys mo Nakloz
bio: Kraznys mo Nakloz is a slaver in Astapor. He deals primarily in Unsullied. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Kraznys is portrayed by Dan Hildebrand.

name: Kromm
bio: Kromm is part of the force that accompanies Theon_Greyjoy, first in the harrying of the Stony Shore and then in the capture of Winterfell. Theon uses Kromm as a guard within Winterfell. Kromm remains with Theon after Ser Rodrik_Cassel comes to retake Winterfell, and he informs Theon about the battle outside the gates of Winterfell. Kromm is presumably slain during the sack of Winterfell by House Bolton.

name: Kurleket
bio: Kurleket is a man-at-arms sworn to House Bracken. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Kurleket is portrayed by Kevin Keenan. Kurleket is a fat man short-cropped hair and a pig's face. Tyrion_Lannister considers him a dim oaf. Kurleket's dirk is made of black iron.

name: Kurz
bio: Kurz is a criminal taken from the dungeons of King's Landing by Yoren to join the Night's Watch. Kurz had been arrested as a poacher.

name: Kyle
bio: Kyle, known as the Cat of Misty Moor or Kyle the Cat, was a hedge knight from Misty Moor. According to a semi-canon source, he blazoned his arms with a silver cat facing to its left on a red field. Ser Kyle once served House Caswell as a sworn sword. He claimed to have saved Lord Armond_Caswell's life during the First Blackfyre Rebellion. He made the first sword for Armond's three-year-old son, Joffrey_Caswell, carving a small wooden sword out of pine to fit his hand. Ser Kyle was one of several hedge knights headed to Lord Ambrose_Butterwell's wedding at Whitewalls in 212 AC. He hoped to take part in the wedding tourney, and possibly win himself a place in service to a lord. Ser Duncan_the_Tall and his squire Aegon_V_Targaryen, unable to stay at the old inn by the Gods Eye, found Kyle and others in a hedge knights' camp in a ring of weirwood stumps. Kyle introduced himself and his fellows, Ser Maynard_Plumm and Ser Glendon Ball, and they shared a meal and conversation about the politics of the day. During their talk, Kyle accidentally offended Ser Glendon when he referred to Blackfyre rebels as traitors, as Glendon claimed to be the bastard son of the great knight Quentyn_Ball, who had supported Daemon_I_Blackfyre. As the hedge knights waited for the ferry for the lake the next morning, Kyle tried to ingratiate himself with Lord Smallwood, who he claimed to know. At Whitewalls, Kyle looked to encounter Lord Joffrey_Caswell, who fell asleep in his wine at the wedding feast. The morning after, Kyle had the luck to face Joffrey Caswell in his first joust of the tourney. Though it would be an easy win, Kyle planned to throw the match in order to compliment Lord Joffrey on his growth as a warrior, and become a Caswell man again. And so, though Joffrey's horse was slow, his lance was wobbly, and he barely struck Kyle's shoulder, the Cat fell off his horse into the dirt, just as planned. However, when Kyle came to Joffrey to pay the forfeit, and told him about how he had once made his first sword, Joffrey did not recognize him, and said there was no place at Bitterbridge for knights as feeble as Kyle the Cat had shown himself to be. He still took Kyle's arms, armor, and horse, since Kyle could not pay the ransom for them. Kyle expressed his regrets to Duncan, wondering what he would do at his age without a horse or arms. Though Dunk tried to reassure him, saying he would find employment soon, Kyle wished he had instead faced John the Fiddler, a chivalrous competitor who took no ransoms. During the tourney, the dragon egg meant to be a prize for the winner disappeared, and Ser Glendon was arrested for its theft. Duncan revealed in the great hall of Whitewalls that John the Fiddler was actually Daemon Blackfyre, Second of His Name, intended by his supporters to win the tourney, and that Glendon had been removed because he was a competitor who could not be bought off. Duncan demanded justice for Glendon, and while many in the hall claimed that all bastards were thieves and could not be trusted, Kyle supported Duncan's defense. Daemon agreed to joust with Glendon in a trial by combat to determine his guilt or innocence. When Glendon was brought up from the dungeons where he had been tortured, Kyle helped dress his wounds and got him ready for the lists. Glendon soon defeated Daemon, and the attempted Second Blackfyre Rebellion was dispersed by the surprise arrival of the Hand of the King, Brynden_Rivers, and an army of riverlords and crownlands lords. Afterwards, Kyle waited for judgement, along with Duncan and Glendon. When Bloodraven questioned Dunk and Egg how they had been able to thwart the conspiracy all by themselves, Dunk said they had been helped by the hedge knights, including Kyle.

name: Kyle
bio: Kyle is a member of the brotherhood without banners.

name: Kyle Condon
bio: Kyle Condon is a knight of House Condon in service to House Cerwyn. According to Roose_Bolton, he is Lord Medger_Cerwyn's right hand man. However, the Appendix of A Storm of Swords lists him as a knight in the service of Ser Wylis_Manderly.

name: Kyle Connington
bio: Kyle Connington was the Lord of Griffin's Roost and the head of House Connington during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Lord Kyle attended the Golden Wedding, where the king entertained the lords in the intimacy of his solar, encouraging them to speak freely and share their views on the problems of the realm and how they might best be overcome. Lord Kyle thought the king to be witty and good-humored.

name: Kyle Royce
bio: Kyle Royce was a member of House Royce during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. His relation to Yohn_Royce, the Lord of Runestone, is unknown. When news of Lyanna_Stark's kidnapping by Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen reached her brother, Brandon_Stark, he raced off to King's Landing accompanied by a party of nobles, which included Kyle. Brandon and his party rode into the Red Keep demanding Rhaegar's head. King Aerys_II_Targaryen had them all arrested and demanded that their fathers come to King's Landing to answer for their sons' crimes. Aerys had all of them killed save for Ethan_Glover.

name: Kyleg of the Wooden Ear
bio: Kyleg of the Wooden Ear is a free folk leader.

name: Kym
bio: Kym was the maester assigned to Riverrun when Lord Hoster_Tully's children were young. He is deceased, and Riverrun's maester is now Vyman. Old Maester Kym prepared moon tea for Lysa_Arryn when she was pregnant with Petyr_Baelish's child.

name: Kyra
bio: Kyra is a tavern wench who works at the Smoking Log in the winter town outside of Winterfell.

name: Kyra Frey
bio: Kyra Frey is a noblewoman of House Frey, the daughter of Ser Jared_Frey and Alys_Frey. Kyra is married to Ser Garse_Goodbrook, with whom she has two children; Walder_Goodbrook and Jeyne_Goodbrook.

name: Kyrie
bio: Kyrie was a maester of the Citadel who served House Baratheon of Storm's End during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Maester Kyrie served Lord Rogar_Baratheon at Storm's End. In 54 AC, Rogar's pregnant wife, Lady Alyssa_Velaryon, fell ill, and went into labor a month early. Maester Kyrie, a midwife, and three bedmaids attended to her, but could only relieve her pain. When King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen arrived at Storm's End, Maester Kyrie told them that there was nothing he could do anymore for Alyssa, but that, if he was allowed to cut her open, he might be able to save her child. Lord Rogar told him to save his child, and fled while Kyrie delivered Alyssa's child, Jocelyn_Baratheon.

name: Lacey
bio: Lacey was an inhabitant of Pennytree.

name: Lady Farts-a-Lot
bio: "Lady Farts-a-Lot" was the nickname Princess Aerea_Targaryen gave a member of Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I)'s court in Dragonstone.

name: Lady of the Leaves
bio: The Lady of the Leaves is the leader of a hidden village that rests in the trees. The village is located in the riverlands near the road to Hayford in the crownlands.

name: Lady Stork
bio: Lady Stork is a mummer in Braavos. She is part of the mummer's troupe at the Gate. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, she is renamed Lady Crane and is portrayed by Essie Davis.

name: Laena Penrose
bio: Laena Penrose was a member of House Penrose and the first daughter of Lord Ronnel_Penrose and his wife Princess Elaena_Targaryen.

name: Laena Velaryon
bio: Lady Laena Velaryon was the eldest child and only daughter of Lord Corlys_Velaryon and Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon). She was the second wife of Prince Daemon_Targaryen and gave birth to his twin daughters, Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon) and Baela_Targaryen. Laena was a dragonrider and rode the she-dragon Vhagar. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Laena is portrayed by Nanna Blondell (adult), Savannah Steyn (teen), and Nova Foueillis-Mosé (child). Laena was born in late 92 AC.[N 1] She was the daughter of Lord Corlys_Velaryon and Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon), and the great-granddaughter of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Princess Rhaenys's father, Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I), the eldest son and heir of King Jaehaerys I, died shortly after Rhaenys learned she was pregnant. Although Rhaenys was Aemon's heir, Jaehaerys I named his second son, Prince Baelon, as his new heir, passing over Rhaenys and her potential unborn son. The unborn Laena, also referred to as "the boy in the belly", was subject of much debate on the succession until her birth. In 101 AC, Laena's claim to the Iron Throne was put forward once more during the Great Council, but she was passed over, together with her mother, on account of her sex. Before the age of twelve, Laena had become a dragonrider by claiming the largest Targaryen dragon left alive, Vhagar. Following the death of Queen Aemma_Arryn in late 105 AC, Grand Maester Runciter urged King Viserys_I_Targaryen to remarry, and suggested the just-turned-twelve and newly flowered Laena as a bride, so that Viserys could heal the rift between House Targaryen and House Velaryon. However, Viserys chose Alicent_Hightower, and though this angered Laena's father, Laena herself was untroubled. According to a letter the maester of High Tide wrote to the Citadel, Laena was interested more in flying than boys. When King Viserys I married Alicent_Hightower in 106 AC, House Velaryon was noticeably absent from the wedding.

name: Laena Velaryon
bio: Laena Velaryon was a noblewoman of House Velaryon, the daughter of Lord Alyn_Velaryon and his wife, Lady Baela_Targaryen, and the half-niece of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. She was named after her paternal great-aunt and maternal grandmother, Lady Laena_Velaryon. Laena was born while her father, Lord Alyn_Velaryon, was on his voyage to the Iron Islands and then Lys. Alyn first met his daughter when he returned to King's Landing with the previously-missing Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen. Laena and her parents departed soon after to return to Driftmark. Laena had been presented a dragon egg upon her birth. At Driftmark in 135 AC, it hatched a blind and wingless wyrm, white as a maggot. After the monster tore a bloody chunk from young Laena's arm, Alyn Oakenfist hacked it to pieces.

name: Laenor Velaryon
bio: Laenor Velaryon was the only son of Lord Corlys_Velaryon and Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon). He was the heir to Driftmark and the first husband of his second cousin, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen. Rhaenyra gave birth to three sons during their marriage, Jacaerys_Velaryon, Lucerys_Velaryon, and Joffrey_Velaryon, although it was rumored that they had been fathered by Rhaenyra's Harwin_Strong instead of Laenor. Laenor was a dragonrider, whose dragon was Seasmoke. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Laenor is portrayed by John Macmillan (adult), Theo Nate (teen), and Matthew Carver (child).

name: Lambert Turnberry
bio: Lambert Turnberry is a knight from House Turnberry. He wears a patch over his perfectly good right eye.

name: Lambert Whitewater
bio: Ser Lambert Whitewater is a knight in the service of King Stannis_Baratheon. He is one of the queen's men.

name: Lamprey
bio: Lamprey is a gaoler on Dragonstone. He receives his nickname from Ser Davos_Seaworth; the gaoler's given name is unknown.

name: Lancel Lannister
bio: Lancel Lannister is a member of House Lannister of Casterly Rock and is the eldest son of Ser Kevan_Lannister. Lancel and his cousin, Tyrek_Lannister, are squires to King Robert_I_Baratheon. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Lancel is portrayed by Eugene Simon. Queen Cersei_Lannister insisted that King Robert_I_Baratheon take her cousins, Lancel and Tyrek_Lannister, as squires. Cersei's father, Lord Tywin_Lannister, ordered his nephew to obey all of Cersei's instructions.

name: Lancel I Lannister
bio: Lancel I Lannister, known as Lancel the Lion, was a King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Lancel rode to war against the Gardener Kings of the Reach and conquered as far south as Old Oak but was later slain in combat. His conquest were lost by his son, King Loreon_III_Lannister, however.

name: Lancel IV Lannister
bio: Lancel IV Lannister was a King of the Rock from House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Lancel IV is said to have beheaded the ironborn king Harrald_Halfdrowned and his heir during the Battle of Lann's Point., with a single stroke of Brightroar, the Valyrian steel greatsword of House Lannister (however, it was probably a common steel sword of the same name, which has been conflated with the Valyrian blade).[N 1] When Gyles_III_Gardener, King of the Reach, was away battling the Storm King in the east, Lancel attempted to cut a piece out of the Reach for himself. According to Ser Eustace_Osgrey, his ancestor Wilbert_Osgrey fought against Lancel as the Osgreys were Marshalls of the Northmarch. Although Lancel nearly cut Ser Wilbert in half with Brightroar, the dying Little Lion was able to stab his dagger through a gap in Lancel's armor to kill the king. Eustace claimed Lancel's men returned to the westerlands after Lancel's death, as they did not want to fight on after the death of their king. According to Maester Yandel, Lancel IV died at Red Lake during his invasion of the Reach. Yandel writes that Gyles halted his own conquest and returned home to the Reach to deal with Lancel's westermen. Had Lancel not invaded the Reach, Gyles might have been able to complete his conquest in the stormlands.

name: Lancel V Lannister
bio: Lancel V Lannister was a King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock. When Eustace_Osgrey tells Ser Duncan_the_Tall about the battle between Wilbert_Osgrey, the Little Lion, and a King of the Rock, he is unsure which King Lancel Lannister Wilbert fought against, and claims it was either Lancel_IV_Lannister or Lancel V. In truth, Wilbert had battled against Lancel IV.

name: Lann the Clever
bio: Lann the Clever is the legendary hero who founded House Lannister in the Age of Heroes, winkling Casterly Rock from the Casterlys with only his wits. Lann is popular amongst singers and storytellers. He was portrayed by Jamie Campbell Bower in the rejected pilot episode of Bloodmoon.

name: Lanna
bio: Lanna is a pretty fourteen-year-old prostitute at the Happy Port brothel in Braavos.

name: Lanna
bio: Lanna is a serving wench and prostitute at the Peach in Stoney Sept.

name: Lanna Lannister
bio: Lanna Lannister is a noblewoman of House Lannister and a distant cousin to the main branch, being a first cousin twice removed of Tywin_Lannister, the Lord of Casterly Rock. She is the daughter of Ser Damion_Lannister and Shiera_Crakehall and the sister of Ser Lucion_Lannister. She is married to the head of House Jast, Lord Antario_Jast; it is unconfirmed whether Antario's sons are from Lanna or an earlier wife.

name: Larence Snow
bio: Larence Snow is the natural son of Lord Halys_Hornwood. He is under the guardianship of Galbart_Glover, Master of Deepwood Motte. He has a half-brother, Daryn_Hornwood.

name: Larissa Velaryon
bio: Larissa Velaryon was a noblewoman of House Velaryon. She became a close friend and favorite of her cousin Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). In 35 AC, Larissa became the first true friend of her cousin, Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). For a time the two girls were inseparable, until Larissa was suddenly recalled to Driftmark to be wed to the second son of the Evenstar of Tarth. By 55 AC, Lady Larissa's husband had died, and their daughter had recently been wed to the elderly Lord Estermont. Rather than remain on Tarth or return to Driftmark, the widowed Larissa chose to stay with her daughter at Greenstone after the wedding. Her presence on Greenstone drew her cousin Rhaena, whose companions had recently been murdered, and whose daughter Aerea_Targaryen had recently disappeared on Balerion. Rhaena lingered on Estermont for a year, seeking the solace of her childhood companion Larissa. After Aerea's return, death, and funeral in 56 AC, Queen Alysanne_Targaryen asked Rhaena if she wished to return to Greenstone. Rhaena dismissed the idea, referring to Larissa as a ghost, though a kind one.

name: Lark
bio: Lark, known as the Sisterman and Lark the Sisterman, is a ranger of the Night's Watch. Lark is a thin man, with sharp features and nervous eyes. He is considered mean. Lark comes from the Three Sisters, a group of islands ruled by the Vale of Arryn. His cousin is Rolley of Sisterton.

name: Larra Blackmont
bio: Larra Blackmont is the Lady of Blackmont and the head of House Blackmont. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Larra is genderswapped as "Lord Blackmont" and portrayed by Daniel Rabin.

name: Larra Rogare
bio: Lady Larra Rogare was a member of House Rogare of Lys. She married Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, the younger brother of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. With Viserys, Larra had three children: King Aegon_IV_Targaryen, Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II), and Queen Naerys_Targaryen. She eventually abandoned Viserys and returned to Lys. Larra was born in 115 AC, the youngest daughter of Lysandro_Rogare, the head of the wealthy and influential banking family in Lys. When Lysandro came into the possession of the captive Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, he quickly[N 1] married him to his daughter Larra, despite her being seven years his senior. Not long thereafter, Larra's uncle, Drazenko_Rogare, approached Lord Alyn_Velaryon at Sunspear, informing him where Viserys could be found. Lysandro Rogare's ransom terms included that Velaryon had to swear upon his honor that Larra's marriage to Viserys would not be set aide, for whatever reason. When Velaryon agreed to Lysandro's terms, he brought Viserys back to King's Landing in 134 AC. Larra accompanied him, with three of her brothers, Moredo_Rogare, Lotho_Rogare, and Roggerio_Rogare. King Aegon_III_Targaryen, his court, and the city celebrated Prince Viserys's return, as he had been believed dead for five years. The king's brother's "revival", along with the fact that Larra was of childbearing age, helped resolve the question of succession to the Iron Throne, open since the end of the Dance of the Dragons, and particularly since King Aegon III had married the young Daenaera_Velaryon. The king's regents quickly confirmed the legitimacy of Larra's marriage to Viserys. Viserys also confided to Grand Maester Munkun that he had consummated his marriage. Larra moved into the Red Keep with her husband, but did not manage to adapt to the customs of the Seven Kingdoms. All her clothing came from Lys, even her smallclothes; Rogare ships delivered the latest Lysene fashions to her three times a year. She made no attempt to learn the Common Tongue, and instead used translators. She kept only Lysene servants, and even her protectors were Lysene, under the command of her brother Moredo_Rogare and Sandoq_the_Shadow. Larra also had no interest in the Faith of the Seven or the old gods of the north, but continued to worship Lysene gods, to whom she did obeisances. Though the people of King's Landing rejoiced when Prince Viserys announced to the royal court that Larra was pregnant, they soon came to dislike and mistrust her. Cats were frequently seen coming and going from Larra's chambers, and people began to whisper that they were her spies. Some even claimed that Larra was able to transform herself into a cat, and darker rumors followed soon after. Her worship of the god Yndros caused men to whisper that Larra would transform herself into a man to visit the brothels on the Street of Silk. Because of her worship of Saagael, she was blamed every time a child went missing. Larra's brothers were also greatly disliked, as they were proud, grasping, and ambitious, and their foreign origin and practices led to suspicion and mistrust. By late 134 AC, some of King's Landing began to fear that the Rogares would soon rule the Seven Kingdoms, with Larra holding the strings. Larra gave birth during the third month of 135 AC to a son whom Viserys named Aegon_IV_Targaryen, after his brother, King Aegon III. Prince Aegon was anointed with the seven oils of the Faith of the Seven, but when it became known that Larra wished to have Aegon blessed by her own gods as well, people began to whisper about obscene ceremonies and blood sacrifices. Following the sudden deaths of Larra's father Lysandro and her uncle Drazenko, Larra's eldest brother Lysaro_Rogare drained the gold held by the Rogare Bank. When rumors began to spread that the Rogare Bank was unsound, House Rogare fell from power in Lys. The consequences extended to Westeros, where lords and merchants realized that the coin they had entrusted to the Rogare Bank was lost, and Lotho and Roggerio were arrested. Ser Marston_Waters of the Kingsguard, the new Hand of the King, also ordered Larra's arrest, but when Ser Amaury_Peake arrived at Maegor's Holdfast to arrest her, he found himself denied entrance by Larra's husband Viserys, and Viserys's brother, King Aegon III. During the secret siege, Larra, Viserys, Aegon III, Queen Daenaera_Velaryon, and several servants were trapped in Maegor's Holdfast for eighteen days. According to Marston Waters, Lord Thaddeus_Rowan, the previous Hand, had confessed to having plotted with Larra's three brothers to poison King Aegon III and Queen Daenaera in order to crown Viserys king, with Larra as his queen. When Rowan was brought before Maegor's Holdfast to confirm his confessions, Viserys was quick to reveal Rowan had been tortured into giving a false confession. Six days after Marston fulfilled his king's command to arrest those who had falsely implicated Lord Rowan and the Rogares, the siege came to an end. After his arrest, the conspirator Septon Bernard confessed that he had been motivated by his religion, as he felt that Larra and her foreign gods had no place in Westeros. He claimed that it had been intended for Larra to die, so Viserys could ascend the throne and take a "proper Westerosi queen." In 136 AC, when trials were held for those implicated in the siege and turmoil that had gone before, Larra was pregnant with her second child. She attended the trials together with her husband Viserys, her young son Aegon, and his wet nurse. Larra was also present when Lord Torrhen_Manderly passed his judgment on Lotho and Roggerio, and wept when the sentences were proclaimed. On the eve of Smith's Day in 136 AC, Larra gave birth to her second son, Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II). When Aegon struck the infant Aemon with the dragon egg that had been placed in his cradle, she rushed to separate them. Two years later, Larra gave birth to a daughter, Naerys_Targaryen. Larra never felt part of the court at King's Landing, and was never truly happy in Westeros. In 139 AC, a year after having given birth to Naerys, Larra returned to Lys. Viserys, an otherwise charming man, grew stern after Larra left him and their children. Larra eventually died in 145 AC of unknown causes.

name: Larraq
bio: Larraq, known as the Lash for his weapons of choice, is a former slave now in training with Ser Barristan_Selmy to become a knight. He serves Barristan as a squire in Meereen.

name: Larys Strong
bio: Larys Strong, known as Larys Clubfoot or the Clubfoot, was the Lord of Harrenhal and head of House Strong during the latter part of the reign of Viserys_I_Targaryen and the reign of Aegon_II_Targaryen. He served on the small council as the master of whisperers and Lord Confessor for both kings. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Larys is portrayed by Matthew Needham.

name: Last hero
bio: The last hero of the First Men lived during the Long Night according to Westerosi legends. It is unknown if there is a connection with Azor_Ahai, a person who saves the world from the darkness in legends from Essos.

name: Laswell Peake
bio: Laswell Peake is an exiled lord and sellsword in service to the Golden Company. His brothers Pykewood_Peake and Torman_Peake serve in the company with him. Laswell has an unspecified familiar relationship to the main branch of House Peake of Starpike. In the appendix of A Dance with Dragons, Laswell is described as an "exile lord". Given that the Peakes were stripped of two of their three castles (Dunstonbury and Whitegrove) by the Iron Throne for their support to the pretender Daemon_I_Blackfyre during the First Blackfyre Rebellion, it is possible Laswell's ancestor was the lord of one of these castles who chose to follow Aegor_Rivers in exile at the end of the war.

name: Laurent
bio: Archmaester Laurent was an archmaester of the Citadel. He wrote the book Old Places of the Trident, which states that there were thirty one weirwoods crowning High Heart at the time Erreg the Kinslayer came across the great hill and slaughtered the Children of the Forest and First Men defending it.

name: Layna
bio: Layna is the young daughter of an owner of an alehouse on the Goldroad.

name: Leaf
bio: Leaf is one of the children of the forest in service to the last greenseer. Her true name is too long for the human tongue. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Leaf is portrayed by Octavia Alexandru in Season 4 and Kae Alexander in Season 6. She was also portrayed by Doyin Ajiboye in the rejected pilot episode of Bloodmoon. Leaf was born in the time of House Targaryen's dynasty in the Seven Kingdoms, and walked the world of men for two hundred years to watch, listen, and learn. She says she might be walking still, but her legs were sore and her heart was weary, so she turned her feet for home.

name: Leana Frey
bio: Leana Frey is a noblewoman of House Frey, the youngest daughter of Lothar_Frey and Leonella_Lefford. She is an infant.

name: Leathers
bio: Leathers  is a member of the free folk of the haunted forest who joins the Night's Watch.

name: Lelia Lannister
bio: Lelia Lannister was a noblewoman from House Lannister and Queen of the Iron Islands through her marriage to King Harmund_II_Hoare. Harmund_II_Hoare, known as Harmund the Haggler, spent his youth as a ward of the Lannisters at Casterly Rock. Once King of the Iron Islands, Harmund married Lady Lelia, the daughter of the King of the Rock and the "fairest flower of the west". Their children were raised in the Faith of the Seven. When Lelia's eldest son became king as Harmund_III_Hoare, he was opposed by many ironborn and the priests of the Drowned God for his opposition to reaving, salt wives, and thralls. A priest known as the Shrike led a quick and successful rebellion which crowned Harmund's younger brother, Hagon_Hoare, who restored ironborn traditions. Harmund III and the Dowager Queen Lelia were both mutilated for having turned away from the Drowned God. Lelia's lips, ears, eyelids, and tongue were removed, after which she was sent by longship back to the Rock. The then-King of the Rock, Lelia's outraged nephew, went to war against the ironborn. Hagon the Heartless was defeated by Ser Aubrey_Crakehall in the seventh year of fighting. Before being hanged, he suffered the same mutilations which had been done to Lelia.

name: Lem
bio: Lem, also known as Lem Lemoncloak, is a one-time soldier. It is unknown if Lem is his given name; according to Tom_of_Sevenstreams, Lem is named after the yellow cloak he wears. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Lem is portrayed by Jóhannes Haukur Jóhannesson.

name: Lem
bio: Lem was a peasant sworn to House Osgrey. After being trained by Ser Eustace_Osgrey's sons with other peasants from his lands, Lem went off to fight for House Osgrey during the Blackfyre Rebellion. He was one of the only three surviving men that returned with Ser Eustace, along with Dake and Pate_(Standfast). Lem brought back a knife and a fine pair of boots from the war. The boots were too small to fit him, so he hung them on his wall for display.

name: Lemore
bio: Lemore, also known  as Lady Lemore, is a septa of the Faith of the Seven and the spiritual tutor for Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar).

name: Lenn
bio: Lenn is the son of a woods witch. People believe he has a gift for foretelling the weather. Lenn is one of the raiders who is ordered by Mance_Rayder to climb the Wall and attack Castle Black from the south. He survives the climb and arrives at Queenscrown with the others. Lenn dies during the attack on Castle Black.

name: Lennocks
bio: Lennocks was the brother of Jack-Be-Lucky and Lister. He was killed by the forces of House Lannister.

name: Lenwood Tawney
bio: Lenwood Tawney, nicknamed Little Lenwood Tawney, is a fiddler from House Tawney in the Iron Islands.

name: Lenyl
bio: Lenyl is a wildling raider. He is a part of the war band lead by Rattleshirt.

name: Leo Blackbar
bio: Leo Blackbar is a knight from House Blackbar. He is married to Olene_Tyrell, a cousin of Lord Mace_Tyrell.

name: Leo Costayne
bio: Leo Costayne, known as the Sea Lion, was a knight of House Costayne and lord admiral of the Reach during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 133 AC, when Lord Alyn_Velaryon was dispatched to the westerlands to battle Lord Dalton_Greyjoy, twenty Hightower war galleys commanded by Ser Leo joined the Velaryon fleet. However, Dalton was slain before the fleet arrived and the his ships returned to the Iron Islands. With the Lannister fleet having been destroyed during the Dance of the Dragons, House Lannister was reluctant to see the Velaryon fleet depart in fear of the ironborn returning. Until the Lannisters and Farmans could rebuild their ships, Alyn agreed to allow a third of his own fleet remain in the westerlands, under the command of Leo. While Alyn had refused to attack the Iron Islands, Leo proved more amenable to Lady Johanna Westerling, the Dowager Lady of Casterly Rock and regent for her young son, Lord Loreon_Lannister. Swayed perhaps by Johanna's promise to marry him if he conquered the Iron Islands for her son, Leo invaded the isles with his fleet in 134 AC. In the middle of the ironmen's struggle for power, Leo and his army took them unawares and put many to the sword, landing thousands of Lannister soldiers on Pyke, Great Wyk, and Harlaw. Ultimately, Leo was killed by Arthur_Goodbrother in the hills of Great Wyk. Leo's host was largely destroyed and three quarters of his fleet were seized or sunk, but a portion of his fleet returned to Lannisport with highborn captives aboard. Amongst the notable hostages was Rodrik_Greyjoy_(son_of_Dalton), a salt son of Lord Dalton that Lady Lannister would geld and make her son's fool.

name: Leo Lefford
bio: Leo Lefford is the Lord of the Golden Tooth and the head of House Lefford. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Leo is portrayed by Vinnie McCabe.

name: Leo Tyrell
bio: Leo Tyrell was a head of House Tyrell and the second Tyrell High Steward of the Reach. He was the son of Gareth_Tyrell. Gareth and Leo performed their duties to House Gardener so well, the Gardeners made the office of High Steward hereditary.

name: Leo Tyrell
bio: Leo Tyrell, known as Lazy Leo, is a member of House Tyrell, a first cousin of Mace_Tyrell, the Lord of Highgarden. He is the second son of Ser Moryn_Tyrell, the commander of Oldtown's City Watch. Leo is a novice at the Citadel. Leo was acquainted with Samwell_Tarly during their childhood. Sam does not have fond memories of their interactions. At some point in their time at the Citadel, Leo nicknamed Alleras "the Sphinx", as he was "a bit of this, a bit of that", like a sphinx is made of multiple creatures. Their fellow students picked up on the nickname, and Alleras himself even uses it. However, Leo's nickname of "Hopfrog" for Mollander (for his clubfoot) is detested.

name: Leo Tyrell
bio: Leo Tyrell, known as Leo Longthorn, was the Lord of Highgarden and head of House Tyrell during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Leo is portrayed by Steve Wall. During the wedding tourney at Whitewalls in 212 AC, Ser Duncan_the_Tall and his squire, Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen, drank a toast to the health of Leo, as there were rumors that he was ailing.

name: Leo Tyrell
bio: Leo Tyrell is a knight of House Tyrell, the son of Ser Victor_Tyrell. He is married to Alys_Beesbury, with whom he has five children, Alla_Tyrell, Leona_Tyrell, Lyonel_Tyrell_(son_of_Leo), Lucas_Tyrell, and Lorent_Tyrell.

name: Leobald Tallhart
bio: Leobald Tallhart is the younger brother of Ser Helman_Tallhart and is the castellan of Torrhen's Square.

name: Leona Tyrell
bio: Leona Tyrell is a member of House Tyrell. She is the daughter of Ser Leo_Tyrell_(son_of_Victor) and Alys_Beesbury, making her a distant cousin to the ruling branch.

name: Leona Woolfield
bio: Leona Woolfield is a noblewoman of House Woolfield and is the wife of Ser Wylis_Manderly, the heir to White Harbor. They have two daughters, Wynafryd_Manderly and Wylla_Manderly.

name: Leonella Lefford
bio: Leonella Lefford is a noblewoman of House Lefford married to Lothar_Frey, with whom she has four daughters: Tysane_Frey, Walda_Frey_(daughter_of_Lothar), Emberlei_Frey and Leana_Frey.

name: Leonette Fossoway
bio: Leonette Fossoway is the wife of Ser Garlan_Tyrell. She is a noblewoman of House Fossoway, though it is unclear which branch of the family she is from.

name: Leowyn Corbray
bio: Leowyn Corbray was the Lord of Heart's Home and the head of House Corbray during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons, later becoming Protector of the Realm in the minority of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. He had a younger brother, Ser Corwyn_Corbray, who wielded Lady Forlorn. In 130 AC, Lady Jeyne_Arryn gave Leowyn and his younger brother, Ser Corwyn_Corbray command of her army of ten thousand men sent forth from Gulltown. After the death of King Aegon_II_Targaryen in 131 AC, Lord Leowyn stood at the prow of a Braavosi cog in the Gullet and watched a line of Velaryon warships tear down  Aegon II's sigil and raise up the red dragon of House Targaryen in its place, to mark the ascension of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Leowyn and his brother divided the army between them. The Braavosi landed Leowyn at Duskendale and Corwyn at Maidenpool.[N 1] Both brothers were welcomed with feasts and flowers. Soon after Lord Cregan_Stark's hold over court had begun, Leowyn left Maidenpool accompanied by Lord Manfryd_Mooton, Lord Brune, and Ser Rennifer_Crabb. He met up with his brother on the march to King's Landing. When they arrived at the capital, they joined the ruling council and added their voices to those of Lady Jeyne Arryn and the three Lads. This contributed to power beginning to slip from Lord Stark's hands. Leowyn, as a supporter of the black faction, was named Protector of the Realm, whilst Ser Tyland_Lannister, a green, was named Hand of the King. By law, both of them were beholden to the council of regents. Ser Tyland quickly achieved an unspoken dominance over Leowyn, gathering more power to himself as time passed. In 133 AC, Leowyn died of the Winter Fever, alongside his mistress and several of his servants.

name: Leslyn
bio: Leslyn works at the Peach in Stoney Sept. The character's gender and occupation are not mentioned. Leslyn told Bella that the girl's mother was Robert_I_Baratheon's favorite at the Peach when he was hiding in Stoney Sept before the Battle of the Bells.

name: Leslyn Haigh
bio: Leslyn Haigh is a knight of House Haigh and the husband of Perriane_Frey. He is the father of Ser Harys_Haigh, Ser Donnel_Haigh and Alyn_Haigh.

name: Lester
bio: Lester, known as Red Lester, is a guard sworn to House Lannister.

name: Lester Dabell
bio: Ser Lester Dabell was a knight during the reign of Daeron_II_Targaryen. He had two brothers: Ser Ernest_Dabell and Ser Pascal_Dabell. He participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Lester Morrigen
bio: Lester Morrigen is the Lord of Crow's Nest and the head of House Morrigen in the stormlands. His heir is his younger brother, Ser Richard_Morrigen.

name: Lew
bio: Lew is a member of the guard at Winterfell.

name: Left Hand Lew
bio: Left Hand Lew is a steward of the Night's Watch.

name: Long Lew
bio: Long Lew is a guard at Riverrun.

name: Lewis Lanster
bio: Lewis Lanster is a sellsword and member of the Windblown. He is said to be the company's best archer.

name: Lewyn Martell
bio: Prince Lewyn Nymeros Martell, better known simply as Lewyn Martell or Lewyn of Dorne, was a knight from House Martell who served as a member of Aerys_II_Targaryen's Kingsguard. He was the uncle of the current Prince of Dorne, Doran_Martell. Prince Lewyn was the younger brother of the former ruling Princess_of_Dorne_(mother_of_Doran). Lewyn was among the Dornishmen who came to court at King's Landing with his niece, Princess Elia_Martell, who was betrothed in early 279 AC and married a year later in 280 AC, to Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen. He became a confidante of Rhaegar's. Lewyn was appointed a member of the Kingsguard in the service of King Aerys_II_Targaryen at an unknown time. Lewyn was present at the tourney at Harrenhal in 281 AC. During Robert's Rebellion, Aerys used Lewyn's niece, Elia, as a hostage against him, and sent him to command the ten thousand Dornishmen that were to join Rhaegar's force that was assembling to take on Lord Robert_I_Baratheon. Lewyn died during the Battle of the Trident in 283 AC at the hands of Ser Lyn_Corbray, although he was mortally wounded before his final duel with Lyn. Dorne was incensed by the deaths of Lewyn and Elia during the war, and Prince Oberyn_Martell tried to raise the kingdom to support Viserys_Targaryen, the surviving son of the late Aerys. In response, Lord Jon_Arryn travelled to Sunspear the year after Robert took the throne (284 AC) to return the bones of Lewyn and was able to broker a peace with Doran_Martell, the ruling Prince of Dorne. Arianne_Martell recalls her great-uncle fondly, remembering when she was a little girl how her great-uncle Lewyn would tower over her and tickle her till she could barely breathe from laughter. It was a closely held secret that Prince Lewyn kept a paramour despite the Kingsguard oaths of chastity. Ser Barristan_Selmy and his other sworn brothers were aware of this but kept their sworn brother's secret.

name: Lewys the Fishwife
bio: Lewys, known as Lewys the Fishwife, is a man sworn to House Baratheon of Dragonstone.

name: Lewys Lydden
bio: Lewys Lydden is the Lord of the Deep Den and the head of House Lydden.

name: Lewys Piper
bio: Lewys Piper, better known as Lew Piper or Little Lew Piper, is a member of House Piper, a younger son of Lord Clement_Piper.

name: Leygood (father of Lucas)
bio: Leygood was the head[N 1] of House Leygood during the Dance of the Dragons. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Leygood declared for King Aegon_II_Targaryen and the greens. Like the rest of the lords of the Reach who supported the greens, he joined the host of Lord Ormund_Hightower on his march to King's Landing.
