After the First Battle of Tumbleton and the death of Lord Ormund, the army was paralyzed in the city without a clear commander and without knowing with certainty the intentions of the Two Betrayers, Hugh_Hammer and Ulf_White. One night, Leygood and twelve other lords and landed knights of the Reach assembled at an inn, the Bloody Caltrops, to conspire against Hugh and Ulf. The Second Battle of Tumbleton broke out before the Caltrops could carry out their plans, however. Leygood and most of his companions died during the battle. Years later, during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen, Lord Unwin_Peake, a fellow Caltrops, assumed the position of Hand of the King and appointed Leygood's son, Ser Lucas_Leygood, as the new Commander of the City Watch of King's Landing.

name: Leyla Hightower
bio: Leyla Hightower is a daughter of Lord Leyton_Hightower of Oldtown. She is married to Ser Jon_Cupps.

name: Leyton Hightower
bio: Leyton Hightower, known as the Old Man of Oldtown, is the Lord of the Hightower and head of House Hightower. His other titles include the Voice of Oldtown, Lord of the Port, Defender of the Citadel, and Beacon of the South. Leyton's flagship is Honor of Oldtown. Leyton has been married four times; his current wife is Rhea_Florent. One of Leyton's daughters, Alerie_Hightower, is married to Lord Mace_Tyrell of Highgarden. Lord Leyton was unhorsed at a tourney at Storm's End by Ser Barristan_Selmy. Lord Tywin_Lannister offered his dwarf son, Tyrion_Lannister, to Leyton for marriage to one of his daughters, but Leyton, feeling insulted by the suggestion, rejected Tywin's offer. Leyton was present for the tourney at Lannisport held to celebrate the victory of the Iron Throne over the Greyjoy rebels during Greyjoy's Rebellion in 289 AC. There, his youngest daughter, Lynesse_Hightower, was named the queen of love and beauty at the tourney's end by Ser Jorah_Mormont, who had become smitten with Lynesse. Jorah asked Leyton permission to marry her that same night, and Leyton accepted the offer. By 300 AC, Leyton has not descended from the Hightower in more than a decade.

name: Lharys
bio: Lharys is a man-at-arms in service to House Bracken.

name: Lia Serry
bio: Lia Serry is a noblewoman of House Serry. She is married to Ser Theodore_Tyrell. They have two children, Elinor_Tyrell and Luthor_Tyrell_(son_of_Theodore).

name: Liane Vance
bio: Liane Vance is a noblewoman of House Vance of Wayfarer's Rest. She is the eldest daughter of the heir of Wayfarer's Rest, Ser Karyl_Vance.

name: Lianna Velaryon
bio: Lianna Velaryon was a noblewoman of House Velaryon during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. She was one of the ladies-in-waiting to her cousin, Queen Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). Lianna was the niece of Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan), Lord of the Tides. After the death of Dowager Queen Alyssa_Velaryon, her daughter Queen Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) retreated to Dragonstone, and found consolation over her mother's death with new companions, including her young cousin Lianna. In 54 AC, Rhaena's husband Androw_Farman used the tears of Lys to poison his wife's companions, having grown jealous of their friendships and weary of their mockery toward him. Initially believing there to be an illness spreading across Dragonstone, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen ordered his Hand of the King, Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan), to blockade the island. The King's Hand did as commanded, though not without grief, for his niece was still on the island. She was the last of Rhaena's companions to be poisoned by Androw. Maester Anselm purged her, bled her, and covered her with ice, but nothing worked. She died in the arms of Rhaena, convulsing, even as her uncle's galleys were pushing off from Driftmark.

name: The Liddle
bio: The Liddle is a traveler in the northern mountains. Bran_Stark presumes he is a member of House Liddle. His name is unknown. The man learned the phrase "dark wings, dark words" from his mother, who died nine years ago.

name: Lily
bio: Lily was the second of the four bastard children of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen and his second mistress, Megette. Lily's mother Megette was the mistress of Prince Aegon for four years. Lily was the second of four daughters. After Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen discovered this, Megette was returned to her husband in the riverlands, while Lily and her three sisters were given to the Faith to be trained as a septa.

name: Liomond Lashare
bio: Liomond Lashare, dubbed the Lord of Battles, was a sellsword captain who became one of the infamous Band of Nine, the instigators of the War of the Ninepenny Kings. The Band of Nine were a group of merchants, sellswords and pirates who joined together to carve kingdoms out for themselves.

name: Lister
bio: Lister was the brother of Jack-Be-Lucky and Lennocks. He was killed by the forces of House Lannister.

name: Little Lord Lazyeye
bio: Little Lord Lazyeye was the nickname Prince Oberyn_Martell gave to an unidentified suitor of his sister Elia_Martell during the trip the siblings took in 273 AC, during which their mother hoped to betroth either one of them, or both. As the Martells stopped at Starfall, the Arbor, Oldtown, the Shield Islands and Crakehall before arriving to Casterly Rock, Little Lord Lazeye was either a Dayne, a Redwyne, a Hightower, a Grimm, a Chester, a Hewett, a Serry or a Crakehall.

name: Lo Bu
bio: Lo Bu, known as the Boy Too Bold By Half, was a God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti and the forty third and final ruler of his dynasty. When he ascended to the throne, the nomadic Jogos Nhai had become bolder and more rapacious, causing the young emperor to seek to exterminate them. He assembled a host said to be three hundred thousand strong and swept the plains with it, leaving a burning wasteland behind, unswayed by tributes, hostages, oaths of fealty or offerings of peace. When the Jogos Nhai resorted to their traditional tactic of melting away before an army, Lo Bu divided his host into thirteen smaller and sent them to hunt down the nomads. History tells that one million of the Jogos Nhai perished at their hands. Thus, the rival clans of zorse-riders united and raised up a jhattar, Zhea. Though Lo Bu was courageous and skilled at arms like no other, he was not as cunning as Zhea, who in two years isolated and destroyed each of Lo Bu's armies one by one, slaying scouts and foragers, starving them, denying them water, or leading them into wastelands and traps. Finally her riders fell upon Lo Bu's own army and slaughtered it, the young emperor included. When Lo Bu's severed head was presented to her, Zhea commanded that the flesh be stripped from the bone and the skull dipped in gold and made into a drinking cup. Since that day, every jhattar has drunk fermented zorse milk from the gilded skull of Lo Bu, who is remembered as the Boy Too Bold By Half.

name: Lo Doq
bio: Lo Doq, called Lo Lackwit, was a God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti and the thirty-fourth ruler of his dynasty. Doq was a simpleton cursed with an affliction that caused him to jerk and stagger when he walked, and drool when he tried to speak. Nonetheless he ruled wisely for more than thirty years. There are those, however, that suggest the true ruler was his formidable wife, Empress Bathi_Ma_Lo.

name: Lo Han
bio: Lo Han was a God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti and the forty second ruler of his dynasty. Three times he led armies into the Plains of the Jogos Nhai with the goal of bringing the Jogos Nhai to heel, even compeling handfuls of jhats to sweat fealty to him and forswear raiding forever. Yet by the time of his death the Jogos Nhai had become bolder and more rapacious than when he had first donned the imperial regalia. He was succeeded by his son Lo_Bu.

name: Lo Tho
bio: Lo Tho, called Lo Longspoon and Lo the Terrible, was a God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti and the twenty-second ruler of his dynasty. Tho was a reputed sorcerer and cannibal, said to have supped upon the living brains of his enemies with a long, pearl-handled spoon, after the tops of their skulls had been removed, earning his monikers.

name: Lodos
bio: Lodos was a priest of the Drowned God who claimed to be his god's living son. Harren_Hoare, King of the Isles and the Rivers, was killed during the burning of Harrenhal. Although Qhorin_Volmark insisted he was Harren's heir, Lodos rejected the claim. A barefoot Lodos was crowned King of the Iron Islands with a driftwood crown by twoscore priests gathered at Nagga's Bones on Old Wyk. During the invasion of the Iron Islands by King Aegon_I_Targaryen to put down several rebellious would-be kings, Lodos turned to his god and called on the krakens of the deep to drag down Aegon's warships. When the beasts failed to appear, Lodos filled his robes with stones and walked into the sea to "take counsel" with his claimed father, the Drowned God. Thousands followed Lodos. Their corpses would wash up on the shores for years to come, except for Lodos's own body.

name: Lodos the Twice-Drowned
bio: Lodos the Twice-Drowned was a man who boasted of being the priest-king "Lodos" reborn, during the reign of King Aenys_I_Targaryen. The would-be monarch Lodos had walked into the Sunset Sea and disappeared, early in the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. After King Aenys_I_Targaryen succeeded to the Iron Throne in 37 AC, a man claiming to be Lodos the Twice-Drowned, returned from the halls of the Drowned God, led a revolt in the Iron Islands against the new king.  The revolt, however, was swiftly put down by Goren_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands, who descended with a hundred longships on Old Wyk and Great Wyk where most of Lodos's followers were concentrated. Thousands of his disciples were put to the sword. Lord Goren sent the pickled head of Lodos to the Iron Throne.

name: Lollys Stokeworth
bio: Lollys Stokeworth is the youngest daughter of Lady Tanda_Stokeworth of Stokeworth. She has an elder sister, Falyse_Stokeworth. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Lollys is portrayed by Elizabeth Cadwallader.

name: Lomas Estermont
bio: Lomas Estermont is a knight of House Estermont and is the father of Ser Andrew_Estermont.

name: Lomas Longstrider
bio: Lomas Longstrider was a famous scribe and traveler of Westerosi origin. He is famously quoted as saying, "The gods made seven wonders and mortal man made nine". Lomas traveled the known world and wrote two famous books, Wonders and Wonders Made by Man, in which he cataloged sixteen sights he considered wonders. One of the wonders Lomas recorded are the Valyrian roads. He also saw and described the triple walls of Qarth. Lomas visited the colossal ruins of the Great Pyramid of Ghis. Lomas interviewed descendants of the Rhoynar living in the ruins of Chroyane. He visited Norvos and named its three bells a wonder made by man. He also visited the large cavern system a hundred leagues northwest of Norvos, and listed it as one of the seven natural wonders. Lomas marveled at the Titan of Braavos and named the Long Bridge of Volantis as one of the wonders made by man. He also named the Palace With a Thousand Rooms in Sarnath among the man-made wonders. Lomas visited the Summer Isles, where he interviewed sages of the isles. Lomas passed east of the massive Bone Mountains but he did not do so by taking a ship in the Shivering Sea. Like many others, he either went through the mountain passes or by way of the Jade Gates. It is said that at the sight of the Bones, Lomas lost heart, believing he had reached the ends of the earth. Lomas visited Yi Ti, where he was awestruck by its wealth and rich culture. Lomas also apparently visited the successor cities of the Patrimony of Hyrkoon—Bayasabhad, Samyriana, and Kayakayanaya—as he said that there are no fiercer fighters in the world than their warrior women.[N 1] Lomas claimed that Leng is an island of "ten thousand tigers and ten million monkeys". In his The World of Ice & Fire, Maester Yandel is contradictory as to whether or not Lomas visited Asshai. In his chapter on the westerlands, Yandel writes that merchants in Asshai asked Lomas about the famous gold of Casterly Rock. In the book's section on Asshai and the Shadow Lands, however, Yandel writes that Lomas never reached the eastern city.

name: Lommy Greenhands
bio: Lommy Greenhands is one of the orphan boys Yoren recruited for the Night's Watch in King’s Landing and is part of the band marching north to the Wall. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is played by Eros Vlahos. Lommy was a dyer's apprentice before he was caught stealing.

name: Lomys
bio: Maester Lomys is a maester of the Citadel. He serves House Tyrell at Highgarden.

name: Longwater Pyke
bio: Longwater Pyke is an ironborn raider in Victarion_Greyjoy's crew aboard the Iron Victory. He is a bastard from the Iron Islands.

name: Longwaters
bio: Longwaters was a knight of great renown and son of Ser Jon_Waters, the bastard son of Princess Elaena_Targaryen and Lord Alyn_Velaryon. His given name is unknown. As a trueborn son, he added "Long" to his "Waters" surname to show he was not himself a bastard. His line survives today in Rennifer_Longwaters.

name: Lonnel Snow
bio: Lonnel Snow, known as Lonny, was a bastard son of Lord Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan) and Lady Wylla_Fenn. He had three younger half-siblings by his father; Rodwell_Stark, Beron_Stark and Arsa_Stark.

name: Loras Tyrell
bio: Loras Tyrell, also known as the Knight of Flowers, is a knight from House Tyrell and the third son of Lord Mace_Tyrell of Highgarden and his wife, Lady Alerie_Hightower. He is a highly skilled knight and jouster. His tournament successes, dazzling good looks, and ostentatious showmanship has made him a celebrated figure in the courts of the Seven Kingdoms. His personal coat of arms is three golden roses on a field of green, indicating his position as a third son. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Loras is portrayed by Finn Jones. Loras was born at Highgarden in 282 AC. He has two older brothers, Willas_Tyrell and Ser Garlan_Tyrell, and one younger sister, Margaery_Tyrell. Loras squired for Lord Renly_Baratheon at Storm's End, and grew close to him. According to a semi-canon source, he soon became Renly's lover. At the young age of fifteen, Loras received his knighthood. Loras purchased his armor from Tobho_Mott's shop in the Street of Steel in King's Landing. During the tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day in 298 AC, at the age of fifteen, Loras defeated Ser Hosteen_Frey and Ser Jaime_Lannister of the Kingsguard in the joust. Renly's brother, King Robert_I_Baratheon, won a Valyrian steel dagger from Loras's victory.

name: Lorcas
bio: Lorcas is an old acolyte of the Citadel. Lorcas has been an acolyte for fifty years, forging nine links in his maester's chain.

name: Lorcas the Learned
bio: Lorcas the Learned was a member of the Poor Fellows during the Faith Militant uprising. Following the death of Septon Moon_(septon), Lorcas became one of his would-be successors. He conflicted with Rob_the_Starvling over the possession of Septon Moon's corpse. Lorcas claimed to have had a vision that Moon would deliver Oldtown to his followers, even after his death. Lorcas seized Moon's body from Rob the Starvling, and strapped it atop a destrier (still naked, bloody, and rotting), to storm the gates of the city. Fewer than a hundred men joined the attack, and most were shot before they even got close to Oldtown's walls. Lorcas was among the few who did reach the walls, and died after being doused with boiling oil or set on fire with burning pitch.

name: Lord of Bones
bio: The Lord of Bones, also mocked as Rattleshirt, is a free folk leader of renown. His given name is unknown. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is portrayed by Edward Dogliani (Seasons 2 and 3) and Ross O'Hennessy (Season 5). Before Mance_Rayder made peace between them while becoming King-Beyond-the-Wall, Rattleshirt warred with Harma.

name: Lord Pigface
bio: "Lord Pigface" was the nickname Princess Aerea_Targaryen gave a member of Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I)'s court in Dragonstone.

name: Lordling
bio: "Lordling" is how Arya_Stark refers to a secret servant of the Many-Faced God in the House of Black and White in Braavos and a Faceless Man.

name: Loren I Lannister
bio: Loren I Lannister, called Loren the Last, was the last King of the Rock and the head of House Lannister at the time of Aegon's Conquest. He submitted to the rule of House Targaryen after surviving the Field of Fire, and became the first Lord of Casterly Rock and Warden of the West. Having taken up arms against Aegon the Conqueror alongside the King of the Reach, Loren I and King Mern_IX_Gardener are remembered collectively as the Two Kings.

name: Lorence Roxton
bio: Lorence Roxton was a knight from House Roxton and a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 49 AC, the young king Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen sought to fill five slots within his Kingsguard. At the wedding of his mother Queen Alyssa_Velaryon and Lord Rogar_Baratheon, Jaehaerys declared that the knights would fight on foot over a position in his Kingsguard. In the subsequent War for the White Cloaks in which hundreds of knights competed for the honor of serving in the Kingsguard, Ser Lorence Roxton was one of the five champions given the white cloak of the Kingsguard, being the only victor from a noble house.[N 1] Lorence and Ser Joffrey_Doggett protected Jaehaerys I when the king met with Rogar Baratheon, the Lord of Storm's End, after he had attempted to place Princess Rhaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon) on the throne. During the tourney celebrating the king's tenth anniversary of his coronation in 58 AC, Lorence was unhorsed by young Ser Ryam_Redwyne.

name: Lorent Caswell
bio: Lorent Caswell is the Lord of Bitterbridge and the head of House Caswell. Lorent became a page, a squire, and finally a knight in service to his father, Lord Caswell. Lorent had four sisters, but no brothers, so the other squires of Bitterbridge would barely lay a finger on him in the yard. One day, Lorent claimed a longsword that was a gift for the castle armorer's son, Rolly_Duckfield, who was a member of the Bitterbridge garrison, and told Rolly he was fit to hold a hammer, not a sword. Rolly then beat Lorent with a hammer until both arms and half his ribs were broken.

name: Lorent Grandison
bio: Lorent Grandison was the Lord of Grandview and the head of House Grandison. He was a regent for Aegon_III_Targaryen. During the Great Council of 136 AC, three regents were selected by lot to serve in the regency for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Lord Lorent Grandison was among the three regents selected, considered an inoffensive, though undistinguished, choice. When Lady Cassandra_Baratheon was found guilty of conspiring to poison Queen Daenaera_Velaryon, three bannermen of House Baratheon spoke for Storm's End at the trial. Through them and Lord Grandison, Lady Cassandra was arranged to marry Ser Walter_Brownhill, a minor landed knight. While the regents and Lord Torrhen_Manderly, the Hand of the King, were discussing King Aegon III's first royal progress, the young king entered the room and dismissed them all, for he had come of age.

name: Lorent Lorch
bio: Lorent Lorch is a landed knight and the head of House Lorch.

name: Lorent Marbrand
bio: Lorent Marbrand was a knight from House Marbrand, a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen, and a Lord Commander of the Queensguard of Rhaenyra_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Lorent is portrayed by Max Wrottesley. When the Dance of the Dragons began in 129 AC, Ser Lorent and Ser Erryk_Cargyll were on Dragonstone. They chose to side with Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen and the blacks, joining her Queensguard under Lord Commander Ser Steffon_Darklyn. Ser Steffon died during the Sowing of the Seeds, and Queen Rhaenyra named Lorent the Lord Commander of the Queensguard after she ascended to the Iron Throne following the fall of King's Landing. Ser Lorent was tasked with finding six worthy knights to serve beside him in Rhaenyra's Queensguard.[N 1] After the Treasons of Tumbleton by the Two Betrayers, Lorent was among those who counseled Queen Rhaenyra to arrest the other two baseborn dragonriders, Ser Addam_Velaryon and Nettles. Lorent died during the riot of King's Landing, leading a hundred knights and men-at-arms into Flea Bottom, of which only sixteen returned. According to Mushroom, Rhaenyra wept when she was told of the manner of Lorent's death. His body was retrieved by his sworn brother Ser Glendon_Goode, to prevent the rioters from despoiling it. Ser Glendon succeeded Lorent as Lord Commander, but less than a day later, the young knight also died in the riot.

name: Lorent Tyrell
bio: Lorent Tyrell is a member of House Tyrell, a distant cousin to the ruling branch. Lorent is the third son and youngest child of Ser Leo_Tyrell_(son_of_Victor) and Alys_Beesbury.

name: Lorent Tyrell
bio: Lorent Tyrell was a member of House Tyrell and a High Steward of the Reach during the reign of Mern_VI_Gardener, King of the Reach. Lorent succeeded his father, Ser Robert_Tyrell, as High Steward. He continued his father's and Osmund_Tyrell work in aiding his king, Mern_VI_Gardener, in the reconstruction of the Reach after the Dornish raid that had sacked Highgarden and killed the senile old king Garth_X_Gardener.

name: Loreon Lannister
bio: Loreon Lannister may refer to:

name: Loreon I Lannister
bio: Loreon Lannister, known as Loreon the Lion, was the first true King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock in historical record. Loreon made House Reyne of Castamere his vassals by wedding a daughter of that house. King Loreon fought and defeated Morgon_Banefort, the Hooded King, and his thralls, in a war that lasted twenty years. According to a semi-canon source, Morgon was said to have been a powerful necromancer who cursed his vanquishers as he died, claiming he would return from the grave. In response, Loreon had Morgon's body cut into pieces and fed to his lions. Two years later the lions broke free within Casterly Rock, slaying three of Loreon's sons.

name: Loreon II Lannister
bio: Loreon II Lannister was a King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Loreon held the first tourney ever seen in the Westerlands and defeated every knight that rode against him.

name: Loreon III Lannister
bio: Loreon III Lannister, known as Loreon the Limp, was a King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Loreon lost all of Lancel_I_Lannister's conquests, earning him his mocking epithet.

name: Loreon IV Lannister
bio: Loreon IV Lannister, known as Loreon the Lackwit, was a King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock. He was the grandfather of Loreon_V_Lannister.

name: Loreon V Lannister
bio: Loreon V Lannister, called Queen Lorea, was a King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock. He was the grandson of Loreon_IV_Lannister. Loreon V Lannister was a grandson of King Loreon_IV_Lannister, known as "the Lackwit". Loreon V was fond of dressing in his wife's clothing and wandering the docks of Lannisport in the guise of a common whore, for which he was dubbed "Queen Lorea". After the reigns of Loreon V and his grandsire, the name Loreon became noticeably less common among Lannister princes.

name: Loreth Lansdale
bio: Loreth Lansdale was a knight from House Lansdale and a member of Rhaenyra_Targaryen's Queensguard. During the Dance of the Dragons, Ser Loreth sided with the blacks. After Rhaenyra seized King's Landing, Ser Loreth was made one of her Queensguard. He was one of the Seven Who Rode, seven knights that went after Prince Joffrey_Velaryon when the prince mounted the dragon Syrax to fly to the Dragonpit, during the Riot of King's Landing. Ser Loreth was killed in defense of Rhaenyra by the men of Ser Alfred_Broome at Dragonstone. He managed to kill two of his opponents before falling. He was the last of Rhaenyra's Queensguard.

name: Loreza Sand
bio: Loreza Sand is the youngest bastard daughter of Prince Oberyn_Martell and his longtime paramour, Ellaria_Sand. She and her sisters are known as the Sand Snakes.

name: Lorimar Peake
bio: Lorimar Peake was the Lord of Starpike and Dunstonbury, and the head of House Peake during the reign of Perceon_III_Gardener, King of the Reach. Lord Lorimar drove House Manderly into exile at the behest of King Perceon_III_Gardener, who feared their swelling power in the Reach. This allowed House Peake to acquire the Manderly seat of Dunstonbury. Lorimar's daughter married Perceon's son, Gwayne_Gardener_(son_of_Perceon_III), and she became the seventh Peake maiden to become Queen of All the Reach.

name: Lorimas Mudd
bio: Lorimas Mudd is a sellsword in service to the Golden Company. As a serjeant, he is a high-ranking officer. Lorimas is a Westerosi exile. His surname Mudd was a house that once loomed large in the histories of the Seven Kingdoms. He may or may not be of House Mudd, as in the free companies a man can call himself whatever he chooses. He may be related to another serjeant in the company, Young_John_Mudd.

name: Lorimer the Belly
bio: Lorimer the Belly is a hedge knight in service to Lord Leo_Lefford.

name: Lormelle Long Lance
bio: Lormelle Long Lance was once defeated by Ser Barristan_Selmy. It is unknown if this was in combat or during a tourney.

name: Loron Greyjoy
bio: Loron Greyjoy, known as the Old Kraken, was a High King of the Iron Islands from House Greyjoy. Loron was chosen by a kingsmoot. He led an invasion of the western shores of the north, retaking Bear Island and Cape Kraken in the process. After the Old Kraken died, King Rodrik_Stark reclaimed Bear Island, while his sons and grandsons battled for Cape Kraken.

name: Loron Greyjoy
bio: Loron Greyjoy, known as the Bard, was a Lord of the Iron Islands and head of House Greyjoy. According to Archmaester Haereg's History of the Ironborn, Loron had a great and tragic friendship with Ser Desmond_Mallister.

name: Lorra Royce
bio: Lorra Royce was a noblewoman from House Royce who married Lord Beron_Stark, the second son of Lord Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan). Lorra and Beron had seven children: Donnor_Stark, Willam_Stark, Artos_Stark, Berena_Stark, Alysanne_Stark, Errold_Stark, and Rodrik_Stark_(son_of_Beron).

name: Black Lorren
bio: Black Lorren is a famous ironborn fighter. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Lorren is portrayed by Forbes KB.

name: Lorren Longaxe
bio: Lorren Longaxe is one of the crew on Asha_Greyjoy's ship, Black Wind.

name: Loso
bio: Loso, dubbed Loso the Lame, was a Dothraki khal who lived during the Century of Blood. He was one of the four khals that fought in the Field of Crows. Khal Loso joined forces with Khals Haro, Qano, and Zhako to fight against High King Mazor_Alexi, who had united all of the Sarnori lesser kings to defend their homeland against the Dothraki hordes. The battle took place halfway between Sarnath and the ruins of Kasath. The Sarnori chariots smashed the center of the Dothraki horde. Haro was trampled and died, while his riders fled, chased by Alexi and his cavalry. However, the escape was feigned and Haro's riders turned back, raining arrows on the Sarnori, while the other khals surrounded the Tall Men on all sides, with Loso's khalasar attacking the rear. Less than a fortnight later, Loso sacked Sarnath and put the city to the torch. Not even the Palace With a Thousand Rooms was spared.

name: Lothar
bio: Lothar was the maester at Whitewalls during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. During the wedding tourney at Whitewalls, Lothar took care of the parade of wounded, among them Ser Duncan_the_Tall. While in his care he let Duncan know of his disdain for the martial arts. After Duncan lost his armor and horse to Ser Uthor_Underleaf, Duncan's squire, Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen, went to Lothar and showed him the dragon ring that had been given to him by his father, Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen, hoping to enlist Lothar's help in sending a raven for help. However, Lothar delivered the Targaryen prince to Lord Ambrose_Butterwell upon seeing the ring.

name: Lothar Bracken
bio: Lothar Bracken was a Lord of the Stone Hedge and head of House Bracken. Harwyn_Hoare, King of the Iron Islands, invaded the northern riverlands when they were under the rule of the Storm King Arrec_Durrandon. Lothar's neighbour, Lady Agnes_Blackwood, led the rivermen in resisting Harwyn's ironborn, but Lord Lothar attacked her army from behind. Some have theorized that Lothar hoped to become King of the Trident once Harwyn defeated Arrec. However, Harwyn claimed the riverlands for House Hoare. Six months later, Lothar rebelled against this new King of the Isles and the Rivers, but Harwyn crushed the rebellion, sacking and destroying Stone Hedge, and imprisoning Lothar in a crow cage. Lothar died of starvation most of a year later.

name: Lothar Frey
bio: Lothar Frey, often called Lame Lothar, is a member of House Frey, the twelfth son of Lord Walder_Frey, and his eldest son by his fourth wife Lady Alyssa_Blackwood. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Lothar is portrayed by Tom Brooke (Season 3) and Daniel Tuite (Season 6). Lothar has served as his father's steward at the Twins for twelve years. He married Leonella_Lefford, and they have four daughters together: Tysane_Frey, Walda_Frey_(daughter_of_Lothar), Emberlei_Frey, and Leana_Frey.

name: Lothar Mallery
bio: Lothar Mallery is the lord and the head of House Mallery.

name: Lotho Lornel
bio: Lotho Lornel is a dealer in old books and scrolls in Braavos.

name: Lotho Rogare
bio: Lotho Rogare was a member of House Rogare, a Lysene noble banking family. He was the son of Lysandro_Rogare, the First Magister of Lys. Lotho was the son of Lysandro Rogare. He had five trueborn brothers: Lysaro_Rogare, Moredo_Rogare, Drako_Rogare, Fredo_Rogare, and Roggerio_Rogare; and three trueborn sisters: Larra_Rogare, Lysara_Rogare, and Marra_Rogare, as well as sixteen bastard siblings. When his sister Larra came to King's Landing as the wife of Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, Lotho, Moredo, and Roggerio came with her. Lotho and Rogerrio were fluent in the Common tongue, although Moredo was not. Lotho set about establishing a branch of the Rogare Bank of Lys atop Visenya's Hill. With the Rogares growing in power late in 134 AC, it was feared by some that they would soon rule Westeros as well, though Archmaester Gyldayn notes that suspicion was largely caused from them being foreign. However, it is known that Lotho used gold to buy friends at court. After Lotho's father Lysandro_Rogare drowned, his brother Lysaro_Rogare inherited the Rogare Bank, quickly running it dry with debt and embezzlement. Lotho attempted to depart King's Landing before he could be punished, but was arrested by the Commander of the City Watch, Ser Lucas_Leygood. Because of Lotho's relationship to Prince Viserys's wife, he was imprisoned in the Tower of the Hand rather than the black cells. After the events of the secret siege, Lotho was tried, accused of fraud and theft, and found guilty. Lord Torrhen_Manderly gave him the chance of joining the Night's Watch or losing his right hand like a common thief; as Lotho was left-handed, he chose the latter. He was then taken up by Lady Samantha_Tarly, paramour of Lord Lyonel_Hightower, who had him establish the Bank of Oldtown.

name: Lothor Brune
bio: Lothor Brune is a freerider. He is related to the Brunes of Brownhollow in the Crackclaw Point region of the crownlands. Lothor is of low birth and is a distant, poorer cousin to House Brune. After his father's death, Lothor went to the Brunes of Brownhollow in an attempt to form a family bond, but they said he was no kin of theirs. He also states that he learned what he knows of arms the hard way.

name: Lothor Burley
bio: Lothor Burley was Lord Commander of the Night's Watch during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He was originally from House Burley. Lothor hosted Alysanne_Targaryen when the queen visited Castle Black in 58 AC. Lord Alaric_Stark of Winterfell had sent word to the Night's Watch beforehand, so Lothor honored Alysanne with eight hundred black brothers when she arrived at the Wall. The lord commander served mammoth, mead, and stout at the welcoming feast. Burley considered his accommodations to be unbecoming of a queen, but Alysanne gracefully praised his efforts. Alysanne suggested to Lothor that the Nightfort, the oldest and largest castle at the Wall, which was too costly to maintain, be replaced with a smaller castle nearby. She used her own jewels (including her own crown) to finance the construction of the castle, which was named Deep Lake. The queen also persuaded Burley to escort her to Mole's Town, where she held one of her women's courts amongst the prostitutes who lived there.

name: Loyal Spear
bio: Loyal Spear is an Unsullied sworn to Daenerys_Targaryen.

name: Lucamore Strong
bio: Lucamore Strong, also known as Lucamore the Lusty, was a knight from House Strong and a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. When it was discovered he had broken the vows of the Kingsguard, he was sent to the Night's Watch by King Jaehaerys I. In 55 AC, a great tourney was held to celebrate the completion of the Dragonpit. Ser Lucamore was the victor of the great melee in the pit. After the death of Ser Willam_the_Wasp the following year, Jaehaerys awarded a white cloak to Ser Lucamore. Ser Lucamore was on duty when Balerion returned to King's Landing in 56 AC with the afflicted Princess Aerea_Targaryen barely clinging to him. Lucamore shoved through the onlookers and carried the girl to Grand Maester Benifer. He later told anyone who'd listen that the princess was stick thin, so hot with fever he could feel the heat through his armor, there was blood in her eyes and something was moving beneath her skin. He was summoned by King Jaehaerys the next day and commanded not to speak of Aerea anymore. After Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), who had ruled as de facto Lady of Harrenhal, died in 73 AC, King Jaehaerys named Lucamore's brother, Ser Bywin_Strong, the new Lord of Harrenhal. It was discovered by Ser Ryam_Redwyne of the Kingsguard near the end of 73 AC that Ser Lucamore had broken his Kingsguard vows: he had wed in secret, not once, but thrice, with each woman being ignorant of the other two. On these three wives, he had fathered a total of sixteen children. Ser Ryam brought the revelation to the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, Ser Gyles_Morrigen, who in turn had Ser Lucamore seized and brought before the Iron Throne, along with his three wives and all his children. This scandal shocked both the court and city, although it was eventually a cause of great laughter and merriment among the smallfolk. Ser Gyles requested that Ser Lucamore be put to death. In front of the king, Lucamore fell to his knees, confessed his guilt, and begged for mercy. According to Septon Barth, the king might have granted it, had Lucamore not been foolish enough to add "for the sake of my wives and children" to the end of his plea, as this was tantamount to throwing his crimes in the king's face. Jaehaerys declared he would have no oathbreakers serving him, and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen added that Lucamore had not only broken the vows of the Kingsguard, but also his marriage vows. None of the three marriages could be declared lawful, and thus all the children were bastards. Alysanne also declared that his wives were not entirely innocent: while each woman had been unaware of the other two, they had known that Lucamore was a knight of the Kingsguard and thus not allowed to wed. She declared some mercy would be granted for them, but none for him. Ser Lucamore was gelded by his former sworn brothers of the Kingsguard, after which King Jaehaerys sent Lucamore to the Wall to join the Night's Watch. Two of his elder sons chose to accompany him. A daughter chose to become a septa. His three wives were each sent into exile away from King's Landing, the first given to the charge of Lord Bywin Strong of Harrenhal, Lucamore's brother in the riverlands, the second to Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan) of Driftmark in the crownlands, and the third wife was sent to Ser Garon_Baratheon and Lord Boremund_Baratheon of Storm's End in the stormlands, along with their children, who were forbidden to continue using the surname Strong from that day forth. The children of his first wife were known as Rivers, the children of his second as Waters, and the children of his third as Storm. Lucamore forever more became known as Lucamore the Lusty. His deceits are recorded in the White Book and are sung about in a humorous song.

name: Lucan
bio: Lucan is a blacksmith at Harrenhal. He is master at the armory.

name: Lucan (Grand Maester)
bio: Lucan was a Grand Maester of the Citadel who served on the small council under an unknown Targaryen King. By the end of the First Dornish War, King Aegon_I_Targaryen was weary of war, but according to Grand Maester Lucan he did not want to grant the Dornishmen peace without submission as he did not want his beloved sister Queen Rhaenys_Targaryen to have died in vain. Moreover, the lords of Aegon's small council further cautioned that any such peace could be seen as a sign of weakness and might encourage fresh rebellions, which would then need to be put down. For all these reasons, Grand Maester Lucan wrote later, Aegon was on the point of refusing the Dornish proposals and continuing the war. However, King Aegon changed his mind and granted peace to the Dornish when Princess Deria_Martell came to King's Landing with a letter from Nymor_Martell.

name: Lucantine Woodwright
bio: Lucantine Woodwright is a knight of House Woodwright. He is the brother of Ser Portifer_Woodwright and is a household knight in King's Landing. Taena of Myr reported that Ser Lucantine is often among the group of household knights which seek Queen Margaery_Tyrell's attention.

name: Lucas Blackwood
bio: Lucas Blackwood is the second son of Lord Tytos_Blackwood of Raventree Hall.

name: Lucas Codd
bio: Lucas Codd, known as Left-Hand Lucas Codd, is an ironborn raider and member of House Codd.

name: Lucas Corbray
bio: Lucas Corbray is a knight of House Corbray. He is the younger brother of Lord Lyonel_Corbray and Ser Lyn_Corbray.

name: Lucas Harroway
bio: Lucas Harroway was the Lord of Harrenhal and the head of House Harroway during the reigns of kings Aenys_I_Targaryen and Maegor_I_Targaryen. He served as Hand of the King for Maegor I, his son-by-marriage. During the reign of King Aenys_I_Targaryen, Lucas was named the Lord of Harrenhal after the violent death of Lord Gargon_Qoherys in 37 AC. In 39 AC, he saw his daughter Lady Alys_Harroway wed to Prince Maegor_I_Targaryen in a Valyrian wedding ceremony on Dragonstone. As Maegor was already married to Lady Ceryse_Hightower, the High_Septon_(Aenys_I) denounced the marriage as "sin and fornication" and condemning Alys as the "this whore of Harroway". Pious lords across the realm began to turn against the crown and in a fruitless effort to pacify the Faith, Aenys exiled Maegor and Alys to Pentos. Aenys further exacerbated the situation in 41 AC by incestuously wedding his daughter Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), to his son and heir, Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I). The young couple embarked on a royal progress across the realm and upon reaching Harrenhal, Lord Lucas refused to admit them to his castle unless they agreed to acknowledge his daughter as Maegor's true and lawful wife. Aegon and Rhaena refused and were forced to spend a cold night in tents beneath Harrenhal. Aenys died the following year and Maegor claimed the Iron Throne, making Alys queen. In 43 AC, Lucas was named Hand of the King by Maegor, but it was said that Lucas did not have the ear of the king, who was instead ruled by Alys and his third wife, Queen Tyanna_of_the_Tower, and his mother, Queen Dowager Visenya_Targaryen. When Prince Aegon attempted to claim the crown from his uncle, Lucas raised his levies from Harrenhal and joined Lord Tully in support of Maegor, advancing on Prince Aegon's host from the north. In the resulting Battle Beneath the Gods Eye, Aegon was slain by Maegor, securing the king's place on the Iron Throne. While Maegor focused on the completion of the construction of the Red Keep, Lucas was left to govern the realm for a while. In 44 AC, after Alys had a stillborn, monstrously deformed baby, Queen Tyanna convinced Maegor that the boy had not been of his seed, but the result of one of Alys's many affairs, and that Lord Lucas himself had helped his daughter conceive a son for the king by sending men of proven fertility to her chambers. Tyanna provided the names of the men she had supposedly been sleeping with, with one man found with gold paid to him by Lord Harroway. The questioning and torture was done in secret and swiftly so the Harroways had no inkling of the king's suspicions until the Kingsguard was dispatched to seize the Harroways. Alys was taken and tortured to death, while Lord Harroway's children, brothers, and nephews at King's Landing were executed. Lucas himself was thrown from the roof of the Tower of the Hand by knights of the Kingsguard, while he was inspecting the tower's construction.[N 1] Maegor then marched upon Harrenhal, held by his unsuspecting nephew, and Lord Harroway's Town, and executed every man, woman, and child with a drop of Harroway blood, thus completing the destruction of House Harroway.

name: Lucas Inchfield
bio: Lucas Inchfield, known as Lucas Longinch, was a knight from House Inchfield and was the castellan of Coldmoat for Lady Rohanne_Webber. Lucas served Wyman_Webber, Lord of Coldmoat. The two men laughed after Ser Eustace_Osgrey, Knight of Standfast, asked Wyman about betrothing their children, Rohanne_Webber and Addam_Osgrey. On his deathbed, Wyman tasked Lucas with protecting Rohanne from unworthy suitors. Lucas remained as castellan of Coldmoat after Wyman's passing. Rohanne worried that Lucas would attempt to marry her by force. When Eustace and Lady Rohanne feuded over the Chequy Water, Ser Lucas informed Eustace about the death of Dake. Lucas was present in the yard at Coldmoat when Ser Duncan_the_Tall arrived to discuss terms with Rohanne, and the castellan took an immediate dislike to the taller hedge knight. Lucas presented Duncan to Lady Helicent_Uffering, letting him believe that it was Rohanne he was talking to and making a fool out of Duncan. He was dismissed by Rohanne and told to find Maester Cerrick. Lucas commanded the force brought by Rohanne to the Chequy Water to take Ser Bennis by force. He championed House Webber in the trial by combat, expecting that he would marry Rohanne after defeating Duncan. The Longinch was stabbed and drowned in the Chequy Water by the hedge knight in their duel, however.

name: Lucas Leygood
bio: Lucas Leygood was a knight from House Leygood who served as Commander of the City Watch during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Lucas was the son of the Leygood_(father_of_Lucas) during the Dance of the Dragons, one of the Caltrops who died at Second Tumbleton. Lord Unwin_Peake, a fellow Caltrops, named Lucas Commander of the City Watch when he became Hand of the King in 133 AC. To replace the men who had died during the Winter Fever and the Moon of Madness, the Hand bestowed gold cloaks on five hundred of his own men. Lucas remained as Commander of the City Watch after Lord Peake resigned as Hand in 134 AC. After Lotho_Rogare attempted to depart King's Landing after the Rogare Bank ran dry, he was arrested by Lucas and his gold cloaks. Lucas supported the attempted coup by Ser Marston_Waters, the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard. Lucas and his gold cloaks of over two thousand men kept a firm grip on King's Landing. When Lord George_Graceford was questioned about this, he named Lucas a conspirator, along with six of the seven captains of the city gates. When men came for him, a short, savage battle was fought at the Gate of the Gods, leaving nine dead, Lucas among them.

name: Lucas Lothston
bio: Lucas Lothston was the Lord of Harrenhal and the head of House Lothston during the reign of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen. He served Aegon as Hand of the King. Ser Lucas was the grandson of a hedge knight. In 136 AC, he was appointed the new master-at-arms of the Red Keep by Lord Torrhen_Manderly, the Hand of the King, replacing the harsh and disgraced Ser Gareth_Long. The patient Lucas became a favorite of Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, and the knight won grudging respect from the prince's solemn elder brother, King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 149 AC, Falena_Stokeworth became the first mistress of Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen, making him "a man" when the prince was fourteen. When a knight of the Kingsguard found them abed together in 151 AC, his father, Prince Viserys, desired to remove Falena from court at King's Landing. Viserys arranged for Falena to marry Lucas, and convinced King Aegon III to gift Lucas with Harrenhal, as the previous holders of the castle, House Strong, had died out as a result of the Dance of the Dragons. House Lothston became the sixth noble house to rule as Lords of Harrenhal. Falena gave birth to a daughter, Jeyne_Lothston, in 164 AC. Although Prince Aegon did not have any acknowledged children with Falena, some suspected Jeyne's father was the prince, not Lucas. In 172 AC, Prince Aegon succeeded to the Iron Throne as King Aegon IV. Falena brought her fourteen-year-old daughter to court in 178 AC. The king took Jeyne as his eighth mistress, and named Lord Lucas as Hand of the King. It was rumored but not proven that King Aegon enjoyed mother and daughter together in the same bed. This possibly resulted in Lucas receiving the derogatory title "The Pander".[N 1] However, Aegon soon gave Jeyne a pox, and Lucas was banished from court along with his wife and daughter, having served less than a year as Hand.

name: Lucas Lothston
bio: Lucas Lothston, also known as the Pander, was a Lord of Harrenhal and head of House Lothston. He is remembered for bad deeds and for fathering Manfryd_Lothston.

name: Lucas Nayland
bio: Lucas Nayland was a knight from House Nayland during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. In 212 AC, Lucas came from Hag's Mire to Whitewalls to attend the wedding of Lord Ambrose_Butterwell and the Lady_Frey_(wife_of_Ambrose_Butterwell) of Lord_Frey. He participated in the wedding tourney, and was considered one of the better jousters. During the event, he also played a drinking game with Ser Kirby_Pimm.

name: Lucas Roote
bio: Lucas Roote is a member of House Roote. He is squiring for Ser Andrey_Charlton.

name: Lucas Tyrell
bio: Lucas Tyrell is a member of House Tyrell, a distant cousin of the ruling branch. He is the son of Ser Leo_Tyrell_(son_of_Victor) and Alys_Beesbury.

name: Luceon Frey
bio: Luceon, born Luceon Frey, is a septon of the Faith of the Seven. He serves at the Great Sept of Baelor and is among the Most Devout. He is the fifth son of Lord Walder_Frey and the second son born of Walder's second wife, Cyrenna_Swann.

name: Luceon of Tarth
bio: Luceon, better known as Luceon of Tarth, was a singer from the island of Tarth. Long after the Dance of the Dragons, Luceon composed a sad ballad called “Farewell, My Brother”, still sung today. The song purports to relate the last meeting between Ser Arryk_Cargyll and Ser Erryk_Cargyll, twin brothers and knights of the Kingsguard. During the Dance of the Dragons, Ser Arryk had sworn his sword to King Aegon_II_Targaryen, but Ser Erryk sided with his rival, Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen. Arryk was in the retinue of Grand Maester Orwyle when he traveled from King's Landing to Dragonstone in an unsuccessful attempt to reconcile the rival Targaryens. In the song, before Arryk boards the ship to return to King's Landing, the brothers try to persuade each other to change sides. When this attempt fails, they exchange declarations of love and part, knowing that when they meet next, it will be as enemies. It is possible that such a farewell did indeed take place that day on Dragonstone, but no historical records mention it.

name: Luceon Templeton
bio: Luceon Templeton was an Andal knight, the Knight of Ninestars and the head of House Templeton during the latter stage of the Andal invasion in the Vale. Luceon fought in the Battle of Seven Stars beneath the banner of Ser Artys Arryn. He is one of the candidates for the man who slew King Robar_II_Royce.

name: Lucerys Velaryon
bio: Prince Lucerys Velaryon, also known as Luke, was the secondborn son of Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen and her first husband, Ser Laenor_Velaryon. He was a dragonrider whose dragon was Arrax. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Lucerys is portrayed by Elliot Grihault and Harvey Sadler (young).

name: Lucerys Velaryon
bio: Lucerys Velaryon was the Lord of the Tides and head of House Velaryon during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen, serving on the small council as the king's master of ships. Serving on the small council as the master of ships, Lucerys was a strong supporter of King Aerys_II_Targaryen during the Year of the False Spring. Called "lickspittle lords" by Maester Yandel, Lucerys and the lords of the small council gained much from the king's madness, eagerly seizing opportunities to speak ill of Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen and inflaming the king's suspicion against his son. In return for Lucerys's support, King Aerys II showered him and his other supporters with honors, offices, and lands. The fate of Lucerys during Robert's Rebellion is as yet unknown, although the royal fleet of House Targaryen, anchored at Dragonstone, was destroyed by a great storm when Princess Daenerys_Targaryen was born.

name: Lucias Vypren
bio: Lucias Vypren is the Lord and the head of House Vypren. He is married to Lythene_Frey, the second daughter of Lord Walder_Frey. They have two children together: Elyana_Vypren and Damon_Vypren.

name: Lucifer Dryland
bio: Lucifer Dryland was a King of the Brimstone and Lord of Hellgate Hall. The last of House Dryland, he was one of the six kings sent to the Wall by Nymeria throughout Nymeria's War.

name: Lucifer Hardy
bio: Lucifer Hardy was a lord and head of House Hardy, ruling at an unknown time. Lucifer was a champion of Crackclaw Point. He is remembered for bringing peace to Crackclaw Point, though it did not survive him long.

name: Lucifer Justman
bio: Lucifer Justman, known as Lucifer the Liar, was a presumed member of House Justman living four centuries prior to Aegon's Conquest. Lucifer claimed the title of river king or King of the Trident in the generations after the riverlands were conquered by the Storm King Arlan_III_Durrandon. Lucifer had a brief, inglorious reign before being put down by the power of Storm's End. His connection with the extinct Justman dynasty is unconfirmed.

name: Lucifer Long
bio: Lucifer Long is a Westerosi knight and sellsword and is a member of the Windblown.

name: Lucifer Massey
bio: Lucifer Massey was the Lord of Stonedance and the head of House Massey during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Lord Lucifer was present at Visenya's Hill when King Maegor was challenged by Ser Damon_Morrigen, Grand Captain of the Warrior's Sons, to a trial of seven. He initially kept quiet when Maegor called upon those who would stand beside him. But he was shamed by the example of Dick_Bean, a simple man-at-arms, and the appeal of Ser Bernarr_Brune, and became one of the king's champions. According to several chroniclers, Lord Lucifer hacked off an arm of Ser Harys_Horpe, one of the Warrior's Sons. According to one of these accounts, Massey was killed after Horpe tossed his battle-axe into the other hand and buried it between Lord Massey's eyes.

name: Lucimore Botley
bio: Lucimore Botley is the half-brother of Lord Sawane_Botley.

name: Lucinda Broome
bio: Lucinda Tully, née Broome, was a lady of House Broome who wed Prentys_Tully, Lord of Riverrun. Lucinda's husband, Lord Prentys_Tully of Riverrun, was one of the first great lords to abandon King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Although the Faith Militant had been outlawed by the Iron Throne, Ser Joffrey_Doggett roamed the riverlands and the westerlands with the support of the pious Lucinda. After the death of Maegor the Cruel, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen refused the request of Lucinda and Joffrey to reinstate the Faith Militant. When Prentys was called to serve as the master of laws and lord justiciar for Jaehaerys in 49 AC, Lucinda accompanied her husband to King's Landing. Lord Tully suggested that the king wed his wife's younger sister, Ella_Broome, but Jaehaerys instead married his own sister, Alysanne_Targaryen. Dowager Queen Alyssa_Velaryon sent Lucinda to take charge of Alysanne's household at Dragonstone. Rogar_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End, wanted Lucinda, one of the so-called Wise Women, to report to him about the royal couple's behavior. When Jaehaerys reached his majority in 50 AC, he dismissed Prentys from the small council, so Lucinda and Prentys returned to Riverrun. After the resignation of Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan) as Hand of the King in 54 AC, Lucinda and Prentys supported his replacement by Myles_Smallwood, Lord of Acorn Hall. Lucinda and her husband died from the Shivers a day apart from each other at Riverrun in 59 AC.

name: Lucinda Penrose
bio: Lucinda Penrose was a noblewoman from House Penrose during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. She was a companion to Queen Daenaera_Velaryon. In 133 AC, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Many maidens across Westeros came to King's Landing in hopes of marrying the young king. Lucinda was one of the many maidens who suffered what was called the "Maiden's Day curse" by being injured or scandalized after the ball was announced. In Lucinda's case, she was set upon by outlaws whilst hawking along Blackwater Bay, not half a day's ride from the castle. Her hawk was killed, her horse was stolen, and a man held her down while another slit her nose open. It is possible that the attack was engineered by Lord Unwin_Peake, the Hand of the King, as it was heavily rumored that he set to ruin various maidens to promote his Myrielle_Peake as Aegon III's second queen. After the king married Lady Daenaera_Velaryon, Lucinda became a lady companion to the new queen. After a poisoning attempt on the king and queen, Lucinda admitted to wanting Daenaera dead, since if her nose had not been slit, she would be queen and Daenaera would be serving her. Lucinda lamented that no man wished to marry her. Lucinda was deemed to have only played a minor role in the plot, and on account of her sex, Lord Torrhen_Manderly gave her a minor punishment. She was condemned to have her nose cut off, with the understanding that the punishment would be stayed if she gave herself to the Faith, so long as she remained true to her vows.

name: Lucion Lannister
bio: Lucion Lannister is a knight of House Lannister. He is the son of Ser Damion_Lannister and Shiera_Crakehall.

name: Luco Prestayn
bio: Luco Prestayn is a member of House Prestayn of Braavos and is the captain of the trading galley Lady Bright.

name: Lucos
bio: Lucos is a septon of the Faith of the Seven. He is currently assigned to Runestone.

name: Lucos Chyttering
bio: Lucos Chyttering, called Little Lucos, is the heir of House Chyttering.

name: Likely Luke
bio: Likely Luke is a member of the Brotherhood without Banners.

name: Luke of Longtown
bio: Luke of Longtown is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Lum
bio: Lum is a guard in service to House Lannister.

name: Luthor Largent
bio: Luthor Largent was a lowborn knight who served as an officer and later the commander of the City Watch of King's Landing during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen and the Dance of the Dragons. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Luthor is portrayed by Tom Cullen. During the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen, Luthor was a captain of the City Watch commanding one of the seven city gates. He was promoted to the position of Commander in 129 AC, as he was seen by the green council as the more fearsome of the officers sympathetic to Prince Aegon_II_Targaryen's cause. The Hand of the King, Ser Otto_Hightower, named his son, Ser Gwayne_Hightower, as Luthor's second to keep an eye on him. During the fall of King's Landing, Luthor and his gold cloaks turned on the greens, as they were loyal to Prince Daemon_Targaryen, their former commander. Luthor personally killed his second-in-command, Gwayne. As a reward for his loyalty, Luthor was ennobled by Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen, forming House Largent. When Queen Helaena_Targaryen committed suicide, many of the smallfolk believed that Luthor killed her under the orders of Rhaenyra. Archmaester Gyldayn dismisses the idea, for there was proof Luthor was eating with three hundred gold cloaks at the barracks by the Gate of the Gods at the time of her death. During the riots in the city, Luthor took four hundred spears to disperse sailors attacking the River Gate in an attempt to return to their ships. Then he led five hundred gold cloaks down Cobbler's Square to disperse the thousands gathered around the Shepherd. Some say the first victim of the confrontation was a little girl trodden under Luthor's warhorse. When Luthor ordered his men to apprehend the escaping Shepherd, the crowd attacked the guardsmen. The gold cloaks, despite being well-armed and well-disciplined, were no match against ten thousand rioters. Luthor was pulled down from his armored warhorse, stabbed in the stomach, and bludgeoned to death. When his body was recovered by the corpse wagons the next day, his head had been so badly crushed that his body was only recognized by its size. The Captain of the Old Gate, Ser Balon Byrch, succeeded Luthor as Commander of the City Watch.

name: Luthor Tyrell
bio: Luthor Tyrell was the Lord of Highgarden and the head of House Tyrell. He is the father of the current lord, Mace_Tyrell. Luthor was betrothed to Princess Shaera_Targaryen in 237 AC when he was the heir to Highgarden, but the pact was broken in 240 AC when she married her brother, Prince Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen. He later married Olenna_Redwyne, who gave him three children, and at some point became the Lord of Highgarden. Luthor rode off a cliff to his death while hawking. He had been looking up at the sky, and not paying attention to where his horse was going. After his death he was succeeded by his only son, Mace Tyrell.

name: Luthor Tyrell
bio: Luthor Tyrell was a knight of House Tyrell, a first cousin of Mace_Tyrell, the Lord of Highgarden. He is the first son of Ser Moryn_Tyrell, the commander of Oldtown's City Watch. Luthor was married to Elyn_Norridge, with whom he had three children, Ser Theodore_Tyrell, Olene_Tyrell, and Medwick_Tyrell. He is deceased.

name: Luthor Tyrell
bio: Luthor Tyrell is a member of a junior branch of House Tyrell, being the grandson of Ser Luthor_Tyrell_(son_of_Moryn), a first cousin of Mace_Tyrell, the Lord of Highgarden. The only son of Ser Theodore_Tyrell and his wife, Lia_Serry, Luthor has an older sister, Elinor_Tyrell.

name: Luton
bio: Luton is a man-at-arms sworn to House Bolton. He is one of the Bastard's Boys.

name: Luwin
bio: Luwin is a maester in the service of House Stark at Winterfell. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Luwin is portrayed by Donald Sumpter. Presumably the replacement at Winterfell for Maester Walys, Luwin delivered all of the children of Lord Eddard_Stark and Lady Catelyn_Stark: Robb_Stark (who was born at Riverrun), Sansa_Stark, Bran_Stark, Arya_Stark, and Rickon_Stark. Luwin educated the Stark children, as well as Eddard's bastard, Jon_Snow, and Eddard's ward, Theon_Greyjoy. For instance, he taught about maesters and stars to Jon, the history of the Night's Watch to Bran, and about the historical lords of Winterfell and the beliefs of the Dothraki to Theon. Luwin also treated Arya's cuts and scrapes. Luwin once made a boy out of pottery and threw it from Winterfell's walls to show Bran what would happen if the boy were to fall while climbing, but Bran ignored the warning.

name: Lyanna Mormont
bio: Lyanna Mormont is the youngest daughter of Lady Maege_Mormont of Bear Island. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Lyanna is portrayed by Bella Ramsey.

name: Lyanna Stark
bio: Lyanna Stark was the only daughter of Lord Rickard_Stark and his wife, Lady Lyarra_Stark. She had two older brothers, Brandon_Stark and Eddard_Stark, and one younger brother, Benjen_Stark. Her abduction by Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen ultimately triggered Robert's Rebellion and led to the downfall of the Targaryen dynasty. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Lyanna is portrayed by both Cordelia Hill (as a child) in Season 6 and Aisling Franciosi (as a young adult) during Season 6 and Season 7. Born at Winterfell, Lyanna was the only daughter of Lord Rickard_Stark and Lady Lyarra_Stark. She had two older brothers, Brandon_Stark and Eddard_Stark, and one younger brother, Benjen_Stark. Eddard was fostered at the Eyrie by Lord Jon_Arryn since the age of eight, where he befriended his fellow ward, Robert_I_Baratheon. Eddard brought Robert's suit to Winterfell, and Rickard agreed to betroth his daughter to the young Lord of Storm's End. However, that night Lyanna confided to Eddard that she believed Robert would never be able to keep to one bed. She had heard that Robert had Mya_Stone in the Vale of Arryn, which Eddard could not deny. He attempted to persuade her that what Robert had done before their betrothal was insignificant and that he would love her, but Lyanna informed him that love could not change a man's nature.

name: Lyanna Stark
bio: Lyanna Stark was a member of House Stark. She was the only daughter of Lord Cregan_Stark and his third and last wife, Lady Lynara_Stark.

name: Lyanne Glover
bio: Lyanne Glover was a noblewoman from House Glover during the early part of the third century after Aegon's Conquest. Lyanne married Willam_Stark, the Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North. She gave him a son, Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Willam), but died during his birth. After Lyanne's death, Lord Willam remarried to Melantha_Blackwood.

name: Lyarra Stark
bio: Lyarra Stark was a noblewoman from House Stark during the reign of Kings Aegon_V_Targaryen, Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen and Aerys_II_Targaryen. She was a daughter of Rodrik_Stark_(son_of_Beron) and Arya_Flint, and was the younger sister of Branda_Stark. Lyarra married her first cousin once removed, Rickard_Stark, the Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North. Together they had four children: Brandon_Stark, Eddard_Stark, Lyanna_Stark, and Benjen_Stark.

name: Lyessa Flint
bio: Lyessa Flint is the Lady of Widow's Watch and the head of House Flint of Widow's Watch. She is the mother of Robin_Flint.

name: Lyle Bracken
bio: Lyle Bracken was a knight of House Bracken and a member of the Warrior's Sons at the start of the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. In 42 AC, Ser Lyle was stationed at the King's Landing chapter of the Warrior's Sons, based in the Sept of Remembrance. He fought in the trial of seven in which seven of the Warrior's Sons faced King Maegor_I_Targaryen and his six champions. Ser Lyle died during the fight.

name: Lyle Crakehall
bio: Lyle Crakehall, better known as Strongboar, is a knight of House Crakehall. He is the middle son of Lord Roland Crakehall. At the tourney at Lannisport celebrating the victory of King Robert_I_Baratheon over Balon_Greyjoy and his rebellion, Ser Lyle was unhorsed by the eventual champion, Lord Jorah_Mormont.

name: Lyman Beesbury
bio: Lyman Beesbury was the Lord of Honeyholt and the head of House Beesbury during the reigns of kings Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and his grandson, Viserys_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Lyman is portrayed by Bill Paterson. Lyman served as master of coin and lord treasurer almost uninterrupted since the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen until the end of King Viserys_I_Targaryen's reign. Lyman was a man of eighty when King Viserys I passed away prior to the Dance of the Dragons. When the small council gathered in the chambers of Queen Alicent_Hightower, Lyman was the only one to espouse the claim of Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, Viserys's daughter and preferred heir, over the claim of Prince Aegon_II_Targaryen, Alicent's eldest son. According to Grand Maester Orwyle, who was present in the chamber, Ser Otto_Hightower, the Hand of the King, had Lyman arrested and sent to the black cells, where he eventually died of a chill. In contrast, Septon Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons) claimed that Ser Criston_Cole, the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, slit Lyman's throat with his dagger. Court fool Mushroom alleges that Criston instead tossed Lyman from the window onto the spikes of the Maegor's Holdfast's moat. Eustace and Mushroom were not present, however. Lyman was succeeded as master of coin by Ser Tyland_Lannister. Lyman's death was kept secret, with most believing he was languishing in a black cell. Lyman's heir, Ser Alan_Beesbury, loudly demanded the release of his grandsire.

name: Lyman
bio: Lyman was an archmaester of the Citadel and the author of Kingdoms of the Sky. Grand Maester Munkun presented King Aegon_III_Targaryen with Lyman's book Kingdoms of the Sky but the king showed no interest.

name: Lyman Darry
bio: Lyman Darry is a nobleman of House Darry, the son of Ser Raymun_Darry and the grandson of Lord_Darry_(father_of_Raymun).[N 1]

name: Lyman Lannister
bio: Lyman Lannister was the Lord of Casterly Rock, Warden of the West, Shield of Lannisport, and head of House Lannister during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen and the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He was married to Lady Jocasta_Lannister.

name: Lymond Fisher
bio: Lymond Fisher was a Knight of Oldstones four centuries prior to Aegon's Conquest. Lymond claimed the title of river king or King of the Trident in the generations after the riverlands were conquered by the Storm King Arlan_III_Durrandon. Lymond had a brief, inglorious reign, presumably ruling from Oldstones, before being put down by the might of Storm's End. His connection with the extinct Fisher dynasty is unconfirmed.

name: Lymond Goodbrook
bio: Lymond Goodbrook is the head of House Goodbrook. He is a good friend of Ser Edmure_Tully.

name: Lymond Hightower
bio: Lymond Hightower, known as the Sea Lion, was the last of the independent Hightower kings to rule over Oldtown. Lymond fought against King Theon_III_Greyjoy after ceasing paying tribute to the High King of the Iron Islands, defeating Greyjoy's ironborn and slaying the High King. He revived the First Men practice of thralldom, still practiced by the ironborn, long enough to set the ironmen captured during the battle to hard labor strengthening the walls of Oldtown. Through long negotiations and marriage arrangements, Garland_II_Gardener brought Oldtown into the Kingdom of the Reach by marrying his daughter to Lymond, while putting aside his own wives to wed Lymond's daughter. Taking on the title of Lord of the Hightower, the Hightowers were reduced from wealthy, relatively minor kings to the greatest lords in the Reach, vassals of House Gardener. The Hightowers were the last of the ancient realms of the Reach to swear fealty to Highgarden and join the Kingdom of the Reach. By terms of the marriage treaty, the Gardeners swore to defend Oldtown, allowing Lymond to turn his attention to his "great purpose", the building of ships and conquest by sea. By the end of his reign, no lord or king in Westeros could match the strength of the Hightowers' fleet. To this day, a great statue of Lymond stands overlooking Oldtown's harbor, gazing off down Whispering Sound.

name: Lymond Lychester
bio: Lymond Lychester is the lord and head of House Lychester. Lord Lymond once held Ser Maynard at the bridge, taking six wounds before killing the other knight. There is no further information as to who Maynard was or where the bridge is located, though Lem and Anguy believe it to be the small bridge near Lychester's keep. All of Lord Lychester's sons died during Robert's Rebellion, fighting for one side or the other. The old knight has since become senile with age, and is now cared for by young Maester Roone_(maester).

name: Lymond Lynderly
bio: Lymond Lynderly is a knight of House Lynderly.

name: Lymond Mallister
bio: Lymond Mallister was the Lord of Seagard and the head of House Mallister during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. During the tourney at Maidenpool to honor the accession of King Viserys_I_Targaryen to the Iron Throne, Lord Lymond unhorsed Ser Criston_Cole.

name: Lymond Pease
bio: Lymond Pease is a sellsword in service to the Golden Company.

name: Lymond Vikary
bio: Lymond Vikary is a landed knight and the head of House Vikary.

name: Lyn Corbray
bio: Lyn Corbray is a knight from House Corbray who wields Lady Forlorn, a famed sword of Valyrian steel. He is the heir of his elder brother, Lord Lyonel_Corbray of Heart's Home. Ser Lyn won his knighthood during Robert's Rebellion. At the start of the war, he fought for House Targaryen alongside Lord Marq_Grafton against Lord Jon_Arryn at the gates of Gulltown. After being pardoned, Lyn joined Jon against the Targaryens. Lord_Corbray_(father_of_Lyonel) was badly wounded during the Battle of the Trident. As Lyonel_Corbray led their father back to the rear, Lyn took up the family's Valyrian steel longsword, Lady Forlorn, led a charge on the Dornish lines, and cut down the previously-wounded Prince Lewyn_Martell of the Kingsguard. Honoring Lyn's achievements on the battlefield, his father bequeathed him Lady Forlorn upon his death. Lyn's elder brother, Lyonel inherited Heart's Home and the rest of the family estate and titles, but still resented the loss of the family blade. Lyn has slain many men in duels.

name: Lynara Stark
bio: Lynara Stark was a noblewoman of House Stark during the second century after Aegon's Conquest, and the third wife of Lord Cregan_Stark of Winterfell. Lynara was a descendant of a younger son of Lord Brandon_Stark_(the_Boisterous). Lynara married her distant cousin, Cregan_Stark, Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North, becoming his third wife. They had five children together; Jonnel_Stark, Edric_Stark_(son_of_Cregan), Lyanna_Stark_(daughter_of_Cregan), Barthogan_Stark and Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan). The main line of House Stark's lineage descends from her.

name: Lync
bio: Lync was a member of the Night's Watch. Lync was sent to the Wall by Ser Malcolm for stealing a pinch of pepper from his table. His brother is an innkeeper who owns an ivy-covered inn north of King's Landing.

name: Lynesse Hightower
bio: Lynesse Hightower is the youngest daughter of Lord Leyton_Hightower of Oldtown. She was the second wife of Ser Jorah_Mormont and is now the chief concubine of Tregar_Ormollen in Lys. During the tourney at Lannisport, Lynesse allowed her favor to be worn by Ser Jorah_Mormont, Lord of Bear Island, who was twice her age and widowed. Jorah had recently been knighted for his bravery during Greyjoy's Rebellion. Jorah crowned Lynesse the queen of love and beauty after winning the tourney and asked for her hand in marriage, to which Lord Leyton_Hightower consented. Lynesse and Jorah married shortly thereafter in Lannisport. Although they had married for love, the marriage did not remain happy for long. Having grown up in House Hightower in prosperous Oldtown, Lynesse was unprepared for life at poor Bear Island in the remote north, and she quickly became miserable. They visited Winterfell for feasts, and during a stay which lasted a fortnight, a drunken Lynesse confessed her unhappiness to Lady Catelyn_Stark, a fellow southron. Catelyn tried to reassure Lynesse, explaining she once felt similar but came to love parts of the north. Ser Jorah attempted to reproduce the luxurious lifestyle his wife was accustomed to, sending for a cook from Oldtown and a harper from Lannisport and building her a fine ship. Jorah lavished Lynesse with expensive gifts and voyages to Lannisport, Oldtown, and even Braavos, where he borrowed from moneylenders. Lynesse was unimpressed with Jorah's defeats in other tourneys, each of which meant the knightly lord had to ransom or replace his horse and jousting armor at great expense. The situation became worse when Lynesse and Jorah returned to Bear Island. Lord Mormont had driven himself into financial ruin, and Lynesse refused to sell her jewelry. In desperation, Jorah attempted to raise money by selling some convicted poachers to a Tyroshi slaver. Learning that one of his bannermen had engaged in the slave trade, Lord Eddard_Stark declared Jorah's life forfeit. Rather than face the Warden of the North, however, Jorah fled with Lynesse to Essos in 293 AC. Ser Jorah would have preferred to live in Braavos, but to make Lynesse happy he instead chose the warmer Lys, where he sold his ship. Jorah became a sellsword, but Lynesse outspent his earnings tenfold. Lynesse moved into the manse of Tregar_Ormollen, a Lysene merchant prince, while Jorah was fighting Braavosi on the Rhoyne. When Jorah finally returned to Lys, Tregar threatened to enslave the northman if he did not give up Lynesse. Jorah choose to abandon Lys for Volantis. Lynesse is now Tregar's chief concubine; even his wife goes in fear of her.

name: Lynora Hill
bio: Lynora Hill is the bastard daughter of Ser Jason_Lannister_(son_of_Gerold) and a serving girl, who worked at Casterly Rock according to a semi-canon source. It is unknown if Lynora is still alive during the time of A Song of Ice and Fire.

name: Lyonce
bio: Lyonce was an archmaester of the Citadel who became Grand Maester under King Aegon_I_Targaryen. The ring, rod, and mask he wore were gold. Lyonce was appointed Grand Maester during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen following the death of Grand Maester Ollidar, the first Grand Maester of the Seven Kingdoms. Lyonce held the office for seven years, until he slipped, broke his hip, and died.

name: Lyonel
bio: Lyonel is a knight in Lord Tywin_Lannister's army at Harrenhal.

name: Lyonel Baratheon
bio: Lyonel Baratheon, known as the Laughing Storm, was the Lord of Storm's End and the head of House Baratheon during the reign of King Aegon_V_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Lyonel is portrayed by Daniel Ings.

name: Lyonel Bentley
bio: Lyonel Bentley was a knight and member of Rhaenyra_Targaryen's Queensguard. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Lyonel Bentley will be portrayed by Joe Gaminara. Ser Lyonel Bentley was inducted into the Queensguard after Queen Rhaenyra seized King's Landing and commanded Ser Lorent_Marbrand to recruit six knights to serve as members. Lyonel accompanied Rhaenyra as her party escaped King's Landing after the storming of the Dragonpit. On the road between Stokeworth and Duskendale, they were attacked by broken men and Lyonel suffered a blow that cracked his helmet. He died raving the following day.

name: Lyonel Corbray
bio: Lyonel Corbray is the Lord of Heart's Home and the head of House Corbray, an ancient but impoverished family. He has two younger brothers, Ser Lyn_Corbray and Ser Lucas_Corbray. When Lord_Corbray_(father_of_Lyonel) was wounded during the Battle of the Trident, Lyonel carried his father back to the maesters in the rear, while his younger brother, Ser Lyn_Corbray, grabbed their father's sword, Lady Forlorn, and led a charge against the Dornishmen. When Lord Corbray died, Lyonel inherited the titles and lands while Lyn received Lady Forlorn. Lyonel felt he was cheated of his birthright. Lyonel's wife bore a sickly child who died in infancy, so Lyonel's heir is his brother, Lyn.

name: Lyonel Deddings
bio: Lyonel Deddings was the head of House Deddings during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. During the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Lyonel Deddings sided with the blacks and Rhaenyra_Targaryen. At the Second Battle of Tumbleton, Lord Deddings joined the host led by Ser Addam_Velaryon and scraped together fresh levies of greybeards and green boys, despite the grievous losses he had suffered in the autumn battles. During the battle, Lyonel and Lord Stanton_Piper seated together atop a low rise and burned with their squires, servants, and sworn shields when the riderless Vermithor took note of them.

name: Lyonel Frey
bio: Lyonel Frey is a knight of House Frey. The son of Lord Emmon_Frey (second son of Lord Walder_Frey) and Genna_Lannister (only daughter of Lord Tytos_Lannister), he has three brothers; Ser Cleos_Frey, Tion_Frey and Walder_Frey_(son_of_Emmon).

name: Lyonel Hightower
bio: Lyonel Hightower was Lord of the Hightower and head of House Hightower at the end of the Dance of the Dragons and during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. A High_Septon_(Aegon_III) eventually reversed the position of his predecessor, allowing Lyonel to marry Sam, and by the same occasion legitimized their six chidren.

name: Lyonel Lorch
bio: Lyonel Lorch was a knight of House Lorch during the Faith Militant uprising. During the Faith Militant uprising, Ser Lyonel took up arms on behalf of the High_Septon_(Aenys_I) and the Starry Sept against the Iron Throne. He fought in the battle at the Great Fork alongside Lord Rupert_Falwell and other knights from the westerlands and riverlands, as well as hundreds of Poor Fellows, against the royal army led by King Maegor_I_Targaryen himself. It is unknown if he survived.

name: Lyonel Selmy
bio: Lyonel Selmy was a knight and possibly the head of House Selmy after the reign of King Aegon_V_Targaryen.[N 1]

name: Lyonel Strong
bio: Lyonel Strong was the Lord of Harrenhal and the head of House Strong during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. He served as both master of laws and Hand of the King for Viserys. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Lyonel is portrayed by Gavin Spokes. As a youth, Lyonel studied at the Citadel, where he earned six links on his chain before deciding that the maester's life was not for him. Lyonel became the Lord of Harrenhal during the later reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In late 105 AC, Lord Lyonel was invited to court at King's Landing to serve as master of laws for King Viserys_I_Targaryen, on account of his exhaustive knowledge of laws within the Seven Kingdoms. Lyonel brought his four children to court with him; his maiden daughters became handmaids to Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, his elder son Ser Harwin_Strong was made a captain in the City Watch, and his second son Larys_Strong joined the king's confessors. Lyonel served ably in his office. In 109 AC, after Ser Otto_Hightower overreached himself by pushing Viserys to name Otto's grandson Prince Aegon_II_Targaryen as his heir over Princess Rhaenyra, Viserys stripped him of his office as Hand of the King and appointed Lyonel in his place, favoring Lyonel's stoic nature. In 111 AC, when the allegation that Prince Daemon_Targaryen had taken Princess Rhaenyra's maidenhead came before the small council, Lord Lyonel's counsel to Viserys was that Daemon should be executed for treason. Septon Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons), however, reminded the council about the taint of kinslaying, and Daemon was exiled instead.
