As the council discussed a consort for Rhaenyra, Viserys confided to Lyonel that Queen Alicent_Hightower was pushing for a marriage between their son Aegon and Rhaenyra to ensure her son would succeed to the throne. Ultimately, the council selected Ser Laenor_Velaryon as Rhaenyra's husband. After the wedding, the couple moved to Dragonstone accompanied by Lyonel's daughters, as well as Harwin, Rhaenyra's sworn shield. When the exiled Prince Daemon had twin daughters with his wife Lady Laena_Velaryon in 116 AC, Daemon wrote to Viserys asking leave to present his daughters to court. While Lyonel and the council argued heatedly against it, Viserys consented and reconciled with his brother. Meanwhile, as Rhaenyra's sworn shield and constant companion, Harwin was rumored to be the father of the princess' three sons, for the boys lacked the Valyrian traits that Rhaenyra and Laenor shared. In 120 AC, over mounting rumors of his grandsons' parentage, Viserys removed Ser Harwin as Princess Rhaenyra's sworn shield. Lyonel left the court to accompany Harwin on his return to Harrenhal. Shortly after their arrival, a fire broke out in the tower they were sleeping in, and killed both Lyonel and Harwin, along with three retainers and a dozen servants. The fire was considered an accident, and blamed on the curse of Harrenhal. However, some records of the time suggested it was a murder, and listed various suspects. The court fool Mushroom's Testimony claimed that Lord Corlys_Velaryon was the man behind the deaths, stating that the Sea Snake sought vengeance against the man who cuckolded his son. Septon Eustace's writings suggested it was Prince Daemon, seeking to eliminate a rival for Rhaenyra's affections. Others suspected Lyonel's younger son Larys, who stood to inherit Harrenhal with his father and elder brother dead, or King Viserys, who only sought to have Harwin killed, with Lyonel's death being an unfortunate accident.

name: Lyonel Tyrell
bio: Lyonel Tyrell was the Lord of Highgarden and the head of House Tyrell during the Dance of the Dragons and the conquest of Dorne.

name: Lyonel Tyrell
bio: Lyonel Tyrell is a member of House Tyrell, a distant cousin of the main branch. He is the eldest son of Ser Leo_Tyrell_(son_of_Victor) and Alys_Beesbury, he has four siblings: Alla_Tyrell, Leona_Tyrell, Lucas_Tyrell, and Lorent_Tyrell.

name: Lyra
bio: Lyra was a novice, and later septa of the Faith of the Seven, who was a beloved religious tutor of Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. In 50 AC, Lyra was one of three women sent to Queen Alysanne_Targaryen on Dragonstone by Dowager Queen Alyssa_Velaryon, to see to Queen Alysanne's religious instruction. Her companions were Septa Ysabel, the novice Edyth, and the famously pious Lady Lucinda Tully. Queen Regent Alyssa hoped that the four women could make Alysanne, and perhaps even King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, understand that the Faith considered their incestuous marriage an abomination. Alyssa hoped her children would repent their marriage as a result, before it would tear the realm apart again. However, Lyra and the other women were moved by Alysanne's devotion to her husband and her kindness, and became her loyal servants instead. By 51 AC, Lyra had sworn her vows to become a septa. She attended Queen Alysanne at Maidenpool along with Septa Edyth, and defended the pregnant queen when she was attacked by women while attending Jonquil's Pool. In 55 AC, when Alysanne was pregnant again, Septa Lyra accompanied her to Dragonstone, where the queen eventually gave birth to Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I). Lyra comforted Alysanne after she lost her three month old baby, Gaemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I) in 73 AC, telling Alysanne that Gaemon was with the Mother Above. Following the birth of the sickly and frail Princess Gael_Targaryen in 80 AC, Lyra assisted Grand Maester Elysar in nursing the child during the first year of her life, watching over the babe night and day.

name: Lyra Hayford
bio: Lyra Hayford was a member of House Hayford during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 133 AC, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Many maidens across Westeros came to King's Landing in hopes of marrying the young king. Lyra was one of the many maidens who suffered what was called the "Maiden's Day curse" by being injured or scandalized after the ball was announced. In Lyra's case, she a was accused of having smothered her infant brother in a fit of jealousy. It is possible that this was a rumor fabricated by Lord Unwin_Peake, the Hand of the King, who wished to promote his Myrielle_Peake as Aegon III's second queen.

name: Lyra Mormont
bio: Lyra Mormont is the third daughter of Lady Maege_Mormont.

name: Lysa Arryn
bio: Lysa Arryn, née Tully, is a noblewoman of House Tully. She is the youngest daughter of Lord Hoster_Tully of Riverrun and his wife, Lady Minisa_Whent, and wife of Jon_Arryn, the Lord of the Eyrie and Hand of the King to King Robert_I_Baratheon. Since her husband's death, she is the Lady Regent of the Vale of Arryn. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Lysa is portrayed by Kate Dickie. Lysa was born as the second daughter of Lord Hoster_Tully, head of House Tully and Lord of Riverrun, and his wife, Lady Minisa_Whent. She has one older sister, Catelyn_Stark, and one younger brother, Edmure_Tully. Her mother, who had given birth to two boys previously (who had both died in infancy), died giving birth to her sixth child, who died with her. Lysa was raised at Riverrun alongside her two siblings and her father's young ward, Petyr_Baelish. As a young girl, Lysa would run and hide whenever she had done something wrong, so her father could not find her. Just as her siblings and her father's ward, Petyr, were wont to do, Lysa always went to her uncle, Ser Brynden_Tully, with her tears and tales, whenever Lord Hoster was too busy, and Lady Minisa too ill. Lysa and Catelyn played together and even had a secret language. Once they made mud pies in the godswood of Riverrun and served them to Petyr, who was sick for a week after eating them. On one occasion, Lysa and Catelyn accompanied their father to Seagard, but on their way back to Riverrun, the sisters became lost due to the mist and Lysa started to cry. Petyr, who had accompanied Lord Hoster as well, eventually found the girls. Being raised alongside Petyr, Lysa fell in love with him, but her feelings were not reciprocated. Instead, Petyr fell in love with Lysa's older sister Catelyn. When they were still young Lysa and Catelyn had first learnt to kiss by taking turns kissing Petyr, making Petyr the first man Lysa ever kissed. Catelyn recalls it as having been innocent, though Petyr attempted to kiss them with tongue, which Lysa allowed. When Lords Blackwood and Bracken were at Riverrun to settle a feud with Hoster's help, Catelyn and Petyr danced six dances. However, when he tried to kiss her, she pushed him away and laughed at him. Petyr, wounded by the rejection, became drunk and had to be brought to bed by Lysa's uncle, Ser Brynden_Tully. Lysa followed, and climbed into Petyr's bed to "give him comfort." Petyr took her maidenhead that night, telling her he loved her whilst he drunkenly believed that she was Catelyn, going as far as calling her "Cat" before he fell asleep. Despite this, Lysa stayed with him until sunrise, and would later come to remember the night fondly.[N 2] When Catelyn's wedding date to Brandon was eventually announced in 282 AC, Petyr challenged the northman for Catelyn's hand. Brandon wounded Petyr severely, after which Lysa helped nurse him. In the following fortnight, they slept together again. Lysa became pregnant with Petyr's child as a result of this. After Hoster had Petyr sent back to the Fingers, Lysa revealed her pregnancy to her father, hoping that Hoster would relent and let her wed Petyr. Hoster, who had always felt Petyr was too lowborn, instead forced Lysa to abort the child with moon tea provided by Maester Kym. Lysa nearly died from this. Lords Hoster_Tully and Tywin_Lannister had been discussing a betrothal between Lysa and Jaime_Lannister, the heir to Casterly Rock. This potential betrothal was off the table when King Aerys_II_Targaryen named Jaime to his Kingsguard in 281 AC. Lord Tywin offered his younger son, Tyrion_Lannister, instead but was rebuffed by Lord Hoster, who stated that he wanted "a whole man" for Lysa instead of a dwarf.

name: Lysa Farman
bio: Lysa Farman was a noblewoman of House Farman during the Dance of the Dragons. She became a salt wife to Lord Dalton_Greyjoy during the conflict. Lysa was the daughter of Lord_Farman_(father_of_Lysa) of Faircastle. During the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Dalton_Greyjoy captured Fair Isle claimed Lysa and three of her sisters as salt wives and gave another to his brother Veron_Greyjoy. By 133 AC two of her sisters and her father had perished. One of Lord Dalton's other salt wives, Tess, slit his throat, making Lysa a salt widow. The smallfolk of Fair Isle and the few knights that remained on the island rose in rebellion and killed the remaining ironmen. Though the garrison at Faircastle was maintained by Alester_Wynch and Gunthor_Goodbrother, the latter slew the former in a quarrel over Lysa. Lysa's fate after Johanna_Lannister's liberation of Fair Isle and invasion of the Iron Islands is unknown.

name: Lysa Locke
bio: Lysa Locke was a noblewoman from House Locke. She was married to Lord Benjen_Stark_(lord), with whom she had two sons; Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Benjen) and Bennard_Stark.

name: Lysa Meadows
bio: Lysa Meadows is a noblewoman of House Meadows. She is married to Ser Olymer_Tyrell and together they have three children, Raymund_Tyrell, Rickard_Tyrell and Megga_Tyrell.

name: Lysandro Rogare
bio: Lysandro Rogare, known as Lysandro the Magnificent, was a wealthy Lysene banker and head of House Rogare. His brother, Drazenko_Rogare, was the consort of Aliandra_Martell, Princess of Dorne. Lysandro's children were Larra_Rogare, Lysaro_Rogare, Moredo_Rogare, Lotho_Rogare, Roggerio_Rogare, Lysara_Rogare, Drako_Rogare, Fredo_Rogare, and Marra_Rogare. Lysandro Rogare was the patriarch of the rich and powerful Rogare dynasty and a magister of Lys during the Dance of the Dragons and the years that followed. His family owned a many holdings, including a famed pillow house called the Perfumed Garden, and controlled the powerful Rogare Bank. After the death of Bambarro_Bazanne, the captive Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen passed into the hands of Lysandro. Initially, he considered putting the boy to work in his pleasure house on account of his striking appearance, but quickly changed plans after Viserys identified himself. Instead, he wed the boy to his youngest daughter, Lady Larra_Rogare. Lysandro was the head of the Rogare Bank, which was even more powerful than the Iron Bank of Braavos for a time. After Larra's marriage to Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, Lysandro styled himself First Magister for Life and people called him the Magnificent. However, he and his brother died within a day of each other and thus the fall of the Rogares began both in Lys and the Seven Kingdoms.

name: Lysara Karstark
bio: Lysara Karstark was a noblewoman from House Karstark during the third century of the Targaryen era. She was married to Artos_Stark, the third son of Lord Beron_Stark of Winterfell, and had twin sons by him; Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Artos) and Benjen_Stark_(son_of_Artos).

name: Lysara Rogare
bio: Lysara Rogare was a noblewoman from House Rogare, a Lysene noble banking family. She was the daughter of Lysandro_Rogare, the First Magister of Lys. Lysara was the daughter of Lysandro Rogare. She had six brothers Lysaro_Rogare, Moredo_Rogare, Lotho_Rogare, Drako_Rogare, Fredo_Rogare, and Roggerio_Rogare, and two sisters, Larra_Rogare and Marra_Rogare, as well as sixteen bastard siblings. Upon the death of her father, Lysandro_Rogare, he bequeathed his palace to Lysara. When the Rogare Bank ran dry in 135 AC, Lysaro Rogare's brothers and sisters claimed to have no part in it, but many doubted their claims of innocence. She was seized and put on trial. When she claimed she "did not know," Magister Tigaro_Moraqos responded that she should have, and the mob roared in approval. Her palace was seized, and she was deprived of her gold, gems, and gowns. It is written that Lysara availed herself of being able to whip her brother Lysaro when he was chained naked to a pillar before the Temple of Trade.

name: Lysaro Rogare
bio: Lysaro Rogare was a member of House Rogare, a Lysene noble banking family. He was the heir to Lysandro_Rogare, the nephew of Drazenko_Rogare, and the elder brother of Larra_Rogare (who married Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen before his ascent to the Iron Throne). After the death of his father, Lysandro_Rogare who claimed to be First Magister for Life, the ambitious Lysaro was a spendthrift who angered other magisters in Lys. His embezzlement weakened the Rogare Bank, causing it to fail. Although Lysaro was killed by scourging at the Temple of Trade, his siblings escaped execution.

name: Lysono Maar
bio: Lysono Maar is a sellsword from Lys in service to the Golden Company as spymaster.

name: Lythene Frey
bio: Lythene Frey is the second daughter of Lord Walder_Frey, the only daughter of his third marriage to Lady Amarei_Crakehall. She is the wife of Lord Lucias_Vypren. They have two children, Elyana_Vypren and Damon_Vypren.

name: Mace Tyrell
bio: Mace Tyrell is the Lord of Highgarden, Lord Paramount of the Mander, Defender of the Marches, High Marshal of the Reach, Warden of the South, and head of House Tyrell. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Mace is portrayed by Roger Ashton-Griffiths. Mace became Lord of Highgarden once his father, Lord Luthor_Tyrell, rode off a cliff while hawking. Mace was one of the attendees at the tourney at Harrenhal in 281 AC. During Robert's Rebellion, Mace was in command of troops from the Reach who marched against Lord Robert_I_Baratheon of Storm's End. Mace is credited with defeating Robert in the Battle of Ashford, although the van under Lord Randyll_Tarly achieved most of the victory before Mace's arrival at Ashford. He then led the siege of Storm's End, which was successfully held by Robert's brother, Stannis_Baratheon. Mace and his closest bannerman, Lord Paxter_Redwyne, surrendered to Robert's ally, Lord Eddard_Stark, after the Sack of King's Landing. According to Prince Oberyn_Martell, Mace encouraged his heir, Willas_Tyrell, to participate in tourneys before he was ready, leading to the accidental crippling of Willas by Oberyn. Some of the Iron Throne's debts under the new king, Robert I Baratheon, are owed to Lord Mace.

name: Mad Axe
bio: Mad Axe was a member of the Night's Watch. He is famous for murdering his fellow brothers at the Nightfort. According to the story, Mad Axe took his boots off and prowled the halls of the Nightfort silently at night and murdered his brothers. The only noise he made came from the blood dripping from his axe, his elbows and his wet beard.

name: Mad Huntsman
bio: The Mad Huntsman, also known as the Huntsman,  is a farmer and owner of a pack of dogs from Stoney Sept. His given name is unknown.

name: Maddy
bio: Maddy is a servant of House Arryn in service at the Eyrie.

name: Maege Mormont
bio: Maege Mormont, known as the She-Bear, is the head of House Mormont and the Lady of Bear Island. She inherited her title from her nephew, Ser Jorah_Mormont, after he fled into exile. She is the younger sister of the Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, Jeor_Mormont. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Maege is portrayed by Elizabeth Barrett. Maege inherited the title of head of House Mormont and ruler of Bear Island after her brother's only heir, Jorah Mormont, fled to the Free Cities to escape justice for unlawfully selling poachers into slavery to pay for his debts. She worked hard to regain the family's noble name, and to help the estate recover from the state of near-poverty Jorah left behind. Maege has five daughters: her eldest and heir, Dacey_Mormont; and Alysane_Mormont, Lyra_Mormont, Jorelle_Mormont, and Lyanna_Mormont. She has no sons. No one knows who the father of Maege's children is, or even if she ever married. There are rumors that she beds with a bear; her daughter, Alysane, smilingly shares the same rumor, claiming that Mormont women are skinchangers who turn into bears and find mates in the woods. Maege prefers to keep her own counsel. Nonetheless, all of Maege's daughters carry the surname "Mormont", and none appear to be considered bastards. It is possible that Maege's husband came from a family of lower nobility than the Mormonts, or no nobility at all, in which case a woman can prefer to retain her own surname for herself and for her children.

name: Maegelle Frey
bio: Maegelle Frey was the daughter of Ser Stevron_Frey and Jeyne_Lydden. She married Ser Dafyn_Vance, with whom she had three children: Marianne_Vance, Walder_Vance and Patrek_Vance.

name: Maegelle Targaryen
bio: Septa Maegelle, born Maegelle Targaryen, was the sixth born child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. As a child, Maegelle attached herself to her elder sister, Princess Alyssa_Targaryen, much as Alyssa herself had done to Prince Baelon when she was younger. Alyssa did the best she could to evade Maegelle, bristling at "the baby" at her skirts. Later, Maegelle was the "guiding star" of her younger sister, Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I). In 73 AC, at the age of ten, Maegelle was promised to the Faith of the Seven as a novice, which Queen Alysanne announced was in thanks for the Mother blessing her with many children. Maegelle eventually became a septa. When Queen Alysanne was having a difficult time finding a husband for Princess Daella, Maegelle told her mother that the Faith would not want Daella because of her illiteracy, as she could not read her prayers. After Princess Saera_Targaryen was involved in a scandal in 84 AC, it was announced that her sister Septa Maegelle was to take charge of her instruction as a novice silent sister in Oldtown. In 87 AC, Maegelle took leave from her sept to stay with her grieving mother, who had lost her daughters Daella, Alyssa, and Viserra_Targaryen in the space of five years. In 89 AC, Jaehaerys visited his daughter in Oldtown during the First Quarrel. It was Maegelle who helped reconcile the king and queen, telling her father to conciliate with Alysanne in time for the wedding of her niece, Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon), the following year. Maegelle also played a role in ending the Second Quarrel, which had begun in 92 AC, when her father, King Jaehaerys, chose Maegelle's brother, Prince Baelon, as his heir over Maegelle's niece, Princess Rhaenys. This decision angered Queen Alysanne, as she felt there was no reason to favor a man over a woman. Maegelle brought her parents back together, solving the Quarrel in 94 AC. Maegelle nursed children afflicted with greyscale, but became afflicted with greyscale herself and died in 96 AC, her arms and legs turned to stone.

name: Maegon Targaryen
bio: Maegon Targaryen was a member of House Targaryen during the Century of Blood, the first son of Lord Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Gaemon) and Lady Elaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Gaemon) of Dragonstone. He had one younger brother, Aerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon). Maegon inherited the lordship of Dragonstone from his father and was himself succeeded by his younger brother, Aerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon).

name: Maegor I Targaryen
bio: Maegor I Targaryen, also known as Maegor the Cruel, was the third Targaryen king to sit the Iron Throne. He was the son of King Aegon_I_Targaryen and his elder sister-wife, Queen Visenya_Targaryen. He had an older half-brother, Aenys_I_Targaryen. The polygamous Maegor had six wives: Ceryse_Hightower, Alys_Harroway, Tyanna_of_the_Tower, Elinor_Costayne, Jeyne_Westerling_(wife_of_Maegor_I), and his niece Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), the last three of whom are known as the Black Brides.

name: Maegor Targaryen
bio: Prince Maegor Targaryen was the son of Prince Aerion_Targaryen and Princess Daenora_Targaryen. Born in 232 AC, Maegor was given his name by his sire who died the same year he was born. According to a semi-canon source, this name was a provocation, since King Maegor_I_Targaryen had a poor reputation. Prince Aerion_Targaryen died later that year from drinking wildfire. After the death of his grandfather, King Maekar_I_Targaryen, Maegor was one of the claimants in the Great Council of 233 AC. Only a few spoke for him, and his claim was turned down because he was an infant and there were fears Maegor would inherit his father's madness. His uncle Aegon_V_Targaryen was instead chosen king.

name: Maegor Towers
bio: Maegor Towers was the Lord of Harrenhal and the head of House Towers during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Named after King Maegor_I_Targaryen, he was the last of his line. Born in 44 AC to Lord Jordan_Towers, Maegor was named after King Maegor_I_Targaryen, who had granted his grandsire, Ser Walton_Towers, the lordship of Harrenhal that same year. Lord Jordan was a staunch supporter of the king and one of the last lords of the Seven Kingdoms who remained loyal to Maegor when the great lords of the realm flocked to Prince Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Lord Jordan was one of the few lords that remained at the Red Keep to turn it over to Jaehaerys, and he was pardoned in exchange for certain holdings and for sending the young Maegor to court to serve as a hostage. As his elder brothers died in King Maegor's wars, the young Maegor became Lord of Harrenhal after his father passed away from a chest congestion. When King Jaehaerys began a royal progress in 53 AC with his dragon Vermithor to celebrate the new year, his first stop was to see the new Lord of Harrenhal, then only nine years of age. In a castle built to house thousands, Maegor was an impoverished lord who resided in the Tower of Dread with only a cook and three elderly men-at-arms. Since the rest of Harrenhal was empty, King Jaehaerys settled his widowed sister, Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), in the Widow's Tower in 56 AC. Maegor and Rhaena eventually became friends, and she cared for his servants after Maegor passed away in 61 AC. As Maegor was last of his line with no heirs, Harrenhal was granted to House Strong after Rhaena passed away in 73 AC.

name: Maekar I Targaryen
bio: Maekar I Targaryen was Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and the fourteenth Targaryen king to sit the Iron Throne. He was the fourth son of King Daeron_II_Targaryen, and was given the Targaryen seat of Summerhall, and was thus known as the Prince of Summerhall. Maekar was a capable army commander who served in two of the Blackfyre Rebellions. After a number of unexpected deaths in the royal family, he came to the throne in 221 AC. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Maekar is portrayed by Sam Spruell. Maekar's father, King Daeron_II_Targaryen, and nephews, Valarr_Targaryen and Matarys_Targaryen died from the Great Spring Sickness in 209 AC. Maekar's brother succeeded to the throne as Aerys_I_Targaryen and appointed their bastard uncle, Brynden_Rivers (called Bloodraven), as Hand of the King. Maekar had expected to be appointed Hand and could not get along with Lord Bloodraven, so he withdrew from court to his residence at Summerhall. According to Ser Eustace_Osgrey, Lord Gormon_Peake, and Ser Kyle the Cat, Maekar sulked and brooded at Summerhall. Ser Duncan_the_Tall and Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen attended the wedding tourney at Whitewalls in 212 AC. When Ser Mallor's squire suggested that Maekar desired to kill his brother Aerys, "Egg" defended his father's reputation by striking the other squire. When the Second Blackfyre Rebellion began to collapse, Lord Ambrose_Butterwell feared Maekar's reaction. After the accidental deaths of Daeron's third son, Rhaegel_Targaryen, in 215 AC and Rhaegel's son Aelor_Targaryen in 217 AC, the childless King Aerys recognized his sole surviving brother Maekar as his heir, and Maekar became Prince of Dragonstone. During the Third Blackfyre Rebellion in 219 AC, Maekar was recognized for his leadership. Ser Aegor_Rivers was captured in battle, and Bloodraven and Maekar's son, Aerion_Targaryen, both urged that Aegor be executed. King Aerys was merciful, however, and allowed Bittersteel to join the Night's Watch, a fate he managed to escape.

name: Maelor Targaryen
bio: Prince Maelor Targaryen was the second son and third child of King Aegon_II_Targaryen and his sister-wife and Queen Helaena_Targaryen. Maelor had older twin siblings, brother Jaehaerys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon_II), and sister Jaehaera_Targaryen.

name: Maelys Blackfyre
bio: Maelys I Blackfyre, better known as Maelys the Monstrous, was the last of the Blackfyre Pretenders, and the last known member of the male line of House Blackfyre. Maelys was a descendant of the male line of Daemon_I_Blackfyre. According to a semi-canon source, he supposedly devoured his twin in the womb (making him a kinslayer, albeit not by choice), which resulted in his grotesquely large upper body and vestigial second head. Maelys grew up in exile in Essos, and eventually became a kinslayer deliberately, when he fought his own cousin, Daemon_Blackfyre_(cousin_of_Maelys_I), for command of the Golden Company. Maelys killed Daemon's destrier with a single punch, and then twisted Daemon's head until he tore it from his shoulders. Following Daemon's death, Maelys assumed command of the Golden Company. In 258 AC, Maelys gathered with eight other outlaws, exiles and sellsword captains beneath the Tree of Crowns in the Disputed Lands in Essos. There, they formed an alliance called the Band of Nine, promising to aid each other in carving out a kingdom for each member. The Band of Nine pledged to help Maelys in conquering the Seven Kingdoms. While it was generally believed that the Band of Nine would founder in Essos, they managed to sack and take Tyrosh, and seize the Stepstones, which made them a real threat to Westeros. Maelys declared himself to be King Maelys I Blackfyre. In 260 AC, King Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen sent an army to the Stepstones to meet the Band of Nine in battle. The war, dubbed the War of the Ninepenny Kings, was fought entirely on the Stepstones. Maelys personally killed Lord Ormund_Baratheon, Jaehaerys's Hand of the King, early in the war. Towards the war's end, Maelys himself was killed by Ser Barristan_Selmy, a young knight from the stormlands, who thereby ended the Blackfyre threat to the Iron Throne. Maelys's death decided the war, as the other members of the Band of Nine were barely interested in Westeros, and fell back to their own domains. Their union would not last much longer.

name: Maerie
bio: Goodwife Maerie is an inhabitant of the riverlands.

name: Maerie the Whore
bio: Maerie the Whore is an inhabitant of the riverlands.

name: Maezon zo Rhaezn
bio: Maezon zo Rhaezn is a Yunkish nobleman.

name: Mag Mar Tun Doh Weg
bio: Mag Mar Tun Doh Weg, called Mag the Mighty, is something of a chieftain among the giants. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Mag is portrayed by Neil Fingleton. Mag the Mighty has a grey pelt streaked with white. His mouth is full of huge square teeth. His laughter sounds like a rumbling belch. The giant speaks the Old Tongue. Mag is smaller than Wun_Weg_Wun_Dar_Wun. Mag's mammoth has the same coloring and is larger than other mammoths, denoting its older age.

name: Maggy the Frog
bio: Maggy the Frog was a reputed sorceress who dealt in fortunes, cures, and love potions at Lannisport. Her true name has been forgotten, with "Maggy" likely being a bastardization of the word maegi. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Maggy is portrayed by Jodhi May. "Maggy", whose real name is unknown, came to Lannisport as a young, beautiful woman, who may have been a priestess. She was brought to Westeros by her husband, who had been trading saffron and pepper in Essos. Some claim that she enchanted him with a spell, though more likely she only needed her sexual charms. Many people of Lannisport visited her for her cures and love potions, calling her "Maegi", as no one could pronounce her real name. It was said that Maggy could curse a man or make him fall in love, summon demons and foretell the future. She eventually lost her beauty through old age and reportedly by the practice of her evil sorcery. Maggy and the trader's wealthy merchant son was ennobled by Lord Tytos_Lannister, founding House Spicer. Through her son, the petty lord, Maggy was the grandmother of Ser Rolph_Spicer and Sybell_Spicer. She was also the grandmother of Ser Samwell_Spicer through another of her children.

name: Mago
bio: Mago is a dothraki rider in the khas of Ko Jhaqo. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Mago is portrayed by Ivailo Dimitrov.

name: Maia Stokeworth
bio: Maia Stokeworth was a noblewoman from House Stokeworth during the reign of kings Baelor_I_Targaryen and Viserys_II_Targaryen. After King Baelor's death and his uncle Viserys_II_Targaryen's ascension to the Iron Throne, Maia claimed that Viserys had poisoned his nephew to gain the throne after more than a decade of waiting.

name: Maisie
bio: Maisie is a serving girl at Winterfell.

name: Maladon Moore
bio: Maladon Moore was a knight from House Moore and a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. When Queen Ceryse_Hightower suddenly passed away in 45 AC, some alleged that King Maegor_I_Targaryen tried to have her tongue removed after a shrewish remark, and Maladon held her down while Ser Owen_Bush accidentally slit her throat. However, this rumor was likely fabricated to further blacken Maegor's reign. When the king suspected Queen Tyanna_of_the_Tower of treason, Maladon and Owen were dispatched to seize the queen and deliver her to the dungeons, where Maegor was said to have slain her while Maladon was present. After Maegor died in 48 AC and his nephew King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen took the Iron Throne, Maladon was accused of being involved in the death of Queen Ceryse, allegedly restraining her when Ser Owen accidentally killed her. Maladon denied these charges, insisting she died of "shrewishness". While the charges were never proven, Maladon lost his head for his involvement in Queen Tyanna's death, of which he was guilty.

name: Malaquo Maegyr
bio: Malaquo  Maegyr is one of the current ruling triarchs of the Free City of Volantis. Malaquo has been re-elected as triarch many times.

name: Malazza
bio: Malazza, known as the Girl General, is a Yunkish noble.

name: Malcolm
bio: Malcolm was a landed knight who lived near King's Landing. Malcolm was considered a hard man. Malcolm sent Lync to the Wall for stealing pepper.

name: Maldon IV Durrandon
bio: Maldon IV Durrandon was a Storm King of House Durrandon. During the Andal invasion of the stormlands, as neither side defeated the other, Maldon took an Andal maiden as his wife. Durran_XXIV_Durrandon was born of this union.

name: Maldon Massey
bio: Maldon Massey was the head and founder of House Massey during the reign of King Durran_Durrandon_(the_Ravenfriend). Maldon built Stonedance and established his lordship over Massey's Hook.

name: Malegorn of Redpool
bio: Malegorn of Redpool is a knight from Redpool.

name: Malentine Velaryon
bio: Malentine Velaryon was a knight from House Velaryon during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen, and Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 126 AC, Malentine's uncle, Lord Corlys_Velaryon, fell ill and the succession of Driftmark was called into question. Both of Corlys's children had died, and Corlys's eldest grandson stood to inherit the Iron Throne. When Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen suggested Corlys's second grandson, Lucerys_Velaryon, instead, Malentine's cousin, Ser Vaemond_Velaryon, protested on the grounds that Lucerys and his Velaryon brothers were bastards. After Vaemond was executed at the behest of Princess Rhaenyra, Malentine and his siblings brought their case before the sick and failing King Viserys_I_Targaryen at King's Landing. However, they made the mistake of questioning the legitimacy of Rhaenyra’s children in front of the king. Viserys had their tongues removed for this insolence, as per his earlier decree. Malentine and his four brothers were thereafter known as the "silent five". When Lord Corlys died in 132 AC, he was succeeded as Lord of the Tides by his bastard grandson, Alyn_Velaryon. This succession was not uncontested. Malentine and his only surviving brother, Ser Rhogar_Velaryon, yet again took their own claim to the small council in King’s Landing. When the Hand of the King and the regents ruled against them, Malentine and Rhogar refused to accept the decision. As Mushroom put it in his testimony, "lacking tongues with which to make their appeal, they preferred to argue with swords". The plot of the silent brothers to assassinate Alyn failed when the guards at Castle Driftmark proved loyal to the Sea Snake's memory and his chosen heir. Ser Malentine was slain during the attempt, his brother Rhogar captured and later sent to the Night's Watch.

name: Mallador Locke
bio: Mallador Locke[N 1] is a knight of the Night's Watch from House Locke. Donnel_Hill is his squire. Loyal to the Night's Watch, Mallador has a longsword and helm and rides a horse.

name: Malleon
bio: Malleon was a Grand Maester of the Citadel. He wrote a book on the lineages of the Seven Kingdoms, The Lineages and Histories of the Great Houses of the Seven Kingdoms, With Descriptions of Many High Lords and Noble Ladies and Their Children. Malleon was likely King Daeron_II_Targaryen's Grand Maester, because his book was over a century old, and Eddard_Stark thought scarcely a man now alive had yet been born when Malleon was working on it, which suggests his time was between 100 and 120 years before Eddard's time. Moreover, Malleon's book describes the marriage of Gowen_Baratheon and Tya_Lannister, which occured at the end of Daeron's reign.[N 1]

name: Malliard
bio: Wisdom Malliard is a member of the Alchemists' Guild in King's Landing. He is in charge of keeping track and projecting production of the wildfire at the Guild Hall.

name: Mallor
bio: Mallor was a knight during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Ser Mallor attended the wedding tourney at Whitewalls. His squire was attacked by Aegon_V_Targaryen after he made a remark about Maekar_I_Targaryen killing Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II).

name: Mallor the Dornishman
bio: Mallor the Dornishman is a sellsword in service to House Lannister.

name: Mallow
bio: Septon Mallow was a member of the Faith of the Seven that lived in the Vale during the Andal invasion. He wrote an account of the Battle of the Seven Stars.

name: Malo Jayn
bio: Malo Jayn is a sellsword and a serjeant in service to the Golden Company.

name: Malora Hightower
bio: Malora Hightower, called the Mad Maid, is a noblewoman of House Hightower and the eldest daughter of Lord Leyton_Hightower of Oldtown.

name: Malthar Xaq
bio: Malthar Xaq was a prince from the Summer Isles and the ruler of Koj. After the first contact with people from beyond the seas, the princes of the Summer Isles began the construction of large, strong ships that could cross long stretches of ocean and resist the fiercest storms. Prince Malthar was the greatest of these shipbuilders and is remembered as "Malthar the Windrider" and "Malthar the Mapmaker".

name: Malwyn Frey
bio: Malwyn Frey is a member of House Frey, the second son of Ser Raymund_Frey, the eleventh son of Lord Walder_Frey, and his wife Beony_Beesbury.

name: Mancaster
bio: Mancaster was an archmaester of the Citadel and the author of Sea Demons: A History of the Children of the Drowned God of the Isles.

name: Mance Rayder
bio: Mance Rayder was once a man of the Night's Watch before breaking his vows and abandoning his post. He has since become extremely influential among the free folk, and is called the "King-Beyond-the-Wall". In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Mance is portrayed by Ciarán Hinds. According to Selyse_Florent, Mance's parents were a common woman of the free folk and a man of the Night's Watch. After a group of raiders were put to the sword when Mance was a child, he was taken by the Night's Watch and raised as one of them. According to a semi-canon source, his last name of "Rayder" comes from this origin. A ranger of the Shadow Tower, Mance often sang of Bael_the_Bard after returning from rangings beyond the Wall. Some time between 286 AC and 288 AC, Lord_Commander_Qorgyle traveled to Winterfell to meet with Lord Eddard_Stark, with Mance among the black brothers escorting him. In Winterfell, he encountered the young Robb_Stark and Jon_Snow playing a prank, and promised not to tell. Years later, Mance was attacked by a shadowcat while ranging, and was healed by a wildling woman, a Wisewoman's_daughter. While he recuperated, she mended his torn cloak with swatches of red silk. Upon returning to the Wall, Ser Denys_Mallister required Mance to replace his mended cloak with one of uniform black. This infringement of freedom caused Mance to abandon the Shadow Tower and live with the free folk in the way he wished. Mance spent years gaining gathering various tribes into one host, seeking support from clan mothers and magnars. He made peace between  Harma Dogshead and the Lord_of_Bones, the Hornfoots and the Nightrunners, and the men of the Frozen Shore and the ice-river clans. Opposed by five other would-be Kings-Beyond-the-Wall, Mance gained the support of Tormund of Ruddy Hall and Styr of Thenn and slew the three rivals who refused to submit. Mance is familiar with hiding places east of Long Lake from his secret expeditions into the north.

name: Mandon Moore
bio: Mandon Moore is a knight from House Moore. He is a member of King Robert_I_Baratheon's Kingsguard. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Mandon is portrayed by James Doran. Ser Mandon came from the Vale of Arryn and is familiar with Ser Vardis_Egen. He was brought to King's Landing by the Hand of the King, Lord Jon_Arryn, and King Robert_I_Baratheon named Mandon to the Kingsguard. Neither of the two are particularly fond of Mandon, however.

name: Manfred Dondarrion
bio: Manfred Dondarrion was a knight from House Dondarrion and the son and heir of Lord_Dondarrion_(father_of_Manfred) during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Manfred is portrayed by Daniel Monks. In 206 AC, Ser Manfred accompanied the host of his Lord_Dondarrion_(father_of_Manfred) and Lord Pearse_Caron that defeated the Vulture_King_(Daeron_II), burning him out of the Red Mountains. Manfred attended the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC. Having served with the Dondarrions against the Vulture King, Ser Duncan_the_Tall thought Manfred could vouch for his knighthood to the Plummer at the tourney, however Manfred did not remember him. Ser Duncan recited the origins of House Dondarrion hoping to impress him, but Manfred was uninterested and claimed that he could not help. Later, when Ser Duncan searched for a seventh knight for his trial of seven, Manfred ignored his call for help, making a comment to the noblewoman beside him.

name: Manfred Hightower
bio: Manfred Hightower[N 1] was the Lord of the Hightower and the head of House Hightower during Aegon's Conquest. He had at least four sons, including Addam_Hightower and Garmon_Hightower, and several daughters. When news arrived in Oldtown of the landing of Aegon_I_Targaryen and his sisters, Visenya_Targaryen and Rhaenys_Targaryen, the High_Septon_(Aegon's_Conquest) fasted and prayed for seven days and nights under the dome of the Starry Sept. When he emerged he announced that the Faith would not oppose the Targaryens, because the Crone had shown him that to do so would mean the destruction of Oldtown in dragonflame. Lord Manfred, who was a godly man, heeded the High Septon's prophecy when he heard it. He kept his forces at Oldtown and when Aegon marched south towards the city, he freely opened his gates submitting to Aegon's authority. Some say that Manfred also offered his youngest daughter's hand in marriage to Aegon_I_Targaryen, which Aegon declined so as not to offend his queens. Lord Manfred died during the First Dornish War, soon after the death of his son Garmon_Hightower in 10 AC, and was succeeded by another son, Ser Addam_Hightower.

name: Manfred Hightower
bio: Manfred Hightower was Lord of the Hightower and head of House Hightower during the reign of Kings Aegon_I_Targaryen and Aenys_I_Targaryen. He had two sons, Martyn_Hightower and Morgan_Hightower, and a daughter, Ceryse_Hightower. In 23 AC, when Queen Visenya_Targaryen suggested a betrothal between her son Maegor_I_Targaryen and her newborn niece Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), the High_Septon_(Aegon_I) objected to the match and suggested instead his own niece, Manfred's daughter Ceryse_Hightower. She and Maegor were wed in 25 AC. In 39 AC, when Maegor took a second bride by wedding Alys_Harroway, Lord Manfred made protest to King Aenys_I_Targaryen, demanding that Lady Alys be put aside. Manfred died in 41 AC and was succeeded by his son Martyn_Hightower.

name: Manfred Lothston
bio: Manfred Lothston was a member of House Lothston during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Manfred sided with Daemon_I_Blackfyre during the First Blackfyre Rebellion, but betrayed him in the end.

name: Manfred Swann
bio: Manfred Swann was the Lord of Stonehelm and the head of House Swann after the reign of King Aegon_V_Targaryen. Ser Barristan_Selmy squired for Ser Manfred in his youth. Manfred Swann became the Lord of Stonehelm no earlier than 260 AC.[N 1]

name: Manfrey Martell
bio: Manfrey Martell is a knight of House Martell and a cousin to Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne. Manfrey is the castellan of Sunspear. When Prince Doran_Martell withdrew to the Water Gardens in 298 AC, he left Manfrey as castellan of Sunspear. Although Prince Oberyn_Martell had command in Doran's absence, Manfrey helped to govern with Maester Myles, Ricasso the seneschal, and the treasurer Alyse_Ladybright.

name: Manfryd Lothston
bio: Manfryd Lothston, better known as Manfryd o' the Black Hood, was the son of Lord Lucas_Lothston_(father_of_Manfryd) of Harrenhal. He is not remembered fondly.

name: Manfryd Merlyn
bio: Manfryd Merlyn is the captain of the ship Kite in the Iron Fleet and is under the command of Victarion_Greyjoy.

name: Manfryd Mooton
bio: Manfryd Mooton was the Lord of Maidenpool and the head of House Mooton during the Dance of the Dragons and the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, a character named Ser Manfryd Mooton is portrayed by Turlough Convery.

name: Manfryd Redwyne
bio: Manfryd Redwyne was the Lord of the Arbor and the head of House Redwyne during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Lord Manfryd was named master of ships, as well as lord admiral, by King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen after the former master of ships, Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan), was raised to Hand of the King. This made Manfryd the first man raised to the position of master of ships that was not a member of House Velaryon. Manfryd had at least three sons, all squires when he brought them to court with him in 50 AC: Robert_Redwyne, Rickard_Redwyne, and Ryam_Redwyne. After Daemon resigned the office of Hand, some suggested that rivalry with Lord Manfryd played a part in his decision. Lord Manfryd served as master of ships until his own death in 89 AC. His eldest son, Robert, succeeded him as Lord of the Arbor whereas Daemon's grandson, Corlys_Velaryon, followed him as master of ships.

name: Manfryd Yew
bio: Manfryd Yew is a landed knight and the head of House Yew.

name: Manly Stokeworth
bio: Manly Stokeworth was a member of House Stokeworth who served as Commander of the City Watch of King's Landing. Manly once led the gold cloaks. After Manly's death, Lord Jon_Arryn, the Hand of the King to Robert_I_Baratheon, named Janos_Slynt—then captain of the Iron Gate—as the new commander. Whether Manly perished in the Sack of King's Landing during Robert's Rebellion, or later on during Robert's reign, is unknown.

name: Mara Manderly
bio: Mara Manderly was a noblewoman of House Manderly and a daughter of Lord Theomore_Manderly. Mara served as a companion to Alysanne_Targaryen at Dragonstone when the queen was pregnant with Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I) in 55 AC.

name: Mara Martell
bio: Mara Nymeros Martell was the ruling Princess of Dorne and head of House Martell during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Mara became the ruling Princess of Dorne in 83 AC after the death of Prince Morion_Martell in the Fourth Dornish War.

name: Marei
bio: Marei is a whore at Chataya's brothel in King's Landing. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Marei is portrayed by Josephine Gillan.

name: Margaery Tyrell
bio: Margaery Tyrell is the only daughter of Lord Mace_Tyrell and his wife, Lady Alerie_Hightower. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Margaery is portrayed by Natalie Dormer. Margaery was born at Highgarden in 283 AC to Lord Mace_Tyrell and Lady Alerie_Hightower. She has three older brothers, Willas_Tyrell, Ser Garlan_Tyrell, and Ser Loras_Tyrell. When she was a little girl, Willas used to read to her, and draw her pictures of the stars. Margaery has been riding horses since she was old enough to walk.

name: Margaret Karstark
bio: Margaret Karstark was a noblewoman from House Karstark. She married Bennard_Stark, second son of Lord Benjen_Stark_(lord) and Lady Lysa_Locke, with whom she had three sons; Benjen_Stark_(son_of_Bennard), Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Bennard) and Elric_Stark.

name: Margate
bio: Archmaester Margate is an archmaester of the Citadel. He has suggested that the tales of freezing mists, ice ships and Cannibal Bay told by sailors who have visited the Shivering Sea are distorted reports of ice dragon activity.

name: Marghaz zo Loraq
bio: Marghaz zo Loraq is a Meereenese noble from the House of Loraq. He is a cousin to Hizdahr_zo_Loraq.

name: Margot Lannister
bio: Lady Margot Lannister is a member of House Lannister, a distant cousin of the main branch. She is married to Lord Titus_Peake of Starpike.

name: Marianne Vance
bio: Marianne Vance is the daughter of Ser Dafyn_Vance and Maegelle_Frey.

name: Maric Seaworth
bio: Maric Seaworth is the fourth son of Ser Davos_Seaworth and his wife Marya_Seaworth. He is the oarmaster on the war galley Fury.

name: Marilda of Hull
bio: Marilda of Hull, also called Mouse, was a trader and captain from Hull on Driftmark. She was the mother of Ser Addam_Velaryon, a dragonrider, and Alyn_Velaryon, Lord of the Tides. Marilda was the daughter of a shipwright at Hull. She was frequently seen at the shipyards, often underfoot. Marilda was sixteen years old when she gave birth to Addam_Velaryon in 114 AC, and she was barely eighteen when Alyn_Velaryon was birthed in 115 AC. She refused to name the father of her sons. After her father's death a decade later, Marilda sold his shipyard and used the proceeds to captain a trading cog she called Mouse. By 130 AC Marilda owned seven ships, which her sons served on. When Prince Jacaerys_Velaryon called for dragonseeds during the Dance of the Dragons, Marilda claimed that her children, who had silver hair and purple eyes, had been fathered by Ser Laenor_Velaryon, who had died in 120 AC. Grand Maester Munkun and Septon Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons) agree with Marilda's claim, but Mushroom wrote that Addam and Alyn had actually been fathered by Laenor's own sire, Lord Corlys_Velaryon, a frequent visitor to Driftmark's shipyards. After Addam became a dragonrider on Seasmoke, who was Laenor's own mount, Corlys and Jacaerys convinced Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen to legitimize the bastard brothers as Velaryons. Addam eventually died in the Second Battle of Tumbleton. After the death of Rhaenyra in the fall of Dragonstone and the pardoning of Corlys by Queen Alicent_Hightower, Alyn agreed to transport King Aegon_II_Targaryen from Dragonstone back to King's Landing. Protected by a dozen Velaryon galleys, Marilda sailed the king to the capital aboard Mouse. When Corlys died in King's Landing in 132 AC, Marilda and Lord Alyn sailed his body back to Driftmark aboard Mermaid's Kiss. Corlys's remains were then buried at sea near Dragonstone aboard Sea Snake. Marilda constructed the Mouse House at Hull during the Lysene Spring. When Alyn Oakenfist led a fleet of merchantmen to Dorne and the Free Cities in 136 AC, the Lord of the Tides commanded from a galley named Bold Marilda.

name: Marillion
bio: Marillion is a singer and a musician. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Marillion is portrayed by Emun Elliott. Marillion was in King's Landing for the tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day. He earned a great deal of silver with his singing during the tourney, but lost it when he bet that Ser Jaime_Lannister would win his joust against Ser Loras_Tyrell.

name: Maris
bio: Mother Maris was the leader of a motherhouse in Gulltown during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Maris taught generations of orphaned girls at a motherhouse in Gulltown. The building later came to be called the Motherhouse of Maris. After wedding his sister, Alysanne_Targaryen, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen sent seven preachers to walk the realm and win smallfolk over to the marriage, not with swords, but with words. Mother Maris was among these seven. They traveled the Seven Kingdoms alone, walking from village to village, telling smallfolk of Jaehaerys's wisdom and Alysanne's kindness. When challenged on the issue of incest, the seven would say the Valyrians mounted dragons and thus were not like other men, and as such, an exception had to be made.

name: Maris the Maid
bio: Maris the Maid, called the Most Fair, is a legendary daughter of Garth_Greenhand and ancestor of House Hightower. Her beauty was so renowned that fifty lords vied for her hand in the first tourney held in Westeros. The victor was Argoth_Stone-Skin, but Maris wed King Uthor_of_the_High_Tower before he could claim her, and Argoth spent the rest of days raging outside the walls of Oldtown, roaring for his bride.

name: Maris Baratheon
bio: Maris Baratheon was a noblewoman from House Baratheon during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen and Aegon_III_Targaryen. She was one of the four daughters of Lord Borros_Baratheon of Storm's End. She and her sisters were known as the Four Storms. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Prince Aemond_Targaryen was sent by the green council to Storm's End to gain the support of its lord for the greens. Lord Borros offered the hand of any of his four daughters to the prince, noting that Maris would make a clever wife. Aemond agreed to a betrothal, though the identity of the daughter is not known, save it was not Maris. The lord and prince were haggling over dates and dowries the morning Prince Lucerys_Velaryon arrived as an envoy representing the blacks. Lucerys claimed he could not marry Maris or any of her sisters due to his betrothal to his cousin, Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon), and was sent away by Lord Borros. After Aemond and Lucerys started to confront one another, Maris taunted Aemond, asking if it was his eye that Lucerys had taken, "or one of [his] balls." Aemond was enraged, and took his leave. Mushroom claims that Prince Aemond cut out the eyes from Lucerys's corpse after killing him, and presented them to Maris on a bed of seaweed, though Archmaester Gyldayn notes that this seems excessive. By the time of King Aegon_III_Targaryen's coronation, Maris had joined the silent sisters. Mushroom claims that her mother, Lady Elenda, had Maris's tongue removed, but Gyldayn notes that this can be discounted, as the belief all silent sisters are tongueless is no more than a myth.

name: Black Maris
bio: Black Maris is a spearwife of the free folk.

name: Marissa Frey
bio: Marissa Frey is a noblewoman of House Frey and the third daughter of Merrett_Frey and Mariya_Darry.

name: Mariya Darry
bio: Mariya Darry is a noblewoman of House Darry, the wife of Merrett_Frey, the ninth son of Lord Walder_Frey of the Twins. They have four children who lived past infancy. Lord Walder_Frey managed to arrange a marriage between his ninth son, Merrett_Frey, and one of the daughters of the Lord_Darry_(father_of_Raymun), Mariya. The marriage was an excellent match for Merrett, for the Darrys were a wealthy and prominent house that stood high in the favor of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. However, almost immediately after the marriage, Robert's Rebellion broke out in 282 AC, leading to the fall of House Targaryen; the Darrys suffered grievously for their staunch support of the Mad King, costing them half their lands and most of their wealth. Since the start of their marriage, Mariya quickly found Merrett to be a great disappointment and, to Merrett's displeasure, the marriage initially only produced daughters, as well as a stillbirth and a girl that died in infancy, before finally producing a son, called Walder_Frey_(son_of_Merrett).

name: Mark Mullendore
bio: Mark Mullendore is a knight from House Mullendore in the Reach. His relation to Martyn_Mullendore, the Lord of Uplands and the head of House is unknown.

name: Mark Ryswell
bio: Mark Ryswell was a knight from House Ryswell. His relation to Rodrik_Ryswell, Lord of the Rills, is unknown. Mark was one of Lord Eddard_Stark's companions, fighting alongside him during Robert's Rebellion. He was slain at the tower of joy in the Red Mountains of Dorne during a fight with three of King Aerys_II_Targaryen's Kingsguard. Afterwards, Eddard had the tower pulled down, and used its stones for cairns for the fallen.

name: Marla Prester
bio: Marla Prester was a member of House Prester, and the second wife of Ser Jason_Lannister_(son_of_Gerold). According to semi-canon sources, after the death of Alys_Stackspear in childbirth, Jason Lannister was quickly married to Marla Prester, who was twice his age, to prevent him from fathering more bastards. She gave birth to Joanna_Lannister, Stafford_Lannister, two more sons and two more daughters.

name: Marla Sunderland
bio: Marla Sunderland was a noblewoman of House Sunderland who briefly ruled the Three Sisters as queen. At the start of Aegon's Conquest, the lords of the Three Sisters took advantage of the chaos in Westeros and rebelled against young King Ronnel Arryn, installing Lady Marla as their queen and launching the Sistermen's Rebellion. As their fleet had largely been destroyed during the Conquest, the Arryns had little means of quelling the rebellion themselves. With the Conquest over and the Vale now part of the Seven Kingdoms, King Aegon_I_Targaryen tasked Lord Torrhen_Stark, newly named by the Conqueror as Warden of the North, with suppressing the Sistermen's Rebellion. Led by Ser Warrick_Manderly, the northern host sailed to the Three Sisters aboard galleys hired from Braavos. The sight of the sails, and the sudden appearance of Queen Visenya_Targaryen mounted on her dragon Vhagar, took the heart out of the Sistermen. Queen Marla was deposed by her own people with her younger brother Steffon_Sunderland bending the knee and renewing fealty to House Arryn. The deposed Marla was exiled and imprisoned. After five years, in 6 AC, she had her tongue removed and ended her days with the silent sisters, tending to the noble dead.

name: Marlon Manderly
bio: Marlon Manderly is a knight of House Manderly and cousin to the head of the house, Lord Wyman_Manderly. He is the commander of the garrison at the New Castle in White Harbor.

name: Marna Locke
bio: Marna Locke was a noblewoman from House Locke. She married Lord Edwyle_Stark, with whom she had one son, Rickard_Stark.

name: Maron Botley
bio: Maron Botley, also known as Old Botley or Fishwhiskers, is a member of House Botley. He has three sons who accompany him on raids.

name: Maron Greyjoy
bio: Maron Greyjoy was the second son of Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands, and Lady Alannys_Harlaw. He died during Greyjoy's Rebellion. According to Asha_Greyjoy, Theon_Greyjoy lived in awe and fear of their elder brothers, Rodrik_Greyjoy and Maron. Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands, rose in rebellion against Robert_I_Baratheon, thinking the new king did not have great support amongst the nobility of the Seven Kingdoms and that Balon would be allowed to go his own way. Rodrik was slain during the storming of Seagard, however. When Robert marshaled his forces and invaded the Iron Islands, Maron was along the walls where Robert's breach was made along the old south tower during the battle at Pyke. Maron was crushed when the tower collapsed during the fighting. Balon blames Eddard_Stark, Lord of Winterfell, for the deaths of Rodrik and Maron during Greyjoy's Rebellion, though neither was actually slain by Eddard. Alannys_Harlaw, Balon's grieving wife, eventually began to roam the halls of Pyke in search of her lost sons, even walking the rope bridge to the Sea Tower while barefoot; a maester often had to draw splinters from her heels.

name: Maron Martell
bio: Maron Nymeros Martell was the ruling Prince of Dorne and head of House Martell during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In 161 AC, Prince_of_Dorne_(father_of_Maron), the Prince of Dorne, signed the marriage treaty which brought peace to Dorne after four years of war. In the treaty, which he made with King Baelor_I_Targaryen, he agreed to marry his daughter and eldest child, Princess Myriah_Martell, to Prince Daeron_II_Targaryen, the grandson of Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, Baelor's uncle and Hand. This agreement removed Myriah from the succession to the throne of Dorne, making Maron the Prince's new heir. By 174 AC, Maron had succeeded his father as Prince of Dorne. Baelor's cousin, King Aegon_IV_Targaryen, was notoriously corrupt, and showed ambitions of war and conquest towards Dorne. His son and heir, Prince Daeron, opposed him and spoke out against his father's misrule, with the backing of his allies, Prince Maron chief among them. After the death of Aegon IV in 184 AC, Daeron, now King Daeron II Targaryen, began one of his earliest significant acts after assuming the throne — negotiations with Prince Maron, his brother-in-law and ally, to bring Dorne under the rule of House Targaryen. The negotiations lasted two years, and the treaty included the proposal of a marriage between Maron and Daeron's sister, Princess Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_IV), once she was of age. This marriage would formally make Dorne part of the Seven Kingdoms. Prince Maron's wedding to Princess Daenerys was held a year later, in 187 AC, in King's Landing. After the ceremony, Prince Maron knelt before the Iron Throne to swear his oaths of fealty. King Daeron raised up Maron to great acclaim, and together they went to pay their respects to the statue of the late King Baelor the Blessed at the Great Sept, laying a golden wreath at his feet and proclaiming, "Baelor, your work is done." To celebrate the wedding, a tourney was held in the capital, where Maron's nephew, Prince Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II), won the name "Breakspear" and the victory, defeating Daemon_I_Blackfyre in the lists in the final tilt. Prince Maron had won a few concessions in his accord with King Daeron II to unite Dorne with the Seven Kingdoms. House Martell held significant rights and privileges that the other Great Houses of Westeros did not — the right to keep their royal title first among them, but also the autonomy to maintain their own laws, the right to assess and gather the taxes with only irregular oversight from the crown, and other such matters. Certain lords were dissatisfied by these concessions, and the belief that Dorne held too much influence over the king, as King Daeron brought many Dornishmen to the court, granting some notable offices. Some were particularly incensed by King Daeron's marriage to a Dornishwoman, and how he had "sold his sister to the Prince of Dorne", believing that Daenerys had loved her half-brother, Daemon Blackfyre, and that King Dareon had denied them. These elements were among the seeds that led to the First Blackfyre Rebellion in 196 AC, led by Daemon.

name: Maron Martell's son
bio: Maron Martell's son was the eldest child and heir of Maron_Martell, Prince of Dorne, and his wife, Princess Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_IV). The eldest son of Prince Maron and Princess Daenerys was their heir, and he had multiple younger siblings. After observing the children at play in the Water Gardens, his mother came to the realization that she could not tell the high-born children from the low-born and concluded that all the children were deserving of long life, love, and protection. Showing her son the children in the Water Gardens, Daenerys told him that they were his realm and he must remember them in everything that he did. He presumably succeeded his father as Prince of Dorne.

name: Maron Volmark
bio: Maron Volmark is the Lord of Volmark and the head of House Volmark.

name: Marq Ambrose
bio: Marq Ambrose was the head[N 1] of House Ambrose during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. During the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons, Marq was loyal to the greens. When the Caltrops assembled to conspire against the Two Betrayers, Marq was of the opinion to take King's Landing before killing them. Marq was slain two days later during the Second Battle of Tumbleton.

name: Marq Farman
bio: Marq Farman was the Lord of Fair Isle and the head of House Farman during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen and the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Marq was born a second son of the Lord of Fair Isle. His father and elder brother perished during the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye while fighting for Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I), which made Marq the new lord. His eldest son and now his heir, Ser Franklyn_Farman, returned the bodies of his kin to Fair Isle for burial. In 47 AC Lord Marq hosted Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). During the princess's stay at Faircastle, she befriended his daughter Elissa_Farman and was courted by his second son Androw_Farman. They married in 49 AC; however, there were tensions between them and Lord Marq's heir, Ser Franklyn. Marq died in 50 AC, choking on a fish bone at his own table. He was forty-six years old at the time of his death.

name: Marq Grafton
bio: Marq Grafton was a member of House Grafton during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. At the start of Robert's Rebellion, Lord Grafton stayed loyal to King Aerys_II_Targaryen instead of following his liege, Lord Jon_Arryn of the Eyrie, into rebellion. Marq was slain by Lord Robert_I_Baratheon at the Gulltown walls when the rebels took the city.

name: Marq Piper
bio: Marq Piper is a knight from House Piper. He is the heir of Clement_Piper, Lord of Pinkmaiden Castle. Ser Daven_Lannister once beat the elder brother of Lewys_Piper, presumably Marq, in a mêlée.

name: Marq Merryweather
bio: Marq Merryweather was the Lord of Longtable and the head of House Merryweather during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. During the Great Council of 136 AC, Lord Marq was among three regents selected by lot to serve in the regency for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Marq was considered an inoffensive, though undistinguished, choice. While the regents and Lord Torrhen_Manderly, the Hand of the King, were discussing Aegon's first royal progress, the young king entered the room and dismissed them all, for he had come of age and no longer wanted their oversight.

name: Marq Mudd
bio: Marq Mudd, known as the Mad Bard, was an alleged member of House Mudd who claimed to be a river king in the generations after the riverlands were conquered by the Storm King Arlan_III_Durrandon. Marq had a brief, inglorious reign before being put down. His connection with the extinct Mudd dynasty is unconfirmed.

name: Marq Rankenfell
bio: Marq Rankenfell, better known as Mad Marq Rankenfell, is a former Lord Commander of the Night's Watch. He is considered one of the worst commanders the Night's Watch ever had.

name: Marqelo Tagaros
bio: Marqelo Tagaros was one of the ruling triarchs of the Free City of Volantis during the reign of Tommen_II_Lannister, King of the Rock. According to a chronicle titled The Glory of Volantis, Tagaros dispatched a squadron toward Valyria to find any sign of the golden fleet of Tommen_II_Lannister a year after he sailed away to the Valyrian peninsula, but the squadron returned empty handed.

name: Marra Rogare
bio: Marra Rogare was a member of House Rogare, a Lysene noble banking family. She was the daughter of Lysandro_Rogare, the First Magister of Lys. Marra was the daughter of Lysandro Rogare. She had six brothers, Lysaro_Rogare, Moredo_Rogare, Lotho_Rogare, Drako_Rogare, Fredo_Rogare, and Roggerio_Rogare, and two sisters, Larra_Rogare and Lysara_Rogare, as well as sixteen bastard siblings. After the death of her father, Lysandro_Rogare, Marra inherited his library. When the Rogare Bank ran dry in 135 AC, Lysaro Rogare's brothers and sisters claimed to have no part in it, but many doubted their claims of innocence. She claimed sanctuary at the Temple of Yndros in man's garb. When the Rogares were tried, Marra was deprived of the books she had inherited.

name: Marrigo
bio: Marrigo is a pit fighter in Meereen.

name: Marro
bio: Marro is a mummer in Braavos. He is part of the mummer's troupe at the Gate.

name: Marselen
bio: Marselen is a Naathi member of the Unsullied. His living siblings include Mossador and Missandei. Missandei and her three brothers, including Marselen and Mossador, were captured on their native island of Naath by raiders from the Basilisk Isles, and sold into slavery in Astapor. Missandei was trained as a scribe, but Marselen and his two brothers were gelded and trained by the Good Masters to be Unsullied. Marselen and Mossador became Unsullied, and the third brother died at some point.

name: Marsella Waynwood
bio: Marsella Waynwood was a noblewoman of House Waynwood. Her relation to Anya_Waynwood, the Lady of Ironoaks, is unknown. She was the third wife of Ser Stevron_Frey, and had one son by him, Walton_Frey.

name: Marston Waters
bio: Marston Waters was a knight of Aegon_II_Targaryen's Kingsguard. He served briefly as Lord Commander and Hand of the King for Aegon_III_Targaryen.

name: Prince of Dorne (father of Maron)
bio: Prince Nymeros Martell was the ruling Prince of Dorne and the head of House Martell during the reign of kings Daeron_I_Targaryen and Baelor_I_Targaryen. He ruled Dorne when the Young Dragon, King Daeron_I_Targaryen, started his conquest of Dorne in 157 AC. The following year, he and forty of the most powerful Dornish lords bent their knees in the Submission of Sunspear. After several long years of war, Daeron's brother and successor, King Baelor_I_Targaryen, walked barefoot from King's Landing to Sunspear in 161 AC to forge a peace between the Iron Throne and Dorne. The Prince accepted Baelor's proposal of peace, and betrothed his daughter and heir, Myriah_Martell, to Baelor's cousin, Prince Daeron_II_Targaryen. After the terms were arranged, he offered Baelor a galley to take him back to King's Landing, but Baelor insisted to walk. The Prince made arrangements to insure Baelor's safely on his way back and commanded Lord_Wyl_(conquest_of_Dorne) to released Baelor's cousin, Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II). By 174 AC, his son, Maron_Martell, had succeeded him as Prince of Dorne and Lord of Sunspear.

name: Princess of Dorne (mother of Doran)
bio: The Princess of Dorne was the unnamed ruler of Dorne during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. She was the mother of Doran_Martell, Elia_Martell, and Oberyn_Martell. Her brother was Prince Lewyn_Martell of the Kingsguard. In 247 AC or 248 AC, the Princess gave birth to her firstborn child, Doran_Martell. In the years that followed, she gave birth to two more sons, Mors_Martell and Olyvar_Martell, who died during infancy, and she miscarried several times. Nine years after the birth of Doran, the Princess gave birth to a daughter, Elia_Martell. Although born one month premature, the girl survived. A year later, the Princess gave birth to another son, Oberyn_Martell. The Princess was one of the ladies-in-waiting of Princess Rhaella_Targaryen. She became friends with Joanna_Lannister, another of Rhaella's ladies.[N 1] In 270 AC, during a visit to Sunspear, King Aerys_II_Targaryen told the Princess that he would "make the Dornish deserts bloom" by digging a great underground canal beneath the Red Mountains to bring water down from the rainwood. But like most of his grandiose plans, it was quickly forgotten. Several years later, the Princess was accompanied on a journey by her daughter Elia, her younger son Oberyn, and her consort. From Dorne, they travelled to several potential suitors for both Elia and Oberyn. Amongst the places they visited were Starfall, the Arbor, Oldtown, the Shield Islands, Crakehall, and finally Casterly Rock. Although she had learned of Joanna Lannister's Tyrion_Lannister while at Oldtown in 273 AC, the Princess decided to travel onwards to Casterly Rock. Oberyn believes that his mother had planned with Joanna to marry either Elia or Oberyn to one of Joanna's twins, Jaime_Lannister and Cersei_Lannister, since there had been an empty cabin on their ship, meant for someone of high birth. Waiting until it was politely decent, the Princess eventually broached the subject of marriage to Lord Tywin_Lannister, Joanna's husband. She first suggested a match between Cersei and Oberyn, next a match between Elia and Jaime. Tywin refused both matches brusquely, informing the Princess how Cersei was meant to marry the crown prince, Rhaegar_Targaryen. When Tywin suggested his newborn dwarf son Tyrion_Lannister to be betrothed to Elia instead, the Princess took it as an insult. In 279 AC, the Princess succeeded in betrothing Elia to Rhaegar. By the time Elia married the prince in early 280 AC, the Princess had already died.[N 2] On her deathbed, she revealed to her son Oberyn how Tywin Lannister had brusquely refused her suggestions for a betrothal, and that she felt she had defeated him with Elia's betrothal to Rhaegar.

name: Martyn Cassel
bio: Martyn Cassel was a member of House Cassel and the brother of Ser Rodrik_Cassel. Martyn fathered four sons, but only Jory_Cassel lived to be a man. Martyn was one of Lord Eddard_Stark's companions, fighting alongside him during Robert's Rebellion. He was slain at the tower of joy in the Red Mountains of Dorne during a fight with three of King Aerys_II_Targaryen's Kingsguard. Afterwards, Eddard had the tower pulled down, and used its stones for cairns for the fallen.

name: Martyn Hightower
bio: Martyn Hightower was Lord of the Hightower and head of House Hightower at the beginning of the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. He was the brother of Queen Ceryse_Hightower, one of Maegor's wives. Martyn inherited the Hightower in 41 AC, following the death of his father, Lord Manfred_Hightower_(son_of_Addam). In 43 AC[N 1] when King Maegor, Queen Dowager Visenya, their dragons, and the royal army made their way to Oldtown during the Faith Militant uprising, Lord Martyn called his banners with a fire atop the Hightower. By the morning of their arrival, however, the city gates were open, and the banners of Houses Targaryen, Tyrell, and Hightower flew atop the city walls. After word of the sudden death of the High_Septon_(Aenys_I) reached him, Martyn at once dispatched his own knights to arrest and disarm all of the Warrior's Sons, including his brother Ser Morgan_Hightower. Martyn sent his men to rouse the Most Devout from their beds and march them at spearpoint to the Starry Sept to choose a new High Septon. Through unspecified circumstances, by the end of Maegor's reign, Martyn died and was succeeded by Lord Donnel_Hightower, whose relationship to Martyn has not been specified.

name: Martyn Hightower
bio: Martyn Hightower was a squire from House Hightower during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Martyn was born to Lord Ormund_Hightower and his first wife, who died in childbed at some point. He had an elder brother, Lyonel_Hightower, a younger brother, Garmund_Hightower, and a sister, Bethany_Hightower. During the Dance of the Dragons, his father married again to Samantha_Tarly, known as Lady Sam. Martyn was sent to be a squire to Lord_Redwyne on the Arbor.

name: Martyn Lannister
bio: Martyn Lannister is a squire from House Lannister. He is the third son of Ser Kevan_Lannister and Lady Dorna_Swyft and twin to Willem_Lannister. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Martyn is portrayed by Dean-Charles Chapman. Lord Gawen_Westerling once proposed that his daughter Jeyne_Westerling wed Martyn or his brother, Willem_Lannister, but the twins' father, Ser Kevan_Lannister, refused because of House Westerling's connection with House Spicer.

name: Martyn Mullendore
bio: Martyn Mullendore is the Lord of Uplands and the head of House Mullendore.

name: Martyn Rivers
bio: Martyn Rivers is a bastard son of Lord Walder_Frey. Martyn's mother has not been mentioned.

name: Martyn Tyrell
bio: Martyn Tyrell was a member of House Tyrell during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He was a younger son of Lord Bertrand_Tyrell of Highgarden and was married to the clever Florence_Fossoway. After the murder of Lord Rego_Draz, a new master of coin was needed. Septon Barth put forward Martyn who he knew from his time serving at Highgarden; while not praising his wits, he mentioned that Martyn's wife, the Lady Florence_Fossoway, had been handling the Reach's accounts for years and had increased the incomes of Highgarden by a third. King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen agreed with the septon and summoned Martyn and his wife to court, believing that Queen Alysanne_Targaryen would be fond of the intelligent Lady Florence. Martyn—aided and abetted by his clever wife—proved almost as able as Rego, the so-called Lord of Air, had been as master of coin. This made the Seven Kingdoms prosperous and allowed King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen to expand his project of roads, creating the roseroad, the ocean road, the river road, and the goldroad once the kingsroad was completed.

name: Marwyn
bio: Marwyn, also known as Marwyn the Mage, is an archmaester of the Citadel. Marwyn has traveled the lands beyond the narrow sea. He spent eight years in the east, mapping distant lands, searching for lost books, and studying with warlocks and shadowbinders. He encountered Mirri_Maz_Duur during his travels in Asshai, and taught her the Common Tongue and the secrets of the human body. Marwyn's accounts of his travels confirmed reports that there are no living animals in Asshai. Marwyn was nicknamed "Marwyn the Mage" by Archmaester Vaellyn after his return to the Citadel. The name was soon all over Oldtown, much to Vaellyn's displeasure. Before Qyburn was expelled from the Citadel for necromancy and vivisection, Marwyn was the only archmaester who liked his thoughts about dead souls leaving some residue in this world. Maryn is rumored to have killed a man with his fists. Marwyn is the author of a book, The Book of Lost Books.

name: Marwyn Belmore
bio: Marwyn Belmore is a knight from House Belmore.

name: Mary Mertyns
bio: Mary Mertyns is the Lady of Mistwood and the head of House Mertyns.

name: Marya Seaworth
bio: Marya Seaworth is the wife of Ser Davos_Seaworth, and mother of his seven sons. Marya is the daughter of a carpenter. She married Davos_Seaworth, who was a smuggler at the time. They had seven children together: Dale_Seaworth, Allard_Seaworth, Matthos_Seaworth, Maric_Seaworth, Devan_Seaworth (b. 287 AC), Stannis_Seaworth (b. 289 AC or 290 AC), and Steffon_Seaworth (b. 293 AC). In 283 AC, after the end of Siege of Storm's End during Robert's Rebellion, Davos was knighted by Stannis_Baratheon, and received a keep and lands at Cape Wrath. Marya became mistress of the keep, where she now resides with their two youngest children. Allard's ship, Lady Marya, is probably named after his mother.

name: Maryam
bio: Maryam was a septa at Dragonstone and a member of Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I)'s household. Septa Maryam was one of Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) newer companions at the time of her death in 54 AC. At first, it was thought she had died of an illness. Later, it was revealed that she had been poisoned with the tears of Lys by Rhaena's husband, Androw_Farman, who was jealous of Rhaena's friendships with her ladies and weary of their mockery toward him. Rhaena much lamented her death, and Septa Edyth was sent to Dragonstone in her place.

name: Masha Heddle
bio: Masha Heddle is the innkeeper of the crossroads inn in the riverlands. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Masha is portrayed by Susie Kelly. Masha let Septon Meribald sleep beside the hearth when she did not have room for him, and gave him food when he left the inn. At some point, Masha told him the history of the inn. When Catelyn_Stark was a child, Masha was kind to her, and gave her and other children sweet cakes.

name: Maslyn
bio: Maslyn is a steward of the Night's Watch.

name: Master of the White Hart
bio: The master of the White Hart is a ship captain of the royal fleet, in service at King's Landing.

name: Matarys Targaryen
bio: Matarys Targaryen, called  the Even Younger Prince by Lord Gawen_Swann's fool, was the second son of Prince Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II) and Lady Jena_Dondarrion. As the second son to King Daeron_II_Targaryen's heir, Matarys was in line for the Iron Throne. While his brother Valarr_Targaryen was nicknamed the Young Prince, Matarys was dubbed the Even Younger Prince by Lord Gawen_Swann's fool. However, after his father Baelor had died in 209 AC during the Ashford Tourney, the Great Spring Sickness struck Westeros. Matarys died from the illness, as did his older brother Valarr_Targaryen and grandfather, King Daeron II, in King's Landing. This ended the line of Prince Baelor, and led to the ascent of his brother as King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Matarys was likely a teenager at the time of his death.[N 1] It is unknown whether Matarys passed away before or after his older brother Valarr. Matarys was mourned after his death. Septon Sefton_Staunton, who was in King's Landing during the Great Spring Sickness, recalled Matarys's among the thousands of deaths during that time. Later, a Hunchbacked_septon preaching against the new Hand of the King, Lord Brynden_Rivers, alluded to Matarys's death. Afer alluding to Bloodraven's history of kinslaying during the First Blackfyre Rebellion, the septon implied that both Matarys's death and the other recent deaths his near kin were Bloodraven's work too.

name: Mathis
bio: Mathis was a maester of the Citadel who wrote The Nine Voyages.

name: Mathis Frey
bio: Mathis Frey is a member of House Frey. He is the son of Ser Jammos_Frey and Sallei_Paege. He is the twin of Dickon_Frey

name: Mathis Rowan
bio: Mathis Rowan is the Lord of Goldengrove and the head of House Rowan. He is married to Lady Bethany_Redwyne and has three children by her, including at least Mathis_Rowan's_daughter. During Robert's Rebellion, Mathis stayed loyal to King Aerys_II_Targaryen. He assisted his liege lord, Mace_Tyrell, during the siege of Storm's End, which was defended by Stannis_Baratheon.
