Lord Eddard_Stark lifted the siege following the Sack of King's Landing, and Rowan bent the knee to the new king, Robert_I_Baratheon. Lord Mathis found Dareon in bed with Mathis_Rowan's_daughter, who was two years older than the singer. Under the eyes of her father, she claimed rape and Dareon was sent to the Wall to join the Night's Watch. Dareon insists the relationship was consensual.

name: Mathis Rowan's daughter
bio: Mathis Rowan's daughter is one of the children of Mathis_Rowan, Lord of Goldengrove, and Bethany_Redwyne. Her given name is unknown. Lord Mathis_Rowan found Dareon in bed with his daughter, who was two years older than the singer. Under the eyes of her father, she claimed rape and Dareon was sent to the Wall to join the Night's Watch.

name: Mathos II Arryn
bio: Mathos II Arryn was a King of Mountain and Vale from House Arryn. During the Rape of the Three Sisters, the name by which the Northern conquest of the isles is best known, the atrocities done by the Kings of Winter were horrible enough, that the remaining lords of the Three Sisters asked King Mathos II for his help, help he gladly gave. The help came, provided upon the condition that the Three Sisters agreed to do fealty to him and House Arryn and acknowledge the right of the Eyrie to rule them. His wife questioned the wisdom of fighting this War Across the Water, but he replied: "that he would sooner have a pirate than a wolf for his neighbour". He set sail for Sisterton with a hundred warships but he never returned. His sons carried on the war after him.

name: Mathos Mallarawan
bio: Mathos Mallarawan is a member of the Pureborn of Qarth.

name: Matrice
bio: Matrice is a servant in service to House Baratheon on Dragonstone.

name: Little Matt
bio: Little Matt is an inhabitant of the riverlands.

name: Matteno Orthys
bio: Matteno Orthys was a nobleman from House Orthys during the Lysene Spring. During the bloody power struggle in Lys after the downfall of the Rogare family, the cage of Matteno's shadowcat was unaccountably left open one night, and the cat mauled and partly devoured him.

name: Matthar
bio: Matthar, sometimes called Matt, is a recruit of the Night's Watch.

name: Matthar
bio: Archmaester Matthar was an archmaester at the Citadel. He wrote The Origins of the Iron Bank and Braavos.

name: Mattheus
bio: Mattheus was a septon of the Faith of the Seven and a member of the Most Devout during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Septon Mattheus claimed to be a descendant of House Gardener, the extinguished family who once ruled as Kings of the Reach. In 49 AC many felt he was almost guaranteed to be the next High Septon. He became a part of the court of King's Landing during the regency of the young King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Mattheus accompanied Lord Rogar_Baratheon and Alyssa_Velaryon to Dragonstone when the small council learned that King Jaehaerys and his younger sister, Alysanne_Targaryen, had fled to the island fortress to wed. The incestuous marriage enraged the septon, who threw insults at the couple. Jaehaerys would not allow his bride to be insulted, however, and ordered Mattheus to be silent or he would have his lips sewn shut. In 50 AC, Septon Mattheus was dismissed from court by Jaehaerys  and replaced with Septon Barth, who came from Highgarden. According to legend, Mattheus passed Barth coming in the other direction whilst crossing the Mander at Bitterbridge. After the death of the High_Lickspittle, Mattheus became a likely candidate to become his successor as High Septon. During the first of the four ballots required for the election, Mattheus was the lead candidate, but his supporters dwindled thereafter with each ballot. On the fourth ballot, Septon Alfyn_(septon) was elected.

name: Matthew Mullendore
bio: Matthew Mullendore was a knight of House Mullendore during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Matthew participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Matthos Seaworth
bio: Matthos Seaworth is the third son of Ser Davos_Seaworth and his wife Marya_Seaworth. He is the second on his father's ship, Black Betha. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Matthos is Davos's only son, and is portrayed by Kerr Logan.

name: Matthos Tyrell
bio: Matthos Tyrell was the Lord of Highgarden and the head of House Tyrell in the late reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Lord Matthos attended the Great Council of 101 AC with a huge retinue of five hundred bannermen, men-at-arms, and servants, outshining Lord Tymond_Lannister, who only numbered three hundred retainers.

name: Mawney
bio: Mawney is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Maynard
bio: Maynard was a knight slain by Lord Lymond_Lychester. He was held at a bridge by Lord Lymond_Lychester. Lord Lymond took six wounds holding the bridge against the other knight, before he killed Ser Maynard.

name: Maynard Holt
bio: Maynard Holt is a knight originally from House Holt. He is captain of Talon, a ship of the Night's Watch at Eastwatch-by-the-Sea.

name: Maynard Plumm
bio: Maynard Plumm was a hedge knight from House Plumm during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. He claimed to be distantly related to Lord Viserys_Plumm. Ser Maynard was among the hedge knights encamped by the Gods Eye near Whitewalls. He defended the actions of Lord Brynden_Rivers, the Hand of the King known as Bloodraven, to Ser Kyle the Cat and Ser Duncan_the_Tall. Maynard attended the wedding of Lord Ambrose_Butterwell and his Lady_Frey_(wife_of_Ambrose_Butterwell), but he did not take part in the tourney. After Duncan the Tall lost to Ser Uthor_Underleaf in the lists, Dunk refused Maynard's advice to leave. When Dunk was cornered by Lord Alyn_Cockshaw, Maynard watched until Alyn had fallen into the well, then proceeded to help the injured Duncan and inform him his Aegon_V_Targaryen was safe in the castle's sept. Maynard claimed that Bloodraven knew of the conspiracy to crown Daemon_II_Blackfyre, and the Second Blackfyre Rebellion was indeed squashed by Lord Brynden's army the next morning. Duncan, Kyle, and Ser Glendon Ball looked for Maynard, but he had disappeared during the night. Duncan praised Maynard's assistance when he spoke with Bloodraven.

name: Mazdhan zo Loraq
bio: Mazdhan zo Loraq, known as Mazdhan the Magnificent, was a famous Ghiscari citizen of the city of Meereen. Mazdhan is an ancestor of Hizdahr_zo_Loraq.

name: Mazor Alexi
bio: Mazor Alexi was the last High King of the Kingdom of Sarnor. Prior to the fall of Mardosh to the Dothraki, the kings of the Sarnori had remained divided. The destruction of the "City of Soldiers" and all its population led them to put aside their petty rivalries and ambitions, however, and unite under their High King. Mazor Alexi assembled a great army from up and down the Sarne and struck out east. In the tall grass between Sarnath and the ruins of Kasath, the Tall Men faced four khalasars, with a combined might of eighty thousand horselords, in a battle that would be known as the Field of Crows. The Sarnori chariots smashed through the center of the Dothraki horse, killing Khal Haro and causing his khalasar to retreat. Alexi and his mounted cavalry plunged in after them, followed by the Sarnori foot. This was a trap, however, as the fleeing Dothraki turned and unleashed a storm of arrows. Meanwhile, the khalasars of Khals Qano and Zhako swept in from north and south and Khal Loso and his screamers struck the Sarnori rear. Completely surrounded, Alexi's host was cut to pieces. A hundred thousand men died, Mazor Alexi among them.

name: Mebble
bio: Mebble, better known as Pinkeye, is a servant at Harrenhal.

name: Medgar Tully
bio: Medgar Tully[N 1] was the Lord of Riverrun and the head of House Tully during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen.  In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, he is portrayed by Russell Simpson. In 209 AC, Lord Medgar participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow. He challenged Ser Humfrey_Hardyng in his first tilt but was knocked from the saddle on the second lance. When they continued their bout on foot with swords, Humfrey beat Medgar into submission. Medgar died within two years after the tourney, because in 211 AC the Lord_Tully_(successor_of_Medgar) was a boy of eight.

name: Medger Cerwyn
bio: Medger Cerwyn is the Lord of Cerwyn and the head of House Cerwyn. He has a daughter, Jonelle_Cerwyn, and a son, Cley_Cerwyn. Medger and his son Cley_Cerwyn have often visited Winterfell.

name: Medrick
bio: Medrick is a maester of the Citadel in service to House Hornwood.

name: Medrick Manderly
bio: Medrick Manderly was the Lord of White Harbor and the head of House Manderly. The eldest son and heir of Lord Desmond Manderly, Medrick served as Lord of White Harbor for only four days before passing away from the Winter Fever. Prince Jacaerys_Velaryon visited White Harbor and Winterfell and enlisted the north for the blacks during the Dance of the Dragons. Lord Desmond Manderly sent his sons, Ser Medrick and Ser Torrhen_Manderly, to aid Rhaenyra_Targaryen in King's Landing. After Rhaenyra lost two dragons to the Two Betrayers, the Manderly brothers argued that Addam_Velaryon and Nettles were also untrustworthy. During the riots at King's Landing, Medrick led a hundred White Harbor men and restored order in the area between Aegon's High Hill and the Iron Gate. Medrick was one of The Seven Who Rode in an unsuccessful effort to rescue Prince Joffrey_Velaryon. The Manderly brothers accompanied Rhaenyra when she fled the capital after the Storming of the Dragonpit. Medrick asked Rhaenyra to travel to White Harbor, but she insisted on sailing instead from Duskendale to Dragonstone on the Violande without the Manderlys. During the Hour of the Wolf after the war, Medrick agreed to transport those men taking the black from King's Landing to White Harbor on his galley, North Star. Orwyle had escaped imprisonment, however, so Medrick sentenced a guard who had aided the vanished Grand Maester to join the Night's Watch in his place. Lord Desmond and the childless Medrick died of Winter Fever four days apart in 132 AC, causing his brother Torrhen to resign from the regency of Aegon III and return to White Harbor.

name: Medwick Tyrell
bio: Medwick Tyrell is a member of House Tyrell and a maester of the Citadel. He is the second son of Ser Luthor_Tyrell_(son_of_Moryn), and a first cousin once removed to Mace_Tyrell, the current Lord of Highgarden.

name: Meera Reed
bio: Meera Reed is a member of House Reed. She is Lord Howland_Reed's daughter and oldest child; her younger brother is Jojen_Reed. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Meera is portrayed by Ellie Kendrick.

name: Megette
bio: Megette, also known as Merry Meg, was the young and buxom wife of a blacksmith. She was the second of Aegon_IV_Targaryen's mistresses. While riding near Fairmarket in 155 AC Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen horse threw a shoe, and when he sought out the local smith, he came to notice the man's young wife. He went on to buy her for seven gold dragons along with the threat of Ser Joffrey_Staunton of the Kingsguard. Megette was installed in a house in King’s Landing; she and Aegon were even "wed" in a secret ceremony conducted by a mummer playing a septon. Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen put an end to it, returning Megette to her husband and placing their daughters with the Faith to be trained as septas. Megette was beaten to death within a year by the blacksmith.

name: Megga Tyrell
bio: Megga Tyrell is a member of a junior branch of House Tyrell. She is the granddaughter of Ser Quentin_Tyrell, a cousin of Mace_Tyrell, the Lord of Highgarden, and is the only daughter of Ser Olymer_Tyrell and his wife, Lysa_Meadows. Megga has two older brothers, Raymund_Tyrell and Rickard_Tyrell.

name: Meha
bio: Meha was the elder sister of Varamyr and Bump. Meha gave both her brothers their nicknames, per wildling tradition, while they were still in her mother's womb. Her first brother, who she named Lump, would eventually become the skinchanger Varamyr Sixskins.

name: Meizo Mahr
bio: Meizo Mahr is a eunuch in service to Salladhor_Saan.

name: Mela
bio: Mela is a serving woman in service with House Arryn at the Eyrie.

name: Melantha Blackwood
bio: Melantha Blackwood was a noblewoman from House Blackwood during the first half of the third century after Aegon's Conquest. Melantha married the widowed Willam_Stark, Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North, after the death of his first wife, Lady Lyanne_Glover. Together they had two children, Edwyle_Stark and Jocelyn_Stark.

name: Melaquin
bio: Melaquin was a maester in service at Summerhall during the reigns of Daeron_II_Targaryen and Aerys_I_Targaryen. Melaquin was a tutor to the sons of Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen. While a child, the future Aegon_V_Targaryen would re-enact the Battle of the Redgrass Field on Melaquin's green table with painted soldiers.

name: Melara Crane
bio: Melara Crane is a noblewoman of House Crane and is married to Lord Alester_Florent. She has one son, Alekyne_Florent, and two daughters, Melessa_Florent and Rhea_Florent. Melara is also the maternal grandmother of Samwell_Tarly.

name: Melara Hetherspoon
bio: Melara Hetherspoon was a noblewoman of House Hetherspoon and a childhood friend of Cersei_Lannister. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Melara is portrayed by Isabella Steinbarth. Melara was a ward at Casterly Rock, a childhood bedmaid of Cersei_Lannister, and her close friend during their youths. Another of Cersei's bedmaids, Jeyne_Farman, would always hang back or hide behind them. At Casterly Rock, Melara heard the serving girls say that the witch woman Maggy_the_Frog in Lannisport could curse a man, make him fall in love, summon demons, and foretell the future. In 276 AC, when Cersei was ten years old and Melara was eleven, the girls attended the tourney hosted by Lord Tywin_Lannister in Lannisport in honor of Prince Viserys's birth. There, Cersei was secretly told that she would be betrothed to Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen at the end of the tourney. Cersei was so happy that she decided to visit Maggy the Frog with Melara and Jeyne to have their fortunes told, after Melara told them tales of Maggy's powers she had heard from the Casterly Rock serving girls, and because Cersei wanted to show her friends that a queen and a lioness had no fears. That night, Cersei and her friends sneaked out of their beds to seek Maggy's tent on the tourney grounds, and entered it to find that the sorceress was sleeping. Cersei kicked her bed and demanded she wake up and tell their fortunes. After Maggy opened her yellow and crusted eyes, the sight of them caused Jeyne to flee in fear. However, Cersei and Melara stayed, asking to know their futures and what men they would marry, despite Maggy ordering them to leave. After Cersei threatened to tell her father Tywin to have Maggy whipped for insolence, Maggy grudgingly agreed, saying she must taste their blood. She provided a twisted iron dagger, which Cersei used to prick her thumb and Melara's. Maggy sucked the blood with her toothless mouth, then told the girls they had three questions. Cersei asked when she would marry Prince Rhaegar and about the children she would have, and received a frightening prophecy about marrying a king, the deaths of her children, a younger queen who would supplant her, and the valonqar that would kill her. Cersei wanted to leave after this, but Melara then demanded her own questions. She asked if she would marry Jaime_Lannister, Cersei's twin, a question that angered Cersei. However, Maggy prophesied that Melara would not marry anyone and would die a maiden, and that her death was was very close, close enough to "smell her breath". Cersei followed this by throwing a jar of potion into Maggy's eyes, and the girls fled the tent while Maggy screamed and cursed at them in a foreign language. Afterwards, Cersei and Melara laughed at the "stupid", nonsensical prophecies, until the end of the tourney, when Cersei learned that King Aerys_II_Targaryen had refused to consider Tywin's proposal and that she would not marry Prince Rhaegar. After that, Melara insisted that if they never spoke about the prophecies, they would be forgotten and would not come true. However, Melara fell down a well and drowned soon afterwards. Cersei recalls that Melara was not silent in the well, and that she had screamed and shouted. After Melara's death, Cersei asked her septa, Saranella, the meaning of the word valonqar, who told her it was High Valyrian for "little brother", which Cersei took to mean her younger dwarf brother, Tyrion_Lannister.

name: Meldred Merlyn
bio: Meldred Merlyn, also known as the Merlyn, is the Lord of Pebbleton on Great Wyk and is the head of House Merlyn.

name: Melesa Crakehall
bio: Melesa Crakehall is a member of House Crakehall and the wife of Ser Lyonel_Frey, the second son of Ser Emmon_Frey, himself the second son of Lord Walder_Frey. They have no progeny. Her relation to the main Crakehalls is currently unknown.

name: Melessa Florent
bio: Melessa Florent is a noblewoman of House Florent, the middle child and eldest daughter of Lord Alester_Florent of Brightwater. She is married to Lord Randyll_Tarly and the mother of Samwell_Tarly, Talla_Tarly, and Dickon_Tarly, as well as two other daughters. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Melessa is portrayed by Samantha Spiro. After the birth of Samwell, she gave her lord husband, Randyll_Tarly, three girls in as many years (including Talla_Tarly), then a second son, Dickon. Randyll once took Samwell with him on a voyage to the Arbor. After they came back, Melessa revealed to Samwell that his father wanted him to remain on the island and become Lord Paxter_Redwyne's page and squire. Randyll used to complain to Melessa about Samwell's cowardice.

name: Meliana
bio: Meliana, known as Lady Meliana, is a prostitute of Mole's Town.

name: Melicent
bio: Melicent is a septa of the Faith of the Seven in King's Landing. She might be one of the Most Devout.[N 1]

name: Melisandre
bio: Melisandre is a red priestess of R'hllor and a shadowbinder, hailing from the city of Asshai in the Further East of Essos. She has joined the court of Stannis_Baratheon, Lord of Dragonstone, believing him to be Azor_Ahai reborn, a hero destined to defeat the Great Other. She has become an influential advisor to him and his family. She becomes a POV character in A Dance with Dragons. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Melisandre is portrayed by Carice van Houten. As a slave called Melony of "Lot Seven", Melisandre was sold to a red temple at an early age. She has learned the Common Tongue, High Valyrian, and the tongue of Asshai. Melisandre has practiced her art for "years beyond count". Melisandre travels to Dragonstone on her own in search of a prophesied prince, Azor_Ahai, who will lead the war for the dawn against the Great Other, the enemy to her god, R'hllor. She is convinced that Dragonstone is the place of smoke and salt amidst which Azor Ahai shall be reborn. Melisandre comes to believe that Stannis_Baratheon, the Lord of Dragonstone, is Azor Ahai reborn, and she becomes a confidant to Stannis's wife, Selyse_Florent.

name: Melissa Blackwood
bio: Melissa Blackwood, nicknamed Missy, was a noblewoman from House Blackwood during the reign of Aegon_IV_Targaryen. She was the sixth of Aegon's Aegon_IV_Targaryen, and the best loved of them. Melissa became the sixth mistress of Aegon_IV_Targaryen early in the king's reign, in 172 AC, replacing Lady Barba_Bracken, who had been sent from court in disgrace. Even though she was Aegon's mistress, Melissa's kind heart and generosity led her to befriend Aegon's sister-wife Queen Naerys_Targaryen, her brother Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II), and her son Prince Daeron_II_Targaryen. While Melissa was slender of frame, Barba had been buxom. When Barba was heard to joke that Melissa was flat as a boy, King Aegon, who previously had renamed the hills known as the Mother's Teats after Barba ("Barba's Teats"), renamed the hills for Melissa instead and granted them to House Blackwood. Members of House Bracken still refer to the hills as "Barba's Teats", while members of House Blackwood continue to call them "Missy's Teats". Melissa was King Aegon's mistress for five years, during which she gave birth to three children: two daughters, Mya_Rivers and Gwenys_Rivers, and a son, Brynden_Rivers. Brynden would later be able to maintain close relations at court, due to the fact that his mother had been well loved during her time there, even after her dismissal. Lord_Bracken, with the help of his daughter Barba, groomed his younger daughter Lady Bethany_Bracken to replace Melissa. In 177 AC, King Aegon visited Stone Hedge to see his bastard son by Barba, Aegor_Rivers, and Bethany caught his eye and returned with him to King's Landing. A statue of Melissa can be found in the godswood of Raventree Hall, the seat of House Blackwood.

name: Mellara Rivers
bio: Mellara Rivers is a bastard daughter of Lord Walder_Frey. Mellara's mother has not been mentioned.

name: Mellario
bio: Lady Mellario is a noblewoman of the Free City of Norvos, and the estranged wife of Doran_Martell, the Prince of Dorne. She had three children by him: Arianne_Martell, Quentyn_Martell, and Trystane_Martell. Mellario was born in Norvos. Accompanied by her guardsman, Areo_Hotah, she met Prince Doran_Martell when he was visiting the city of her birth. Doran caught her eye during a festival, where the three bells of Norvos were ringing and the bears were dancing. When Doran returned to Dorne, Mellario went with him, escorted by Areo Hotah. By 273 AC, Doran and Mellario were betrothed. They would marry not long after, as their first child, Princess Arianne_Martell was born in 276 AC. Two more children would follow: Prince Quentyn_Martell, born in 281 AC, and Prince Trystane_Martell, born in 287 AC. Despite the fact that Mellario and Doran had married for love, it was not a good marriage. Half of their marriage, Mellario, and Doran spent arguing with each other. The customs in Dorne were much different from those in Norvos, including the fostering away of children. When Doran sent their eldest son, Quentyn, to be fostered by Lord Anders_Yronwood, Mellario heavily protested. Norvoshi did not foster out their children, and Mellario believed her son to be much too young. Doran would send Quentyn away regardless, and Mellario never forgave him. When Doran planned to send their daughter, Arianne, to Tyrosh, to serve as the Archon's cupbearer, Mellario threatened to harm herself, should Doran steal another one of her children. This time, Doran relented, and Arianne remained in Dorne. Despite this, however, the issues in their marriage were still unsolved. Since divorce in Westeros is uncommon, Mellario eventually returned to Norvos.[N 1] There's some bitterness about this from Mellario, as Doran was the Prince of Dorne, which enabled his children to stay with him, while she had to leave them behind.

name: Mellei
bio: Mellei is a servant of House Martell at Sunspear. She is the sister of Morra.

name: Mellos
bio: Mellos was a Grand Maester during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Mellos is portrayed by David Horovitch. Maester Mellos replaced Runciter as Grand Maester in 112 AC. He encouraged the match between Laenor_Velaryon and Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, despite the fact that Laenor was known not to be interested in women. He also counselled forgiveness for Prince Daemon_Targaryen, the controversial brother of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. In 120 AC, Mellos in his writings is the one that suggested that the fire at Harrenhal that killed Lord Lyonel_Strong and his heir, Ser Harwin_Strong, was ordered by Viserys. Mellos implies that the king had come to accept the rumors that his grandchildren by his daughter, Rhaenyra,  were really bastards sired by Harwin, thus he desired to keep the truth concealed and kill the man who had dishonored his daughter. When King Viserys fell and sliced his hand open on the Iron Throne, Mellos was unsuccessful in treating the laceration. Princess Rhaenyra flew her own maester, Gerardys, from Dragonstone to treat her father. Gerardys swiftly amputated two of the king's fingers, saving his life. Mellos passed away one night in 127 AC, collapsing while climbing the serpentine steps. Rhaenyra recommended that Gerardys take his place as Grand Maester. Queen Alicent_Hightower, on the other hand, wanted Maester Alfador, who served House Hightower, named to the office. Beset from both sides, Viserys chose neither, reminding both his wife and daughter that the Citadel would decide who filled the office. Archmaester Orwyle, in the end, was selected by the Conclave as the new Grand Maester. Mellos's written accounts were used frequently by Archmaester Gyldayn in his own writings. Gyldayn considered Mellos's frequent leechings to be less effective than Maester Gerardys's potions and tinctures.

name: Melly
bio: Melly is a member of the brotherhood without banners.

name: Melony Piper
bio: Melony Piper was a member of House Piper during the reigns of kings Aenys_I_Targaryen and Maegor_I_Targaryen. She was the sister of Lord Jon_Piper of Pinkmaiden, and had at least two other brothers. She became a favorite of Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). It was only after Queen Alyssa_Velaryon invited Melony, Rhaena's closest companion at the time, to join the royal party on King Aenys_I_Targaryen coronation progress, that Rhaena finally put aside her sullenness to join the celebrations. During Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) and Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) progress in the westerlands, Melony arrived at Lannisport with her brothers to swear the loyalty of House Piper. When Prince Aegon rose against King Maegor_I_Targaryen, it was widely believed that Lord Jon joined due to Rhaena's childhood friend Melony winning him over to the cause. Melony donned man's mail, with a spear, and led the Piper levies against Maegor. She was slain in the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye in 43 AC along with three brothers.

name: Melwyn Sarsfield
bio: Melwyn Sarsfield is a knight from House Sarsfield. He is married to Shierle_Swyft.

name: Melwys Rivers
bio: Melwys, born Melwys Rivers, is a bastard son of Lord Walder_Frey of the Crossing and a maester of the Citadel in service to House Rosby at Rosby. Melwys's mother has not been mentioned.

name: Mengo
bio: Mengo was a  Dothraki khal who lived during the Century of Blood. His mother was Doshi, a reputed witch queen, and his son was Moro_(Century_of_Blood). Compelled by his mother Doshi, Mengo united all Dothraki tribes and khalasars under his rule, becoming the first great khal. He enslaved or killed those who refused to follow him. In his old age, he turned his attentions westward and began to move against the Kingdom of Sarnor. As the Sarnori were contemptuous of the horselords, they sought to use the Dothraki in their own wars, offering them gold, slaves, and other gifts to fight their rivals. Khal Mengo took these gifts and then took the conquered lands as well, burning fields and towns and farms to return the grasslands to their former state, as Dothraki consider it sinful to cut the flesh of the earth with plows, spades, and axes. In time he was succeeded by his son Moro_(Century_of_Blood).

name: Mengo Quen
bio: Mengo Quen, known as the Glittering God, was a God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti and the third member of his dynasty. Quen earned his moniker because he ruled from a palace where the floors, walls, and columns were covered in gold leaf, and all furnishings were made of gold, even the chamber pots.

name: Meralyn
bio: Meralyn, more commonly called Merry, is the proprietor of the Happy Port, a brothel near Ragman's Harbor in Braavos.

name: Meredyth Crane
bio: Meredyth Crane, known as Merry Crane, is a noblewoman of House Crane.

name: Meredyth Darklyn
bio: Meredyth Darklyn was a member of House Darklyn and Lady of Duskendale through her marriage to Lord Gunthor_Darklyn. Early in the Dance of the Dragons, Meredyth became Lady of Duskendale after Ser Criston_Cole captured the town and had Lord Darklyn beheaded. Alarmed by reports that Prince Aemond_Targaryen was burning the riverlands atop Vhagar, Lady Meredyth sent requests for help to Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen. Rhaenyra fled King's Landing after the Storming of the Dragonpit and traveled toward Duskendale. Ser Harrold_Darke, once the squire of Ser Steffon_Darklyn, convinced Meredyth to allow the queen's party within the town walls, but only for a short time. Rhaenyra continued on to Dragonstone after leaving Duskendale.

name: Meria Martell
bio: Meria Nymeros Martell[N 1] was the ruling Princess of Dorne from House Martell during the invasion of Westeros by Aegon_I_Targaryen.

name: Merianne Frey
bio: Merianne Frey, called Merry by her family, is a noblewoman of House Frey, the daughter of Ser Whalen_Frey and Sylwa_Paege. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Merianne is portrayed by Grace Hendy.

name: Meribald
bio: Meribald is a wandering septon of the riverlands. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Meribald is merged with the Elder_Brother_(Quiet_Isle) as Ray, and portrayed by Ian McShane. Meribald was born and has lived his entire life in the riverlands. As a boy, Meribald went to fight in the War of the Ninepenny Kings with his brothers. His older brother, Willam, agreed to jokingly take Meribald as his squire. During the war, however, both Willam and another brother, Robin, died from a fever. His last brother, Owen_(brother_of_Meribald), was killed in battle and Owen's friend, Jon_Pox, was hanged for rape. Meribald may have become a broken man because of this. When he returned to the riverlands, he joined the Faith of the Seven, but used his position as a world-savvy septon to convince young women to have sex with him. He now walks barefoot as penance for these sins he committed as a youth. He cannot read or write, but he knows The Seven-Pointed Star by memory which seemed to make him capable enough to minister the Faith to smallfolk. He has been walking the countryside of the riverlands as a wandering septon in exchange for food and shelter for nearly forty years.

name: Merion
bio: Merion was a Grand Maester of the Citadel. Merion was made Grand Maester at the age of sixty-six and sent to serve the court of King Aegon_V_Targaryen in King's Landing to succeed Grand Maester Ellendor. He died on his way to King's Landing and was succeeded by Grand Maester Pycelle. Merion's work The Red Dragon and the Black was left unfinished. Merion once referred to the custom of Lys, Myr, and Tyrosh to grant licenses on ships to prey upon those of their foes as "piracy with a wax seal".

name: Meris
bio: Meris, mockingly nicknamed Pretty Meris, is a sellsword of the Windblown. Originally from Westeros, Meris has been with the free companies for twenty years. She was reportedly raped by half the members of a sellsword company. Meris eventually joined the Windblown, and now serves as the company's torturer.

name: Merle I Gardener
bio: Merle I Gardener, called Merle the Meek, was a King of the Reach and a head of House Gardener. Along with his Garth_IX_Gardener and Gwayne_V_Gardener, he is remembered as one of the Three Sage Kings who dealt with the Andal invasion of the Reach with a policy of accord and assimilation rather then armed resistance. After his father had brought a septon to court and made him one of his counselors and built the first sept at Highgarden, Merle converted to the Faith of the Seven and helped fund the construction of septs, septries, and motherhouses all over the Reach. Merle also took an Andal maiden to wife as a means of binding the bride's father to the realm, as well as other Andals into his service as knights and retainers, and bequeathed lands and wives and lordships for the more powerful of the Andal kings and other Andal adventurers descending on the Reach in exchange for pledges of fealty. He also sought after Andal craftsmen, especially blacksmiths and stonemasons, to settle throughout the Reach, supplying his bannermen with iron instead of bronze and strengthening their castles and holdfasts with Andal masonry.

name: Merling Queen
bio: The Merling Queen is a famous courtesan in Braavos. Her given name is unknown. One of the Merling Queen's Mermaids drowned at some point.

name: Merlon Crakehall
bio: Merlon Crakehall is a knight of House Crakehall. He is the youngest son of Lord Roland Crakehall.

name: Mern II Gardener
bio: Mern II Gardener, called Mern the Mason, was a King of the Reach and a head of House Gardener. Before the Andals arrived in the Reach, Mern built a new curtain wall about Highgarden and commanded his lords bannermen to see to their own defenses.

name: Mern III Gardener
bio: Mern III Gardener, called the Madling, was a King of the Reach and a head of House Gardener. Before the Andals arrived in the Reach, Mern showered gold and honors on a woods witch who boasted she could raise armies of the dead to fight off the invaders.

name: Mern VI Gardener
bio: Mern VI Gardener was a King of the Reach and a head of House Gardener. Mern was a second cousin of the late Garth_X_Gardener, who had died after becoming a tool in a war between his goodsons from House Peake and House Manderly for supremacy. Ser Osmund_Tyrell, High Steward, had led an alliance of houses against the two factions, defeated them, and placed Mern on the throne as King Mern VI Gardener. Although a man of modest gifts, Mern ruled well, rebuilding Highgarden and doing much and more to restore the power of the Reach and House Gardener, relying on the counsel of his stewards: Ser Osmund, and later Osmund's son Ser Robert_Tyrell, and finally Robert's son Lorent_Tyrell_(steward). King Mern was so pleased with them that he gave Ser Robert the hand of his youngest daughter in marriage, thereby allowing the following generations of Tyrells to claim decent from Garth_Greenhand. Mern VI was succeeded by his son Garth_XI_Gardener.

name: Mern IX Gardener
bio: Mern IX Gardener was the last King of the Reach from House Gardener. He and King Loren_I_Lannister are remembered collectively as the Two Kings. After Aegon_I_Targaryen landed in Westeros, set on conquering the Seven Kingdoms. King Mern IX of the Reach and King Loren_I_Lannister of the Rock agreed to join forces and throw the invaders back into the sea. The two armies gathered near Goldengrove in the Reach. Since he had brought half as many more men, Mern had the honor of commanding the center of the combined army from the Reach and the westerlands. Edmund_Gardener, Mern's son and heir, was to lead the vanguard, while another son, Gawen_Gardener, accompanied the king with the banner. Mern led the charge against Aegon's army alongside his kin. The combined might of the Reach and the Rock began to break the Targaryen host, but Aegon and his sisters, Visenya_Targaryen and Rhaenys_Targaryen, took to the sky with their dragons. The beasts killed 4,000 men of the combined army at the Field of Fire, among them Mern and his sons, grandsons, brothers, uncles, cousins, and other kin. Another thousand men perished from sword and spears and arrows. One nephew of King Mern survived, but he died of his burns three days later, ending House Gardener.

name: Mero
bio: Mero, better known as the Titan's Bastard, is the commander of the Second Sons, a sellsword company. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Mero is portrayed by Mark Killeen.

name: Merrel
bio: Merrel is a guard of House Baratheon of Dragonstone and a queen's man.

name: Merrell the Bold
bio: Merrell the Bold was a landed knight from the Blackwater Rush during the Dance of the Dragons. When the host of blacks led by Ser Garibald_Grey and Lord Roderick_Dustin marched south from the riverlands to Tumbleton, they were joined by men from the Blackwater Rush, including Merrell. Lord Owain_Bourney betrayed Merrell during the First Battle of Tumbleton, stabbing him in the back with his spear and opening the gates of Tumbleton's castle.

name: Merrell Bullock
bio: Merrell Bullock was a knight in service to House Targaryen. He was the commander of the castle garrison at Dragonstone during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He had two sons, Ser Alyn_Bullock and Ser Howard_Bullock. When King Jaehaerys was on Dragonstone during his regency, he spent every morning until midday in the castle yard training with sword, shield, and any other armament. Ser Merrell, along with his two sons, Ser Elyas_Scales the master-at-arms, and knights of the Kingsguard sparred with the king. When Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I) was given Dragonstone as her seat, Ser Merrell continued in his duties as the commander of the castle's garrison. In 54 AC, after the theft of three dragon eggs from Dreamfyre's clutch by Elissa_Farman, Ser Merrell was suddenly relieved of his position, as Rhaena, furious by the betrayal by her former friend, became suspicious of everyone.

name: Merrell Florent
bio: Merrell Florent is the youngest son of Ser Colin_Florent.

name: Merrett Frey
bio: Merrett Frey is a member of House Frey and a POV character in the epilogue of A Storm of Swords. Merrett is the ninth son of Lord Walder_Frey and the fourth son born of Walder's marriage to Amarei_Crakehall.

name: Merriman
bio: Merriman was an inhabitant of the riverlands.

name: Merriman's widow
bio: Merriman's widow is an inhabitant of the riverlands and the widow of Merriman.

name: Merrit
bio: Merrit o' Moontown is a member of the brotherhood without banners from Moontown.

name: Lady Merryweather
bio: Lady Merryweather was the Lady of Longtable and the head of House Merryweather during the reign of King Aegon_II_Targaryen. Her given name is unknown. Lady Merryweather became the head of her house after the execution of Lord_Merryweather at the start of the Dance of the Dragons. In 130 AC, she was besieged in her own castle by the green army led by Lord Ormund_Hightower.  Lady Merryweather eventually yielded Longtable to Ormund, who claimed the castle's wealth and food, but did no harm to the Merryweathers. Later, during the First Battle of Tumbleton, Lady Merryweather sent levies from Longtable to join the blacks's host, fortifying Tumbleton against the Hightowers. By 136 AC, Marq_Merryweather had succeeded the lordship of Longtable.

name: Lord Merryweather
bio: Lord Merryweather was the Lord of Longtable and the head of House Merryweather during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, he is portrayed by Paul Clayton. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Merryweather was arrested and put in the Red Keep's dungeons by Aegon_II_Targaryen's supporters for supporting his half-sister, Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen. When given the choice between swearing his allegiance to Aegon II or being killed by the King's Justice, Lord Merryweather was one of the nobles who chose to keep their sworn words and were beheaded for it. Their heads were mounted on spikes above the city’s gates. His House, now led by Lady_Merryweather, followed his example and continued to support Rhaenyra during the war.

name: Mervyn Flowers
bio: Mervyn Flowers was the bastard son of Lord_Peake_(father_of_Unwin) of Starpike and half-brother of Unwin_Peake. He became a knight of Aegon_III_Targaryen's Kingsguard. During the regency of Aegon III in 133 AC, Ser Willis_Fell, the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, died of the Winter Fever alongside two of his sworn brothers. In their stead, Aegon_III_Targaryen appointed Ser Robin_Massey as Lord Commander and Ser Robert_Darklyn to the white cloaks. With the support of Grand Maester Munkun, Lord Unwin_Peake, the Hand of the King, dismissed all the appointments and arrangements of the twelve-year-old king, who had not consulted with his regents. His Kingsguard appointments were reversed, with Ser Marston_Waters being elevated to Lord Commander while Lord Peake's nephew, Ser Amaury_Peake, and bastard-half brother, Ser Mervyn, were made knights of the Kingsguard. Mervyn was standing guard at the door to Queen Jaehaera_Targaryen's chambers on the occurrence of her death. In 135 AC after the secret siege ended, the real conspirators were gathered up. Mervyn killed his own sworn brother and Lord Commander, Ser Marston Waters, who had tried to arrest Mervyn in the stables, by plunging his dagger into his belly. Mervyn tried to escape, but he was found saddling his horse by a drunk man-at-arms and two stableboys. Mervyn slew them, but the commotion alerted others who overwhelmed and beat him to death, still wearing the white cloak that he had dishonored.

name: Meryn Trant
bio: Meryn Trant is a knight from House Trant and a member of the Kingsguard of Robert_I_Baratheon. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Meryn is portrayed by Ian Beattie and takes on some aspects of fellow Kingsguard member Ser Boros_Blount.

name: Meryn III Gardener
bio: Meryn III Gardener was a King of the Reach and head of House Gardener. Mern brought the Arbor into the Kingdom of the Reach and made House Redwyne his vassals after his cousin, the King of the Arbor, was lost at sea.

name: Meryn Peake
bio: Meryn Peake, known as Meryn the Scribe, was a Lord of Starpike. He was an illustrious and legendary ancestor of House Peake.

name: Methyso
bio: Methyso is a freeborn citizen of the Free city of Volantis.

name: Mezzara Merreq
bio: Mezzara Merreq is a child from the House of Merreq, one of the noble ruling families of Meereen. Mezzara is the daughter of the patriarch of her family.

name: Michael Mertyns
bio: Michael Mertyns, better known as the White Owl, was a knight from House Mertyns. He was a member of the Kingsguard at an unknown time.

name: Michael Miller
bio: Ser Michael Miller was a knight during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Michael participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Michael Crowell
bio: Ser Michael Crowell was a knight during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Michael participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Michael Manwoody
bio: Michael Manwoody was a knight of House Manwoody during the reign of Daeron_II_Targaryen. He later married Princess Elaena_Targaryen. In his early years, Michael studied at the Citadel. He was a cultured man of great learning and wit. He had attended Queen Myriah_Martell at her court, and served at the court of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He became a great confidant of the king. On several occasions, Michael was sent to Braavos, to negotiate on behalf of the Iron Throne to the Iron Bank of Braavos. There are letters recorded, of correspondence between him and the keyholders of the Iron Bank. While it is Michael's name and seal on the letters, the handwriting appears to belong to Princess Elaena_Targaryen. Michael and Elaena were wed with the blessing of King Daeron II, shortly after the death of Elaena's Ronnel_Penrose. Their marriage was a marriage of love. According to Princess Elaena, she had fallen in love with Michael because of his passion for music, not his intelligence. Michael would often play the harp for her. When Michael died, Elaena commanded that his effigy be made carrying a harp, instead of the sword and spurs which is commonly used in a knight's effigy. Elaena and Michael had no children together.

name: Mikken
bio: Mikken is the blacksmith at Winterfell. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Mikken is portrayed by Boyd Rankin.

name: Miklaz
bio: Miklaz is a boy of one of the noble ruling families of Meereen.

name: Miller's sons
bio: The miller's sons are two boys from the mill by the Acorn Water near Winterfell in the north.

name: Miller's wife
bio: An unidentified miller's wife is the mother of Ramsay_Snow. She was a young woman who married the old miller of the mill by the Weeping Water stream, who was twice her age. Soon after the marriage, Lord Roose_Bolton of the Dreadfort was hunting a fox along the Weeping Water, when he came upon the mill and saw the miller's wife washing clothes in the stream. The moment Roose saw her, he desired her. According to the outlawed first night custom, it was Roose's right to bed his vassals' new brides. As the miller had married without his lord's permission or knowledge, Roose felt he had been cheated, and had him hanged. Roose then raped the miller's wife under the tree where her husband was hanging. A year later, the miller's wife came to the Dreadfort with her infant son, Ramsay_Snow, who she claimed was Roose's bastard. Roose considered having her whipped for her impudence, and killing her son, until he saw that the child had the same pale blue eyes as him. The woman claimed that when her dead husband's brother had seen the baby's eyes, he had beaten her and sent her away from the mill. Roose was annoyed by this, so he gave the woman possession of the mill, and cut out the tongue of the old miller's brother, so that he could not relate Lord Bolton's unlawful activities to his liege lord, Rickard_Stark of Winterfell. Every year, Roose Bolton would send the woman some piglets and chickens, and a bag of stars, on the understanding that she was never to tell Ramsay who his father was. However, she disobeyed him, and often told Ramsay about his rights. One day, she returned to the Dreadfort to demand that Roose provide a servant for Ramsay, who was growing up wild and unruly. Roose gave her his foul-smelling servant Reek, as a joke, but the man and Ramsay became inseparable. Ramsay stayed with his mother until Domeric_Bolton returned to the Dreadfort after his fostering in the Vale of Arryn with House Redfort, and came to the mill by the Weeping Water to seek out his bastard brother for companionship; after Domeric died, Roose brought Ramsay to the Dreadfort as he lacked any other heirs.

name: Miller's wife from Acorn Water
bio: The miller's wife from Acorn Water is the wife of a miller in the lands near Winterfell. She has Miller's_sons and resides at a mill by the Acorn Water. Theon_Greyjoy has known her for years and bedded her several times, and thinks there was nothing special about her. However, he later recalls her features, and the way she would claw at his back while they had sex, but he cannot recall her name.

name: Mina Tyrell
bio: Mina Tyrell is a noblewoman of House Tyrell, the eldest daughter of Lord Luthor_Tyrell and Lady Olenna_Redwyne. She has two siblings: Mace_Tyrell, the current Lord of Highgarden, and a younger sister, Janna Tyrell. Mina married her first cousin, Lord Paxter_Redwyne of the Arbor. Together, they have three children, Ser Horas_Redwyne, Ser Hobber_Redwyne, and Desmera_Redwyne.

name: Minisa Whent
bio: Minisa Tully, née Whent, was a noblewoman of House Whent who became the wife of Hoster_Tully, Lord of Riverrun, and the mother of Catelyn_Stark, Lysa_Arryn, and Edmure_Tully. Minisa was born into House Whent, the Lords of Harrenhal, and she wed their liege, Hoster_Tully, the Lord of Riverrun and Lord Paramount of the Trident. Minisa's first two children were sons who died in infancy, but her later children Catelyn_Stark, Lysa_Arryn, and Edmure_Tully grew to adulthood. Minisa died in childbirth with her fourth son, who also died shortly after. The sept of Riverrun is located in Minisa's gardens.

name: Mirri Maz Duur
bio: Mirri Maz Duur is a Lhazareen godswife and maegi. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Mirri is portrayed by Mia Soteriou. Mirri was a godswife in the temple of the Great Shepherd, a Lhazareen deity. Her mother had been the godswife before her, and taught her all the spells and songs, as well as how to make the sacred smokes and ointments. As a young woman, Mirri traveled to Asshai to learn the arts of the shadowbinders. In Asshai, a moonsinger of the Jogos Nhai taught her their birthing songs, a woman of the Dothraki taught her their herb lore, and Maester Marwyn taught her Westerosi medicine and human anatomy. Marwyn also taught Mirri the Common Tongue of Westeros.

name: Missandei
bio: Missandei is a Naathi scribe in service to Kraznys_mo_Nakloz, one of the Good Masters of Astapor. In the television adaptation, Game of Thrones, Missandei is portrayed by Nathalie Emmanuel and depicted as a young woman instead of a child. Missandei and her three brothers were born on the island of Naath. Missandei remembers Mossador teaching her to climb trees. The four siblings were eventually captured by raiders from the Basilisk Isles and sold into slavery in Astapor. Her brothers became Unsullied, but one has died and only Marselen and Mossador remain. Missandei has a noticeable gift, easily learning languages. Consequently, the Good Masters chose to train her as a scribe and translator. Missandei has been trained to speak the Common Tongue of Westeros, High Valyrian, and bastard Valyrian. She has some knowledge of Ghiscari. Missandei has been seen reading scrolls, so she is literate in at least one writing system.

name: Moelle
bio: Moelle is a septa of the Faith of the Seven in King's Landing. She might be one of the Most Devout.[N 1] In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Moelle is portrayed by Maggie Hayes.

name: Mohor
bio: Mohor is a man-at-arms sworn to House Bracken.

name: Mollander
bio: Mollander, often called Hopfrog, is a novice at the Citadel.

name: Mollono Yos Dob
bio: Mollono Yos Dob is a former slave freed by Daenerys_Targaryen.

name: Mollos
bio: Mollos is an archmaester at the Citadel.

name: Monford Velaryon
bio: Monford Velaryon is the Lord of the Tides, the Master of Driftmark, and the head of House Velaryon. Aurane_Waters, the Bastard of Driftmark, is his bastard half-brother.

name: Monfryd I Durrandon
bio: Monfryd I Durrandon, also known as Monfryd the Mighty, was a Storm King of House Durrandon. Monfryd crossed the Blackwater Rush and defeated the petty kings of House Darklyn and House Mooton in a series of wars and seized the port towns of Duskendale and Maidenpool. He was succeeded by Durran_XI_Durrandon.

name: Monfryd V Durrandon
bio: Monfryd V Durrandon was a Storm King of House Durrandon during the Andal invasion and the heir and successor of King Qarlton_III_Durrandon. Like his father and Qarlton_II_Durrandon, Monfryd V reigned in times of war against the Andals. He defeated the Holy Brotherhood of the Andals, an alliance of seven petty kings and warlords, at the Battle of Bronzegate, though he lost his life in the process.

name: Monster
bio: The wildling infant son of Gilly and her father-husband Craster has not received a given name yet. Some members of the Night's Watch have called him the abomination, as he is a product of incest. Val thinks of him as a sweet little monster and the monster, using it as a milk name. Jon_Snow then thinks of the infant as Monster. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is named Little Sam and portrayed by Arya Hasson (Season 3), William and James Wilson (Seasons 6 - 7), and Finn and Logan Watson (Season 8).

name: Monterys Velaryon
bio: Monterys Velaryon is a member of House Velaryon and is the young heir of Monford_Velaryon, Lord of the Tides.

name: Moon
bio: Moon was a septon and a member of the Poor Fellows during the Faith Militant uprising against the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Following the Faith Militant's defeats in 42 AC in the battles at Stonebridge and the Great Fork of the Blackwater, new leaders in the rebellion arose the following year. Among these was Septon Moon. Moon was seen as hardly distinguishable from an outlaw. Septon Moon was part of a band of outlaws who crowned him as their own High Septon, in defiance of the "High_Lickspittle" that King Maegor_I_Targaryen had forced the Most Devout in Oldtown to elect to the office. Moon roamed the riverlands and Reach, and whenever he emerged from the woods to preach, he drew huge crowds to hear his condemnations of Maegor. At Stoney Sept in 47 AC, he denounced Maegor upcoming polygamous wedding, and was cheered on by hundreds of smallfolk. In 48 AC, Moon led thousands of followers of the Faith across the Reach to Oldtown, with the openly announced intent of "bearding the Lickspittle in the Starry Sept", to demand that he lift the ban on the military orders and denounce Maegor. While encamped outside of Oldtown, his forces were joined by the armies of Lord Torgen_Oakheart and Lord Rickard_Rowan. After Maegor's death, Moon remained encamped outside of Oldtown with his forces. Moon was considered the greatest threat to the Maegor's successor, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Moon continued to denounce the Targaryens and the High Lickspittle, who allowed the abominations to continue. Although Lord Donnel_Hightower had closed his gates to Moon and his men, he also refused to attack them. Jaehaerys's small council decided that King Jaehaerys needed to be anointed by the High Septon, but Moon had to be dealt with before this could happen. The suggestion of attacking Moon's host with dragons were rejected. Lord Rogar_Baratheon declared that, if he were to lead his host against Moon and his followers, the cost would be great. While the small council in King's Landing still discussed how to deal with Moon, Moon was approached by a young woman who requested Moon's help in exchange for a flagon of wine. After a while, the woman emerged, half-naked and screaming, and fled the scene. Moon emerged shortly after, with his throat cut. He staggered through half the camp as he died, in an attempt to catch the woman. The woman could not be found afterwards, and Moon's followers could not even agree on what she had looked like. Of the flagon of wine that the woman had brought, half still remained. The four Poor Fellows who drank it afterwards all died soon after, revealing the wine had been poisoned. Many stories and rumors were soon spread about Septon Moon's sudden death. The would-be successors in Moon's host fought over Moon's bones. One of them, Lorcas_the_Learned, claimed he had had a vision of Moon, in which the deceased septon deliver Oldtown into the hands of his followers. He seized the septon's body from Rob_the_Starvling, strapped it atop a destrier, and stormed the gates of Oldtown in a failed attack. When most of the few men who joined the attack were dead, Lord Hightower sent out a dozen knights to seize Moon's body and remove the head. Moon's head was later presented as a gift to the High Septon, tanned and stuffed.

name: Moon Boy
bio: Moon Boy is the royal jester and fool of King Robert_I_Baratheon's court in the Red Keep. His given name is unknown.

name: Moonshadow
bio: The Moonshadow is a famous courtesan in Braavos. Her given name is unknown.

name: Moqorro
bio: Moqorro is a red priest in service to the faith of R'hllor, from the Temple of the Lord of Light in Volantis. He is the right hand of the High Priest, Benerro.

name: Mord
bio: Mord is a gaoler of House Arryn, who is in charge of the upkeep and maintenance of the sky cells of the Eyrie. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Mord is portrayed by Ciaran Bermingham.

name: Mordane
bio: Mordane is the septa at Winterfell. She serves as tutor to the Stark girls, Sansa_Stark and Arya_Stark. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Mordane is portrayed by Susan Brown. Mordane has tutored Sansa_Stark and Arya_Stark in the gentle arts and turning them into noblewomen. When Arya wanted to go boar hunting in the wolfswood, Mordane explained that boar hunting was not for ladies. Arya has often daydreamed when Mordane tries to teach the girls about noble houses. Mordane once went to the market square of the winter town with Lady Catelyn_Stark, Bran_Stark, and Hodor. The simple Hodor wandered off, and when they eventually found the stableboy he was being teased with sticks by youths.

name: Morden II Durrandon
bio: Morden II Durrandon was a Storm King from House Durrandon. During his reign the decline of House Durrandon turned back, thanks in part to Morden's baseborn half brother Ronard_Storm, whom he named as his castellan. A fearsome warrior, Ronard became ruler of the Stormlands in all but name and took Morden's sister to wife. Within five years, he had usurped kingship as well. Morden's own queen placed the crown on Ronard's head and, if songs are true, she shared his bed as well. In the meantime, Morden, deemed harmless, was confined to a cell in the tower.

name: Moredo Prestayn
bio: Moredo Prestayn is a member of House Prestayn and a merchant of Braavos. He is the Tradesman-Captain of the Vixen.

name: Moredo Rogare
bio: Moredo Rogare was a scion of House Rogare, a Lysene noble banking family. He was the fourth of six trueborn sons of Lysandro_Rogare.[N 1] In 134 AC, Moredo and his brothers, Roggerio and Lotho, accompanied their sister, Larra_Rogare, when she came to King's Landing to live at the Red Keep with her husband, Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen. Moredo was the commander of his sister's guards. The Iron Throne respected all the conditions for Prince Viserys's return, as negotiated between Lord Alyn_Velaryon and Lysandro, these included granting lordships to Moredo and his two brothers. A war of succession broke out in the Vale of Arryn after the death of Lady Jeyne_Arryn, and the killing of King Aegon_III_Targaryen's regent, Ser Corwyn_Corbray. Lord Thaddeus_Rowan, Aegon's Hand, gave Moredo the command of the army carried with the Velaryon fleet to the Vale. It was a very poor choice as Moredo was not fluent in the Common Tongue and had great difficulty communicating with his Westerosi men. He lost hundreds of men taking the harbor and city of Gulltown. When word of the fall of House Rogare reached Gulltown, Moredo yielded up his command to Lord Alyn and sailed for Braavos. Once in Braavos, he treated with the keyholders of the Iron Bank and recieved gold from them. He then used the Braavosi gold to hire ships and swords in Tyrosh, and prepared to attack Lys in retaliation for what happened to his family. Archmaester Gyldayn does not go into the details of Moredo's attack in his book, however. In 138 AC, Moredo interred the bones of his dead Lysaro_Rogare in the family crypt.

name: Moreo Dagareon
bio: Moreo Dagareon was a Lyseni gonfaloniere. Moreo was slain by his own elite guards in 135 AC during the bloody power struggle in the city after the downfall of the Rogare family.

name: Moreo Tumitis
bio: Moreo Tumitis is captain of the ship Storm Dancer. Moreo has sailed the narrow sea for roughly thirty years, first as oarman, then as quartermaster, and now as captain of trading galleys. Storm Dancer is his fourth ship and the fastest of them.

name: Morgan
bio: Morgan is a guard of House Baratheon of Dragonstone, and a queen's man. He is a hopeless drunkard.

name: Morgan Dunstable
bio: Morgan Dunstable was a knight from Tumbler's Falls during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Morgan knighted Glendon_Flowers in front of two dozen witnesses at the Pussywillows,  but supposedly only because Glendon's sister gave him her maidenhead.

name: Morgan Hightower
bio: Morgan Hightower was a knight from House Hightower and the commander of the Warrior's Sons chapter at Oldtown during the Faith Militant uprising. He was a son of Lord Manfred_Hightower_(son_of_Addam), and the younger brother of Lord Martyn_Hightower and Queen Ceryse_Hightower.[N 1] When King Maegor_I_Targaryen and his army approached Oldtown during the uprising of the Faith Militant, Ser Morgan's elder brother, Lord Martyn_Hightower, called the banners. Morgan led the Warrior's Sons of Oldtown in surrounding the Starry Sept to protect the High_Septon_(Aenys_I). By morning, however, the High Septon was dead. Morgan and his sworn brothers were arrested under orders of Lord Martyn and put on trial by Maegor the Cruel. Morgan was the only one pardoned, while the rest were given the choice between death or taking the black. Morgan's pardon led to the suspicion that he had murdered the High Septon at the behest of his lordly brother, as the knight was seen entering and leaving the privy chambers of His High Holiness that night. Patrice_Hightower, Visenya_Targaryen, and the archmaesters of the Citadel have also been suspected of killing the High Septon. Morgan was later cut down and butchered on the road to Honeyholt by the Warrior's Sons who remained loyal to Ser Joffrey_Doggett.

name: Morgan Liddle
bio: Morgan Liddle is the second son of Lord Torren_Liddle, known as the Liddle, the clan chief of House Liddle, a mountain clan in the North. Other northmen call him Middle Liddle, though not often in his hearing. He is the heir apparant of his father Torren, as his elder brother, Duncan, is a sworn brother of the Night's Watch.

name: Morgan Martell
bio: Morgan Martell was an Andal adventurer and the founder of House Martell. Morgan and his kin descended on lands loosely held by House Wade and House Shell, defeated them in battle, seized their villages, burned their castles and established his dominion over a strip of coastland fifty leagues long and ten leagues wide.

name: Morgan Wylde
bio: Morgan Wylde was the Lord of the Rain House and the head of House Wylde during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. After his daughter, Coryanne_Wylde, was deflowered by a Surly_lad, Lord Morgan had the man gelded, and Coryanne was confined to her chambers.

name: Morgarth
bio: Morgarth the Merry is a hedge knight.

name: Morghaz zo Zherzyn
bio: Morghaz zo Zherzyn, or Morghar zo Zherzyn, is a Yunkish nobleman called the Drunken Conqueror.

name: Morgil Hastwyck
bio: Morgil Hastwyck was a knight from House Hastwyck who lived during the reign of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen. Ser Morgil is renowned for slandering Queen Naerys_Targaryen and being killed by her brother, Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II), in trial by combat. According to Grand Maester Kaeth's Lives of Four Kings, these accusations were instigated by Aegon IV himself, who was at the time quarreling with his son and heir, Prince Daeron_II_Targaryen.

name: Morgo
bio: Morgo is a slave soldier owned by Yezzan_zo_Qaggaz. He is commanded by the serjeant Scar.

name: Morgon Banefort
bio: Morgon Banefort was the last Hooded King of House Banefort during the Age of Heroes. King Morgon and his thralls were defeated in a twenty-years-long war with King Loreon_I_Lannister of House Lannister. According to a semi-canon source, Morgon was said to have been a powerful necromancer who cursed his vanquishers as he died, claiming he would return from the grave. In response, Loreon had Morgon's body cut into pieces and fed to his lions. Two years later the lions broke free within Casterly Rock, slaying three of Loreon's sons.

name: Moriah Qorgyle
bio: Moriah Qorgyle was a noblewoman from House Qorgyle during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. After the death of Queen Jaehaera_Targaryen in 133 AC, Moriah was presented to King Aegon III as a potential bride at the Maiden's Day Ball. According to Archmaester Gyldayn, she had the boldest tongue present, as she requested that the king come down from the Iron Throne and kiss her. Aegon did not oblige her request.

name: Moribald Chester
bio: Moribald Chester is the Lord of Greenshield and the head of House Chester.

name: Morion Martell
bio: Morion Nymeros Martell was the ruling Prince of Dorne and head of House Martell during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 61 AC, knights of the Seven Kingdoms marched into the Red Mountains to eliminate a Vulture_King_(Jaehaerys_I). Morion's father, the Prince of Dorne, kept Dorne out of the conflict, leaving the Vulture King to his fate in Lord Rogar's War. Prince Morion bristled at his father's apparent cowardice. When his elderly father died, Morion succeeded him as the Prince of Dorne and Lord of Sunspear during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He was still angered by his father's inaction during Lord Rogar's War and wished to wash the slight away with an invasion of the stormlands. Assembling his hosts at Ghost Hill and the Tor, Morion loaded his Dornishmen onto the ships of pirates from the Stepstones, sellsails from Myr, and corsairs of the Pepper Coast. He intended to land his armies on Cape Wrath, hoping to take the stormlords and King Jaehaerys unaware. However, it took the Prince of Dorne nearly a year to load his hosts aboard the ships and, by that time, spies and friends of the Iron Throne among Morion's court and bannermen had informed the king of the prince's plans. While Lord Boremund_Baratheon awaited the Dornish on the shores of Cape Wrath, King Jaehaerys and his sons took their dragons to burn the Prince of Dorne's fleet. Not a single Dornishmen made it to shore alive, nor did the Iron Throne lose a single man. Morion died during his attempted invasion, called the Fourth Dornish War, in 83 AC. He was succeeded by Mara_Martell.

name: Lady Mormont (wife of Alaric Stark)
bio: Lady Mormont was the wife of Lord Alaric_Stark and the Lady of Winterfell during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Her given name is unknown. When she was twelve, she took an axe to a pack of wolves, killed two of them, and sewed a cloak from their skins. At some point, she married Alaric Stark, the second son of Lord Brandon_Stark_(father_of_Walton). They had three children together, two sons and a daughter, Alarra_Stark. In 50 AC, she became the Lady of Winterfell after the death of her brother-in-law, Lord Walton_Stark_(son_of_Brandon). She died five years later.

name: Morna White Mask
bio: Morna White Mask is a free folk leader, warrior witch, and raider.

name: Moro
bio: Moro is a Dothraki khal. He is a sometime ally of Khal Drogo. He has a son, Rhogoro. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Moro is portrayed by Joe Naufahu.

name: Moro
bio: Moro was a khal who lived during the Century of Blood. He was the son of Khal Mengo, who had united all Dothraki in a single khalasar. Moro led the Dothraki to the gates of Sathar, the "Waterfall City" of the Sarnori. All men were put to the sword and their women and children carried off as slaves to the marts of the hill city of Hazdahn Mo, a colony of the Ghiscari Empire. Sathar was burned to the ground, and it is written that Moro gave the ruins a new name: Yalli Qamayi, the place of Wailing Children. Six years later, Moro razed Kasath to the ground, with the aid of the King of Gornath, who had taken one of Moro's daughters as wife and made common cause with him and sent aid to Moro's riders. Twelve years later, however, Moro was slain by Khal Horro.

name: Morosh the Myrman
bio: Morosh the Myrman is a sellsail admiral from Myr.

name: Morra
bio: Morra is a servant of House Martell at Sunspear. She is the sister of Mellei.

name: Morrec
bio: Morrec is a servant of Tyrion_Lannister on his travels.

name: Morros Slynt
bio: Morros Slynt is the eldest son of Janos_Slynt.

name: Mors Manwoody
bio: Mors Manwoody is the eldest son and heir of Lord Dagos_Manwoody of Kingsgrave.

name: Mors Martell
bio: Mors Martell was the head of House Martell and Lord of the Sandship who married the warrior queen Nymeria. Together they conquered the rest of Dorne in Nymeria's War. When Nymeria led the Rhoynar refugees to Dorne in southern Westeros, the lands of the Martells paled in comparison to those of House Yronwood. The marriage of Lord Mors to Nymeria gave the Martells the manpower to conquer the rest of Dorne and gave the Rhoynar a new homeland. The Dornish rulers have since styled themselves "Prince" in the Rhoynish fashion rather than "King". Mors was slain by King Yorick_V_Yronwood in the Third Battle of the Boneway, but Nymeria forced Yorick to submit two years later.

name: Mors II Martell
bio: Mors II Martell was a Prince of Dorne and head of House Martell. He was the grandson of Nymeria and successor to her eldest daughter.

name: Mors Martell
bio: Prince Mors Nymeros Martell was the younger brother of Prince Doran_Martell. He died as an infant.

name: Mors Umber
bio: Mors Umber, known as Mors Crowfood, is a member of House Umber, the uncle of Lord Greatjon Umber, and the joint castellan of Last Hearth with his younger brother, Hother_Umber. A crow once took Mors for dead and pecked out his eye. According to Nan, he grabbed the bird at its feet and bit its head off, earning him the nickname Crowfood. Mors was a fierce fighter in his youth.  His wife died in childbirth, his sons were killed in the Battle of the Trident during Robert's Rebellion, and his only daughter was abducted in 270 AC by wildlings, leaving Mors with a deep hatred of the free folk.

name: Mortimer Boggs
bio: Mortimer Boggs was a knight from House Boggs during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Ser Mortimer attended the wedding tourney at Whitewalls in 212 AC. He was defeated in the lists by Ser Glendon_Flowers, and had to forfeit his horse and armor for his loss. However, because Glendon was a bastard, Ser Mortimer refused to send over his horse, and the armor he sent over had holes hammered through it.

name: Morton Caron
bio: Morton Caron was the Lord of Nightsong and the head of House Caron early in the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Lord Morton attended the Golden Wedding, where the Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen entertained the lords in the intimacy of his solar, encouraging them to speak freely and share their views on the problems of the realm and how they might best be overcome. Lord Morton thought the king to be cautious and shrewd.

name: Morton Waynwood
bio: Morton Waynwood is a knight of House Waynwood. He is the eldest son and heir of Lady Anya_Waynwood, the Lady of Ironoaks.

name: Morya Frey
bio: Morya Frey is the third daughter of Lord Walder_Frey, first daughter born of his marriage to Alyssa_Blackwood. She is married to Ser Flement_Brax. They have three children, Robert_Brax_(son_of_Flement), Walder_Brax and Jon_Brax.

name: Moryn Tyrell
bio: Moryn Tyrell is a knight from House Tyrell. The uncle of  Mace_Tyrell, Lord of Highgarden, Moryn is the Lord Commander of the City Watch of Oldtown.

name: Moslander of Yore
bio: Moslander of Yore was a landed knight or petty lord from Yore along the Blackwater Rush during the Dance of the Dragons. When the host of blacks led by Ser Garibald_Grey and Lord Roderick_Dustin marched south from the riverlands to Tumbleton, they were joined by men from the Blackwater Rush, including Moslander. It is unknown if he survived the First Battle of Tumbleton.

name: Mossador
bio: Mossador is a Naathi member of the Unsullied. His living siblings include Marselen and Missandei. When they were young, Mossador taught his sister Missandei how to climb trees on Naath. Missandei once found him sleeping in their garden with butterflies crawling on him. Missandei and her three brothers, including Marselen and Mossador, were captured by raiders from the Basilisk Isles and sold into slavery in Astapor. Missandei was trained as a scribe, but Mossador and his two brothers were gelded and trained by the Good Masters to be Unsullied. Marselen and Mossador became Unsullied, and the third brother died at some point.

name: Mother Mole
bio: Mother Mole is a wildling leader and woods witch who is given to prophecy. She makes her home in a burrow beneath a hollow tree.

name: Motho
bio: Motho is a khal who leads a Dothraki khalasar. It is said that he is an old man and most of his riders are as old as him.

name: Mudge
bio: Mudge is a member of the brotherhood without banners.

name: Mudge
bio: Mudge is an inhabitant of Donnelwood.

name: Mudge
bio: Mudge was a swineherd in service to House Osgrey during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Mudge once traveled to Oldtown. He led devotions in the local sept every seventh day for those sworn to House Osgrey. However, twice a year, a real septon came through to forgive people's sins.

name: Mullin
bio: Mullin is the maester for the Night's Watch at the Shadow Tower. Mullin was the maester at the Shadow Tower when Mance_Rayder was stationed at the castle. When Mance was wounded by a shadowcat while ranging beyond the Wall, his fellow rangers took him to a nearby wildling village for treatment because they feared Mance would die before they could return to the Shadow Tower for Mullin's healing.

name: Mully
bio: Mully is a steward of the Night's Watch. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Mully is portrayed by Andy Moore.

name: Munciter
bio: Wisdom Munciter is a member of the Alchemists' Guild in King's Landing. He is in charge of keeping an inventory of the amount of wildfire produced by the Guild.

name: Munda
bio: Munda is  the daughter of Tormund, a chief of the free folk.

name: Munkun
bio: Munkun was a Grand Maester of the Seven Kingdoms. He wrote the book The Dance of the Dragons, A True Telling about the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. The book contains some inaccuracies. Maester Alford was Grand Maester in 153 AC, but by 171 AC Munkun was Grand Maester again. When King Baelor_I_Targaryen collapsed from fasting after the birth of Daemon_I_Blackfyre, Munkun tried to heal the king, but Baelor died.

name: Murch
bio: Murch is part of Yoren's band when it is marching north to the Wall to join the Night’s Watch. It is not known whether he is a recruit, a criminal or one of the orphan boys.

name: Murch
bio: Murch is a man sworn to House Stark. He is one of the best huntsmen in service to the Starks.

name: Murenmure
bio: Murenmure is a maester of the Citadel. He is in service at Hammerhorn to House Goodbrother.

name: Murmison
bio: Murmison was a renowned septon of the Faith, who served as the Hand of the King for King Aenys_I_Targaryen, from 39 AC until his death in 41 AC. He is now used as a warning about the makings of a good Hand. In 39 AC, the Faith was furious when King Aenys_I_Targaryen's brother, Prince Maegor_I_Targaryen, took a second wife, Lady Alys_Harroway. Maegor arguing that his first wife, Lady Ceryse_Hightower, was barren, but the High_Septon_(Aenys_I) denounced the marriage as "sin and fornication" and pious lords across the realm began to turn against the crown. Aenys tried to placate the Faith by stripping Maegor of his office as Hand of the King and sending him into exile to Pentos, appointing the pious cleric Murmison in his brother's place. Aenys had Murmison lay hands on Lady Ceryse's belly every night in the hope that Prince Maegor might repent if his lawful wife became fertile. However, Murmison only succeeded in making Ceryse grow weary of the nightly ritual and she departed King's Landing for Oldtown. However, not even the appointment of the reputed miracle-worker Murmison, could repair the breach with the Faith. Murmison could do little to heal the discontent across the realm, nor did his reputed miracles make Ceryse fertile. The High Septon continued to speak against the crown while lords across the realm spoke of the king's weakness and inability to rule. Aenys enraged the Faith even further in 41 AC when he wed his daughter, Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), to his son and heir, Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I), and was denounced as "King Abomination". Pious lords and the smallfolk who once loved Aenys turned against him. For performing the incestuous marriage ceremony, Murmison was expelled from the Faith as the High Septon sent out decrees to septons across the realm condemning the marriage. Aenys took it upon himself to write to the High Septon asking for Murmison to be restored to his status, explaining the history of sibling marriages in Old Valyria, but the High Septon's response was to declare the king a pretender and a tyrant. The Faithful responded to the High Septon's denunciations; less than a fortnight later, as Septon Murmison was crossing King's Landing in his litter, a group of Poor Fellows came swarming from an alleyway and hacked the septon to pieces with their axes. The Faith Militant uprising had begun and Aenys was forced to flee to Dragonstone, the office of Hand remaining vacant for the rest of the king's reign.

name: Mushroom
bio: Mushroom was a dwarf who served as a fool at the courts of Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen, Rhaenyra_Targaryen, and Aegon_III_Targaryen. He allegedly recorded the events that occurred during his time at their courts in The Testimony of Mushroom, written by an unknown scribe. Mushroom's Testimony is filled with tales of plots, murders, trysts, debaucheries, and many other salacious claims. The fool's account is frequently cited by Archmaester Gyldayn in his Targaryen history, in which it is often compared to the accounts of other historians, like Septon Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons) and Grand Maester Munkun. Many of the wild claims of Mushroom, who according to Gyldayn loved Rhaenyra greatly, are disputed by the account of Eustace, a supporter of Aegon II, Rhaenyra's half-brother and enemy. Mushroom outlived the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. After spending time at the Merman's Court in White Harbor, Mushroom visited the Sealord's Palace in Braavos and Port of Ibben on the island of Ib. He also spent time amongst the mummers of the Lisping Lady.

name: Mya Rivers
bio: Mya Rivers was the eldest of the three bastard children of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen and his sixth mistress, Lady Melissa_Blackwood. Melissa Blackwood "reigned" for five years as the mistress of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen, during which she gave birth to three children: Mya, Gwenys_Rivers, and Brynden_Rivers. Aegon's bastards born of noblewomen were legitimized as Great Bastards on the king's deathbed.

name: Mya Stone
bio: Mya Stone is a young woman serving House Royce of the Gates of the Moon. She is the eldest of the bastards of King Robert_I_Baratheon. In service to Lord Nestor_Royce at the Gates of the Moon, Mya serves as a guide on the treacherous, rocky journey between the Gates of the Moon and the Eyrie by leading trains of mules, such as Whitey. Mya also transports foodstuffs from the foot of the Giant's Lance to the Eyrie. Young Robert_I_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End, fathered Mya, his first child, on Mya_Stone's_mother while he and Eddard_Stark were being fostered in the Vale of Arryn at the court of Jon_Arryn, Lord of the Eyrie. When Lyanna_Stark, Ned's sister, heard of Mya's birth and parentage, she became less enthusiastic about her betrothal to Robert. Robert often brought Ned along during his daily visits to see Mya, even after he lost interest in her mother. Mya remained in the Vale at the Gates of the Moon when Robert became king after Robert's Rebellion and took up residence in the Red Keep of King's Landing. At some point after the birth of Prince Joffrey_Baratheon, Robert expressed interest in taking one of his baseborn daughters (apparently Mya) to court in King's Landing. Queen Cersei_Lannister did not protest, but spitefully stated that "the city is not a healthy place for a growing girl", inferring that Cersei would harm her. Robert grew enraged enough to strike his wife, but did not press the point. Mya is somewhat openly known to be the bastard daughter of the king, although she was not officially acknowledged by her father. She has vague memories of him, as a big strong man tossing her in the air and catching her. While Maddy knows the truth, it is unclear if Mya is aware that the king is her father. Lord Nestor_Royce has tried to arrange matches for Mya, but she has declined them all.

name: Mya Stone's mother
bio: Mya Stone's mother is a commoner from the Vale of Arryn who is the mother of Mya_Stone. Her given name is unknown. Robert_I_Baratheon fathered Mya_Stone on a common woman while he and Eddard_Stark were living at the Eyrie as wards of Lord Jon_Arryn.
