Robert made daily visits to the babe Mya, sometimes with Eddard, even after he lost interest in her mother. Robert never publicly acknowledged Mya, however. Mya has been told stories about the Giant's Lance by her mother.

name: Mycah
bio: Mycah is the son of a butcher who befriends Arya_Stark. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Mycah is portrayed by Rhodri Hosking.

name: Mychel Redfort
bio: Mychel Redfort is a member of House Redfort, the youngest son of Lord Horton_Redfort and squire to Ser Lyn_Corbray. Mychel took Mya_Stone's virginity.

name: Mylenda Caron
bio: Mylenda Caron is a noblewoman of House Caron, married to Petyr_Frey, a great-grandson of Lord Walder_Frey of the Crossing. They have one child, Perra_Frey. Mylenda married Petyr_Frey, called "Petyr Pimple", when he was ten and she was three times his age. Rumors at the Twins claim that Mylenda is sleeping with Petyr's older brother, Walder_Frey_(son_of_Ryman).

name: Myles
bio: Myles is a maester in service to House Martell, along with the older Caleotte.

name: Myles
bio: Myles is the squire for Ser Desmond_Grell, the master-at-arms of Riverrun.

name: Myles Greenfield
bio: Myles Greenfield was a knight of House Greenfield during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Myles participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Myles Hightower
bio: Myles Hightower was a knight from House Hightower during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Ser Myles grew furious when his cousin, Lord Lyonel_Hightower, agreed to Lord Corlys_Velaryon's terms to end the Dance of the Dragons, including returning the Crown's gold, as Myles had stolen a good part of it.

name: Myles Manwoody
bio: Myles Manwoody is a knight of House Manwoody. He is the brother of Lord Dagos_Manwoody of Kingsgrave.

name: Myles Mooton
bio: Myles Mooton was a famous knight from House Mooton. His brother is Lord William_Mooton of Maidenpool. Myles was the squire of Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen, and received his knighthood from Rhaegar himself. As one of the younger men at court, he remained one of Rhaegar's close companions after having been knighted. Ser Myles fought in the Battle of the Bells during Robert's Rebellion. He was one of the six men killed in the battle by Lord Robert_I_Baratheon that day.

name: Myles Smallwood
bio: Myles Smallwood was the Lord of Acorn Hall and the head of House Smallwood during the reigns of the kings Aenys_I_Targaryen, Maegor_I_Targaryen, and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Renowned for his courage, he briefly served as the Hand of the King to Jaehaerys I. In 37 AC, Lord Myles rode beside Lord Alyn_Stokeworth, the Hand of the King for Aenys_I_Targaryen, when Alyn was hunting for the outlaw, Harren_the_Red. In 42 AC, he faced Wat_the_Hewer in single combat in the battle at Stonebridge during the Faith Militant uprising. Myles fought on the side of Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) in the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye against King Maegor_I_Targaryen. One of Myles's sworn swords, Ser Willam_the_Wasp, was a victor in the War for the White Cloaks. Willam joined the Kingsguard of Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. When Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan) resigned in 54 AC, Myles was chosen as the new Hand. Since many of Jaehaerys's advisors were bookish, the small council desired that the king also be advised by a martial man such as Lord Smallwood. Jaehaerys was convinced by the recommendations of Ser Willam the Wasp and his liege lords, Lord Prentys_Tully and Lady Lucinda_Broome. Myles disapproved of the conduct of the king's sister, Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), toward her host at Faircastle, Lord Franklyn_Farman. Jaehaerys did not take issue with Smallwood's point, only his words. After Willam was slain by outlaws in the Velvet Hills, Jaehaerys declined Myles's advice to punish Pentos. Although undoubtedly well-intentioned and a leal man, Myles was dismissed as Hand of the King in 57 AC, as the Lord of Acorn Hall proved unsuited for the office, a fact that even Myles had acknowledged. Lord Smallwood was succeeded in the office by Septon Barth.

name: Myles Toyne
bio: Myles Toyne, also known as Blackheart, was an exiled knight from House Toyne and a captain-general of the Golden Company. At a time when Myles would have been captain-general, Viserys_Targaryen feasted the captains of the Golden Company in the hopes they might take up his cause to reclaim the Iron Throne from King Robert_I_Baratheon. His sister Daenerys_Targaryen, then a little girl, witnessed them eat his food and hear his pleas and laugh at him. Myles secretly plotted with Magister Illyrio_Mopatis, Lord Varys, and the exiled lord Jon_Connington to have the Golden Company aid Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar) in claiming the Iron Throne. After Myles's death in 296 AC, his skull was dipped in gold and placed upon a company standard. He was succeeded as captain-general by the company paymaster, Ser Harry_Strickland.

name: Myranda Lefford
bio: Myranda Lefford is a noblewoman of House Lefford and the wife of Ser Stafford_Lannister. Myranda is married to Ser Stafford_Lannister, and has three children by him; Ser Daven_Lannister, Cerenna_Lannister and Myrielle_Lannister.

name: Myranda Royce
bio: Myranda Royce, called Randa, is the daughter of Lord Nestor_Royce, the High Steward of the Vale. Myranda was once married to an elderly lord. Her husband died while having sex with her. She maintains the Gates of the Moon for her father, Lord Nestor_Royce.

name: Lord Myre
bio: Lord Myre is the head of House Myre and a bannerman to Lord Rodrik_Harlaw of Harlaw.

name: Myrcella Baratheon
bio: Princess Myrcella Baratheon is known to the Seven Kingdoms as the middle child of King Robert Baratheon and Queen Cersei_Lannister. A member of House Baratheon of King's Landing, her siblings are Princes Joffrey_Baratheon and Tommen_Baratheon. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Myrcella is portrayed by Aimee Richardson (Seasons 1 and 2) and Nell Tiger Free (Season 5). Following the tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day, Myrcella and her siblings traveled with Cersei_Lannister to Casterly Rock with their grandfather, Lord Tywin_Lannister. Myrcella has a garden at the Red Keep.

name: Myria Jordayne
bio: Myria Jordayne is a member of House Jordayne and the daughter and heir of Lord Trebor_Jordayne of the Tor.

name: Myriah Martell
bio: Princess Myriah Nymeros Martell was a Dornish princess, and the wife and queen consort of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Myriah was the eldest daughter of the Prince_of_Dorne_(father_of_Maron) during the reign of the Young Dragon, King Daeron_I_Targaryen. She had a younger brother, Prince Maron_Martell. In 161 AC, after Daeron was killed during his attempted conquest of Dorne, King Baelor_I_Targaryen freed the Dornish hostages in King's Landing and returned them to Dorne personally, walking barefoot. Once he reached Sunspear, Baelor spoke with the Prince of Dorne, and agreed upon a peace, which included the betrothal of Myriah to Baelor's cousin, Prince Daeron_II_Targaryen. As both Daeron and Myriah were children at the time, the two were to marry once they were both of age. Princess Myriah eventually wed Prince Daeron, and their marriage was happy and fruitful. They would have four sons together: Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II) (born 170 AC), Aerys_I_Targaryen (born 172–177 AC), Rhaegel_Targaryen (born 173–178 AC), and Maekar_I_Targaryen (born 174–179 AC). Myriah's marriage to Daeron would pave the way for Dorne to come under the rule of the Iron Throne. In 184 AC, after the death of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen, Daeron succeeded him as King Daeron_II_Targaryen, and Myriah became queen. Many noble Dornishmen and women flocked to Daeron's court, which served to give life in the Red Keep a foreign flavor; however, this alienated many of Daeron's other nobles and courtiers. Some also looked upon Prince Baelor's dark hair and eyes and muttered he was more Martell than Targaryen. These seeds of dissent would eventually grow into the First Blackfyre Rebellion. It is unknown whether Myriah survived her husband, who died of the Great Spring Sickness in 209 AC.

name: Myriah Stark
bio: Myriah Stark[N 1] was a member of House Stark. She was the fourth daughter of Lord Cregan_Stark and Lady Alysanne_Blackwood.

name: Myriame Manderly
bio: Myriame Manderly was a noblewoman from House Manderly and Lady of Winterfell. She married Lord Rodwell_Stark, son of Lord Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan), but they had no issue.

name: Myrielle Lannister
bio: Myrielle Lannister is a member of House Lannister. She is the daughter of Ser Stafford_Lannister and Myranda_Lefford. She is the younger sister of Ser Daven_Lannister and Cerenna_Lannister.

name: Myrielle Peake
bio: Myrielle Peake was the daughter of the Hand of the King, Lord Unwin_Peake of Starpike, and a proposed bride for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. After her brother, Ser Titus_Peake_(son_of_Unwin) died in the Dance of the Dragons, Myrielle became Lord Unwin_Peake's last surviving child. As Hand of the King, Unwin attempted to promote Myrielle as a bride for King Aegon_III_Targaryen after the death of Queen Jaehaera_Targaryen in 133 AC. He claimed that Myrielle's personality, her age, and her mother's fertility made her the perfect candidate in marriage to Aegon. When Aegon expressed doubt about liking Myrielle, Lord Peake likened a potential marriage to the king having to eat turnips when his cooks prepared them. When word got out, Myrielle was soon known throughout the Seven Kingdoms as Lady Turnips. Many lords disapproved of Peake trying to forward the match so soon after the suspicious death of Queen Jaehaera. Lord Cregan_Stark suggested the north would look on the match with disfavor, Lord Kermit_Tully called it presumptuous, and Grand Maester Munkun's support began to waver, acknowledging that the match would be seen as advancing House Peake's interests rather than being for the good of the realm. Due to pressure from the lords and ladies of the realm, Uwin instead announced the Maiden's Day Ball. Myrielle arrived a fortnight before the ball, and Unwin made sure that his daughter spent as much time as possible with the king. Half a dozen dinners together were held, and it seemed like the king was fonder of Myrielle than he ever was of Jaehaera. Three days before the ball, he gave her one of Jaehaera's dolls. Myrielle took this as a token of affection when she was presented at the Maiden's Day ball, cradling the doll in her arms like a babe. The king greeted her by name, and told her he was pleased she liked the doll. Aegon III did not marry Myrielle, however, instead wedding Lady Daenaera_Velaryon. Lord Peake took the rejection of his daughter as a personal affront. This was partly the reason for his resignation as Hand. As Lord Unwin's only surviving child, it is possible that Myrielle inherited Starpike, Dunstonbury and Whitegrove at some point.

name: Myrio Myrakis
bio: Myrio Myrakis is a Pentoshi sellsword and member of the Windblown. Myrio_Myrakis's_cousin is the cupbearer for the Tattered_Prince.

name: Myrio Myrakis's cousin
bio: Myrio Myrakis's cousin is a Pentoshi member of the Windblown. His given name is unknown.

name: Myrmadora Haen
bio: Myrmadora Haen was a Lyseni noblewoman of House Haen, a possible marriage candidate for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Myrmadora was the daughter of Torreo_Haen, a Lyseni magister. She attended the Maiden's Day Ball wearing a gown of translucent blue-green silk that matched her eyes, with only a jeweled girdle underneath. The dress shocked others, but the Kingsguard barred her from King Aegon_III_Targaryen's throne room until she changed clothes. In 135 AC, Myrmadora was poisoned along with her mother, sister(s), father, her father's siblings, and her father's supporters at the feast he held to celebrate his becoming First Magister of Lys.

name: Myrmello
bio: Myrmello is a mummer of the Ship.

name: Myros
bio: Myros[N 1] was a maester of the Citadel who served as Grand Maester during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. After returning to King's Landing following his victory at the Battle at the Great Fork, King Maegor_I_Targaryen declared his intent to take Tyanna_of_the_Tower as his third wife. Myros dared to speak against the marriage openly, stating that Maegor's true wife, Ceryse_Hightower, awaited at Oldtown. Maegor heard him out in silence before drawing Blackfyre and killing Myros where he stood. Myros was one of three Grand Maesters were executed during Maegor's reign, the others being Gawen and Desmond_(Grand_Maester).

name: Myrtle
bio: Myrtle is a wildling spearwife.

name: Mysaria
bio: Mysaria, also called Lady Misery, was a dancer from Lys who became the paramour of Prince Daemon_Targaryen and the unofficial mistress of whisperers to Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen during the Dance of the Dragons. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Mysaria is portrayed by Sonoya Mizuno.

name: Naerys Targaryen
bio: Queen Naerys Targaryen was a member of House Targaryen and the sister-wife to King Aegon_IV_Targaryen, who was known as the Unworthy. Naerys was the sister of Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II), and the mother of King Daeron_II_Targaryen and Princess Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_IV). On the last day of 153 AC, Naerys gave birth for the first time, to a son named Daeron_II_Targaryen. His birth was difficult for Naerys, however, and Grand Maester Alford warned her that another pregnancy could kill her. Naerys thus went to her husband, and asked him, as he now had an heir, if they could live as brother and sister, instead of husband and wife. Aegon refused. The marriage of Aegon_IV_Targaryen and Naerys was an unhappy one. Aegon is known to have stated that he had loved nine women in his entire lifetime, yet Naerys was not counted as being among those. While unhappy in her marriage, Naerys still had her brother Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II) and her son Daeron, who both knew how to make her smile. Throughout the years, Naerys went through several more difficult pregnancies, despite Alford's warning that another pregnancy could kill her. In 161 AC, Naerys nearly died giving birth to twins, and the twins' deaths shortly after birth led King Baelor_I_Targaryen to fast for a moon's turn. Baelor also sent Naerys's husband, Aegon, to Braavos on a diplomatic mission after this pregnancy. Accounts at the time suggest it was an excuse to make sure Aegon left Naerys alone as she recovered from the difficult childbirth. In 172 AC, Naerys gave birth to a second set of twins. The boy was stillborn, but the girl, Princess Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_IV), survived. Yet again, this delivery caused Naerys to linger near death, and Aegon's Hand of the King, Lord_Bracken, went as far as to speak openly about marrying his own daughter Barba_Bracken (who had given birth to the king's bastard Aegor_Rivers a fortnight before Naerys had gone into labor) to Aegon, after Naerys's expected eventual death. However, Naerys eventually recovered, and the scandal caused Lord Bracken to be removed from his office. Naerys died in childbirth a year after Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II)'s death. The child, never mentioned again, most likely died as well.

name: Nage
bio: Nage is a guard of House Bolton.

name: Naggle
bio: Naggle is the innkeep of the Old Stone Bridge, an inn that lies along the road to Duskendale.

name: Nail
bio: Nail is an apprentice to the Second Sons' blacksmith and armorer known as Hammer.

name: Nan
bio: Nan, generally referred to as Old Nan, is a servant woman at Winterfell. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Nan is portrayed by Margaret John, and in a Season 6 flashback by Annette Tierney. Old Nan is the oldest person at Winterfell. She came to the castle to nurse a Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Willam); she does not recall which one, only that Lyanne_Glover had died birthing him. Sometimes she says that Brandon Stark was the older brother of Lord Rickard_Stark, or perhaps his younger brother, or a brother of Edwyle_Stark.[N 1] Nan lost both of her sons during Robert's Rebellion, her grandson at the siege of Pyke during Greyjoy's Rebellion, and her daughters moved away and died. Her only living relative is her great-grandson, Hodor, who was called "Hodor" after he started to say that word. When Lord Eddard_Stark was young, Old Nan taught him the proverb "dark wings, dark words". Old Nan sometimes eats at the table of Lord Eddard. The elderly servant often tells stories to Eddard's children, as well as his ward, Theon_Greyjoy. Theon once carelessly leapt down the stairs of the Great Keep and accidentally knocked Old Nan to the ground, for which he was punished with a thrashing, the worst he ever had at Winterfell. The elderly woman sometimes confuses Bran_Stark with other Brandon Starks she has known, as well as the various Brandon Starks with each other. When Lord Eddard would not forbid Bran from climbing the walls of Winterfell, Catelyn_Stark, Bran's mother, instead turned to others to convince him otherwise, and Old Nan told him a story about a boy who climbed too high and was struck by lightning.

name: Narbert
bio: Narbert is a brother and proctor of the order on the Quiet Isle.

name: Narbert Grandison
bio: Narbert Grandison  is a knight from House Grandison in the service of King Stannis_Baratheon. He is one of the queen's men.

name: Narbo
bio: Little Narbo is a dockside cutpurse and thief in Braavos. Narbo was Tagganaro's partner until he lost the use of three fingers after being stabbed in the hand by the Drunken_Daughter.

name: Narha Otherys
bio: Narha Otherys was the second of the three bastard children of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen and his fourth mistress, Bellegere_Otherys. After Princess Naerys_Targaryen had almost died, giving birth in 161 AC, her husband, Prince Aegon_IV_Targaryen was send to Braavos as an envoy, so Naerys would have sufficient time to recover. In Braavos, Aegon met Bellegere Otherys, with whom he started an affair. Aegon and Bellegere had an affair for ten years, during which Bellegere gave birth to three children: Bellenora_Otherys, Narha and Balerion_Otherys. Their paternity remains doubtful, as Bellegere is said to have had a husband in every port she visited.

name: Nate
bio: Nate is a serving man and maester's assistant in service of House Martell. He is known as Feathers because he has long worked with the ravens at the Water Gardens.

name: Naylin
bio: Naylin was the maester who wrote Rubies and Iron.

name: Ned
bio: Ned was the ferryman at the old inn by the lakeshore near Whitewalls during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. In 212 AC, Ned ferried several guests across the lake for the wedding tourney at Whitewalls, including Ser John the Fiddler, Lord_Shawney, Lord_Costayne_(Aerys_I), Lord Smallwood, and various hedge knights.

name: Ned Bean
bio: Ned Bean, called Blackbean, was a notorious sellsail who became second-in-command of the royal fleet during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. When Lord Unwin_Peake became Hand of the King, he made his uncle Ser Gedmund_Peake commander of the royal fleet with the more experienced Ned Bean as his second-in-command. Ser Gedmund and Ned, with reinforcement from the Velaryon fleet commanded by Lord Alyn_Velaryon, were sent against Racallio_Ryndoon, the self-proclaimed King of the Narrow Sea. When Racallio allied with Tyrosh, Ser Gedmund and Ned remained at Tarth to await further instructions from Lord Unwin. Lord Alyn protested they would lose time, however, and left them behind. Lord Alyn's attack was a great victory against the Braavosi.

name: Ned Woods
bio: Ned Woods, known as Noseless Ned, is a member of House Woods. A man of the wolfswood sworn to House Glover of Deepwood Motte.

name: Nella
bio: Nella is a wildling wife of Craster. Nella has borne six sons for Craster, all of them left to die.

name: Nestor Royce
bio: Nestor Royce is the senior member of the junior branch of House Royce and is the Keeper of the Gates of the Moon. A widower, Lord Nestor is the cousin of Yohn_Royce, Lord of Runestone. Nestor has served as High Steward of the Vale for the fourteen years prior to Lord Jon_Arryn's death. Lord Jon_Arryn made Nestor Royce Keeper of the Gates of the Moon and High Steward of the Vale, to rule the Vale while Jon served as Hand of the King in King's Landing. Nestor served in that position for fourteen years. Nestor hoped to marry his daughter, Myranda_Royce, to Harrold_Hardyng. However, Harry's guardian, Lady Anya_Waynwood, rejected the offer, though it is uncertain whether she was displeased with Myranda's character or her dowry. Nestor was somewhat annoyed that his daughter was widowed so quickly after her marriage.

name: Nettles
bio: Nettles, also known as Netty, was a dragonseed and the first and possibly last dragonrider of the dragon Sheepstealer. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Nettles is merged with Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon), who is portrayed by Phoebe Campbell. According to Mushroom's Testimony, Nettles was a bastard of uncertain birth, born to a dockside whore. She grew up homeless in Spicetown and Hull on the island of Driftmark. According to Septon Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons)'s history, Nettles's nose was slit after she was caught thieving. During the Dance of the Dragons, when Prince Jacaerys_Velaryon decided that the blacks needed more dragonriders in 129 AC, several "dragonseeds" on Dragonstone came forward to answer the prince's call. Nettles, sixteen years old, was able to tame the wild dragon Sheepstealer by bringing it a freshly slaughtered sheep each morning, causing Sheepstealer to become accustomed to the girl. Nettles fought valiantly on behalf of Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen in the Battle of the Gullet in 130 AC. While her fellow dragonriders Hugh_Hammer and Ulf_White celebrated after the battle, Nettles was tearful, perhaps upset by the death of Jacaerys as well as the destruction of Spicetown and the butchering of its people. During the fall of King's Landing, Nettles landed Sheepstealer on Visenya's Hill. Nettles and Sheepstealer joined Prince Daemon_Targaryen, Rhaenyra's husband, and his dragon Caraxes in hunting Prince Aemond_Targaryen and Vhagar along the Trident, as Daemon felt at least two dragons were necessary to confront Vhagar. The two dragonriders stayed at House Mooton's castle at Maidenpool, from where they continued their search every morning. According to Mushroom, Daemon had come to love Nettles, and took her as his bedmate, despite the fact that she was an unlikely paramour for a prince. Though Mushroom's veracity is often questionable, Gyldayn considers this story to be far more likely than most of his tales. Gyldayn further references Maester Norren_(House_Mooton)'s Chronicles of Maidenpool, which recorded that "the prince and his bastard girl" slept in adjoining bedrooms, but that they ate together each night and morning. Norren wrote that Daemon doted upon Nettles "as a man might dote upon his daughter", and described Daemon teaching Nettles common courtesies and giving her fine gifts, such as an ivory hairbrush, a silvered looking glass, a velvet and satin cloak, and leather riding boots. Norren further wrote that Daemon showed Nettles how to wash herself properly, and recorded that maidservants reported they often shared a single tub, washing each other. After the treason of the dragonseeds Hugh Hammer and Ulf White at the First Battle of Tumbleton, Queen Rhaenyra's court at the Red Keep was outraged. Lady Mysaria, once Daemon's paramour, fed Rhaenyra's growing distrust of the dragonseeds with rumors of Nettles and Daemon's relationship. Rhaenyra raged, declaring that Nettles had used spells to bond with Sheepstealer and to seduce her husband, and that Daemon could not be relied upon while in the girl's thrall. The queen sent a raven to Manfryd_Mooton, Lord of Maidenpool, ordering him to deliver Nettles's head but not to do Daemon any harm, only to tell him to return to the capital. However, according to Maester Norren, Manfryd and his advisors, fearful of how Daemon slept beside Nettles, unwilling to kill a guest or a young girl (or two guests, to prevent Daemon from becoming enraged at the death of the girl he was "more than fond of" and taking out his wrath on Maidenpool), disobeyed the queen and followed Norren's advice to pretend that they had never received the letter. When Maester Norren informed Daemon that Rhaenyra had declared Nettles a traitor, Daemon recognized Mysaria's actions in Rhaenyra's words, and and thanked the maester for the warning, before he allowed Nettles to escape the next morning. It is unknown how the two spent their last night together, but at dawn Daemon helped Nettles saddle Sheepstealer one last time. Nettles killed a large black ram and fed it to her dragon, then tearfully flew Sheepstealer into the morning mists of the Bay of Crabs. According to Norren, no word of farewell was spoken between Daemon and Nettles, but as Sheepstealer flew away, Caraxes let out a scream that shattered all the windows of Jonquil's Tower. That same day, Daemon did not return to his wife in King's Landing, but publicly announced his departure for Harrenhal. He perished fighting Aemond in the Battle Above the Gods Eye. Since Daemon's body was never found in the Gods Eye, some songs claim that he survived the encounter and found his way back to Nettles. Rhaenyra was enraged by the disappearance of Nettles, and her husband's betrayal. When King Aegon_II_Targaryen regained the Iron Throne after Rhaenyra's death, there were reports that Sheepstealer had been seen at Crackclaw Point and the Mountains of the Moon. In 134 AC, during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen, Ser Robert_Rowan led a royal army to the Vale of Arryn to support Ser Joffrey_Arryn. Robert's men encountered Sheepstealer and a ragged Nettles in a cave, and in the ensuing fight sixteen men were slain and threescore more were wounded. Nettles and her dragon were last seen flying deeper into the Mountains of the Moon. No sighting of Nettles and Sheepstealer was ever again recorded in the chronicles of Westeros, but to this day, the Vale mountain clans tell stories about a "fire witch" who once lived in a hidden valley within the mountains. An offshoot clan of the Painted Dogs, one of the most savage of the mountain clans, worshipped her. Their boys would prove their courage by bringing her gifts, and were only considered men after they had faced the flames of her dragon and returned with burns. Some of the maesters of the Seven Kingdoms believe that this practice was the origin of the tradition held nowadays by the Burned Men, where youths must give some part of their body "to the fire" to prove they have the courage to be a man.

name: Nettles (free folk)
bio: Nettles is a spearwife of the free folk.

name: Nevio Narratys
bio: Nevio Narratys was a Prince of Pentos during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Nevio was chosen as Prince during the latest war between Pentos and Braavos over slavery, which ended in 209 AC. He was the fifth prince chosen in the span of that single year, for the war had been a disaster for Pentos, where Magisters sacrifice the Prince should a crop be lost or a war be lost. Nevio convinced the magisters to sue for peace after a rare victory - one that is rumored to have been purchased by Nevio himself through bribery. In these peace accords Pentos was forced to abolish slavery and withdraw from the slave trade.

name: Nicol
bio: Nicol was a maester of the Citadel who wrote the book The Measure of the Days in which he argued that seasons of the world might once have been of regular length.

name: Night's King
bio: Night's King, also called the Night's King, is a legendary Lord Commander of the Night's Watch who lived during the Age of Heroes. His given name is unknown. The legend of the Night's King is frequently told, and in it the Night's King may be a Bolton, a Woodfoot, an Umber, a Flint, a Norrey, or even a Stark, depending on where the tale is told and who tells it. The legend is recorded in Archmaester Harmune's book, Watchers on the Wall, which includes many stories of the Nightfort. In the Citadel, archmaesters generally dismiss the tale as only fiction, though some consider the possibility that an early Lord Commander did indeed try to create a kingdom for himself. Some suggest that the corpse queen was actually a barrowlander, a daughter of the Barrow King. If the event truly happened, it is possible that it is what led the Night's Watch to never construct walls at their keeps, ensuring the keeps would always be accessible from the south.

name: Nightingale (courtesan)
bio: The Nightingale is a famous courtesan in Braavos. Her given name is unknown.

name: Nine Eyes
bio: The man known as Nine Eyes became one of the infamous Band of Nine, the instigators of the War of the Ninepenny Kings. The Band of Nine were a group of merchants, sellswords and pirates who joined together to carve kingdoms out for themselves. Nine Eyes was captain of the Jolly Fellows, a possible pirate mercenary company.

name: Nissa Nissa
bio: Nissa Nissa was the wife of Azor_Ahai. He sacrificed her to create the famous sword, Lightbringer.

name: Noho Dimittis
bio: Noho Dimittis is a Braavosi representative of the Iron Bank of Braavos.

name: Nolla
bio: Goodwife Nolla is an inhabitant of the riverlands.

name: Norbert Vance
bio: Norbert Vance is the Lord of Atranta and the head of House Vance of Atranta. Norbert squired for Lord Darry in his youth, alongside a young Brynden_Tully.

name: Norjen
bio: Norjen is one of Aeron_Greyjoy's drowned men.

name: Norman Hightower
bio: Norman Hightower was a knight from House Hightower during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He was a grandson of Lord Donnel_Hightower and an accomplished mariner. In 56 AC, Norman and his brother, Ser Eustace_Hightower, accompanied Lady Elissa_Farman, under the alias Alys Westhill, on her voyage to discover new lands. Lord Donnel_Hightower received a raven from the Iron Throne that Elissa was to be arrested and brought back to King's Landing in chains, but by the time Donnel acted, Norman and his brother had already set sail with her. Sailing west and south of Westeros into the Sunset Sea, the expedition began suffering from storms during their second week of sailing. Norman's Autumn Moon foundered during one storm and sank under a wave. Some of the men on Eustace's ship, Lady Meredith, claimed that a kraken had brought down Norman's ship.

name: Normund Tyrell
bio: Normund Tyrell is a maester of the Citadel in service at Blackcrown to House Bulwer. He is a cousin of Lord Mace_Tyrell.

name: Norne Goodbrother
bio: Norne Goodbrother is the Lord of Shatterstone and the head of House Goodbrother of Shatterstone.

name: Norren
bio: Norren is an Archmaester of the Citadel. His ring, mask and rod are made of electrum.

name: Norren (House Mooton)
bio: Norren was a maester in service to Lord Manfryd_Mooton of Maidenpool during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. He was also the keeper of the Chronicles of Maidenpool. When Lord Manfryd_Mooton received a letter from Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen, ordering him to execute her husband Daemon_Targaryen's paramour Nettles, Maester Norren is among those who he asked for their counsel. When Lord Manfryd, deeply disturbed by the request to break the sacred laws of hospitality, expressed how he wished he had never read the letter, Norren suggested that "Mayhaps you never did." Norren later showed Prince Daemon_Targaryen and Nettles the letter, claiming he had not shown it to anyone else. Daemon called him a bad maester but a good man, and requested that Norren not say a word to anyone until the next day. The following morning, both Nettles and Daemon departed Maidenpool. After they were gone, Norren went to Lord Manfryd, asking him to take his maester's chain off his neck, bind his hands with it, and to deliver him to Rhaenyra for allowing a traitor to escape. Lord Manfryd replied "Keep your chain. We are all traitors here." Maidenpool declared for King Aegon_II_Targaryen that night, forsaking Rhaenyra's cause.

name: Norwin Lannister
bio: Norwin Lannister was a King of the Rock of House Lannister of Casterly Rock.

name: Notch
bio: Notch is a longbowman of the brotherhood without banners. Notch was born near the lands of House Goodbrook.

name: Nurse
bio: Nurse is a Ghiscari commoner and the overseer of Yezzan_zo_Qaggaz's slaves.

name: Nute
bio: Nute the Barber is an ironborn raider and the best man of Lord Victarion_Greyjoy's crew aboard the Iron Victory. Nute earned his nickname of "the Barber" due to his ability in his youth to throw an axe so well that it was said he could shave you with it.

name: Nyessos Vhassar
bio: Nyessos Vhassar is one of the current ruling triarchs of the Free City of Volantis. He is of the elephant party.

name: Nyles Rowan
bio: Nyles Rowan was a knight of House Rowan during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Nyles participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Nymella Toland
bio: Nymella Toland is the Lady of Ghost Hill and the head of House Toland. She has two daughters, Valena_Toland and Teora_Toland.

name: Nymeria
bio: Princess Nymeria of Ny Sar was a princess of the Rhoynar. After the Rhoyne was conquered by the Valyrian Freehold, Nymeria led the Rhoynar to Dorne, where she took Lord Mors_Martell of the Sandship as her husband. After years of war and conquest, House Nymeros Martell established supremacy over Dorne, with Nymeria ruling for well over two decades as the Princess of Dorne. Through Nymeria's rule, the Dornish adopted many Rhoynish customs and laws that remain to this day.

name: Nymeria (direwolf)
bio: Nymeria is a direwolf bonded to Arya_Stark. She is a litter-mate of Grey Wind, Lady, Summer, Shaggydog, and Ghost. She is named after Princess Nymeria of the Rhoynar.

name: Nymeria Sand
bio: Nymeria Sand, nicknamed Lady Nym, is a bastard daughter of Oberyn_Martell and a a noblewoman from Volantis. She is the second oldest of the infamous Sand Snakes, so named for their bastard origin and the nickname of their father, the "Red Viper". In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Nymeria is portrayed by Jessica Henwick and stated to be the daughter of a YiTish noblewoman instead of a Volantene one. Nymeria's mother comes from the noblest blood of Volantis, the Free City where Nym was born. Young Nym sometimes joined in play at the Water Gardens with her sister, Tyene_Sand, her cousin, Princess Arianne_Martell, Garin_(orphan), Andrey_Dalt, Sylva_Santagar, the Fowler twins, and the Archon's_green-haired_daughter. Arianne keeps in her boot a throwing knife gifted to her by Nymeria.

name: Nymor Martell
bio: Nymor Martell was the ruling Prince of Dorne and head of House Martell during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. During the War of Conquest, Dorne had defied the Targaryens and their dragons, but in 4 AC, King Aegon_I_Targaryen began his campaign on Dorne, in an attempt to finalize his Conquest. This First Dornish War would last until 13 AC. In 13 AC, Nymor's mother, Princess Meria_Martell, the Yellow Toad of Dorne, died, and Nymor inherited the rule of Dorne. Already old and failing, Nymor wanted peace. He sent a delegation, led by his daughter and heir, Princess Deria_Martell, to King's Landing, to negotiate a peace with King Aegon - a peace between two kingdoms, two equals, not a peace between a vassal and its overlord. It is said that King Aegon was about to refuse, when Deria handed him a letter from Nymor. King Aegon read the letter on the Iron Throne, allegedly clenching the bladed seat so hard his hand started to bleed. Once finished, he burned the letter, and flew to Dragonstone on Balerion without a word. When he returned the next morning, he agreed to the peace and signed a treaty. It is unknown what content was in the letter that Nymor had written, but many have speculated. Some claim it was a simple plea that touched Aegon’s heart. Others insist it was a list of all those lords and noble knights who had lost their lives during the war. Some believe it said that Queen Rhaenys_Targaryen had survived her fall at Hellholt, but was being kept captive in extreme torment, and the Dornish promised to end her suffering if Aegon agreed to end the war. Others suspect the letter stated that Nymor was willing to spend all of Dorne's gold to hire the Faceless Men of Braavos to assassinate Aegon's young son and heir, Prince Aenys_I_Targaryen, if Aegon did not sign the peace. Certain septons even suggest that the missive was ensorceled. The answer to those questions is unknown, but the peace that Nymor and Aegon agreed on in 13 AC would last throughout Aegon's entire reign.

name: Nymos
bio: Archmaester Nymos is an archmaester at the Citadel in Oldtown.

name: Nysseos Qoheros
bio: Nysseos Qoheros was a Volantene and the author of Journals. Among the events he reports the fate of Triarch Horonno, who tried to declare himself triarch for life and was executed for it.

name: Nysterica
bio: Nysterica is a septa assigned to House Tyrell.

name: Obara Sand
bio: Obara Sand is the bastard daughter of Oberyn_Martell and Obara_Sand's_mother from Oldtown. She is the eldest of the infamous Sand Snakes. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Obara is portrayed by Keisha Castle-Hughes. Obara was born to a prostitute in Oldtown. When Prince Oberyn_Martell came to claim the girl, Obara_Sand's_mother did not want to let her go and insisted that Obara's father was one of the other men she had been with. Oberyn tossed his spear at Obara's feet and slapped her mother in the face. He then told the girl to choose, pointing to the spear and her mother's tears. Obara chose the former, and her mother drank herself to death within a year. Obara spent part of her youth at the Water Gardens, but not as long a time as her kin.

name: Obara Sand's mother
bio: Obara Sand's mother was a prostitute in Oldtown who was the mother of Obara_Sand. Her given name is unknown. Obara_Sand was fathered by Prince Oberyn_Martell on a prostitute in Oldtown. When Oberyn came to claim Obara, her mother disputed that Oberyn was actually the father, as she had slept with numerous other men. Oberyn tossed a spear on the ground and struck the mother, causing her to cry. When Oberyn gave Obara the choice of the spear or her mother's tears, the girl chose the weapon. The mother drank herself to death within the year after Obara left for Dorne. Obara now hates the city of Oldtown.

name: Obella Sand
bio: Obella Sand is the second bastard daughter of Prince Oberyn_Martell and his paramour Ellaria_Sand. She is the sixth of the famous Sand Snakes.

name: Oberyn Martell
bio: Prince Oberyn Nymeros Martell, also known as the Red Viper, is a member of House Martell and is the younger brother of Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne. Oberyn has eight bastard daughters called the Sand Snakes, the four youngest of whom are by his current paramour, Ellaria_Sand. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Oberyn is portrayed by Pedro Pascal. As a child, Oberyn Martell played the shoulder-riding game in the pools of the Water Gardens. His older brother Doran_Martell had long left the pools by the time Oberyn was old enough to play, but would watch him while visiting Princess_of_Dorne_(mother_of_Doran). Oberyn fought fiercely and quickly, often toppling boys much bigger than himself. In his youth, Oberyn was fostered at Sandstone. When in his teens, Oberyn traveled with Princess_of_Dorne_(mother_of_Doran), the ruling Princess of Dorne, and his sister, Princess Elia_Martell, and met multiple potential marriage candidates for his sister, visiting castles at Starfall, the Arbor, Oldtown, the Shield Islands, and Crakehall. Oberyn spent his time mocking Elia's suitors. The last castle they visited was Casterly Rock, where they arrived shortly after the death of Lady Joanna_Lannister in childbirth. According to Oberyn, his mother and Joanna had planned to wed either Oberyn to Cersei_Lannister, or Elia to Jaime_Lannister, or both. Lord Tywin_Lannister, however, was unreceptive of the suggestion, stating that his daughter would marry Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen. He offered the newborn Tyrion_Lannister as a husband for Elia instead, which was seen as an insult. At the age of sixteen, Oberyn was found in bed with the paramour of Lord Edgar_Yronwood, so the lord challenged him to a duel. The duel was to first blood, given the prince's youth and high birth, and both took cuts. Edgar's wounds festered and killed him. Oberyn has been known as the "Red Viper" ever since by friends and foes alike, due to rumors that he fought the duel with a poisoned blade. Afterward, Oberyn was sent to Oldtown and then to Lys in temporary exile (though none called it such) in order for House Martell to make peace with House Yronwood, and Prince Quentyn_Martell was sent as a ward to Yronwood. Oberyn traveled extensively in the Free Cities, where he learned much about poisons and perhaps even darker arts. He had studied at the Citadel, going so far as to forge six links of a maester's chain before he grew bored. He soldiered in the Disputed Lands across the narrow sea, riding with the Second Sons for a time before forming his own company. It is said that he beds both men and women, and has begotten eight bastard daughters, called the Sand Snakes. Oberyn, however, does not let his children grow up wanting: he takes responsibility for them, and actively seeks to help raise all his children (something few lords do when it comes to their bastard offspring). He teaches them how to defend themselves and allows them great independence in growing up. Prince Oberyn was unhorsed by Ser Barristan_Selmy in Lord Steffon's tourney at Storm's End.[N 1] He was also present during the tourney at Harrenhal in 281 AC, where he danced with Lady Ashara_Dayne.

name: Ocley
bio: Archmaester  Ocley is an archmaester at the  Citadel in Oldtown.

name: Ogo
bio: Ogo is a Dothraki khal. He has a son, Fogo.

name: Old Grey Gull
bio: The Old Grey Gull is a priest of the Drowned God. His true name is unknown.

name: Old Mother
bio: The Old Mother was a pirate queen who became one of the infamous Band of Nine, the instigators of the War of the Ninepenny Kings. The name of the Old Mother's Son, a galley in the fleet of Salladhor_Saan, seems to be inspired by her. The Band of Nine were a group of merchants, sellswords and pirates who joined together to carve kingdoms out for themselves.

name: Old Pate
bio: Old Pate has a cottage in the riverlands near the Inn of the Kneeling Man.

name: Old Tattersalt
bio: Old Tattersalt is a member of the Night's Watch. He is the captain of the Blackbird.

name: Olene Tyrell
bio: Olene Tyrell is the only daughter of Ser Luthor_Tyrell_(son_of_Moryn) and Elyn_Norridge. She is married to Ser Leo_Blackbar.

name: Olenna Redwyne
bio: Olenna Tyrell, née Redwyne, is the widow of Lord Luthor_Tyrell and the mother of Mace_Tyrell, Lord of Highgarden. Born into House Redwyne of the Arbor, she is commonly known as the Queen of Thorns because of her sharp tongue. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Olenna is portrayed by Dame Diana Rigg. Olenna Redwyne was betrothed to Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon_V) when they were both nine, but she claims she did everything she could to make sure it never came to fruition. According to maesters, it was Daeron who broke the betrothal when they were eighteen in 246 AC, but Olenna speaks contemptuously of the "queer notions" of House Targaryen and Baratheon, presumably referring to Targaryen madness as well as to Renly_Baratheon and Daeron's homosexuality. Olenna later married Luthor_Tyrell, the heir to Highgarden, who had also been spurned by his own Shaera_Targaryen. At some point Luthor became Lord of Highgarden, and he and Olenna had three children together, Mace_Tyrell, Mina_Tyrell, and Janna_Tyrell. Olenna says she loved Luthor well enough, as he was kind and not unskilled in bed, but she still considered him an oaf. An example she cites is that Luthor rode off a cliff to his death while hawking, because he had been looking up at the sky and not paying attention to where his horse was going. Olenna feels that her son Mace is also an oaf, and regrets that she did not beat him more often when he was a child, as that would have made him more ready to heed her advice as an adult. Olenna's nephew is Paxter_Redwyne, Lord of the Arbor, who is also married to her daughter Mina Tyrell. Olenna has twin guardsmen named Erryk_(guard) and Arryk_(guard), whom she refers to as "Left and Right" because she cannot tell them apart.

name: Ollidar
bio: Ollidar was an archmaester of the Citadel who became the first Grand Maester. Although King Aegon_I_Targaryen already had maesters of his own, in 5 AC he asked the Citadel for an archmaester to advise him on governing the Seven Kingdoms. The Conclave selected the experienced Archmaester Ollidar, the keeper of histories. The new Grand Maester was exceptionally old, however, and he died less than a year after his appointment. Ollidar was succeeded as Grand Maester by Archmaester Lyonce.

name: Ollidor
bio: Septon Ollidor is a follower of the Faith of the Seven. He is stationed in King's Landing and a member of the Most Devout.

name: Ollo Lophand
bio: Ollo, dubbed Ollo Lophand, is a ranger of the Night's Watch. Ollo is from Tyrosh. He lost a hand after being caught for thievery and was sent to the Wall after being found abed with a knight's wife.

name: Olymer Tyrell
bio: Olymer Tyrell is the only child of Ser Quentin_Tyrell. The knight is married to Lysa_Meadows, and they have three children, Raymund_Tyrell, Rickard_Tyrell and Megga_Tyrell.

name: Olyvar Frey
bio: Olyvar Frey is a member of House Frey. He is the eighteenth son of Lord Walder_Frey, the fourth with his sixth wife, Bethany_Rosby.

name: Olyvar Martell
bio: Prince Olyvar Nymeros Martell was a younger brother of Prince Doran_Martell. He died as an infant.

name: Olyvar Oakheart
bio: Olyvar Oakheart, known as the Green Oak, was a knight from House Oakheart. He was a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Daeron_I_Targaryen. Olyvar died at the side of his king, Daeron_I_Targaryen, as depicted in a tapestry at Old Oak. King Daeron was slain under a banner of peace when the Dornishmen betrayed and attacked him during the Conquest of Dorne in 161 AC. Three knights of his Kingsguard died in his defense, and Olyvar Oakheart may have been of these three men.

name: Olyvar Yronwood
bio: Olyvar Yronwood was a King of Yronwood from House Yronwood. Olyvar repeatedly led incursions into the stormlands during the reign of the Storm King Erich_VII_Durrandon.

name: Olyver Bracken
bio: Olyver Bracken was a knight from House Bracken, and a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Ser Olyver and his sworn brother of the Kingsguard, Ser Raymund_Mallery, abandoned King Maegor_I_Targaryen in 48 AC when Prince Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen made his claim for the Iron Throne. Since both men had broken their Kingsguard oaths by not protecting Maegor, and Jaehaerys did not wish for oathbreakers to serve him, after taking the throne he offered them the choice of execution or taking the black. They both chose to join the Night's Watch. The Lord Commander of the Night's Watch gave Ser Olyver command of Rimegate and Ser Raymund command of Sable Hall, with orders to restore the crumbling forts of the Wall. However, in 50 AC, Olyver and Raymund led a rebellion of former Faith Militant in the Watch, trying to make themselves lords of their castles. Lord Walton_Stark_(son_of_Brandon) rode north to aid the loyal brothers with ending the mutiny. The defeated rebels at Rimegate were hanged, with the exception of Olyver, who was beheaded by Lord Stark with the Valyrian steel greatsword Ice.

name: Omer Blackberry
bio: Omer Blackberry is a sworn sword from House Blackberry in service at Dragonstone.

name: Omer Florent
bio: Omer Florent is the eldest son of Ser Colin_Florent, a younger brother of Lord Alester_Florent of Brightwater Keep. Omer is a maester of the Citadel in service at Old Oak to House Oakheart.

name: Ondrew Locke
bio: Ondrew Locke is the Lord of Oldcastle and the head of House Locke.

name: One-eyed crone
bio: The one-eyed crone is an ancient crone of the dosh khaleen who dwell in Vaes Dothrak. Her given name is unknown.

name: Onqo
bio: Onqo was a Dothraki khal who lived during the Century of Blood. Onqo led a particularly huge khalasar. Onqo began to make incursions into the Ibbenese territories in Essos, only to be killed by the Ibbenese in an epic battle that destroyed his khalasar. His grandson was Khal Dhako.

name: Onyx Emperor
bio: The Onyx Emperor was the legendary fifth ruler of the mythic Great Empire of the Dawn. He succeeded the Tourmaline_Emperor and was in turn succeeded by the Topaz_Emperor.

name: Opal Emperor
bio: The Opal Emperor was the legendary seventh ruler of the mythic Great Empire of the Dawn and one of its last emperors. He succeeded the Topaz_Emperor and was in turn succeeded by his daughter, the Amethyst_Empress, though his second son would usurp his sister and crown himself as the Bloodstone_Emperor.

name: Oppo
bio: Oppo, better known as Groat, is a dwarf from across the narrow sea. Oppo was the son of Hop-Bean. Oppo and his sister Penny make their living as entertainers who ride atop a pig and a dog while mock fighting each other. Oppo rides the Pig. During these fights they used the names Groat and Penny after the two smallest coins.

name: Orbelo
bio: Orbelo has fair hair and wears a jacket made of green velvet and cloth of silver. Orbelo and his companion Terro confront Samwell_Tarly outside the Cattery, a brothel in Braavos. Due to the timely intervention of Arya_Stark, the bravos decide to leave Samwell alone.

name: Orbert Caswell
bio: Orbert Caswell, known as the Black Centaur, was a member of House Caswell who became Lord Commander of the Night's Watch. Lord Commander Orbert served nine years in the position. He died in the night. The book Annals of the Black Centaur by Septon Jorquen was written about his era.

name: Ordello
bio: Ordello is a magister of Pentos.

name: Orell
bio: Orell is a free folk raider and a skinchanger who controls an eagle. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Orell is portrayed by Mackenzie Crook.

name: Orivel
bio: Ser Orivel the Open-Handed was a knight of the Kingsguard at an unknown time. He is remembered as a knight who shamed the white cloak of the Kingsguard. History remembers him as a coward.

name: Orland of Oldtown
bio: Orland of Oldtown, also known as Ormond of Oldtown, is a singer. He is the royal harper and bard for House Baratheon of King's Landing.

name: Orlos
bio: Orlos the Catamite is a pit fighter in Meereen.

name: Ormond
bio: Ormond is a knight from the riverlands.

name: Ormond Osgrey
bio: Ormond Osgrey was the Lord of Coldmoat and the head of House Osgrey during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. During the Faith Militant uprising, Lord Ormond spoke out against King Maegor's laws that suppressed the Poor Fellows and the Warrior's Sons, the militant orders of the Faith of the Seven. Maegor took Coldmoat away from House Osgrey in retaliation for the outburst.

name: Ormond Westerling
bio: Ormond Westerling was a knight from House Westerling during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Ser Ormond participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow of 209 AC.

name: Ormond Yronwood
bio: Ormond Yronwood was the Lord of Yronwood and the head of House Yronwood. He was the father of Anders_Yronwood and the son of Lord Edgar_Yronwood.

name: Ormund Baratheon
bio: Ormund Baratheon was the Lord of Storm's End and the head of House Baratheon during the reign of kings Aegon_V_Targaryen and Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen. He served as Hand of the King for Jaehaerys. Ormund was the heir of Lyonel_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End. Duncan_Targaryen, Prince of Dragonstone, was betrothed to Lyonel's daughter, but in 239 AC, he spurned her to elope with a smallfolk woman named Jenny_of_Oldstones. Though Duncan's father, King Aegon V Targaryen, did all he could to have the marriage undone, Duncan stubbornly refused to give up Jenny, and instead abdicated his claim to the throne in favor of Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen. Still, this did not restore the peace — Lyonel, infuriated at the insult to his daughter and himself, rebelled against the Iron Throne and crowned himself as a new Storm King. The short and bloody rebellion was eventually settled when Duncan_the_Tall defeated Lyonel in single combat, and King Aegon V promised that his youngest daughter, Princess Rhaelle_Targaryen, would marry Lord Lyonel's heir, Ormund.[N 1] To confirm the promise, Princess Rhaelle was sent to live at Storm's End as Lyonel's cupbearer and companion to his lady wife. In 245 AC, Rhaelle and Ormund were married, and their son, and only known child, Steffon_Baratheon, was born a year later. Ormund had inherited Storm's End already prior to his marriage. He became Hand of the King to his brother-in-law, King Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen, following the tragedy at Summerhall. When Jaehaerys decided to intervene militarily in the Stepstones to defeat the Band of Nine, Ormund was made commander of the expedition. He was one of the first to be killed, struck down by Maelys I Blackfyre, and died in the arms of his son and heir, Steffon. Command of the expedition passed to Ser Gerold_Hightower, the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, and Ormund was succeeded as Hand by Edgar_Sloane.

name: Ormund III Durrandon
bio: Ormund III Durrandon was a Storm King of House Durrandon during the coming of the Andals in the stormlands. Ormund and his queen abandoned the old gods and converted to the Faith of the Seven. The stormlanders followed their example.

name: Ormund Hightower
bio: Ormund Hightower was the Lord of the Hightower and the head of House Hightower during the Dance of the Dragons. He wielded the Valyrian steel blade Vigilance. Ormund was the nephew of Ser Otto_Hightower, Hand of the King to Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Ormund is portrayed by James Norton. Lord Ormund had three sons, Lyonel_Hightower, Martyn_Hightower_(son_of_Ormund), and Garmund_Hightower, and at least one daughter, Bethany_Hightower with his first wife. After she died in childbed, he married again to Samantha_Tarly, who was only two years older than his eldest son. Ormund led the Reach forces for the greens during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons, supporting his kinsman, King Aegon_II_Targaryen. His host included a thousand knights, a thousand archers, three thousand men-at-arms, and thousands more of less discipline. Not all of the Reach's lords supported him, however. Ser Alan_Beesbury and Lord Alan_Tarly raided Ormund's camps and killed his scouts, while Lord Owen_Costayne attacked his baggage train. Ormund wanted to wait for reinforcements from King's Landing. During the Battle of the Honeywine, Ormund and his forces were trapped on the banks of the river between the forces of Lord Thaddeus_Rowan and Tom_Flowers on one side and Owen and the two Alans on the other. Lord Hightower's lines were starting to crumble and defeat seemed all but a foregone conclusion. Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I), Ormund's squire and Aegon's brother, turned up on his dragon, Tessarion, however, and turned the battle for the greens. After the battle Ormund knighted Daeron with Vigilance, naming the prince "Ser Daeron the Daring". After the fall of King's Landing, Ser Criston_Cole marched south from Harrenhal with the intention of joining Ormund's host, but Criston was slain en route at the Butcher's Ball. After the siege of Longtable, Ormund spared Lady Merryweather. Ormund and Daeron led the sack of Bitterbridge, however, as Daeron's nephew, Prince Maelor_Targaryen, had been killed in that town. During the First Battle of Tumbleton, Lord Ormund's archers and heavy horse defeated the six thousand blacks of Ser Garibald_Grey. After the surviving rivermen retreated into Tumbleton, Roderick_Dustin, Lord of Barrowton, led his Winter Wolves rode out to meet the greens. Screaming war cries, the northmen fought through ten times their own numbers till Roderick came face to face with Lord Hightower. Ormund's cousin, Ser Bryndon_Hightower, put himself between the northman and his liege, taking off his arm with his longaxe, yet Roddy the Ruin slew both before succumbing to his wounds. After losing their general at First Tumbleton, Lord Ormund's host was divided and without direction, with the Caltrops and the Two Betrayers competing for leadership. Ser Hobert_Hightower, Ormund's cousin, ineffectually claimed command. The army finally dissolved following the Second Battle of Tumbleton. The fifteen-year-old Lyonel succeeded his father as Lord of the Hightower and took as his paramour Lady Sam, Ormund's second wife.

name: Ormund Wylde
bio: Ormund Wylde is a knight from House Wylde. He is the uncle of Casper_Wylde, Lord of the Rain House. The cautious Ormund is a cadaverous old man.

name: Oro Tendyris
bio: Oro Tendyris is a merchant from Braavos.

name: Orryn Baratheon
bio: Orryn Baratheon was a knight from House Baratheon and a younger brother of Lord Rogar_Baratheon of Storm's End. When Orryn's older brother, Lord Rogar_Baratheon, was Hand of the King, he wanted to betroth Orryn to Princess Alysanne_Targaryen. When Rogar was dismissed as Hand, Orryn returned to Storm's End along with his brothers, Ser Borys_Baratheon, Ser Garon_Baratheon, and Ser Ronnal_Baratheon. In 50 AC Orryn arrived at the motherhouse attached to the Starry Sept in Oldtown demanding Princess Rhaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon), a novice of the Faith, be given into his custody. However, the Faith refused, and Orryn and his men-at-arms were instead captured by thirty armed septons led by the High_Lickspittle steward, Casper_Straw. Orryn was questioned and confessed to a plot: he was to bring Rhaella to Storm's End, where Rogar planned to have her confess that she was really her older twin sister, Princess Aerea_Targaryen, and then he meant to name her queen. For this, Lord Donnel_Hightower imprisoned Orryn. When he came of age, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen decided to be merciful and pardoned Lord Rogar for his plots. Orryn was also pardoned by the king, but was exiled to Essos for ten years. Orryn and the dozen men who had accompanied him to Oldtown traveled to the Free City of Tyrosh and went into the service of its Archon. Within a year Orryn married the Archon's buxom blue-green haired maiden Archon's_daughter_(Jaehaerys_I), who his brother Rogar had once hoped to wed to King Jaehaerys. Orryn had a daughter by her, but there were doubts if he was her true father, as his wife was known to be free with her favor. When the Archon's term ended Orryn left Tyrosh for Myr and joined the Maiden's Men. He was slain soon after in the Disputed Lands in a battle against the Men of Valor. Nothing is known of the fate of his wife and daughter.

name: Orson Stone
bio: Orson Stone is an exiled knight from Westeros, a sellsword and member of the Windblown. At some time the Tattered_Prince dispatched Orson's brother to the Sorrows.

name: Orton Merryweather
bio: Orton Merryweather is the Lord of Longtable and the head of House Merryweather. Orton is the grandson of Lord Owen_Merryweather, a Hand of the King to Aerys_II_Targaryen who was exiled from Westeros for failing to contain Robert's Rebellion. Orton went into exile with his grandfather where he found a Myrish bride, Taena. During his reign,[N 1] King Robert_I_Baratheon was persuaded by Orton to restore the lands, castle and titles stripped by Aerys to the Merryweathers. Robert did not grant them all of their lands back, however, and also emptied the family treasury, leaving Orton a less wealthy and powerful lord than his grandfather had been.

name: Orwyle
bio: Orwyle was an archmaester of the Citadel who became Grand Maester. He served during the reigns of Kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Orwyle is portrayed by Kurt Egyiawan.

name: Orys Baratheon
bio: Orys Baratheon, also known as Orys One-Hand, was the founder of House Baratheon and was the first Lord of Storm's End after House Durrandon. During the Wars of Conquest, he slew the last Storm King, Argilac_Durrandon, married his daughter Argella_Durrandon, and became the first Lord Paramount of the Stormlands. Orys is also considered the first Hand of the King, serving Aegon_I_Targaryen. Famed for his prowess at arms, he was believed to be the bastard brother of Aegon I. Although these rumors were never proven, maesters and members of House Targaryen insist on their authenticity. He distinguished himself fighting for the Targaryens during Aegon's Conquest, the First Dornish War, and the Vulture Hunt.

name: Osbert Serry
bio: Osbert Serry is the Lord of Southshield and the head of House Serry, from the Shield Islands.

name: Oscar Tully
bio: Oscar Tully was a knight from House Tully during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen and Aegon_III_Targaryen. He was the younger brother of Lord Kermit_Tully of Riverrun. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Oscar (merged with Elmo_Tully and Kermit_Tully) is portrayed by Archie Barnes. Oscar was knighted on the battlefield after slaying three men in the Battle of the Kingsroad. He was one of the Lads who marched to King's Landing after the battle, but they later deferred to the formidable Lord Cregan_Stark. After the Dance of the Dragons ended, Oscar created the Stormbreakers, a free company which left Westeros for the Free Cities.

name: Osfryd Kettleblack
bio: Osfryd Kettleblack is the second oldest of the Kettleblack brothers. He is the son of Oswell_Kettleblack, and he has two brothers, Ser Osmund_Kettleblack and Osney_Kettleblack. Osfryd's father, Oswell_Kettleblack, has been in the service of Lord Petyr_Baelish for a long time. Osfryd and his brothers have known Petyr since they were children.

name: Osgood Arryn
bio: Osgood Arryn, known as the Old Falcon, was a King of Mountain and Vale from House Arryn. Osgood warred with House Stark of Winterfell and besieged the Wolf's Den. His son, Oswin_Arryn, burned the Wolf's Den down.

name: Osgood III Shett
bio: Osgood III Shett was a First Man king of Gulltown and the head of House Shett during the early stages of the Andal Invasion. He was styled Osgood Shett, Third of His Name, King of the True Men. King Osgood and his forebears had been waging an intermittent war against the Bronze Kings of House Royce of Runestone. Under Osgood's rule, the Shetts were pushed back inside their town walls. Osgood turned to Andalos for help in recovering the lands lost to King Yorwyck_VI_Royce. Seeking to avoid the mistakes made by Jon_Brightstone and Dywen_Shell, who had hired Andal warlords with gold, he sought to bind them with blood. He gave his daughter in marriage to the Andal knight Ser Gerold Grafton, took Grafton's eldest daughter as his own bride, and married a younger daughter of Grafton to his own son and heir. All these marriages were performed by septons, according to the rites of the "Seven From Across the Sea". Shett even converted to the Faith and swore to build a great sept in Gulltown should the Seven grant him victory. And so he sallied forth with his allies to face the Bronze King. King Osgood won his victory, but did not survive the battle. It was rumored that Ser Gerold himself struck him down, for upon his return, the Andal knight claimed his good-father's crown for his own, dispossessing Osgood's lawful heir and confining him to his bedchamber until he had gotten his wife (Ser Gerold's daughter) with child. After the daughter was impregnated, Osgood's heir dissappeared from the pages of history.

name: Osha
bio: Osha is a spearwife of the free folk from north of the Wall.  In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, she is portrayed by Natalia Tena. Osha was born beyond the Wall and has lived her life there. Osha has fought men of the Night's Watch, and has killed one of them. Her brother killed a ten-foot-tall female giant.

name: Osmund Frey
bio: Osmund Frey is a member of House Frey. He is the second child of Ser Benfrey_Frey and his wife and cousin, Jyanna_Frey.

name: Osmund Kettleblack
bio: Osmund Kettleblack is a member of House Kettleblack who claims to be a hedge knight. He is the son of Oswell_Kettleblack, and he has two brothers, Osfryd_Kettleblack and Osney_Kettleblack. Osmund's father, Oswell_Kettleblack, has been in the service of Lord Petyr_Baelish for a long time. Osmund and his brothers have known Petyr since they were children. Ser Osmund served in the Stepstones and the Disputed Lands with the Gallant Men fighting for Lys and then Tyrosh. He claims to have been knighted on a battlefield by Ser Robert_Stone, who is now dead.

name: Osmund Strong
bio: Osmund Strong was a knight from House Strong and the fourth Hand of the King to Aegon_I_Targaryen. Ser Osmund was named Hand of the King by Aegon_I_Targaryen in 17 AC, following the death of Lord Alton_Celtigar in office. After Aegon heard of an attack on Tall Trees Town in the Summer Isles, Ser Osmund and Grand Maester Gawen were charged with the overseeing and raising of walls around King's Landing. Work on the walls commenced in 20 AC and continued until 26 AC. In 29 AC and again 30 AC, Ser Osmund and Lord Aethan_Velaryon, lord admiral and master of ships, led campaigns in the Stepstones to root out the Lysene pirate king Sargoso_Saan. They were accompanied by the young Prince Maegor_I_Targaryen. In 34 AC, Ser Osmund died in office and was replaced as Hand by Lord Alyn_Stokeworth.

name: Osmund Tyrell
bio: Osmund Tyrell was a knight of House Tyrell and the High Steward of the Reach during the reigns of Garth_X_Gardener and Mern_VI_Gardener, Kings of the Reach. Lords Peake and Manderly instigated a civil war to see their respective wives, both daughters to the senile old King Garth_X_Gardener, succeed as Queen of the Reach. Ser Osmund led an alliance of twoscore houses to put down the rebels and reclaim the ruins of Highgarden, as a Dornish king and his raiders had sacked the seat of House Gardener and killed Garth X. Ser Osmund placed a second cousin of the late Garth X upon the throne as King Mern_VI_Gardener. With the counsel of Osmund, Mern VI began the reconstruction of the Reach and Highgarden and the power of House Gardener. Osmund was succeeded by his son, Ser Robert_Tyrell.

name: Osmynd
bio: Osmynd was the septon at Riverrun when Catelyn Tully was a child. Septon Osmynd instructed Catelyn Tully in the Faith of the Seven when she was a girl. He taught her that the seven-faced-god was a single deity with seven aspects, just as a sept was a single building with seven walls. He told Catelyn that, though seven different aspects of the Seven existed, each embodied all of the Seven. By 299 AC, Septon Osmynd was long-dead.

name: Osney Kettleblack
bio: Osney Kettleblack is the youngest of the Kettleblack brothers. He is the son of Oswell_Kettleblack, and he has two older brothers, Ser Osmund_Kettleblack and Osfryd_Kettleblack. Osney's father, Oswell_Kettleblack, has been in the service of Lord Petyr_Baelish for a long time. Osney and his brothers have known Petyr since they were children.

name: Osric V Arryn
bio: Osric V Arryn was a King of Mountain and Vale of House Arryn. Osric ordered the reconstruction of the Bloody Gate from the rough-hewn, unmortarted wall that it was, to the vaunted gate on which many a army smashed themselves to pieces.

name: Osric Stark
bio: Osric Stark was a member of House Stark who became Lord Commander of the Night's Watch around 400 BC. Osric was only ten years old when he was elected Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, making him the youngest chosen for that position. He was a son or brother of a Stark King in the North. Maester Balder wrote The Edge of the World during Osric's rule of sixty years.

name: Osric Umber
bio: Osric Umber was the Lord of the Last Hearth and the head of House Umber during the latter half of the second century after Aegon's Conquest. Osric was married to Arrana_Stark, the older daughter of Edric_Stark and Serena_Stark. Together, the couple had issue.

name: Orphan Oss
bio: Orphan Oss is a steward of the Night's Watch.

name: Ossifer Lipps
bio: Ossifer Lipps is a knight from House Lipps.

name: Ossifer Plumm
bio: Ossifer Plumm was the Lord and head of House Plumm during the reign of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen. He married the king's cousin, Princess Elaena_Targaryen. Ossifer was a wealthy but aged man when he wed Princess Elaena_Targaryen in 176 AC. He is said to have died during the bedding, after seeing his wife naked for the first time. Elaena became pregnant, and gave birth to a child, Viserys_Plumm. However, rumors later suggested that Lord Ossifer had died before he was able to consummate the marriage, and that the child instead had been fathered by King Aegon_IV_Targaryen. The claim that Ossifer fathered a child after his death led to the saying that he had had "a cock six feet long". Regardless of his true paternity, Viserys Plumm inherited Ossifer's lands and titles.

name: Ossy
bio: Ossy is one of the mule-handlers in service to House Royce of the Gates of the Moon.

name: Oswald Wode
bio: Oswald Wode was a knight from House Wode and a supporter of the blacks during the Dance of the Dragons. When Ser Criston_Cole marched from King's Landing toward Harrenhal in 130 AC, his vanguard defeated Oswald, Lord_Darry_(Dance), and Lord Roote in a short and sharp battle on the eastern shore of the Gods Eye.

name: Oswell I Arryn
bio: Oswell I Arryn was a King of Mountain and Vale from House Arryn. In the North, they tell the tale of the Rat_Cook, who served an unknowing Andal king the flesh of his son, which had been baked into a pie. Some say this king was Oswell I of House Arryn.

name: Oswell Kettleblack
bio: Oswell Kettleblack, called Oswell and Kettleblack, is a member of House Kettleblack and the father of Ser Osmund_Kettleblack, Ser Osfryd_Kettleblack, and Ser Osney_Kettleblack. He is a man-at-arms in the service of Lord Petyr_Baelish. Oswell has been in the service of Lord Petyr_Baelish for a long time.

name: Oswell Whent
bio: Oswell Whent was a knight from House Whent and a member of King Aerys_II_Targaryen's Kingsguard. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Oswell is merged with Gerold_Hightower, who is portrayed by Eddie Eyre. Lord Walter_Whent of Harrenhal, Oswell's older brother, announced the tourney at Harrenhal shortly after a visit from Oswell. It was proposed by Varys and believed by some that Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen was secretly sponsoring the tourney in order to gather lords together to deal with the matter of his mad father, King Aerys_II_Targaryen, and used Oswell as a go-between to Lord Whent. Oswell was present at the tourney when Ser Jaime_Lannister became a member of the Kingsguard. According to a semi-canon source, Oswell, along with his other sworn brother Ser Arthur_Dayne, aided Rhaegar in the kidnapping of Lyanna_Stark near Harrenhal as the year of the false spring was ending. Robert's Rebellion began shortly after. At the end of the rebellion, Lord Eddard_Stark encountered Ser Oswell and two other knights of the Kingsguard, Arthur and Ser Gerold_Hightower, at the tower of joy in Dorne. Eddard told them that King's Landing had already fallen and that King Aerys II Targaryen was dead. In the ensuing conflict, all three Kingsguard were killed. Eddard had been with six northern companions, but he and Howland_Reed were the only survivors. Stones from the tower were used to build Oswell's cairn upon a nearby ridge.

name: Oswin Arryn
bio: Oswin Arryn, nicknamed the Talon, was a King of Mountain and Vale from House Arryn. During the wars between House Stark of the North and House Arryn of the Vale of Arryn, King Osgood_Arryn laid siege to the Wolf's Den. His son, King Oswin the Talon, later captured and burned the castle.

name: Oswyck
bio: Oswyck was a septon and a member of the Faith of the Seven during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Oswyck had been the septon at Dragonstone for some time. He was the tutor of the royal children in the Faith of the Seven during the reign of Aenys_I_Targaryen and was a novice in the court of Queen Rhaenys_Targaryen during the rule of Aegon_I_Targaryen. When Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Alysanne_Targaryen fled to Dragonstone in 49 AC and asked to be wed, he personally performed the ceremony. Later in King Jaehaerys's reign, Oswyck, along with Septon Barth, assisted the king in the development of the Doctrine of Exceptionalism, which would later be added to the teachings of the Faith of the Seven. Septon Oswyck passed away in 54 AC. His was the first death that year which would become known as the Year of the Stranger, though his death was due to natural causes. At the king's request, his remains were interred on Dragonstone.

name: Oswyn
bio: Oswyn Longneck the Thrice-Hanged was a famous member of the Kingswood Brotherhood.

name: Othell Yarwyck
bio: Othell Yarwyck is a member of House Yarwyck who serves as the First Builder of the Night's Watch. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Othell is portrayed by Brian Fortune.

name: Othgar Hoare (Demonlover)
bio: Othgar Hoare, known as Othgar Demonlover, was a King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. The Hoare kings of his era were opposed by the priests of the Drowned God because of their support for the Faith of the Seven, discouraging of reaving, and promotion of trade.

name: Othgar Hoare (the Soulless)
bio: Othgar Hoare, known as Othgar the Soulless, was a King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. The Hoare kings of his era were opposed by the priests of the Drowned God because of their support for the Faith of the Seven, discouraging of reaving, and promotion of trade.

name: Otho Bracken
bio: Otho Bracken, called the Brute of Bracken, was a knight of House Bracken during the reign of kings Daeron_II_Targaryen and Aerys_I_Targaryen. He was the second son of Lord_Bracken_(father_of_Otho) of Stone Hedge and eventually succeeded him. After slaying Lord Quentyn_Blackwood in 206 AC in a tourney at King's Landing, Otho became known as "the Brute of Bracken". Although he used a blunted longaxe, Otho struck Lord Quentyn so hard that he stove in his helm's visor and the face beneath. Otho participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC, his brown tent beneath a red stallion erected far away from the tents of House Blackwood. Ser Duncan_the_Tall watched Otho and Lord Pearse_Caron tilting against a quintain the day before the tourney began. While searched for knights for his trial of seven, Ser Steffon_Fossoway believed he could bring the Brute of Bracken to Duncan's cause, but ended up betraying Duncan in favor of Prince Aerion_Targaryen. Still in need of a seventh knight, Duncan reined up before Otho and pleaded for his assistance; unlike other knights, Otho had the grace to respond, but rejected him on account of not knowing the hedge knight. Otho's older brother died in the Great Spring Sickness, which made Otho his Lord_Bracken_(father_of_Otho) new heir. In 211 AC, Otho's father was slowly dying, and Septon Sefton_Staunton predicted that "The Blackwoods will never stomach the Brute of Bracken as a neighbor", insisting it would mean war. Early the following year, it was rumored at the wedding tourney at Whitewalls that Otho might come. He did not, however, and it was suggested that the tourney was "hardly worth the bother" for Otho. At the tourney, Ser Duncan the Tall overheard a discussion between Lord Gormon_Peake and Ser Tommard_Heddle, where the belief was expressed that Otho would have to follow the Blackfyre cause, once the Freys and Lothstons had joined it.

name: Otho II Hightower
bio: Otho II Hightower was a King of the High Tower and head of House Hightower. Otho had the wooden palisades and ditch protecting Oldtown replaced with massive stone walls, thicker and higher than any seen in Westeros in that time, as the city had previously been sacked three times in a single century. Otho's efforts beggared the city for three generations, but succeeded in their purpose of turning back would-be invaders and raiders.

name: Othor
bio: Othor is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Otter Gimpknee
bio: Otter Gimpknee is an innkeeper and whoremonger in Lordsport. Otter's inn doubled in size when Lordsport was rebuilt after Greyjoy's Rebellion.

name: Otto Hightower
bio: Otto Hightower was a knight from House Hightower of Oldtown who served as Hand of the King to three kings, although two of the kings removed him from office at various times. He was the younger brother of Lord_Hightower, and the uncle of Lord Ormund_Hightower. His children included Alicent_Hightower, the second queen of King Viserys_I_Targaryen, and Ser Gwayne_Hightower, second in command of the gold cloaks. Otto is remembered as a warning that knowledge is not everything. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Otto is portrayed by Rhys Ifans.

name: Ottomore
bio: Ottomore is a maester of the Citadel.

name: Ottyn Wythers
bio: Ottyn Wythers is a knight from House Wythers and a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Owain Bourney
bio: Owain Bourney was a lord from the Blackwater Rush and the head of House Bourney during the Dance of the Dragons. When the host of blacks led by Ser Garibald_Grey and Lord Roderick_Dustin marched south from the riverlands to Tumbleton, they were joined by men from the Blackwater Rush, including Lord Bourney and Ser Roger_Corne. Owain was secretly a supporter of King Aegon_II_Targaryen, however. When the Two Betrayers began attacking the blacks during the First Battle of Tumbleton, Roger ordered his men to open Tumbleton's gates to the greens, while Owain did the same at the town's castle. Owain killed Ser Merrell_the_Bold with his spear. Lord Bourney later claimed responsibility for the greens' victory. The greens' commanders regrouped in Tumbleton and planned what to do next. Owain desired to take command of the host, but he was opposed by Ser Hobert_Hightower, Lord Unwin_Peake, and Ser Jon_Roxton. Unwin eventually stabbed Owain in the eye during a war council.

name: Owen the Oaf
bio: Owen, called the Oaf and Owen the Oaf, is a steward of the Night's Watch. Owen's mother dropped him on his head as a baby and left him dimwitted.

name: Owen
bio: Owen was the brother of Septon Meribald. He died in the War of the Ninepenny Kings. Owen and his brothers, Meribald, Robin, Willam and Owen's friend Jon_Pox all went off to fight in the War of the Ninepenny Kings. During the War, Willam and Robin died from a fever. Owen died in battle after receiving a blow from a mace and Jon was hanged for rape. Meribald survived to become a septon.

name: Owen
bio: Owen is a knight from the Vale of Arryn.

name: Owen Bush
bio: Owen Bush was a knight of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. In 44 AC, when Queen Alyssa_Velaryon escaped from King Maegor_I_Targaryen's imprisonment, the king ordered his knights to bring her son, Prince Viserys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I), to his torturers to discover where she was hiding. In 45 AC, Queen Ceryse_Hightower died suddenly. A rumor went around the Red Keep that she had said something to offend Maegor. It was widely believed that the king had ordered Ser Owen to remove her tongue, but Ceryse's struggles caused his knife to slip and slash her throat.
