However, most historians believe this was a slander concocted by the king's enemies to damage Maegor's reputation. In 48 AC, Maegor suspected Queen Tyanna_of_the_Tower of betrayal. He had Owen and his sworn brother, Ser Maladon_Moore, bring her to the dungeons, where she confessed. That same year, Maegor the Cruel gradually lost political support, resulting in a rival threat in his nephew, Prince Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Two of his Kingsguard defected to Jaehaerys, and Maegor lost a third guard when Owen was found dead outside a brothel in 48 AC, his member cut off and stuffed in his mouth.

name: Owen Costayne
bio: Owen Costayne was the Lord of the Three Towers and the head of House Costayne during the Dance of the Dragons. Lord Costayne supported Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen and the blacks. Owen, along with Lord Alan_Tarly and Ser Alan_Beesbury, trapped Lord Ormund_Hightower's forces between them and Lord Thaddeus_Rowan and the Tom_Flowers and the banks of the Honeywine. Lord Hightower's lines were starting to crumble and defeat seemed all but a forgone conclusion. Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I) joined the Battle of the Honeywine on his dragon Tessarion, however, turning the battle for the greens. Owen died slowly from a wound given by Jon_Roxton blade, Orphan-Maker.

name: Owen Fossoway
bio: Owen Fossoway was the Lord of Cider Hall and the head of House Fossoway during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. During the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Owen was loyal to King Aegon_II_Targaryen and the greens, marching with Lord Ormund_Hightower's host from the Reach. After the deaths of Lord Ormund and his cousin, Ser Bryndon_Hightower, during the First Battle of Tumbleton, the host needed a new commander to lead them. Old Ser Hobert_Hightower thought that as the most senior Hightower left alive, the command should pass on to him. However, when Hobert took charge of the men, he failed miserably and the Two Betrayers, Ulf_White and Hugh_Hammer, took the command instead. This strongly displeased Ser Hobert as well as other nobles, Lord Unwin_Peake chief among them. One night, Hobert and Unwin organised a secret meeting with other lords and landed knights in the cellar of the Bloody Caltrops, an inn in Tumbleton. The thirteen assembled nobles (later known as "the Caltrops") debated all night on what to do with the Two Betrayers. Lord Owen was dispatched to wake Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I) and bring him back so that the plotters might inform him of their plans. Unfortunately, the Second Battle of Tumbleton occured before the conspirators could execute their plan, and Lord Owen was among the casualties of the battle.

name: Owen Inchfield
bio: Owen Inchfield is a knight from House Inchfield.

name: Owen Merryweather
bio: Owen Merryweather was the Lord of Longtable and the head of House Merryweather during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. He also served as Hand of the King for Aerys. During a feast King Aerys_II_Targaryen held when Cersei_Lannister came to court for the first time, Lord Owen talked about raising taxes on wine, which led to Lord Rykker joking about Lord Tywin_Lannister shitting gold. Ser Bonifer_Hasty once served Lord Merryweather. When Tywin resigned his position as Hand of the King in 281 AC, the aged Owen was named the new Hand by Aerys. With Tywin gone from court, the new focus of Aerys's mistrust and paranoia was his own son and heir, Rhaegar_Targaryen, Prince of Dragonstone. Lord Merryweather and Grand Maester Pycelle attempted to keep peace between the factions loyal to the king and those loyal to Rhaegar. Owen proved ineffectual in preventing the uprising that came to be known as Robert's Rebellion. When Lords Jon_Arryn, Robert_I_Baratheon, and Eddard_Stark began their rebellion, Owen sent missives to the lords of the Seven Kingdoms declaring them outlaws and demanding their heads. Some of the stormlords were encouraged by Owen, but they were defeated by Robert at Summerhall. Owen never stirred from King's Landing, and this coupled with the news of Ned and Robert's successful return to their respective seats of power made the paranoid Aerys believe that Lord Merryweather was conspiring with them. Owen was subsequently stripped of lands and titles and exiled for his failure, and was replaced as Hand by the younger Jon_Connington, Lord of Griffin's Roost. King Robert I Baratheon eventually restored the Merryweather titles and some of their lands to Owen's grandson, Orton_Merryweather.

name: Owen Norrey
bio: Owen Norrey is a member of House Norrey and one of Robb Stark's battle guard. His relation to Lord Brandon_Norrey, the head of the House is unknown.

name: Oznak zo Pahl
bio: Oznak zo Pahl is a noble from Meereen, a scion of the House of Pahl, an ancient slaving family. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Oznak is portrayed by Daniel Naprous. Oznak once cut the liver out of a man, Scarb, who had supposedly looked at a Meereenese lady wrongly. He claimed he was defending her honor. His father commands the city guard of Meereen and his uncle is the richest man in the city.

name: Paezhar zo Myraq
bio: Paezhar zo Myraq is a Yunkish noble known as the Little Pigeon.

name: Palla
bio: Palla is a kennel girl at Winterfell. She is the daughter of Farlen. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Palla is portrayed by Joy Carleton.

name: Parmen Crane
bio: Parmen Crane, also known as Parmen the Purple, is a knight of House Crane. He is named to King Renly_Baratheon's Rainbow Guard.

name: Parquello Vaelaros
bio: Parquello Vaelaros is a candidate to become a triarch of Volantis.

name: Lord Parren
bio: Lord Parren was the head of House Parren during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Lord Parren fought for Prince Aegon the Uncrowned during the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye against King Maegor_I_Targaryen.

name: Pascal Dabell
bio: Ser Pascal Dabell was a knight during the reign of Daeron_II_Targaryen. He had two brothers: Ser Ernest_Dabell and Ser Lester_Dabell. He participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Patchface
bio: Patchface, nicknamed Patches, is Lord Stannis_Baratheon's court fool and jester at Dragonstone. His face is tattooed in motley, by which he received his name. His given name is unknown. Patchface was a jester slave in Volantis. He was a clever and skilled boy, with astonishing wit. He was reportedly "nimble as a monkey", could juggle and tell riddles and do magic tricks, and could sing well in four languages. Patchface's freedom was bought by Steffon_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End, who was impressed with the boy during his trip to the Free Cities. Steffon wrote to his maester, Cressen, saying he believed his son Robert_I_Baratheon would be delighted with Patchface, and that he hoped the fool might be able to make his serious son Stannis_Baratheon laugh. However, while Lord Steffon was returning to Westeros, his ship was struck by a sudden storm in Shipbreaker Bay. Steffon's eldest sons, Robert and Stannis, watched from the parapets of Storm's End while the Windproud was smashed against the rocks of the bay. All aboard the ship were believed killed, including Lord Steffon, his wife Cassana_Estermont, and over a hundred soldiers and sailors. Patchface washed up among the dead three days later, naked, his skin white and wrinkled. Maester Cressen believed him to be dead as well, but when the Baratheon's servant Jommy grabbed him to put him on the burial wagon, Patchface coughed up water and sat up. Jommy would swear for the rest of his life that the boy's flesh was clammy cold. What happened to Patchface during the two days he was lost in the sea is unknown, but the fisherfolk around Storm's End often tell a story that a mermaid had taught him to breathe water in return for his seed. Though Patchface had survived the shipwreck, he was broken in mind and body; the ordeal had taken his memories, and half his wit. He matched none of what Lord Steffon had written in his letter, and was only recognized by his tattooed face. It was suggested by Ser Harbert, the castellan of Storm’s End, that it would be kindest for Cressen to euthanize the mad boy with milk of the poppy, but the maester refused to do so. Despite his condition, Patchface is employed as court fool and jester at Dragonstone, the castle of Lord Stannis Baratheon. He spends most of his time with Shireen_Baratheon, Lord Stannis's daughter. He offers strange insights and stranger songs, sometimes touching upon what may have really happened to the boy at the bottom of the bay.

name: Pate (novice)
bio: Pate is a novice of the Citadel. He is the POV Character of the Prologue of A Feast for Crows. Pate was originally from the westerlands. He came to the Citadel at the age of thirteen, but he has not earned any links for his maester's chain in five years. He has twice tried for a link without success. The first time was with Archmaester Vaellyn, to demonstrate his knowledge of astronomy, but fell to "Vinegar" Vaellyn's acid tongue. Two years later, Pate finally regained the courage to try with Archmaester Ebrose, for a silver link for healing, but Ebrose's disappointed sighs were just as painful. Afterwards, Pate felt he was lucky when he was chosen to help old senile Archmaester Walgrave with his ravens, but soon his duties included fetching Walgrave's meals, sweeping out his chambers, and dressing him every morning. When Walgrave broke the lock on his own strongbox, after losing the key that opened it, Maester Gormon_Tyrell suspected Pate of breaking it, and accused him of theft. And though Pate hoped that at least he would be guaranteed a black iron link for ravencraft, Walgrave's subject, he soon learned that the senile Walgrave remained an archmaester only by courtesy, and Maester Gormon was the judge of those who seek such links. In addition to looking after Archmaester Walgrave, who often forgets who Pate is and thinks he is someone called Cressen, Pate's daily duties include tending the ravens in the Ravenry. The white ravens know his name, and often quork "Pate, Pate, Pate" whenever they see him. Pate's sleeping cell is below Walgrave's chambers at the top of the west rower of the Ravenry.

name: Pate (orphan)
bio: Pate is a boy orphaned by the War of the Five Kings who is currently living at the crossroads inn.

name: Pate (ranger)
bio: Pate is a ranger in the Night's Watch.

name: Pate (whipping boy)
bio: Pate is a whipping boy in service to House Baratheon of King's Landing. He is the whipping boy for King Tommen_Baratheon and the former whipping boy for King Joffrey_Baratheon.

name: Pate the Plowman
bio: Pate the Plowman is a legendary figure of the Age of Heroes who founded House Moreland.

name: Pate the Woodcock
bio: Pate the Woodcock was a knight of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. By 49 AC, the young king Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen still had five vacant spots in the Kingsguard to fill. At the wedding of his mother Alyssa_Velaryon and Lord Rogar_Baratheon, it was decided to fill the remaining five slots by a test of arms since knights from all over the realm had come to attend the wedding. Jaehaerys agreed, but with a twist - the knights must fight on foot, not in the joust, for foes seldom strike a king from horseback. The wild melees and bloody duels that followed were dubbed by maesters as the War for the White Cloaks, with hundreds of knights competing for the honor of serving in the Kingsguard. Ser Pate the Woodcock was one of the five champions. He was the youngest of all the champions, and his weapon of choice was a spear instead of a sword. Some questioned whether he truly was a knight, but such was his skill that Ser Joffrey_Doggett personally knighted the lad himself to end the accusation while hundreds of onlookers cheered, after which Pate was given the white cloak of the Kingsguard. During King Jaehaerys' training at Dragonstone, Ser Pate was the one who won the castle garrison's wagers on who would "kill" (defeat) the young king the most. In the book allegedly written by Lady Coryanne_Wylde, A Caution for Young Girls, the author claimed to have bedded both Ser Pate and his sworn brother Ser Joffrey_Doggett, although most maesters dismiss these claims. After Jaehaerys reached maturity and sat on the Iron Throne in King's Landing, the king summoned Lord Rogar_Baratheon and granted him a full pardon, on the condition that he be a faithful husband to Jaehaerys's mother, Alyssa_Velaryon, to which Lord Rogar agreed. Ser Pate led the escort of a hundred men-at-arms to take Lord Rogar and Queen Alyssa safely to Storm's End.

name: Pate (Standfast)
bio: Pate was a peasant sworn to House Osgrey. After being trained by Ser Eustace_Osgrey's sons with other peasants from his lands, Pate went off to fight for House Osgrey during the Blackfyre Rebellion. He was one of the only three surviving men that returned with Ser Eustace, along with Dake and Lem_(Standfast).

name: Pate of the Blue Fork
bio: Pate of the Blue Fork, also known as Ser Pate of Sevenstreams, is a hedge knight from Sevenstreams in the northern riverlands. Pate the hedge knight married Amerei_Frey, the eldest daughter of Merrett_Frey, after she was caught in the stables of the Twins having sex with three grooms at the same time. The bard Tom_of_Sevenstreams, Pate's cousin, sang at his wedding to Amerei, but afterwards Pate did not want to pay for his singing.

name: Pate of Fairmarket
bio: Pate of Fairmarket was a lowborn king from the the town of Fairmarket in the riverlands. Pate claimed the title of river king or King of the Trident in the generations after the riverlands were conquered by the Storm King Arlan_III_Durrandon. Pate even had a brief, inglorious reign, presumably ruling from Fairmarket, before being put down by the might of Storm's End.

name: Pate of Lancewood
bio: Pate of Lancewood is an inhabitant of Lancewood in the riverlands.

name: Pate of Longleaf
bio: Pate of Longleaf, known as Longleaf the Lionslayer, was a knight from Longleaf during the Targaryen civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. Pate served as a squire in the Battle at the Red Fork, during which he mortally wounded Lord Jason_Lannister in the third attack by the greens. Lord Petyr_Piper granted knighthood to Pate afterward. Longleaf the Lionslayer led a ragged band of survivors from earlier battles to join Lords Lord_Bigglestone, Lord_Chambers, and Lord_Perryn at the Battle by the Lakeshore. Ser Criston_Cole offered single combat to Pate, Lord Roderick_Dustin, and Ser Garibald_Grey at the Butcher's Ball, but all refused him. Criston was instead slain by Robb_Rivers' arrows and his host was destroyed. When Tessarion began attacking during the First Battle of Tumbleton, Pate was unhorsed and trampled.

name: Pate of Mory
bio: Pate of Mory is an inhabitant of Mory in the riverlands.

name: Pate of Shermer's Grove
bio: Pate of Shermer's Grove is an inhabitant of Shermer's Grove in the riverlands.

name: Pater
bio: The High Septon, formerly known as Septon Pater, was the second High Septon of the Faith of the Seven during the reign of Maegor_I_Targaryen. In 43 AC,[N 1] during the Faith Militant uprising, King Maegor_I_Targaryen and his mother, Dowager Queen Visenya_Targaryen, after several victorious battles against the Faith Militant, turned towards Oldtown, threatening to incinerate the Starry Sept in response to the High_Septon_(Aenys_I)'s condemnation of Maegor's polygamous marriages. The High Septon died suddenly before the Targaryens and their dragons arrived, allowing Lord Martyn_Hightower to open his gates before Balerion and Vhagar could burn the city. Rumors about the nature of the High Septon's death still persist today. As he had been in good health, some have suggested that he had committed suicide, either to save himself from Maegor's wroth or to spare Oldtown from  dragonflame. Others believe that the gods themselves had struck him down. Most believe he had been murdered, although opinions differ about the murderer's identity. In any case, the Most Devout were roused from their beds and marched at spearpoint by House Hightower men-at-arms to the Starry Sept to elect a new High Septon. The frail and amiable Septon Pater was named the new High Septon by a single ballot, though he nearly collapsed under the weight of the crystal crown when it was placed on his head. The new High Septon was happy to welcome Maegor when he arrived at Oldtown, and blessed and anointed his head with holy oils, although he forgot the words of the blessing. He formally dissolved the Warrior's Sons and Poor Fellows, commanding the surviving members of the Faith Militant to lay down their arms in the name of the gods. He proclaimed that the Seven had no more need of warriors, and that henceforth the Iron Throne would protect and defend the Faith. King Maegor allowed the surviving members of the Faith Militant until year's end to surrender their weapons, but afterwards a bounty would be placed on those remaining defiant. Neither the new High Septon nor the Most Devout disagreed with the king. The High Septon died in 44 AC, most likely due to old age, and was replaced by High_Lickspittle who spoke no word against King Maegor or his queens.

name: Patrek Mallister
bio: Patrek Mallister is a member of House Mallister. He is the son and heir of Jason_Mallister, Lord of Seagard.

name: Patrek of King's Mountain
bio: Ser Patrek of King's Mountain is a knight from King's Mountain, in the service of King Stannis_Baratheon. He is one of the queen's men.

name: Patrek Vance
bio: Patrek Vance is the second son of Ser Dafyn_Vance and Maegelle_Frey.

name: Paxter Redwyne
bio: Paxter Redwyne is the Lord of the Arbor and head of House Redwyne. He is married to his cousin, Mina_Tyrell, the sister of his liege lord, Mace_Tyrell; he is thus twice a nephew of the Queen of Thorns, Olenna_Redwyne. Paxter and Mina have two sons, Ser Horas_Redwyne and Ser Hobber_Redwyne, and a daughter, Desmera_Redwyne. Paxter commands the Redwyne fleet from his galleas, the Arbor Queen. His healer is Maester Ballabar. Following the lead of his liege lord, Mace_Tyrell, Paxter remained loyal to the Targaryens during Robert's Rebellion. During the year-long siege of Storm's End, the Redwyne fleet blockaded Shipbreaker Bay to prevent Stannis_Baratheon from being resupplied by sea. Paxter and Mace feasted outside Storm's End while the Baratheon garrison starved. The siege was nearly successful, but Storm's End was resupplied when the smuggler Davos_Seaworth slipped through the Redwyne blockade. Following the defeat of the Targaryens through the Battle of the Trident and the Sack of King's Landing, Paxter swore fealty to the new king, Robert_I_Baratheon, when Lord Eddard_Stark arrived to break the siege. During Greyjoy's Rebellion, warships from the Arbor supported Stannis, Robert's master of ships, in the sea battle off Fair Isle. When Samwell_Tarly was ten, Lord Randyll_Tarly brought his son to the Arbor on Paxter's ship, Arbor Queen. Paxter's twins Horas_Redwyne and Hobber_Redwyne bullied Sam, howevever, and Randyll brought him home to Horn Hill. Sam's mother Melessa_Florent revealed that Randyll had meant for him to remain at the Arbor as Lord Redwyne's page and squire. Had Sam pleased Paxter, he would have been betrothed to his daughter, Desmera_Redwyne. Ser Stafford_Lannister has been negotiating to betroth his son, Ser Daven_Lannister, to Desmera, although an agreement has not been reached with her father.

name: Patrice Hightower
bio: Patrice Hightower was a noblewoman from House Hightower during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. She was the sister of Lord Manfred_Hightower_(son_of_Addam) of Oldtown and the aunt of Lord Martyn_Hightower, Queen Ceryse_Hightower, and Ser Morgan_Hightower. Patrice is considered one of the suspects in the death of the High_Septon_(Aenys_I) in 43 AC,[N 1] as she had sought an audience with His High Holiness at the Starry Sept on the day of his death. However, he was known to be alive when she departed. According to Maester Yandel, the suspicion only rests in the belief that poison is a woman's weapon.

name: Patricia Redwyne
bio: Patricia Redwyne was a noblewoman of House Redwyne during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. When Lord Unwin_Peake, the Hand of the King, sought to make his daughter Myrielle_Peake the queen to King Aegon_III_Targaryen, Lady Samantha Hightower nominated nubile maidens from House Redwyne as potential queens for Aegon. Patricia was later presented to the king at the Maiden's Day Ball. When Alyssa_Royce told the king she had come all the way from Runestone, Lady Patricia went her one better by declaring that her party had traveled from the Arbor, had thrice been forced to beat back attacks by outlaws, and that she shot one in the rear.

name: Lord Peake (father of Unwin)
bio: Lord Peake was the Lord of Starpike, Dunstonbury, Whitegrove, and the head of House Peake during the late reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. After the death of Prince Baelon Targaryen, Prince of Dragonstone and Hand of the King, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen summoned the nobility of Westeros at Harrenhal to form a Great Council and choose the new heir to the Iron Throne. Lord Peake came and voted for Prince Viserys_I_Targaryen instead of Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon) or her son Laenor_Velaryon, as he did not believe woman should rule over men. He was eventually succeeded by his eldest son, Unwin_Peake.

name: Pearl Emperor
bio: The Pearl Emperor was the legendary second ruler of the mythic Great Empire of the Dawn. According to the tales told by the priestly scribes of Yin, he was the eldest son and the successor of the God-on-Earth, the son of the Maiden-Made-of-Light and the Lion of Night, who ruled for ten thousand years before ascending to the heavens. The Pearl Emperor then ruled for a thousand years and was followed by the Jade_Emperor. He is credited by some for the construction of the Five Forts, to keep the Lion of Night and his demons away from the realms of men.

name: Pearse Caron
bio: Pearse Caron was the Lord of Nightsong, Lord of the Marches, and the head of House Caron during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In 206 AC, Lord Pearse and Lord Lord_Dondarrion_(father_of_Manfred) raised a host and defeated the Vulture_King_(Daeron_II), burning him out of the Red Mountains. Lord Pearse participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC, challenging Lord Leo_Tyrell_(Longthorn) in the second tilt. They fought afoot with blunted longaxes, but Pearse was defeated after his axehead caught in Leo's shield. After Ser Humfrey_Hardyng's injury, Lord_Ashford considered naming Pearse as a replacement champion, though Prince Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II) did not find it seemly to remove Hardyng as a champion. When Ser Duncan_the_Tall searched for a seventh knight for his trial of seven, Pearse ignored his call for help, whispering a comment to Lord Gawen_Swann.

name: Penny
bio: Penny is a dwarf woman from across the narrow sea. Penny and her brother Oppo are trained by their father, Hop-Bean, to mock-joust on a pig and dog to make their living.

name: Perceon III Gardener
bio: Perceon III Gardener was a King of the Reach and a head of House Gardener. Fearing House Manderly's swelling power in the Reach, Perceon had Lord Lorimar_Peake drive the Manderlys into exile. Perceon III's son Gwayne_Gardener_(son_of_Perceon_III) married Lord Lorimar's daughter and in time succeeded Perceon.

name: Peremore Hightower
bio: Peremore Hightower, known as Peremore the Twisted, was the second son of King Uthor_of_the_High_Tower. When Peremore died, his brother, King Urrigon_Hightower, bequeathed a large tract of land beside the Honeywine to "Peremore's pets", that they might establish and continue in their quest for knowledge. Thus the Citadel and the order of maesters was established.

name: Pereno Pendaerys
bio: Pereno Pendaerys was a Lyseni during the Lysene Spring. Pereno was garroted in a pillow house in 135 AC during the bloody power struggle in the city after the downfall of the Rogare family. His brother Silvario_Pendaerys was also murdered.

name: Perestan
bio: Perestan is one of the archmaesters at the Citadel. He wrote A Consideration of History. Archmaester Perestan claims the world is forty thousand years old, though his colleague Mollos believes it is five hundred thousand years old. Because the double-bladed axe was a symbol used by the Andals of old and the modern Norvoshi see the axe as a symbol of power, Perestan proposed that Andals were the first to settle Norvos. The archmaester suggested the bearded priests of Norvos took the emblem from ruins they found as they established the city. He also suggested that the name "Erreg" was a corruption of an Andal title. Perestan suggested that the ancient Valyrians once reached Oldtown but turned away after an unknown calamity. The archmaester theorized that Lann_the_Clever inherited Casterly Rock after marrying the daughter of the last Casterly. Regarding the legend of Ser Artys Arryn riding a huge falcon, Perestan suggests the storytellers were inspired by dragonriders.

name: Perfumed Hero
bio: The Perfumed Hero is a name the Windblown, a sellsword company hired by Yunkai, mockingly gave to a Yunkish nobleman commanding some of the Yunkish slave solders.

name: Perianne Moore
bio: Perianne Moore was a member of House Moore during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and a lady companion to Jaehaerys's daughter, Princess Saera_Targaryen. Perianne was a maiden of an age with Princess Saera_Targaryen, attending the royal court at the Red Keep in King's Landing. In 82 AC, Perianne became a favorite and dearest friend of the princess, along with Alys_Turnberry.  Saera nicknamed Perianne "Pretty Peri". Saera and her five favorites — Perianne, Alys, Jonah_Mooton, Lord Roy_Connington, and Ser Braxton_Beesbury, called Stinger — became inseparable at every feast and ball. They hunted and hawked together, and once sailed across Blackwater Bay to Dragonstone together. When the young lords rode at rings or crossed swords in the yards, the three girls cheered them on. King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen was pleased at his daughter's popularity and friendship, but Queen Alysanne_Targaryen was concerned about the character of Perianne, Alys, and the three boys. Nevertheless, King Jaehaerys felt they could not get up to trouble, with all the servants attending them watching. In 84 AC, an incident occurred between Saera's male favorites and Tom_Turnip, King Jaehaerys's fool, at the Blue Pearl, a brothel in King's Landing, something Jonah claimed had been the princess's idea. Perianne and Alys were woken in the middle of the night and questioned by Queen Alysanne, telling them to tell the truth or they would join their male friends in the dungeons. Before long, the girls were weeping and pleading for forgiveness, although their confessions said almost nothing about the incident they were being questioned about. Perianne explained that Saera had been teaching Alys how to kiss, and she asked to be taught to kiss too, to train for their future marriages. Alys revealed that one night the girls had started kissing each other with their clothes off, taking turns pretending they were boys. Then, Saera dared Alys to kiss a real boy, and she dared Perianne, and both dared Saera, who said she would kiss a man grown, a knight. This began the ladies' affairs with Red Roy, Jonah, and Stinger. Perianne explained that Stinger did the "training" for all of them. Queen Alysanne then demanded to know where their septas and servants had been during all of this. Perianne, confused, explained that servants do what you tell them, and they had sent them out of the room. As for the ones who knew, Stinger had threatened to have their tongues out if they talked. Alys then confessed to being with child, and that she did not know who the father was. Alysanne sent both girls to bed, and told them that the next morning they would make a confession to a septa and be forgiven by the Mother. Hasty marriages were arranged for the two girls. Perianne, who was not pregnant, was wed to another of Saera's favorites, Jonah Mooton, the heir to Maidenpool. The king told Jonah that since he had played a part in Perianne's ruin, he should play a part in her redemption. The marriage was successful, and eventually Jonah and Perianne ruled together as Lord and Lady of Maidenpool. A year and a half after the scandal, Saera fled from her punishment in Oldtown. King Jaehaerys thought she might seek out her friends, and told Jonah and Perianne to keep watch for her in Maidenpool, but Saera did not go there.

name: Perkin Follard
bio: Perkin Follard is a knight from House Follard, in the service of King Stannis_Baratheon. He is one of the queen's men.

name: Perkin the Flea
bio: Perkin the Flea was a hedge knight who lived during the Dance of the Dragons.

name: Perra Frey
bio: Perra Frey is the only child of Petyr_Frey and Mylenda_Caron.

name: Perra Royce
bio: Perra Royce was a noblewoman from House Royce. She married Lord Walder_Frey of the Twins, and was the first of his eight wives.

name: Perriane Frey
bio: Perriane Frey is the eldest daughter of Lord Walder_Frey and Perra_Royce. She is married to Ser Leslyn_Haigh and they have three sons Harys_Haigh, Donnel_Haigh and Alyn_Haigh.

name: Perros Blackmont
bio: Perros Blackmont is the son of the Lady of Blackmont and a member of House Blackmont. He is a squire.

name: Lord Perryn
bio: Lord Perryn was the head of House Perryn during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. During the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Perryn sided with the blacks and Rhaenyra_Targaryen. Lord Perryn, alongside with Lord_Bigglestone and Lord_Chambers, joined Ser Pate_of_Longleaf and his ragged band of survivors from earlier battles and fought together in the Battle by the Lakeshore. It is unknown if he survived the war.

name: Perwyn Frey
bio: Perwyn Frey is a knight from House Frey. He is the fifteenth son of Lord Walder_Frey and the first son born in the marriage between Walder and Bethany_Rosby. Ser Perwyn rode in the tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day in 297 AC.

name: Perwyn Osgrey
bio: Perwyn Osgrey, also known as Perwyn the Proud, was a head of House Osgrey prior to Aegon's Conquest and the first Lord of Coldmoat. Lord Perwyn ruled House Osgrey when the Gardener kings still ruled from Highgarden. He built the greatest of the Osgrey holds, Coldmoat.

name: Peter Plumm
bio: Peter Plumm is a knight from House Plumm and is the second son of Lord Philip_Plumm.

name: Petyr Baelish
bio: Petyr Baelish is the Lord and head of House Baelish. He serves as master of coin on the small council of King Robert_I_Baratheon. Petyr wears a mockingbird as his personal sigil; he has also replaced the traditional arms of House Baelish, depicting the Titan's head, with a field of silver mockingbirds on green. Petyr is commonly known as Littlefinger, a nickname given to him in his youth by Edmure_Tully on account of his short size and slender build, as well as the small size and location of his family's holdings on the smallest peninsula of the Fingers. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Petyr is portrayed by Aidan Gillen. Petyr's Baelish_(sellsword) was a Braavosi sellsword in the service of Lord Corbray; when his Baelish_(hedge_knight) became a hedge knight, he took the stone head of the Titan of Braavos as his sigil. Petyr's Baelish_(lord), in turn, was the smallest of small lords of a few rocky acres on the smallest of the Fingers, Petyr's mother was named Alayne_Baelish. Petyr's father once took him to a hermit's cave to see a man said to have the gift of prophecy. Lord Baelish gave the hermit a skin of wine after he groped the boy a bit and said he would be a great man. Petyr's father befriended Hoster_Tully during his service in the War of the Ninepenny Kings, and this friendship led to Petyr being sent to be fostered as a ward at Riverrun.

name: Petyr Frey
bio: Petyr Frey, also called Petyr Pimple, is a member of House Frey and is the youngest of Ser Ryman_Frey's three sons. His elder brothers are Edwyn_Frey and Walder_Frey_(son_of_Ryman). Petyr is married to Mylenda_Caron, and they have one daughter, Perra_Frey. Petyr is only a year or two older than Robb_Stark, but he was married at the age of ten to Mylenda_Caron, a woman three times his age.

name: Petyr Mallister
bio: Petyr Mallister was a Lord of Seagard and a head of House Mallister. Petyr claimed the title of river king or King of the Trident in the generations after the riverlands were conquered by the Storm King Arlan_III_Durrandon. Lord Petyr had a brief, inglorious reign before being put down.

name: Petyr Piper
bio: Petyr Piper was the Lord of Pinkmaiden and the head of House Piper during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Petyr is portrayed by Antonio Magro. In 105 AC, Lord Petyr swore his fealty to Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen as the heir of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Petyr stayed true to his word and supported the blacks and Rhaenyra's claim to the Iron Throne. At the Battle at the Red Fork, Lord Petyr and Lord Tristan_Vance faced the westermen army commanded by Jason_Lannister, Lord of Casterly Rock. In the aftermath of the battle Petyr knighted Pate_of_Longleaf, a squire who killed Lord Jason, dubbing him "Longleaf the Lionslayer". Lord Piper died in a battle at Acorn Hall against the invading westermen. According to Mushroom, his heart burst at the sight of his favorite grandson's head upon a spear. Petyr was succeeded by Lord Stanton_Piper.

name: Petyr Plumm
bio: Petyr Plumm was a knight from House Plumm during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Petyr participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Phario Forel
bio: Phario Forel is a Braavosi playwright.

name: Philip Foote
bio: Philip Foote is a knight from House Foote in the westerlands.

name: Philip Plumm
bio: Philip Plumm is the head of House Plumm. He has three sons.

name: Pia
bio: Pia, also known as Pretty Pia, is a servant of House Whent who works in the buttery at Harrenhal. Pia was born at Harrenhal. She was a young girl during the tourney at Harrenhal arranged by Lord Walter_Whent in 281 AC.

name: Pilman
bio: Pilman was a Westerosi ship's captain from Lannisport. Pilman provided the Citadel with an account of a water dancer duel. He claimed that the water dancers seemed to barely skim upon the surface, and insisted he never saw anything like it for grace and skill, however.

name: Pimply squire
bio: A pimply squire from House Sarsfield is part of the Mountain's men. He is a squire to Ser Gregor_Clegane, along with Joss_Stilwood. The given name of the boy is unknown.

name: Pinchbottom Pate
bio: Pinchbottom Pate was an old man who lived in Little Dosk. When ironmen raided Little Dosk, they attempted to take Pate's daughter. Pate tried to stop them, but was killed instead. He owed Ser Bennis a silver.

name: Lady Piper (wife of Quellon Greyjoy)
bio: Lady Piper was a member of House Piper, the daughter of a Lord of Pinkmaiden Castle. She was the third and final wife of Lord Quellon_Greyjoy. Her given name is unknown. Lady Piper was wed by a septon to Lord Quellon_Greyjoy at Pinkmaiden Castle. She brought with her a maester to Pyke. While Lord Quellon and his three oldest sons, Balon_Greyjoy, Euron_Greyjoy and Victarion_Greyjoy, were away fighting in Robert's Rebellion, Urrigon_Greyjoy lost half his hand playing the finger dance with his younger brother, Aeron_Greyjoy. Lady Piper sent her maester to sew the missing fingers back but the wound became infected and Urrigon died. After his return to the Iron Islands, Balon heard what had happened to his brother. As an act of revenge, he cut three of the maester's fingers and forced Lady Piper to sew them back. Lady Piper died not long after while giving birth to a stillborn daughter.

name: Pisswater prince
bio: "Pisswater prince" is the moniker that Tyrion_Lannister gives the nameless tanner's son from Pisswater Bend who, according to Illyrio_Mopatis, was swapped with Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar) when he was an infant. His mother died birthing him and his father sold him to Lord Varys for a jug of Arbor gold—his father had other sons but had never tasted Arbor gold. Varys gave the pisswater prince to Princess Elia_Martell and carried Aegon away for his own safety. During the Sack of King's Landing, Ser Gregor_Clegane scaled Maegor's Holdfast and entered the nursery of the infant Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar), son of Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen, and killed the baby present by dashing the boy's head against a wall. He then raped Elia, his hands still covered with the blood of the murdered baby. After the boy was dead, Varys smuggled Aegon across the narrow sea to his ally, Magister Illyrio_Mopatis of Pentos.

name: Plague face
bio: Plague face is how Arya_Stark refers to a secret servant of the Many-Faced God in the House of Black and White in Braavos and a Faceless Man. He has the face of a plague victim.

name: Plummer
bio: Plummer was the steward of Ashford during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Plummer is portrayed by Tom Vaughan-Lawlor. Plummer was the master of the games for the tourney at Ashford Meadow. He initially rejected Ser Duncan_the_Tall's entry into the tourney on the grounds that no one knew who he was, until Prince Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II) vouched for him.

name: Podrick Payne
bio: Podrick Payne, frequently called Pod, is a squire from a lesser branch of House Payne, and he is a distant cousin of Ser Ilyn_Payne. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Podrick is portrayed by Daniel Portman. Podrick was born a member of a lesser branch of House Payne. His father was a squire to richer cousins and his mother was a chandler's daughter. Podrick's father died in Greyjoy's Rebellion, and his mother abandoned him when he was four years old to run away with a wandering singer. Podrick was left with Ser Cedric_Payne, one of the cousins his father had squired for. The knight took care of Podrick, although Cedric might have treated the boy more like a servant than a relative. Podrick had a good dog named Hero when he was young.

name: Poetess
bio: The Poetess is a famous courtesan in Braavos. Her given name is unknown.

name: Pol
bio: Pol was a maester who wrote a treatise on Qohorik metalworking. Pol spent many years of residence in Qohor, investigating the secrets of Qohorik blacksmiths. He was thrice publicly whipped, and cast out for making too many inquiries. The final time, his hand was also cut off, per the allegation he had stolen a Valyrian steel blade. According to him, the true reason for his final exile was the discovery of blood sacrifices — including that of infant slaves — which the Qohorik smiths use in their efforts to produce steel equal to the original Valyrian steel.

name: Pol Qo
bio: Pol Qo, called Hammer of the Jogos Nhai, is a YiTish general who has claimed the title of God-Emperor. Pol Qo is the first of the orange emperors of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti, in defiance of Bu_Gai, the seventeenth azure emperor, enthroned in the capital city of Yin. Qo's seat is located in the garrison city of Trader Town. Another pretender is a Sorcerer_lord, claiming to be the sixty-ninth yellow emperor despite the fall of that dynasty a thousand years ago, who is exiled to the city of Carcosa, far to the east of Yi Ti. It is unknown which of the three emperors will prevail.

name: Pollitor
bio: Pollitor was a Wisdom of the Alchemists' Guild in King's Landing. Wisdom Pollitor trained Widsom Hallyne and claimed that the decline in the potency of wildfire was linked to the extinction of dragons.

name: Polliver
bio: Polliver is a man-at-arms among the Mountain's men in service to Ser Gregor_Clegane. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Polliver is portrayed by Andy Kellegher.

name: Pono
bio: Pono is a ko in Khal Drogo's khalasar.

name: Porridge
bio: Porridge is Ser Davos_Seaworth's nickname for a gaoler at Dragonstone.

name: Porther
bio: Porther is a member of the household guard of Lord Eddard_Stark at Winterfell.

name: Portifer Woodwright
bio: Portifer Woodwright is a knight of House Woodwright. He is the brother of Ser Lucantine_Woodwright and is a household knight in King's Landing.

name: Poul Pemford
bio: Poul Pemford is a member of House Pemford and a guard at Riverrun.

name: Poxy Tym
bio: Poxy Tym is a member of the household guard sworn to House Stark of Winterfell.

name: Praed
bio: Praed is a sellsword recruited by Yoren for the Night's Watch and is part of the group marching north to the Wall.

name: Prendahl na Ghezn
bio: Prendahl na Ghezn is a Ghiscari captain of the mercenary company Stormcrows. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Prendahl is portrayed by Ramon Tikaram.

name: Prendos
bio: Mad Prendos was a pirate known for his black humor. Mad Prendos, one of Salladhor_Saan's galleys, is named after him.

name: Prentys Tully
bio: Prentys Tully was the Lord of Riverrun and the head of House Tully during the later reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen and the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He was married to the pious Lady Lucinda_Broome, of House Broome. On account of his undistinguished term sitting on the small council, Prentys was passed over during the consideration of a new Hand of the King, after the resignation of Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan) in 54 AC. However, Lord Tully had a favorable opinion of his bannerman, Lord Myles_Smallwood of Acorn Hall, as the new Hand of the King. King Jaehaerys visited Lord Tully at Riverrun during a royal progress across the riverlands. In late 58 AC, Prentys attended the tourney at King's Landing commemorating the tenth anniversary of the king's coronation. Prentys and Lucinda died at Riverrun from the Shivers a day apart from each other in the winter of 59 AC.

name: Preston Greenfield
bio: Preston Greenfield is a knight from House Greenfield. He is a member of King Robert_I_Baratheon's Kingsguard. Preston's fellow Kingsguard were aware that he broke his vows by meeting a Draper's_wife in King's Landing whenever her husband was away.

name: Priscella Hogg
bio: Priscella Hogg was a noblewoman from House Hogg during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. She was a companion to Aegon's second wife, Queen Daenaera_Velaryon. In 135 AC, Priscella was accused of a poisoning attempt against members of the royal household. She had conceived the notion that Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen would marry her if only Larra_Rogare were dead. She told the court that he smiled whenever he saw her, and his shoulder once brushed against her bosom when he passed her on the steps. Priscella was deemed to have only played a minor role in the plot, and on account of her sex, Lord Torrhen_Manderly, the Hand of the King, gave her a minor punishment. She was condemned to have her nose cut off, with the understanding that the punishment would be put aside should she give herself to the Faith and so long as she remained true to her vows.

name: Prudence Celtigar
bio: Lady Prudence Celtigar was a noblewoman from House Celtigar during the reigns of kings Maegor_I_Targaryen and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. She later became the Lady of Gulltown. Prudence was the daughter of Lord Edwell_Celtigar of Claw Isle. She had at least one sister, Prunella_Celtigar, and a brother. In 47 AC, her father urged King Maegor_I_Targaryen to marry either Prudence or Prunella, or both if he so desired. The king did not choose Prudence or her sister, instead marrying the Black Brides, Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), Jeyne_Westerling_(wife_of_Maegor_I) and Elinor_Costayne. In 49 AC, her father made the same offer to Lord Rogar_Baratheon and Queen Regent Alyssa_Velaryon, that King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen marry either Prudence or Prunella; Lord Baratheon rebuffed this offer. Prudence became a lady companion to Queen Alysanne_Targaryen during her time on Dragonstone. During King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne's second wedding, she disrobed the king during the bedding ceremony. At Maidenpool, Prudence was stabbed through the shoulder defending the queen when she was attacked by septas attending Jonquil's Pool who wished to kill the "Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I)" Alysanne was carrying. In 52 AC, the betrothal of Prudence to Lord Grafton was arranged.

name: Prunella Celtigar
bio: Prunella Celtigar was a noblewoman from House Celtigar during the reigns of kings Maegor_I_Targaryen and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. She later became the Lady of Starpike, Dunstonbury, and Whitegrove by marrying Lord Uther_Peake. Prunella was the daughter of Lord Edwell_Celtigar of Claw Isle. She had at least one sister, Prudence_Celtigar, and a brother. In 47 AC, her father urged King Maegor_I_Targaryen to marry either Prunella or Prudence, or both if he so desired. The king did not choose Prunella or her sister, instead marrying the Black Brides, Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), Jeyne_Westerling_(wife_of_Maegor_I) and Elinor_Costayne. In 49 AC, her father made the same offer to Lord Rogar_Baratheon and Queen Regent Alyssa_Velaryon, that King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen marry either Prunella and Prudence; Lord Baratheon rebuffed this offer. Prunella became a lady companion to Queen Alysanne_Targaryen during her time on Dragonstone. During King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne's second wedding, she disrobed the king during the bedding ceremony. In 55 AC, Alysanne arranged a marriage between Prunella and Lord Uther_Peake. It was considered an exceptional match for her, and a triumph for the queen.

name: Puckens
bio: Puckens is a guard in service to House Lannister.

name: Pudding
bio: Pudding was a street child from Flea Bottom in King's Landing during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. When Ser Duncan the Tall was an orphan child in Flea Bottom, Pudding was one of his friends, along with Ferret and Rafe. They once stole a rotting head from a spike atop one of the walls of King's Landing. Ferret climbed the wall to grab the head, after Rafe and Pudding dared him, but when the guards came, he threw the head down and Dunk caught it. The four friends used the head to terrorize the girls of Flea Bottom, chasing them through the alleys and making them give the head a kiss before they let them go. Dunk and his friends kept the head until it turned black and the flesh started falling off, which made it less fun to chase girls. One night, they broke into a pot shop and threw what was left of the head into the kettle. In 212 AC, Duncan saw the decapitated head of the Hunchbacked_septon on the walls of Stoney Sept, and was reminded of his childhood friends' adventures with the head in King's Landing.

name: Puddingfoot
bio: Puddingfoot is a member of the brotherhood without banners.

name: Pyat Pree
bio: Pyat Pree is a warlock from the city of Qarth. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Pyat is portrayed by Ian Hanmore.

name: Pycelle
bio: Pycelle is a Grand Maester of the Citadel who has served in King's Landing and on the small council for over forty years. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Pycelle is portrayed by Julian Glover. Pycelle forged his maester's chain in Oldtown as a young man during the hot summer in the reign of King Maekar_I_Targaryen. After Grand Maester Merion died on his way to serve Aegon_V_Targaryen, Pycelle was granted his position. He has been the Grand Maester of the Seven Kingdoms for forty years, serving Aegon V and Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen before Aerys_II_Targaryen. Pycelle wrote Observations Upon the Recent Blood-Letting on the Stepstones about the War of the Ninepenny Kings. During the reign of Aerys_II_Targaryen, Pycelle came to greatly admire and respect Aerys's Hand, Tywin_Lannister. Out of all the members of the small council, Pycelle assisted Tywin the most in the governance of the realm. Pycelle wrote the Citadel often, praising Tywin's leadership, stating that the man was born to rule. While Aerys grew increasingly jealous and resentful of Tywin's capability, Pycelle became a staunch ally to the Hand, until Tywn resigned his office in 281 AC. Aerys also mistrusted his own son and heir, Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen, who had become wary of his father's behavior. At court tensions were growing between the factions loyal to the king and those loyal to Rhaegar. It fell to Pycelle and the new Hand, Lord Owen_Merryweather, to try and keep the peace between the factions. Pycelle even dispatched a letter to the Citadel writing that tensions and division at court strongly resembled those before the Dance of the Dragons. Pycelle was fearful that a civil war between those loyal to the king and Rhaegar's supporters would break out unless some accord could be reached that would satisfy both factions. Near the end of Robert's Rebellion, Pycelle convinced King Aerys to open his gates to his former Hand, Tywin, in the hope that Tywin would take the throne. This led to the Sack of King's Landing. During the coronation of Robert I Baratheon, Pycelle was pardoned for his service under Aerys II and allowed to continue on the small council. Despite his oath to serve the realm, Pycelle has generally promoted the interests of House Lannister on the council for years.

name: Pycelle's serving girl
bio: Pycelle's serving girl is Grand Maester Pycelle's servant girl in the Red Keep. She is also Pycelle's bedwarmer, although the Citadel would not approve, as maesters are supposed to be celibate. Her name is not mentioned.

name: Pyg
bio: Pyg is a member of the Brave Companions.

name: Pykewood Peake
bio: Pykewood Peake is a sellsword in service to the Golden Company. He is one of the brothers of the exiled Lord Laswell_Peake.

name: Pylos
bio: Pylos is a maester of the Citadel, newly sent to serve Stannis_Baratheon at Dragonstone. He came to Dragonstone to be the eventual replacement for Maester Cressen. When Maester Cressen became ill in 298 AC, Pylos was sent by the Citadel to Dragonstone. It was said that he was to be Cressen's assistant, but Cressen was aware that truly meant that Pylos was to be his replacement after he died. Pylos's rooms are only half a turn down the stairs from Cressen's, in earshot of the elderly maester.

name: Pynto
bio: Pynto is the innkeep of a tavern in Braavos.

name: Pypar
bio: Pypar, commonly known as Pyp, is a member of the Night's Watch. He is in the same recruit class as Jon_Snow. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Pypar is portrayed by Jozef Altin. Before coming to the Wall, Pypar had traveled the Seven Kingdoms with a mummers' troupe.

name: Pytho Malanon
bio: Pytho Malanon is a sculptor of Essos. Pytho sculpted a statue of Illyrio_Mopatis when Illyrio was sixteen years old. The statue is magnificently carved and still graces the court in Illyrio's manse.

name: Qalen
bio: Qalen was a maester who served at Pyke for House Greyjoy. After Greyjoy's Rebellion, Tristifer_Botley and four other boys were brought to Pyke to be fostered by Lady Alannys_Harlaw. After Maester Qalen found Tristifer inappropriately touching Asha_Greyjoy, the daughter of Lord Balon_Greyjoy, the boy was promptly shipped off to Blacktyde.

name: Qano
bio: Qano was a Dothraki khal who lived during the Century of Blood. He was one of the four khals that fought in the Field of Crows. Khal Qano joined forces with Khals Haro, Loso, and Zhako to fight against High King Mazor_Alexi, who had united all of the Sarnori lesser kings to defend their homeland against the Dothraki hordes. The battle took place halfway between Sarnath and the ruins of Kasath. The Sarnori chariots smashed the center of the Dothraki horde. Haro was trampled and died, while his riders fled, chased by Alexi and his cavalry. However, the escape was feigned and Haro's riders turned back, raining arrows on the Sarnori, while the other khals surrounded the Tall Men on all sides, with Qano's khalasar attacking from the north.

name: Qarl the Maid
bio: Qarl, widely known as Qarl the Maid, is an ironborn warrior sworn to Balon_Greyjoy. He is the grandson of a thrall. When Asha_Greyjoy first met Qarl in 293 AC or 294 AC, she called his attempt at growing a beard "peach fuzz". Since he had never seen a peach, she encouraged him to join her crew. They traded at Fair Isle, Lannisport, and the Arbor, and raided Lyseni pirates in the Stepstones. Qarl and Asha began sleeping together after sampling peaches at the Arbor.

name: Qarl the Thrall
bio: Qarl the Thrall is an ironborn raider. He presumably is, or once was, a thrall.

name: Qarl Corbray
bio: Qarl Corbray was the Lord of Heart's Home and the head of House Corbray during the reign of the kings Maegor_I_Targaryen and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Jaehaerys made him the Commander of the City Watch of King's Landing at the beginning of his reign.

name: Qarl Correy
bio: Qarl Correy was a household knight in service to House Velaryon at High Tide during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Qarl is portrayed by Arty Froushan. Laenor had always favored handsome knights of his household, whom he likely took as lovers. The first prominent knight was Ser Joffrey_Lonmouth, whom Laenor's third son, Prince Joffrey_Velaryon, was later named after. After Ser Joffrey's death, Ser Qarl Correy became Laenor's new favorite. Qarl slew Laenor over a quarrel at a fair at Spicetown. The reasons for the quarrel are not known. Correy was never seen again after escaping. According to The Testimony of Mushroom, Prince Daemon_Targaryen paid Correy to kill Laenor to free Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen for himself and offered him escape from Spicetown on a boat but had him murdered and the body disposed of at sea instead.

name: Qarl Kenning
bio: Qarl Kenning, known as Queer Qarl Kenning, is a member of House Kenning of Harlaw.

name: Qarl Quickaxe
bio: Qarl Quickaxe is a resident of the Iron Islands. His house and allegiance are unknown. He is mentioned by Asha_Greyjoy as one of many Qarls who live on the Iron Islands.

name: Qarl Shepherd
bio: Qarl Shepherd is a member of House Shepherd in the Iron Islands.

name: Qarlon
bio: Qarlon the Great was an Andal warrior who united the Lorathi isles and dreamed of making himself "King of All Andals". His seat was a large wooden keep at Lorassyon. After twenty years of warfare against petty kings of Andalos, Qarlon's domain extended along the Shivering Sea from the future site of the Braavosi lagoon in the west and the Axe in the east. To the south Qarlon controlled the headwaters of the Upper Rhoyne and the Noyne, but this led to conflict with other Andal kings and the Free City of Norvos. When the Norvoshi closed the Noyne, Qarlon defeated them twice in the Hills of Norvos. Unwisely, however, he then marched against the city of Norvos. The Norvoshi sent to distant Valyria for help, and the Freehold rose in defense of its colony. According to The Fires of the Freehold, a hundred dragonlords burned Qarlon the Great and his Andal host outside Norvos. The dragonlords then attacked the Lorathi isles, burning Qarlon's keep. It is said nobody survived the Scouring of Lorath.

name: Qarlton Chelsted
bio: Qarlton Chelsted was a Lord of House Chelsted who served as master of coin to King Aerys_II_Targaryen. He was named to the office of Hand of the King during Robert's Rebellion.

name: Qarlton II Durrandon
bio: Qarlton II Durrandon, self-styled Qarlton the Conqueror, was a Storm King of House Durrandon during the Andal invasion and a grandson of King Erich_VII_Durrandon. Qarlton was the first Storm King to face the Andals in battle. He also, after four generations of war, completed the reconquest  of Massey's Hook after a year's siege and slaying the last Massey king, Josua_Massey. However, he held his conquest for less than two years, as Togarion_Bar_Emmon threw the stormlanders away from Massey's Hook. Qarlton II, however, soon had to face the Andals, whose longships had begun to land on the Stormlands' shores. Qarlton II was succeeded by Qarlton_III_Durrandon.

name: Qarlton III Durrandon
bio: Qarlton III Durrandon was a Storm King of House Durrandon during the Andal invasion and the heir and successor of King Qarlton_II_Durrandon. Like his father, Qarlton III reigned in times of war. He was succeeded by his son Monfryd_V_Durrandon.

name: Qarro Volentin
bio: Qarro Volentin is the current First Sword of Braavos, protector of Ferrego_Antaryon, the Sealord of Braavos.

name: Qavo Nogarys
bio: Qavo Nogarys is a customs officer of Selhorys near Volantis.

name: Qezza Galare
bio: Qezza Galare is a girl of the House of Galare, one of the noble ruling families of Meereen. Her brother is Grazhar_zo_Galare. She is a cousin to Galazza_Galare, the Green Grace of Meereen.

name: Qhored I Hoare
bio: Qhored I Hoare, known as Qhored the Cruel, was one of the greatest High Kings of the Iron Islands. While he is usually associated with House Hoare, some accounts state he was actually of House Greyiron or House Blacktyde. The young Qhored sacked Oldtown, taking thousands of women as captives. When he was thirty, Qhored defeated King Bernarr_II_Justman and took his three sons as hostages. Three years later he murdered the boys in the Bloody Keep of Pyke, crushed Bernarr's vengeful army, and drowned the river king as a sacrifice to the Drowned God, putting an end to House Justman. During the reign of King Qhored, the ironborn could rightfully claim that his writ ran "wherever men can smell salt water or hear the crash of waves." The ironborn were at their peak of expansion, ruling territory along the Sunset Sea from the Arbor to Bear Island. Many men abandoned the shores for fear of Qhored's reavers, while those who remained were forced to pay tribute. The Kingdom of the Reach called him Qhored the Cruel. King Qhored died at the age of ninety, having ruled for seventy five years. Qhored's conquests were gradually lost by his successors and descendants, however. According to a semi-canon source, after the reign of Qhored the Cruel there were other kings from House Hoare that were named Qhored, both in the driftwood king era and the iron king era.

name: Qhorin Halfhand
bio: Qhorin, known as Qhorin Halfhand and the Halfhand, is a ranger of the Night's Watch and second-in-command at the Shadow Tower. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones he is portrayed by Simon Armstrong. Qhorin lost all the fingers on his right hand except his thumb and forefinger after catching a wildling axe before it would strike his skull. Qhorin then thrust his maimed hand into his adversary's face and slew the man while he was blinded by blood. The Halfhand trained how to fight left-handed and became just as proficient with his left hand as he had been with his right. Qhorin has explored deep into the lands beyond the Wall. Qhorin was a good friend to Mance_Rayder before Mance deserted the Night's Watch. The Halfhand also knows Eddard_Stark, Lord of Winterfell, and his late father, Lord Rickard_Stark.

name: Qhorin Volmark
bio: Qhorin Volmark was the Lord of Volmark and the head of House Volmark during Aegon's Conquest. Qhorin's grandmother had been the younger sister of Harwyn_Hoare, King of the Isles and the Rivers. After the death of Harwyn's grandson, King Harren_Hoare, in the burning of Harrenhal during Aegon's Conquest, Qhorin claimed to be the heir of the black line of House Hoare. He was the first of many rival claimants to the title King of the Iron Islands. During his invasion of the Iron Islands in 2 AC, King Aegon_I_Targaryen flew to Great Wyk and slew Qhorin with Blackfyre. Aegon allowed Qhorin's infant son to succeed to the Volmark inheritance. Goren_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands, easily defeated a conspiracy to crown Qhorin's son in 33 AC.

name: Qhorwyn Hoare
bio: Qhorwyn Hoare, known as Qhorwyn the Cunning, was a King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. Qhorwyn was a shrewd King of the Iron Islands who avoided war and instead gathered wealth during his reign. He tripled the size of House Hoare's fleets and ordered the creation of additional armament to dissuade enemies from attacking. Qhorwyn's eldest son died of greyscale. When his third son, Harwyn_Hoare, returned to the Iron Islands from adventuring two years later, Qhorwyn was dying. The king's middle son, Prince Harlan_Hoare, died after falling from a horse while Qhorwyn was lying ill. Though it was said to be an accident, Harlan's death was considered suspicious by some, especially when King Qhorwyn passed six days later. Harwyn succeeded Qhorwyn as king, and the Hardhand soon used his father's strengthened military to conquer the riverlands from House Durrandon of the stormlands and become the first King of the Isles and the Rivers.

name: Qoren Martell
bio: Qoren Nymeros Martell was the ruling Prince of Dorne, the Lord of Sunspear, and the head of House Martell during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen as well as during the Dance of the Dragons. In 110 AC, Prince Qoren Martell joined the War for the Stepstones in support of the Triarchy when they warred against Prince Daemon_Targaryen and Lord Corlys_Velaryon, leading the Dornishmen in battle over the Stepstones. King Viserys_I_Targaryen considered wedding his daughter Rhaenyra_Targaryen to Prince Qoren, as a way of bringing Dorne into the realm. During the Dance of the Dragons, both the greens and the blacks courted Dorne's support, but Qoren refused to take part. When Ser Otto_Hightower reached out to the Prince of Dorne, Qoren spurned his offer as he refused to partake in a war with dragons, recalling the First Dornish War. By 132 AC, Qoren had passed away and was succeeded by his eldest child, Princess Aliandra_Martell.

name: Lord Commander Qorgyle
bio: Lord Commander Qorgyle was a member of House Qorgyle and was the 996th Lord Commander of the Night's Watch. His given name has not been revealed. Lord Commander Qorgyle entered the Night's Watch some time before Ser Denys_Mallister. During his term, Castle Black sent patrols east towards Eastwatch-by-the-Sea every third day and west towards the Shadow Tower every second day. The Lord Commander once visited Winterfell with an escort of a dozen men, including Mance_Rayder, to meet with Lord Eddard_Stark. During the visit, Eddard's sons Robb_Stark and Jon_Snow piled up snow above a gate to dump on a unsuspecting passerby. Mance caught them but did not reveal their prank, and the boys eventually dropped the snow onto Tomard. After Qorgyle's death, Jeor_Mormont was chosen as the new Lord Commander in 288 AC.

name: Qos
bio: Qos is the captain of the Wind Witch, a galley out of Braavos or Myr.

name: Qotho
bio: Qotho is a bloodrider to Khal Drogo. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Qotho is portrayed by Dar Salim.

name: Quaithe
bio: Quaithe, also known as Quaithe of the Shadow, is a shadowbinder from Asshai. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Quaithe is portrayed by Laura Pradelska.

name: Quaro
bio: Quaro is a Dothraki warrior assigned to the khas of Daenerys_Targaryen within the khalasar of Khal Drogo.

name: Queen o' whores
bio: The queen o' whores is a prostitute with Ser Ryman_Frey during the siege of Riverrun. Her true name is unknown.

name: Queen Whore
bio: Queen Whore was the concubine to Cleon_the_Second. Her given name is unknown.

name: Quellon Botley
bio: Quellon Botley is the second son of Lord Germund_Botley.

name: Quellon Greyjoy
bio: Quellon Greyjoy was a Lord of the Iron Islands, Lord Reaper of Pyke, and head of House Greyjoy. He was the father of Balon_Greyjoy, Euron_Greyjoy, Victarion_Greyjoy, and Aeron_Greyjoy. The longship Lord Quellon is presumably named in his honor. Quellon was the grandson of Lord Dagon_Greyjoy. As a youth, he fought corsairs and slavers in the Summer Sea. Years later, Quellon commanded a hundred longships during the War of the Ninepenny Kings, playing a crucial part in the fighting around the Stepstones. As a lord, Quellon preferred a peaceful coexistence with the rest of the realm. Quellon tried to reform the ancient, insular traditions of the ironborn and integrate them with the rest of the Seven Kingdoms, hoping to forge stronger bonds between his own domains and the mainland. Quellon brought on reform by freeing the remaining thralls on the islands and outlawing the practice of thralldom, though he was not wholly successful. He forbade most reaving except by his own leave, and discouraged the taking of salt wives, taxing heavily for the privilege. He brought scores of maesters to the Iron Islands, employing them as healers and tutors, while their ravens tied the islands even closer to the mainland. For some time in his elder years, Quellon was troubled with stomach pains that were so excruciating that he required milk of the poppy to sleep every night. Quellon's health and strength were failing when Robert's Rebellion broke out, and the cautious lord resolved to take no part in the war. It was only after the Battle of the Trident and the death of Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen that he was convinced by his eldest sons to join the war against House Targaryen, least the Greyjoys lose any hope in sharing in the spoils of victory. Quellon led fifty longships against the Reach, keeping most of the ironmen home to guard against House Lannister. Quellon died at sea in a battle at the Mander's mouth against the Shield Islands, and the ironmen's contribution to the war turned out to be minimal. With Quellon's death, the title of head of House Greyjoy and Lord of the Iron Islands passed to Balon_Greyjoy, his eldest surviving son. Lord Balon rejected most of his father's reforms, and his desire to return to the Old Way led to Greyjoy's Rebellion against the rule of King Robert_I_Baratheon.

name: Quellon Humble
bio: Quellon Humble is an ironborn raider and a member of House Humble.

name: Quence
bio: Quence, is a mummer of the Ship.

name: Quenn
bio: Quenn  is a member of the free folk.

name: Quent
bio: Quent  is a member of the household guard sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Quent is portrayed by Aidan Crowe.

name: Quenten Banefort
bio: Quenten Banefort is the Lord of Banefort and the head of House Banefort.

name: Quentin Tyrell
bio: Quentin Tyrell was a knight of House Tyrell during the reign of Aerys_II_Targaryen. He was a cousin of Lord Mace_Tyrell. He has one son, Ser Olymer_Tyrell. Quentin died at the Battle of Ashford during Robert's Rebellion.

name: Quenton Corbray
bio: Quenton Corbray was the Lord of Heart's Home and the head of House Corbray during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In the dispute over succession after Lady Jeyne_Arryn's death, Lord Quenton's uncle, Ser Corwyn_Corbray, was killed at Runestone by a crossbowman whilst trying to confront a claimant to the Eyrie, Ser Arnold_Arryn. As Ser Corwyn was one of the king's regents, killing him was an act of treason, and House Corbray rallied to Lady Jeyne's chosen heir, Ser Joffrey_Arryn. In 136 AC, Lord Quenton made his way to King's Landing with Lord Arryn, Lord Gunthor_Royce, and Isembard_Arryn when the new regents were to be chosen by lot.

name: Quenton Greyjoy
bio: Quenton Greyjoy was a member of House Greyjoy, the second son of Lord Quellon_Greyjoy and his first wife, Lady_Stonetree_(wife_of_Quellon_Greyjoy).

name: Quenton Greyjoy (cousin)
bio: Quenton Greyjoy is a distant cousin to Asha_Greyjoy. He is captain of the longship Salty Wench.

name: Quenton Hightower
bio: Quenton Hightower was the Lord of the Hightower and the head of House Hightower during the reign of King Maekar_I_Targaryen. When Grand Maester Pycelle was a boy, the grey plague hit Oldtown. Lord Quenton had the city gates closed, burned every ship in the port, and ordered that anyone trying to flee was to be killed. Once the plague burned itself out, Quenton rescinded those orders. On the day he did so, however, he and his young son were pulled from their horses by the survivors and had their throats slit.

name: Quenton Qoherys
bio: Quenton Qoherys was the first Lord of Harrenhal and the head of House Qoherys. Ser Quenton was the master-at-arms at Dragonstone during Aegon's Conquest.[N 1] He was named Lord of Harrenhal by King Aegon_I_Targaryen after Aegon had extinguished House Hoare during his conquest. Lord Quenton had two strong sons and a plump grandson to continue the family line, but as his first wife had died from spotted fever in 1 BC, he agreed to wed a daughter of his liege lord, Edmyn_Tully of Riverrun. In 9 AC, he died from a fall from his horse and was succeeded by his grandson, Gargon_Qoherys.

name: Quentyn Ball
bio: Quentyn Ball, known as Fireball, was a knight from House Ball during the reigns of kings Aegon_IV_Targaryen and Daeron_II_Targaryen. His personal coat-of-arms was a fireball blazing red and yellow across a night black field. He was one of the key supporters of Daemon Blackfyre during the First Blackfyre Rebellion.

name: Quentyn Blackwood
bio: Quentyn Blackwood was the Lord of Raventree Hall and the head of House Blackwood during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Lord Quentyn was killed by Ser Otho_Bracken in 206 AC during a tourney at King's Landing. This act reignited the feud between the Blackwoods and Brackens.

name: Quentyn Martell
bio: Prince Quentyn Nymeros Martell, better known simply as Quentyn Martell, is a knight from House Martell. He is the second child and eldest son of Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne, and Lady Mellario of Norvos. Quentyn was born the second child of Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne, and his wife, Lady Mellario of Norvos. At a young age, Quentyn was sent to be fostered by Anders_Yronwood, Lord of Yronwood, whom he served as a page and later squire. Quentyn was fostered at Yronwood to repair the relationship between Houses Martell and Yronwood, which had been damaged by a duel between Quentyn’s uncle, Prince Oberyn_Martell, and Lord Edgar_Yronwood, more than a decade before. Edgar and Oberyn had both taken cuts during this duel, but while Oberyn recovered, Edgar's wounds festered and he eventually died. This led to whispers that Oberyn had fought with a poisoned blade, so sending Quentyn to Yronwood was to settle this blood debt. Mellario felt Quentyn was too young to be sent away, however, and the boy's fosterage was one of the strains on his parents' marriage. When Quentyn first arrived at Yronwood, he was smitten with Lord Anders's eldest daughter, Ynys_Yronwood. His crush lasted until the day she wed Ryon_Allyrion and left Yronwood. Quentyn received his first kiss from one of the Drinkwater_twins, though he never knew which one. About 290 AC, Prince Doran wrote a letter in which he instructed his son to do all required of him by the maester and master-at-arms at Yronwood, as Quentyn would one day rule Dorne, and he therefore needed to be strong of both mind and body. Quentyn's elder sister, Princess Arianne_Martell, then fourteen years old, secretly discovered the letter while it was still being written, however. Arianne is Doran's heir by Dornish custom, and she has since resented Quentyn under the belief that Doran intends to be succeeded by her younger brother. Arianne is unaware, however, that the Prince of Dorne had negotiated a secret marriage pact by which Arianne is to wed the exiled Viserys_Targaryen and Quentyn would instead become the successor to Dorne. At Yronwood, Quentyn befriended Anders's eldest son, Cletus_Yronwood, Cletus's cousin Archibald_Yronwood, Gerris_Drinkwater, and Willam_Wells. Quentyn received his knighthood at the age of eighteen, from Lord Anders Yronwood, after turning down the same offer made by his uncle, Prince Oberyn. Quentyn has been riding horses since he was six, and has been trained with spear and sword and shield since he was old enough to walk. Amongst the people in Dorne, Quentyn is little known.

name: Quentyn Qorgyle
bio: Quentyn Qorgyle is the Lord of Sandstone and the head of House Qorgyle in Dorne.

name: Quhuru Mo
bio: Quhuru Mo is the captain of the Cinnamon Wind out of Tall Trees Town in the Summer Islands. He has a daughter, Kojja_Mo.

name: Quickfinger
bio: Quickfinger was a the nickname of a thief who lived during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. During the First Blackfyre Rebellion, Quickfinger sided with the rebels, supporting Daemon_I_Blackfyre. Quickfinger was caught with stolen dragon eggs. Later, Eustace_Osgrey lamented that if Quickfinger had not been caught, it might have changed the tide of the war.

name: Quill
bio: Quill is a Braavosi playwright who writes bawdy plays for the mummers of the Ship.

name: Quillion
bio: Archmaester Quillion is an Archmaester of the Citadel. He has put forth the theory that the mysterious black stone fortress of Battle Isle that serves as the foundation of the Hightower of Oldtown was built by the mazemakers, citing its labyrinthine nature.

name: Quince
bio: Quince was the first maester to serve at the Eyrie, the seat of House Arryn. He spoke highly of the castle and regarded it the finest work ever built by men and worth even of the gods.

name: Quincy Cox
bio: Quincy Cox is the Knight of Saltpans and the head of House Cox.

name: Quort
bio: Despite still being a boy around the age of fourteen, Quort already has a wife and a child. Quort is of the raiders from the free folk who goes over the Wall with Jarl and Styr. He fights in the assault on Castle Black, but is killed in battle. His body is later found by Jon_Snow.

name: Qyburn
bio: Qyburn is a former maester who has joined the Brave Companions. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Qyburn is portrayed by Anton Lesser. Qyburn studied at the Citadel, and was as skilled a healer as Ebrose. However, he sought to surpass the archmaester, and instead of opening dead bodies to discover the secrets of the living as the Citadel has done for centuries, Qyburn opened living bodies to discover the secrets of death. When these unethical experiments, as well as his studies of necromancy, were discovered, Qyburn was stripped of his chain and his position as a maester. Nevertheless, he claims to understand the nature of life and death better than any man in Oldtown. Following his exile from Oldtown, Qyburn joined the Brave Companions. He has respect for Marwyn of the Citadel, the only archmaester who liked Qyburn's way of thinking about the residue of souls after death, but he calls the other archmaesters "grey sheep".

name: Qyle
bio: Qyle is one of the recruits for the Night’s Watch found by Yoren and is part of the band marching north to the Wall.

name: Qyle Corbray
bio: Qyle Corbray was the King of the Fingers and a monarch of House Corbray during the Andal invasion. King Qyle was the first of the Andal kings to fall to Robar_II_Royce, after uniting the First Men of the Vale. According to legend, King Robar II personally slew King Qyle after striking Lady Forlorn from his hand. Afterwards, Robar claimed Lady Forlorn for himself.

name: Qyle Martell
bio: Qyle Nymeros Martell was a Dornish prince and younger brother to Princess Aliandra_Martell. Prince Qyle was a son of Prince Qoren_Martell. His elder sister, Aliandra_Martell became Princess of Dorne after their father's death. The small council of the Iron Throne suggested Qyle as a possible husband for Lady Baela_Targaryen and father of a potential male heir to the Seven Kingdoms, but he was soon dismissed. Baela was instead arranged to marry Lord Thaddeus_Rowan, though she broke the betrothal by marrying Lord Alyn_Velaryon without the consent of the council. Qyle and his sister Coryanne_Martell were later angered by the attentions the flirtatious Aliandra lavished upon Lord Alyn when he came to Sunspear.

name: Racallio Ryndoon
bio: Racallio Ryndoon was a devious Tyroshi captain-general who declared himself King of the Narrow Sea. He is remembered in the Free Cities through two scholarly studies and numerous poems, romances, and songs.

name: Rafe
bio: Rafe was a street child from Flea Bottom in King's Landing during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In The Mystery Knight, Rafe is implied to be a boy, having a male given name. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Rafe is portrayed in flashbacks by actress Chloe Lea. When Ser Duncan_the_Tall was an orphan child in Flea Bottom, Rafe was one of his friends, along with Ferret and Pudding. They once stole a rotting head from a spike atop one of the walls of King's Landing. Ferret climbed the wall to grab the head, after Rafe and Pudding dared him, but when the guards came, he threw the head down and Dunk caught it. The four friends used the head to terrorize the girls of Flea Bottom, chasing them through the alleys and making them give the head a kiss before they let them go. Dunk and his friends kept the head until it turned black and the flesh started falling off, which made it less fun to chase girls. One night, they broke into a pot shop and threw what was left of the head into the kettle. In 212 AC, Duncan saw the decapitated head of the Hunchbacked_septon on the walls of Stoney Sept, and was reminded of his childhood friends' adventures with the head in King's Landing.

name: Rafford
bio: Rafford, known as Raff the Sweetling, is one of the Mountain's men, the men-at-arms in service to Ser Gregor_Clegane.

name: Ragnor Pyke
bio: Ragnor Pyke is an ironborn raider and a member of Victarion_Greyjoy's crew on board the Iron Victory. He is bastard-born.

name: Ragwyle
bio: Ragwyle is a wildling spearwife.

name: Rainbow Knight
bio: The Rainbow Knight is the focus of a famous love story with Lady Shella.

name: Rakharo
bio: Rakharo is a Dothraki horserider in the khalasar of Khal Drogo. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Rakharo is portrayed by Elyes Gabel.

name: Ralf
bio: Ralf the Limper is an ironborn raider and is the captain of the Lord Quellon.

name: Ralf
bio: Ralf of Lordsport is an ironborn raider from Lordsport.

name: Ralf
bio: Ralf the Shepherd is an ironborn raider.

name: Ragged Ralf
bio: Ragged Ralf is a legendary ironborn raider who supposedly lived during the Age of Heroes. He was from Old Wyk.

name: Ralf Kenning
bio: Ralf Kenning is an ironborn captain, raider and a member of House Kenning of Harlaw. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Ralf is portrayed by Grahame Fox.

name: Ralf Stonehouse
bio: Ralf Stonehouse, better known as Red Ralf Stonehouse, is an ironborn raider and a member of House Stonehouse. He is captain of the ship Red Jester of the Iron Fleet.

name: Ralph Buckler
bio: Ralph Buckler is the Lord of Bronzegate and the head of House Buckler. His cousin is Ser Brus_Buckler.

name: Ramsay Snow
bio: Ramsay Snow is the bastard son of Roose_Bolton, Lord of the Dreadfort and head of House Bolton, and is known as the Bastard of Bolton and the Bastard of the Dreadfort. Ramsay has a group of men-at-arms who stand high in his favor and follow him loyally, although their true allegiance lies with Lord Roose. They are called the Bastard's Boys, though never within their or Ramsay's hearing, and are just as depraved as Ramsay. Ramsay's personal steed is named Blood, and he keeps a pack of female hunting hounds. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Ramsay is portrayed by Iwan Rheon. Ramsay is the product of rape between Roose_Bolton, Lord of the Dreadfort, and a Miller's_wife.
