While hunting along the Weeping Water, Roose saw the miller's wife and decided to illicitly practice the banned tradition of the first night, wherein a lord had the right to bed the commoner's bride. He hanged the old miller under a tree for not informing his lord of the new marriage and raped the young bride beneath his swaying body. A year later the woman arrived at the Dreadfort with the newborn Ramsay. Roose nearly killed her and the babe, but when he saw the child had his eyes, the taboo of kinslaying stayed his hand. The woman claimed her husband's brother had beaten her when he saw the babe's eyes and cast her out. Angered by this, Roose had the man's tongue removed so he would spread no tales to Roose's liege lord, Rickard_Stark, Lord of Winterfell. Roose then gave the woman the mill by the Weeping Water along with some piglets, chickens, and a bag of stars every year on the condition that she never reveal to Ramsay the truth about who his father was. Ramsay's mother returned to the Dreadfort years later claiming she needed help in raising Ramsay, who was growing up up wild and unruly. Roose sent Ramsay a servant known as Reek. Reek, despite taking constant washes, always smelled bad due to some "unknown birth condition" that caused his skin to reek, thus his nickname. Giving him to Ramsay and his mother was actually a cruel jest by Roose, but Ramsay and Reek grew inseparable. Roose would later reflect on whether Ramsay had corrupted Reek or whether Reek had corrupted Ramsay, though Reek would follow Ramsay's orders quite faithfully; Ramsay mentioned once that Reek knew better than to deny him. Despite Roose's instruction to Ramsay's mother, either she or Reek eventually informed him of his true parentage. Roose believes that both Reek and Ramsay's mother were urging Ramsay on, and constantly reminding the increasingly violent bastard of his "rights." In 297 AC, Roose's heir, Domeric_Bolton, attempted to forge a sibling connection with his younger half-brother, despite Roose's order not to do so. Domeric soon died of a sickness of the bowels, according to Maester Uthor, although Roose believes his heir had been poisoned by Ramsay, making the bastard a kinslayer. Ramsay also earned the enmity of House Dustin, as Barbrey_Dustin, Lady of Barrowton, was fond of her late nephew. Roose brought Ramsay to the Dreadfort as he had no other sons, trueborn or otherwise. Ramsay doubtless learned to read and write while there. Ser Rodrik_Cassel is not aware of Roose having ever acknowledged his bastard son. Ramsay is accompanied by Dreadfort men called the Bastard's Boys, such as Sour_Alyn, Luton, and Skinner. However, they are tasked by Roose with spying on Ramsay on behalf of the Lord of the Dreadfort.

name: Randa
bio: Randa is an inhabitant of the riverlands.

name: Randor the Exile
bio: Randor the Exile was a knight during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Randor participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Randyll Tarly
bio: Randyll Tarly is Lord of Horn Hill and head of House Tarly. He is married to Lady Melessa_Florent, and is the father of the POV character Samwell_Tarly. Randyll wields the Tarly ancestral blade Heartsbane, a Valyrian steel greatsword, in battle. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Randyll is portrayed by James Faulkner. Randyll married Lady Melessa_Florent, the eldest daughter of Alester_Florent, Lord of Brightwater Keep. Their first-born child, Samwell_Tarly, was born in 283 AC. They would later have three daughters, born within three years time, and a second son, Dickon_Tarly. As one of Lord Mace_Tyrell's bannermen, Randyll fought in Robert's Rebellion on the side of House Targaryen. He defeated Lord Robert_I_Baratheon's forces at the Battle of Ashford, cutting down Lord_Cafferen with Heartsbane during the battle. Randyll insisted that young Sam attend him while holding court in the audience chamber at Horn Hill, and he brought Sam to Highgarden during a visit to see Mace. A martinet, Randyll became disappointed in his gentle, timid firstborn son and made several harsh attempts to engage him in manly, martial pursuits, but all to no effect. A dozen masters-at-arms failed to toughen Sam. Attempts such as dressing him in his mother's clothes, forcing him to sleep in chainmail, and even being bathed in aurochs blood by two warlocks of Qarth failed to raise his valor. Samwell even wept to see a chicken slaughtered. When Lord Tarly threw Sam into Horn Hill's fishpond to teach him to swim, Ser Hyle_Hunt had to rescue the boy. Randyll forbade Sam from sleeping in the same bed as his sisters, and he reprimanded Sam and his mother when he heard them singing "The Song of the Seven" to the infant Dickon. Randyll began to ignore Samwell, leaving him to his own devices and paying more attention to Dickon after his second son was born. When Sam was no more than ten, Lord Tarly brought his heir with him to the Arbor, where Samwell was mocked endlessly by Horas_Redwyne and Hobber_Redwyne as well as by the fool of House Redwyne. Randyll had planned to leave his son at the Arbor as Lord Paxter_Redwyne's page and squire, but he instead brought Samwell back to Horn Hill with him. Randyll forbade Sam from traveling to Oldtown to study at the Citadel, horrified at the notion of a member of House Tarly wearing a maester's chain. To discourage the boy, Randyll had Sam chained and manacled to a wall for three days. When Samwell turned fifteen, three men-at-arms escorted him to his father in a nearby forest. While skinning a deer, Randyll offered his eldest son the choice of either joining the Night's Watch, thereby nullifying his claim to Horn Hill and allowing Dickon to become Randyll's lawful heir, or dying in a supposed accident while hunting the following day. Sam agreed to leave for the Wall.

name: Rast
bio: Rast is a recruit of the Night's Watch. Ser Alliser_Thorne refers to him as Rat. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Rast is portrayed by Luke McEwan. According to a semi-canon source, Rast comes from the Fingers. Rast was sentenced to the Wall for being a rapist.

name: Rat Cook
bio: The Rat Cook was an infamous member of the Night's Watch. According to legend, the man who would later be known as the Rat Cook was a simple cook at the Nightfort. He became infamous when he served an Andal king (identified either as Tywell_II_Lannister, King of the Rock, or Oswell_I_Arryn, King of Mountain and Vale) a pie that was made of bacon and, unknown to the king, the king's son. The cook killed the prince in revenge for a wrong the king supposedly did to him. The king was unaware of this, however, as he ate and praised the taste and asked for a second piece. The gods were angered—not because the cook had committed murder, nor because he had made the king a cannibal—but because the cook had slain a guest beneath his roof. They cursed the cook and transformed him into a massive rat who was doomed to be unable to eat anything but his own young. According to the story, the Rat Cook is an enormous white rat who still lives in the Nightfort today, and all the other rats that inhabit the Nightfort are his descendants. Such is the infamy of the tale that there is a song about the Rat Cook that is still sung in the Seven Kingdoms, despite the fact that this incident supposedly happened hundreds of years before Aegon's Landing. The song is used to represent the repercussions to those who violate guest right, the sacred laws of hospitality.

name: The Rat, the Hawk, and the Pig
bio: The Rat, the Hawk, and the Pig are three men who are mentioned in the history of Westeros. Their real names are not known. As their only known actions were at least thirty years apart, it may be possible that different individuals used these names at different times.[N 1] Some time between 217 AC and 221 AC, these three men attacked Aelora_Targaryen, Princess of Dragonstone, at a masked ball. She committed suicide soon afterward. In 251 AC, the trio led a rebellion that was crushed by Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon_V). Daeron perished in the final battle, alongside his lover, Ser Jeremy_Norridge, but the rebels were slain or hanged.

name: Rattleshirt
bio: The Lord of Bones, also mocked as Rattleshirt, is a free folk leader of renown. His given name is unknown. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is portrayed by Edward Dogliani (Seasons 2 and 3) and Ross O'Hennessy (Season 5). Before Mance_Rayder made peace between them while becoming King-Beyond-the-Wall, Rattleshirt warred with Harma.

name: Ravella Swann
bio: Ravella Smallwood, née Swann, is the Lady of Acorn Hall. She is the wife of Lord Theomar_Smallwood and the mother of Carellen_Smallwood. Ravella had a son who died when he was seven. According to Anguy, Ravella and Tom_of_Sevenstreams were once lovers. According to Tom, Ravella enjoyed his playing of the song "Let Me Drink Your Beauty".

name: Ravos Hoare
bio: Ravos Hoare, known as Ravos the Raper, was an ironborn raider and the son of Harrag_Hoare, an Iron King. Ravos ruled Bear Island during the ironborn invasion of the western coast of the north. Theon_Stark, a King of Winter, personally slew Ravos, but ironborn raids continued under Harrag's grandson, Erich_Hoare.

name: Rawney
bio: Rawney is a brother of the order on the Quiet Isle.

name: Raya Stark
bio: Raya Stark was a noblewoman of House Stark. She was the third daughter of Lord Cregan_Stark and Lady Alysanne_Blackwood.

name: Rayford Rosby
bio: Rayford Rosby was a knight from House Rosby during the final days of King Aenys_I_Targaryen and the coronation of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Ser Rayford was present at Visenya's Hill when King Maegor was challenged by Ser Damon_Morrigen, Grand Captain of the Warrior's Sons, to a trial of seven. He kept quiet when Maegor called upon those who would stand beside him but was shamed by the example of Dick_Bean, a simple man-at-arms, and the appeal of Ser Bernarr_Brune and became one of the king's champions. During the fight against the Warrior's Sons, Rayford was killed.

name: Raylon Rivers
bio: Raylon Rivers was a knight from House Bracken during the Dance of the Dragons. He was the bastard half-brother of Ser Amos_Bracken and the son of Lord Humfrey_Bracken of Stone Hedge. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Raylon Rivers is not present. However, a son of Lord Amos_Bracken is named Ser Raylon Bracken, who is portrayed by Aedan Day. House Bracken declared for the greens during the Dance of the Dragons. Ser Amos_Bracken was slain during the Battle of the Burning Mill, after which his half-brother Raylon led survivors back toward Stone Hedge. Prince Daemon_Targaryen had taken Stone Hedge and Lord Humfrey_Bracken in the meantime, however, so Raylon surrendered to spare his kin from harm.

name: Raymar Royce
bio: Raymar Royce was a former head of a junior branch of House Royce at the start of the third century after Aegon's Conquest, that may or may not be the same junior branch that becomes House Royce of the Gates of the Moon in 300 AC.[N 1] One of his younger sons, Benedict_Royce, married Jocelyn_Stark, a sister of Eddard_Stark's grandfather Lord Edwyle_Stark. They had three daughters, one of which married a Corbray, another a Waynwood and the third might have married a Templeton.

name: Raymond Nayland
bio: Raymond Nayland is a knight of House Nayland.

name: Raymond Rychard
bio: Raymond Rychard was a knight during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Raymond participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Raymont Baratheon
bio: Raymont Baratheon was a knight from House Baratheon and a younger son of Lord Orys_Baratheon and Lady Argella_Durrandon.[N 1] He was a member of King Aenys_I_Targaryen's Kingsguard during the Faith Militant uprising. Ser Raymont Baratheon served in the Kingsguard when the Faith Militant rebelled against King Aenys_I_Targaryen in 41 AC. Three days before King Aenys and his family were to set sail for Dragonstone, two Poor Fellows scaled the walls of the manse atop Visenya's Hill where the royal family resided during the construction of the Red Keep, and broke into the king's bedchamber to assassinate the king. Only the timely intervention of Ser Raymont saved the life of the king.

name: Raymun Redbeard
bio: Raymun Redbeard was a King-Beyond-the-Wall. He is renowned for having led an invasion of the north in the time of Jon_Snow's grandfather's grandfather. Raymun united the free folk clans beyond the Wall as King-Beyond-the-Wall. He had seen the Night's Watch decline and growing laxness and in 226 AC took advantage of the situation. Sending his reavers over after establishing a toehold for themselves on the Wall, they used thrown ramparts of their own and dropped ropes and ladders for his people to cross over and march south. Because the Night's Watch was caught off guard as Redbeard's horde came down upon the north, its lord commander, Jack_Musgood, was henceforth called Sleepy Jack. Eventually Raymun's host met a bloody end on the shores of Long Lake, caught between Lord Willam_Stark of Winterfell and the Drunken Giant, Lord Harmond_Umber. During the fight, Willam was slain, and his younger brother, Artos_Stark, known as the Implacable, slew Raymun Redbeard. Raymun's sons were slain as well, however his brother fled from battle and was known after as the Red_Raven_(free_folk). The Watch arrived too late to fight the wildlings but in time to bury them, the task that Artos assigned them in his wroth as he grieved above the headless corpse of his fallen brother Willam.

name: Raymun Darry
bio: Raymun Darry is a knight of House Darry[N 1] Ser Raymun's three older brothers all died fighting for the Targaryens in the Battle of the Trident. Under Raymun, House Darry has secretly kept its loyalty to the Targaryens after Robert's Rebellion.

name: Raymun Fossoway
bio: Raymun Fossoway was a knight of House Fossoway of Cider Hall, who founded House Fossoway of New Barrel some time after 209 AC. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Raymun is portrayed by Shaun Thomas. Raymun attended the tourney at Ashford Meadow as the squire of Ser Steffon_Fossoway, his older cousin, who promised to eventually knight him. Upon entering the tourney grounds, Ser Duncan_the_Tall watched Steffon defeat Raymun in a spar. Noticing the hedge knight, Steffon challenged Duncan to spar and Raymun urged him on, hoping for Duncan to win against his boastful cousin. Duncan declined to involve himself in their quarrel and, as Steffon challenged Ser Grance_Morrigen instead, Raymun caught up to him and apologized for urging him on against his cousin. In the final tilt of the first day, Raymun watched Prince Aerion_Targaryen intentionally kill Ser Humfrey_Hardyng's horse, much to his disgust. Spotting Duncan among the smallfolk, Raymun sought him out and invited him to the Fossoway pavilion for wine. Raymun expressed his disgust for Aerion, and gave Duncan his opinion on the Targaryen princes and their personalities, believing Prince Maekar Targaryen to be disgraced by his sons. The conversation was interrupted when Maekar's youngest, Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen disguising himself as Duncan's squire "Egg", burst into the Fossoway pavilion to seek out Duncan's help against Aerion, who was assaulting the Dornish puppeteer Tanselle. Raymun warned Duncan against laying a hand against the prince, but Duncan intervened and attacked the prince. Raymun and Steffon arrived shortly after armed with their men-at-arms, while Aegon finally revealed himself as a prince to save Duncan's life. Raymun took care of Duncan's horses while he was imprisoned at Ashford Castle, where Aerion challenged Duncan to a trial of seven. Returning to the Fossoway pavilion, Steffon pledged to join Duncan's side and left to seek out five more knights, though Raymun doubted Steffon's intentions. Raymun sought out additional knights with Aegon, fetching the wounded Humfrey Hardyng and his good-brother Ser Humfrey_Beesbury, seeking revenge against Aerion, while Aegon recruited Ser Robyn_Rhysling and Ser Lyonel_Baratheon. Upon the day of the trial, Steffon betrayed Duncan and sided with Aerion, who promised him a lordship. Disgusted with Steffon, Raymun took his cousin's place. When Duncan hesitated in honoring Raymun's request to be knighted, Lyonel Baratheon intervened and granted knighthood on the youth, with Prince Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II) filling the seventh slot. To avoid confusion with his cousin, Raymund repainted the red apple on his shield green, to the approval of Baelor. During the trial he fought Steffon, matching him blow for blow. Both left the field standing, with Raymun believing he broke a few of his cousin's ribs. After the trial, Prince Baelor asked Raymun for assistance in removing his helm, where it was revealed that the prince had been mortally wounded in the combat and promptly died in Duncan's arms. House Fossoway of New Barrel, the "green-apple Fossoways", was eventually recognized as a cadet branch of House Fossoway of Cider Hall.

name: Raymund Connington
bio: Raymund Connington is the younger brother and heir of Ser Ronnet_Connington, the Knight of Griffin's Roost. He resides in Griffin's Roost.

name: Raymund Frey
bio: Raymund Frey is a knight from House Frey and is the eleventh son of Lord Walder_Frey, the sixth born of his marriage to Amarei_Crakehall. Raymund is married to Beony_Beesbury.

name: Raymund Mallery
bio: Raymund Mallery was a knight from House Mallery, and a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Ser Raymund and his sworn brother of the Kingsguard, Ser Olyver_Bracken, abandoned King Maegor_I_Targaryen in 48 AC when Prince Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen made his claim for the Iron Throne. Since both men had broken their Kingsguard oaths by not protecting Maegor, and Jaehaerys did not wish for oathbreakers to serve him, after taking the throne he offered them the choice of execution or taking the black. They both chose to join the Night's Watch. The Lord Commander of the Night's Watch gave Ser Raymund command of Sable Hall and Ser Olyver command of Rimegate, with orders to restore the crumbling forts of the Wall. However, in 50 AC, Raymund and Olyver led a rebellion of former Faith Militant in the Watch, trying to make themselves lords of their castles. Lord Walton_Stark_(son_of_Brandon) rode north to aid the loyal brothers with ending the mutiny. After Sable Hall was informed of Olyver's execution at Rimegate, Raymund and his rebels fled beyond the Wall to join the free folk. Lord Stark marched north in pursuit, but was slain in the haunted forest by giants. Half a year later, Raymund's head was delivered to Eastwatch-by-the-Sea by a wildling chieftain, who claimed his people had eaten Raymund's rebels.

name: Raymund Tyrell
bio: Raymund Tyrell is a member of House Tyrell, a distant cousin of the ruling line. He is the son of Ser Olymer_Tyrell and Lysa_Meadows.

name: Raynald Westerling
bio: Raynald Westerling is a knight from House Westerling, the eldest son and heir of Lord Gawen_Westerling and Lady Sybell_Spicer.

name: Raynard
bio: Raynard is a septon of the Faith of the Seven. He is stationed in King's Landing and is of the Most Devout.

name: Raynard Ruttiger
bio: Raynard Ruttiger is a landed knight and the head of House Ruttiger.

name: Raynard Ruskyn
bio: Raynard Ruskyn was a knight from House Ruskyn. He became Lord Commander of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. By 135 AC, Ser Raynard was a sworn brother of the Kingsguard. King Aegon_III_Targaryen defied an attempted coup against the Rogare family and the Hand of the King, Lord Thaddeus_Rowan, beginning a secret siege. Raynard was imprisoned in Maegor's Holdfast after being overwhelmed and wounded by the Lysene guards of Larra_Rogare. In the aftermath of the coup, Raynard was released from captivity, and Ser Marston_Waters, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, was mortally wounded when he confronted Ser Mervyn_Flowers, a fellow Kingsguard and one of the conspirators. At some point, Marston was heard to say to Raynard about Mervyn, "We are the both of us bastards and Sworn Brothers besides". Several royal offices were filled at the Great Council of 136 AC, including the promotion of Ser Raynard to Lord Commander, left vacant by Marston's death.

name: Red Flea
bio: Red Flea is one of the daily-assigned names of an Unsullied purchased by Daenerys_Targaryen. He has previously been known as Black Rat, Brown Flea, and possibly Blue Toad or Blue Worm.

name: Red Lamb
bio: The Red Lamb is a former slave in Meereen training to become a knight with Ser Barristan_Selmy. His real name is not known.

name: Red Oarsman
bio: The Red Oarsman is an ironborn captain.

name: Red Raven (free folk)
bio: The Red Raven was a member of the free folk and was the younger brother of Raymun_Redbeard, a legendary King-Beyond-the-Wall. He is an ancestor of House Redbeard. His given name is unknown.

name: Red Rolfe
bio: Red Rolfe is an ironborn warrior.

name: Redtusk
bio: Redtusk was a supporter of Daemon_I_Blackfyre during the First Blackfyre Rebellion. At the battle of the Redgrass Field, Daemon ordered Redtusk to bring Ser Gwayne_Corbray of the Kingsguard to the rear to see to his wounds after their fight. He was considered one of the finest knights of his time. He may be a member of House Crakehall.

name: Redwyn
bio: Redwyn was a ranger of the Night's Watch. Redwyn lived during the reign of Dorren_Stark, a King in the North. He wrote an account of a ranging from the Shadow Tower to Lorn Point on the Frozen Shore. During that ranging they fought giants and traded with the children of the forest. Maester Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) sent a letter to the Citadel, early in the reign of King Aegon_V_Targaryen, reporting of Redwyn's account.

name: Lord Redwyne
bio: Lord Redwyne was the Lord of the Arbor and head of House Redwyne during the reign of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen, Aegon_II_Targaryen and Aegon_III_Targaryen. Lord Unwin_Peake's second son served Lord Redwyne as a page and squire until his death in a sailing accident. When the Dance of the Dragons began, Lord Redwyne declared for King Aegon_II_Targaryen. It is unknown to what extent the Redwynes were involved in the war. By the end of the war, Lord Ormund_Hightower's second son, Martyn_Hightower_(son_of_Ormund), was sent to be a squire to Lord Redwyne on the Arbor. In 131 AC, Lord Redwyne along with Lords Beesbury and Costayne escorted the High Septon to King's Landing for the wedding of King Aegon_III_Targaryen and Queen Jaehaera_Targaryen. On the trip back to the Reach, they were joined by Lords Rowan and Tarly. In 133 AC, Lord Lyonel_Hightower promised Lord Alyn_Velaryon that the Hightowers would supply twenty warships and that Lord Redwyne would supply thirty. Redwyne's ships were slow to show, however, and the impatient Lord Alyn departed Oldtown without them. After the death of Queen Jaehaera, some of Lord Redwyne's relatives were proposed by Lady Samantha_Tarly as a potential new bride for King Aegon_III_Targaryen.

name: Reek
bio: Reek is a man-at-arms in service to House Bolton. His real name may be Heke. According to Lord Roose_Bolton, Reek always smelled appalling, apparently born with that condition.[N 1] To try to hide the smell, Reek bathed three times a day and wore flowers in his hair, but nothing helped. Reek once doused himself with perfume stolen from Roose's second wife, Bethany_Bolton. When he was caught and whipped, even his blood smelled bad, according to Roose. Reek tried it again a year later, this time almost dying from drinking the perfume. Although Maester Uthor diagnosed the stench as the result of some sickness, Reek was otherwise healthy and strong. No one in the Dreadfort could stand to be near him, so he slept in a pig sty. Roose fathered a bastard son, Ramsay_Snow, on a Miller's_wife after hanging her husband. When the widow came to the Dreadfort from the mill by the Weeping Water and asked for a servant to help her raise her unruly and wild son, Roose gave her Reek as a jape. However, Ramsay and Reek became inseparable. According to Roose, Reek never had any training in arms, though he was Ramsay's tutor in martial prowess, which explains Ramsay's vicious and overly aggressive style of swordsmanship. Reek stayed with Ramsay, even after Ramsay was brought to the Dreadfort in 297 AC and was made Roose's unofficial heir. From that time on, Ramsay and Reek became infamous in the region for the "hunts" they undertook together. They caught peasant girls, then set them free and told them to run for their lives. The pair chased after the girls until they caught them, with Ramsay raping and killing the girls, then Reek having his way with the dead bodies while they were still warm. Only a few of the girls escaped.

name: Regenard Estren
bio: Regenard Estren is the Lord of Wyndhall and the head of House Estren.

name: Regis Groves
bio: Regis Groves was a knight from House Groves. He became a Kingsguard during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Ser Regis supported the blacks during the Dance of the Dragons. King Aegon_III_Targaryen appointed him and four other blacks to the Kingsguard in 131 AC after the civil war ended. Harrenhal had fallen under the sway of an alleged "witch queen", Alys_Rivers, after the Battle Above the Gods Eye in 130 AC. Since broken men, outlaws, and robber knights preyed upon the countryside, Ser Tyland_Lannister, the Hand of the King, tasked Regis with reclaiming Harrenhal in 132 AC. Regis marched with half a hundred men to Castle Darry, where he was joined by a like number under Ser Damon_Darry. When the king's men arrived at Harrenhal they found the gates barred and six hundred people within, a third of them warriors. Alys claimed to be the widow of Prince Aemond_Targaryen, who had died during the Dance, and that Alys_Rivers's_son with her was her trueborn son by Aemond. Alys commanded Regis to kneel to her son and swear his sword to the rightful king, but Regis laughed at her and refused to do so. Some say the sorceress raised a hand and caused Regis's skull to burst, while others state Regis was killed by a crossbow bolt through his eye. The fool Mushroom, who was not present, claims it was a lead ball from a sling. Alys's riders charged out through Harrenhal's gates and defeated the king's men in a bloody rout, with Damon leading the survivors back to Darry. Regis's death disturbed Tyland, and Aegon's regents agreed that a larger army should be gathered to reclaim Harrenhal.

name: Regnar Drumm
bio: Regnar Drumm, known as Raven-feeder, was a High King of the Iron Islands from House Drumm. Erich_I_Greyiron seized the crown of his father, Urras_Greyiron, upon Ironfoot's death and declared himself High King of the Iron Islands. Galon_Whitestaff commanded that kings may only be made by kingsmoot, however, and the ironborn captains and kings cast Erich down from power. Regnar, the rock king of Old Wyk, was chosen the new High King at the kingsmoot.

name: Rego Draz
bio: Rego Draz was a merchant-trader and money-changer from Pentos. He became the master of coin in King's Landing during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen after the king reached his majority. Rego started out with nothing, but rose to become the richest man in Pentos. However, due to his low birth he was largely shunned by the other wealthy Pentoshi, and denied a seat on the city's council of magisters. Because he had enough of the scorn of the other Pentoshi, in 50 AC Rego was glad to accept the offer of the Westerosi King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen to come to King's Landing and become the new master of coin on the king's small council. Rego moved to King's Landing with his family and fortune, and was granted the title of lord by Jaehaerys. As he was a lord without lands, castle or sworn men, some wit japed he was the "Lord of Air", which amused Rego. Although offered residence within the Red Keep on many occasions, Rego chose to live in a manse on the Street of Silk, below the Dragonpit. There, he regaled his concubines without having to deal with the court's condemnation of his activities. Rego excelled as master of coin, and was one of the key men responsible for rebuilding the realm's economy after the wars of King Maegor_I_Targaryen had left the crown severely in debt. He reached out to the Iron Bank of Braavos, and its rivals in Tyrosh and Myr, arranging three substantial loans. With these loans, the construction of the Dragonpit could finally continue. Together with King Jaehaerys, Rego abolished the deeply unpopular trade taxes of Lord Edwell_Celtigar, who had been the previous master of coin. In their place, they instituted a series of luxury taxes on expensive foreign imports, as well as on castle crenellations. However, Draz quickly grew to be as unpopular as his predecessor had been, on account of his alleged corruption, his religion, his ancestry, his wealth, and his foreign origins. When the taxes on silk, spice, and crenellations proved to be insufficient to cover all of the crown's expenses, Lord Rego imposed a new levy: a gate fee, required of passing the gates of the city, with additional fees for animals, wagons, and carts. After Elissa_Farman stole three dragon eggs from House Targaryen and fled Dragonstone in 54 AC, Rego aided Grand Maester Benifer by reaching out to his agents across the narrow sea for any news on the eggs, dragons, or Elissa herself, to no avail. That same year, Rego suggested bribing some of the Most Devout to assure that the new High Septon elected would be favorable for the Iron Throne, but the suggestion was rejected by the king. When several mysterious deaths on Dragonstone were put before the small council, Rego read Maester Anselm's accounts of the illness, and declared it to be no sickness but caused by the tears of Lys, a poison. After Lord Daemon_Velaryon_(son_of_Aethan) stepped down as Hand of the King (after his niece Lianna_Velaryon was murdered on Dragonstone), King Jaehaerys initially looked to members of his small council for his replacement. However, he knew that despite Rego's qualifications, his low birth and foreign origin made him unsuitable. Near the end of 54 AC, when Princess Aerea_Targaryen disappeared with the dragon Balerion, Rego's agents were reassigned to find them, but not even a trace of information was found. Rego was entertaining a prostitute at his manse the day that Aerea and Balerion returned to King's Landing in 56 AC. In 57 AC, after Septon Barth returned from Braavos with a deal from the Iron Bank to forgive the crown's loans, Rego calculated that the coin could now be used for King Jaehaerys's project to improve King's Landing. However, Rego was reluctant to pay for Barth and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen's project to dig wells and build fountains for drinking water, until Alysanne served him a tankard of foul water from the Blackwater Rush and dared him to drink it. During the harsh winter of 59 AC and the ensuing plague of the Shivers, famine set in throughout the Seven Kingdoms. So many of the City Watch of King's Landing had died of the sickness that the city became lawless outside of the Red Keep. One day, as he was traveling in his palanquin through Flea Bottom, Rego came upon a starving mob who became enraged at the sight of him. They dragged him from his palanquin and killed him, blaming him (as a foreigner) for bringing the plague to Westeros. Rego's head was bashed in using a cobblestone from one of the new streets he himself had helped pay for, and his fingers were cut off to take his jewels. Learning of Rego's death, an enraged King Jaehaerys went forth into the city with what few guards were left, found the men responsible, and had them hung from the walls of the castle with their entrails hanging out.

name: Renfred Rykker
bio: Renfred Rykker is the Lord of Duskendale and the head of House Rykker, a noble house of the crownlands.

name: Renly Baratheon
bio: Renly Baratheon is the Lord of Storm's End and Lord Paramount of the Stormlands. The younger brother of King Robert_I_Baratheon and Lord Stannis_Baratheon, Renly serves as master of laws on Robert's small council. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Renly is portrayed by Gethin Anthony. Renly was born in 277 AC at Storm's End, the youngest of the three sons of Lord Steffon_Baratheon and Lady Cassana_Estermont. Less than a year later, King Aerys_II_Targaryen sent his cousin Steffon to Volantis to find a bride of Valyrian blood for Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen. Steffon was unable to do so, although he and Cassana did send a message to Storm's End saying that they had found a very talented fool. On their return journey from the Free Cities in 278 AC, Steffon and Cassana's ship, the Windproud, was caught in a severe storm and sank in Shipbreaker Bay, with no survivors except the fool, Patchface. Renly grew up at Storm's End. Young Renly loved bright colors and fabrics. He loved games as well, and would run laughing through the halls of Storm's End, pretending to be a dragon or a wizard or the rain god. He was still a young boy at the time of his eldest brother Robert_I_Baratheon's rebellion against King Aerys in 282–283 AC. Renly and his older brother Stannis_Baratheon spent the war under siege in Storm's End. Renly was witness to the desperation of the starving garrison, while Lord Mace_Tyrell and his bannermen feasted within sight of the walls. In 285 AC or 286 AC, King Robert_I_Baratheon granted his brother Stannis the lordship of Dragonstone, traditionally the holding of the king's heir, and granted his youngest brother Renly the seat of Storm's End and named him Lord Paramount of the Stormlands. This upset Stannis, who felt that since he was the elder of Robert's younger brothers, Storm's End was his by right. When Renly turned sixteen in 293 AC, he made a lord's progress across the stormlands to mark his coming of age. During this progress, he visited the isle of Tarth, where Renly courteously danced with Brienne of Tarth, and she fell in love with him. Renly also visited Sunspear once; Princess Arianne_Martell recalls that she had tried her best to seduce him, but remembers that Renly had seemed more bemused than inflamed by her attempts. During his time as Lord of Storm's End, Renly took Loras_Tyrell as his squire. Eventually, Renly and Loras became lovers. Although it is not widely known, various others at court are aware of their forbidden relationship. Renly was eventually named to the small council, where he occupies the position of master of laws. While he is at court, his castellan at Storm's End is Ser Cortnay_Penrose, who also has been given the charge of raising King Robert's bastard son, Edric_Storm. Brella runs Renly's household in King's Landing for him. Renly has thirty men in his personal guard at the capital.

name: Renly Norcross
bio: Renly Norcross is the second son of Ser Hosman_Norcross and lady Delena_Florent. He is the younger brother of Alester_Norcross, and a half-brother of Edric_Storm.

name: Rennifer Crabb
bio: Rennifer Crabb was a knight from House Crabb during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. In 131 AC, at the end of the Dance, Ser Rennifer accompanied Lord Leowyn_Corbray, Lord Manfryd_Mooton, and Lord Brune when they marched to King's Landing from Maidenpool.

name: Rennifer Longwaters
bio: Rennifer Longwaters is the chief undergaoler of the dungeons beneath the Red Keep. He is descended from Ser Jon_Waters, the bastard son of Princess Elaena_Targaryen and Lord Alyn_Velaryon. Rennifer first came to work in the Red Keep in 288 AC.

name: Reynard Reyne
bio: Reynard Reyne was a knight from House Reyne and the younger son of Lord Robert_Reyne. Reynard had two older sibings, Roger_Reyne and Ellyn_Reyne. Reynard and his elder brother, Lord Roger_Reyne, were often at Casterly Rock once their sister Ellyn_Reyne married Tion_Lannister, the son of Lord Gerold_Lannister and heir to the Rock. The brothers returned to Castamere after Tion's death, however, as Gerold began to groom Tytos_Lannister for leadership. Ellyn was later rewed to Walderan_Tarbeck, Lord of Tarbeck Hall. Ca. 255 AC, House Tarbeck angered King Aegon_V_Targaryen through their territorial demands, according to a semi-canon source. Lord Tytos sent Lord Denys Marbrand to capture the Tarbecks, but Roger came to his sister's aid and his soldiers slew the Marbrand men in the night before they reached Tarbeck Hall. Reynard convinced Tytos that the attack was a misunderstanding, claiming that Roger was unaware of Denys's identity and thought he was attacking outlaws. After Reynard offered apologies and a blood price to House Marbrand, Tytos pardoned Roger Reyne, and Lord and Lady Tarbeck as well. After the War of the Ninepenny Kings, Tytos's heir, Ser Tywin_Lannister, attempted to bring order to the westerlands. During the ensuing crisis, Reynard oversaw the safe exchange of hostages held by the Lannisters and the Tarbecks at Roger's seat, Castamere. During the Reyne-Tarbeck rebellion, Tywin extinguished the Tarbecks by killing Lord Walderan in the field and burning Tarbeck Hall. The outnumbered army of House Reyne then attacked Tywin's host, but were forced to flee after the Lannisters withstood the charge. Reynard took command of the Reynes since Roger became feverish after a crossbow wound and was no longer capable of leading. Reynard abandoned the surface fortifications of Castamere and led the survivors into the mines of the Reynes. According to a semi-canon source, Reynard sent terms to Tywin offering peace if Tytos's other sons were sent into subterranean Castamere as hostages. Tywin disregarded the offer and instead ordered his miners to block Castamere's entrances with stone. The Lannisters then diverted a local stream into an entrance, flooding Castamere and drowning all within.

name: Reynard Webber
bio: Reynard Webber was the Lord of Coldmoat and the head of House Webber during the middle of the second century. Lord Reynard was the lord of Coldmoat during the time of Ser Eustace_Osgrey's father. When Eustace Osgrey's father took him to call on old Reynard Webber, he showed his son the chequy lion of Osgrey carved above the gates of the castle. His relation to Lord Wyman_Webber is unknown.

name: Lord Reyne
bio: Lord Reyne was the Lord of Castamere and head of House Reyne during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. Lord Reyne declared for the greens during the Dance of the Dragons. He was part of the westermen host led by Lord Jason_Lannister then by Ser Adrian_Tarbeck and Lord Humfrey_Lefford. He was one of the two thousand men who died in the battle by the Lakeshore.

name: Reysen
bio: Reysen is one of the recruits for the Night’s Watch found by Yoren and is part of the band marching north to the Wall.

name: Reznak mo Reznak
bio: Reznak mo Reznak is the Ghiscari seneschal of the city of Meereen.

name: Rhae Targaryen
bio: Princess Rhae Targaryen was a noblewoman of House Targaryen, the second daughter and youngest child of King Maekar_I_Targaryen and Lady Dyanna_Dayne. Rhae was the youngest child of Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen and his wife, Lady Dyanna_Dayne. She had four older brothers, Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I), Aerion_Targaryen, Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) and Aegon_V_Targaryen, and one older sister, Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Maekar_I). Once, in her youth, Rhae gave a love potion to her youngest brother, Aegon_V_Targaryen, so he would marry her instead of Daella, to whom Aegon may have been betrothed.[N 1] Later in life, Rhae was married, and had children.

name: Rhaegar Frey
bio: Rhaegar Frey is a member of House Frey and the second son of Ser Aenys_Frey and Tyana_Wylde, who died in childbirth. Rhaegar is married to Jeyne_Beesbury and has three children with her, Robert_Frey_(son_of_Rhaegar), Walda_Frey_(daughter_of_Rhaegar), and Jonos_Frey.

name: Rhaegar Targaryen
bio: Prince Rhaegar Targaryen was the firstborn son of King Aerys_II_Targaryen and his sister-wife, Queen Rhaella_Targaryen. As heir-apparent, he was the Prince of Dragonstone and crown prince for the Iron Throne. Rhaegar was popular with the smallfolk during his lifetime, but he died in Robert's Rebellion which was triggered in part by his abduction of Lyanna_Stark. He was slain in single combat by Robert_I_Baratheon at the ruby ford during the Battle of the Trident. Rhaegar was the elder brother of the exiled last members of House Targaryen, Prince Viserys_Targaryen and Princess Daenerys_Targaryen. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Rhaegar is portrayed by Wilf Scolding in flashbacks. Prince Rhaegar was the firstborn son of King Aerys_II_Targaryen and Queen Rhaella_Targaryen. He was born at Summerhall in 259 AC, on the same day as the great tragedy there. As a child he read obsessively, to the point that jests were made about his habits. He became a noted warrior later in life, although he did not initially seem inclined to martial habits. However, apparently by something he had read, Rhaegar became motivated to become a warrior. At the age of seventeen, Rhaegar was knighted, and from all reports grew into a skilled and capable fighter. The prince always distinguished himself well at tourneys, although he seldom entered the lists. Unlike warriors such as Robert_I_Baratheon or Jaime_Lannister, Rhaegar was not enthusiastic about fighting. Rhaegar's squires were Myles_Mooton and Richard_Lonmouth, and after he knighted them they remained close companions. Jon_Connington, whom he had squired with, was a good friend to Rhaegar as well. Returning from a trip to Dorne, Rhaegar once visited the Connington seat of Griffin's Roost. His songs brought the castle's women to tears, while Lord Armond_Connington sought House Targaryen's support against rival House Morrigen. Rhaegar's closest and oldest friend, however, was Ser Arthur_Dayne, the Sword of the Morning, trusting him more than Ser Barristan_Selmy. Rhaegar often liked to visit the ruins of Summerhall with only his harp and when he returned he sang songs of such beauty they could reduce women to tears. Although Rhaegar was often dour, private and bookish, Cersei_Lannister noted at the tournament in honor of Viserys's birth in Lannisport in 276 AC that the smallfolk cheered for Lord Tywin_Lannister twice as much as for King Aerys II, but only half as loudly for Tywin as for Rhaegar. Rhaegar fought well in the tourney besting a dozen skilled knights, among them Barristan Selmy, Gerion_Lannister, and Tygett_Lannister but was defeated in the champion's tilt by his friend Arthur Dayne. Aerys refused Tywin's offer at the tourney to betroth Cersei to Rhaegar. Rhaegar was also defeated by Barristan Selmy in the champion's tilt during a tourney at Storm's End. Rhaegar was born at a time when the Targaryens had declined, and once he came of age, there was no sister, or anyone else of their bloodline, available for him to marry. King Aerys sent his first cousin, Lord Steffon_Baratheon, to seek a bride for Rhaegar. Despite Valyrian blood still being present in Essos, Steffon could not find appropriate females of noble-enough birth for Rhaegar to wed. After this in early 279 AC, Rhaegar was formally betrothed to the Dornish princess, Elia_Martell, the younger sister of Doran_Martell, Prince of Dorne. They married the following year in 280 AC, a lavish ceremony was held at the Great Sept of Baelor. His father did not attend the wedding as he was paranoid about an assassination attempt and nor did he permit the young Prince Viserys_Targaryen to attend. Rhaegar and his father's relationship was straining at this point, and he and his new bride took up residence on Dragonstone instead of King's Landing. Rhaegar and Elia had their first child, a girl named Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar), in 280 AC. When the babe was presented at court, Rhaegar's mother, Queen Rhaella, embraced her grandchild warmly while King Aerys remarked: "she smells Dornish". The relationship between Aerys and Rhaegar became more and more estranged. Elia and Rhaegar had a son they named Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar). Elia, due to her delicate health, was bed-ridden for half a year after giving birth to Rhaenys and nearly died giving birth to Aegon, after which the maesters told Rhaegar she would be unable to have any more children. Maester Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I), whom Rhaegar corresponded with via raven messages, remembers that Rhaegar believed his child Aegon to be the prince that was promised. When Lord Tywin resigned his position as Hand of the King and left court, the new focus of King Aerys's mistrust and paranoia was his own son and heir, Prince Rhaegar. At court, there was growing tension between factions loyal to the king and to the prince. Grand Maester Pycelle dispatched a letter to the Citadel, writing that tensions and division at court strongly resembled those before the Dance of the Dragons. Pycelle was fearful a civil war would break out unless some accord could be reached that would satisfy both factions.

name: Rhaegel Targaryen
bio: Prince Rhaegel Targaryen was the third son of King Daeron_II_Targaryen and Queen Myriah_Martell. Like his brothers, Prince Rhaegel learned how to fight from Ser Quentyn_Ball, the master-at-arms of the Red Keep, but he lacked martial prowess. Rhaegel was married to Lady Alys_Arryn_(wife_of_Rhaegel) and had three children: the twins Aelor_Targaryen and Aelora_Targaryen and daughter Daenora_Targaryen. He and his brother Aerys seldom left the court, leading their father to pass the castle of Summerhall instead to their youngest brother, Maekar. Rhaegel became Prince of Dragonstone in 209 AC, when his older brother Prince Aerys_I_Targaryen ascended the Iron Throne, as Aerys had no children of his own. Despite his position as the heir and the common Targaryen practice of naming close blood relations as Hand of the King, Rhaegel was seemingly not in contention for the Handship when Aerys took the throne, for when Aerys named Brynden_Rivers as Hand, their younger brother Maekar quarreled bitterly as he had expected the position to be granted to him. Some even mistook Maekar as Aerys's true heir, forgetting both Rhaegel and his children. Those in the realm who mistrusted Bloodraven as Aerys's Hand and felt he held an inordinate amount of power considered Rhaegel impotent to oppose him on account of his meek nature and madness. Such individuals even suggested that Rhaegel and his children were at risk of being killed by Bloodraven or his brother Maekar, though the claim infuriated Maekar's son, Aegon. Rhaegel died in 215 AC, whilst choking on a lamprey pie. His son, Prince Aelor, the new Prince of Dragonstone, would not long outlive him, and his daughter Aelora's death by suicide would soon follow.

name: Rhaego
bio: Rhaego is the son of Daenerys_Targaryen and Khal Drogo. He was named after his late uncle, Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Rhaego is portrayed by Wren Ros Elliot-Sloan.

name: Rhaella Targaryen
bio: Queen Rhaella Targaryen was the sister, wife, and queen of King Aerys_II_Targaryen and the only daughter of King Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen and his sister-wife, Queen Shaera_Targaryen. Her grandfather was King Aegon_V_Targaryen. Rhaella was the mother of Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen, Prince Viserys_Targaryen, and Princess Daenerys_Targaryen. She died shortly after Daenerys' birth. Rhaella was born in either 245 AC, 246 AC or 247 AC to Prince Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen and Princess Shaera_Targaryen. She had an older brother, Aerys_II_Targaryen. According to Ser Barristan_Selmy, Rhaella had been in love with Ser Bonifer_Hasty, a young landed knight from the stormlands, during her youth. Bonifer once named her his queen of love and beauty. Their affair was a short one, however, as Bonifer was of too low birth to ever be seriously considered a suitor for Rhaella. Rhaella was married at a young age to her older brother, Aerys_II_Targaryen, on the command of their father, Prince Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen. Jaehaerys had been influenced by a Ghost_of_High_Heart brought to court by Jenny_of_Oldstones. The woods witch prophesied that the prince that was promised would be born from Aerys's and Rhaella's line, making Jaehaerys determined to marry his daughter to his son. King Aegon_V_Targaryen, whilst frustrated with his son's decision, allowed Jaehaerys to do as he wished. According to Barristan, there was no fondness between Aerys and Rhaella on the day of their wedding. As a princess, and eventual queen, Rhaella had multiple ladies in waiting. At some point, they included the mother of Prince Doran_Martell, and, from 259 AC to 263 AC, Lady Joanna_Lannister from Casterly Rock. In 259 AC, whilst present at Summerhall, Rhaella gave birth to the first of her children, Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen, whilst the tragedy of Summerhall raged. During the tragedy, Rhaella's grandfather, King Aegon_V_Targaryen, died, leading to her own father, ascending the throne as Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen. Following Jaehaerys's own death, in 262 AC, Rhaella's brother and husband ascended the throne as King Aerys_II_Targaryen, and Rhaella became queen. Following Rhaegar's birth, Rhaella and Aerys had trouble where childbirth was concerned. In the seventeen years following Rhaegar's birth, Rhaella went through multiple pregnancies, stillbirths and miscarriages: While at first, her brother comforted Rhaella in her grief, the multiple stillbirths, miscarriages and dead children eventually caused Aerys to become suspicious. He accused her of having been unfaithful, and claimed that the dead children had been bastards. Rhaella was confined to Maegor's Holdfast (roughly around 270 AC), and Aerys decreed that two septas would sleep in Rhaella's bed every night, to ensure she would remain faithful. Despite Aerys's accusations against Rhaella, it was actually he who was unfaithful to her. The king kept many mistresses, both before and after 270 AC. Rhaella was willing to look the other way for most such indiscretions, but she greatly disapproved whenever her husband turned his eye towards her ladies. On multiple occasions, women were abruptly dismissed from her service following incidents with the king. Notably, Lady Joanna Lannister was dismissed shortly after Aerys made suggestive comments and took liberties with her person at Joanna's wedding to Tywin_Lannister. The death of Prince Jaehaerys in 274 AC shifted Aerys' suspicions to others. He first blamed Jaehaerys's wet nurse, and had her beheaded in a black rage. He later became convinced that Jaehaerys had been poisoned by his mistress, and had the girl and her family tortured to death. Aerys then fasted for a fortnight, made a walk of repentance across the city to the Great Sept of Baelor, and prayed with the High Septon. Upon his return, he declared that he would only sleep with Rhaella, his wife, from that day forth. As far as is known, Aerys remained true to this vow, no longer taking any mistress from 275 AC forth. In 276 AC, Rhaella gave birth to Prince Viserys_Targaryen. While he was small and robust, he was healthy. However, Aerys feared that Viserys would die like his brothers, and so tasked the Kingsguard to watch over the infant. Even Rhaella was forbidden to be alone with her son. Aerys insisted that his food taster suckle at the breasts of the wet nurse, to be certain that there was no poison on her nipples. When Lord Tywin Lannister, Hand of the King, held a tournament in honor of Viserys's birth at Lannisport, Rhaella was ordered to remain at King's Landing with Viserys. Following their cousin Steffon_Baratheon's death at sea in 278 AC, Aerys's paranoia and madness deepened. No longer trusting his wife or elder son and convinced they may be conspiring with the lords and smallfolk against him, Aerys recruited the eunuch Varys to act as his spymaster. In 281 AC, during the tourney at Harrenhal, Rhaella again remained in the capital with Viserys. In the later years of the marriage of Aerys and Rhaella, the Mad King's insanity set in. Never in love or even especially fond of each other, she and Aerys grew even more distant because of his increasingly erratic and violent behavior. He would treat her with greater and greater cruelty as his madness deepened. According to Ser Barristan_Selmy, Rhaella did her best to protect Viserys from the worst of Aerys's excesses, keeping her child oblivious to the fact his father was sliding deeper into madness. Despite Rhaella's efforts, Selmy believed that Viserys showed signs of the same madness that afflicted his father. According to Ser Jaime_Lannister, Rhaella's eyes had been closed for years by the time Aerys ordered caches of wildfire be placed all over King's Landing. In the final years of Aerys' reign, relations between king and queen were strained. The couple slept apart and both did their best to avoid each other during the day. However, Aerys would inevitably seek her out every night after he executed someone by burning. He grew sexually abusive and violent toward Rhaella, aroused by the fire and death. The last time was during Robert's Rebellion after Aerys burned his then-Hand, Lord Qarlton_Chelsted, for opposing the Mad King's wildfire plot. Jaime recalls listening outside Rhaella's bedchamber as she cried as the king raped her; when Jaime protested that the Kingsguard were sworn to defend the queen as well, his sworn brother Ser Jonothor_Darry replied, "We are, but not from him."

name: Rhaella Targaryen
bio: Septa Rhaella, born Rhaella Targaryen, was a daughter of Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) and Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). She had an older twin sister, Aerea_Targaryen.

name: Rhaelle Targaryen
bio: Princess Rhaelle Targaryen was the youngest child of King Aegon_V_Targaryen and Queen Betha_Blackwood. She was married to Lord Ormund_Baratheon of Storm's End. In 237 AC, Rhaelle's four elder siblings had been betrothed: Prince Duncan_Targaryen to the daughter of Lord Lyonel_Baratheon, Prince Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen to Celia_Tully, Princess Shaera_Targaryen to Luthor_Tyrell, and Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon_V) to Olenna_Redwyne. It was Rhaelle's eldest brother, Duncan, who broke his betrothal first, by marrying Jenny_of_Oldstones in 239 AC. This greatly angered Lord Lyonel, who rebelled against his king. A trial by combat, and the word of King Aegon_V_Targaryen ended Lord Lyonel's rebellion, with an end result a betrothal, of Rhaelle to Lord Lyonel's son and heir, Ormund_Baratheon. To seal the bargain, Rhaelle was sent to Storm's End, where she would serve as a cupbearer, and be a companion to Lord Lyonel's wife. In 245 AC, the promise of King Aegon V was fulfilled, and Rhaelle and Ormund were wed. Their son, Steffon_Baratheon, was born the next year. Rhaelle's marriage to Ormund would eventually be part of the end of the rule of House Targaryen. Rhaelle's grandchildren through Steffon would be Robert, Stannis_Baratheon and Renly_Baratheon. It would be young Lord Robert, who would eventually lead the rebellion against Rhaelle's nephew, King Aerys_II_Targaryen.

name: Rhaena Targaryen
bio: Rhaena Targaryen was the eldest of the six children of King Aenys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alyssa_Velaryon. She was a dragonrider, and rode the dragon Dreamfyre. Rhaena was forcefully married to her uncle, King Maegor_I_Targaryen, who had usurped and killed her husband Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I), becoming one of his Black Brides. Later, in the reign of her brother, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, Rhaena was called the Queen in the West during the time she spent in the westerlands, and the Queen in the East during the years she held Dragonstone. In 23 AC, Rhaena was born at Dragonstone to Prince Aenys_I_Targaryen and Lady Alyssa_Velaryon. She was named in honor of Aenys's late mother, Queen Rhaenys_Targaryen. Rhaena was the first grandchild of King Aegon_I_Targaryen, and it is said that he wept the first time he held her. Following Rhaena's birth, many wondered if her uncle Prince Maegor_I_Targaryen would fall behind her in the line of succession, as she was the eldest (and at the time, only) child of Aenys, the heir to the throne. That year,[N 1] Maegor's mother, Queen Visenya_Targaryen, proposed a betrothal between her son and Rhaena in order to settle the succession issue. However, both Rhaena's parents and the High_Septon_(Aegon_I) protested the match. The High Septon instead suggested his niece, Lady Ceryse_Hightower, as a bride for Maegor. As a child, Rhaena was shy, preferring the presence of animals instead of other people. At age of nine, Rhaena was presented with a dragon hatchling. She bonded instantly with the dragon, whom she named Dreamfyre. At the age of twelve, Rhaena flew upon Dreamfyre for the first time. Shortly thereafter, Rhaena became good friends with her cousin Larissa_Velaryon. The two girls were inseparable, until Larissa was suddenly sent back to Driftmark to marry the second son of the Evenstar of Tarth. Rhaena soon befriended Samantha_Stokeworth, the daughter of Lord Alyn_Stokeworth, the Hand of the King. Rhaena would go on to have a number of favorites, all of whom she would shower with affection and attention to such extents that it was cause for whispering at the court. Rhaena and her next-eldest brother Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) were close during their childhood, and Rhaena would sometimes take him with her to fly upon Dreamfyre. Rhaena doted on her siblings, and it was said that she placed a dragon egg in the cradles of her brother Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and sister Alysanne_Targaryen, from which their dragons Vermithor and Silverwing hatched. Although she would sometimes take others flying with her upon her dragon, Rhaena most often flew Dreamfyre by herself.

name: Rhaena Targaryen
bio: Lady Rhaena Targaryen, also known as Rhaena of Pentos and Rhaena Corbray, was one of the twin daughters of Prince Daemon_Targaryen and Lady Laena_Velaryon; Baela_Targaryen was her older twin. Rhaena was the niece of King Viserys_I_Targaryen and the cousin of King Aegon_II_Targaryen. She was also the half-sister of Kings Aegon_III_Targaryen and Viserys_II_Targaryen, as well as the stillborn Visenya_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon). Rhaena was a dragonrider who rode Morning. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Rhaena is portrayed by Phoebe Campbell and Eva Ossei-Gerning (young). When preparations were being made in 136 AC for a royal progress to be held after Aegon turned sixteen, Rhaena desired to accompany the king on Morning. However, this suggestion was delicately deflected by the council of regents. Later in life, Rhaena married Garmund_Hightower, the younger brother of Lord Lyonel_Hightower, by whom she had six daughters.

name: Rhaena Targaryen
bio: Septa Rhaena, born Rhaena Targaryen, was the second daughter of King Aegon_III_Targaryen and his second wife, Queen Daenaera_Velaryon. Born in 147 AC, Rhaena was the second daughter of King Aegon_III_Targaryen and Queen Daenaera_Velaryon. She had two older brothers, Daeron_I_Targaryen and Baelor_I_Targaryen, and two sisters, Daena_Targaryen, who was older, and Elaena_Targaryen, who was younger. During the reign of their brother Baelor, Rhaena and her sisters were confined in the keep within the Red Keep that became known as the Maidenvault. According to Baelor, this was done to preserve their innocence from the wickedness of the world and the lusts of impious men, but many suspected he feared that he himself would be tempted to sin by the sight of their beauty. Rhaena, unlike her sisters, never chafed at her confinement. She was almost as pious as Baelor, and she eventually became a septa. After the death of Baelor, Daena was briefly considered as a possible heir to the throne. However, the memories of the Dance of the Dragons and of Rhaenyra_Targaryen, the last woman to sit the Iron Throne, left many leery of the idea of a ruling queen. The claims of Rhaena and her sisters were set aside, and Rhaena's uncle was crowned King Viserys_II_Targaryen.

name: Rhaenyra Targaryen
bio: Rhaenyra Targaryen, cheered as the Realm's Delight when she was young, and later called the Half-Year Queen, was the first-born child of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. Rhaenyra was King Viserys I's only living child by his first wife, Queen Aemma_Arryn of House Arryn. At the age of eight, she was made her father's heir, and grew up expecting to become the first ruling Queen of Westeros. Rhaenyra was a dragonrider whose mount was Syrax. Her claim to the Iron Throne was challenged by her younger half-brother, Aegon_II_Targaryen, sparking the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. She was the mother of two kings, Aegon_III_Targaryen and Viserys_II_Targaryen. Rhaenyra's personal sigil was the standard Targaryen heraldry (a three-headed red dragon on a black field), quartered with the moon-and-falcon sigil of House Arryn (for her mother Aemma Arryn), and the silver seahorse on sea green of House Velaryon (for her first husband Laenor_Velaryon, as well as for the Velaryons in general, who were among her major supporters). During the Dance, her faction used her personal heraldry as their battle-flag. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Rhaenyra is portrayed by Emma D'Arcy and Milly Alcock (young). Born in 97 AC, Rhaenyra was the only living child of King Viserys_I_Targaryen by his first wife, Lady Aemma_Arryn of House Arryn.  Rhaenyra had two brothers, both of whom died in the cradle. Rhaenyra became a dragonrider at the age of seven, when she flew into the sky on the back of a young dragon, whom she named Syrax. A year later, when she was eight, Rhaenyra became a cupbearer for her father, serving him at the table, at tourneys, and at court. From that point onward, King Viserys was seldom seen without Rhaenyra. At a young age, Rhaenyra became enamored with her uncle, Prince Daemon_Targaryen. The roguish prince often brought her exotic gifts from his trips across the narrow sea. During the tourney for King Viserys I's accession, Ser Criston_Cole won the melee by disarming Daemon. The knight granted the victor's laurel to Rhaenyra and asked the seven-year-old girl for her favor in the lists. Rhaenyra became smitten with Criston when he joined the Kingsguard in 105 AC, calling him her white knight, and Viserys allowed the charming knight to become Rhaenyra's personal shield and protector. From that moment on, Cole always wore Rhaenyra's favor in the lists and protected the girl during public events. Late in 105 AC, Rhaenyra's mother, Aemma, died birthing Rhaenyra's second brother, Prince Baelon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I), who died a day later. King Viserys then proclaimed Rhaenyra his heir, having all the lords of the Seven Kingdoms swear fealty to her, promising to honor and defend her rights of succession. In addition, Rhaenyra was named Princess of Dragonstone. King Viserys remarried in 106 AC, to Lady Alicent_Hightower, and stepmother and stepdaughter initially got along well. That changed when Alicent birthed the king two sons, Aegon_II_Targaryen in 107 AC and Aemond_Targaryen in 110 AC, yet Viserys did not proclaim either boy his heir. People continued to ask Viserys about the succession, though Viserys was unwilling to discuss it. Ser Otto_Hightower, Alicent's father and Viserys's Hand, was sent away from court for pushing the issue too much.  But even after he left, clearly two parties formed at court: the party of the Queen, and the party of the Princess. In 111 AC, a great tourney was held at King's Landing on the fifth anniversary of King Viserys's marriage to Queen Alicent. At the opening feast, the queen wore a green gown, whilst Princess Rhaenyra dressed dramatically in Targaryen red and black. Note was taken, and thereafter it became the custom to refer to “greens” and “blacks” when talking of the queen's party and the party of the princess, respectively. The blacks had greater success in the tourney as Criston, wearing Rhaenyra's favor, unhorsed all of the queen's champions, including two of her cousins and her youngest brother, Ser Gwayne_Hightower. According to the accounts of Septon Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons), prior to her turning sixteen, Rhaenyra lost her virginity to her uncle, Prince Daemon, after he seduced her. The account of the fool Mushroom is more scandalous, however, claiming that Daemon gave Rhaenyra lessons on how to seduce and please a man, lessons in which Mushroom insists he took part as well. Mushroon further claimed that Daemon secretly smuggled Rhaenyra from her rooms dressed as a page boy, and took her to brothels on the Street of Silk, where she could observe the act of love and learn the "womanly arts" from prostitutes. Mushroom's account differs from Eustace's account, in that Mushroom claims Rhaenyra remained a virgin during these lessons. According to Mushroom, Rhaenyra received those lessons from Daemon because she was in love with Criston Cole, and she wanted him to see her as a woman. Whichever account is true, (Grand Maester Runciter simply claims that the two brothers quarreled), King Viserys sent his brother into exile.

name: Rhaenys Targaryen
bio: Queen Rhaenys Targaryen was the youngest sister of King Aegon_I_Targaryen, the first Lord of the Seven Kingdoms. Both she and her older sister Visenya_Targaryen were married to Aegon, and it was said that she was Aegon's favorite. Through her son with Aegon, Aenys_I_Targaryen, the line of the dragonkings continued, with fifteen of the sixteen Targaryen kings being direct descendants of Rhaenys and Aegon. She was a dragonrider who rode the dragon Meraxes. Rhaenys was born on the island Dragonstone to Aerion_Targaryen_(son_of_Daemion), Lord of Dragonstone, and his wife, Lady Valaena_Velaryon. She had two older siblings: Visenya_Targaryen and Aegon_I_Targaryen. Although according to Valyrian marriage customs Aegon was expected to marry his eldest sister, Visenya, he took both his sisters to wife. People claimed that Aegon wed Visenya out of duty and Rhaenys out of desire. Prior to her wedding, Rhaenys became a dragonrider, having bonded with the dragon Meraxes.

name: Rhaenys Targaryen
bio: Princess Rhaenys Targaryen, known as the Queen Who Never Was, was the daughter of Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I) and Lady Jocelyn_Baratheon. She was married to Lord Corlys_Velaryon of Driftmark. She was a dragonrider whose dragon was Meleys, the Red Queen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Rhaenys is portrayed by Eve Best. Rhaenys was born in 74 AC on the seventh day of the seventh moon of the year. As her father was the Prince of Dragonstone, and she was his firstborn child, many considered her as being next in line for the Iron Throne, after her father Aemon. Queen Alysanne_Targaryen, when being presented with her granddaughter, called her "our queen to be". Rhaenys became a dragonrider in 87 AC when she claimed the dragon Meleys, that had been ridden by her late aunt Alyssa_Targaryen. In 89 AC, Rhaenys joined King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen for part of his royal progress. She flew on Meleys to Highgarden to meet up with Jaehaerys, and together they flew to all four of the Shield Islands. On Greenshield she informed the king about her intention to marry Lord Corlys_Velaryon, and received her grandfather's blessing. Rhaenys married Corlys in 90 AC at the age of sixteen. She insisted on arriving at the wedding on the back of Meleys. In early 92 AC, Rhaenys learned that she was pregnant with her first child.

name: Rhaenys Targaryen
bio: Rhaenys Targaryen was the daughter of Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen and Princess Elia_Martell. She was murdered in the Sack of King's Landing when she was only a toddler. In 280 AC, Rhaenys was born on Dragonstone. After her birth, her Elia_Martell was bedridden for half a year. Rhaenys was presented at court by her father, Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen. Whilst her grandmother, Queen Rhaella_Targaryen, embraced her warmly, her grandfather, King Aerys_II_Targaryen, refused to touch or hold her, claiming she "smelled Dornish". Rhaenys had a small black kitten that she named Balerion, after Balerion the Black Dread, the dragon of Aegon_I_Targaryen. Princess Arianne_Martell has been told that she held Rhaenys once, when she was too young to remember. During Robert's Rebellion, Elia, Rhaenys, and her infant brother, Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar), were brought to the Red Keep on orders of King Aerys II Targaryen. In preparation for the Battle of the Trident, Aerys used them as hostages, gracelessly reminding Rhaenys's great-uncle, Prince Lewyn_Martell of the Kingsguard, that the three were in Aerys's hands. Lewyn took command of ten thousand Dornishmen, but died in the battle, as did Rhaenys's father, Rhaegar. Aerys became convinced that the Dornishmen had betrayed him, and refused to allow Rhaenys and her family to flee to Dragonstone alongside Queen Rhaella_Targaryen and Prince Viserys_Targaryen. Rhaenys and her mother and brother were kept at King's Landing as hostages to ensure continued Dornish loyalty. During the Sack of King's Landing, a fortnight after the royal forces had left for the Trident, Ser Jaime_Lannister of the Kingsguard slew King Aerys in the throne room of the Red Keep. Though Rhaegar had left the safety of his family to Jaime, the only Kingsguard member left in the city, Jaime did not believe that his father Tywin_Lannister, whose troops were attacking King's Landing, would harm Elia or her children. However, Tywin commanded the deaths of Rhaenys and Aegon to prove his loyalty to Robert_I_Baratheon. While Jaime was killing Aerys, Tywin's men Ser Gregor_Clegane and Ser Amory_Lorch were scaling the walls of Maegor's Holdfast. Rhaenys hid under her father's bed, a floor above the nursery where her mother and brother were located. While Gregor Clegane slew Aegon and Elia, Rhaenys was discovered and dragged out by Amory Lorch, who proceeded to stab her with "half a hundred" thrusts. Tyrion_Lannister recalls hearing of other men-at-arms present during the murder, but does not know their names. Amory brought Rhaenys's body to Tywin afterwards. Tywin claims to have been shocked at the brutality of the deaths of Elia and her children. In Rhaenys's case, he believed Lorch, who tried to justify his savagery by claiming the girl kicked him and would not stop screaming, could simply have offered a few sweet words to calm her, and a pillow to suffocate her. After Rhaenys and Aegon's deaths, Tywin Lannister presented their bodies to Robert, laying them beneath the Iron Throne wrapped in crimson Lannister cloaks, to better conceal the blood. Rhaenys's body was distinctly recognizable, despite its condition.

name: Rhea Florent
bio: Rhea Florent is the second daughter of Lord Alester_Florent. She is the fourth wife of Leyton_Hightower, Lord of the Hightower. It is unknown if Rhea is the mother of any of Leyton's ten children.

name: Rhea Royce
bio: Rhea Royce was the Lady of Runestone and the head of House Royce during the reigns of kings Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Viserys_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Rhea is portrayed by Rachel Redford.

name: Rhialta Vance
bio: Rhialta Vance is a noblewoman of House Vance of Wayfarer's Rest. She is the second daughter of the heir of Wayfarer's Rest, Ser Karyl_Vance.

name: Rhodry
bio: Rhodry is a maester of the Citadel in service to House Cerwyn.

name: Rhogar Velaryon
bio: Rhogar Velaryon was a knight from House Velaryon. He was a nephew of Lord Corlys_Velaryon by his youngest brother. He had four brothers, including Ser Malentine_Velaryon, and several cousins. In 126 AC, When Rhogar's uncle, Lord Corlys_Velaryon, fell ill, the succession of Driftmark was called into question. Both of Corlys's children had died, and Corlys's eldest grandson stood to inherit the Iron Throne. When Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen suggested Corlys's second grandson, Lucerys_Velaryon, instead, Rhogar's cousin, Ser Vaemond_Velaryon, protested on the grounds that Lucerys and his Velaryon brothers were bastards. After Vaemond was executed at the behest of Princess Rhaenyra, Rhogar and his siblings brought their case before the sick and failing King Viserys_I_Targaryen at King's Landing. However, they made the mistake of questioning the legitimacy of Rhaenyra’s children in front of the king. Viserys had their tongues removed for this insolence, as per his earlier decree. Rhogar and his four brothers were thereafter known as the "silent five". When Lord Corlys died in 132 AC, he was succeeded as Lord of the Tides by his bastard grandson, Alyn_Velaryon. This succession was not uncontested. Rhogar and his only surviving brother, Ser Malentine_Velaryon, yet again took their own claim to the small council in King’s Landing. When the Hand of the King and the regents ruled against them, Rhogar and Malentine refused to accept the decision. As Mushroom put it in his testimony, "lacking tongues with which to make their appeal, they preferred to argue with swords". The plot of the silent brothers to assassinate Alyn failed when the guards at Castle Driftmark proved loyal to the Sea Snake's memory and his chosen heir. Ser Rhogar was captured during the attempt, and his brother was slain. Rhogar was condemned to death, but choose to take the black.

name: Rhogoro
bio: Rhogoro is the son and khalakka of the Dothraki horselord, Khal Moro.

name: Rhonda Rowan
bio: Rhonda Rowan is a noblewoman of House Rowan. She is married to Ser Baelor_Hightower, the heir of House Hightower. Rhonda married Baelor at some time in or after 273 AC.

name: Ricasso
bio: Ricasso is Prince Doran_Martell's seneschal at Sunspear.

name: Richard Farrow
bio: Richard Farrow is a knight of House Farrow.

name: Richard Horpe
bio: Richard Horpe is a knight of House Horpe, in service to Stannis_Baratheon. Richard wanted to join the Kingsguard when he was a squire, but King Robert_I_Baratheon chose another knight after Queen Cersei_Lannister objected to him.

name: Richard Lonmouth
bio: Richard Lonmouth was a knight from House Lonmouth. Richard followed Myles_Mooton in squiring for Rhaegar_Targaryen, Prince of Dragonstone, and each man received his knighthood from Rhaegar when they were ready. Richard and Ser Myles were close friends of Rhaegar and strong supporters of the prince at court, as were Lord Jon_Connington, Prince Lewyn_Martell, and Ser Arthur_Dayne. Richard was present at the tourney at Harrenhal, where he played a drinking game with Robert_I_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End. Halfway through the tourney, he and Robert each promised Rhaegar's father, King Aerys_II_Targaryen, they would find and discover the true identity of the Knight of the Laughing Tree. It is unknown if Richard chose to follow his liege lords, House Baratheon, during Robert's Rebellion, or if he stayed loyal to Prince Rhaegar and House Targaryen.

name: Richard Morrigen
bio: Richard Morrigen is a knight from House Morrigen. He is the brother and heir to Lester_Morrigen, Lord of Crow's Nest.

name: Richard Rodden
bio: Richard Rodden was the head[N 1] of House Rodden during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. Richard was loyal to King Aegon_II_Targaryen and the greens. A member of the Caltrops, he took an arrow during the Second Battle of Tumbleton and died the following day.

name: Richard Roote
bio: Richard Roote was a knight from House Roote during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen who served in his Kingsguard. Richard was chosen by Queen Visenya_Targaryen to be one of the first sworn brothers of the Kingsguard when the order was founded in 10 AC by King Aegon_I_Targaryen.

name: Rickard Karstark
bio: Rickard Karstark is the Lord of Karhold and the head of House Karstark. He has three sons, Harrion_Karstark, Eddard_Karstark, and Torrhen_Karstark, and one daughter, Alys_Karstark. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Rickard is portrayed by Steven Blount (Season 1) and John Stahl (Season 2 and 3). Named after Lord Rickard_Stark. Rickard Karstark fought for his son, Lord Eddard_Stark, when the north rebelled against the Mad King, Aerys_II_Targaryen, during Robert's Rebellion. He participated in the Battle of the Trident. Rickard visited Winterfell with his daughter, Alys_Karstark, when she was six. He expected her to charm Eddard's son Robb_Stark, despite Alys's age, in hopes of arranging a betrothal.

name: Rickard Liddle
bio: Rickard Liddle known as Little Liddle, is the youngest son of Lord Torren_Liddle, known as the Liddle, the clan chief of House Liddle, a mountain clan in the north.

name: Rickard Redwyne
bio: Rickard Redwyne was a knight from House Redwyne during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. He was the second son of Lord Manfryd_Redwyne of the Arbor.[N 1] While still a squire, he came to King's Landing with his father and brothers, Robert_Redwyne and Ryam_Redwyne, when Lord Manfryd was appointed to the small council in 50 AC. In 55 AC Rickard took part in the squire's tourney held as part of the celebrations for the completion of the Dragonpit. He was subsequently knighted on the field by King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 73 AC, Rickard took part at a tourney at Old Oak, where he unhorsed the mystery knight called the Silver Fool. Rickard unmasked the mystery knight and revealed him to be Prince Baelon Targaryen. He then knighted Prince Baelon.

name: Rickard Rowan
bio: Rickard Rowan was the Lord of Goldengrove and the head of House Rowan during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen and the beginning of the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 48 AC, Lord Rowan and Lord Torgen_Oakheart marched with Moon_(septon) against the increasingly brutal King Maegor, defending the septon and his followers when they camped beneath the walls of Oldtown. After the death of King Maegor, Septon Moon continued to denounce the Targaryens and the High Septon. Lord Donnel_Hightower refused to take up arms against Moon's followers, with many claiming the real reason was not wishing to offer battle to Lord Rowan and Lord Oakheart. However, the lords were reluctant to offer their armies to the septon when he planned to attack Oldtown itself; Moon was later assassinated with the guards, ending his uprising. Lord Rickard attended the Golden Wedding in 49 AC, where the king entertained the lords in the intimacy of his solar, encouraging them to speak freely and share their views on the problems of the realm and how they might best be overcome. Lord Rowan found the king gentle and soft-spoken. Lord Rowan was among those lords whose sisters and daughters were honored by becoming companions to Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. In 58 AC, he was present in King's Landing to celebrate the ten year anniversary of King Jaehaerys I coming to the throne.

name: Rickard Ryswell
bio: Rickard Ryswell is a son of Lord Rodrik_Ryswell and a member of House Ryswell.

name: Rickard Stark
bio: Rickard Stark, known as the Laughing Wolf for his good nature, was a King in the North and head of House Stark. He was the son of King Jon_Stark. King Rickard took the Neck away from the Marsh King and married his daughter. After his death, he was buried in the crypts of Winterfell.

name: Rickard Stark
bio: Rickard Stark was Lord of Winterfell, Warden of the North, and head of House Stark during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. He was the only child of Lord Edwyle_Stark and Lady Marna_Locke. Rickard was the father of the current Lord of Winterfell, Eddard_Stark. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Rickard is portrayed by Wayne Foskett in a Season 6 flashback. Rickard was born at Winterfell the only child of Lord Edwyle_Stark and Lady Marna_Locke.
