Rickard married his cousin once removed, Lyarra_Stark, and had four children: Brandon_Stark (b. 262 AC), Eddard_Stark (b. 263 AC), Lyanna_Stark (b. 266 AC or 267 AC), and Benjen_Stark. Lord Rickard visited King's Landing in 264 AC, leading King Aerys_II_Targaryen to consider building another Wall and claiming more land in the far north. Lady Olenna Redwyne was loosely acquainted with Rickard. After killing a miller and raping Miller's_wife, Lord Roose_Bolton had the tongue cut out from the miller's brother to prevent Lord Rickard from learning what happened. Rickard negotiated the betrothal of his heir Brandon to Catelyn_Stark of Riverrun. According to a semi-canon source, Rickard accepted a betrothal between his daughter Lyanna and Lord Robert_I_Baratheon of Storm's End when his younger son Eddard carried Robert's proposal to Winterfell. According to Barbrey_Dustin, both proposed marriages were the idea of Rickard's maester at the time, Walys_Flowers. In 282 AC, Brandon was traveling to meet Rickard's party coming down from the north in preparation of Brandon's wedding to Catelyn, when news of Lyanna's abduction at the hand of Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen reached him. Brandon went to King's Landing with a small company of young nobles to challenge Rhaegar, but was imprisoned with his companions by King Aerys II Targaryen on the charge of conspiring to kill the crown prince in the Red Keep. Aerys next summoned Rickard and the fathers of Brandon's companions to come and answer for the crimes of their sons. Rickard demanded a trial by combat, which was granted by the king. According to Ser Jaime_Lannister, Rickard expected to fight one of the Kingsguard. Dressed in his steel armor, he was brought to the throne room, where King Aerys II declared that fire was the champion of House Targaryen. Rickard was suspended from the rafters, and a fire was lit beneath him by the pyromancers. Rickard was slowly cooked to death in his armor, while his son Brandon, with a Tyroshi noose around his neck and a sword just out of his reach, was made to watch his father roast. Trying to reach the sword to save his dying father, Brandon strangled himself. Ser Gerold_Hightower and Ser Jaime Lannister of the Kingsguard were among those present for Rickard's and Brandon's execution. The other nobles and their sons were also executed by Aerys, with the exception of Ethan_Glover. Rickard was succeeded by his second son, Eddard. Together with Lords Jon_Arryn, Hoster_Tully, and Robert Baratheon, Eddard was a leader of Robert's Rebellion, the revolt against the Targaryens that overthrew their dynasty. Some have blamed Rickard's alliances for the near fall of House Stark. Lord Eddard did not reveal the true manner of Rickard's death to his family; both his wife Catelyn and his son Bran_Stark believed Rickard was beheaded.

name: Rickard Thorne
bio: Rickard Thorne was a knight from House Thorne and a member of the Kingsguard during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Rickard is portrayed by Vincent Regan. After the death of King Viserys_I_Targaryen in 129 AC, Rickard choose the side of King Aegon_II_Targaryen and the greens in the ensuing civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. In 130 AC, during the fall of King's Landing, when Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen's dragons first appeared in the skies above the city, Lord Larys_Strong, the master of whisperers, smuggled King Aegon II, Princess Jaehaera_Targaryen, and Prince Maelor_Targaryen to safety through a secret passage known only to Larys within Maegor's Holdfast. The party was accompanied by two members of the Kingsguard, Ser Rickard and Ser Willis_Fell. Ser Willis was charged to bring Jaehaera to Storm's End, and Maelor was put in Rickard's charge. Rickard swore to bring the three-year-old prince safely to Oldtown. After the escape, Queen Rhaenyra posted huge rewards for information leading to the capture of Aegon, Jaehaera, Maelor, and Larys, as well as the "false knights" Willis and Rickard. When that failed to produce anything, Rhaenyra sent out hunting parties of "knights inquisitor" to seek out the escapees, and punish anyone who helped them. Rickard Thorne traveled incognito, and claimed Maelor was his son. At the blacks-held Bitterbridge, Rickard attempted to stay at the Hogs Head, a disreputable inn, but there were no rooms left. Rickard showed a silver stag to the innkeep, Ben_Buttercakes, and Ben allowed him to stay in the stables, if he cleaned them first. Once Rickard did so, Ben offered him a drink, as a pretext for his stableboy to search for more money in Rickard's belongings. Sly_(stableboy) did not find any coins, but he did find Maelor's dragon egg, wrapped in Rickard's white cloak. When Sly informed the inn's common room of his discovery, Rickard fled the inn with Maelor, slaying Ben on his way out and stealing a horse from the stables, while a mob of people followed. Not long after, Rickard was killed by crossbowmen on the city's stone bridge, though the Kingsguard clung to his charge until the end. A washerwoman, Willow_Pound-Stone, had to tear a crying Maelor from his arms. However, once the mob killed Rickard and seized Maelor, they did not know what to do with him. They debated whether to bring him to far away King's Landing for Rhaenyra's reward, or take him to nearby Longtable and the camp of the green Lord Ormund_Hightower, who might pay more for Maelor. A fight broke out between Sly the stableboy, the crossbowman who had killed Rickard, and Willow Pound-Stone, who said no one was going to hurt her new son. It is disputed what happened next: Mushroom claims Willow Pound-Stone accidentally crushed Maelor to death, Eustace_(Dance_of_the_Dragons) says he was chopped into six pieces by a butcher so everyone might have a piece, and Munkun writes that Maelor was torn apart by the mob. All that is known for sure is that by the time Lady_Caswell and her knights arrived, Maelor was dead. Lady Caswell, after hanging those responsible for the prince's death, had Ser Rickard's corpse and Maelor's head sent to Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen at King's Landing.

name: Rickard Tyrell
bio: Rickard Tyrell is a member of House Tyrell, a distant cousin of the ruling line. He is the son of Ser Olymer_Tyrell and Lysa_Meadows.

name: Rickard Wylde
bio: Rickard Wylde is the son of Ser Jon_Wylde and Elyana_Vypren.

name: Rickon Stark
bio: Rickon Stark is Eddard_Stark and Catelyn_Stark's youngest child, only three years old when the series begins. Rickon is a naturally aggressive and strong-willed child, but his youth makes it difficult for him to cope with the terrible changes that come to his family and life.  He is usually accompanied by his direwolf, Shaggydog. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Rickon is portrayed by Art Parkinson. Born at Winterfell in 295 AC, Rickon is the third son and youngest child of Catelyn_Stark and Eddard_Stark, Lord of Winterfell. Maester Luwin helped deliver the child. Rickon's brothers are Robb_Stark and Bran_Stark, while his sisters are Sansa_Stark and Arya_Stark. Rickon also has a bastard half-brother, Jon_Snow, from whom Rickon begs sweets.

name: Rickon Stark (son of Benjen)
bio: Rickon Stark was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Rickon is portrayed by David Hounslow. Rickon succeeded his father, Lord Benjen_Stark_(lord), as the Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North.[N 1] After Lord Rickon passed away in 121 AC, he was succeeded as Lord of Winterfell by his thirteen-year-old son and heir, Cregan_Stark. Rickon's  brother, Bennard_Stark, served as regent during Cregan's minority until 126 AC, when Cregan rose against his uncle for he was slow to surrender power. Lord Cregan named his eldest son and heir Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan), after his lord father.

name: Rickon Stark (son of Cregan)
bio: Rickon Stark was a member of House Stark, the eldest son and heir of Lord Cregan_Stark and Lady Arra_Norrey. He married Jeyne_Manderly, with whom he had two daughters, Sansa_Stark_(daughter_of_Rickon) and Serena_Stark. Rickon Stark was born in 128 AC, the firstborn son of Lord Cregan_Stark and Lady Arra_Norrey, who died giving birth to him. Cregan named his son after his father, Lord Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Benjen). When the Dance of the Dragons begun in 129 AC, Prince Jacaerys_Velaryon gained Lord Cregan's support in the war with the Pact of Ice and Fire, to be sealed with a marriage. Under the terms of the pact, the first daughter Jacaerys sired would be sent north at the age of seven, to be fostered at Winterfell until she was of age to marry young Rickon. However, Jacaerys died childless at the Battle of the Gullet in early 130 AC. In 132 AC, after Lord Cregan remarried to Lady Alysanne_Blackwood, the young Rickon sang a song for his new stepmother at the feast that followed the wedding.

name: Rigney
bio: Rigney was an archmaester of the Citadel.

name: Rob
bio: Rob, better known as Big Rob, was a peasant in service to House Osgrey during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Squinty_Jeyne was his mother. Big Rob was among the smallfolk called up by Ser Eustace_Osgrey to defend his lands against House Webber when a dispute over the Chequy Water escalated into threats of war. Big Rob was trained for battle by Eustace's sworn swords, Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield and Ser Duncan_the_Tall. Because several of the recruits had the same first name, Duncan's squire, Egg, suggested that they be called by "village names" to distinguish them. However, because the villages had no names, the men were instead given surnames derived from the crop planted in their village. The men were excited to have been given "lord's names", except for Big Rob, who could not seem to remember whether he was a "Bean" or a "Barleycorn". During the training, Big Rob was always the first to drop his spear and run, and was chased and dragged back by Bennis, in tears. After the first day of training, Duncan had a nightmare where he saw Big Rob had been gutted, lying dead at the bottom of a grave, and that all the other men had also been killed and he had to bury them. After Ser Duncan realized that it would be a hopeless fight where all the smallfolk would certainly die, he sent Rob home with the others. As Rob ran off, he stepped on a chicken.

name: Rob the Starvling
bio: Rob, better known as Rob the Starvling, was a member of the Poor Fellows during the Faith Militant uprising. Following the death of Septon Moon_(septon), Rob the Starvling became one of his would-be successors. He conflicted with Lorcas_the_Learned over the possession of Septon Moon's corpse. Rob was not among the Poor Fellows killed when they attacked Oldtown, nor those one of those killed when Donnel_Hightower bestirred himself to slaughter the survivors. During the time the newly crowned King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen journeyed from Oldtown to King's Landing, Rob the Starvling was among the Poor Fellows who presented themselves to the king to beg for clemency. They were pardoned, on the condition that they agreed to join the Night's Watch.

name: Robar Royce
bio: Robar Royce, also known as Robar the Red, is a knight from  House Royce. He is the second son of Yohn_Royce, Lord of Runestone.

name: Robar II Royce
bio: Robar II Royce was a Bronze King from House Royce. Robar became High King of the Vale, the Fingers, and the Mountains of the Moon and nearly held back the coming of the Andals, but he was ultimately slain in the Battle of the Seven Stars. Robar, the grandson of Yorwyck_VI_Royce, inherited the crown of the Bronze King less than a fortnight before his sixteenth nameday. Robar united the houses and clans of the First Men that still held out against the Andals, most prominently the Belmores, Coldwaters, Hunters, Redforts, and Upcliffs. Robar brought many together with marriages, grants of land or gold, and, according to legend, he cheated in an archery contest against Lord Hunter. Robar was proclaimed High King of the Vale, the Fingers, and the Mountains of the Moon. King Robar warred upon one enemy at a time, as the Andal houses had fallen to petty rivalries and infighting. According to legend, Robar slew Qyle_Corbray, King of the Fingers, after striking Lady Forlorn from Qyle's hand. Gulltown was retaken when Robar's sister infiltrated the city and convinced the surviving Shetts to rise against the Graftons. Robar defeated the Hammer_of_the_Hills at Ironoaks. The Andals responded by uniting under Ser Artys_I_Arryn, the Falcon Knight. According to singers, Robar's army threw back six Andal charges in the Battle of Seven Stars, but the seventh one broke through. Nevertheless, Robar ordered a counterattack and led the charge himself, with his champions at his side, slaying Torgold_Tollett with Lady Forlorn. Then he spied the Falcon Knight's armor across the field and spurred toward him. Robar managed to slay Artys, but then five hundred knights charged down the slopes of the Giant's Lance to take Robar's host in the rear. Leading the attack was Artys, as the man slain by Robar had been a decoy. It is unknown who slew Robar in the ensuing rout, with Artys, Lord_Ruthermont, Ser Luceon_Templeton, or Ser Jaime_Corbray, who reclaimed Lady Forlorn, often being suggested. With Robar dead, Artys I claimed the title of King of Mountain and Vale.

name: Robard Cerwyn
bio: Robard Cerwyn was the Lord of Cerwyn and the head of House Cerwyn during the latter half of the second century after Aegon's Conquest. Robard was married to Argelle_Stark, the younger daughter of Edric_Stark and Serena_Stark. Together, the couple had issue.

name: Robb Reyne
bio: Robb Reyne was a knight from House Reyne during the reign of Daeron_II_Targaryen. He was considered one of the finest knights of his time. Robb sided with Daemon_I_Blackfyre during the First Blackfyre Rebellion in 196 AC.

name: Robb Rivers
bio: Robb Rivers, known as Red Robb Rivers and the Bowman of Raventree, was a bastard of House Blackwood of Raventree Hall. Fighting on the side of the blacks, Robb brought three hundred archers to the Battle by the Lakeshore during the Dance of the Dragons. Caught by armies from the riverlands and the north, Lord Humfrey_Lefford sent ravens to Prince Aemond_Targaryen at Harrenhal for assistance. However, Robb used his archery to prevent any of the ravens from reaching Aemond. Robb and two of his archers later slew Ser Criston_Cole during the Butcher's Ball. Robb's bowmen covered Ser Garibald_Grey's men as they retreated during the First Battle of Tumbleton. It is probable Robb did not survive the battle, which was a victory for the greens.[N 2]

name: Robb Stark
bio: Robb Stark is the eldest son of Lord Eddard_Stark and Lady Catelyn_Stark and is the heir of House Stark to Winterfell and the north. He is called the Young Wolf by friend and foe alike. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Robb is portrayed by Richard Madden. Robb was conceived during the first fortnight of the marriage of Lord Eddard_Stark and Lady Catelyn_Stark—on the wedding night at Riverrun, according to Catelyn. While Eddard rode south to fight in Robert's Rebellion, Catelyn remained at House Tully's castle. Near the end of the war, she gave birth at Riverrun with the aid of Maester Luwin. After war ended Catelyn traveled to Winterfell with Robb. Robb was raised at Winterfell as his father's heir, and is the closest of companions with his half-brother Jon_Snow. Robb and Jon trained together at arms ever since they were big enough to walk, and played at being mighty knights and heroes. Once, Robb and Jon built a great mountain of snow above a gate. They were spotted by Mance_Rayder, then a brother of the Night's Watch, who had accompanied the visiting Lord_Commander_Qorgyle. Mance promised not to tell on them, and the two boys eventually dumped the snow on Tomard, the slowest of Lord Eddard's household guards, who chased them around afterward. After Greyjoy's Rebellion in 289 AC, Eddard brought to Winterfell a hostage, Theon_Greyjoy, the last surviving son of Lord Balon_Greyjoy. Robb came to befriend Theon and enjoy the elder boy's company. Theon joined Robb and Jon in their training at arms under the tutelage of Ser Rodrik_Cassel, and Robb and Theon trained at sword fighting at the staircases of Winterfell. The Stark children, Jon, and Theon were also educated by Maester Luwin. Catelyn resented whenever Jon defeated Robb in a task put before the two youths. When Bran_Stark was only a few years old, Robb took his siblings into the crypt of Winterfell to show them the empty tombs that would be theirs one day. Jon, having covered himself with flour to appear as a ghost, tried to scare his half siblings by emerging from a tomb, and a sobbing Bran wrapped his arms around the legs of a laughing Robb. When Alys_Karstark was six, Lord Rickard_Karstark brought his daughter to Winterfell in an attempt to betroth her to Eddard's heir. Although no betrothal was arranged, Robb courteously told Alys that she danced beautifully. Robb has often attended Lord Stark's councils, and he has hunted in the wolfswood with his father, Jon, and Jory_Cassel. Eddard enjoys hawking with his heir.

name: Robert Arryn
bio: Robert Arryn is the only son of Lord Jon_Arryn and Lady Lysa_Arryn. He is named after King Robert_I_Baratheon. Robert is the new head of House Arryn, Lord of the Eyrie, Defender of the Vale, and Warden of the East. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones the character is named Robin Arryn to prevent confusion with the king, and he is portrayed by Lino Facioli. Robert has been sickly all his days. Prince Joffrey_Baratheon calls him cruel names and once slapped Robert with a wooden sword. The boy was brought to the Vale of Arryn from King's Landing at the age of six.

name: Robert Ashford
bio: Robert Ashford was a knight from House Ashford, and the younger son of the ruling Lord_Ashford during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Robert participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC. Along with his older brother, Ser Androw_Ashford, he was one of Lord_Ashford's_daughter champions in the tourney. In the first tilt he was challenged by Ser Lyonel_Baratheon, who beat him after ten lances and a fight afoot.

name: Robert I Baratheon
bio: Robert I Baratheon is the King of the Andals, the Rhoynar, and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm of Westeros, and the head of House Baratheon of King's Landing. Robert was crowned king after King Aerys_II_Targaryen, his first cousin once removed, was killed during Robert's Rebellion. Following the war, Robert married Cersei_Lannister to ensure political stability. With her, Robert has three children: Joffrey_Baratheon, Myrcella_Baratheon, and Tommen_Baratheon. On account of his fighting prowess during his youth, he was dubbed the Demon of the Trident, while those that opposed his ascension to the Iron Throne referred to him as the Usurper. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Robert is portrayed by Mark Addy. Born in 262 AC, Robert was the first born child of Cassana_Estermont and Steffon_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End, Lord Paramount of the Stormlands, and head of House Baratheon. He was a scant year older than his brother, Stannis_Baratheon, who was born in 264 AC. When Robert was already in his teens, his youngest brother, Renly_Baratheon, was born. According to Stannis, when Robert was five or six years old, the two brothers were brought to court by their father, Steffon, where they witnessed the Hand of the King, Lord Tywin_Lannister, sitting on the Iron Throne and holding court. Young Robert also played with an Estermont Estermont_(cousin_of_Robert) whose father was good to the future king. Robert had a gyrfalcon named Thunderclap who never missed her strike, and he mocked Stannis's goshawk, Proudwing, as "Weakwing". From an early age onwards, Robert had been fostered by Lord Jon_Arryn at the Eyrie in the Vale of Arryn, together with Eddard_Stark of Winterfell. The two boys became fast friends, and Jon, who had no children of his own, became like a second father to them. During the period in which he was fostered, Robert visited Storm's End on multiple occasions. At some point, Robert was knighted. During a tourney at Storm's End, Robert was defeated by Ser Barristan_Selmy. After reaching manhood, Robert divided his time between Storm's End and the Eyrie. In 278 AC, Robert's father was sent on a mission by King Aerys_II_Targaryen, to travel to Volantis to find a bride for Robert's cousin, Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen. The mission failed, and Steffon, accompanied by his wife, returned home. Robert and Stannis watched their parents' ship, Windproud, sink in a storm in Shipbreaker Bay while nearing Storm's End. With his father's death, Robert became the Lord of Storm's End.

name: Robert Blackwood
bio: Robert Blackwood is the sixth and youngest son of Lord Tytos_Blackwood of Raventree Hall.

name: Robert Blackwood (209)
bio: Robert Blackwood was a nobleman of House Blackwood during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Robert participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.[N 1]

name: Robert Brax
bio: Robert Brax is a knight from House Brax. He is the second son of Lord Andros_Brax.

name: Robert Brax
bio: Robert Brax is a member of House Brax, the eldest son of Ser Flement_Brax and Morya_Frey. He is a page at Casterly Rock.

name: Robert Darklyn
bio: Robert Darklyn was a knight from House Darklyn and was briefly a member of the Kingsguard during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. After the death of Ser Willis_Fell and two of other members of the Kingsguard from the Winter Fever in 133 AC, King Aegon_III_Targaryen bestowed white cloaks on Ser Robert and Ser Robin_Massey, making Ser Robin Lord Commander of the Kingsguard. However, the king's regent, Lord Unwin_Peake, with the support of Grand Maester Munkun, dismissed all the king's appointments on the grounds that no boy of twelve had the judgment to decide such weighty matters himself. Ser Marston_Waters was then confirmed as Lord Commander of the Kingsguard. The king protested the dismissal of the Kingsguard knights, saying they served for life, but Lord Peake replied that was only when they had been properly appointed. It is unknown whether House Darklyn included Ser Robert in their count of the the seven Darklyn knights who served in the Kingsguard.

name: Robert Flowers
bio: Robert Flowers, known as Red Robert Flowers, was a knight of the Kingsguard and a bastard from the Reach. Despite his bastard origin, he rose to become Lord Commander of the Kingsguard at an unknown time.

name: Robert Frey
bio: Robert Frey is a member of House Frey, the eldest son of Rhaegar_Frey and Jeyne_Beesbury. He is one of the numerous great-grandchildren of Lord Walder_Frey.

name: Robert Frey
bio: Robert Frey is the eldest son of Ser Raymund_Frey, the eleventh son of Lord Walder_Frey, and his wife, Beony_Beesbury. He has six younger siblings: Malwyn_Frey, Sarra_Frey, Serra_Frey, Cersei_Frey, Tywin_Frey_(son_of_Raymund) and Jaime_Frey.

name: Robert Paege
bio: Robert Paege is a knight of House Paege. His relation to the head of the House, Ser Halmon_Paege, is unknown.

name: Robert Quince
bio: Robert Quince was a knight and the castellan of Dragonstone during the Dance of the Dragons. When Rhaenyra_Targaryen went forth to seize the Iron Throne and conquer King's Landing, she named Ser Robert as castellan of Dragonstone, passing Ser Alfred_Broome over despite the fact that he was the most senior of the knights on the island and expected to be named castellan. Rhaenyra chose Robert because of his more affable character. When Robert heard the news that the Grey Ghost had been killed by another dragon he was quick to name the Cannibal as the killer. The fisherfolk urged Robert to dispatch knights to the dragon's lair but he refused, reasoning that if they did not trouble the Cannibal he would not trouble them. He then forbade fishing in the waters beneath the Dragonmont's eastern face where the Grey Ghost's body lay rotting. Although Robert was loyal to the queen and her cause some of the men under him were less than leal and harbored certain resentments and grudges for old wrongs whether real or imagined. Prominent amongst them was Alfred. Prior to, and during, the fall of Dragonstone to King Aegon_II_Targaryen, Alfred proved more than willing to betray his queen. He led the men who burst into Robert's chambers and slew him, driving a spear into his huge pale belly. The thrust was delivered with such force that the spear went out Robert's back, through the feather-bed and straw mattress, and into the floor beneath. Rhaenyra eventually fled back to Dragonstone. When she came ashore Rhaenyra was met by Ser Alfred Broome.  She asked why Robert had not come himself to meet her, to which Alfred replied that the queen would be seeing "our fat friend" at the castle. Rhaenyra and her party were taken by escort to Robert's charred corpse, which was burned beyond all recognition, and hanging from the battlements of the gatehouse beside Dragonstone's steward, master-at-arms, captain of guards and the upper torso of Grand Maester Gerardys. Only by the enormous size of the corpse did they recognize that it was Robert's.

name: Robert Redwyne
bio: Robert Redwyne was a knight from House Redwyne during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Robert was the eldest son of Manfryd_Redwyne, the Lord of the Arbor and master of ships to King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. While still a squire, he came to King's Landing with his father and brothers, Rickard_Redwyne and Ryam_Redwyne, when Lord Manfryd was appointed to the small council in 50 AC. After the death of Ser Qarl_Corbray, who succumbed to the Shivers in 59 AC, Robert, by now a knight, was appointed to command the City Watch of King's Landing. Robert's father, Lord Manfryd_Redwyne, passed away in 89 AC, making Robert the new Lord of the Arbor. Robert followed his father into the grave soon thereafter, however.

name: Robert Reyne
bio: Robert Reyne was the Lord of Castamere and head of House Reyne during the time Gerold_Lannister was Lord of Casterly Rock. He was the father of Roger_Reyne, Reynard_Reyne, and Ellyn_Reyne. Lord Robert arranged the betrothal of his daughter Ellyn_Reyne to Lord Gerold_Lannister's son and heir, Tywald_Lannister. Tywald served Robert as a squire. During the Peake Uprising both Robert and Tywald were killed during the Storming of Starpike. Upon Robert's death, his son Roger_Reyne became the new Lord of Castamere. In the aftermath of Starpike, the vengeful Roger executed seven captive Peakes before Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen prevented him from harming others.

name: Robert Rowan
bio: Robert Rowan was a knight from House Rowan and the heir to Goldengrove during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Ser Robert was the eldest son of Lord Thaddeus_Rowan of Goldengrove and his first wife. He had one full brother, and five younger half-brothers by his father's second wife. In 132 AC, Princess Baela_Targaryen claimed to have bedded Robert and a brother of his when a marriage was proposed between her and Lord Thaddeus. However, Archmaester Gyldayn notes the princess was deliberately provocative at times. Robert's father instead married Lady Floris_Baratheon. In 134 AC, a war of succession broke out in the Vale of Arryn after the death of Lady Jeyne_Arryn, and the killing of the king's regent, Ser Corwyn_Corbray, at Runestone. As the son of the Hand of the King, Robert was given command of an army of five thousand men. They set out on the kingsroad to the Bloody Gate to restore the King's Peace, as it was thought that the Mountains of the Moon would be passable with the coming of spring. Levies from Maidenpool, Darry, and Hayford joined them on the march, and six hundred Freys and a thousand Blackwoods joined them at the Trident, making them nine thousand strong entering the mountains. A naval attack was also launched at the command of Ser Gedmund_Peake. The high road proved far less open than had been assumed. Robert's host found itself struggling through deep snows in the mountain passes, slowing their advance to a crawl, and the baggage train was often attacked by mountain clans. Once, whilst looking for shelter, they roused the dragon Sheepstealer and saw Nettles. Robert was crushed by a falling boulder when the clansmen toppled half a mountainside down upon the column. Lord Benjicot_Blackwood took charge of his host, and a third of the men had died by the time they reached the Bloody Gate.

name: Robert Stone
bio: Robert Stone was a knight mentioned by Ser Osmund_Kettleblack.

name: Robert Tyrell
bio: Robert Tyrell was a knight of House Tyrell and the High Steward of the Reach during the reign of Mern_VI_Gardener, King of the Reach. Ser Robert succeeded his father, Ser Osmund_Tyrell, as High Steward. He continued his father's work in aiding his king, Mern_VI_Gardener, in the reconstruction of the Reach after the Dornish raid that had sacked Highgarden and killed the senile old king, Garth_X_Gardener. King Mern VI was so pleased with the Tyrells that he gave Robert the hand of his youngest daughter in marriage, thereby allowing the following generations of Tyrells to claim descent from Garth_Greenhand. Robert was succeeded by his eldest son, Lorent_Tyrell_(steward).

name: Robert Vance
bio: Robert Vance was the head of one of the branches of House Vance four centuries before the Aegon's Conquest. Robert claimed the title of river king or King of the Trident in the generations after the riverlands were conquered by the Storm King Arlan_III_Durrandon. Lord Robert had a brief and inglorious reign before being put down.

name: Robeson
bio: Robeson was a septon during the rule of Lord Damon_Hightower over Oldtown. When Damon_Hightower, Lord of the Hightower, died of a bad belly, Robeson became regent for Damon's newborn son, Triston_Hightower. Robeson ruled Oldtown in all but name for twenty years and ultimately became the first High Septon. Lord Triston raised the Starry Sept in Robeson's honor after his passing.

name: Robett Glover
bio: Robett Glover is a member of House Glover. He is the brother of Galbart_Glover, the husband of Sybelle_Glover, and the father of two children, Gawen_Glover and Erena_Glover. The appendices of A Clash of Kings and A Storm of Swords state that Gawen is the heir to Deepwood Motte, while the appendices of A Feast for Crows and A Dance with Dragons state that Robett is the heir of Galbart. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Robett is portrayed by Tim McInnerny.

name: Robin
bio: Robin was a brother of Septon Meribald. He died in the War of the Ninepenny Kings. Robin and his brothers, Meribald, Willam, Owen_(brother_of_Meribald) and Owen's friend Jon_Pox all went off to fight in the War of the Ninepenny Kings. During the War, Willam and Robin died from a fever. Owen died in battle after receiving a blow from a mace and Jon was hanged for rape. Meribald survived to become a septon.

name: Black Robin
bio: Black Robin was an outlaw. The singers made a sad song about him "The Day They Hanged Black Robin". It is unknown if Black Robin really existed.

name: Robin
bio: Robin was a candlemaker's daughter who lived in Flea Bottom during the Dance of the Dragons. According to legend, Robin comforted Prince Joffrey_Velaryon as he was dying after his fall from Rhaenyra_Targaryen's dragon, Syrax.

name: Robin Arryn
bio: Robin Arryn was a King of Mountain and Vale from House Arryn. Robin was the younger brother of King Roland_II_Arryn. When his older brother was killed by King Tristifer_IV_Mudd, Robin took the Falcon Crown and resumed work on the Eyrie.

name: Robin Darklyn
bio: Robin Darklyn, known as Darkrobin, was a knight from House Darklyn during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. Robin was chosen by Queen Visenya_Targaryen to be one of the first knights of the Kingsguard when the order was founded in 10 AC by King Aegon_I_Targaryen. Robin was the first of the eventual seven Darklyns who served in the Kingsguard.

name: Robin Flint
bio: Robin Flint is a member of House Flint of Widow's Watch and the son of Lyessa_Flint, Lady of Widow's Watch.

name: Robin Greyjoy
bio: Robin Greyjoy was the ninth son of Lord Quellon_Greyjoy, and his only son by his third wife, Lady_Piper_(wife_of_Quellon_Greyjoy). Robin was the youngest son of Lord Quellon_Greyjoy of Pyke, and the only son by his third wife, Lady_Piper_(wife_of_Quellon_Greyjoy) of House Piper. He was a sickly child, with a large soft head, and died in infancy. His older brother Aeron_Greyjoy dismisses Robin as a "sickly idiot boy", who is best forgotten.

name: Robin Hill
bio: Robin Hill was a bastard from the westerlands who rose to become Lord Commander of the Night's Watch. He is considered one of the worst Lord Commanders after almost destroying the Watch.

name: Robin Hollard
bio: Robin Hollard was a member of House Hollard and a squire during the Defiance of Duskendale. When King Aerys_II_Targaryen was a captive in the Defiance of Duskendale, Robin would dance around the king and pull on his beard. After the Defiance had been put down, Robin died on the rack.

name: Robin Massey
bio: Robin Massey was a knight from House Massey, who was briefly made Lord Commander of the Kingsguard during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. After the death of Ser Willis_Fell and two other members of the Kingsguard from the Winter Fever in 133 AC, King Aegon_III_Targaryen bestowed white cloaks on Ser Robin and Ser Robert_Darklyn. Aegon made Robin Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, thereby passing over the second-in-command, Ser Marston_Waters. However, the king's regent, Lord Unwin_Peake, with the support of Grand Maester Munkun, dismissed all the king's appointments on the grounds that no boy of twelve had the judgment to decide such weighty matters himself. Marston Waters was then confirmed as Lord Commander of the Kingsguard. The king protested the dismissal of the Kingsguard knights, saying they served for life, but Lord Peake replied that was only when they had been properly appointed. Tessario later slew Robin in a dispute over a horse both men wished to buy.

name: Robin Moreland
bio: Robin Moreland is the head of House Moreland in the westerlands.

name: Robin Peasebury
bio: Robin Peasebury is the Lord of Poddingfield and the head of House Peasebury.

name: Robin Penrose
bio: Robin Penrose was the only son and eldest child of Lord Ronnel_Penrose and his wife Princess Elaena_Targaryen. He was presumably the heir of Parchments.

name: Robin Potter
bio: Robin Potter is a knight of House Potter.

name: Robin Ryger
bio: Robin Ryger is a knight of House Ryger who serves as the captain of the guard at Riverrun.

name: Robyn Rhysling
bio: Robyn Rhysling was a knight from House Rhysling during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, he is portrayed by William Houston. Ser Robyn lost an eye to a splinter from a broken lance in 204 AC. During the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC, Robyn challenged Lord Leo_Tyrell_(Longthorn) to a tilt. Robyn lost his helm to Leo Longthorn's lance in their first course, but refused to yield. Three times more they rode at each other, yet Leo was too chivalrous to aim at Robyn's head. Finally, the Lord of Highgarden struck Robyn's breastplate and sent him to the ground. Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen brought Robyn to Ser Duncan_the_Tall's side during the trial of seven, having known him from previous tourneys. Robyn knocked Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) from his horse and also fought one of the Kingsguard.

name: Robin Shaw
bio: Robin Shaw was a knight of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 59 AC the winter disease known as the Shivers killed many including Ser Samgood_of_Sour_Hill and Ser Victor_the_Valiant of the Kingsguard. Once the disease passed, the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, Ser Gyles_Morrigen presented Ser Robin and Ser Ryam_Redwyne before King Jaehaerys and both men joined the sworn brotherhood.

name: Robyn Ryswell
bio: Robyn Ryswell was a noblewoman from House Ryswell during the latter half of the second century after Aegon's Conquest. Robyn married Jonnel_Stark of Winterfell after the death of his first wife, Sansa_Stark_(daughter_of_Rickon), but they had no issue.

name: Roderick Blackwood
bio: Roderick Blackwood was a Lord of Raventree Hall and head of House Blackwood four centuries before Aegon's Conquest. Roderick led the Blackwoods when Humfrey_I_Teague was King of the Rivers and the Hills. When Humfrey began to repress worship of the old gods, Roderick rose in rebellion, supported by House Vance of Atranta and House Tully of Riverrun. Nearly defeated by Humfrey and the Faith Militant, Roderick sought the aid of the Storm King Arlan_III_Durrandon, who had married one of Roderick's daughters. Arlan intended to restore the riverlands to House Blackwood, but Roderick was slain in battle. After the Teagues were all killed in the Battle of Six Kings, Roderick's heir was a boy of only eight and Arlan distrusted Roderick's brothers. The river lords rejected Arlan's suggestion of having Roderick's eldest child, Shiera_Blackwood, rule the riverlands with Arlan's son by her side. Arlan then decided to add the riverlands to the realm of the Storm Kings.

name: Roderick Dustin
bio: Roderick Dustin, known as Roddy the Ruin, was the Lord of Barrowton and the head of House Dustin during the Targaryen civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Roderick is portrayed by Tommy Flanagan. The north supported the blacks during the Dance of the Dragons. Roderick led two thousand northern soldiers, known as the Winter Wolves, south of the Neck to support the claim of Rhaenyra_Targaryen. They marched first to the Twins, where they met Lady Sabitha Frey, the wife of Lord Forrest_Frey. In the Battle by the Lakeshore along the western Gods Eye, Lord Dustin's Winter Wolves led the attack and charged at Lannister spearmen five times, with more than two-thirds of the northern force killed or wounded in their victory. Lord Frey was also slain in the battle. Roderick was scarred in the fighting against the Lannisters. After the Kingmaker, Ser Criston_Cole, was slain by archers, Roddy the Ruin sounded the charge which began the Butcher's Ball. Roderick died during the First Battle of Tumbleton while leading his remaining Winter Wolves against Lord Ormund_Hightower. Although Ser Bryndon_Hightower chopped off Roderick's shield arm, the frenzied Lord Dustin was able to slay both Hightowers before dying himself.

name: Roderick Stark
bio: Roderick Stark was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the first half of the first century after Aegon's Conquest. Roderick was succeeded as Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North by Brandon_Stark_(father_of_Walton).

name: Rodrik Arryn
bio: Rodrik Arryn was the Lord of the Eyrie and the head of House Arryn during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Rodrik became the Lord of the Eyrie at the age of ten after the death of his Darnold_Arryn and Rymond_Arryn at the hands of mountain clan raiders. Rodrik's sister served as a companion to Queen Alysanne_Targaryen at Dragonstone when the queen was pregnant with Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I) in 55 AC. Rodrik was named lord justiciar and master of laws by King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen in 59 AC. He had four children with his first wife: two daughters, Elys_Arryn and Amanda_Arryn, and two sons. As King Jaehaerys I's and his sister-wife, Queen Alysanne's, daughter Princess Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I) sixteenth nameday approached a suitable candidate for her to wed was needed by the end of the year 80 AC. Among the lords that contended for her hand were, Boremund_Baratheon of Storm's End, Tymond_Lannister of Casterly Rock, and Rodrik. Daella chose Rodrik for she found him the kindest. Rodrik then took his wife back to the Eyrie, resigning his position on the small council. Daella fell pregnant after a year and a half of marriage and died of childbed fever after their daughter, Aemma_Arryn, was born in 82 AC.

name: Rodrik Cassel
bio: Rodrik Cassel is a knight from House Cassel and a member of the Stark household. He is the uncle of Jory_Cassel and is the master-at-arms of Winterfell. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Rodrik is portrayed by Ron Donachie and in a Season 6 flashback by Fergus Leathem. Rodrik's mother died forty years ago. According to Maester Luwin, Rodrik has served House Stark all his life. Rodrik trained Robb_Stark, Jon_Snow, and Theon_Greyjoy. Eddard_Stark, Lord of Winterfell, sometimes invites Rodrik to join him for meals. When Ser Waymar_Royce joined the Night's Watch, Lord Yohn_Royce accompanied his son to the north. During a sparring session at Winterfell, Bronze Yohn defeated first Eddard and then Rodrik.

name: Rodrik Flint
bio: Rodrik Flint was a member of House Flint who became Lord Commander of the Night's Watch. It is unknown from which branch of House Flint he came from. Lord Commander Flint attempted to make himself King-Beyond-the-Wall. His efforts almost destroyed the Night's Watch. He is considered one of the worst commanders the Night's Watch has ever had.

name: Rodrik Freeborn
bio: Rodrik Freeborn is an ironborn raider. He descends from either thralls or salt wives.

name: Rodrik Greyjoy
bio: Rodrik Greyjoy was a claimant Lord of the Iron Islands from House Greyjoy during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Rodrik was a salt son of Lord Dalton_Greyjoy, the Red Kraken. In 133 AC,  Dalton was slain by one of his salt wives, Tess, at Faircastle. A bloody power struggle ensued on the Iron Islands. By law, the Seastone Chair fell to Rodrik's elder half-brother, Toron_Greyjoy, but Rodrik's father's cousins raised him up as Lord of the Iron Islands. After half a year, Lady Johanna_Lannister sent westermen to invade the Iron Islands in 134 AC. Rodrik was captured, gelded, and made a court fool to Johanna's son, Lord Loreon Lannister, at Casterly Rock.

name: Rodrik Greyjoy
bio: Rodrik Greyjoy was a member of House Greyjoy and the oldest son of Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands, and Lady Alannys_Harlaw. He was killed during Greyjoy's Rebellion. He may have been named after his maternal uncle, Lord Rodrik_Harlaw of Harlaw. According to Asha_Greyjoy, Theon_Greyjoy lived in awe and fear of their elder brothers, Rodrik and Maron_Greyjoy. Rodrik was good friends with Harras_Harlaw in their youth. Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands, rose in rebellion against Robert_I_Baratheon, thinking the new king did not have great support amongst the nobility of the Seven Kingdoms and that Balon would be allowed to go his own way. Rodrik led longships in the storming of Seagard, causing the bronze bell of the Booming Tower at Seagard to be rung for the first time in three hundred years. Jason_Mallister, Lord of Seagard, killed Rodrik beneath the castle walls and threw the ironmen back into the sea. Maron was later killed in the battle at Pyke. Balon blames Eddard_Stark, Lord of Winterfell, for the deaths of Rodrik and Maron during Greyjoy's Rebellion, though neither was actually slain by Eddard. Alannys_Harlaw, Balon's grieving wife, eventually began to roam the halls of Pyke in search of her lost sons, even walking the rope bridge to the Sea Tower while barefoot; a maester often had to draw splinters from her heels.

name: Rodrik Harlaw
bio: Rodrik Harlaw is the Lord of Harlaw, Lord of the Ten Towers, and head of House Harlaw. He is nicknamed Rodrik the Reader because he is always reading, which is uncommon amongst the ironborn. He is the captain of the Sea Song. Lord Harlaw's kin include Boremund_Harlaw, Ser Harras_Harlaw, Hotho_Harlaw, and Sigfryd_Harlaw. Other bannermen include Lords Myre, Kenning, Stonetree, and Maron_Volmark. Rodrik unsuccessfully advised Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands, not to rebel against King Robert_I_Baratheon. Rodrik had two tall sons who died in the sea battle off Fair Isle during Greyjoy's Rebellion. When the Faith of the Seven was still tolerated in the Iron Islands, Rodrik kept septons at Ten Towers to help care for his books, which he reads in the Book Tower. Rodrik's sister, Gwynesse_Harlaw, insists that she should rule Ten Towers, as she is seven years his elder. Rodrik is a widower, who does not expect to wed again, and his heir is his cousin Harras_Harlaw, the Knight of Grey Garden.

name: Rodrik Ryswell
bio: Rodrik Ryswell is the Lord of the Rills and the head of House Ryswell in the north. He has at least two daughters, the late Bethany_Bolton, Lady of the Dreadfort, and Barbrey_Dustin, Lady of Barrowton, and three sons, Roger_Ryswell, Rickard_Ryswell, and Roose_Ryswell.[N 1] Rodrik's personal coat of arms is an alteration of the Ryswell horse head, golden instead of black. Rodrik's elder daughter, Bethany_Bolton, was the second wife of Roose_Bolton, Lord of the Dreadfort. Brandon_Stark, eldest son of Rickard_Stark, Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North, was fostered at Barrowton with House Dustin. Brandon spent much of his time riding the Rills, and Lord Ryswell enjoyed hosting the heir to Winterfell during his visits. The ambitious Rodrik wanted to wed his younger daughter, Barbrey_Dustin, to Brandon, and Barbrey eventually gave her maidenhead to Brandon. Lord Stark, ambitious as well, did not want Brandon to marry one of his bannermen, however, and Rickard instead betrothed Brandon to Catelyn_Stark, eldest daughter of Hoster_Tully, Lord of Riverrun and Lord Paramount of the Trident. Lord Rodrik next unsuccessfully hoped to betroth Barbrey to Rickard's middle son, Eddard_Stark, and she instead wed Willam_Dustin, Lord of Barrowton. Robert's Rebellion began after the deaths of Rickard and Brandon in King's Landing. Barbrey gave the pride of Rodrik's herds, a red stallion, to her husband when he marched south with Lord Eddard. Willam ended up dying in combat at the tower of joy. Barbrey resents that Eddard returned the stallion to the north, but not Lord Dustin's bones.

name: Rodrik Sparr
bio: Rodrik Sparr is a member of House Sparr from the Iron Islands. He is the captain of the ship Grief in the Iron Fleet and is under the command of Victarion_Greyjoy. His crew call him the Vole.

name: Rodrik Stark
bio: Rodrik Stark was a King in the North and head of House Stark. His statue is in the crypts of Winterfell, near that of Torrhen_Stark. After the death of the Old Kraken, Loron_Greyjoy, King Rodrik is claimed to have won Bear Island from the ironborn and granted the island to House Mormont. The histories of the north claim that Rodrik won Bear Island back from the ironborn in a wrestling match. Some maesters believe there is truth to this tale, for the driftwood kings were often moved to prove their prowess and right to their crown with feats of strength. Other scholars question the tale and suggest that Rodrik had won the island back "with words". After his death, Rodrik's sons and grandsons battled the ironborn for Cape Kraken, though the resulting battles were less decisive.

name: Rodrik Stark
bio: Rodrik Stark, known as the Wandering Wolf, was a member of House Stark, the fifth son and youngest child of Lord Beron_Stark and Lady Lorra_Royce. He married Arya_Flint of the mountain clans and had two daughters, Branda_Stark and Lyarra_Stark. Rodrik served with the Second Sons, a sellsword company.

name: Rodwell Stark
bio: Rodwell Stark was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Rodwell was the eldest son of Lord Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan) and his lady wife, Alys_Karstark_(wife_of_Brandon), and he had two younger siblings, Beron_Stark and Arsa_Stark, as well as an older bastard half-brother, Lonnel_Snow. Lord Rodwell married Lady Myriame_Manderly, but had no issue. His brother Beron followed him as Lord of Winterfell. Rodwell is buried in the crypts of Winterfell.

name: Roelle
bio: Roelle was a septa of the Faith for Brienne of Tarth when she was growing up at Evenfall Hall. Roelle once gave advice to Brienne that all compliments from men are lies and that if she wanted the truth all she had to do was to look in the mirror in the morning, a lesson Brienne took to heart.

name: Rogar Baratheon
bio: Rogar Baratheon was the Lord of Storm's End and head of House Baratheon during the reigns of kings Maegor_I_Targaryen and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. During the minority of Jaehaerys I, Rogar served as Hand of the King and Protector of the Realm. Throughout the long reign of King Jaehaerys I, Lord Rogar proved to be both a loyal friend and brief adversary to the king. A grandson of Lord Orys_Baratheon and the eldest of five brothers, Rogar married Queen Dowager Alyssa_Velaryon and fathered two children, Boremund_Baratheon and Jocelyn_Baratheon. Boremund succeeded his father as Lord of Storm's End, while Jocelyn married Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I). Rogar Baratheon was born in 17 AC, the eldest of five brothers and the grandson of Orys_Baratheon, the first Lord of Storm's End of House Baratheon and the reputed bastard half-brother of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. As Lord Orys had taken Storm King Argilac_Durrandon's Argella_Durrandon to wife, Rogar could claim that both the blood of House Targaryen and House Durrandon flowed through his veins. Rogar's first wife died young, succumbing to a fever less than a year after her wedding to the Lord of Storm's End. The marriage was childless.

name: Rogar Bolton
bio: Rogar Bolton, known as Rogar the Huntsman, was the last Red King of the Dreadfort from the dynasty of House Bolton. Rogar the Huntsman submitted to the Kings of Winter, the Stark of Winterfell, and sent them his sons as hostages when the Andal invasion was beginning.

name: Roger of Pennytree
bio: Roger of Pennytree was the squire and nephew of Ser Arlan_of_Pennytree. Roger died at the Battle of the Redgrass Field after Lord Gormon_Peake smashed his head with a mace. Ser Duncan_the_Tall served as Arlan's squire after the war until the old knight's death. As Duncan played a role in events that led to Gormon's execution, he felt he had avenged Roger's death.

name: Roger Blackwood
bio: Roger Blackwood was a member of House Blackwood during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Roger participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.[N 1]

name: Roger Corne
bio: Roger Corne was a landed knight from the Blackwater Rush during the Dance of the Dragons. When the host of blacks led by Ser Garibald_Grey and Lord Roderick_Dustin marched south from the riverlands to Tumbleton, they were joined by men from the Blackwater Rush, including Roger and Lord Owain_Bourney. Roger was secretly a supporter of King Aegon_II_Targaryen, however. When the Two Betrayers began attacking the blacks during the First Battle of Tumbleton, Roger ordered his men to open Tumbleton's gates to the greens, while Owain did the same at the town's castle. The greens' commanders regrouped in Tumbleton and planned what to do next, with Roger among those wanting to attack the black-held King's Landing. The two baseborn dragonriders Ulf_White and Hugh_Hammer proved difficult to deal with. Ulf desired Highgarden, while Hugh had designs on claiming the Iron Throne. Hugh wore a black iron crown, enraging Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I). Roger angered Hugh by knocking off his crown, which led to Hugh nailing three horseshoes to Roger's skull. Roger's supporters tried to intervene, resulting in three dead and twelve wounded.

name: Roger Hogg
bio: Roger Hogg is the Knight of Sow's Horn and the head of House Hogg. He is a bannerman of House Hayford.

name: Roger Reyne
bio: Roger Reyne, known as the Red Lion, was a Lord of Castamere and head of House Reyne. He was a famous knight, and considered one of the deadliest men of the westerlands.

name: Roger Ryswell
bio: Roger Ryswell is a son of Lord Rodrik_Ryswell and a member of House Ryswell.

name: Roggerio Rogare
bio: Roggerio Rogare was a member of House Rogare, a Lysene noble banking family. He was the son of Lysandro_Rogare, the First Magister of Lys. Roggerio was the son of Lysandro Rogare. He had five trueborn brothers, Lysaro_Rogare, Moredo_Rogare, Lotho_Rogare, Drako_Rogare, and Fredo_Rogare, and three trueborn sisters, Larra_Rogare, Lysara_Rogare, and Marra_Rogare, as well as sixteen bastard siblings. When his sister Larra came to King's Landing as the wife of Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, Roggerio, Moredo, and Lotho came with her. Roggerio and Lotho were fluent in the common tongue, although Moredo was not. Roggerio opened a high-class pleasure house which he called the Mermaid near the River Gate in the capital. The Mermaid was no common brothel, but an opulent establishment, full of exotic animals such as parrots and monkeys. Its prostitutes were exotic too, from every corner of the world and cost ten times more than common whores. Despite this Roggerio never lacked for customers, mainly high lords, knights and rich merchants. Roggerio banned Tessario the Tiger from the Mermaid when the sellsword struck one of his girls. In 135 AC Roggerio was arrested at the Mermaid by Ser Marston_Waters when House Rogare was accused of treason. Although the treason against King Aegon_III_Targaryen was later proven unfounded, the new Hand of the King, Lord Torrhen_Manderly still sentenced Roggerio to seven lashes, when Roggerio asked "for what?", Lord Manderly replied "for being a thrice damned Lyseni". After his punishment and release, he sold his brothel and everything in it to purchase a great cog which he named the Mermaid's Daughter, which effectively was the pillowhouse reborn with sails. Roggerio departed King's Landing and sailed the sea, trading in wines and carnal pleasures at every port he visited.

name: Roggo
bio: Red Roggo is a cutpurse in Braavos.

name: Roggon Rustbeard
bio: Roggon Rustbeard is a member of the crew on Asha_Greyjoy's ship, Black Wind.

name: Rogin Salt-Beard
bio: Rogin Salt­-Beard is an ironborn raider. He is one of Euron_Greyjoy's captains or sworn swords.

name: Rognar II Greyiron
bio: Rognar II Greyiron was the last King of the Iron Islands from House Greyiron. Rognar II was brought down by pirates, sellswords, and warlords, Andals who came to the Iron Islands and allied with Orkwoods, Drumms, Hoares, and Greyjoys. He was succeeded by Harras_Hoare, who, according to the tale, won the crown in a contest of the finger dance.

name: Rogo
bio: Rogo was a Dothraki khal who lived during the Century of Blood. He sacked the city of Ibbish a generation after Khal Scoro, putting the city to the torch and marching ten thousand women into slavery.

name: Rohanne of Tyrosh
bio: Rohanne of Tyrosh was the wife of Daemon_I_Blackfyre, with whom she had seven sons and several daughters. Rohanne's betrothal to Daemon_I_Blackfyre was arranged by King Aegon_IV_Targaryen, Daemon's father, and the Archon of Tyrosh. Following the death of King Aegon IV in 184 AC, his successor and Daemon's half-brother, King Daeron_II_Targaryen, paid the dowry the late King Aegon IV had promised the Archon, and saw his half-brother wed. Rohanne was married to the thirteen-year old[N 1] Daemon in 184 AC. Their first children, the twins Aegon_Blackfyre and Aemon_Blackfyre, were born that same year. In total, Rohanne would birth seven sons for Daemon, and at least two daughters. After the First Blackfyre Rebellion ended with the deaths of Daemon and his eldest two sons, Rohanne and her remaining children fled to Tyrosh along with another one of Daemon's half-brothers, Aegor_Rivers. They remained in Tyrosh for years after. In 212 AC, when Daemon_II_Blackfyre was captured at Whitewalls by the crown after the abortive Second Blackfyre Rebellion, Brynden_Rivers noted if he executed him, his mother would mourn.

name: Rohanne Tarbeck
bio: Rohanne Tarbeck was a member of House Tarbeck and the elder daughter of Lord Walderan_Tarbeck and Ellyn Tarbeck. According to a semi-canon source, Lady Ellyn_Reyne named her after Rohanne_Webber, an attack against the widowed Gerold_Lannister, Lord of Casterly Rock, as his wife Rohanne had disappeared mysteriously. During the Reyne-Tarbeck rebellion, Lord Tywin_Lannister ordered the executions of Rohanne's unnamed husband and her father, Lord Walderan_Tarbeck. Rohanne and her sister Cyrelle_Tarbeck were taken captive during Tywin's assault on Tarbeck Hall. Some stories claim that Tywin ordered their tongues removed before they were forced to join the silent sisters. Rohanne's three-year-old son, the Last_Lord_Tarbeck, disappeared during the assault.

name: Rohanne Webber
bio: Rohanne Webber, known as the Red Widow, was the Lady of Coldmoat and the head of House Webber during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. She was the daughter of Lord Wyman_Webber. Later, she married Lord Gerold_Lannister and became Lady of Casterly Rock. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Rohanne will be portrayed by Lucy Boynton. Rohanne was the only living child of Lord Wyman_Webber. She had five or six brothers, but they all died in infancy. Later, rumors claimed she had killed her brothers because they stood in the way of her succession. During her childhood, Rohanne was fond of young Addam_Osgrey, who served as her father's page and later squire at Coldmoat. The two had an innocent romance, though it never went past a few kisses. Because of this relationship, Addam's father and their neighbor, the landed knight Ser Eustace_Osgrey, proposed that they marry, but Lord Wyman refused him. Instead, Wyman had Rohanne married to a different squire of her father's. During the First Blackfyre Rebellion, Rohanne's father and husband fought in the Battle of the Redgrass Field in 196 AC, on the side of House Targaryen. Rohanne's husband, twelve years old, was slain in battle, and she became a widow for the first time at the age of ten. Addam also died during the battle, fighting on the side of House Blackfyre as his father's squire. Rohanne wept only for Addam, not for the husband she barely knew; she blamed Ser Eustace for his death, and held a grudge against the knight thereafter. At age thirteen, Rohanne married her second husband, a fifty-five-year-old man who soon died of a chill. Six months after his death, she gave birth to their son, who was weak and died within three days. Rohanne's third husband, Ser Simon_Staunton, choked to death on a chicken bone, and her fourth husband, Ser Rolland_Uffering, died of the Great Spring Sickness. Rohanne bore a daughter by either her third or fourth husband, but the girl did not live a year. Because of the unfortunate deaths of her husbands and children, Rohanne was called the Red Widow, and the smallfolk accused her of poisoning her husbands and being a witch who sold her unborn babies to the Lord of the Seven Hells so that he would teach her the black arts. Various kin of of Rohanne's husbands came to Coldmoat with the marriages, and remained living at the castle even after the men died. These included Septon Sefton_Staunton, Rohanne's good-brother through her husband Simon Staunton, and Lady Helicent_Uffering, sister of Ser Roland. Lord Wyman still wanted grandsons to continue the Webber line, so before his death, he attempted to marry Rohanne to his castellan, Ser Lucas_Inchfield, but she refused him. Wyman then stated in his will that Rohanne had two years to marry after his death. If she remained unwed after that time, Coldmoat would be granted to her cousin, Ser Wendell_Webber. On his deathbed, Rohanne's father charged Lucas to scare off unworthy suitors. In the two years that followed, Lucas took Wyman's instruction to mean all suitors, threatening to fight any man who came to Coldmoat. The behavior of "the Longinch", in addition to the rumors concerning the deaths of Rohanne's husbands, caused the number of suitors to be lower than would be expected for her beauty and status. Some rumors even claimed that she was barren. Nevertheless, Rohanne did have several suitors, including Cleyton_Caswell and Simon_Leygood, both rather persistent, though she did not care for them. Another suitor was Ser Gerold_Lannister, who sent her flattering letters from Casterly Rock, but Rohanne did not believe he was willing to leave the westerlands and his position at the seat of his brother, Lord Tybolt_Lannister. In 209 AC, not long after Rohanne became the Lady of Coldmoat, a peasant of House Osgrey's villages, Dake, was accused of stealing sheep from House Webber's neighboring lands. Rohanne sent her knights to seize Dake and bring him to Coldmoat for punishment. When Ser Eustace Osgrey arrived at Coldmoat to demand Dake's release, he learned that Rohanne had him sewn into a sack with rocks, which was sunk in the moat of Coldmoat. Eustace then swore a holy vow that he would never return to Coldmoat again, except to take possession of it.

name: Roland of the Horn
bio: Roland of the Horn is a legendary knight of the Reach who lived during the Age of Heroes. Tales describe him as a knight even though knights came to Westeros thousands of years later with the Andal invasion.

name: Roland I Arryn
bio: Roland I Arryn was the fourth King of Mountain and Vale from House Arryn. He is noted for ordering construction of the Eyrie. The grandson of King Artys_I_Arryn, Roland fostered with an Andal king in the riverlands as a boy. He traveled far and wide after becoming a knight, including visits to the Hightower, Casterly Rock, and the castles of the Trident. Returning to the Vale to don the Falcon Crown, Roland felt that the Gates of the Moon, the seat of his house, was ugly compared to the grander castles of the First Men. He initially wanted to tear down the Gates of the Moon and rebuild it, but was reminded of the site's vulnerability after thousands of wildlings descended from the Mountains of the Moon in search of food during winter. Legends say Roland's future wife, Teora_Hunter, reminded him how his grandsire Artys had defeated Robar_II_Royce, by attacking from high ground during the Battle of Seven Stars. Roland began the slow development of the Eyrie and its approaches atop the highest ground possible, the Giant's Lance. The king preferred marble from Tarth instead of the Vale. After a twenty-six year reign, Roland was killed in a surprise attack by Painted Dogs, who pulled him from his horse and crushed his head with a stone maul before he could draw his longsword. Roland I had lived long enough to see the first stones laid for the castle he envisioned, and he was succeeded by his son.

name: Roland II Arryn
bio: Roland II Arryn was a King of Mountain and Vale from House Arryn. Roland II was the great-grandson of King Roland_I_Arryn, who first started constructing the Eyrie. He was a man whose passions were not building, but war and wenching. As the cost of building the Eyrie had become too much, he had need for money to finance a campaign in the riverlands. So it came to be that all work on the Eyrie was halted for the better part of four years. Conquests proved harder to achieve and after small victories over petty kings, King Roland found himself facing Tristifer_IV_Mudd, the Hammer of Justice, the last great king of the First Men. However, Roland's forces were shattered by Tristifer's rivermen. After fleeing to one of his allies, the King of Mountain and Vale found himself betrayed and delivered back to Tristifer in chains. Four years after leaving the Vale, King Roland II was beheaded at Oldstones, the seat of House Mudd, by Tristifer. Few mourned his passing. He was succeeded by his younger brother, Robin_Arryn, who resumed work on the Eyrie.

name: Roland Blackwood
bio: Roland Blackwood was a member of House Blackwood during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Roland participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.[N 1]

name: Roland Crakehall
bio: Roland Crakehall is the Lord of Crakehall and the head of House Crakehall. During the Sack of King's Landing, Roland, Ser Elys_Westerling, and other westermen entered the Red Keep's throne room shortly after Ser Jaime_Lannister of the Kingsguard killed King Aerys_II_Targaryen. Unsurprised to find the Mad King dead at Jaime's hand, Roland informed Jaime that King's Landing had fallen to the forces of his father, Lord Tywin_Lannister. Jaime seated himself on the Iron Throne and waited for more men to arrive, which turned out to be Lord Eddard_Stark with his northmen.

name: Roland Crakehall
bio: Roland Crakehall was a knight from House Crakehall and a member of the Kingsguard during the reigns of the kings Daeron_II_Targaryen and Aerys_I_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Roland is portrayed by Wade Briggs. Ser Roland, along with his two fellow brothers of the Kingsguard, Ser Willem_Wylde and Ser Donnel_of_Duskendale, escorted the royal Targaryen princes to Ashford for the tourney in 209 AC. He helped Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen search for his sons Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) and Aegon_V_Targaryen, who were supposed to be at the Ashford tourney. All three Kingsguard present fought on behalf of Prince Aerion_Targaryen in the trial of seven. Ser Roland was among three Kingsguard who marched on Whitewalls with Lord Brynden_Rivers to put down the Second Blackfyre Rebellion. He found Ser Duncan_the_Tall amongst the prisoners and escorted him to the Hand's pavilion to meet with him.

name: Roland Darry
bio: Roland Darry was a Lord of Darry and head of House Darry during the Dance of the Dragons. A Lord Darry and his heir were slain during the Dance when Prince Aemond_Targaryen burned Darry with the dragonflame of Vhagar, but Lady Darry and her younger children survived in the castle's vaults. Lord Derrick_Darry gathered men for Ser Addam_Velaryon's host before the Second Battle of Tumbleton. Derrick may have died during the battle, as Lord Roland later joined the host of Lord Elmo_Tully. Roland was slain by Lord Borros_Baratheon during the Battle of the Kingsroad.

name: Roland Waynwood
bio: Roland Waynwood is a knight of House Waynwood. He is a grandson of Anya_Waynwood, the Lady of Ironoaks. Roland appears to be the son of Anya's heir, Ser Morton_Waynwood.[N 1]

name: Roland Westerling
bio: Roland Westerling was the Lord of the Crag and the head of House Westerling during the Dance of the Dragons. With the coronation of the young Aegon_III_Targaryen, Lord Roland was one of the seven regents that would rule until Aegon came of age. Roland's daughter Johanna_Lannister was married to Jason_Lannister, the Lord of Casterly Rock and Warden of the West. After the death of Lord Jason during the Dance of the Dragons, rule of the westerlands passed to Lady Johanna and Lord Roland, for Jason's son and heir Loreon was a boy of four. Roland and Johanna later attended the coronation of the young Aegon_III_Targaryen at King's Landing in 131 AC. A council of regents was selected to rule the realm during Aegon's minority, with Roland selected as one of the seven regents. Lord Westerling's daughter Johanna was also a consideration for the regency. When the council discussed Aegon III's heir, Roland was against naming Princess Baela_Targaryen, for her dragon had brought down the late Aegon_II_Targaryen. He feared that crowning Baela would open old wounds among the greens. When the Winter Fever struck the capital in 133 AC, Roland grew ill and later succumbed to the disease, partly due to his age.

name: Rolder
bio: Rolder is a guard sworn to House Lannister.

name: Rolfe
bio: Rolfe is an ironborn warrior. It is unknown if he is the same character as Red_Rolfe or Rolfe_the_Dwarf.

name: Rolfe the Dwarf
bio: Rolfe the Dwarf is one of the crew on Asha_Greyjoy's ship, Black Wind.

name: Rollam Westerling
bio: Rollam Westerling is the second son of Gawen_Westerling, Lord of the Crag, and Lady Sybell_Spicer.

name: Rolland Darklyn
bio: Rolland Darklyn was a knight of House Darklyn. He was a member of the Kingsguard at an unknown time, one of seven members from House Darklyn. Ser Rolland was given his white cloak on a battlefield. Until the naming of Ser Jaime_Lannister to the Kingsguard, he had the distinction of being the youngest to ever don the white cloak. He died within an hour of receiving it.

name: Rolland Longthorpe
bio: Rolland Longthorpe is the Lord of Longsister and the head of House Longthorpe.

name: Rolland Storm
bio: Rolland Storm, called the Bastard of Nightsong, is a knight of House Caron. He is the bastard son of the late Bryen_Caron, Lord of Nightsong, and is the half-brother of Lord Bryce_Caron.

name: Rolland Uffering
bio: Rolland Uffering was a knight from House Uffering during the reign of Daeron_II_Targaryen. He was Lady Rohanne_Webber's fourth husband. Ser Rolland Uffering was the fourth husband of Lady Rohanne Webber of Coldmoat. His sister was Lady Helicent_Uffering. Ser Rolland and his wife had not been married long when he died during the Great Spring Sickness. After his death, his sister remained at Coldmoat, as the kin of other deceased husbands of Lady Rohanne had done before her. It was rumored after his death that Rohanne killed him, possibly by poison, as she supposedly had multiple husbands before him.

name: Rolley
bio: Rolley of Sisterton is a steward of the Night's Watch. His cousin is Lark. Rolley comes from Sisterton on Sweetsister, the main port of the Three Sisters.

name: Rollo
bio: Rollo was a septon of the Faith of the Seven during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. After King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen wed his sister, Alysanne_Targaryen, Jaehaerys sent seven preachers to walk the realm and win the small folk over to the marriage, not with swords, but with words. Septon Rollo was among these seven. They traveled the realm alone, walking from village to village, telling the smallfolk of Jaehaerys's wisdom and Alysanne's kindness. When challenged on the issue of incest, the seven would say the Valyrians mounted dragons and thus were not like other men, and as such, an exception had to be made.

name: Rolly Duckfield
bio: Rolly Duckfield, also known as Duck, is a knight in service to Young Griff. Rolly was born in the Reach. His father was a blacksmith at Bitterbridge in service to old Lord_Caswell. Rolly's strength and skill with a blade earned him a place in the castle garrison, but Rolly had dreams of knighthood. He resented seeing Lord Caswell's weakling son Lorent_Caswell become a page, a squire, then a knight. Lord Caswell had only one son, so no one in the castle was allowed to say a word against Lorent. In the yard the other squires barely touched him. On his sixteenth name day, Rolly's father made a longsword for Rolly as a present, but Lorent Caswell saw it and claimed it for his own, saying to Rolly that he was only fit to wield a hammer, not a sword. Rolly then fetched a hammer and broke both of Lorent's arms and half his ribs. Afterwards, Rolly quickly fled across the narrow sea to escape Lord Caswell's wrath. He joined the Golden Company as an apprentice smith for a few years, and then served Ser Harry_Strickland as a squire. When Jon_Connington needed someone to train his son Young Griff in skill at arms, Harry Strickland sent him Rolly. A year later Rolly was knighted by Griff. Having no surname, Rolly chose the name Duckfield because he was in a field with some ducks when he was knighted, earning him the nickname "Duck".

name: Rolph Spicer
bio: Rolph Spicer is a knight from House Spicer and is the castellan of the Crag for House Westerling. He is the brother of Sybell_Spicer and grandson of Maggy_the_Frog.

name: Rommo
bio: Rommo is a jaqqa rhan of the Dothraki.

name: Romny Weaver
bio: Romny Weaver is an ironborn raider from House Weaver.

name: Ronald Connington
bio: Ronald Connington was the Knight of Griffin's Roost and the head of House Connington during the reign of King Robert_I_Baratheon. He had three children: Ronnet_Connington, Raymund_Connington and Alynne_Connington. When his cousin, Lord Jon_Connington, went to King's Landing to attend Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen, Ronald was made castellan of Griffin's Roost. Lord Jon was exiled by King Aerys_II_Targaryen during Robert's Rebellion, following the loss at the Battle of the Bells in the riverlands, and stripped of his lands, titles, and wealth. Griffin's Roost next passed to Ronald. Ronald joined the rebellion, but following the conclusion of the war, the new King Robert_I_Baratheon completed the destruction of House Connington which Aerys II had begun; While Ronald was allowed to keep both his head and Griffin's Roost, he lost his lordship and a lot of the Connington wealth, while nine-tenths of the Connington lands were given to neighboring lords who had been more fervent supporters to Robert's claim. This reduced Ronald to a landed knight, carrying the title Knight of Griffin's Roost. Ronald once attempted to betroth his eldest son, Ronnet_Connington, to Brienne_Tarth, the daughter and heir to Lord Selwyn_Tarth of Tarth. Though Ronnet objected, stating that he had heard how ugly Brienne was, Ronald would not relent. Ronnet did eventually travel to Tarth to meet with Brienne, but informed her immediately that he had no interest in marrying her. Ronald was eventually succeeded as the Knight of Griffin's Roost by his heir, Ronnet.

name: Ronald Storm
bio: Ronald Storm is the bastard son of Ser Ronnet_Connington, the Knight of Griffin's Roost. He is most likely named for his grandfather, Ronald_Connington. He resides in Griffin's Roost.

name: Ronald Vance
bio: Ronald Vance, known as Ronald the Bad, is a knight from House Vance of Atranta and the eldest son and heir of Lord Norbert_Vance. He has four brothers; Ser Hugo_Vance, Ser Ellery_Vance, Ser Kirth_Vance, and Maester Jon_Vance.

name: Ronard Storm
bio: Ronard Storm, known as Ronard the Bastard, was a bastard of House Durrandon who became a Storm King. After being named castellan of Storm's End by his half-brother, King Morden_II_Durrandon, Ronard effectively ruled the stormlands and helped to slow the decline of House Durrandon. Ronard was wed to Morden's sister. Within five years Ronard had replaced Morden as Storm King, with Morden's queen placing Morden's crown atop Ronard's head. She also allegedly slept with the new king as well. Instead of executing Morden, Ronard confined his harmless half-brother to a tower cell. During Ronard's rule of nearly thirty years, he defeated several rebel bannermen and petty kings in battle, taking a daughter from each adversary who surrendered. According to singers, Ronard had twenty-three wives and ninety-nine sons, most of them bastards without inheritance. Many smallfolk of the stormlands thus claim to have royal blood thousands of years later.

name: Ronel Rivers
bio: Ronel Rivers is a member of House Frey and a bastard son of Lord Walder_Frey. Ronel's mother has not been mentioned.

name: Ronnal Baratheon
bio: Ronnal Baratheon was a knight from House Baratheon and a younger brother of Lord Rogar_Baratheon of Storm's End. When Lord Rogar_Baratheon was dismissed as Hand of the King, he sent his brother Ronnal to retrieve Princess Aerea_Targaryen from her quarters, but the girl had been spirited away on the orders of Alyssa_Velaryon, the Queen Regent. Thereafter Lord Rogar and his brothers returned to Storm's End. When Rogar was summoned to King's Landing by King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen in 50 AC, Ronnal and his brother Ser Garon_Baratheon urged Rogar to defy the summons. The Shivers hit Westeros during the winter of 59–60 AC, Ronnal, his wife, his sons, as well as Garon's wife all perished of the disease at Storm's End. In the aftermath of the winter, Lord Rogar came to King's Landing with Ronnal's two surviving daughters and his own daughter, Jocelyn_Baratheon, to ask King Jaehaerys I and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen to foster the three Baratheon girls at court. The royal couple accepted.

name: Ronnel Arryn
bio: Ronnel Arryn was the last King of Mountain and Vale, reigning until Aegon's Conquest. Since Ronnel was a boy king, rule of the Vale of Arryn fell to his mother, Queen Regent Sharra_Arryn, regarded as one of the most beautiful women of Westeros.

name: Ronnel Arryn
bio: Ronnel Arryn was a knight of House Arryn and was the younger brother of Lord Jon_Arryn. He was wed to a noblewoman from House Belmore. They had one son, Elbert_Arryn. After Jon_Arryn ascended to the lordship he named Ronnel as the Keeper of the Gates of the Moon. Ronnel died of a bad belly at roughly the same time as his son Elbert_Arryn was born.

name: Ronnel Harclay
bio: Ronnel Harclay is a ranger of the Night's Watch from House Harclay.

name: Ronnel Penrose
bio: Ronnel Penrose was the Lord of Parchments and the head of House Penrose during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. He later married Princess Elaena_Targaryen and had four children together. Though Ronnel Penrose sat on the king's small council as master of coin, it was widely known that his wife Elaena was the one performing the duties of the office.

name: Ronnel Stout
bio: Ronnel Stout is a member of House Stout.

name: Ronnel Vance
bio: Ronnel Vance was the head of House Vance during the reign of Maegor_I_Targaryen. It is unknown if he was the Lord of Atranta or Wayfarer's Rest. After declaring his uncle Maegor_I_Targaryen a usurper and a tyrant, Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) gathered his supporters at Pinkmaiden Castle in the riverlands. Lord Ronnel Vance was one of the several lords from the riverlands to join his strength to Aegon's cause. However, Ronnel died with the rebels at the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye in 43 AC, slain by Maegor's loyalists alongside the prince. On account of Ronnel's support to Prince Aegon, Dowager Queen Alyssa_Velaryon later considered wedding her son King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen to a Vance, posthumously rewarding Ronnel's loyalty to her late son.

name: Ronnet Connington
bio: Ronnet Connington, also known as Red Ronnet, is the Knight of Griffin's Roost. He is a landed knight and the head of House Connington. Ronnet has a bastard son, Ronald_Storm. Once lords, House Connington were demoted to landed knights as a result of Robert's Rebellion. Ser Ronald_Connington, the Knight of Griffin's Roost, betrothed his eighteen-year-old son Ronnet to Brienne_Tarth, the daughter of Selwyn_Tarth, the Lord of Tarth. Ronnet was opposed, having been told that Brienne was ugly. When they first met at Evenfall Hall, the two had the same height, although Ronnet was six years older. Brienne remembers how Ronnet brought a rose to give to his betrothed. She was not able to look him in the eye or speak the simple words of welcome that she had been taught, however. When Ronnet handed Brienne the rose, he told her that this was all she would ever have from him, as he had no real interest in marrying her.

name: Rook
bio: Rook is one of the crew on Asha_Greyjoy's ship, Black Wind.

name: Roone
bio: Roone is a young novice at the Citadel.

name: Roone
bio: Roone is the maester at Lord Lychester's keep.

name: Roose Bolton
bio: Roose Bolton is the Lord of the Dreadfort and the head of House Bolton. Some refer to him as Lord Leech or the Leech Lord, though not to his face. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Roose is portrayed by Michael McElhatton. Roose practices the abolished tradition of the first night, but he is discreet with his activities and amusements in order to avoid the attention and possible ire of the Wardens of the North, House Stark.
