While hunting along the Weeping Water, Roose once raped a young Miller's_wife and hanged the miller for not gaining his approval for their marriage. When the woman later presented a baby to Roose, he spared the bastard boy Ramsay_Snow because he had the same pale eyes as Roose. Lord Bolton granted the mill to the woman, sent her annual supplies and funds, and had the tongue of the miller's brother cut out to prevent Lord Rickard_Stark from learning what had happened. Roose fought at the Battle of the Trident during Robert's Rebellion. When Ser Barristan_Selmy, seriously wounded, was brought before Lord Robert_I_Baratheon, Roose counselled that they should kill him. Robert ignored Roose and spared Barristan's life, sending his own maester to tend to the Kingsguard's wounds. After the death of his first wife, Roose was married to Bethany_Bolton, with whom he had one son survive the cradle, Domeric_Bolton. Roose's children who did not survive the cradle have been buried below the Dreadfort. Roose had Domeric serve as a page to Bethany's sister, Lady Barbrey_Dustin, in Barrowton and as a squire to Lord Horton_Redfort at the Redfort. Roose had Reek, a servant at the Dreadfort who always smelled terrible, whipped for stealing Bethany's perfume in an attempt to mask his stench. When the mother of his bastard son appeared at the Dreadfort demanding Roose provide her with a servant to help in raising her son, Roose sent her Reek, an act which he intended to be amusing. Roose eventually became a widower when his wife Bethany died of a fever. In 297 AC,[N 1] Domeric died after visiting his half brother, Ramsay. Maester Uthor blamed a bad belly, but Lord Bolton attributes his heir's death to poisoning by Ramsay. Having no other trueborn heirs, Roose brought Ramsay to the Dreadfort but is not believed to have acknowledged him.

name: Roose Ryswell
bio: Roose Ryswell is a son of Lord Rodrik_Ryswell and a member of House Ryswell.

name: Rorge
bio: Rorge is one of the criminals Yoren recruits to the Night's Watch when he cleans out the dungeons of King’s Landing. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Rorge is portrayed by Andy Beckwith. According to a semi-canon source, Rorge owned a pot shop or tavern in Flea Bottom, that also served as a rat pit where he would stage rat fights, dog fights, and bear cub fights for his profit. At some point, he found the orphan child Biter, and raised him ferally to compete in these fights.

name: Roro Uhoris
bio: Roro Uhoris was a Tyroshi smuggler and pirate, captain of the Cobblecat. He was known as the Blind Bastard up and down the narrow sea though he was neither blind nor a bastard. Roro had sailed the Cobblecat past the Wall, into a hundred little coves that had never seen a trading ship before, and traded arms to the wildlings in exchange for furs and other precious goods. On his return south of the Wall, the Cobblecat was intercepted by a Night's Watch fleet and escorted to Eastwatch-by-the-sea. There, Roro was executed on the deck of his ship for trading arms to the wildlings, and all the goods that he had received were confiscated.

name: Rory
bio: Rory is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Roryn Drumm
bio: Roryn Drumm, known as Roryn the Reaver, was a member of House Drumm.

name: Rosamund Ball
bio: Rosamund Ball was a noblewoman of House Ball during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Rosamund served as a lady and companion for Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. When her queen was attacked by the order of holy sisters while attempting to bathe in the sacred waters of Jonquil's Pool at Maidenpool, Rosamund, like the rest of the queen's ladies, did not hesitate to put herself between the queen and her would-be assassins. Rosamund was stabbed in the stomach during the attack. The shouts alerted the Kingsguard who were guarding the entrance of the bathhouse, and the knights intervened and ended the assassins plot. Rosamund died a few days later from the wound.

name: Rosamund Darry
bio: Rosamund Darry was a member of House Darry during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 133 AC, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Many maidens across Westeros came to King's Landing in hopes of marrying the young king. Rosamund was one of the many maidens who suffered what was called the "Maiden's Day curse" by being injured or scandalized after the ball was announced. In Rosamund's case, she was rumored to have six nipples, as her mother had supposedly lain with a dog. It is possible that this was a rumor fabricated by Lord Unwin_Peake, the Hand of the King, who wished to promote his Myrielle_Peake as Aegon III's second queen.

name: Rosamund Lannister
bio: Rosamund Lannister is a noblewoman of House Lannister of Lannisport. Rosamund accompanies Myrcella_Baratheon as a handmaid when the princess goes to Dorne. She is chosen by Tyrion_Lannister, the acting Hand of the King, to accompany Myrcella due to similarities in height and looks from afar, although Rosamund's hair is straight while Myrcella's is curly. Because of the risk of the Seaswift being taken by Stannis_Baratheon's forces on the way to Braavos, Septa Eglantine puts brown dye in Myrcella's hair to disguise the princess, and Rosamund wears her clothes.

name: Lord Rosby
bio: Lord Rosby was the Lord of Rosby and the head of House Rosby during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen.

name: Rose of Red Lake
bio: Rose of Red Lake is a legendary daughter of Garth_Greenhand and ancestor of House Crane of Red Lake. According to legend, Rose could transform into a crane at will. Some say this ability manifests in women of House Crane from time to time.

name: Rosey
bio: Rosey is the daughter of Emma, a serving wench at the Quill and Tankard in Oldtown.

name: Rosey (daughter of Aegon IV)
bio: Rosey was the youngest of the four bastard children of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen and his second mistress, Megette. Rosey's mother Megette was the mistress of Prince Aegon for four years. Rosey was the youngest of four daughters. After Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen discovered this, Megette was returned to her husband in the riverlands, while Rosey and her three sisters were given to the Faith to be trained as a septa.

name: Roslin Frey
bio: Roslin Frey is a noblewoman of House Frey, the only daughter of Lord Walder_Frey by his sixth wife, Lady Bethany_Rosby, but his fifth overall. Roslin has four full older brothers—Ser Perwyn_Frey, Ser Benfrey_Frey, Maester Willamen_Frey, and Olyvar_Frey—and many half-siblings. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Roslin is portrayed by Alexandra Dowling.

name: Rossart
bio: Lord Rossart was a member of the Alchemists' Guild in King's Landing. He served as the Hand of the King to Aerys_II_Targaryen for a fortnight. Rossart was the Grand Master of the Alchemists' Guild during the reign of Aerys II, who granted him the title of Lord and a seat on the small council. He was also the pyromancer who had cooked Lord Rickard_Stark in his armor, and one of the key players in the wildfire plot. During the latter stage of Robert's Rebellion, Lord Qarlton_Chelsted, the Hand of the King at that time, was burned alive by order of Aerys before the Battle of the Trident, and after the battle the king made Rossart his new Hand. As his personal arms he chose a burning torch. As the Sack of King's Landing began, Rossart disguised as a common man-at-arms rushed to fulfill Aerys's command to burn the city down, but he was killed by Ser Jaime_Lannister before he could carry out his orders, Rossart futilely tried to put up a fight. In the recorded history, the Maesters, ignorant of the wildfire plot write that Rossart was killed while attempting to flee the castle. They do not even speculate who killed him and apparently no one even inquired.

name: Rowan
bio: Rowan is a spearwife of the free folk.

name: Rowan Gold-Tree
bio: Rowan Gold-Tree is a legendary daughter of Garth_Greenhand. According to legend, Rowan was so bereft when her lover left her for a rich rival that she wrapped an apple in her golden hair and planted it upon a hill. From the apple grew a tree whose bark, leaves and fruit were yellow gold. House Rowan traces their roots to Rowan's daughters. In some tales of the Reach, Lann_the_Clever was a bastard born to Rowan or her sister, Florys_the_Fox.

name: Rowena Arryn
bio: Rowena Arryn was a noblewoman of House Arryn, the cousin and second wife of Lord Jon_Arryn. Rowena died childless of a winter chill.

name: Rowley
bio: Rowley was a maester who became Lord Confessor for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Rowley studied at the Citadel under Archmaester Sandeman, a wise healer. After the downfall of George_Graceford, young Rowley was appointed as the new Lord Confessor by Lord Torrhen_Manderly, the Hand of the King, in 136 AC.

name: Roy Connington
bio: Roy Connington, known as Red Roy Connington, was the Lord of Griffin's Roost and the head of House Connington during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Lord Roy, Jonah_Mooton, and Ser Braxton_Beesbury became the three male favorites of Princess Saera_Targaryen in 82 AC. Joined by Alys_Turnberry and Perianne_Moore, the young men and women became inseparable at every feast and ball, hunted and hawked together, and once sailed across Blackwater Bay to Dragonstone. In 84 AC, Roy, Jonah, and Braxton were found drunk at the Blue Pearl, a brothel in King's Landing, laughing as the king's fool, Tom_Turnip, was surrounded by whores. When asked by men of the City Watch, Roy admitted they thought it would be funny to see the fool do the deed, and Jonah admitted it was Princess Saera's idea. When Perianne and Alys were questioned about the incident, it was revealed that Alys was pregnant outside of wedlock, and that Saera had coerced them into sexual acts with her male favorites. Roy, Jonah, and Braxton were then imprisoned in the dungeons for one night. When questioned by King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen, Saera defended herself by saying Roy would marry her. She confessed that she had bedded all three of her male favorites, with each of them thinking he was the first to take her maidenhead. She proposed a solution of redemption by marrying all three of her favorites, like King Maegor_I_Targaryen and his six wives, at which the king grew furious. A hasty marriage was proposed between Lord Roy and the pregnant Alys Turnberry, but he refused, claiming the child was Braxton's bastard whom he would not make heir to Griffin's Roost. Nevertheless, the child born to Alys was a girl with red hair, signifying she was likely fathered by Lord Roy. King Jaehaerys gave Roy the choice of his punishment: joining the Night's Watch or ten years of exile. Roy chose exile, going first to Pentos and then Myr. He never returned to Westeros, as he was stabbed to death by a whore only half a year before the end of his exile.

name: Royce Baratheon
bio: Royce Baratheon[N 1] was the Lord of Storm's End and the head of House Baratheon during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Royce was the posthumous son of Lord Borros_Baratheon of Storm's End by his wife, Lady Elenda_Caron. Born seven days after his father died at the Battle of the Kingsroad, Royce succeeded him as the Lord of Storm's End and Lord Paramount of the Stormlands at birth. If a boy, Lord Borros had instructed Elenda to name their child Aegon, after King Aegon_II_Targaryen. However, she instead named him after her father, Lord Royce_Caron of Nightsong. During Royce's infancy, his mother Elenda ruled as regent. With the death of Aegon II and the ascension of King Aegon_III_Targaryen, Lady Elenda decided to not renew the war, fearing for her son's safety, for he was the future of House Baratheon.

name: Royce II Bolton
bio: Royce II Bolton was a Red King of the Dreadfort from the dynasty of House Bolton. During one of the wars between Houses Bolton and Stark, King Royce succeeded in taking and burning Winterfell. His namesake King Royce_IV_Bolton did the same three hundred years later.

name: Royce IV Bolton
bio: Royce IV Bolton was a Red King of the Dreadfort from the dynasty of House Bolton. During one of the wars between Houses Bolton and Stark, King Royce succeeded in taking and burning Winterfell just like his namesake King Royce_II_Bolton had done three hundred years earlier.

name: Royce Blackwood
bio: Royce Blackwood was a member of House Blackwood during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Royce's father, Lord Blackwood, attempted to betroth his son to Princess Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I) when the girl was brought to Raventree Hall by Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. Although Daella was charmed by Royce's singing, the princess lost interest when she learned that House Blackwood followed the old gods instead of the Faith of the Seven.

name: Royce Caron
bio: Royce Caron was the Lord of Nightsong, Lord of the Marches, and head of House Caron during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons and the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. After the Hour of the Wolf, lords and ladies from all around Westeros came to King's Landing to assist to the coronation of Prince Aegon_III_Targaryen and his marriage to Princess Jaehaera_Targaryen. Royce's daughter, Lady Elenda, gave birth to a son seven days after the death of her husband, Lord Borros_Baratheon of Storm's End. Lord Borros had wished Elenda to name their son Aegon after King Aegon_II_Targaryen but she named him Royce after her father instead. Weakened by the delivery, the newly widowed Lady of Storm's End could not make the travel to the capital. Instead she sent her daughters Cassandra_Baratheon, Floris_Baratheon and Ellyn_Baratheon to represent House Baratheon with Lord Royce escorting his granddaughters to the city and acting as their guardian. After the coronation of King Aegon_III_Targaryen, Royce became a member of the council of seven regents that ruled until the child-king came of age. Lord Royce, however, resigned his post in 132 AC to return to the Dornish Marches where the Dornishmen, encouraged by their new ruler Princess Aliandra_Martell, raided and plundered the region. He was not able to return to court the following year after the death of the Hand of the King, Ser Tyland_Lannister, as he was still dealing with the Dornishmen.

name: Royce Coldwater
bio: Royce Coldwater is the Lord of Coldwater Burn and the head of House Coldwater.

name: Rudge
bio: Rudge is a steward of the Night's Watch.

name: Rue
bio: Rue was the author of Bastard Born. Rue may or may not have been a septa, and may or may not have been one of Lord Alyn_Velaryon's paramours. Rue wrote an account of Lord Alyn_Velaryon's life called Bastard Born. Archmaester Gyldayn considers this account to be colorful, but unreliable.

name: Rufus Leek
bio: Rufus Leek is a knight of House Leek and the castellan of the Dun Fort in Duskendale.

name: Rugen
bio: Rugen is an undergaoler in charge of the third level of the dungeons of the Red Keep, known as the black cells. Rugen was appointed as an undergaoler during the reign of Aerys_II_Targaryen.

name: Runceford Redwyne
bio: Runceford Redwyne was a Lord of the Arbor and a head of House Redwyne. He was the father of Lady Olenna Tyrell and grandfather of Lord Paxter_Redwyne.

name: Runcel Hightower
bio: Runcel Hightower was a nobleman of House Hightower and a former Lord Commander of the Night's Watch. Some five hundred years ago, Lord Commander Runcel forgot his vows and attempted to bequeath Castle Black and the Night's Watch to his bastard son. Runcel's ambitions almost destroyed the Night's Watch, but his efforts to subvert the traditional electoral system of the Night's Watch ultimately failed.

name: Runciter
bio: Runciter was a Grand Maester during the late reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and the early reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. Runciter was appointed in 101 AC after the death of Grand Maester Allar. He had only been in King's Landing a short time before Prince Baelon Targaryen fell ill, complaining of severe pain in his stomach. Runciter treated Baelon and was able to bring his fever down and eased his pain with milk of the poppy, but the prince's condition continued to worsen until he died on the fifth day of his illness. Runciter opened the corpse and attributed the prince's death to a burst belly. Runciter later served King Viserys_I_Targaryen after Jaehaerys passed away in 103 AC. In his records, he only cites a quarrel with King Viserys as the reason for Prince Daemon_Targaryen's second exile. He died in 112 AC and was replaced by Grand Maester Mellos.

name: Rupert Brax
bio: Rupert Brax is a knight from House Brax. He is the brother of Lord Andros_Brax of Hornvale.

name: Rupert Crabb
bio: Rupert Crabb was a knight from House Crabb, who became a member of the Kingsguard at an unknown time.

name: Rupert Falwell
bio: Rupert Falwell, known as the Fighting Fool, was a Lord of House Falwell during the Faith Militant uprising. At the battle at the Great Fork of the Blackwater in 42 AC, Lord Rupert led the ranks of the pious that had answered the High_Septon_(Aenys_I) and the Starry Sept's call to took up arms against the Iron Throne. Those that rode with him against the royal army led by King Maegor_I_Targaryen himself, included Ser Lyonel_Lorch, Ser Alyn_Terrick, Lord Tristifer_Wayn, Lord Jon_Lychester, along with many other puissant knights. The famed Fighting Fool slew two knights of the Kingsguard before being cut down by Lord Mooton of Maidenpool.

name: Rus
bio: Rus is one of Aeron_Greyjoy's drowned men.

name: Russ Barleycorn
bio: Russ Barleycorn is a member of the Night's Watch. He is captain of the Storm Crow at Eastwatch-by-the-Sea. He shares a surname with Tom_Barleycorn, a ranger.

name: Russell Merryweather
bio: Russell Merryweather is the sole child of Lord Orton_Merryweather and Lady Taena of Myr. He is the heir to Longtable. Russell was born at Longtable.

name: Russell Stillman
bio: Russell Stillman was the former squire of Lord Alyn_Velaryon and the author of Hard as Oak. During Alyn_Velaryon's fifth voyage, Russell was knighted by Lord Alyn before losing a leg. He later wrote a book about Lord Alyn Velaryon's life called Hard as Oak. Archmaester Gyldayn considers this account to be colorful, but unreliable.

name: Rusty Flowers
bio: Rusty Flowers is a member of the Night's Watch. His surname suggests he is a bastard from the Reach.

name: Lord Ruthermont
bio: Lord Ruthermont was an Andal lord during the Andal invasion of the Vale. During the Andal Invasion of the Vale, Lord Ruthermont was part of the Andal coalition led by Ser Artys_I_Arryn. He fought in the Battle of the Seven Stars and some named him as the killer of King Robar_II_Royce, the leader of the First Men coalition.

name: Ryam
bio: Ryam is an archmaester of the Citadel. He has a poor opinion of Archmaester Marwyn. His ring, rod, and mask are of yellow gold. His maester's chain has no link of Valyrian steel.

name: Ryam Florent
bio: Ryam Florent was a knight of House Florent, the father of Selyse_Florent, Imry_Florent and Erren_Florent. He was a younger brother of Lord Alester_Florent. Ryam died of a fall from a horse.

name: Ryam Redwyne
bio: Ryam Redwyne was a famed knight from House Redwyne who served as Lord Commander of the Kingsguard under kings Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Viserys_I_Targaryen. He also briefly served as Hand of the King for Jaehaerys I. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Ryam is portrayed by Garry Cooper. Ryam and his older brothers, Robert_Redwyne and Rickard_Redwyne, accompanied their father, Lord Manfryd_Redwyne, to King's Landing as squires when King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen named Manfryd as master of ships in 50 AC. During the tourney celebrating Jaehaerys's tenth year of coronation, young Ryam unhorsed Ser Ronnal_Baratheon, Ser Arthor_Oakheart, Lord Simon_Dondarrion, and Ser Harys_Hogg, as well as Ser Lorence_Roxton and Ser Lucamore_Strong of the Kingsguard. Ryam crowned Queen Alysanne_Targaryen as the tourney's queen of love and beauty. Ser Gyles_Morrigen, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, recommended that Ryam and Ser Robin_Shaw join the white cloaks in 60 AC after two previous knights had perished from the Shivers. In late 73 AC Ryam discovered that his sworn brother, Ser Lucamore Strong, had broken his Kingsguard vows of not taking a wife or fathering children. Lucamore the Lusty had fathered sixteen children among three women he had secretly wed, with each woman unaware of the others. Ryam brought the revelation to an enraged Gyles. Lucamore was gelded by his former sworn brothers of the Kingsguard, after which King Jaehaerys sent the disgraced knight to the Wall to join the Night's Watch. Ryam was the victor of a tourney held in Jaehaerys's honor at Oldtown in 89 AC. In a tourney at King's Landing to celebrate the fiftieth year of Jaehaerys's reign in 98 AC, Ryam and Ser Clement_Crabb broke thirty lances against each other before the king proclaimed them co-champions. It is often called the finest display of jousting in the history of Westeros. In 99 AC, Ryam, the Lord Commander, was named Hand of the King by Jaehaerys, after the death of the previous Hand, Septon Barth. Considered one of the worst Hands the Seven Kingdoms has seen, Ryam was replaced by the king's son and heir, Prince Baelon Targaryen, less than a year after having been raised to the office. Ryam served King Viserys_I_Targaryen after the death of Jaehaerys in 103 AC until he died in 105 AC. His slot in the Kingsguard was taken by Ser Criston_Cole. Ser Duncan_the_Tall once recalled that Ryam won victories against terrible foes. When playing as children, Robb_Stark pretended to be King Daeron_I_Targaryen, while Jon_Snow pretended to be Ser Ryam.

name: Ryben
bio: Ryben was a maester of the Citadel. Maester Ryben is dismissive of the story told by Coryanne_Wylde in a A Caution for Young Girls that she was ordered by Lord Rogar_Baratheon to try and seduce King Jaehaerys while he was in his minority on Dragonstone. Ryben considered it nothing more than a tale to excite people.

name: Rycherd Crane
bio: Rycherd Crane is a knight from House Crane and is the husband of Rylene_Florent, the sister of Lord Alester_Florent of Brightwater Keep.

name: Ryella Frey
bio: Ryella Frey is the eldest child of Ser Arwood_Frey and Ryella_Royce.

name: Ryella Royce
bio: Ryella Royce is a noblewoman of House Royce and the wife of Ser Arwood_Frey, a grandson of Lord Walder_Frey of the Twins. They have four young children together; Ryella_Frey, Androw_Frey, Alyn_Frey and Hostella_Frey.

name: Ryger Rivers
bio: Ryger Rivers is a bastard son of Walder_Frey, Lord of the Crossing. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Ryger is portrayed by Bryan McCaugherty. Ryger's mother was a peasant who milked goats before Lord Walder got her pregnant with Ryger.

name: Ryk
bio: Ryk, known as Longspear Ryk, is a free folk raider and a member of Rattleshirt's war band. Ryk is from the same village as Ygritte, who thinks of him as a brother. He broke the arm of Ygritte's first lover after he tried to take her a second time; both of them deemed him a weak partner for her.

name: Rylene Florent
bio: Rylene Florent is the only sister of Lord Alester_Florent of Brightwater Keep. She is married to Ser Rycherd_Crane.

name: Ryles
bio: Ryles is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Rylona Rhee
bio: Rylona Rhee is a former slave freed by Daenerys_Targaryen. Rylona was enslaved and trained in Yunkai to play music for many of the noble families of the city.

name: Ryman Frey
bio: Ryman Frey is a knight from House Frey of the Twins. He is the eldest son of Ser Stevron_Frey, and second in line to inherit the title of Lord of the Crossing. Ser Ryman participated in the tourney at Lannisport, the tourney that was held to celebrate King Robert_I_Baratheon's victory over Greyjoy's Rebellion. He was defeated by the eventual champion, Lord Jorah_Mormont.

name: Rymolf Stormdrunk
bio: Rymolf Stormdrunk is an ironborn steersman in the crew of Victarion_Greyjoy.

name: Rymond Arryn
bio: Rymond Arryn was a knight from House Arryn during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 54 AC, Ser Rymond and his brother, Lord Darnold_Arryn, were killed at the hands of mountain clan raiders they had unwisely pursued into the Mountains of the Moon. After their deaths, Rymond's ten-year-old son Rodrik_Arryn became Lord of the Eyrie.

name: Rymund the Rhymer
bio: Rymund the Rhymer is a singer and harper.

name: Anvil Ryn
bio: Anvil Ryn is an inhabitant of the riverlands.

name: Ryon Allyrion
bio: Ryon Allyrion is a knight from House Allyrion. He is the son and heir of Delonne_Allyrion, Lady of Godsgrace. Ryon is married to Ynys_Yronwood, with whom he has children, at least two sons. He also has a bastard son, Ser Daemon_Sand.

name: Lord Ryswell (Night's Watch)
bio: Lord Ryswell was lord of House Ryswell at some point, and his Lord_Ryswell's_youngest_son had joined the Night's Watch. His given name is unknown. Lord Ryswell's Lord_Ryswell's_youngest_son was one of the legendary seventy-nine sentinels who deserted their posts at the Nightfort and went south to the lands of House Ryswell. They hoped that Lord Ryswell would aid them, but instead they were captured and imprisoned within the Wall. Lord Ryswell, as he was dying, had himself carried to the Wall to take the black of the Night's Watch, so he could stand beside the son that he had condemned to certain death.

name: Lord Ryswell's youngest son
bio: Lord Ryswell's youngest son was a son to Lord_Ryswell_(Night's_Watch), and eventually joined the Night's Watch. His given name is unknown. Lord Ryswell's son was one of the legendary seventy-nine sentinels who deserted their posts at the Nightfort and went south of the Wall. He led them to the lands of House Ryswell, hoping his father would aid them, but instead they were captured and brought back to the Wall. The son and his fellow deserters were then sealed within the Wall as punishment.

name: S'vrone
bio: S'vrone is a dockside whore in Braavos of a murderous bent.

name: Saathos the Wise
bio: Saathos the Wise was a visitor to Qarth. According to legend, he thought the city so beautiful that he blinded himself because he knew that all he would see after would be ugly in comparison.

name: Saathos Saan
bio: Saathos Saan was a Lysene pirate-lord who named himself King of the Basilisk Isles. He was a member of the Saan family and ancestor of Salladhor_Saan. The exact time when he lived is not clear, but it was apparently between the reign of Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and the War of the Five Kings. The pirates of the Basilisk Isles profited from the chaos of the Century of Blood, until seventy-seven years after the Doom of Valyria, when a great plague known as the Red Death swept out of the slave pens and killed nine out of every ten persons, with the rest fleeing. The isles remained totally uninhabited for a full century afterwards, when (during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen) the Qartheen pirate Xandarro_Xhore established himself on the Isle of Toads and the Brotherhood of Bones established themselves on the isles to the west. Ever since, once every generation a major fleet has been sent from the Free Cities to clear out the pirates. Some have ended in failure, but even the successful campaigns seem ultimately futile because new pirates always refill the isles shortly thereafter. One infamous expedition was led by Saathos Saan. Though he was sent to eliminate the pirates, Saan and his fleet instead turned pirate themselves. He rose to such prominence that he united and reigned the islands as King of the Basilisk Isles for thirty years.

name: Sabitha Vypren
bio: Sabitha Frey, née Vypren, was a noblewoman of House Vypren and the wife of Lord Forrest_Frey, the head of House Frey and the Lord of the Crossing during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Sabitha is portrayed by Sarah Woodward.

name: Saera Targaryen
bio: Princess Saera Targaryen was the ninthborn child and fifthborn daughter of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. Saera was born in 67 AC as the ninth child and fifth daughter of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. She was a difficult child, and refused to be weaned until past the age of four. She was also given to mischief and pranks. Saera always sneaked cats into the bedchamber of her sister Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I), knowing that Daella was afraid of cats, and once filled Daella's chamberpot with bees.[N 1] When she was ten years old, Saera slipped into White Sword Tower, stole all the white cloaks of the Kingsguard that she could get her hands on and dyed them pink. When she was seven, Saera learned when and how to slip into the kitchens, making off with all kinds of treats. Before she was eleven, she had taken to stealing alcoholic beverages instead. By the time she was twelve, she was often drunk when she was summoned to the royal sept. A frequent victim of Saera's pranks was Tom_Turnip, the court fool. Once, after a particularly cruel prank which resulted in Tom being injured, Saera's septa declared her to be "an evil child". Before she had even turned thirteen, Saera had already had half a dozen septas and as many bedmaids. In 73 AC, after her parents had concluded that her siblings Vaegon_Targaryen and Daella should not wed each other, Saera was briefly considered as a bride for Vaegon. After her flowering, Saera took to the men at the royal court. When she was fourteen years old, Saera told her father that she wanted to become a queen like her mother, by wedding the Prince of Dorne or the King-Beyond-the-Wall. When she saw a trader from the Summer Islands, she declared that she might want to marry him as well. By the time she was fifteen, however, she had come to entertain other fantasies. At home, she could have as many young men, squires, knights and lords, as she liked. She quickly had three favorites of all the men who attended her: Jonah_Mooton, the heir to Maidenpool, Roy_Connington, the Lord of Griffin's Roost, and Ser Braxton_Beesbury, the heir to Honeyholt. Meanwhile, two girls her own age, Perianne_Moore and Alys_Turnberry, became Saera's closest friends. For more than a year, the three girls and young lordlings did everything together. King Jaehaerys considered all three of Saera's male favorites promising when considering a future husband for Saera. Queen Alysanne did not agree, and spoke of her doubts on Saera's companions, especially Ser Braxton. Jaehaerys waved her concerns away, claiming that Saera had never been alone with any of the men, as she was always attended by servants, and thus could not possibly get herself in trouble.

name: Saffron
bio: Saffron is the daughter of a spice merchant from Gulltown, the richest man in the city.

name: Sailor's Wife
bio: The Sailor's Wife is a prostitute who works at the Happy Port brothel in Braavos. The Sailor's Wife's first husband was lost at sea when she was no older than fourteen. Every month when her flower is in bloom, she visits the temples on the Isle of the Gods to pray for his return. However, according to the prostitute Yna who has known her the longest and who tasted her blood, her first husband is dead.

name: Salladhor Saan
bio: Salladhor Saan, known as Salla to his friends, is a notorious Lysene pirate, sellsail, smuggler, trader, and banker. He styles himself the Prince of the Narrow Sea and is a descendant of the Saan family. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Salladhor is portrayed by Lucian Msamati. The Saan family have a long and distinguished history as pirate-lords stretching back at least three centuries to the reign of Aegon_I_Targaryen. One of his relatives, Samarro_Saan, was one of the infamous Band of Nine. Before becoming a knight during Robert's Rebellion, the one-time smuggler Ser Davos_Seaworth often bought cargo from Salladhor.

name: Sallei Paege
bio: Sallei Paege is a noblewoman of House Paege and is married to Ser Jammos_Frey with whom she has three children, Walder_Frey_(son_of_Jammos), Dickon_Frey and Mathis_Frey

name: Sallor the Bald
bio: Sallor the Bald is a Qartheen captain of the mercenary company, Stormcrows.

name: Salloreon
bio: Salloreon is a master armorer in King's Landing.

name: Sam Stoops
bio: Sam Stoops was the steward of Standfast for House Osgrey during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. When Ser Duncan_the_Tall trained smallfolk, Sam stuffed some old sacks with soiled straw for them to practice on. he also provided straw pallets in the undercroft of Standfast for the recruits to rest upon. He began praying when he saw Wat's Wood afire. While Ser Eustace_Osgrey was away, Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield tied up Sam and Sam_Stoops'_wife, and stole the remaining valuables from Standfast.

name: Sam Stoops' wife
bio: Sam Stoops' wife was an old serving woman at Standfast during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. The name of Sam_Stoops' spouse is unknown. Ser Duncan_the_Tall observed the woman cleaning the hearth at Standfast and washing clothes in the undercellar. She provided bowls of stew for all, thick with carrots, onions, and barley for Duncan's recruits. The servant also sewed the clothes of the late Addam_Osgrey so they would be wearable for Aegon_V_Targaryen. While Ser Eustace_Osgrey was away, Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield tied up Sam and his wife, and stole the remaining valuables from Standfast.

name: Sam Salt
bio: Sam Salt was a pretender Lord of the Iron Islands who claimed descent from the black line, House Hoare. In 133 AC, Lord Dalton_Greyjoy was murdered at Faircastle by his salt wife, Tess. In the bloody struggle for succession that ensued on the Iron Islands between his salt sons, sisters, and cousins, Sam Salt emerged as a pretender.

name: Samantha Stokeworth
bio: Samantha Stokeworth was a member of House Stokeworth in the reigns of kings Aenys_I_Targaryen, Maegor_I_Targaryen, and Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Samantha was the daughter of Lord Alyn_Stokeworth of Stokeworth, who was Hand of the King at the end of King Aegon_I_Targaryen's reign, and at the beginning of the reign of his successor, King Aenys_I_Targaryen. At court, she became a favorite of Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I). Samantha arrived on Fair Isle at the beginning of 49 AC for Rhaena's wedding to Androw_Farman. She stayed with the princess at Faircastle, where she, Rhaena, Alayne_Royce, and Elissa_Farman became nigh inseparable and often rode Rhaena's dragon Dreamfyre together. Ser Franklyn_Farman dubbed the four friends as, "the Four-Headed Beast". When Rhaena left Fair Isle upon the ascension of Lord Franklyn, Samantha came with her. At Casterly Rock, she was asked by a castle septa if the marriage between Princess Rhaena and Androw had ever been consummated. Samantha was later part of Rhaena's court on Dragonstone. Samantha died at the end of 54 AC. At first, it was thought she had died of an illness. Later, it was revealed that she had been poisoned by Androw Farman, who was jealous of Rhaena's friendships with her ladies and weary of their mockery toward him.

name: Samantha Tarly
bio: Samantha Tarly, known as Lady Sam, was a member of House Tarly who wed first Lord Ormund_Hightower and later his son, Lord Lyonel_Hightower.  She was the founder of the Bank of Oldtown. A High_Septon_(Aegon_III) eventually reversed the position of his predecessor, allowing Lyonel to marry Sam, and by the same occasion legitimized their six chidren. Along with Johanna_Lannister, Sharis_Footly, and Alys_Rivers, Samantha is featured in Archmaester Abelon's When Women Ruled: Ladies of the Aftermath.

name: Samarro Saan
bio: Samarro Saan, known as the Last Valyrian, was a Lysene pirate who became one of the infamous Band of Nine, the instigators of the War of the Ninepenny Kings. He is an ancestor of Salladhor_Saan. The Band of Nine were a group of merchants, sellswords, and pirates, who joined together to carve kingdoms out for themselves. Samarro Saan was one of them, a notorious pirate from a notorious family of pirates from Lys.

name: Samgood of Sour Hill
bio: Samgood of Sour Hill, known as Sour Sam, was a knight of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Prior to this, he was a hedge knight and boasted to be a veteran of hundreds of battles. He was a guarded man and considered it no one's business whose side he had fought on in any of his historical battles. By 49 AC the young king Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen still had five vacant spots in the Kingsguard to fill. At the wedding of his mother Queen Regent Alyssa_Velaryon and Lord Rogar_Baratheon, it was decided to fill the remaining five slots by a test of arms since knights from all over the realm had come to attend the wedding. Jaehaerys agreed, but with a twist - the knights must fight on foot, not the joust, for foes seldom strike a king from horseback. The wild melees and bloody duels that followed were dubbed by maesters as the War for the White Cloaks with hundreds of knights competing for the honor of serving in the Kingsguard. While Ser Samgood of Sour Hill was the eldest of the five champions to win and be given a white cloak, his skills had been honed from a lifetime of battles and he was quicker than men half his age. During Jaehaerys's minority on Dragonstone, Samgood and his sworn brothers sparred with their young king, who spent every morning until midday in the castle yard training with sword, shield and any other weapon. Samgood accompanied young Prince Baelon when the lad visited the Dragonpit and stated that the prince was either brave or mad after he saw the child smack Balerion the Black Dread on the snout. Prince Baelon was known as Baelon the Brave after this. In 59 AC when the winter disease known as the Shivers came to King's Landing, Samgood and his sworn brother Ser Victor_the_Valiant were among those carried off by the disease. Samgood was seventy-three years old.

name: Samwell Blackwood
bio: Samwell Blackwood was the Lord of Raventree Hall and head of House Blackwood at the start of the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. When Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen visited the Trident in 112 AC, Samwell lost a duel to Amos_Bracken as the rivals unsuccessfully fought for the young princess's favor and hand in marriage. Samwell later succeeded his father as Lord of Raventree Hall.

name: Samwell Dayne
bio: Samwell Dayne, known as the Starfire, was member of House Dayne who ruled as King of the Torrentine and Lord of Starfall.

name: Samwell Rivers
bio: Samwell Rivers was a knight and a natural son of Lord Tommen_Tully of Riverrun. When Harwyn_Hoare, King of the Iron Islands, invaded the Riverlands, Ser Samwell assembled a small host and faced King Harwyn on the Tumblestone. Samwell's lines shattered when the Hardhand charged. Hundreds drowned attempting to flee, while Rivers was hacked in two, so that half of his body might be delivered to each of his parents.

name: Samwell Spicer
bio: Samwell Spicer is a knight of House Spicer. He is the cousin of Sybell_Spicer and Rolph_Spicer.

name: Samwell Stokeworth
bio: Samwell Stokeworth was a knight of House Stokeworth during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Ser Samwell participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Samwell Stone
bio: Samwell  Stone, better known as Strong Sam Stone, is a well-known bastard in the Vale. He is the master-at-arms at Runestone. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Samwell is portrayed by Allan Gildea.

name: Samwell Tarly (lord)
bio: Samwell Tarly, known as Savage Sam, was the Lord of Horn Hill and head of House Tarly during the reign of King Aenys_I_Targaryen. When the Vulture_King_(Aenys_I) began raiding the Dornish Marches in 37 AC, Lord Orys_Baratheon was joined by marcher lords in the Second Dornish War. The Vulture King soon abandoned his siege of Nightsong, but Lord Harmon_Dondarrion and Lady Ellyn_Caron cut off his retreat, while Samwell suddenly appeared in the Dornish line of march with several thousand knights and archers. In what became known as the Vulture Hunt, it is claimed that Savage Sam's Valyrian steel sword, Heartsbane, was crimson from hilt to point with the blood of the dozens of Dornish outlaws he had slain. The Dornish host was defeated and the Vulture King was taken alive and tied naked between two posts by Sam. The Vulture King soon perished of thirst and exposure. Samwell was among those granted gold, honors, and offices by a grateful King Aenys_I_Targaryen. The Lord of Horn Hill was present at the Golden Wedding in 49 AC, but it is unknown if this was Samwell.

name: Samwell Tarly
bio: Samwell Tarly is the eldest son of Lord Randyll_Tarly and his wife, Lady Melessa_Florent. A recruit of the Night's Watch originally from House Tarly, he is popularly called Sam. He becomes a POV character in A Storm of Swords. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Samwell is portrayed by John Bradley. Samwell was the first child of Randyll_Tarly, Lord of Horn Hill, and his wife, Melessa_Florent. Randyll, regarded as one of the finest military commanders in the Seven Kingdoms, did everything in his power to raise his son into what he deemed a proper heir, but Samwell frustrated him at every opportunity, resisting every effort made to change his nature. A dozen masters-at-arms failed to toughen him. Attempts such as beatings, starving him, dressing him in his mother's clothes and parading him, forcing him to sleep in chainmail, and being bathed in aurochs blood by two Qartheen warlocks failed to raise his valor. He even wept to see a chicken slaughtered. When Lord Tarly had Sam thrown into Horn Hill's pond to teach his son to swim, Ser Hyle_Hunt had to rescue the boy. Randyll insisted that young Sam attend him while holding court at Horn Hill, and he brought Sam to Highgarden during a visit to see Lord Mace_Tyrell. When he was ten, Sam accompanied his father on a trip to the Arbor, but the Redwyne twins Horas_Redwyne and Hobber_Redwyne bullied him and Lord Randyll brought him back home. Sam's mother revealed to him that Randyll had meant for Samwell to remain at the Arbor, to serve as Lord Paxter_Redwyne's page and squire. Had Sam pleased Paxter, he would have been betrothed to his daughter, Desmera_Redwyne. After Lady Melessa bore her husband three girls in three consecutive years, Samwell's brother Dickon_Tarly was born. When Sam and his mother sang "The Song of the Seven" to the baby, Randyll reprimanded his wife and his heir. Randyll also forbade Sam from sleeping in the same bed as his sisters. Dickon showed all of the physical vigor that Samwell lacked, and Randyll's attentions turned to raising his more promising younger son to take Sam's place. For a time Sam was left to enjoy his music, food, and other soft pursuits, though Randyll forbade him from traveling to Oldtown to study at the Citadel, horrified at the notion of a member of House Tarly wearing a maester's chain. To discourage the boy, Randyll had Sam chained and manacled to a wall for three days. When Sam turned fifteen, his father bluntly told him that he was not worthy of their house's ancestral Valyrian steel greatsword, Heartsbane, and that he must join the Night's Watch and renounce his family name so that he would not stand in the way of Dickon's inheritance. Failing that, Randyll promised that Sam would suffer an unfortunate hunting accident on the morrow. Sam chose to take the black.

name: Samwyle Tarly
bio: Samwyle Tarly was a knight from House Tarly and the heir to Horn Hill during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Samwyle is listed as competing in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Sandeman
bio: Sandeman was an archmaester of the Citadel. In 136 AC, one of Sandeman's former students, Maester Rowley, became King Aegon_III_Targaryen's new Lord Confessor.

name: Sandoq the Shadow
bio: Sandoq the Shadow was a former pit fighter in service to Lady Larra_Rogare and House Rogare during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Sandoq's history before his service to House Rogare is uncertain, but it is said that he had been a veteran pit fighter in Meereen, with a hundred wins to his name. In 135 AC, during the plot against the Rogare family amidst the Lysene Spring, Sandoq the Shadow protected his lady's husband, Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, and his brother, King Aegon_III_Targaryen, from Ser Amaury_Peake of the Kingsguard and a dozen guardsmen, slaying them all on the drawbridge of Maegor's Holdfast. Thus began an eighteen-day standoff before the plot unraveled. Sandoq, alongside several of the white cloaks, accompanied King Aegon upon his sixteenth nameday when he entered the small council chambers. Sandoq's presence unnerved the Torrhen_Manderly and the regents as the king dismissed them from their service.

name: Sandor Clegane
bio: Sandor Clegane is a member of House Clegane and is the younger brother of Ser Gregor_Clegane. Sandor is nicknamed the Hound for his fierce nature and unquestioning obedience to House Lannister and for the three dogs featured in his family's arms. As a child, Sandor received gruesome facial burns when Gregor shoved his face into a brazier. He has come to loathe fire, his brother, and the hypocrisy of knighthood in general. The sworn shield of Prince Joffrey_Baratheon, Sandor is considered one of the most dangerous fighters in Westeros. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Sandor is portrayed by Rory McCann. When Sandor was seven years old, a woodcarver in the village by the family keep gifted toys to the Cleganes to buy favor. Sandor desired his brother Gregor_Clegane gift, a jointed wooden knight, and took it to play with, though he was afraid of what the brutal Gregor might do. When Gregor found him, he shoved the side of Sandor's face into the burning coals of a brazier and held him there while he screamed, until three grown men were able to drag him off. The Cleganes' maester attempted to heal the burns with ointments. Sandor's Clegane_(father_of_Gregor_and_Sandor) explained the burns by claiming that his bedding caught fire, though there are still rumors that Gregor was involved. There are also rumors of a sister who died young in mysterious circumstances, and their father was killed in a hunting accident, which is also implied to have been suspicious, leaving the family estates to Gregor. According to a semi-canon source, Sandor admired knights when he was a child, but became greatly disillusioned when Gregor was knighted by Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen. Sandor left to join the Lannister household as a sworn sword on the day Gregor came into his inheritance, and he has never returned to his family home. He claims to have killed a man when he was twelve years old, possibly during Robert's Rebellion, as he was part of Lord Tywin_Lannister's host during the Sack of King's Landing. Sandor has never taken a knight's vows. Queen Cersei_Lannister assigned Sandor to protect her eldest son, Prince Joffrey_Baratheon, and the sworn shield has protected the boy for many years. Joffrey is very fond of Sandor and often calls him "dog", but his younger brother, Prince Tommen_Baratheon, is frightened of Sandor's face and voice. Ser Barristan_Selmy unhorsed Sandor during a tourney in 297 AC.

name: Sandor Frey
bio: Sandor Frey is a member of House Frey, the only son of Ser Geremy_Frey and Carolei_Waynwood. Sandor is the squire of Ser Donnel_Waynwood.

name: Sansa Stark
bio: Sansa Stark is a noblewoman of House Stark and is the elder daughter of Lady Catelyn_Stark and Lord Eddard_Stark. She has three brothers: Robb_Stark, Bran_Stark and Rickon_Stark; a younger sister: Arya_Stark; and a half-brother: Jon_Snow. Sansa Stark is one of the major POV characters in the books. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Sansa is portrayed by Sophie Turner. Sansa was born at Winterfell, with Maester Luwin aiding Catelyn_Stark during the delivery. In her eleven years, she has been tutored by Septa Mordane in the traditional womanly activities. Luwin has also educated the Stark children. Sansa shared a bedchamber with her younger sister, Arya_Stark, before getting her own. Jon_Snow, having covered himself with flour to appear as a ghost, once tried to scare his younger siblings in the crypt of Winterfell. While Sansa and Bran_Stark were frightened, Arya instead punched their half-brother. Sansa became infatuated with Ser Waymar_Royce when the young man stopped at Winterfell on his way to Castle Black. Theon_Greyjoy, the ward of Lord Eddard_Stark, has pondered marrying Sansa and being accepted by Eddard as a son.

name: Sansa Stark (daughter of Rickon)
bio: Sansa Stark was a noblewoman from House Stark, the oldest daughter of Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan), heir to Lord Cregan_Stark of Winterfell, and his wife, Jeyne_Manderly. She had a younger sister, Serena_Stark.[N 1] After the death of her father Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan) in the Conquest of Dorne, Sansa was set to inherit Winterfell after her grandfather, Lord Cregan. However, the lordship of Winterfell instead passed to Cregan's second son, Jonnel_Stark, Sansa's father's half-brother. Jonnel also married Sansa. The north lamented the death of Rickon, for the reigns of Jonnel and his brothers after him were troublesome. Sansa had no issue with Jonnel. After her death, Lord Jonnel married again to Robyn_Ryswell. It is unknown if Sansa was still alive when her husband succeeded Lord Cregan.

name: Sansara Tarly
bio: Sansara Tarly was a noblewoman from House Tarly during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Sansara was the daughter of Lord Donald Tarly and Lady Jeyne_Rowan. Her sister, Samantha_Tarly, married Lord Ormund_Hightower and then his son, Lord Lyonel_Hightower. After the death of Queen Jaehaera_Targaryen in 133 AC, Lady Sam boldly wrote a letter proposing that King Aegon_III_Targaryen should marry both her sister Sansara and her step-daughter, Bethany_Hightower.

name: Sara Snow
bio: Sara Snow was, according to Mushroom, a bastard daughter of Lord Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Benjen) of Winterfell. By Mushroom's account, Jacaerys_Velaryon, the Prince of Dragonstone, fell in love with Sara when he arrived at Winterfell in 129 AC to secure the support of Sara's half-brother, Lord Cregan_Stark, for Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen during the Dance of the Dragons. According to Mushroom, Sara and Jacaerys slept together, and Cregan grew angry when he learned about this. However, he calmed when Sara claimed that she and Jacaerys had secretly wed, having spoken their vows in the godswood of Winterfell before she and Jacaerys had sex amidst the snows. Archmaester Gyldayn questions whether Sara ever existed and dismisses the tale of marriage as Mushroom's "fevered imaginings", stating that what is known about Jacaerys's character makes it unlikely he would break his betrothal of eleven years to Lady Baela_Targaryen to protect the virtue of the northern bastard girl. Although Gyldayn concedes that it is possible Jacaerys had bedded Sara, if she indeed existed, he considers the talk of marriage to be "preposterous".

name: Saranella
bio: Saranella was a septa of the Faith of the Seven in service to House Lannister when Cersei_Lannister was a young girl. After Melara_Hetherspoon drowned, Cersei_Lannister asked Septa Saranella the meaning of the word valonqar. She responded that it means "little brother" in High Valyrian.

name: Sarella Sand
bio: Sarella Sand is the fourth eldest of the Sand Snakes, the bastard daughters of Oberyn_Martell. Her mother is the captain of the Feathered Kiss. Sarella is Prince Oberyn_Martell's fourth daughter. Her mother, the trader captain of the Feathered Kiss, comes from the Summer Isles. Sarella sometimes played with her cousin, Princess Arianne_Martell, at the Water Gardens, although Arianne was always closer to Sarella's half-sister, Tyene_Sand. Sarella once visited the abandoned holdfast Shandystone with her father, Tyene, and Arianne. Sarella was interested in the history of the ruins, and turned over rocks and brushed sand off its mosaics, wanting to know everything about the people who had lived there.

name: Sargon Botley
bio: Sargon Botley is the half-brother of Lord Sawane_Botley. He has a bastard son, Wex_Pyke.

name: Sargoso Saan
bio: Sargoso Saan was a  Lysene pirate king in the Stepstones during the reign of Aegon_I_Targaryen. Sargoso was a member of the Saan family. In 29 AC and 30 AC, Prince Maegor_I_Targaryen, Lord Aethan_Velaryon, and Ser Osmund_Strong led punitive campaigns to drive out Sargoso from the Stepstones.

name: Sarra Frey
bio: Sarra Frey is twin sister of Serra_Frey and daughter of Ser Raymund_Frey and Beony_Beesbury.

name: Sarra Stark
bio: Sarra Stark was a member of House Stark. She was the eldest daughter of Lord Cregan_Stark and Lady Alysanne_Blackwood.

name: Sarya Whent
bio: Sarya Whent was a noblewoman of House Whent, and the fifth wife of Lord Walder_Frey. She is deceased, with no progeny. Her relationship to other members of House Whent is unknown.

name: Satin
bio: Satin is a recruit of the Night's Watch, who was once a prostitute in Oldtown. Satin received his name while working as a whore at the Oldtown brothel where he had been born and raised.

name: Sauron Salt-Tongue
bio: Sauron Salt-Tongue was a priest of the Drowned God. Sauron declared the ironborn to have originated in the Drowned God's watery halls, made in the god's image, emerging from beneath the seas with dominion over all the waters of the earth.

name: Sawane Botley
bio: Sawane Botley is the Lord of Lordsport and the head of House Botley. He is the captain of the Swiftfin. During Greyjoy's Rebellion, the timber-and-wattle castle of House Botley was burned to the ground by King Robert_I_Baratheon during a battle at Lordsport. Lord Sawane rebuilt in stone after the war. Sawane bought a difficult horse at Lannisport in 298 AC.

name: Sawwood
bio: Sawwood is a steward of the Night's Watch. A forester, he earned his name more from his snoring than his ability to cut wood.

name: Scales
bio: Scales is one of the children of the forest in service to the last greenseer. Their true name is too long for the human tongue, according to Leaf.

name: Scar
bio: Scar is a slave soldier and serjeant owned by Yezzan_zo_Qaggaz.

name: Scarb
bio: Oznak_zo_Pahl beheaded and cut the liver out of Scarb after he had looked at a Meereenese lady wrongly. His friend, Ben_Plumm, escaped the city through its sewers to avoid a similar fate.

name: Scolera
bio: Scolera is a septa of the Faith of the Seven in service at the Great Sept of Baelor in King's Landing. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Scolera is portrayed by Mary Jordan.

name: Scoro
bio: Scoro was a Dothraki khal who lived during the Century of Blood. Scoro broke through the Whalebone Gates of Ibbish and sacked the city, looting temples and treasuries and carrying off the city's gods to Vaes Dothrak.

name: Sebastion Errol
bio: Sebastion Errol is the Lord of Haystack Hall and the head of House Errol.

name: Sebaston Farman
bio: Sebaston Farman is the Lord of Fair Isle and the head of House Farman. His sister, Jeyne_Farman, is married to Ser Gareth_Clifton, one of his bannermen.

name: Sedgekins
bio: Sedgekins is a servant at the Twins, serving House Frey.

name: Sefton Staunton
bio: Sefton, born Sefton Staunton, was a septon of the Faith of the Seven during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Sefton was the brother of Ser Simon_Staunton. Sefton served many years at the Great Sept of Baelor in King's Landing, attending the High Septon. He was in King's Landing throughout the Great Spring Sickness and witnessed much of the city's misery. He came to serve at Coldmoat when his brother, Ser Simon_Staunton, married Lady Rohanne_Webber.

name: Selmond Stackspear
bio: Selmond Stackspear is the head of House Stackspear.

name: Selwyn Tarth
bio: Selwyn Tarth, or Selywn of Tarth, is the Lord of Tarth, Lord of Evenfall, and the head of House Tarth, known as the Evenstar. Selwyn has one remaining child, his daughter and heir, Brienne_Tarth. Selwyn's daughters Alysanne_Tarth and Arianne_Tarth died in infancy. His son Galladon_Tarth drowned when he was eight years old, leaving Brienne as his only living child. After the death of his wife, Selwyn took a new mistress every year. One mistress came from across the narrow sea; she had a slim waist, purple eyes, and a lovely voice. Lord Tarth had a septa, Roelle, who educated Brienne. He also allowed his master-at-arms, Ser Goodwin, to train Brienne at arms. He kept a singer every year of Brienne's childhood, which led her to memorize all the songs. Selwyn betrothed Brienne to the second son of Lord Bryen_Caron, but the boy died of a chill when he was twelve. Selwyn then tried to wed Brienne to Ser Ronnet_Connington, but the knight rejected her for her appearance. He last tried an older man for her, Ser Humfrey_Wagstaff, but Brienne broke her betrothal to Wagstaff by defeating him in combat. After that, Lord Selwyn stopped trying to arrange a marriage for Brienne. Selwyn hosted Renly_Baratheon at Evenfall Hall during the Lord Paramount of the Stormlands's coming of age progress. Some time later, Selywn sent Brienne to serve Renly at Storm's End.

name: Selyse Florent
bio: Selyse Florent, also known as Selyse Baratheon, is a noblewoman of House Florent and a niece of Alester_Florent, Lord of Brightwater Keep. She is the wife of Stannis_Baratheon, Lord of Dragonstone, and together they have one daughter, Shireen_Baratheon. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Selyse is portrayed by Sarah MacKeever (Season 2) and Tara Fitzgerald (Seasons 3 - 5). Selyse married Stannis in either 286 AC or 287 AC. During the wedding feast, Stannis's elder brother, King Robert_I_Baratheon, deflowered Delena_Florent, a cousin of Selyse, in the bed made for the newlyweds. Delena became pregnant and gave birth to a boy, Edric_Storm. Stannis is not fond of his wife. They sleep together only once or twice a year, and despite their hopes they have only a daughter, Shireen_Baratheon; Selyse believes that Edric's existence has cursed them to have no sons. Stannis rarely sends letters from King's Landing to his wife at Dragonstone. Some years before the start of A Song of Ice and Fire, Lord Varys informed Robert's small council that Selyse had taken up with a red priestess, Melisandre of Asshai, in Dragonstone. Selyse has asked Stannis that they be wed again in a ceremony of the Lord of Light.

name: Senelle
bio: Senelle is a maid in service to Queen Cersei_Lannister.

name: Senerra She-Snake
bio: Senerra She-Snake is a pit fighter in Meereen.

name: Septon of Sallydance
bio: The septon of Sallydance serves at the sept of Sallydance, a village in the riverlands.

name: Ser Stupid
bio: "Ser Stupid" was the nickname Princess Aerea_Targaryen gave a knight of Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I)'s court in Dragonstone.

name: Serala
bio: Lady Serala of Myr, known as the Lace Serpent, was the wife of Lord Denys_Darklyn, the last Darklyn lord of Duskendale. The smallfolk blame Serala for the fall of House Darklyn, claiming she put the seed of rebellion in her husband's ear with Myrish poison, leading to the Defiance of Duskendale. Her actual role in the matter is not truly known, but from that belief held by the populace came her name, the Lace Serpent. During the Defiance, her husband, Lord Denys_Darklyn, made the mistake of holding captive King Aerys_II_Targaryen. Following his liberation and the end of the Defiance, Aerys went on a killing rampage. Serala's husband and all of his house were beheaded, while she was burned alive, though her tongue was torn out first, along with her female parts, with which it was said she enslaved her lordly husband. Half of Duskendale stated that Aerys was still too kind to her, believing the Defiance would have never happened had Lord Denys married a Stokeworth or a Staunton instead.

name: Serena Stark
bio: Serena Stark was a noblewoman from House Stark, the youngest daughter of Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan), heir to Lord Cregan_Stark of Winterfell, and his wife, Jeyne_Manderly. She had an older sister, Sansa_Stark_(daughter_of_Rickon).[N 1] After the death of her Rickon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan) in the Conquest of Dorne, Serena's sister Sansa was set to inherit Winterfell after her grandfather, Lord Cregan. However, the lordship of Winterfell instead passed to Cregan's second son, Jonnel_Stark, who also married Sansa. The north lamented the death of Rickon, for the reigns of Jonnel and his brothers after him were troublesome. Serena married twice in her life. She first married Lord Jon_Umber_(husband_of_Serena) with whom she did not have issue, and then she married her father's half-brother, Edric_Stark_(son_of_Cregan), with whom she had four children: first the twin sons Torrhen_Stark_(son_of_Edric) and Cregard_Stark, then daughters Arrana_Stark and Argelle_Stark.

name: Serenei of Lys
bio: Serenei of Lys, also called Sweet Serenei, was the ninth and last mistress of Aegon_IV_Targaryen. There was one child of their union, Shiera_Seastar, the last of the king's Great Bastards. Lady Serenei was the last daughter from an ancient but impoverished Valyrian noble family of Lys. She was brought to King's Landing by Lord Jon_Hightower, the new Hand of the King. Rumors spread that Serenei was much older than the king, practicing dark arts to retain her youth and beauty. She died giving birth to the last of the king’s bastard children, a girl called Shiera_Seastar.

name: Serra
bio: Serra was the second wife of Illyrio_Mopatis. Illyrio found Serra in a Lysene pillow house, and enjoyed her so much, he brought her home as one of his bedwarmers. He grew to care for her and eventually married her, upsetting the cousin of his Illyrio_Mopatis's_first_wife, the Prince of Pentos, who closed the palace to him forever. Still, this bothered Illyrio not at all, for he was happy with Serra. However, the Braavosi trading galley, Treasure, stopped over in Pentos and brought the grey plague with it. Even though they killed every crew member who tried to leave the ship, the rats aboard came ashore and brought the plague with them. Of the two thousand who died from the plague, Serra was among them. After her death, Illyrio kept her stone hands in his bedchamber, and also kept a silver locket with a painting of her inside.

name: Serra Frey
bio: Serra Frey is twin sister of Sarra_Frey and daughter of Ser Raymund_Frey and Beony_Beesbury.

name: Lord Serrett
bio: Lord Serrett is the Lord of Silverhill and the head of House Serrett.

name: Serwyn of the Mirror Shield
bio: Serwyn of the Mirror Shield is a legendary figure from the Age of Heroes, although singers now claim he was a knight of the Kingsguard. He is a favorite of the smallfolk. Serwyn served the ancient Kings of the Reach from House Gardener.

name: Shadd
bio: Shadd is a member of the household guard sworn to House Stark of Winterfell.

name: Shadrick
bio: Shadrich, known as Shadrich of the Shady Glen and the Mad Mouse, is a hedge knight. The location of the Shady Glen is unknown.

name: Shae
bio: Shae is a young camp follower who comes into the service of Tyrion_Lannister. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Shae is portrayed by Sibel Kekilli and is stated to be from Lorath instead of Westeros. Shae claims she ran away from home when her father attempted to turn her into his kitchen wench and his whore.

name: Shaena Targaryen
bio: Princess Shaena Targaryen was the stillborn second child of King Aerys_II_Targaryen and Queen Rhaella_Targaryen. Shaena was stillborn in 267 AC, while her father Aerys_II_Targaryen, her brother Rhaegar_Targaryen, and half the court were present in the westerlands. Shaena's mother, Queen Rhaella_Targaryen, had remained in King's Landing.

name: Shaera Targaryen
bio: Shaera Targaryen was the eldest daughter and thirdborn child of King Aegon_V_Targaryen and Queen Betha_Blackwood. She became the queen of her brother, King Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen. Princess Shaera was the eldest daughter of Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen and his wife, Lady Betha_Blackwood. She had two older brothers, Duncan_Targaryen and Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen, and two younger siblings, Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Aegon_V) and Rhaelle_Targaryen. Shaera's grandfather, King Maekar_I_Targaryen, died in 233 AC, when Shaera was seven years old, and the Great Council selected her father to be the new king, Aegon V Targaryen, called "Aegon the Unlikely" as he was the fourth son of a fourth son. It had long been the custom of House Targaryen to wed brother to sister to keep the blood of the dragon pure, but Aegon V had become convinced that such unions did more harm than good. Instead, he chose to betroth his children to some of the Great Houses of the Seven Kingdoms, in hopes of winning their support for his reign. In 237 AC, when Shaera was eleven, her mother Queen Betha arranged her betrothal to Luthor_Tyrell, heir to Highgarden. Shaera's older brother, Prince Jaehaerys, was betrothed to Celia_Tully, the daughter of the Lord of Riverrun. However, from a young age, Jaehaerys had been of a more traditional frame of mind, as he was in love with his sister, Shaera. Shaera, in turn, desired him. Once King Aegon and Queen Betha were aware of this feeling, they did everything they could to separate the two siblings, yet this only served to inflame their passion. In 239 AC, Shaera's eldest brother, Prince Duncan, broke his betrothal to the daughter of Lord Lyonel_Baratheon and married a common-born girl, Jenny_of_Oldstones. This caused much trouble and a small rebellion by House Baratheon, but in the end, King Aegon V yielded to his son's desires, though Duncan was excluded from the line of succession. Jaehaerys, the new Prince of Dragonstone and heir to the Iron Throne, noted this reaction of the king and court. In 240 AC, when Shaera was fourteen and Jaehaerys was fifteen, they eluded their guards and were secretly married. By the time this became known to their royal parents, Jaehaerys and Shaera had already consummated the marriage, forcing Aegon to accept it, despite the anger of House Tyrell and House Tully. In 244 AC, when Shaera was eighteen, she and Jaehaerys had their first child, a son who they named Aerys_II_Targaryen. A daughter, Rhaella_Targaryen, followed within the next two years. While Shaera and Jaheaerys had married for love, Jaehaerys eventually commanded his children to marry each other, after hearing a Ghost_of_High_Heart of Jenny of Oldstones prophesy that the prince that was promised would be born of their line. Although King Aegon was frustrated by Jaehaerys's decision, he let his son have his way. In 259 AC, Aegon summoned many of those closest to him to Summerhall, reportedly all the descendants of House Targaryen, to celebrate the impending birth of Aerys and Rhaella's Rhaegar_Targaryen, his first great-grandchild and Shaera's first grandchild. Unfortunately, a tragic fire occurred, killing King Aegon V, Prince Duncan, and many others of the court. It is not certain whether Shaera or Jaehaerys were in attendance at Summerhall, but it is known that both survived the conflagration, as did Aerys and Rhaella and their newborn son. Though it is unknown when Queen Shaera died, she is mentioned to have remained in King's Landing with her brother and husband, King Jaehaerys II, when the armies marched out to the Stepstones to fight the War of the Ninepenny Kings in 260 AC.

name: Shagga
bio: Shagga, known as Shagga son of Dolf because of Dolf, is a member of the Stone Crows, one of the mountain clans who dwell in the Mountains of the Moon. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Shagga is portrayed by Mark Lewis Jones.

name: Shagwell
bio: Shagwell, also called Shagwell the Fool and Shags, is a psychotic jester and member of the Brave Companions mercenary company. There are rumors said that Shagwell once killed a man for not laughing at one of his japes.

name: Sharako Lohar
bio: Sharako Lohar was a Lysene admiral for the Triarchy. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Lohar is merged with Racallio_Ryndoon and the character's sex has been changed to female; though she personally identifies as a woman, her society genders her as a man because of her leadership role. She is portrayed by Abigail Thorn. During the Westerosi civil war, Admiral Lohar commanded a fleet of ninety Myrish, Lysene and Tyroshi warships under the banners of the Three Daughters who had allied themselves to King Aegon_II_Targaryen and his supporters. On a mission to break the blacks's blockade of the Gullet, Sharako Lohar took the fleet of ninety warships up from the Stepstones, bending their oars for battle. Admiral Lohar was in command during what turned out to be one of the bloodiest sea battles in all of history. The Three Daughters emerged victorious. Of the ninety original warships, twenty-eight survived to limp home. Of these, all but three were crewed by Lyseni. This fact caused some in Myr and Tyrosh to turn against Lohar, accusing him of holding back his Lysene crews while sending the other men of the Triarchy to their doom. This sparked a quarrel that would result in the Daughters' War two years later, shattering the Triarchy. During the battle, the fleet captured the cog Gay Abandon. Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen was made the captive of a Tyroshi captain, but Admiral Lohar soon took the Prince into his charge. Wanting for gold and allies due to his disgraceful defeat in the Gullet, he sold Viserys to Bambarro_Bazanne, in exchange for the prince's weight in gold and a promise of support. Eventually, he was murdered by one of his rivals for the favor of the courtesan known as the Johanna_Swann.

name: Sharis Footly
bio: Sharis Footly was the Lady of Tumbleton by her marriage to Lord_Footly during the Dance of the Dragons, and regent of Tumbleton during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Bold Jon_Roxton became enamored of Lady Sharis after the First Battle of Tumbleton, and claimed her as a "prize of war." When her lord husband protested, Ser Jon cut him nigh in two with Orphan-Maker. Sharis wept as he tore her gown off. Six months after the Second Battle of Tumbleton, Lady Sharis gave birth to a lusty dark-haired boy who she proclaimed to be her late lord husband's trueborn heir, though it was far more likely that he had been sired by Roxton. She ruled in his name as regent and became famous for her efforts to restore the shattered town. She pulled down the burned shells of shops and houses, rebuilt the town walls, buried the dead, planted wheat and barley and turnips in the fields where the camps had been, and had the heads of the dragons Seasmoke and Vermithor cleaned and displayed in the town square, where travelers paid a penny to look at them, and a star to touch them. After the death of Queen Jaehaera_Targaryen in 133 AC, Lady Sharis made so bold as to nominate herself as the second wife of King Aegon_III_Targaryen.

name: Sharna
bio: Sharna is the innkeep of the Inn of the Kneeling Man. She is married to Husband. Sharna and her family were displaced during the War of the Five Kings. She had two sons, one of whom was killed by Lannister men, whilst the other died of the flux. She and her Husband later adopted an orphaned Boy whose mother had been killed by the Brave Companions. They came upon the Inn of the Kneeling Man and found the previous owners dead, so buried them and took over running the establishment.

name: Sharra Arryn
bio: Sharra Arryn, known as the Flower of the Mountain, was Queen Regent of the Kingdom of Mountain and Vale, ruling in the name of her son, the boy-king Ronnel_Arryn_(king), during the invasion of Westeros by Aegon_I_Targaryen. In her youth, Sharra was regarded as one of the most beautiful women of Westeros, known as the "the Flower of the Mountain". Sharra married the King of Mountain and Vale and had two sons with him, Ronnel_Arryn_(king) and Jonos_Arryn. Sharra was widowed when Ronnel was only a boy, and so rule of the Vale of Arryn fell to Sharra.

name: Sharra the Witch Queen
bio: Sharra the Witch Queen is one of the many legendary heroes of the riverlands, whose tales date back to the Age of Heroes.

name: Lord Shawney
bio: Lord Shawney was the head of House Shawney during the reign of Kings Daeron_II_Targaryen and Aerys_I_Targaryen. Lord Shawney supported Daemon_I_Blackfyre during the First Blackfyre Rebellion in 196 AC. During the Battle of the Redgrass Field, he was on the right of the Blackfyre host, under the command of Ser Aegor_Rivers. Lord Shawney almost died from his wounds in the battle, and he was pardoned by King Daeron_II_Targaryen after the war. In 212 AC, Lord Shawney came with his retinue to Whitewalls to attend the wedding of Lord Ambrose_Butterwell and his second wife, the daughter of Lord_Frey. On his way, Lord Shawney had to cross the Gods Eye via a ferry. He quarreled with Lord_Costayne_(Aerys_I) on the matter of which lord should cross first; Shawney argued that as the older of the two he should go first, whereas Costayne believed that as his House was nobler then Shawney's, he had the precedence. During the wedding feast, Lord Shawney was seated on the dais. The next day, he attend the wedding tourney and bet on Ser Duncan_the_Tall to win (five to one) against Ser Uthor_Underleaf in the joust. The fate of Lord Costayne following the arrival at Whitewalls of the Hand of the King, Brynden_Rivers, and the suppression of the Second Blackfyre Rebellion is unknown.

name: Shella
bio: Lady Shella is the focus of a famous love story with the Rainbow_Knight.

name: Shella Whent
bio: Shella Whent is the Lady of Harrenhal and head of House Whent. According to a semi-canon response from George R. R. Martin, Shella was married to Lord Walter_Whent, the brother of Ser Oswell_Whent, and she was the mother of the fair maid of the tourney at Harrenhal. In A Game of Thrones and A Clash of Kings it is stated that Shella is from House Whent, and that her father and grandfather held Harrenhal before her.

name: Shepherd
bio: The Shepherd was a prophet and begging brother who lived in King's Landing during the Dance of the Dragons. He was a follower of the Faith, although his name has been lost to history.

name: False Shepherd
bio: The false Shepherd was one of the men claiming to be the reincarnation of the Shepherd after his death. After the arrest and execution of the prophet known as the Shepherd on the last day of 130 AC, several individuals in King's Landing claimed to be the reincarnation of the late prophet. One such was executed by Ser Victor_Risley, the King's Justice, by order of Lord Unwin_Peake in 133 AC.

name: Shepherd reborn
bio: The Shepherd reborn was one of the men claiming to be the reincarnation of the Shepherd during the Hour of the Wolf. After the arrest and execution of the prophet known as the Shepherd on the last day of 130 AC, several individuals in King's Landing claimed to be the reincarnation of the late prophet. One such preached against the northmen in Lord Cregan_Stark's army, as they believed in the old gods rather than following the Faith of the Seven. Hundreds flocked to hear him but the gold cloaks of the City Watch captured him and removed his tongue.

name: Sherrit
bio: Sherrit was an ancient king who, while at the Nightfort, called a curse down on the Andals.

name: Shiera Blackwood
bio: Shiera Blackwood was a member of House Blackwood who married into House Durrandon. Shiera was the eldest child of Lord Roderick_Blackwood, who was allied with the Storm King Arlan_III_Durrandon. Arlan had married one of Roderick's daughters, while Shiera was herself married to Arlan's son. Arlan intended to restore the riverlands to House Blackwood, but Roderick was slain in battle. After the Battle of Six Kings, Roderick's heir was a boy of only eight and Arlan distrusted Roderick's brothers. Arlan considered granting the riverlands to his good-daughter Shiera and having his own son rule by her side, but the other river lords opposed being ruled by a woman. Arlan instead added the riverlands to the domains of House Durrandon.

name: Shiera Crakehall
bio: Shiera Crakehall is the wife of Ser Damion_Lannister, a cousin of the main branch of House Lannister. They have two children, Ser Lucion_Lannister and Lanna_Lannister.

name: Shiera Seastar
bio: Shiera Seastar was the last of the Great Bastards of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen, born to his last mistress, Serenei_of_Lys. The known lover of Brynden_Rivers, she is said to have been the most beautiful woman of the Seven Kingdoms. King Aegon_IV_Targaryen sired Shiera upon the ninth and last of his mistresses, Lady Serenei_of_Lys. Serenei died giving birth to the last of the king's bastard children, naming her child Shiera, Star of the Sea. Shiera never married, but took many lovers, and numerous duels were fought for her favor. Her half-brother Brynden_Rivers, known as Bloodraven, was her most ardent suitor, and repeatedly proposed marriage to her. Although she refused to marry him, Shiera did share her bed with him and was amused by his jealousy. Her other half-brother Aegor_Rivers, known as Bittersteel, also desired her, which only served to increase the hatred between Bloodraven and Bittersteel. There were rumors that Shiera used sorcery to aid Bloodraven when he served as Hand of the King and master of whisperers to King Aerys_I_Targaryen, and that she bathed in blood to retain her beauty.

name: Shierle Swyft
bio: Shierle Swyft is the daughter of Ser Harys_Swyft, the Knight of Cornfield. She is married to Ser Melwyn_Sarsfield.

name: Shireen Baratheon
bio: Shireen Baratheon is a noblewoman of House Baratheon of Dragonstone, and is the daughter and only child of Lord Stannis_Baratheon and Lady Selyse_Florent. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Shireen is portrayed by Kerry Ingram. Shireen was born at Dragonstone in 289 AC to Lord Stannis_Baratheon and Lady Selyse_Florent. She contracted greyscale as an infant. The disease almost killed her, and left her disfigured. Cressen, Dragonstone's maester, feels that her sadness is a mark of his failure. As the daughter and heir of Stannis, Shireen resides in Dragonstone and is educated by Maester Cressen. She is usually seen in the company of Patchface, their mad fool that she calls "Patches", though his ravings sometimes frighten her.

name: Shirei Frey
bio: Shirei Frey is the seventh and youngest daughter of Lord Walder_Frey, the second born daughter of his seventh spouse, Lady Annara_Farring. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Shirei is portrayed by Maria Laird.

name: Shitmouth
bio: Shitmouth is one of the Mountain's men, the men-at-arms in service to Ser Gregor_Clegane. His real name is not known.

name: Shortear
bio: Shortear is a guard in service to House Lannister.

name: Shrike
bio: The Shrike was a priest of the Drowned God who lived during the reign of Harmund_III_Hoare, King of the Iron Islands. His given name is unknown. Going against tradition, King Harmund_III_Hoare announced that ironborn reavers would be hanged as pirates. The king outlawed the taking of salt wives, declaring the children of such unions no more than bastards with no right to inheritance. Harmund also considered ending the practice of thralldom. The Shrike began to preach against Harmund, and the lords of the Iron Islands listened when other priests of the Drowned God took up the cry. Septons and their followers stood by King Harmund, but the Shrike managed to overthrow Harmund bloodlessly. Afterward, the Shrike took the deposed king's tongue so he may never again speak "lies and blasphemies".
