Harmund was also blinded and his nose cut off so "all men might see him for the monster he is". Harmund's brother, Hagon_Hoare, was declared king. Hagon allowed the mutilation of his own mother, Dowager Queen Lelia_Lannister, whom the Shrike blamed for turning her husband, Harmund_II_Hoare, and their sons away from the Drowned God to the Faith of the Seven. Hagon the Heartless was brought down by the westermen led by Ser Aubrey_Crakehall, who wanted vengeance for the mutilation of Lelia. Surprisingly, Aubrey then claimed the kingship of the Iron Isles for himself. The reign of "King Aubrey" lasted less than half a year before he was captured by the ironborn and sacrificed to the sea by the Shrike.

name: Shrouded Lord
bio: The Shrouded Lord, also known as His Grey Grace and the Prince of Sorrows, is a mysterious figure said to inhabit the Sorrows.  Many say he is just a legend. The Shrouded Lord is said to rule the mists around the Sorrows since the time of Garin and to spread greyscale through the grey kiss, although he does not bestow his kiss lightly according to Haldon. According to some rumors, he is thought to be Prince Garin himself, risen from his watery grave. Others believe that there have been numerous Shrouded Lords, and when one dies another one takes his place; the one currently holding the title is a corsair from the Basilisk Isles. Rolly_Duckfield likes yet another version of the tale, in which the Shrouded Lord was a statue at first, and a grey woman from the fog kissed life to it with lips as cold as ice. They say the Shrouded Lord will grant a boon to any man who can make him laugh. According to Septa Lemore the stone men were proud, and the Shrouded Lord was the proudest of all.

name: Shyra
bio: Shyra is the twin sister of Bandy and daughter of Joseth, Winterfell's master of horse.

name: Shyra Errol
bio: Shyra Errol is the Lady of Haystack Hall and the head of House Errol.

name: Sigfry Stonetree
bio: Sigfry Stonetree is an ironborn raider and a member of House Stonetree.

name: Sigfryd Harlaw
bio: Sigfryd Harlaw, called Sigfryd Silverhair, is a nobleman of House Harlaw and the master of Harlaw Hall. His personal sigil is per bend black and silver, two scythes counterchanged.

name: Sigorn
bio: Sigorn is the son of Styr, the Magnar of Thenn.

name: Sigrin
bio: Sigrin, also known as Sigrin the Shipwright, is a shipwright in Lordsport sworn to Balon_Greyjoy. The first ship Sigrin built was called Esgred, named after Esgred. He also built Sea Bitch.

name: Ragged Silas
bio: Ragged Silas, was a leader of the Poor Fellows during the Faith Militant uprising against the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Following the Faith Militant's defeats in the battles at Stonebridge and the Great Fork of the Blackwater the previous year, new leaders arose across the Seven Kingdoms in 43 AC, one of which was Ragged Silas. He and his followers were hardly distinguishable from outlaws, ambushing royal forces then disappearing into the greenwood. Smallfolk gave them aid wherever they went, while the knights and men-at-arms who were sent out to bring them to justice often disappeared. The exact region that Ragged Silas and his followers were active in hasn't been stated. Silas was still alive at the time of Maegor's death, but his ultimate fate has not been revealed.

name: Silvario Pendaerys
bio: Silvario Pendaerys was a Lyseni magister during the Lysene Spring. Silvario was stabbed through the eye leaving the Temple of Trade in 135 AC during the bloody power struggle in the city after the downfall of the Rogare family. His brother Pereno_Pendaerys was also murdered.

name: Silver haired whore in Selhorys
bio: The silver haired whore is an enslaved prostitute working in a modest Selhorys brothel. Her true name is unknown.

name: Simon Dondarrion
bio: Simon Dondarrion was the Lord of Blackhaven and the head of House Dondarrion during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. When his father Lord_Dondarrion_(father_of_Simon) hosted King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen at Blackhaven in 54 AC, Simon charmed the royal couple with sad songs of star-crossed lovers and fallen kings. The queen was so taken with Simon that the royal party lingered at Blackhaven longer than they intended. Simon was the victor at the tourney celebrating the completion of the Dragonpit in 55 AC. He won the love of the commons and queen alike when he crowned Princess Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I) as his queen of love and beauty. He was defeated by Ser Ryam_Redwyne at the tourney celebrating the tenth year of Jaehaerys's coronation in late 58 AC, however. During the Third Dornish War in 61 AC, Lord Simon led a small force of marcher knights into the western Red Mountains of Dorne to cut off any means of escape for the Vulture_King_(Jaehaerys_I) men, while King Jaehaerys and Lord Rogar_Baratheon pursued the outlaws from the east.

name: Simon Leygood
bio: Simon Leygood was a knight from House Leygood during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Simon was a suitor of Lady Rohanne_Webber, attending her at Coldmoat. He was deeply in debt and hoped to repay them by marrying her.

name: Simon Staunton
bio: Simon Staunton was a knight from House Staunton. He was married to Lady Rohanne_Webber becoming her third husband. His brother was Septon Sefton_Staunton.

name: Simon Staunton (squire)
bio: Simon Staunton was a squire from House Staunton during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Simon was among the group of squires who kept company with Princess Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I). The princess fell out with Simon after he tried to make her drink wine.

name: Simon Strong
bio: Simon Strong was a knight from House Strong during the Dance of the Dragons. He was the great-uncle to the ruling Lord Larys_Strong, and the castellan of Harrenhal. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Simon is portrayed by Simon Russell Beale. During the Dance of the Dragons, Simon surrendered Harrenhal at the sight of Caraxes, Prince Daemon_Targaryen's dragon. Simon and his grandsons were then held as hostages by Daemon. Simon was freed from captivity when Ser Criston_Cole, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, recovered the castle. However, Prince Aemond_Targaryen, angry after the fall of King's Landing, believed Simon to be a traitor who had too easily yielded Harrenhal to Daemon. Aemond forced Simon into a duel, and then after he slew the aged knight, fed his remains to his dragon Vhagar. Aemond then ordered the executions of the remaining Strongs at Harrenhal, except for Alys_Rivers, whom he took as his bedmate.

name: Simon Toyne
bio: Simon Toyne was the leader of the Kingswood Brotherhood, an outlaw group active during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. Simon was a member of House Toyne, a family fallen into disgrace after Terrence_Toyne with House Targaryen. Under Simon's leadership, the Kingswood Brotherhood achieved no small measure of infamy as they kidnapped various nobles and evaded capture. According to Ser Barristan_Selmy, Simon entered a tourney at Storm's End as a mystery knight, where he was unhorsed by Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen; a semi-canon source, however, states that Barristan may have misremembered the tourney. The forest folk of the kingswood had looked to Simon and his outlaws for protection. When Ser Arthur_Dayne led a campaign into the forest, however, the Sword of the Morning gained the support of these smallfolk by paying them for supplies, bringing their grievances to King Aerys_II_Targaryen, and allowing them to gain some additional rights. When Barristan rescued Lady Jeyne_Swann and her septa from the outlaws, he defeated the Smiling_Knight and slew Simon. Ulmer of the Kingswood has often told the men at Castle Black about his adventures with Simon and other outlaws from the Brotherhood.

name: Skahaz mo Kandaq
bio: Skahaz mo Kandaq, also known as the Shavepate, is a Ghiscari noble of the city of Meereen.

name: Skinner
bio: Skinner  is a man-at-arms sworn to House Bolton. He is one of the Bastard's Boys.

name: Skittrick
bio: Skittrick is a member of the household guard sworn to House Stark of Winterfell.

name: Skyte
bio: Skyte is an ironborn sailor sworn to Balon_Greyjoy.

name: Lord Slate
bio: Lord Slate is the head of House Slate of Blackpool.

name: Sloey
bio: Sloey  is a mummer of the Ship.

name: Sly
bio: Sly was a stableboy at Hogs Head, a disreputable inn in Bitterbridge, during the Dance of the Dragons. The disguised Ser Rickard_Thorne of the Kingsguard and his charge, Prince Maelor_Targaryen, tried to stay overnight in the Hogs Head while journeying to the safety of Lord Ormund_Hightower's host at the nearby siege of Longtable. Sly was instructed by the owner of the inn, Ben_Buttercakes, to search for coins in Rickard's belongings, but he found Maelor's dragon egg wrapped in Rickard's white cloak instead. When he informed the inn's common room of his discovery, Rickard fled with Maelor, slaying Ben on his way out. Afterward, Sly was hanged from the center span of the old bridge at the command of Lady_Caswell.

name: Small Paul
bio: Small Paul is a steward of the Night's Watch. His nickname is ironic, as he is a very big man.

name: Smike
bio: Smike was a maester of the Citadel who served House Farman of Fair Isle during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Maester Smike served Lord Marq_Farman of Fair Isle, as well as his eldest son, Ser Franklyn_Farman, who succeeded his father as lord in 50 AC. Smike wrote to the Citadel after Lord Franklyn brusquely commanded his late father's guest, Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), to depart Fair Isle. In the letter, Smike states that Franklyn did not realize how close he came to death, for the maester could see the fire in the princess's eyes, who took his lordship's words and tone as a great slight against her.

name: Smiling Knight
bio: The Smiling Knight was an infamous outlaw and robber knight of the Kingswood Brotherhood. His given name has not been published. During the time of the Kingswood Brotherhood, the Smiling Knight killed Ser Victor_Tyrell, a cousin of Mace_Tyrell, Lord of Highgarden. While rescuing Lady Jeyne_Swann and her septa from the outlaws, Ser Barristan_Selmy of the Kingsguard defeated the Smiling Knight and slew Simon_Toyne, chief of the Brotherhood. Jaime_Lannister, then a squire of Lord Sumner_Crakehall and part of Ser Arthur_Dayne's campaign against the Brotherhood, desired to be the one to slay the Smiling Knight in combat, and the youth held his own in a clash against the outlaw. Arthur next fought an extended exchange with the Smiling Knight until the outlaw bled from a dozen wounds. The chivalrous Arthur paused their fight and allowed the Smiling Knight to take a fresh blade to replace his notched longsword. The mad outlaw did so, but remarked that it was Arthur's famous sword, Dawn, that he truly desired. The Smiling Knight was then slain by Arthur, the Sword of the Morning. Ulmer of the Kingswood has often told the men at Castle Black about his adventures with the Smiling Knight and other outlaws from the Brotherhood.

name: Snatch
bio: Snatch is a sellsword of the Second Sons. His rank is serjeant.

name: Snowylocks
bio: Snowylocks is one of the children of the forest in service to the last greenseer. Her true name is too long for the human tongue, according to Leaf.

name: Softfoot
bio: Softfoot is a ranger of the Night's Watch.

name: Sorcerer lord
bio: A sorcerer lord claims to be the sixty-ninth yellow God-Emperor of Yi Ti, despite the fact that the dynasty of the yellow emperors fell a thousand years ago. The sorcerer lord lives in exile, far to the east of of the Golden Empire, in the city of Carcosa in the Further East of Essos. It is unknown whether he will prevail over Bu_Gai, the seventeenth azure emperor, enthroned in Yin, or another pretender, General Pol_Qo, who claims to be the first of the orange emperors.

name: Soren Shieldbreaker
bio: Soren Shieldbreaker is a free folk leader.

name: Spare Boot
bio: Spare Boot is a builder of the Night's Watch.

name: The Sparr
bio: The Sparr is the head and lord of House Sparr of Great Wyk. His son, Steffarion_Sparr, is his heir. His given name has not been published. The Sparr was ritually drowned in service to the Drowned God as a boy.

name: Spotted Cat
bio: The Spotted Cat is a pit fighter famed in Meereen. His origin is unknown, but he supports the pit fighting culture of the city.

name: Spotted Pate
bio: Spotted Pate is a hero from a thousand stories. Pate is a pig boy, a good-hearted, empty-headed lunkhead, who always manages to succeed, often when his stupidity turns out to be a clumsy sort of cunning. He usually bests fat lords, haughty knights, and pompous septons. The stories all end well for Spotted Pate, including sitting on a high lord's seat or bedding a knight's daughter. Because of his legendary status, "Pate" is a popular name among the smallfolk of Westeros.

name: Spotted Pate of Maidenpool
bio: Spotted Pate of Maidenpool is a builder of the Night's Watch. He comes from Maidenpool in the riverlands.

name: Squint
bio: Squint is among Theon_Greyjoy's force that participates in the harrying of the Stony Shore and the capture of Winterfell. While he and Drennan are on guard duty at the Hunter's Gate, Squint is killed on the wallwalk by the direwolves Summer and Shaggydog. Since Squint's body is found in the moat, Theon conjectures that Osha pushed his remains into the water.

name: Squinter
bio: The squinter is a secret servant of the Many-Faced God in the  House  of Black and White in Braavos and a Faceless Man.

name: Squire Squishlips
bio: Squire Squishlips was the nickname Prince Oberyn_Martell gave to an unidentified suitor of his sister Elia_Martell during the trip the siblings took in 273 AC, during which their mother hoped to betroth either one of them, or both. As the Martells stopped at Starfall, the Arbor, Oldtown, the Shield Islands and Crakehall before arriving to Casterly Rock, Squire Squishlips was either a Dayne, a Redwyne, a Hightower, a Grimm, a Chester, a Hewett, a Serry or a Crakehall.

name: Squirrel
bio: Squirrel is a spearwife of the free folk. Squirrel was twelve when she first climbed the Wall with her brother to raid in the Seven Kingdoms.

name: Stableboy at King's Landing
bio: A stableboy at King's Landing serves at the stables of the Red Keep. His given name is unknown.

name: Lord Stackhouse
bio: Lord Stackhouse was the head of House Stackhouse during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Lord Stackhouse coveted the Horseshoe Hills who belong to House Webber of Coldmoat.

name: Lord Stackspear (father of Alys)
bio: Lord Stackspear was the head of House Stackspear during the reign of King Aegon_V_Targaryen. Lord Stackspear's daughter, Alys_Stackspear, was hastly married to the fifteen-year-old Jason_Lannister_(son_of_Gerold) after he took her maidenhood and got her pregnant. The marriage was short lived as Alys died giving birth to their son, Damon_Lannister_(son_of_Jason). During the misrule of the westerlands by Lord Tytos_Lannister, Lord Stackspear doubled the taxes on his smallfolk. Ignoring his liege lord's protests, he hired a company of Volantene sellswords to enforce his extortion. Lord Stackspear and his levies were later part of the host of Lord Tytos's son, Ser Tywin_Lannister, when he suppressed the Reyne-Tarbeck revolt in 261 AC.

name: Stafford Lannister
bio: Stafford Lannister is a knight of House Lannister. He is the good-brother to the Warden of the West as his sister, Lady Joanna_Lannister, married their cousin, Lord Tywin_Lannister. When he was a young squire, Stafford and two Lannisters of Lannisport were captured by Lady Ellyn_Reyne in retaliation for the imprisonment of her husband, Lord Walderan_Tarbeck, by Ser Tywin_Lannister. Lord Tytos_Lannister forced the prisoners to be exchanged, however. Hostilities later erupted with the Reyne-Tarbeck rebellion, which Tywin suppressed. Stafford has been negotiating to betroth his son, Ser Daven_Lannister, to Desmera_Redwyne, although an agreement has not been reached with Lord Paxter_Redwyne.

name: Stalwart Shield
bio: Stalwart Shield is an Unsullied sworn to Daenerys_Targaryen. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is renamed White Rat and portrayed by Marcos James.

name: First Ranger Stane
bio: First Ranger Stane was a member of House Stane who briefly served as First Ranger of the Night's Watch over a thousand years ago. His given name has not been revealed.

name: Stannis Baratheon
bio: Stannis Baratheon is the head of House Baratheon of Dragonstone and the Lord of Dragonstone. He is the secondborn son of Steffon_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End, and Lady Cassana_Estermont. He has an older brother, King Robert_I_Baratheon, and a younger brother, Lord Renly_Baratheon. Stannis serves on Robert's small council as the master of ships. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Stannis is portrayed by Stephen Dillane. Born in 264 AC, Stannis is the second son of Lady Cassana_Estermont and Steffon_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End and head of House Baratheon. He has an older brother, Robert_I_Baratheon, and a younger brother, Renly_Baratheon. When Stannis was no more than four years old, his father took him and Robert to the Red Keep in King's Landing. Stannis believed he saw King Aerys_II_Targaryen on the Iron Throne, holding court. Stannis was impressed, and he and Robert agreed that the king had been as noble as the skulls of the Targaryen dragons were fearsome. It was years later that Steffon told him that it had been the Hand of the King, Lord Tywin_Lannister, whom they had seen, as King Aerys had cut himself on the throne earlier that day and Tywin had taken over for him. As a lad, Stannis found an injured goshawk and nursed it back to health. He named the bird Proudwing, but his brother Robert called it Weakwing, as it would never fly higher than the treetops and never soared. In contrast, Robert owned a gyrfalcon named Thunderclap which never missed a strike. Eventually, Stannis's great-uncle, Ser Harbert, convinced him to abandon the bird, stating that Stannis was making a fool of himself with it. Growing up, Stannis always stood in the shadow of his older brother. Whatever Stannis did, Robert had done first and better. These feelings remained until adulthood. Cressen, the maester at Storm's End, came to care deeply for Stannis, moreso than for his brothers, as he felt Stannis was the one who needed him the most, as the most unloved of the three. Even in his youth, Stannis had been an introverted child, who was mature beyond his years, solemn and joyless. In 278 AC, Stannis's parents were sent on a mission by King Aerys II to Volantis to find a bride for Rhaegar_Targaryen, Stannis's cousin and the crown prince, but they were unsuccessful. Maester Cressen remembers how Lord Steffon had written to him a fortnight before his return, stating that he had found a splendid fool in Volantis who would delight Robert and who might even teach Stannis how to laugh, in time. Stannis and Robert were both present at Storm's End when Steffon and Cassana were expected to return from their voyage, looking on from the parapets of the castle. When the Windproud came in sight of Storm's End, the two brothers witnessed how the ship was smashed against the rocks in Shipbreaker Bay and swallowed by the sea, with Steffon and Cassana drowning. According to Stannis, he lost his faith in the Seven that day. The only survivor of the wreck was the fool, Patchface, who washed up on the shore on the third day. Despite his loyalty and service to his elder brother, Stannis has always felt underappreciated by King Robert_I_Baratheon. Instead of thanking Stannis for holding Storm's End against the Tyrells during Robert's Rebellion, the king thanked his old friend, Lord Eddard_Stark, for lifting the siege. Instead of thanking Stannis for capturing Dragonstone for him, Robert blamed Stannis for the escape of Viserys_Targaryen and Daenerys_Targaryen, even though they left Dragonstone before Stannis had set sail. Stannis has helped Lord Jon_Arryn, Robert's Hand of the King, in ruling the Seven Kingdoms, but he has received little or no acknowledgment or thanks from his elder brother, who spends his time hunting, drinking, and whoring. Stannis feels that Storm's End, the ancestral seat of House Baratheon, was his by rights after Robert took the Iron Throne, and has told Robert so on multiple occasions. In due time, Storm's End was given to young Renly_Baratheon instead. King Robert once rejected Stannis's proposal to outlaw brothels. Stannis has outlawed prostitution on his island of Dragonstone. Dragonstone's maester, the elderly Cressen, fell and broke his hip in 297 AC. Eventually, Stannis began to suspect that the children of Queen Cersei_Lannister—Prince Joffrey_Baratheon, Princess Myrcella_Baratheon, and Prince Tommen_Baratheon—were not actually Robert's. Lord Varys, the master of whisperers, suggests the idea had been mentioned to Stannis by an unnamed individual. Convinced that Robert would not believe him, Stannis confided his suspicions with Lord Arryn, the King's Hand. Stannis and Jon investigated the matter, visiting several of Robert's baseborn children in the capital, including visits to the shop of Tobho_Mott and to Chataya's brothel. According to Gendry, Tobho's apprentice, Stannis and Jon often went riding together, with Renly excluded. During their investigations, Jon agreed with Stannis that his young son and heir, Robert_Arryn, would be fostered with Stannis on Dragonstone. Jon had begun to gather proof when he suddenly died, with Stannis believing that Cersei had Jon killed.

name: Stannis Seaworth
bio: Stannis Seaworth is the sixth son of Ser Davos_Seaworth and his wife Marya_Seaworth.

name: Stanton Piper
bio: Stanton Piper was the Lord of Pinkmaiden and the head of House Piper during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. Lord Stanton succeeded Lord Petyr_Piper after he died in battle against westermen at Acorn Hall in 130 AC. During the Second Battle of Tumbleton, Lord Stanton scraped together fresh levies of greybeards and green boys, though had suffered grievous losses in the autumn battles. Seated atop a low rise, Lord Stanton and Lord Lyonel_Deddings were burned to death by the riderless dragon Vermithor along with their squires, servants, and sworn shields.

name: Starved man
bio: The starved man is a secret servant of the Many-Faced God in the House of Black and White in Braavos and a Faceless Man.

name: Lady Staunton
bio: Lady Staunton was the wife of Lord_Staunton, the ruling Lord of Rook's Rest and head of House Staunton during the Dance of the Dragons. Her given name and birth house are unknown. After the execution of her Lord_Staunton at the Battle of Rook's Rest, Lady Staunton took charge of House Staunton. During the Hour of the Wolf, when Lord Cregan_Stark and his northmen took control of King's Landing and secured the young king Aegon_III_Targaryen, she rode from Duskendale to King's Landing with the armies of Ser Corwyn_Corbray and Lord Clement_Celtigar.

name: Lord Staunton
bio: Lord Staunton was the Lord of Rook's Rest and the head of House Staunton during the Dance of the Dragons. His given name has not been revealed. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, he is named Simon Staunton and portrayed by Michael Elwyn. At the outbreak of the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Staunton was one of the lesser lords that sat on the black council in support of Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen at Dragonstone. Lord Staunton counted himself among Rhaenyra's loyalists, well-aware that King Aegon_II_Targaryen would name him a traitor, having already received a summons from King's Landing demanding an oath of loyalty from him. At the first meeting of the black council, Lord Staunton agreed with Princess Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon) that Rhaenyra would need to win the war with her superior number of dragons. After the sack of Duskendale, Ser Criston_Cole advanced with his host towards Rook's Rest. Having already received word of Cole's march, Lord Staunton closed his gates, but could not stop the greens from burning his lands and attacking his smallfolk and herds. He sent a raven to Dragonstone when supplies ran low, and Princess Rhaenys and her dragon Meleys arrived nine days later. However, Aegon II with Sunfyre and Prince Aemond_Targaryen with Vhagar joined the battle, killing Rhaenys and Meleys. Criston and Aemond then took Rook's Rest and put the garrison to the sword. Lord Staunton's head was taken to King's Landing and mounted above the Old Gate. After his death, his Lady_Staunton took charge of Rook's Rest and rode from Duskendale to King's Landing with Ser Corwyn_Corbray after the ascension of King Aegon_III_Targaryen.

name: Lord Staunton (father of Cassella)
bio: Lord Staunton was the Lord of Rook's Rest and the head of House Staunton during the reigns of King Maegor_I_Targaryen and King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. In 48 AC, Prince Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen pressed his claim to the throne against his usurping uncle, King Maegor_I_Targaryen, and high lords of the realm flocked to his cause. Maegor commanded a score of lesser lords, including Lord Staunton, to gather at King's Landing, but their numbers were few and many soon lost heart. Shortly afterwards, Maegor was found dead upon the Iron Throne, and the prince ascended as King Jaehaerys I Targaryen. In the chaos that followed Maegor's death, many lords fled the capital, but Lord Staunton remained. Along with Lord Jordan_Towers and Lord_Darklyn_(father_of_Jonquil_Darke), he yielded the Red Keep to Prince Jaehaerys, Princess Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I), and Princess Alysanne_Targaryen. The three lords were sent to the black cells, but were eventually pardoned by King Jaehaerys I after surrendering some of their land and sending a child to court to serve as a hostage. In 50 AC, Lord Staunton was among the lords who visited Jaehaerys at Dragonstone. This displeased Jaehaerys' Hand, Lord Rogar_Baratheon, who referred to them as the "lords lickspittle". Lord Staunton's daughter, Cassella_Staunton, became a companion to Princess Rhaena on Dragonstone in 54 AC. She died of poison that same year, however. Five years later, Lord Staunton, his lady wife, and his remaining children all died of the Shivers at Rook's Rest.

name: Steelskin
bio: Steelskin  is a younger pit fighter in Meereen, not regarded as the first rank.

name: Steely Pate
bio: Steely Pate was a blacksmith in the Reach during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Pate is portrayed by Youssef Kerkour. Steely Pate sold Ser Duncan_the_Tall new armor at the tourney at Ashford Meadow. Duncan sold the palfrey Sweetfoot to Henly_(House_Ashford) so that he could afford the armor. After Duncan was arrested for defending the puppeteer Tanselle from Prince Aerion_Targaryen, Pate put a new steel rim on Duncan's oaken shield, and reinforced the back with steel bands, for the price of just a copper penny. The smith accompanied Duncan to the trial of seven held at Ashford Meadow, and he tried to help the mortally-wounded Prince Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II) after the battle. Duncan lost his armor, sword, and horse Thunder when he lost to Ser Uthor_Underleaf in the wedding tourney at Whitewalls. Uthor was dismissive of the armor's quality, but Duncan defended it, saying it was made by Steely Pate. Later, Duncan paid his ransom to Uthor with gold loaned by Lord Brynden_Rivers, who was grateful to Duncan for his help in suppressing the Second Blackfyre Rebellion.

name: Steffar Stammerer
bio: Steffar Stammerer is an ironborn raider in Victarion_Greyjoy's crew.

name: Steffarion Sparr
bio: Steffarion Sparr is the son and heir of The_Sparr, the Lord of House Sparr. Steffarion was ritually drowned in service to the Drowned God on his name day.

name: Steffon Baratheon
bio: Steffon Baratheon was the Lord of Storm's End and the head of House Baratheon during the reign of kings Jaehaerys_II_Targaryen and Aerys_II_Targaryen. To make peace with Lord Lyonel_Baratheon, King Aegon_V_Targaryen betrothed his youngest daughter, Princess Rhaelle_Targaryen, to Lyonel's heir Ormund_Baratheon, and sent her to live at Storm's End. In 245 AC, Rhaelle married now-Lord Ormund, and their son Steffon was born a year later. In his youth, Steffon served as a page and squire in King's Landing, where he became good friends with his cousin, Prince Aerys_II_Targaryen, and another page, Tywin_Lannister. In 260 AC, Steffon participated as a squire in the War of the Ninepenny Kings in the Stepstones. Unfortunately, his father, Ormund Baratheon, Hand of the King and commander of the expedition, was one of the first to perish in the war. Ormund was struck down by Maelys I Blackfyre, commander of the Band of Nine, and died in Steffon's arms. With the death of his father, Steffon became Lord of Storm's End and Lord Paramount of the Stormlands.

name: Steffon Connington
bio: Steffon Connington was a knight from House Connington and the second husband of the Dowager Lady Elenda Baratheon of Storm's End. Ser Steffon was the second son of Lord Connington of Griffin's Roost. With Lord Borros_Baratheon dead after the Dance of the Dragons and his son, Lord Royce_Baratheon a mere infant, attacks from Dornish raiders in the Marches became increasingly common. Lady Elenda_Caron, regent of Storm's End, felt she needed a strong man's hand to keep the peace, and thus married Steffon, who was twenty years her junior. In 133 AC he was slain by Wyland_Wyl in the Dornish Marches while pursuing raiders across the marches, and Elenda was widowed once more.

name: Steffon Darklyn
bio: Steffon Darklyn was a knight from House Darklyn and a member of the Kingsguard, one of the seven Darklyns to serve the order. He was the uncle of Gunthor_Darklyn, the Lord of Duskendale. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Steffon is portrayed by Anthony Flanagan. Ser Steffon was a knight of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. Ser Harrold_Darke, a distant kinsman of the Darklyns of Duskendale, was once his squire. When the king died in his sleep, Steffon was one of the five knights of the Kingsguard in King's Landing, the other two with Viserys' chosen heir and daughter Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen on Dragonstone. Upon learning of the king's death, Ser Criston_Cole dispatched Steffon and the other Kingsguard knights to rouse the members of the small council, intent on crowning Princess Rhaenyra's half-brother as King Aegon_II_Targaryen, usurping the Iron Throne. However, House Darklyn were among Rhaenyra's greatest supporters; under the cover of darkness during the night, Steffon slipped away from the capital to attend Rhaenyra at Dragonstone with his squire, two stewards, four guardsmen, and Viserys' crown, fleeing out a postern gate to a fisherman's skiff to cross the Blackwater Bay. The arrival of Steffon at Dragonstone was an occasion of much joy among Rhaenyra's supporters, especially when it was learned that he had brought her father's crown with him, allowing for a hasty coronation among her supporters. He served as the first Lord Commander of Rhaenyra's Queensguard. When Prince Jacaerys_Velaryon called for dragonriders, Steffon made an attempt to mount Seasmoke during the Sowing, but he was burned to death by the dragon. He was succeeded as Lord Commander by Ser Lorent_Marbrand.

name: Steffon Fossoway
bio: Steffon Fossoway was a knight and heir to House Fossoway of Cider Hall during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Steffon is portrayed by Edward Ashley. Ser Steffon was one of the entrants in the tourney at Ashford Meadow, attended by his cousin Raymun_Fossoway as his squire, promising to eventually knight him. Upon entering the tourney grounds, Ser Duncan_the_Tall watched Ser Steffon defeat Raymun in a spar. Noticing the imposing hedge knight, Steffon challenged Duncan to spar, urged on by Raymun. Not wishing to involve himself in their quarrel, Duncan declined and Steffon challenged Ser Grance_Morrigen instead. After watching the first day of tilts, Steffon considered challenging Ser Tybolt_Lannister on the second day, for they were evenly matched. However, Raymun noted that Steffon intended to look for a wounded or exhausted knight for an easier challenge. While Raymun had hosted Duncan in the Fossoway pavilion, Prince Aerion_Targaryen assaulted the Dornish puppeteer Tanselle, leading to Duncan attacking the prince in her defense. Raymun sought his cousin and together, the Fossoway cousins parted the crowd with their men-at-arms in defense of Duncan. Disgusted by Aerion's actions and seeking glory, Steffon pledged to fight for Ser Duncan_the_Tall during the resulting trial of seven, and promised to find five other great knights to join them, though Raymun doubted Steffon would be successful. The day of the trial, Steffon switched sides when Aerion promised to make him a lord. Raymun, horrified by his cousin's betrayal, joined Duncan and was promptly knighted by Ser Lyonel_Baratheon. Raymun painted the apple on his shield green to mark the difference between the two Fossoways. During the trial, the evenly matched cousins fought each other and both left the field standing, though Raymun hoped he had broken a few of his cousin's ribs. Several years later, Duncan still looked back bitterly on Steffon's betrayal, making him reluctant to trust those of high birth.

name: Steffon Frey
bio: Steffon Frey, also known as Steffon the Sweet, is the son of Walton_Frey and Deana_Hardyng. He is one of the numerous great-grandchildren of Lord Walder_Frey.

name: Steffon Hollard
bio: Steffon Hollard was a knight from House Hollard. He was the twin brother of Ser Symon_Hollard, as well as brother of Ser Jon_Hollard,[N 1] and father of Ser Dontos_Hollard. Ser Steffon died a few years before the Defiance of Duskendale and therefore took no part in it. This played a large role in King Aerys_II_Targaryen sparing the life of young Dontos_Hollard in the aftermath of the defiance.

name: Steffon Seaworth
bio: Steffon Seaworth is the seventh son of Ser Davos_Seaworth and his wife Marya_Seaworth. He is presumably named after Steffon_Baratheon. He lives at the family keep on Cape Wrath with his mother and his older brother Stannis_Seaworth.

name: Steffon Stackspear
bio: Steffon Stackspear is a knight from House Stackspear. He is the eldest son and heir of Lord Selmond_Stackspear.

name: Steffon Sunderland
bio: Steffon Sunderland was the Lord of the Three Sisters and the head of House Sunderland during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. During Aegon's Conquest the lords of the Three Sisters declared their independence from King Ronnel_Arryn_(king), and installed Lady Marla_Sunderland, Steffon's older sister, as their queen. However, when the conquest was done and House Arryn swore their allegiance to House Targaryen, King Aegon I sent an army under the command of Ser Warrick_Manderly and Queen Visenya_Targaryen, which was tasked with putting the rebellion down. The lords of the Sisters were not willing to face the queen's dragon Vhagar, and deposed Queen Marla in favor of Steffon. Lord Steffon renewed his fealty to House Arryn and bent the knee to Queen Visenya. He had to give over two of his sons as hostages for his good behavior, one with the Manderlys at White Harbor, the other to the Eyrie with the Arryns.

name: Steffon Swyft
bio: Steffon Swyft is a knight of House Swyft, the son and heir of Ser Harys_Swyft of Cornfield.

name: Steffon Varner
bio: Steffon Varner is the Lord and the head of House Varner in the Reach.

name: Stern face
bio: The stern face is a secret servant of the Many-Faced God in the House of Black and White in Braavos and a Faceless Man.

name: Stevron Frey
bio: Stevron Frey is a knight from House Frey, Lord Walder_Frey's firstborn son, and heir to the Twins. His mother was Lady Perra_Royce. Stevron has had several children through his marriages to Corenna_Swann, Jeyne_Lydden, and Marsella_Waynwood. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Stevron is portrayed by Colin Carnegie. Stevron was too old to ride in the tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day in 297 AC.

name: Stiv
bio: Stiv is a deserter of the Night's Watch. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is portrayed by Stephen Don. Stiv deserted from the Night's Watch. He has slept with Osha.

name: Lord Stokeworth
bio: Lord Stokeworth was the Lord of Stokeworth and the head of House Stokeworth during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen.

name: Stone Thumbs
bio: Stone Thumbs is a wildling raider.

name: Stonehand
bio: Stonehand is an ironborn raider.

name: The Stonehouse
bio: The Stonehouse is the head and lord of House Stonehouse of Old Wyk. His given name is unknown.

name: Stonesnake
bio: Stonesnake is a veteran ranger of the Night's Watch stationed at the Shadow Tower.

name: Lady Stonetree (wife of Quellon Greyjoy)
bio: Lady Stonetree was a noblewoman of House Stonetree. She was the first rock wife of Lord Quellon_Greyjoy. Her given name is unknown. Lady Stonetree was wed by a priest of the Drowned God to Lord Quellon_Greyjoy. She was the mother of Harlon_Greyjoy, Quenton_Greyjoy and Donel_Greyjoy. After her death, Lord Quellon took a new rock wife, Lady_Sunderly_(wife_of_Quellon_Greyjoy)

name: Lord Stonetree
bio: Lord Stonetree is the head of House Stonetree and a bannerman to Lord Rodrik_Harlaw of Harlaw.

name: Stormbreaker
bio: The Stormbreaker was a famous knight from House Baratheon. Stormbreaker brought glory onto House Baratheon.

name: Stygg
bio: Stygg is a ironborn raider sworn to House Greyjoy. He is a crew member of Sea Bitch.

name: Styr
bio: Styr is the Magnar of Thenn and leader of the Thenns, a tribe of free folk. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Styr is portrayed by Yuri Kolokolnikov. Mance_Rayder had to defeat Styr three times before the Magnar agreed to support him as King-beyond-the-Wall.

name: Sky Blue Su
bio: Sky Blue Su is a whore from Mole's Town.

name: Sumner Crakehall
bio: Sumner Crakehall was the Lord of Crakehall and the head of House Crakehall during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. Ser Jaime_Lannister squired for Sumner for four years; Merrett_Frey was also sent to Crakehall as a page and made a squire by Lord Crakehall. During the campaign against the outlaws of the Kingswood Brotherhood, Sumner had to ransom Merrett back after he was captured by Wenda the White Fawn. After receiving a hard blow to the head from a mace in the next battle, Merrett was no longer able to fight. Unwilling to grant knighthood to Merrett, Sumner kindly sent Merrett back to the Twins. Jaime stopped Big Belly Ben from killing the Lord of Crakehall in battle. Sumner's fate is unknown. Lord Roland Crakehall led the house during the Sack of King's Landing.

name: Lady Sunderly (wife of Quellon Greyjoy)
bio: Lady Sunderly was a noblewoman of House Sunderly. She was the second rock wife of Lord Quellon_Greyjoy. Her given name is unknown. Lady Sunderly was wed by a priest of the Drowned God to Lord Quellon_Greyjoy, after the death of his Lady_Stonetree_(wife_of_Quellon_Greyjoy). She was the mother of Balon_Greyjoy, Euron_Greyjoy, Victarion_Greyjoy, Urrigon_Greyjoy and Aeron_Greyjoy. After her death, Lord Quellon took a new rock wife, Lady_Piper_(wife_of_Quellon_Greyjoy) of Lord Piper of Pinkmaiden Castle.

name: Lord Sunderly
bio: Lord Sunderly is a lord from Saltcliffe in the Iron Islands and is the head of House Sunderly. His relation to Lord Balon_Greyjoy, whose mother was a Sunderly, is unknown.

name: Lord Sunglass
bio: Lord Sunglass is the brother and heir of Lord Guncer_Sunglass.

name: Sure Spear
bio: Sure Spear is a captain of the Unsullied sworn to Daenerys_Targaryen.

name: Surly lad
bio: "Surly lad" is the nickname given by Archmaester Gyldayn to a stableboy in service to House Wylde during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. He deflowered and impregnated Coryanne_Wylde, the younger daughter of Lord Morgan_Wylde. He was gelded shortly after, and sent to the Wall.

name: Sunset girl
bio: The "sunset girl" is a redheaded slave-whore in a Selhorys brothel. Her true name is not mentioned.

name: Lord Sweet
bio: Lord Sweet is the head of House Sweet and one of the queen's men. He may come from the stormlands or the crownlands. His given name is unknown.

name: Sweets
bio: Sweets is a hermaphrodite[N 2] slave owned by Yezzan_zo_Qaggaz.

name: Lord Swyft
bio: Lord Swyft was the Lord of Cornfield and head of House Swyft during the Dance of the Dragons. Lord Swyft declared for the greens during the Dance of the Dragons. He was part of the westermen host led by Lord Jason_Lannister then by Ser Adrian_Tarbeck and Lord Humfrey_Lefford. He was one of the two thousand men who died in the battle by the Lakeshore.

name: Sybassion
bio: Blind Sybassion the Eater of Eyes is a resident of Qarth.

name: Sybell Spicer
bio: Sybell Spicer, also known as Sybell Westerling, is the wife of Lord Gawen_Westerling and is the Lady of the Crag. Sybell's grandfather was a lowborn trader in saffron and pepper, her grandmother a woman he had brought with him from the east; the people of Lannisport called her Maggy_the_Frog. Sybell's father was a wealthy merchant raised to be a petty lord by Lord Tytos_Lannister. Sybell wed Lord Gawen_Westerling, whose family has lost much of their wealth.

name: Sybelle Glover
bio: Sybelle Glover, originally from House Locke, is the wife of Robett_Glover and the mother of Gawen_Glover and Erena_Glover. In the absence of the brothers Galbart_Glover and Robett, Sybelle is the Lady of Deepwood Motte, although it is Glover_steward who truly rules at Deepwood.

name: Sylas Flatnose
bio: Sylas Flatnose was a High King of the Iron Islands. He was chosen by a kingsmoot.

name: Sylas the Grim
bio: Sylas the Grim was a free folk raider during King Aegon_III_Targaryen's regency. In 133 AC, Sylas led three thousand free folk against the Wall, overwhelming the black brothers at Queensgate and spreading out across the Gift. Lord Cregan_Stark rode forth from Winterfell and joined with the Glovers, the Flints, the Norreys, and a hundred rangers from the Night's Watch to hunt Sylas's men and put an end to them.

name: Sylas Sourmouth
bio: Sylas Sourmouth was the steward of Pyke for House Greyjoy.

name: Sylva Santagar
bio: Sylva Santagar, known as Spotted Sylva, is a member of House Santagar. She is the heir of Ser Symon_Santagar, the Knight of Spottswood. Sylva is a childhood friend of Princess Arianne_Martell. They were children at the Water Gardens together, usually a group of five, along with Tyene_Sand, Garin, and Andrey_Dalt. Because of her freckles, Sylva received the nickname "Spotted Sylva". Her friends like to pretend the nickname comes from the fact that she is the heir to Spottswood, as the Santagars' sigil is a spotted leopard.

name: Sylvenna Sand
bio: Sylvenna Sand was a bastard from Dorne and a whore at the House of Kisses, a brothel in King's Landing during the Dance of the Dragons. Sylvenna was the paramour of Essie, another whore and the mother of Gaemon_Palehair, a pretender king during the Dance of the Dragons. Gaemon was claimed to be a bastard of King Aegon_II_Targaryen. During the Moon of the Three Kings, after Rhaenyra_Targaryen's escape from King's Landing, Gaemon gathered thousands of followers and issued a series of edicts, quite likely the work of Sylvenna, for some of them reflected the Rhoynish views on women inherited by the Dornish. After the city was taken by Lord Borros_Baratheon in Aegon II's name, Sylvenna was arrested alongside "King" Gaemon, Essie, and the rest of his "court". After Aegon's return to the capital, Sylvenna was hanged from the battlements of the Red Keep alongside Essie and twenty seven followers of the false king.

name: Sylwa Paege
bio: Sylwa Paege is a noblewoman of House Paege, her relation to the head of House, Ser Halmon_Paege, is unknown. She is the wife of Ser Whalen_Frey, with whom she has two children, Hoster_Frey and Merianne_Frey.

name: Symeon Star-Eyes
bio: Symeon Star-Eyes is a legendary figure from the Age of Heroes who was blind. He is described in tales as a knight even though chivalry came to Westeros thousands of years after.

name: Symon Hollard
bio: Symon Hollard was a knight of House Hollard. The former master-at-arms at the Dun Fort in Duskendale, he played a large role in the Defiance of Duskendale. When Lord Denys_Darklyn defied King Aerys_II_Targaryen and captured him, imprisoning him within the Dun Fort, Ser Symon slew Ser Gwayne_Gaunt of the Kingsguard during the taking of the King. When Ser Barristan_Selmy, another knight of the Kingsguard, entered the Dun Fort and escaped with the King, he avenged his sworn brother by killing Ser Symon.

name: Symon Santagar
bio: Ser Symon Santagar, the Knight of Spottswood is head of House Santagar in Dorne. He has a daughter, Sylva_Santagar, who is his heir.

name: Symon Silver Tongue
bio: Symon Silver Tongue is a singer and harper. Symon learned to play the harp from an old master who believed each man has his song.

name: Symon Stripeback
bio: Symon Stripeback is the commander of the Free Brothers, a company of freedmen. Symon is a former slave of Astapor. He is called Stripeback due to the scars he received by being whipped in the past by his slave masters. He was freed by Daenerys_Targaryen.

name: Symond Botley
bio: Symond Botley is the third son of Lord Sawane_Botley.

name: Symond Crayne
bio: Symond Crayne was a knight from House Crayne and a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Ser Symond served in the Kingsguard during Maegor's reign. After Maegor's mysterious death, the new young king Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen offered Maegor's surviving Kingsguard a choice between execution or taking the black. Symond chose the latter.

name: Symond Frey
bio: Symond Frey is a member of House Frey and is the seventh son of Lord Walder_Frey, the second born of Walder's marriage to Amarei_Crakehall. Symond is married to Betharios_of_Braavos and they have three children, Alesander_Frey, Alyx_Frey and Bradamar_Frey.

name: Symond Staunton
bio: Symond Staunton was the Lord of Rook's Rest and the head of House Staunton during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. He served Aerys as master of laws. When the tourney at Harrenhal was announced, based on the notion that Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen was secretly funding the tourney as a way to call a Great Council to discuss the rule of King Aerys_II_Targaryen, Symond unsuccessfully suggested to prohibit tourneys. When Rhaegar won the final joust, Symond and Lord Qarlton_Chelsted inflamed the king's suspicions by declaring that Rhaegar had taken part only to win the favor of the smallfolk and remind the lords present that he was a true heir to King Aegon_I_Targaryen. Because Rhaegar crowned Lyanna_Stark as the queen of love and beauty, Symond considered it an attempt to win support from House Stark of Winterfell. Symond's fate during Robert's Rebellion is as yet unknown.

name: Symond Templeton
bio: Symond Templeton is the Knight of Ninestars and the head of House Templeton.

name: Syrio Forel
bio: Syrio Forel is a bravo from Braavos, a master fencer and former first sword to the Sealord of Braavos. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Syrio is portrayed by Miltos Yerolemou. The day the old first sword died, the Sealord of Braavos sent for Syrio. The bravo, upon entering the Sealord's presence, was asked what was special about the cat, claimed to be from a far off land, seated on the Sealord's lap. Syrio answered him truthfully, telling him that he has seen tomcats like him a thousand times in the alleys of Braavos. Because he saw the reality of what the cat was, common instead of special and male instead of female, Syrio was named first sword. Syrio has seen the Sealord's menagerie.

name: Taena Merryweather
bio: Taena Merryweather, also known as Taena of Myr, is the Myrish wife of Lord Orton_Merryweather and is the Lady of Longtable. She and Orton have one child, Russell_Merryweather. Taena met Lord Orton_Merryweather during his exile from the Seven Kingdoms. She accompanied Orton when King Robert_I_Baratheon allowed him to return to Westeros.

name: Tagganaro
bio: Tagganaro is a dockside entertainer, cutpurse, and thief in Braavos.

name: Tal Toraq
bio: Tal Toraq is a former slave, originally of the Summer Isles, who was freed by Daenerys_Targaryen.

name: Talbert Serry
bio: Talbert Serry is a knight of House Serry. He is the son and heir of Lord Osbert_Serry of Southshield.

name: Talea
bio: Talea is the younger daughter of Brusco, a fishmonger. She has one older sister, Brea, and a couple of brothers.

name: Talla Tarly
bio: Talla Tarly  is the daughter of Lord Randyll_Tarly and his wife Lady Melessa_Florent. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Talla is portrayed by Rebecca Benson.

name: Tallad
bio: Tallad, called Tallad the Tall, is a hedge knight of common origin. His personal coat-of-arms is checkered blue and white, with three golden eyes on a black fess.

name: Tanda Stokeworth
bio: Tanda Stokeworth is the Lady of Stokeworth and the head of House Stokeworth.

name: Tanselle
bio: Tanselle, called Tanselle Too-Tall, was a Dornish puppeteer during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, Tanselle is portrayed by Tanzyn Crawford.

name: Tansy (innkeep)
bio: Tansy is an innkeep at the Peach, a brothel in Stoney Sept.

name: Tansy (orphan)
bio: Tansy is a girl orphaned by the War of the Five Kings who is currently living at the crossroads inn.

name: Tanton Fossoway
bio: Tanton Fossoway is a knight from House Fossoway of Cider Hall in service to Lord Mace_Tyrell of Highgarden.

name: Last Lord Tarbeck
bio: The "last Lord Tarbeck" is the semi-canonical designation for the three-year-old son of Rohanne_Tarbeck. His given name is unknown. Lord Walderan_Tarbeck was executed during the Reyne-Tarbeck rebellion, and his wife, Lady Ellyn_Reyne, and last surviving son, Tion_Tarbeck, died during the assault on Tarbeck Hall by Lord Tywin_Lannister. Ellyn's daughters, Rohanne_Tarbeck and Cyrelle_Tarbeck, were taken captive and forced to join the silent sisters. Rohanne's husband had been executed alongside Walderan, and their three-year-old son disappeared during the assault. Although the boy was probably thrown down a well by Ser Amory_Lorch, one of Tywin's knights, some stories claim the boy was smuggled to Essos and became a bard popular for his sad ballads.

name: Lord Tarbeck (son of Alyn)
bio: Lord Tarbeck was the Lord of Tarbeck Hall and the head of House Tarbeck during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Lord Tarbeck was born a few months after the death of his father, Lord Alyn_Tarbeck, who died during the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye in 43 AC. In 47 AC, King Maegor_I_Targaryen summoned his mother, Lady Jeyne_Westerling_(wife_of_Maegor_I), and him to court, where he married the widow as one of his Black Brides, using the young Lord Tarbeck as a hostage to prevent any defiance. Lord Tarbeck was later confirmed as Alyn's heir and Lord of Tarbeck Hall. He was sent to Casterly Rock as a ward to Lord Lyman_Lannister. A Lord Tarbeck died of the Shivers in 59 AC, it is unknown if it was him or another lord.

name: Tarber
bio: Tarber is one of the orphan boys Yoren recruits for the Night’s Watch in King’s Landing.

name: Targon
bio: Targon the Halfwild is a knight from the Vale of Arryn.

name: Tarle the Thrice-Drowned
bio: Tarle the Thrice-Drowned is a priest of the Drowned God. He is renowned for his incredible success rate when performing the kiss of life, a rate only surpassed by Aeron_Greyjoy. When Balon_Greyjoy first named himself King of the Iron Islands at the Grey King's Hall, Tarle the Thrice-Drowned placed a driftwood crown upon his head.

name: Tarth triplets
bio: The Tarth triplets were members of House Tarth during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. Their given names have not been revealed. During the early reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen, the wife of the Evenstar of Tarth gave birth to triplets daughters. Aegon's sister-wife, Queen Rhaenys_Targaryen, arranged betrothals for the girls with members of Houses Corbray, Hightower, and Harlaw.

name: Tattered Prince
bio: The Tattered Prince, also known as Rags or Tatters, is the first and only commander of the Windblown. He is well past sixty and keeps his true name to himself. In 262 AC, when the Tattered Prince was twenty-three, the magisters of Pentos chose him to be their new Prince, mere hours after they'd beheaded the previous one. Instead of accepting their offer and be their disposable puppet, he fled to the Disputed Lands and never returned to Pentos. Taking up the life of a sellsword, over the years he rode with the Second Sons, the Iron Shields, and the Maiden's Men. Circa 270 AC, along with five brothers-in-arms, he established the Windblown. Out of the six founders of the company, by 300 AC he is the only one alive. There is enmity between the Windblown and the Company of the Cat. In 299 AC, the two sellsword companies had been on opposite sides of the battle lines in the Disputed Lands, and bad blood continues to linger. Bloodbeard, the leader of the Company of the Cat, has a strong dislike for the Tattered Prince, and makes no secret of this, referring to his rival as old grey-beards in rags.

name: Temmo
bio: Temmo was a Dothraki khal who lived four hundred years before the War of the Usurper, during the Century of Blood after the Doom of Valyria. He was a great conqueror, but today is best remembered for his loss in the Battle of the Three Thousand. Temmo came into conflict with Khal Dhako, the Dragon of the North, who was old by then. Temmo's khalasar broke Dhako's and Temmo took the elder khal captive, and fed Dhako to the flames, cutting off his hands, feet, and genitals, and roasting them before his eyes, after first burning his wives and sons in the same manner. Khal Temmo led his khalasar to Qohor to sack the city. He destroyed the city's defenders on the first day and retired to his camps for the night, planning to sack the city the following day. The next morning he awoke to find three thousand Unsullied barring the way. Eighteen times the khalasar attacked the Unsullied, to no avail. Khal Temmo was slain in the battle, along with all his sons, and bloodriders. His khalasar suffered grievous losses and eventually chose to retreat.

name: Teora Hunter
bio: Teora Hunter was a lady of House Hunter who became the queen consort of Roland_I_Arryn, King of Mountain and Vale. According to legend, Teora reminded her future husband of how his grandfather, Artys_I_Arryn, the first King of Mountain and Vale, had defeated Robar_II_Royce, High King of the Vale, by attacking from the high ground in the Battle of the Seven Stars. Thanks to Teora's words, King Roland_I_Arryn decreed that the Eyrie should be built on the Giant's Lance high above the Gates of the Moon.

name: Teora Kyndall
bio: Teora Kyndall was a noblewoman from House Kyndall who was married to Tybolt_Lannister, Lord of Casterly Rock. They had one daughter, Cerelle_Lannister_(daughter_of_Tybolt).

name: Teora Toland
bio: Teora Toland is the younger daughter of Lady Nymella_Toland of Ghost Hill. Teora dislikes Ghost Hill's maester, Toman, due to the fact that he dismisses her dreams and attributes them to her adolescence and eating sweet foods.

name: Ternesio Terys
bio: Ternesio Terys is the captain of the Titan's Daughter. He is the father of Yorko_Terys and Denyo_Terys. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Ternesio is portrayed by Gary Oliver.

name: Terrance Lynderly
bio: Terrance Lynderly is a member of House Lynderly and the son and heir of Lord Jon_Lynderly.

name: Terrence Kenning
bio: Terrence Kenning is the Lord of Kayce and the head of House Kenning of Kayce.

name: Terrence Toyne
bio: Terrence Toyne was a knight from House Toyne. He was a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen. Ser Terrence Toyne was found abed with King Aegon_IV_Targaryen's mistress, Lady Bethany_Bracken. Even though they proclaimed their love, Aegon had them executed. Terrence was dismembered piece by piece, while Bethany was forced to watch, before meeting her own death. Ser Terrence's brothers attempted to avenge him by killing King Aegon, but were stopped by Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II), who died defending him. The brothers paid for their treason with their lives; this event was key in the downfall of House Toyne.

name: Terrio Erastes
bio: Terrio Erastes was a Braavosi adventurer who lived during the Century of Blood, and the author of the chronicle Fire Upon the Grass, about his time as a guest of the Dothraki Khal Dhako. Terrio witnessed the destruction of Ibbish that led to Dhako being called "the Dragon of the North". He wrote that the khal took great pride in his moniker, but came to rue it when the younger Khal Temmo broke his khalasar, took him captive and fed him to the flames, cutting off his hands, feet, and genitals and roasting them before his eyes, after killing his wives and sons in the same manner.

name: Terro
bio: Terro has dark hair and wears a burgundy brocade and yellow cloak. Terro and his companion Orbelo confront Samwell_Tarly outside the Cattery, a brothel in Braavos. Due to the timely intervention of Arya_Stark, Terro and Orbelo decide to leave Samwell alone.

name: Tess
bio: Tess was a woman of Fair Isle who became a salt wife to Lord Dalton_Greyjoy and murdered him. Following the end of the Dance of the Dragons, Dalton_Greyjoy kept raiding the western coast of Westeros, based at Faircastle. Tess was a woman of humble birth, taken as one of his twenty-two salt wives. Accounts differ as to what is known of her; whether she was thirteen or thirty, pretty or plain, virgin or widow, where Lord Greyjoy found her, how long she had been a salt wife, whether she was mad with jealousy or despised him for his reaving and raping. All that is known for certain is that she cut his throat open from ear to ear with his own dagger after he had his pleasure with her, then threw herself naked and bloody into the sea below in 133 AC.

name: Tessario
bio: Tessario, known as Tessario the Tiger and later Tessario the Thumb, was a sellsword from the free city of Volantis. Tessario was employed in the service of Lord Unwin_Peake during the early reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. After Unwin became Hand of the King, Tessario was made the captain of Lord Peake's personal guard, known as the Fingers. Mushroom mocked him as Tessario the Thumb in reference to the name of the Hand's guard. Tessario is considered by Maester Yandel to be the most likely suspect in the murder of Queen Jaehaera_Targaryen. Tessario killed Ser Robin_Massey, a knight Aegon III wanted to name to the Kingsguard, over a horse that both men wished to purchase. King Aegon III strongly disliked Tessario and the Fingers. Tessario was banned from the Mermaid by Roggerio_Rogare when Tessario struck one of the prostitutes there. After the events of the secret siege, Tessario was named by Lord George_Graceford as being involved in a plot against House Rogare. He was arrested at a tavern near the River Gate, trying to gain passage on an Ibbenese whaler to flee to the Port of Ibben. Under torture and interrogation, he confessed to wanting the Rogares dead, stating he did it for gold, girls and vengeance, being promised the manhood and life of Roggerio and ownership of his brothel. When asked who promised him this, he only grinned. He was tortured more and named several other names before dying, although Lord Unwin Peake was not one of the names he gave up.

name: Thaddeus Rowan
bio: Thaddeus Rowan was the Lord of Goldengrove and the head of House Rowan during the Dance of the Dragons. He served as a Hand of the King during the regency of Aegon III. Lord Thaddeus supported Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen during the Dance of the Dragons. He led one of the armies that marched against Lord Ormund_Hightower's host in the Reach in 129 AC. The armies fought one another during the Battle of the Honeywine, during which Thaddeus and the other blacks seemed to be victorious, until the arrival of Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_I) on his dragon Tessarion turned the battle for the Hightowers and the other greens. Thaddeus retreated north with the remnants of his host. Thaddeus had two sons by his first wife, and five by his second. His second wife died in 131 AC. In 132 AC, King Aegon_III_Targaryen's small council and regents agreed to marry him to Lady Baela_Targaryen. However, Baela was not satisfied with the proposed marriage, and fled to her cousin, Lord Alyn_Velaryon, who she married a fortnight later. Thaddeus's wounded pride was appeased by the council by a betrothal to the fourteen-year-old Lady Floris_Baratheon. She died in childbirth two years later. In 133 AC, after the death of Ser Tyland_Lannister, the Hand of the King, King Aegon summoned Thaddeus to King's Landing to serve as his Hand. King Aegon's summons was overruled by two of his remaining regents, Lord Unwin_Peake and Grand Maester Munkun. To prevent offending Thaddeus, he was offered a place on the small council as lord justiciar and master of laws, and replaced Lord Roland_Westerling as one of the king's regents. After Unwin resigned as Hand in 134 AC, Thaddeus was named to the position. However, Thaddeus, though well-liked, was not feared by the realm, and it was soon rumored that Lady Larra_Rogare, the wife of Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen, and her three brothers were in charge of the kingdom during the Lysene Spring. In 135 AC Lord Thaddeus, accused of treason and being involved in a plot with House Rogare, was tortured for information and stripped of his position of Hand. On day twelve of the secret siege, Lord Thaddeus was brought before the king and prince. Thaddeus face was so bruised and swollen he was near unrecognizable, his speech so slurred he was thought drunk but it was because all his teeth had been knocked out of his mouth. He falsely confessed to King Aegon and Prince Viserys in his involvement in the plot. It was soon clear to Viserys though that Lord Thaddeus had been broken by his experience and simply responded affirmatively when accused of anything, a point that was exemplified when the court fool Mushroom asked him if he poisoned King Viserys_I_Targaryen, to which Thaddeus said he had. After the siege ended and the real conspirators were caught, Thaddeus was reinstated in his post as a regent and King's Hand. It soon became clear he was no longer suited to his office, as one moment he seemed his old self, only to burst into tears the next. Mushroom mocked him at times, and once got him to confess to causing the Doom of Valyria. King Aegon politely dismissed him from the office and Munkun took the position of Hand of the King for the remainder of that year. Lord Thaddeus vowed he would return once his health had improved, only to die on his return journey back to Goldengrove.

name: Theo Bolling
bio: Theo Bolling was a knight of House Bolling during the reign of Maegor_I_Targaryen. Theo was a landed knight during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. He was married to Elinor_Costayne, with who he had three sons. Ser Theo participated in one of King Maegor I's campaigns against the Poor Fellows. In 47 AC, Theo was arrested by knights of the Kingsguard and accused of conspiring with Dowager Queen Alyssa_Velaryon to place Prince Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen on the throne. Although he declared his innocence, Theo  was found guilty and beheaded that same day. Seven days after Theo's execution, King Maegor I announced that he would wed Theo's widow, Lady Elinor. After the wedding, Maegor dispatched one of Theo's sons to be fostered at the Eyrie by House Arryn and the other at Highgarden by Lord Tyrell, while the youngest was given to a wetnurse.

name: Theo Charlton
bio: Theo Charlton was a Lord and a head of House Charlton living around one century before Aegon's Conquest. Theo joined King Harwyn_Hoare in the battle at Fairmarket against King Arrec_Durrandon.

name: Theo Frey
bio: Theo Frey is a knight of House Frey. His relationship with Lord Walder_Frey is not specified, though he is probably a grandson.

name: Theo Teague
bio: Theo Teague was the fourth monarch of House Teague to rule the riverlands. He was known as Theo the Saddle-sore because he spent his entire reign on horse, crushing rebellions and hanging hostages.

name: Theo Tyrell
bio: Theo Tyrell was the Lord of Highgarden and the head of House Tyrell during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. He was the son of Lord Harlan_Tyrell, the first Lord Paramount of the Mander. Theo became the Lord of Highgarden in 5 AC after his father, Lord Harlan_Tyrell, perished in the First Dornish War. King Aegon_I_Targaryen flew Balerion to Highgarden to take counsel with the young Warden of the South, but Theo was reluctant to take any further part in the war against Dorne after the fate that had befallen his father. However, House Tyrell eventually became embroiled when the conflict spilled out beyond the Red Mountains. After the Targaryens made peace with the Dornishmen, Theo spent his time consolidating Tyrell power. He called a council of septons and maesters to examine and dismiss the claims put forth by other houses of the Reach trying to claim Highgarden.

name: Theo Wull
bio: Theo Wull, also known as Buckets because of his clan's sigil, was a member of the Wulls. His relation to Hugo_Wull, the clan chief of the Wulls, is unknown. Theo was one of Lord Eddard_Stark's companions, fighting alongside him during Robert's Rebellion. He was slain at the tower of joy in the Red Mountains of Dorne during a fight with three of Aerys_II_Targaryen's Kingsguard. Afterwards, Eddard had the tower pulled down, and used its stones for cairns for the fallen.

name: Theobald
bio: Theobald is an archmaester of the Citadel. His ring, mask and rod are made of lead.

name: Theodan Wells
bio: Theodan Wells, called Theodan the True, is a pious knight.  Theodan is either from House Wells of the north or from House Wells of Dorne.

name: Theodore Tyrell
bio: Theodore Tyrell is a knight of a junior branch of House Tyrell. He is the eldest son of Ser Luthor_Tyrell_(son_of_Moryn) and Elyn_Norridge. He is married to Lia_Serry and they have two children, Elinor_Tyrell and Luthor_Tyrell_(son_of_Theodore).

name: Theomar Smallwood
bio: Theomar Smallwood is the Lord of Acorn Hall and the head of House Smallwood. He is married to Lady Ravella Swann and is the father of Carellen_Smallwood. Lord Theomar's son and heir died at the age of seven.

name: Theomore
bio: Theomore, born Theomore Lannister, is a maester of the Citadel, in service to House Manderly of White Harbor. He is a member of House Lannister of Lannisport.

name: Theomore Harlaw
bio: Theomore Harlaw was a Lord of Harlaw Hall and a head of House Harlaw during the second century. He raised the Ten Towers to replace Harlaw Hall. He had six wives and was the great-great grandfather of Lord Rodrik_Harlaw. After losing three sons in the cradle, Lord Theomore decided that the dampness and constant flooding of the ancient seat of House Harlaw, Harlaw Hall, was to blame. He ordered the construction of the Ten Towers, a better sited castle as his seat.

name: Theomore Manderly
bio: Theomore Manderly was the Lord of White Harbor and the head of House Manderly during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. One of Lord Theomore's household knights fostered a wildling girl who been captured by rangers of the Night's Watch. Theomore's daughter Mara_Manderly served as a companion to Queen Alysanne_Targaryen at Dragonstone in 55 AC. Lord Manderly lavishly entertained Alysanne when the queen visited White Harbor in 58 AC during a royal progress. Theomore served an aurochs at the welcoming feast, and his daughter, Jessamyn_Manderly, acted as the queen's cupbearer. Theomore held a small tourney to honor the queen and present his knights. Alysanne hosted a women's court in Lord Manderly's hall, something previously unheard of in the north. Theomore and Alysanne negotiated the betrothals of two of her ladies-in-waiting to Theomore's younger sons and a third to a nephew. His eldest daughter and three nieces joined the queen's own party as well, with the expectation they would be pledged to suitable lords and knights at the King's Landing court of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 58 AC Lord Manderly attended a tourney in the capital to celebrate the tenth anniversary of Jaehaerys's coronation. One of Theomore's daughters married Lord Darklyn at Duskendale in 72 AC. Queen Alysanne betrothed her daughter, Princess Viserra_Targaryen, to Theomore, who had been four times a widower, in 86 AC. But the headstrong girl died before her departure to White Harbor, when she was thrown from her horse's saddle in the streets of King's Landing, in 87 AC.

name: Theon Greyjoy
bio: Theon Greyjoy is a member of House Greyjoy and is the sole surviving son and heir apparent of Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands. At the end of Greyjoy's Rebellion, Theon was taken to Winterfell as a hostage and ward to Lord Eddard_Stark. He becomes a POV character in A Clash of Kings. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Theon is portrayed by Alfie Allen. Theon refers to Theon_Stark, a King in the North, as his namesake. Theon received beatings at Pyke from his older brothers, Rodrik_Greyjoy and Maron_Greyjoy. He received his first sword from Dagmer Cleftjaw. Theon was a boy of ten when Greyjoy's Rebellion was crushed and his brothers were killed, making him the heir to House Greyjoy and the Iron Islands. Theon was taken from his father, Lord Balon_Greyjoy, as a hostage and ward by Lord Eddard_Stark and has been raised at Winterfell for nine years since. Considered to be in fosterage, Theon was raised with the Stark children and tutored with them in all disciplines, including instructions from Maester Luwin and training at arms from Ser Rodrik_Cassel. His relationship with the Starks is respectful, and he has come to consider Robb_Stark a close friend and younger brother. Although they trained together, Theon ignored the sullen Jon_Snow, Lord Eddard's bastard son and another outsider in the family. However, though he claims to Catelyn_Stark that Lord Eddard was like a second father to him, and although Eddard indeed attempted to be like a father to him from time to time, Theon still thought Eddard to be cold. As a child, Theon used to think that Eddard might marry him to his eldest daughter Sansa_Stark and claim him for a son. Theon and Robb trained with wooden swords on the steps of Winterfell, and together with Jon Snow were trained at arms by Ser Rodrik Cassel. He played in the castle's godswood, and participated in melees during tourneys. In 296 AC Theon traveled with Lord Eddard to Torrhen's Square, where he spent a fortnight with Benfred_Tallhart, whom Theon teased for his large neck. Theon has slept with numerous women, including Kyra and Bessa_(Winterfell) of the winter town and a Miller's_wife_from_Acorn_Water along the Acorn Water. He lost his virginity to the Barth_(brewer) wife in Winterfell's godswood.

name: Theon III Greyjoy
bio: Theon III Greyjoy was a High King of the Iron Islands from House Greyjoy. Theon III was chosen at a kingsmoot. His reign was among those which saw the decline of ironborn power over the western coast of Westeros, including Houses Gardener and Hightower ceasing to pay tribute. Theon sailed against them, only to be slain by Lord Lymond_Hightower, who revived the practice of thralldom long enough to set the ironmen captured in battle to hard labor strengthening Oldtown's walls.

name: Theon Stark
bio: Theon Stark, known as the Hungry Wolf, was a King in the North and head of House Stark. Theon_Greyjoy refers to Theon Stark as his namesake. King Theon defended the north during the coming of the Andals. Aided by House Bolton, Theon defeated the Andal warlord Argos_Sevenstar in the Battle of the Weeping Water. The King of Winter then sailed across the narrow sea to the coast of Andalos with Argos's body displayed on the prow of his ship like a figurehead. Theon burned a score of Andal villages, killing hundreds and capturing three tower houses and a fortified sept. The king displayed the spiked heads of his victims along his coastline to deter future invaders. Theon later conquered the Three Sisters in the Bite and landed an army on the Fingers, possibly beginning the War Across the Water. He also defeated rebels from the Rills and aided the Night's Watch in imposing a generational defeat on wildlings beyond the Wall. Harrag_Hoare, King of the Iron Islands, led a fleet of ironborn longships against the western coast of the north, conquering the Stony Shore and burning the wolfswood. Harrag's son, Ravos_Hoare, used Bear Island as a base. Theon eventually slew Ravos, however, and expelled the ironmen from his shores, including Bear Island and Cape Kraken.

name: Theron Pyke
bio: Theron, born Theron Pyke, was a maester of the Citadel who lived a century ago. Theron Pyke was born a bastard of the Iron Islands. In his manuscript Strange Stone, Maester Theron postulated the existence of Deep Ones, which he described as a "queer, misshapen race of half men sired by creatures of the salt seas upon human women". Theron suggested they inspired the legend of merlings and that the sea creatures who fathered them inspired the religion of the Drowned God. Theron also noted a certain likeness between the black stone of the ancient fortress on Battle Isle that serves as foundation to the Hightower and that of the Seastone Chair, both of unknown origin. He postulated they had been constructed by the Deep Ones.

name: Therry
bio: Therry is a young turnkey in the Wolf's Den.

name: Thistle
bio: Thistle is a wildling spearwife.

name: Thomas the Threadbare
bio: Thomas the Threadbare was a knight during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Thomas participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC. His shield displayed a frayed maunch, white on brown.

name: Thomax
bio: Maester Thomax was a maester and the author of the book Dragonkin, Being a History of House Targaryen from Exile to Apotheosis, with a Consideration of the Life and Death of Dragons.

name: Thoren Smallwood
bio: Thoren Smallwood is a member of House Smallwood, and a ranger of the Night's Watch. Long ago, Thoren told Jeor_Mormont, Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, that Craster gives his sons to the wood.

name: Thormor Ironmaker
bio: Thormor Ironmaker is an ironborn raider from House Ironmaker. He is a grandson of Erik_Ironmaker.

name: Thoron True
bio: Thoron True was a knight and a sworn sword to Corlys_Velaryon, Lord of the Tides and Hand of the Queen to Rhaenyra_Targaryen, during the Dance of the Dragons. After Lord Corlys was imprisoned under charges of treason for warning Addam_Velaryon of his impending arrest, Ser Thoron and Ser Denys_Woodwright, another sworn sword to the Sea Snake, cut their way into the dungeons of the Red Keep to liberate their lord. However, their plans had been betrayed to Mysaria by a whore Ser Thoron had been bedding. Ser Thoron and Ser Denys were taken and hanged from the walls of the Red Keep, dying at dawn.

name: Thoros of Myr
bio: Thoros of Myr is a red priest of R'hllor from the Free City of Myr, and a member of King Robert_I_Baratheon's court at King's Landing. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Thoros is portrayed by Paul Kaye. Thoros was born in the Free City of Myr, the youngest of eight children, and he was given to a red temple of R'hllor at a young age. He earned his priesthood but was never overly pious due to his tastes for fighting, drinking, and women. Thoros was sent to King's Landing in hopes he could convert the fire-obsessed King Aerys_II_Targaryen, but he was unsuccessful and started to question his own faith. During Robert's Rebellion, Thoros was present when the corpses of Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar) and Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar), clothed in red cloaks to hide their wounds, were presented by Lord Tywin_Lannister to Lord Robert_I_Baratheon before the Iron Throne. Thoros has never forgotten Ser Gregor_Clegane's brutality during the Sack of King's Landing. Thoros became a frequent drinking companion of the new king, Robert I. During Greyjoy's Rebellion, Thoros earned recognition for being the first over the wall at Pyke. Thoros is a frequent tourney mêlée champion, using cheap swords set aflame with wildfire to spook the other combatants' horses. Three times Thoros defeated Sandor_Clegane in the mêlées. At the tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day, Thoros was defeated by Lord Yohn_Royce with a common mace after the fire of his sword guttered out. Tobho_Mott, displeased with the burning of swords, overcharges Thoros for replacements, but Robert is glad to provide for his friend.

name: Three Toes
bio: Three Toes is a member of the Brave Companions.

name: Three-Tooth
bio: Three-Tooth is the steward of Ten Towers for Lord Rodrik_Harlaw. She has been the steward since she was known as Twelve-Tooth.

name: Thurgood
bio: Archmaester Thurgood was an archmaester of the Citadel. He wrote the book Inventories, which listed the existence of 227 Valyrian steel blades in all of Westeros.

name: Thurgood Fell
bio: Thurgood Fell was a knight from House Fell during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Ser Thurgood participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Tickler
bio: The Tickler is a man-at-arms and torturer in service to Ser Gregor_Clegane. One of the Mountain's men, his given name is unknown. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is portrayed by Anthony Morris.

name: Tigaro Moraqos
bio: Tigaro Moraqos was a Lysene magister. Magister Tigaro was present at the trial for the members of the Rogare family who had not fled the city.

name: Tim Stone
bio: Tim Stone is a ranger of the Night's Watch. He is a bastard from the Vale of Arryn.

name: Tim Tangletongue
bio: Tim Tangletongue is a steward of the Night's Watch.

name: Timeon
bio: Timeon, also known as Timeon of Dorne, is a Dornish sellsword of the Brave Companions.

name: Timett
bio: Timett is a red hand, a type of war chief, of the Burned Men, one of the most-feared Vale clans of the Mountains of the Moon. He is commonly known as Timett son of Timett, after Timett_(father), and is also called Timett One-Eye. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Timett is portrayed by Tobias Winter. During the rite of manhood for his clan, Timett burned one of his eyes out of its socket with a hot knife. Because the typical sacrifice for the rite is a nipple or finger, the impressed clan elders immediately named him a red hand of the tribe. Timett was young for this position, but he inspired fear even among his fellow clansmen.

name: Timett (father)
bio: Timett is the father of Timett of the Burned Men in the Vale of Arryn.

name: Timon
bio: Timon the Scrapesword, also called Timon Scrapesword, is a noble knight.

name: Timoth
bio: Timoth is a servant of House Martell at Sunspear.

name: Timotty
