bio: Urrathon IV Goodbrother,[N 1] also known as Badbrother, was a High King of the Iron Islands who ruled during the Age of Heroes. When King Urragon_III_Greyiron died, a kingsmoot was called by his family while one of the king's sons, Torgon_Greyiron, was raiding the Mander. The king's younger sons were hoping that one of them would be elected king, but the ironborn chose Urrathon Goodbrother of Great Wyk instead. Urrathon IV's first act was to have all of the late king's sons at the kingsmoot executed. Thus Urrathon became known as "Badbrother". Urrathon ruled for two years and made many enemies due to his cruelty. Torgon, the only surviving son of the previous king, returned to the Iron Islands and declared the kingsmoot decision unlawful as he had not been present to put forth his claim as was his right. The priests, smallfolk, and lords rallied to Torgon's banner and Urrathon was cast down, hacked to pieces by his own captains. Torgon the Latecomer succeeded Badbrother as king.

name: Urrathon Night-Walker
bio: Urrathon Night-Walker is an inhabitant of Qarth.

name: Urrathon Peake
bio: Urrathon Peake, known as Urrathon the Shieldsmasher, was a knight and an illustrious and legendary ancestor of House Peake.

name: Urreg
bio: Urreg is part of Yoren's band when it is marching north to the Wall to join the Night’s Watch. It is not known whether he is a recruit, a criminal or one of the orphan boys.

name: Urrigon Greyjoy
bio: Urrigon Greyjoy, affectionately called Urri by his brother Aeron_Greyjoy, was the fourth son of Lord Quellon_Greyjoy and Lady Sunderly. He died in his childhood from an infection. In a sample chapter for The Winds of Winter, he is called Urrathon Greyjoy. Urrigon and his younger brother Aeron_Greyjoy were great friends during their childhood. They were both sexually abused by their brother Euron_Greyjoy as children. Urrigon and Aeron often played at the finger dance together. Once, Aeron threw an axe that shaved off part of Urrigon's hand. The maester of Quellon_Greyjoy new wife, Lady_Piper_(wife_of_Quellon_Greyjoy), insisted on sewing Urrigon's fingers back on instead of treating the amputation. Urrigon's hand became infected. They eventually amputated his arm, but it was already too late, and Urrigon died at the age of fourteen as a result of the infection. Urrigon's father, Lord Quellon_Greyjoy, and older brothers, Balon_Greyjoy, Euron, and Victarion_Greyjoy, had been away from Pyke at the time, fighting the final battles of Robert's Rebellion. Lord Quellon died, and Urrigon's brother Balon returned home as the new Lord of the Iron Islands. To avenge his brother's death, he took off the maester's fingers and had Lady Piper sew them back on. The maester died raving.

name: Urrigon Hightower
bio: Urrigon Hightower was a King of the High Tower and head of House Hightower. He was the eldest son and the successor of Uthor_of_the_High_Tower, as well as the brother of Peremore_Hightower. Urrigon's brother Peremore_Hightower had an insatiable thirst for knowledge and therefore surrounded himself with scholars, healers, priests and sorcerers. After Peremore died, King Urrigon granted a tract of land beside the Honeywine to "Peremore's pets" so they might establish and continue their quest for knowledge. Thus the Citadel was established and the Order of Maesters was born.

name: Urron Greyiron
bio: Urron Greyiron, known as Urron Redhand and Urron of Orkmont, was a member of House Greyiron. He was the first King of the Iron Islands to make the throne hereditary, five thousand years before the War of the Five Kings. Urron was the salt king of Orkmont during the rule of his great-uncle, Urragon_IV_Greyiron, who had assumed the title High King of the Iron Islands without being chosen in a kingsmoot. It was the dying Urragon's wish for Urron to succeed him, but the priests of the Drowned God were determined not to lose the power of kingmaking for a third time after Urragon's assumption of the throne and, before that, Torgon_Greyiron's usurpation from Urrathon_IV_Goodbrother. Urron put an end to the institution of kingsmoot when he descended with his axemen on Nagga's Hill on Old Wyk, slaying all captains, the thirteen salt and rock kings, and half a hundred priests and prophets assembled for the choosing. Urron made the high kingship hereditary. Calling himself simply King of the Iron Islands, Urron had his crown be made of black iron instead of the traditional driftwood. During Urron's reign of twenty-two years, the rulers of the various Iron Islands were reduced to lords, and several ancient lines that refused to bend the knee were extinguished. Along with the kingsmoot, Galon Whitestaff's decree against ironborn making war upon each other ended. Urron Redhand and his descendants had to deal with half a dozen major rebellions and at least two major thrall uprisings. House Greyiron is said to have ruled the islands for a thousand years, until the coming of the Andals.

name: Ursula Upcliff
bio: Ursula Upcliff was a member of House Upcliff of Witch Isle. A reputed sorceress who lived during the coming of the Andals to Westeros, Ursula called herself bride of the Merling King. Ursula Upcliff joined the alliance of First Men houses, petty kings, and clans summoned by Robar_II_Royce, High King of the Vale, the Fingers, and the Mountains of the Moon, to fight against the Andals. According to tradition, Robar won her alliance due to his "honeyed tongue." Ursula rode a bloodred horse in the Battle of the Seven Stars, during which she tried to curse Torgold_Tollett. However, according to singers, Torgold leapt upon Ursula's horse, grasped her face, and tore her head from her shoulders as she screamed for succor.

name: Urswyck
bio: Urswyck, known as Urswyck the Faithful and Faithful Urswyck, is a member of the Brave Companions. He is Vargo_Hoat's lieutenant.

name: Urzen
bio: Urzen is a sinewy man with a scrawny throat. He carries a round shield. Urzen is among the ironborn who raid the Stony Shore. He and Black_Lorren silence animals too badly wounded to be saved. Urzen is part of Theon_Greyjoy's force that captures Winterfell. Theon has him investigate the godswood of Winterfell when Summer and Shaggydog are no longer heard. When the disemboweled body of Squint is found, Theon mocks and shoves Urzen for not understanding the dangers of direwolves. Urzen serves as one of Theon's guards at Winterfell. When northmen led by Ser Rodrik_Cassel arrive to retake Winterfell, Theon gives a speech asking ironborn to fight beside him. Urzen shuffles his feet and chooses to depart instead. Urzen is a member of the crew of the Sea Bitch.

name: Uther Peake
bio: Uther Peake was the Lord of Starpike, Dunstonbury, and Whitegrove, and the head of House Peake during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. In 55 AC, Queen Alysanne_Targaryen arranged a marriage between Lord Uther Peake and Lady Prunella_Celtigar, a lady-in-waiting to the queen and the daughter of Lord Edwell_Celtigar. The marriage was considered an exceptional match for Prunella and a triumph on the queen's behalf.

name: Uther Shett
bio: Uther Shett is a knight from House Shett of Gulltown.

name: Uthero Zalyne
bio: Uthero Zalyne was a Sealord of Braavos, in the century after the founding of Braavos. Uthero dispatched envoys of the Iron Bank to Valyria to pay settlements to the grandchildren of the owners of the ships Braavos' founders had seized - but not the value of the escaped slaves. The dragonlords turned out to have little interest in the descendants of slaves who had escaped a century before. Several years later, Uthero sent forth ships throughout the world, announcing the existence[N 1] of Braavos, and inviting the men of all nations to celebrate the 111th anniversary of the city's founding. However, even after it unveiled itself, Braavos continued to be known as the Secret City, with its exact location hidden for another two centuries. The anniversary of the Uncloaking of Uthero is celebrated every year, in a ten day festival of masks and revelry. At midnight on the tenth day, the Titan of Braavos roars, and all celebrants and revelers remove their masks as one.

name: Utherydes Wayn
bio: Utherydes Wayn is the steward of Riverrun under Lords Hoster_Tully and Edmure_Tully. Utherydes is an old man. He is grave, gaunt and sour.

name: Uthor
bio: Uthor was a maester of the Citadel who served Lord Roose_Bolton at the Dreadfort. Uthor's fate is unknown, with Maester Tybald now serving as the Dreadfort's maester. The old maester insisted that Reek's stench was a sign of sickness. When Domeric_Bolton died, Uthor claimed it was a sickness of the bowels. Lord Roose_Bolton, however, suspected his son had been poisoned by Ramsay_Snow.

name: Uthor of the High Tower
bio: Uthor of the High Tower was a legendary king from House Hightower. According to legend, Uthor married Maris_the_Maid, a daughter of Garth_Greenhand, before she could be claimed by Argoth_Stone-Skin, who had won Maris's hand in the first tourney ever held in Westeros. Uthor is credited with commissioning Bran the Builder or his son Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Bran_the_Builder) to rebuild the Hightower, turning it from a tall timber tower and a beacon to a structure of stone that rose two hundred feet above the Whispering Sound.

name: Uthor Tollett
bio: Uthor Tollett is the Lord of the Grey Glen and the head of House Tollett.

name: Uthor Uller
bio: Uthor Uller was the Lord of Hellholt and the head of House Uller during the First Dornish War. During the First Dornish War, King Aegon_I_Targaryen sent Lord Harlan_Tyrell against Lord Uller, though found Hellholt abandoned when he reached the castle. After the death of Queen Rhaenys_Targaryen, four successive Lords of the Hellholt were murdered, for the Iron Throne's ransom on the head of any Dornish lords. It is likely that Lord Uthor was the first of these lords.

name: Uthor Underleaf
bio: Uthor Underleaf, sometimes called the Snail for his personal emblem, was a tourney knight during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Uthor was a professional tourney knight who made his living wagering on jousts and the ransoms he gained for his wins. He preferred not to become a tourney champion, as he did not want to become famous, but was satisfied with the prize granted to the knight who came second. Uthor would bribe the master of the games so that he would face the opponents he preferred—big men, older champions past their prime, and "village heroes"—where the odds would appear to be against him. Ser Uthor attended the wedding of Lord Ambrose_Butterwell and Lady_Frey_(wife_of_Ambrose_Butterwell). He participated in the wedding tourney at Whitewalls in celebration of the marriage. He was not part of the conspiracy to start the Second Blackfyre Rebellion, only attending the tourney for the money he planned to win there. In his first joust, Uthor unhorsed Ser Duncan_the_Tall in the first tilt with a blow to the head. In his second joust, he defeated Ser Addam_Frey. Afterwards, Duncan came to Uthor's tent to forfeit his arms, armor, and horse, Thunder. He saw Uthor chastise his squire, Will_(squire), for accepting "traitor's gold", a dragon with the face of Daemon_I_Blackfyre. Uthor would have preferred to receive a ransom for Dunk's possessions, but Dunk had no money to pay it. Uthor would not accept a promise on parchment, but instead offered Dunk a place in his service. Uthor wanted someone who would appear to be a fearsome knight, who would get excellent odds when faced with Uthor at minor tourneys across Westeros. Uthor thought that by the time news got around that Dunk was a hopeless jouster, they would have won enough money to buy a dragon egg. Dunk refused the offer, however. Uthor also revealed how Ser Glendon_Flowers had achieved his knighthood, and the fact that Dunk had a Alyn_Cockshaw, as someone had bribed Uthor to kill Dunk in the joust. Uthor then defeated Ser Buford_Bulwer in five tilts. For his next tilt, he unexpectedly was to go against Ser Tommard_Heddle, as Lord Gormon_Peake had bribed the master of the games with a better offer than Uthor's, to make sure that Uthor would not face Ser Daemon_II_Blackfyre or win the tourney. However, the tourney was interrupted by claims that a thief had stolen the grand prize of the tourney, Lord Butterwell's dragon egg. While Uthor was at the feast, Duncan infiltrated the knight's pavilion and reclaimed his sword and shield from Will, using them to defend his own squire, Prince Aegon_V_Targaryen, from Tom Heddle. After the defeat of the Second Blackfyre Rebellion, Aegon convinced Lord Brynden_Rivers, the Hand of the King, to loan Dunk the gold to pay Uthor's ransom.

name: Utt
bio: Utt is a disgraced septon, and lieutenant of the Brave Companions mercenary company.

name: Vaegon Targaryen
bio: Vaegon, born Prince Vaegon Targaryen, was an archmaester of the Citadel. He was the seventhborn child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. He was also called Vaegon the Dragonless. Vaegon was born in 63 AC, the fourth son and seventh child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. King Jaehaerys and Queen Alysanne assumed that Vaegon would one day marry his sister Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I), who was only one year younger than him. When Vaegon was five years old, Jaehaerys told him to be kind to Daella, as she would one day become “his Alysanne”. However, as time passed it became clear that the two children did not like one another. Vaegon tolerated Daella's presence, but no more, and considered her to be stupid. Daella, on the other hand, was frightened of Vaegon, and considered him to be mean. Although Jaehaerys believed that the two children would eventually warm to one another, in truth their dislike only increased. In 73 AC, one of Queen Alysanne's lady companions asked Vaegon and Daella when they would be wed. Vaegon's public reaction was a grave insult to Daella, insisting that she needed to find a husband who wanted stupid children, as those were the only kind she would be able to birth. After Daella fled the hall in tears, Vaegon's older sister Alyssa_Targaryen emptied a flagon of wine over Vaegon's head, which did not impress him. Instead, Vaegon simply told his sister that she had wasted Arbor gold, before leaving to change his clothing. Following this incident, Jaehaerys and Alysanne agreed that they would need to find other spouses for Vaegon and Daella. Vaegon's other sisters, Saera_Targaryen and Viserra_Targaryen, were briefly considered, but Alysanne said that Vaegon had never taken any notice of them. King Jaehaerys decided that Vaegon would benefit from spending less time in the library, and asked his elder son Baelon to train Vaegon at arms. Baelon trained Vaegon for a year, during which time both were miserable. One day, Baelon brought their sister Alyssa, dressed in mail, onto the yard as a challenger to Vaegon. Still recalling the incident with the Arbor gold, Alyssa laughed and mocked as she humiliated Vaegon in the yard, as Princess Daella looked on down at them from the castle. When he could take no more, Vaegon threw down his sword and left the yard forever. As Vaegon grew older and began to receive more attention from the young ladies at court, it became apparent that he was not interested in finding a bride. He was polite to the girls, but also did not pay them any attention. His primary interest continued to be books. Once, Grand Maester Elysar gave Vaegon a volume of erotic drawings in the hope that pictures of “naked maidens comporting with men and beasts and one another” might increase Vaegon's interest in women. Although Vaegon kept the book, he did not show a change in behavior. No further mention is made of Vaegon after the Great Council of 101 AC, as he apparently returned to obscurity in his studies at the Citadel. It is unknown if he was still alive during the Dance of the Dragons, though it is not implausible: he was only 38 years old at the time of the Great Council, and would have been 66 years old by the outbreak of the civil war in 129 AC. For that matter, the first volume of Archmaester Gyldayn's history, Fire & Blood, stops in 136 AC, when the long regency of Aegon III ended, at which time Vaegon would have been 73 years old. It is possible that Vaegon's ultimate fate is detailed in the second volume of Gyldayn's history, at some point later in the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen.

name: Vaella Targaryen (daughter of Aenys I)
bio: Princess Vaella Targaryen was the youngest child of King Aenys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alyssa_Velaryon. She died in the cradle. Vaella was born in 39 AC to King Aenys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alyssa_Velaryon. She died not long after her birth. When she was pregnant the first time by her second husband, Lord Rogar_Baratheon, Alyssa recalled Vaella's death, claiming that she could not suffer another child to die during infancy as it would rip her heart apart. Alyssa gave birth to a son, Boremund_Baratheon.

name: Vaella Targaryen (daughter of Daeron)
bio: Princess Vaella Targaryen, called Vaella the Simple, was the daughter of Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I) and  Kiera_of_Tyrosh. Vaella was born in 222 AC to Prince Daeron_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I), the eldest son of King Maekar_I_Targaryen, and Kiera_of_Tyrosh, the widow of Prince Valarr_Targaryen, Daeron's cousin. Her father, then Prince of Dragonstone and Maekar's heir, died before his own father when Vaella was still young, leaving her as his only heir. When King Maekar died in 233 AC in battle against a rebel lord, a Great Council was called to settle the murky question of his successor. As the only child of Maekar's eldest son, Vaella's claim to the Iron Throne was considered, but dismissed immediately by the lords before any of the other candidates, both on account of her mental capacity and gender. Ultimately, the Great Council instead chose Vaella's uncle, Maekar's youngest son Aegon_V_Targaryen, and declared him Aegon the Fifth of His Name.

name: Vaellyn
bio: Archmaester Vaellyn, also known as Vinegar Vaellyn, is one of the teachers at the Citadel of Oldtown. Vaellyn gave Archmaester Marwyn the nickname "Marwyn the Mage" when Marwyn returned from spending eight years in the east. However, he was annoyed when the nickname soon spread all over of Oldtown. The first time the novice Pate_(novice) tried out for a link of his chain, he went before Vaellyn, who criticized him harshly. When Pate was later accused of theft, Vaellyn was among the archmaesters who interrogated him.

name: Vaemond Velaryon
bio: Vaemond Velaryon was a knight from House Velaryon during the reign of King Viserys_I_Targaryen. He was the son of the elder of Lord Corlys_Velaryon's brothers. Vaemond had two sons, Daemion_Velaryon and Daeron_Velaryon. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Vaemond is the younger brother of Corlys instead of his nephew and he is portrayed by Wil Johnson. In 126 AC, when Lord Corlys_Velaryon, Lord of the Tides, was struck by a sudden fever and fell gravely ill, a discussion began about the succession to Driftmark. Though both of Corlys's children, Laena_Velaryon and Laenor_Velaryon, had died six years before, Laena's two daughters (Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon) and Baela_Targaryen) and Laenor's three sons (Jacaerys_Velaryon, Lucerys_Velaryon, and Joffrey_Velaryon) still lived. Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen, who had been married to Laenor, urged Corlys to choose Lucerys as his heir. Vaemond, the eldest of Lord Velaryon's six nephews, insisted he should be Corlys's chosen successor, claiming that Laenor's three sons were not fathered by Laenor at all, and accusing Rhaenyra of having committed adultery with the late Ser Harwin_Strong (this would have ignored the fact that Laena's daughters still stood ahead of Vaemond in the line of succession). In response, Rhaenyra dispatched her new husband, Prince Daemon_Targaryen, to seize Ser Vaemond. She had Vaemond's head removed and fed his carcass to her dragon, Syrax. Vaemond's five younger cousins had their tongues removed afterwards by King Viserys_I_Targaryen, Rhaenyra's father, for repeating the rumors about her three sons by Laenor. Vaemond's sons, Daemion_Velaryon and Daeron_Velaryon, presented their own claims to Driftmark after Corlys died in 132 AC, but the regency of Aegon III chose Alyn_Velaryon to be the new Lord of the Tides instead.

name: Vaggoro
bio: Vaggoro is the writer of Ruined Cities, Stolen Gods, detailing the fall of the Kingdom of Sarnor.

name: Lord Vaith (father of Cassella)
bio: Lord Vaith was the Lord of the Red Dunes and the head of House Vaith during the Conquest of Dorne. Lord Vaith was among the forty powerful Dornish lords who bent their knees with their liege lord, the Prince_of_Dorne_(father_of_Maron), to King Daeron_I_Targaryen in the Submission of Sunspear. In order to insure their loyalties, Daeron took fourteen highborn as hostages, including Lord Vaith's daughter, Cassella_Vaith.

name: Val
bio: Val is a member of the free folk. She is the sister of Dalla, the wife of Mance_Rayder.

name: Valaena Velaryon
bio: Valaena Velaryon was a noblewoman from House Velaryon living at the end of the Century of Blood. She was the wife of Aerion_Targaryen_(son_of_Daemion), Lord of Dragonstone, and the mother of the three conquerors of Westeros: Visenya_Targaryen, Aegon_I_Targaryen, and Rhaenys_Targaryen. Valaena was half-Targaryen herself, through her mother, an as of yet unidentified member of House Targaryen. The marriage between Velaena's grandson, Prince Aenys_I_Targaryen, and Alyssa_Velaryon was considered a cousin to cousin marriage because the bride was kin to the groom's grandmother.

name: Valarr Targaryen
bio: Prince Valarr Targaryen, known as the Young Prince, was the oldest son of Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II), Prince of Dragonstone, and Lady Jena_Dondarrion. In the television adaptation A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms, he is portrayed by Oscar Morgan. Prince Valarr was born the eldest son of Prince Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II), and his wife, Lady Jena_Dondarrion. He had one younger brother, Prince Matarys_Targaryen. At some point, Valarr was married to Kiera_of_Tyrosh. They had sons, but all were stillborn.

name: Valena Toland
bio: Valena Toland is the eldest daughter of the Lady Nymella_Toland of Ghost Hill.

name: Valerion Targaryen
bio: Prince Valerion Targaryen was the twelfth born child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. Valerion was born in 77 AC after a troubled labor that left his mother, Queen Alysanne_Targaryen, bedridden for half a year. Valerion was small at birth, similar to his brother Gaemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Jaehaerys_I). The sickly babe was attended by half a dozen nurses. However, he died the following year, a fortnight before his first nameday.

name: Lord Vance
bio: Lord Vance is the Lord of Wayfarer's Rest and the head of House Vance.

name: Vanyon
bio: Vanyon was a maester of the Citadel of Oldtown, who wrote Against the Unnatural.

name: Varamyr
bio: Varamyr, better known as  Varamyr Sixskins, is a member of the free folk, and a skinchanger who controls three wolves, a snow bear, a shadowcat, and an eagle. He was known as Lump until he took the name Varamyr. The man who would eventually call himself Varamyr was born the second child of a wildling family beyond the Wall, and nicknamed "Lump" by his older sister Meha per wildling tradition. As he was born a month premature, and often ill, his mother waited until he was four to name him properly, by which time it was too late and everyone in his village only called him Lump. When Lump was six, he was jealous of his two-year-old brother Bump, who was much more vigorous and healthy, and skinchanged into one of his family's dogs (Loptail, Sniff, or Growler) to kill him. When Lump's father came upon Bump's body, the dogs were sniffing around it. Not knowing which dog had killed his son, he put down all three, killing them with an axe. Right before he split Loptail's skull, Lump slipped inside the dog's skin and felt his death. His reaction, a scream, informed Lump's parents that he was a warg. Lump's father dragged him through the woods until he came to Haggon, an elderly warg who informed Lump that he belonged with his own kind. Haggon taught him how to skinchange properly and the rules of the ability, and introduced him to other skinchangers. He also instructed him on the strengths and weaknesses of different animals, and gave him rudimentary survival skills. Haggon often traded with the Night's Watch, and it was on one of those trips where Lump began to dream of living south of the Wall. He named himself Varamyr when he was ten years old. When Haggon lay dying, Varamyr, resentful, stole his wolf, Greyskin, who Haggon had intended as his second life. At that point Varamyr was calling himself Varamyr Threeskins, and then Fourskins after taking Greyskin. Over the years, Varamyr died eight more times while wearing the skins of other creatures. However, his power grew and he was known to ride into battle on the back of a 13-foot tall female snow bear. He had a hall of moss, mud, and hewn logs, which had been Haggon's before he died. He became a lord, with a dozen villages paying him homage. When he wanted to have sex with a woman from the villages, he would send his shadowcat to stalk her until she came to him, whether she wanted to or not. A few of the women had relatives who would try to save them by slaying the warg; Varamyr killed them, but claimed not to harm the women, only taking a hank of hair from each. Some of the women became pregnant, but all of Varamyr's children were small and weak, and none had the ability to skinchange. Eventually Mance_Rayder sought an alliance with Varamyr. Varamyr agreed and was a part of Mance's host beneath the Wall.

name: Vardis Egen
bio: Vardis Egen is a knight of House Egen and captain of the household guard at the Eyrie. He has at least one young son. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Vardis is portrayed by Brendan McCormack. During Lord Jon_Arryn's tenure as Hand of the King at King's Landing, Vardis served as his captain of the guards. Lord Robert_Arryn and his mother, Lady Lysa_Arryn, returned to the Vale of Arryn after Jon's death in the capital. Robert played with Vardis's son and the sons of the steward of the Eyrie, but Lysa sent them away, judging them too rough with her son.

name: Vargo Hoat
bio: Vargo Hoat, sometimes called the Goat, is a sellsword from Qohor who leads the Brave Companions mercenary company. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Vargo is replaced by the original character Locke. Vargo and the Brave Companions were brought across the narrow sea by Lord Tywin_Lannister. After having caught a venereal disease, Vargo began sending women to be examined by Qyburn as a precautionary measure, before raping them.

name: Varly
bio: Varly is a member of the household guard of Lord Eddard_Stark at Winterfell. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Varly is portrayed by Graham Charles.

name: Varys
bio: Varys, also known as the Spider, is the master of whisperers on the small council of King Robert_I_Baratheon, a position he previously held for King Aerys_II_Targaryen. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones he is played by Conleth Hill.

name: Vayon Poole
bio: Vayon Poole is the steward of Winterfell under Lord Eddard_Stark. He has one daughter, Jeyne_Poole. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Vayon is portrayed by Matthew Scurfield. In Winterfell, Lord Eddard_Stark reserves one place at his table so that each day he invites a different servant to join him and his family during the meal. When Vayon sits with them, the talk is usually about money, stores, and servants.

name: Veiled Lady
bio: The Veiled Lady is a famous courtesan in Braavos. Her given name is unknown.

name: Veron Greyjoy
bio: Veron Greyjoy was a member of House Greyjoy and the brother of Lord Dalton_Greyjoy. When Lord Dalton_Greyjoy took Fair Isle and Faircastle during the Dance of the Dragons, the Red Kraken claimed four daughters of Lord_Farman_(father_of_Lysa) as salt wives, including Lysa_Farman. A fifth daughter, whom Dalton considered "homely", was given to Veron. Archmaester Gyldayn does not list Veron as one of his brother's heirs after the Red Kraken's death in 133 AC, indicating that Veron had predeceased his elder brother.

name: Vhalaso
bio: Vhalaso was one of the ruling triarchs of the Free City of Volantis before the end of the Rhoynish Wars. Vhalaso ordered the construction of the Long Bridge of Volantis, but did not live long enough to see it finished.

name: Vickon Botley
bio: Vickon Botley is the fifth son of Lord Sawane_Botley.

name: Vickon Greyjoy
bio: Vickon Greyjoy was the Lord Reaper of Pyke and the head of House Greyjoy during Aegon's Conquest. After the demise of House Hoare, Vickon was elected as the first Lord of the Iron Islands. After the demise of House Hoare in the burning of Harrenhal, the Iron Islands became engulfed in chaos as claimants battled over the Seastone Chair. In 2 AC, Aegon_I_Targaryen descended on the islands and put an end to the fighting. Afterward, the issue arose as to who should rule the Iron Islands and Aegon allowed the ironborn to choose their own lord paramount. They elected Vickon, the Lord Reaper of Pyke, a famous captain who boasted a distinguished lineage and descent from the Grey_King. Vickon did homage to Aegon and became the first Lord of the Iron Islands. Ruling from Pyke, Vickon's writ only extended to the Iron Islands; the riverlands which had also been part of the kingdom of House Hoare had already been given to House Tully of Riverrun. Vickon commanded that the practice of reaving be confined to distant waters, forbidding his men to reave the shores of the Seven Kingdoms. He also angered the priests of the Drowned God and many pious lords by allowing the Faith of the Seven to return to the Iron Islands. Vickon paid them no heed, maintaining his loyalty to Aegon the Conqueror. Vickon counselled his son, Goren_Greyjoy, that only a fool would rise against Aegon and his dragons. Vickon died in 33 AC and was succeeded by Goren.

name: Victaria Tyrell
bio: Victaria Tyrell is the daughter of Ser Victor_Tyrell, and is a distant cousin of the main branch of House Tyrell. She is the widow of Lord Jon_Bulwer. They have one daughter, Alysanne_Bulwer, and possibly a Lady_Bulwer's_brother.

name: Victarion Greyjoy
bio: Victarion Greyjoy is a member of House Greyjoy and is a younger brother of Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands. Victarion is the Lord Captain of the Iron Fleet, and his flagship is the Iron Victory. Two of Victarion's brothers, Euron_Greyjoy and Aeron_Greyjoy, often mocked him when they were boys. Euron later gave Victarion his first woman to make him a man. Victarion and his two elder brothers, Balon_Greyjoy and Euron, convinced their father Quellon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands, to join Robert's Rebellion, and the ironborn raided the Reach late in the war. After Quellon was killed in a battle at the Mander, however, the new Lord Balon returned to the Iron Islands with his younger brothers. Victarion's first wife died in childbirth, birthing a stillborn daughter, and his second wife was stricken by a pox. Victarion recognized the greatness of Balon and followed him loyally. He commanded the great fleet constructed by Balon, the Iron Fleet, during Greyjoy's Rebellion. During the war, using a plan Euron had concocted, they sailed into Lannisport and burned the Lannister fleet at anchor. Victarion tossed the first torch onto the flagship of Lord Tywin_Lannister. Later in the rebellion, the Iron Fleet was smashed by Stannis_Baratheon in a sea battle off Fair Isle, during which Golden Storm, the vessel of Victarion's younger brother Aeron, was chopped in half by Stannis's warship, Fury. Victarion continued to serve Balon faithfully as Lord Captain of the Iron Fleet after the rebellion's end, and he took a Victarion_Greyjoy's_third_wife, a salt wife. According to a semi-canon source, she had an affair with Euron, however, and was impregnated by him. When Victarion confronted his brother, Euron laughed and claimed the woman was willing. To retain his honor and prevent the cuckoldry from becoming public, a sobbing Victarion beat his love to death and left her body on the shore for crabs to eat. Only the taboo against kinslaying kept Victarion from doing the same to Euron. Victarion has not touched a woman since, nor has he forgiven Euron, who was forced into exile by Balon in 297 AC. Balon commanded that neither speak of what happened. In addition to his third wife, Victarion has beaten four men to death with his fists. Victarion thrashed Harras_Harlaw on multiple occasions when Harras was young.

name: Victarion Greyjoy's third wife
bio: The given name of Victarion_Greyjoy's third wife has not been revealed in A Song of Ice and Fire. She was beaten to death by her husband. After his first wife died in childbirth and his second wife died from the pox, Victarion_Greyjoy took an unnamed third woman as a salt wife. In 297 AC, Victarion's new wife was seduced or raped by Victarion's elder brother, Euron_Greyjoy, becoming pregnant with his child. According to a semi-canon source, their relationship was an affair. Victarion beat his wife to death with his fists, sobbing himself each time he struck the sobbing woman. Victarion then carried her body down to the rocks to give it to the crabs. Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands, commanded his younger brothers to never speak of the incident. Victarion would have killed Euron, but Balon would have no kinslaying in his hall. Lord Balon sent Euron into exile, never to return while Balon lived. The day that Balon sent Euron off to sea, he reminded Victarion that a kinslayer is accused in the eyes of gods and men. Balon has refused to speak of her and Victarion has not touched a woman since.

name: Victor the Valiant
bio: Victor the Valiant was a knight of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. By 49 AC the young king Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen still had five vacant spots in the Kingsguard to fill. At the wedding of his mother Alyssa_Velaryon and Lord Rogar_Baratheon, it was decided to fill the remaining five slots by a test of arms since knights from all over the realms had come to attend the wedding. Jaehaerys agreed, but with a twist - the knights must fight on foot, not the joust, for foes seldom strike a king from horseback. The wild melees and bloody duels that followed were dubbed by maesters as the War for the White Cloaks with hundreds of knights competing for the honor of serving in the Kingsguard. Ser Victor the Valiant was one of the five champions and was given a white cloak. Prior to this event, Victor was in the employ of the Royces of Runestone. In 59 AC when the winter disease known as the Shivers came to King's Landing, Victor and his sworn brother Ser Samgood_of_Sour_Hill were among those carried off by the disease.

name: Victor Risley
bio: Victor Risley was the Knight of Risley Glade and the head of House Risley during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. He became the King's Justice during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Ser Victor was one of the Caltrops during the Dance of the Dragons. When Lord Unwin_Peake became Hand of the King during the regency of Aegon_III_Targaryen, he had Victor named the King's Justice. During public executions, Victor used his headsman's axe to behead the False_Shepherd and a Pentoshi captain accused of bringing the Winter Fever from Sisterton to King's Landing. Victor killed Orwyle, a former Grand Maester, with a sword. He retained his office after Lord Peake resigned as Hand. During a plot against House Rogare in King's Landing in 135 AC, Victor was named by Ser Gareth_Long as being an accomplice and he was arrested. The trial of the people involved in the plot took place the following year after a Great Council named a new Hand of the King and regents for Aegon III. During the trial, rather than join the Night's Watch, Victor demanded a trial by combat to prove his innocence. Victor was quickly slain in the duel by his accuser, Gareth, who had agreed to take the black.

name: Victor Tyrell
bio: Victor Tyrell was a knight from House Tyrell. He was a distant cousin of Lord Mace_Tyrell of Highgarden. He had two children, Victaria_Tyrell and Ser Leo_Tyrell_(son_of_Victor). Victor was slain by the Smiling_Knight of the Kingswood Brotherhood.

name: Victor Velaryon
bio: Victor Velaryon was a member of House Velaryon during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Victor and his brothers Jorgen_Velaryon and Corwyn_Velaryon escorted King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen when they flew their dragons to Driftmark in 50 AC and toured the shipyards of the island.

name: Violante
bio: Violante was a septa of the Faith of the Seven during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. After King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen wed his sister, Alysanne_Targaryen, he sent seven preachers, including Septa Violante, to walk the realm with the mission of winning over the smallfolk on the issue of their incestious marriage. The royal couple thought it would be easier to spread the values of the Doctrine of Exceptionalism to their people with words rather than swords. Violante and the others traveled the realm alone, walking from village to village, praising Jaehaerys's wisdom and Alysanne's kindness.

name: Violet
bio: Violet was an old widowed peasant woman. Violet used to visit Riverrun years ago in search of shoes that needed new soles.

name: Visenya Targaryen
bio: Queen Visenya Targaryen was the older sister and wife of King Aegon_I_Targaryen, the first Lord of the Seven Kingdoms. Her younger sister, Rhaenys_Targaryen, was married to Aegon as well. Visenya was a dragonrider, and rode the dragon Vhagar. Born in 29 BC on the island Dragonstone, Visenya was the eldest child of Lord Aerion_Targaryen_(son_of_Daemion) and Lady Valaena_Velaryon. Her two younger siblings, Aegon_I_Targaryen and Rhaenys_Targaryen, were born within a few years following her birth. Since young age, Visenya trained together with Aegon at arms, and she became a skilled warrior. As expected, Visenya was wed to her brother Aegon following Valyrian custom. However, Aegon took their younger sister as a wife as well. People claimed that he had wed Visenya out of duty, and Rhaenys out of desire. Prior to her wedding, Visenya became a dragonrider, having bonded with the dragon Vhagar. In her youth Visenya was reported to have visited the Citadel of Oldtown and Lord Redwyne of the Arbor together with her brother Aegon.

name: Visenya Targaryen (daughter of Daemon)
bio: Princess Visenya Targaryen was the daughter of Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen and Prince Daemon_Targaryen. Princess Rhaenyra_Targaryen labored for three days in her apartments in the Sea Dragon Tower of Dragonstone. One month premature, Visenya was a stillborn. The labour had begun when Princess Rhaenyra heard the news from King's Landing, that her father, King Viserys_I_Targaryen, had died, and that her half-brother, Prince Aegon_II_Targaryen, had claimed the throne and had been crowned King, despite Rhaenyra having been Viserys' heir. According to Mushroom, Princess Rhaenyra had cursed Visenya while giving birth, calling her a monster. He also claims that Visenya had dragon-like birth defects, describing her as having been twisted and malformed, with a hole in her chest where her heart should have been and a stubby, scaled tail. Mushroom also claims that it had been he who brought Visenya to the yard of Dragonstone for burning.

name: Viserra Targaryen
bio: Princess Viserra Targaryen was the tenth-born child of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen. Viserra was born to King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alysanne_Targaryen in 71 AC. She was their tenth child and sixth daughter. In 73 AC, after Viserra's brother Vaegon_Targaryen had refused their sister Daella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Jaehaerys_I) as a bride, Viserra and her older sister Saera_Targaryen were briefly considered as a potential spouse for Vaegon. However, Saera was only six years old and Viserra two, and Vaegon had never looked at either of them. As such, the notion was rejected. According to Alysanne, Viserra aimed to become a queen and had her eye set on her fourteen-year older brother, Baelon, who had been a widower since the death of his sister-wife Alyssa_Targaryen two years before. Viserra saw no reason as to why she could not be wed to an older brother. Queen Alysanne, however, was determined to prevent such a marriage, and betrothed the fifteen-year old Viserra in 86 AC to Theomore_Manderly, Lord of White Harbor. The betrothal was possibly also a reaction to Viserra's elder sister Saera's fall from grace. Viserra was not excited about the marriage, as Theomore had grown very stout during his life and had been widowed four times already. Viserra first turned to her father for help, but Jaehaerys agreed with the match and refused to interfere. Viserra then turned to Baelon, hoping for him to rescue her according to court gossip. One night, she slipped past Baelon's guards and climbed naked into his bed, but Baelon sent the drunk girl away when he arrived. In 87 AC, not long after the incident with Baelon, as arrangements were being made for Viserra's departure to White Harbor, Viserra traded clothes with one of her maids and escaped her guards. She slipped from the Red Keep for "one last night of laughter" with her companions, two minor lordlings and four young knights. Near midnight the princess and her remaining companions raced back to the Red Keep. At the foot of Aegon's High Hill, Viserra's palfrey collided with the mare of one of her companions. Viserra was thrown from her saddle into a wall, and she died of a broken neck at the age of fifteen.

name: Viserys Plumm
bio: Viserys Plumm was the head of House Plumm during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Viserys's father, Lord Ossifer_Plumm, was a wealthy but aged man when he wed Princess Elaena_Targaryen in 176 AC. He is said to have died during the bedding, after seeing his wife naked for the first time. Nevertheless, Elaena did conceive, and Viserys was eventually born. However, rumors later suggested that Lord Ossifer had died before he was able to consummate the marriage, and that Viserys instead had been fathered by King Aegon_IV_Targaryen. The claim that Ossifer fathered a child after his death led to the saying that he had had "a cock six feet long". Ser Maynard_Plumm claimed to be distantly related to Lord Viserys, though doubted the lord would admit to it.

name: Viserys Targaryen
bio: Prince Viserys Targaryen is the heir of his father, King Aerys_II_Targaryen, following the death of his older brother, Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen, during Robert's Rebellion. The war turned Viserys and his younger sister, Daenerys_Targaryen, Princess of Dragonstone, into exiles from the Seven Kingdoms who can only plot to regain control of the Iron Throne. He styles himself as "Viserys of the House Targaryen, the Third of his Name, King of the Andals, the Rhoynar, and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm", as he had been declared king on Dragonstone after his father's death. In the Free Cities Viserys was mockingly dubbed the "Beggar King", a name which spread towards the Seven Kingdoms. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Viserys is portrayed by Harry Lloyd. Prince Viserys was born in King's Landing in 276 AC to King Aerys_II_Targaryen and his sister-wife Queen Rhaella_Targaryen, their first child to survive in the seventeen years since their son Rhaegar_Targaryen's birth, after multiple stillbirths, miscarriages, and infant deaths. The celebrated Viserys was small but robust and beautiful. Aerys's paranoia made him fear for the infant's life beyond reason, however. The Kingsguard watched him day and night, and Rhaella was forbidden to be alone with her son. Aerys insisted that his food taster drink from the breasts of the prince's wet nurse, to make sure she had not smeared poison on her nipples. The king burned gifts sent by Westerosi lords, thinking they might be cursed or. Lord Tywin_Lannister hosted a tournament in honor of Viserys's birth in Lannisport, but only Aerys and Rhaegar attended from the royal family. When Rhaegar married Elia_Martell in the Great Sept of Baelor in 280 AC, Aerys refused to allow Viserys to attend. In the years following the wedding, rumors spread that Aerys intended to disinherit his estranged heir in favor of Viserys, even by the king's own men.

name: Viserys I Targaryen
bio: Viserys I Targaryen was the fifth Targaryen king to sit the Iron Throne, ruling from 103 AC to 129 AC. Upon his ascension, he was known as the Young King to differentiate him from his predecessor, his grandfather Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen, who was called "the Old King". Viserys continued the prosperous peace and legacy of his grandsire, but the seeds for the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons that would later engulf the realm were inadvertently sown in his reign. Viserys was a dragonrider who rode Balerion until the dragon died of old age during the reign of Jaehaerys I. Viserys never bonded with another dragon after Balerion's death. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, Viserys is portrayed by Paddy Considine.

name: Viserys II Targaryen
bio: Viserys II Targaryen was the tenth Targaryen king to sit the Iron Throne as Lord of the Seven Kingdoms. He was the son of Rhaenyra_Targaryen and Daemon_Targaryen, the brother and Hand of Aegon_III_Targaryen, and the father of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen, Queen Naerys_Targaryen, and Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II).

name: Viserys Targaryen (son of Aenys I)
bio: Prince Viserys Targaryen was the second son of King Aenys_I_Targaryen and Queen Alyssa_Velaryon.

name: Vogarro
bio: Vogarro was a triarch of Volantis. Vogarro was an elephant, the party of merchants, and was elected triarch seven times. Vogarro owned piers and storehouses, brokered cargoes, changed money and insured shipowners against the hazards of the sea. He also dealt in slaves. He fell in love with a pleasure slave who was taught the way of the seven sighs in Yunkai. He freed and married her, causing a scandal. When he died he left his businesses to his wife, the former slave. She began to manage them and became known as the Widow_of_the_waterfront. She had to sell Vogarro's manse inside the Black Wall because freedmen are not allowed inside.

name: Vorian Dayne
bio: Vorian Dayne, called the Sword of the Evening, was the last King of the Torrentine from House Dayne in Dorne. After Princess Nymeria and Prince Mors_Martell declared supremacy over Dorne, they faced the Kings of the Torrentine in war. Vorian was ultimately defeated in Nymeria's War and was one of the six kings sent to the Wall in golden fetters by Nymeria. The Daynes were subsequently reduced from Kings of the Torrentine to powerful lord bannermen to House Martell, and Ser Davos_Dayne later wed Princess Nymeria.

name: Vorian Vypren
bio: Vorian Vypren was a member of House Vypren and a warlord of the Andals. During the Andal Invasion, he was one of the many warlords that conquered the Riverlands. At the Widow's Ford, Vypren and his knights were held back by three sons of Lord Darry for a day and a night before being victorious.

name: Vortimer Crane
bio: Vortimer Crane is a knight of House Crane and the master-at-arms at Highgarden.

name: Vulture King (Aenys I)
bio: The Vulture King was a Dornish outlaw who gathered thousands of followers to rise against King Aenys_I_Targaryen in the Second Dornish War. His given name is unknown. After the passing of King Aegon_I_Targaryen in 37 AC, the Vulture King raised an army in the Red Mountains against his son, King Aenys_I_Targaryen. The Dornishmen sought vengeance for House Targaryen's attacks during the First Dornish War. Deria_Martell, Princess of Dorne, denounced the insurrection, but she did nothing to stop this Second Dornish War. After burning Blackhaven and slicing off the nose of Lord Harmon_Dondarrion, the Vulture King divided his host in two. Lord Walter_Wyl led one army to Stonehelm, but he was defeated and captured by Orys_Baratheon, Lord Paramount of the Stormlands. The Vulture King led the other host toward Nightsong and Horn Hill, but the outlaw was unable to capture Nightsong. Lord Dondarrion, Lady Caron, and Lord Samwell_Tarly_(lord) defeated the Vulture King's army in a bloody battle. The marcher lords pursued the rebels during the "Vulture Hunt". Savage Sam executed the captured Vulture King by tying him naked between two posts and leaving him to die from exposure. The singers like to say the Vulture King was torn apart and consumed by the same vultures from which he took his name, though in truth, he died from thirst after a few days with no water, and the vultures feasted on his corpse long after he was dead. In later generations, several other leaders of rebels and outlaw bands that arose in the Red Mountains called themselves "Vulture Kings", after the first. None of these later Vulture Kings were as powerful as the first, however, nor could anyone say if they were actually related to him by blood.

name: Vulture King (Jaehaerys I)
bio: The second Vulture King was a self-proclaimed Dornish king who raided in the Third Dornish War during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Whether he was related by blood to other "Vulture Kings" is unknown. The second Vulture King was believed to be the minor son of a minor house in Dorne. While the first Vulture_King_(Aenys_I) had commanded an army of thousands, the second had a force of a few hundred brigands and outlaws who shared his taste for robbery and rape. His band was later joined by Ser Borys_Baratheon, an embittered younger brother of Rogar_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End. With Borys's knowledge of the stormlands, the Vulture King raided with more success, always retreating back to the Red Mountains before a force could be mustered to attack him. He knew the mountain terrain well and always disappeared when pursued, and he launched cunning ambushes upon would-be pursuers. King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen used Vermithor to burn the Vulture King's scattered mountain camps. When the outlaw king was captured at the end of the Third Dornish War, Lord Rogar ordered his chains removed and that he be given a spear and shield. Rogar commanded that if the Vulture King managed to slay him, he was to be set free. The Vulture proved unable for the task and was cut down by Rogar with ease. In 83 AC Morion_Martell, Prince of Dorne, began the ill-fated Fourth Dornish War, as he resented that his princely father had not supported the second Vulture King.

name: Vulture King (Aegon II)
bio: The third Vulture King was a Dornish rebel active during the Dance of the Dragons. Borros_Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End, gathered six thousand men in support of King Aegon_II_Targaryen during the Dance of the Dragons. Instead of marching against Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen and her dragons, however, Borros led his host south into the Red Mountains to retaliate for incursions from Dorne. Borros was victorious in his campaign against the Vulture King, during which Ser Steffon_Connington also proved his valor. Whether the rebel was related by blood to other "Vulture Kings" is unknown.

name: Vulture King (Daeron II)
bio: The Vulture King was a self-proclaimed king who rose up against the Iron Throne from the Red Mountains of Dorne during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. In 206 AC the Lords Lord_Dondarrion_(father_of_Manfred) and Pearse_Caron joined forces and defeated the Vulture King, burning him out of the Red Mountains. Whether he was related by blood to other "Vulture Kings" is unknown.

name: Vylarr
bio: Vylarr is captain of the House Lannister guards in King's Landing.

name: Vyman
bio: Vyman is the maester at Riverrun. Vyman presumably succeeded Kym as the maester at Riverrun.

name: Vyron
bio: Archmaester Vyron is an Archmaester of the Citadel and the author of Triumphs and Defeats, where he speculates that the claim of the tale of Storm's End that the final form of the castle was the seventh, shows a clear Andal influence, and suggests the possibility that it was achieved in Andal times..

name: Lord Vyrwel
bio: Lord Vyrwel[N 1] was the Lord of Darkdell and the head of House Vyrwel during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. His given name is unknown. In 212 AC, Lord Vyrwel came with his lady wife to Whitewalls to attend the wedding of the daughter of Lord_Frey to Lord Ambrose_Butterwell. During the wedding feast, Lord Vyrwel was seated at the top of the hall in a place of high honor. Ser Glendon_Flowers was imprisoned after being accused of stealing the prize of the wedding's tourney, Ambrose's treasured dragon egg. When Ser Duncan_the_Tall publicly declared that Glendon had been falsely accused, Daemon_II_Blackfyre ordered Lords Vyrwel, Sunderland, and Smallwood to fetch Glendon from the dungeons. After the Second Blackfyre Rebellion was put down, a man in service to Lord Vyrwel claimed to have been one of the spies of Lord Brynden_Rivers. He was promptly killed by a knight in service to Lord_Costayne_(Aerys_I). It is unknown if Lord Vyrwel suffered any punishment for the part he played in the rebellion.

name: Waif
bio: The waif is the name given by Arya_Stark to a priest of the Many-Faced God in the House of Black and White. Her given name is unknown. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, she is portrayed by Faye Marsay, as well as various additional actors when she is wearing other faces, including Margaret Jackman and Adam Turns. Based on a tale told by herself, the waif was once the only child of an ancient noble house, her father's heir. Her mother died when she was too young to remember her. When the waif was six years old, her father remarried. Her new stepmother treated her well, until she gave birth to a daughter of her own. Her stepmother then wanted the girl to die, so that her own daughter would inherit her husband's wealth. The stepmother could not bear the sacrifice that the Faceless Men would ask of her, so she tried to poison her stepdaughter herself. She did not succeed, although the poison left the girl with a permanent child-like appearance. When the healer in the House of the Red Hands told the girl's father what the stepmother had done, he offered a sacrifice to the Many-Faced God, offering two-thirds of his wealth and his daughter, so that the Faceless Men would deliver the gift of death to his wife. His prayer was accepted, the stepmother was killed, and the girl came to the House of Black and White to serve. However, the waif herself specifies that her story contains an untruth. She is now a woman grown, who has spent her life serving the Many-Faced God, and will never have any children. Some days she leads the dawn prayer of the priests and acolytes within the House of Black and White. She frequently deals with poisons, including emptying a flagon of poison into the pool of dark water in the temple every evening. According to the kindly man, those poisons are what gives the waif her appearance of a child, which could mean that the lie in her tale is about the effect of the original poisoning by her stepmother.

name: Walda Frey
bio: Walda Frey is the only child of Edwyn_Frey and Janyce_Hunter. As of A Dance with Dragons, she is second in line to inherit the Twins, following her father Edwyn. She is in line before her uncle, Walder_Frey_(son_of_Ryman).

name: Walda Frey
bio: Walda Frey is a member of House Frey, the second daughter of Lothar_Frey and Leonella_Lefford.

name: Walda Frey
bio: Walda Frey, better known as Fair Walda, is the daughter of Walton_Frey and Deana_Hardyng. She is called Fair Walda as she is considered one of the more beautiful daughters of House Frey. It is common knowledge around the Twins that Walda has occasionally slept with her first cousin, Walder_Frey_(son_of_Ryman).

name: Walda Frey
bio: Walda Frey, known as Fat Walda, is the second daughter of Merrett_Frey and Mariya_Darry, and a granddaughter of Lord Walder_Frey, the head of House Frey. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Walda is portrayed by Elizabeth Webster.

name: Walda Frey
bio: Walda Frey, better known as White Walda, is the daughter of Rhaegar_Frey, a grandson of Lord Walder_Frey, and his wife Jeyne_Beesbury.

name: Walda Rivers
bio: Walda Rivers is the daughter of a lady of House Charlton and Ser Walder_Rivers, the eldest natural son of Lord Walder_Frey.

name: Walda Rivers
bio: Walda Rivers is the daughter of Ser Aemon_Rivers. She is a great-granddaughter of Walder_Frey, Lord of the Crossing.

name: Walder Brax
bio: Walder Brax is the second son of Ser Flement_Brax and Morya_Frey.

name: Walder Frey
bio: Walder Frey, nicknamed Big Walder, is the son of Ser Jammos_Frey and Sallei_Paege. His personal coat of arms, depicted on his shield and surcoat, quarters the twin towers of House Frey with the tree and ravens of House Blackwood and the twining snakes of House Paege.

name: Walder Frey
bio: Walder Frey, called Black Walder to differentiate him from others with the same name, is a member of House Frey. He is the second son of Ser Ryman_Frey, grandson to Ser Stevron_Frey, and great-grandson of Lord Walder_Frey. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Black Walder is not present as a standalone character, but he is merged with Walder_Rivers as Black Walder Rivers, who is portrayed by Tim Plester. Black Walder is known to be resolute and easy to anger, feared by many including among his own family. Enmity runs deep between Walder and his elder brother, Edwyn_Frey. Black Walder refuses to marry, according to Merrett_Frey. Walder is rumored to instead have maintained numerous affairs with several women, including cousins and the wives of his close relations. There is talk that he bedded the wives of his brothers Edwyn (Janyce_Hunter) and Petyr_Frey (Mylenda_Caron), his cousin Walda_Frey_(daughter_of_Walton), and his half-cousin once removed Amerei_Frey, among others. Black Walder is even rumored to have been unusually close with Lord Walder's seventh wife, Annara_Farring. The illicit relations may cast doubt over the paternity of many of the younger Freys, including Edwyn's daughter, the young heiress Walda_Frey_(daughter_of_Edwyn), as well as her cousin Perra_Frey (Petyr's daughter), and Lord Walder's six children with Annara.

name: Walder Frey
bio: Walder Frey, nicknamed Little Walder, is the son of Merrett_Frey and Mariya_Darry. His personal coat of arms, depicted on his shield and surcoat, quarters the twin towers of House Frey with the brindled boar of House Crakehall and the plowman of House Darry.

name: Walder Frey
bio: Walder Frey, known as Red Walder, is the youngest son of Ser Emmon_Frey, the second son of Lord Walder_Frey, and Genna_Lannister, the sister of Lord Tywin_Lannister. He has three brothers: Ser Cleos_Frey, Ser Lyonel_Frey, and Tion_Frey.

name: Walder Frey
bio: Walder Frey is the Lord of the Crossing and head of House Frey. Though almost ninety years old and infirm, he still maintains an active hand in the running of his house at the Twins. Walder has become famous for siring many children and surviving many wives. He is to marry his eighth wife and has over a hundred descendants, base and trueborn. Walder has had twenty-two trueborn sons and seven trueborn daughters from his marriages, with an unknown number of bastard sons and daughters. He places a great emphasis on family loyalty, though his descendants jockey ruthlessly for his favor. Many Freys name their sons Walder and daughters Walda hoping that the lord will favor them. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Walder is portrayed by David Bradley. As a toddler Walder was present at the wedding of Lady_Frey_(wife_of_Ambrose_Butterwell) to Lord Ambrose_Butterwell at Whitewalls.[N 1] It is said he caused the marriage when he discovered his sister having intimate relations with a servant. Ser Duncan_the_Tall thought that Walder was an extremely annoying child, and was sorely tempted to throw him down a well. When Lord Tytos_Lannister acquiesced to Walder's proposal to marry his second son, Emmon_Frey, to Tytos's daughter Genna_Lannister, the ten-year-old Tywin_Lannister spoke out against the match. During Robert's Rebellion, Walder and his levies arrived at the Battle of the Trident after the rebels had already won. Hoster_Tully, the Lord Paramount of the Trident, has since called his bannerman "the Late Lord Frey", a name Walder despises. Lord Jon_Arryn refused to foster Walder's grandsons at court in King's Landing and his own son, Robert_Arryn, at the Twins. Lord Hoster Tully refused a Frey bride for his son Edmure_Tully, and Hoster did not attend Walder's wedding to his seventh wife, Annara_Farring.

name: Walder Goodbrook
bio: Walder Goodbrook is a member of House Goodbrook. He is the only son of Ser Garse_Goodbrook and Kyra_Frey.

name: Walder Haigh
bio: Walder Haigh is a member of House Haigh, a son of Ser Harys_Haigh.

name: Walder Rivers
bio: Walder Rivers, also known as Bastard Walder, is a knight and the eldest of Lord Walder_Frey's bastards. He is married to a lady of House Charlton and has two children, Walda_Rivers and Aemon_Rivers. Walder's personal coat of arms is a silver-grey castle on blue (the colors of House Frey reversed) beneath a red bend sinister. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Walder Rivers is not present as a standalone character, but he is merged with Walder_Frey_(son_of_Ryman) as Black Walder Rivers, who is portrayed by Tim Plester. Walder's mother died after falling from a horse. When Chett was caught for murder near Sevenstreams, Walder was sent from the Twins to judge him. Chett ended up being sent to the Wall in the company of Yoren.

name: Walder Stackspear
bio: Walder Stackspear was a knight of House Stackspear during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Walder participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Walder Vance
bio: Walder Vance is the eldest son of Ser Dafyn_Vance and Maegelle_Frey.

name: Walder of Woodmere
bio: Ser Walder of Woodmere was a knight from Woodmere during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Walder was listed as a participant of the tourney at Ashford Meadow of 209 AC.

name: Walderan Tarbeck
bio: Walderan Tarbeck was the last confirmed Lord of Tarbeck Hall and head of House Tarbeck, who ruled during the reign of King Aegon_V_Targaryen.

name: Waldon Wynch
bio: Waldon Wynch is the Lord of Iron Holt and the head of House Wynch.

name: Walgram
bio: Archmaester Walgram was an archmaester of the Citadel, and the author of The Reckoning of Time. The book delves deeply into the problem of studying ancient records when various cultures reckon days, seasons, and years differently.

name: Walgrave
bio: Walgrave is an elderly archmaester of the Citadel who has lost his wits. Walgrave is considered by many to have the foremost knowledge in ravencraft, and he wrote a book about the subject called Black Wings, Swift Words. His chambers are located in the west tower of the Ravenry, below the rookery of the white ravens. The birds are Walgrave's pride, and he wants them to eat him when he dies. Walgrave once taught Maester Yandel how to read. However, he is now an old man and keeps the title archmaester only by courtesy, his wits having left him a long time ago. He often soils his smallclothes, and has been found weeping in the Citadel's Library, unable to remember the way back to his chambers. The novice Pate_(novice) has been assigned to help him, tending the ravens, fetching Walgrave's meals, cleaning his chambers, and dressing him every morning. Walgrave cannot recall names very well (though he can easily tell one raven from another), and often calls Pate by the name of Cressen. Maester Gormon_Tyrell now judges those seeking a black iron link in Walgrave's place. There is a strongbox underneath Walgrave's bed, containing a bag of silver stags, a lock of yellow hair tied up in a ribbon, a painted miniature of a woman who resembles Walgrave (even to her mustache), and a lobstered steel knight's gauntlet that supposedly once belonged to a prince. The box also contains Walgrave's archmaester's key, which can open any door in the Citadel. The strongbox's lock was broken by Walgrave after he lost the key that opened it.

name: Wallace Massey
bio: Wallace Massey is a steward of the Night's Watch. Wallace is the squire of Ser Denys_Mallister, the commander of the Shadow Tower.

name: Wallace Waynwood
bio: Wallace Waynwood is the youngest son of Lady Anya_Waynwood.

name: Wallen
bio: Wallen is a deserter from the Night's Watch. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is portrayed by Barry O'Connor. Wallen deserted from the Night's Watch.

name: Walter Brownhill
bio: Walter Brownhill was a minor landed knight and the head of House Brownhill during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Ser Walter ruled a few hides of land on Cape Wrath from a castle described as being made of "mud and tree roots". By 136 AC, Walter had been married and widowed thrice. Thirteen of his sixteen children still lived. After Lady Cassandra_Baratheon was found guilty of attempting to poison King Aegon_III_Targaryen and Queen Daenaera_Velaryon, Lord Lorent_Grandison and three other stormlords arranged for her to marry Walter instead of facing a harsher punishment, due to her high birth. Lady Elenda Baratheon thought caring for Walter's children would end Cassandra's scheming and plotting, and it did.

name: Walter Whent
bio: Walter Whent was the Lord of Harrenhal and the head of House Whent during the reign of King Aerys_II_Targaryen. According to a semi-canon source, Walter was married to Shella_Whent.[N 1] According to A Game of Thrones and A Clash of Kings, Shella is also from House Whent. In 281 AC, the year of the false spring, Lord Whent hosted the great tourney at Harrenhal to honor his maiden daughter. Ser Barristan_Selmy recalls that the tourney was announced by Walter shortly after he was visited by his brother, Ser Oswell_Whent of the Kingsguard. Most people believed that Walter was attempting to outdo the Anniversary Tourney hosted by Lord Tywin_Lannister in 272 AC, but some believed that a "shadow host"—possibly Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen—provided the funding for Walter's extravagant prizes. Lord Whent's daughter reigned as queen of love and beauty when the tourney began. Her four brothers and her uncle, Oswell, swore to defend her crown, but Prince Rhaegar won the joust and named Lyanna_Stark the new queen of love and beauty. King Aerys_II_Targaryen, Rhaegar's father, unexpectedly attended the tourney and was suspicious of Walter. Ser Jorah_Mormont defeated a Lord Whent in the tourney at Lannisport in 289 AC; it is unknown if it was Walter or one of his descendants.

name: Walter Wyl
bio: Walter Wyl was the Lord of Wyl and the head of House Wyl during the reign of King Aenys_I_Targaryen. Walter was a son of the infamous lord known as the Wyl_of_Wyl, who passed away in 27 AC. Lord Walter was among the handful of Dornish lords that joined the host of the first Vulture_King_(Aenys_I) during the reign of King Aenys_I_Targaryen in 37 AC. Walter had heeded the Vulture King's call for all "true" Dornishmen to join his cause against the Seven Kingdoms, despite Princess Deria_Martell denouncing the outlaw "king". As the Vulture King's host grew in size, he divided his strength and marched against Nightsong and Horn Hill, while Walter commanded the other half to besiege Stonehelm, the seat of House Swann. Lord Orys_Baratheon rode forth from Storm's End and smashed Walter's host beneath the walls of Stonehelm. Walter was injured and captured during the battle and brought before Orys. Walter's father had chopped off Orys's sword hand during the First Dornish War and, in retaliation, Orys chopped off Walter's sword hand, then his other hand, followed by both feet. Orys deemed this "usury".

name: Steelshanks Walton
bio: Walton, known as Steelshanks Walton, is a captain in service to Lord Roose_Bolton of the Dreadfort. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Walton is portrayed by Jamie Michie.

name: Walton Frey
bio: Walton Frey is a member of House Frey, third born son of Ser Stevron_Frey and first born to Marsella_Waynwood. He is married to Deana_Hardyng, with whom he has three children, Steffon_Frey, Walda_Frey_(daughter_of_Walton) and Bryan_Frey.

name: Walton Stark
bio: Walton Stark, known as Walton the Moon King, was a King in the North. He is buried in the crypt of Winterfell.

name: Walton Stark
bio: Walton Stark was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the early reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Walton attended the Golden Wedding in King's Landing with his father, Lord Brandon_Stark_(father_of_Walton), and younger brother, Alaric_Stark, in 49 AC. Brandon died soon after returning to the north and was succeeded by Walton. When the disgraced former Kingsguard knights, Ser Olyver_Bracken and Ser Raymund_Mallery, launched a mutiny of the Night's Watch in 50 AC, Lord Walton rode north to aid loyal black brothers in putting down the rebellion. After Walton beheaded Olyver with Ice at Rimegate, other mutineers fled beyond the Wall from Sable Hall. Walton pursued them into the haunted forest, but his party came under attack by giants. It is claimed that Lord Stark slew two giants before he was torn apart, with the surviving northmen bringing his body parts back to Castle Black. Alaric, who succeeded Walton as Lord of Winterfell, blamed King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen for his brother's death, as the mutineers' lives had been spared by the young king after the wars of Maegor_I_Targaryen. Alaric showed Walton's tomb in the crypt of Winterfell to Jaehaerys when the king visited Winterfell in 58 AC.

name: Walton Towers
bio: Walton Towers was a household knight of King Maegor_I_Targaryen, rising to be the short-lived Lord of Harrenhal and the founder of House Towers. After King Maegor_I_Targaryen wiped out House Harroway in 44 AC, he grew weary of the ambitious king's men entreating him for the lordship of Harrenhal. Instead, Maegor decreed that the strongest of his household knights would have Harrenhal. Ser Walton was one of twenty-three knights of the king's household who fought in the blood-soaked streets of Lord Harroway's Town for the prize. Though gravely wounded in the melee, Walton emerged victorious and was named Lord of Harrenhal, though its domains were much diminished as the Harroway holdings held separately from Harrenhal were granted to Lord Alton_Butterwell and Lord Darnold_Darry. Walton did not live long enough to enjoy his lordship, as he died within a fortnight from the wounds he had taken in gaining his prize, and Harrenhal subsequently passed to Walton's eldest son, Jordan_Towers. House Towers was the fourth house to rule Harrenhal, but Walton's line faltered after only two generations and died with Maegor_Towers in 61 AC.

name: Waltyr Frey
bio: Waltyr Frey, called Tyr, is the twenty-first born son of Lord Walder_Frey, the third son of his marriage to his seventh wife, Lady Annara_Farring. However, rumor in the Twins is that Annara was sleeping with Walder_Frey_(son_of_Ryman) making Waltyr's parentage dubious.

name: Walys Flowers
bio: Walys, born Walys Flowers, was a maester of the Citadel. Walys was born a bastard son of a Hightower girl and an archmaester of the Citadel. After forging his chain, he served at Winterfell when Lord Rickard_Stark was head of House Stark.

name: Walys Mooton
bio: Walys Mooton was the Lord of Maidenpool and the head of House Mooton during the early part of the Dance of the Dragons. In the television adaptation House of the Dragon, a character named Ser Walys Mooton will be portrayed by Richard Riddell. Following the battle of Rook's Rest in the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Walys desired to recover Rook's Rest for the blacks and Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen. Walys led a hundred knights from Maidenpool and joined with soldiers from the Crabbs, Brunes, and Celtigars. They took the greens' garrison at Rook's Rest by surprise and recovered the castle. Afterwards, Walys led his bravest soldiers to the field west of Rook's Rest to kill the injured dragon Sunfyre. The attackers failed to kill the dragon, however. Three score were killed, including the charred Lord Walys, before the survivors fled. Walys was succeeded as Lord of Maidenpool by his brother, Manfryd_Mooton, who found Walys's body when he arrived at the castle a fortnight later.

name: Ward of Gyles Rosby
bio: The ward of Gyles Rosby is a young male living at Rosby in the crownlands. His name has not yet been mentioned. Lord Gyles_Rosby has no immediate heir.

name: Warg King
bio: The Warg King was a monarch and a skinchanger living in the North at some point after the Long Night. His given name is unknown. Documents of the Nightfort indicate that the Warg King ruled Sea Dragon Point. He was allied with the children of the forest, but they were defeated by the Kings of Winter of House Stark. The Starks killed his sons, beasts, and greenseers, but took his daughters as prizes.

name: Warren
bio: Warren is a landed knight from the riverlands.

name: Warrick Manderly
bio: Warrick Manderly was a knight from House Manderly during the reign of King Aegon_I_Targaryen. Since the Three Sisters had declared Marla_Sunderland their queen during Aegon's Conquest, King Aegon_I_Targaryen tasked Lord Torrhen_Stark, his new Warden of the North, with ending the rebellion. Torrhen granted command of a northern army to Warrick, who sailed from White Harbor to the Bite on Braavosi galleys. The Sistermen's Rebellion ended when Sisterton spotted Warrick's fleet and Queen Visenya_Targaryen on her dragon, Vhagar. Marla's younger brother, Steffon_Sunderland, became the new Lord of the Three Sisters and sent one of his sons to be fostered by the Manderlys at White Harbor for his good behavior.

name: Warryn Beesbury
bio: Warryn Beesbury is the Lord of Honeyholt and the head of House Beesbury.

name: Blue Bard
bio: The Blue Bard is a young singer in service with House Tyrell. His real name is Wat. Wat was the son of a cooper and was raised in the trade, but he found he had more talent for making lutes than barrels. When he was twelve years old, he ran away to join a musician troupe. He wandered half the Reach before coming to King's Landing in search of patronage.

name: Wat (Night's Watch)
bio: Wat is the brother of Jack-Be-Lucky. He was sent to the Wall.

name: Wat (orphan)
bio: Wat is a boy orphaned by the War of the Five Kings who is currently living at the Inn at the crossroads.

name: Wat (sailor)
bio: Wat is a member of the crew of the Brazen Monkey.

name: Wat (Standfast)
bio: Wat was a peasant in service to House Osgrey during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Wat was among the smallfolk called up by Ser Eustace_Osgrey to defend his lands against House Webber when a dispute over the Chequy Water escalated into threats of war. Wat was trained for battle by Eustace's sworn swords, Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield and Ser Duncan_the_Tall. Because several of the recruits had the same first name, Duncan's squire, Egg, suggested that they be called by "village names" to distinguish them. However, because the villages had no names, the men were instead given surnames derived from the crop planted in their village. The men were excited to have been given "lord's names", and Wat was given the surname "Bean" or "Melon", distinguishing him from the brothers Wat_Barleycorn and Wet_Wat. After the first day of training, Duncan had a nightmare that all the men had been killed and he had to bury them. After Ser Duncan realized that it would be a hopeless fight where all the smallfolk would certainly die, he sent Wat home with the others.

name: Wat Barleycorn
bio: Wat, also known as Wat Barleycorn, was a peasant in service to House Osgrey. He had a younger brother, Wet_Wat, who was also called Wat Barleycorn. Wat and his younger brother, Wet_Wat, were among the smallfolk called up by Ser Eustace_Osgrey to defend his lands against House Webber when a dispute over the Chequy Water escalated into threats of war. Will was trained for battle by Eustace's sworn swords, Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield and Ser Duncan_the_Tall. Bennis laughed when he learned the brothers had the same first name, joking that their mother must not have known any other name but Wat. Because several of the other recruits also had the same first name, Duncan's squire, Egg, suggested that they be called by "village names" to distinguish them. However, because the villages had no names, the men were instead given surnames derived from the crop planted in their village. The men were excited to have been given "lord's names", and Wat was given the surname "Barleycorn". Since Wat's younger brother was also named Wat Barleycorn, he was additionally dubbed "Wet Wat", after he said he had once fallen down the village well. After the first day of training, Duncan had a nightmare that all the men had been killed and he had to bury them. After Ser Duncan realized that it would be a hopeless fight where all the smallfolk would certainly die, he sent Wat home with the others.

name: Wat the Hewer
bio: Wat the Hewer was a leader of the Poor Fellows during the Faith Militant uprising. During the Faith Militant uprising, Wat the Hewer led Poor Fellows out of the Reach on a march towards King's Landing. In the battle at Stonebridge he killed half a dozen knights, including Lord Meadows, the commander of King Maegor_I_Targaryen's army, Wat also faced Myles_Smallwood in single combat with both men surviving. Wat was taken alive and delivered to King's Landing in chains. Maegor cut off Wat's limbs with his own axe, then commanded his maesters to keep Wat alive so he could attend the king's wedding to Tyanna_of_the_Tower. When Maegor later took Tyanna as his third wife on Rhaenys's Hill, surrounded by the remains of the Warrior's Sons who had died there when Maegor burned down the Sept of Remembrance, Wat was present as a witness.

name: Wat the Tanner
bio: Wat the Tanner was a simple tanner who lived in King's Landing during the Dance of the Dragons. When riots broke out in King's Landing in 130 AC, Wat was among the mob that stormed the manse of Lord Bartimos_Celtigar, the despised master of coin. After Bartimos was captured and tortured, Wat declared to Bartimos that he had not paid the "cock tax" and thus his manhood was forfeit. Wat later rode through the streets upon a stolen horse, displaying Lord Bartimos's severed head and genitals to the crowds and declaring an end to all taxes. Due to his profession, he was dubbed Wat the Tanner by historians. During the second day of the riot, Wat and his followers overwhelmed the Gate of the Gods and poured out into the countryside up the kingsroad. Wat still carried Lord Celtigar's rotting head.

name: Wet Wat
bio: Wat, better known as Wet Wat and Wat Barleycorn, was a peasant in service to House Osgrey. He had an older brother, who was also called Wat_Barleycorn. Wat and his older brother, Wat_Barleycorn, were among the smallfolk called up by Ser Eustace_Osgrey to defend his lands against House Webber when a dispute over the Chequy Water escalated into threats of war. Will was trained for battle by Eustace's sworn swords, Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield and Ser Duncan_the_Tall.
