Bennis laughed when he learned the brothers had the same first name, joking that their mother must not have known any other name but Wat. Because several of the other recruits also had the same first name, Duncan's squire, Egg, suggested that they be called by "village names" to distinguish them. However, because the villages had no names, the men were instead given surnames derived from the crop planted in their village. The men were excited to have been given "lord's names", and Wat was given the surname "Barleycorn". Since Wat's older brother was also named Wat Barleycorn, the younger Wat was additionally dubbed "Wet Wat", after he said he had once fallen down the village well. After the first day of training, Duncan had a nightmare where he saw Wet Wat with his head cut nearly in half, and that all the other men had also been killed and he had to bury them. Wet Wat was assigned to stand watch atop Standfast to look out for Lady Rohanne_Webber's forces. However, after Ser Duncan realized that it would be a hopeless fight where all the smallfolk would certainly die, he sent Wet Wat home with the others.

name: Whitesmile Wat
bio: Whitesmile Wat is a singer with a high, sweet voice.

name: Wate
bio: Wate was a guardsmen in service to House Targaryen during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Wate is one of the guardsmen who accompanied Prince Aerion_Targaryen to Ashford for the tourney at Ashford Meadow. He helped Prince Aerion destroy the puppeteer's stall after Aerion took issue with their skit involving a dragon.

name: Watkyn
bio: Watkyn is a sellsword in service to the Golden Company. He is cupbearer and squire to the company commander Harry_Strickland.

name: Watt of Long Lake
bio: Watt of Long Lake is a member of the Night's Watch from Long Lake in the north.

name: Watty the Miller
bio: Watty the Miller is a member of the brotherhood without banners.

name: Waymar Royce
bio: Waymar Royce is a knight of House Royce. The third and youngest son of Lord Yohn_Royce of Runestone, he became a ranger of the Night's Watch. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Waymar is portrayed by Rob Ostlere. He was previously played by Jamie Campbell Bower in the rejected pilot episode of the series. As a third son of a lord, Waymar had few chances at wealth or land and so joined the Night's Watch. He was escorted by his father, Lord Yohn_Royce, north to the Wall. While they stayed at Winterfell as guests on the way, Sansa_Stark fell wildly in love with Waymar. Among the brothers of the Night's Watch, Waymar's behavior and appearance earned him some ridicule behind his back, including from Gared and Will. Shortly before A Song of Ice and Fire begins, Lord Commander Jeor_Mormont gave Waymar the command of a ranging in the haunted forest in search of a band of wildling raiders. He was accompanied by Gared and Will. Waymar was the least experienced of the three, having been in the Night's Watch for less than half a year, whereas Jeor counted Gared and Will among his best men. However, Waymar felt it was his due to have a command because he was a knight, and Jeor accepted because he did not want to offend Waymar's father.

name: Wayn
bio: Wayn is a guard sworn to House Stark.

name: Weasel
bio: Weasel, also known as the crying girl, is a girl from the riverlands found by Yoren on his march north from King's Landing. Her real name is unknown.

name: Webber
bio: Webber is a sellsword of the Windblown of Westerosi origin. It is unknown if he has any connection with House Webber, as men of the free companies can take any name they want.

name: Weeper
bio: The Weeper, also known as the Weeping Man, is a notorious free folk raider and leader of a war band. Lord Torghen_Flint hates the Weeper for blinding girls and for raping, stealing, or slaying three of his kin. Jarl has gone over the Wall with raiders like Alfyn_Crowkiller and the Weeper.

name: Weese
bio: Weese is the understeward of the Wailing Tower at Harrenhal. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, he is renamed Weasel and portrayed by Paul Kealyn.

name: Wenda
bio: Wenda the White Fawn was a famous member of the Kingswood Brotherhood, an outlaw band. It is unknown if she was connected with House Cafferen of Fawnton, whose sigil includes white fawns. Wenda branded her trademark, a fawn, on the buttocks of Merrett_Frey, a squire captured by the Kingswood Brotherhood. Lord Sumner_Crakehall ransomed Merrett back from her. Merrett was unable to sit for a fortnight. It is not clear what happened to Wenda when the outlaws were destroyed.

name: Wendamyr
bio: Wendamyr is a maester who serves House Greyjoy at Pyke. He tends the ravens, acts as a healer, and serves as a counselor to Balon_Greyjoy, Lord of the Iron Islands. Wendamyr came to his post after the death of Maester Qalen.

name: Wendel Frey
bio: Wendel Frey is the nineteenth son of Lord Walder_Frey, the first son born of his marriage to Annara_Farring. He is a page at Seagard. Rumor at the Twins is that Annara slept with Walder_Frey_(son_of_Ryman), making Wendel's parentage dubious.

name: Wendel Manderly
bio: Wendel Manderly is a knight of House Manderly. He is the second son of Wyman_Manderly, Lord of White Harbor. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Wendel is portrayed by Oddie Braddell.

name: Wendell Webber
bio: Wendell Webber was a knight of House Webber during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. He was the cousin of Wyman_Webber, Lord of Coldmoat. Wendell was married to the sister of Lord Rowan of Goldengrove. He and his wife had many sons and daughters. Lord Wyman_Webber dictated in his will that his daughter, Lady Rohanne_Webber, must have married before the second anniversary of his death or Coldmoat and its lands would go to his cousin, Ser Wendell.

name: Wendello Qar Deeth
bio: Wendello Qar Deeth is a member of the Pureborn in Qarth.

name: Wendeyne
bio: Wendeyne is a mummer at the Gate  in Braavos.

name: Werlag
bio: Werlag is an ironborn raider sworn to House Greyjoy.

name: Wex Pyke
bio: Wex Pyke is the bastard son of Sargon_Botley of Lordsport.

name: Whale That Walks
bio: The Whale That Walks was the nickname Prince Oberyn_Martell gave to an unidentified suitor of his sister Elia_Martell during the trip the siblings took in 273 AC, during which their Princess_of_Dorne_(mother_of_Doran) hoped to betroth either one of them, or both. As the Martells stopped at Starfall, the Arbor, Oldtown, the Shield Islands and Crakehall before arriving to Casterly Rock, the Whale That Walks was either a Dayne, a Redwyne, a Hightower, a Grimm, a Chester, a Hewett, a Serry or a Crakehall.

name: Whalen Frey
bio: Whalen Frey is a knight from House Frey. He is the fourteenth son of Lord Walder_Frey, the third by Walder's fourth wife, Alyssa_Blackwood. He is married to Sylwa_Paege, with whom he has had two children.

name: Wick Whittlestick
bio: Wick Whittlestick is a steward of the Night's Watch.

name: Widow of the waterfront
bio: The widow of the waterfront is a businesswoman and former bedslave who runs several docks, piers, and shipping lanes in a section of western Volantis. Her late husband was Vogarro, a triarch of Volantis. Her given name is unknown. The widow of the waterfront was once a pleasure slave taught the way of the seven sighs in Yunkai. She was purchased by Vogarro, a Triarch of Volantis. Vogarro fell in love with her, causing a great scandal, and an even greater scandal when he freed her and married her. Vogarro owned piers, storehouses, brokered cargoes, changed money, and insured shipowners against the hazards of the sea. When he died in 268 AC, his widow took over his business. She had to sell his manse, because no freedman may live within the Black Wall in eastern Volantis, regardless of wealth. She took up residence at the Merchant's House west of the Rhoyne, and set up her business in its common room. The widow once had a monkey which performed clever tricks.

name: Widower
bio: The Widower is a member of the Stormcrows, the second-in-command to Daario_Naharis.

name: Wilbert
bio: Wilbert is a knight from Stoney Sept. He has some authority over the town. It is possible that he belongs to a house of landed knights, and that Stoney Sept is part of their domain.

name: Wilbert Osgrey
bio: Wilbert Osgrey, also known as the Little Lion, was a knight from House Osgrey during the reign of Gyles_III_Gardener, King of the Reach. According to a story told by Ser Eustace_Osgrey, a King of the Rock, either Lancel_IV_Lannister or Lancel_V_Lannister, attempted to cut a piece out of the Reach while Gyles_III_Gardener, King of the Reach, was battling the Storm King in the east. It fell to Wilbert to stop Lancel as his his older brothers were off in the east with Gyles. During the battle, Wilbert came face to face with the King of the Rock. It is said the king had the Valyrian steel sword Brightroar; however, it was probably a common steel sword of the same name, which has been conflated with the Valyrian blade.[N 1]  Lancel fought Wilbert for half a day, breaking the knight's shield and sword and chopping the Little Lion to bits. As Wilbert was dying, however, he drove his dagger into a gap in Lancel's armor and killed the king, causing the westermen to retreat. According to Maester Yandel, Lancel IV yielded the Valyrian steel sword Brightroar. When the King of the Rock invaded the Reach, Gyles abandoned his siege of Storm's End to deal with the westermen. Yandel writes that Lancel died in battle at Red Lake.

name: Will
bio: Will is a ranger of the Night's Watch. He is the POV character for the prologue of A Game of Thrones. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Will is portrayed by Bronson Webb. Will had been a poacher before joining the Night's Watch, hunting in House Mallister's woods. After Lord Mallister's freeriders caught him skinning a buck, Will choose to taking the black instead of losing a hand. During his first ranging, a scared Will remembered old stories he had heard about the lands beyond the Wall. At the start of A Song of Ice and Fire, however, he has already been in the Night's Watch for four years and is a veteran of a hundred rangings. Will's stealth and hunting experience proved useful for scouting as a ranger. Shortly before the series starts, Will, Gared, and Ser Waymar_Royce were sent ranging into the haunted forest by Jeor_Mormont, Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, in search of a band of wildling raiders. Jeor gave the command to Waymar, although he was the least experienced of the three, whereas Jeor counted Gared and Will among his best men. However, Waymar felt it was his due to have a command because he was a knight, and the Lord Commander accepted because he did not want to offend Waymar's father, Lord Yohn_Royce.

name: Treb
bio: Will, called Treb, was a peasant in service to House Osgrey. Will was among the smallfolk called up by Ser Eustace_Osgrey to defend his lands against House Webber when a dispute over the Chequy Water escalated into threats of war. Will was trained for battle by Eustace's sworn swords, Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield and Ser Duncan_the_Tall. Because several of the recruits had the same first name, Duncan's squire, Egg, suggested that they be called by "village names" to distinguish them. However, because the villages had no names, the men were instead given surnames derived from the crop planted in their village. The men were excited to have been given "lord's names", and Will was given the surname "Barleycorn" or "Melon", distinguishing him from the Will_Bean.[N 1] Additionally, after Will said he was good at throwing rocks, Bennis laughed and said they now had a trebuchet, and Will was called "Treb" by everyone afterwards. During the training, only Treb was fast enough to get a spear past Duncan's shield, but he only did it once. After the first day of training, Duncan had a nightmare where he saw Treb had an arrow in his eye, and that all the other men had also been killed and he had to bury them. Treb was assigned to stand watch atop Standfast to look out for Lady Rohanne_Webber's forces. However, after Ser Duncan realized that it would be a hopeless fight where all the smallfolk would certainly die, he sent Treb home with the others.

name: Will (orphan)
bio: Will is among the orphans taken in by Jeyne_Heddle and Willow_Heddle at the crossroads inn. When Brienne of Tarth and her party arrive at the inn, Will is told by Willow to drop the rock he holds for defense.

name: Will (squire)
bio: Will was the squire of Ser Uthor_Underleaf during the reign of King Aerys_I_Targaryen. Ser Duncan_the_Tall threatened him and took back the armor he had lost to Uthor_Underleaf in the Whitewalls Tourney.

name: Will Bean
bio: Will, also known as Will Bean, was a peasant in service to House Osgrey. Will was among the smallfolk called up by Ser Eustace_Osgrey to defend his lands against House Webber when a dispute over the Chequy Water escalated into threats of war. Will was trained for battle by Eustace's sworn swords, Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield and Ser Duncan_the_Tall. Because several of the recruits had the same first name, Duncan's squire, Egg, suggested that they be called by "village names" to distinguish them. However, because the villages had no names, the men were instead given surnames derived from the crop planted in their village. The men were excited to have been given "lord's names", and Will was given the surname "Bean", distinguishing him from the Treb. After the first day of training, Duncan had a nightmare that all the men had been killed and he had to bury them. After Ser Duncan realized that it would be a hopeless fight where all the smallfolk would certainly die, he sent Will home with the others. As Will ran off, he was almost accidentally disemboweled by Pate_(Standfast) when he tripped over his spear.

name: Fletcher Will
bio: Fletcher Will is an inhabitant of the Riverlands.

name: Hookface Will
bio: Hookface Will is a guard usually posted to the gate at Dragonstone. A fish hook made a long scar on his face.

name: Will the Stork
bio: Will, better known as Will the Stork, is a hedge knight.

name: Will of the Woods
bio: Will of the Woods is a sellsword and member of the Windblown.

name: Will Cole
bio: Will Cole is a sellsword in service to the Golden Company. As a serjeant, he is a high-ranking officer. Will is a Westerosi exile. His surname Cole is of a house that was once prominent in the histories of the Seven Kingdoms. House Cole served as stewards to House Dondarrion in the years preceding the Dance of the Dragons, and their most prominent member was Ser Criston_Cole, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard under Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. By the present day, their arms are obscure, and they may be extinct. Will may or may not belong to House Cole, as in the free companies a man can call himself whatever he chooses, and not all are genuine. He might be related to another serjeant in the company, Dick_Cole.

name: Will Humble
bio: Will Humble is an ironborn member of House Humble.

name: Will Short
bio: Will Short is a man of the North.

name: Willam
bio: Willam was the brother of Septon Meribald. He died in the War of the Ninepenny Kings. William and his brothers, Meribald, Robin, Owen_(brother_of_Meribald) and Owen's friend Jon_Pox all went off to fight in the War of the Ninepenny Kings. During the War, Willam and Robin died from a fever. Owen died in battle after receiving a blow from a mace and Jon was hanged for rape. Meribald survived to become a septon.

name: Willam the Wanderer
bio: Willam the Wanderer was a knight of the Warrior's Sons at the start of the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. In 42 AC, Ser Willam was stationed at the King's Landing chapter of the Warrior's Sons, based in the Sept of Remembrance. He fought in the trial of seven in which seven of the Warrior's Sons faced King Maegor_I_Targaryen and his six champions. Willam and Ser Damon_Morrigen were the last Warrior's Sons standing against Maegor, whose six champions had all been killed. Badly wounded, the two were killed by Maegor with Blackfyre, the king's Valyrian steel sword. As he fell, however, Ser Willam dealt the king a terrible blow to the head, cracking his helm and leaving him insensate. However, this was not enough to kill the king and Maegor was declared the winner.

name: Willam the Wasp
bio: Willam the Wasp was a knight of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Willam was a sworn sword to Lord Myles_Smallwood of Acorn Hall. In 49 AC, he was one of the five champions in the War for the White Cloaks and was given the white cloak of the Kingsguard. When rumors reached King's Landing that Balerion the Black Dread had been sighted in the Velvet Hills north of Pentos, King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen sent a dozen men under the command of Willam to investigate. A guide who claimed to know where the beast was offered to escort Willam and his party to the dragon's lair. In truth, brigands had been spreading rumors of a dragon in the area in order to turn others away. Willam and his men were led into a trap by the guide and ambushed. Willam was the last of his party to die and it was said he gave a good account of himself. An agent of Lord Rego_Draz, the master of coin, managed to retrieve the knight's head, confirming Willam's death. Lord Smallwood, the Hand of the King, wanted Pentos to be punished, but Jaehaerys declined.

name: Willam Dustin
bio: Willam Dustin was the Lord of Barrowton and the head of House Dustin during Robert's Rebellion. He was married to Barbrey_Dustin, now the Lady of Barrowton. Young Lord Willam had been married to Barbrey_Dustin for less than half a year before the outbreak of Robert's Rebellion. Barbrey urged him to send one of his kin in his place, but Willam insisted on leading the levies from Barrowton himself. Barbrey gave her husband a fine red stallion, the pride of the herds of her father, Lord Rodrik_Ryswell. Willam vowed he would return to her mounted on the steed. Willam was present at the wedding of his liege, Lord Eddard_Stark, to Lady Catelyn_Stark at Riverrun. During the bedding custom, Willam praised Catelyn's breasts to Eddard. Willam was one of six companions who accompanied Eddard to the tower of joy in the Red Mountains of Dorne, where Lord Stark sought his sister, Lyanna_Stark. They were opposed by three Kingsguard, however, and only Eddard and Lord Howland_Reed survived the ensuing battle. Eddard buried the fallen in cairns raised from the tower's stones. Lord Stark returned the horse to Willam's widow, Barbrey, when he returned to the north, and told her that Willam had died an honorable death.

name: Willam Foxglove
bio: Willam Foxglove, also known as Will Foxglove, is a knight sworn to King Stannis_Baratheon.

name: Willam Royce
bio: Willam Royce[N 1] was a knight from House Royce, loyal to Rhaenyra_Targaryen and the blacks during the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons. During the storming of the Dragonpit in 130 AC, Prince Joffrey_Velaryon attempted to save the dragons in the Dragonpit from the crazed mob, by flying there from the Red Keep on his mother's dragon, Syrax. Queen Rhaenyra_Targaryen sent the Seven Who Rode, including Ser Willam, to rescue her son and heir. Willam and the other six knights faced many perils as they fought their way across King's Landing, while the city burned in riot. Unfortunately, Prince Joffrey fell from Syrax and died from the impact, and the mob began tearing apart his remains. While trying to recover Joffrey's body, three of the Seven died fighting the mob. Willam Royce was killed when a man leaped down from a rooftop to land on his back. Willam's Valyrian steel sword Lamentation was torn from his hand and carried off, never to be found again.

name: Willam Stackspear
bio: Willam Stackspear was the Lord and head of House Stackspear during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. He became one of Aegon's regents. During the Great Council of 136 AC, three regents were selected by lot to serve in the regency for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Lord Willam Stackspear was among the three regents selected, considered an inoffensive, though undistinguished, choice. While the regents and Lord Torrhen_Manderly, the Hand of the King, were discussing King Aegon III's first royal progress, the young king entered the room and dismissed them all, for he had come of age.

name: Willam Stafford
bio: Willam Stafford, known as the Drunken Knight and the Keg o' Ale, was a knight during the reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Willam participated in the War for the White Cloaks in 49 AC during the wedding of Queen Regent Alyssa_Velaryon and Lord Rogar_Baratheon, hoping to win one of the vacant places in the Kingsguard. Willam quickly became a favorite of the smallfolk, who started calling him "the Keg o' Ale" and would sing the "Hail, Hail Keg o' Ale" whenever he took the field. In the end did not win a white cloak. In 55 AC, Willam participated in the tourney held to celebrate the completion of the Dragonpit, to the joy of the smallfolk. When he fell, he was replaced as a favorite of the commoners by Ser Harys_Hogg.

name: Willam Stark
bio: Willam Stark was the Lord of Winterfell and the head of House Stark during the reign of King Maekar_I_Targaryen. Willam became Lord of Winterfell after the death of his older brother, Donnor_Stark. Willam first married Lyanne_Glover and had one child with her, Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Willam), but Lyanne died while giving birth to the boy. Nan came to Winterfell to be Brandon's wet nurse, but he died at age three of a summer chill. Willam then took Melantha_Blackwood as his second wife, and together they had two children, Edwyle_Stark and Jocelyn_Stark. When Raymun_Redbeard, the King-Beyond-the-Wall, went over the Wall, Lord Willam called his banners and marched with Lord Harmond_Umber, with the northmen meeting the wildlings on the shores of Long Lake in 226 AC. Willam was killed and beheaded in the battle at Long Lake, but his younger brother, Artos_Stark, avenged him by slaying Raymun. Willam was succeeded as Lord of Winterfell by his son, Edwyle_Stark. Willam is buried in the crypts of Winterfell.

name: Willam Wells
bio: Willam Wells, known as Will, is a knight of House Wells from Dorne.

name: Willam Whistler
bio: Willam Whistler was a knight during the reign of King Maegor_I_Targaryen. Willam supported Prince Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I) when he rebelled against his uncle, Maegor_I_Targaryen. Aegon and Willam both died in the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye in 43 AC.

name: Willam Wythers
bio: Willam Wythers is a knight from House Wythers.

name: Willamen Frey
bio: Willamen, born Willamen Frey, is a maester of the Citadel in service at Longbow Hall to House Hunter. He is the seventeenth son of Lord Walder_Frey and the third son born in the marriage between Walder and Bethany_Rosby. Willamen took his maester's vows in 298 AC.

name: Willas Tyrell
bio: Willas Tyrell is a member of House Tyrell and the eldest son of Lord Mace_Tyrell and Lady Alerie_Hightower. He is the heir to Highgarden. Willas is the eldest son of Mace_Tyrell, Lord of Highgarden, and Lady Alerie_Hightower. During his first tourney, which Willas entered as a squire of a young age due to his father's wishes, he competed against Prince Oberyn_Martell of Dorne, who knocked Willas from his horse. Unfortunately, Willas's foot caught in his stirrup as he fell, and he pulled his horse on top of him, crushing his leg and leaving him a cripple. Though the incident fanned the flames of the considerable and historical enmity between Houses Tyrell and Martell, Willas bore Oberyn no personal ill will, and the two long remained in correspondence, sharing a passion for horseflesh. Ever since his accident, Willas has occupied himself with scholarly studies, as he cannot be a knight. Willas's grandmother, Olenna Tyrell, despite having a low opinion of her son Mace, has a high opinion of her grandson. His younger brother, Garlan_Tyrell, was plump as a boy. To protect him from a potential denigrating nickname, such as given to his uncle Garth_Tyrell, Willas was the first to call him "Garlan the Gallant". When Willas's sister Margaery_Tyrell was a girl, he would often read to her and draw her pictures of the stars. Princess Arianne_Martell once considered marrying Willas, but her father Prince Doran_Martell refused to send her to Highgarden to meet him. Nevertheless, she tried to travel there on her own, with the help of her cousin Tyene_Sand, but her uncle Prince Oberyn_Martell caught them at Vaith and brought them back to Sunspear.

name: Willem Darry
bio: Willem Darry was a knight from House Darry who served as the Red Keep's master-at-arms for King Aerys_II_Targaryen. He remained deeply loyal to House Targaryen during and after Robert's Rebellion. According to semi-canon sources, he was the brother of Ser Jonothor_Darry of the Kingsguard. When a new master-at-arms of the Red Keep needed appointing, Lord Tywin_Lannister, the Hand of the King, wished to name his brother Ser Tygett_Lannister to the post, but King Aerys_II_Targaryen chose Willem instead. When young Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen decided that he must be a knight, he went to Willem to be trained. After King Aerys II learned of the death of Prince Rhaegar at the Battle of the Trident, the king sent Queen Rhaella_Targaryen and Prince Viserys_Targaryen to Dragonstone, along with Willem. After Aerys's death in the Sack of King's Landing and Rhaella's death in childbirth, the garrison of Dragonstone considered selling the two Targaryen children, Viserys and the infant Princess Daenerys_Targaryen, to the new king, Robert_I_Baratheon. However, before the garrison could come to a decision and before Stannis_Baratheon arrived for his assault on Dragonstone, Willem and four loyal men broke into the nursery at Dragonstone, and escaped with Viserys and Daenerys under cover of darkness, sailing for the safety of the Braavosian coast. Willem and the Targaryen children then settled in Braavos, living in a house with a red door. While in Braavos, Willem was visited by Prince Oberyn_Martell, and together, with the Sealord of Braavos as witness, they signed a pact promising that Viserys Targaryen would one day wed Princess Arianne_Martell in return for the support of Dorne in winning Viserys the Iron Throne. However, Willem became ill from a wasting sickness when Daenerys was five years old (either in 289 AC or 290 AC). He slowly wasted away, dying an unknown amount of time later, after which the servants of the house stole their money, and Viserys and Daenerys were thrown into the streets to fend for themselves.

name: Willem Frey
bio: Willem Frey is a member of House Frey. The second son of Ser Cleos_Frey and Jeyne_Darry, he is a page at Ashemark.[N 1]

name: Willem Lannister
bio: Willem Lannister is a squire from House Lannister. He is the second son of Ser Kevan_Lannister and Lady Dorna_Swyft and twin to Martyn_Lannister. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Willem is portrayed by Timothy Gibbons. Lord Gawen_Westerling once suggested that Willem or his twin Martyn_Lannister marry Gawen's daughter, Jeyne_Westerling. Ser Kevan_Lannister refused this offer because he considered Jeyne of dubious blood due to the lineage of her mother, Sybell_Spicer.

name: Willem Wylde
bio: Willem Wylde was a knight from House Wylde and a member of the Kingsguard during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. When a place opened in the Kingsguard, Daeron_II_Targaryen gave the position to Ser Willem. Either Daeron did not know, or willfully ignored his father, Aegon_IV_Targaryen, promise to Ser Quentyn_Ball that he would be raised to the Kingsguard. This was one of the contributing factors to Ser Quentyn siding with Daemon_I_Blackfyre during the First Blackfyre Rebellion.

name: William Mooton
bio: William Mooton is the Lord of Maidenpool and the head of House Mooton. He is the brother of the late Ser Myles_Mooton.

name: William Tortoll
bio: Ser William Tortoll was a knight during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. William participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC.

name: Willifer
bio: Archmaester Willifer is an archmaester at the  Citadel in Oldtown.

name: Willis Fell
bio: Willis Fell was a knight from House Fell and a member of the Kingsguard during the reigns of kings Viserys_I_Targaryen and Aegon_II_Targaryen. He served as Lord Commander of Aegon_III_Targaryen's Kingsguard.

name: Willis Wode
bio: Willis Wode is a knight from House Wode in the service of House Whent. He is possibly one of the brothers Wode who are landed knights sworn to House Whent of Harrenhal. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Willis is portrayed by Ryan McKenna.

name: Willit
bio: Goodman Willit is a man-at-arms in service to House Swyft.

name: Willow (daughter of Aegon IV)
bio: Willow was the third of the four bastard children of King Aegon_IV_Targaryen and his second mistress, Megette. Willow's mother Megette was the mistress of Prince Aegon for four years. Willow was the third of four daughters. After Prince Viserys_II_Targaryen discovered this, Megette was returned to her husband in the riverlands, while Willow and her three sisters were given to the Faith to be trained as a septa.

name: Willow Pound-Stone
bio: Willow Pound-Stone was a washerwoman in the town of Bitterbridge during the Dance of the Dragons. In The Testimony of Mushroom, the dwarf asserts it was Willow who killed Prince Maelor_Targaryen by clutching him so tightly that she broke his back and crushed him to death. Afterward, Willow was hanged from the center span of the old bridge at the command of Lady Caswell.

name: Willow Witch-eye
bio: Willow, better known as Willow Witch-eye is a wildling spearwife.

name: Willow Heddle
bio: Willow Heddle is a member of the Heddle family. She has an older sister, Jeyne_Heddle. According to the Elder Brother, the sisters are nieces of Masha_Heddle, the innkeep of the inn at the crossroads. According to a semi-canon source, Masha is their great-aunt.

name: Willum
bio: Willum is a knight in the riverlands. He may possibly be the Knight of Sherrer.

name: Lord Willum
bio: Lord Willum is the head of House Willum from the Reach. He has at least two sons, Josua_Willum and Elyas_Willum.

name: Wine merchant
bio: The wineseller is a wine merchant who attempts to assassinate Daenerys_Targaryen in Vaes Dothrak.

name: Wisewoman's daughter
bio: A wisewoman's daughter was a wildling healer in a village beyond the Wall. During the time when Mance_Rayder was still a member of the Night's Watch, he was on a ranging beyond the Wall when he was attacked by a shadowcat. The shadowcat's claws shredded his cloak, arm, and back, and his other brothers of the Night's Watch were afraid he would die before he could be brought to Maester Mullin at the Shadow Tower. They brought him to a wildling village where they knew an old wisewoman lived, and though she had passed away, her daughter took care of Mance. She cleaned his wounds and sewed them up, and fed him porridge and potions until he had recovered. While he recuperated, she mended his torn cloak with swatches of her treasured red silk, as a gift to him. Upon his return to the Shadow Tower, Mance was given a new black wool cloak to replace his mended one. Ser Denys_Mallister, his commander, admonished him that the Night's Watch only wore black, and that the red-patched cloak was not uniform, now only suitable for burning. Because of this infringement of his freedoms, Mance deserted the Night's Watch to live with the free folk in the way he wished.

name: Wolmer
bio: Wolmer was a peasant in service to House Webber of Coldmoat. Wolmer defied Ser Bennis of the Brown Shield and refused to take down the dam that was being built across the Chequy Water. Ser Bennis slashed his face for his refusal.

name: Woth
bio: Woth is one of the recruits for the Night’s Watch found by Yoren and is part of the band marching north to the Wall. Woth pulled an oar on a galley once.

name: Wulfe
bio: Wulfe One-Ear is an ironborn raider in Victarion_Greyjoy's crew on the Iron Victory.

name: Wulfgar Hoare
bio: Wulfgar Hoare, known as Wulfgar the Widowmaker, was a King of the Iron Islands from House Hoare. The Hoare kings of his era were opposed by the priests of the Drowned God because of their support for the Faith of the Seven, discouraging of reaving, and promotion of trade. Wulfgar allowed the construction on Great Wyk of the first sept in the Iron Islands. His great-grandson was King Horgan_Hoare.

name: Wun Weg Wun Dar Wun
bio: Wun Weg Wun Dar Wun, nicknamed Wun Wun, is a giant who lives beyond the Wall. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Wun Wun is portrayed by Ian Whyte.

name: Wyl
bio: Wyl is a young member of the household guard of Lord Eddard_Stark at Winterfell.

name: Wyl the Whittler
bio: Wyl the Whittler is a blind inhabitant of the riverlands.

name: Wyl Waynwood
bio: Wyl Waynwood, better known as Wild Wyl Waynwood, was a member of House Waynwood, during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Wild Wyl fought as a loyalist in Lord Donnel_Arryn's van at the Battle of the Redgrass Field. There, he was slain by Daemon_I_Blackfyre.

name: Lord Wyl (conquest of Dorne)
bio: Lord Wyl was the Lord of Wyl and the head of House Wyl during the Conquest of Dorne. His given name is unknown. After the death of King Daeron_I_Targaryen in Dorne under a peace banner, Prince Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II) was imprisoned by Lord Wyl in a cage. King Baelor_I_Targaryen tried to obtain Aemon's release during his journey to Sunspear, but Lord Wyl refused. After negotiating peace with the Prince_of_Dorne_(father_of_Maron), Baelor found that Aemon's cage was now suspended over a pit of vipers. Lord Wyl obeyed the king's command to release the Dragonknight from captivity, but gave the key to Baelor instead of having his men open the cage. The Wyls are said to have wagered upon the Targaryen cousins' chances during their successful escape from the pit of venomous snakes.

name: Wyl of Wyl
bio: The Wyl of Wyl was the Lord of Wyl and head of House Wyl during the First Dornish War. He was also known as Widow-lover, and had at least one son, Walter_Wyl. His given name is unknown.[N 1] In the First Dornish War, Lord Wyl ambushed the forces of Lord Orys_Baratheon as they tried to assault the Boneway. The cunning Dornishmen launched a night raid, raining rocks, arrows and spears from above. In the end, the Boneway was blocked both before and behind, and Orys was captured by the Wyl of Wyl. Orys remained the Wyl's captive for years, until the Targaryens ransomed him and his men back in 7 AC for each man's weight in gold. When the captives were freed, however, the Wyl had their sword hands chopped off so they could not be used again against Dorne. It is said that, after the release of Orys and the other lords, the war entered a new stage, as King Aegon_I_Targaryen was by now intent on revenge. According to Maester Yandel, the Wyl of Wyl committed infamous deeds which are still remembered in Fawnton and Old Oak. According to Archmaester Gyldayn, the Wyl of Wyl turned up uninvited to the wedding of Ser Jon_Cafferen, the heir to Fawnton, and Alys_Oakheart, the daughter of Lord Oakheart, in 12 AC. He murdered Lord Oakheart and most of the guests, forcing Alys to watch as Jon was castrated. Alys and her handmaids were then raped by the Wyl and his men and sold off to a Myrish slaver. Gyldayn calls this "the most infamous act" of the First Dornish War. Lord Walter_Wyl, the son of the Widow-lover, led a host for the Vulture_King_(Aenys_I) in 37 AC during the Second Dornish War. After defeating the Dornishmen at Stonehelm, Orys Baratheon chopped off Walter's hands and feet as vengeance for his father's behavior years earlier.

name: Wyland Wyl
bio: Wyland Wyl was a nobleman of House Wyl during the regency of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. The young Princess Aliandra_Martell encouraged Dornish noblemen to raid the stormlands. In 133 AC, Ser Steffon_Connington pursued a small band of Dornish raiders across the Dornish Marches, but rode too far. Ignorant of what lied ahead, Steffon was ambushed and slain by Wyland Wyl.

name: Wylis Manderly
bio: Wylis Manderly is a knight of House Manderly. He is the eldest son and heir of Wyman_Manderly, Lord of White Harbor. Wylis is married to Leona_Woolfield and has two daughters, Wynafryd_Manderly and Wylla_Manderly.

name: Wylla
bio: Wylla was the wet nurse to Edric_Dayne, who believes her to be the mother of Jon_Snow. Wylla was the wet nurse of Edric_Dayne. She worked at Starfall for many years.

name: Wylla Fenn
bio: Wylla Fenn was a noblewoman from House Fenn. She was the mother of Lord Brandon_Stark_(son_of_Cregan)'s bastard son, Lonnel_Snow.

name: Wylla Manderly
bio: Wylla Manderly is the second daughter of Ser Wylis_Manderly and Leona_Woolfield.

name: Wylla of Wyl
bio: Wylla of Wyl was a warrior-maid of House Wyl. At the Battle by the Bloody Pool, she fought beside Yoren_Yronwood, and was turned back by King Durran the Young of the Stormlands.

name: Wyllis
bio: Wyllis was a maester of the Citadel. Wyllis journeyed to Hardhome on a Pentoshi trader and established himself among the Free Folk, under the protection of the chieftain Gorm, to write down their customs while practicing as a healer and counselor. When Gorm was murdered in a drunken brawl, Wyllis found himself in mortal danger and fled back to Oldtown, where he wrote Hardhome: An Account of Three Years Spent Beyond-the-Wall among Savages, Raiders, and Woods-witches. The year after the illuminations were done, Wyllis vanished. It was said in the Citadel that he was last seen at the docks, looking for a ship that would take him to Eastwatch-by-the-Sea

name: Wyman Manderly
bio: Wyman Manderly is the Lord of White Harbor and the head of House Manderly. He also holds several honorary titles: Warden of the White Knife, Shield of the Faith, Defender of the Dispossessed, Lord Marshal of the Mander, and Knight of the Order of the Green Hand. A widower for the past eight years, Wyman has two adult sons, Ser Wylis_Manderly and Ser Wendel_Manderly. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Wyman is portrayed by Sean Blowers. Wyman loved to ride horses as a boy and he won some small acclaim in the lists when he was a young man. However, he has grown too big to ride. Wyman participated in the Battle of the Trident during Robert's Rebellion. He granted the Wolf's Den to Ser Bartimus after the latter saved Wyman's life in the battle. Lord Manderly has been a widower since about 291 AC.

name: Wyman Webber
bio: Wyman Webber was the Lord of Coldmoat and the head of House Webber during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Wyman was good friends with Ser Eustace_Osgrey until the First Blackfyre Rebellion. He fought at the Battle of the Redgrass Field for House Targaryen. Lord Wyman died in the Great Spring Sickness. He dictated, in his will, that his daughter Rohanne_Webber would have to be married by the second anniversary of his death, or else the lands and income of Coldmoat would pass to his cousin, Ser Wendell_Webber.

name: Wynafrei Whent
bio: Wynafrei Whent is a noblewoman of House Whent and the wife of Ser Danwell_Frey. Wynafrei has no children but had many stillbirths and miscarriages.

name: Wynafryd Manderly
bio: Wynafryd Manderly is the eldest daughter of Ser Wylis_Manderly and Leona_Woolfield.

name: Wynton Stout
bio: Wynton Stout is a knight from House Stout and a ranger of the Night's Watch. Thirty years before the War of the Five Kings, Ser Wynton came within a dozen votes of being named Lord Commander of the Night's Watch. He has been a ranger in the Night's Watch for nearly eighty years. His mental state has significantly deteriorated within the last ten years.

name: Xanda Qo
bio: Xanda Qo, Princess of Sweet Lotus Vale, was a warrior woman from the Summer Isles. She was the mother of Chatana_Qo, the Arrow of Jhahar. Xanda Qo, herself being a slave for a time, ended slavery on the Summer Isles and united all the islands under her rule. She invented the famous goldenheart bows, from a wood found only on Jhala and Omboru. She also built the tall, graceful ships known as swan ships, named for their curved prows carved into the shapes of birds and beasts.

name: Xandarro Xhore
bio: Xandarro Xhore was a Qartheen pirate who lived nearly two centuries after the Doom of Valyria and one century after the  Red Death killed most people in the Basilisk Isles. Xhore was the first to raise his banner on the Basilisk Isles since the Red Death, a century after the plague swept across the Basilisk Isles. He raised a grim black fort above his anchorage on Ax Isle, using the stones he found on the islands.

name: Xaro Xhoan Daxos
bio: Xaro Xhoan Daxos is a merchant prince of Qarth. He is a member of the Thirteen, a prominent group of traders within the city. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Xaro is portrayed by Nonso Anozie as an immigrant from the Summer Isles.

name: Xhobar Qhoqua
bio: Xhobar Qhoqua, known as the Ebon Prince, was a member of the Band of Nine. He was an exile prince from the Summer Isles who found fortune in the Disputed Lands leading a sellsword company. The Band of Nine were a group of merchants, sellswords and pirates who joined together to carve kingdoms out for themselves. After conquering the Disputed Lands, they took over Tyrosh. Next, they seized the Stepstones, from where they stood poised to attack Westeros. The Band of Nine was defeated during the War of the Ninepenny Kings.

name: Xhondo Dhoru
bio: Xhondo Dhoru is a mate on the Summer Islander ship Cinnamon Wind.

name: Yandel
bio: Yandel is a maester of the Citadel. He wrote the book The World of Ice & Fire. A foundling from his birth, left in an empty stall in the Scribe's Hearth of the Citadel, Yandel was found by an acolyte who took him to the Seneschal of that year, Archmaester Edgerran. Edgerran announced that Yandel might prove of use, which when Yandel first heard the story, he believed to mean that Edgerran foresaw his destiny as a maester. It would be only later that Yandel would discover that Edgerran had been writing a treatise on the swaddling of infants at the time, and had wished to test certain theories. Yandel was given to the care of servants, and received the occasional attention of maesters. He was raised as a servant amongst the halls, chambers, and libraries. Archmaester Walgrave taught him how to read and write. Yandel desired nothing more than to become one of the maesters. He wanted to read about faraway places and long-dead men, to gaze at the stars and measure the passing of the seasons. At the age of thirteen, in 285 AC, Yandel forged the first link of his chain, and other links would follow, until he completed his studies in 292 AC. Having finished his studies, Yandel remained at the Citadel, serving the archmaesters and aiding them in all that they required. Meanwhile, he set about completing his work, The World of Ice & Fire. Archmaester Perestan reminded Yandel that Archmaester Gyldayn was working on a history of the Targaryen kings, namely, Fire & Blood, Being a History of the Targaryen Kings of Westeros. Yandel persuaded Gyldayn to permit him to use fragments of the uncompleted Fire & Blood in his The World of Ice & Fire. Yandel dedicated The World of Ice & Fire to King Robert_I_Baratheon. Upon Robert's death, however, someone had his name replaced with Joffrey_Baratheon's and later Tommen_Baratheon's. Yandel himself, for fear of angering the Lannisters, also cut out the vast majority of material on Eddard_Stark, Stannis_Baratheon and Renly_Baratheon, and several others.

name: Yandry
bio: Yandry is the master and captain of the poleboat, Shy Maid. He is married to Ysilla.

name: Yezzan zo Qaggaz
bio: Yezzan zo Qaggaz is an extremely wealthy slave trader and one of the Wise Masters of Yunkai. He is said to be the richest man in the Yellow City, and has great influence because of this. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Yezzan is portrayed by Enzo Cilenti. Yezzan has come to own a Goat_boy, a Bearded_woman, a feeble-minded Two-headed_girl from Mantarys, and a hermaphrodite named Sweets, who also sleeps with him. Yezzan used to own a giant, who he would make copulate with slave women so he could watch. However, the giant died, and Yezzan would pay much gold to acquire a new one. According to Sweets, Yezzan contracted some disease ten years ago while in Sothoryos, and has been dying ever since, "rotting from the inside out". If anyone makes him forget this even for a little while, they are well rewarded by him.

name: Ygon Farwynd
bio: Ygon Farwynd is the son of Lord Gylbert_Farwynd.

name: Ygon Oldfather
bio: Ygon Oldfather is a wildling clan chief.

name: Ygritte
bio: Ygritte is a spearwife of the free folk. She is a member of Rattleshirt's war band. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Ygritte is portrayed by Rose Leslie. Ygritte learned the song of Bael_the_Bard from her mother. Ygritte came from the same village as Longspear Ryk, whom she regards as a brother. Ygritte lost her maidenhead to a red-haired youth at a feast five years ago, but she was unimpressed with her lover. When the lover returned, Longspear broke his arm and chased him off.

name: Yna
bio: Yna, sometimes called Yna One-Eye, is a prostitute at the Happy Port brothel in Braavos. Of all the prostitutes in the Happy Port, Yna has known the Sailor's_Wife the longest. Yna once tasted the blood of the Sailor's Wife, and saw that her first husband, who was lost at sea years ago, is dead. The Sailor's Wife still prays for her husband's return, but Yna prays it never happens.

name: Ynys Yronwood
bio: Ynys Yronwood is the eldest daughter of Lord Anders_Yronwood. She is married to Ser Ryon_Allyrion, the heir to Godsgrace, with whom she has children, at least two sons. Prince Quentyn_Martell became smitten with Ynys when he first came to Yronwood to be fostered, but he never spoke of his feelings to her.

name: Yohn Farwynd
bio: Yohn Farwynd is the son of Lord Gylbert_Farwynd.

name: Yohn Royce
bio: Yohn Royce, known as Bronze Yohn, is the Lord of Runestone and the head of the senior branch of House Royce. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Yohn is portrayed by Rupert Vansittart. Yohn took part in the tourney at Harrenhal, where he was defeated by Prince Rhaegar_Targaryen. Yohn also participated in the tourney at Lannisport, where he was defeated by the eventual champion, Lord Jorah_Mormont. He defeated Thoros_of_Myr in the melee at the tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day with a common mace, after the fire of Thoros's sword had guttered out. Lord Tywin_Lannister offered his son Tyrion_Lannister for marriage within House Royce, but was rejected. When Yohn's youngest son, Ser Waymar_Royce, joined the Night's Watch in 296 AC, his father escorted him north. They stayed in Winterfell on their way, where Yohn participated in hunts, and spoke with Lady Catelyn_Stark. Bronze Yohn practiced sparring with Lord Eddard_Stark and Ser Rodrik_Cassel, defeating one and then the other.

name: Yorbert Royce
bio: Yorbert Royce was the Lord of Runestone and the head of House Royce during the late reign of King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen. Lord Yorbert Royce was the regent and the Lord Protector of the Vale during the minority of Lady Jeyne_Arryn, after her Lord_Arryn_(father_of_Jeyne) and elder brothers were slain by the Stone Crows. In that capacity, he attended the Great Council of 101 AC in her stead. The text does not state the candidate Yorbert supported in the final vote at the council, however.[N 1] Rhea_Royce, the heir to Runestone, was married to Prince Daemon_Targaryen in 97 AC. By the time of the accession of King Viserys_I_Targaryen in 103 AC, Rhea had succeeded Yorbert as Lady of Runestone, indicating that he had passed away.

name: Yoren
bio: Yoren is a sworn brother of the Night's Watch. A recruiter, or wandering crow, he travels the Seven Kingdoms, and is responsible for collecting recruits and prisoners who would take the black. He then escorts them to Castle Black for training. In the television adaptation Game of Thrones, Yoren is portrayed by Francis Magee. Yoren suffered a shoulder injury that made it impossible for him to fight well any longer. He has spent the past thirty years traveling the Seven Kingdoms and recruiting for the Night's Watch, losing only three recruits in that time. His travels frequently bring him to Winterfell, thus he knows the members of House Stark and has a certain loyalty to them. Yoren brought Chett to the Wall after the criminal was judged by Walder_Rivers. Jeor_Mormont, Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, would be willing to have Yoren or Conwy collect Craster's sons, but the Night's Watch instead allows Craster to give his sons to the wood so that Craster's Keep remains a refuge beyond the Wall.

name: Yoren Yronwood
bio: Yoren Yronwood was a member of House Yronwood. At the Battle by the Bloody Pool, Yoren fought alongside the warrior-maid Wylla_of_Wyl, and was turned back by King Durran the Young of the Stormlands.

name: Yorick V Yronwood
bio: Yorick V Yronwood was the Lord of Yronwood and the last King of Yronwood from House Yronwood in Dorne, until his defeat during Nymeria's War. As the most powerful of the Dornish kings of his time, Yorick V styled himself as the Bloodroyal, Lord of Yronwood, Warden of the Stone Way, Knight of the Wells, King of Redmarch, King of the Greenbelt, and King of the Dornish. The Kings of Yronwood ruled half of Dorne, and Yorick was the richest and most powerful of the Dornish kings at the start of Nymeria's War, when Lord Mors_Martell and Princess Nymeria warred to conquer Dorne. The Martells met and subdued all the petty kings of Dorne, until only Yorick V remained, the greatest of the Dornish kings. For nine years, the Yronwoods warred against the Martells and their supporters; Yorick's own bannermen included the Jordaynes and the Wyls, as well as the Blackmonts and Qorgyles. In the Third Battle of the Boneway. Yorick V himself slew Mors and Princess Nymeria assumed command of the Martell armies. Two years of battle followed, though ultimately Yorick V was forced to submit and bend the knee to Nymeria, and the Yronwoods became bannermen of House Martell. After his defeat, Yorick was sent to the Wall to join the Night's Watch, the same fate that befell the five kings that submitted before him.

name: Yorkel
bio: Yorkel was a guardsmen in service to House Targaryen during the reign of King Daeron_II_Targaryen. Yorkel was among the guardsmen that accompanied Prince Aerion_Targaryen to Ashford for the tourney. He was one of the guardsmen who helped Prince Aerion destroy Tanselle's stall after Aerion took issue with their skit involving a dragon.

name: Yorko Terys
bio: Yorko Terys is the oldest son of Ternesio_Terys. He works aboard the Titan's Daughter.

name: Yormwell
bio: Yormwell was a maester in service of Prince Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II). After Ser Humfrey_Hardyng's leg was broken when Prince Aerion_Targaryen killed his horse during a tilt at the tourney at Ashford Meadow, Prince Baelor sent his own maester to tend to him. Yormwell treated Prince Maekar_I_Targaryen after the trial of seven. Prince Baelor intended to send Yormwell to treat Ser Duncan_the_Tall's injuries when he finished with his younger brother but died before he could.

name: Yorrick
bio: Maester Yorrick was a maester of the Citadel who served at White Harbor. He wrote the book Wed to the Sea, Being an Account of the History of White Harbor from Its Earliest Days.

name: Yorwyck VI Royce
bio: Yorwyck VI Royce was a Bronze King of House Royce of Runestone who lived during the Andal invasion. He claimed the Runic Crown when his sire died in battle against House Shett. King Yorwyck was embroiled in a war against Osgood_III_Shett, ruler of Gulltown and King of the True Men. A redoubtable foe, Yorwyck VI defeated the Shetts in several battles and drove them inside their town walls. Osgood turned to the Andals and made a marriage alliance with Ser Gerold_Grafton_(king), securing a victory against King Yorwyck, but being betrayed afterward. Yorwyck would lead the Royces to several notable victories over the Andals, at one point destroying seven longships that landed on his shores and decorating the walls of Runestone with the heads of the captains and crews. His son succeeded him as Bronze King.

name: Yrma Peake
bio: Yrma Peake, known as Yrma of the Golden Bowl, was a Lady of Starpike. She was an illustrious and legendary ancestor of House Peake.

name: Ysabel
bio: Ysabel was a septa of the Faith of the Seven during the reign of Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen and one of the "Wise Women". Ysabel was part of the retinue sent to Dragonstone by Queen Regent Alyssa_Velaryon, alongside novices Lyra and Edyth. She was sent to see to Alysanne's religious instruction and expected to impress upon Alysanne, and perhaps even Jaehaerys, that the Faith considered incest an abomination so the couple would then consent to the annullment of their marriage. After King Jaehaerys_I_Targaryen wed his sister, Alysanne_Targaryen, Jaehaerys sent seven preachers to walk the realm and win the small folk over to the marriage, not with swords, but with words. Septa Ysabel was among these Seven Speakers. They traveled the realm alone, walking from village to village, telling the smallfolk of Jaehaerys's wisdom and Alysanne's kindness. When challenged on the issue of incest, they would spread the Doctrine of Exceptionalism.

name: Ysabel Staunton
bio: Ysabel Staunton was a member of House Staunton during the reign of King Aegon_III_Targaryen. In 133 AC, the Maiden's Day Ball was held at the Red Keep to find a second wife and new queen for King Aegon_III_Targaryen. Many maidens across Westeros came to King's Landing in hopes of marrying the young king. Lyra was one of the many maidens who suffered what was called the "Maiden's Day curse" by being injured or scandalized after the ball was announced. In Ysabel's case, her supposed fondness for wine was gossiped about. It is possible that this was a rumor fabricated by Lord Unwin_Peake, the Hand of the King, who wished to promote his Myrielle_Peake as Aegon III's second queen.

name: Ysilla
bio: Ysilla is Yandry's wife. She is one of the crew of the  Shy Maid. She is an orphan of the Greenblood come home to Mother Rhoyne.

name: Ysilla Royce
bio: Ysilla Royce is a noblewoman of House Royce and is the daughter of Lord Yohn_Royce of Runestone.

name: Yurkhaz zo Yunzak
bio: Yurkhaz zo Yunzak is a Yunkish noble and the Supreme Commander of the Armies and Allies of Yunkai. Yurkhaz is an old Yunkish hero. He owns a group of Yunkish castrati slaves who sing songs in the ancient tongues of the Old Empire of Ghis.

name: Zachery Frey
bio: Zachery Frey is a member of House Frey, the eldest son of Ser Tytos_Frey and Zhoe_Blanetree. He is studying at the Sept of Oldtown.

name: Zahrina
bio: Zahrina is a slave trader from Slaver's Bay. She purchases cheap slaves for the fighting pits, expecting them to be slain by heroes. She is also either the owner or proprietor of the Purple Lotus, a winesink in Meereen.

name: Zarabelo
bio: Archmaester Zarabelo is an archmaester at the  Citadel in Oldtown.

name: Zeggo
bio: Zeggo was a Dothraki khal who lived during the Century of Blood. Zeggo sacked the city of Sarys, the last city of the Kingdom of Sarnor to fall to the Dothraki.

name: Zei
bio: Zei is a whore in Mole's Town.

name: Zekko
bio: It is said every three to four years Zekko visits Qohor to get a sack of gold so his khalasar will turn away.

name: Zhako
bio: Zhako was a Dothraki khal who lived during the Century of Blood. He was one of the four khals that fought in the Field of Crows. Khal Zhako joined forces with Khals Haro, Loso, and Qano to fight against High King Mazor_Alexi, who had united all of the Sarnori lesser kings to defend their homeland against the Dothraki hordes. The battle took place halfway between Sarnath and the ruins of Kasath. The Sarnori chariots smashed the center of the Dothraki horde. Haro was trampled and died, while his riders fled, chased by Alexi and his cavalry. However, the escape was feigned and Haro's riders turned back, raining arrows on the Sarnori, while the other khals surrounded the Tall Men on all sides, with Zhako's khalasar attacking from the south.

name: Zharaq zo Loraq
bio: Zharaq zo Loraq, known as Zharaq the Liberator, was a famous Ghiscari citizen of the city of Meereen. Zharaq is an ancestor of Hizdahr_zo_Loraq.

name: Zhea
bio: Zhea, also known as Zhea the Barren, Zhea the Cruel, and Zhea Zorseface, was the first jhattar of the Jogos Nhai. Zhea was a woman of the Jogos Nhai who chose the life of a male warrior. When a thousand rivals unified to fight against the massive army of Lo_Bu, the God-Emperor of the Golden Empire of Yi Ti which threatened the hunt down the Jogos Nhai into extinction, they raised up Zhea as a jhattar. When Lo Bu divided his massive armies into thirteen hosts, Zhea isolated each of the emperor's armies, slaying their scouts and foragers, starving them, denying them water, leading them into wastelands and traps, and so each of the YiTish armies was destroyed. Finally his riders descended on Lo Bu's own host. Lo Bu's severed head was presented to Zhea, who commanded that the flesh be stripped from the bone and his skull be dipped in gold and made into his drinking cup, which has passed from jhattar to jhattar through generations. To this day, Zhea is remembered in the Golden Empire, where mothers whisper his name to frighten unruly children into obedience.

name: Zhoe Blanetree
bio: Zhoe Blanetree is a noblewoman of House Blanetree and the wife of Ser Tytos_Frey, a grandson of Lord Walder_Frey. They have two children together: Zia_Frey and Zachery_Frey.

name: Zia Frey
bio: Zia Frey is the daughter of Ser Tytos_Frey and Zhoe_Blanetree.

name: Zollo
bio: Zollo, better known as Zollo the Fat, is a Dothraki member of the Brave Companions.
